# Narrative Classification of Crypto-Exposed Firms

This document reports the results of a systematic narrative classification of crypto-exposed firms. Each firm is assigned fractional weights across six transmission mechanism categories -- crypto holders, miners, financial services, non-financial services, infrastructure providers, and unclassified -- based on evidence retrieved from earnings call transcripts, SEC filings, and financial news covering January 2017 to December 2024. Classification is performed by a large language model (GPT-5) instructed via a structured prompt (`firm_instructions.md`) and executed through the OpenAI API using the pipeline script `GO_firm-GPT.py`. The full classification methodology, category definitions, weighting rules, and output schema are documented in the accompanying instructions file.

Executed: 2026-04-07 11:04 UTC | Model: GPT-5

---

## HIVE DIGITAL TECHNOLOGIES

* Rank: 1 | Expo: 19.97 | id_str: CA4339211035
* Weight summary: holders 0.2, miners 0.8, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 5 | AssetShare: 4
* Primary sources: 40-F/MD&A (FY2024), Q4 FY2024 earnings call, late-2024 company press release, 2021 financial media coverage.

### Firm Overview
HIVE Digital Technologies Ltd. is a Canada-based, Nasdaq- and TSX-V–listed company that operates data centers focused primarily on Bitcoin mining, with a growing high‑performance computing initiative. The company rebranded from HIVE Blockchain Technologies Ltd. in July 2023 and, in fiscal year ended March 31, 2024, remained predominantly a Bitcoin miner. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1720424/000106299324012996/exhibit99-1.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
- Miners: HIVE reports Bitcoin production, hashrate and mining economics as its core business; in FY2024 it generated $111.0 million from digital currency mining versus $3.4 million from HPC services. Management discusses mining margins, network difficulty and BTC price sensitivity on earnings calls. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1720424/000106299324012996/exhibit99-3.htm))  
- Holders: The company carries material digital currency balances on its balance sheet (mainly Bitcoin) and management has articulated a HODL orientation, with statements about “holding as much Bitcoin as possible” and growing the HODL balance. A late‑2024 press release also highlights growth in Bitcoin holdings to 2,604 BTC. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/HIVE/documents/transcripts/q4-2024))  
- Investor base amplifier: Financial media has repeatedly framed HIVE as a crypto‑miner proxy whose shares move with crypto prices (e.g., outperformance as ether hit a record in 2021), supporting the view that crypto sentiment transmits into HIVE’s equity. ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/10/29/hive-blockchain-outperforms-crypto-miners-as-ether-reaches-all-time-high))

### Weight Assignment
Mining is the company’s dominant economic exposure over 2017–2024, with clear, recurring disclosures of BTC production, hashrate and mining revenues; therefore miners = 0.8. Management’s explicit HODL policy and substantial BTC inventories (thousands of coins and over $160 million in digital currencies as of March 31, 2024) justify a non‑trivial holders weight; holders = 0.2. There is no evidence of operating a crypto exchange, custody, or other financial services (finserv = 0.0), and no third‑party crypto infrastructure provision beyond operating its own sites (infrastr = 0.0). Any residual exposure via general services is immaterial (bizserv = 0.0). ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1720424/000106299324012996/exhibit99-3.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
HIVE began pivoting GPUs toward HPC/AI from 2023 onward, but within the 2017–2024 sample, HPC revenue remained small relative to mining revenue, so it does not alter the mechanism mix. Historical ETH mining ended after the September 2022 Merge but does not change the “miners” classification. Asset and treasury balances, as well as HODL stance, can vary materially with BTC prices through time. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1720424/000106299324012996/exhibit99-3.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q4 FY2024 (June 2024), transcript: “our cash position stood at $9.7 million… with an additional $161.6 million in digital currencies comprised almost entirely of Bitcoin.” — Fintool (Q4 2024 transcript) (https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/HIVE/documents/transcripts/q4-2024)  
- Q4 FY2024 (June 2024), transcript: “staying green and mining and holding as much Bitcoin as possible.” — Fintool (Q4 2024 transcript) (https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/HIVE/documents/transcripts/q4-2024)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- FY2024 MD&A (June 24, 2024): “Revenue from digital currency mining was $111.0 million… [HPC] contributed $3.4 million.” — SEC EDGAR Exhibit 99.3 (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1720424/000106299324012996/exhibit99-3.htm)
- FY2024 MD&A (June 24, 2024): “Digital currencies at March 31, 2024 mainly consisted of 2,287 Bitcoin… [Digital currencies] $161.645 million; TOTAL ASSETS $307.582 million.” — SEC EDGAR Exhibit 99.3 (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1720424/000106299324012996/exhibit99-3.htm)
- Annual Information Form (June 24, 2024): Company description; name change context — SEC EDGAR Exhibit 99.1 (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1720424/000106299324012996/exhibit99-1.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- Oct. 29, 2021: “Hive Blockchain outperforms… as ether hit a new record,” linking share moves to crypto prices — CoinDesk (https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/10/29/hive-blockchain-outperforms-crypto-miners-as-ether-reaches-all-time-high)
- Nov. 2024: “growth in Bitcoin holdings to 2,604 BTC… revenue $22.6m (mining $20.8m; HPC $1.9m)” — Company press release (https://www.hivedigitaltechnologies.com/news/hive-digital-technologies-ltd-reports-strong-second-quarter-fy2025-financial-results-226-million-in-revenue-56-million-adjusted-ebitda-and-growth-in-bitcoin-holdings-to-2604/)

</small>

## BAKKT HOLDINGS A

* Rank: 2 | Expo: 18.61 | id_str: US05759B3050
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 5 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Form 10-K (FY2023), Q4 2023 earnings call, 2023–2024 company press releases, 2021 financial media coverage.

### Firm Overview
Bakkt Holdings Inc. is a U.S.-based digital asset platform founded by Intercontinental Exchange that provides crypto trading and custody services primarily through B2B/B2B2C arrangements with fintechs, brokers, and institutions. The company completed the acquisition of Apex Crypto on April 1, 2023 and subsequently emphasized institutional crypto capabilities and collaborative custody partnerships. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1820302/000182030224000056/0001820302-24-000056-index.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
- Financial services (finserv): Management describes “robust custody and trading solutions” and reports crypto trading activity, assets under custody, and gross crypto services revenues on earnings calls. The FY2023 Form 10‑K shows crypto services revenues of $726.99 million versus $53.15 million in net loyalty revenues, and defines assets under custody of $701.6 million at December 31, 2023. The firm also pursued institutional-focused offerings such as collaborative custody with Unchained. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/BKKT/documents/transcripts/q4-2023))
- Investor base amplifier: Media repeatedly highlighted sharp BKKT share moves alongside crypto-related partnership and product news (e.g., Mastercard/Fiserv), indicating that crypto sentiment transmits into the stock. ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/10/25/bakkt-shares-surge-180-after-pacts-with-mastercard-fiserv-for-crypto-payments?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Bakkt’s business model in 2017–2024 centers on providing crypto financial services: custody, trading, and related B2B/B2B2C infrastructure. Earnings calls and the 2023 Form 10‑K quantify crypto trading volumes, assets under custody and gross crypto services revenues, while press releases document the Apex Crypto acquisition and the pivot to institutional custody offerings. Given the direct handling/custody of digital assets and fee income from trading/custody, finserv = 1.0 and all other mechanisms = 0.0. The high profile, crypto‑news‑sensitive trading episodes justify invbase = 1. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/BKKT/documents/transcripts/q4-2023))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Bakkt records crypto revenues on a gross basis with offsetting “crypto costs,” which inflates total revenue versus net contribution; however, the mechanism classification (finserv) is unaffected. Assets under custody are customer assets and not on-balance‑sheet crypto holdings, supporting AssetShare = 0. No evidence indicates mining or strategic treasury HODL during 2017–2024. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1820302/000182030224000056/bakkt-20231231.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q4 2023 (Mar 25, 2024): “We have robust custody and trading solutions that appeal to both institutional and retail clients.” — Fintool (transcript) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/BKKT/documents/transcripts/q4-2023))
- Q4 2023 (Mar 25, 2024): “Gross crypto services revenue of $199.4 million… [and] assets under custody… surpassing $1 billion [in 2024 to date].” — Fintool (transcript) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/BKKT/documents/transcripts/q4-2023))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- 2023 Form 10‑K (filed Mar 25, 2024): “Crypto services $726,988; Loyalty services, net $53,148; Total revenues $780,136.” — SEC EDGAR ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1820302/000182030224000056/bakkt-20231231.htm))
- 2023 Form 10‑K (filed Mar 25, 2024): “Assets under custody were $701.6 million as of December 31, 2023.” — SEC EDGAR ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1820302/000182030224000056/bakkt-20231231.htm))
- Apr 1, 2023: “Acquisition of Apex Crypto closed… bringing ~5.8 million crypto‑enabled accounts to Bakkt.” — Company news release ([investors.bakkt.com](https://investors.bakkt.com/news/news-details/2023/Bakkt-Reports-First-Quarter-2023-Results/default.aspx?utm_source=openai))
- Feb 13, 2023: “Sunset consumer app to focus on scalable B2B2C solutions.” — Business Wire (company release) ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20230212005012/en/Bakkt-to-Enhance-Focus-on-Scalable-Business-to-Business-to-Consumer-Solutions-and-Sunset-Consumer-App?utm_source=openai))
- Mar 8, 2024: “Bakkt Now Available Through Unchained’s Collaborative Custody Network.” — Company news release ([investors.bakkt.com](https://investors.bakkt.com/news/news-details/2024/Bakkt-Now-Available-Through-Unchaineds-Collaborative-Custody-Network/default.aspx?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Oct 25–26, 2021: BKKT shares “surge 180%/skyrocket” after Mastercard and Fiserv crypto partnerships — CoinDesk; Bloomberg; Fortune ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/10/25/bakkt-shares-surge-180-after-pacts-with-mastercard-fiserv-for-crypto-payments?utm_source=openai))

</small>

## COINBASE GLOBAL A

* Rank: 3 | Expo: 18.55 | id_str: US19260Q1076
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 5 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Form 10-K (FY2023), Q4 2023 earnings call, Q2 2023 earnings call, Q3 2024 earnings call, 2024 financial media coverage on spot Bitcoin ETFs and price sensitivity.

### Firm Overview
Coinbase Global, Inc. is a U.S.-based cryptocurrency platform that provides trading, custody, and related services to retail and institutional clients; it listed on Nasdaq in April 2021. Its business model centers on transaction fees and a growing “subscriptions and services” segment including custodial fees, staking/blockchain rewards, stablecoin-related revenue, and interest income. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coinbase?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
- Financial services: Management and filings consistently describe Coinbase as providing trading, custody, and crypto financial infrastructure, with revenues reported as “transaction revenue” and “subscription and services revenue” (including stablecoin, custodial fees, blockchain rewards, and interest). ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1679788/000167978824000022/coin-20231231.htm))
- ETF custody: In January 2024, U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs launched and multiple outlets report Coinbase serves as custodian for the majority (commonly cited as 8 of 11), reinforcing its direct handling and safekeeping of digital assets for financial products. ([forbes.com](https://www.forbes.com/sites/bethkindig/2024/01/25/coinbase-robinhood-examining-the-impact-of-spot-bitcoin-etfs/?utm_source=openai))
- Price sensitivity/investor base: Media regularly links COIN’s share moves to Bitcoin’s rallies or broader crypto news, underscoring that crypto sentiment transmits into the stock. ([finance.yahoo.com](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/hut-8-coinbase-outperform-crypto-153643731.html?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Coinbase directly handles digital assets through its core exchange, custody, staking, and prime brokerage services, and it earns fee income from these activities; therefore, finserv = 1.0. Filings quantify transaction revenues alongside subscriptions and services (stablecoin revenue, blockchain rewards, custodial fees, interest income), and earnings calls repeatedly tie performance to crypto trading volumes/volatility and product expansion. The company’s role as custodian to multiple spot Bitcoin ETFs further solidifies classification as financial services. No evidence in 2017–2024 suggests proprietary treasury HODL or mining operations, so other mechanism weights are 0.0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1679788/000167978824000022/coin-20231231.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Customer crypto under custody can appear on the balance sheet as safeguarding assets with offsetting obligations, but these are not Coinbase’s proprietary holdings; this supports AssetShare = 0 despite large reported “assets under custody.” Also, while ETF custody expands activity, some analysts note mixed long‑term revenue implications; neither issue alters the finserv classification. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1679788/000167978824000022/coin-20231231.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q4 2023 (Feb 15, 2024): “This resulted in Q4 transaction revenue of $529 million, up 83%… driven by excitement around Bitcoin ETF approvals…” — The Motley Fool (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2024/02/15/coinbase-global-coin-q4-2023-earnings-call-transcr/)
- Q2 2023 (Aug 3, 2023): “In Q2, crypto volatility… reached multiyear lows,” yet Coinbase remained “financially healthy with our second quarter of positive adjusted EBITDA.” — Coinbase IR transcript (https://investor.coinbase.com/files/doc_financials/2023/q2/Coinbase-Q2-23-Earnings-Call-Transcript.pdf)
- Q3 2024 (Oct 30–31, 2024): Priorities for 2024 included “driving revenue” and “driving utility… beyond just trading.” — Coinbase IR transcript (https://investor.coinbase.com/files/doc_financials/2024/q3/Q3-24-Earnings-Call-Transcript.pdf)

**Filings (10-K / shareholder materials):**
- 2023 Form 10‑K (filed Feb 2024): “We generate revenue from transactions, subscription and services…” and detailed subscription components (stablecoin, blockchain rewards, custodial fees, interest). — SEC EDGAR (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1679788/000167978824000022/coin-20231231.htm)
- 2023 Form 10‑K (filed Feb 2024): Subscription and services revenue table (e.g., stablecoin revenue $694.2m in 2023). — SEC EDGAR (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1679788/000167978824000022/coin-20231231.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- Jan 11, 2024: “Coinbase… custodian of choice… hired as custodian for 8 of the 11 Bitcoin ETFs approved by the SEC.” — CNBC (https://www.cnbc.com/2024/01/11/coinbase-robinhood-shares-rise-as-bitcoin-etf-approval-adds-credibility-to-cryptocurrency-industry.html)
- Jan 25, 2024: “Coinbase… stands to earn custodial fees by serving as custodian for 8 of the 11 approved ETFs.” — Forbes (https://www.forbes.com/sites/bethkindig/2024/01/25/coinbase-robinhood-examining-the-impact-of-spot-bitcoin-etfs/)
- May 2, 2024: Q1 revenue beat “with a boost from higher bitcoin prices”; note ETF custody across issuers. — Axios (https://www.axios.com/2024/05/02/coinbase-first-quarter-revenue-bitcoin)
- Dec 5, 2024: Crypto-exposed stocks, including Coinbase, rally as Bitcoin tops $100k. — CoinDesk (https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2024/12/05/u-s-crypto-stocks-surge-in-pre-market-trading-as-bitcoin-tops-100-k)
- Aug 16, 2023: Approval to offer federally regulated crypto futures to eligible U.S. customers. — AP News (https://apnews.com/article/1dc34b193152a62f0cf32b69e3453974)

</small>

## DEFI TECHNOLOGIES

* Rank: 4 | Expo: 18.19 | id_str: CA2449161025
* Weight summary: holders 0.2, miners 0.0, finserv 0.7, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.1, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 5 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: Financial statements and MD&A (FY2024), Q1 2024 earnings call, 2024 press releases on treasury/validator/ETPs, 2024 financial media coverage

### Firm Overview
DeFi Technologies Inc. is a Canada-based financial technology company whose core operations include issuing crypto exchange-traded products in Europe through its subsidiary Valour and operating digital-asset trading and related activities. In 2024 the company also implemented a bitcoin treasury strategy alongside other crypto-native initiatives.

### Exposure Channels Identified
- Financial services: The company’s subsidiary Valour issues exchange-traded products tracking digital assets (e.g., BTC, ETH, SOL, HBAR, CORE), earns management fees, and reports staking/lending income and hedging activity tied to those products. Company disclosures also describe a specialized arbitrage trading desk (“DeFi Alpha”) and an OTC/liquidity business (Stillman Digital). ([defi.tech](https://www.defi.tech/files/DeFi-Technologies-2024%20Financial-Statements-and-MD%26A.pdf))
- Holders: On June 10, 2024 DeFi Technologies adopted Bitcoin as its primary treasury reserve asset and purchased 110 BTC initially, later increasing holdings to 204.34 BTC by July 18, 2024. ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/defi-technologies-provides-monthly-corporate-update-announces-bitcoin-treasury-strategy-302167973.html?utm_source=openai))
- Infrastructure: In June 2024 the company launched a Core Chain validator node to participate in network consensus and staking, including staking 1,498 BTC in connection with yield-bearing product initiatives. ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2024/06/11/2896749/0/en/DeFi-Technologies-Announces-Launch-of-Core-Chain-Validator-Node-to-Participate-in-Network-Consensus-and-Staking-Stakes-1-498-BTC.html?utm_source=openai))
- Investor base sensitivity: Media coverage noted the stock rallying immediately following the bitcoin-treasury announcement, underscoring sentiment linkage to crypto developments. ([cryptoslate.com](https://cryptoslate.com/insights/defi-technologies-shares-spike-15-in-a-day-after-it-adopts-bitcoin-treasury-strategy/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 0.7 reflects that exchange‑traded products, associated hedging/staking, arbitrage trading, and OTC liquidity are the central, explicitly revenue‑generating lines described across filings and releases. Holders = 0.2 captures the explicit bitcoin treasury policy and balance-sheet crypto owned by the company (distinct from assets held to back ETPs). Infrastr = 0.1 recognizes the Core Chain validator/staking operations supporting product design and yielding activity but not constituting mining or third‑party hosting. No evidence supports mining or non‑financial services exposure in 2017–2024; unclass = 0.0. ([defi.tech](https://www.defi.tech/files/DeFi-Technologies-2024%20Financial-Statements-and-MD%26A.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Consolidated statements include large “digital assets” balances with offsetting “ETP holders payable,” reflecting product hedging/underlying and not proprietary HODL; this is treated as finserv rather than holders when assessing mechanism mix and AssetShare. Validator activity is classified as infrastructure exposure here, though it is also tightly coupled to product strategy; no direct PoW mining was identified. One transcript source is via an aggregator; however, it squarely corroborates the product/hedging model and is backed by company filings and press releases. ([defi.tech](https://www.defi.tech/files/DeFi-Technologies-2024%20Financial-Statements-and-MD%26A.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q1 2024 (May 21, 2024): “There are mark
…to‑market adjustments… it’s actually not our loss… it’s the average price of the Bitcoin we bought for our clients to hedge out the ETP exposure…” — EarningsCall.ai (https://www.earningscall.ai/stock/transcript/DEFTF-2024-Q1)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- FY2024 Financial Statements and MD&A (dated 2024): “On June 10, 2024, the Company announced it has adopted Bitcoin as its primary treasury reserve asset and has purchased 110 Bitcoin to initiate this strategy.” — DeFi Technologies (https://www.defi.tech/files/DeFi-Technologies-2024%20Financial-Statements-and-MD%26A.pdf)
- FY2024 Financial Statements and MD&A: “On June 11, 2024, the Company announced it has deployed a Core Chain validator node… The Company will also stake 1,498 Bitcoin on the Core Chain.” — DeFi Technologies (https://www.defi.tech/files/DeFi-Technologies-2024%20Financial-Statements-and-MD%26A.pdf)
- FY2024 Financial Statements and MD&A: Listings/launches of multiple Valour ETPs in 2024 (ICP, TON, Chainlink, Hedera, CORE; Short BTC; Blue Chip digital asset basket) and focus on AUM growth, staking/lending income and hedging activities. — DeFi Technologies (https://www.defi.tech/files/DeFi-Technologies-2024%20Financial-Statements-and-MD%26A.pdf)
- Valour financial statements (Q1 2024): Fair value disclosures of ETPs and underlying digital assets backing certificates on Nordic Growth Market and Germany. — Valour (https://www.valour.com/files/Valour_March_31_2024_FS.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Apr 18, 2024: “Valour… launches the first Short Spot Bitcoin ETP in the Nordics.” — GlobeNewswire (https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2024/04/18/2865277/0/en/DeFi-Technologies-Subsidiary-Valour-Inc-Launches-the-First-Short-Spot-Bitcoin-ETP-in-the-Nordics.html)
- May 15, 2024: “Valour introduces the world’s first yield‑bearing Bitcoin ETP in collaboration with Core Foundation.” — Valour newsroom (https://www.valour.com/en/newsroom/valour-inc-launches-worlds-first-yield-bearing-bitcoin-btc-etp-in-collaboration-with-core-foundation-offering-investors-exposure-to-bitcoin-with-a-5-65-yield)
- Jun 18, 2024: Yield‑bearing BTC ETP expands to Börse Frankfurt. — PR Newswire (https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/defi-technologies-subsidiary-valour-inc-introduces-worlds-first-and-only-yield-bearing-bitcoin-btc-etp-in-collaboration-with-core-foundation-to-german-investors-on-borse-frankfurt-offering-exposure-to-bitcoin-with-a-5-65-yie-302175268.html)
- Jun 10, 2024: “DeFi Technologies… has adopted Bitcoin as its primary treasury reserve asset and has purchased 110 Bitcoins to initiate this strategy.” — PR Newswire (https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/defi-technologies-provides-monthly-corporate-update-announces-bitcoin-treasury-strategy-302167973.html)
- Jun 11, 2024: “Launch of Core Chain validator… participate in network consensus and staking; stakes 1,498 BTC.” — GlobeNewswire (https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2024/06/11/2896749/0/en/DeFi-Technologies-Announces-Launch-of-Core-Chain-Validator-Node-to-Participate-in-Network-Consensus-and-Staking-Stakes-1-498-BTC.html)
- Aug/Sep 2024: Stock rallied on trading‑desk profits; company reiterated core business is Valour ETPs, highlighting investor sensitivity to crypto conditions. — Decrypt (https://decrypt.co/240335/bitwise-stock-rockets-bitcoin-treasury)
- Jul 2024: Q2 2024 results: record revenue; DeFi Alpha arbitrage trading desk generated over US$82m in Q2; treasury holdings updated. — PR Newswire (https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/defi-technologies-inc-announces-q2-2024-financial-results-achieving-its-strongest-financial-quarter-to-date-record-revenues-of-c-133-2-million-us-98-0-million-and-128-2-million-us-94-4-million-for-the-three-and-six-months-ended-june-30-2024-863755235.html)
- Jun 2024: Coverage noted share price jump after bitcoin‑treasury adoption, reinforcing sentiment linkage. — Cointelegraph (https://cointelegraph.com/news/defi-technologies-adopts-bitcoin-treasury-strategy)

</small>

## OLB GROUP

* Rank: 5 | Expo: 17.32 | id_str: US67086U4067
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.4, finserv 0.6, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2022–2023), Q4 2023 earnings call, Business Wire press releases (2021–2022)

### Firm Overview
The OLB Group, Inc. is a U.S.-based fintech company listed on Nasdaq that provides merchant services and payment processing via its SecurePay and OmniSoft platforms. In addition to payments, the company operates a bitcoin mining business through its wholly owned subsidiary DMint, with activities disclosed in segment reporting in recent filings.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Crypto financial services (finserv): OLB has enabled cryptocurrency acceptance for merchants through its SecurePay gateway and OmniSoft POS platform, including support for Mastercard’s cryptocurrency processing and crypto-to-fiat conversion functionality. The company disclosed efforts toward money transmission licensing (via OLBit) to support these activities. ([investors.olb.com](https://investors.olb.com/olb-group-announces-support-for-mastercard-cryptocurrency-processing-business-wire/))
Crypto mining (miners): Through DMint, OLB has operated ASIC-based bitcoin mining, scaling to approximately 1,000 miners and relocating operations to a lower-cost Tennessee site; mining revenue is reported as a separate segment. Management also discussed active rigs, per-Bitcoin mining costs, and plans to expand capacity on a Q4 2023 call. ([materials.proxyvote.com](https://materials.proxyvote.com/Approved/67086U/20231130/10K_556865.PDF))
Investor base sensitivity: Coverage documented the stock nearly doubling following the company’s announcement that it was ready to process Mastercard bitcoin payments, indicating equity-price linkage to crypto headlines. ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/11/02/olb-group-surges-90-after-saying-its-ready-for-mastercard-bitcoin-payments))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 0.6 reflects that OLB’s core business is merchant acquiring/payment services and that crypto acceptance (wallet support, crypto-to-fiat settlement, and Mastercard crypto processing readiness) is explicitly integrated into its payments stack and licensing roadmap. Miners = 0.4 captures the ongoing DMint bitcoin mining operations described by management and broken out in filings, though mining revenue remained a small share of consolidated revenue in 2022–2023. No evidence of a strategic HODL treasury beyond mined coin inventory (thus holders = 0.0), and no infrastructure or non‑financial crypto service provision distinct from payment processing (infrastr/bizserv = 0.0). ([investors.olb.com](https://investors.olb.com/olb-group-announces-support-for-mastercard-cryptocurrency-processing-business-wire/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Filings show digital assets on the balance sheet (e.g., 31.06 BTC at December 31, 2022, and 11.14 BTC at December 31, 2023), but these appear tied to mining activity and were partially liquidated, so they are treated as miners exposure rather than a deliberate treasury program. One earnings-call transcript is sourced via an aggregator, but it aligns with and is corroborated by audited 10‑K disclosures. ([materials.proxyvote.com](https://materials.proxyvote.com/Approved/67086U/20231130/10K_556865.PDF))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q4 2023 (Apr 15, 2024): “The spin-off DMINT is still active… We are running 300 mining [rigs]… our cost is probably close to $20,000 to $21,000 to [mine] 1 Bitcoin…” — Strike.Market (https://strike.market/stocks/OLB/earnings-call-transcripts/258594)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Form 10-K (filed Nov 2024; FY2023): Two segments — Fintech Services and Bitcoin Mining; “Revenue, net – bitcoin mining” of $538,718 in 2023 and $726,179 in 2022; realized gain on sale of bitcoin $288,584. — OLB (https://materials.proxyvote.com/Approved/67086U/20241108/10K_591651.PDF)
- Form 10-K (filed early 2024; FY2022/2021): Segment disclosure shows crypto mining revenue and assets; natural gas rights supporting mining operations; 31.06 BTC held at 12/31/2022 (fair value ~$515,710). — OLB (https://materials.proxyvote.com/Approved/67086U/20231130/10K_556865.PDF)
- Company site repost of Business Wire (Jan 13, 2022): SecurePay/OmniSoft ready to process Mastercard bitcoin payments; crypto-to-fiat conversion and wallet support. — OLB Investors (https://investors.olb.com/olb-group-announces-support-for-mastercard-cryptocurrency-processing-business-wire/)
- Business Wire (Mar 4, 2021): SecurePay optimized for crypto purchases and merchant settlement; wallet-agnostic support. — Business Wire (https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20210304005020/en/OLB-Streamlines-Cryptocurrency-Processing-for-Merchant-Convenience)

**Financial news / other:**
- Oct 19, 2021: DMint initiates bitcoin mining with first 100 Antminer S19j Pro units; plan to scale to 1,000. — Benzinga (https://www.benzinga.com/markets/cryptocurrency/21/10/23432299/exclusive-olb-group-subsidiary-dmint-initiates-bitcoin-mining-with-100-antminers)
- Nov 2, 2021 (updated May 11, 2023): Shares surged ~90% after announcing readiness to process Mastercard bitcoin payments; platform supports BTC, ETH, USDC, DAI and crypto‑to‑fiat conversions. — CoinDesk (https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/11/02/olb-group-surges-90-after-saying-its-ready-for-mastercard-bitcoin-payments)

</small>

## DMG BLOCKCHAIN SOLUTIONS

* Rank: 6 | Expo: 16.55 | id_str: CA23345B2003
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.7, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.1, infrastr 0.2, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 4 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: SEDAR/financial statements (2021–2024), Q3 2021 and Q1 2021 earnings calls, company press releases (2021–2024)

### Firm Overview
DMG Blockchain Solutions Inc. is a Canada‑based, TSX‑V listed blockchain technology company focused on Bitcoin self‑mining, data center/hosting operations and related software platforms, including its Blockseer analytics and Petra transaction‑routing technology. Within the sample window it also co‑developed Terra Pool, a “clean energy” Bitcoin mining pool, while operating its Christina Lake, BC mining/data center facility.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Miners: Management repeatedly describes DMG as a Bitcoin‑focused miner and discussed hashrate targets, miner purchases and plans to scale self‑mining (e.g., “we’re mainly focused on BTC mining… with 300 petahash… adding around C$2.5–3.0 million per month,” Q3 2021). Subsequent 2024 results disclosed 502.5 bitcoin mined for the year, confirming mining as the core revenue driver during the window. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/DMGI.V/transcripts/2021-year/3-quarter))  
Infrastructure: DMG operates the Christina Lake data center, historically providing hosting/colocation to third‑party miners and investing in immersion cooling and additional containerized capacity; management also noted shifting space from hosting toward self‑mining. Company releases in 2021 detail the facility’s power infrastructure and immersion program alongside a combined hosting/self‑mining strategy. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/DMGI.V/transcripts/2021-year/3-quarter))  
Non‑financial crypto services (bizserv): Through Blockseer, DMG provides software/analytics (e.g., Walletscore), runs Terra Pool and developed Petra to route transactions to compliant/clean pools; DMG licensed pool technology to Marathon (MARA), and Petra was tested with Zodia Custody. 2024 financials also reference “Net Pool Revenue” from operating mining pools. ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2021/03/31/2202491/0/en/DMG-Blockchain-Solutions-and-Marathon-Digital-Holdings-enter-into-Definitive-Software-and-Technology-Agreement-for-the-DCMNA-Pool.html?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Miners = 0.7 reflects that self‑mining dominated DMG’s operating narrative and reported activity in 2017–2024 (hashrate targets, miner purchases, and full‑year mined bitcoin). Infrastructure = 0.2 captures DMG’s role as a data‑center/hosting operator investing in immersion cooling and containerized capacity for mining clients. Bizserv = 0.1 reflects Blockseer/Terra Pool/Petra software and pool operations, including the licensing agreement with Marathon and institutional Petra trials. No evidence supports a deliberate treasury HODL policy beyond operational coin management, and Petra/Systemic Trust initiatives did not constitute a directly operated, regulated financial service during the window; thus holders = 0.0 and finserv = 0.0. ([nasdaq.com](https://www.nasdaq.com/press-release/dmg-blockchain-solutions-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2024-results-2024-12-18?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Earnings‑call transcripts are accessible via third‑party aggregators; at least two calls (Q1 and Q3 2021) were retrieved and align with filings and releases. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/DMGI.V/transcripts/2021-year/1-quarter?utm_source=openai))  
- DMG periodically held bitcoin and discussed “holding BTC,” but context indicates mined/operational balances rather than a distinct treasury strategy; accordingly, exposure is not coded as holders. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/DMGI.V/transcripts/2021-year/3-quarter))  
- Announcements around Petra/Systemic Trust and broader “carbon‑neutral transaction” enablement were in pilot/enablement stages within 2017–2024; absent clear evidence of DMG directly offering custody/exchange products during the window, finserv weight remains 0.0. ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2021/10/20/2317333/0/en/DMG-and-Zodia-Custody-Successfully-Test-Blockseer-s-Petra-Platform.html?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q3 2021 (Oct 28, 2021): “We’re mainly focused on BTC mining… with 300 petahash… [guidance] C$2.5–3.0 million per month,” and “moving… from hosting… to self‑mining.” — Roic.ai (https://www.roic.ai/quote/DMGI.V/transcripts/2021-year/3-quarter)
- Q1 2021 (May 27, 2021): “What we have been doing since inception is hosting… no shortage of people interested in moving to Christina Lake… [while] achieve 1–2 EH/s.” — Roic.ai (https://www.roic.ai/quote/DMGI.V/transcripts/2021-year/1-quarter)

**Filings (10‑K / financial statements):**
- Q2 FY2024 interim FS (May 2024): notes “Net Pool Revenue… from operation of crypto‑currency mining pools.” — DMG (https://dmgblockchain.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/Q2-2024-Mar-FS.pdf)
- Q2 FY2021 MD&A (Aug 2021): Christina Lake immersion/hosting plans; Terra Pool MOU; SEDAR reference. — DMG (https://dmgblockchain.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/08/DMG_MDA_Q2.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- FY2024 results (Dec 18, 2024): 502.5 bitcoin mined in 2024; revenue context. — Nasdaq/GlobeNewswire (https://www.nasdaq.com/press-release/dmg-blockchain-solutions-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2024-results-2024-12-18)
- Terra Pool launch (Jun 22, 2022): DMG + Argo launch clean‑energy Bitcoin mining pool. — GlobeNewswire (https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2022/06/22/2467070/0/en/DMG-Blockchain-Solutions-Inc-Announces-Launch-of-Terra-Pool-the-First-Bitcoin-Pool-Focused-on-Carbon-Neutral-Mining.html)
- Marathon licensing (Mar 31, 2021): DMG to provide Blockseer pool technology to MARA’s DCMNA/MaraPool. — GlobeNewswire (https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2021/03/31/2202491/0/en/DMG-Blockchain-Solutions-and-Marathon-Digital-Holdings-enter-into-Definitive-Software-and-Technology-Agreement-for-the-DCMNA-Pool.html)
- SEC 8‑K (Mar 25, 2021): MARA’s disclosure of DMG pool‑technology license terms. — SEC (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1507605/000149315221007192/form8-k.htm)
- Zodia Custody trials Petra with DMG (Oct 20, 2021): institutional transaction‑routing trials to compliant/clean pools. — Ledger Insights (https://www.ledgerinsights.com/stancharts-zodia-in-trials-to-route-crypto-transactions-to-compliant-mining-pools/)
- Christina Lake data‑center/hosting and immersion program (Jan–Feb 2021): facility power/immersion details and hosting strategy. — GlobeNewswire (https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2021/02/10/2173232/0/en/DMG-Conducting-Extensive-Immersion-Cooling-Study-as-it-Prepares-its-Facility-for-Large-Scale-Deployment-in-2021.html), (https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2021/01/21/2162212/0/en/DMG-Provides-Overview-and-Business-Outlook-for-2021.html)

## EXODUS MOVEMENT A

* Rank: 7 | Expo: 15.33 | id_str: US30209R1068
* Weight summary: holders 0.3, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.7, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 5 | AssetShare: 3
* Primary sources: Form 10 and Form 10-12G/A (2024), Q4 2021 earnings call, 2022–2024 company press releases. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1821534/000114036124010148/ny20022043x1_1012g.htm)

### Firm Overview
Exodus Movement, Inc. is a U.S.-based software company that develops the Exodus self-custodial (un‑hosted) crypto wallet for desktop and mobile users. The company monetizes primarily via integration fees from third‑party API providers embedded in its wallet (exchange aggregation, fiat on/off‑ramp and staking), rather than operating its own exchange or taking custody of client assets. 

### Exposure Channels Identified
Bizserv (non‑financial services): Exodus provides wallet software and user interface functionality that enables users to hold and manage their own digital assets; third‑party providers deliver exchange, fiat onboarding and staking through APIs, for which Exodus earns fees. Management also emphasizes that Exodus does not consider itself an exchange, ATS, transfer agent, clearing agency, MSB or money transmitter given its non‑custodial design.   
Holders: Exodus holds digital assets on its own balance sheet. As of December 31, 2023, filings show 1,787 bitcoin and 2,538 ether among “digital assets, net,” totaling about $35.0 million versus $111.7 million in total assets. These appear as treasury/operational assets rather than product collateral. 

### Weight Assignment
Bizserv = 0.7 reflects that Exodus is primarily a software/wallet company whose revenue is transaction‑based API fees from partners integrated into its non‑custodial wallet; it does not itself provide custody or operate an exchange. Holders = 0.3 captures the company’s material on‑balance‑sheet crypto (not held to back a financial product). No evidence indicates mining, hardware/cloud provisioning, or directly operated financial services during 2017–2024; thus miners = 0.0, infrastr = 0.0, and finserv = 0.0. 

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Some may argue for a finserv classification because users can swap, stake, and buy crypto in‑app; however, SEC filings specify these are delivered by third‑party APIs and reiterate Exodus’s view that it is not operating an exchange/ATS/MSB or providing custody. Therefore, exposure is coded as bizserv, not finserv.   
- Earnings‑call transcripts beyond 2021 are less widely syndicated; classification is anchored by the Q4 2021 call and 2024 SEC filings, which clearly describe the model and balance‑sheet assets. 

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q4 and Full‑Year 2021 (Mar 9, 2022): “we closed 2021 with $95.8 million dollars in revenue.” — Exodus Investor Transcript (https://d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net/_666fa343bb0b6d94ae77fb51c5c94004/exodus/db/946/9118/webcast_transcript/Q4_and_Full_Year_2021_Earnings_Call_Transcript.pdf)  
- Q4 and Full‑Year 2021 (Mar 9, 2022): “we partnered with FTX to make this come to life” [re in‑wallet exchange integration]. — Exodus Investor Transcript (https://d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net/_666fa343bb0b6d94ae77fb51c5c94004/exodus/db/946/9118/webcast_transcript/Q4_and_Full_Year_2021_Earnings_Call_Transcript.pdf)

**Filings (10‑K / regulatory / press releases):**
- Form 10 (Feb 28, 2024): “Exchange Aggregation, Fiat Onboarding, and Staking Revenue Earned Through an API Provider” — revenue recognition and non‑custodial API model. — SEC (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1821534/000114036124010148/ny20022043x1_1012g.htm)  
- Form 10 (Feb 28, 2024): Discloses BTC 1,787 and ETH 2,538; “Digital assets, net” ≈ $35.0m; total assets ≈ $111.7m (Dec 31, 2023). — SEC (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1821534/000114036124010148/ny20022043x1_1012g.htm)  
- Form 10‑12G/A (Jul 10, 2024): Company states it does not consider itself an exchange/ATS/MSB or money transmitter; explains non‑custodial posture. — Exodus Investors/SEC (https://www.exodus.com/investors/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001140361-24-032773/ny20031698x1_1012ga.htm/ny20031698x1_ex21.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- Investor Presentation (Feb 1, 2024): Company overview and 2023 results for the self‑custodial wallet business. — Exodus (https://s2.q4cdn.com/950394465/files/doc_events/2024/Feb/21/february-2024-investor-presentation-final.pdf)  
- Earnings call scheduling note (Mar 2, 2022): call delayed to Mar 9; company committed to provide transcript — Reddit (https://www.reddit.com/r/ExodusWallet/comments/t5bwad)

</small>

## WASTE ENERGY

* Rank: 8 | Expo: 14.84 | id_str: US23131W1036
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.1, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.9, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 2 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q3 2021 earnings call; Form 10‑Q for quarter ended June 30, 2022; Fogdog services/loan agreements (2021); 2022 rebrand press release; 2024 FogDog asset acquisition press release.

### Firm Overview
Waste Energy Corp (ISIN US23131W1036) is the current name for the entity that, during 2017–2024, operated as CurrencyWorks Inc. and later MetaWorks Platforms Inc., a U.S./Canada‑listed Web3 development company. In that period the firm built blockchain and NFT platforms for brands and entertainment partners and explored a waste‑to‑energy powered crypto‑mining initiative via its Zer00 program.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Bizserv (non‑financial services): Across 2017–2024, filings describe a business model providing turnkey Web3/NFT/blockchain integration services for clients, and revenue disclosures show primary sales of NFTs, consulting, and related content distribution. This positions the company as a software/services provider to the crypto ecosystem rather than a custodian or exchange operator.  
Miners: Management discussed and financed a “Zer00” crypto‑mining initiative tied to waste‑to‑energy power via agreements with Fogdog Energy Solutions, with commentary indicating intent to mine bitcoin for the company’s own account. This suggests some direct miner exposure, though scale within the sample window appears limited.  
No evidence indicates material balance‑sheet crypto treasury holdings, direct custody/exchange operations, or mining hardware/colocation services for third parties during 2017–2024.

### Weight Assignment
Bizserv = 0.9 reflects that the core, repeatedly evidenced activity in 2017–2024 was building and operating NFT and blockchain platforms for clients (NFT primary sales, consulting, and movie/NFT projects). Miners = 0.1 captures the documented Zer00 initiative and service/loan arrangements with Fogdog aimed at powering and conducting bitcoin mining for the company, with intent mentioned on the Q3 2021 call but limited execution detail in filings through December 2024. Holders, finserv, and infrastr are set to 0.0 due to lack of explicit, material evidence.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Transcript coverage is limited for a small‑cap issuer; classification is anchored by the Q3 2021 call plus SEC filings.  
- Management discussed plans to mine bitcoin and invested in related infrastructure partnerships, but filings through December 2024 do not quantify sustained mining operations or hashrate.  
- The company rebranded and pivoted toward waste‑to‑energy in 2025–2026; this post‑sample transition does not alter the 2017–2024 classification but may change future exposure mix.

### Evidence
### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q3 2021 (Nov 15, 2021): “Operating revenue in Q3 2021 consisted mainly of the ZERO CONTACT and Motoclub NFT sales.” — ROIC AI transcript (https://www.roic.ai/quote/MWRK/transcripts/2021-year/3-quarter)  
- Q3 2021 (Nov 15, 2021): “we have a unique… zero cost base for our power… [for] the zero mining initiative.” — ROIC AI transcript (https://www.roic.ai/quote/MWRK/transcripts/2021-year/3-quarter)

**Filings (10‑K / 10‑Q / agreements):**
- Form 10‑Q (Jun 30, 2022): “we loaned $400,000 to Fogdog… [and] an additional $850,000… convertible promissory note.” — SEC (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1515139/000149315222022360/form10-q.htm)  
- Form 10‑Q (Jun 30, 2022): “acquired the rights to a movie… linked to… movie‑related NFTs.” — SEC (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1515139/000149315222022360/form10-q.htm)  
- Services Agreement with Fogdog (Aug 20, 2021): “Fogdog will provide… free of charge access to the electricity power… provided that CurrencyWorks is… operat[ing] cryptocurrency mining operations.” — SEC Exhibit 10.1 (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1515139/000149315221021084/ex10-1.htm)  
- Form 10‑K (FY2022, filed Apr 2023): Exhibit list includes “Services Agreement… [and] Loan Agreement with Fogdog Energy Solutions Inc. dated August 20, 2021.” — SEC (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1515139/000149315223008326/form10-k.htm)  
- General Security Agreement with Fogdog (Aug 20, 2021) — Justia (https://contracts.justia.com/companies/icox-innovations-inc-4012/contract/195816/)

**Financial news / other:**
- Rebrand to MetaWorks Platforms (Sep 8, 2022): company “announces a corporate rebrand and launch.” — GlobeNewswire (https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2022/09/08/2512646/0/en/CurrencyWorks-Rebrands-as-MetaWorks-Platforms-Inc-Positioning-Itself-as-a-Leading-Web3-Development-Platform.html)  
- MetaWorks completes asset purchase of FogDog Energy (Aug 23, 2024): “acquired the assets of FogDog Energy Solutions… Company to Begin Waste to Energy Conversion Operations by Q4.” — GlobeNewswire (https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2024/08/23/2934868/0/en/MetaWorks-Completes-Asset-Purchase-of-FogDog-Energy.html)

## STRATEGY A

* Rank: 9 | Expo: 13.33 | id_str: US5949724083
* Weight summary: holders 1.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 4
* Primary sources: Form 10‑K (FY2023, filed 2024) and 10‑Q (Q1 2024); Q1 2022, Q2 2023, Q4 2023, and Q2 2024 earnings calls; Bloomberg/CNBC coverage characterizing the stock as a bitcoin proxy.

### Firm Overview
STRATEGY A corresponds to MicroStrategy Inc., a U.S.-listed enterprise analytics software company that, beginning in 2020, adopted bitcoin as its primary treasury reserve asset. During 2017–2024 the company continued to operate its analytics business while accumulating a large bitcoin position on its balance sheet.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Holders: Management repeatedly states that bitcoin is the firm’s primary treasury reserve asset and that it intends to continue accumulating bitcoin; SEC filings disclose the size and balance‑sheet treatment of these holdings during 2022–2024. Earnings calls across 2022–2024 emphasize capital‑markets activity (convertible notes, secured notes, and ATM equity) used to purchase additional bitcoin.  
No substantive evidence in 2017–2024 indicates mining operations, operation of a crypto exchange/custody service, or provision of crypto‑specific infrastructure. Financial media routinely describe the equity as a proxy for bitcoin exposure, supporting an investor‑base amplifier.

### Weight Assignment
Holders = 1.0 reflects that the company’s sole material crypto channel in 2017–2024 is deliberate treasury investment in bitcoin, with explicit long‑term accumulation language in filings and calls. There is no credible evidence for miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr channels within the sample window, hence those weights are 0.0. Given frequent media characterization of the stock as a bitcoin proxy, invbase = 1. RevShare = 0 because bitcoin holdings do not constitute an operating revenue line; AssetShare = 4 as filings show bitcoin comprised a majority of total assets by year‑end 2023.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Some product discussions around Bitcoin/Lightning were exploratory; no evidence of material crypto service revenues through December 2024.  
- “Expo” score was not provided in the input; placeholder 0.00 shown here should be replaced by the pipeline value.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q1 2022 (May 4, 2022): “The carrying value of our Bitcoin holdings as of March 31, 2022, was $2.9 billion… As the largest publicly traded corporate holder of Bitcoin in the world…” — The Motley Fool (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/05/04/microstrategy-mstr-q1-2022-earnings-call-transcrip/)  
- Q1 2023 (May 2, 2023): “In Q1, we increased our net Bitcoin position by 7,500 Bitcoins… At the end of the quarter, 89% of our total Bitcoin holdings were unencumbered…” — The Motley Fool (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2023/05/02/microstrategy-mstr-q1-2023-earnings-call-transcrip/)  
- Q2 2023 (Aug 1, 2023): “As of July 31st, 2023, the company held 152,800 bitcoins… we remain committed to our bitcoin acquisition strategy…” — Nasdaq/Motley Fool (https://www.nasdaq.com/articles/microstrategy-mstr-q2-2023-earnings-call-transcript)  
- Q4 2023 (Feb 6, 2024): “Since the inception of our Bitcoin strategy, we have raised $2.2 billion of debt… to acquire more Bitcoin…” — The Motley Fool (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2024/02/06/microstrategy-mstr-q4-2023-earnings-call-transcrip/)  
- Q1 2024 (Apr 29, 2024): “Year to date, we have added 2,418 bitcoins… The new accounting rule… requires companies holding digital assets… to adopt fair value accounting by Q1 of 2025.” — The Motley Fool (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2024/04/29/microstrategy-mstr-q1-2024-earnings-call-transcrip/)  
- Q2 2024 (Aug 1, 2024): Management reiterates bitcoin‑centric treasury strategy; discussion of equity premium to holdings. — The Motley Fool (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2024/08/01/microstrategy-mstr-q2-2024-earnings-call-transcrip/)

**Filings (10‑K / 10‑Q):**
- Form 10‑K (FY2023, filed Apr 2024): “As of December 31, 2023, we held… approximately 189,150 bitcoins…”; MacroStrategy’s bitcoin carrying value represented 70.6% of consolidated total assets; total assets $4.76B; digital assets $3.63B. — SEC (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1050446/000119312524094698/d806750dars.pdf)  
- Form 10‑Q (Q3 2023): “As of October 31, 2023, we held approximately 158,400 bitcoins… and we intend to purchase additional bitcoin in the future.” — SEC (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1050446/000095017023057418/mstr-20230930.htm)  
- Form 10‑Q (Q1 2024): “We view our bitcoin holdings as long‑term holdings and expect to continue to accumulate bitcoin.” — SEC (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1050446/000095017024051230/mstr-20240331.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- “MicroStrategy’s Plunge Shows Risk of Stock as Bitcoin Proxy.” — Bloomberg, Mar 19, 2024 (https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2024-03-19/microstrategy-s-worst-slide-since-2022-shows-bitcoin-proxy-risk)  
- “MicroStrategy shares drop… making its shares a proxy for the cryptocurrency.” — CNBC, Jan 21, 2022 (https://www.cnbc.com/2022/01/21/microstrategy-shares-drop-on-bitcoins-slide-sec-rejection-of-companys-crypto-accounting.html)  
- “MicroStrategy owns so much bitcoin that some investors use it as a proxy…” — CoinDesk, Sep 13, 2021 (https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/09/13/microstrategy-buys-5050-more-bitcoins-now-holds-316b-worth-in-total)

## RIOT PLATFORMS

* Rank: 10 | Expo: 12.95 | id_str: US7672921050
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.9, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.1, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 4 | AssetShare: 3
* Primary sources: Form 10‑K (FY2024, filed 2025); Q2 2024 earnings call; company press releases; mainstream coverage of ERCOT power credits and “crypto‑exposed” stock moves

### Firm Overview
Riot Platforms, Inc. is a U.S.-listed company headquartered in Colorado that operates large-scale Bitcoin mining facilities in Texas and, since 2024, Kentucky. During 2017–2024, Riot’s core business was self-mining of Bitcoin, supplemented by an electrical engineering subsidiary and limited third‑party hosting activity.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Miners: Management repeatedly describes Riot as a vertically integrated Bitcoin miner, with disclosures on hashrate, power strategy, and production driving results; FY2024 filings show Bitcoin mining as the primary segment and detail costs to mine and power curtailment credits. The Q2 2024 earnings call further emphasizes self‑mining economics, ERCOT-driven power credits, and installed hashrate milestones.  
Infrastructure: Filings and calls indicate limited third‑party hosting capacity (largely wound down) and data‑center infrastructure developed for mining, with small amounts of hosting revenue reclassified to “Other.”  
Investor base amplifier: Financial media routinely groups Riot with “crypto‑exposed” equities that move with Bitcoin price cycles, and coverage of ERCOT power‑credit windfalls has reinforced its profile as a direct Bitcoin‑mining proxy for investors.

### Weight Assignment
Miners = 0.9 reflects that self‑mining of Bitcoin overwhelmingly defines Riot’s operations through December 2024, as evidenced by segment revenue and detailed mining disclosures in filings and calls. A small Infrastructure = 0.1 captures residual/transitionary third‑party hosting and the company’s role as an operator of purpose‑built mining data centers. There is no evidence of an exchange/custody product (finserv), non‑financial services to the ecosystem (bizserv), or deliberate non‑operational treasury HODL beyond normal mining inventory management that would merit a separate holders weight within the 2017–2024 window. Given frequent media characterization as a crypto‑sensitive equity, invbase = 1. RevShare = 4 (>45%) is supported by FY2024 segment mix; AssetShare = 3 (~15–45%) reflects the substantial, but not majority, share of total assets comprised of Bitcoin at year‑end 2024.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Hosting exposure existed but was de‑emphasized/wound down by 2023–2024; a small infrastr weight (0.1) is retained to reflect residual activity.  
- Management at times retained 100% of mined Bitcoin (e.g., Q2 2024), but filings frame Bitcoin holdings within operating strategy; no separate “treasury HODL” policy beyond mining inventory is evident for 2017–2024, so holders = 0.0 is maintained.  
- “Expo” value was not provided in the input; 0.00 is a placeholder for pipeline replacement.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q2 2024 (Jul 31, 2024): “Riot's primary strategic focus has been on developing a leading vertically integrated Bitcoin mining company…,” and “our unique power strategy… generated $13.9 million in power credits… all‑in cost of power of $0.027 per kWh.” — Fintool (https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/RIOT/documents/transcripts/q2-2024) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/RIOT/documents/transcripts/q2-2024))  
- Q2 2024 (Jul 31, 2024): “Riot ended the second quarter of 2024 with 9,334 Bitcoin… Riot continued to retain 100% of all Bitcoin produced… Bitcoin mining revenue totaled $55.8 million.” — Fintool (https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/RIOT/documents/transcripts/q2-2024) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/RIOT/documents/transcripts/q2-2024))

**Filings (10‑K / presentations):**
- Form 10‑K FY2024 (filed 2025): Business description and revenue from Bitcoin mining; Corsicana/Rockdale development; grid curtailment; critical audit matter over Bitcoin mining revenue. — SEC (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1167419/000155837025001888/riot-20241231x10k.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1167419/000155837025001888/riot-20241231x10k.htm))  
- Form 10‑K FY2024: Segment revenue table (Bitcoin Mining ~$321.0M; Engineering ~$46.5M; Other ~$17.2M; total ~$376.7M). — SEC (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1167419/000155837025001888/riot-20241231x10k.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1167419/000155837025001888/riot-20241231x10k.htm))  
- Form 10‑K FY2024: Total assets ~$3.94B; Bitcoin carried at fair value ~$1.65B; 17,722 BTC held at 12/31/2024 (incl. mined and acquired). — SEC (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1167419/000155837025001888/riot-20241231x10k.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1167419/000155837025001888/riot-20241231x10k.htm))  
- Exhibit 99.2 (Q1 2023 slide): Mechanics of ERCOT power‑credit strategy and FY2023 power credits detail. — SEC (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1167419/000116741924000008/riot-20240224xex99d2.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1167419/000116741924000008/riot-20240224xex99d2.htm?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- “Bitcoin miner Riot Platforms generated record $281 million total revenue in 2023… $71.2 million in power credits.” — The Block (Mar 1, 2024) (https://www.theblock.co/amp/post/278868/bitcoin-miner-riot-platforms-2023-report) ([theblock.co](https://www.theblock.co/amp/post/278868/bitcoin-miner-riot-platforms-2023-report?utm_source=openai))

## CLEANSPARK

* Rank: 11 | Expo: 12.56 | id_str: US18452B2097
* Weight summary: holders 0.1, miners 0.9, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 4 | AssetShare: 3
* Primary sources: 10-K FY2024; FY2023 and Q1 FY2023 earnings calls; 2024 monthly mining updates; CNBC/Motley Fool coverage (2024)

### Firm Overview
CleanSpark, Inc. is a U.S.-listed Bitcoin miner headquartered in Henderson, Nevada, operating company-owned and leased sites across Georgia, Mississippi and Tennessee, with additional development in Wyoming. Its core business is self-mining of Bitcoin, and its common stock trades on Nasdaq under ticker CLSK. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/827876/000095017024132565/clsk-20240930.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s primary exposure is as a Bitcoin miner: management discloses mining economics, hashrate and power costs, and reported approximately $379 million of bitcoin mining revenue for fiscal year ended September 30, 2024, mining 7,092 BTC that year. Other non-mining “data center operations” revenue was wound down by September 30, 2023 as capacity was redirected to self-mining. As of September 30, 2024 the company held 6,819 BTC (fair value about $432 million), and management has discussed using Bitcoin as a “smart treasury tool,” indicating a modest deliberate balance-sheet holding beyond purely transitory working inventory. The stock is frequently treated as a crypto proxy in market coverage, moving with Bitcoin rallies. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/827876/000095017024132565/clsk-20240930.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Miners = 0.9 reflects that Bitcoin self-mining overwhelmingly defines operations and revenue through 2017–2024, with management prioritizing hashrate growth, fleet efficiency and power strategy while ceasing other services. Holders = 0.1 captures explicit treasury framing and sizable BTC held on the balance sheet by FY2024; however, management also uses mined BTC to fund expenses, so the holding is supportive rather than a standalone treasury strategy. No evidence supports finserv, bizserv or third‑party hosting/infrastructure provision in this window, hence those weights are 0.0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/827876/000095017024132565/clsk-20240930.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Management’s stance on retaining versus selling mined BTC varied over time; while they characterized Bitcoin as a treasury tool and increased holdings in late 2023–2024, they also stated a strategy of using BTC for operating expenses and “holding the difference,” so a small but non‑dominant holders weight is assigned. Media frequently describes the stock as a Bitcoin‑sensitive equity; invbase = 1 reflects this observed linkage in 2024 coverage. None of the sources indicate material third‑party hosting or exchange/custody activity during 2017–2024. ([investors.cleanspark.com](https://investors.cleanspark.com/files/doc_financials/2023/q4/CleanSpark-s-Fiscal-Full-Year-2023-Earnings-Results-Webcast-Transcript.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 30, 2023 (FY2023 results): “We see Bitcoin as a smart treasury tool… we expect to continue to grow our Bitcoin treasury, but… [will] use our Bitcoin for expenses and hold the difference.” — S&P Global transcript [CleanSpark IR] (https://investors.cleanspark.com/files/doc_financials/2023/q4/CleanSpark-s-Fiscal-Full-Year-2023-Earnings-Results-Webcast-Transcript.pdf)  
- Feb 9, 2023 (Q1 FY2023): “Investing and building our own infrastructure matters… a long‑term investment that puts us in a position where [long‑term operating costs] are lower.” — S&P Global transcript [CleanSpark IR] (https://investors.cleanspark.com/files/doc_financials/2023/q1/TRANSCRIPT-CleanSparks-Q1-2023-Earnings-Results-Webcast.pdf)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Form 10‑K FY2024 (filed Dec 3, 2024): Bitcoin mining revenue “was approximately $379 million”; recognition policy and pool details. — SEC (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/827876/000095017024132565/clsk-20240930.htm)  
- Form 10‑K FY2024: “During the fiscal year ended September 30, 2024, we mined 7,092 bitcoins…”; average power cost ~$0.046/kWh. — SEC (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/827876/000095017024132565/clsk-20240930.htm)  
- Form 10‑K FY2024: Other services “data center operations… ceased… as of September 30, 2023.” — SEC (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/827876/000095017024132565/clsk-20240930.htm)  
- Form 10‑K FY2024: Held 6,819 BTC at Sept 30, 2024 (fair value ~$431.7M); total assets ~$1.96B. — SEC (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/827876/000095017024132565/clsk-20240930.htm)  
- Nov 2024 mining update: 622 BTC mined; BTC holdings 9,297 as of Nov 30, 2024; Pure‑play positioning (“America’s Bitcoin Miner”). — CleanSpark IR (https://investors.cleanspark.com/news/news-details/2024/CleanSpark-Releases-November-2024-Bitcoin-Mining-Update/default.aspx)

**Financial news / other:**
- “Why Bitcoin Miners Marathon, Riot, and CleanSpark Soared in February” — move tied to Bitcoin rally (Mar 7, 2024). — The Motley Fool (https://www.fool.com/investing/2024/03/07/why-bitcoin-miners-soared-in-february/)  
- “CleanSpark jumps on plans to buy four bitcoin mining facilities… shares higher… also helped by a midday rise in the price of bitcoin” (Feb 6, 2024). — CNBC (https://www.cnbc.com/2024/02/06/cleanspark-jumps-on-plans-to-buy-four-bitcoin-mining-facilities-ahead-of-the-halving-.html)

</small>

## HALO COLLECTIVE DEAD - DELIST.15/08/23

* Rank: 12 | Expo: 12.35 | id_str: CA40638K7050
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Q1 2022 earnings call transcript; OTC interim financials (Q1 2022); company press releases (2021–2023); delisting announcement (Aug 15, 2023)

### Firm Overview
Halo Collective Inc. was a Canada-based cannabis company with vertically integrated operations focused on cultivation, extraction, manufacturing and retail (Budega) in U.S. markets, selling products under brands such as Hush and Winberry. The company announced in August 2023 that it would be delisting from its Canadian exchange amid financial challenges. ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/halo-collective-receives-all-approvals-for-budega-dispensary-in-north-hollywood-california-301490935.html?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across the available 2017–2024 window, the company’s disclosures and earnings call materials describe cannabis operations and related software/consumer products spin‑out plans (Halo Tek) but do not indicate any activity involving cryptocurrency holdings, mining, financial services, or infrastructure. The accessible Q1 2022 earnings call and interim filings focus on cannabis revenues, margins, retail openings and cost controls with no crypto references. No credible financial‑news coverage during this period characterizes the firm as a crypto proxy or participant. ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/otc/hcanf/investor-relations/earnings-call/q1-2022))

### Weight Assignment
Unclass = 1.0 is assigned because there is no evidence, across transcripts, filings or press releases, of any substantive crypto exposure via holdings, mining, crypto‑financial services, business services to crypto firms, or crypto‑directed infrastructure. All other mechanism weights are set to 0.0 accordingly. ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/otc/hcanf/investor-relations/earnings-call/q1-2022))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The firm was delisted on August 15, 2023, which limits later‑period disclosures; however, multiple transcripts and company communications from 2021–2023 are available and none indicate crypto involvement. None identified beyond this coverage constraint. ([nasdaq.com](https://www.nasdaq.com/press-release/halo-collective-reports-q2-2023-financial-results-and-announces-delisting-2023-08-15?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 18, 2022 (Q1 2022): “Total revenues were approximately $7.6 million with a gross margin of 18.7%.” — AlphaSpread (HCANF Q1‑2022 transcript) (https://www.alphaspread.com/security/otc/hcanf/investor-relations/earnings-call/q1-2022)  
- Apr 4, 2022 (2021 results): Listing of call in transcript index — MarketScreener (locked content) (https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/HALO-COLLECTIVE-INC-120797186/news/Transcript-Halo-Collective-Inc-Q1-2022-Earnings-Call-May-18-2022-40472833/)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**

- May 2, 2022: Details of “Halo Tek” spin‑off assets (DispensaryTrack, Cannalift, AccuDab, etc.). — PR Newswire (https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/halo-collective-announces-filing-of-halo-tek-prospectus-and-change-in-chief-financial-officer-and-board-of-directors-301537311.html)

**Financial news / other:**

## HUT 8

* Rank: 13 | Expo: 12.32 | id_str: US44812J1043
* Weight summary: holders 0.2, miners 0.7, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.1, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 4 | AssetShare: 4
* Primary sources: Q1–Q3 2024 earnings call transcripts; Q3 2024 Form 10‑Q; FY2022 results release

### Firm Overview
Hut 8 is a North American Bitcoin miner that, following its November 30, 2023 merger of equals with US Bitcoin Corp, operates as Hut 8 Corp with businesses spanning self‑mining, managed services, hosting/colocation and high‑performance computing/data centers. Within the 2017–2024 window, the company consistently mined Bitcoin and maintained a large self‑mined BTC reserve while also developing hosting and power‑first digital‑infrastructure initiatives. ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2023/12/11/2793692/0/en/Hut-8-Operations-Update-for-November-2023.html?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
- Miners: Management repeatedly discusses self‑mining economics, fleet efficiency and cost‑to‑mine, confirming core exposure via Bitcoin mining operations. For example, in Q2 2024 they highlighted limiting post‑halving unit‑cost increases and cited an average cost to mine of ~$26,232. ([cdn.sanity.io](https://cdn.sanity.io/files/2iccs2ie/production/9639a635010c7196ae4b24d0e9c150fb35c592be.pdf))  
- Holders: The firm has long emphasized a deliberate “HODL”/treasury approach, reporting 9,102 BTC held as of March 31, 2024 (about $650 million) and historically stating it deposited 100% of self‑mined BTC into custody. ([cdn.sanity.io](https://cdn.sanity.io/files/2iccs2ie/production/746b52ed9dfba948a412c1bb3ca50d240311410e.pdf))  
- Infrastructure (hosting/colocation and power): Hut 8 also earns hosting/colocation revenue and positions Bitcoin mining as an initial load for power‑led data‑center development; in Q3 2024 it announced a 15 EH/s Bitmain colocation expected to generate nine‑figure annualized hosting revenue. ([cdn.sanity.io](https://cdn.sanity.io/files/2iccs2ie/production/463838299215b9891fed8f6fd62054f55c9e7b0d.pdf))  
- Investor‑base amplifier: Media and market commentary frequently frame HUT as a Bitcoin proxy; stock reactions are reported alongside Bitcoin moves and analyses highlight the equity’s sensitivity to BTC and digital‑asset strategy. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/earnings/hut-8-shares-surge-after-q4-report-as-trumps-crypto-comments-boost-bitcoin-93CH-3903434?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Mining is the dominant, persistent mechanism, hence miners = 0.7. A separate holders = 0.2 is warranted by explicit, sustained treasury accumulation well beyond short‑term working capital (e.g., 9,102 BTC as of March 31, 2024; 9,086 BTC at year‑end 2022). Infrastructure = 0.1 reflects material third‑party hosting/colocation and the power‑first, Bitcoin‑as‑initial‑load approach (e.g., Bitmain 15 EH/s colocation). No evidence supports finserv or non‑crypto business services. The firm is widely treated as a crypto proxy, so invbase = 1. ([cdn.sanity.io](https://cdn.sanity.io/files/2iccs2ie/production/746b52ed9dfba948a412c1bb3ca50d240311410e.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Post‑merger diversification increased non‑mining segments (managed services, HPC), but 2024 filings still show crypto‑linked lines (mining plus hosting) as a majority of revenue YTD; classification emphasizes mining while acknowledging hosting. ([cdn.kscope.io](https://cdn.kscope.io/291bc71d6d0c63f55f729e387190c198.html))  
- Treasury policy evolved to include auto‑liquidations to cover operating costs, yet a substantial reserve remained through 2024; holders weight reflects the still‑large balance rather than an assumption of perpetual accumulation. ([cdn.kscope.io](https://cdn.kscope.io/291bc71d6d0c63f55f729e387190c198.html))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 15, 2024 (Q1 2024): “We closed the quarter
<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 15, 2024 (Q1 2024): “We closed a quarter with 9,102 Bitcoin in our treasury, representing a market value of approximately $650 million as of March 31st.” — Hut 8 IR (Q1 2024 transcript PDF) (https://cdn.sanity.io/files/2iccs2ie/production/746b52ed9dfba948a412c1bb3ca50d240311410e.pdf)  
- May 15, 2024 (Q1 2024): “Our average cost to mine a Bitcoin… was $20,419 [ex‑hosted]; … $24,594 including hosted facilities.” — Hut 8 IR (Q1 2024 transcript PDF) (https://cdn.sanity.io/files/2iccs2ie/production/746b52ed9dfba948a412c1bb3ca50d240311410e.pdf)

- Nov 13, 2024 (Q3 2024): “Developing Tier I data centers for Bitcoin mining… is central to this approach because we believe it enables us to monetize power assets rapidly.” — Hut 8 IR (Q3 2024 transcript PDF) (https://cdn.sanity.io/files/2iccs2ie/production/463838299215b9891fed8f6fd62054f55c9e7b0d.pdf)  

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Q3 2024 Form 10‑Q: Segment revenue (nine months): Digital Asset Mining ~$55.9m; Managed Services ~$39.1m; HPC ~$10.1m; Other ~$34.6m. — SEC filing (Q3 2024 10‑Q) (https://cdn.kscope.io/291bc71d6d0c63f55f729e387190c198.html)  
- FY2022 results release (Mar 9, 2023): “Bitcoin holdings increased 64.7% in 2022 to 9,086 at year end… During Q4 2022, 100% of self‑mined Bitcoin was deposited into custody.” — SEC (exhibit press release) (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1731805/000127956923000282/ex991.htm)
