## CIPHER MINING

* Rank: 14 | Expo: 12.11 | id_str: US17253J1060
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 1.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 5 | AssetShare: 2
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (FY2023), Q1 2024 earnings call, monthly operations updates (Mar–Apr 2024)

### Firm Overview
Cipher Mining Inc. is a U.S.-listed company that develops and operates industrial‑scale data centers dedicated to Bitcoin mining, with core operations in Texas. Management describes Bitcoin mining as the company’s principal revenue‑generating activity during the 2017–2024 window. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1819989/000095017024025442/cifr-20231231.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
- Miners: Filings and calls consistently state that Cipher’s business model centers on mining Bitcoin (self‑mining using ASIC fleets powered by long‑term power arrangements). In FY2023, revenue was earned entirely via a mining pool counterparty, confirming mining as the revenue engine. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1819989/000095017024025442/cifr-20231231.htm))  
- Treasury/holders (excluded): The company carries “bitcoin inventory” and references custody/treasury management but sells mined BTC periodically; monthly updates show balances fluctuating due to routine sales. This reflects operational inventory management rather than a deliberate long‑term treasury HODL policy. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1819989/000095017024025442/cifr-20231231.htm))  
- No evidence during 2017–2024 of operating a crypto exchange, issuing crypto investment products, or providing third‑party hosting at scale that would shift exposure into finserv, bizserv, or infrastr within the sample period. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1819989/000095017024025442/cifr-20231231.htm))  
- Investor‑base amplifier: Financial media routinely treats listed miners (including Cipher) as leveraged proxies for Bitcoin; coverage in late 2024 linked miner stock moves and analyst actions to Bitcoin price dynamics. ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2024/12/10/bitcoin-miners-cipher-clean-spark-and-maras-upgraded-at-jp-morgan?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Mining is the clear and dominant mechanism throughout 2017–2024, so miners = 1.0. Although Cipher reports BTC balances, disclosures frame them as inventory managed for liquidity and operating needs (including routine sales), not as a strategic treasury reserve; therefore holders = 0.0 under the exclusion rule. There is no
... credible evidence of material finserv, bizserv or infrastructure revenue within 2017–2024; while management began outlining an HPC data center development strategy on the Q3 2024 call, mining remained the operative driver in this window. Accordingly, miners = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. The stock is also treated in market coverage as a Bitcoin proxy, so invbase = 1. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1819989/000095017024025442/cifr-20231231.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Treasury vs. inventory: Cipher reported 1,508 BTC “in our treasury” at September 30, 2024, but also described an opportunistic approach that included liquidating coins to fund site acquisitions; its 10‑K details routine sales treatment in cash flows. This points to operating inventory management rather than a deliberate HODL policy. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-cipher-mining-pivots-to-hpc-amid-bitcoin-volatility-93CH-3697800))  
- Infrastructure pivot: Late‑2024 calls highlight a shift toward developing HPC data centers, but monetization fell largely beyond 2024; the classification therefore emphasizes mining for the 2017–2024 sample. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-cipher-mining-pivots-to-hpc-amid-bitcoin-volatility-93CH-3697800))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 1, 2024 (Q3 2024): “As of September 30th, we held 1,508 Bitcoin in our treasury.” — Investing.com transcript/article (Q3 2024) (https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-cipher-mining-pivots-to-hpc-amid-bitcoin-volatility-93CH-3697800)  
- Nov 1, 2024 (Q3 2024): “Our business model has rapidly evolved from being just a Bitcoin miner to being a developer of HPC data centers…” — Investing.com transcript/article (Q3 2024) (https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-cipher-mining-pivots-to-hpc-amid-bitcoin-volatility-93CH-3697800)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Mar 2024, FY2023 Form 10‑K: “Our business model centers on bitcoin mining.” — SEC (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1819989/000095017024025442/cifr-20231231.htm)  
- Mar 2024, FY2023 Form 10‑K: “We use third‑party mining pools, including the Foundry USA Pool (‘Foundry’) and Luxor…” — SEC (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1819989/000095017024025442/cifr-20231231.htm)  
- Mar 2024, FY2023 Form 10‑K: Bitcoin included in current assets; proceeds from coins sold promptly in operating cash flows; FIFO sales — SEC (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1819989/000095017024025442/cifr-20231231.htm)  
- Mar 2024, FY2023 Form 10‑K: Agreement to purchase 37,396 Antminer T21 miners for H1 2025 delivery (≈7.1 EH/s) — SEC (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1819989/000095017024025442/cifr-20231231.htm)  
- May 7, 2024: “First Quarter 2024 GAAP Net Income of $40m; Revenues of $48m; current self‑mining hash rate ~7.7 EH/s.” — Cipher IR press release (https://investors.ciphermining.com/news-releases/news-release-details/cipher-mining-provides-first-quarter-2024-business-update/)

**Financial news / other:**
- Oct 3, 2024: “Cipher … mined ~155 bitcoin in September 2024.” — GlobeNewswire (September operational update) (https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2024/10/03/2957741/0/en/Cipher-Mining-Announces-September-2024-Operational-Update.html)  
- Nov 1, 2024: Market wrap around Q3 call and strategy (HPC development, BTC inventory context). — Investing.com (https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-cipher-mining-pivots-to-hpc-amid-bitcoin-volatility-93CH-3697800)  
- Jan 2026: “Bitcoin mining stock prices rally… Core Scientific and Cipher Mining recorded gains…” — Yahoo Finance (proxy behavior of miner equities) (https://finance.yahoo.com/news/bitcoin-mining-stock-prices-rally-144548975.html)

</small>

## MONEX GROUP

* Rank: 15 | Expo: 10.87 | id_str: JP3869970008
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 3 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q3 FY2020 earnings call (Jan–Mar 2021), FY2020–FY2021 results presentations/analyst call transcripts, integrated report, financial news (2021)

### Firm Overview
Monex Group, Inc. is a Tokyo‑based financial services holding company listed on TSE Prime that operates online brokerage and related businesses in Japan, the U.S. and Asia. In April 2018 it acquired Coincheck, a major Japan‑regulated crypto‑asset exchange; during 2017–2024, crypto activities were consolidated within Monex through the Crypto Asset segment led by Coincheck.

### Exposure Channels Identified
- Crypto financial services (finserv): Monex directly provides crypto trading, market‑making/RFQ, custody and related retail services in Japan through Coincheck; management repeatedly highlights the Crypto Asset segment as a core pillar of group profit alongside Japan and U.S. brokerage. The Q3 FY2020 call explicitly attributes profit growth to Coincheck and details spreads/market‑making economics; FY2020/FY2021 results presentations quantify the segment’s revenue surge and drivers (booming crypto markets, altcoins, marketing). ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/tse/8698/investor-relations/earnings-call/q3-2021))  
- Other categories: No evidence of mining operations or crypto‑specific hardware/hosting; activities such as NFT marketplace and IEOs remained within Coincheck’s platform and thus fall under financial services rather than non‑financial “bizserv.” ([monexgroup.jp](https://www.monexgroup.jp/en/investor/ir_library/presentation/main/06/teaserItems1/0/linkList/010/link/SA000277_2021Apr27_a_02_pdf_eng.pdf))  
- Investor‑base amplifier: In February 2021, Bloomberg reported Monex shares “tracking the ups and downs of Bitcoin” and rallying alongside the crypto surge due to its Coincheck unit, supporting that the stock acts as a crypto proxy for investors. ([bloomberg.com](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2021-02-24/bitcoin-bet-sparks-rally-at-japan-s-most-expensive-brokerage?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Monex’s crypto exposure in 2017–2024 is unambiguously through operating a regulated exchange/brokerage (Coincheck) and related platform services (custody, RFQ/market‑making, NFT marketplace, IEOs). Earnings calls and IR materials show crypto financial services as a key profit driver in bull periods and a managed contributor otherwise; there is no evidence of balance‑sheet HODL distinct from operations, mining, or crypto‑targeted infrastructure sales. Accordingly, finserv = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. The Bloomberg coverage of shares moving with Bitcoin justifies invbase = 1. ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/tse/8698/investor-relations/earnings-call/q3-2021))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Transcript availability is mixed for earlier years, but the Q3 FY2020 call transcript and FY2020–FY2021 results/analyst call documents contain clear, direct quotes linking profits to Coincheck and detailing crypto economics. ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/tse/8698/investor-relations/earnings-call/q3-2021))  
- Coincheck’s subsequent listing steps occurred after 2024; this classification focuses strictly on the 2017–2024 window when Coincheck remained consolidated within Monex. ([coincheckgroup.com](https://www.coincheckgroup.com/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001213900-22-069898/ea168210-425_coincheck.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jan 2021 (Q3 FY2020): “The biggest driver for the growth of the profitability was … the Crypto Asset business or Coincheck… pretax profit became 3.3x Q‑on‑Q, ending JPY 2.4 billion.” — AlphaSpread transcript (Q3 FY2020) (https://www.alphaspread.com/security/tse/8698/investor-relations/earnings-call/q3-2021)  
- Jan 2021 (Q3 FY2020): “The major factor of this revenue increase came from net trading income in Crypto Asset segment, where the cryptocurrency market was very active.” — AlphaSpread transcript (Q3 FY2020) (https://www.alphaspread.com/security/tse/8698/investor-relations/earnings-call/q3-2021)  
- Apr 27, 2021 (FY2020 results/analyst call transcript): “Operating revenue [crypto asset segment] increased significantly… Altcoin’s revenue growth contributed… [Q&A:] profitability has increased because Altcoins have a wider spread than Bitcoin.” — Monex IR presentation with transcripted remarks (https://www.monexgroup.jp/en/investor/ir_library/presentation/main/06/teaserItems1/0/linkList/010/link/SA000277_2021Apr27_a_02_pdf_eng.pdf)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Apr 2018: Monex “Successfully Completed Acquisition of Coincheck, Inc.” — IR News (https://www.monexgroup.jp/en/news_release/irnews/auto_20180416412613.html)  
- FY2021 Integrated Report: Crypto Asset segment overview and KPIs (Coincheck within Monex Group). — Monex IR (https://www.monexgroup.jp/en/investor/ir_library/annual_report/main/0/teaserItems1/00/linkList/0114/link/IntegratedReport2021_EN_A3.pdf)  
- Nov 8, 2022: SEC 425 filing (Coincheck SPAC materials) reproducing Monex segment data, including Crypto Asset metrics (historical). — SEC (https://www.coincheckgroup.com/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001213900-22-069898/ea168210-425_coincheck.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- Feb 24, 2021: “Shares of Monex Group Inc. have been tracking the ups and downs of Bitcoin… crypto exchange Coincheck has seen profit jump.” — Bloomberg (https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2021-02-24/bitcoin-bet-sparks-rally-at-japan-s-most-expensive-brokerage)  
- Jul 28, 2021: “Monex Nets $100M First‑Quarter Profit Driven by Crypto Trading,” attributing results to Coincheck and crypto volatility. — CoinDesk (https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2021/07/28/monex-nets-100m-first-quarter-profit-driven-by-crypto-trading)  
- Oct 27, 2023: Monex quarterly highlights showing Crypto Asset segment contribution within consolidated results. — TDnet/Monex IR (https://post.tokyoipo.com/tdnet/20231027/202310271515/20231026572547/140120231026572547.pdf)

</small>

## INTCHAINS GROUP ADR 1:2

* Rank: 16 | Expo: 10.85 | id_str: US45828E1047
* Weight summary: holders 0.2, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.8, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 5 | AssetShare: 2
* Primary sources: 20-F (FY2024), Q4 2024 earnings call transcript, Q4 2024 results press release, Goldshell asset acquisition release

### Firm Overview
Intchains Group Limited is a Cayman Islands–incorporated, Shanghai‑based company listed on Nasdaq (ICG) that develops and sells altcoin mining machines incorporating its own high‑performance ASIC chips, operating under a fabless model. In 2024 the company also began a strategic program to accumulate Ethereum on its balance sheet while continuing to expand Web3‑adjacent products under the Goldshell brand. ([ir.intchains.com](https://ir.intchains.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/20-F-2024.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
- Crypto infrastructure (infrastr): Management states that since mid‑2024 revenue has been generated entirely from altcoin mining machines integrating the company’s ASICs; the firm also acquired Goldshell brand assets to expand downstream manufacturing and overseas sales of mining hardware. ([ir.intchains.com](https://ir.intchains.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/20-F-2024.pdf))  
- Crypto holders (holders): The company adopted an Ethereum accumulation strategy in 2024 and reported non‑stablecoin cryptocurrency assets of RMB148.8 million as of December 31, 2024 (primarily ETH, including staked ETH), and recognized fair‑value gains on cryptocurrency in 2024. ([ir.intchains.com](https://ir.intchains.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/%E3%80%90V2-Final%E3%80%9124Q4-earnings.pdf))  
- Other categories: There is no evidence of own‑account crypto mining, and while wallet/payments pilots (Goldshell Wallet/Goldshell Pay) were launched, management indicated these were early‑stage and not expected to contribute materially in 2024; thus no finserv weight is assigned for the sample period. ([ir.intchains.com](https://ir.intchains.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/%E3%80%90V2-Final%E3%80%9124Q4-earnings.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Intchains’ economic exposure in 2017–2024 is dominated by sales of crypto‑mining hardware, clearly fitting the infrastructure channel; management disclosures show all 2024 revenue coming from integrated altcoin mining machines after discontinuing standalone chip sales. At the same time, the firm’s deliberate Ethereum treasury program (including staking) created a measurable balance‑sheet position and P&L fair‑value impact by year‑end 2024. Accordingly, infrastr = 0.8 to reflect the core revenue driver, and holders = 0.2 to capture the treasury exposure; miners, finserv and bizserv remain 0.0. ([ir.intchains.com](https://ir.intchains.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/20-F-2024.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Transcript coverage before the 2023 IPO is naturally limited; classification relies on FY2024 transcript and filings.  
- Web3/payments initiatives (e.g., Goldshell Wallet/Pay) were in trial in late 2024; if these become revenue‑generating later, a future finserv weight could be warranted, but not within the 2017–2024 window. ([ir.intchains.com](https://ir.intchains.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/%E3%80%90V2-Final%E3%80%9124Q4-earnings.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 27, 2025 (Q4 2024 call): “We will keep accumulating Ethereum and develop our new miners… we remain committed to upgrading our Dogecoin series mining machines.” — Investing.com transcript (https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-intchains-adr-reports-strong-q4-2024-growth-93CH-3898523) ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-intchains-adr-reports-strong-q4-2024-growth-93CH-3898523))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- FY2024 Form 20‑F: “We are an innovative altcoins development company… provision of altcoin mining products, the strategic acquisition and holding of Ethereum‑based cryptocurrencies…” and disclosure of crypto holdings (ETH, staked ETH, BTC; non‑stablecoin fair value RMB148.8m as of Dec 31, 2024). — Intchains IR (PDF) (https://ir.intchains.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/20-F-2024.pdf) ([ir.intchains.com](https://ir.intchains.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/20-F-2024.pdf))  
- Feb 27, 2025: Q4 & FY2024 results release noting “Cryptocurrency Assets… approximately 5,702 ETH‑based cryptocurrencies” and fair‑value gains. — GlobeNewswire (https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/02/27/3034261/0/en/Intchains-Group-Limited-Reports-Fourth-Quarter-and-Full-Year-2024-Unaudited-Financial-Results.html) ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/02/27/3034261/0/en/Intchains-Group-Limited-Reports-Fourth-Quarter-and-Full-Year-2024-Unaudited-Financial-Results.html?utm_source=openai))  
- May 2023 (FY2022) Form 20‑F: business description as fabless designer of high‑performance ASICs for blockchain applications. — SEC EDGAR (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1895597/000119312523123984/d486158d20f.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1895597/000119312523123984/d486158d20f.htm))

**Financial news / other:**
- Dec 8, 2023: Asset acquisition agreement for Goldshell brand–related Web3 infrastructure assets to expand mining‑hardware footprint and downstream operations. — GlobeNewswire (https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2023/12/08/2793087/0/en/Intchains-Expands-WEB3-Industry-Presence-Acquires-Goldshell-Brand-related-Assets.html) ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2023/12/08/2793087/0/en/Intchains-Expands-WEB3-Industry-Presence-Acquires-Goldshell-Brand-related-Assets.html?utm_source=openai))  
- Feb 27, 2025: Company investor deck and call materials highlighting 2024 shift to mining machines and ETH treasury strategy. — Intchains IR (PDF) (https://ir.intchains.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/%E3%80%90V2-Final%E3%80%9124Q4-earnings.pdf) (https://ir.intchains.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/ICG_Q4%2724_PPT.pdf) ([ir.intchains.com](https://ir.intchains.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/%E3%80%90V2-Final%E3%80%9124Q4-earnings.pdf))

## FRMO

* Rank: 17 | Expo: 10.83 | id_str: US30262F2056
* Weight summary: holders 0.4, miners 0.6, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: 10‑K/annual report (FY2024), Q3 2024 and Q3 2018/Q3 2021 earnings call transcripts, FRMO crypto exposure summaries (2023–2024), Winland Holdings OTC filings (2024), FY2024 results press release

### Firm Overview
FRMO Corp is a U.S. OTC‑listed holding company affiliated with Horizon Kinetics that owns stakes in exchanges and investment vehicles and, since the late 2010s, has built meaningful exposure to digital assets and cryptocurrency mining directly and through investees. As of May 31, 2024, FRMO reported $10.98 million of digital assets at fair value and recognized dedicated “digital mining assets,” reflecting its mining activities. ([frmocorp.com](https://www.frmocorp.com/_content/10k/FRMO_Annual_Report_2024.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
- Miners: Management states FRMO is “building a crypto business” and aims to be “a cryptocurrency mining company,” with mining conducted directly (digital mining assets of about $1.14 million and FY2024 mining revenue of ~$450,000) and indirectly via significant ownership in Winland Holdings and an investment in Consensus Mining & Seigniorage. ([frmocorp.com](https://frmocorp.com/_content/letters/2024_Q3_FRMO_Transcript.pdf))  
- Holders: FRMO holds cryptocurrencies on balance sheet and via investment vehicles; the FY2024 annual report discloses 157.4 BTC and other coins at fair value totaling $10.98 million, and the company periodically publishes crypto holding summaries. ([frmocorp.com](https://www.frmocorp.com/_content/10k/FRMO_Annual_Report_2024.pdf))  
- Supporting evidence: Winland’s OTC filings document multiple transfers of mining rigs from FRMO to Winland and detail Winland’s mining operations, reinforcing FRMO’s miner exposure through affiliates. ([otcmarkets.com](https://www.otcmarkets.com/file/company/financial-report/398517/content))

### Weight Assignment
Within the 2017–2024 window, FRMO’s narrative and disclosures emphasize cryptocurrency mining as a developing operating focus, executed both directly (dedicated mining equipment and revenue line) and via material stakes in mining entities; therefore miners = 0.6. Concurrently, FRMO maintains a deliberate on‑balance‑sheet crypto position (primarily BTC) and reports fair‑value changes, warranting holders = 0.4. No evidence supports finserv, bizserv, or infrastr classifications for the sample period. ([frmocorp.com](https://frmocorp.com/_content/letters/2024_Q3_FRMO_Transcript.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
A portion of exposure is indirect (e.g., Winland/Consensus Mining stakes and crypto held via funds/ETPs), which can blur look‑through measurement versus FRMO’s standalone operations; weighting emphasizes stated strategy and audited balance‑sheet items. FRMO’s own transcripts and filings provide most evidence; third‑party transcript coverage is limited for some years. Figures cited (e.g., BTC units, digital asset fair value) reflect the FY2024 reporting date and may have changed subsequently, but remain within the January 2017–December 2024 sample. ([frmocorp.com](https://www.frmocorp.com/_content/10k/FRMO_Annual_Report_2024.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Apr 16, 2024 (Q3 FY2024): “We’re actually building a crypto business… Winland is now largely a crypto miner… about $1.3 million of digital mining assets that we actually mine for ourselves.” — FRMO transcript (PDF) (https://frmocorp.com/_content/letters/2024_Q3_FRMO_Transcript.pdf) ([frmocorp.com](https://frmocorp.com/_content/letters/2024_Q3_FRMO_Transcript.pdf))  
- Apr 22, 2021 (Q3 FY2021): “The following coins we have owned directly in FRMO itself. We have 88.5 bitcoin that we mined… 714 Litecoin… 35 Ethereum…” — FRMO transcript (PDF) (https://frmocorp.com/_content/letters/2021_Q3_FRMO_Transcript.pdf) ([frmocorp.com](https://frmocorp.com/_content/letters/2021_Q3_FRMO_Transcript.pdf))  
- Apr 17, 2018 (Q3 FY2018): “The computer equipment is comprised entirely of the servers that we’re using for cryptocurrency mining… In the HK Cryptocurrency Mining LLC, the idea is to mine the cryptocurrency, then sell it that day… On the equity side… the idea… is to hang onto it.” — FRMO transcript (PDF) (https://frmocorp.com/_content/letters/2018_Q3_FRMO_Transcript.pdf) ([frmocorp.com](https://frmocorp.com/_content/letters/2018_Q3_FRMO_Transcript.pdf))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- FY2024 Annual Report (audited): Digital assets at fair value $10,978,796; Bitcoin 157.4 units; digital mining assets $1,139,972; mining revenue $450,086. — FRMO (PDF) (https://www.frmocorp.com/_content/10k/FRMO_Annual_Report_2024.pdf) ([frmocorp.com](https://www.frmocorp.com/_content/10k/FRMO_Annual_Report_2024.pdf))  
- Aug 15, 2024: Fiscal 2024 results and annual meeting announcement referencing financials and digital assets caution. — Nasdaq Press Release (https://www.nasdaq.com/press-release/frmo-corp-announces-fiscal-2024-results-and-annual-meeting-2024-08-15) ([nasdaq.com](https://www.nasdaq.com/press-release/frmo-corp-announces-fiscal-2024-results-and-annual-meeting-2024-08-15?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Feb 29, 2024: FRMO Cryptocurrency & TPL Holdings Summary (BTC and other assets held directly/indirectly). — FRMO (PDF) (https://frmocorp.com/_content/letters/FRMO%20exposure%20to%20crypto%20Feb%2029%202024.pdf) ([frmocorp.com](https://frmocorp.com/_content/letters/FRMO%20exposure%20to%20crypto%20Feb%2029%202024.pdf))  
- Nov 30, 2023: FRMO Top Five Holdings + CMSC (Winland, GBTC among top positions). — FRMO (PDF) (https://frmocorp.com/_content/letters/FRMO%20Corp%20-%20Top%205%2020231130.pdf) ([frmocorp.com](https://frmocorp.com/_content/letters/FRMO%20Corp%20-%20Top%205%2020231130.pdf))  
- Mar 31, 2024: Winland Holdings OTC quarterly filing documenting multiple 2023–2024 miner transfers from FRMO to Winland and Winland’s mining fleet. — OTC Markets (PDF) (https://www.otcmarkets.com/file/company/financial-report/398517/content) ([otcmarkets.com](https://www.otcmarkets.com/file/company/financial-report/398517/content))

## MARA HOLDINGS

* Rank: 18 | Expo: 10.30 | id_str: US5657881067
* Weight summary: holders 0.2, miners 0.8, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 5 | AssetShare: 3
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (FY2021, FY2023), Q4 2023 earnings call, monthly production/treasury updates (Jan–May 2024), financial news (Forbes Mar 2024; CNBC Jan 2024)

### Firm Overview
MARA Holdings is a U.S.-listed company that operates large-scale Bitcoin mining facilities and holds Bitcoin on its balance sheet; it was formerly Marathon Patent Group and changed its name to Marathon Digital Holdings in March 2021 before rebranding to MARA Holdings in August 2024. The company’s SEC filings describe a business dedicated to securing and supporting the Bitcoin ecosystem through mining and related activities, with Bitcoin holdings managed as part of its corporate treasury. ([materials.proxyvote.com](https://materials.proxyvote.com/Approved/565788/20240429/10K_580207.PDF))

### Exposure Channels Identified
- Miners: Management consistently frames the business around Bitcoin production, hash rate growth, and site acquisitions; FY2023 revenue and operating metrics were driven primarily by increased Bitcoin production and higher BTC prices. Monthly updates in early 2024 detail production and energized capacity expansions. ([insidermonkey.com](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/marathon-digital-holdings-inc-nasdaqmara-q4-2023-earnings-call-transcript-2-1267441/))  
- Holders: The FY2023 Form 10‑K discloses fair value accounting for crypto assets and a long‑term treasury approach, with $639.7 million fair value of Bitcoin holdings at December 31, 2023; filings also note hedging of a portion of holdings and that the company may sell BTC periodically for operating needs. Production updates in 2024 state the intention to sell a portion of holdings to support operations, while March–April 2024 press releases reported 15,000–17,000+ BTC held. ([materials.proxyvote.com](https://materials.proxyvote.com/Approved/565788/20240429/10K_580207.PDF))  
- Investor base linkage: Financial media frequently characterizes Marathon as closely tied to Bitcoin price moves; coverage in March 2024 noted its shares “likely to move in sync” with BTC, and miners’ stocks moved with BTC in January 2024. ([forbes.com](https://www.forbes.com/sites/petercohan/2024/03/30/marathon-digitals-stock-price-outpaces-bitcoins-150-rise-as-halving-looms/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence from earnings calls and SEC filings shows Marathon as a pure‑play Bitcoin miner through 2017–2024, with operations, capex, and guidance centered on mining; thus miners = 0.8. The firm also maintains a deliberate on‑balance‑sheet Bitcoin position (fair‑value accounted, hedged at times) and articulates a treasury strategy (accumulate via production, sell selectively for operating needs), warranting holders = 0.2. There is no credible evidence that the company directly provides financial services, non‑financial services to third parties, or sells infrastructure to other miners during the sample window; those categories remain 0.0. Media depiction of the stock as a Bitcoin proxy supports invbase = 1. ([insidermonkey.com](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/marathon-digital-holdings-inc-nasdaqmara-q4-2023-earnings-call-transcript-2-1267441/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Marathon’s BTC holdings fluctuate due to operating sales and market conditions, and hedging may affect reported gains/losses; the weighting emphasizes sustained mining operations while acknowledging an explicit treasury posture. Some third‑party transcript archives restrict access; however, at least two independent transcript sources were located for the period (2017, 2023). Name changes in 2021 and 2024 required care in locating filings and press releases but do not affect classification. ([materials.proxyvote.com](https://materials.proxyvote.com/Approved/565788/20240429/10K_580207.PDF))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 28, 2017 (Q3 2017): “Marathon Patent Group has traditionally been in the business of patent licensing. The company that we’re buying, Global Bit Ventures, is … the business of digital assets and cryptocurrency mining.” — Marathon Patent Group Q3 2017 call (The Motley Fool) [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2017/11/28/marathon-patent-group-mara-q3-2017-earnings-confer.aspx) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2017/11/28/marathon-patent-group-mara-q3-2017-earnings-confer.aspx))  
- Feb 28, 2024 (Q4 2023): “Fourth quarter revenues were a record $157 million… driven by a 172% increase in Bitcoin production… In 2023, we increased our Bitcoin holdings 24%… As one of the largest holders of Bitcoin among publicly traded companies, we expect the new fair‑value accounting… to impact our bottom line.” — Marathon Q4 2023 call [Insider Monkey](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/marathon-digital-holdings-inc-nasdaqmara-q4-2023-earnings-call-transcript-2-1267441/) ([insidermonkey.com](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/marathon-digital-holdings-inc-nasdaqmara-q4-2023-earnings-call-transcript-2-1267441/))

**Filings (10‑K / SEC / press releases):**

[Marathon 10‑K PDF](https://materials.proxyvote.com/Approved/565788/20240429/10K_580207.PDF) ([materials.proxyvote.com](https://materials.proxyvote.com/Approved/565788/20240429/10K_580207.PDF))  
- FY2023 Form 10‑K: “The Company hedged a portion of its bitcoin holdings… while maintaining a long‑term strategy of maximizing the size and value of the Company’s treasury… [and] intends to sell bitcoin… to cover monthly operating costs…” [Marathon 10‑K PDF](https://materials.proxyvote.com/Approved/565788/20240429/10K_580207.PDF) ([materials.proxyvote.com](https://materials.proxyvote.com/Approved/565788/20240429/10K_580207.PDF))  
- Jan 5, 2024 update: “The Company intends to continue to sell a portion of its bitcoin holdings in future periods to support monthly operations…” — January 2024 production/treasury update. [Marathon IR](https://ir.mara.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/1341/marathon-digital-holdings-announces-bitcoin-production-and-mining-operation-updates-for-january-2024) ([ir.mara.com](https://ir.mara.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/1341/marathon-digital-holdings-announces-bitcoin-production-and-mining-operation-updates-for-january-2024?utm_source=openai))  
- Apr 4, 2024 update: “As of March 31, 2024 the Company holds a total of 17,381 unrestricted BTC.” — March 2024 production update. [GlobeNewswire](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2024/04/04/2857772/0/en/Marathon-Digital-Holdings-Announces-Bitcoin-Production-and-Mining-Operation-Updates-for-March-2024.html) ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2024/04/04/2857772/0/en/Marathon-Digital-Holdings-Announces-Bitcoin-Production-and-Mining-Operation-Updates-for-March-2024.html?utm_source=openai))

## VOYAGER DIGITAL (OTC)

* Rank: 19 | Expo: 9.76 | id_str: CA92919V4055
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 5 | AssetShare: 4
* Primary sources: MD&A filings (FY2021 interim), FY2022 earnings call (Feb 2022), financial news (2022–2023)

### Firm Overview
Voyager Digital Ltd. was a Canada-listed, U.S.-operated cryptocurrency brokerage that offered retail trading, custody, and yield-earning programs via a mobile app. The company filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection in July 2022 following industry turmoil and counterparty exposures, and its OTC shares subsequently traded under the VYGVQ symbol. ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2022/07/06/crypto-firm-voyager-digital-files-for-chapter-11-bankruptcy-protection.html?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Voyager’s business model centered on crypto financial services: operating a brokerage platform, safeguarding customer digital assets, and generating fee and interest income tied to crypto trading and lending programs. Management disclosures show revenue driven by trading spreads/fees and interest from custodians as assets under management grew, and balance sheet line items included “digital currencies and fiat” and “digital currencies and fiat on loan.” These are operational holdings that back products, falling squarely under the finserv mechanism rather than holders. Media and court coverage throughout 2022–2023 consistently described Voyager as a crypto broker/lender undergoing restructuring after counterparties defaulted. ([webfiles.thecse.com](https://webfiles.thecse.com/Voyager_Digital_Ltd_MDA_3.31.21.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv is assigned 1.0 given Voyager directly provided crypto brokerage, custody and yield products, and held digital assets and loans to support those products. No evidence indicates proprietary mining or hardware/infrastructure provision, so miners and infrastr remain 0.0. Any crypto assets on the balance sheet existed to serve the brokerage/lending operations and therefore do not trigger a separate holders weight under the exclusion rules. Given frequent characterization of the stock as a crypto play, invbase is set to 1. ([webfiles.thecse.com](https://webfiles.thecse.com/Voyager_Digital_Ltd_MDA_3.31.21.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Post–July 2022 bankruptcy, investor-relations materials and transcripts became less accessible; some transcript archives are gated, though at least one 2021–2022 earnings-call transcript was identified. Voyager filed primarily on Canadian platforms rather than the U.S. SEC, so MD&A documents were used in lieu of 10‑K/20‑F filings. AssetShare is inferred from March 31, 2021 disclosures showing digital assets and loans dominated current assets; later balances during bankruptcy may differ, but classification remains finserv. ([seekingalpha.com](https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VYGVQ/earnings/transcripts?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 15, 2022 (Q2 FY2022): “Good day and welcome to this Voyager Digital Fiscal Year Second Quarter 2022 Earnings Conference Call.” — SA transcript heading. [Seeking Alpha](https://seekingalpha.com/article/4487075-voyager-digital-ltd-vygvf-ceo-steve-ehrlich-on-q2-2022-results-earning-calls-transcript) ([seekingalpha.com](https://seekingalpha.com/article/4487075-voyager-digital-ltd-vygvf-ceo-steve-ehrlich-on-q2-2022-results-earning-calls-transcript?utm_source=openai))  
- Nov 16, 2021 (Q1 FY2022): Transcript listing (gated) — confirms call and timing. [MarketScreener](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/VOYAGER-DIGITAL-LTD-66346263/news/Transcript-Voyager-Digital-Ltd-Q1-2022-Earnings-Call-Nov-16-2021-37755485/) ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/VOYAGER-DIGITAL-LTD-66346263/news/Transcript-Voyager-Digital-Ltd-Q1-2022-Earnings-Call-Nov-16-2021-37755485/))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- MD&A for the three and nine months ended March 31, 2021: Current assets $2.7B “primarily a result of $2.4B increase in digital currencies and fiat and digital currencies and fiat on loan”; quarterly revenue $60.4M driven by fees ($53.7M) and interest ($6.7M); AUM $2.45B. [Voyager Digital MD&A PDF](https://webfiles.thecse.com/Voyager_Digital_Ltd_MDA_3.31.21.pdf) ([webfiles.thecse.com](https://webfiles.thecse.com/Voyager_Digital_Ltd_MDA_3.31.21.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**
- Jul 6, 2022: Voyager files for Chapter 11; described as a crypto brokerage. [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2022/07/06/crypto-firm-voyager-digital-files-for-chapter-11-bankruptcy-protection.html) ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2022/07/06/crypto-firm-voyager-digital-files-for-chapter-11-bankruptcy-protection.html?utm_source=openai))  
- Jul 6, 2022: Bankruptcy filing context and causes; described as crypto broker. [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2022-07-06/voyager-digital-holdings-files-for-chapter-11-bankruptcy) ([bloomberg.com](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2022-07-06/voyager-digital-holdings-files-for-chapter-11-bankruptcy?utm_source=openai))  
- Apr 25, 2023: Binance.US terminates agreement to acquire Voyager assets; plan to return assets directly to customers. [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2023-04-25/binance-us-terminates-deal-to-buy-bankrupt-crypto-firm-voyager) | [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/policy/2023/04/26/voyager-lawyers-surprised-binanceus-canceled-1b-deal) ([bloomberg.com](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2023-04-25/binance-us-terminates-deal-to-buy-bankrupt-crypto-firm-voyager?utm_source=openai))  
- Jul 29, 2022: FDIC and Federal Reserve order Voyager to remove misleading deposit‑insurance claims. [Axios](https://www.axios.com/2022/07/29/voyager-bankruptcy-regulator-deposits) ([axios.com](https://www.axios.com/2022/07/29/voyager-bankruptcy-regulator-deposits?utm_source=openai))  
- Nov 8, 2021: “When Bitcoin and Ethereum hit all-time highs, Voyager stock also did the same.” — investor-oriented coverage linking stock to crypto moves. [The Motley Fool Canada](https://www.fool.ca/2021/11/08/why-voyager-digital-stock-soared-48-in-october/) ([fool.ca](https://www.fool.ca/2021/11/08/why-voyager-digital-stock-soared-48-in-october/?utm_source=openai))

## CANAAN ADR 1:15

* Rank: 20 | Expo: 9.75 | id_str: US1347481020
* Weight summary: holders 0.1, miners 0.2, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.7, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 5 | AssetShare: 3
* Primary sources: 20-F filings (2022–2024), Q1 2024 earnings call, Q4 2024 earnings call, company press releases, CoinDesk coverage

### Firm Overview
Canaan Inc. is a Nasdaq‑listed designer and manufacturer of ASIC‑based Bitcoin mining machines sold under the Avalon brand, with operations based in Singapore and global sales to mining customers. In recent years it has also developed a company‑operated Bitcoin mining business and reports cryptocurrency assets and crypto‑related receivables on its balance sheet. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1780652/000141057825000805/can-20241231x20f.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
- Infrastructure: Management discloses that sales of Bitcoin mining machines and related parts comprised roughly 83% of revenue in 2024 (and 95%/84% in 2022/2023), indicating the core business is supplying ASIC hardware to miners. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1780652/000141057825000805/can-20241231x20f.htm))  
- Miners: The company operates self‑mining, reports deployed/energized hashrate, and recognizes “mining revenue” (US$32.5m in 2022, US$34.0m in 2023, US$44.0m in 2024). Q4 2024 remarks note 186 BTC mined and hashrate around 5.4 EH/s. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1780652/000141057825000805/can-20241231x20f.htm))  
- Holders: On the Q1 2024 call, management stated it held over 1,000 BTC and that policy was to hold mined coins (using BTC primarily to pay electricity and otherwise “no plans to sell”), and the 20‑F shows cryptocurrency assets and crypto receivables recognized on the balance sheet. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/CAN/documents/transcripts/q1-2024))

### Weight Assignment
Infrastructure is set to 0.7 reflecting that ASIC miner sales dominate revenue and are emphasized across filings. The firm also runs a self‑mining segment with disclosed BTC mined/operational hashrate, warranting 0.2 to miners. A 0.1 holders weight captures the explicit treasury‑style policy to retain BTC beyond operational needs and the reported cryptocurrency asset balances. No evidence supports finserv or non‑financial business services activities. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1780652/000141057825000805/can-20241231x20f.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Some earnings transcripts are hosted by third‑party aggregators; quotes are taken from accessible Q1 2024 and Q4 2024 materials. The line between miners and holders is judged based on management’s stated HODL policy and balance‑sheet presentation; mined coin held temporarily would otherwise fall under miners only. Regulatory changes (e.g., Kazakhstan licensing) have intermittently affected self‑mining operations but do not alter the dominant infrastructure exposure. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1780652/000110465923121395/tm2331640d1_ex99-1.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q1 2024 (May 2024): “The Bitcoins we held as our own holding asset kept growing… reached a record high of 1,057… our company policy is only use the Bitcoin to pay the mining electricity bill… we will hold [the rest]… we currently have no plans to sell.” — Fintool transcript. [Fintool](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/CAN/documents/transcripts/q1-2024) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/CAN/documents/transcripts/q1-2024))  
- Q4 2024 results call: “We mined 186 bitcoins… our total installed mining hash rate reached 5.4 exahash per second… our efficient mining operations also helped grow our bitcoin holdings.” — Investing.com transcript. [Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-canaan-inc-reports-q4-2024-results-misses-eps-forecast-93CH-3949647) ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-canaan-inc-reports-q4-2024-results-misses-eps-forecast-93CH-3949647))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- 2024 Form 20‑F: “In 2022, 2023 and 2024, sales of our Bitcoin mining machines and related parts and accessories accounted for 94.8%, 83.1% and 82.6% of our revenues, respectively.” [SEC 20‑F](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1780652/000141057825000805/can-20241231x20f.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1780652/000141057825000805/can-20241231x20f.htm))  
- 2024 Form 20‑F: “We… started with the Bitcoin mining business… aim to develop the Bitcoin mining business as the second engine…”; revenue breakdown includes “Mining revenue” for 2022–2024. [SEC 20‑F](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1780652/000141057825000805/can-20241231x20f.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1780652/000141057825000805/can-20241231x20f.htm))  
- 2024 Form 20‑F: “As of December 31, 2024, we held cryptocurrency assets with a fair value of US$61.8 million and cryptocurrency receivables with an aggregate fair value of US$69.6 million”; total assets ~US$463.0m. [SEC 20‑F](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1780652/000141057825000805/can-20241231x20f.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1780652/000141057825000805/can-20241231x20f.htm))  
- 6‑K (Nov 2023): Temporary shutdown of ~2.0 EH/s in Kazakhstan; later obtained Type II license for mining hardware owners to conduct bitcoin mining. [SEC 6‑K exhibit](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1780652/000110465923121395/tm2331640d1_ex99-1.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1780652/000110465923121395/tm2331640d1_ex99-1.htm))

**Financial news / other:**
- Jun 23, 2021: Company begins self‑mining operations in Kazakhstan as part of diversification. [Nasdaq](https://www.nasdaq.com/articles/canaan-to-begin-mining-bitcoin-itself-setting-up-operations-in-kazakhstan-2021-06-23) ([nasdaq.com](https://www.nasdaq.com/articles/canaan-to-begin-mining-bitcoin-itself-setting-up-operations-in-kazakhstan-2021-06-23?utm_source=openai))  
- Nov 22, 2021: Canaan delivers 2,000 Avalon miners to Kazakhstan; joint operations planned. [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/11/22/asic-maker-canaan-delivers-2000-mining-rigs-to-kazakhstan) ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/11/22/asic-maker-canaan-delivers-2000-mining-rigs-to-kazakhstan?utm_source=openai))  
- Dec 2024 update: Month‑end deployed hashrate ~5.44 EH/s; BTC holdings disclosure and FY2025 guidance. [Canaan IR](https://investor.canaan-creative.com/news-releases/news-release-details/canaan-inc-announces-december-2024-bitcoin-production-and-mining/) ([investor.canaan-creative.com](https://investor.canaan-creative.com/news-releases/news-release-details/canaan-inc-announces-december-2024-bitcoin-production-and-mining/?utm_source=openai))  
- FY/Q4 2024 results release: mining revenue US$15.3m; 186 BTC mined in Q4. [Canaan IR](https://investor.canaan-creative.com/news-releases/news-release-details/canaan-inc-reports-unaudited-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2024/) ([investor.canaan-creative.com](https://investor.canaan-creative.com/news-releases/news-release-details/canaan-inc-reports-unaudited-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2024/?utm_source=openai))  
- Sep 16, 2021: Coverage of Canaan as mining‑rig maker; investor reaction tied to guidance and sector conditions. [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/09/16/mining-rig-maker-canaans-stock-fell-11-despite-record-q2-profits) ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/09/16/mining-rig-maker-canaans-stock-fell-11-despite-record-q2-profits?utm_source=openai))

## WEM ADE

* Rank: 21 | Expo: 9.50 | id_str: KR7112040001
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.7, bizserv 0.3, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: Company IR presentations (2022 Q4; 2023 Q4), earnings call media coverage (Q4 2022 results), official WEMIX documentation

### Firm Overview
Wemade Co., Ltd. is a South Korean game developer and publisher listed on KOSDAQ that operates popular MMORPG IP while expanding into blockchain-based services under the WEMIX brand. The company runs its own mainnet (WEMIX 3.0) and related platforms including WEMIX PLAY, WEMIX.Fi, a stablecoin initiative (WEMIX$), and community/NFT services, tying game economies to digital-asset activity. ([wemade.com](https://wemade.com/wemix))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Finserv exposure is clear: management materials explicitly state the launch of the WEMIX 3.0 mainnet and WEMIX Dollar (stablecoin), plus the opening of WEMIX.Fi and NILE, and they disclose “Blockchain Platform” revenue recognized using a base WEMIX price and transactions through DEX/NFT/swap features on WEMIX PLAY. These activities involve issuing and handling digital assets, on-chain transactions, wallet services and DeFi functionality. ([irsvc.teletogether.com](https://irsvc.teletogether.com/wemade/pdf/wemade2022Q4_eng.pdf?1=&utm_source=openai))
Non-financial business services also apply: Wemade provides developer onboarding and platform tooling for games to integrate blockchain features and NFT trading into gameplay via WEMIX PLAY and broader ecosystem infrastructure. ([wemade.com](https://wemade.com/wemix))
Investor base sensitivity to crypto developments is documented: when Korean exchanges moved to delist WEMIX in late 2022, Wemade’s shares dropped sharply alongside the token, and management addressed the issue on the quarterly earnings call. ([koreajoongangdaily.joins.com](https://koreajoongangdaily.joins.com/2022/11/25/business/tech/Korea-Wemade-Wemix/20221125095404911.html?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv is set to 0.7 because the firm directly operates a blockchain mainnet, stablecoin and DeFi/NFT platforms and reports a specific “Blockchain Platform” revenue stream linked to WEMIX-based transactions. Bizserv is assigned 0.3 to capture the game-onboarding, developer tools and non-custodial platform services that support third‑party titles in the ecosystem. No evidence supports miners or hardware/energy infrastructure roles, and holdings are treated as operational to the platform rather than treasury-style investments, so holders = 0.0.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Earnings call quotes are sourced from reputable media coverage of the Q4 2022 results call; a full transcript PDF was not publicly retrievable from the IR site, though detailed IR slide decks were available. Segment disclosures show blockchain platform revenue but do not provide a clear balance-sheet split for digital assets, so AssetShare is conservatively coded as minor. Crypto exchange listing status for WEMIX changed multiple times after 2022; the classification focuses on 2017–2024 evidence. ([koreajoongangdaily.joins.com](https://koreajoongangdaily.joins.com/2023/02/15/culture/gamesWebtoons/Wemade/20230215160543593.html))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 15, 2023, Q4 2022 results call coverage: “I am sorry for everything that happened last year… We will create a transparent economical platform where anyone can easily check the exchange records.” — Korea JoongAng Daily. [Korea JoongAng Daily](https://koreajoongangdaily.joins.com/2023/02/15/culture/gamesWebtoons/Wemade/20230215160543593.html) ([koreajoongangdaily.joins.com](https://koreajoongangdaily.joins.com/2023/02/15/culture/gamesWebtoons/Wemade/20230215160543593.html))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- 4Q FY2023 & Annual Earnings Results deck
- 4Q FY2023 & Annual Earnings Results deck (Feb 6, 2024): “Base price of WEMIX for blockchain platform sales recognition in 4Q’23 is KRW 2,267… Blockchain platform sales is recognized from unearned revenue”; WEMIX PLAY transaction value defined as the sum of DEX, NFT and SWAP, with blockchain platform revenue +276% QoQ in 4Q’23. [Wemade IR deck PDF](https://file.wemade.com/homepage/upload/20240206223201032_FY23%204Q%20Earnings%20Release%20PT%28ENG%29.pdf).   
- 4Q FY2023 & Annual Earnings Results deck: update highlights include “Listed on KRW-based crypto exchange GOPAX and relisted on Korbit, Bithumb” and “Zero Minting of WEMIX to achieve true deflationary economy.” [Wemade IR deck PDF](https://file.wemade.com/homepage/upload/20240206223201032_FY23%204Q%20Earnings%20Release%20PT%28ENG%29.pdf). 

**Financial news / other:**
- Dec 8, 2022: Seoul court upholds DAXA exchanges’ decision to delist WEMIX; coverage details investor reaction and delisting context. [Korea JoongAng Daily](https://koreajoongangdaily.joins.com/2022/12/08/business/tech/Korea-Wemade-Wemix/20221208101718343.html). ([koreajoongangdaily.joins.com](https://koreajoongangdaily.joins.com/2022/12/08/business/tech/Korea-Wemade-Wemix/20221208101718343.html?utm_source=openai))  
- Feb 15, 2023: Results coverage notes loss in 2022, WEMIX delisting, share price impact, and CEO remarks on the call. [Korea JoongAng Daily](https://koreajoongangdaily.joins.com/2023/02/15/culture/gamesWebtoons/Wemade/20230215160543593.html).   
- Jan 2023: WEMIX Vesting Plan outlines token lockups, including WEMIX owned by Wemade locked for three years from Jan 2023. [PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/wemix-vesting-plan-2023-revealed-for-a-transparent-and-deflationary-tokenomics-301708867.html). ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/wemix-vesting-plan-2023-revealed-for-a-transparent-and-deflationary-tokenomics-301708867.html?utm_source=openai))  
- WEMIX ecosystem overview: WEMIX 3.0 mainnet (SPoA with 40 WONDERS), WEMIX PLAY, and other services supporting token swaps, NFTs, and developer integration. [Wemade (WEMIX) official site](https://wemade.com/wemix). 

**Earnings calls (transcript):**
- Q4 2023 call (Feb 6, 2024): “this year will be the year that the blockchain division solidifies the foundation for sustainable growth… revenue [grew] from $200,000 in July 2023 to $2 million in December”; “the blockchain business based on WEMIX 3.0 mainnet continues to expand,” and updates on exchange listings and “Zero Minting.” [AlphaSpread transcript](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/kosdaq/112040/investor-relations/earnings-call/q4-2023). ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/kosdaq/112040/investor-relations/earnings-call/q4-2023))

## LM FUNDING AMERICA

* Rank: 22 | Expo: 9.20 | id_str: US5020745031
* Weight summary: holders 0.3, miners 0.7, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 5 | AssetShare: 3
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2022–2023), Q2 2024 earnings call, company press releases (2023–2024)

### Firm Overview
LM Funding America, Inc. is a U.S.-based, Nasdaq‑listed company that operates a Bitcoin mining business alongside a legacy technology‑enabled specialty finance operation serving community associations. The company announced its mining strategy in September 2021 and commenced Bitcoin mining in late September 2022 through its US Digital Mining and Hosting subsidiary. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1640384/000095017023011149/lmfa-20221231.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Mining is the primary channel: management details mining fleet deployments, petahash capacity, quarterly Bitcoin production and power strategy on earnings calls, and the 10‑K breaks out “digital mining revenues” and cost metrics tied to hosting and energy. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-lm-funding-america-reports-q2-2024-results-93CH-3572801))  
A secondary channel is treasury-style Bitcoin holdings: management refers to a growing “hodl balance,” discloses coins held on the balance sheet, and discusses gains/losses tied to fair value or impairment, indicating balance‑sheet sensitivity to Bitcoin prices beyond purely transitory working inventory from mining. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-lm-funding-america-reports-q2-2024-results-93CH-3572801))  
There is no evidence the firm operates exchanges, custody for third parties, or crypto financial products, nor does it provide hardware/hosting to others as a standalone business segment in the 2017–2024 window. ([lmfunding.com](https://www.lmfunding.com/investors/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0000950170-24-038953/lmfa-20231231.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Miners = 0.7 reflects that Bitcoin mining is the dominant operating activity and revenue driver (2023 digital mining revenues ≈ $12.3 million of ≈ $13.0 million total). Holders = 0.3 captures explicit emphasis on maintaining and discussing a Bitcoin “hodl balance” and the impact of Bitcoin valuation changes on results, indicating balance‑sheet exposure that can independently move equity value. No support was found for finserv, bizserv or infrastr roles during 2017–2024, so those weights are set to 0.0. ([lmfunding.com](https://www.lmfunding.com/investors/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0000950170-24-038953/lmfa-20231231.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Some detailed transcript repositories are paywalled; however, the Q2 2024 earnings call transcript is publicly accessible and sufficient to anchor classification. The “holders” share is conservatively limited given most coins originate from mining, though management’s “hodl” framing and marked valuation effects justify a non‑zero allocation. Evidence after December 2024 (e.g., 2025 calls) was not used for coding per the sample period. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-lm-funding-america-reports-q2-2024-results-93CH-3572801))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 14, 2024, Q2 2024 earnings call: “we… successfully mined 44.1 Bitcoins during the quarter, valued approximately $2.9 million.” — Investing.com. [Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-lm-funding-america-reports-q2-2024-results-93CH-3572801) ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-lm-funding-america-reports-q2-2024-results-93CH-3572801))  
- Aug 14, 2024, Q2 2024 earnings call: “As of June 30, 2024, the company held 160.4 Bitcoins, valued at approximately $10 million.” — Investing.com. [Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-lm-funding-america-reports-q2-2024-results-93CH-3572801) ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-lm-funding-america-reports-q2-2024-results-93CH-3572801))

- Apr 1, 2024, Form 10‑K (FY2023): Digital mining revenues ≈ $12.29m of ≈ $12.98m total; cost metrics and bitcoin mined disclosed. [LM Funding IR (10‑K FY2023)](https://www.lmfunding.com/investors/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0000950170-24-038953/lmfa-20231231.htm) ([lmfunding.com](https://www.lmfunding.com/investors/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0000950170-24-038953/lmfa-20231231.htm))

[LM Funding IR (10‑K FY2023)](https://www.lmfunding.com/investors/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0000950170-24-038953/lmfa-20231231.htm) ([lmfunding.com](https://www.lmfunding.com/investors/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0000950170-24-038953/lmfa-20231231.htm))

- Nov 2024 8‑K/IR update: As of Sept 30, 2024, held ≈ 142.3 BTC (≈ $12.4m); combined cash+BTC ≈ $14.9m. [LM Funding IR (8‑K/press PDF)](https://www.lmfunding.com/investors/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0000950170-24-125982/0000950170-24-125982.pdf).

## ARGO BLOCKCHAIN

* Rank: 23 | Expo: 8.61 | id_str: GB00BZ15CS02
* Weight summary: holders 0.2, miners 0.8, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 5 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: 2023 Form 20‑F/A (SEC), 2023 annual report, Q4 2023 earnings call, Q4 2021 earnings call, 2022 Helios sale press releases, financial news

### Firm Overview
Argo Blockchain plc is a London‑based, dual‑listed company focused on large‑scale Bitcoin mining, historically operating sites in Quebec, Canada and at the Helios facility in Texas under hosting arrangements. The company listed on Nasdaq in 2021 and its financial performance is primarily tied to Bitcoin mining output and market conditions.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The core exposure is Bitcoin mining: management repeatedly reports BTC mined, mining margins, and power/hosting cost dynamics as the key business drivers. Q4 2023 remarks note “Argo mined 1,760 Bitcoin during the year” with more than $50 million of revenue, and discuss curtailment credits and cost per BTC, underscoring mining as the dominant activity. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/ARBK/transcripts/2023/4))  
A secondary channel is treasury‑style holdings: in 2021 management highlighted a deliberate HODL strategy and disclosed “2,595 bitcoin and bitcoin equivalents on the balance sheet” at year‑end; the company also explicitly describes holding Bitcoin in treasury that it does not sell to fund operating expenses. While subsequent years saw material reductions in coin balances, the earlier policy and related disclosures establish a non‑zero balance‑sheet exposure channel. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/05/02/argo-blockchain-plc-arbk-q4-2021-earnings-call-tra/?source=stocklight&utm_campaign=article&utm_medium=feed&utm_source=stocklight))  
There is no evidence during 2017–2024 that Argo operated exchanges, custody for third parties, or issued crypto financial products; nor did it supply hardware/hosting to others as a standalone external segment (the Helios facility was sold in December 2022 with Argo continuing as a hosted miner there). ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1841675/000110465925037403/arbk-20231231x20fa.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Miners = 0.8 reflects that Bitcoin mining is the overwhelming driver of revenue and management emphasis throughout the sample period, with detailed disclosures on BTC mined, mining margins, and power/hosting arrangements. Holders = 0.2 captures the explicit 2021 HODL/treasury stance (including a cash‑purchase of BTC and thousands of coins held) and language in filings about holding Bitcoin in treasury; this share is limited because balances were reduced sharply by 2023 (9 BTC year‑end). No support was found for finserv, bizserv or infrastr roles in 2017–2024. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/ARBK/transcripts/2023/4))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Argo’s treasury exposure varied materially over time: a large HODL position in 2021 was largely unwound in 2022–2023; the 0.2 holders weight balances this temporal shift. Some transcript repositories are paywalled; however, Q4 2021 (Motley Fool) and Q4 2023 (Roic AI) provide sufficient public quotations to anchor classification. Evidence after December 2024 (e.g., 2025 RNS) is not used for coding. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/05/02/argo-blockchain-plc-arbk-q4-2021-earnings-call-tra/?source=stocklight&utm_campaign=article&utm_medium=feed&utm_source=stocklight))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Apr 28, 2022 (FY 2021 results): “At the end of the year, we held 2,595 bitcoin and bitcoin equivalents on the balance sheet.” — The Motley Fool (Q4 2021). [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/05/02/argo-blockchain-plc-arbk-q4-2021-earnings-call-tra/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/05/02/argo-blockchain-plc-arbk-q4-2021-earnings-call-tra/?source=stocklight&utm_campaign=article&utm_medium=feed&utm_source=stocklight))  
- Apr 25, 2024 (Q4 2023): “Argo mined 1,760 Bitcoin during the year and we generated more than $50 million of revenue.” — Roic AI transcript. [Roic AI](https://www.roic.ai/quote/ARBK/transcripts/2023/4) ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/ARBK/transcripts/2023/4))

**Filings (10‑K/20‑F / annual report):**
- Apr 30, 2024 (as amended Apr 2025), Form 20‑F/A: Restates accounting for mined cryptocurrencies; describes Bitcoin held in treasury and hosting arrangements at Helios following 2022 sale. [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1841675/000110465925037403/arbk-20231231x20fa.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1841675/000110465925037403/arbk-20231231x20fa.htm))  
- 2023 Annual Report: “As at 31 December 2023, digital assets comprised of 9 Bitcoin (2022: 25 Bitcoin).” [AnnualReports.com PDF](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/a/LSE_ARB_2023.pdf) ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/a/LSE_ARB_2023.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**
- Dec 28, 2022: Argo sells Helios facility to Galaxy for ~$65m and enters two‑year hosting agreement for its miners. [Galaxy press release](https://www.galaxy.com/newsroom/galaxy-to-acquire-helios-bitcoin-mining-facility-from-argo-blockchain/) | [Argo press release](https://www.argoblockchain.com/news-room/2022-december-helios-sale-to-galaxy) ([galaxy.com](https://www.galaxy.com/newsroom/galaxy-to-acquire-helios-bitcoin-mining-facility-from-argo-blockchain/?utm_source=openai))

- Jan 2021: Argo purchased 172.5 BTC as reserve asset (deliberate balance‑sheet exposure). ## COINSHARES INTERNATIONAL

* Rank: 24 | Expo: 8.03 | id_str: JE00BLD8Y945
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 5 | AssetShare: 4
* Primary sources: 2021–2023 earnings call transcripts; 2022 Annual Report; product/press releases (2022–2024); exchange listing notices

### Firm Overview
CoinShares International Limited is a Jersey‑based financial services company listed in Stockholm that specialises in digital assets. Its core business is issuing and managing crypto investment products (ETPs/ETFs) across European venues and, from 2024, the U.S. via the Valkyrie acquisition.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s primary exposure is financial services: it issues physically backed crypto ETPs/ETFs (e.g., CoinShares Physical and XBT Provider) and earns management and staking‑related fees; transcripts and filings describe fee income tied to AUM, custody and issuer costs, and daily note economics. Management also discusses capital‑markets activities that deploy group capital into DeFi staking, delta‑neutral trading, liquidity provisioning and hedging that support its product platforms, reinforcing that the business directly handles and custodies digital assets to collateralise products. There is no evidence of mining operations or hardware provision, and no separate non‑financial services segment serving crypto clients. ([s3.amazonaws.com](https://s3.amazonaws.com/a.storyblok.com/f/155294/x/27329d5c46/coinshares_q122_transcript_cterree.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 reflects that CoinShares directly issues and manages crypto ETPs/ETFs, custodies underlying coins to back notes, recognizes management fees (and shares staking rewards with noteholders), and operates supporting capital‑markets activities; these are repeatedly and explicitly described in earnings calls and the annual report. No support was found for holders (treasury HODL outside product or trading operations), mining, infrastructure supply, or non‑financial business services. ([s3.amazonaws.com](https://s3.amazonaws.com/a.storyblok.com/f/155294/x/27329d5c46/coinshares_q122_transcript_cterree.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified. Transcripts and filings clearly position the firm as a crypto financial services issuer/manager; any digital assets on the balance sheet are held to hedge/operate products or strategies rather than as treasury investments. Evidence is confined to January 2017–December 2024 sources and contemporaneous documents. ([a.storyblok.com](https://a.storyblok.com/f/155294/x/851df4f2ab/coinshares_annual_report_2022.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 3, 2022 (Q1 2022): “The platform as a whole generated combined management fees of £17.2 million… Direct costs… comprise predominantly custody fees and issuer costs… It’s a highly scalable business model with higher levels of AUM translating to the group’s bottom line.” — Transcript (English). [CoinShares IR (PDF)](https://s3.amazonaws.com/a.storyblok.com/f/155294/x/27329d5c46/coinshares_q122_transcript_cterree.pdf) ([s3.amazonaws.com](https://s3.amazonaws.com/a.storyblok.com/f/155294/x/27329d5c46/coinshares_q122_transcript_cterree.pdf))  
- Aug 1, 2023 (Q2 2023): “Management fees… in Q2 of GBP10.6 million… CoinShares Physical generated inflows… Staking income… just short of GBP6 million… Liquidity provisioning… from supporting the group’s ETPs.” — Transcript (English). [CoinShares IR (PDF)](https://s3.amazonaws.com/a.storyblok.com/f/155294/x/f5c60503d8/q2-earnings-english-transcript.pdf) ([s3.amazonaws.com](https://s3.amazonaws.com/a.storyblok.com/f/155294/x/f5c60503d8/q2-earnings-english-transcript.pdf))  
- Nov 1, 2022 (Q4 2022): “Approximately 60% [of revenue] came from our asset management business… Direct costs… comprised custody fees, trading fees and issuer expenses incurred by the two issuing entities of the group’s ETPs.” — Transcript (English). [CoinShares IR (PDF)](https://s3.amazonaws.com/a.storyblok.com/f/155294/x/22c07271c4/q4-earnings-english-transcript.pdf) ([s3.amazonaws.com](https://s3.amazonaws.com/a.storyblok.com/f/155294/x/22c07271c4/q4-earnings-english-transcript.pdf))

**Filings (Annual report):**
- 2022 Annual Report: “The Group earns revenue by issuing ETPs which synthetically track the performance of digital assets… fee income… based on the market value of the ETP.” — Revenue recognition; auditor notes ETP liability valuation (~£987m). [CoinShares 2022 Annual Report (PDF)](https://a.storyblok.com/f/155294/x/851df4f2ab/coinshares_annual_report_2022.pdf) ([a.storyblok.com](https://a.storyblok.com/f/155294/x/851df4f2ab/coinshares_annual_report_2022.pdf))

[CoinShares 2022 Annual Report (PDF)](https://a.storyblok.com/f/155294/x/851df4f2ab/coinshares_annual_report_2022.pdf) ([a.storyblok.com](https://a.storyblok.com/f/155294/x/851df4f2ab/coinshares_annual_report_2022.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**
- Mar 27, 2023: Deutsche Börse Xetra lists new CoinShares crypto ETNs (Top10 basket and smart‑contracts), evidencing ongoing ETP issuance and distribution. [Deutsche Börse Xetra press release](https://www.xetra.com/xetra-en/newsroom/press-releases/list-press-releases/Two-new-crypto-ETNs-from-CoinShares-on-Xetra-access-to-ten-largest-crypto-assets-and-smart-contracts-3480906) ([xetra.com](https://www.xetra.com/xetra-en/newsroom/press-releases/list-press-releases/Two-new-crypto-ETNs-from-CoinShares-on-Xetra-access-to-ten-largest-crypto-assets-and-smart-contracts-3480906?utm_source=openai))

- Mar 12, 2024: Completion of Valkyrie ETF business acquisition, expanding U.S. crypto ETF footprint (finserv scope). [CoinShares press release](https://investor.coinshares.com/pressreleases/coinshares-strengthens-its-global-reach-by-completing-acquisition-of-valkyrie-etf-business) ([investor.coinshares.com](https://investor.coinshares.com/pressreleases/coinshares-strengthens-its-global-reach-by-completing-acquisition-of-valkyrie-etf-business?utm_source=openai))

## SILVERGATE CAPITAL A

* Rank: 25 | Expo: 8.00 | id_str: US82837P4081
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 3 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K/Annual Report (2021), Q3 2020 and Q3 2022 earnings calls, company press releases, CNBC/Bloomberg/Reuters coverage

### Firm Overview
Silvergate Capital Corporation was a California-based bank holding company for Silvergate Bank that specialized in providing banking services to the digital asset industry, including real‑time U.S. dollar settlement via the Silvergate Exchange Network (SEN). On March 8, 2023, the company announced it would wind down operations and voluntarily liquidate Silvergate Bank. ([silvergate.com](https://silvergate.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/Annual-Report-2021.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s exposure was through financial services: it provided fiat banking rails, real‑time payments (SEN/Euro SEN), and bitcoin‑collateralized lending (SEN Leverage) to crypto exchanges, market‑makers, institutional investors and stablecoin issuers, and earned deposit‑related fees and net interest income funded by digital‑asset customer deposits. Management repeatedly described SEN transfer volumes, digital‑asset customer deposits, and growth of SEN Leverage on earnings calls, and the 2021 Annual Report details the digital‑currency customer base and fee income. There is no evidence of proprietary crypto mining or provision of mining hardware/compute, and the firm did not present itself as a corporate “HODL” treasury investor; any token initiative (a “tokenized dollar”) was exploratory and never launched prior to the 2023 wind‑down. ([silvergate.com](https://silvergate.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/Earnings-Transcript-2022-Q3.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 reflects that Silvergate directly provided banking/payment rails and bitcoin‑collateralized lending to crypto clients, reported deposit‑related fees “substantially all” from digital‑currency customers, and tied business performance to activity and volumes on the SEN; no credible evidence supports holders, miners, infrastr, or non‑financial bizserv categories. The stock also functioned as a crypto sentiment proxy per the company’s own risk disclosures and market coverage, supporting invbase = 1. ([silvergate.com](https://silvergate.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/Annual-Report-2021.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The company announced an orderly liquidation on March 8, 2023 and discontinued SEN shortly before; classification relies on activities within the January 2017–December 2024 sample, primarily pre‑liquidation operations. Revenue attribution is bank‑model specific: net interest income was driven by securities funded with digital‑asset customer deposits, so RevShare is coded as “significant” (3) rather than “major.” ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2023/03/09/bitcoin-ether-fall-after-go-to-crypto-bank-silvergate-announces-liquidation.html?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 18, 2022 (Q3 2022): “Turning to SEN Leverage, our Bitcoin‑collateralized lending product, we saw continued strong demand… commitments growing to $1.5 billion.” — Transcript (company PDF). [Silvergate IR](https://silvergate.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/Earnings-Transcript-2022-Q3.pdf)  
- Oct 18, 2022 (Q3 2022): “Average deposits from digital asset customers declined to $12 billion in the third quarter… The number of digital asset customers… 1,677.” — Transcript (company PDF). [Silvergate IR](https://silvergate.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/Earnings-Transcript-2022-Q3.pdf)  
- Oct 26, 2020 (Q3 2020): “We’re excited to be out of the pilot for SEN Leverage and… scale the product offering… two strategic partners… Bitstamp and Anchorage.” — Transcript (company PDF). [Silvergate IR](https://silvergate.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/Earnings-Transcript-2020-Q3.pdf)  
- Apr 21, 2022 (Q1 2022): “Opening the account… and the API integrations with the SEN… we had made a loan to Marathon Digital… a bitcoin mining company.” — Transcript. [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/04/21/silvergate-capital-corporation-si-q1-2022-earnings/)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**

- Nov 7, 2022 (Q3 2022) 10‑Q: SEN Leverage loans of ~$302m (Sept 30, 2022) disclosed in loan detail. [Company 10‑Q (HTML mirror)](https://silvergate.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/quarterly1172022.html)  
- Oct 19, 2021 8‑K/press release: “Average digital currency customer deposits grew to $11.2 billion… increased SEN Leverage commitments and balances.” [Silvergate press release (PDF)](https://silvergate.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/current10192021.pdf)  
- Mar 8, 2023 press release: Intent to wind down operations and voluntarily liquidate Silvergate Bank. [Silvergate IR](https://silvergate.com/2023/03/08/)

**Financial news / other:**
- Mar 9, 2023: “Go‑to crypto bank” Silvergate to liquidate; SEN shutdown noted. [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2023/03/09/bitcoin-ether-fall-after-go-to-crypto-bank-silvergate-announces-liquidation.html)  
- Mar 8, 2023: Plans to wind down and liquidate bank operations. [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2023-03-08/silvergate-plans-to-wind-down-bank-operations-and-liquidate)  
- Jan 5, 2023: Crypto‑related deposits plunged by ~$8B; shares fell >40% (Reuters report). [Investing.com (Reuters)](https://www.investing.com/news/economy/silvergate-capitals-cryptorelated-deposits-plunge-in-fourth-quarter-2973890)

## IREN

* Rank: 26 | Expo: 7.97 | id_str: AU0000185993
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 1.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 5 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Form 20-F (FY2024), H1 FY2022 and FY2023 earnings calls, monthly investor updates (2024), Reuters/Bloomberg coverage

### Firm Overview
IREN (formerly Iris Energy Limited) is an Australia‑headquartered operator of Bitcoin mining data centers, listed on Nasdaq, with facilities primarily in North America. Within the 2017–2024 sample window it focused on self‑mining and infrastructure development, with early-stage AI/HPC cloud efforts emerging in 2024.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s exposure is through direct Bitcoin mining: management repeatedly describes the business as a sustainable bitcoin miner that builds and operates its own data centers, tracks hashrate and monthly coins mined, and reports mining revenue as the dominant line. Filings also state the company typically liquidates mined Bitcoin daily and thus carries little to no digital‑asset inventory on the balance sheet, indicating no treasury “HODL” posture. There is no evidence in the period of operating an exchange, custody service, or issuing crypto investment products, nor of supplying mining hardware to others. While the company began to pilot AI/HPC cloud services in 2024, these are not crypto‑mechanism exposures and were immaterial to revenue that year.

### Weight Assignment
Miners = 1.0 reflects clear, repeated disclosures that IREN runs self‑mining operations and derives substantially all revenue from Bitcoin mining during FY2024. Holders = 0.0 is warranted because management states it sells mined Bitcoin and did not hold BTC on balance sheet at period end. Finserv, bizserv, and infrastr = 0.0 because the firm neither provides financial services around digital assets nor supplies mining hardware/hosting as a third‑party infrastructure vendor in a way that constitutes a separate crypto channel in the sample period. Financial media coverage regularly frames IREN as a “bitcoin miner” and reports share reactions around sector events, supporting invbase = 1. ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/2203358/q2-2022-iris-energy-ltd-earnings-call-transcript))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
By late 2024 the company was developing AI/HPC cloud services; however, FY2024 filings show these contributed minimally relative to Bitcoin mining, so they do not change the crypto‑mechanism classification. Some transcript sources are hosted by third‑party aggregators (access to certain pages may be rate‑limited), but multiple independent sources corroborate the key statements used here. ([ih.advfn.com](https://ih.advfn.com/stock-market/NASDAQ/iris-energy-IREN/stock-news/94456509/form-6-k-report-of-foreign-issuer-rules-13a-16?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

**Earnings calls:**

[GuruFocus](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/2203358/q2-2022-iris-energy-ltd-earnings-call-transcript) ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/2203358/q2-2022-iris-energy-ltd-earnings-call-transcript))  
- Sep 13, 2023 (FY2023): “We don’t believe in holding bitcoin on our balance sheet.” — Transcript. [ROIC.AI](https://www.roic.ai/quote/IREN/transcripts/2023/3) ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/IREN/transcripts/2023/3?utm_source=openai))
