## GMO INTERNET GROUP

* Rank: 27 | Expo: 7.74 | id_str: JP3152750000
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.1, finserv 0.8, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.1, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: FY2024 Q2–Q3 earnings call transcripts; FY2023 consolidated results statement; 2018 mining-business extraordinary loss disclosure; 2020 NYDFS approval for GYEN/ZUSD stablecoins; CoinDesk coverage (2019)

### Firm Overview
GMO Internet Group, Inc. is a Japan‑based internet conglomerate listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange (9449) with businesses spanning internet infrastructure, online advertising/media, and internet finance. Within 2017–2024 it also operated a Cryptoassets segment comprising exchange (GMO Coin), mining, and payments/stablecoins via a U.S. trust subsidiary.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The dominant crypto exposure is financial services via GMO Coin, the group’s regulated cryptoasset exchange in Japan, which management repeatedly ties to transaction volumes and segment profitability. The group also pursued fiat‑backed stablecoins (GYEN, ZUSD) through its New York‑chartered GMO‑Z.com Trust Company, reinforcing its direct handling/custody of digital assets. A smaller, declining mining operation persisted through the period, and earlier in the window the company manufactured/sold mining hardware before exiting that activity after incurring losses in 2018. There is no evidence of a treasury “HODL” policy; references to digital-asset balances are operational (exchange/mining), not discretionary holdings.

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 0.8 reflects that crypto exposure primarily arises from operating a spot/leveraged trading exchange (GMO Coin) and issuing regulated stablecoins, with management explicitly linking profits to exchange volumes throughout 2023–2024. Miners = 0.1 recognizes the continuing but small crypto‑mining sub‑segment during the window. Infrastr = 0.1 captures the 2017–2018 period when GMO produced/sold mining hardware before exiting, a channel that was time‑limited but present within the sample. Holders and bizserv = 0.0 due to lack of treasury HODL posture and because activities that touch assets are financial rather than non‑financial services. Given GMO’s diversification and crypto’s modest revenue share (low‑single‑digit percent in 2024), invbase = 0.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Mining activity diminished markedly by 2023–2024, while hardware supply ceased after 2018; the small non‑zero weights for miners/infrastr reflect presence within the 2017–2024 sample but are economically secondary to finserv. English transcripts are IR-provided translations; however, multiple quarters consistently describe the Cryptoassets segment and GMO Coin’s role. Stablecoin issuance is via a U.S. subsidiary; disclosures focus on regulatory status rather than consolidated revenue contribution.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 12, 2024 (FY2024 Q3): “Next, cryptoassets is marked as good… [due to] expansion of transaction revenues in the exchange business. We have established a system that allows us to make profit… as long as there is a certain level of transaction volume.” — Transcript. [GMO IR](https://ir.group.gmo/en/pdf/presen/GMO_FY24_Q3_script_241112_e.pdf) ([ir.group.gmo](https://ir.group.gmo/en/pdf/presen/GMO_FY24_Q3_script_241112_e.pdf))  
- Aug 8, 2024 (FY2024 Q2): “Next is crypto assets… The booming crypto asset market led to an increase in trading volume and revenues from the exchange business GMO Coin developed.” — Transcript. [GMO IR](https://ir.group.gmo/en/pdf/presen/GMO_FY24_Q2_script_240808_e.pdf) ([ir.group.gmo](https://ir.group.gmo/en/pdf/presen/GMO_FY24_Q2_script_240808_e.pdf))  
- Feb 13, 2024 (FY2023): “The next is cryptoassets… Cryptoassets has three businesses, which are mining, exchange, and payment.” — Transcript. [GMO IR](https://ir.group.gmo/en/pdf/presen/GMO_FY23_Q4_script_240213_e.pdf) ([ir.group.gmo](https://ir.group.gmo/en/pdf/presen/GMO_FY23_Q4_script_240213_e.pdf))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Feb 13, 2024: FY2023 Consolidated Results Statement — “The Cryptoassets segment operates business pertaining to ‘mining,’ ‘exchange’ and ‘payment’… [exchange] primarily through GMO Coin… accounts 601 thousand; mining net sales ¥577m.” [GMO IR filing](https://ir.gmo.jp/en/pdf/presen/GMO_FY23_Q4_Consolidated_Results_Statement_240213_e.pdf) ([ir.gmo.jp](https://ir.gmo.jp/en/pdf/presen/GMO_FY23_Q4_Consolidated_Results_Statement_240213_e.pdf))  
- Aug 8, 2024: FY2024 Q2 Consolidated Results Statement — reiterates exchange sub‑segment “mainly through GMO Coin.” [GMO IR filing](https://ir.group.gmo/en/pdf/presen/GMO_FY24_Q2_Consolidated_Results_Statement_240808_e.pdf) ([ir.group.gmo](https://ir.group.gmo/en/pdf/presen/GMO_FY24_Q2_Consolidated_Results_Statement_240808_e.pdf?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**

## BITFARMS

* Rank: 28 | Expo: 7.61 | id_str: CA09173B1076
* Weight summary: holders 0.2, miners 0.8, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 5 | AssetShare: 3
* Primary sources: 2024 Annual Information Form (Form 40-F exhibits); Q3 2022 and Q2 2023 earnings call transcripts; June 2022 HODL strategy press release; CoinDesk coverage (June 2022)

### Firm Overview
Bitfarms Ltd. is a Canada-based, publicly listed Bitcoin self-mining company with operations in Canada, the United States, Paraguay and Argentina. Within the 2017–2024 window, its business model centered on operating owned mining facilities powered largely by hydroelectricity and holding a variable portion of self‑mined bitcoin on the balance sheet.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The dominant exposure is as a Bitcoin miner: management repeatedly discusses hashrate, energy capacity, production volumes, and mining economics on earnings calls. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/BITF/transcripts/2022-year/3-quarter)) The firm also held bitcoin as a balance‑sheet asset at year‑end 2022–2024, with disclosed values rising to about $120.1 million at December 31, 2024, indicating non‑trivial treasury exposure alongside operations. ([investor.bitfarms.com](https://investor.bitfarms.com/static-files/7954ad94-bf22-4a90-8f4e-a2103c793b84)) In June 2022 Bitfarms explicitly “adjusted” its prior HODL strategy to enhance liquidity during the downturn, and contemporaneous reporting noted sizable BTC sales to reduce debt; treasury exposure later rebuilt into 2023–2024. ([investor.bitfarms.com](https://investor.bitfarms.com/news-releases/news-release-details/bitfarms-adjusts-hodl-strategy?utm_source=openai)) There is no evidence in 2017–2024 of the firm running exchanges, custody for third parties, or supplying mining hardware/hosting to others as a primary business line, so finserv, bizserv, and infrastr are set to 0.0.

### Weight Assignment
Miners = 0.8 reflects that essentially all revenue in 2017–2024 derived from mining activity (“sale of computational power used for hashing”) and related operations, as emphasized in filings and calls. ([investor.bitfarms.com](https://investor.bitfarms.com/static-files/7954ad94-bf22-4a90-8f4e-a2103c793b84)) Holders = 0.2 captures the deliberate bitcoin treasury component that, while paused/downsized in mid‑2022, is clearly present and material by late 2024 ($120.1m BTC against total assets of ~$667.6m, i.e., high‑teens percent). ([investor.bitfarms.com](https://investor.bitfarms.com/static-files/7954ad94-bf22-4a90-8f4e-a2103c793b84)) Finserv, bizserv, and infrastr remain at 0.0 due to lack of qualifying activities during the sample. invbase = 1 is supported by management’s own positioning of Bitfarms as providing investors “excellent exposure to rising Bitcoin prices,” indicating the stock is used as a proxy for BTC sentiment. ([insidermonkey.com](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/bitfarms-ltd-nasdaqbitf-q2-2023-earnings-call-transcript-1180314/?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The proportion of BTC held versus sold varied materially over time (e.g., HODL adjustment and debt reduction in 2022), so the holders weight reflects presence within the window rather than a constant policy. ([investor.bitfarms.com](https://investor.bitfarms.com/news-releases/news-release-details/bitfarms-adjusts-hodl-strategy?utm_source=openai)) Late‑2025/2026 commentary about HPC/AI pivots falls outside the 2017–2024 window and is not considered in weights. Form 40‑F/A restatements in late 2024 relate to control/financial statement classification issues but do not change the directional classification as a miner with some treasury BTC. ([quantisnow.com](https://quantisnow.com/insight/amendment-sec-form-6ka-filed-by-bitfarms-ltd-5806437?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 14, 2022 (Q3 2022): “We ended the third quarter with cash of $36 million and 2,064 bitcoin valued… at $40 million… We sold a total of 2,595 bitcoins… reducing indebtedness.” — Transcript. [Roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/BITF/transcripts/2022-year/3-quarter)  
- Aug 8, 2023 (Q2 2023): “Our portfolio of high‑quality assets offers exposure to rising Bitcoin prices…” — Transcript. [Insider Monkey](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/bitfarms-ltd-nasdaqbitf-q2-2023-earnings-call-transcript-1180314/)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Apr 1, 2025 (covers FY2024): AIF (Form 40‑F exhibit) — “Revenues $192.9m… Revenues are from the sale of computational power…”; “Value of Bitcoin held $120.1m… Total assets $667.6m.” [Bitfarms IR (PDF)](https://investor.bitfarms.com/static-files/7954ad94-bf22-4a90-8f4e-a2103c793b84)  
- Mar 21, 2023: FY2022 40‑F/AIF reference (company background and operations). [Bitfarms IR (PDF)](https://investor.bitfarms.com/es-LA/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001213900-23-021559/0001213900-23-021559.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Jun 21, 2022: “Bitfarms Adjusts HODL Strategy… no longer HODLing all daily BTC production.” — Press release. [Bitfarms IR](https://investor.bitfarms.com/news-releases/news-release-details/bitfarms-adjusts-hodl-strategy)  
- Jun 21, 2022: “Miner Bitfarms sold almost half its bitcoin to reduce debt.” [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2022/06/21/miner-bitfarms-sold-almost-half-its-bitcoin-to-reduce-debt)  
- Nov 14, 2022: Q3 2022 results release (deleveraging and operational metrics). [Bitfarms IR](https://investor.bitfarms.com/news-releases/news-release-details/bitfarms-reports-third-quarter-2022-results-positive-adjusted)  
- Nov 2023 (Q3 2023 call summary): Sold 1,018 of 1,172 BTC; 154 BTC to treasury; initiated “Synthetic HODL.” [Investing.com transcript summary](https://ng.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-bitfarms-outlines-growth-strategy-reports-q3-2023-earnings-93CH-1135350)

## BIT DIGITAL

* Rank: 29 | Expo: 7.37 | id_str: KYG1144A1058
* Weight summary: holders 0.3, miners 0.7, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 4 | AssetShare: 3
* Primary sources: FY2024 Form 10-K (filed March 14, 2025, covering 2024); Q4 2023 earnings call transcript; FY2023 results press release; 2021–2023 production/news releases

### Firm Overview
Bit Digital, Inc. is a Nasdaq‑listed company headquartered in New York that, within 2017–2024, primarily operated as a Bitcoin self‑miner and began accumulating and staking Ethereum alongside its treasury strategy. Following China’s 2021 mining ban, the company migrated its fleet to North America and later added Ethereum staking (2022) and AI/HPC services (2024) while continuing to generate substantial revenue from Bitcoin mining.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The dominant channel is miners: management discloses hashrate, BTC production and mining revenue as the core of operations through 2024 (e.g., $58.6 million bitcoin mining revenue in FY2024). ([stocklight.com](https://stocklight.com/stocks/us/nasdaq-btbt/bit-digital/annual-reports/nasdaq-btbt-2025-10K-25737585.pdf))  
A secondary channel is holders: the firm accumulated BTC and ETH on its balance sheet and staked ETH on its own account; digital assets reached about $161.4 million as of December 31, 2024 (roughly 30% of total assets), and management described an intentional conversion of BTC to ETH for staking. ([stocklight.com](https://stocklight.com/stocks/us/nasdaq-btbt/bit-digital/annual-reports/nasdaq-btbt-2025-10K-25737585.pdf))  
There is no evidence in 2017–2024 of Bit Digital operating an exchange, providing third‑party custody, or issuing crypto investment products; ETH staking activity reflected the company’s own treasury, not client services. ([stocklight.com](https://stocklight.com/stocks/us/nasdaq-btbt/bit-digital/annual-reports/nasdaq-btbt-2025-10K-25737585.pdf))  
Investor sentiment has historically treated BTBT as a crypto proxy, with media and management commentary linking miner equities to Bitcoin exposure. ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2021/01/10/bitcoin-miner-bit-digital-passes-1b-market-cap?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Miners = 0.7 reflects that mining was the principal economic driver in 2017–2024 (e.g., $58.6m bitcoin mining revenue in 2024 versus ~$1.8m from ETH staking), and repeated operational focus on hashrate and production. Holders = 0.3 captures the deliberate BTC/ETH treasury and on‑balance‑sheet exposure (digital assets ~$161.4m vs. total assets ~$538.2m at December 31, 2024) and management’s explicit strategy of converting BTC to ETH for staking. Finserv, bizserv, and infrastr remain 0.0 due to lack of qualifying third‑party financial services, support‑only services, or hardware supply roles. invbase = 1 is supported by the firm’s positioning amid crypto‑sensitive investor flows and media characterization as a bitcoin miner. ([stocklight.com](https://stocklight.com/stocks/us/nasdaq-btbt/bit-digital/annual-reports/nasdaq-btbt-2025-10K-25737585.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
ETH staking commenced in late 2022 and grew through 2023–2024, but revenues remained small relative to mining in 2024; the holders weight reflects balance‑sheet significance rather than revenue magnitude. The company’s AI/HPC services expanded in 2024, which dilutes crypto revenue mix but does not create a new crypto mechanism. Announced strategic shifts after December 2024 (e.g., 2025 Ethereum‑focused transition) are outside the sample and not used in weighting. ([stocklight.com](https://stocklight.com/stocks/us/nasdaq-btbt/bit-digital/annual-reports/nasdaq-btbt-2025-10K-25737585.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q4 2023 (Roic.ai transcript): “We take the rewards from Bitcoin, we put a material portion of that into Ethereum, then we stake a lot of that Ethereum…”; “Ethereum’s generating about 4% of revenues… 95%+ is through native staking.” — [Roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/BTBT/transcripts/2023-year/4-quarter) ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/BTBT/transcripts/2023-year/4-quarter))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Mar 14, 2025 (FY2024 10‑K): “Revenue from bitcoin mining… $58.6 million”; cloud services $45.7m; ETH staking $1.82m; total assets $538.2m; digital assets $161.4m (current assets). — [Form 10‑K PDF](https://stocklight.com/stocks/us/nasdaq-btbt/bit-digital/annual-reports/nasdaq-btbt-2025-10K-25737585.pdf) ([stocklight.com](https://stocklight.com/stocks/us/nasdaq-btbt/bit-digital/annual-reports/nasdaq-btbt-2025-10K-25737585.pdf))

## BITDEER TECHNOLOGIES GROUP A

* Rank: 30 | Expo: 6.67 | id_str: KYG114481008
* Weight summary: holders 0.1, miners 0.6, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.3, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 5 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: Earnings calls (Q3 2024; Q4 2024 call script), 6‑K and IR press releases (2023–2024)

### Firm Overview
Bitdeer Technologies Group is a Singapore‑headquartered bitcoin mining and computing infrastructure company listed on Nasdaq (ticker BTDR). It operates self‑mining, cloud hash rate and hosting businesses with datacenters in the United States, Norway and Bhutan, and in 2024 also emphasized an internal ASIC roadmap and power infrastructure buildout. ([bitdeer.gcs-web.com](https://bitdeer.gcs-web.com/news-releases/news-release-details/bitdeer-reports-financial-results-full-year-2023-and-operational?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The dominant mechanism is miners: management repeatedly discloses self‑mining hashrate and segment revenue as core to operations (e.g., Q3 2024 self‑mining revenue of $31.5 million; FY2024 self‑mining revenue of $163.1 million). ([ir.bitdeer.com](https://ir.bitdeer.com/static-files/d9e6643d-30a0-4fec-a5a7-911d2e101731))  
A second channel is infrastr: Bitdeer provides hosting/colocation capacity and power‑intensive datacenter infrastructure purpose‑built for mining clients, with FY2024 hosting revenue of about $131.6 million and a pipeline exceeding 1.7 GW. ([ir.bitdeer.com](https://ir.bitdeer.com/news-releases/news-release-details/bitdeer-reports-unaudited-financial-results-fourth-quarter-and/))  
A smaller holders channel is present: the company reported $77.5 million of cryptocurrencies on the balance sheet as of December 31, 2024 and disclosed 594 bitcoin held at year‑end 2024. ([ir.bitdeer.com](https://ir.bitdeer.com/news-releases/news-release-details/bitdeer-reports-unaudited-financial-results-fourth-quarter-and/))  
There is no evidence in 2017–2024 of Bitdeer operating an exchange, offering third‑party custody, or issuing crypto investment products; cloud hash rate is presented as hash rate subscription/mining‑share arrangements rather than a fund product. ([ir.bitdeer.com](https://ir.bitdeer.com/static-files/68eca638-3e89-4ced-8479-ec444823134f))

### Weight Assignment
Miners = 0.6 reflects self‑mining as the largest single revenue source in 2024 and the centrality of hashrate/production in calls. Infrastr = 0.3 captures substantial hosting/colocation revenue and the explicit build‑out of mining‑directed power/datacenter capacity. Holders = 0.1 recognizes on‑balance‑sheet digital assets (bitcoin held and crypto balance) that create mark‑to‑market exposure but are smaller relative to total assets (~$77.5 million crypto vs. ~$1.56 billion total assets at December 31, 2024). Finserv and bizserv remain 0.0 due to lack of qualifying client‑facing financial products or support‑only services. invbase = 1 is supported by crypto‑native investor attention, including Tether’s strategic equity investment in 2024, and coverage that treats BTDR as a bitcoin‑miner proxy. ([ir.bitdeer.com](https://ir.bitdeer.com/news-releases/news-release-details/bitdeer-reports-unaudited-financial-results-fourth-quarter-and/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Cloud hash rate straddles service and mining economics; here it is grouped with mining/infrastructure rather than “finserv” since management frames it as hash rate subscriptions with mining‑income sharing, not a custody/trading/investment product. AI/HPC initiatives expanded in 2024 but do not constitute a crypto exposure mechanism under the taxonomy. The Q4 2024 call was held on February 25, 2025 but discusses 2024 results; the Q3 2024 transcript (November 18, 2024) anchors the 2017–2024 sample directly. ([ir.bitdeer.com](https://ir.bitdeer.com/static-files/d9e6643d-30a0-4fec-a5a7-911d2e101731))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 18, 2024 (Q3 2024): “Self‑mining revenue was $31.5 million… Cloud Hash Rate revenue was $7.1 million… General Hosting revenue was $9.6 million; Membership Hosting revenue was $9.9 million.” — [Bitdeer IR (Q3 2024 transcript)](https://ir.bitdeer.com/static-files/d9e6643d-30a0-4fec-a5a7-911d2e101731) ([ir.bitdeer.com](https://ir.bitdeer.com/static-files/d9e6643d-30a0-4fec-a5a7-911d2e101731))  
- Feb 25, 2025 (Q4 2024 call script): “For Q4 2024, total revenue was $69.0 million… Self‑mining revenue was $41.5 million… Cloud Hash Rate revenue was $2.3 million… We have secured… over 2.6 GWs of total power capacity.” — [Bitdeer IR (Q4 2024 script)](https://ir.bitdeer.com/static-files/1f54c14d-da8d-4a68-bbc5-e9e82df97264) ([ir.bitdeer.com](https://ir.bitdeer.com/static-files/1f54c14d-da8d-4a68-bbc5-e9e82df97264))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Jan 8, 2025 (Form 6‑K, Dec 2024 production update): “Bitcoins held 594… [excludes customer deposits].” — [SEC/Bitdeer 6‑K Exhibit 99.1](https://ir.bitdeer.com/static-files/68eca638-3e89-4ced-8479-ec444823134f) ([ir.bitdeer.com](https://ir.bitdeer.com/static-files/68eca638-3e89-4ced-8479-ec444823134f))  
- Mar 2025 (IR release for Q4/FY2024): FY2024 segment revenue — self‑mining $163.1m; cloud hash rate $39.8m; hosting $67.6m (general) + $64.0m (membership); crypto balance $77.5m; total assets ~$1.56b. — [Bitdeer IR](https://ir.bitdeer.com/news-releases/news-release-details/bitdeer-reports-unaudited-financial-results-fourth-quarter-and/) ([ir.bitdeer.com](https://ir.bitdeer.com/news-releases/news-release-details/bitdeer-reports-unaudited-financial-results-fourth-quarter-and/))  
- Mar 18, 2024 (FY2023 results): self‑mining revenue $111.7m; membership hosting revenue $79.9m; commentary on business lines. — [Bitdeer IR](https://ir.bitdeer.com/news-releases/news-release-details/bitdeer-reports-financial-results-full-year-2023-and-operational/) ([ir.bitdeer.com](https://ir.bitdeer.com/news-releases/news-release-details/bitdeer-reports-financial-results-full-year-2023-and-operational/?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- May 31, 2024: “Tether acquires $100 million stake in U.S.-listed bitcoin miner Bitdeer, with option for $50 million more.” — [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2024-05-31/tether-acquires-100-million-stake-in-us-listed-bitcoin-miner-bitdeer) ([bloomberg.com](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2024-05-31/tether-acquires-100-million-stake-in-us-listed-bitcoin-miner-bitdeer?utm_source=openai))

</small>

## CORE SCIENTIFIC

* Rank: 31 | Expo: 6.56 | id_str: US21874A1060
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.8, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.2, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 5 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: 2024 Form 10‑K (filed Feb 27, 2025), Q4 2024 earnings call (Feb 26, 2025), Q2/Q3 2024 results and investor materials

### Firm Overview
Core Scientific is a U.S.-based operator of purpose-built data centers focused on bitcoin mining and, increasingly, high‑performance computing hosting. The company emerged from Chapter 11 and relisted on Nasdaq in January 2024, with operations across multiple U.S. sites and segment reporting that separates self‑mining, hosted mining, and HPC hosting. ([bloomberg.com](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2024-01-16/crypto-miner-core-scientific-to-leave-bankruptcy-as-coins-rally?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The dominant channel is miners: management discloses substantial bitcoin self‑mining operations and segment revenue tied to digital asset self‑mining (e.g., FY2024 self‑mining revenue of about $408.7 million). ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250226238202/en/Core-Scientific-Announces-Fiscal-Fourth-Quarter-and-Full-Fiscal-Year-2024-Results/?utm_source=openai))  
A secondary channel is infrastr: Core Scientific provides hosting/colocation and power‑intensive data center capacity tailored for third‑party bitcoin miners, with FY2024 hosted mining revenue of roughly $77.6 million and detailed fleet/hosting disclosures in filings. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250226238202/en/Core-Scientific-Announces-Fiscal-Fourth-Quarter-and-Full-Fiscal-Year-2024-Results/?utm_source=openai))  
Holdings are not treated as a separate mechanism: the company reported 256 bitcoin at December 31, 2024, but also states it sells digital assets “as soon as practicable” after receipt, indicating operational balances rather than a treasury strategy. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1839341/000162828025008302/core-20241231.htm))  
There is no evidence in 2017–2024 of operating an exchange, third‑party crypto custody, or issuing crypto investment products. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1839341/000162828025008302/core-20241231.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Miners = 0.8 reflects that bitcoin self‑mining was the clear majority of crypto‑related revenue in 2024. Infrastr = 0.2 captures material hosted mining/colocation activity and the purpose‑built mining infrastructure footprint documented in filings. Holders = 0.0 is applied because year‑end bitcoin on the balance sheet appears to be operational inventory with an explicit policy to sell promptly, rather than a treasury HODL strategy. Finserv and bizserv are 0.0 due to a lack of qualifying financial or ancillary service lines. invbase = 1 is warranted given repeated media framing of CORZ as a bitcoin miner proxy and observable stock moves alongside bitcoin/mining metrics. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250226238202/en/Core-Scientific-Announces-Fiscal-Fourth-Quarter-and-Full-Fiscal-Year-2024-Results/?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The company’s rapid pivot into non‑crypto HPC hosting expanded during 2024; those activities are not a crypto exposure mechanism under this taxonomy and were excluded from the weights except insofar as they reduced hosted mining revenue. The anchor transcript citing segment figures is the Q4 2024 call held on February 26, 2025 (discussing 2024 results); Q3 2024 transcripts are listed but less accessible. Minimal year‑end bitcoin holdings could be misread as a holders channel, but filings emphasize near‑term sale of mined assets. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-core-scientific-q4-2024-sees-stock-surge-postrestructuring-93CH-3894514))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 26, 2025 (Q4 FY2024): “Digital asset self‑mining revenue was $79.9 million… hosted mining revenue was $6.5 million… HPC hosting revenue was $8.5 million.” — [Investing.com transcript] (https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-core-scientific-q4-2024-sees-stock-surge-postrestructuring-93CH-3894514) ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-core-scientific-q4-2024-sees-stock-surge-postrestructuring-93CH-3894514))

- Feb 26, 2025 (FY2024 results): FY2024 revenue by segment (self‑mining ~$408.7m; hosted
- Feb 26, 2025 (FY2024 results): FY2024 segment revenue — self‑mining $408.7m; hosted mining $77.6m; HPC hosting $24.4m; total revenue $510.7m. — [Business Wire press release](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250226238202/en/Core-Scientific-Announces-Fiscal-Fourth-Quarter-and-Full-Fiscal-Year-2024-Results)

- Feb 26, 2025: FY2024 results press release PDF (archived). — [Core Scientific IR (PDF)](https://d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net/_5ac9e262697f54db3dcf4bf920cc5684/corescientific/news/2025-02-26_Core_Scientific_Announces_Fiscal_Fourth_Quarter_109.pdf)  
- Nov 6, 2024: Q3 2024 press release filed as 8‑K exhibit. — [SEC 8‑K Exhibit 99.1](https://investors.corescientific.com/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001628280-24-045714/q32024corescientificinc-ea.htm)

## BANXA HOLDINGS

* Rank: 32 | Expo: 5.96 | id_str: CA06683R1010
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 5 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: FY2023 MD&A; Q2–Q3 FY2024 earnings calls; FY2024 press releases and interim financials

### Firm Overview
Banxa Holdings Inc. is a Canada‑incorporated fintech that operates a global fiat‑to‑crypto on/off‑ramp platform, supplying payment, compliance and liquidity infrastructure to exchanges, wallets and Web3 applications. Its filings describe a flow‑based model earning revenue from the sale of cryptocurrencies plus commissions/spreads, with operations across Australia, Europe and North America. ([investor.banxa.com](https://investor.banxa.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/Banxa-FY23-MDA-Final.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company directly enables customers of partner platforms to purchase and sell digital assets via cards, bank transfers and local payment rails, and it holds the regulatory registrations needed to operate these services. Filings state Banxa “earns revenue from the sale of crypto‑currencies, commission fees and/or spread,” and show small balances of “cryptocurrencies held for resale” and liquidity inventories used in operations. Earnings calls through 2024 emphasize embedded crypto/on‑ramp services and scaling of processed crypto sales to partner ecosystems. These activities squarely place Banxa in the crypto financial services channel rather than mining, hardware infrastructure or passive holding. ([investor.banxa.com](https://investor.banxa.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/Banxa-FY23-MDA-Final.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Banxa is a pure‑play provider of fiat‑to‑crypto on/off‑ramp and embedded crypto payments/custody rails for partners, a business that by definition involves direct handling of digital assets for customer transactions. No evidence indicates strategic coin‑treasury activity, mining, or crypto‑specific hardware provision. Accordingly, 1.0 weight is assigned to finserv; all other mechanism weights are 0.0. ([investor.banxa.com](https://investor.banxa.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/Banxa-FY23-MDA-Final.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Seeking Alpha pages listing multiple 2024–2021 transcripts are access‑restricted; transcript quotations are therefore anchored to Investing.com’s call write‑ups that reproduce portions of the remarks. Small crypto inventories are held for resale/liquidity per filings; under the taxonomy these are operational to back products and remain within finserv, not holders. None identified that would shift the mechanism mix. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/BANXA-HOLDINGS-INC-49479466/news-call-transcripts/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 22, 2024 (Q3 FY2024): “I’m pleased to present the quarter three FY ’24 unaudited financials… we’ve achieved profitability in this latest quarter.” — [Investing.com] (https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-banxa-reports-profitability-and-strategic-growth-in-q3-fy-24-93CH-3454654) ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-banxa-reports-profitability-and-strategic-growth-in-q3-fy-24-93CH-3454654))

## US GLOBAL INVRS.

* Rank: 33 | Expo: 5.51 | id_str: US9029521005
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (FY2021), 10-Q (Q1 2024), Q3 2021 and Q4 2020 earnings calls, company webcast slides (3Q 2021), press releases (2018)

### Firm Overview
U.S. Global Investors, Inc. is a U.S.-based registered investment adviser and ETF sponsor focused on niche themes such as airlines and precious metals. Its crypto linkage during 2017–2024 came primarily from a strategic investment relationship with HIVE Blockchain (now HIVE Digital), including equity and convertible debentures, rather than from managing client-facing crypto products. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/754811/000118518521001814/usglobal20210630_10k.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management repeatedly discussed HIVE/crypto as a driver of volatility in GROW’s results and share price, noting “it’s impacting our stock price because behind every share of GROW, there is almost 0.7 shares of HIVE.” The firm also received consulting income from HIVE and provided governance/strategic support via its CEO’s board role at HIVE. These are services around the ecosystem, not direct custody, issuance, mining or coin‑treasury activity. Accordingly, exposure fits the non‑financial “business services” channel under the taxonomy. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/GROW/transcripts/2021-year/3-quarter?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence in filings shows USGI held HIVE common shares historically and, in January 2021, purchased HIVE 8% unsecured convertible debentures with attached warrants; later disclosures document the debentures’ fair value and the January 2024 expiry of the warrants with a realized loss. None of this is direct coin holding, mining, exchange/custody, or a crypto ETP the firm manages for clients. Given the explicit consulting income from HIVE and the broader advisory/governance linkage, 1.0 weight is assigned to bizserv; all other categories are 0.0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/754811/000118518521001814/usglobal20210630_10k.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
USGI is a financial firm, and some might argue its balance‑sheet financing of a crypto miner is “financial services,” but the taxonomy’s finserv category requires directly handling or issuing crypto products, which is not evidenced here in 2017–2024. Transcript coverage is adequate, though some materials are webcasts rather than verbatim earnings call transcripts. The investor‑base effect is strong per management’s remarks, hence invbase = 1. 

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q3 2021: “it’s impacting our stock price because behind every share of GROW, there is almost 0.7 shares of HIVE.” — [Roic AI transcript](https://www.roic.ai/quote/GROW/transcripts/2021-year/3-quarter)  
- Q4 2020: “we became the largest crypto minor investor and we launched HIVE …” — [AlphaStreet transcript](https://news.alphastreet.com/us-global-investors-inc-grow-q4-2020-earnings-call-transcript/)

**Filings (10‑K / 10‑Q):**
- FY2021 10‑K: “During the fiscal year ended June 30, 2021, the Company purchased convertible securities of HIVE … for $15.0 million,” with details of debentures and warrants. — [SEC 10‑K](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/754811/000118518521001814/usglobal20210630_10k.htm)  
- Q1 2024 10‑Q: HIVE debentures fair value; 5M warrants expired January 2024 with realized loss recognized. — [USGI 10‑Q (Mar 31, 2024)](https://www.usfunds.com/app/uploads/2024/05/USGI-10-Q-03.31.24.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- 3Q 2021 Webcast: slides on “GROW’s strategy for HODLing HIVE,” sale of 10M HIVE shares and subsequent debenture investment; “Volatility in cryptocurrency space.” — [USGI 3Q 2021 Webcast PDF](https://www.usfunds.com/GROW/assets/img/3Q2021-GROW-Webcast.pdf)  
- Feb 14, 2018: Company highlights strategic investments in HIVE, the first publicly traded crypto miner. — [GlobeNewswire](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2018/02/14/1348369/0/en/U-S-Global-Investors-Reports-Significantly-Improved-Net-Income-for-Second-Quarter-2018.html)

</small>

## REALBOTIX

* Rank: 34 | Expo: 5.31 | id_str: CA75604K1075
* Weight summary: holders 0.8, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.2, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 3 | AssetShare: 3
* Primary sources: MD&A for FY2024 (filed Jan 28, 2025), Q2 2024 earnings call, press releases (2021–2024)

### Firm Overview
Realbotix Corp is a Canadian-listed issuer on the TSX Venture Exchange (formerly Tokens.com Corp) that pivoted from crypto staking and Web3 initiatives toward AI-powered humanoid robotics in 2024. During 2017–2024, its crypto linkage came primarily from holding and staking digital assets, alongside metaverse-related activities through its Metaverse Group subsidiary. ([uk.marketscreener.com](https://uk.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/REALBOTIX-CORP-120793726/news/Tokens-com-Corp-has-Changed-its-Name-to-Realbotix-Corp-47345979/?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company held an inventory of cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet and generated staking revenue, with management disclosing revaluation gains/losses on digital assets and detailing custody/staking practices in filings. On the Q2 2024 call, management reported “revenue for the quarter was $286,000 from staking … [and] $4 million in a revaluation of our digital assets,” underscoring direct balance‑sheet exposure. Earlier releases also framed the business as one that “owns cryptocurrency for the purpose of generating revenue,” noting correlation of asset value and share price to crypto markets. Separately, Tokens.com/Metaverse Group executed and monetized metaverse/NFT land acquisitions and client activations, consistent with non‑financial services to the crypto/Web3 ecosystem. ([financialreports.eu](https://financialreports.eu/filings/realbotix-corp/management-reports/2025/13766939/))

### Weight Assignment
Holders = 0.8 reflects the centrality of treasury crypto assets and staking rewards to results, including recurring mark‑to‑market effects and explicit balance‑sheet discussions in FY2024 materials and the Q2 2024 call. Bizserv = 0.2 captures the 2021–2023 metaverse/Web3 services provided via Metaverse Group (e.g., Decentraland assets and brand activations), which are services around the crypto economy rather than custody, exchange, or client ETPs. No evidence supports miners, finserv, or infrastr classifications in 2017–2024 under this taxonomy. ([financialreports.eu](https://financialreports.eu/filings/realbotix-corp/management-reports/2025/13766939/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The company rebranded to Realbotix in 2024 and began reporting non‑crypto product revenue; it later exited staking in 2025, but that falls outside the 2017–2024 sample window. Earnings‑call coverage is available for 2022–2024; core figures are corroborated by filings and press releases. Given management’s explicit statement that share price tracked crypto markets, invbase = 1 is warranted. ([uk.marketscreener.com](https://uk.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/REALBOTIX-CORP-120793726/news/Tokens-com-Corp-has-Changed-its-Name-to-Realbotix-Corp-47345979/?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q2 2024: “revenue for the quarter was $286,000 from staking … [and] $4 million in a revaluation of our digital assets.” — [AlphaSpread transcript] (https://www.alphaspread.com/security/xtsx/xbot/investor-relations/earnings-call/q2-2024) ([financialreports.eu](https://financialreports.eu/filings/realbotix-corp/management-reports/2025/13766939/))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- FY2024 MD&A (dated Jan 28, 2025): Staking revenue disclosed; gains/losses on revaluation; custody and staking policy (Fireblocks, self‑custody); “Digital Asset Inventory.” — [FinancialReports.eu (MD&A FY2024)] (https://financialreports.eu/filings/realbotix-corp/management-reports/2025/13766939/) ([financialreports.eu](https://financialreports.eu/filings/realbotix-corp/management-reports/2025/13766939/))
- Name change to Realbotix Corp (ISIN CA75604K1075). — [MarketScreener] (https://uk.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/REALBOTIX-CORP-120793726/news/Tokens-com-Corp-has-Changed-its-Name-to-Realbotix-Corp-47345979/) ([uk.marketscreener.com](https://uk.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/REALBOTIX-CORP-120793726/news/Tokens-com-Corp-has-Changed-its-Name-to-Realbotix-Corp-47345979/?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- May 9, 2024: Q2 2024 results; revenue $286k; $4.0m gain on revaluation of cryptocurrencies. — [Business Wire] (https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20240509724814/en/Tokens.com-Reports-Financial-Results-for-Q2-2024) ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20240509724814/en/Tokens.com-Reports-Financial-Results-for-Q2-2024?utm_source=openai))
- Nov 8, 2023: Corporate update; company “owns an inventory of crypto assets” largely staked; details on ETH held at Genesis. — [Business Wire] (https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20231108753201/en/Tokens.com-Provides-Corporate-Update) ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20231108753201/en/Tokens.com-Provides-Corporate-Update?utm_source=openai))
- Aug 13, 2021: Q2 2021 results; “owns cryptocurrency for the purpose of generating revenue” and notes share price correlation to crypto markets. — [Business Wire] (https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20210813005036/en/Tokens.com-Announces-Q2-2021-Financial-Results) ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20210813005036/en/Tokens.com-Announces-Q2-2021-Financial-Results?utm_source=openai))
- Nov 23, 2021: Metaverse Group (subsidiary) closes large Decentraland land acquisition for 618k MANA. — [Business Wire] (https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20211123005825/en/Tokens.coms-Subsidiary-Closes-the-Largest-Metaverse-Land-Acquisition-in-History) ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20211123005825/en/Tokens.coms-Subsidiary-Closes-the-Largest-Metaverse-Land-Acquisition-in-History?utm_source=openai))

## WISDOMTREE

* Rank: 35 | Expo: 5.09 | id_str: US97717P1049
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Form 10-K for FY2023 (filed Apr 29, 2024), Q2 2023 and Q4 2020 earnings calls, press releases and market news (2019–2024)

### Firm Overview
WisdomTree, Inc. is a U.S.-based asset manager and exchange-traded product sponsor listed on the NYSE (WT). Between 2017 and 2024, its crypto linkage came from issuing physically backed crypto ETPs in Europe and, in 2023–2024, expanding into blockchain‑enabled retail products (WisdomTree Prime) and a U.S. spot Bitcoin ETF (BTCW) launched following SEC approval on January 10, 2024. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/880631/000121465924007721/wt422241ars10k.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Finserv: The company directly offers crypto financial products, including European ETPs providing exposure to bitcoin, ether and crypto baskets, which totaled about $0.4 billion of AUM as of December 31, 2023 and exceeded $1.5 billion by late 2024. In the U.S., WisdomTree launched the spot Bitcoin ETF (BTCW) in January 2024. Its blockchain‑native Prime app provides access to bitcoin, ether, tokenized gold and U.S. dollar tokens, plus 13 blockchain‑enabled Digital Funds, with affiliated regulated entities (WT Securities and a NYDFS‑chartered limited purpose trust company) supporting distribution and custody. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/880631/000121465924007721/wt422241ars10k.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 reflects that WisdomTree’s crypto exposure is through issuing and managing exchange‑traded crypto products and on‑chain funds/wallet services, including BTCW and European physically backed ETPs, as well as the Prime platform. Per the taxonomy’s exclusion rules, any crypto held to back these products is operational to the products and thus counted as finserv, not holders. No evidence supports miners, infrastructure, or non‑financial business services as primary exposure channels in 2017–2024. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/880631/000121465924007721/wt422241ars10k.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Transcript availability varies by outlet; accessible calls used here include Q4 2020 and Q2 2023. BTCW’s January 2024 launch falls within the sample, but crypto revenues remain a minor share relative to overall AUM; the 2023 10‑K discloses $0.4 billion in cryptocurrency product AUM versus $100.1 billion total AUM at year‑end 2023. Holdings that collateralize ETPs/ETFs are product‑level and not treasury assets of the parent, so holders = 0.0 under the rules. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/01/29/wisdomtree-investments-inc-wetf-q4-2020-earnings-c/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q2 2023 (July 28, 2023): “we launched WisdomTree Prime … The initial launch included 9 blockchain‑enabled funds … as well as Bitcoin and Ether.” — [Fintool transcript](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/WT/documents/transcripts/q2-2023) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/WT/documents/transcripts/q2-2023))
- Q4 2020 (Jan 29, 2021): “We will maintain our leadership position in crypto ETPs … I often say, the essence of the ETF structure is about simplifying and democratizing access… Our European listed Bitcoin ETP has gathered almost $200 million…” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/01/29/wisdomtree-investments-inc-wetf-q4-2020-earnings-c/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/01/29/wisdomtree-investments-inc-wetf-q4-2020-earnings-c/))

**Filings (10‑K / product filings):**
- FY2023 Form 10‑K (filed Apr 29, 2024): Prime provides access to bitcoin, ether, tokenized gold/USD and 13 Digital Funds; WT Securities as mutual‑fund retailer for Digital Funds; NYDFS limited purpose trust charter; cryptocurrency ETP AUM $0.4bn at 12/31/2023; total AUM $100.1bn. — [SEC 10‑K PDF](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/880631/000121465924007721/wt422241ars10k.pdf) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/880631/000121465924007721/wt422241ars10k.pdf))
- FY2022 Form 10‑K: outlines early milestones in digital assets, tokenization and EU crypto ETPs. — [SEC 10‑K HTML](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/880631/000119312523054126/d426509d10k.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/880631/000119312523054126/d426509d10k.htm?utm_source=openai))
- WisdomTree Bitcoin Fund: Q1 2024 filing notes SEC approval Jan 10, 2024 and trading inception Jan 11, 2024. — [BTCW filing](https://www.wisdomtree.com/investments/-/media/us-media-files/documents/resource-library/crypto-assets/btcw-10-q-q1-2024-as-filed.pdf) ([wisdomtree.com](https://www.wisdomtree.com/investments/-/media/us-media-files/documents/resource-library/crypto-assets/btcw-10-q-q1-2024-as-filed.pdf?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Jan 10, 2024: SEC approved 11 spot bitcoin ETPs including WisdomTree’s BTCW. — [Congressional Research Service brief](https://www.congress.gov/crs-product/IF12573) ([congress.gov](https://www.congress.gov/crs-product/IF12573?utm_source=openai))
- Dec 2019: WisdomTree Bitcoin ETP listed on SIX Swiss Exchange. — [SIX media release](https://www.six-group.com/en/newsroom/media-releases/2019/20191203-wisdom-tree-etp.html) ([six-group.com](https://www.six-group.com/en/newsroom/media-releases/2019/20191203-wisdom-tree-etp.html?utm_source=openai))
- Apr 14, 2021: WisdomTree lists Bitcoin ETP on Xetra (EU passporting). — [Company IR press release](https://ir.wisdomtree.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/79/wisdomtree-lists-bitcoin-etp-on-brse-xetra) ([ir.wisdomtree.com](https://ir.wisdomtree.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/79/wisdomtree-lists-bitcoin-etp-on-brse-xetra?utm_source=openai))
- Q4 2024 results: crypto ETP range exceeded $1.5bn AUM; BTCW >$1bn and led European bitcoin ETP flows. — [Company press release](https://ir.wisdomtree.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/721/wisdomtree-announces-fourth-quarter-2024-results) ([ir.wisdomtree.com](https://ir.wisdomtree.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/721/wisdomtree-announces-fourth-quarter-2024-results))

## ROBINHOOD MARKETS A

* Rank: 36 | Expo: 4.97 | id_str: US7707001027
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 3 | AssetShare: 4
* Primary sources: 10-K and 10-Q filings (2022–2024), Q3 2021 and Q4 2023–Q2 2024 earnings calls, company press/newsroom, AP News (2024)

### Firm Overview
Robinhood Markets, Inc. is a U.S.-based retail brokerage and financial services company listed on Nasdaq (HOOD). Between 2017 and 2024 it expanded beyond equities and options to offer crypto trading, wallets/transfers and, from late 2023, an EU crypto platform, making crypto a recurring driver of transaction-based revenues.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Finserv: Robinhood directly provides crypto brokerage, custody and transfer services through Robinhood Crypto in the U.S. and launched crypto trading in the European Union in December 2023. Management highlighted crypto engagement and volumes on earnings calls and added access to all 11 U.S. spot bitcoin ETFs on day one of trading in January 2024, alongside direct crypto trading on-platform. Filings detail that crypto trading revenues are earned via market‑maker rebates, and that user‑custodied cryptocurrencies (recognized per SAB 121) appear on the balance sheet with a corresponding safeguarding liability. There is no evidence of proprietary coin mining or treasury HODL outside of operational needs. ([newsroom.aboutrobinhood.com](https://newsroom.aboutrobinhood.com/robinhood-launches-crypto-trading-in-the-eu/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 reflects that Robinhood’s crypto exposure arises from operating a crypto brokerage/custody business (including wallets/transfers and EU crypto trading) and offering trading access to spot bitcoin ETFs through its brokerage. Per the rules, safeguarded user crypto recorded on the balance sheet under SAB 121 is product‑/platform‑related and does not constitute treasury “holders” exposure; there is no evidence of mining, infrastructure provisioning, or non‑financial business services as a primary channel in 2017–2024. ([app.stocklight.com](https://app.stocklight.com/stocks/us/nasdaq-hood/robinhood-markets/annual-reports/nasdaq-hood-2023-10K-23675518.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
SAB 121 caused large “asset related to user cryptocurrencies safeguarding obligation” balances to appear on Robinhood’s balance sheet; these reflect customer assets with an offsetting liability and are treated here as finserv exposure rather than holders. Reported crypto revenue mix fluctuates with market conditions; mid‑2024 filings show meaningful crypto transaction revenue growth, and late‑2024 saw a sharp rebound, but exact full‑year shares vary quarter to quarter. ([app.stocklight.com](https://app.stocklight.com/stocks/us/nasdaq-hood/robinhood-markets/annual-reports/nasdaq-hood-2023-10K-23675518.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q4 2023 (Feb 13, 2024), Motley Fool: “we’re really pleased to be offering all 11 of the Bitcoin ETFs on the first day of trading… The one thing… I’d highlight is that the crypto volume on Robinhood nearly doubled in Q4.” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2024/02/13/robinhood-markets-hood-q4-2023-earnings-call-trans/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2024/02/13/robinhood-markets-hood-q4-2023-earnings-call-trans/?utm_source=openai))
- Q2 2024 (Aug 7, 2024), Motley Fool: “In July, trading volumes were more than 20% higher than June across equities, options, and crypto…” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2024/08/07/robinhood-markets-hood-q2-2024-earnings-call-trans/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2024/08/07/robinhood-markets-hood-q2-2024-earnings-call-trans/?utm_source=openai))
- Q3 2021 (Oct 27, 2021), Motley Fool: “crypto wallets will allow customers to… take coins… to participate in… decentralized applications”; “crypto volume is off meaningfully…” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/27/robinhood-markets-inc-hood-q3-2021-earnings-call-t/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/27/robinhood-markets-inc-hood-q3-2021-earnings-call-t/?utm_source=openai))
- Q2 2021 (Aug 2021), Company IR transcript: “Notably, 62% of crypto trading volume was in Dogecoin in Q2.” — [IR transcript PDF](https://s28.q4cdn.com/948876185/files/doc_financials/2021/q2/Q2-2021-Earnings-Call-Transcript.pdf) ([s28.q4cdn.com](https://s28.q4cdn.com/948876185/files/doc_financials/2021/q2/Q2-2021-Earnings-Call-Transcript.pdf?utm_source=openai))

**Filings (10‑K / 10‑Q / exhibits):**
- FY2023 Annual Report (Form 10‑K): “as a result of… SAB 121, the cryptocurrency we custody for users now appears on our balance sheet as an asset.” — [SEC filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1783879/000119312524118494/d828738dars.pdf) ([app.stocklight.com](https://app.stocklight.com/stocks/us/nasdaq-hood/robinhood-markets/annual-reports/nasdaq-hood-2023-10K-23675518.pdf))
- Q2 2024 Form 10‑Q (filed Aug 8, 2024): crypto transaction‑based revenues $81m in Q2 2024 vs $31m in Q2 2023; YTD $207m vs $69m. — [Fintel copy of SEC 10‑Q](https://fintel.io/doc/sec-robinhood-markets-inc-1783879-10q-2024-august-08-19943-9036) ([fintel.io](https://fintel.io/doc/sec-robinhood-markets-inc-1783879-10q-2024-august-08-19943-9036?utm_source=openai))
- Q3 2024 exhibit (Oct 30, 2024): “Asset related to user cryptocurrencies safeguarding obligation” $19,456m (vs $14,708m at Dec 31, 2023). — [SEC exhibit](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1783879/000178387924000295/q32024robinhoodexhibit991.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1783879/000178387924000295/q32024robinhoodexhibit991.htm?utm_source=openai))
- Q1 2024 Form 10‑Q (May 2024): describes RHC crypto brokerage/custody; revenue model includes crypto trade order rebates. — [SEC 10‑Q](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1783879/000178387924000145/hood-20240331.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1783879/000178387924000145/hood-20240331.htm?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Dec 7, 2023: Robinhood launches crypto trading in the EU. — [Company newsroom](https://newsroom.aboutrobinhood.com/robinhood-launches-crypto-trading-in-the-eu/) ([newsroom.aboutrobinhood.com](https://newsroom.aboutrobinhood.com/robinhood-launches-crypto-trading-in-the-eu/?utm_source=openai))
- Jan 11, 2024: Robinhood adds all newly approved spot bitcoin ETFs for trading. — [Company newsroom](https://robinhood.com/us/en/newsroom/robinhood-adds-new
- Jan 11, 2024: Robinhood adds all 11 newly approved spot Bitcoin ETFs for trading on day one. — [Company newsroom](https://robinhood.com/us/en/newsroom/robinhood-adds-new-spot-bitcoin-etfs/)
- Dec 7, 2023: Robinhood launches crypto trading service in the EU. — [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2023/12/07/robinhood-launches-crypto-trading-service-in-the-eu.html)
- Dec 7, 2023: Robinhood launches zero‑fee crypto app in Europe. — [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2023-12-07/robinhood-hood-launches-zero-fee-crypto-trading-app-in-europe)
- Oct 1, 2024: Robinhood enables crypto deposits/withdrawals for EU customers. — [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/10/01/robinhood-launches-crypto-transfers-in-europe-in-push-overseas.html)
- Jun 6, 2024: Robinhood to acquire Bitstamp to accelerate global crypto expansion. — [Investor relations release](https://investors.robinhood.com/news-releases/news-release-details/robinhood-acquire-bitstamp)
- Jun 6, 2024: Robinhood buying cryptocurrency exchange Bitstamp for about $200 million. — [AP News](https://apnews.com/article/df2aaf86ffadf2e0ce78747c59f88a4a)
- May 6, 2024: SEC sends Wells Notice to Robinhood’s crypto unit (regulatory context). — [Axios](https://www.axios.com/2024/05/06/robinhood-crypto-sec-wells-notice)
- Dec 25, 2024: Robinhood shares more than tripled in 2024 as “all things tied to crypto rallied.” — [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/12/25/ai-crypto-top-tech-stocks-applovin-microstrategy-palantir-nvidia.html)

## GOLDMONEY

* Primary sources: Company press releases (2017–2019), FY2018 year-end earnings call, FY2024 annual report

### Firm Overview
Goldmoney Inc. is a Canada-based precious metals financial services company listed on the TSX (XAU) that provides online access to buy, sell, and custody physical bullion for clients globally; in recent years it has also added a UK real-estate segment. Its FY2024 annual report describes operations centered on Goldmoney.com (precious metals platform) and Goldmoney Properties, with no ongoing cryptocurrency line of business. ([goldmoney.com](https://www.goldmoney.com/docs/goldmoney-annual-report-2024.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
From September 2017, Goldmoney offered a cryptocurrency custody and dealing service within the Goldmoney Holding, powered by its BlockVault subsidiary and marketed as insurable, auditable, AML-compliant cold‑storage for assets such as Bitcoin and Ethereum. Management characterized this as a “cryptocurrency custody exchange trading business,” and disclosed meaningful cryptocurrency transaction revenue during FY2018–FY2019. On March 6, 2019, the company formally exited the cryptocurrency business, asking clients to liquidate or withdraw positions and ceasing crypto activity on its platform by May 6, 2019. Across the 2017–2019 window, the mechanism was financial services (custody/dealing); there is no evidence of mining operations or treasury HODL. ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/fr/news-release/2017/9/28/1134284/0/en/Goldmoney-Inc-Adds-Bitcoin-and-Ethereum-to-the-Goldmoney-Holding.html))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 reflects that Goldmoney’s crypto exposure was through running a custody/dealing service for clients (including institutional clients), with explicit disclosure of crypto transaction revenues (e.g., $30.6m in a quarter and $17m in Q1 FY2019). No sources indicate company-run mining, hardware/compute provisioning, or strategic balance‑sheet HODL beyond operational facilitation, and the firm exited the line in March 2019, leaving no other crypto mechanism. ([goldmoney.com](https://www.goldmoney.com/investors/news/goldmoney-inc-year-end-2018-earnings-conference-call-transcript))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The crypto exposure was time‑bounded (late‑2017 launch to March–May 2019 wind‑down); classification therefore reflects historical exposure within the 2017–2024 sample. Reported crypto revenues were presented as exchange/custody activity and, per company disclosures, certain elements were reported on a net basis, which can complicate precise revenue‑share estimation. Earnings call transcript coverage is strongest for FY2018; later calls are sparse, but company press releases and filings corroborate the timeline and substance. ([goldmoney.com](https://www.goldmoney.com/investors/news/goldmoney-inc-reports-financial-results-for-fiscal-third-quarter-2019?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- FY2018 year-end call (June 22, 2018): “We saw $30.6 million of cryptocurrency revenue compared to $22 million in the prior quarter… We’ve built out an entire cryptocurrency custody exchange trading business.” — [Goldmoney transcript](https://www.goldmoney.com/investors/news/goldmoney-inc-year-end-2018-earnings-conference-call-transcript) ([goldmoney.com](https://www.goldmoney.com/investors/news/goldmoney-inc-year-end-2018-earnings-conference-call-transcript))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**

- Aug 8, 2018: Q1 FY2019 results; “Cryptocurrency Business Revenue of $17 million, compared to $31 million in Q4 2018.” — [Company news](https://www.goldmoney.com/investors/news/goldmoney-inc-reports-financial-results-for-first-quarter-2019) ([goldmoney.com](https://www.goldmoney.com/corporate/news/goldmoney-inc-reports-financial-results-for-first-quarter-2019))
- Mar 6, 2019: “Goldmoney Inc. has decided to exit the cryptocurrency business… all cryptocurrency related activity on the Goldmoney.com platform will cease [by May 6, 2019].” — [Company news](https://www.goldmoney.com/investors/news/goldmoney-inc-formally-exits-cryptocurrency-business) ([goldmoney.com](https://www.goldmoney.com/investors/news/goldmoney-inc-formally-exits-cryptocurrency-business))
- FY2024 Annual Report: Company description focused on precious metals platform (Goldmoney.com) and Goldmoney Properties; no active crypto segment. — [Annual report PDF](https://www.goldmoney.com/docs/goldmoney-annual-report-2024.pdf) ([goldmoney.com](https://www.goldmoney.com/docs/goldmoney-annual-report-2024.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**

## MOGO

* Rank: 38 | Expo: 4.23 | id_str: CA60800C2085
* Weight summary: holders 0.2, miners 0.0, finserv 0.8, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: Earnings calls (Q3 2021), SEC filings (2019 20‑F; 2024 20‑F), Company press releases (2018–2023)

### Firm Overview
Mogo Inc. is a Canadian fintech company listed in Toronto and on Nasdaq that offers consumer financial services including a spending account and prepaid Visa card, credit services, investing/trading products and other digital finance tools. Within the 2017–2024 period, Mogo added cryptocurrency features (buy/sell bitcoin and bitcoin rewards) and made a strategic equity investment in Canadian crypto exchanges (initially Coinsquare, later WonderFi via merger). ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/mogo-announces-fourth-quarter--full-year-2018-financial-results-300811289.html?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Mogo launched MogoCrypto in 2018 to let Canadian members buy and sell bitcoin in-app, and in 2021 it introduced a bitcoin cashback rewards program tied to its MogoCard; these are crypto financial services exposures rather than mining or infrastructure. Management also emphasized building crypto functionality alongside equities in its trading platform roadmap. ([blog.mogo.ca](https://blog.mogo.ca/mogocrypto-is-here/?utm_source=openai))  
Separately, Mogo completed a strategic investment in Coinsquare in 2021 (ultimately near 39%), recording equity method results; after the 2023 Coinsquare–WonderFi–CoinSmart combination, Mogo became the largest shareholder of WonderFi during 2024. This ties Mogo’s performance partly to Canada’s regulated crypto‑trading ecosystem. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20210416005258/en/Mogo-Completes-Strategic-Investment-in-Crypto-Platform-Coinsquare?utm_source=openai))  
Management also disclosed holding a small amount of bitcoin and ether within its investment portfolio during 2021, indicating limited direct crypto asset exposure on the balance sheet. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/MOGO.TO/transcripts/2021/3))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 0.8 reflects that Mogo’s primary crypto channel was operating crypto financial services for consumers (MogoCrypto buy/sell, bitcoin rewards) and owning a significant stake in crypto exchanges (Coinsquare, later WonderFi), with management commentary tying results and strategy to crypto trading volumes and regulated exchange assets. Holders = 0.2 captures the disclosed, but small, direct holdings of bitcoin and ether within Mogo’s investment portfolio during the period. There is no evidence of company‑run mining, hardware provision or purely ancillary business services. ([blog.mogo.ca](https://blog.mogo.ca/mogocrypto-is-here/?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Bitcoin cashback on MogoCard was altered/discontinued in late 2021, and the prominence of in‑app retail crypto trading fluctuated with market conditions; thus the finserv channel was more significant in 2018–2021 and later centered on Mogo’s exchange equity exposure (Coinsquare/WonderFi). The precise size of Mogo’s direct crypto holdings is not broken out in filings, but management described them as part of a broader investment portfolio, implying a minor balance‑sheet share. ([finder.com](https://www.finder.com/ca/news/how-to-earn-bitcoin-on-everyday-purchases?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

Earnings calls:
- Nov 10, 2021 (Q3 2021): “A significant and perhaps underappreciated asset on our balance sheet is the 39% ownership in crypto platform, Coinsquare… Although we do have crypto related revenue… less than 5% of our total revenue.” — [ROIC.ai transcript](https://www.roic.ai/quote/MOGO.TO/transcripts/2021/3) ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/MOGO.TO/transcripts/2021/3))

Filings (10‑K / press releases):
- 2018 Form 20‑F: Describes MogoCrypto as a way for Canadians to add bitcoin via the MogoAccount. — [SEC filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0001602842/000147793219002153/mogo_20f.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1602842/000147793219002153/mogo_20f.htm?utm_source=openai))
- Apr 16, 2021: “Mogo Completes Strategic Investment in Crypto Platform, Coinsquare.” — [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20210416005258/en/Mogo-Completes-Strategic-Investment-in-Crypto-Platform-Coinsquare) ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20210416005258/en/Mogo-Completes-Strategic-Investment-in-Crypto-Platform-Coinsquare?utm_source=openai))
- Sept 2021: Q3 2021 results filed on EDGAR note completion of 19.99% initial investment and equity‑method pickup related to Coinsquare. — [SEC exhibit](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1602842/000156459021055920/mogo-ex992_8.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1602842/000156459021055920/mogo-ex992_8.htm?utm_source=openai))
- 2024 Form 20‑F: Coinsquare shares exchanged for ~87 million WonderFi shares; Mogo largest WonderFi shareholder in 2024. — [SEC filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1602842/000095017025060208/mogo-20241231.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1602842/000095017025060208/mogo-20241231.htm?utm_source=openai))
- 2018: “MogoCrypto is here (and what it’s all about).” — [Company blog](https://blog.mogo.ca/mogocrypto-is-here/) ([blog.mogo.ca](https://blog.mogo.ca/mogocrypto-is-here/?utm_source=openai))
- Jan 20, 2021: “Mogo Introduces 1% Bitcoin Cashback Rewards.” — [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20210120005390/en/Mogo-Introduces-1-Bitcoin-Cashback-Rewards) ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20210120005390/en/Mogo-Introduces-1-Bitcoin-Cashback-Rewards?utm_source=openai))

## XUNLEI CL.A ADR 1:5

* Rank: 39 | Expo: 4.20 | id_str: US98419E1082
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.2, infrastr 0.8, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 20-F filings (2018–2024), Q2 2018 and Q1 2019 and Q3 2020 earnings calls

### Firm Overview
Xunlei Limited is a China-based internet technology company listed on Nasdaq (XNET) best known for download acceleration, subscription services, and distributed/edge cloud computing. From 2017 onward it developed blockchain infrastructure (ThunderChain) and related enterprise solutions that sit alongside its core cloud and media businesses. ([annualreports.co.uk](https://www.annualreports.co.uk/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/x/NASDAQ_XNET_2023.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The dominant channel is crypto infrastructure: management launched and repeatedly discussed ThunderChain, a high-performance blockchain infrastructure/open platform, later adding a Blockchain-as-a-Service layer to simplify enterprise deployment. The company also commercialized blockchain tools in non-financial enterprise use cases (e.g., a 2022 “digital collection” minting/showcase/management platform running on ThunderChain), which fits non-financial business services to the crypto ecosystem. There is no evidence of Xunlei operating a crypto exchange, offering custody/investment products, mining proof‑of‑work assets, or holding crypto as a treasury asset during 2017–2024; its 2017–2019 LinkToken initiative was characterized in filings as a blockchain-based “digital ticket” that was disposed of in 2019. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/08/15/xunlei-limited-xnet-q2-2018-earnings-conference-ca.aspx))

### Weight Assignment
Infrastr = 0.8 reflects sustained emphasis on ThunderChain infrastructure (high TPS, smart‑contract/EVM compatibility) and the 2020 BaaS rollout highlighted across earnings calls and filings. Bizserv = 0.2 captures enterprise blockchain services built on that stack, including the NFT‑style “digital collection” platform launched in 2022. No allocation to finserv/holders/miners is warranted given the lack of exchange/custody, treasury crypto holdings, or mining operations; LinkToken’s disposal in 2019 further limits any residual financial‑services characterization. ([annualreports.co.uk](https://www.annualreports.co.uk/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/x/NASDAQ_XNET_2023.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Regulatory treatment of blockchain services in China evolved over the period; filings note historical LinkToken-related uncertainties despite the 2019 disposal, but there is no indication of material crypto assets on balance sheet. The company does not break out a discrete “crypto” revenue line; blockchain-related revenues appear embedded within cloud/enterprise services and are likely minor relative to total, so RevShare is coded conservatively. Investor sentiment temporarily treated Xunlei as a “crypto proxy” during 2017’s token launch frenzy, justifying invbase = 1. ([annualreports.co.uk](https://www.annualreports.co.uk/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/x/NASDAQ_XNET_2023.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

Earnings calls:
- Aug 15, 2018 (Q2 2018): “We launched a high performance blockchain platform, ThunderChain Open Platform, and the ThunderChain File System… [to] create large-scale practical blockchain applications.” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/08/15/xunlei-limited-xnet-q2-2018-earnings-conference-ca.aspx) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/08/15/xunlei-limited-xnet-q2-2018-earnings-conference-ca.aspx))
- May 13, 2019 (Q1 2019): “In May 2018, we launched ThunderChain, a high‑performance blockchain platform… we are leading the charge on the commercialization of blockchain by establishing cooperation and partnerships.” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/05/13/xunlei-limited-xnet-q1-2019-earnings-call-transcri.aspx) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/05/13/xunlei-limited-xnet-q1-2019-earnings-call-transcri.aspx))
- Nov 12, 2020 (Q3 2020): “During the third quarter, we launched a Blockchain as a Service or BaaS platform for our blockchain business.” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/11/12/xunlei-limited-xnet-q3-2020-earnings-call-transcri/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/11/12/xunlei-limited-xnet-q3-2020-earnings-call-transcri/))

- Apr 23, 2024 (Form 20‑F FY2023): “LinkToken, which we developed in 2017 and disposed of in 2019, is essentially a type of digital ticket… [used by] OneThing Cloud [users].” — [Annual report PDF](https://www.annualreports.co.uk/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/x/NASDAQ_XNET_2023.pdf) ([annualreports.co.uk](https://www.annualreports.co.uk/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/x/NASDAQ_XNET_2023.pdf))

## SWISSQUOTE 'R'

* Rank: 40 | Expo: 4.17 | id_str: CH0010675863
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 2 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Annual report (FY2024), press releases (2017–2022), H1 2023 earnings call listing

### Firm Overview
Swissquote Group Holding SA is a SIX‑listed Swiss online bank and brokerage headquartered in Gland that offers multi‑asset trading, e‑banking and related services to retail and institutional clients. Within this model, the firm has developed extensive digital‑asset capabilities, including crypto trading, custody, staking and, since 2022, operation of its own crypto exchange.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company provides crypto financial services directly to clients: it enabled bitcoin trading in 2017, expanded to multiple cryptocurrencies and launched structured products tied to crypto indices. It also delivers custody solutions and operates “SQX,” a centralized exchange where Swissquote clients and market makers match orders; the 2024 annual report discloses dedicated “net crypto assets income” and SQX‑related trading gains. There is no evidence that Swissquote strategically holds crypto on its own balance sheet beyond client‑asset custody (which is treated in nominee/fiduciary capacity), nor any mining or hardware/energy provisioning activities. Accordingly, the exposure channel is squarely financial services.

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 reflects the breadth and materiality of Swissquote’s crypto trading, custody, staking and exchange operations, including CHF 85.5m of net crypto‑asset income in 2024 (~13% of operating income) and explicit disclosure of SQX trading gains. No allocation is warranted to holders (client assets are held in fiduciary capacity and not recognized on the balance sheet), miners (no mining operations), bizserv (services directly involve trading/custody of digital assets), or infrastr (SQX is a financial marketplace rather than compute/energy/hardware infrastructure). ([carbonaccountingfinancials.com](https://carbonaccountingfinancials.com/files/institutions_downloads/Swissquote-Group-Holding-Annual-Report-2024-1.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Full earnings‑call transcripts with crypto‑specific quotes for 2017–2024 were behind paywalls; a transcript page for H1 2023 was located but only the opening lines were publicly viewable, so certainty is capped at 2. Crypto revenue contribution varies with market cycles; the 2024 mix (13% of operating income) may not generalize to other years. Client crypto assets (CHF 5.9bn at year‑end 2024) are custody balances, not company assets, which motivates AssetShare = 0. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/SWISSQUOTE-GROUP-HOLDING--71211/news/Transcript-Swissquote-Group-Holding-Ltd-H1-2023-Earnings-Call-Aug-09-2023-44564171/))

### Evidence

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 9, 2023 (H1 2023): “So ladies and gentlemen, very good morning to our press conference for half year results for 2023.” — [MarketScreener transcript page] (login‑restricted) (MarketScreener) ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/SWISSQUOTE-GROUP-HOLDING--71211/news/Transcript-Swissquote-Group-Holding-Ltd-H1-2023-Earnings-Call-Aug-09-2023-44564171/))

- Annual Report 2024: Client assets disclosure — “Of which crypto assets: CHF 5.9 billion (2023: CHF 2.6 billion).” — [Annual Report 2024 PDF] (Swissquote) ([carbonaccountingfinancials.com](https://carbonaccountingfinancials.com/files/institutions_downloads/Swissquote-Group-Holding-Annual-Report-2024-1.pdf))
- Annual Report 2024: “The Group operates a crypto exchange [SQX] …” and notes CHF 6.8m gains related to operation of SQX. — [Annual Report 2024 PDF] (Swissquote) ([carbonaccountingfinancials.com](https://carbonaccountingfinancials.com/files/institutions_downloads/Swissquote-Group-Holding-Annual-Report-2024-1.pdf))
- Annual Report 2024: Holdings in crypto assets are kept “solely as a nominee (fiduciary capacity) on behalf of its clients” and not recognized on the Group’s balance sheet. — [Annual Report 2024 PDF] (Swissquote) ([carbonaccountingfinancials.com](https://carbonaccountingfinancials.com/files/institutions_downloads/Swissquote-Group-Holding-Annual-Report-2024-1.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**
- Oct 3, 2022: “Swissquote launches its own crypto exchange: SQX.” — [Media release] (Swissquote)
- Oct 3, 2022: “Swissquote launches its own crypto exchange: SQX.” — [Media release](https://resources.swissquote.com/sites/default/files/2022-10/media-release_swissquote-sqx_en.pdf)
