## RIVALRY

* Rank: 41 | Expo: 3.91 | id_str: CA76803P1036
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings-call transcripts (Q1–Q2 2024), MD&A filings (2022–2023), 2024 press releases, product pages

### Firm Overview
Rivalry Corp is a Toronto-based online sportsbook and iGaming operator focused on esports and Gen‑Z bettors, listed on the TSX Venture Exchange and operating internationally under an Isle of Man license. In 2024 the company expanded into crypto‑enabled betting features and a native platform token initiative alongside accepting cryptocurrency deposits and withdrawals.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company directly enables crypto payments on its platform, offering deposit and withdrawal options in major cryptocurrencies and rolling out a “crypto wallet and payment experience” for users. In 2024 it announced and began pre‑launch activity for a native platform token (initially “Rivalry Token,” later “NUTZ”) integrated across its product suite, with related deferred revenue disclosed in Q3 2024 materials. There is no evidence that Rivalry operates mining hardware, supplies mining infrastructure, or strategically holds cryptocurrency on its own balance sheet beyond operational needs associated with payments or token activities. Accordingly, exposure is squarely via crypto financial services.

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 reflects Rivalry’s direct involvement with digital assets through crypto payments and the native token program integrated with its betting products, as evidenced in 2024 earnings calls and press releases. No allocation is warranted to holders (no strategic treasury HODL disclosed), miners (no mining operations), bizserv (services are not ancillary to third‑party crypto businesses), or infrastr (no hardware/energy/colocation offering). The token‑related deferred revenue noted in late 2024 had not yet translated into recognized revenue within the 2017–2024 window, supporting RevShare = 0 and AssetShare = 0.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Token launch timing and accounting recognition largely fall after the sample period (anticipated 2025), so quantified crypto revenue contribution for 2017–2024 is limited. Some on‑site product pages are marketing‑oriented; however, they are corroborated by earnings‑call transcripts and press releases. No evidence indicates treasury‑style crypto holdings during the period.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 29, 2024 (Q2 2024): “In addition to releasing a more fulsome crypto wallet and payment experience last quarter, we also announced Rivalry Token. Rivalry Token will be our native cryptocurrency integrated across our platform…” — [Rivalry Q2 2024 transcript PDF](https://s28.q4cdn.com/890135940/files/doc_financials/2024/q2/Rivalry_Q2_2024_Transcript_August_29_2024.pdf)
- Aug 29, 2024 (Q2 2024): “The last one would be… the timing of when the token gets released… exchange listings… you list your token on crypto exchanges.” — [Rivalry Q2 2024 transcript PDF](https://s28.q4cdn.com/890135940/files/doc_financials/2024/q2/Rivalry_Q2_2024_Transcript_August_29_2024.pdf)
- May 30, 2024 (Q1 2024): “The more NUTZ you collect, the more tokens you receive on the eventual airdrop… connect your crypto wallet to get started.” — [Rivalry Q1 2024 transcript PDF](https://s28.q4cdn.com/890135940/files/doc_financials/2024/q1/Rivalry_Q1_2024_Transcript_May_30_2024.pdf)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- MD&A 2023: “It is currently intended that Rivalry will accept cryptocurrencies such as Bitcoin and Ethereum as a payment… [Risks Relating to Cryptocurrency Transactions].” — [MD&A 2023 PDF](https://s28.q4cdn.com/890135940/files/doc_financials/2023/q4/Rivalry-Corp-MDA-for-the-Year-ended-December-31-2023.pdf)
- MD&A 2022: Same risk disclosure on accepting cryptocurrencies and related operational risks. — [MD&A 2022 PDF](https://s28.q4cdn.com/890135940/files/doc_financials/2022/q4/Rivalry-Corp-MD-A-for-the-year-ended-December-31-2022.pdf)
- Nov 29, 2024: “NUTZ (Rivalry Token) captured $3.0 million in deferred revenue… supporting a global, crypto‑first strategy.” — [Q3 2024 press release PDF](https://s28.q4cdn.com/890135940/files/doc_financials/2024/q3/RVLY-Q3-2024-Press-Release-3.pdf)
- May 30, 2024: “Expands into crypto segment through Rivalry Token… overhauled homepage and more fulsome crypto wallet experience.” — [GlobeNewswire release](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2024/05/30/2890591/0/en/Rivalry-Reports-First-Quarter-2024-Results-Expands-Into-Crypto-Segment-Through-Rivalry-Token.html)

**Financial news / other:**
- Nov 29, 2024: Coverage of Q3 results highlighting “crypto‑first strategy” and token momentum. — [GlobeNewswire](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2024/11/29/2989095/0/en/Rivalry-Reports-Third-Quarter-2024-Results-Rivalry-Token-Momentum-Leading-Company-s-Crypto-Native-Future-Reveals-Organizational-Realignment-Major-Product-Revamp.html)
- Dec 1, 2024: “Crypto‑native transformation… NUTZ cornerstone; planned launch early 2025.” — [Covers.com](https://www.covers.com/industry/rivalry-reports-q3-results-amid-crypto-native-transformation-dec-1-2024)
- Product page: “We accept many popular deposit methods including cryptocurrencies such as Bitcoin, Ethereum, Solana, USDT…” — [Rivalry site](https://www.rivalry.pro/)

## WISDOMTREE PHYSICAL PLATINUM ETC

* Rank: 42 | Expo: 3.88 | id_str: JE00B1VS2W53
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Issuer annual report (2024), product page, base prospectus

### Firm Overview
WisdomTree Metal Securities Limited is a Jersey‑incorporated special purpose issuer of physically backed precious‑metal exchange‑traded commodities listed across European and other exchanges. The specific security, WisdomTree Physical Platinum (ISIN JE00B1VS2W53), provides exposure to the platinum spot price and is backed by allocated platinum held with HSBC Bank plc as custodian. ([dataspanapi.wisdomtree.com](https://dataspanapi.wisdomtree.com/pdr/documents/ANNUAL_REPORT/MSL/GB/EN-GB/JE00BN2CJ301/))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across filings and product documentation, the issuer’s activity is confined to issuing debt securities backed by physical bullion (gold, silver, platinum, palladium). No sources indicate any activity involving digital assets, crypto custody, trading, mining, token issuance, or crypto‑backed products for this issuer or this ETC. The appropriate classification is therefore unclassified (no crypto exposure) within the January 2017–December 2024 sample window. ([dataspanapi.wisdomtree.com](https://dataspanapi.wisdomtree.com/pdr/documents/ANNUAL_REPORT/MSL/GB/EN-GB/JE00BN2CJ301/))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of any crypto‑related operations or balance‑sheet exposure, all mechanism weights are set to zero with unclass = 1.0. There is no basis for allocations to holders (no treasury crypto), miners (no mining), finserv (no crypto financial products or custody), bizserv (no ancillary services to crypto firms), or infrastr (no hardware/energy services). The investor‑base amplifier is set to 0 due to lack of evidence that equity flows in this issuer are tied to crypto sentiment.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
No earnings‑call transcripts exist for this product‑issuer SPV; evidence relies on regulatory filings and product documentation. Note that other WisdomTree group entities offer crypto ETPs in Europe, but the entity and security classified here are precious‑metals only, not crypto—care was taken to disambiguate. ([dataspanapi.wisdomtree.com](https://dataspanapi.wisdomtree.com/pdr/documents/ANNUAL_REPORT/MSL/GB/EN-GB/JE00BN2CJ301/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Filings (annual/financial reports):**
- Annual Report, year ended Dec 31, 2024: “The Company’s principal activity is the issue and listing of metal securities (‘Metal Securities’).” — [WisdomTree Metal Securities Limited 2024 Annual Report (PDF)](https://dataspanapi.wisdomtree.com/pdr/documents/ANNUAL_REPORT/MSL/GB/EN-GB/JE00BN2CJ301/)
- Annual Report 2024: table of metals in issue (incl. platinum balances and USD values) — [WisdomTree Metal Securities Limited 2024 Annual Report (PDF)](https://dataspanapi.wisdomtree.com/pdr/documents/ANNUAL_REPORT/MSL/GB/EN-GB/JE00BN2CJ301/)

**Product / prospectus:**
- Product page: “backed by physical, allocated Physical Platinum held by HSBC Bank plc (the custodian).” — [WisdomTree Europe — Physical Platinum (PHPT/PPTX)](https://www.wisdomtree.eu/en-gb/products/ucits-etfs-unleveraged-etps/commodities/wisdomtree-physical-platinum)
- Listings and identifiers (ISIN JE00B1VS2W53; LSE tickers PHPT/PPTX, etc.) — [WisdomTree Europe — Physical Platinum (PHPT/PPTX)](https://www.wisdomtree.eu/en-gb/products/ucits-etfs-unleveraged-etps/commodities/wisdomtree-physical-platinum)
- Base prospectus (example edition): includes class “WisdomTree Physical Platinum — JE00B1VS2W53” among metal securities — [WisdomTree Metal Securities Limited prospectus (PDF)](https://www.wisdomtree.eu/-/media/eu-media-files/key-documents/prospectus/etf-securities/prospectus---etfs-metal-securities-limited.pdf)

## VIVOPOWER INTERNATIONAL

* Rank: 43 | Expo: 3.47 | id_str: GB00BD3VDH82
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 1.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q4 2022 earnings call; company press releases (Dec 2021, Dec 2024); 2024 Form F‑1; 2023 annual report

### Firm Overview
VivoPower International PLC is a U.K.-headquartered sustainable energy solutions company listed on Nasdaq, with activities spanning critical power services in Australia, electric light‑vehicle conversions (Tembo) and U.S. solar development (Caret). Within the 2017–2024 window, management advanced plans for a renewable‑powered digital asset mining venture housed under Caret.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company signed a December 2021 letter of intent to launch “Caret Decimal,” a renewable‑powered digital asset mining business, stating capabilities to mine cryptocurrencies and aligning it with its Power‑to‑X strategy. ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2021/12/08/2348437/0/en/VivoPower-Subsidiary-Caret-Announces-LOI-to-Launch-Renewable-Powered-Digital-Asset-Mining-Business.html?utm_source=openai)) In its FY2022 results call on August 29, 2022, management said it had “pivoted to [a] Power‑to‑X Strategy with a view to [a] renewable powered digital asset mining operation to be spun‑off,” noting this was on hold at that time due to market conditions. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/VVPR/transcripts/2022/4)) Corporate materials and filings in 2022–2024 reference Caret Decimal/Caret Digital as a group subsidiary and outline plans to apply Caret’s U.S. solar sites to crypto‑mining use cases. ([vivopower.com](https://vivopower.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/VivoPower-1H22-Earnings-Presentation-FINAL.pdf?utm_source=openai)) In December 2024, the company announced Caret Digital would activate Dogecoin mining with revenue and cash flow expected from early January 2025, underscoring miner‑type exposure even if commercial production fell just after the sample period. ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2024/12/05/2992313/0/en/VivoPower-s-Caret-Digital-to-activate-Dogecoin-mining-operations-with-revenue-and-profitable-cashflow-generation-by-early-January-2025.html?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence across company releases, a transcript, and filings consistently ties VivoPower’s crypto exposure to operating its own renewable‑powered mining through Caret Decimal/Caret Digital. There is no support for treasury crypto holdings, crypto financial services, or third‑party infrastructure supply during 2017–2024. Accordingly, miners is assigned 1.0, with all other mechanisms at 0.0. Given that operational ramp was largely planned and the activation milestone fell at the very end of the window (with revenues expected from January 2025), materiality scores are set to 0 and certainty to 3 (clear evidence plus a transcript anchor, but limited in‑period operating scale). ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/VVPR/transcripts/2022/4))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Mining activity within the sample window appears preparatory, with activation slated for early January 2025; measurable crypto revenue or asset balances were not evident for 2017–2024. Transcript coverage is available but limited; the key FY2022 call confirms intent while noting a temporary pause due to market conditions. These factors constrain materiality estimates and certainty. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/VVPR/transcripts/2022/4))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 29, 2022 (FY2022 results), transcript: “re‑branded … and pivoted to Power‑to‑X Strategy with a view to [a] renewable powered digital asset mining operation to be spun‑off… That is on hold at the moment due to market conditions.” — [Roic.ai transcript](https://www.roic.ai/quote/VVPR/transcripts/2022/4) ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/VVPR/transcripts/2022/4))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**

- Nov 2024 filing: Lists “Caret Decimal, LLC” among consolidated subsidiaries. — [SEC Form F‑1 (2024)](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1681348/000149315224029294/formf-1.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1681348/000149315224029294/formf-1.htm))
- FY2023 Annual Report: references Caret Decimal within group structure/portfolio context. — [Annual Report FY2023 (PDF)](https://vivopower.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/11/231127-VVPR-Annual-Report-30-June-2023-FINAL-PRINTED.pdf) ([vivopower.com](https://vivopower.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/11/231127-VVPR-Annual-Report-30-June-2023-FINAL-PRINTED.pdf?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**

## TERAWULF

* Rank: 44 | Expo: 3.47 | id_str: US88080T1043
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.9, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.1, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 5 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: 10-K filing (FY2023, filed March 19, 2024), Q2 2023 and Q3 2024 earnings calls, company press releases (2023–2024)

### Firm Overview
TeraWulf Inc. is a U.S.-listed bitcoin mining company that owns and operates data center capacity primarily at its Lake Mariner facility in New York and previously through a joint venture interest at the Nautilus Cryptomine in Pennsylvania. The company emphasizes low-cost, predominantly zero‑carbon power and in 2024 began developing high‑power compute (HPC) capacity alongside its core bitcoin self‑mining activities. ([investors.terawulf.com](https://investors.terawulf.com/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001083301-24-000072/wulf-20231231.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The dominant channel is miners: management repeatedly details self‑mined bitcoin volumes, hashrate and power costs on earnings calls. The company also provides miner hosting services to third parties, creating a secondary infrastructure exposure via datacenter/colocation services for crypto clients. Filings specify that earned bitcoin are routinely sold for U.S. dollars (i.e., no deliberate treasury HODL), and there is no evidence of crypto financial services such as exchange, custody, or investment products. In October 2024 TeraWulf sold its 25% Nautilus JV stake, but core crypto exposure remained mining at Lake Mariner. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/WULF/transcripts/2023-year/2-quarter))

### Weight Assignment
Earnings‑call transcripts in 2023–2024 focus overwhelmingly on self‑mining output, costs, and fleet efficiency, justifying miners = 0.9. The 2023 10‑K explicitly discloses hosting revenue to third parties, supporting a modest infrastructure allocation (infrastr = 0.1). There is no support for holders (treasury accumulation), finserv, or bizserv exposure within 2017–2024; the 10‑K states mined bitcoin are routinely sold, and no product/custody activities are described. Accordingly: holders 0.0, miners 0.9, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.1, unclass 0.0. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/WULF/transcripts/2023-year/2-quarter))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
From late 2024 the company began building non‑crypto HPC capacity; this diversification does not alter the crypto classification for the 2017–2024 window but may dilute crypto revenue share afterward. The relative scale of third‑party hosting versus self‑mining is disclosed qualitatively across sources but precise revenue mix granularity within the sample period is limited in public summaries. None identified regarding transcript availability (multiple calls retrieved). ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/WULF/transcripts/2024/3))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 12, 2024 (Q3 2024), transcript: “In the third quarter of 2024, we self‑mined 442 Bitcoin … our net share of Bitcoin mined at Nautilus was 113.” — [Roic.ai transcript](https://www.roic.ai/quote/WULF/transcripts/2024/3) ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/WULF/transcripts/2024/3))
- Aug 14, 2023 (Q2 2023), transcript: “we mined 506 Bitcoin at Lake Mariner, and our net share … at Nautilus was 403 … for a total of 909.” — [Roic.ai transcript](https://www.roic.ai/quote/WULF/transcripts/2023-year/2-quarter) ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/WULF/transcripts/2023-year/2-quarter))

**Filings (10‑K / prospectus / IR):**
- Mar 19, 2024 (FY2023 Form 10‑K): “The Company generates revenue in the form of bitcoin… The earned bitcoin are routinely sold for U.S. dollars. The Company also earns revenue by providing miner hosting services to third parties.” — [SEC filing](https://investors.terawulf.com/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001083301-24-000072/wulf-20231231.htm) ([investors.terawulf.com](https://investors.terawulf.com/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001083301-24-000072/wulf-20231231.htm))
- Dec 1, 2023 (prospectus): Nautilus was “powered by 100% ‘behind the meter’ zero‑carbon nuclear energy … at a fixed rate of 2.0 cents per kWh for a term of five years.” — [SEC filing](https://investors.terawulf.com/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001628280-23-040459/0001628280-23-040459.pdf) ([investors.terawulf.com](https://investors.terawulf.com/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001628280-23-040459/0001628280-23-040459.pdf?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Mar 6, 2023: Company release notes energization at Nautilus with “fixed power cost … $0.02 per kilowatt hour.” — [Press release PDF](https://d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net/_e730d89937b3d29b0ab43471138e97ac/terawulf/news/2023-03-06_TeraWulf_Announces_Energization_and_Rapid_13.pdf) ([d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net](https://d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net/_e730d89937b3d29b0ab43471138e97ac/terawulf/news/2023-03-06_TeraWulf_Announces_Energization_and_Rapid_13.pdf?utm_source=openai))
- Oct 3, 2024: Law‑firm client news confirms sale of TeraWulf’s 25% Nautilus stake (~$92M). — [Paul, Weiss client news](https://www.paulweiss.com/insights/client-news/terawulf-sells-minority-stake-in-nautilus-cryptomine-joint-venture) ([paulweiss.com](https://www.paulweiss.com/insights/client-news/terawulf-sells-minority-stake-in-nautilus-cryptomine-joint-venture?utm_source=openai))

## WISEKEY N

* Rank: 45 | Expo: 3.40 | id_str: CH1276062754
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.3, bizserv 0.7, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Annual reports/SEC exhibits (2022–2024), H1 2024 earnings call, company press releases (2020–2024), financial-news transcript summaries (2024)

### Firm Overview
WISeKey International Holding AG is a Switzerland-based cybersecurity and digital identity company listed on SIX (WIHN) and on Nasdaq (WKEY). Its activities span IoT secure chips, PKI/Root-of-Trust software, and blockchain-enabled platforms including an NFT marketplace (WISe.ART) and the SEALCOIN transactional IoT initiative.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Crypto exposure arises primarily through non-financial services to the digital-asset ecosystem: WISeKey provides identity, PKI certificates and secure-element technology that underpin blockchain applications and NFT authenticity, and it positions SEALCOIN and satellite connectivity as part of a broader Web3/DePIN strategy. Management also operates WISe.ART, a marketplace for NFTs and tokenized assets, and has pursued tokenization of WISe.ART equity on Hedera, which constitutes limited crypto financial-services exposure. Earnings calls and filings discuss SEALCOIN, WISe.ART and blockchain integrations, but there is no indication of mining operations or treasury “HODL” of crypto. Across 2017–2024, crypto is strategic but economically peripheral relative to the core semiconductor/cybersecurity business.

### Weight Assignment
Given the firm’s focus on identity/PKI, device security and authenticity services used around blockchain and NFTs, bizserv = 0.7. Operating the WISe.ART marketplace and tokenization initiatives on Hedera justify a smaller finserv = 0.3. There is no evidence of deliberate crypto treasury holdings or mining, and hardware activities are not miner/compute infrastructure, so holders = 0.0, miners = 0.0, infrastr = 0.0. unclass = 0.0.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Transcript access for smaller Swiss issuers can be fragmented; at least one 2024 call transcript was retrieved from third‑party aggregators. WISe.ART was characterized as pre‑revenue in 2024, so crypto revenue materiality remains low and timing-sensitive. SEALCOIN and tokenization projects are evolving; classification reflects the 2017–2024 window and may shift if these initiatives scale.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 2, 2024 (H1 2024), transcript: “With SEALCOIN, we are not only redefining the IoT ecosystem, but also unlocking new revenue avenues for WISeKey.” — [Fintool transcript](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/WKEY/documents/transcripts/h1-2024) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/WKEY/documents/transcripts/h1-2024))
- Apr 22, 2025 (FY2024), transcript: “WISe.ART is the trusted blockchain and NFT segment of the group… Blockchain and identity platforms like SEALCOIN and WISeID are being deployed…” — [Roic.ai transcript](https://www.roic.ai/quote/WKEY/transcripts/2024-year/4-quarter) ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/WKEY/transcripts))

**Filings (10‑K / annual report / exhibits):**

## DOLPHIN ENTERTAINMENT

* Rank: 46 | Expo: 3.33 | id_str: US25686H3084
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.3, bizserv 0.7, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2021–2023), Q1–Q3 2021 earnings calls, company/news releases (2021–2022)

### Firm Overview
Dolphin Entertainment is a U.S.-listed entertainment marketing and content production holding company, with operations centered on publicity, influencer/social campaigns, and branded content for film, TV, music and lifestyle clients. Management launched “Dolphin 2.0” initiatives to own and market proprietary assets alongside its core services, which from 2021 included non-fungible token (NFT) projects. ([fintel.io](https://fintel.io/doc/sec-dolphin-entertainment-inc-1282224-10k-2023-march-31-19447-6114))

### Exposure Channels Identified
- Crypto financial services: In 2021 Dolphin announced a partnership with FTX US to co-develop consumer-facing NFT marketplaces for major sports and entertainment brands and later minted and sold its own NFT collection, Creature Chronicles: Exiled Aliens, recognizing gross sales of about $429,000 (≈13,175 SOL). These activities involve issuing and selling digital assets and operating programs tied to an exchange partner, fitting the finserv mechanism. ([newswire.com](https://www.newswire.com/news/dolphin-entertainment-and-ftx-announce-partnership-to-create-nft-marketplace?utm_source=openai))  
- Non-financial services to crypto ecosystem: Dolphin created an internal “NFT creation and marketing division” to design, produce, release and promote NFTs for itself and partners (e.g., Hall of Fame Resort NFTs; culinary and arts drops), reflecting marketing, creative and PR services around Web3 without custodying client assets. ([dolphinentertainment.com](https://dolphinentertainment.com/press/dolphin-entertainment-launches-non-fungible-tokens-nfts-creation-and-marketing-division-expects-first-partnership-in-collectibles/?utm_source=openai))  
- No evidence indicates mining operations or deliberate corporate crypto treasury holdings beyond transactional proceeds from NFT sales. Management also disclosed halting additional NFT collections amid a more uncertain crypto/NFT environment after 2022, implying exposure is strategic but peripheral to group revenue and assets. ([fintel.io](https://fintel.io/doc/sec-dolphin-entertainment-inc-1282224-10k-2023-march-31-19447-6114))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence shows two channels: (i) finserv via launching/operating NFT marketplace programs with FTX US and acting as principal in minting/selling NFT collections, and (ii) larger-scale non-financial services via creative, marketing and publicity work around NFTs for third parties. Given the emphasis and economics across 2017–2024, assign bizserv = 0.7 and finserv = 0.3. There is no support for miners, infrastruct, or holders (no sustained treasury “HODL”), so those remain 0.0; unclass = 0.0. ([newswire.com](https://www.newswire.com/news/dolphin-entertainment-and-ftx-announce-partnership-to-create-nft-marketplace?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The FTX marketplace partnership (announced August 2, 2021) became non-operational following the broader FTX collapse in late 2022; Dolphin’s 2023 10‑K states “no immediate plans” to design/sell additional NFT collections due to uncertainty, so post‑2022 finserv activity is limited. ([newswire.com](https://www.newswire.com/news/dolphin-entertainment-and-ftx-announce-partnership-to-create-nft-marketplace?utm_source=openai))  
- Transcript coverage is strong for 2021 but thinner for 2023–2024 on crypto topics; however, filings quantify 2022 NFT revenue, anchoring materiality. None of the sources indicate material crypto assets retained on balance sheet. ([fintel.io](https://fintel.io/doc/sec-dolphin-entertainment-inc-1282224-10k-2023-march-31-19447-6114))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 17, 2021 (Q1 2021): “In Q1 we announced the formation of our new division dedicated to designing, producing, releasing and promoting NFTs for ourselves and our partners…” — [Company transcript PDF] ([dolphinentertainment.com](https://dolphinentertainment.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/2021_q1_earnings-call.pdf))
- Aug 16, 2021 (Q2 2021): “Together, we will develop and program global NFT marketplaces… Dolphin will develop the creative branding… FTX will use their crypto exchange services and technical development as the backbone of the marketplaces.” — [Company transcript PDF] ([dolphinentertainment.com](https://dolphinentertainment.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/2021_Q2-Earnings_Call_-_8-16-21__Transcript_-_FINAL_.pdf))
- Nov 22, 2021 (Q3 2021): “Creature Chronicles: Exiled Aliens… priced at $200… payments via credit/debit cards and crypto… NFTs minted and stored on the Solana blockchain.” — [Company transcript PDF] ([dolphinentertainment.com](https://dolphinentertainment.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/q3_2021_Dolphin_Entertainment_Transcript_112221.pdf))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- 2023 Form 10‑K (covering FY2022 results): “minted… 7,777 NFTs… generated approximately 13,175 SOL (≈$429,000)… Company acted as principal and recorded gross revenues.” — [SEC/Fintel-hosted 10‑K] ([fintel.io](https://fintel.io/doc/sec-dolphin-entertainment-inc-1282224-10k-2023-march-31-19447-6114))
- 2021 Form 10‑K: notes share price jumped from $5.45 (Mar 22, 2021) to $18.33 (Mar 23, 2021) after announcing the NFT division — [AnnualReports.com PDF] ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/d/NASDAQ_DLPN_2021.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**
- Mar 23, 2021: “Dolphin Entertainment Launches Non‑Fungible Tokens (NFTs) Creation and Marketing Division.” — [Company press release] ([dolphinentertainment.com](https://dolphinentertainment.com/press/dolphin-entertainment-launches-non-fungible-tokens-nfts-creation-and-marketing-division-expects-first-partnership-in-collectibles/?utm_source=openai))
- Mar 23, 2021: Hall of Fame Resort partners with Dolphin to create NFTs (H2H platform). — [HOFV press release] ([ir.hofreco.com](https://ir.hofreco.com/press-releases/press-release/2021/Hall-of-Fame-Resort--Entertainment-Company-Signs-Partnership-with-Dolphin-Entertainment/default.aspx?utm_source=openai))
- Aug 2, 2021: “Dolphin Entertainment and FTX announce partnership to create NFT marketplace programs…” — [Newswire press release] ([newswire.com](https://www.newswire.com/news/dolphin-entertainment-and-ftx-announce-partnership-to-create-nft-marketplace?utm_source=openai))
- Nov 2021: “Dolphin Entertainment, FTX launching NFT marketplace for sports and entertainment brands.” — [Company repost of Variety exclusive] ([dolphinentertainment.com](https://dolphinentertainment.com/press/dolphin-entertainment-ftx-launching-nft-marketplace-for-sports-and-entertainment-brands-exclusive/?utm_source=openai))
- Mar 2022: Dolphin partners with female‑led NFT collection The Flower Girls. — [Benzinga] ([benzinga.com](https://www.benzinga.com/markets/cryptocurrency/22/03/25988806/dolphin-entertainment-confirms-earlier-report-co-partners-with-female-led-nft-collection-t?utm_source=openai))
- Mar 24, 2021: “Dolphin Entertainment shares soared… after NFT partnership news.” — [TheStreet] ([thestreet.com](https://www.thestreet.com/crypto/investing/dolphin-entertainment-dlpn-hall-of-fame-resort-nft?utm_source=openai))

## GMO GLOBALSIGN HOLDINGS KK

* Rank: 47 | Expo: 3.04 | id_str: JP3386040004
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Financial reports and IR tool (2017–2024), Q3 2023 financial results briefing transcript, company/market profiles

### Firm Overview
GMO GlobalSign Holdings K.K. is a Japan-listed IT company that operates GlobalSign, a global certificate authority and identity/security provider, alongside related businesses in e-signature, single sign-on/IDaaS, cloud infrastructure and digital transformation. The company is part of GMO Internet Group and reports segments centered on digital certificates/e-signature, cloud infrastructure and DX solutions.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024 investor materials and transcripts, the company’s discussion centers on PKI, SSL/TLS, e-signature and access management, with no references to cryptocurrency mining, exchange/brokerage, custody, token issuance, or corporate crypto holdings. Its offerings are general-purpose internet security and cloud services rather than crypto-specific products, and there is no evidence that crypto-related clients represent a disclosed or material segment. While the broader GMO Internet Group operates crypto businesses (e.g., stablecoins, exchange) via separate subsidiaries, these activities are not within GMO GlobalSign Holdings’ reporting perimeter. Therefore, no crypto-exposure mechanism applies for this entity. ([gmogshd.com](https://www.gmogshd.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/2023Q3_FinancialResultsBriefingTranscription.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Given the lack of substantive, entity-level evidence for any of the six crypto mechanisms during the 2017–2024 sample window, assign unclass = 1.0. Financial reports and the Q3 2023 briefing focus on PKI/e-signature and cloud/DX metrics with no crypto-related revenue lines or asset disclosures; parent-group crypto activities are outside this issuer’s scope. Consequently, holders, miners, finserv, bizserv and infrastr are all 0.0. ([gmogshd.com](https://www.gmogshd.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/2023Q3_FinancialResultsBriefingTranscription.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The parent GMO Internet Group engages in crypto initiatives (e.g., GMO-Z.com Trust stablecoins, GMO Coin exchange). These could be conflated with GMO GlobalSign Holdings, but sources indicate such activities sit in other subsidiaries, not this issuer. ([stablecoin.z.com](https://stablecoin.z.com/company/?utm_source=openai))  
- Parent-level materials sometimes use “crypto security (SSL)” to denote cryptography/PKI, which is not cryptocurrency exposure. ([ir.gmo.jp](https://ir.gmo.jp/en/pdf/presen/GMO_FY22_Q4_script_230213_e.pdf?utm_source=openai))  
- No explicit disclosure rules in filings segregate sales to crypto-native clients; incidental demand from crypto firms (if any) is not disclosed and appears immaterial within reported segments. ([gmogshd.com](https://www.gmogshd.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/FY2024-Q4-Financial-Report.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 13, 2023 (Q3 FY2023): “Sales of GMO Sign and GMO Trust Login, our focus businesses, grew strongly… the electronic authentication and seals business increased…” — [Q3 2023 Financial Results Briefing Transcript] (https://www.gmogshd.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/2023Q3_FinancialResultsBriefingTranscription.pdf)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- FY2024 Q4 Financial Report: segment overview (Digital Certificate & eSignature; Cloud Infrastructure; DX); parent ownership context — [Company IR PDF] (https://www.gmogshd.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/FY2024-Q4-Financial-Report.pdf)
- FY2024 Q1 Financial Report: emphasis on certification authority and e-signature growth; SSL recovery in Europe/US — [Company IR PDF] (https://www.gmogshd.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/FY2024-1Q_Financial-Report.pdf)
- IR Tool archive (2017–2024) listing transcripts/presentations — [Company IR page] (https://www.gmogshd.com/english/ir/tool)

**Financial news / other:**
- Company profile confirming business scope (formerly GMO Cloud KK; IT services/security) — [CNBC GMOGF] (https://www.cnbc.com/quotes/GMOGF)
- GlobalSign 2024 highlights (identity security, digital signing, IoT solutions) — [GlobalSign News] (https://www.globalsign.com/en/company/news-events/news/gmo-globalsign-caps-year-expansion)
- Group-level crypto activity for context: GMO-Z.com Trust (GYEN, ZUSD) — [GMO Trust] (https://stablecoin.z.com/company/)

## SBI HDG.

* Rank: 48 | Expo: 2.77 | id_str: JP3436120004
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.2, finserv 0.8, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Integrated Report 2024, FY2022 financial results presentation, Q1 FY2025 results briefing transcript (Aug 2, 2024)

### Firm Overview
SBI Holdings, Inc. is a Tokyo‑listed diversified financial group spanning securities, banking and insurance, with additional businesses in asset management, investments and next‑generation fields. Within this structure it operates a defined Crypto‑asset Business segment housing exchange/brokerage platforms, an institutional market‑maker and related Web3 services. ([sbigroup.co.jp](https://www.sbigroup.co.jp/english/investors/library/annualreport/pdf/2024ar_e-all.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
- Financial services: SBI operates retail and institutional crypto trading and related services via SBI VC Trade and BITPOINT Japan, and controls U.K.-based institutional market‑maker B2C2; management highlights staking products and growing customer balances, and the segment delivered ¥57.1bn revenue in FY2023. ([sbigroup.co.jp](https://www.sbigroup.co.jp/english/investors/library/annualreport/pdf/2024ar_e-all.pdf))  
- Financial infrastructure/venues: Through SBI Digital Asset Holdings, the group co‑founded AsiaNext (with SIX) to offer institutional digital‑asset derivatives, expanding crypto‑related trading services for professional clients. ([sbidm.com](https://sbidm.com/press-releases/asianext-to-start-trading-crypto-derivatives/?utm_source=openai))  
- Mining: SBI Crypto operates a mining business and previously launched a public mining pool; management materials also note mining‑related impairments and Russia exit during FY2022’s downturn. ([sbigroup.co.jp](https://www.sbigroup.co.jp/english/investors/disclosure/presentation/pdf/230512presentations.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
The dominant exposure is crypto financial services: consolidated materials describe the Crypto‑asset Business as primarily an exchange/trading business (
[continued]

### Weight Assignment
The dominant exposure is crypto financial services: consolidated materials describe the Crypto‑asset Business as primarily an exchange/trading business with staking and lending features at SBI VC Trade and BITPOINT; this segment generated ¥57.1 billion of revenue in FY2023, making finserv the clear majority of crypto exposure. The group also operates mining via SBI Crypto; however, FY2022 presentations note mining‑related impairments and completion of the Russia exit, indicating a reduced but present mining footprint, which merits a smaller miners weight. There is no evidence of treasury HODL outside operational needs, nor of crypto‑specific hardware supply, so holders, bizserv and infrastr remain 0.0. 

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Transcript access relies on a third‑party aggregator (Roic AI) for the Q1 FY2025 briefing held in August 2024; the quoted portions fall within the sample window (April–June 2024 quarter).   
- Management materials indicate downsizing/impairments and exit from Russia in the mining business during FY2022; residual mining scope by geography is not fully detailed in 2023–2024 public slides.   
- Reported crypto balances at exchanges are customer assets; no separate disclosure suggests deliberate corporate treasury accumulation beyond operational requirements. Segment descriptions frame activity as exchange/trading services. 

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 2, 2024 (Q1 FY2025 results briefing): “This is number of customers in crypto‑assets… plus 10% Q‑o‑Q increase.” / “SBI VC Trade and BITPOINT achieved a customer balance of ¥100 billion.” — [Roic AI transcript] (https://www.roic.ai/quote/8473.T/transcripts/2025-year/1-quarter) 

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Integrated Report 2024: Crypto‑asset Business revenue ¥57.1bn (+88.5% YoY) and profit before income tax expense ¥8.4bn; segment overview and service lineup snapshots. — [SBI Holdings IR PDF] (https://www.sbigroup.co.jp/english/investors/library/annualreport/pdf/2024ar_e-all.pdf) 
- FY2022 Financial Results Presentation: Crypto‑asset Business performance; notes on losses at B2C2 linked to counterparty bankruptcies; mining impairments and “withdrawal from the mining business in Russia has been completed”; constituent companies include SBI VC Trade, B2C2, BITPoint, and mining. — [SBI Holdings IR PDF] (https://www.sbigroup.co.jp/english/investors/disclosure/presentation/pdf/230512presentations.pdf) 
- SBI Crypto announces launch of public mining pool service (Mar 18, 2021). — [PR Newswire release] (https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/sbi-crypto-announces-start-of-its-mining-pool-service-301250316.html) ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/sbi-crypto-announces-start-of-its-mining-pool-service-301250316.html?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Dec 15, 2020: SBI Financial Services acquires B2C2, positioning SBI at the center of institutional crypto trading. — [Business Wire] (https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20201215005758/en/Japanese-Powerhouse-SBI-Becomes-Worlds-First-Financial-Conglomerate-to-Open-Cryptocurrency-Dealing-Desk-by-Acquiring-Pioneering-Liquidity-Provider-B2C2) ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20201215005758/en/Japanese-Powerhouse-SBI-Becomes-Worlds-First-Financial-Conglomerate-to-Open-Cryptocurrency-Dealing-Desk-by-Acquiring-Pioneering-Liquidity-Provider-B2C2?utm_source=openai))
- Jul 3, 2020: SBI takes $30m minority stake in B2C2 to expand crypto offerings for customers. — [Business Wire] (https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20200630006130/en/Financial-Conglomerate-SBI-to-Acquire-%2430MM-Stake-in-Digital-Asset-Firm-B2C2) ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20200630006130/en/Financial-Conglomerate-SBI-to-Acquire-%2430MM-Stake-in-Digital-Asset-Firm-B2C2?utm_source=openai))
- Dec 15, 2020: Coverage of SBI’s B2C2 acquisition and strategic rationale. — [CoinDesk] (https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2020/12/15/sbi-financial-acquires-institutional-crypto-desk-b2c2) ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2020/12/15/sbi-financial-acquires-institutional-crypto-desk-b2c2?utm_source=openai))

</small>

## SEMLER SCIENTIFIC

* Rank: 49 | Expo: 2.72 | id_str: US81684M1045
* Weight summary: holders 1.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 5
* Primary sources: 10-K filing (FY2024), Q2–Q3 2024 earnings calls, 2024 press releases, mainstream financial news (May 2024)

### Firm Overview
Semler Scientific is a U.S.-based medical technology company listed on Nasdaq that develops and markets diagnostic products for chronic disease detection, notably the QuantaFlo test for peripheral arterial disease. In 2024 the company also adopted a bitcoin treasury strategy, adding digital assets to its balance sheet alongside its healthcare operations.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s crypto exposure is through treasury holdings: management adopted bitcoin as the primary treasury reserve asset in May 2024 and has since accumulated bitcoin held on the balance sheet, recognizing fair value changes in earnings. As of December 31, 2024, the 10-K reports 2,298 bitcoins with an aggregate fair value of approximately $214.6 million, and bitcoin comprised the overwhelming majority of reported assets at year‑end 2024. There is no evidence that Semler Scientific operates mining, offers exchange/custody/brokerage or other crypto financial services, or supplies crypto-specific hardware infrastructure. Share-price reactions and management remarks indicate that crypto market sentiment influences the investor base following the treasury shift. ([ir.semlerscientific.com](https://ir.semlerscientific.com/news-releases/news-release-details/semler-scientificr-announces-bitcoin-treasury-strategy?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Holders = 1.0 reflects explicit board adoption of bitcoin as the primary treasury reserve asset and substantial bitcoin reported on the balance sheet by year‑end 2024. Miners, finserv, bizserv and infrastr are 0.0 due to no credible evidence of mining operations, customer-facing crypto financial services, non‑financial crypto services, or crypto‑specific hardware/hosting. The investor‑base amplifier is set to 1 in light of immediate media‑documented stock moves on the May 28, 2024 announcement and management’s comments about strong investor response and embrace by the bitcoin community. ([ir.semlerscientific.com](https://ir.semlerscientific.com/news-releases/news-release-details/semler-scientificr-announces-bitcoin-treasury-strategy?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The FY2024 Form 10-K was filed in March 2025 but covers balances as of December 31, 2024; later 2025 activity is outside the stated 2017–2024 sample and is not used for weighting.  
- Earnings-transcript access relies on a third‑party aggregator (Roic AI) for Q2 and Q3 2024 calls; quotations align with the sample window.  
- No evidence was found of mining or customer crypto services; classification would need revision only if the company were to launch such activities in future periods. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1554859/000155485925000011/smlr-20241231x10k.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 5, 2024 (Q2 2024): “used most of our cash and cash equivalents to purchase bitcoin… we may now hold 929 bitcoins as of August 5th, 2024.” — [Roic AI transcript] (https://www.roic.ai/quote/SMLR/transcripts/2024/2) ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/SMLR/transcripts/2024/2))  
- Nov 4, 2024 (Q3 2024): “used most of our cash and cash equivalents to buy Bitcoin… as of November 5, 2024, we hold 1,058 Bitcoins.” — [Roic AI transcript] (https://www.roic.ai/quote/SMLR/transcripts/2024/3) ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/SMLR/transcripts/2024/3))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- May 28, 2024 press release: board “adopted bitcoin as its primary treasury reserve asset” and initial purchase of 581 BTC for $40m. — [Semler Scientific IR] (https://ir.semlerscientific.com/news-releases/news-release-details/semler-scientificr-announces-bitcoin-treasury-strategy) ([ir.semlerscientific.com](https://ir.semlerscientific.com/news-releases/news-release-details/semler-scientificr-announces-bitcoin-treasury-strategy?utm_source=openai))  
- Aug 5, 2024 Q2 results: total holdings 929 BTC as of Aug 5, 2024 (aggregate purchase $63m). — [PR Newswire copy] (https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/semler-scientific-reports-second-quarter-2024-financial-results-and-additional-bitcoin-purchases-302214705.html) ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/semler-scientific-reports-second-quarter-2024-financial-results-and-additional-bitcoin-purchases-302214705.html?utm_source=openai))  
- Dec 16, 2024 update: now holds 2,084 BTC; average purchase price ~$80,916/BTC. — [Semler Scientific IR] (https://ir.semlerscientific.com/news-releases/news-release-details/semler-scientificr-announces-updated-btc-and-atm-activity-1) ([ir.semlerscientific.com](https://ir.semlerscientific.com/news-releases/news-release-details/semler-scientificr-announces-updated-btc-and-atm-activity-1?utm_source=openai))  
- FY2024 10-K: 2,298 bitcoins with fair value ~$214.6m at 12/31/2024; total assets ~$218.3m. — [SEC 10‑K] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1554859/000155485925000011/smlr-20241231x10k.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1554859/000155485925000011/smlr-20241231x10k.htm))  
- FY2024 results release: “2024 was a transformative year… embraced a bitcoin treasury strategy”; pre‑tax income includes $24.9m unrealized gain from change in fair value of bitcoin holdings. — [Semler Scientific IR] (https://ir.semlerscientific.com/news-releases/news-release-details/semler-scientificr-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2024) ([ir.semlerscientific.com](https://ir.semlerscientific.com/news-releases/news-release-details/semler-scientificr-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2024?utm_source=openai))

## COMPOSECURE A

* Rank: 50 | Expo: 2.52 | id_str: US20459V1052
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (FY2024), Q1 2022 and Q3 2024 earnings calls, CompoSecure press releases (2021–2024)

### Firm Overview
CompoSecure Inc. is a U.S.-based manufacturer of premium metal payment cards serving major banks and fintechs; it also develops Arculus, a security platform featuring a credit‑card‑shaped hardware device and app for digital asset cold storage and authentication launched in 2021. Management reports Arculus as a separate operating segment alongside the core Payment Card business. ([fintel.io](https://fintel.io/doc/sec-composecure-inc-1823144-10k-2025-march-05-20152-4218))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Crypto exposure arises from providing non‑custodial hardware wallet and authentication technology (Arculus) to consumers and enterprise partners; the company does not custody, broker, or exchange digital assets. The FY2024 Form 10‑K reports two segments—Payment Card and Arculus—with Arculus generating $10.5 million of 2024 net sales out of $420.6 million total and identifiable assets of $5.6 million at year‑end 2024. Earnings calls across 2022–2024 repeatedly discuss Arculus cold storage and authentication use cases for crypto platforms and users. There is no evidence of mining, crypto treasury holdings, or crypto‑specific compute/energy infrastructure. ([fintel.io](https://fintel.io/doc/sec-composecure-inc-1823144-10k-2025-march-05-20152-4218))

### Weight Assignment
Bizserv = 1.0 reflects that CompoSecure supplies non‑financial products and services (hardware wallet, authentication, white‑label solutions) enabling others to access and secure digital assets without CompoSecure taking custody or transacting in the assets itself. Holders, miners, finserv and infrastr are set to 0.0 due to lack of credible evidence of treasury holdings, mining, customer‑facing exchange/custody/brokerage activity, or crypto‑mining hardware/compute or energy services during 2017–2024. ([fintel.io](https://fintel.io/doc/sec-composecure-inc-1823144-10k-2025-march-05-20152-4218))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The Arculus app includes “buy/swap” functionality via partners, but filings and disclosures indicate CompoSecure provides the wallet/authentication layer and not asset custody or brokerage; therefore exposure is classified as bizserv rather than finserv. ([ir.composecure.com](https://ir.composecure.com/news-releases/news-release-details/composecures-arculus-cold-storage-wallet-debut-september-9/?utm_source=openai))  
- FY2024 results and segment detail are reported in a 10‑K filed March 5, 2025 but cover activity through December 31, 2024, which remains within the stated sample window. ([fintel.io](https://fintel.io/doc/sec-composecure-inc-1823144-10k-2025-march-05-20152-4218))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 9, 2024 (Q3 2024): “The benefit to Arculus… is it’s using the FIDO standard… incorporating it into a payment card.” — [Investing.com] (https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-composecure-reports-growth-amid-market-challenges-93CH-3713303) ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-composecure-reports-growth-amid-market-challenges-93CH-3713303))  
- May 9, 2022 (Q1 2022): “payment card plus Arculus digital authentication and/or crypto cold storage has been selected by several cryptocurrency platforms and fintech partners…” — [MLQ.ai transcript] (https://mlq.ai/stocks/CMPOV/earnings-call-transcript/Q1-2022) ([mlq.ai](https://mlq.ai/stocks/CMPOV/earnings-call-transcript/Q1-2022))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- FY2024 10‑K (filed Mar 5, 2025): reports two segments (Payment Card, Arculus); 2024 net sales: Payment Card $410.1m, Arculus $10.
- FY2024 10‑K (filed Mar 5, 2025): two reportable segments; 2024 net sales Payment Card $410.1m and Arculus $10.5m; Arculus identifiable assets $5.6m at 12/31/2024. — [SEC 10‑K] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1823144/000182314425000004/cmpo-20241231.htm)  
- Nov 7, 2024: Q3 2024 results release referencing Arculus security/authentication; updated FY2024 outlook. — [CompoSecure IR] (https://ir.composecure.com/news-releases/news-release-details/composecure-reports-strong-third-quarter-2024-financial-results/)  
- Mar 5, 2025: FY2024 results release notes first quarter of positive net contribution from Arculus in Q4 2024. — [GlobeNewswire] (https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/03/05/3037742/0/en/CompoSecure-Reports-Fourth-Quarter-and-Full-Year-2024-Financial-Results.html)  
- Oct 13, 2021: Announces general availability and shipping of Arculus Key Card and wallet app. — [Business Wire] (https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20211013005251/en/CompoSecure-Announces-General-Availability-and-Shipping-of-Its-Arculus-KeyTM-Card-for-%2499-Enabling-Consumers-to-Manage-Cryptocurrency-in-One-Secure-Place)  
- Sept 9, 2021: Arculus Wallet debut announcement. — [CompoSecure IR] (https://ir.composecure.com/news-releases/news-release-details/composecures-arculus-cold-storage-wallet-debut-september-9/)

**Financial news / other:**
- Jan 28, 2022: Needham note says Arculus could boost CompoSecure revenue as a new hardware wallet entrant. — [CoinDesk] (https://www.coindesk.com/business/2022/01/28/arculus-crypto-wallet-to-boost-payment-card-firm-composecures-revenue-needham)

- June 7, 2022: Arculus expands to NFTs and WalletConnect; positions wallet as a secure Web3 gateway. — [CompoSecure IR] (https://ir.composecure.com/news-releases/news-release-details/composecure-expands-arculustm-capabilities-launching-nft-support/)

## NANO LABS A

* Rank: 51 | Expo: 2.49 | id_str: KYG6391Y1281
* Weight summary: holders 0.3, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.7, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 4 | AssetShare: 3
* Primary sources: Form 20‑F (FY2024), H2 2023 earnings call transcript, press releases (2024)

### Firm Overview
Nano Labs Ltd is a China‑based fabless integrated circuit design company that develops high‑throughput computing (HTC) and high‑performance computing (HPC) chips and sells iPollo‑branded blockchain mining machines targeting cryptocurrencies such as Ethereum Classic, Grin and Bitcoin. In late 2024, the company also adopted Bitcoin as its primary reserve asset. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1872302/000121390025031065/ea0235323-20f_nanolabs.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
- Infrastructure: Filings describe multiple generations of “Cuckoo” HTC chips and Darkbird HPC chips, and a full iPollo product lineup for ETC/Grin/BTC mining (e.g., V1/V2 and B1L models), indicating direct exposure via supplying mining hardware. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1872302/000121390025031065/ea0235323-20f_nanolabs.htm))  
- Holders: The FY2024 Form 20‑F states Nano Labs “adopted Bitcoin as [its] primary reserve asset” and, as of December 31, 2024, held approximately 361 BTC (fair value ~RMB 242.9 million) versus total assets of ~RMB 615.9 million (roughly 39% of assets). A November 18, 2024 press release also announced the Bitcoin reserve strategy. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1872302/000121390025031065/ea0235323-20f_nanolabs.htm))  
- No evidence was found that the company operates mining farms, provides exchange/custody/brokerage services, or offers non‑financial services to crypto clients beyond its hardware footprint. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1872302/000121390025031065/ea0235323-20f_nanolabs.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Infrastructure = 0.7 reflects that Nano Labs’ core business throughout 2017–2024 is designing chips and selling iPollo mining machines, which drive the majority of revenue. Holders = 0.3 captures the late‑2024 decision to adopt Bitcoin as a primary reserve asset with 361 BTC recorded at year‑end 2024, a significant share of the balance sheet. Miners, finserv and bizserv are set to 0.0 due to lack of credible evidence of self‑mining, exchange/custody/brokerage activities, or non‑financial support services as a revenue line during the sample window. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1872302/000121390025031065/ea0235323-20f_nanolabs.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Management expanded crypto‑treasury activities after 2024 as reported in later news, but classification here relies on disclosures through December 31, 2024 per the sample window. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1872302/000121390025031065/ea0235323-20f_nanolabs.htm))  
- Some transcripts are hosted via third‑party mirrors; however, their contents align with the company’s IR announcements and SEC filings. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-nano-labs-reports-mixed-results-amid-market-recovery-93CH-3342968))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Mar 18, 2024 (H2 2023): “Net revenue was RMB26.1 million… The decrease… was primarily due to the drop of sales volume of iPollo V1 Series and B1 Series.” — [Investing.com] (https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-nano-labs-reports-mixed-results-amid-market-recovery-93CH-3342968) ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-nano-labs-reports-mixed-results-amid-market-recovery-93CH-3342968))  
- Mar 14, 2023 (Q4 2022): “The fourth quarter of 2022 was a difficult quarter for our industry and overall crypto market… the conversion of Ethereum from POW to POS… partially impacted our business.” — [StockInsights transcript] (https://www.stockinsights.ai/us/NA/earnings-transcript/fy22-q4-988b) ([stockinsights.ai](https://www.stockinsights.ai/us/NA/earnings-transcript/fy22-q4-988b))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- FY2024 Form 20‑F (filed Apr 2025): Adopted Bitcoin as primary reserve asset; held ~361 BTC and 9 USDT at 12/31/2024 (fair value ~RMB 242.9m) vs total assets ~RMB 615.9m; detailed Cuckoo/Darkbird chips and iPollo mining machines. — [SEC 20‑F] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1872302/000121390025031065/ea0235323-20f_nanolabs.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1872302/000121390025031065/ea0235323-20f_nanolabs.htm))  
- Nov 18, 2024: Announces Bitcoin as strategic reserve asset. — [GlobeNewswire] (https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2024/11/18/2982722/0/en/Nano-Labs-Announces-Bitcoin-as-Its-Strategic-Reserve-Asset.html) ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2024/11/18/2982722/0/en/Nano-Labs-Announces-Bitcoin-as-Its-Strategic-Reserve-Asset.html?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Mar 25, 2025: H2 2024 results release notes ongoing product developments (covers period within 2024). — [Nano Labs IR] (https://ir.nano.cn/node/7906/pdf) ([ir.nano.cn](https://ir.nano.cn/node/7906/pdf?utm_source=openai))

## METROPOLITAN BANK HDG.

* Rank: 52 | Expo: 2.43 | id_str: US5917741044
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10‑K filing (FY2024), Q4 2021 and Q1/Q3 2023 earnings calls, company press release (Jan 9, 2023), Reuters/industry coverage

### Firm Overview
Metropolitan Bank Holding Corp. is the New York‑based parent of Metropolitan Commercial Bank, a commercial bank focused on lending and deposit verticals and a Global Payments Group that serves non‑bank financial services companies. The company completed its exit from business associated with digital currency entities in 2023, as noted in its FY2024 Form 10‑K. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0001476034/000155837025001884/mcb-20241231x10k.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
- Financial services: Management described providing “basic banking services, including deposit accounts and payment rail access to non‑bank financial service companies” engaged in retail and commercial activities, with explicit discussion of crypto‑related clients and flows during the Q1 2023 call. Q4 2021 remarks detailed crypto‑related deposits and the issuance of prepaid debit cards linked to crypto platforms, as well as settling buy/sell transactions for exchanges. ([insidermonkey.com](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/metropolitan-bank-holding-corp-nysemcb-q1-2023-earnings-call-transcript-1141957/))  
- Exit from crypto vertical: On January 9, 2023 the company announced it would fully exit its crypto‑asset related vertical, stating it had four active institutional crypto clients accounting for about 1.5% of total revenues and 6% of deposits; later calls and filings confirmed the wind‑down and completion in 2023. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1476034/000094337423000007/ex99-1.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- No evidence indicates proprietary crypto holdings, mining operations, non‑financial services to crypto firms, or provision of mining hardware/compute; management also emphasized the bank does not hold crypto‑assets on its balance sheet. ([cryptonews.com](https://cryptonews.com/news/metropolitan-commercial-bank-closes-crypto-vertical-emphasises-it-no-liabilities/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 reflects that the company’s sole crypto‑related activity during 2017–2023 was banking‑as‑a‑service/payment rails and deposit accounts for crypto platforms (including card issuing), which generated fee income and crypto‑related deposits but not proprietary asset exposure. The January 2023 exit and 2024 filing confirming completion fall within the sample window yet do not change the mechanism: exposure, when present, was entirely through financial services rather than holdings, mining, infrastructure, or non‑financial services. Holders, miners, bizserv, and infrastr are set to 0.0 accordingly. ([tickertrends.io](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/MCB/Q4-earnings-transcript-2021))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Management reported the crypto vertical was small (about 1.5% of revenue and 6% of deposits at announcement), which constrains materiality estimates; detailed product‑level revenue splits beyond transcript figures are limited. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1476034/000094337423000007/ex99-1.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- Some transcripts are hosted by third‑party mirrors, but quoted passages are consistent with company IR materials and SEC filings. ([tickertrends.io](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/MCB/Q4-earnings-transcript-2021))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q4 2021 (Jan 2022), transcript: “Crypto‑related deposits nearly doubled in the quarter to $1.5 billion… [we] help settle transactions for exchanges,” and explanation of prepaid debit cards tied to crypto accounts. — [TickerTrends] (https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/MCB/Q4-earnings-transcript-2021) ([tickertrends.io](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/MCB/Q4-earnings-transcript-2021))  
- Q1 2023 (Apr 26, 2023): “We are very pleased with the progress we made this quarter in exiting our crypto space… expect to complete our exit by the end of the second quarter… Crypto‑related deposits… $278 million at March 31,” with most remaining balances in FBO accounts subject to BIN transfer. — [Insider Monkey] (https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/metropolitan-bank-holding-corp-nysemcb-q1-2023-earnings-call-transcript-1141957/) ([insidermonkey.com](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/metropolitan-bank-holding-corp-nysemcb-q1-2023-earnings-call-transcript-1141957/))  
- Q3 2023 (Oct 23, 2023): “Excess liquidity… allowed us to pay down… borrowings, which were used specifically to off‑ramp our previous on‑balance sheet crypto deposits,” and “noninterest income… down… due largely to the exit from crypto.” — [StockInsights] (https://www.stockinsights.ai/us/MCB/earnings-transcript/fy23-q3-9755) ([stockinsights.ai](https://www.stockinsights.ai/us/MCB/earnings-transcript/fy23-q3-9755))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Jan 9, 2023 press release: decision to “fully exit the crypto‑asset related vertical”; four active institutional crypto clients; ~1.5% of revenues and 6% of deposits. — [Company press release (Q4 CDN)] (https://s206.q4cdn.com/586823346/files/doc_news/Metropolitan-Bank-Holding-Corp--to-Exit-Crypto-Asset-Related-Vertical-2023.pdf) ([s206.q4cdn.com](https://s206.q4cdn.com/586823346/files/doc_news/Metropolitan-Bank-Holding-Corp--to-Exit-Crypto-Asset-Related-Vertical-2023.pdf?utm_source=openai))  
- FY2024 Form 10‑K: “In 2023, the Company completed the exit from the business associated with digital currency entities.” — [SEC] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0001476034/000155837025001884/mcb-20241231x10k.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0001476034/000155837025001884/mcb-20241231x10k.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- Q2/Q3 2023 results releases: deposit changes reflect “final exit from the crypto‑related vertical.” — [IR (Q2 2023)] (https://investors.mcbankny.com/news-events/news/news-details/2023/Metropolitan-Bank-Holding-Corp.-Reports-Second-Quarter-2023-Results/default.aspx) • [IR (Q3 2023)] (https://s206.q4cdn.com/586823346/files/doc_news/Metropolitan-Bank-Holding-Corp--Reports-Third-Quarter-2023-Results-2023.pdf) ([investors.mcbankny.com](https://investors.mcbankny.com/news-events/news/news-details/2023/Metropolitan-Bank-Holding-Corp.-Reports-Second-Quarter-2023-Results/default.aspx?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Jan 9, 2023: Reuters coverage of the exit announcement (four clients; ~1.5% revenue; 6% deposits). — [Reuters syndication] (https://kelo.com/2023/01/09/regional-lender-metropolitan-bank-to-exit-crypto-related-asset-vertical/) ([kelo.com](https://kelo.com/2023/01/09/regional-lender-metropolitan-bank-to-exit-crypto-related-asset-vertical/?utm_source=openai))  
- Apr 20, 2023: Exit “almost complete,” with crypto‑related deposits ~4% of total at March 31. — [CoinDesk] (https://www.coindesk.com/business/2023/04/20/metropolitan-banks-exit-from-crypto-almost-complete) ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2023/04/20/metropolitan-banks-exit-from-crypto-almost-complete?utm_source=openai))

## FLOW TRADERS

* Rank: 53 | Expo: 2.42 | id_str: BMG3602E1084
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: Annual Reports (2021–2022), Q2 2021, Q2 2022, Q2 2023, Q2 2024 earnings calls

### Firm Overview
Flow Traders N.V. is a Netherlands‑based proprietary trading firm and leading market maker, historically focused on exchange‑traded products and now active across multiple asset classes. Since 2017 it has built a dedicated digital assets capability spanning market making in crypto ETPs, OTC trading in digital assets, and strategic investments aligned with its trading franchise.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s crypto exposure is through financial services: it provides liquidity and market making in crypto ETPs across Europe and other regions, trades spot and derivatives in digital assets with institutional counterparties, and operates an OTC offering. Management repeatedly discusses crypto/digital assets alongside fixed income and FX as one of three trading pillars and highlights registrations and partnerships that enable regulated participation (e.g., DNB registration; TP ICAP and Boerse Stuttgart Digital partnerships). There is no evidence of crypto mining, hardware/compute provisioning, or non‑financial services to crypto firms, and crypto positions are held for trading rather than as treasury assets. ([flowtraders.com](https://www.flowtraders.com/news/flow-traders-remains-committed-to-the-digital-asset-ecosystem/))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 reflects that all substantiated activity is the direct handling and trading of digital assets and liquidity provision for crypto ETPs/ETFs and OTC markets. Earnings calls across 2021–2024 describe crypto market making, expanding coverage of crypto ETPs, and acting as lead market maker on launches (including supporting a US Bitcoin ETF), while filings state Flow Traders is the number one liquidity provider in European crypto ETPs and explain accounting for digital asset trading positions. No evidence supports holders, miners, infrastr, or bizserv exposures; these are set to 0.0 accordingly. ([flowtraders.com](https://www.flowtraders.com/media/g32prq22/flow-traders-q221-results-analyst-call-transcript.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The firm does not disclose net trading income by asset class; management has explicitly declined to break out crypto’s NTI contribution, so revenue materiality can only be coded conservatively. ([flowtraders.com](https://www.flowtraders.com/media/g32prq22/flow-traders-q221-results-analyst-call-transcript.pdf))  
- Annual reporting confirms digital asset positions and borrowings are part of trading activities, but balance‑sheet shares versus total assets are not separately quantified in the public summary sections reviewed. ([flowtraders.com](https://www.flowtraders.com/media/y5uotj1i/flow-traders-annual-report-2022.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q2 & H1 2021 (Jul 23, 2021): “In Europe… robust trading performance in Q2 with contribution from crypto market making activities… partnered with TP ICAP on the launch of [a] new crypto platform… commenced coverage of Grayscale products and Canadian crypto ETPs… further develop our crypto business… maintaining our number one market maker position in crypto ETPs in Europe.” — [Flow Traders IR transcript] (https://www.flowtraders.com/media/g32prq22/flow-traders-q221-results-analyst-call-transcript.pdf) ([flowtraders.com](https://www.flowtraders.com/media/g32prq22/flow-traders-q221-results-analyst-call-transcript.pdf))  
- Q2 & H1 2022 (Jul 22, 2022): “Bitcoin… experienced a sharp price decline… [which] impacted crypto ETP value traded… we continued to provide liquidity across the crypto markets… we remain convinced of the long‑term potential of digital assets.” — [Flow Traders IR transcript] (https://www.flowtraders.com/media/uzsdgdnl/flow-traders-q222-results-call-transcript-vf.pdf) ([flowtraders.com](https://www.flowtraders.com/media/uzsdgdnl/flow-traders-q222-results-call-transcript-vf.pdf))  
- Q2 2023 (Jul 28, 2023): “Value traded… saw corresponding declines… with crypto seeing the largest decline… we retain our long‑term conviction… continue to expand our coverage of crypto ETPs… and expand the OTC bilateral counterparty business.” — [Flow Traders IR transcript] (https://www.flowtraders.com/media/wufkianh/flow-traders-q2-2023-results-28-july-2023.pdf) ([flowtraders.com](https://www.flowtraders.com/media/wufkianh/flow-traders-q2-2023-results-28-july-2023.pdf))  
- Q2 2024 (Jul 26, 2024): “Crypto saw the largest increase year‑on‑year… trading volume and volatility in digital assets was up significantly YoY… we assisted our partners in the US with a Bitcoin ETF launched in the half as a lead market maker… regulatory approval of the first spot Bitcoin ETFs in the US… anticipate growing investor demand for digital assets.” — [Flow Traders IR transcript] (https://www.flowtraders.com/media/dqkf0203/flow-traders-q2-2024-results-conference-call-transcript.pdf) ([flowtraders.com](https://www.flowtraders.com/media/dqkf0203/flow-traders-q2-2024-results-conference-call-transcript.pdf))

**Filings (Annual reports / corporate documents):**
- Annual Report 2022: “Flow Traders’ crypto liquidity provision commenced 5 years ago and spans OTC spot as well as ETPs… connected to 20 trading venues… trade 300 coins… number 1 liquidity provider in crypto ETPs in Europe with ~45% market share… digital asset positions presented as other assets held for trading; loans in digital assets presented as other liabilities held for trading.” — [Flow Traders 2022 Annual Report] (https://www.flowtraders.com/media/y5uotj1i/flow-traders-annual-report-2022.pdf) ([flowtraders.com](https://www.flowtraders.com/media/y5uotj1i/flow-traders-annual-report-2022.pdf))  
- Corporate news (Nov 28, 2022): “Flow Traders has been trading cryptocurrencies since 2017… secured crypto registration from the Dutch Central Bank (DNB) in 2021.” — [Flow Traders News] (https://www.flowtraders.com/news/flow-traders-remains-committed-to-the-digital-asset-ecosystem/) ([flowtraders.com](https://www.flowtraders.com/news/flow-traders-remains-committed-to-the-digital-asset-ecosystem/))

**Financial news / other:**
- Feb 10, 2022: “Flow Traders… becoming a significant player in European crypto ETPs… 45% market share among 125 crypto ETP listings in EMEA.” — [Reuters via Euronews] (https://www.euronews.com/next/2022/02/10/flow-traders-results) ([euronews.com](https://www.euronews.com/next/2022/02/10/flow-traders-results?utm_source=openai))  
- Jun 29, 2021: TP ICAP announces crypto trading platform partnership including Flow Traders as liquidity provider. — [TP ICAP press release] (https://tpicap.com/tpicap/media/press-releases/2021/tp-icap-launch-cryptoasset-trading-platform-collaboration-fidelity-digital-assets-zodia-custody-and-flow-traders) ([tpicap.com](https://tpicap.com/tpicap/media/press-releases/2021/tp-icap-launch-cryptoasset-trading-platform-collaboration-fidelity-digital-assets-zodia-custody-and-flow-traders?utm_source=openai))

## ZYMEWORKS

* Rank: 54 | Expo: 2.18 | id_str: US98985Y1082
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filing (2023), Q2 2024 and Q3 2023 earnings call transcripts

### Firm Overview
Zymeworks is a royalty-driven biopharmaceutical company focused on developing antibody-based therapeutics for difficult-to-treat cancers, led by the HER2‑targeted bispecific zanidatamab and supported by multiple R&D programs and collaborations. Its business model centers on internal R&D plus partnering (e.g., licensing of zanidatamab to Jazz Pharmaceuticals), with no indication of activities in digital assets or blockchain. ([zymeworks.com](https://www.zymeworks.com/))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across the reviewed earnings call transcripts within the 2017–2024 sample and the company’s 2023 Form 10‑K, there are no references to cryptocurrency, digital assets, mining, exchanges, custody, or blockchain-related initiatives. The calls and filings focus on oncology pipeline progress, regulatory milestones, collaborations, and conventional corporate finance matters. Accordingly, no crypto-exposure mechanisms (holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, infrastr) are supported by evidence in the sample period. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-zymeworks-reports-progress-and-strategic-shifts-in-q2-2024-93CH-3553102))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of any substantive crypto links in transcripts, SEC filings, or credible news during January 2017–December 2024, the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism weights set to 0.0. There is likewise no evidence that Zymeworks trades as a crypto proxy or has a crypto‑sensitive investor base, so invbase = 0. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-zymeworks-reports-progress-and-strategic-shifts-in-q2-2024-93CH-3553102))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q2 2024 (Aug 1, 2024): Transcript centers on zanidatamab regulatory timelines, pipeline updates, and a share repurchase; no mentions of cryptocurrency, blockchain, or digital assets. — [Investing.com transcript] (https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-zymeworks-reports-progress-and-strategic-shifts-in-q2-2024-93CH-3553102) ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-zymeworks-reports-progress-and-strategic-shifts-in-q2-2024-93CH-3553102))  
- Q3 2023 (Nov 7, 2023): “Data presented by our partners… highlight zanidatamab’s continued efficacy and tolerability.” No crypto/digital-asset references in the call. — [Fintool transcript] (https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/ZYME/documents/transcripts/q3-2023) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/ZYME/documents/transcripts/q3-2023))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Mar 6, 2024: Annual Report on Form 10‑K (FY2023) — business description, risk factors, and MD&A contain no references to cryptocurrency, blockchain, or digital assets (keyword search “cryptoc*”, “blockchain”, “bitcoin”, “digital asset” returns no matches). — [SEC filing] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1937653/000193765324000019/zyme-20231231.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1937653/000193765324000019/zyme-20231231.htm))

**Financial news / other:**
- Oct 19, 2022: Jazz Pharmaceuticals and Zymeworks announce exclusive license agreement for zanidatamab, underscoring core oncology focus. — [Jazz Pharmaceuticals press release PDF] (https://investor.jazzpharma.com/node/19551/pdf) ([investor.jazzpharma.com](https://investor.jazzpharma.com/node/19551/pdf))  
- Company overview describing royalty-driven R&D model in oncology; no mention of crypto activities. — [Zymeworks website] (https://www.zymeworks.com/) ([zymeworks.com](https://www.zymeworks.com/))
