## NUVEI SUBORDINATE VOTING DEAD - DELIST.19/11/24

* Rank: 55 | Expo: 2.18 | id_str: CA67079A1021
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 2 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings call transcripts (Q1 2022, Q3 2022, Q2 2023), MD&A/press releases (2021–2023)

### Firm Overview
Nuvei Corporation is a Canada‑based global payments technology provider offering merchant acquiring, alternative payment methods, card issuing, and payout solutions across regions and verticals; it was listed on the TSX and Nasdaq until completing a go‑private transaction with Advent International in November 2024. Within 2017–2024, Nuvei developed dedicated solutions for the digital‑asset ecosystem (on‑ramps/off‑ramps, crypto card issuing and settlement connections) alongside its core non‑crypto payments services. ([nuvei.com](https://www.nuvei.com/posts/nuvei-announces-completion-of-going-private-transaction?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Nuvei’s exposure to crypto is through financial services it provides to crypto platforms and merchants: fiat‑to‑crypto on‑ramp/off‑ramp processing (via its 2021 acquisition of Simplex), branded Visa crypto‑friendly debit cards in the EEA/UK, and support for pay‑ins/payouts in dozens of cryptoassets. It also participated in Visa’s stablecoin settlement expansion as a processing partner, reflecting direct involvement in crypto payment flows. Management consistently referred to a “digital assets and cryptocurrencies” vertical on earnings calls and in disclosures, including breakouts of revenue attributable to that activity. There is no evidence of proprietary coin holdings as treasury, mining operations, or hardware/datacenter provisioning. ([nuvei.com](https://www.nuvei.com/posts/nuvei-to-acquire-simplex-a-payment-solution-provider-to-the-cryptocurrency-industry?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence shows Nuvei directly enables, processes and monetizes crypto transactions (exchanges, wallets, NFT platforms, crypto payout rails), which squarely fits crypto financial services. Earnings calls detail customer wins in crypto (e.g., Crypto.com, Pionex, Ledger integrations) and discuss revenue impacts from “digital assets,” while filings and press releases explicitly separate “revenue from digital assets and cryptocurrencies.” Accordingly, finserv = 1.0 and all other mechanisms = 0.0. The stock was covered as having crypto exposure, but there is no explicit evidence that Nuvei traded primarily as a crypto proxy or that management characterized a crypto‑centric shareholder base; therefore invbase = 0. ([earningscall.biz](https://earningscall.biz/e/nasdaq/s/nvei/y/2022/q/q1))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified on mechanism classification. Crypto revenue contribution fluctuated materially with market conditions (e.g., declines in 2022), but remained a distinct, disclosed vertical; no balance‑sheet crypto holdings were identified. Note: Nuvei was delisted in November 2024 following completion of its go‑private transaction, which does not affect classification within the 2017–2024 sample. ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2023/03/08/payments-technology-firm-nuvei-q4-crypto-revenue-drops-58-from-year-ago?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q1 2022 (May 2022): “In digital assets, we added several new customers, including cryptocurrency exchange, Crypto.com, and Pionex… We’ve also partnered with Ledger… enabling [users] to purchase cryptocurrencies through our vast supported payment rails.” — [EarningsCall.biz] (https://earningscall.biz/e/nasdaq/s/nvei/y/2022/q/q1) ([earningscall.biz](https://earningscall.biz/e/nasdaq/s/nvei/y/2022/q/q1))  
- Q3 2022 (Nov 2022): “Digital assets continued to decline in the quarter… same‑store sales in digital assets were down almost 70% year‑over‑year.” — [AlphaSpread transcript] (https://www.alphaspread.com/security/tsx/nvei/investor-relations/earnings-call/q3-2022) ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/tsx/nvei/investor-relations/earnings-call/q3-2022))  
- Q2 2023 (Aug 2023): “EMEA… was negatively impacted… by volatility in digital assets… excluding digital assets… growth would have been 17% in the quarter.” — [AlphaSpread transcript] (https://www.alphaspread.com/security/tsx/nvei/investor-relations/earnings-call/q2-2023) ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/tsx/nvei/investor-relations/earnings-call/q2-2023?utm_source=openai))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Mar 8, 2023: FY2022 results release notes FX headwinds and “volatility from digital assets and cryptocurrencies.” — [Nuvei press release] (https://www.nuvei.com/posts/nuvei-announces-fourth-quarter-and-fiscal-year-2022-results) ([nuvei.com](https://www.nuvei.com/posts/nuvei-announces-fourth-quarter-and-fiscal-year-2022-results?utm_source=openai))  
- Dec 13, 2021: “Nuvei and Visa join forces to launch crypto‑friendly debit cards” via Simplex subsidiary. — [GlobeNewswire] (https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2021/12/13/2350936/0/en/Nuvei-and-Visa-Join-Forces-to-Launch-Crypto-Friendly-Debit-Cards.html) ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2021/12/13/2350936/0/en/Nuvei-and-Visa-Join-Forces-to-Launch-Crypto-Friendly-Debit-Cards.html?utm_source=openai))  
- Mar 29, 2021: “Adds pay‑in and payout support for nearly 40 cryptocurrencies.” — [GlobeNewswire] (https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2021/03/29/2201145/0/en/Nuvei-Adds-Payment-Solution-for-Nearly-40-Cryptocurrencies.html) ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2021/03/29/2201145/0/en/Nuvei-Adds-Payment-Solution-for-Nearly-40-Cryptocurrencies.html?utm_source=openai))  
- Apr 24, 2024 filing pack (FY2023): Results presentation includes line items “Revenue from digital assets and cryptocurrencies.” — [PR Newswire release] (https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/nuvei-announces-fourth-quarter-and-fiscal-2023-results-302080637.html) ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/nuvei-announces-fourth-quarter-and-fiscal-2023-results-302080637.html?utm_source=openai))  
- 2023 Management circular referencing “volatility from digital assets and cryptocurrencies” in FY2022 performance. — [SEC EDGAR EX‑99.3] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1765159/000119312523109257/d459645dex993.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1765159/000119312523109257/d459645dex993.htm))

**Financial news / other:**
- Sept 5, 2023: Visa expands stablecoin settlement with new partners including Nuvei. — [Payments Dive] (https://www.paymentsdive.com/news/visa-stablecoin-digital-payments-cryptocurrency-circle-ethereum-solana-worldpay-nuvei/692701/) ([paymentsdive.com](https://www.paymentsdive.com/news/visa-stablecoin-digital-payments-cryptocurrency-circle-ethereum-solana-worldpay-nuvei/692701/?utm_source=openai))  
- Mar 8, 2023: “Nuvei Q4 crypto‑related revenue $19.2M, down 58% y/y.” — [CoinDesk] (https://www.coindesk.com/business/2023/03/08/payments-technology-firm-nuvei-q4-crypto-revenue-drops-58-from-year-ago) ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2023/03/08/payments-technology-firm-nuvei-q4-crypto-revenue-drops-58-from-year-ago?utm_source=openai))  
- May 6, 2021: “Nuvei to acquire Simplex, a payment solution provider to the cryptocurrency industry.” — [Nuvei press release] (https://www.nuvei.com/posts/nuvei-to-acquire-simplex-a-payment-solution-provider-to-the-cryptocurrency-industry) ([nuvei.com](https://www.nuvei.com/posts/nuvei-to-acquire-simplex-a-payment-solution-provider-to-the-cryptocurrency-industry?utm_source=openai))

## RYVYL

* Rank: 56 | Expo: 2.17 | id_str: US39366L3078
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2023), Q4 2022 earnings call, press releases (2021–2023)

### Firm Overview
RYVYL Inc. is a U.S.-based financial technology company listed on Nasdaq that develops and sells blockchain-based payment solutions, including a closed‑loop, tokenized payments platform branded as coyni. The company describes its systems as proprietary, blockchain‑based ledgers that facilitate, record and store tokenized assets for payment processing. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1419275/000118518524000318/ryvyl20231231_10k.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management presents RYVYL as a payments provider that directly leverages blockchain and USD‑pegged stablecoin technology in its products, notably through the coyni platform and related banking‑as‑a‑service offerings. Earnings‑call remarks describe packaging services “from card issuing, IBAN and bank account issuing to FX and cryptocurrency” and offering on/off‑ramp capabilities using the company’s technology. Company filings further state that “the coyni platform features a digital token supported by our blockchain technology” within a closed‑loop ecosystem. There is no evidence of crypto mining or treasury HODL activity; any tokenization is product‑related rather than balance‑sheet investment. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/RVYL/documents/transcripts/q4-2022))

### Weight Assignment
The firm’s exposure to crypto arises from operating and monetizing crypto‑adjacent financial services: blockchain‑based acquiring, stablecoin/token rails (coyni), and on/off‑ramp functionality. Transcripts and filings consistently characterize coyni and the blockchain ledger as core to RYVYL’s payments offering, which squarely fits the “finserv” mechanism. No sources indicate mining, hardware provision, or discretionary crypto treasury holdings; therefore, finserv = 1.0 and all other mechanisms = 0.0. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/RVYL/documents/transcripts/q4-2022))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
RYVYL discussed a coyni spin‑off in 2023 but later disclosed it would retain coyni and not pursue a spin‑off; this does not alter the mechanism classification but underscores product strategy shifts. The company does not break out a precise revenue share attributable specifically to stablecoin/crypto versus traditional acquiring, so RevShare is coded conservatively. No evidence was found of material crypto assets held on the balance sheet outside of operational uses backing products. ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/de/news-release/2023/04/12/2645296/0/en/RYVYL-Initiates-coyni-Spin-Off-and-Value-Creation-Strategy.html?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q4 2022 (Apr 17, 2023): “The expansion and growth of our advanced blockchain ledger‑based payment solutions…”; and “package… services from card issuing… to FX and cryptocurrency to offer different on and off‑ramp capabilities utilizing our technology.” — [Fintool transcript](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/RVYL/documents/transcripts/q4-2022) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/RVYL/documents/transcripts/q4-2022))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Mar 26, 2024 10‑K: “RYVYL Inc. is a financial technology company that develops… blockchain‑based payment solutions…,” and “The coyni platform features a digital token supported by our blockchain technology.” — [SEC EDGAR 10‑K](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1419275/000118518524000318/ryvyl20231231_10k.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1419275/000118518524000318/ryvyl20231231_10k.htm))  
- Mar 26, 2024 10‑K: Company “no longer plans to pursue a spin‑off of Coyni PubCo.” — [SEC EDGAR 10‑K](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1419275/000118518524000318/ryvyl20231231_10k.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1419275/000118518524000318/ryvyl20231231_10k.htm))

**Financial news / other:**
- Jun 22, 2021: “Pilot launch of its COYNI… a stablecoin token, to registered early adopters.” — [GlobeNewswire](https://www.globenewswire.com/de/news-release/2021/06/22/2250995/0/en/GreenBox-POS-Announces-Launch-of-its-Stablecoin-Token-COYNI-to-Early-Adopters.html) ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/de/news-release/2021/06/22/2250995/0/en/GreenBox-POS-Announces-Launch-of-its-Stablecoin-Token-COYNI-to-Early-Adopters.html?utm_source=openai))  
- Sept 29, 2022: “GreenBox POS launches the coyni platform… stablecoin backed one‑to‑one by the U.S. dollar with near real‑time attestation.” — [GlobeNewswire](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2022/09/29/2525513/0/en/GreenBox-POS-launches-coyni-platform-featuring-one-of-the-industry-s-few-stablecoins-backed-one-to-one-by-the-U-S-dollar-with-near-real-time-attestation.html) ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2022/09/29/2525513/0/en/GreenBox-POS-launches-coyni-platform-featuring-one-of-the-industry-s-few-stablecoins-backed-one-to-one-by-the-U-S-dollar-with-near-real-time-attestation.html?utm_source=openai))  
- Apr 17, 2023: FY2022 results release highlights blockchain‑based solutions and coyni v2 go‑live. — [Nasdaq/GlobeNewswire](https://www.nasdaq.com/press-release/ryvyl-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2022-financial-results-2023-04-17) ([nasdaq.com](https://www.nasdaq.com/press-release/ryvyl-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2022-financial-results-2023-04-17?utm_source=openai))  
- Apr 12, 2023: Company “initiates coyni spin‑off and value creation strategy.” — [GlobeNewswire](https://www.globenewswire.com/de/news-release/2023/04/12/2645296/0/en/RYVYL-Initiates-coyni-Spin-Off-and-Value-Creation-Strategy.html) ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/de/news-release/2023/04/12/2645296/0/en/RYVYL-Initiates-coyni-Spin-Off-and-Value-Creation-Strategy.html?utm_source=openai))

## SPRINGWORKS THERAPEUTICS DEAD - DELIST.02/07/25

* Rank: 57 | Expo: 2.13 | id_str: US85205L1070
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filing (2023), FDA approval press release (2023), quarterly results press releases (2024)

### Firm Overview
SpringWorks Therapeutics, Inc. is a U.S.-based commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on precision medicines for severe rare diseases and oncology, notably OGSIVEO (nirogacestat) for desmoid tumors and the MEK inhibitor mirdametinib for NF1‑PN. Its 2023 Form 10‑K and corporate communications describe a pipeline and revenue model centered on drug development and commercialization, with no references to digital assets or blockchain activities. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/s/NASDAQ_SWTX_2023.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across filings and investor materials from 2017–2024, the company’s activities are exclusively biopharmaceutical: R&D, clinical development, regulatory approvals, and product sales. There is no evidence that it holds crypto on its balance sheet, operates mining, provides crypto financial services, or supplies crypto‑specific infrastructure. Public disclosures during 2023–2024 focus on FDA approval and commercialization of OGSIVEO and regulatory milestones for mirdametinib, with financial updates tied to product revenue and typical biotech expenses. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/s/NASDAQ_SWTX_2023.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of any substantive crypto-related operations or treasury activity in filings, earnings materials, or reputable news coverage over the 2017–2024 sample, the firm is classified as unclassified for crypto exposure. Accordingly, unclass = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/s/NASDAQ_SWTX_2023.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified. Company disclosures and coverage reviewed consistently describe a conventional biopharma business with no crypto touchpoints during the 2017–2024 window. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/s/NASDAQ_SWTX_2023.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings/IR and results:**
- Nov 12, 2024: Q3 2024 results press release highlighting OGSIVEO product revenue and business updates; conference call held the same day — [SpringWorks IR](https://news.springworkstx.com/news-releases/news-release-details/springworks-therapeutics-reports-third-quarter-2024-financial) ([news.springworkstx.com](https://news.springworkstx.com/news-releases/news-release-details/springworks-therapeutics-reports-third-quarter-2024-financial?utm_source=openai))

**Filings (10‑K / corporate):**

## APPLIED DIGITAL

* Rank: 58 | Expo: 1.99 | id_str: US0381692070
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 1.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 4 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 2023 Form 10-K; Q4 2023 and Q2 2024 earnings calls; 2022–2024 IR and press releases

### Firm Overview
Applied Digital Corporation is a U.S. designer, builder and operator of next‑generation data centers whose business during 2023–2024 centered on hosting infrastructure for crypto mining customers alongside emerging HPC/AI services. The company ceased its own crypto mining in March 2022 and focuses on multi‑year, fixed‑price hosting contracts and HPC build‑outs. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1144879/000114487923000176/apld-20230531.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The dominant channel is infrastructure: Applied Digital provides power, space and operations for third‑party crypto miners through large‑scale hosting sites in North Dakota and Texas, contracted on multi‑year terms. Management repeatedly details contracted megawatt capacity and states that demand for hosting rises when Bitcoin prices increase, while pricing for its Garden City site was fixed, insulating it from crypto price volatility. There is no evidence of treasury crypto holdings or of operating a crypto exchange/custody service, and the firm has not self‑mined since March 9, 2022. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/APLD/documents/transcripts/q4-2023))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence from filings and multiple earnings calls shows Applied Digital’s crypto touchpoint is providing purpose‑built datacenter and colocation capacity for mining clients; therefore, infrastr = 1.0. The company explicitly exited self‑mining in 2022 (miners = 0.0), does not describe holding crypto as treasury assets (holders = 0.0), and does not run financial‑service offerings such as exchanges or ETPs (finserv = 0.0). General business services do not apply, and unclass = 0.0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1144879/000114487923000176/apld-20230531.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
In fiscal 2024 the firm also generated revenue from AI/HPC cloud services and began constructing HPC data centers, which are non‑crypto activities; classification here isolates the crypto‑related hosting exposure. In April 2024 it sold its Garden City hosting facility to Marathon, but the mechanism remains infrastructure hosting across its portfolio. No explicit evidence was found that investor flows into APLD are a dedicated “crypto proxy,” so invbase is set to 0. ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2024/08/28/2937374/0/en/Applied-Digital-Reports-Fiscal-Fourth-Quarter-and-Full-Year-2024-Results.html))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jan 16, 2024 (Q2 FY2024), transcript: “With the increase in the cost of Bitcoin, we are seeing demand increase significantly for hosting services… [Garden City] is fully contracted with fixed prices, so we are not exposed to volatility…” — [Fintool](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/APLD/documents/transcripts/q2-2024) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/APLD/documents/transcripts/q2-2024))  
- Jul 24, 2023 (Q4 FY2023), transcript: “This brings Applied Digital to 280 megawatts of hosting capacity… all of which are contracted out to customers on multi‑year terms.” — [Fintool](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/APLD/documents/transcripts/q4-2023) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/APLD/documents/transcripts/q4-2023))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Aug 2023, Form 10‑K: “On March 9, 2022, we ceased all crypto mining operations and completed the sale of all crypto mining equipment….” — [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1144879/000114487923000176/apld-20230531.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1144879/000114487923000176/apld-20230531.htm))  
- Aug 2023, Form 10‑K: “Our crypto datacenter hosting business provides infrastructure and colocation services to crypto mining customers.” — [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1144879/000114487923000176/apld-20230531.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1144879/000114487923000176/apld-20230531.htm))  
- Nov 17, 2022, corporate rebrand from Applied Blockchain to Applied Digital — [Applied Digital IR](https://ir.applieddigital.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/44/applied-blockchain-announces-official-name-change-to) ([ir.applieddigital.com](https://ir.applieddigital.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/44/applied-blockchain-announces-official-name-change-to?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Jul 18, 2022: Marathon Digital announces ~200 MW hosting with Applied (90 MW Texas; ≥110 MW North Dakota) — [GlobeNewswire](https://www.globenewswire.com/f4/news-release/2022/07/18/2481355/0/en/Marathon-Digital-Holdings-Secures-Hosting-Capacity-To-Support-All-23-3-EH-s-of-Bitcoin-Mining.html) ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/f4/news-release/2022/07/18/2481355/0/en/Marathon-Digital-Holdings-Secures-Hosting-Capacity-To-Support-All-23-3-EH-s-of-Bitcoin-Mining.html?utm_source=openai))  
- Aug 28, 2024: FY2024 results; total revenue $165.6m including $29.0m from Cloud Services, ongoing Data Center Hosting operations — [GlobeNewswire](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2024/08/28/2937374/0/en/Applied-Digital-Reports-Fiscal-Fourth-Quarter-and-Full-Year-2024-Results.html) ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2024/08/28/2937374/0/en/Applied-Digital-Reports-Fiscal-Fourth-Quarter-and-Full-Year-2024-Results.html))

## ASX

* Rank: 59 | Expo: 1.78 | id_str: AU000000ASX7
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.8, bizserv 0.2, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: FY2019 and FY2022 results speaking notes; 2018 Annual Report; Nov 2022 and Nov 2023 CHESS replacement releases; June 2024 ASX media release

### Firm Overview
ASX Limited operates Australia’s primary securities exchange and associated clearing and settlement infrastructure. During 2017–2024, the company pursued a distributed‑ledger replacement for its CHESS post‑trade system and launched a DLT‑as‑a‑service platform, and in June 2024 it admitted the first spot bitcoin ETF to trade on the ASX market. ([www2.asx.com.au](https://www2.asx.com.au/content/dam/asx/about/media-releases/2019/ASX2019FullYearResultsPresentationSpeakingNotes.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The dominant channel is financial services: ASX provides listing and trading of crypto‑linked investment products by admitting spot bitcoin ETFs (e.g., VanEck Bitcoin ETF, ticker VBTC) to its exchange in June 2024, which ties ASX trading and listing revenues to crypto investor interest and activity even though ASX does not custody crypto itself. ([asx.com.au](https://www.asx.com.au/content/dam/asx/about/media-releases/2024/20-jun-asx-admits-first-bitcoin-etf.pdf))  
A secondary, smaller channel is non‑financial business services via enterprise blockchain: management discussed and launched Synfini, a “DLT as a service” platform, and for several years pursued a DLT‑based CHESS replacement before pausing in November 2022 and later moving to a non‑DLT product solution in 2023. These DLT efforts relate to blockchain infrastructure and services rather than holding or trading crypto assets. ([www2.asx.com.au](https://www2.asx.com.au/content/dam/asx/about/financial-results/2022/full-year/37-18-august-2022-full-year-results-presentation-and-speaking-notes.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 0.8 reflects that the clearest crypto‑linked mechanism affecting ASX is exchange admission and trading of spot bitcoin ETFs beginning June 20, 2024, which can influence volumes, fees and issuer activity when crypto prices and interest rise. Bizserv = 0.2 captures the enterprise‑DLT exposure from Synfini and the long‑running CHESS DLT work discussed in results materials; these are blockchain services and internal technology programs that do not involve ASX holding or directly transacting in crypto assets. No evidence supports holders or miners, and infrastructure hardware/hosting is not ASX’s role, so those weights are 0.0. ([asx.com.au](https://www.asx.com.au/content/dam/asx/about/media-releases/2024/20-jun-asx-admits-first-bitcoin-etf.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
There is a definitional nuance between “providing trading of crypto investment products” (ETFs on ASX) and “directly handling digital assets”; here, finserv is applied since ASX lists and operates the trading venue for spot bitcoin ETFs, despite not issuing or custoding them. The CHESS DLT initiative was paused in November 2022 and replaced in November 2023 by a TCS product solution, reducing forward‑looking blockchain linkage; Synfini remains enterprise DLT and not crypto‑asset specific. Earnings‑call style evidence is sourced from ASX’s official results “speaking notes” PDFs rather than third‑party transcript sites. ([asx.com.au](https://www.asx.com.au/content/dam/asx/about/media-releases/2022/60-17-november-2022-CHESS-Replacement-ASX-reassessing-financial-derecognition_.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 18, 2022 (FY2022 results speaking notes): “In November 2021, we launched our ‘DLT as a service’ platform, Synfini.” — [ASX](https://
<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 18, 2022 (FY2022 results speaking notes): “In November 2021, we launched our ‘DLT as a service’ platform, Synfini… the first of those DLT applications [has] go[ne] live on the platform.” — [ASX](https://www2.asx.com.au/content/dam/asx/about/financial-results/2022/full-year/37-18-august-2022-full-year-results-presentation-and-speaking-notes.pdf)  
- Aug 15, 2019 (FY2019 results speaking notes): “The DLT infrastructure we are building ensures data consistency through sharing the same data with permissioned parties… using smart contracts.” — [ASX](https://www2.asx.com.au/content/dam/asx/about/media-releases/2019/ASX2019FullYearResultsPresentationSpeakingNotes.pdf)  
- Feb 16, 2024 (1H FY2024 results speaking notes): “In November last year we announced the selection of the TCS BaNCS for Market Infrastructure product as the solution for the CHESS replacement project… Release 1 [clearing] indicative timeframe of 2026.” — [ASX](https://www.asx.com.au/content/dam/asx/about/financial-results/2024/half-year/4-asx-half-year-results-presentation-and-speaking-notes-february-2024.pdf)

**Filings (Annual reports / market announcements):**
- Nov 17, 2022, market announcement: ASX pauses CHESS DLT solution and derecognises $245–255m pre‑tax capitalised software (1H23). — [ASX](https://www.asx.com.au/content/dam/asx/about/media-releases/2022/60-17-november-2022-CHESS-Replacement-ASX-reassessing-financial-derecognition_.pdf)  
- Nov 20, 2023, media release: ASX selects TCS BaNCS product solution; clearing targeted first, settlement/sub‑register to follow. — [ASX](https://www.asx.com.au/content/dam/asx/about/media-releases/2023/70-20-november-2023-chess-replacement-solution-announced-and-2024-consultation.pdf)  
- Aug 17, 2023, FY23 results market release: statutory NPAT impacted by significant items including CHESS replacement derecognition charge. — [ASX](https://announcements.asx.com.au/asxpdf/20230817/pdf/05sqj6bj52g4v0.pdf)  
- FY2023 Annual Report: notes derecognition of CHESS replacement project costs and related governance/remediation actions. — [ASX](https://www.asx.com.au/content/dam/asx/annual-reports/01-asx-2023-annual-report.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Jun 20, 2024: ASX admits first spot Bitcoin ETF; VanEck Bitcoin ETF (ASX: VBTC). — [ASX media release](https://www.asx.com.au/content/dam/asx/about/media-releases/2024/20-jun-asx-admits-first-bitcoin-etf.pdf)  
- Jun 20, 2024: “Bitcoin ETF lists on main Australian stock market for first time” (VBTC on ASX). — [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2024-06-20/bitcoin-btc-etf-lists-on-main-australian-stock-market-for-first-time)  
- Jun 20, 2024: VanEck’s spot Bitcoin ETF goes live on ASX; context vs Cboe Australia. — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2024/06/20/vanecks-spot-bitcoin-etf-goes-live-on-australias-biggest-stock-exchange)  
- Jul 12, 2024: DigitalX Bitcoin ETF (ASX: BTXX) admitted and commences trading; second spot Bitcoin ETF on ASX. — [ASX notice (ASX Online)](https://asxonline.com/public/notices/2024/july/0779.24.07.html), [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2024-07-08/digitalx-to-list-bitcoin-etf-on-australia-s-main-stock-market)

</small>

## VIRTU FINANCIAL CL.A

* Rank: 60 | Expo: 1.75 | id_str: US9282541013
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K/annual reports (2021–2024), Q1–Q2 2021 earnings calls, financial news (2021–2022)

### Firm Overview
Virtu Financial is a U.S.-based electronic market maker and execution services firm active across multiple asset classes globally, including equities, ETFs, FX, futures, fixed income and commodities. Company materials and filings during 2021–2024 indicate that cryptocurrencies became one of the asset classes served as Virtu expanded into digital-asset market making and related ETF/futures activity. ([s21.q4cdn.com](https://s21.q4cdn.com/422114427/files/doc_financials/2021/ar/041922Virtu_AnnualReport_2021.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company explicitly discusses “cryptocurrency market making” in earnings remarks, describing activity in bitcoin and ether across spot venues, ETFs and futures, and plans to stream crypto liquidity to institutional clients via its VFX platform. These are direct financial services (trading/liquidity, ETF/AP support) rather than custody or treasury holding. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/08/04/virtu-financial-inc-virt-q2-2021-earnings-call-tra/))  
Filings and investor materials list “cryptocurrencies” among the multi‑asset classes served, and an SEC comment letter describes Virtu’s crypto market making across spot, perpetuals, futures and ETPs on dozens of venues. News coverage further shows Virtu’s role tied to crypto ETPs, including selection as a market maker for a Canadian bitcoin ETF and being named as an authorized participant partner for a proposed U.S. spot bitcoin ETF. ([s21.q4cdn.com](https://s21.q4cdn.com/422114427/files/doc_financials/2021/ar/041922Virtu_AnnualReport_2021.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 reflects that Virtu’s crypto linkage is through providing financial services: proprietary market making in crypto spot/derivatives, streaming liquidity to institutions, and ETF/AP activities. There is no evidence of treasury crypto holdings (holders) or coin mining (miners), and the firm does not supply hardware/hosting (infrastr) nor only peripheral services (bizserv), so those remain 0.0. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/08/04/virtu-financial-inc-virt-q2-2021-earnings-call-tra/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
No evidence indicates material balance-sheet crypto assets; activities appear trading/flow‑driven and therefore revenue contribution is likely minor relative to total. Some sources (e.g., ETF/AP roles) are contingent on product approvals or issuer arrangements during the period, but the core crypto market‑making statements come directly from management and filings. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/08/04/virtu-financial-inc-virt-q2-2021-earnings-call-tra/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 4, 2021 (Q2 2021): “Our expansion into cryptocurrency market making… focus has been on market making in Bitcoin and Ether… including spot… as well as ETF and futures… [and] developing our ability to stream cryptocurrencies over our… VFX platform.” — [Motley Fool transcript] ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/08/04/virtu-financial-inc-virt-q2-2021-earnings-call-tra/))  
- May 4, 2021 (Q1 2021): “We’re very supportive of crypto as a new asset class… an entirely new addressable market… people are going to want to access it as an ETF, as a spot position and as a future.” — [Motley Fool transcript] ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/05/04/virtu-financial-inc-virt-q1-2021-earnings-call-tra/))

**Filings (10-K / press releases / investor materials):**
- 2021 Annual Report: lists “cryptocurrencies” among asset classes clients can trade via Virtu’s platform. — [Virtu 2021 Annual Report PDF] ([s21.q4cdn.com](https://s21.q4cdn.com/422114427/files/doc_financials/2021/ar/041922Virtu_AnnualReport_2021.pdf))  
- 2024 Form 10-K (Business overview for year ended Dec 31, 2024): product offerings across asset classes include “cryptocurrencies”; market‑making segment includes cryptocurrencies. — [SEC 10‑K] ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1592386/000159238625000055/virt-20241231.htm))  
- Apr 4, 2022: Virtu comment letter to SEC on GBTC conversion notes “cryptocurrency market making includes spot, perpetuals, futures, and ETPs across more than fifty…” — [Virtu submission] ([virtu-www.s3.amazonaws.com](https://virtu-www.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/documents/Virtu-Financial-Regulation-GBTC-Comment-Letter-20220404.pdf?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Feb 23, 2021: Virtu chosen as a market maker for Evolve’s Canadian bitcoin ETF. — [iHodl] ([ihodl.com](https://ihodl.com/topnews/2021-02-23/virtu-financial-chosen-market-maker-bitcoin-etf/?utm_source=openai))  
- Jun 9, 2022: CEO says crypto presents market‑making opportunity; discussion of potential crypto marketplace efforts. — [CoinDesk] ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2022/06/09/virtu-sees-crypto-market-making-opportunity-has-little-confidence-in-incumbents-ceo-says?utm_source=openai))  
- 2022: Grayscale names Virtu (with Jane Street) as AP partners for its proposed U.S. spot bitcoin ETF. — [Markets Media] ([marketsmedia.com](https://www.marketsmedia.com/grayscale-appoints-virtu-and-jane-street-ahead-of-secs-etf-decision/?utm_source=openai))

## CLPS

* Rank: 61 | Expo: 1.72 | id_str: KYG316421042
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 20-F/annual reports (2021–2023), FY2021–FY2023 earnings call transcripts, 2021 JV press release, company case studies

### Firm Overview
CLPS Incorporation is a Hong Kong–headquartered IT consulting and solutions provider focused on financial institutions across banking, wealth management, e‑commerce and related verticals, and is listed on Nasdaq under ticker CLPS. Company filings and IR materials describe offerings spanning core banking, credit card and loan systems, with R&D in areas including blockchain and digital currency applications. ([ir.clpsglobal.com](https://ir.clpsglobal.com/?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management established a blockchain-focused joint venture in 2021 to develop “digital asset solutions for financial institutions,” including custody, exchange, payments and NFT distribution platforms. Subsequent earnings calls highlighted a dedicated blockchain subsidiary (LinkCrypto) that was “committed to [an] NFT platform” and had “initially generated revenue” in fiscal H1 2022. In FY2022 H2 and FY2023 H1 calls, CLPS described integrating blockchain into loan systems and delivering “scenario‑based digital currency application solutions,” including a completed digital renminbi project for a Chinese bank. Company case studies further reference a digital RMB mall implementation and a blockchain points system for a rural commercial bank. Collectively, these indicate exposure via building technology and solutions for clients, not by directly operating exchanges, custody, or holding crypto on the balance sheet. ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/clps-incorporation-and-columbus-announce-joint-venture-to-develop-and-upgrade-blockchain-based-digital-asset-solutions-301307576.html))

### Weight Assignment
Bizserv = 1.0 reflects that CLPS’s crypto linkage during 2017–2024 is through non‑financial services to the ecosystem: consulting, software development and delivery of blockchain/digital‑currency solutions for financial institutions (e.g., NFT platform support, CBDC/digital RMB projects, blockchain‑enabled loan systems). There is no evidence the firm itself ran a crypto exchange or custody service (finserv), mined coins (miners), held crypto as treasury (holders), or supplied mining hardware/hosting (infrastr) in this period. ([ir.clpsglobal.com](https://ir.clpsglobal.com/resources/presentation-events/document/CLPS%20Incorporation%20-%20Second%20Half%20and%20Full%20Year%20of%20Fiscal%202022%20Earnings%20Call%20-%20SCRIPT.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Disclosures indicate only early, small‑scale revenues from blockchain initiatives (e.g., LinkCrypto’s “initially generated revenue” in H1 FY2022), with no segment share quantified; accordingly RevShare is coded as minor. Public filings list blockchain‑related subsidiaries/JVs but do not show material crypto assets on the balance sheet. Evidence of any direct, regulated financial‑services role in digital assets was not found through December 2024. ([ir.clpsglobal.com](https://ir.clpsglobal.com/resources/presentation-events/document/CLPS%20Incorporation%20-%20First%20Half%20of%20Fiscal%20Year%202022%20Earnings%20Call%20-%20SCRIPT.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 15, 2021 (FY2021 H2/FY): “set up CLPS Beefinance… a blockchain technology solutions provider… develop and upgrade blockchain‑based digital assets solutions for financial institutions.” — [CLPS IR transcript PDF] (https://ir.clpsglobal.com/resources/presentation-events/document/CLPS%20Incorporation%20-%20Second%20Half%20and%20Full%20Year%20of%20Fiscal%202021%20Earnings%20Call%20-%20SCRIPT.pdf) ([ir.clpsglobal.com](https://ir.clpsglobal.com/resources/presentation-events/document/CLPS%20Incorporation%20-%20Second%20Half%20and%20Full%20Year%20of%20Fiscal%202021%20Earnings%20Call%20-%20SCRIPT.pdf))  
- Mar 4, 2022 (FY2022 H1): “our blockchain solution company LinkCrypto… committed to [an] NFT platform… LinkCrypto has initially generated revenue during the first half of Fiscal Year 2022.” — [CLPS IR transcript PDF] (https://ir.clpsglobal.com/resources/presentation-events/document/CLPS%20Incorporation%20-%20First%20Half%20of%20Fiscal%20Year%202022%20Earnings%20Call%20-%20SCRIPT.pdf) ([ir.clpsglobal.com](https://ir.clpsglobal.com/resources/presentation-events/document/CLPS%20Incorporation%20-%20First%20Half%20of%20Fiscal%20Year%202022%20Earnings%20Call%20-%20SCRIPT.pdf))  
- Oct 20, 2022 (FY2022 H2/FY): “loan management system… powered by… blockchain… [and] we have successfully completed the digital renminbi project for a well‑known bank in China.” — [CLPS IR transcript PDF] (https://ir.clpsglobal.com/resources/presentation-events/document/CLPS%20Incorporation%20-%20Second%20Half%20and%20Full%20Year%20of%20Fiscal%202022%20Earnings%20Call%20-%20SCRIPT.pdf) ([ir.clpsglobal.com](https://ir.clpsglobal.com/resources/presentation-events/document/CLPS%20Incorporation%20-%20Second%20Half%20and%20Full%20Year%20of%20Fiscal%202022%20Earnings%20Call%20-%20SCRIPT.pdf))

**Filings (20‑F / annual reports):**

## BLOCK A

* Rank: 62 | Expo: 1.67 | id_str: US8522341036
* Weight summary: holders 0.2, miners 0.0, finserv 0.7, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.1, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 3 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2022–2023), Q1 2021 and Q3 2023 earnings calls, shareholder letters, 2024 IR press release on mining chip

### Firm Overview
Block, Inc. is a U.S.-listed financial technology company whose Square ecosystem serves merchants and whose Cash App serves consumers. Within Cash App, the company enables customers to buy, sell, send, and withdraw bitcoin; bitcoin sales are recognized as revenue, and historically comprised a large share of total net revenue. ([s29.q4cdn.com](https://s29.q4cdn.com/628966176/files/doc_financials/2023/ar/2023-Annual-Report-Proxy_10K.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
- Crypto financial services (finserv): Cash App offers bitcoin trading and transfers, generating bitcoin revenue and related gross profit; management frequently discusses bitcoin as part of the Cash App product set on earnings calls. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/05/07/square-sq-q1-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))  
- Treasury holdings (holders): Block purchased bitcoin for corporate investment beginning in 2020 and disclosed cumulative purchases of $220 million with associated impairment accounting during 2021–2022. ([s21.q4cdn.com](https://s21.q4cdn.com/114365585/files/doc_financials/2020/Q2/Q3/3Q20-Shareholder-Letter-SQ.pdf))  
- Infrastructure (infrastr): In July 2024 Block announced a new bitcoin mining ASIC and a project with Core Scientific to deploy its chips, positioning Block as an emerging supplier to miners. ([investors.block.xyz](https://investors.block.xyz/investor-news/news-details/2024/Blocks-New-Bitcoin-Mining-Chip-to-Be-Part-of-an-Ongoing-Project-With-Core-Scientific-to-Decentralize-Mining-Hardware/default.aspx?utm_source=openai))  
- Investor base amplifier: Financial media notes the stock “often trades like a proxy for Bitcoin,” reflecting crypto‑sensitive investor interest. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/investing/2022/09/14/reasons-buy-block-stock-not-bitcoin/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv receives the largest weight (0.7) because Cash App’s bitcoin brokerage and transfer features are ongoing, repeatedly emphasized by management, and contribute materially to reported revenue. Holders (0.2) reflects a deliberate treasury investment policy begun in 2020 and carried through 2022 within the sample period. Infrastr (0.1) captures Block’s move into supplying bitcoin‑mining chips/platforms announced in 2024; the modest weight reflects limited commercialization evidence through 2024. No evidence indicates Block operated mining at scale during 2017–2024, so miners = 0.0. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/05/07/square-sq-q1-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Reported “bitcoin revenue” is gross sales to customers; Block’s 2023 10‑K clarifies bitcoin revenue comprised 41% and 57% of total revenue in 2022 and 2021, while contributing only a low-single‑digit share of gross profit, so RevShare is coded from revenue even though economics are thinner. ([s29.q4cdn.com](https://s29.q4cdn.com/628966176/files/doc_financials/2023/ar/2023-Annual-Report-Proxy_10K.pdf))  
- The ASIC/mining initiative was announced in 2024 with a partner agreement; revenue impact within 2017–2024 is not quantified, hence a small infrastr weight. ([investors.block.xyz](https://investors.block.xyz/investor-news/news-details/2024/Blocks-New-Bitcoin-Mining-Chip-to-Be-Part-of-an-Ongoing-Project-With-Core-Scientific-to-Decentralize-Mining-Hardware/default.aspx?utm_source=openai))  
- Treasury holdings sizing post‑2022 is not used here; classification anchors to clearly disclosed 2017–2024 figures. ([s29.q4cdn.com](https://s29.q4cdn.com/628966176/files/doc_financials/2023/ar/2023-Annual-Report-Proxy_10K.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 7, 2021 (Q1 2021): “we launched bitcoin peer‑to‑peer functionality… Customers can now send bitcoin to friends and family for free within the app.” — [Motley Fool transcript] (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/05/07/square-sq-q1-2021-earnings-call-transcript/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/05/07/square-sq-q1-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))  
- May 7, 2021 (Q1 2021): “We see bitcoin as the Internet’s potential to have a native currency… We started with buying and selling of bitcoin in Cash App.” — [Motley Fool transcript] (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/05/07/square-sq-q1-2021-earnings-call-transcript/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/05/07/square-sq-q1-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))  
- Nov 3, 2023 (Q3 2023): “customers… may participate on our… Bitcoin investing [in Cash App].” — [Motley Fool transcript] (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2023/11/03/block-sq-q3-2023-earnings-call-transcript/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2023/11/03/block-sq-q3-2023-earnings-call-transcript/))  
- Nov 3, 2023 (Q3 2023): “we have a focus on Bitcoin… We started… providing an exchange for people to buy and sell…” — [Motley Fool transcript] (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2023/11/03/block-sq-q3-2023-earnings-call-transcript/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2023/11/03/block-sq-q3-2023-earnings-call-transcript/))

**Filings (10‑K / shareholder materials):**
- 2023 Form 10‑K: “While bitcoin contributed 41% and 57% of the total revenue in 2022 and 2021… gross profit generated from bitcoin was only 3% and 5%…” — [Block 2023 10‑K PDF] (https://s29.q4cdn.com/628966176/files/doc_financials/2023/ar/2023-Annual-Report-Proxy_10K.pdf) ([s29.q4cdn.com](https://s29.q4cdn.com/628966176/files/doc_financials/2023/ar/2023-Annual-Report-Proxy_10K.pdf))  
- 2023 Form 10‑K: “As of December 31, 2022, we have purchased a cumulative $220.0 million in bitcoin for investment purposes.” — [Block 2023 10‑K PDF] (https://s29.q4cdn.com/628966176/files/doc_financials/2023/ar/2023-Annual-Report-Proxy_10K.pdf) ([s29.q4cdn.com](https://s29.q4cdn.com/628966176/files/doc_financials/2023/ar/2023-Annual-Report-Proxy_10K.pdf))  
- Q3 2020 Shareholder Letter: “In October 2020, we invested $50 million in bitcoin… We expect to hold this investment for the long term.” — [Shareholder letter] (https://s21.q4cdn.com/114365585/files/doc_financials/2020/Q2/Q3/3Q20-Shareholder-Letter-SQ.pdf) ([s21.q4cdn.com](https://s21.q4cdn.com/114365585/files/doc_financials/2020/Q2/Q3/3Q20-Shareholder-Letter-SQ.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**
- July 10, 2024: “Block’s New Bitcoin Mining Chip… Core Scientific becomes the first to procure Block’s new ASIC technology…” — [Block IR press release] (https://investors.block.xyz/investor-news/news-details/2024/
- July 10, 2024: “Block’s New Bitcoin Mining Chip to Be Part of an Ongoing Project With Core Scientific to Decentralize Mining Hardware.” — [Block IR press release] (https://investors.block.xyz/investor-news/news-details/2024/Blocks-New-Bitcoin-Mining-Chip-to-Be-Part-of-an-Ongoing-Project-With-Core-Scientific-to-Decentralize-Mining-Hardware/default.aspx)  
- May 4, 2023: Cash App bitcoin revenue $2.16B in Q1 2023; bitcoin gross profit $50M. — [CoinDesk] (https://www.coindesk.com/business/2023/05/04/blocks-q1-bitcoin-revenue-rises-18-from-q4-gains-25-from-a-year-ago)  
- May 5, 2022: “Ten million accounts have bought bitcoin on Cash App,” per Block’s shareholder letter. — [Axios] (https://www.axios.com/2022/05/05/ten-million-accounts-have-bought-bitcoin-on-cash-app)  
- Aug 3, 2023: Q2 2023 shareholder letter (exhibit 99.1): disclosure including Cash App metrics and note on bitcoin revenue definition. — [SEC exhibit 99.1] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1512673/000119312523202864/d469227dex991.htm)

</small>

## PLAYBOY

* Rank: 63 | Expo: 1.47 | id_str: US72814P1093
* Weight summary: holders 0.2, miners 0.0, finserv 0.8, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: 2022–2023 Form 10‑K filings; Q1 2021 earnings call; October 2021 Rabbitars press release; crypto/tech media coverage (CoinDesk, Blockworks)

### Firm Overview
PLBY Group Inc. is a U.S.-listed lifestyle and consumer brand company that owns Playboy and monetizes the brand through licensing, digital subscriptions/content, and other initiatives. During 2021–2023 it experimented with Web3 products, including NFT collections sold under the Playboy brand. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1803914/000180391424000022/ply-20231231.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Beginning in 2021, management launched Playboy-branded NFTs (e.g., the 11,953‑piece “Rabbitars” collection) and accepted ether (ETH) as payment, reflecting direct participation in crypto payments and digital-asset product issuance. In its 2022 Form 10‑K, the company disclosed it accounted for ETH as digital assets and recorded a $4.9 million impairment tied to those holdings, while noting ETH price volatility could materially affect results. PLBY also partnered with The Sandbox to develop a “MetaMansion” experience, tying its NFT community to metaverse activations. In 2023, filings indicated digital‑asset impairments were immaterial and the net carrying value of digital assets had fallen to de minimis levels. ([investors.playboy.com](https://investors.playboy.com/news-releases/news-release-details/playboy-launches-new-nft-project-rabbitars/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv (0.8) is primary because the company directly issued and sold NFT products and accepted cryptocurrency as a means of payment—activities squarely within crypto financial services. Holders (0.2) reflects on‑balance‑sheet exposure to ETH acquired via NFT sales and subsequently measured with impairment (not a long‑term treasury program, but a non‑trivial balance‑sheet link during 2021–2022). No evidence indicates mining or crypto‑specific infrastructure operations (miners/infrastr = 0.0), nor a distinct non‑financial service provided to crypto firms (bizserv = 0.0). ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1803914/000180391423000017/ply-20221231.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- ETH holdings arose from NFT proceeds rather than a stated treasury strategy; under the framework this is reflected with a smaller Holders weight.  
- NFT revenue was episodic (not recurring), and crypto activity intensity declined by 2023; weights anchor to clear disclosures within 2017–2024.  
- Only one earnings call with detailed Web3 commentary was retrievable; additional transcripts likely wouldn’t change the classification but would raise certainty. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1803914/000180391424000022/ply-20231231.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 12, 2021 (Q1 2021): “sold out in less than three minutes and generated almost $1 million in sales.” — [TickerTrends transcript] (https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/PLBY/Q1-earnings-transcript-2021) ([tickertrends.io](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/PLBY/Q1-earnings-transcript-2021))  
- May 12, 2021 (Q1 2021): “we are… leveraging… blockchain technology, immersive virtual worlds, digital goods…” — [TickerTrends transcript] (https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/PLBY/Q1-earnings-transcript-2021) ([tickertrends.io](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/PLBY/Q1-earnings-transcript-2021))  
- May 12, 2021 (Q1 2021): “partnered with Decentraland to create Playboy’s first metaverse experience.” — [TickerTrends transcript] (https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/PLBY/Q1-earnings-transcript-2021) ([tickertrends.io](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/PLBY/Q1-earnings-transcript-2021))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- 2022 Form 10‑K: “During the fourth quarter of 2021 we released ‘Rabbitars’… and accepted Ethereum as payment… [we] incurred an impairment loss of $4.9 million on our Ethereum.” — [SEC 10‑K] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1803914/000180391423000017/ply-20221231.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1803914/000180391423000017/ply-20221231.htm))  
- 2023 Form 10‑K: “Recognized losses related to the impairment of our digital assets during the year ended December 31, 2023 were immaterial… digital assets, net $5 [2023] vs $327 [2022].” — [SEC 10‑K] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1803914/000180391424000022/ply-20231231.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1803914/000180391424000022/ply-20231231.htm))  
- Oct 20, 2021: Rabbitars launch; 11,953 NFTs priced at 0.1953 ETH; crypto or USD checkout. — [Playboy press release] (https://investors.playboy.com/news-releases/news-release-details/playboy-launches-new-nft-project-rabbitars/) ([investors.playboy.com](https://investors.playboy.com/news-releases/news-release-details/playboy-launches-new-nft-project-rabbitars/?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Mar 16, 2023: “Playboy lost $4.9M on ether it accepted as NFT payments.” — [CoinDesk] (https://www.coindesk.com/business/2023/03/16/playboy-lost-49m-on-ethereum-it-accepted-as-nft-payments) ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2023/03/16/playboy-lost-49m-on-ethereum-it-accepted-as-nft-payments?utm_source=openai))  
- Mar 16, 2023: “Playboy held all ETH earned from NFTs — but it cost them $5M.” — [Blockworks] (https://blockworks.com/news/playboy-held-nft-eth) ([blockworks.com](https://blockworks.com/news/playboy-held-nft-eth?utm_source=openai))  
- Jul 2022: “Playboy and The Sandbox collaborate to create ‘MetaMansion’ digital gaming experience.” — [Animoca Brands] (https://www.animocabrands.com/announcement/playboy-and-the-sandbox-collaborate-to-create-metamansion-digital-gaming-experience) ([animocabrands.com](https://www.animocabrands.com/announcement/playboy-and-the-sandbox-collaborate-to-create-metamansion-digital-gaming-experience?utm_source=openai))

</small>

## ROCKETFUEL BLOCKCHAIN

* Rank: 64 | Expo: 1.42 | id_str: US77313J1088
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 4 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: SEC 10‑K filings (FY2021, FY2023); Q3 2022 earnings call; GlobeNewswire press releases (2022–2023); industry trade coverage (Digital Transactions)

### Firm Overview
RocketFuel Blockchain, Inc. is a U.S.-based payments company quoted on OTCQB (RKFL) that provides crypto-enabled checkout, payouts, and cross-border B2B settlement for merchants. Its filings describe solutions that route customer crypto through exchanges and stablecoins with merchant settlement largely in fiat, positioning the firm as a crypto payments facilitator rather than a crypto holder. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/823546/000149315223029497/form10-k.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management characterizes RocketFuel as a “payment infrastructure company,” emphasizing that it processes crypto/stablecoin payments and does not hold crypto on its balance sheet; the platform earns fees on processed volume similar to card networks. The FY2023 10‑K explains operational flows: crypto received from shoppers is transferred to a regulated exchange and converted to USDT or similar stablecoin, with merchant settlements typically via ACH or wire in fiat; the company also launched a payouts product that can use stablecoins. In July 2022 RocketFuel announced a cross-border B2B solution facilitating settlement with stablecoins (USDT/USDC), and in November 2023 it partnered with Ripple to integrate Ripple Payments across its products. Together these sources point to direct provision of crypto financial services (payments, settlement) rather than mining, hardware, or treasury holding. ([insidermonkey.com](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/rocketfuel-blockchain-inc-pnkrkfl-q3-2022-earnings-call-transcript-1138310/))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv (1.0) is assigned because RocketFuel directly enables crypto/stablecoin payments, payouts, and cross‑border settlements for merchants, earns transaction and related fees, and integrates exchange and stablecoin rails operationally. Holders (0.0) is supported by management’s explicit statement that the company does not own crypto, and filings indicating rapid conversion and fiat settlement; there is no evidence of mining, hardware provision, or non‑financial services to crypto firms, so miners/bizserv/infrastr remain 0.0. ([insidermonkey.com](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/rocketfuel-blockchain-inc-pnkrkfl-q3-2022-earnings-call-transcript-1138310/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Public transcripts are limited for a micro‑cap issuer; one detailed Q3 2022 call was located and used alongside SEC filings and press releases.  
- Some products also support ACH/fiat rails; without a disclosed revenue split, RevShare is coded as 4 (>45%) rather than 5 to avoid overstatement.  
- Stablecoin conversions may involve temporary operational holdings, but filings and management statements indicate no strategic crypto asset holdings on the balance sheet. ([insidermonkey.com](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/rocketfuel-blockchain-inc-pnkrkfl-q3-2022-earnings-call-transcript-1138310/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- April 2023 (Q3 FY2022): “We do not own crypto… we’re like Visa and Mastercard, but for modern payment solutions where we charge a percentage of the money that we process.” — [Insider Monkey transcript] (https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/rocketfuel-blockchain-inc-pnkrkfl-q3-2022-earnings-call-transcript-1138310/) ([insidermonkey.com](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/rocketfuel-blockchain-inc-pnkrkfl-q3-2022-earnings-call-transcript-1138310/))  
- April 2023 (Q3 FY2022): “More and more… ‘can you just send it to me in crypto, maybe a stable coin’… payouts product… we expect a lot from that.” — [Insider Monkey transcript] (https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/rocketfuel-blockchain-inc-pnkrkfl-q3-2022-earnings-call-transcript-1138310/) ([insidermonkey.com](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/rocketfuel-blockchain-inc-pnkrkfl-q3-2022-earnings-call-transcript-1138310/))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- FY2023 10‑K (filed Aug 18, 2023): “Cryptocurrency received… are transferred immediately to an account on Coinbase… then converted to USDT or another stablecoin… settlements to merchants are made by ACH or wire transfer of fiat currency.” — [SEC 10‑K] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/823546/000149315223029497/form10-k.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/823546/000149315223029497/form10-k.htm))  
- FY2023 10‑K: “Our blockchain‑based solution enables… transfers using a stablecoin tied to the U.S. dollar… B2B cross‑border payments… payouts solution.” — [SEC 10‑K] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/823546/000149315223029497/form10-k.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/823546/000149315223029497/form10-k.htm))  
- FY2021 10‑K (filed Jul 22, 2021): “Merchants do not need to… hold the different cryptocurrencies because RocketFuel exchanges these currencies to… USD and allow[s] settlement with fiat… [solution is] non‑custodial.” — [SEC 10‑K] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/823546/000149315221017524/form10-k.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/823546/000149315221017524/form10-k.htm))

**Financial news / other:**
- Jul 26, 2022: “New B2B cross‑border settlements… facilitates settlement using stablecoins like USDT and USDC.” — [GlobeNewswire] (https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2022/07/26/2486278/0/en/RocketFuel-Announces-New-B2B-Cross-Border-Settlements-Solution.html) ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2022/07/26/2486278/0/en/RocketFuel-Announces-New-B2B-Cross-Border-Settlements-Solution.html?utm_source=openai))  
- Nov 8, 2023: “RocketFuel Partners with Ripple… adopting Ripple Payments into its suite of payment products.” — [GlobeNewswire] (https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2023/11/08/2776592/0/en/RocketFuel-Partners-with-Ripple-to-Revolutionize-Global-Payments.html) ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2023/11/08/2776592/0/en/RocketFuel-Partners-with-Ripple-to-Revolutionize-Global-Payments.html?utm_source=openai))  
- Nov 9, 2023: Trade coverage of Ripple partnership and RocketFuel’s crypto processor positioning. — [Digital Transactions] (https://www.digitaltransactions.net/eye-on-crypto-bitcoin-depot-seals-cord-kiosk-deal-ripple-in-rocketfuel-partnership/) ([digitaltransactions.net](https://www.digitaltransactions.net/eye-on-crypto-bitcoin-depot-seals-cord-kiosk-deal-ripple-in-rocketfuel-partnership/?utm_source=openai))

## ZIMVIE DEAD - DELIST.21/10/25

* Rank: 65 | Expo: 1.39 | id_str: US98888T1079
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10‑K filings (FY2023), Q4 2022 and Q4 2023 earnings calls; company press releases (Dec 2023–Apr 2024)

### Firm Overview
ZimVie Inc is a U.S.-based medical technology company that, during the 2017–2024 sample period, developed and sold dental and spine products, and in December 2023 agreed to sell its spine segment to H.I.G. Capital, closing in April 2024 to become a pure‑play dental business. Its filings and investor communications focus on implants, biomaterials, and digital dentistry solutions; there is no indication of cryptocurrency-related activities. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1876588/000095017024022103/zimv-20231231.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across multiple earnings-call transcripts in 2023–2024 and the FY2023 Form 10‑K, management consistently discusses dental implants, biomaterials, digital workflow software, and (pre-divestiture) spine devices, with no references to crypto assets, mining, blockchain operations, custody, trading, or payments. The Q4 2023 call explicitly frames the business as transitioning to a “pure‑play dental company,” again without any crypto touchpoints. The absence of crypto mentions in core filings and calls over the sample window supports classification as unclassified for crypto exposure. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2024/02/28/zimvie-zimv-q4-2023-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Weight Assignment
Unclass (1.0) is assigned because searches of 10‑K filings (2017–2024 coverage available for ZimVie as an independent company beginning 2022), press releases, and multiple transcripts revealed no substantive crypto exposure via treasury holdings, mining, financial services, non‑financial crypto services, or infrastructure supply. All mechanism categories are therefore set to 0.0, with unclass at 1.0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1876588/000095017024022103/zimv-20231231.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified. ZimVie became a standalone public company on March 1, 2022 following a spin from Zimmer Biomet; coverage prior to that date relates to its former parent, but within the sample period as an independent issuer there are no crypto‑related disclosures. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1876588/000095017024022103/zimv-20231231.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Mar 1, 2023 (Q4 2022): “These launches marked the beginning of the next wave of innovation in our dental portfolio… Moving to spine, we’re equally focused on refreshing our spine portfolio.” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2023/03/01/zimvie-zimv-q4-2022-earnings-call-transcript/)  
- Feb 28, 2024 (Q4 2023): “We now look very optimistically to 2024 as a pure‑play dental company with a comprehensive and industry‑leading portfolio.” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2024/02/28/zimvie-zimv-q4-2023-earnings-call-transcript/)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- FY2023 Form 10‑K (filed Mar 2024): background on the spin from Zimmer Biomet and agreement to sell the spine business; business focused on dental and spine within the period. — [SEC 10‑K](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1876588/000095017024022103/zimv-20231231.htm)  
- Apr 1, 2024: Closing of the spine sale to H.I.G. Capital; company positions as a dental pure‑play. — [Company press release](https://investor.zimvie.com/news-releases/news-release-details/zimvie-announces-closing-sale-spine-business/)

**Financial news / other:**
- Dec 18, 2023: Announcement of definitive agreement to sell the spine business for $375 million. — [Company press release](https://investor.zimvie.com/news-releases/news-release-details/zimvie-announces-definitive-agreement-sell-spine-business-hig)  
- Apr 1, 2024: H.I.G. Capital announces completion of ZimVie spine acquisition (rebranded Highridge Medical). — [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20240401181472/en/H.I.G.-Capital-Acquires-the-Spine-Business-of-ZimVie-Rebranded-as-Highridge-Medical)

## ZEDGE 'B'

* Rank: 66 | Expo: 1.38 | id_str: US98923T1043
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10‑K filing (FY2023), Q2 FY2022 and Q4 FY2022 earnings calls; company press releases (2021–2022)

### Firm Overview
Zedge Inc is a U.S.-based app publisher best known for the Zedge Ringtones & Wallpapers app and related creator marketplace, Zedge Premium. During 2021–2023 it added an NFT feature (“NFTs Made Easy”) that allowed select creators to mint and sell NFTs to Zedge users within its marketplace. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1667313/000121390023081544/f10k2023_zedgeinc.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management introduced and discussed NFT minting and sales in late 2021–2022, describing an easy on‑ramp where non‑crypto experts could “Publish as an NFT” and users could buy those NFTs using Zedge’s in‑app virtual currency (Zedge Credits/Tokens), with plans for auctions, numbered editions, trading, and potential crypto payment support. This constitutes direct participation in digital‑asset issuance and marketplace activity, i.e., crypto financial services. There is no evidence that Zedge mined crypto or held crypto assets on its balance sheet as treasury or product collateral. ([irp.cdn-website.com](https://irp.cdn-website.com/53275870/files/uploaded/Transcript_Zedge_Mar15_2022.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv is set to 1.0 because Zedge operated an NFT minting/sales feature inside its marketplace and explicitly framed future functionality around trading and crypto payment acceptance, which aligns with the financial‑services mechanism. Other channels (holders, miners, bizserv, infrastr) are 0.0 due to lack of evidence. Although the NFT initiative was a small revenue contributor relative to total company revenue, the mechanism classification is based on the nature of exposure rather than its size; therefore RevShare is coded as 1 and AssetShare as 0. ([irp.cdn-website.com](https://irp.cdn-website.com/53275870/files/uploaded/Transcript_Zedge_Mar15_2022.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Zedge processed NFT transactions using its own virtual credits rather than on‑chain payments during the period, and filings do not specify custody architecture; however, management’s descriptions and filings clearly indicate NFT minting/sales and planned crypto payment support within the marketplace. ([irp.cdn-website.com](https://irp.cdn-website.com/53275870/files/uploaded/Transcript_Zedge_Mar15_2022.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Mar 15, 2022 (Q2 FY2022): “We rolled out our potentially disruptive NFTs Made Easy platform… To date, we have sold close to 600 video wallpaper NFTs… [users] purchase these NFTs by buying Zedge Tokens, our existing virtual currency.” — [Company transcript PDF](https://irp.cdn-website.com/53275870/files/uploaded/Transcript_Zedge_Mar15_2022.pdf)  
- Nov 2022 (Q4 FY2022): “We introduced ‘NFTs Made Easy’… non‑crypto experts… tokenize and sell their art… We’ve been somewhat insulated from the crypto winter… we will add features… including… the ability to use crypto as a payment type.” — [Insider Monkey transcript](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/zedge-inc-amexzdge-q4-2022-earnings-call-transcript-1100736/)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Oct 30, 2023: “In late 2021 we introduced NFT functionality… All NFT Made Easy transactions are made using Zedge Credits.” — [SEC 10‑K FY2023](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1667313/000121390023081544/f10k2023_zedgeinc.htm)  
- Dec 13, 2021: “Tomorrow morning, we will launch… ‘NFTs Made Easy’ to select Zedge Premium artists.” — [Newswire press release](https://www.newswire.com/news/zedge-announces-record-first-quarter-results)

**Financial news / other:**
- Aug 8, 2022: “Zedge Introduces ‘NFTs Made Easy’ Version 2.0… available exclusively for purchase in Zedge Premium.” — [PressRelease.com](https://www.pressrelease.com/news/zedge-introduces-nfts-made-easy-version-2-0)  
- Nov 2021: “Zedge Celebrates the Sale of the 500th NFT through its ‘NFTs Made Easy’ Platform on Zedge Premium.” — [Newswire](https://www.newswire.com/news/zedge-celebrates-the-sale-of-the-500th-nft-through-its-nfts-made-easy-platform)

</small>

## CONTANGO ORE

* Primary sources: 10‑K/10‑KT filings (FY2023 and 2023 transition period), company press releases (Q3 2024), corporate overview page

### Firm Overview
Contango ORE, Inc. is a U.S.-based minerals company focused on gold exploration and development in Alaska. As of the 2023 transition-period filing, it held a 30% interest in the Peak Gold JV (Manh Choh) operated by a Kinross subsidiary and advanced additional Alaska projects including Lucky Shot and Johnson Tract. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1502377/000095017024031469/ctgo-20231231.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024 filings and company communications, Contango ORE describes a conventional gold mining and exploration business with no indication of cryptocurrency-related activities. The 2023 transition 10‑K and the June 30, 2023 Form 10‑K contain no references to cryptoassets, mining of digital assets, custody, NFT products, or blockchain-enabled financial services. Public updates in 2024 emphasize the Manh Choh first gold pour and operational milestones, not digital-asset initiatives. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1502377/000095017024031469/ctgo-20231231.htm))

### Weight Assignment
No credible evidence of crypto exposure was identified in any mechanism category for the 2017–2024 sample window. Accordingly, all mechanism weights are set to 0.0 and unclass is set to 1.0. With no crypto mechanism present, the investor‑base amplifier is 0 and materiality metrics are not applicable.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 15, 2024: Company scheduled a conference call and webcast to discuss Q3‑2024 results; no crypto‑related content indicated. — [Contango press release](https://www.contangoore.com/press-release/contango-announces-earnings-for-the-quarter-ended-september-30-2024)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Transition Form 10‑K (filed Mar 14, 2024): Business overview focused on Alaska gold projects (Peak Gold JV/Manh Choh, Lucky Shot, Johnson Tract). — [SEC 10‑KT](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1502377/000095017024031469/ctgo-20231231.htm)  
- FY2023 Form 10‑K (year ended Jun 30, 2023): Conventional gold mining focus; details on gold hedge agreements; no crypto references. — [SEC 10‑K](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1502377/000143774923025785/conta20230630_10k.htm)  
- Jul 8, 2024: First gold poured at Manh Choh; project milestone update. — [Company press release](https://www.contangoore.com/press-release/manh-choh-mine-pours-first-gold)

**Financial news / other:**
- Jul 31, 2024: Kinross update notes Manh Choh first gold bar poured on July 8, 2024. — [Kinross news release](https://www.kinross.com/news-and-investors/news-releases/press-release-details/2024/Kinross-reports-strong-2024-second-quarter-results/)  
- Company overview: Alaska-focused gold exploration and development; production profile context. — [Corporate overview](https://www.contangoore.com/corporate/overview)

## FLATEXDEGIRO N

* Rank: 68 | Expo: 1.34 | id_str: DE000FTG1111
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Annual Report 2024, Q3 2024 earnings call, pricing/fee schedule (Dec 2024), DEGIRO helpdesk page

### Firm Overview
flatexDEGIRO AG is a Frankfurt‑based pan‑European online broker operating the flatex, DEGIRO, and ViTrade brands, offering access to exchanges in Europe, North America and APAC. In December 2024 the group added native crypto‑asset trading in Germany under the flatex brand, with expansion to other markets planned subsequently.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s exposure to crypto arises through financial services: it offers customers the ability to trade crypto assets directly and, prior to that, provided access to crypto‑linked ETPs/ETNs on its brokerage platforms. Management discussed the Q4 2024 launch and near‑term rollout plans on earnings calls, and the 2024 Annual Report documents the new product and its pricing model. There is no evidence that flatexDEGIRO mines crypto, holds crypto on its own balance sheet as a treasury asset, or supplies mining/compute infrastructure.

### Weight Assignment
Evidence supports a single mechanism: crypto financial services via brokerage/trading access. Direct crypto trading began on December 16, 2024, and DEGIRO previously offered crypto exposure through trackers/ETPs; therefore finserv = 1.0 and all other categories = 0.0. Given the late‑2024 start and management’s guidance that significant crypto revenues would scale in 2025, crypto revenue within 2017–2024 appears minor (RevShare = 1) and no material crypto assets are indicated on balance sheet (AssetShare = 0). No clear signs that the stock trades as a crypto proxy were identified (invbase = 0).

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Direct crypto trading under flatex launched very late in 2024, so in‑period revenue contribution is small; management describes a partner‑based setup for execution/custody, which limits on‑balance‑sheet exposure. Earlier crypto access via ETPs/ETNs is indirect and routed through listed securities, but still fits the financial‑services mechanism as brokerage of crypto investment products.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 23, 2024 (Q3 2024): “We will launch [native] crypto trading in Germany still this year.” — [ROIC.ai transcript](https://www.roic.ai/quote/FTK.DE/transcripts/2024/3) ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/FTK.DE/transcripts/2024/3))  
- Oct 23, 2024 (Q3 2024): “As a first step, we will make crypto trading available for our German clients at the flatex and ViTrade brands.” — [ROIC.ai transcript](https://www.roic.ai/quote/FTK.DE/transcripts/2024/3) ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/FTK.DE/transcripts/2024/3))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Annual Report 2024: “16 December 2024 [the company] launched trading in 20 cryptocurrencies… total trading cost… 0.6%… 0.7% for less liquid cryptocurrencies.” — [Annual Report 2024 PDF] ([companiesmarketcap.com](https://companiesmarketcap.com/annual-reports/44677.ar.en.2024.pdf))  
- Annual Report 2024: “At the end of 2024, flatexDEGIRO also began enabling its customers to trade directly in crypto assets.” — [Annual Report 2024 PDF] ([companiesmarketcap.com](https://companiesmarketcap.com/annual-reports/44677.ar.en.2024.pdf))  
- Annual Report 2024 (Outlook): “Since December 2024, customers of the flatex brand in Germany have been able to trade directly in crypto assets.” — [Annual Report 2024 PDF] ([companiesmarketcap.com](https://companiesmarketcap.com/annual-reports/44677.ar.en.2024.pdf))  
- Fee schedule: “Ergänzendes Preis‑ und Leistungsverzeichnis … Handel von Kryptowerten … gültig ab 01.12.2024.” — [flatex fee schedule PDF] ([konto.flatex.de](https://konto.flatex.de/formularcenter_bank/public/0005100_de.pdf?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- DEGIRO helpdesk: “We do not offer cryptocurrencies… we offer several crypto trackers (ETPs) on our platform.” — [DEGIRO help page] ([degiro.ie](https://www.degiro.ie/helpdesk/trading-possibilities/can-i-trade-cryptocurrencies-degiro?utm_source=openai))

</small>
