## 1STDIBS COM

* Rank: 69 | Expo: 1.22 | id_str: US3205511047
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q1 2022 and Q2 2022 earnings calls; FY2022 Form 10‑K (filed Mar 3, 2023); Aug 11, 2021 NFT platform launch press release; NFT FAQ/terms pages

### Firm Overview
1stdibs.com, Inc. is a New York–based online marketplace for luxury design, connecting buyers with vetted sellers of furniture, decor, jewelry, art and fashion. Within the 2017–2024 window, the company briefly operated a curated NFT marketplace (launched August 2021) before ceasing further investment in January 2023.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s only direct crypto exposure in the period stems from financial services: an NFT marketplace that minted and auctioned digital art on Ethereum and required buyers to connect a crypto wallet (e.g., MetaMask) and pay in ETH. Management highlighted “NFTs” as a core strategic focus in Q1 2022 and noted self‑minting features to grow NFT supply; by Q2 2022 they stated the NFT market had slowed and they paused incremental investment. The FY2022 Form 10‑K (filed March 3, 2023) formally records that, in January 2023, the company ceased further investment in the NFT platform, though previously minted NFTs remained available for sale. There is no evidence that 1stdibs mined crypto, held crypto as a treasury asset, or provided mining/compute infrastructure. ([investors.1stdibs.com](https://investors.1stdibs.com/news/news-details/2021/1stDibs-Launches-NFT-Platform-Introducing-Auctions-Featuring-Group-and-Solo-Exhibitions/default.aspx))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence supports a single mechanism: crypto financial services via an NFT marketplace operating from August 2021 with activity de‑emphasized from mid‑2022 and further investment ceased in January 2023. Accordingly, finserv = 1.0 and all other categories = 0.0. Given management’s disclosure that the NFT initiative was paused and later wound down from an investment perspective, crypto‑related revenue within 2017–2024 appears minor (RevShare = 1), and there is no indication of material on‑balance‑sheet crypto assets (AssetShare = 0). ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/08/10/1stdibscom-inc-dibs-q2-2022-earnings-call-transcri/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The NFT marketplace used self‑custody (MetaMask) and ETH payments, limiting on‑balance‑sheet exposure; disclosures do not quantify NFT‑related revenue. Although further investment ceased in January 2023, previously minted NFTs could still trade; ongoing economic materiality appears limited. No evidence indicates broader crypto payment acceptance for the core physical‑goods marketplace. ([1stdibs.com](https://www.1stdibs.com/info/nft-faq/?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 11, 2022 (Q1 2022), Motley Fool: “Our plan for 2022 and beyond is to … focus on four strategic areas: supply growth, auctions, international expansion, and NFTs.”; “In early March, we launched self‑minting capabilities and creator profiles.” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/05/11/1stdibscom-inc-dibs-q1-2022-earnings-call-transcri/)  
- Aug 10, 2022 (Q2 2022), Motley Fool: “Over the past few months, the market for NFTs has slowed dramatically… we have paused incremental investment in our NFT platform.” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/08/10/1stdibscom-inc-dibs-q2-2022-earnings-call-transcri/)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Mar 3, 2023 (FY2022 10‑K): “In January 2023, we determined to cease further investment in the NFT platform; however, previously‑minted NFTs remain available for sale.” — [SEC filing (FY2022 10‑K)](https://fintel.io/doc/sec-1stdibscom-inc-1600641-10k-2023-march-03-19419-4597)  
- Aug 11, 2021: “1stDibs Launches NFT Platform… The buying process… leveraging Ethereum cryptocurrency accessed through a MetaMask wallet.” — [Company press release](https://investors.1stdibs.com/news/news-details/2021/1stDibs-Launches-NFT-Platform-Introducing-Auctions-Featuring-Group-and-Solo-Exhibitions/default.aspx)

**Financial news / other:**
- NFT FAQ: “To place a bid on an NFT work on 1stDibs, you must have a crypto wallet, like MetaMask, and connect it to 1stDibs.” — [1stDibs NFT FAQ](https://www.1stdibs.com/info/nft-faq/)  
- NFT Terms of Use: Ethereum wallet connection via MetaMask and smart‑contract risks outlined. — [1stDibs NFT Terms](https://www.1stdibs.com/about/nft-terms-of-use/)

</small>

## PHUNWARE

* Rank: 70 | Expo: 1.16 | id_str: US71948P2092
* Weight summary: holders 0.6, miners 0.0, finserv 0.4, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: FY2021 and FY2023 10‑K filings; Q4 2021 and Q4 2023 earnings calls; Nov–Dec 2021 bitcoin acquisition and payment press releases

### Firm Overview
Phunware, Inc. is an Austin‑based enterprise mobile software company that provides a Multiscreen‑as‑a‑Service platform for applications, location, and engagement. Within 2021–2023 it added blockchain initiatives (PhunWallet, PhunToken, PhunCoin) and purchased bitcoin for treasury; by December 31, 2023, its digital asset holdings had been largely reduced to de minimis levels. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1665300/000162828022008722/phun-20211231.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Crypto holders: In 2021 the company adopted bitcoin as a treasury reserve asset; its 2021 10‑K discloses digital assets equal to about 33% of total assets and details impairment accounting, and press releases show cumulative purchases reaching roughly 630 BTC by December 9, 2021. By year‑end 2023, filings show it had disposed of essentially all such holdings (ending balance ≈ $75 across BTC/ETH/other). ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1665300/000162828022008722/phun-20211231.htm))  
Crypto financial services: Management launched and “commercially… started scaling” PhunWallet together with its PhunCoin (security token) and PhunToken (utility token) ecosystem, and later noted 2022 revenue benefited from $1.5 million of PhunToken sales; the company also accepted bitcoin as payment for its Lyte PCs. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/PHUNW/transcripts/2021-year/4-quarter))

### Weight Assignment
Two mechanisms are supported. Holders receives the larger weight (0.6) because bitcoin treasury activity in 2021–2022 was large and explicitly emphasized (including adoption of bitcoin as a primary treasury asset and detailed impairment effects), even though balances were largely exited by late 2023. Finserv is assigned 0.4 reflecting the launch and monetization of the PhunWallet/PhunToken/PhunCoin ecosystem and acceptance of bitcoin for payments, which contributed meaningfully to 2022 revenue but appears far less material thereafter. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1665300/000162828022008722/phun-20211231.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Digital asset exposure declined sharply by December 31, 2023 (≈$75 remaining), so balance‑sheet sensitivity to crypto prices during the back end of the sample is minimal despite earlier materiality. Token‑related revenue was lumpy (e.g., $1.5 million in 2022) and not clearly sustained in 2023; Lyte (which accepted bitcoin) was shut down in late 2023. Management’s 2024 commentary about a “global crypto passport” points to potential future finserv initiatives but without quantification in the 2017–2024 window. ([investors.phunware.com](https://investors.phunware.com/sec-filings/annual-reports/xbrl_doc_only/1465))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Mar 12, 2024 (Q4 2023), Strike.Market: “Net revenues… 2023 totaled $4.8 million… The decline… is due to the sale of $1.5 million of PhunToken in the prior year.” — [Q4 2023 transcript](https://strike.market/stocks/PHUN/earnings-call-transcripts/243980)  
- Mar 23, 2022 (Q4 2021), Roic AI: “We commercially launched and started scaling our PhunWallet… powered by our PhunCoin and PhunToken cryptocurrencies.” — [Q4 2021 transcript](https://www.roic.ai/quote/PHUNW/transcripts/2021-year/4-quarter)  
- Mar 23, 2022 (Q4 2021), Roic AI: “Expect to see more purchases of Bitcoin… and strategic use of… DeFi.” — [Q4 2021 transcript](https://www.roic.ai/quote/PHUNW/transcripts/2021-year/4-quarter)  
- Aug 11, 2022 (Q2 2022), Roic AI: “Digital currency balance of more than 653 Bitcoin, 753 Ethereum…” and token/Wallet scaling remarks. — [Q2 2022 transcript](https://www.roic.ai/quote/PHUNW/transcripts/2022/2)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- FY2021 10‑K: “As of December 31, 2021, we held $32.6 million of digital assets… carrying value… approximately 33% of our total assets… We have adopted bitcoin as our primary treasury reserve asset.” — [SEC 10‑K (2021)](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1665300/000162828022008722/phun-20211231.htm)  
- FY2023 10‑K XBRL note: Digital assets roll‑forward ending Dec 31, 2023: BTC $17, ETH $52, Other $6 (total $75). — [SEC 10‑K (2023) note](https://investors.phunware.com/sec-filings/annual-reports/xbrl_doc_only/1465)  
- Dec 9, 2021: “Phunware holds an aggregate of approximately 630 bitcoin… average purchase price ~$58,513.” — [Press release](https://investors.phunware.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/222/phunware-acquires-additional-bitcoin)  
- Oct 29, 2021: “Now accepts bitcoin for purchases of high performance computers… Lyte Technology.” — [Press release](https://investors.phunware.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/213/phunware-now-accepts-bitcoin-for-lyte-personal-computers)

**Financial news / other:**
- Nov 22, 2021: “Buys 398 more bitcoins… now holds about 529 bitcoin.” — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/11/22/cloud-software-firm-phunware-buys-398-more-bitcoins-for-about-24m)

</small>

## USIO

* Rank: 71 | Expo: 1.14 | id_str: US9173131080
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.8, bizserv 0.2, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10‑K filings (2022–2023), Q4 2021 and Q4 2022 earnings calls, Aug 4, 2021 Voyager–Usio crypto payments press release

### Firm Overview
Usio, Inc. is a U.S.-based fintech that provides integrated electronic payment solutions including ACH processing, PayFac/credit card acquiring, prepaid card issuing/program management, and bill print-and-mail services. Within 2021–2022, Usio partnered with crypto platforms and launched capabilities to enable crypto payments, but exited this area after its largest crypto customer filed for bankruptcy in July 2022. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/u/NASDAQ_USIO_2022.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Crypto financial services: Management stated that Usio processed ACH payments for Voyager Digital and was “on the cusp of launching the Voyager prepaid card… [a] Mastercard that actually settles in USDC,” indicating direct operational involvement in crypto-linked payment flows. In August 2021, a joint press release announced a Voyager–Usio program to let merchants and ISVs accept many cryptocurrencies, with Usio supporting exchanges, brokerages and related organizations. Post‑bankruptcy of Voyager on July 6, 2022, Usio disclosed withdrawal from the cryptocurrency space and a related decline in ACH revenues. There is no evidence of crypto mining or treasury crypto holdings. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/USIO/transcripts/2021-year/4-quarter))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv receives 0.8 because the company’s exposure came from providing payments rails and card program management directly tied to crypto activity (processing for a crypto exchange, crypto‑settled card program, and a merchant acceptance initiative). Bizserv gets 0.2 to reflect broader, more indirect support to crypto ecosystem clients via general ACH/PayFac services and program operations that may not always have involved handling digital assets. No evidence supports holders, miners, or infrastr. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/USIO/transcripts/2021-year/4-quarter))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The extent to which Usio itself “touched” digital assets versus facilitating fiat on/off‑ramps is partly ambiguous; however, management’s reference to USDC settlement for the Voyager card suggests at least some direct crypto‑linked operations. Exposure declined sharply after July 2022, as the firm exited the crypto space, so sensitivity later in the sample is minimal. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/USIO/transcripts/2021-year/4-quarter))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Mar 18, 2022 (Q4 2021), Roic AI: “Today, we process their ACH payments, and we’re on the cusp of launching the Voyager prepaid card… that actually settles in USDC.” — [Q4 2021 transcript] ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/USIO/transcripts/2021-year/4-quarter))  
- Mar 8, 2023 (Q4 2022), Fintool: “We’re doing minor wind down activities for the bankruptcy court for Voyager, but it’s not significant at all.” — [Q4 2022 transcript] ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/USIO/documents/transcripts/q4-2022))

**Filings (10‑K / 10‑Q):**
- FY2022 10‑K: “On July 6, 2022, our largest cryptocurrency customer filed for bankruptcy… we… pulled out of the cryptocurrency space,” noting ≈$0.8m impact to ACH/complementary services and exit from crypto in July 2022. — [SEC-filed annual report PDF] ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/u/NASDAQ_USIO_2022.pdf))  
- FY2023 10‑K: Notes Voyager’s $1.0m equity investment in Usio in Nov 2021, evidencing the commercial linkage. — [SEC 10‑K 2023] ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1088034/000143774924009583/usio20231231_10k.htm))  
- Q2 2023 10‑Q: “Decreases… were primarily attributable to our withdrawal from the cryptocurrency space following the… bankruptcy… by Voyager Digital on July 6, 2022.” — [SEC 10‑Q] ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1088034/000143774923023653/usio20230630_10q.htm?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Aug 4, 2021: “Voyager Digital partners with Usio to enable merchants and ISVs to accept cryptocurrency as a form of payment,” with Usio supporting exchanges/brokerages. — [PR Newswire] ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/voyager-digital-partners-with-usio-to-enable-merchants-and-isvs-to-accept-cryptocurrency-as-a-form-of-payment-301348459.html))

</small>

## GAMESTOP 'A'

* Rank: 72 | Expo: 1.13 | id_str: US36467W1099
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10‑K/10‑Q filings (2022–2024), Q1 2022 and Q2 2022 earnings calls, company and industry news (2022–2024)

### Firm Overview
GameStop Corp. is a U.S. specialty retailer of video games, consumer electronics, and collectibles listed on the NYSE. Between 2022 and 2024, the company pursued digital‑asset initiatives, launching an NFT marketplace and a non‑custodial crypto wallet and later winding down both offerings amid shifting market and regulatory conditions. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1326380/000132638023000019/gme-20230128.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Crypto financial services: In 2022 GameStop launched a non‑custodial digital asset wallet and an NFT marketplace enabling users to buy, sell and trade NFTs, supported by partnerships with Immutable X and Loopring. Management framed these as part of growth “in the cryptocurrency, NFT and Web3 gaming verticals.” The company also announced a retail partnership with the crypto exchange FTX US in September 2022, which it terminated following FTX’s collapse in November 2022. In filings, GameStop disclosed it received IMX tokens under its Immutable agreements but liquidated all such tokens during fiscal 2022, and subsequently wound down first the wallet (2023) and then the NFT marketplace (February 2024). ([nasdaq.com](https://www.nasdaq.com/articles/gamestop-gme-q2-2022-earnings-call-transcript))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 reflects GameStop’s direct operation of an NFT trading venue and digital wallet—core financial‑service functions involving digital assets. There is no evidence of crypto mining or of deliberate balance‑sheet crypto treasury holdings; IMX tokens received for the marketplace partnership were treated as digital assets and fully liquidated in 2022, supporting zero weight for holders/miners and an AssetShare of 0 based on the most recent disclosures in the 2017–2024 window. Revenue contribution from these initiatives appears minor, consistent with a RevShare of 1. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1326380/000132638023000019/gme-20230128.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Exposure was time‑limited: products launched in 2022 and were wound down in 2023–2024, so sensitivity later in the sample is minimal. The wallet was non‑custodial, which limits direct asset handling risk versus custodial exchanges, but the NFT marketplace still constitutes crypto financial services under our definitions. None identified regarding mining or treasury crypto holdings. ([arstechnica.com](https://arstechnica.com/gaming/2023/08/gamestop-citing-regulatory-uncertainty-winds-down-its-crypto-and-nft-wallet/?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Sep 7, 2022 (Q2 2022), Nasdaq/Motley Fool: “The launch of our NFT marketplace supports GameStop’s pursuit of long‑term growth in the cryptocurrency, NFT and Web3 gaming verticals.” — [Nasdaq transcript] (https://www.nasdaq.com/articles/gamestop-gme-q2-2022-earnings-call-transcript) ([nasdaq.com](https://www.nasdaq.com/articles/gamestop-gme-q2-2022-earnings-call-transcript))  
- Jun 1, 2022 (Q1 2022), Motley Fool: “Our growth and the launch of new tech products, such as our digital asset wallet and the upcoming NFT marketplace…” — [Motley Fool transcript] (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/06/01/gamestop-gme-q1-2022-earnings-call-transcript//) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/06/01/gamestop-gme-q1-2022-earnings-call-transcript//?utm_source=openai))

**Filings (10‑K / 10‑Q / 8‑K):**
- Feb 3, 2022 (8‑K): Partnership with Immutable X; grant agreements including “up to $150 million in IMX tokens” upon milestones. — [SEC 8‑K] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1326380/000132638022000012/gme-20220128.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1326380/000132638022000012/gme-20220128.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- Mar 28, 2023 (FY2022 10‑K): “We liquidated all tokens received during fiscal 2022 and have no IMX token assets recorded at January 28, 2023.” — [SEC 10‑K] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1326380/000132638023000019/gme-20230128.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1326380/000132638023000019/gme-20230128.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- Oct 2023 (Q3 2023 10‑Q): Reiterates IMX token liquidation and references the digital asset wallet and NFT marketplace within segment discussion. — [SEC 10‑Q] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1326380/000132638023000063/gme-20231028.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1326380/000132638023000063/gme-20231028.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- FY2021 10‑K (filed Mar 17, 2022): Note on “Digital Assets” and recognition of an IMX‑related receivable/deferred revenue. — [10‑K PDF] (https://stocklight.com/stocks/us/nyse-gme/gamestop/annual-reports/nyse-gme-2022-10K-22748602.pdf) ([stocklight.com](https://stocklight.com/stocks/us/nyse-gme/gamestop/annual-reports/nyse-gme-2022-10K-22748602.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**
- Oct 31, 2022: Official launch of GameStop NFT Marketplace with ImmutableX. — [GameStop IR press release] (https://investor.gamestop.com/news-releases/news-details/2022/GameStops-NFT-Marketplace-Launches-with-ImmutableX-10-31-2022/default.aspx) ([investor.gamestop.com](https://investor.gamestop.com/news-releases/news-details/2022/GameStops-NFT-Marketplace-Launches-with-ImmutableX-10-31-2022/default.aspx?utm_source=openai))  
- Sep 7, 2022: GameStop–FTX US partnership announcement. — [GameStop IR press release] (https://investor.gamestop.com/news-releases/news-details/2022/GameStop-Forms-Partnership-with-FTX-09-07-2022/default.aspx) ([investor.gamestop.com](https://investor.gamestop.com/news-releases/news-details/2022/GameStop-Forms-Partnership-with-FTX-09-07-2022/default.aspx?utm_source=openai))  
- Nov 11, 2022: GameStop to end FTX.US ties and refund customers after FTX collapse. — [CoinDesk] (https://www.coindesk.com/business/2022/11/11/gamestop-to-end-ftxus-ties-refund-customers) ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2022/11/11/gamestop-to-end-ftxus-ties-refund-customers?utm_source=openai))  
- Aug 4, 2023: GameStop winds down its crypto/NFT wallet citing regulatory uncertainty. — [Ars Technica] (https://arstechnica.com/gaming/2023/08/gamestop-citing-regulatory-uncertainty-winds-down-its-crypto-and-nft-wallet/) ([arstechnica.com](https://arstechnica.com/gaming/2023/08/gamestop-citing-regulatory-uncertainty-winds-down-its-crypto-and-nft-wallet/?utm_source=openai))  
- Jan 2024: GameStop to wind down NFT marketplace (closure effective Feb 2, 2024). — [The Block] (https://www.theblock.co/post/272529/gamestop-axes-nft-marketplace-citing-regulatory-uncertainty) ([theblock.co](https://www.theblock.co/post/272529/gamestop-axes-nft-marketplace-citing-regulatory-uncertainty?utm_source=openai))

## GLIMPSE GROUP

* Rank: 73 | Expo: 1.12 | id_str: US37892C1062
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (FY2024), Q1 FY2022 earnings call, company press releases (2021–2023)

### Firm Overview
The Glimpse Group is a U.S.-based immersive technology company listed on Nasdaq that provides enterprise-focused virtual reality, augmented reality and spatial computing software and services across multiple subsidiaries. In recent years, management has emphasized a strategic focus on spatial computing, cloud and AI, with filings also noting interconnections with emerging technologies such as blockchain. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1854445/000149315224038786/form10-k.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Glimpse launched an internal initiative in August 2021 to develop VR/AR software and services for the NFT and crypto segments, including multi‑user VR art galleries, AR NFT display systems and high‑resolution NFT asset creation. In January 2022 it announced creation of a limited‑edition “Glimpse Medal” NFT intended for use across AR/VR platforms and existing NFT virtual worlds. In February 2023, the company stated it was integrating NFT/blockchain features into v‑commerce applications within its XR software portfolio. These activities constitute non‑financial, enterprise software/services provided to crypto/Web3 clients rather than custody, exchange, or token issuance. ([theglimpsegroup.com](https://www.theglimpsegroup.com/news/press-releases/nft))

### Weight Assignment
Bizserv = 1.0 reflects that Glimpse’s crypto linkage occurs through AR/VR software development and integration for NFTs/Web3 (galleries, displays, v‑commerce), without evidence it operates a marketplace, provides custody or holds crypto as treasury assets. Earnings remarks have referenced “blockchain” and “NFT” as part of the technology landscape coalescing with VR/AR, while filings frame blockchain as an adjacent emerging technology; neither suggests mining, balance‑sheet coin holdings, or financial intermediation. Revenue contribution from these efforts appears minor, hence RevShare = 1 and AssetShare = 0. ([tickertrends.io](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/VRAR/Q1-earnings-transcript-2022))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Crypto/NFT activity appears most pronounced during 2021–2023; the November 2023 earnings call and the FY2024 10‑K emphasize spatial computing, cloud and AI rather than NFTs, suggesting the crypto‑adjacent work may be opportunistic or de‑emphasized later in the sample. No evidence was found of Glimpse operating a crypto exchange, offering custodial services, mining, or adopting a treasury crypto strategy. ([ir.theglimpsegroup.com](https://ir.theglimpsegroup.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/11/Glimpse-Novemember-2023-transcript.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 15, 2021 (Q1 FY2022), TickerTrends: “And as other emerging technologies, AI, computer vision, blockchain, NFT potentially [coalesce] together with VR and AR over time.” — [TickerTrends transcript](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/VRAR/Q1-earnings-transcript-2022) ([tickertrends.io](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/VRAR/Q1-earnings-transcript-2022))  
- Nov 14, 2023 (Q1 FY2024), Company IR PDF: strategy “driven by spatial computing, cloud and AI.” — [IR transcript PDF](https://ir.theglimpsegroup.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/11/Glimpse-Novemember-2023-transcript.pdf) ([ir.theglimpsegroup.com](https://ir.theglimpsegroup.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/11/Glimpse-Novemember-2023-transcript.pdf))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Sep 30, 2024 (FY2024 10‑K): Company overview; notes immersive tech interconnecting with “AI… and blockchain.” — [SEC 10‑K](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1854445/000149315224038786/form10-k.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1854445/000149315224038786/form10-k.htm))

**Financial news / other:**
- Aug 31, 2021: Launch of enterprise‑focused NFT/crypto VR & AR software/services initiative. — [Company press release](https://www.theglimpsegroup.com/news/press-releases/nft) ([theglimpsegroup.com](https://www.theglimpsegroup.com/news/press-releases/nft))  
- Jan 20, 2022: “Glimpse Medal” NFT created; “metaverse‑ready” across AR/VR and NFT worlds (Decentraland, CryptoVoxels, The Sandbox). — [Company press release](https://www.theglimpsegroup.com/news/press-releases/glimpse-nft) ([theglimpsegroup.com](https://www.theglimpsegroup.com/news/press-releases/glimpse-nft))  
- Feb 14, 2023: Notes “NFT/blockchain integration for V‑Commerce applications” among tech initiatives. — [Company press release](https://www.theglimpsegroup.com/news/press-releases/q2-2023-results) ([theglimpsegroup.com](https://www.theglimpsegroup.com/news/press-releases/q2-2023-results))

## TASKUS A

* Rank: 74 | Expo: 1.10 | id_str: US87652V1098
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (FY2023), Q2 2022 earnings call, Q4 2023 earnings call

### Firm Overview
TaskUs is a U.S.-listed business process outsourcing company that provides digital customer experience, trust and safety, and AI services from global delivery centers. Its exposure to crypto arises indirectly through service work for fintech platforms, including some with crypto or trading features.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management explicitly discussed serving “crypto and equity trading” clients and quantified their revenue contribution during 2022, indicating TaskUs provides customer support and related operations for such platforms rather than transacting in digital assets itself. The FY2023 10-K notes U.S. revenue declines tied to reduced spending by “FinTech, particularly crypto and equity trading clients,” reinforcing that crypto exposure is via service delivery to those customers. Company marketing materials also highlight support for crypto exchanges within its fintech offerings. These activities fit non‑financial services to crypto ecosystem firms, not custody, mining, or product issuance. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/08/09/taskus-inc-task-q2-2022-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Weight Assignment
Bizserv = 1.0 reflects that TaskUs’s linkage to crypto is through outsourced CX/risk and response work for fintech/crypto platforms. Earnings commentary shows this segment was significant at points in 2022 but shrank to a smaller share by late 2022; no evidence indicates crypto asset holdings, mining, or direct financial intermediation. Accordingly, RevShare = 1 (minor) based on guidance that crypto/equity trading clients together were expected to fall to about 5% of revenue by Q4 2022, and AssetShare = 0. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/08/09/taskus-inc-task-q2-2022-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Disclosures aggregate crypto with equity trading, so the precise crypto-only revenue share is not reported. Later calls emphasize “non‑crypto fintech” growth and declines at an equity trading client, suggesting crypto exposure remained peripheral after 2022. Litigation disclosures related to a third‑party crypto hardware wallet data breach involve client work but do not imply balance‑sheet crypto exposure. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2024/02/29/taskus-task-q4-2023-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 9, 2022 (Q2 2022), Motley Fool: “Most acute among our crypto and equity trading clients… [TaskUs has] a low double‑digit number of clients” and expects this group to be ~5% of Q4 revenue. — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/08/09/taskus-inc-task-q2-2022-earnings-call-transcript/)  
- Feb 29, 2024 (Q4 2023), Motley Fool: notes signings with “non‑crypto fintech” clients and volume declines at the “largest equity trading client.” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2024/02/29/taskus-task-q4-2023-earnings-call-transcript/)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Mar 2024 (FY2023 10‑K): U.S. segment impacted by decreased spending from “FinTech, particularly crypto and equity trading clients.” — [SEC 10‑K](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1829864/000182986424000011/task-20231231.htm)  
- Mar 2024 (FY2023 10‑K): Added as defendant in litigation relating to 2020 Ledger cryptocurrency wallet data breach (client work context). — [SEC 10‑K](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1829864/000182986424000011/task-20231231.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- FinTech microsite: positions TaskUs as a partner to “crypto exchanges” for CX and compliance operations. — [TaskUs FinTech microsite](https://taskus.com/wp-content/uploads/microsites/Fintech/fintech-microsite/)

</small>

## VERSABANK

* Rank: 75 | Expo: 1.10 | id_str: CA92512J1066
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.9, bizserv 0.1, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: MD&A filings (Q3 2022), Q1 2022 earnings call, Q4 2024 earnings call, 2021–2022 press releases

### Firm Overview
VersaBank is a Canada‑based, branchless digital bank listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange and Nasdaq that focuses on business‑to‑business digital banking and also owns a cybersecurity subsidiary. Its crypto linkage stems from the bank’s development of blockchain‑based digital deposit receipts (tokenized deposits) and related security technology.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management repeatedly discussed “Digital Deposit Receipts” (also referred to as tokenized deposits) that are one‑for‑one digital representations of cash on deposit with the bank, built on Algorand, Ethereum and Stellar, and supported by the bank’s SOC 2‑audited VersaVault technology. These remarks appear on earnings calls in 2022 and 2024, and filings highlight the SOC 2 Type I audit completion that underpins the product. This activity constitutes direct crypto financial services (issuance/custody of blockchain‑based deposit instruments), not mining or proprietary crypto holding. VersaBank’s cybersecurity arm (DRT Cyber/VersaVault) also markets security technology for digital assets, which is supportive to the ecosystem but secondary to the bank’s financial product focus. ([versabank.com](https://www.versabank.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/2022-Q1-ConferenceCallTranscript.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 0.9 reflects that VersaBank’s principal crypto mechanism is issuing and potentially custodizing tokenized deposits (DDRs), with extensive discussion by management and in filings through 2021–2024, including multi‑chain operation and SOC 2 validation. A small Bizserv = 0.1 captures ancillary exposure via the bank’s cybersecurity technology/services (VersaVault/DRT Cyber) marketed for digital asset security. No evidence indicates balance‑sheet crypto treasury holdings or any mining operations; infrastructure exposure (e.g., hardware/hosting for miners) is not present. Given pilots and pre‑commercial positioning in 2017–2024, crypto revenue/asset materiality appears negligible, so RevShare = 0 and AssetShare = 0. ([versabank.com](https://www.versabank.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/2022-Q1-ConferenceCallTranscript.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Within the January 2017–December 2024 sample, DDRs were in closed‑ecosystem testing and pilot phases with anticipated commercial launch timing linked to SOC 2 completion and evolving regulation; disclosures do not quantify any meaningful DDR revenue. DRT Cyber’s digital‑asset security offerings blur the line between financial and non‑financial services, but available evidence shows the crypto touchpoint mainly as bank‑issued, blockchain‑based deposit instruments rather than third‑party client services. ([versabank.com](https://www.versabank.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/2022-Q1-ConferenceCallTranscript.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Dec 9, 2024 (Q4 FY2024), Company IR PDF: “the world’s first digital deposit receipts… Our digital deposit receipts are on Algorand, Ethereum, and Stellar… SOC2 Type1 compliant using VersaVault.” — [IR transcript PDF](https://www.versabank.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/VersaBank-Fourth-Quarter-and-Fiscal-Year-2024-Financial-Results-Conference-Call-December-9-2024.pdf)  
- Mar 2, 2022 (Q1 FY2022), Company IR PDF: “completed… testing for… VCAD… remains on Ethereum, Algorand, and Stellar… preparing for commercial launch… following SOC2… audit… a significantly better stablecoin… a one‑for‑one representation of a fiat currency on deposit.” — [IR transcript PDF](https://www.versabank.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/2022-Q1-ConferenceCallTranscript.pdf)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Q3 FY2022 MD&A: notes completion of SOC 2 Type I audit of VersaVault “which underpins… Digital Deposit Receipts.” — [MD&A PDF](https://www.versabank.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/Q322MDA.pdf)  
- Mar 28, 2022 press release: announces successful completion of SOC 2 Type I audit for VersaVault supporting DDRs, including VCAD/VUSD on Stellar, Algorand and Ethereum. — [PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/versabank-announces-successful-completion-of-soc2-audit-ahead-of-anticipated-commercial-launch-of-revolutionary-digital-deposit-receipts-301511413.html)

**Financial news / other:**
- Feb 24, 2021: Coverage of VersaBank’s plan to launch bank‑issued, deposit‑backed digital currency (VCAD) in partnership with Stablecorp. — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/02/24/canadian-bank-launching-fiat-backed-digital-currency-in-claimed-world-first)  
- May 27, 2021 investor materials: VCAD highlighted as bank‑issued, deposit‑based digital currency; testing on Stellar and plans for Algorand. — [Company release (Q2 FY2021)](https://www.versabank.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/VBQ221ER.pdf)  
- Jun 1, 2022 (Q2 FY2022) call deck: “Revolutionary Digital Deposit Receipts (DDRs)… actual fiat currency on deposit with VersaBank.” — [Conference presentation](https://www.versabank.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/VersaBank-Q2-22-Conf.-Call-Pres-FINAL-2.pdf)

</small>

## GB GROUP

* Rank: 76 | Expo: 1.10 | id_str: GB0006870611
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings call transcripts (Nov 2024, Nov 2022), Annual Report 2024, Company press releases and product pages (2017–2024)

### Firm Overview
GB Group plc (GBG) is a UK‑based software company listed in London that provides digital identity verification, location intelligence and fraud prevention solutions to businesses globally. Its linkage to crypto arises from supplying KYC/AML, document, and biometric verification to cryptocurrency exchanges and related platforms as enterprise clients, rather than by holding or transacting in digital assets itself. ([gbgplc.com](https://www.gbgplc.com/media/fi4krtl1/fy24_annual_report_final.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management explicitly discussed exposure to crypto exchanges on earnings calls, noting that crypto‑exchange volumes were a headwind in FY2023 and that by H1 FY2025 revenue from “crypto exchanges” was around 1% of Group revenue. This is consistent with GBG’s role as an identity/KYC provider to exchanges and DeFi partners via acquired units such as IDology and Acuant. Marketing and partnership materials further show GBG positioning its verification stack for “crypto & FX” onboarding and integrations with blockchain/DeFi projects. These activities constitute non‑financial services sold into the crypto ecosystem (Bizserv), not custody, brokerage, issuance, or mining. ([gbgplc.com](https://www.gbgplc.com/media/jq4dl0wd/gbg_1h_fy23_presentation_trancript_291122.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Bizserv = 1.0 reflects that GBG’s crypto linkage is as an external software/compliance vendor to exchanges and digital‑asset platforms (KYC/AML, document and biometric checks). Earnings transcripts confirm crypto‑exchange customers as a distinct vertical with immaterial revenue contribution (~1% in H1 FY2025), and the 2024 annual report references prior “exceptional cryptocurrency volume” when adjusting FY2022 pro‑forma revenue. There is no evidence of GBG holding crypto on its balance sheet, running financial products that custody crypto, or operating mining/infrastructure. Accordingly, other mechanism weights are 0.0; RevShare is coded 1 (<5%) and AssetShare is 0. ([gbgplc.com](https://www.gbgplc.com/media/vtyhs3eq/gb-group-plc-fy25-half-year-results-transcript-19-11-2024.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The boundary between financial services and non‑financial services can blur for compliance tooling; however, GBG’s disclosures show it sells verification/compliance software rather than directly issuing, trading, or custodying digital assets, so Finserv is not assigned. Crypto exposure has been volatile over time (FY2022 “exceptional cryptocurrency volume” vs. ~1% in H1 FY2025), but within the 2017–2024 sample crypto revenue appears minor, supporting the chosen materiality codes. ([gbgplc.com](https://www.gbgplc.com/media/fi4krtl1/fy24_annual_report_final.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 19, 2024 (H1 FY2025): “We did not see any increase in revenue from crypto exchanges, with revenue from this sector remaining at around 1% of the total Group revenue.” — [Company IR transcript PDF] (https://www.gbgplc.com/media/vtyhs3eq/gb-group-plc-fy25-half-year-results-transcript-19-11-2024.pdf)  
- Nov 19, 2024 (H1 FY2025): “The driver behind that drop in NRR during calendar year 2022 was predominantly the reduction in volumes… These were crypto exchanges, fintech businesses…” — [Company IR transcript PDF] (https://www.gbgplc.com/media/vtyhs3eq/gb-group-plc-fy25-half-year-results-transcript-19-11-2024.pdf)  
- Nov 29, 2022 (H1 FY2023): “Activity volumes with customers involved in cryptocurrency trading decline[d] much more significantly and more abruptly than we had anticipated.” — [Company IR transcript PDF] (https://www.gbgplc.com/media/jq4dl0wd/gbg_1h_fy23_presentation_trancript_291122.pdf)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- FY2024 Annual Report: pro‑forma presentation “to remove… exceptional cryptocurrency volume that arose during the year to 31 March 2022.” — [Annual Report 2024 PDF] (https://www.gbgplc.com/media/fi4krtl1/fy24_annual_report_final.pdf)  
- Nov 18, 2021 press release (Acuant acquisition): Acuant “powers trust globally for industry leaders in finance, crypto…” — [GBG press release] (https://www.gbg.com/en/news/gbg-announces-it-has-agreed-to-acquire-acuant/)

**Financial news / other:**
- “Onboarding Crypto & FX Traders” page: step‑by‑step IDV/KYC process for crypto/FX platforms — [GBG product page] (https://www.gbg.com/en/identity-verification/onboarding-traders-crypto-fx/)  
- Mar 9, 2021: Acuant partners with Scalable Solutions (digital‑asset exchange provider) to streamline AML/KYC — [Cointelegraph PR] (https://cointelegraph.com/press-releases/acuant-and-scalable-solutions-partner-to-streamline-aml-kyc-compliance)  
- AllianceBlock x GBG: integration of GBG’s identity verification with a DeFi protocol — [GBG blog] (https://www.gbg.com/en/blog/allianceblock-closing-blockchains-kyc-gaps/)

</small>

## VISA 'A'

* Rank: 77 | Expo: 1.07 | id_str: US92826C8394
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings call transcripts (Q4 FY2021, Q1 FY2022, Q2 FY2023), Form 10‑K 2021, Visa press releases (2021, 2023), CNBC Jan 2022

### Firm Overview
Visa Inc. is a U.S.-based global payments network listed on the NYSE that enables digital payments among consumers, merchants, financial institutions and governments worldwide. Its crypto linkage comes from partnering with exchanges and wallets to issue Visa credentials and from piloting/expanding stablecoin (USDC) settlement within its treasury and settlement operations, rather than holding crypto as a treasury asset. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/27/visa-v-q4-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management has repeatedly discussed crypto as an emerging area: by FY2021 Visa had “nearly 60 crypto platform partners” issuing Visa credentials and captured over $3.5 billion of payment volume from crypto‑linked programs; in Q1 FY2022, crypto wallets on Visa generated $2.5 billion of payments volume in the quarter. Visa also launched a USDC settlement pilot with Crypto.com and Anchorage on Ethereum (2021) and in September 2023 expanded stablecoin settlement capabilities to merchant acquirers Worldpay and Nuvei, utilizing the Solana blockchain. These activities constitute direct crypto financial services (payments/settlement and on/off‑ramps), not mining, hardware infrastructure, or proprietary coin holdings. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/27/visa-v-q4-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 reflects that Visa’s measurable crypto exposure arises from enabling fiat on/off‑ramps for exchanges via Visa credentials, operating crypto‑linked card programs that drive fee income, and incorporating stablecoins (USDC) into settlement workflows with issuers/acquirers. Earnings transcripts, the FY2021 10‑K, and Visa press releases substantiate that exposure; there is no evidence that Visa holds crypto as a treasury investment, operates mining, or supplies mining/compute infrastructure, hence other categories are 0.0. Revenue and balance‑sheet materiality appear minor relative to Visa’s scale, so RevShare is coded 1 (<~5%) and AssetShare is 0. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/27/visa-v-q4-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Stablecoin settlement initiatives were in pilot/expansion phases during 2021–2023 and disclosures do not isolate a precise revenue share from crypto‑related programs; classification relies on qualitative descriptions and selected volume figures. Crypto volumes can be volatile, and management frames the space as “early days,” but the mechanism (payments/settlement) is clear and recurring within the sample period. ([s1.q4cdn.com](https://s1.q4cdn.com/050606653/files/doc_financials/2023/q2/CORRECTED-TRANSCRIPT-Visa-Inc-V-US-Q2-2023-Earnings-Call-25-April-2023-500-PM-ET.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 27, 2021 (Q4 FY2021): “We have nearly 60 crypto platform partners… capturing over $3.5B of payment volume in FY ’21.” — [Motley Fool] (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/27/visa-v-q4-2021-earnings-call-transcript/)  
- Jan 28, 2022 (Q1 FY2022): “There are over 65 crypto platforms… This quarter, Visa credentials and crypto wallets had more than $2.5 [billion] in payments volume.” — [Motley Fool] (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/01/28/visa-v-q1-2022-earnings-call-transcript/)  
- Apr 25, 2023 (Q2 FY2023): “It’s still early days… We’re enabling on and off ramps on crypto… and developing the capability… to settle in stable coins.” — [Company IR (FactSet transcript PDF)] (https://s1.q4cdn.com/050606653/files/doc_financials/2023/q2/CORRECTED-TRANSCRIPT-Visa-Inc-V-US-Q2-2023-Earnings-Call-25-April-2023-500-PM-ET.pdf)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- FY2021 Form 10‑K: “$3.5 billion in payments volume on crypto‑linked card programs… building infrastructure… including future settlement in digital currencies.” — [SEC filing] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1403161/000140316121000060/v-20210930.htm)  
- Mar 29, 2021: “Use of USD Coin (USDC)… to settle a transaction with Visa over Ethereum… pilot with Crypto.com and Anchorage.” — [Visa press release] (https://usa.visa.com/about-visa/newsroom/press-releases.releaseId.17821.html)  
- Sep 5, 2023: “Visa expands stablecoin settlement capabilities… adding pilots with merchant acquirers Worldpay and Nuvei, utilizing the Solana blockchain.” — [Visa press release] (https://usa.visa.com/about-visa/newsroom/press-releases.releaseId.19881.html)

**Financial news / other:**
- Jan 28, 2022: “Customers made $2.5B in payments with crypto‑linked cards in fiscal Q1 2022,” Visa CFO told CNBC. — [CNBC] (https://www.cnbc.com/2022/01/28/visa-says-crypto-linked-card-usage-hit-2point5-billion-in-its-first-quarter.html)  
- Feb 27, 2024: Coinbase to integrate Visa Direct for real‑time account funding; “Purchase crypto on Coinbase directly with an eligible Visa debit card.” — [Visa investor news] (https://investor.visa.com/news/news-details/2024/Coinbase-to-Integrate-Visa-Direct-to-Deliver-Real-Time-Account-Funding-for-Customers/default.aspx)

</small>

## MASTERCARD

* Rank: 78 | Expo: 1.06 | id_str: US57636Q1040
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.9, bizserv 0.1, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings call transcripts (Q3 2021, Q1 2022), 2023 Form 10‑K, Mastercard press releases (2021–2024), Bloomberg/Reuters Aug 2023

### Firm Overview
Mastercard Incorporated is a U.S.-based global payments technology company listed on the NYSE that operates a multi-rail network connecting issuers, acquirers, merchants, consumers and governments worldwide. Its crypto linkage arises from enabling card spending and cash‑outs from crypto wallets, partnering with infrastructure and custody providers, and developing standards and tooling for compliant blockchain interactions.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management described a dedicated crypto program in Q3 2021, noting new wallet/exchange partners and capabilities that let consumers buy crypto with Mastercard, spend converted balances at acceptance points, cash out via Mastercard Send, and even earn crypto/NFT rewards. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/28/mastercard-ma-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))  
In Q1 2022, management reiterated that Mastercard “is enabling consumers to spend their crypto holdings on card and cashing out their crypto wallets via Mastercard Send,” and that it engages central banks on CBDC design. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/04/28/mastercard-ma-q1-2022-earnings-call-transcript/))  
Mastercard launched Crypto Source in 2022 with Paxos to help banks offer crypto trading and custody through Mastercard‑integrated experiences, and introduced Mastercard Crypto Credential in 2023, which went live in 2024 with pilot P2P transfers to verify recipient aliases and wallet/asset compatibility. ([paxos.com](https://www.paxos.com/newsroom/mastercard-to-bring-crypto-trading-capabilities-to-banks?utm_source=openai))  
The company also acquired blockchain analytics firm CipherTrace in 2021 to bolster crypto risk, compliance and monitoring services sold to banks, exchanges and regulators. ([mastercard.com](https://www.mastercard.com/news/press/2021/september/mastercard-acquires-ciphertrace-to-enhance-crypto-capabilities?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 0.9 reflects Mastercard’s primary, repeatedly stated role of enabling crypto on‑ and off‑ramps, wallet cash‑outs, and card spending of converted crypto balances, as well as bank‑facing programs to support trading/custody via partners; these activities are also described in the company’s 2023 Form 10‑K (e.g., enabling purchase of cryptocurrencies/NFTs, spend of converted holdings, and CBDC/crypto participant services). There is no evidence of proprietary crypto mining or treasury HODL activity. Bizserv = 0.1 captures the CipherTrace analytics/compliance offering and related consulting around digital assets that support the ecosystem without directly issuing or custoding assets. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1141391/000114139124000022/ma-20231231.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Mastercard does not disclose a standalone revenue line for crypto‑related programs; the contribution appears minor relative to total revenue. Certain exchange partnerships have been adjusted over time (e.g., ending the Binance co‑branded card in 2023), but these changes do not alter the mechanism of exposure (payments enablement and services). No filings indicate material crypto assets on the balance sheet. ([bloomberg.com](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2023-08-24/mastercard-to-end-its-binance-co-branded-card-partnership?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 28, 2021 (Q3 2021): “We’re making it easier for crypto players to connect to our network… [Our] crypto program allows consumers to buy crypto with their Mastercard, spend their crypto balances wherever Mastercard is accepted, cash out with Mastercard Send, and earn rewards in crypto or even NFT.” — [Motley Fool] (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/28/mastercard-ma-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcript/)  
- Apr 28, 2022 (Q1 2022): “We are enabling consumers to spend their crypto holdings on card and cashing out their crypto wallets via Mastercard Send… [and] engaging with central banks as they design and develop CBDCs.” — [Motley Fool] (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/04/28/mastercard-ma-q1-2022-earnings-call-transcript/)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- 2023 Form 10‑K (filed 2024): Describes solutions “to support blockchain‑based digital currencies,” including helping consumers purchase cryptocurrencies/NFTs, spend converted holdings on Mastercard card offerings, and cash out crypto wallets via Mastercard Send; also outlines CBDC/crypto services. — [SEC filing] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1141391/000114139124000022/ma-20231231.htm)  
- Sep 9, 2021: Mastercard agrees to acquire CipherTrace, a crypto intelligence firm covering 900+ cryptocurrencies. — [Mastercard Newsroom] (https://www.mastercard.com/news/press/2021/september/mastercard-acquires-ciphertrace-to-enhance-crypto-capabilities)  
- Oct 17, 2022: Mastercard introduces Crypto Source with Paxos to enable banks to offer crypto trading/custody to customers. — [Paxos newsroom] (https://www.paxos.com/newsroom/mastercard-to-bring-crypto-trading-capabilities-to-banks)  
- Apr 28, 2023: Mastercard launches “Crypto Credential,” a common standard to bring more trust to blockchain interactions. — [Mastercard Newsroom] (https://www.mastercard.com/news/latin-america/en/newsroom/press-releases/pr-en/2023/april/mastercard-launches-mastercard-crypto-credential-bringing-more-trust-to-the-blockchain-ecosystem)  
- May 29, 2024: Crypto Credential pilot goes live with first P2P transactions and new partners. — [Mastercard Newsroom] (https://newsroom.mastercard.com/news/press/2024/may/mastercard-crypto-credential-goes-live-with-first-peer-to-peer-pilot-transactions-adds-new-partners-to-the-ecosystem/)

**Financial news / other:**
- Aug 24, 2023: Mastercard to end Binance co‑branded card partnership (programs in LATAM and Bahrain). — [Bloomberg] (https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2023-08-24/mastercard-to-end-its-binance-co-branded-card-partnership) | [Investing.com/Reuters] (https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/mastercard-binance-to-end-crypto-card-partnership-3160921)  
- Jan 18, 2022: Mastercard strikes NFT payments deal with Coinbase to enable card purchases on its NFT marketplace. — [CNBC] (https://www.cnbc.com/2022/01/18/mastercard-strikes-nft-payments-deal-with-coinbase-amid-wave-of-crypto-partnerships.html)  
- Jun 22, 2023: Mastercard expands Engage program to help crypto firms launch card programs. — [CoinDesk] (https://www.coindesk.com/business/2023/06/22/mastercard-focuses-its-engage-program-on-crypto)

</small>

## OXBRIDGE RE HOLDINGS

* Rank: 79 | Expo: 1.04 | id_str: KYG6856M1069
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2023), Q3 2024 earnings call, press releases (2023–2024)

### Firm Overview
Oxbridge Re Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands–based holding company whose subsidiaries write fully collateralized property catastrophe reinsurance and, since 2023, operate a Web3/tokenization initiative through SurancePlus Inc. The company’s 2023 Form 10‑K documents the launch of tokenized reinsurance securities (beginning with DeltaCat Re), issued on the Avalanche blockchain and structured as fractional interests in reinsurance contracts. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1584831/000149315224011313/form10-k.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The material crypto linkage is via SurancePlus, which develops and issues tokenized reinsurance securities that represent fractional interests in reinsurance contracts and are recorded on-chain (Avalanche). Management discussed this “RWA/Web3” expansion and the issuance of tokenized securities (DeltaCat Re in 2023 and the follow‑on EpsilonCat Re in 2024) on earnings calls, framing it as a diversification alongside the core reinsurance business. Public filings and press releases corroborate that SurancePlus completed a $2.45 million private offering of DeltaCat Re tokens in June 2023 and launched/raised for EpsilonCat Re in 2024. There is no evidence in the 2017–2024 window of proprietary crypto mining or a treasury HODL program. ([stockinsights.ai](https://www.stockinsights.ai/us/OXBR/earnings-transcript/fy24-q3-4d9b))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 reflects that Oxbridge Re’s crypto exposure arises from issuing and managing tokenized investment products (tokenized reinsurance securities) via SurancePlus, including disclosure of blockchain rails, offering mechanics, and investor return structures in the 2023 Form 10‑K and related 2024 filings/press releases. No credible sources indicate mining, hardware supply, or balance‑sheet crypto holdings within 2017–2024; thus other mechanism weights are 0.0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1584831/000149315224011313/form10-k.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Tokenization activity began only in 2023 and is still relatively small; management and filings indicate offering/management fees associated with SurancePlus but do not break out a standalone crypto revenue line, implying a minor contribution in the period. Coverage for small‑cap earnings calls can be fragmented, though at least one 2024 transcript with direct statements is accessible. Any 2025 announcements about crypto treasury management are outside the stated 2017–2024 sample and are not considered here. ([stockinsights.ai](https://www.stockinsights.ai/us/OXBR/earnings-transcript/fy24-q3-4d9b))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 14, 2024 (Q3 2024): “We have solid rein tranche in the RWA/Web3 space, where we issue tokenized securities as an RWA… Our core business, however, remains reinsurance.” — [StockInsights transcript] (https://www.stockinsights.ai/us/OXBR/earnings-transcript/fy24-q3-4d9b)  
- Nov 14, 2024 (Q3 2024): “In December 2022, we established SurancePlus… to tokenize securities… The initial DeltaCat Re was issued on the Avalanche blockchain… investors… received a 49.11% return… We believe these are the first tokenized reinsurance securities backed by a publicly traded company.” — [StockInsights transcript] (https://www.stockinsights.ai/us/OXBR/earnings-transcript/fy24-q3-4d9b)

**Filings (10‑K / 10‑Q / official):**
- 2023 Form 10‑K (filed Mar 26, 2024): Describes SurancePlus tokenized reinsurance securities, DeltaCat Re issuance on Avalanche, offering structure (Reg D/Reg S), rights, and intended series; includes risk factors specific to blockchain/tokenization. — [SEC filing] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1584831/000149315224011313/form10-k.htm)  
- Q3 2024 Form 10‑Q (filed Nov 2024): Notes EpsilonCat Re 2024 participation‑share tokenized offering and gross proceeds raised in July 2024. — [SEC filing] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1584831/000149315224030747/form10-q.htm)  
- Mar 18, 2024 Form 8‑K (Reg FD): Announces commencement of EpsilonCat Re tokenized participation‑share offering. — [MarketScreener (8‑K excerpt)] (https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/OXBRIDGE-RE-HOLDINGS-LIMI-16451852/news/Oxbridge-Re-Regulation-FD-Disclosure-Form-8-K-46221488/)

**Financial news / other:**
- Jun 28, 2023: SurancePlus closes $2.4M private raise of DeltaCat Re tokenized reinsurance securities. — [GlobeNewswire] (https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2023/06/28/2696192/0/en/Oxbridge-Re-s-Web3-Focused-Subsidiary-SurancePlus-Announces-Closing-of-its-Private-Offering-of-Tokenized-Reinsurance-Securities.html)  
- Aug 8, 2024: Company release notes successful EpsilonCat Re private placement and references 49% payout on 2023 DeltaCat Re. — [Boardroom Alpha (8‑K exhibit 99.1)] (https://sec.boardroomalpha.com/2024/QTR3/0001493152-24-030755/ex99-1.htm)  
- Industry coverage: DeltaCat Re tokenized reinsurance securities issued via Avalanche; details of offering and regulatory exemptions. — [Artemis.bm] (https://www.artemis.bm/news/oxbridge-re-raises-2-4m-for-sidecar-via-deltacat-re-tokenized-reinsurance-securities/)

</small>

## PAYSAFE

* Rank: 80 | Expo: 1.00 | id_str: BMG6964L2062
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 20-F/6-K filings (2021–2024), Q2 2021 earnings call, press releases (2021, 2024), Bloomberg/CoinDesk articles (2023)

### Firm Overview
Paysafe Ltd is a global payments platform listed in the U.S., operating digital wallets (Skrill and NETELLER), eCash solutions, and merchant processing across multiple regions. Crypto-related activity sits within its consumer wallets and partner integrations that enable customers and crypto businesses to buy, sell, and fund digital asset transactions.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s exposure is through crypto financial services: Skrill and NETELLER allow users to buy and sell cryptocurrencies, including via a white‑label liquidity integration with Coinbase for the U.S. in March 2021. Management has discussed adding dozens of supported coins, live integrations with multiple crypto exchanges, and bank partnerships to facilitate acquiring and wallet payments for exchanges. Paysafe has also provided on/off‑ramp capabilities for Web3 and exchanges via partnerships such as Alchemy Pay in 2024, while its prior role as a fiat partner to Binance in Europe/UK wound down in 2023. Filings clarify that cryptocurrency assets purchased on behalf of customers are not recognized on Paysafe’s balance sheet, underscoring an agency model rather than treasury holdings. ([d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net](https://d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net/_9b46327f4e43f198371808480d1d7139/paysafe/news/2021-03-25_Paysafe_s_Skrill_Expands_Crypto_Offering_to_US_18.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 reflects that Paysafe directly enables crypto transactions via its wallets and payments rails, provides fiat on‑ramps/off‑ramps to exchanges and Web3 companies, and has explicit exchange/banking integrations; there is no evidence of proprietary mining, crypto‑specific hardware supply, or balance‑sheet HODL. SEC filings state that crypto purchased for customers is not recorded as company assets, supporting AssetShare = 0, while transcript and product disclosures indicate crypto is a contributory but non‑dominant wallet revenue driver, supporting RevShare = 1. ([news.alphastreet.com](https://news.alphastreet.com/paysafe-limited-psfe-q2-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Public disclosures within 2017–2024 do not isolate a standalone crypto revenue line for wallets, so revenue materiality is inferred as minor from qualitative commentary. Exchange partnerships can change with regulation (e.g., the 2023 wind‑down with Binance), so the mix of crypto partners is dynamic even if the mechanism of exposure remains stable. ([bloomberg.com](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2023-06-29/binance-says-paysafe-to-cease-being-its-euro-payments-provider?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 16, 2021 (Q2 2021): “Since the first quarter, digital wallets added 22 cryptocurrencies… bringing the total to 37… and we are now live on 30 exchanges. Equally, we continue to extend payment processing capability to crypto and are now supporting eight exchanges.” — [AlphaStreet transcript] (https://news.alphastreet.com/paysafe-limited-psfe-q2-2021-earnings-call-transcript/)  
- Aug 16, 2021 (Q2 2021): “We… signed two new banks in Europe to facilitate acquiring and wallet payments for crypto exchanges.” — [AlphaStreet transcript] (https://news.alphastreet.com/paysafe-limited-psfe-q2-2021-earnings-call-transcript/)

**Filings (20‑F / 6‑K / regulatory):**
- Aug 13, 2024 Form 6‑K: “The Company purchases cryptocurrency assets on behalf of its customers… [and] does not recognize either the cryptocurrency asset or liability… on its balance sheet.” — [SEC/IR filing] (https://ir.paysafe.com/financial-info-and-filings/sec-filings/content/0000950170-24-096190/psfe-20240813.htm)  
- Apr 1, 2021 Form 20‑F: Notes wallet use cases including access to crypto exchanges; details regulatory framework for Skrill/NETELLER. — [SEC filing] (https://ir.paysafe.com/financial-info-and-filings/sec-filings/content/0001193125-21-104105/d159702d20f.htm)  
- 2021–2023 20‑F filings: Reference FCA/CBI crypto‑asset business registration for Skrill/NETELLER Cryptocurrency Services. — [SEC filings] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1833835/000095017022004762/psfe-20211231.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- Mar 25, 2021: “Skrill expands crypto offering to the U.S. with Coinbase (white‑label).” — [Business Wire release] (https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20210325005123/en/Paysafes-Skrill-Expands-Crypto-Offering-to-US-With-Coinbase)  
- Jul 25, 2024: Paysafe partners with Alchemy Pay to power fiat on‑ramp options (Skrill/NETELLER, open banking). — [Business Wire release] (https://d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net/_b38080833634c41c4f6ebda128db2870/paysafe/news/2024-07-25_Paysafe_Partners_with_Alchemy_Pay_to_Expand_Its_225.pdf)

## RF CAPITAL GROUP DEAD - DELIST.04/11/25

* Rank: 81 | Expo: 0.98 | id_str: CA74971G4016
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Annual report (FY2023), Q3 2024 earnings call, earnings releases (2023–2024), company articles (2024)

### Firm Overview
RF Capital Group Inc. was a Canadian wealth management company listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX: RCG) and the parent of Richardson Wealth, serving high-net-worth clients across Canada. Company disclosures and results releases position it as a pure-play wealth management platform focused on advisory, fee and commission revenue tied to client assets under administration. ([richardsonwealth.com](https://richardsonwealth.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/02/RF-Capital-Q4-2023-Earnings-Release.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Targeted review of 2017–2024 transcripts, filings, and investor materials found no substantive crypto operations: no exchange or brokerage of digital assets run by the firm, no crypto custody or product issuance, and no mining or balance‑sheet crypto holdings. Management commentary and investor communications during the period focus on advisor recruitment, client service, interest income on client cash, and AUA growth, not crypto activities. Advisor content on the firm’s website discusses Bitcoin ETFs in general market terms and frames crypto as speculative rather than a core offering, indicating any client exposure would be via third‑party listed products through normal brokerage channels. ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/stock/STU%3AG04/transcripts/2594468))

### Weight Assignment
Unclass = 1.0 reflects the absence of evidence tying RF Capital Group to any of the defined crypto‑exposure mechanisms within the 2017–2024 sample: no treasury crypto holdings (holders), no mining (miners), no direct crypto financial services such as exchange, custody or issued products (finserv), no identifiable non‑financial crypto services (bizserv), and no crypto‑directed hardware/hosting/energy supply (infrastr). Company filings and communications emphasize traditional wealth management revenue drivers and do not identify crypto-related segments or assets. ([richardsonwealth.com](https://richardsonwealth.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/2023-Annual-Report-Richardson-Wealth-RF-Capital-Group.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
As a wealth manager, individual advisors may allocate client assets to crypto‑themed ETFs available on public markets; however, this routing does not constitute the firm directly handling or issuing crypto products. No earnings‑call or filing references in 2017–2024 indicated corporate crypto activities, and advisor articles describing Bitcoin ETFs as speculative support the non‑core characterization. ([richardsonwealth.com](https://richardsonwealth.com/articles/its-clearly-not-an-investment-advisors-weigh-in-on-the-impact-of-us-bitcoin-etfs/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 8, 2024 (Q3 2024): “My overarching goal as CEO is to ensure our advisers feel valued and … have the products, services, technology and tools they need.” — [GuruFocus transcript] (https://www.gurufocus.com/stock/STU%3AG04/transcripts/2594468) ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/stock/STU%3AG04/transcripts/2594468))

**Filings (annual reports / releases):**
- Feb 29, 2024: FY2023 Annual Report and audited financial statements; business focused on Richardson Wealth advisory platform; no crypto operations disclosed. — [Annual report PDF] (https://richardsonwealth.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/2023-Annual-Report-Richardson-Wealth-RF-Capital-Group.pdf) ([richardsonwealth.com](https://richardsonwealth.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/2023-Annual-Report-Richardson-Wealth-RF-Capital-Group.pdf))  
- Nov 8, 2024: Q3 2024 earnings release describing the firm as a TSX-listed wealth management company operating under Richardson Wealth. — [Earnings release PDF] (https://richardsonwealth.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/RF-Capital-Q3-2024-Earnings-Release.pdf) ([richardsonwealth.com](https://richardsonwealth.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/RF-Capital-Q3-2024-Earnings-Release.pdf?utm_source=openai))  
- Feb 29, 2024: Q4/FY2023 results release outlining revenue drivers tied to AUA and wealth management; no crypto segment. — [Earnings release PDF] (https://richardsonwealth.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/02/RF-Capital-Q4-2023-Earnings-Release.pdf) ([richardsonwealth.com](https://richardsonwealth.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/02/RF-Capital-Q4-2023-Earnings-Release.pdf?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Jan 17, 2024: Richardson Wealth advisor commentary on US Bitcoin ETFs, characterizing crypto as speculative and not a core portfolio holding. — [Company article summary] (https://richardsonwealth.com/articles/its-clearly-not-an-investment-advisors-weigh-in-on-the-impact-of-us-bitcoin-etfs/) ([richardsonwealth.com](https://richardsonwealth.com/articles/its-clearly-not-an-investment-advisors-weigh-in-on-the-impact-of-us-bitcoin-etfs/))

## CME GROUP

* Rank: 82 | Expo: 0.97 | id_str: US12572Q1058
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Form 10-K (2023), Q1–Q2 2021 earnings calls, CME press releases (2017, 2022–2024), CoinDesk coverage (Nov 2023)

### Firm Overview
CME Group Inc. is a U.S.-based global derivatives marketplace operating exchanges including CME, CBOT, NYMEX and COMEX. Within its multi-asset offering, CME lists cash-settled cryptocurrency derivatives (notably Bitcoin and Ether futures and options) as a regulated venue serving institutional and professional clients.

### Exposure Channels Identified
CME directly provides crypto financial services by listing and clearing cash-settled Bitcoin and Ether futures and options and administering related reference rates/indices in partnership with CF Benchmarks; management repeatedly highlights these products in filings and calls. These activities generate exchange fee income tied to crypto derivatives volumes but do not involve custody of digital assets, mining, or holding tokens on the balance sheet. The 2023 Form 10-K explicitly cites Bitcoin futures volume records and product expansions including Micro Bitcoin/Ether options, Euro‑denominated BTC/ETH futures, and ETH/BTC ratio futures. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1156375/000115637524000010/cme-20231231.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 reflects CME’s role as the listing venue and clearer for regulated, cash‑settled crypto derivatives (Bitcoin and Ether futures/options and extensions), with revenue exposure via transaction and clearing fees. No evidence indicates company crypto treasury holdings (holders = 0.0), mining operations (miners = 0.0), non‑financial crypto services (bizserv = 0.0), or crypto‑directed hardware/hosting (infrastr = 0.0). This assignment is supported by multiple earnings‑call remarks on crypto product launches and volumes, 10‑K product disclosures, and company press releases. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/07/28/cme-group-inc-cme-q2-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
CME does not break out a standalone crypto revenue line in public filings; based on disclosures and scale of the broader franchise, crypto appears minor to low‑single‑digit share of total revenue during 2017–2024. Products are cash‑settled, so there is no balance‑sheet crypto asset exposure. External media periodically note shifts in market share versus offshore venues (e.g., Binance), but these dynamics do not alter the mechanism classification. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1156375/000115637524000010/cme-20231231.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Apr 28, 2021 (Q1 2021), Motley Fool: “there is definitely a lot of interest about the upcoming micro Bitcoin launch… in May.” — [The Motley Fool] (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/04/28/cme-group-inc-cme-q4-2020-earnings-call-transcript/)  
- Jul 28, 2021 (Q2 2021), Motley Fool: “our highly successful Micro Bitcoin contract… RPC of about $1.57… huge success” and “we’ve now grown a substantial [crypto] business.” — [The Motley Fool] (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/07/28/cme-group-inc-cme-q2-2021-earnings-call-transcript/)  
- Feb 10, 2021 (Q4 2020 call), Motley Fool: “we just launched our new Ether futures earlier this week… Bitcoin doing more than 11,000 contracts a day.” — [The Motley Fool] (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/02/10/cme-group-inc-cme-q4-2020-earnings-call-transcript/)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Feb 28, 2024: 2023 Form 10‑K — cites “Bitcoin futures” volume records; lists product launches incl. Micro Bitcoin and Ether options (2022), Euro‑denominated BTC/ETH futures (2022), Ether options (2022), Event contracts on Bitcoin futures (2023), and ETH/BTC ratio futures (2023). — [SEC EDGAR] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1156375/000115637524000010/cme-20231231.htm)  
- Dec 1, 2017: CME self‑certifies Bitcoin futures to launch Dec 18; cash‑settled to CME CF BRR. — [CME press release] (https://www.cmegroup.com/media-room/press-releases/2017/12/01/cme_group_self-certifiesbitcoinfuturestolaunchdec18.html)  
- Apr 17, 2023: CME to expand Bitcoin and Ether options expiries, citing growing client demand. — [CME press release] (https://investor.cmegroup.com/news-releases/news-release-details/cme-group-expand-bitcoin-and-ether-options-expiries-may-22)  
- Feb 2024: CME to launch Micro Euro‑denominated Bitcoin and Ether futures (Mar 18), noting increased micro‑contract demand. — [CME press release] (https://investor.cmegroup.com/news-releases/news-release-details/cme-group-launch-micro-euro-denominated-bitcoin-and-ether)

**Financial news / other:**
- Nov 10, 2023: CME “topples Binance” as top bitcoin futures venue by open interest amid U.S. spot ETF excitement. — [CoinDesk] (https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2023/11/10/cme-topples-binance-as-top-bitcoin-futures-exchange-as-crypto-etf-excitement-soars)  
- CME cryptocurrencies product/benchmarks overview (Bitcoin and Ether futures/options; CME CF reference rates). — [CME product page] (https://www.cmegroup.com/cryptocurrencies)

## NESTE

* Rank: 83 | Expo: 0.95 | id_str: FI0009013296
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0

* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Company press releases (2022), Annual Report release (2024)

### Firm Overview
Neste Oyj is a Finland-based energy company focused on refining renewable diesel and sustainable aviation fuel and developing circular feedstock solutions for polymers and chemicals. The company’s public materials emphasize renewable and circular solutions rather than digital-asset-related businesses.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Searches across 2017–2024 found no evidence that Neste holds cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet, operates mining, offers crypto trading/custody products, or supplies crypto-specific hardware, hosting, or energy infrastructure. Neste did participate in a blockchain traceability pilot with ISCC and Circularise and later invested in Circularise; however, these relate to supply-chain transparency software and certification processes, not crypto-asset exposure or services to the crypto ecosystem. ([neste.com](https://www.neste.com/en-sg/news/iscc-and-circularise-pilot-blockchain-technology-with-10-companies-including-neste-to-complement-mass-balance-certification))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of crypto treasury holdings, mining activities, financial services involving digital assets, or crypto-directed infrastructure, the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0. The identified blockchain items represent enterprise IT/traceability use and minority investment in a software startup, which do not meet the taxonomy thresholds for holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr exposure. ([neste.com](https://www.neste.com/en-sg/news/iscc-and-circularise-pilot-blockchain-technology-with-10-companies-including-neste-to-complement-mass-balance-certification))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Internal or partner-led blockchain traceability initiatives (ISCC/Circularise) are adjacent to, but distinct from, crypto-asset exposure under this framework. ([neste.com](https://www.neste.com/en-sg/news/iscc-and-circularise-pilot-blockchain-technology-with-10-companies-including-neste-to-complement-mass-balance-certification))  
- No earnings-call transcript passages referencing crypto were located for 2017–2024; public disclosures reviewed focus on renewable fuels and circular solutions. ([neste.com](https://www.neste.com/news/neste-has-published-its-annual-report-2023?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Filings (Annual report/news releases):**

**Financial news / other (company press/news):**

- Nov 17, 2022: Neste and partners invest €11 million in Circularise, a blockchain-based traceability startup. — [Neste press release](https://www.neste.com/news/neste-brightlands-venture-partners-4-impact-vc-and-asahi-kasei-invests-combined-eur-11-million-into-startup-circularise-to-accelerate-a-shift-towards-a-circular-economy)
