## LEONTEQ

* Rank: 84 | Expo: 0.95 | id_str: CH0190891181
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: Annual Reports (2018, 2021), H1 2021 earnings call, company press releases (2021–2024), industry media (Structured Retail Products, CoinDesk)

### Firm Overview
Leonteq AG is a Switzerland-based fintech company listed on SIX that designs, issues and distributes structured investment products and operates a white‑label platform for partner banks and insurers. Crypto-linked products form a subset of its participation and tracker offerings within its broader derivatives franchise.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Leonteq has issued crypto‑linked tracker and other structured certificates since 2017 and expanded the lineup over time, including short/mini‑futures and thematic crypto baskets; the firm reported CHF 17.4 million of revenues from products with crypto assets as underlyings in 2021. These products are hedged by holding the relevant digital assets as “trading inventories” measured at fair value less costs to sell, an accounting policy Leonteq detailed in its 2018 annual report. In August 2021, Leonteq also launched an expanded crypto‑asset offering in Germany and Austria via collaboration with ICF Bank. These activities constitute direct provision of crypto financial products and related hedging/custody, consistent with the finserv mechanism rather than holders or infrastr. ([leonteq.com](https://www.leonteq.com/investor-relations/fyr-2021/leonteq%2Bag-full%2Byear%2Breport-2021-en.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Classification is concentrated in finserv because Leonteq structures, issues and distributes crypto‑linked investment products, and holds crypto only to hedge those products (classified as trading inventories), not as balance‑sheet treasury investments. There is no evidence of proprietary crypto mining or crypto‑specific hardware/hosting provision. Accordingly: finserv 1.0, all other mechanism weights 0.0. ([leonteq.com](https://www.leonteq.com/reporting/2018/leonteq-ag-annual-report-2018-en.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Crypto revenue share fluctuates over time and was notably elevated in 2021; more recent disclosures emphasize broader product mix rather than crypto as a core driver. ([leonteq.com](https://www.leonteq.com/news-and-media/news/corporate-news/2024/leonteq-publishes-full-year-2023-results?utm_source=openai))  
- Leonteq’s temporary crypto holdings are operational hedges backing issued products; per taxonomy rules these do not trigger a holders weight. Evidence supports this treatment. ([leonteq.com](https://www.leonteq.com/reporting/2018/leonteq-ag-annual-report-2018-en.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jul 22, 2021 (H1 2021): “Leonteq has also been one of the first innovators in the cryptocurrency area… [and] recorded CHF9 million in revenues from this recently evolving asset class… first short tracker certificate on Bitcoin… in 2017.” — [GuruFocus transcript] (https://www.gurufocus.com/stock/FRA%3AEFL/transcripts/2372856)

**Filings (Annual reports / presentations):**
- 2018 Annual Report, Note 20: “Since 2017, Leonteq is issuing certificates that replicate the performance of cryptocurrencies… [crypto] held as ‘trading inventories’… measured at fair value less costs to sell.” — [Leonteq AR 2018] (https://www.leonteq.com/reporting/2018/leonteq-ag-annual-report-2018-en.pdf)  
- 2021 Annual Report: “continued to build out its market leading position for crypto assets in a securitised format… launched tracker certificates on a total of 25 single crypto assets… Revenues with crypto assets as underlyings grew… to CHF 17.4 million in 2021.” — [Leonteq AR 2021] (https://www.leonteq.com/investor-relations/fyr-2021/leonteq%2Bag-full%2Byear%2Breport-2021-en.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Aug 16, 2021: Leonteq launches crypto‑asset offering in Germany and Austria in collaboration with ICF BANK. — [Leonteq press release] (https://www.leonteq.com/news-and-media/news/corporate-news/2021/leonteq-launches-its-crypto-asset-offering-in-germany-and-austria?language_id=1)  
- Nov 17, 2017: Swiss firms Vontobel and Leonteq to offer Switzerland’s first mini futures to short bitcoin. — [CoinDesk] (https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2017/11/17/swiss-firms-to-let-traders-short-bitcoin-with-new-futures-products)  
- Sep 2021: “Crypto revenues… 15‑fold increase (CHF 9.2m) according to H1 2021 results; Leonteq a pioneer/provider of crypto assets in securitised format.” — [Structured Retail Products] (https://www.structuredretailproducts.com/insights/77607/cryptos-leonteq-launches-20-callable-rev-conv-expands-pool-of-cryptos)  
- Mar 2024: FY2023 results release notes diversification incl. adding crypto assets to AMC platform. — [Leonteq press release] (https://www.leonteq.com/news-and-media/news/corporate-news/2024/leonteq-publishes-full-year-2023-results)

## XINYUAN REAL ESTATE ADR 1:20

* Rank: 85 | Expo: 0.94 | id_str: US98417P2048
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.7, bizserv 0.3, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings call transcripts (Q4 2018, Q3 2019), company press releases (2019), industry press (2019–2020), SEC filings (Form 20‑F FY2020)

### Firm Overview
Xinyuan Real Estate Co., Ltd. is a China‑based residential property developer that listed American depositary shares on the NYSE under ticker XIN, with projects in multiple Chinese cities and select overseas developments. In 2019–2020 the company also explored blockchain applications and real‑estate tokenization alongside its core development activities.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management discussed piloting security token offerings (STOs) tied to company properties, culminating in “Oosten Digital Securities” being listed for secondary trading on the MERJ Exchange in 2020; these activities constitute direct crypto‑financial services (issuance/listing of digital securities). ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20200619005510/en/Oosten-Digital-Securities-Are-Now-Listed-on-MERJ-EXCHANGE-for-Secondary-Trading))  
Beyond tokenization, Xinyuan publicized a blockchain technology program (X‑Bolt/X‑Bolts) and applications such as Golden Bricks and Conbow, positioning itself as a blockchain technology service provider, which is best classified as non‑financial services to the ecosystem. ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/xinyuan-speeds-up-real-estate-innovation-with-blockchain-300959071.html))  
Press coverage in 2019 linked UPRETS, a digital security issuance platform funded by Xinyuan, to tokenizing units of the company’s New York “Oosten” project, reinforcing the finserv channel. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/XINYUAN-REAL-ESTATE-CO-LT-430743/news/UPRETS-Is-Tokenizing-the-Prestigious-Oosten-Property-in-Williamsburg-Brooklyn-29564082/))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv receives the dominant weight (0.7) because the clearest, executed exposure within 2017–2024 was the issuance and exchange listing of real‑estate‑backed digital securities via UPRETS/MERJ. Bizserv is assigned 0.3 reflecting Xinyuan’s parallel push to build and promote blockchain platforms and applications (BAAS/enterprise tools) that support real‑estate processes but do not involve custody or trading of crypto assets themselves. There is no evidence of proprietary crypto mining, hardware provision, or treasury HODL activity.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Management characterized the STO activity as a small‑scale trial in 2019 (about 10 units), suggesting limited commercial scale during the sample period. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/12/06/xinyuan-real-estate-co-ltd-xin-q3-2019-earnings-ca.aspx))  
- Public disclosures emphasize blockchain technology development but provide few quantified revenue figures; by taxonomy rules, holdings or collateral connected to issued digital securities are classified under finserv rather than holders. ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/xinyuan-speeds-up-real-estate-innovation-with-blockchain-300959071.html))  
- Later filings focus on core property operations and do not separately disclose crypto assets or material crypto revenue. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1398453/000114036122009911/brhc10035369_ex99-1.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 15, 2019 (Q4 2018), Motley Fool: “the application for blockchain technology in Xinyuan is now in the very early phase of exploration.” — [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/02/15/xinyuan-real-estate-co-ltd-xin-q4-2018-earnings-co.aspx)  
- Dec 6, 2019 (Q3 2019), Motley Fool: “we launched a trial… to securitize about 10 unit of our property… via… blockchain… STO.” — [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/12/06/xinyuan-real-estate-co-ltd-xin-q3-2019-earnings-ca.aspx)

**Filings (10‑K/20‑F / other):**
- Mar 15, 2022: Company announces filing of FY2020 Form 20‑F (business description; no crypto assets disclosed). — [SEC EX‑99.1](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1398453/000114036122009911/brhc10035369_ex99-1.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- Nov 15, 2019: “Xinyuan Speeds up Real Estate Innovation with Blockchain” (X‑Bolt platform; Golden Bricks; Conbow; patents). — [PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/xinyuan-speeds-up-real-estate-innovation-with-blockchain-300959071.html)  
- Nov 12, 2019: UPRETS to tokenize the Oosten property; UPRETS “funded by NYSE:XIN.” — [MarketScreener/Business Wire](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/XINYUAN-REAL-ESTATE-CO-LT-430743/news/UPRETS-Is-Tokenizing-the-Prestigious-Oosten-Property-in-Williamsburg-Brooklyn-29564082/)  
- Jun 19, 2020: “Oosten Digital Securities Are Now Listed on MERJ EXCHANGE for Secondary Trading” (MERJ listing; Xinyuan cited as the developer). — [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20200619005510/en/Oosten-Digital-Securities-Are-Now-Listed-on-MERJ-EXCHANGE-for-Secondary-Trading)

## SIGNATURE BANK

* Rank: 86 | Expo: 0.92 | id_str: US82669G1040
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 2 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings call transcripts (Q3 2021, Q2 2022), 2021 Annual Report, NYDFS internal review (2023), Businesswire press releases (2018–2020), major media (2022–2023)

### Firm Overview
Signature Bank was a New York–based commercial bank (ticker SBNY) serving private‑client businesses and specialty verticals. From 2018 onward it built a sizeable “digital asset banking” franchise anchored by Signet, a blockchain‑based real‑time USD payments platform; the bank was closed by New York regulators on March 12, 2023, within the sample window.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The bank’s crypto exposure came through providing fiat banking and payments to crypto firms and institutions via its Signet platform, which enabled 24/7/365 real‑time USD transfers between clients. Management repeatedly emphasized that the bank took U.S. dollar deposits from digital‑asset clients, used Signet for payments, and did not hold cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/07/19/signature-bank-sbny-q2-2022-earnings-call-transcri/))  
By 2020–2022, digital‑asset clients became a major source of deposit growth, with management disclosing large quarterly increases tied to the digital asset banking team and the Signet platform; the 2021 Annual Report highlighted $28.7 billion of digital‑related deposits at year‑end 2021. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/19/signature-bank-sbny-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcri/))  
Following industry turmoil in late 2022, the bank announced it would deliberately reduce crypto‑related deposits, and in March 2023 regulators closed Signature, widely described in the press as a key crypto‑industry bank, underscoring the salience of this channel during the period. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20221115006076/en/Signature-Bank-Provides-Digital-Asset-Banking-Update?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv is assigned 1.0 because Signature directly provided financial services to crypto clients: accepting and managing their U.S. dollar deposits and operating the Signet real‑time payments network. There is explicit evidence that the bank held no cryptocurrencies (so holders = 0.0), conducted no mining (miners = 0.0), and did not primarily supply hardware or energy infrastructure (infrastr = 0.0). The investor‑base amplifier is set to 1, as multiple outlets characterized Signature as a “crypto‑friendly” bank and its stock was sensitive to crypto‑sector news, indicating retail and institutional flows treated it as a crypto proxy. RevShare is coded 2 (~5–15%) to reflect meaningful but not dominant contribution from digital‑asset clients to net interest income via deposit funding; AssetShare is 0 because the bank stated it held no crypto assets. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/07/19/signature-bank-sbny-q2-2022-earnings-call-transcri/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The bank did not separately disclose a clean crypto‑revenue line; the RevShare code reflects inferred materiality from deposit mix rather than explicit revenue segmentation. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/19/signature-bank-sbny-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcri/))  
- Signature actively reduced digital‑asset deposits in late 2022, so exposure declined before the March 2023 resolution; however, within 2019–2022, the finserv channel was clearly material. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20221115006076/en/Signature-Bank-Provides-Digital-Asset-Banking-Update?utm_source=openai))  
- Post‑closure handling of Signet (under the FDIC bridge entity) falls outside ongoing operations of the listed firm but does not affect classification within the 2017–2024 sample. ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/zh/business/2023/03/14/signature-banks-signet-platform-still-works-but-some-clients-have-moved-on?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 19, 2021 (Q3 2021), Motley Fool: “This quarter’s growth was driven by the digital asset banking team, which grew deposits $5.1 billion…” — [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/19/signature-bank-sbny-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcri/)  
- Jul 19, 2022 (Q2 2022), Motley Fool: “We hold no cryptocurrencies… our clients use our payments platform, Signet, to transact in real time 24x7x365.” — [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/07/19/signature-bank-sbny-q2-2022-earnings-call-transcri/)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Mar 18, 2022: “Signet… blockchain‑based digital payments platform… Total digital‑related deposits reached $28.7 billion at year‑end 2021.” — [Annual Report 2021 (MarketScreener host)](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/SIGNATURE-BANK-10755/news/Signature-Bank-Annual-Report-2021-39806100/)  
- Dec 4, 2018: NYDFS approval for blockchain real‑time payments system (Signet) — [CoinDesk summary linking NYDFS](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2018/12/04/signature-bank-wins-new-york-approval-for-real-time-blockchain-payments)  
- Jun 23, 2020: “Signet… the first platform of its kind to be approved for use by NYDFS, enables instantaneous payments among Signature Bank clients.” — [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20200623005138/en/Signature-Bank-Launches-Its-Digital-Payments-Platform-Signet-on-the-Fireblocks-Network)

**Financial news / other:**
- Nov 15, 2022: Update after FTX — “deposit relationship with FTX… less than 0.1%,” and plan to reduce digital‑asset deposits — [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20221115006076/en/Signature-Bank-Provides-Digital-Asset-Banking-Update)  
- Dec 21, 2022: S&P Global: Signature to cut crypto‑related deposits to $8–10B; est. $24.67B tied to digital‑asset customers as of Sept. 30, 2022 — [S&P Global MI](https://www.spglobal.com/market-intelligence/en/news-insights/articles/2022/12/banks-active-in-digital-assets-challenged-by-crypto-related-deposit-outflows-73510737)  
- Mar 13, 2023: “Few banks were as deeply embedded within the cryptocurrency firms as Signature Bank and Silvergate.” — [Washington Post](https://www.washingtonpost.com/technology/2023/03/13/signature-bank-collapse-crypto/)  
- Mar 10, 2023: “Crypto bank Signature [shares] slide…” — [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2023/03/10/crypto-bank-signature-slides-on-friday-amid-troubles-at-silicon-valley-bank-silvergate.html)  
- Apr 28, 2023: NYDFS internal review: digital‑asset deposits were the bank’s largest growth segment by 2020; details on growth and Signet — [NYDFS report (PDF)](https://www.dfs.ny.gov/system/files/documents/2023/04/nydfs_internal_review_rpt_signature_bank_20230428.pdf)

</small>

## BLACK HILLS

* Rank: 87 | Expo: 0.90 | id_str: US0921131092
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 1.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2024), Q1–Q3 2023 earnings calls, Q3 2024 earnings call, company press releases (2022), financial news (2022)

### Firm Overview
Black Hills Corporation is a U.S. regulated electric and natural gas utility based in Rapid City, South Dakota, with operations across multiple Midwestern and Mountain states. Its crypto exposure arises from supplying electricity to blockchain/bitcoin mining customers in Wyoming under a dedicated interruptible tariff and related large‑load programs. ([minedocs.com](https://minedocs.com/29/Black-Hill-Form-10K-2024.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management repeatedly described “data centers” and “blockchain” as capital‑light growth areas, citing a blockchain mining customer in Cheyenne that ramped power intake and expanded to 75 MW by late 2023. The 2024 Form 10‑K defines a Wyoming Blockchain Interruptible Service (BCIS) tariff and discloses agreements with two customers totaling up to 130 MW under this tariff. The company also noted serving “data center and blockchain load growth” on its Q3 2024 call. There is no evidence that Black Hills itself mines crypto, holds digital assets, or provides custody/exchange services. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/BKH/documents/transcripts/q1-2023))

### Weight Assignment
Infrastr = 1.0 is assigned because the documented crypto linkage is utility provision of electric power and related interconnection/dispatch arrangements purpose‑built for blockchain mining loads (BCIS tariff and large‑load service), not financial intermediation or asset holding. No transcripts or filings indicate proprietary crypto holdings, mining by the company, or financial services to crypto clients, so all other mechanism weights are 0.0. ([minedocs.com](https://minedocs.com/29/Black-Hill-Form-10K-2024.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The company does not break out a distinct “crypto” revenue line; RevShare = 1 reflects that blockchain‑related sales appear meaningful yet likely <~5% of consolidated revenue during 2017–2024. ([minedocs.com](https://minedocs.com/29/Black-Hill-Form-10K-2024.pdf))  
- Some “data center” load discussed by management is unrelated to crypto (e.g., hyperscale/AI and Meta), so transcript mentions of “data centers and blockchain” combine crypto and non‑crypto drivers. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-black-hills-corporation-reports-q3-2024-financial-results-93CH-3711257))  
- Load served under the BCIS tariff is interruptible and can be curtailed, which may dampen earnings sensitivity in stress scenarios. ([minedocs.com](https://minedocs.com/29/Black-Hill-Form-10K-2024.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 4, 2023 (Q1 2023): “Data centers and blockchain are capital‑light projects that provide recurring earning streams… our first blockchain customer is in service and continues to ramp up their energy intake.” — [Fintool](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/BKH/documents/transcripts/q1-2023)  
- Aug 3, 2023 (Q2 2023): “We have growth when we think about our capital light initiatives, which… we refer to as our data centers, our blockchain.” — [Fintool](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/BKH/documents/transcripts/q2-2023)  
- Nov 2, 2023 (Q3 2023): “Conditions that are ideal for data centers… and blockchain and crypto mining… our blockchain customer has… an additional 30 MW… total of 75 MW.” — [Fintool](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/BKH/documents/transcripts/q3-2023)  
- Nov 8, 2024 (Q3 2024): “Slide 6 provides a summary of our hyperscale data center and blockchain growth opportunities… [we are] serving data center and blockchain load growth.” — [Investing.com transcript](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-black-hills-corporation-reports-q3-2024-financial-results-93CH-3711257)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Feb 12, 2025 filing (FY2024 10‑K): Defines the “Blockchain Interruptible Service (BCIS) Tariff” for Wyoming; “agreements with two customers to provide up to 130 MWs in Cheyenne, Wyoming under this Tariff.” — [Black Hills 2024 Form 10‑K (PDF)](https://minedocs.com/29/Black-Hill-Form-10K-2024.pdf)  
- Jun 21, 2022: “Serve first customer under… Blockchain Interruptible Service Tariff.” — [Black Hills Energy press release](https://www.blackhillsenergy.com/news/black-hills-energy-to-serve-first-customer-under-new-wyoming-blockchain-interruptible-service-tariff)

**Financial news / other:**
- Jun 21, 2022: “Publicly traded utility… inks deal to power bitcoin mining in Wyoming” (first BCIS customer agreement). — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2022/06/21/publicly-traded-utility-company-inks-deal-to-power-bitcoin-mining-in-wyoming)  
- Apr 2024: “CleanSpark executes 75 MW of power contracts and completes acquisition of bitcoin mining site in Wyoming.” — [PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/cleanspark-executes-75-mw-of-power-contracts-and-completes-acquisition-of-bitcoin-mining-site-in-wyoming-302212200.html)  
- Resource page: “Black Hills Energy’s commitment to supporting the blockchain industry… established a new electric tariff to support this industry.” — [BHE Data Centers](https://datacenters.blackhillsenergy.com/resources/black-hills-energys-commitment-supporting-blockchain-industry)

</small>

## MONEYGRAM INTL. DEAD - DELIST.02/06/23

* Rank: 88 | Expo: 0.90 | id_str: US60935Y2081
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings call transcript (Q3 2019), SEC filings (2019–2022), press releases (2019–2022), financial news (2019–2023)

### Firm Overview
MoneyGram International was a U.S.-based global money transfer and cross‑border payments company listed on Nasdaq under ticker MGI until its acquisition by private equity and delisting on February 6, 2023. During 2017–2024, its crypto‑related activity centered on blockchain‑enabled settlement for treasury/FX and retail crypto on/off‑ramp and trading features.

### Exposure Channels Identified
- In 2019, management described using Ripple’s on‑demand liquidity (ODL) product and XRP for a portion of daily FX funding needs (e.g., Mexico) and characterized the Ripple partnership as enabling near real‑time settlement; this was discussed explicitly on the Q3 2019 earnings call. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/11/01/moneygram-international-inc-mgi-q3-2019-earnings-c.aspx))  
- MoneyGram suspended ODL activity in December 2020 amid the SEC’s action against Ripple and formally wound down the partnership in March 2021; company filings noted no Ripple‑related fee benefits in 2021. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1273931/000127393121000021/mgi-20201231.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- Separately, in 2022 MoneyGram launched a global crypto‑to‑cash service on the Stellar network using USDC and added in‑app buy/sell/hold of BTC, ETH and LTC in the U.S. via its Coinme integration, positioning the firm as a fiat–crypto on/off‑ramp. ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news/the-stellar-development-foundation?utm_source=openai))  
- The stock was treated by markets as a crypto proxy in 2019, surging on news of Ripple’s equity investment and commercial tie‑up. ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2019/06/18/blockchain-startup-ripple-buys-30-million-stake-in-moneygram.html?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 is assigned because the firm’s crypto linkage was financial intermediation: use of a blockchain‑based liquidity/settlement product (Ripple ODL) for FX treasury flows and, later, consumer crypto services (buy/sell/hold and cash‑in/cash‑out of stablecoins). There is no evidence of proprietary coin mining or balance‑sheet HODL; SEC materials indicate XRP received under the Ripple agreement was treated as vendor consideration and activity ceased in early December 2020, with no related fees in 2021. Thus, holders, miners, bizserv, and infrastr remain 0.0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1273931/000127393121000021/mgi-20201231.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Reported “market development fees” from Ripple in 2019–2020 reduced expenses rather than constituting a distinct revenue line; RevShare = 1 reflects that crypto‑linked P&L impact appears minor relative to consolidated results. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1273931/000119312522085200/d323273ddef14a.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- The company was taken private and delisted on February 6, 2023; disclosures after that date rely on press and partner releases rather than 10‑K/10‑Q filings. ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news/the-stellar-development-foundation?utm_source=openai))  
- Some Stellar/USDC wallet and on/off‑ramp integrations rolled out across regions and partners over time; precise contribution to revenue is not broken out. ([resources.stellar.org](https://resources.stellar.org/hubfs/Q2%202022_Quarterly%20Report.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 1, 2019 (Q3 2019): “We’ve integrated Ripple onto our standard treasury process… continued to increase our usage of Ripple’s on‑demand liquidity product… driving about 10% of our daily Mexican peso volume through this technology.” — [Motley Fool transcript]([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/11/01/moneygram-international-inc-mgi-q3-2019-earnings-c.aspx))

**Filings (10‑K / 8‑K / proxy):**
- Jun 17, 2019: 8‑K detailing Ripple’s $30M equity investment and commercial agreement. — [SEC EDGAR]([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1273931/000119312519174813/d766773d8k.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- 2019 10‑K: Describes ODL/XRP use under the Ripple agreement. — [Annual Report PDF]([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/m/NYSE_MGI_2019.pdf?utm_source=openai))  
- 2020 10‑K (iXBRL): “Ceased transacting with Ripple under the commercial agreement in early December 2020.” — [SEC EDGAR]([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1273931/000127393121000021/mgi-20201231.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- Mar 2022 DEF 14A: XRP “market development fees” treated as vendor consideration; no such fees in 2021. — [SEC EDGAR]([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1273931/000119312522085200/d323273ddef14a.htm?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Mar 9, 2021: Ripple and MoneyGram agree to end their partnership. — [Ripple press statement]([ripple.com](https://ripple.com/ripple-press/our-statement-on-partnering-with-moneygram/?utm_source=openai))  
- Jun 10, 2022: “MoneyGram launches pioneering global crypto‑to‑cash service on the Stellar Network.” — [PR Newswire]([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news/the-stellar-development-foundation?utm_source=openai))  
- Nov 1, 2022: “MoneyGram debuts crypto purchases on mobile app” (via Coinme). — [CoinDesk]([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2022/11/01/moneygram-debuts-crypto-purchases-on-mobile-app?utm_source=openai))  
- Jun 18, 2019: Shares soar triple‑digits after Ripple deal and investment details. — [Bloomberg]([bloomberg.com](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2019-06-18/it-feels-like-2017-as-moneygram-soars-176-on-ripple-investment?utm_source=openai))  
- Jun 18, 2019: “Ripple buys $30 million stake in MoneyGram; shares surge.” — [CNBC]([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2019/06/18/blockchain-startup-ripple-buys-30-million-stake-in-moneygram.html?utm_source=openai))

</small>

## NVIDIA

* Rank: 89 | Expo: 0.89 | id_str: US67066G1040
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 1.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (FY2022–FY2023), Q1 FY2019 and Q4 FY2021 earnings calls, Q2 FY2022 earnings call/CFO commentary, SEC press release (May 6, 2022)

### Firm Overview
NVIDIA is a U.S.-based semiconductor company listed on Nasdaq under ticker NVDA that designs GPUs and accelerated computing platforms for gaming, data centers, and professional visualization. During 2017–2024, its crypto link was through supplying GPUs widely used for proof‑of‑work mining and, in 2021, a dedicated Cryptocurrency Mining Processor (CMP) product line.

### Exposure Channels Identified
- Management acknowledged significant demand from cryptocurrency miners for its GPUs during the 2017–2018 boom and later created mining‑specific products (CMP) to serve miners directly and protect gaming supply. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/05/16/nvidia-corporation-nvda-q1-2019-earnings-conferenc.aspx))  
- Filings disclose CMP revenue of about $550 million in fiscal year 2022, with OEM & Other growth “primarily driven by CMP,” and note that gaming trends were also influenced by the ability of end users to mine cryptocurrencies. CMP subsequently fell to nominal levels in fiscal 2023. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1045810/000104581022000036/nvda-20220130.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- NVIDIA settled SEC charges in May 2022 regarding inadequate disclosure of the cryptomining impact on its gaming revenue in fiscal 2018, underscoring material historical sensitivity to mining demand. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/newsroom/press-releases/2022-79?utm_source=openai))  
- Financial media repeatedly treated the stock as a crypto proxy during 2018’s “crypto hangover,” with notable share moves tied to mining demand dynamics. ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2018/11/16/nvidia-nurses-crypto-hangover-as-demand-for-mining-chips-evaporates.html?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Infrastr = 1.0 is assigned because NVIDIA’s exposure was through supplying hardware used for crypto mining, including purpose‑built CMP products — squarely a crypto infrastructure role. There is no evidence that NVIDIA mined coins, held crypto on its balance sheet, or provided financial intermediation, so miners, holders, finserv, and bizserv remain 0.0. Given frequent market portrayal of NVDA as a crypto proxy in 2017–2018 and management/filing discussions of mining‑driven demand swings, invbase = 1. Filings show CMP was a small share of consolidated revenue (roughly low‑single‑digit percent in FY2022), so RevShare = 1 and AssetShare = 0. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/02/25/nvidia-corp-nvda-q4-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- NVIDIA has consistently stated limited visibility into how much general‑purpose GPU demand is driven by mining, and CMP revenue quickly became nominal after FY2022; crypto‑related revenue shares are therefore approximate. ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2021/11/17/nvidia-crypto-mining-chip-sales-dropped-60percent-sequentially-in-q3.html?utm_source=openai))  
- From 2023 onward, AI/data‑center demand dwarfed any crypto effect; however, the 2017–2021 period provides clear evidence of crypto‑mining‑driven sensitivity. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1045810/000104581023000175/nvda-20230730.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 16, 2018 (Q1 FY2019): “Cryptocurrency demand was, again, stronger than expected… we were able to fulfill most of it with crypto‑specific GPUs… included in our OEM business at $289 million.” — [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/05/16/nvidia-corporation-nvda-q1-2019-earnings-conferenc.aspx) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/05/16/nvidia-corporation-nvda-q1-2019-earnings-conferenc.aspx))  
- Feb 25, 2021 (Q4 FY2021): “We developed CMP for this very reason… we can sell it directly… to the industrial miners… [it will be] a small part of our business.” — [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/02/25/nvidia-corp-nvda-q4-2021-earnings-call-transcript/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/02/25/nvidia-corp-nvda-q4-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))  
- Aug 18, 2021 (Q2 FY2022): “CMP revenue… was $266 million… and we expect a minimal contribution from CMP going forward.” — [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/08/18/nvidia-corporation-nvda-q2-2022-earnings-call-tran/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/08/18/nvidia-corporation-nvda-q2-2022-earnings-call-tran/?utm_source=openai))

**Filings (10‑K / 10‑Q / CFO commentary):**
- FY2022 Form 10‑K: “OEM and Other revenue was up 84%… primarily driven by CMP sales. CMP revenue was $550 million… Gaming revenue… influenced by… ability of end users to engage in cryptocurrency mining.” — [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1045810/000104581022000036/nvda-20220130.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1045810/000104581022000036/nvda-20220130.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- Q4 FY2022 CFO commentary: “CMP revenue was $24 million in the quarter… $550 million for the fiscal year.” — [SEC (Investor site link)](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1045810/000104581022000008/q4fy22cfocommentary.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1045810/000104581022000008/q4fy22cfocommentary.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- Q2 FY2023 Form 10‑Q: “CMP contributed an insignificant amount… compared to $266 million in [Q2 FY2022].” — [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1045810/000104581022000147/nvda-20220731.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1045810/000104581022000147/nvda-20220731.htm?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- May 6, 2022: SEC press release on settlement over inadequate cryptomining disclosures (FY2018). — [SEC Press Release 2022‑79](https://www.sec.gov/newsroom/press-releases/2022-79) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/newsroom/press-releases/2022-79?utm_source=openai))  
- Nov 16, 2018: “Nvidia nurses ‘crypto hangover’ as demand for mining chips evaporates.” — [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2018/11/16/nvidia-nurses-crypto-hangover-as-demand-for-mining-chips-evaporates.html) ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2018/11/16/nvidia-nurses-crypto-hangover-as-demand-for-mining-chips-evaporates.html?utm_source=openai))  
- Nov 15, 2018: “Shares plunge… lingering loss of demand from the collapse of cryptocurrency mining.” — [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2018-11-15/nvidia-gives-weak-forecast-citing-inventory-stock-slumps) ([bloomberg.com](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2018-11-15/nvidia-gives-weak-forecast-citing-inventory-stock-slumps?utm_source=openai))  
- Jan 2025: Supreme Court allows investor class action over crypto‑mining dependence to proceed. — [AP News](https://apnews.com/article/bd509b33f4b4b5c72101545f6996a0a2) ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/bd509b33f4b4b5c72101545f6996a0a2?utm_source=openai))

</small>

## IG GROUP HOLDINGS

* Rank: 90 | Expo: 0.88 | id_str: GB00B06QFB75
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: Annual reports (2018–2019, 2024), H1 2020 earnings call, financial/regulatory news (2018, 2020–2021)

### Firm Overview
IG Group Holdings plc is a UK‑based online trading provider focused on spread betting, CFDs and exchange‑traded derivatives across global markets, with additional offerings in the US via its tastytrade platform. Within this model, client access to crypto markets has been provided through crypto‑linked derivatives and, in the US, spot crypto executed via a third‑party infrastructure provider.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s crypto exposure is through financial intermediation: it offered crypto‑related spread bets/CFDs (particularly active in FY2018) and later, via tastytrade in the US, enabled spot crypto trading through Zero Hash infrastructure rather than self‑custody. In FY2018, management disclosed that client trading in cryptocurrencies contributed to revenue and that the group recognized crypto assets on balance sheet to support hedging of client positions, indicating operational holdings backing products rather than treasury investment. Following the UK FCA’s ban effective January 6, 2021, IG ceased offering crypto derivatives to UK retail clients, though professional and certain non‑UK offerings continued; the US spot channel persisted via an associate (Zero Hash). These facts place IG squarely in the finserv category, with no evidence of proprietary mining, treasury HODL, or crypto‑specific infrastructure sales. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/i/LSE_IGG_2018.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 is assigned because IG directly intermediates client crypto exposure (derivatives and, in the US, spot via an external custodian/processor), and its on‑balance‑sheet crypto appears solely for hedging associated with these products. Management described crypto as a small revenue contributor by H1 2020, and earlier disclosures indicate the notable but time‑bound spike in FY2018; there is no indication of balance‑sheet HODL, mining, or infrastructure provision. Consequently, holders, miners, bizserv, and infrastr are 0.0. ([bulletin.webull.com](https://bulletin.webull.com/20200121/150376959/5169026bb00a439dcf43e413993450d4.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Regional availability and product mix changed over time (e.g., UK retail ban effective January 6, 2021), so revenue sensitivity to crypto varied across periods and jurisdictions. ([fca.org.uk](https://www.fca.org.uk/news/press-releases/fca-bans-sale-crypto-derivatives-retail-consumers?utm_source=openai))  
- Publicly accessible transcripts are limited; classification relies on one clear earnings‑call quote plus detailed annual‑report disclosures.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- January 21, 2020 (H1 FY2020): “Yes. Cryptos is small numbers still… we’re really not seeing that as a driver in any material way.” — [Thomson Reuters StreetEvents (Webull PDF)](https://bulletin.webull.com/20200121/150376959/5169026bb00a439dcf43e413993450d4.pdf) ([bulletin.webull.com](https://bulletin.webull.com/20200121/150376959/5169026bb00a439dcf43e413993450d4.pdf))

- FY2019 Annual Report: “Other assets are cryptocurrencies and rights to cryptocurrencies… for the purpose of hedging the Group’s exposure to client cryptocurrency trading positions.” — [IG Group Annual Report 2019, Note 15](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/i/LSE_IGG_2019.pdf) ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/i/LSE_IGG_2019.pdf))

- FY2022 Annual Report: records a fair value gain on revaluation of Zero Hash and notes the disposal of Small Exchange; confirms IG’s minority investment exposure to crypto infrastructure supporting tastytrade’s crypto offering. — [IG Group Annual Report 2022](https://www.iggroup.com/~/media/Files/I/IG-Group/newsroom/press-releases/2022/ig-group-ar-2022.pdf)
- FY2019 Annual Report: “Other assets are cryptocurrencies and rights to cryptocurrencies… for the purpose of hedging the Group’s exposure to client cryptocurrency trading positions.” (£33.1m at 31 May 2019; £27.2m at 31 May 2018). — [IG Group Annual Report 2019](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/i/LSE_IGG_2019.pdf)
- FY2018 Annual Report: “Client trading in cryptocurrencies accounted for 7% of OTC leveraged revenue in the 2018 financial year.” and “Revenue from foreign exchange… includes £36.5 million from cryptocurrencies.” — [IG Group Annual Report 2018](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/i/LSE_IGG_2018.pdf)
- FY2025 Annual Report: Accounting policy clarifies “Other assets represent cryptocurrency assets and rights to cryptocurrency assets… [held] as part of… hedging”; also discloses 31 May 2024 balance of £36.6m and notes customers contract directly with a third‑party custodian for crypto custody. — [IG Group Annual Report 2025](https://www.iggroup.com/~/media/Files/I/IG-Group/documents/investors/financial-results/results-reports-and-presentations/2025/annual-report-2025.pdf)

## BROADRIDGE FINL.SLTN.

* Rank: 91 | Expo: 0.86 | id_str: US11133T1034
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2020), Q2 2019 and Q4 2021 earnings calls, trade press and press releases (2020–2021)

### Firm Overview
Broadridge Financial Solutions is a U.S.-based fintech that provides investor communications, capital markets post-trade processing, and wealth management technology to global financial institutions. During the 2017–2024 period it expanded into distributed-ledger initiatives and, following the 2021 acquisition of Itiviti, offered trading/connectivity solutions that include institutional access to cryptocurrency venues.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company provides non-financial technology and connectivity used by digital-asset firms, rather than directly intermediating or custoding cryptocurrencies. Its 2019 acquisition of Shadow Financial added exchange and post-trade capabilities that explicitly include cryptocurrency; Itiviti/NYFIX subsequently enabled institutional connectivity and trading access across major crypto exchanges, including partnerships with Eqonex and Gemini. Management also discussed launching its Distributed Ledger Repo (DLR) platform and broader blockchain work in earnings calls, but DLR addresses repo markets and does not itself handle cryptocurrencies. There is no evidence of proprietary coin holdings, mining, or Broadridge-branded crypto brokerage/custody. ([broadridge-ir.com](https://www.broadridge-ir.com/files/doc_financials/2020/q4/q4-2020.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Bizserv = 1.0 is assigned because Broadridge’s exposure arises from providing software, connectivity and market infrastructure used by crypto market participants (e.g., NYFIX FIX support for crypto, liquidity aggregation and exchange access, and Shadow Financial’s crypto-capable post-trade stack). These activities serve the crypto ecosystem without Broadridge taking asset custody, issuing crypto products, running an exchange, mining, or holding crypto on its balance sheet. Consequently, holders, miners, finserv, and infrastr are 0.0. ([waterstechnology.com](https://www.waterstechnology.com/awards-rankings/7894411/bst-awards-2021-best-low-latency-trading-network-itiviti-a-broadridge-business))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Earnings calls in 2019–2021 emphasize blockchain/DLT (e.g., DLR) more than cryptocurrencies; DLR targets traditional repo markets and is not itself a crypto service. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/08/12/broadridge-financial-solutions-inc-br-q4-2021-earn/))  
- Public sources do not break out crypto-related revenue; available disclosures imply minimal financial materiality within the period. ([broadridge-ir.com](https://www.broadridge-ir.com/files/doc_financials/2020/q4/q4-2020.pdf))  
- Some connectivity evidence comes via Itiviti partnerships announced before Broadridge closed the 2021 acquisition, but subsequent trade-press coverage confirms the crypto access under Broadridge’s umbrella. ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/itiviti-and-gemini-to-offer-nyfix-connectivity-for-cryptocurrency-customers-301026869.html?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- February 8, 2019 (Q2 FY2019): “We intend to… be the on-ramp to new technologies… across artificial intelligence, blockchain… Speaking of blockchain, … the first blockchain proof of concept on proxy voting in Japan last month.” — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/02/08/b
… — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/02/08/broadridge-financial-solutions-br-q2-2019-earnings.aspx)
- August 12, 2021 (Q4 FY2021): “We also recently launched our Digital Ledger Repo platform and are averaging $35 billion worth of transactions daily… While both of these products are small today, each is bringing an innovative and differentiated solution to a multi-billion dollar market.” — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/08/12/broadridge-financial-solutions-inc-br-q4-2021-earn/)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Aug 2020 Form 10‑K: “In addition, the acquisition [of Shadow Financial] adds capabilities across exchange traded derivatives and cryptocurrency.” — [Broadridge IR (FY2020 10‑K PDF)](https://www.broadridge-ir.com/files/doc_financials/2020/q4/q4-2020.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Nov 5, 2021: “NYFIX has added FIX support for crypto… providing liquidity aggregation and trading access to all major crypto exchanges/venues through its partnership with Eqonex. It will continue to develop its crypto and digital assets offering.” — [WatersTechnology](https://www.waterstechnology.com/awards-rankings/7894411/bst-awards-2021-best-low-latency-trading-network-itiviti-a-broadridge-business)
- Mar 19, 2020: Itiviti and Gemini “to offer NYFIX connectivity for cryptocurrency customers,” enabling institutional access to the Gemini exchange via NYFIX. — [PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/itiviti-and-gemini-to-offer-nyfix-connectivity-for-cryptocurrency-customers-301026869.html)
- Mar 17, 2021: Diginex (Eqonex) and Itiviti launch ‘Access,’ enabling trading of cryptocurrencies and crypto derivatives across several platforms. — [PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/diginex-launches-front-to-back-digital-assets-trading-portfolio-management-and-risk-platform-powered-by-itiviti-301229667.html)

* Primary sources: Press releases (2018, 2024), FY 2024 earnings call, Annual Reports (2022–2023), product pages

### Firm Overview
CMC Markets plc is a London‑listed online trading and investing provider offering CFDs, spread betting, and stockbroking across global markets, with operations in the UK, Europe, APAC and elsewhere. Within the 2017–2024 period, the firm added cryptocurrency exposure first via crypto‑CFDs and later by rolling out spot crypto investing in Australia.

### Exposure Channels Identified
CMC directly intermediates client access to digital assets. It launched cryptocurrency trading via financial spread bets/CFDs (e.g., Bitcoin and Ethereum) in 2018, and by FY 2024 it had rolled out cryptocurrencies on its CMC Invest platform in Australia, allowing customers to buy/sell crypto within its regulated brokerage environment. It also maintains a crypto‑CFD product suite, enabling clients to speculate on crypto price movements without taking custody. There is no evidence that CMC mines crypto, holds it as a treasury asset, or supplies hardware/compute to the sector. (2018 launch; 2024 rollout; product page.) ([thearmchairtrader.com](https://www.thearmchairtrader.com/cmc-markets-launches-bitcoin-and-ethereum-trading/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 is assigned because the company’s exposure comes from running crypto trading/investing products (CFDs and, in Australia, spot crypto) that generate client‑driven fee/transaction revenue. There is no indication of proprietary crypto holdings (holders = 0.0), mining operations (miners = 0.0), non‑financial support services (bizserv = 0.0), or crypto‑directed infrastructure supply (infrastr = 0.0). Management’s results communications during FY 2024 support the positioning of crypto as part of its multi‑asset platform offering. ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/stock/LSE%3ACMCX/transcripts/2461704))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Earnings call transcripts rarely break out crypto specifically; classification relies primarily on official product pages and company press releases confirming crypto product launches/availability. ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/stock/LSE%3ACMCX/transcripts/2461704))  
- No disclosed revenue share for crypto; given multi‑asset breadth, crypto appears peripheral in financial materiality during 2017–2024. Annual reports consulted do not present separate crypto line items. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/c/LSE_CMCX.L_2022.pdf))  
- Australia spot crypto availability is clear by FY 2024; availability and scope may differ by jurisdiction due to local regulations. ([cmcmarkets.com](https://www.cmcmarkets.com/group/press-releases/cmc-markets-releases-final-results-for-the-year-ended-31-march-2024?utm_source=openai))

## NCSOFT

* Rank: 93 | Expo: 0.85 | id_str: KR7036570000
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Earnings-call press coverage (2021–2023), Business Wire press release (2022), Earnings releases (2023–2024)

### Firm Overview
NCSOFT is a South Korea–based video game developer and publisher known for major online franchises such as Lineage, Aion, Blade & Soul, and Guild Wars, with global operations across North America, Europe and Asia. Its investor materials in 2023–2024 emphasize core game releases and partnerships rather than dedicated crypto products. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NCSoft?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Within the 2017–2024 window, management discussed exploring blockchain and NFTs for future games: on Nov 11, 2021 the company said it had been preparing to apply NFTs/blockchain and aimed to announce a related game in 2022. On Aug 12, 2022, during its Q2 results call, the CFO clarified the firm’s NFT strategy was “not P2E,” focusing on using NFTs within game ecosystems and examining Web3/mainnet options. In November 2022, NCSOFT entered a strategic partnership with Mysten Labs (Sui) and invested $15 million as part of Mysten’s $300 million Series B. On Nov 9, 2023, the CFO said integrating blockchain into games was “premature” and reiterated that blockchain games are not P2E. Across available filings and earnings releases through 2024, there is no evidence that NCSOFT ran a crypto exchange/custody product, mined crypto, or held crypto as treasury assets. ([cm.asiae.co.kr](https://cm.asiae.co.kr/en/article/2021111111103464834?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
No mechanism category (holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, infrastr) is clearly supported by 2017–2024 evidence. Management statements refer to potential product design choices (e.g., NFTs inside games) and a strategic Web3 partnership investment, but there is no substantiation that NCSOFT directly intermediated crypto financial services, operated mining/infrastructure businesses, or carried crypto assets on its balance sheet. Accordingly, unclass = 1.0 is assigned.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Full, publicly accessible earnings-call transcripts with crypto quotes were not retrievable; quotes are taken from credible Korean/English financial press summarizing the calls.  
- The Mysten Labs partnership could involve future Web3 game features, but within 2017–2024 there is no confirmation of operational crypto services or treasury crypto holdings.  
- Korean regulatory constraints on P2E and tokenization complicate interpretation of management’s comments; classification remains conservative. ([fnnews.com](https://www.fnnews.com/ampNews/202208121144299151?utm_source=openai))

## NETMARBLE

* Rank: 94 | Expo: 0.83 | id_str: KR7251270005
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0

* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Company history/IR (2022–2023), Earnings‑call press coverage (Aug & Nov 2022), Klaytn blog (Mar 2022), MARBLEX official posts (2022–2023)

### Firm Overview
Netmarble is a South Korea–based global video game developer and publisher with a portfolio spanning mobile and PC titles across North America, Europe and Asia. In 2022 the company launched its own blockchain ecosystem and token through subsidiary MARBLEX (MBX), alongside the MBX Wallet and later MBX 2.0, as noted in Netmarble’s corporate history. ([company.netmarble.com](https://company.netmarble.com/en/company/history))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The primary exposure channel is crypto financial services: MARBLEX issues and operates the MBX token and provides a wallet, on‑chain swap/DEX, staking, NFT marketplace and related services, which constitutes direct handling and issuance of digital assets. These services were introduced and expanded in 2022–2023, including the initial launch on Klaytn and subsequent MBX 2.0 ecosystem rollout. ([medium.com](https://medium.com/marblex/introducing-mbx-partners-f044375a7539?utm_source=openai))  
Earnings‑call coverage shows management’s active blockchain push in 2022: on August 11, 2022 (Q2 2022), Netmarble’s CEO said the firm planned to launch six new titles in 2H 2022, three of which would apply blockchain, and described building experience for its blockchain ecosystem. On November 28, 2022, press summarized that during the Q3 2022 call the company said it would not launch one planned P2E title amid market concerns. ([fnnews.com](https://www.fnnews.com/news/202208111634303922))  
Investor materials also label select titles as “Blockchain Applied,” reinforcing that blockchain functionality is part of product roadmaps even as timing and scope have been adjusted in response to market conditions. ([sgimage.netmarble.com](https://sgimage.netmarble.com/images/netmarble/nmOfficial/20230217/2z8g1676613854589.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 is assigned because Netmarble, via MARBLEX, directly issues and operates crypto tokens and platforms (wallet/DEX/staking/NFT marketplace) and therefore intermediates digital assets rather than merely referencing them in game content. There is no evidence of mining operations, crypto treasury holdings independent of product needs, or provision of mining hardware/compute; hence miners = 0.0, holders = 0.0, infrastr = 0.0. Bizserv = 0.0 because the company’s crypto activities go beyond ancillary services and involve direct custody/issuance. ([medium.com](https://medium.com/marblex/introducing-mbx-partners-f044375a7539?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Public, fully transcribed English earnings‑call documents were limited; quotes are taken from reputable Korean financial/news outlets covering the calls, which caps certainty below “unambiguous.” ([fnnews.com](https://www.fnnews.com/news/202208111634303922))  
- Group filings and decks during 2022–2023 do not break out a distinct crypto revenue/asset line, so crypto materiality appears minor and precise shares are not disclosed. ([sgimage.netmarble.com](https://sgimage.netmarble.com/images/netmarble/nmOfficial/20230217/2z8g1676613854589.pdf))  
- Korean regulatory constraints on P2E features and shifting market conditions affected product plans (e.g., canceling a P2E launch), so the operational footprint may vary by title and region over time. ([donga.com](https://www.donga.com/news/Economy/article/all/20221127/116715596/1))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 11, 2022 (Q2 2022) — “In 2H 2022… three new titles applying blockchain… we built experience and a foundation for a blockchain ecosystem.” — Financial News (IR call coverage) [fnnews.com] ([fnnews.com](https://www.fnnews.com/news/202208111634303922))  
- Nov 28, 2022 (Q3 2022) — “During the Q3 earnings call, Netmarble said it would not launch the P2E‑featured ‘Monster Arena’.” — Dong‑A Ilbo (call coverage) [donga.com] ([donga.com](https://www.donga.com/news/Economy/article/all/20221127/116715596/1))

## ENSURGE MICROPOWER

* Rank: 95 | Expo: 0.80 | id_str: NO0013186460
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.1, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.9
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Annual reports (2022–2023), Company press release (May 2018), News coverage on business pivot (2019), Prospectus/IR materials (2020)

### Firm Overview
Ensurge Micropower ASA is a Norway‑listed technology company that develops and manufactures solid‑state lithium microbatteries, with corporate headquarters in Oslo and operational/R&D facilities in San Jose, California. The company was formerly Thin Film Electronics ASA and pivoted from NFC/software offerings to a solid‑state microbattery strategy beginning in 2019–2020, rebranding to Ensurge Micropower in 2021. ([assets.milestoneinternet.com](https://assets.milestoneinternet.com/ensurge-inc/pdf/ensurge-annual-report-2022-final.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The only identified crypto‑adjacent activity within the 2017–2024 window is historical and non‑financial: in May 2018, while operating as Thin Film Electronics, the company launched “CNECT Blockchain Services” to add provenance/traceability and secure transfer‑of‑ownership features to its NFC cloud platform, built on Ethereum. This is a blockchain integration aimed at supply‑chain transparency and consumer engagement, not at issuing or custodying digital assets. ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2018/05/02/1494593/0/en/Thinfilm-Launches-CNECT-Blockchain-Services-to-Enable-Product-Provenance-Supply-Chain-Traceability-and-Secure-Transfer-of-Ownership.html?utm_source=openai))  
Subsequent filings and investor materials during and after the 2019–2021 pivot focus on solid‑state microbatteries and do not indicate ongoing blockchain/crypto operations, mining, custody, or treasury holdings. ([assets.milestoneinternet.com](https://assets.milestoneinternet.com/ensurge-inc/pdf/ensurge-annual-report-2022-final.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
A small bizserv weight (0.1) is assigned to reflect the company’s brief 2018 launch of blockchain‑enabled services for product provenance within its CNECT platform, which constitutes a non‑financial service adjacent to blockchain. All other categories (holders, miners, finserv, infrastr) are set to 0.0 due to a lack of evidence that the firm held crypto assets, mined tokens, operated financial products/exchanges, or supplied crypto‑mining hardware/compute. The residual 0.9 is placed in unclass because the blockchain activity appears short‑lived and immaterial after the strategic pivot to batteries, and no crypto exposure is evident in 2022–2023 reports. ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2018/05/02/1494593/0/en/Thinfilm-Launches-CNECT-Blockchain-Services-to-Enable-Product-Provenance-Supply-Chain-Traceability-and-Secure-Transfer-of-Ownership.html?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Earnings‑call transcripts for 2017–2024 are listed on third‑party sites, but full text access is gated; no call quotes could be retrieved to corroborate or quantify the 2018 blockchain initiative’s materiality. Certainty is capped at 2 per protocol. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/THIN-FILM-ELECTRONICS-ASA-111314113/news-call-transcripts/))  
- Available filings do not disclose any crypto‑related revenue or assets, and post‑pivot materials emphasize batteries; it remains possible the 2018 blockchain feature saw limited or pilot‑level use only. ([assets.milestoneinternet.com](https://assets.milestoneinternet.com/ensurge-inc/pdf/ensurge-annual-report-2022-final.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 16, 2022 (Q3 2022) — Transcript listing (content behind login; no crypto references evident in surrounding IR materials). — MarketScreener (listing) ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/ENSURGE-MICROPOWER-ASA-1413302/news/Transcript-Ensurge-Micropower-ASA-Q3-2022-Earnings-Call-Nov-16-2022-42333041/))  
- Historical transcripts index (2017–2019 listings under Thin Film Electronics/Ensurge). — MarketScreener (index) ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/THIN-FILM-ELECTRONICS-ASA-111314113/news-call-transcripts/))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Annual Report 2023 — Focus on solid‑state microbattery business; no blockchain/crypto operations disclosed. — Ensurge (PDF) ([live.euronext.com](https://live.euronext.com/sites/default/files/company_press_releases/attachments_oslo/2024/04/25/616822_Ensurge_annual_2023.pdf))  
- Annual Report 2022 — R&D and operations centered on SSLB; revenue nil; no crypto holdings or activities disclosed. — Ensurge (PDF) ([assets.milestoneinternet.com](https://assets.milestoneinternet.com/ensurge-inc/pdf/ensurge-annual-report-2022-final.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**

## NTT DATA GROUP DEAD - DELIST.26/09/25

* Rank: 96 | Expo: 0.76 | id_str: JP3165700000
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.8, infrastr 0.2, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings Q&A (FY2018 4Q), Integrated/Annual Reports (2018–2024), press releases (2017–2024), third‑party coverage (Ledger Insights 2023/SWIAT; Global Trade Review 2020)

### Firm Overview
NTT DATA Group Corporation is a Japan‑headquartered global IT services and consulting company within the NTT Group, providing systems integration, application development, consulting, and infrastructure services worldwide. Within 2017–2024, the firm pursued select enterprise blockchain initiatives alongside broader digital and data center growth. ([nttdata.com](https://www.nttdata.com/global/en/-/media/nttdataglobal/1_files/investors/integrated-report/fy2024_ar_p.pdf?rev=94b209bbe1084de8a9d1066485be8830&utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management explicitly identified “trade blockchain” as a focused digital investment area in its FY2018 results Q&A, and the company developed TradeWaltz, a blockchain‑based trade data‑sharing platform that later became a jointly owned venture with Japanese corporates. NTT DATA also engaged in blockchain collaborations and industry bodies (e.g., Enterprise Ethereum Alliance) and ran PoCs across insurance and trade finance. In September 2023, it was recognized as a leader in enterprise blockchain services by Everest Group, and in December 2024 it became a validator node operator on the NEAR network via Meta Pool’s Enterprise Node Operators program, indicating limited direct blockchain infrastructure participation. These activities are professional/IT services around blockchain, not custody, exchange operation, or token issuance. ([nttdata.com](https://www.nttdata.com/global/en/-/media/nttdataglobal/1_files/investors/financial-results/2018/fy2018_qa_4q.pdf?rev=9082fd8e3ec24e24baff775bae88bebe))

### Weight Assignment
The classification is dominated by bizserv (0.8) because NTT DATA primarily designs, integrates, and advises on enterprise blockchain solutions (e.g., TradeWaltz; PoCs; advisory and delivery recognition by Everest/NelsonHall). A smaller infrastr weight (0.2) reflects limited but explicit operation of blockchain network infrastructure as a validator (NEAR) and participation as a validator on tokenization networks (e.g., SWIAT), which goes beyond consulting into operating nodes. No evidence indicates crypto treasury holdings, proof‑of‑work mining, or operating crypto financial products/custody; thus holders, miners, and finserv are set to 0.0. ([us.nttdata.com](https://us.nttdata.com/en/news/press-release/2024/december/ntt-data-collaborates-with-meta-pool-to-drive-data-sovereignty-identity-security-governance?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Earnings briefings infrequently discuss blockchain after 2019; quantified revenue or asset exposure from blockchain was not disclosed in reviewed materials, so materiality is coded as 0.  
- The validator role (NEAR; SWIAT) confirms infrastructure participation but the economic scale and persistence through 2024 are not quantified in filings or calls. ([nttdata.com](https://www.nttdata.com/global/en/-/media/nttdataglobal/1_files/investors/financial-results/2024/fy2024_qa_2q.pdf?rev=1b82e5eeab624079993e8ee9cc531628))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 9, 2019 (FY2018 4Q) — Principal Q&A: “we will invest in the focused fields such as healthcare, connected cars, and trade blockchain.” — NTT DATA IR (PDF) ([nttdata.com](https://www.nttdata.com/global/en/-/media/nttdataglobal/1_files/investors/financial-results/2018/fy2018_qa_4q.pdf?rev=9082fd8e3ec24e24baff775bae88bebe))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**

- Integrated Report 2021 — Describes TradeWaltz and blockchain data‑sharing initiatives. — NTT DATA (PDF) ([nttdata.com](https://www.nttdata.com/global/en/-/media/nttdataglobal/1_files/investors/integrated-report/fy2021_ar_b.pdf?utm_source=openai))  
- Integrated Report 2024 — Company overview and strategy context during the period prior to delisting. — NTT DATA (PDF) ([nttdata.com](https://www.nttdata.com/global/en/-/media/nttdataglobal/1_files/investors/integrated-report/fy2024_ar_p.pdf?rev=94b209bbe1084de8a9d1066485be8830&utm_source=openai))  
- Enterprise Ethereum Alliance membership (Oct 2017) — Press release. — NTT DATA ([nttdata.com](https://www.nttdata.com/global/en/news/press-release/2017/october/ntt-data-joins-the-blockchain-technology-related-organization-enterprise-ethereum-alliance?utm_source=openai))  
- Blockchain insurance PoC (June 2017) — Press release on smart insurance policy prototype. — NTT DATA ([nttdata.com](https://www.nttdata.com/global/en/news/press-release/2017/june/ntt-data-successfully-tests-prototype-of-blockchain-insurance-policy?utm_source=openai))  
- Cross‑industry investment into TradeWaltz (Oct 28, 2020) — Press release announcing JV shareholders and platform scope. — NTT DATA ([nttdata.com](https://www.nttdata.com/global/en/news/press-release/2020/october/cross-industry-investment-by-seven-enterprises?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Dec 2024 — NTT DATA becomes the first global consulting firm to operate a validator node on NEAR via Meta Pool’s ENO program. — NTT DATA US press release ([us.nttdata.com](https://us.nttdata.com/en/news/press-release/2024/december/ntt-data-collaborates-with-meta-pool-to-drive-data-sovereignty-identity-security-governance?utm_source=openai))  
- Sep 19, 2023 — Everest Group names NTT DATA a Leader in Enterprise Blockchain Services PEAK Matrix. — NTT DATA press release ([nttdata.com](https://www.nttdata.com/global/en/news/press-release/2023/september/ntt-data-named-a-leader-in-everest-groups-blockchain-peak-matrix-2023-report?utm_source=openai))

## LESAKA TECHNOLOGIES

* Rank: 97 | Expo: 0.76 | id_str: US64107N2062
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10‑K filings (2019 annual report; 2021 10‑K), Q3 2018–Q1 2020 earnings calls

### Firm Overview
Lesaka Technologies, formerly Net1 UEPS Technologies, is a South Africa–focused fintech listed in the U.S. and on the JSE, providing payments and financial services to consumers and merchants. Within 2017–2024, its international payments arm (IPG) and minority stake in Bank Frick supported select crypto-related initiatives, which were later exited as the company refocused on Southern Africa.

### Exposure Channels Identified
During 2018–2019, management highlighted development of a “crypto asset cold storage” product and noted crypto-exchange processing within the International Payments Group. The 2019 annual report explicitly states IPG provided card acquiring services for cryptocurrency exchanges (e.g., Bitstamp and Bitpanda) and pursued blockchain-based solutions with Bank Frick, including a crypto-asset storage offering and related R&D spending. Earnings calls in 2019–2020 repeatedly referenced these crypto initiatives and the sensitivity of IPG volumes to crypto-exchange activity. In February 2021, the company sold its entire 35% stake in Bank Frick and entered an indemnity and release agreement that terminated all existing arrangements with Bank Frick, indicating that the prior crypto-finserv exposure was wound down. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/n/NASDAQ_UEPS_2019.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv is set to 1.0 because the only substantiated crypto mechanism was direct financial services to crypto clients: acquiring/processing for exchanges and development of a custody-like storage product with a licensed bank partner. There is no evidence of proprietary crypto holdings, mining, or pure infrastructure provision, and later filings confirm disposal of Bank Frick and termination of those arrangements. Although the activity was temporally concentrated (circa 2018–2020), the mechanism itself is unambiguous and therefore receives full weight; materiality is coded separately as minor. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/n/NASDAQ_UEPS_2019.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Crypto exposure was time-limited and largely tied to IPG and Bank Frick; after February 2021, filings indicate those arrangements were terminated. Magnitudes by revenue line for crypto-exchange acquiring were not broken out, so RevShare is coded as minor (1). ([ir.lesakatech.com](https://ir.lesakatech.com/pdf/quarterly-results/2021/net1-2021-10-k.pdf))
- Some 2018–2019 references describe products under development; commercial scale and duration are not fully quantified in public materials. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/05/10/net-1-ueps-technologies-inc-ueps-q3-2019-earnings.aspx?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 10, 2019 (Q3 FY2019): “International transaction processing… down 12%… specifically meaningfully lower crypto exchange… processing.” — The Motley Fool (Transcript) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/05/10/net-1-ueps-technologies-inc-ueps-q3-2019-earnings.aspx?utm_source=openai))  
- May 2018 (Q3 FY2018): “A… highly secure… crypto asset cold storage solution for crypto asset investors and exchanges.” — MLQ.ai (Transcript) ([mlq.ai](https://mlq.ai/stocks/UEPS/earnings-call-transcript/Q3-2018?utm_source=openai))  
- Feb 7, 2019 (Q2 FY2019): “develop bespoke blockchain‑based solutions including a… crypto asset storage solution… filed two patents.” — The Motley Fool (Transcript) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/investing/2019/02/08/net-1-ueps-technologies-inc-ueps-q2-2019-earnings.aspx?utm_source=openai))  
- Nov 9, 2019 (Q1 FY2020): “The new crypto and blockchain products developed by IPG complement Bank Frick’s activities…” — The Motley Fool (Transcript) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/11/09/net1-ueps-technologies-ueps-q1-2020-earnings-call.aspx?utm_source=openai))

**Filings (10‑K / annual report):**
- 2019 Annual Report: “card acquiring services for cryptocurrency exchanges such as Bitstamp and Bitpanda.” — Net1 (PDF) ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/n/NASDAQ_UEPS_2019.pdf))  
- 2019 Annual Report: “prototype crypto‑asset storage product is now ready… incurred R&D of approximately $1.4m in fiscal 2019.” — Net1 (PDF) ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/n/NASDAQ_UEPS_2019.pdf))  
- 2021 Form 10‑K: Sale of Bank Frick; “terminate all existing arrangements… settle all liabilities… through the payment of $3.6m.” — Net1/Lesaka (PDF) ([ir.lesakatech.com](https://ir.lesakatech.com/pdf/quarterly-results/2021/net1-2021-10-k.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**

- Sept 27, 2018 Sidoti presentation: “International processing — Blockchain & crypto expansion.” — Net1 IR (PDF) ([ir.lesakatech.com](https://ir.lesakatech.com/pdf/presentations/sidoti-conf-investor-presentation-2018.pdf))

## RAS TECHNOLOGY HOLDINGS

* Rank: 98 | Expo: 0.76 | id_str: AU0000186496
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: FY2024 Annual Report (Appendix 4E), FY2024 results call/Q&A, ASX announcement (Stake.com partnership)

### Firm Overview
RAS Technology Holdings (ASX: RTH), trading as Racing and Sports, is an Australia‑based provider of racing data, enhanced content, digital media, and wagering technology to bookmakers and racing bodies across multiple jurisdictions. During FY2024 the company highlighted international growth and new managed trading services contracts while remaining focused on B2B solutions for the global racing and wagering industry. ([data-api.marketindex.com.au](https://data-api.marketindex.com.au/api/v1/announcements/XASX%3ARTH%3A3A648461/pdf/inline/rth-appendix-4e-and-2024-annual-report))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Within the 2017–2024 window, the company did not report proprietary crypto holdings, mining, or direct crypto financial products. Instead, its exposure to crypto is indirect via enterprise contracts with operators that accept cryptocurrencies, notably a two‑year agreement to provide Stake.com with a “complete racing solution” including trading/risk management; Stake.com supports both fiat and cryptocurrencies for betting. Management materials also note RAS is positioned to support new sportsbook entrants “looking to leverage crypto and fiat currencies.” Taken together, this constitutes non‑financial services provision to crypto‑native clients. ([aspecthuntley.com.au](https://www.aspecthuntley.com.au/asxdata/20240122/pdf/02764259.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Bizserv is set to 1.0 because the only substantiated crypto channel is RAS supplying data, software, and managed trading services to operators that accept or are native to crypto (e.g., Stake.com). There is no evidence the firm itself custodies, issues, trades, or holds digital assets (finserv/holders), nor that it provides mining hardware or energy (infrastr/miners). Thus 100% of the mechanism weight is assigned to non‑financial services provision around the crypto ecosystem, with crypto‑attributable revenue judged minor during FY2024 given the timing of launches. ([aspecthuntley.com.au](https://www.aspecthuntley.com.au/asxdata/20240122/pdf/02764259.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- ASX‑listed small caps often do not publish full third‑party call transcripts; for FY2024 we rely on a call/Q&A summary that includes a management comment about “crypto sportsbooks,” plus audited filings and ASX announcements. Certainty is set to 3 accordingly. ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/2526665/ras-technology-holdings-ltd-asxrth-q4-2024-earnings-call-transcript-highlights-strong-revenue-growth-and-strategic-partnerships))  
- Magnitudes for revenue sourced specifically from crypto‑native clients were not broken out; RevShare is coded as minor (1) based on qualitative disclosures and contract timing. ([data-api.marketindex.com.au](https://data-api.marketindex.com.au/api/v1/announcements/XASX%3ARTH%3A3A648461/pdf/inline/rth-appendix-4e-and-2024-annual-report))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 22, 2024 (FY2024 results): “Crypto sportsbooks are growing in number and prominence… Racing and Sports… will work with any operator… with rights holders.” — GuruFocus (Transcript highlights) (Q&A) [gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/2526665/ras-technology-holdings-ltd-asxrth-q4-2024-earnings-call-transcript-highlights-strong-revenue-growth-and-strategic-partnerships)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
