## TAIWAN SEMICON.MNFG.

* Rank: 99 | Expo: 0.74 | id_str: TW0002330008
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 1.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q1 2018 and Q2 2018 earnings calls; 2018 Annual Report; financial news (2018 outlook revisions)

### Firm Overview
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (TSMC) is a Taiwan‑based pure‑play semiconductor foundry that manufactures chips for a broad range of fabless and integrated device maker customers across mobile, high‑performance computing, automotive and IoT end markets. The company does not design its own end products and operates globally with ADRs listed on the NYSE under the symbol TSM.

### Exposure Channels Identified
During 2017–2018, management explicitly cited cryptocurrency mining as a source of demand within its high‑performance computing platform, reflecting wafer fabrication for customers’ mining ASICs. On the Q1 2018 call, TSMC noted results were “mainly driven by a strong demand from high‑performance computing such as cryptocurrency mining,” and in Q2 2018 it added that demand “including cryptocurrency mining” helped offset smartphone seasonality while also warning that crypto‑mining demand would decline with weaker coin prices. These disclosures indicate TSMC’s crypto linkage is through supplying leading‑edge chips used in mining hardware, not through holding or transacting in digital assets. Financial press at the time also tied TSMC’s outlook changes to shifts in crypto‑mining demand. ([investor.tsmc.com](https://investor.tsmc.com/english/encrypt/files/encrypt_file/english/2018/Q1/TSMC%201Q18%20transcript.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Infrastr is set to 1.0 because the only substantiated mechanism is hardware supply: TSMC fabricates ASICs used by mining‑equipment vendors and referenced crypto mining within its HPC platform mix. There is no evidence the firm mines coins (miners), holds crypto on its balance sheet (holders), or provides custody/exchange/payment products (finserv). As the crypto‑related demand was a minor, cyclical sub‑segment of HPC and not broken out as a separate line, RevShare is coded as minor (1) and AssetShare as 0. ([investor.tsmc.com](https://investor.tsmc.com/english/encrypt/files/encrypt_file/english/2018/Q2/TSMC%202Q18%20transcript.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Management flagged weakening crypto‑mining demand later in 2018 and does not provide ongoing revenue breakouts for crypto; the assessed exposure is episodic and tied to customer orders rather than a recurring product category. ([investor.tsmc.com](https://investor.tsmc.com/english/encrypt/files/encrypt_file/english/2018/Q2/TSMC%202Q18%20transcript.pdf))  
- Annual reports during the period discuss platforms and nodes but do not quantify cryptocurrency specifically; classification therefore relies on call transcripts plus contemporaneous financial news for calibration. ([investor.tsmc.com](https://investor.tsmc.com/static/annualReports/2018/english/index.html))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Apr 19, 2018 (Q1 2018): “This result was mainly driven by a strong demand from high‑performance computing such as cryptocurrency mining…” — TSMC transcript (Thomson Reuters) [investor.tsmc.com](https://investor.tsmc.com/english/encrypt/files/encrypt_file/english/2018/Q1/TSMC%201Q18%20transcript.pdf)  
- Jul 19, 2018 (Q2 2018): “This result reflected mainly a stronger demand from high‑performance computing, including cryptocurrency mining… [but] cryptocurrency mining demand will decline due to weakening cryptocurrency prices.” — TSMC transcript (Thomson Reuters) [investor.tsmc.com](https://investor.tsmc.com/english/encrypt/files/encrypt_file/english/2018/Q2/TSMC%202Q18%20transcript.pdf)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- 2018 Annual Report (English): platform mix and technology ramp context for HPC; no crypto breakout. — TSMC IR [investor.tsmc.com](https://investor.tsmc.com/static/annualReports/2018/english/index.html)

**Financial news / other:**
- Jul 19, 2018: “Chipmaker Taiwan Semiconductor cuts revenue outlook again on cryptocurrency gloom.” — CNBC [cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2018/07/19/chipmaker-taiwan-semiconductor-cuts-revenue-outlook-again-on-cryptocur.html)  
- Apr 19, 2018: “TSMC blames lowered outlook on bitcoin mining.” — CNBC (earnings‑call coverage) [cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2018/04/19/a-global-chipmaking-giant-blames-lowered-outlook-on-bitcoin-mining.html)

## VINCO VENTURES

* Rank: 100 | Expo: 0.74 | id_str: US9273302094
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.6, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.4, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q1 2022 earnings call; 2021–2022 company press releases; 2023 Form 10‑Q

### Firm Overview
Vinco Ventures, Inc. is a U.S.-based digital media and content technologies holding company (formerly NASDAQ: BBIG, later OTC). During 2021–H1 2022, subsidiaries included EVNT Platform LLC (d/b/a Emmersive Entertainment), which operated the E‑NFT.com marketplace, and Cryptyde, Inc., which housed Bitcoin mining services and Web3 activities prior to Cryptyde’s spin‑off on June 29, 2022. ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2021/11/23/2339436/0/en/Vinco-Ventures-Inc-Reports-Financial-Results-for-the-Third-Quarter-Ended-September-30-2021.html))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Finserv: Through Emmersive/EVNT, Vinco operated the E‑NFT.com marketplace and released artist and media NFTs, positioning the company directly in crypto asset issuance/distribution during 2021–2022. ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2021/08/23/2285117/0/en/Vinco-Ventures-Inc-Reports-Financial-Results-for-the-Second-Quarter-Ended-June-30-2021.html))  
Infrastr: Via Cryptyde’s CW Machines joint venture with Wattum, Vinco participated in the Bitcoin mining equipment ecosystem; management noted “initial crypto mining equipment sales at CW Machines” contributing to Q1 2022 revenue. ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2021/11/23/2339436/0/en/Vinco-Ventures-Inc-Reports-Financial-Results-for-the-Third-Quarter-Ended-September-30-2021.html))  
These crypto‑related lines were separated when Cryptyde was distributed to Vinco shareholders on June 29, 2022, limiting ongoing exposure after that date within the 2017–2024 sample window. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1717556/000149315223005705/form10-q.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Weights are split between finserv (0.6) and infrastr (0.4). Finserv dominates due to direct operation of an NFT marketplace (E‑NFT.com) with multiple releases and platform activity highlighted across 2021–2022. Infrastr is assigned for the mining‑equipment JV and reported initial equipment sales at CW Machines in early 2022. No evidence indicates Vinco mined crypto on its own account (miners) or held crypto on the balance sheet as treasury (holders), and the activities were not generic support services (bizserv). Given financial media frequently framed BBIG as an “NFT play,” invbase is set to 1. ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2021/08/23/2285117/0/en/Vinco-Ventures-Inc-Reports-Financial-Results-for-the-Second-Quarter-Ended-June-30-2021.html))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Crypto exposure was time‑bound: Cryptyde (mining services/equipment) was spun off on June 29, 2022; subsequent crypto exposure at Vinco likely declined materially after the separation. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1717556/000149315223005705/form10-q.htm))  
- The E‑NFT.com marketplace’s management transitioned to Curios in 2023, suggesting reduced direct involvement by Vinco thereafter; however, this does not alter the 2021–2022 exposure within scope. ([prweb.com](https://www.prweb.com/releases/flo-ridas-e-nft-is-alive-curios-assumes-management-of-e-nft-marketplace-from-vinco-ventures-zash-global-and-emmersive-entertainment-301960278.html?utm_source=openai))  
- Limited accessible transcripts exist for smaller‑cap coverage; classification is anchored on the Q1 2022 call plus filings and press releases. ([earningscall.biz](https://earningscall.biz/e/nasdaq/s/bbig/y/2022/q/q1))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 23, 2022 (Q1 2022): “In addition, initial crypto mining equipment sales at CW Machines helped further increase our revenue.” — Earnings call transcript (CFO remarks) [earningscall.biz](https://earningscall.biz/e/nasdaq/s/bbig/y/2022/q/q1)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Nov 22, 2021 (Q3 2021 results): “EVNT Platform… operates the NFT Commerce site, E‑NFT.com… [and] CW Machines, LLC, with Wattum… a global supplier of bitcoin mining equipment and services.” — Company press release [globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2021/11/23/2339436/0/en/Vinco-Ventures-Inc-Reports-Financial-Results-for-the-Third-Quarter-Ended-September-30-2021.html)  
- Aug 23, 2021 (Q2 2021 results): “Utilizing the Emmersive assets, the Company developed the EVNT Platform which operates the NFT Commerce site, E‑NFT.com.” — Company press release [globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2021/08/23/2285117/0/en/Vinco-Ventures-Inc-Reports-Financial-Results-for-the-Second-Quarter-Ended-June-30-2021.html)  
- Apr 18, 2022 (FY2021 results): “Cryptyde’s joint venture CW Machines has launched its crypto mining equipment reselling business and has welcomed its initial customers in Q1 2022.” — Company press release [globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2022/04/18/2423608/0/en/Vinco-Ventures-Inc-Reports-Financial-Results-for-the-Year-and-Fourth-Quarter-Ended-December-31-2021.html)  
- Feb 21, 2023 (Form 10‑Q): “Cryptyde… held our packaging, Bitcoin mining services, and Web3… products businesses… Effective June 29, 2022, Cryptyde separated from the Company…” — SEC filing [sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1717556/000149315223005705/form10-q.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- Aug 9, 2021: “Vinco Ventures Stock Offers an Indirect but Intriguing NFT Play.” — Nasdaq article [nasdaq.com](https://www.nasdaq.com/articles/vinco-ventures-stock-offers-an-indirect-but-intriguing-nft-play-2021-08-09)

## ASUSTEK COMPUTER

* Rank: 101 | Expo: 0.72 | id_str: TW0002357001
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.3, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.7, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings calls (Q2 2018, Q3 2018, Q2 2021), 2023 Annual Report, Q3 2022 investor presentation; tech media/product materials (2017–2018)

### Firm Overview
ASUSTeK Computer Inc. is a Taiwan-based global PC, motherboard and graphics card manufacturer listed on the TWSE. Within the 2017–2024 window, its crypto-related activities were primarily supplying dedicated mining motherboards/GPUs and, from late 2022, operating an NFT marketplace via a wholly owned subsidiary.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Infrastructure: ASUS produced purpose-built mining hardware, including the B250 Mining Expert (19 PCIe slots) and H370 Mining Master (supports up to 20 GPUs), directly targeting cryptocurrency‑mining workloads. These products, along with graphics cards, tied parts of ASUS’s components business to crypto mining cycles. ([asus.com](https://www.asus.com/motherboards-components/motherboards/others/b250-mining-expert/?utm_source=openai))  
Finserv: In October 2022 ASUS established ASUS Metaverse Inc. and launched an NFT trading platform (“Art Black hole”), later operating the ASUS NFT Plaza marketplace, which handles creator royalties and digital-asset trading — activities consistent with crypto financial services. ([asus.com](https://www.asus.com/event/Investor/Content/attachment_en/2023_Annual_Report_en.pdf))  
Management commentary links component demand and margins to cryptocurrency cycles (e.g., crypto demand slump in 2018; 1–2% margin contribution from crypto in 1H 2021), reinforcing infrastructure exposure rather than direct coin holding or mining. ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/twse/2357/investor-relations/earnings-call/q2-2018))

### Weight Assignment
Infrastructure is set to 0.7 reflecting ASUS’s explicit, purpose-built mining motherboards and the documented impact of crypto mining on component demand/margins in multiple earnings calls across 2018 and 2021. Finserv is set to 0.3 for the NFT marketplace launched via ASUS Metaverse Inc. in late 2022 and operated through 2023–2024. No evidence indicates ASUS mines cryptocurrency on its own account (miners) or holds crypto as treasury (holders), and the activities go beyond generic support services (bizserv). ([asus.com](https://www.asus.com/motherboards-components/motherboards/others/b250-mining-expert/?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- NFT marketplace scale/revenue contribution is not broken out in filings; classification relies on corporate disclosures of platform launch/operations rather than segment figures. ([asus.com](https://www.asus.com/event/Investor/Content/attachment_en/2023_Annual_Report_en.pdf))  
- Earnings-call references tie crypto primarily to component demand/margins rather than a dedicated mining operation or treasury holdings. ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/twse/2357/investor-relations/earnings-call/q2-2018))  
- Transcripts for some years are limited; however, coverage in 2018 and 2021 provides clear evidence of mechanism and materiality direction. ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/twse/2357/investor-relations/earnings-call/q2-2018))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q2 2018: “Cryptocurrency’s demand slid substantially… components lost share due to… lower demand for cryptocurrencies.” — Earnings call transcript (CFO) [AlphaSpread] ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/twse/2357/investor-relations/earnings-call/q2-2018))  
- Q3 2018: “Components have fallen… because the demand for cryptocurrency mining has fallen… cryptocurrency… at a very low level in demand.” — Earnings call transcript [AlphaSpread] ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/twse/2357/investor-relations/earnings-call/q3-2018))  
- Q2 2021: “1%–2% of margin was contributed by cryptocurrency… video card business… prices driven by digital currency growth.” — Earnings call transcript [AlphaSpread] ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/twse/2357/investor-relations/earnings-call/q2-2021))

**Filings / investor materials:**
- 2023 Annual Report: “October 2022 ASUS established the NFT trading platform for Web3.0.” — Annual report PDF [ASUS IR] ([asus.com](https://www.asus.com/event/Investor/Content/attachment_en/2023_Annual_Report_en.pdf))  
- Q3 2022 investor presentation: “ASUS announced its first move into Web 3.0 with the launch of ‘Art Black hole’, a new NFT platform.” — Slide deck [ASUS IR] ([asus.com](https://www.asus.com/EVENT/Investor/Content/attachment/IR_2022_3Q_ch.pdf))

**Financial news / product materials:**
- Product page: B250 Mining Expert (19 PCIe slots; mining features). — [ASUS] ([asus.com](https://www.asus.com/motherboards-components/motherboards/others/b250-mining-expert/?utm_source=openai))  
- ASUS Edge Up blog: B250 Mining Expert “blockchain beast” overview (19 PCIe). — [ASUS Edge Up] ([edgeup.asus.com](https://edgeup.asus.com/2017/b250-mining-expert-motherboard-19-pcie-blockchain-beast/?utm_source=openai))  
- Launch coverage: “ASUS Announces B250 Expert Mining Motherboard: 19 Expansion Slots” (Aug 22, 2017). — [AnandTech] ([anandtech.com](https://www.anandtech.com/show/11739/asus-announces-b250-expert-mining-motherboard-19-expansions-slots?utm_source=openai))  
- Launch coverage: “You can plug 20 graphics cards into ASUS’ new monster mining motherboard” (H370 Mining Master, May 30, 2018). — [TechRadar] ([techradar.com](https://www.techradar.com/news/you-can-plug-20-graphics-cards-into-asus-new-monster-mining-motherboard?utm_source=openai))

- ASUS pressroom blog: “In September 2022, ASUS established… ASUS Metaverse… launched an NFT-trading platform: Art Black hole.” — [ASUS Pressroom] ([press.asus.com](https://press.asus.com/blog/asus-empowers-creators-with-new-nft-platform/?utm_source=openai))

## REKLAIM

* Rank: 102 | Expo: 0.71 | id_str: CA7594021007
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.1, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.9
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q3 2023 earnings call; 2023 MD&A (SEDAR+); Reklaim Help Center (Crypto Redemptions); 2024–2025 company press releases

### Firm Overview
Reklaim Ltd. is a Canada-based consumer data privacy and monetization company listed in Canada and the U.S. OTC markets. It operates a platform where users can view, control, and opt in to share elements of their data profile in exchange for compensation paid out through various redemption options. ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/2326586/q3-2023-reklaim-ltd-earnings-call-transcript))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Finserv (minor): Company disclosures state that users who opt in to share data “receive weekly compensation” redeemable “in various forms, including, but not limited to, crypto,” indicating that Reklaim facilitates consumer redemptions in digital assets alongside gift cards and fiat (PayPal/Venmo). This is consistent with Help Center materials that include a Crypto Redemptions section and educational content on Bitcoin. ([newsfile.futunn.com](https://newsfile.futunn.com/public/NN-PersistNoticeAttachment/7781/20241119/SEDAR_PLUS/CSA_SEDAR_PLUS_NOTICE_RECORD_ID_114072.pdf))  
No evidence indicates Reklaim mines cryptocurrencies, holds crypto as a treasury asset, or supplies mining-focused hardware/hosting. Its core business, as described by management on earnings calls and in filings, is data privacy and consented data sales, not crypto operations. ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/2326586/q3-2023-reklaim-ltd-earnings-call-transcript))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv is set to 0.1 to reflect the presence of a crypto payout/redemption rail offered to consumers, which involves handling or arranging crypto payments as one of several redemption options. All other categories are set to 0.0, with the residual 0.9 assigned to unclass because crypto is peripheral to the company’s data‑privacy model and not a core revenue driver or balance‑sheet item during 2017–2024. ([newsfile.futunn.com](https://newsfile.futunn.com/public/NN-PersistNoticeAttachment/7781/20241119/SEDAR_PLUS/CSA_SEDAR_PLUS_NOTICE_RECORD_ID_114072.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The filings confirm crypto as a redemption option but do not specify whether Reklaim itself custodies or sources the digital assets versus using a third‑party processor; thus, the finserv exposure is coded as minor. ([newsfile.futunn.com](https://newsfile.futunn.com/public/NN-PersistNoticeAttachment/7781/20241119/SEDAR_PLUS/CSA_SEDAR_PLUS_NOTICE_RECORD_ID_114072.pdf))  
- Transcript availability is limited; one accessible Q3 2023 call is used to anchor the business description. No earnings-call discussion specifically about crypto redemptions was found. ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/2326586/q3-2023-reklaim-ltd-earnings-call-transcript))  
- Help Center content includes general education (“What is Bitcoin?”) and category pages; these support the existence of crypto redemptions but do not quantify scale. ([help.reklaimyours.com](https://help.reklaimyours.com/en/collections/2941196-crypto-redemptions))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 9, 2023 (Q3 2023): “Reklaim… was really created to try to give consumers some visibility and optionality around the data profile that they have.” — GuruFocus transcript excerpt. [GuruFocus] (https://www.gurufocus.com/news/2326586/q3-2023-reklaim-ltd-earnings-call-transcript) ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/2326586/q3-2023-reklaim-ltd-earnings-call-transcript))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Nov 19, 2024 MD&A (SEDAR+): “they will receive weekly compensation that they can claim and redeem in various forms, including, but not limited to, crypto, gift cards, fiat via PayPal, or charitable donations.” — MD&A PDF. [SEDAR+ via Futu Newsfile] (https://newsfile.futunn.com/public/NN-PersistNoticeAttachment/7781/20241119/SEDAR_PLUS/CSA_SEDAR_PLUS_NOTICE_RECORD_ID_114072.pdf) ([newsfile.futunn.com](https://newsfile.futunn.com/public/NN-PersistNoticeAttachment/7781/20241119/SEDAR_PLUS/CSA_SEDAR_PLUS_NOTICE_RECORD_ID_114072.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**
- Nov 6, 2023: Company set Q3 2023 earnings call (business description consistent with privacy/compensation model). [Reklaim IR] (https://www.investors.reklaimyours.com/press/reklaim-sets-third-quarter-earnings-call-for-thursday-november-9th-at-430-pm-est) ([investors.reklaimyours.com](https://www.investors.reklaimyours.com/press/reklaim-sets-third-quarter-earnings-call-for-thursday-november-9th-at-430-pm-est?utm_source=openai))  
- Aug 2024: “While others have tried this in crypto, we believe the public markets are already the best version of that idea.” — Reklaim Invest (points-for-shares) program. [Reklaim IR] (https://www.investors.reklaimyours.com/press/reklaim-introduces-consumer-ownership-program-a-new-model-for-shareholder-participation) ([investors.reklaimyours.com](https://www.investors.reklaimyours.com/press/reklaim-introduces-consumer-ownership-program-a-new-model-for-shareholder-participation?utm_source=openai))  
- Help Center: Crypto Redemptions collection and “What is Bitcoin?” article indicate active support content for crypto payouts. ## SHIFT4 PAYMENTS A

* Rank: 103 | Expo: 0.70 | id_str: US82452J1097
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.4, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.6
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: Q4 2021 and Q2 2022 earnings calls; Q1 2023 Form 10‑Q; March 1, 2022 acquisition press release; CoinDesk/Digital Transactions coverage

### Firm Overview
Shift4 Payments Inc. is a U.S.-based integrated payments technology company listed on the NYSE that serves merchants across hospitality, restaurants, sports/entertainment and other verticals. In 2022, it expanded into crypto-enabled philanthropy by acquiring The Giving Block, a cryptocurrency donation marketplace for nonprofits. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shift4?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Finserv: Management describes The Giving Block as a crypto donation platform and has stated it would “establish the Shift4 Crypto Innovation Center… to expand crypto acceptance and settlement capabilities across the organization,” indicating direct provision of crypto-related payment services to nonprofits. Subsequent calls reiterated that “going to market with a differentiated offering that includes crypto acceptance” is part of Shift4’s nonprofit strategy. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/03/01/shift4-payments-inc-four-q4-2021-earnings-call-tra/))  
Regulatory filings post-acquisition show “crypto settlement assets and liabilities,” confirming the company handles digital-asset settlement flows associated with this business. ([investors.shift4.com](https://investors.shift4.com/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001794669-23-000011/0001794669-23-000011.pdf))  
No evidence was found that Shift4 mines crypto or holds crypto as a treasury investment; the references to crypto on filings are tied to donation processing/settlement activities and related SaaS, consistent with financial services exposure rather than holders or miners. ([investors.shift4.com](https://investors.shift4.com/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001794669-23-000011/0001794669-23-000011.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv is set to 0.4 to reflect substantive, continuing activity in crypto-enabled donations via The Giving Block, supported by repeated management commentary about crypto acceptance/settlement and by recorded crypto settlement assets on the balance sheet. The remaining 0.6 is assigned to unclass because core revenues remain dominated by traditional card-based processing across non-crypto verticals and management has framed The Giving Block’s contribution as modest during 2022–2023 amid broader crypto volatility. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/03/01/shift4-payments-inc-four-q4-2021-earnings-call-tra/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Custody/processing pathways are not fully detailed in public filings; third-party infrastructure appears to be used for some flows (e.g., materials indicating Gemini involvement), which limits clarity on Shift4’s direct asset handling beyond settlement pass-through. ([7741744.fs1.hubspotusercontent-na1.net](https://7741744.fs1.hubspotusercontent-na1.net/hubfs/7741744/Fundraising%20Success%20Activation%20Kickoff%20Meeting%20Deck.2024.pdf?utm_source=openai))  
- Donation volumes and revenue tied to crypto fluctuate with market conditions; management noted lower donation volume during periods of crypto market stress in late 2022. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/FOUR/documents/transcripts/q4-2022))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**

## ENVIROSUITE SUSP - SUSP.22/08/25

* Rank: 104 | Expo: 0.69 | id_str: AU000000EVS3
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: ASX filings and annual reports (2019–2024), FY24 full-year results release

### Firm Overview
Envirosuite Ltd is an Australia-based environmental intelligence software company that provides real-time monitoring, analytics and decision-support solutions for customers in industries such as aviation, mining, wastewater and industrial operations, and was listed on the ASX under ticker EVS. Its product portfolio (e.g., EVS Industrial/Aviation, EVS IoT) is positioned as cloud/SaaS environmental monitoring rather than financial or digital-asset services. ([envirosuite.com](https://envirosuite.com/?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across the 2017–2024 sample, the company’s public materials consistently emphasize environmental monitoring and ESG-focused software (ARR, recurring revenue, aviation/industrial modules) with no disclosures of cryptocurrency mining, exchange/custody, crypto investment products, or treasury allocations to digital assets. FY24 results and the FY24 Annual Report overview reiterate segment focus on EVS Industrial and EVS Aviation and outline growth metrics without any crypto-related operations. No credible evidence was found of hardware provision targeted at crypto mining or of blockchain-based products integral to Envirosuite’s revenues. Based on these sources, no substantive crypto exposure mechanism is identified. ([announcements.asx.com.au](https://announcements.asx.com.au/asxpdf/20240820/pdf/066sz0l863nx83.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of any documented crypto-related activity in filings and investor materials during 2017–2024, the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0, with all other mechanism weights at 0.0. This reflects that Envirosuite’s activities are environmental intelligence SaaS rather than crypto mining, holding, financial services, business services to crypto, or mining-focused infrastructure. ([announcements.asx.com.au](https://announcements.asx.com.au/asxpdf/20240820/pdf/066sz0l863nx83.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- ASX-listed small/mid-cap coverage means third-party transcript repositories offer limited or no textual earnings-call transcripts; investor briefings are referenced but not routinely transcribed, which marginally constrains negative-evidence checks. ([envirosuite.com](https://envirosuite.com/insights/news/our-fy24-annual-report))  
- Corporate developments around an acquisition and planned delisting in 2025 do not affect the 2017–2024 assessment window but may limit future disclosures in ASX channels. ([envirosuite.com](https://envirosuite.com/insights/news/envirosuite-enters-into-scheme-implementation-deed-with-ideagen?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 20, 2024: “We hosted a webinar for investors following the release of our FY24 Financial Results.” — Investor page post. [Envirosuite] (https://envirosuite.com/insights/news/our-fy24-annual-report)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Aug 20, 2024: FY24 Full Year results ASX release; focus on ARR, recurring revenue, EVS Industrial/Aviation; no crypto-related activities disclosed. [ASX PDF] (https://announcements.asx.com.au/asxpdf/20240820/pdf/066sz0l863nx83.pdf)  
- Aug 20, 2024: Appendix 4E and Annual Report to shareholders (landing page). [MarketIndex] (https://www.marketindex.com.au/asx/evs/announcements/appendix-4e-and-annual-report-to-shareholders-2A1541844)  
- Aug 6, 2020: “Envirosuite acquires water modelling and management software technology…” — strategic product acquisition disclosure. [ASX PDF] (https://announcements.asx.com.au/asxpdf/20200806/pdf/44l7qqvf0gkxlp.pdf)

## ATEAM HOLDINGS

* Rank: 105 | Expo: 0.68 | id_str: JP3160890004
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: IR press releases and TDnet filings (May–June 2024), Q3 FY2024 earnings briefing (June 2024, Logmi Finance)

### Firm Overview
Ateam Holdings Co., Ltd. is a Japan-based IT group whose core businesses span web/media services (e.g., comparison and lead-gen sites, Qiita developer platform), games, and select D2C/e-commerce brands. In 2024, the company broadened into point-based apps convertible to crypto assets via the consolidation of Paddle Inc., the operator of “Bit Start” and “Bit Walk.” ([a-tm.co.jp](https://www.a-tm.co.jp/ir/materials/))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Crypto exposure stems from financial-service-like activities at the acquired Paddle unit. Management’s June 7, 2024 earnings briefing discussed M&A to add media assets, specifically naming Paddle, which operates the “Bit Start” and “Bit Walk” apps that issue points redeemable for crypto assets (e.g., bitcoin). A May 28, 2024 corporate release and a June 7, 2024 financing disclosure further describe the rationale and funding for acquiring a majority stake in Paddle, noting its customer base in the crypto-asset market. There is no evidence of crypto mining, mining-directed infrastructure, or treasury crypto holdings beyond operational needs to support the apps’ redemption mechanism. ([finance.logmi.jp](https://finance.logmi.jp/articles/379793))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 reflects that the sole identified mechanism is Paddle’s crypto-convertible point apps, which constitute a crypto financial service
… that enable users to accrue points convertible into crypto assets; management highlighted the Paddle deal and these apps in the June 7, 2024 briefing, and the company’s May 28, 2024 release plus related TDnet filing confirm the acquisition and its crypto focus. There is no evidence of mining, hardware provision, or treasury crypto holdings during 2017–2024, so all other mechanism weights are 0.0. Given limited disclosed scale within the sample window, RevShare and AssetShare are coded as 1 (minor). 

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Paddle’s apps are points-based with crypto redemption; the extent to which Ateam or Paddle directly custodies or sources crypto versus using third-party exchanges is not fully detailed in public filings, but the core service proposition is crypto-convertible points, hence finserv classification.   
- Ateam Entertainment participated as developer in an NFT/FT-based game project (“Crypt Busters”) announced in 2023; project status and financial materiality within 2017–2024 are unclear, so this was not scored as a separate mechanism.   
- Most transcripts are in Japanese and hosted by Logmi Finance; retrieval is good for 2024, but older calls rarely mention crypto, so the anchor quote relies on FY2024 materials. 

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- June 7, 2024, Q3 FY2024 briefing (Logmi Finance): “すでに『Bit Start』『Bit Walk』などを運営するPaddle社とのM&Aが決まっており…” — [Logmi Finance](https://finance.logmi.jp/articles/379793)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- May 28, 2024: “暗号資産ポイントアプリ『Bit Start』を運営するPaddle社の株式を取得し、連結子会社化” — [Ateam corporate news](https://www.a-tm.co.jp/news/46818/)  
- May 28, 2024: “株式会社Paddleの連結子会社化に関するお知らせ”（適時開示PDF） — [TDnet PDF](https://ssl4.eir-parts.net/doc/3662/tdnet/2449761/00.pdf)  
- June 7, 2024: 資金調達説明資料（第三者割当新株予約権・CB）“Paddle社の買収資金…暗号資産市場において多くの顧客基盤を築いている” — [JPX disclosure PDF](https://www2.jpx.co.jp/disc/36620/140120240607524046.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Apr 2023: “独自トークン(FT)を導入したNFTゲーム『Crypt Busters』を発表（Game Development: Ateam Entertainment Inc.）” — [PR TIMES](https://prtimes.jp/main/html/rd/p/000000019.000103876.html)

## RECORD

* Rank: 106 | Expo: 0.68 | id_str: GB00B28ZPS36
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Annual reports (FY2023–FY2024), Interim results RNS (Nov 2023), FY2024 earnings presentation (July 2024, transcript listing), financial news on Block Scholes funding

### Firm Overview
Record plc is a UK-based specialist in currency and derivatives management serving institutional clients and has been diversifying into broader asset management via European fund platforms. Since 2022 it has operated Record Digital Asset Ventures, a corporate venture arm investing in digital-asset sector companies and funds alongside partners. ([recordfg.com](https://recordfg.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/2023-Annual-Report-Record-plc.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Crypto exposure arises through Record Digital Asset Ventures’ investments in digital-asset businesses and funds, including a direct stake in Block Scholes, a crypto analytics platform; Record led a 2023 funding round alongside other investors. Management disclosures also describe building a Luxembourg fund platform that “embeds” capabilities from digital-asset market leaders, with Block Scholes providing data/analytics to funds’ portfolio managers and risk teams. There is no evidence that Record mines crypto, provides custody/exchange services, or holds crypto on its own balance sheet during 2017–2024. ([investegate.co.uk](https://www.investegate.co.uk/index.php/announcement/rns/record--rec/interim-results/7218823))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 reflects that the only substantiated crypto linkage is financial in nature: Record’s corporate venture investments and product incubation around digital-asset analytics within its asset management platform (e.g., Block Scholes as a service provider). No disclosures indicate crypto treasury holdings, mining, or mining-focused infrastructure, hence all other mechanism weights are 0.0. Given the small, venture-style scale and absence of crypto-backed products within the 2017–2024 window, both RevShare and AssetShare are coded 0 (negligible). ([recordfg.com](https://recordfg.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/2023-Annual-Report-Record-plc.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Public earnings-call transcripts for 2017–2024 are mostly behind paywalls; classification relies on RNS filings and annual reports, so certainty is capped at 2 per protocol. ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/2468558/))  
- Management’s references to “embedding” digital-asset capabilities into Luxembourg funds indicate adjacency but do not confirm Record itself custodies or transacts in crypto assets; exposure is therefore treated as financial-services-linked via investments/partnerships rather than direct crypto products. ([stockopedia.com](https://www.stockopedia.com/share-prices/record-LON%3AREC/news/reg-record-plc-interim-results-urn%3Anewsml%3Areuters.com%3A20231117%3AnRSQ7582Ta/))  
- RDAV’s fund commitments (e.g., Hack VC, Castle Island, Fasanara) and investees (e.g., Block Scholes; Lake Parime mentioned post-period) show ecosystem exposure but limited disclosed financial materiality over 2017–2024. ([investegate.co.uk](https://www.investegate.co.uk/index.php/announcement/rns/record--rec/interim-results/7218823))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jul 2, 2024: “Good morning, and welcome to the Record PLC full-year results investor presentation.” — Transcript listing. [GuruFocus] (https://www.gurufocus.com/news/2468558/full-year-2024-record-plc-earnings-presentation-transcript) ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/2468558/))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- FY2023 Annual Report: establishment of Record Digital Asset Ventures; initial £2m capital; investment and partnership timeline with Block Scholes (incl. tied agency). ## ESPORTS ENTERTAINMENT GROUP

* Rank: 107 | Expo: 0.68 | id_str: US29667K6038
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.6, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.4, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (FY2021, FY2023), corporate press releases on ggCircuit crypto mining (Apr–Oct 2021), investor deck (Oct 2022), third‑party news coverage (Jul–Oct 2021)

### Firm Overview
Esports Entertainment Group is a U.S.-listed company that operated esports-focused iGaming and entertainment businesses, including online casino/sportsbook brands and a B2B esports software unit, ggCircuit, acquired in June 2021. Subsequent filings indicate a streamlined focus on Lucky Dino (iGaming) and ggCircuit (esports software) after divestitures in 2022–2023. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1451448/000149315221025333/form10-k.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company launched a crypto mining initiative in 2021 via ggCircuit: an opt‑in application for esports/LAN centers that used idle gaming PCs to mine Ethereum, with milestones publicly disclosed from beta launch in May 2021 through $1 million mined by October 2021. Management materials and news releases describe this mining feature as an add‑on within the ggLeap subscription platform offered to centers worldwide. Filings further reference small related‑party amounts “for cryptocurrency mining” during FY2022, corroborating operational involvement. There is no evidence in 2017–2024 filings or releases that the company held crypto as treasury assets or provided exchange/custody services. ([esportsentertainmentgroup.com](https://esportsentertainmentgroup.com/2021/04/ggcircuit-announces-crypto-mining-application-for-lan-centers/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Miners = 0.6 reflects direct operational exposure through the 2021 Ethereum mining program run via ggCircuit’s software and distributed across participating centers; disclosures and related‑party notes indicate EEG bore/recognized some mining‑related activity. Bizserv = 0.4 captures that EEG primarily enabled third‑party venues to mine (software feature within ggLeap) rather than building proprietary dedicated mining infrastructure or offering financial products. No support was found for treasury holdings, dedicated mining hardware supply, or crypto financial services during 2017–2024; thus holders, infrastr, and finserv are 0.0. Given the initiative’s limited scale and concentration in 2021, RevShare is coded 1 (<~5%), and AssetShare is 0 (no balance‑sheet crypto holdings identified). ([esportsentertainmentgroup.com](https://esportsentertainmentgroup.com/2021/04/ggcircuit-announces-crypto-mining-application-for-lan-centers/?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Public, full‑text earnings call transcripts for 2017–2024 are difficult to access behind paywalls/aggregators; classification therefore relies on SEC filings and press releases, so certainty is capped at 2 per protocol. ([seekingalpha.com](https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GMBL/earnings/transcripts?utm_source=openai))  
- Evidence of the crypto mining program is concentrated in 2021; later SEC filings emphasize divestitures and do not quantify ongoing crypto‑related revenues, implying the exposure was modest and time‑limited. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1451448/000149315223037224/form10-k.htm))

* Primary sources: Company financial reports (2021–2024), earnings call listings (2021–2023), press releases (2021–2024)

### Firm Overview
Medicenna Therapeutics Corp is a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing engineered interleukin “Superkines” for cancer and autoimmune indications, headquartered in Toronto with operations including the U.S. and Australia. Its shares trade on Nasdaq and the Toronto Stock Exchange under the symbol MDNA. ([medicenna.com](https://www.medicenna.com/?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
A review of earnings call listings, regulatory filings, and company press materials from 2017–2024 shows no indication that Medicenna holds, mines, custodies, trades, or otherwise provides products or services involving cryptocurrencies or blockchain. The company’s disclosures focus on R&D activities, clinical programs, financing transactions, and routine public-company matters, without any crypto-related line items or commentary. Accordingly, none of the defined crypto-exposure mechanisms apply. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/MEDICENNA-THERAPEUTICS-CO-34185015/news-call-transcripts/))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of any credible crypto references across transcripts/IR events, filings, or news within the 2017–2024 window, the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0) with all other mechanism weights at 0.0. No evidence supports investor-base amplification linked to crypto sentiment, so invbase = 0. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/MEDICENNA-THERAPEUTICS-CO-34185015/news-call-transcripts/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified.

## NASDAQ

* Rank: 109 | Expo: 0.67 | id_str: US6311031081
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.3, bizserv 0.7, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2021), Q4 2023 earnings call (Q&A), 2018–2023 press releases and financial news

### Firm Overview
Nasdaq, Inc. operates U.S. and European equity and derivatives exchanges and provides market technology, surveillance and anti–financial‑crime software to financial institutions and market operators globally. Within digital assets, the company’s activities emphasize technology and market‑infrastructure services alongside operating listing and trading venues for crypto‑linked ETPs. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1120193/000112019322000007/ndaq-20211231.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Nasdaq provides market‑technology and surveillance solutions to crypto venues and institutions (e.g., SMARTS surveillance, Verafin AML, marketplace and custody technology), positioning the firm as a non‑financial services provider to the digital‑asset ecosystem. Management stated it continues to sell trading, clearing and surveillance technology to cryptocurrency exchanges, and that its crypto custody has been built as a technology offering for third parties rather than as a Nasdaq‑operated custodian. ([ir.nasdaq.com](https://ir.nasdaq.com/news-releases/news-release-details/nasdaq-establishes-new-digital-assets-business?utm_source=openai))  
On the financial‑services side, Nasdaq serves as the listing and trading venue for spot bitcoin ETFs (e.g., BlackRock’s iShares Bitcoin Trust) and crypto‑linked securities, tying a portion of exchange and options activity to crypto markets. The firm had planned to launch institutional crypto custody in the U.S. but halted that plan in July 2023 amid regulatory conditions. ([blackrock.com](https://www.blackrock.com/ae/intermediaries/products/333011/ishares-bitcoin-trust-etf?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence from the Q4 2023 earnings call and 10‑K disclosures shows Nasdaq’s core crypto exposure stems from technology, surveillance and infrastructure software sold to exchanges and institutions, warranting a primary bizserv weight. A secondary finserv weight reflects Nasdaq’s role operating listing and trading venues for bitcoin ETFs and related listed products, which link a slice of its exchange revenues to crypto cycles. No balance‑sheet coin holdings or mining are indicated. Therefore: bizserv 0.7, finserv 0.3; all other mechanisms 0.0. ([ir.nasdaq.com](https://ir.nasdaq.com/static-files/7745ccc4-92b3-430e-9da7-e2018ceb5c57))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Nasdaq explored becoming a crypto custodian in 2022–2023 but did not launch U.S. custody; it instead commercialized custody as technology for clients. This reduces direct financial‑services exposure versus original plans. ([ir.nasdaq.com](https://ir.nasdaq.com/news-releases/news-release-details/nasdaq-establishes-new-digital-assets-business?utm_source=openai))  
- Revenue attributable specifically to crypto within broader technology and market segments is not broken out; RevShare = 1 reflects minor but recurring contribution inferred from disclosures. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1120193/000112019322000007/ndaq-20211231.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q4 2023 (Q&A), IR PDF: “We are really proud to partner with BlackRock and Valkyrie as they brought their Bitcoin ETFs to Nasdaq… we are a tech provider to the industry… trading, clearing and… surveillance… our specific crypto custody solution, we have built it… a technology offering… we have made a conscious choice not to launch a custody solution [ourselves].” [Nasdaq IR (PDF)](https://ir.nasdaq.com/static-files/7745ccc4-92b3-430e-9da7-e2018ceb5c57) ([ir.nasdaq.com](https://ir.nasdaq.com/static-files/7745ccc4-92b3-430e-9da7-e2018ceb5c57))  
- Q2 2021: Reference to Hashdex partnership “to launch the world’s first ETF available in Brazil to investors utilizing the Nasdaq Crypto Index.” [The Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/07/21/nasdaq-inc-ndaq-q2-2021-earnings-call-transcript/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/07/21/nasdaq-inc-ndaq-q2-2021-earnings-call-transcript/?utm_source=openai))  
- Q3 2022: Discussion of building digital‑asset custody and broader market‑infrastructure opportunity. [AlphaStreet transcript](https://news.alphastreet.com/nasdaq-inc-ndaq-q3-2022-earnings-call-transcript/) ([news.alphastreet.com](https://news.alphastreet.com/nasdaq-inc-ndaq-q3-2022-earnings-call-transcript/?utm_source=openai))  
- Q2 2023: “We continue to build and deliver… digital asset software solutions… advancing our custody solution as a technology platform… and… partnerships with potential ETF issuers.” [MarketBeat transcript excerpt](https://www.marketbeat.com/earnings/transcripts/92927/) ([marketbeat.com](https://www.marketbeat.com/earnings/transcripts/92927/?utm_source=openai))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- 2021 Form 10‑K: Solutions “handle… digital currencies”; Market Technology “offers its services to several digital assets exchanges.” [SEC 10‑K](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1120193/000112019322000007/ndaq-20211231.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1120193/000112019322000007/ndaq-20211231.htm))  
- Sep 20, 2022: “Nasdaq Digital Assets” business established; crypto‑specific surveillance/AML and marketplace solutions highlighted. [Nasdaq press release](https://ir.nasdaq.com/news-releases/news-release-details/nasdaq-establishes-new-digital-assets-business) ([ir.nasdaq.com](https://ir.nasdaq.com/news-releases/news-release-details/nasdaq-establishes-new-digital-assets-business?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Jan 2024: BlackRock iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) listed on Nasdaq. [BlackRock fund page](https://www.blackrock.com/ae/intermediaries/products/333011/ishares-bitcoin-trust-etf) ([blackrock.com](https://www.blackrock.com/ae/intermediaries/products/333011/ishares-bitcoin-trust-etf?utm_source=openai))  
- Jul 19, 2023: Nasdaq halts U.S. crypto‑custody launch due to regulatory conditions. [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2023/07/19/nasdaq-halts-plan-for-crypto-custody-service-due-to-us-regulatory-conditions) ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2023/07/19/nasdaq-halts-plan-for-crypto-custody-service-due-to-us-regulatory-conditions?utm_source=openai))  
- Apr 25, 2018: Gemini adopts Nasdaq SMARTS market‑surveillance technology. ## CBOE GLOBAL MARKETS(BTS)

* Rank: 110 | Expo: 0.67 | id_str: US12503M1080
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2022–2024), Q2 2022 and Q4 2023 earnings calls, Cboe press releases and market notices, SEC/Cboe circulars

### Firm Overview
Cboe Global Markets is a U.S.-based exchange operator spanning options, futures, equities, FX and market data. Within digital assets, Cboe operates Cboe Digital (formerly ErisX) and lists crypto-linked products, including spot bitcoin ETFs, on its BZX exchange.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company is directly exposed to crypto via financial-services activities: operating Cboe Digital’s exchange/clearing for crypto and listing/trading of crypto-linked ETPs. Management highlighted Cboe Digital as offering spot and leveraged derivatives with clearing on a single regulated platform as of early 2024. ([ir.cboe.com](https://ir.cboe.com/files/doc_financials/2023/q4/4q23-earnings-script-final.pdf))  
Cboe completed the acquisition of ErisX in May 2022, rebranding it as Cboe Digital to expand into digital-asset spot and derivatives markets. ([ir.cboe.com](https://ir.cboe.com/news/news-details/2022/Cboe-Global-Markets-Completes-Acquisition-of-ErisX-Entering-Digital-Asset-Market-05-02-2022/default.aspx?utm_source=openai))  
On January 11, 2024, Cboe BZX listed multiple spot bitcoin ETFs and also launched margined bitcoin and ether futures on Cboe Digital, linking a slice of exchange, clearing and data revenues to crypto market activity. ([cdn.cboe.com](https://cdn.cboe.com/resources/regulation/circulars/products/IC-2024-011.pdf?utm_source=openai))  
Earlier, Cboe launched XBT bitcoin futures in December 2017 (later discontinued in 2019), showing a multi‑year pattern of crypto product involvement via regulated markets. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/02/09/cboe-global-markets-inc-cboe-q4-2017-earnings-conf.aspx))

### Weight Assignment
Cboe’s crypto exposure is squarely through financial services: it operates a crypto exchange/clearing venue and lists/trades crypto ETPs, with no evidence of proprietary coin holdings or mining. Earnings-call commentary and filings emphasize regulated market infrastructure, listing, trading and clearing as the drivers, so the assignment is finserv 1.0 and all other mechanisms 0.0. The contribution to overall revenue appears minor relative to the group, hence RevShare = 1; the firm reported zero safeguarded digital assets at December 31, 2024 after winding down its spot market, supporting AssetShare = 0. ([ir.cboe.com](https://ir.cboe.com/files/doc_financials/2023/q4/4q23-earnings-script-final.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Cboe impaired the ErisX goodwill in 2022 amid market stress but reiterated commitment to regulated digital-asset markets; this affects scale but not the mechanism of exposure. ([s202.q4cdn.com](https://s202.q4cdn.com/174824971/files/doc_financials/2022/Q2/2Q22-earnings-call.pdf))  
- In April–July 2024, Cboe announced the wind‑down of its digital spot market and by December 31, 2024 reported zero safeguarded customer digital assets; derivatives and listings remain the ongoing channels. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1374310/000162828024044657/cboe-20240930.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- Cboe’s 2017 XBT futures were discontinued in 2019; crypto exposure later re-emerged via Cboe Digital and ETF listings, so the intensity varies over time but stays within financial services. ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2019/03/18/cboe-to-stop-listing-bitcoin-futures-as-interest-in-crypto-trading-cools.html?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q4 2017, Motley Fool: “our December 10th launch of Cboe bitcoin futures XBT, the world’s first exchange‑listed regulated bitcoin futures product.” [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/02/09/cboe-global-markets-inc-cboe-q4-2017-earnings-conf.aspx)  
- Q2 2022, IR PDF: “closing the acquisition of ErisX on May 2… we remain excited by the compelling strategic opportunity for Cboe in the digital asset space… a trusted, transparent, regulated market for digital assets.” [Cboe IR (PDF)](https://s202.q4cdn.com/174824971/files/doc_financials/2022/Q2/2Q22-earnings-call.pdf)  
- Q4 2023 (Feb 2, 2024), IR script: “a more vibrant crypto ecosystem is advantageous to our recently‑launched margin futures product. In January, Cboe Digital became the only U.S.‑regulated exchange to offer spot, leveraged derivatives, and clearing on a single platform.” [Cboe IR (PDF)](https://ir.cboe.com/files/doc_financials/2023/q4/4q23-earnings-script-final.pdf)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- 2023 Form 10‑K: discussion of customer digital assets held in omnibus wallets and safeguarded‑assets accounting. [SEC 10‑K](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1374310/000155837024001277/cboe-20231231x10k.htm)  
- 2024 Annual Report/10‑K: “On July 1, 2024, the Company liquidated all digital assets held by Cboe Digital for customers… Digital assets — safeguarded assets totaled zero at December 31, 2024.” [SEC Annual Report PDF](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1374310/000110465925031482/tm256640d3_ars.pdf)  
- May 2, 2022: “Cboe Global Markets Completes Acquisition of ErisX, Entering Digital Asset Market.” [Cboe press release](https://ir.cboe.com/news/news-details/2022/Cboe-Global-Markets-Completes-Acquisition-of-ErisX-Entering-Digital-Asset-Market-05-02-2022/default.aspx)  
- Dec 13, 2023: Cboe Digital to launch margined Bitcoin and Ether futures Jan 11, 2024. [Cboe press release](https://ir.cboe.com/news/news-details/2023/Cboe-Digital-to-Launch-Margined-Bitcoin-and-Ether-Futures-on-January-11-2024-Backed-by-Crypto-and-Traditional-Finance-Players/default.aspx)

**Financial news / other:**
- Jan 11–12, 2024: Cboe BZX listings of spot Bitcoin ETFs; Cboe circular for Franklin Bitcoin ETF; Trader E‑News recapping listings and margined futures first trade. [Cboe circular (PDF)](https://cdn.cboe.com/resources/regulation/circulars/products/IC-2024-011.pdf) | [Cboe Trader E‑News (PDF)](https://cdn.cboe.com/resources/trader_news/2024/Trader-E-News-1-12-24.pdf)  
- Mar 2019: Cboe halts new XBT bitcoin futures listings. [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2019/03/18/cboe-to-stop-listing-bitcoin-futures-as-interest-in-crypto-trading-cools.html)

</small>

## BLACKBOXSTOCKS

* Rank: 111 | Expo: 0.66 | id_str: US09229E3036
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2022, 2024), Q3 2022 earnings call

### Firm Overview
Blackboxstocks Inc. is a Dallas‑based fintech and social‑media platform that sells subscriptions for real‑time analytics to stock and options traders; during the sample period it was listed on Nasdaq under BLBX. Company filings describe the core business as software providing market‑data analytics and community features for retail traders. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1567900/000143774923010333/blkbx20221231_10k.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024 sources, there is no evidence that the company held cryptocurrencies, mined digital assets, or directly provided crypto financial services such as exchange, brokerage, custody, or crypto‑backed products. In September 2022, an investor presentation outlined a prospective “Crypto Currency Platform” concept, but on the November 15, 2022 earnings call management explicitly said the “crypto initiative” was put on hold, prioritizing other products. Subsequent filings in 2024 continue to characterize the business as a trading analytics SaaS without disclosing crypto assets or operations. Overall, no substantive crypto exposure channel is supported within the sample window. ([blackboxstocks.com](https://blackboxstocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Investor_Presentation_Sept_2022.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Because the only crypto reference in the sample period is a paused initiative and there is no evidence of operational crypto activity (holding, mining, custody, trading, or hardware/services targeted at crypto), the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0) with all other mechanism weights at 0.0. No investor‑base “crypto proxy” effect is documented, so invbase = 0. Filings show no crypto revenue or balance‑sheet assets, so RevShare and AssetShare are NA for an unclassified firm. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1567900/000143774923010333/blkbx20221231_10k.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Management expressed interest in developing a crypto platform (Sept 2022) but then deferred it on the Nov 15, 2022 call; future execution (outside the 2017–2024 window) could alter exposure. ([blackboxstocks.com](https://blackboxstocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Investor_Presentation_Sept_2022.pdf))  
- Limited third‑party transcript coverage exists for a small‑cap issuer; however, the available Q3 2022 call contains the key statement regarding the paused crypto initiative. ([tickertrends.io](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/BLBX/Q3-earnings-transcript-2022))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 15, 2022, Q3 2022: “We have also … put on hold our crypto initiative for now… we believe that crypto is here to stay… but for now… Stock Nanny and Black Box Pro take precedence.” [TickerTrends](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/BLBX/Q3-earnings-transcript-2022)  
- Nov 15, 2022, Q3 2022 transcript mirror (same remarks): [AlphaSpread](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/nasdaq/blbx/investor-relations/earnings-call/q3-2022)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- 2022 Form 10‑K: “We are a financial technology and social media hybrid platform offering real-time proprietary analytics and news for stock and options traders of all levels.” [SEC 10‑K](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1567900/000143774923010333/blkbx20221231_10k.htm)  
- 2024 Form 10‑K: business description and exhibits; no disclosures of crypto assets or crypto operations. [SEC 10‑K](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1567900/000143774925008773/blkbx20241231_10k.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- Sept 2022 investor deck: “Crypto Currency Platform — Combines a quotation and execution system with the BLBX social media platform.” [Blackboxstocks Investor Presentation (PDF)](https://blackboxstocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Investor_Presentation_Sept_2022.pdf)  
- Apr 14, 2023: Company announces Q4 and full‑year 2022 results; release reiterates core analytics subscription model for stock/options traders. [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20230414005424/en/Blackboxstocks-Announces-Fourth-Quarter-and-Year-End-2022-Financial-Results)

## SANRIO

* Rank: 112 | Expo: 0.66 | id_str: JP3343200006
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: press releases (2022–2024), integrated/annual filings (2024), investor presentation (Aug 2024)

### Firm Overview
Sanrio Co., Ltd. is a Japan‑based character IP company best known for Hello Kitty, operating a global licensing, merchandise, and theme‑park business with listings on the Tokyo Stock Exchange. Company investor materials emphasize licensing and brand engagement as core earnings drivers, supplemented by digital initiatives. ([corporate.sanrio.co.jp](https://corporate.sanrio.co.jp/en/about_sanrio/profile/?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Within the 2017–2024 window, Sanrio engaged with blockchain/Web3 mainly via brand and fan‑community initiatives rather than financial products. In August 2022 it announced a capital and business alliance with Gaudiy to build global fan communities using blockchain technologies such as NFTs and decentralized IDs; Gaudiy’s release described the platform capabilities. In 2024 Sanrio and NTT Digital announced plans to adopt a wallet solution for Sanrio’s next‑generation community service. Separately, Sanrio licensed Hello Kitty NFTs through RECUR (announced late 2021) as a digital collectible initiative; there is no evidence of crypto custody, mining, or exchange operations by Sanrio itself. ([prtimes.jp](https://prtimes.jp/main/html/rd/p/000000424.000037629.html?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
The documented crypto touchpoints are limited to non‑financial, brand/IP licensing and Web3 fan‑engagement projects run with partners (Gaudiy, NTT Digital; previously RECUR). There is no filing or call evidence of treasury crypto holdings, mining, custody, brokerage, or hardware provisioning. Accordingly, exposure is classified as bizserv = 1.0 (non‑financial services/content to the crypto ecosystem), with all other mechanisms at 0.0. Given the lack of disclosed crypto revenue lines or crypto assets on the balance sheet in Sanrio’s filings, RevShare and AssetShare are set to 0; no evidence indicates a “crypto proxy” investor base effect, so invbase = 0. Certainty is capped at 2 due to the absence of a retrievable earnings‑call transcript. ([www2.jpx.co.jp](https://www2.jpx.co.jp/disc/81360/140120240513594121.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- No text transcripts of earnings briefings were retrievable for 2017–2024; Sanrio mainly provides results videos and presentation decks, so certainty is capped at 2. ([corporate.sanrio.co.jp](https://corporate.sanrio.co.jp/ir/library/presentation/202403-movie.html))  
- RECUR, a partner platform that hosted a Hello Kitty NFT drop, announced it would wind down in August 2023; this limits persistence of that specific NFT initiative during the sample window. ([bitcoinist.com](https://bitcoinist.com/nft-giant-recur-faces-abrupt-end/?utm_source=openai))
### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls / briefings:**
- FY2024 (year ended March 2024) results briefing video page (no text transcript provided; video archive only).   
- Aug 2024, Individual investor briefing deck: “テーマパークは、リアルも！バーチャルも！ SANRIO Virtual Festival 2024 in Sanrio Puroland.”

## COOTEK 1:650 ADR

* Rank: 113 | Expo: 0.65 | id_str: US21718L2016
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 20‑F annual reports (2023–2024), earnings call transcript (Q4 2019), press releases (2021–2022)

### Firm Overview
CooTek (Cayman) Inc. is a Shanghai‑based mobile internet company that operates content‑rich applications focused on online literature, scenario‑based apps and casual games, and historically developed the TouchPal keyboard. Its filings describe revenues primarily from mobile advertising and in‑app purchases, with operations organized around online literature and games. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1734262/000141057825001019/ctkyy-20241231x20f.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across the 2017–2024 window, no substantive crypto exposure was identified. The company’s 20‑F discusses monetization via in‑app “virtual currency” used within games/reading apps and advertising; this refers to app‑specific game coins and subscription features rather than blockchain‑based assets. There is no evidence of crypto treasury holdings, mining activities, custody/exchange operations, or issuance of crypto‑backed products in filings or calls. While CooTek issued “metaverse” themed PR in late 2021 describing content IP across novels and games, those materials did not indicate use of cryptocurrencies, NFTs, or blockchain infrastructure by the company. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1734262/000141057825001019/ctkyy-20241231x20f.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of crypto‑related operations in filings, earnings remarks, or credible news within 2017–2024, the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0. All mechanism categories (holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, infrastr) are set to 0.0. No evidence suggests a “crypto proxy” investor base effect, so invbase = 0. As the firm is unclassified for crypto exposure, certainty is NA and materiality scores (RevShare, AssetShare) are NA. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1734262/000141057825001019/ctkyy-20241231x20f.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- “Metaverse” initiatives (2021) can sometimes be conflated with Web3; available materials frame CooTek’s efforts around content/IP without blockchain or tokenization details. ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/the-cootek-metaverse-a-new-entertainment-landscape-that-merges-novels-and-games-301437799.html?utm_source=openai))  
- Multiple historical earnings call transcripts exist, but none reviewed indicated crypto‑related operations; classification remains unclass based primarily on filings and representative call text. ([seekingalpha.com](https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CTK-DEFUNCT-584681/earnings/transcripts))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Mar 12, 2020 (Q4 2019): “Our content‑rich portfolio apps contributed approximately 95% of our total revenue during the quarter.” — [ROIC AI (Q4 2019 transcript)](https://www.roic.ai/quote/CTK/transcripts/2019/4)  
- Index of additional 2018–2021 calls — [Seeking Alpha transcript index](https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CTK-DEFUNCT-584681/earnings/transcripts)

**Filings (10‑K/20‑F / annual reports):**
- Apr 30, 2025: Form 20‑F (FY2024) — describes in‑app purchase revenue “through the sale of in‑app virtual currency in our mobile games and online literature products,” with no crypto custody/mining. [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1734262/000141057825001019/ctkyy-20241231x20f.htm)  
- 2023 Form 20‑F (PDF) — business overview emphasizing online literature/games and advertising. [AnnualReports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/c/NYSE_CTK_2023.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Dec 17, 2021: “A Female‑oriented Game World: CooTek’s First Attempt at Metaverse” — content/IP focus; no blockchain/NFT claims. [Nasdaq (press post)](https://www.nasdaq.com/press-release/a-female-oriented-game-world%3A-cooteks-first-attempt-at-metaverse-2021-12-17)  
- Dec 20, 2021: “The CooTek Metaverse” — outlines long‑term content strategy; no crypto component disclosed. [PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/the-cootek-metaverse-a-new-entertainment-landscape-that-merges-novels-and-games-301437799.html)  
- Sep 28, 2021: Fengdu Novel community features and incentives (app ecosystem context). [PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/fengdu-novel-launches-online-community-to-engage-readers-and-writers-301386359.html)

## EMEMORY TECHNOLOGY

* Rank: 114 | Expo: 0.64 | id_str: TW0003529004
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.1, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.9
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Annual report (2023), Q1–Q4 2022 earnings call transcripts, product/solution pages (NeoPUF/PUFsecurity)

### Firm Overview
eMemory Technology Inc. is a Taiwan-based semiconductor IP company that develops and licenses logic-based embedded non-volatile memory (eNVM) and PUF-based hardware security IP to foundries, IDMs, and fabless chip designers globally. Its business model centers on licensing and royalties; it does not operate manufacturing lines or financial services. ([ememory.com.tw](https://www.ememory.com.tw/Content/Upload/files/20240528170001.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management positions its PUF-based security portfolio (e.g., PUFrt, PUFcc, TRNG) for a range of end-markets and has explicitly cited “Blockchain” among active application areas in investor calls. In Q2 2022, the company described how PUF-based TRNG and key storage can secure crypto transactions and NFT-related activity; Q1 2022 remarks also listed Blockchain among target verticals for PUF-based solutions. That said, eMemory is an IP licensor and does not mine, custody, issue tokens, or hold crypto on balance sheet. On this basis, any crypto linkage is indirect via security IP used by customers building blockchain/crypto products, aligning with a small bizserv exposure. ([ememory.com.tw](https://www.ememory.com.tw/Content/Upload/files/Webcastconference/2022/Q2%202022%20Earnings%20Call/2022%20Q2%20Investor%20Conference%20Transcript_eng_20220810.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Assigned bizserv = 0.1 to reflect explicit, repeated management statements that PUF-based security IPs target blockchain/crypto use cases and can secure crypto transactions/NFTs, supported by product collateral (e.g., PUFcc crypto coprocessor, NeoPUF pages referencing blockchain). No evidence supports holders, miners, finserv, or infrastr exposure; the remaining 0.9 is allocated to unclass to capture that crypto-related demand is indirect and not separably disclosed or material to revenues. invbase = 0 given no indication the stock trades as a crypto proxy, and certainty = 3 based on multiple transcripts plus filings and product materials. ([ememory.com.tw](https://www.ememory.com.tw/Content/Upload/files/Webcastconference/2022/Q2%202022%20Earnings%20Call/2022%20Q2%20Investor%20Conference%20Transcript_eng_20220810.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- References to blockchain/crypto appear in illustrative or marketing-driven security discussions; the 2023 annual report does not break out any crypto-specific revenue, and no customer or product wins are attributed specifically to crypto.  
- As an upstream IP provider, any crypto demand is second-order and may be difficult to quantify; no evidence of crypto treasury holdings, mining, custody, exchange operations, or products collateralized by digital assets. ([ememory.com.tw](https://www.ememory.com.tw/Content/Upload/files/20240528170001.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 11, 2022 (Q1 2022): “Business activities for PUF-based security solutions are continuing to progress in IoT, Industrial IoT, AI, Blockchain, FPGA, DPU, Mobile Storage (UFS) and Automotive applications.” — [eMemory Q1 2022 transcript (PDF)](https://www.ememory.com.tw/Content/Upload/files/Webcastconference/2022/2022%20Q1%20Investor%20Conference%20Transcript_eng_20220511.pdf) ([ememory.com.tw](https://www.ememory.com.tw/Content/Upload/files/Webcastconference/2022/2022%20Q1%20Investor%20Conference%20Transcript_eng_20220511.pdf))  
- Aug 10, 2022 (Q2 2022): “Generating random numbers on the blockchain… The application of PUFtrng can further extend to creating secure private keys… every time a transaction takes place, such as sending cryptocurrencies, the key pair can be used to secure your transactions.” — [eMemory Q2 2022 transcript (PDF)](https://www.ememory.com.tw/Content/Upload/files/Webcastconference/2022/Q2%202022%20Earnings%20Call/2022%20Q2%20Investor%20Conference%20Transcript_eng_20220810.pdf) ([ememory.com.tw](https://www.ememory.com.tw/Content/Upload/files/Webcastconference/2022/Q2%202022%20Earnings%20Call/2022%20Q2%20Investor%20Conference%20Transcript_eng_20220810.pdf))  
- Feb 8, 2023 (Q4 2022): “We have completed a series of PUF-based security IPs and solutions… Our security subsystem SoC IP was created by integrating a crypto engine with the Root of Trust IP.” — [eMemory Q4 2022 transcript (PDF)](https://www.ememory.com.tw/Content/Upload/files/Webcastconference/2022/Q4%202022%20Earnings%20Call/2022%20Q4%20Investor%20Conference%20Transcript_eng_20230208.pdf) ([ememory.com.tw](https://www.ememory.com.tw/Content/Upload/files/Webcastconference/2022/Q4%202022%20Earnings%20Call/2022%20Q4%20Investor%20Conference%20Transcript_eng_20230208.pdf))

**Filings (annual reports):**
- 2023 Annual Report: Business model is eNVM and hardware security IP licensing/royalties; company is an IP provider with “zero-inventory,” not a manufacturer; no crypto holdings or services disclosed. [eMemory Annual Report 2023 (PDF)](https://www.ememory.com.tw/Content/Upload/files/20240528170001.pdf) ([ememory.com.tw](https://www.ememory.com.tw/Content/Upload/files/20240528170001.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**
- Product collateral: PUFsecurity “PUFcc” crypto coprocessor positioned to safeguard Arm ecosystem with PUF-based root of trust. [PUFsecurity PUFcc product page](https://www.pufsecurity.com/products/pufcc/) ([pufsecurity.com](https://www.pufsecurity.com/products/pufcc/?utm_source=openai))  
- Product collateral: NeoPUF overview and resources include blockchain-related use cases. [eMemory NeoPUF page](https://www.ememory.com.tw/3-neo/Products/Security/NeoPUF) ([ememory.com.tw](https://www.ememory.com.tw/3-neo/Products/Security/NeoPUF?utm_source=openai))  
- Feb 8, 2022: eMemory joins Intel Foundry Services Accelerator, promoting OTP/PUF security IP on advanced nodes.
