## MARQETA A

* Rank: 115 | Expo: 0.64 | id_str: US57142B1044
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.3, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.7
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (FY2023), Q3 2021 and Q4 2021 earnings calls, press/news on crypto card programs

### Firm Overview
Marqeta, Inc. is a U.S.-based modern card-issuing and processing platform listed on Nasdaq that provides API-driven tools for companies to create and manage debit, prepaid, and credit card programs across regions. Management emphasizes enabling embedded finance use cases, with customers spanning neobanks, BNPL, expense management, and select crypto-native firms. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2025/02/27/marqeta-mq-q4-2024-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Within the sample period, Marqeta powered multiple crypto spend and rewards cards for exchanges and crypto apps (e.g., Coinbase, Bakkt, Fold, Shakepay, Blockchain.com), enabling real-time conversion of crypto balances to fiat at the point of sale through partners’ wallets. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/11/marqeta-inc-mq-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))  
Crucially, Marqeta discloses it “does not handle or interact with cryptocurrency” and only processes transactions in fiat, while “certain cryptocurrency businesses use our platform to provide card products,” indicating an indirect, support‑service role rather than direct custody, trading, or product issuance. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1522540/000162828024007663/mq-20231231.htm))  
Earnings remarks describe Marqeta’s JIT funding as a “gateway between cryptocurrencies and fiat currencies” for partners like Coinbase, further underscoring that crypto exposure comes via enabling clients’ card programs, not by holding or transacting in digital assets itself. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/11/marqeta-inc-mq-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Weight Assignment
Bizserv = 0.3 reflects repeated, explicit discussion (and third‑party coverage) of Marqeta enabling crypto spend/rewards cards for multiple programs (Coinbase Card in the U.S., Blockchain.com Visa Card, Shakepay, Bakkt, Fold) during 2020–2022, with management noting revenue from these customers reached “millions” by late 2021. No evidence supports holders, miners, finserv, or infrastr under the project’s definitions, particularly given the 10‑K statement that Marqeta does not handle cryptocurrency and processes only fiat. The remaining 0.7 is allocated to unclass to capture that crypto is a peripheral, customer‑driven use case within a broader payments platform. invbase = 0; certainty = 3 based on multiple transcripts plus filings and independent news. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/03/09/marqeta-inc-mq-q4-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Filings do not break out a crypto-specific revenue line; references indicate indirect enablement for crypto clients and note regulatory uncertainty that could affect those customers. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1522540/000162828024007663/mq-20231231.htm))  
- Partnerships and program scopes can evolve; while widely covered in 2020–2022, ongoing contribution levels are not quantified in detail within 2017–2024 filings. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1522540/000162828024007663/mq-20231231.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 11, 2021 (Q3 2021): “JIT… enabling a new wave of innovative cryptocurrency card products… highlighted by companies like Coinbase, Bakkt, Fold and Shakepay.”; “For Coinbase users… transaction funded in fiat currency in real time, based on their cryptocurrency balance.” — [Marqeta Q3 2021 transcript (Motley Fool)](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/11/marqeta-inc-mq-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcript/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/11/marqeta-inc-mq-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))  
- Mar 9, 2022 (Q4 2021): “Our platform acts as a gateway between fiat and cryptocurrencies for partners like Coinbase, Bakkt, Fold, Shakepay… Revenue from these customers is now in the millions…” — [Marqeta Q4 2021 transcript (Motley Fool)](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/03/09/marqeta-inc-mq-q4-2021-earnings-call-transcript/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/03/09/marqeta-inc-mq-q4-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))  
- May 11, 2022 (Q1 2022): “Upgrade… launched four reward card programs… from millennials looking for crypto rewards to savers looking for cash back.” — [Marqeta Q1 2022 transcript (Motley Fool)](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/05/11/marqeta-inc-mq-q1-2022-earnings-call-transcript/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/05/11/marqeta-inc-mq-q1-2022-earnings-call-transcript/))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- FY2023 Form 10‑K: “We do not handle or interact with cryptocurrency and we only process transactions… in fiat currencies… certain cryptocurrency businesses use our platform to provide card products…” — [Marqeta 2023 10‑K (SEC)](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1522540/000162828024007663/mq-20231231.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1522540/000162828024007663/mq-20231231.htm))

**Financial news / other:**
- Oct 28, 2020: “Coinbase to launch crypto debit card in U.S.… issued by MetaBank and powered by Marqeta.” — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2020/10/28/coinbase-to-launch-crypto-debit-card-in-us-for-retail-spending) ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2020/10/28/coinbase-to-launch-crypto-debit-card-in-us-for-retail-spending?utm_source=openai))  
- Oct 19, 2021: “Marqeta powering crypto spending and rewards products for Coinbase, Fold, Shakepay and Bakkt.” — [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20211019005398/en/Cryptocurrency-Innovators-Invest-in-New-Card-Solutions-With-Marqetas-Modern-Card-Issuing-Platform-Powering-Growth-of-New-Category) ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20211019005398/en/Cryptocurrency-Innovators-Invest-in-New-Card-Solutions-With-Marqetas-Modern-Card-Issuing-Platform-Powering-Growth-of-New-Category?utm_source=openai))  
- Oct 2022: “Blockchain.com Visa Card, powered by Marqeta, debuts with 50,000 sign‑ups.” — [PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/blockchaincom-visa-card-powered-by-marqeta-debuts-with-50-000-sign-ups-at-launch-301659945.html) ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/blockchaincom-visa-card-powered-by-marqeta-debuts-with-50-000-sign-ups-at-launch-301659945.html?utm_source=openai))

## ONE UNITED PROPERTIES

* Rank: 116 | Expo: 0.64 | id_str: ROJ8YZPDHWW8
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 2024 annual report, Q3 2024 results call (recording), Romanian financial press (May 2024)

### Firm Overview
One United Properties SA is a Bucharest‑based real estate developer focused on premium residential, mixed‑use, office and commercial projects, listed on the Bucharest Stock Exchange (BVB: ONE). Its investor materials and filings emphasize traditional property development and leasing activities in Romania. ([one.ro](https://www.one.ro/en/?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Searches across 2017–2024 found no evidence that the company holds cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet, operates mining, offers crypto financial services (exchange, brokerage, custody, token issuance), or supplies crypto‑specific infrastructure. The 2024 Annual Report details cash and bank deposits in RON/EUR/USD with no crypto asset line or treasury references to digital assets. A May 2024 press article notes a framework agreement with MetaWealth for listing certain rental apartments on a tokenization platform; however, this is described as distribution access to the developer’s properties and does not indicate One United issuing tokens, handling digital assets, or earning crypto‑linked fee income. Accordingly, no mechanism category is supported within the project definitions. ([bvb.ro](https://www.bvb.ro/infocont/infocont25/ONE_20250429195020_ONE-EN-FY2024-sm.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
With only conventional real‑estate operations evidenced in filings and investor materials, and no substantive crypto activity under holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr, the firm is assigned unclass = 1.0. The 2024 tokenization‑platform news suggests third‑party listing of property exposure but does not meet the threshold for finserv (no custody, trading, product issuance, or crypto collateral managed by the company). All other categories remain 0.0 based on lack of evidence in 2017–2024 sources. ([bvb.ro](https://www.bvb.ro/infocont/infocont25/ONE_20250429195020_ONE-EN-FY2024-sm.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Company hosts recordings of earnings calls, but publicly accessible full transcripts for 2024 were not identified; classification relies primarily on filings and reputable press. ([one.ro](https://www.one.ro/en/investor-relations/))  
- A May 2024 article mentions a framework agreement to list select rentals on a tokenization platform; scope, economics, and whether any digital‑asset handling by One United occurs are not detailed, so this was not treated as finserv exposure. ([revistabiz.ro](https://www.revistabiz.ro/acord-de-20-mil-euro-intre-one-united-properties-si-metawealth-ce-active-vor-fi-listate-pe-platforma-de-tokenizare-imobiliara/?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 12, 2024: Recording of the Conference Call with Analysts (English) — [One United IR page] (see Q3 2024 Results section). ([one.ro](https://www.one.ro/en/investor-relations/))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Apr 30, 2025: 2024 Annual Report — shows cash and bank deposits in RON/EUR/USD; no crypto asset line disclosed. [BVB filing PDF] ([bvb.ro](https://www.bvb.ro/infocont/infocont25/ONE_20250429195020_ONE-EN-FY2024-sm.pdf))

## PEARLABYSS

* Rank: 117 | Expo: 0.63 | id_str: KR7263750002
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Earnings presentations (3Q 2022; 4Q 2022), Korean business/gaming press (Nov 2021–Feb 2022)

### Firm Overview
Pearl Abyss Corp. is a South Korean video game developer and publisher best known for Black Desert Online and EVE Online via subsidiary CCP Games. Public investor materials emphasize online game development and live services, with revenue primarily from game publishing across global regions. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pearl_Abyss?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Searches across 2017–2024 found no evidence that Pearl Abyss holds cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet, operates mining, offers crypto financial services (exchange, brokerage, custody, token issuance), or supplies crypto‑specific infrastructure. Management materials in late 2021–2022 discussed exploring blockchain/NFT concepts and “Web3/Blockchain biz” at CCP Games, but these references were framed as exploration or future business rather than launched products or financial services, and no token issuance or custody by Pearl Abyss was identified in the sample window. Press coverage also noted that Pearl Abyss had “no plans to implement blockchain technology into Black Desert,” underscoring the exploratory nature of comments at the time. An MOU by subsidiary Nettention with PlayDapp to collaborate on a blockchain‑enabled P2P server network is a technology partnership, not a financial service or asset‑holding activity by Pearl Abyss. ([irsvc.teletogether.com](https://irsvc.teletogether.com/pearlabyss/pdf/pearlabyss2022Q3_eng.pdf?2=))

### Weight Assignment
Given only exploratory statements and a technology MOU, with no substantiated balance‑sheet crypto holdings, mining operations, crypto‑asset product issuance, or custody/trading services during 2017–2024, none of the mechanism categories meet the project’s substantive exposure threshold. The firm is therefore assigned unclass = 1.0, with all other mechanism weights at 0.0. ([irsvc.teletogether.com](https://irsvc.teletogether.com/pearlabyss/pdf/pearlabyss2022Q3_eng.pdf?2=))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Management remarks in 2021–2022 referenced NFTs/Web3 and “Web3/Blockchain biz” at CCP, which could imply future activity; however, within the 2017–2024 sample no concrete launch of crypto‑asset products, custody, or exchange services by Pearl Abyss was found. ([view.asiae.co.kr](https://view.asiae.co.kr/en/article/2021111109454230475?utm_source=openai))  
- Subsidiary Nettention’s 2022 blockchain‑P2P engine MOU is technical collaboration rather than direct crypto exposure under this framework. ([medium.com](https://medium.com/playdappgames/playdapp-signs-agreement-with-nettention-to-collaborate-on-a-blockchain-enabled-p2p-server-network-afb88e22217e))  
- Public earnings materials reviewed (including consolidated financial statement slides) did not show a crypto‑asset line; full Korean DART business reports were not relied on for specific asset line items here. ([irsvc.teletogether.com](https://irsvc.teletogether.com/pearlabyss/pdf/pearlabyss2022Q4_eng.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 9, 2022 (3Q22 earnings presentation): “CCP Games: Web3 / Blockchain biz.” — [Pearl Abyss IR PDF] (shows strategic exploration rather than launched services). ([irsvc.teletogether.com](https://irsvc.teletogether.com/pearlabyss/pdf/pearlabyss2022Q3_eng.pdf?2=))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Feb 14, 2023 (4Q22/annual IR deck): Consolidated statements presented with cash/financial assets; no dedicated crypto‑asset line observed. [Pearl Abyss IR PDF] ([irsvc.teletogether.com](https://irsvc.teletogether.com/pearlabyss/pdf/pearlabyss2022Q4_eng.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**
- Nov 11, 2021: CEO said during the Q3 conference call, “We are considering developing and servicing play‑to‑earn (P2E) and NFT games based on blockchain technology.” [Asia Business Daily] ([view.asiae.co.kr](https://view.asiae.co.kr/en/article/2021111109454230475?utm_source=openai))  
- Feb 16, 2022: Update clarified “no plans to implement blockchain technology into Black Desert” and that blockchain was only being explored for future business. [Massively Overpowered] ([massivelyop.com](https://massivelyop.com/2022/02/16/eve-and-black-desert-company-pearl-abyss-says-its-confident-it-can-create-a-stable-blockchain-ecosystem/?utm_source=openai))  
- Mar 30, 2022: Nettention (a Pearl Abyss subsidiary) signed an MOU with PlayDapp to collaborate on a blockchain‑enabled P2P server network (technology partnership). [PlayDapp Medium post] ([medium.com](https://medium.com/playdappgames/playdapp-signs-agreement-with-nettention-to-collaborate-on-a-blockchain-enabled-p2p-server-network-afb88e22217e))

## BNY MELLON STRATEGIC MUNICIPALS

* Rank: 118 | Expo: 0.62 | id_str: US05588W1080
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: SEC N-CSR annual report (FY 2024), Business Wire fund distribution press release (2024)

### Firm Overview
BNY Mellon Strategic Municipals, Inc. is a diversified, closed-end management investment company listed on the NYSE (ticker LEO). The fund seeks to maximize current income exempt from federal income tax by investing primarily in U.S. municipal securities, and is advised by BNY Mellon Investment Adviser, Inc. with Insight North America LLC as sub-adviser.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024, filings show the portfolio consists of long‑term municipal bond investments; no evidence indicates any cryptocurrency holdings, mining, custody, exchange, token issuance, or blockchain-based financial products at the fund level. The FY 2024 N‑CSR details the schedule of investments dominated by municipal securities and contains no references to digital assets or blockchain. Routine distribution announcements in 2024 likewise reference tax‑exempt municipal bond income, not crypto-linked activity. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/818972/000174177324004495/ncsr.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Because the fund operates solely as a municipal bond closed‑end fund with no documented digital-asset activity, none of the six mechanism categories (holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, infrastr) apply under the project’s substantive exposure thresholds. Accordingly, the classification assigns unclass = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/818972/000174177324004495/ncsr.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified specific to this fund within the 2017–2024 sample window; closed‑end funds like LEO typically do not host earnings calls, and none were located on common aggregators during this review.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aggregator check (no transcripts page available for LEO; holdings/quant pages only). [Seeking Alpha – LEO symbol pages](https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LEO/holdings)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Nov 26, 2024: Form N‑CSR (FY ended Sep 30, 2024) — shows long‑term municipal investments and fund details; no crypto references. [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/818972/000174177324004495/ncsr.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- Sep 30, 2024: “BNY Mellon Municipal Bond Closed‑End Funds Declare Distributions” — routine distribution notice for LEO; no digital‑asset products referenced. [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20240927457654/en/BNY-Mellon-Municipal-Bond-Closed-End-Funds-Declare-Distributions)

</small>

## UP FINTECH HOLDING ADR A 1:15

* Rank: 119 | Expo: 0.61 | id_str: US91531W1062
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Form 20-F (FY2023, filed Apr 22, 2024), Q1 2024 earnings call (June 5, 2024), company press releases (Jan 25 and June 5, 2024), financial news (CNBC May 31, 2021)

### Firm Overview
UP Fintech Holding Ltd., known as Tiger Brokers, is a Singapore-headquartered, NASDAQ‑listed online brokerage platform that enables retail and institutional clients to trade equities and other securities across global markets. In 2024, the firm expanded its Hong Kong offering to include spot cryptocurrency trading for professional investors alongside its traditional brokerage services.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s exposure to crypto arises through financial services: in January 2024 its Hong Kong subsidiary obtained a Type 1 license uplift from the Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) to provide virtual asset dealing services to professional investors, and in April 2024 it officially launched spot cryptocurrency trading in Hong Kong. Management reiterated on the Q1 2024 earnings call that the firm had launched this feature and positioned itself among the first mainstream online brokerages in Hong Kong offering spot crypto trading to professional investors. There is no evidence in filings of treasury crypto holdings, mining, or crypto‑specific infrastructure provision during 2017–2024. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/u/NASDAQ_TIGR_2023.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Given the explicit launch of spot cryptocurrency trading services in Hong Kong in April 2024 and the related SFC license uplift, UP Fintech’s crypto exposure is classified entirely as finserv. No transcript, filing, or credible news source indicates balance‑sheet crypto holdings, mining operations, or non‑financial crypto services in the 2017–2024 window. Accordingly, finserv is set to 1.0 and all other mechanism categories to 0.0. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/u/NASDAQ_TIGR_2023.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The Hong Kong spot crypto service was initially limited to professional investors; revenue contribution within 2017–2024 appears minor and is not separately disclosed. ([ir.itigerup.com](https://ir.itigerup.com/node/8196/pdf))  
- Earnings call transcripts were sourced via a third‑party aggregator; however, the statements align with contemporaneous company press releases and the Form 20‑F “Recent Developments” section. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/TIGR/transcripts/2024/1))  
- Earlier reports (2021) discussed intent to add crypto trading pending licenses; these do not on their own evidence exposure but support the timeline to the 2024 launch. ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2021/06/01/chinas-robinhood-rivals-pile-into-the-crypto-craze.html?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jun 5, 2024 (Q1 2024): “we uplifted our Type 1 license to include virtual asset dealing services for professional investors in the first quarter and officially launched this feature in April…” — [Roic.ai transcript]. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/TIGR/transcripts/2024/1))  
- Jun 5, 2024 (Q1 2024, Q&A): “As for crypto… we are now one of the first fintech broker in Hong Kong, allowing clients to trade spot crypto as well as 11 US Bitcoin ETFs.” — [Roic.ai transcript]. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/TIGR/transcripts/2024/1))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Apr 22, 2024: Form 20‑F FY2023 — “In January 2024, [Tiger Brokers HK]… upgraded its Type 1 license to include virtual asset dealing service… opening the door for providing cryptocurrency trading services to Professional Investor clients in Hong Kong.” — [UP Fintech 20‑F]. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/u/NASDAQ_TIGR_2023.pdf))  
- Jun 5, 2024: Q1 2024 results press release — “In Hong Kong, we officially launched spot cryptocurrency trading services for professional investor.” — [Company IR PDF]. ([ir.itigerup.com](https://ir.itigerup.com/node/8196/pdf))  
- Jan 25, 2024: Press release — SFC Type 1 license uplift to include virtual asset dealing service for professional investors. — [GlobeNewswire]. ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2024/1/25/2816350/0/en/UP-Fintech-Holding-Limited-Announces-Uplift-of-Type-1-License-by-Hong-Kong-SFC-to-include-Virtual-Asset-dealing-service-for-Professional-Investors.html?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- May 31, 2021: “Futu and Tiger Brokers said… they are applying for licenses in Singapore and the U.S. that will allow local customers to trade cryptocurrencies.” — [CNBC]. ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2021/06/01/chinas-robinhood-rivals-pile-into-the-crypto-craze.html?utm_source=openai))

</small>

## ADVANCED MICRO DEVICES

* Rank: 120 | Expo: 0.60 | id_str: US0079031078
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 1.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10‑K and 10‑Q filings (2018–2021), Q3 2018 and Q4 2018 earnings calls, Q2 2019 earnings call, AMD press releases (2018–2019), financial news (CNBC 2017; CoinDesk 2018)

### Firm Overview
Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD) is a US‑based semiconductor company listed on NASDAQ that designs CPUs and GPUs for client PCs, gaming, and data center markets. During 2017–2019, AMD’s discrete GPUs were also purchased by cryptocurrency miners, and the company has referenced blockchain as a compute workload for general‑purpose GPUs in its SEC filings.

### Exposure Channels Identified
AMD’s crypto exposure is via infrastructure: it sells general‑purpose GPUs that have at times been used for cryptocurrency mining. Management explicitly quantified “blockchain‑related” GPU revenue in late 2017 and early 2018 and later noted it had become negligible by late 2018/2019; filings also describe blockchain/cryptocurrency mining as a use case for GPUs. There is no evidence the company holds crypto assets on its balance sheet, operates mining, or offers crypto financial services. AMD also participated in blockchain‑adjacent initiatives (e.g., joining the Blockchain Game Alliance) consistent with its role as a compute supplier rather than a market participant. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/10/24/advanced-micro-devices-inc-amd-q3-2018-earnings-co.aspx?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
All identified exposure stems from the sale of compute hardware that can be used for blockchain/crypto workloads, not from owning, mining, or intermediating digital assets. Given repeated earnings‑call references to “blockchain‑related” GPU sales in 2017–2018, subsequent disclosure that such sales were negligible by late 2018/2019, and filings that list blockchain as a GPU workload, AMD is classified entirely as infrastr = 1.0, with all other mechanism categories at 0.0. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/10/24/advanced-micro-devices-inc-amd-q3-2018-earnings-co.aspx?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Crypto‑mining demand was episodic and not broken out as a sustained segment; later disclosures characterize blockchain‑related revenue as negligible. As a result, RevShare is coded 0 using the most recent clear figure in the 2017–2024 window, even though Q1–Q2 2018 briefly reached ~10% and ~6% of revenue, respectively. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/07/30/advanced-micro-devices-amd-q2-2019-earnings-call-t.aspx?utm_source=openai))  
- Transcript quotes are sourced from established aggregators (Motley Fool) rather than AMD’s own transcript PDFs; statements cross‑check with AMD filings referencing blockchain demand trends. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/10/24/advanced-micro-devices-inc-amd-q3-2018-earnings-co.aspx?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 24, 2018 (Q3 2018): “For comparative purposes, third quarter 2017 blockchain‑related GPU sales were approximately high‑single‑digit percent of overall AMD revenue… [Q3 2018] was negligible.” — [Motley Fool transcript]. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/10/24/advanced-micro-devices-inc-amd-q3-2018-earnings-co.aspx?utm_source=openai))  
- Jan 29, 2019 (Q4 2018): “blockchain‑related GPU revenue was negligible… Q2 2018 was still a very strong quarter… significant amount of blockchain sales.” — [Motley Fool transcript]. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/01/30/advanced-micro-devices-amd-q4-2018-earnings-confer.aspx?utm_source=openai))  
- Jul 30, 2019 (Q2 2019): “…lower overall graphics sales due to negligible blockchain‑related revenue in the second quarter of 2019.” — [Motley Fool transcript]. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/07/30/advanced-micro-devices-amd-q2-2019-earnings-call-t.aspx?utm_source=openai))

**Filings (10‑K / 10‑Q / press slides):**
- Oct 31, 2018: Form 10‑Q — notes lower Radeon sales “caused primarily by… the decline in blockchain‑related demand.” — [SEC filing]. ([ir.amd.com](https://ir.amd.com/financial-information/sec-filings/content/0000002488-18-000189/amd0929201810q.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- Jul 31, 2019: Form 10‑Q — “decline in blockchain‑related demand” impacted Radeon channel products. — [SEC filing]. ([ir.amd.com](https://ir.amd.com/financial-information/sec-filings/content/0000002488-19-000104/amd0629201910q.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- Jan 29, 2021: Form 10‑K (FY2020) — describes blockchain as a graphics compute market; blockchain apps can run on CPUs/GPUs. — [SEC filing]. ([ir.amd.com](https://ir.amd.com/sec-filings/content/0001628280-21-001185/amd-20201226.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- Jan 29, 2019: Q4 2018 earnings slides — segment commentary alongside the negligible blockchain‑related revenue noted on the call. — [AMD IR slides]. ([ir.amd.com](https://ir.amd.com/financial-information/sec-filings/content/0000002488-19-000004/q418earningsslidesfinalc.htm?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Jul 26, 2017: “Wall Street stunned over AMD’s cryptocurrency mining demand.” — [CNBC]. ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2017/07/26/wall-street-stunned-over-amds-cryptocurrency-mining-demand.html?utm_source=openai))  
- Oct 25, 2017: “AMD CEO sees ‘leveling off’ in crypto‑mining demand… [and] a commercial blockchain component… likely to continue into the medium term.” — [CoinDesk]. ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2017/10/25/amd-ceo-sees-leveling-off-in-cryptocurrency-mining-demand?utm_source=openai))  
- Apr 26, 2018: “Blockchain was approximately 10% of AMD revenue in Q1 2018,” per CFO on the call. — [CoinDesk]. ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2018/04/26/crypto-mining-made-up-10-of-amds-revenue-in-q1?utm_source=openai))  
- Jul 25, 2018: Q2 2018 call recap — blockchain about 6% of revenue; outlook tempered by lower blockchain GPU sales. — [AlphaSpread (call highlights)]. ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/nasdaq/amd/investor-relations/earnings-call/q2-2018?utm_source=openai))  
- Dec 13, 2019: AMD joins the Blockchain Game Alliance and partners on blockchain‑based gaming platforms. — [AMD press release]. ([ir.amd.com](https://ir.amd.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/922/amd-and-industry-partners-to-develop-new-blockchain-based-gaming-platforms?utm_source=openai))

</small>

## BANK NEGARA INDONESIA

* Rank: 121 | Expo: 0.58 | id_str: ID1000096605
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Company press releases (2018–2019), 3Q 2024 earnings call deck, Indonesian financial media coverage

### Firm Overview
PT Bank Negara Indonesia (Persero) Tbk is a large Indonesian state‑owned commercial bank listed in Jakarta, with operations spanning retail, corporate, transaction banking and international remittances. Its public disclosures indicate selective adoption of blockchain technology to support trade finance and remittance processes, while it remains a conventional bank under Indonesian regulation. ([bni.co.id](https://bni.co.id/en-us/corporate/international/smart-remittance))

### Exposure Channels Identified
BNI disclosed in May 2018 that it “utilizes blockchain technology to improve banking business performance for trade finance and remittance products,” following an MoU with a technology partner; local media the same week reported the initiative would target efficiency in export‑import documentation and remittances. These activities place BNI’s exposure in crypto financial services insofar as blockchain rails are used within banking products, without evidence the bank offers crypto trading, custody, or tokenized investment products. Subsequent communications referenced additional blockchain‑adjacent pilots (e.g., “blockchain financing” in an agripreneur program), but there is no indication of material revenue from digital assets or any balance‑sheet crypto holdings. Indonesian rules during 2017–2024 also prohibited the use of crypto as a means of payment, reinforcing the absence of retail crypto payment services at BNI. ([bni.co.id](https://www.bni.co.id/en-us/home/bni-news/news/articleid/3978))

### Weight Assignment
All identified exposure arises from BNI integrating blockchain into existing financial services (trade finance and remittance), not from issuing crypto products, owning crypto, mining, or supplying hardware/infrastructure. Therefore the classification is concentrated in finserv = 1.0, with all other mechanism categories at 0.0. Materiality appears negligible in reported results; accordingly RevShare = 0 and AssetShare = 0 based on available filings and presentations within the 2017–2024 window. ([bni.co.id](https://www.bni.co.id/en-us/home/bni-news/news/articleid/3978))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- No earnings‑call transcript for 2017–2024 could be retrieved despite search; only an earnings‑call presentation deck for 3Q‑2024 was accessible. Certainty is capped at 2 per protocol. ([bni.co.id](https://www.bni.co.id/Portals/1/BNI/Perusahaan/HubunganInvestor/Docs/2024/BBNI-Corporate-Presentation-3Q24.pdf))  
- Company and media statements describe “blockchain” use but do not specify whether public cryptoassets (e.g., XRP for on‑demand liquidity) were used; related regional initiatives (e.g., by SBI Remit) mention Indonesia as a destination but do not identify BNI as a counterparty. As such, exposure is coded to blockchain‑enabled financial services, not crypto‑asset intermediation. ([remit.co.jp](https://www.remit.co.jp/en/kaigaisoukin/information/release20230906/))  
- Indonesian regulation during the period banned crypto as a payment instrument, limiting the likelihood of bank‑provided crypto payment products. ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2018/01/15/indonesia-central-bank-cryptocurrency-payments-not-legitimate?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- 3Q‑2024, corporate presentation deck: “BNI Open API 280 services (approved by regulator), the most compared to peers… BNI is one of 20 BI Fast Sponsor Bank.” — [BNI 3Q‑2024 Earnings Call deck]. ([bni.co.id](https://www.bni.co.id/Portals/1/BNI/Perusahaan/HubunganInvestor/Docs/2024/BBNI-Corporate-Presentation-3Q24.pdf))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- May 11, 2018, press release: “BNI utilizes blockchain technology to improve… trade finance and remittance products.” — [BNI]. ([bni.co.id](https://www.bni.co.id/en-us/home/bni-news/news/articleid/3978))  
- Oct 15, 2019, press release: agripreneur program to build “blockchain financing” between farmers and ecosystem partners. — [BNI]. ([bni.co.id](https://www.bni.co.id/id-id/beranda/kabar-bni/berita/articleid/6165/bni-inisiasi-program-cetak-1-000-agripreneur-di-pedesaan?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- May 11, 2018: “BNI to adopt blockchain to increase revenue via export‑import recording and remittances.” — [Katadata]. ([katadata.co.id](https://katadata.co.id/digital/nasional/5e9a55f6ef3e6/bni-targetkan-peningkatan-pendapatan-dengan-adopsi-blockchain?utm_source=openai))  
- May 18, 2018: “Anquan (powered by Zilliqa) inks deal with BNI to build blockchain apps for remittance and trade finance.” — [LegitGambling]. ([legitgambling.com](https://www.legitgambling.com/news/anquan-powered-by-zilliqa-inks-deal-with-bni-bank/?utm_source=openai))  
- Jan 15, 2018: Indonesia’s central bank states cryptocurrencies are not a legitimate instrument of payment. — [CoinDesk]. ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2018/01/15/indonesia-central-bank-cryptocurrency-payments-not-legitimate?utm_source=openai))

## WHERE FOOD COMES FROM

* Rank: 122 | Expo: 0.57 | id_str: US96327X2009
* Weight summary: holders 1.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: 10-K (FY2023), Q2 2024 10-Q, Q4 2023 earnings call, Q3 2021 earnings call

### Firm Overview
Where Food Comes From, Inc. is a U.S.-based provider of third‑party verification, certification and related software for food production practices, serving producers, packers and retailers across species and categories. It is listed on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the ticker WFCF. ([library.wherefoodcomesfrom.com](https://library.wherefoodcomesfrom.com/documents/proxy-meeting-materials/2023-Form-10K-Document-02092024-clean.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Within the 2017–2024 sample window, company filings disclose small “digital assets (cryptocurrency)” held on the balance sheet and accounted for as indefinite‑lived intangibles under then‑current GAAP. As of December 31, 2023 (and 2022), the carrying value of digital assets was $116,000; the company reported no sales and no impairment losses related to these holdings, and this balance was unchanged as of June 30, 2024. There is no evidence in filings or calls that the firm mined crypto, intermediated crypto for customers, or provided crypto financial services. ([library.wherefoodcomesfrom.com](https://library.wherefoodcomesfrom.com/documents/proxy-meeting-materials/2023-Form-10K-Document-02092024-clean.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
The only documented crypto exposure mechanism in 2017–2024 is treasury‑style holding of cryptocurrency on the balance sheet; there is no indication of mining activity, exchange/custody operations, or crypto‑specific hardware/infrastructure provisioning. Accordingly, holders = 1.0 and all other mechanism categories = 0.0. Given the very small absolute amount of digital assets relative to total assets of $16.3 million at year‑end 2023, revenue share is coded 0 and asset share is coded 1 (<5%). ([library.wherefoodcomesfrom.com](https://library.wherefoodcomesfrom.com/documents/proxy-meeting-materials/2023-Form-10K-Document-02092024-clean.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Filings do not specify the type(s) of cryptocurrency or any explicit treasury policy; disclosures only note measurement and balances. ([library.wherefoodcomesfrom.com](https://library.wherefoodcomesfrom.com/documents/proxy-meeting-materials/2023-Form-10K-Document-02092024-clean.pdf))  
- Mentions of “Bitcoin” in calls appear incidental and not indicative of operating exposure; no call discussed crypto services, mining, or strategy. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/WFCF/documents/transcripts/q4-2023))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 21, 2024 (Q4 2023), transcript: “We delivered a strong fourth quarter… [and] report increased revenue and profitability for the full year as well.” — [Fintool]. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/WFCF/documents/transcripts/q4-2023))  
- Nov 10, 2021 (Q3 2021), transcript: “Revenue in the third quarter and nine‑month period increased 6% and 11% year‑over‑year.” — [ROIC.ai]. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/WFCF/transcripts/2021/3))

**Filings (10‑K / 10‑Q):**
- Feb 15, 2024, Form 10‑K (FY2023): “As of December 31, 2023 and 2022, the carrying value of our digital assets held was $116,000… we have not sold any digital assets.” — [Company PDF of 10‑K]. ([library.wherefoodcomesfrom.com](https://library.wherefoodcomesfrom.com/documents/proxy-meeting-materials/2023-Form-10K-Document-02092024-clean.pdf))  
- Aug 8, 2024, Form 10‑Q (Q2 2024): “As of June 30, 2024 and December 31, 2023, the carrying value of our digital assets held was $116,000” and no impairment losses. — [SEC XBRL/PDF]. ([d5bp8bijhhba2.cloudfront.net](https://d5bp8bijhhba2.cloudfront.net/pdf/9cbddeeff502df75a8b17501fca523272734849150f50293942cf8ada8e124ca.pdf))  
- FY2023 total assets $16.286 million (for asset share context). — [Form 10‑K FY2023]. ([library.wherefoodcomesfrom.com](https://library.wherefoodcomesfrom.com/documents/proxy-meeting-materials/2023-Form-10K-Document-02092024-clean.pdf))

## AMC ENTERTAINMENT HDG. CL.A

* Rank: 123 | Expo: 0.56 | id_str: US00165C3025
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2022–2023), Q3 2021 earnings call, company press releases; major financial/crypto news coverage (2021–2022)

### Firm Overview
AMC Entertainment Holdings, Inc. is a U.S.-based cinema operator and one of the world’s largest theatrical exhibition companies, listed on the NYSE under the ticker AMC. Within 2017–2024, management engaged in limited crypto‑related initiatives focused on enabling cryptocurrency payments via a third‑party processor and marketing campaigns involving NFTs rather than maintaining digital assets on the balance sheet.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Beginning in Q4 2021, AMC enabled customers in U.S. markets to pay online for tickets, concessions and gift cards using select cryptocurrencies via a processor; company filings emphasize these transactions settle in U.S. dollars and that AMC did not hold cryptocurrency during 2021–2022. ([investor.amctheatres.com](https://investor.amctheatres.com/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001104659-23-105014/0001104659-23-105014.pdf))  
On its Q3 2021 earnings call, management also discussed exploring acceptance of Dogecoin and Shiba Inu, stated explicitly that AMC would not hold crypto on its balance sheet, and noted preliminary discussions around NFTs and even the idea of an AMC‑branded cryptocurrency. ([tickertrends.io](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/AMC/Q3-earnings-transcript-2021))  
AMC and Sony partnered to distribute promotional Spider‑Man NFTs to qualifying ticket buyers, with redemption handled on the WAX blockchain. ([investor.amctheatres.com](https://investor.amctheatres.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/84/members-of-amc-stubs-and-investor-connect-to-receive-an-exclusive-spider-man-nft-with-an-advance-ticket-purchase-for-opening-day-of-sony-pictures-spider-man-no-way-home-at-all-amc-theatres-u-s-locations-while-supplies-last?utm_source=openai))  
There is no evidence of crypto mining, custody/exchange operations, or crypto‑specific hardware/infrastructure provisioning in the firm’s activities through 2024. ([investor.amctheatres.com](https://investor.amctheatres.com/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001104659-23-105014/0001104659-23-105014.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
AMC’s crypto involvement in 2017–2024 is limited to merchant acceptance (facilitated by BitPay) and NFT promotions, with filings explicitly stating that crypto transactions settle in USD and that AMC did not hold cryptocurrencies. As such, none of the mechanism categories (holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, infrastr) apply in a substantive way, and the firm is coded unclass = 1.0. Management commentary and outreach to crypto‑interested retail shareholders support invbase = 1, reflecting a shareholder base that engaged with and influenced crypto‑related decisions (e.g., Dogecoin/Shiba polls and acceptance timeline). ([investor.amctheatres.com](https://investor.amctheatres.com/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001104659-23-105014/0001104659-23-105014.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Management said in November 2021 it was exploring the feasibility of launching an AMC cryptocurrency and pursuing NFT collaborations; no evidence indicates a launched token or balance‑sheet crypto holdings within the 2017–2024 window. ([tickertrends.io](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/AMC/Q3-earnings-transcript-2021))  
- Crypto payment functionality relies on a third‑party processor (BitPay); disclosures do not quantify crypto‑denominated sales, but filings indicate USD settlement and no holdings, suggesting immaterial direct exposure. ([investor.amctheatres.com](https://investor.amctheatres.com/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001104659-23-105014/0001104659-23-105014.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 9, 2021 (Q3 2021), transcript: “While we will accept cryptocurrency, we will not hold it on our balance sheet…” — [Motley Fool]. (([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/09/amc-entertainment-holdings-amc-q3-2021-earnings-ca/?utm_source=openai)))  
- Nov 2021 (Q3 2021), transcript: “We are on track… to accept Bitcoin, Ethereum, Litecoin, Bitcoin Cash… [and] figuring out how to take Shiba Inu… we do not have to hold cryptocurrency on our balance sheet.” — [TickerTrends]. (([tickertrends.io](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/AMC/Q3-earnings-transcript-2021)))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Feb 28, 2023, Form 10‑K (FY2022): “In Q4 2021, we introduced the ability… to pay… with cryptocurrencies… These transactions all settle in U.S. Dollars. We did not hold any cryptocurrency during 2022 and 2021.” — [AMC IR, 10‑K PDF]. (([investor.amctheatres.com](https://investor.amctheatres.com/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001104659-23-105014/0001104659-23-105014.pdf)))  
- Nov 28, 2021, press release: AMC/Sony offer 86,000 Spider‑Man NFTs; redemption on WAX. — [AMC IR press release]. (([investor.amctheatres.com](https://investor.amctheatres.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/84/members-of-amc-stubs-and-investor-connect-to-receive-an-exclusive-spider-man-nft-with-an-advance-ticket-purchase-for-opening-day-of-sony-pictures-spider-man-no-way-home-at-all-amc-theatres-u-s-locations-while-supplies-last?utm_source=openai)))

**Financial news / other:**
- Aug 9–10, 2021: “AMC says it will accept bitcoin as payment by year‑end,” announced on earnings call. — [CNBC]. (([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2021/08/09/amc-says-it-will-accept-bitcoin-as-payment-for-movie-tickets-by-year-end.html?utm_source=openai)))  
- Apr 18, 2022: AMC mobile app accepts Dogecoin and Shiba Inu via BitPay. — [TechCrunch]. (([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2022/04/18/the-amc-mobile-app-for-u-s-theaters-now-accepts-dogecoin-shiba-inu-and-other-cryptocurrencies/?utm_source=openai)))  
- BitPay merchant directory entry confirming AMC acceptance via BitPay. — [BitPay]. (([bitpay.com](https://www.bitpay.com/directory/amctheatres/?utm_source=openai)))

## MEITU

* Rank: 124 | Expo: 0.56 | id_str: KYG5966D1051
* Weight summary: holders 1.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: HKEX announcements (Mar–Apr 2021; Dec 2024), Interim Report 2021, Annual Report 2024; major financial/crypto news coverage (2021, 2024)

### Firm Overview
Meitu Inc. is a Hong Kong–listed technology company best known for its AI‑powered photo, video and design applications, with revenues primarily from premium subscriptions and advertising. Within 2017–2024, the company also made balance‑sheet investments in Bitcoin and Ether in 2021 and subsequently fully exited these crypto holdings in December 2024, realizing gains.

### Exposure Channels Identified
In March–April 2021, Meitu executed a board‑approved “Cryptocurrency Investment Plan,” purchasing approximately 31,000 Ether and 940.9 Bitcoin, accounting for the assets as intangible assets under IFRS and disclosing impairment testing in filings. Coinbase Institutional handled execution and custody for roughly $90 million of those purchases, per contemporaneous reporting and company statements. In December 2024, Meitu announced that it had sold all acquired Ether and Bitcoin and no longer held any cryptocurrencies, allocating 80% of net proceeds to a special dividend and the remainder to working capital. There is no evidence during 2017–2024 that Meitu engaged in crypto mining, exchange/brokerage custody services, or crypto‑specific infrastructure activities. ([www1.hkexnews.hk](https://www1.hkexnews.hk/listedco/listconews/sehk/2021/0307/2021030700045.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
The company’s crypto exposure from 2017–2024 was via direct treasury holdings of BTC and ETH disclosed in 2021, with subsequent impairment/reversal accounting and a complete disposal in December 2024. As such, exposure is assigned entirely to holders (1.0). No services, mining or infrastructure channels are supported by filings or calls. Given the full disposal by December 4, 2024, AssetShare is coded 0 based on the most recent period in‑scope; earlier interim‑2021 figures showed material but time‑limited holdings. ([corp-static.meitu.com](https://corp-static.meitu.com/corp-new/20210923/2021092301104_e.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- An accessible, quotable earnings‑call transcript could not be retrieved (major repositories list the calls but content is gated); classification is therefore anchored on HKEX announcements and annual/interim reports, and certainty is capped at 2. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/MEITU-INC-34353136/news/Transcript-Meitu-Inc-2021-Earnings-Call-Mar-30-2022-39911637/))  
- Investor‑base amplifier is set to 0 despite news‑driven share reactions in 2021/2024, as there is no explicit, persistent management commentary or third‑party characterization of Meitu as a primary crypto proxy. ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2021/03/08/crypto-chinese-app-meitu-buys-millions-worth-of-bitcoin-and-ethereum.html?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Mar 30, 2022 (FY2021) — earnings call listed; transcript content gated (no accessible quote). — [MarketScreener] (https://www.marketscreener.com/quote
…  
- Mar 30, 2022 (FY2021) — earnings call listed; transcript content gated (no accessible quote). — [MarketScreener](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/MEITU-INC-34353136/news/Transcript-Meitu-Inc-2021-Earnings-Call-Mar-30-2022-39911637/)  

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Dec 4, 2024 — “Disposals of Cryptocurrencies (Ether and Bitcoin)… After completion… the Group no longer holds any Ether or Bitcoin.” — [HKEX announcement PDF](https://www1.hkexnews.hk/listedco/listconews/sehk/2024/1204/2024120402120.pdf)  
- Apr 25, 2025 — Annual Report 2024: reversal of impairment losses on BTC of RMB68.1m and gain on disposal of US$79.63m; confirms all crypto sold by Dec 4, 2024. — [Meitu Annual Report 2024 PDF](https://corp-static.meitu.com/corp-new/20250425/2024%20Annual%20Report%20%28E%29.pdf)  
- Sep 23, 2021 — Interim Report 2021: purchased 31,000 ETH and 940.885 BTC; impairment recognized on BTC; accounting as intangible assets under IFRS (cost model). — [Meitu Interim Report 2021 PDF](https://corp-static.meitu.com/corp-new/20210923/2021092301104_e.pdf)  
- Apr 28, 2022 — Annual Report 2021: details of crypto acquisitions in Mar/Apr 2021 per board‑approved plan and HKEX announcements. — [HKEX Annual Report 2021 PDF](https://www.hkexnews.hk/listedco/listconews/sehk/2022/0428/2022042802528.pdf)

## INFOBEANS TECHNOLOGIES

* Rank: 125 | Expo: 0.56 | id_str: INE344S01016
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q4 FY23 earnings call (May 12, 2023); Annual Report FY2022–23; company website solutions/blog pages (NFT marketplace, blockchain initiative)

### Firm Overview
InfoBeans Technologies Limited is an India-based IT services company listed on NSE/BSE that provides digital transformation and product engineering services for enterprise clients across the US, Europe, Middle East and India. Its portfolio spans Salesforce and ServiceNow implementation, UX/design, cloud engineering, automation/QA, and select emerging-tech offerings.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management referenced business development around “blockchain” and “Web3” during the Q4 FY23 earnings call while describing events targeted to generate new client work, indicating consulting/build services linked to crypto-related technologies. ([alphastreet.com](https://alphastreet.com/india/infobeans-technologies-ltd-infobean-q4-fy23-earnings-concall-transcript/))  
The FY2022–23 Annual Report states an increased focus on capabilities in “AI, Blockchain,” notes the company “landed our first client in Enterprise Blockchain,” and lists “Web3 Consulting, Implementation & Enterprise Blockchain” within its services. ([infobeans.ai](https://infobeans.ai/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Annual-Report-FY-2022-2023.pdf))  
InfoBeans’ website markets an “NFT Marketplace” solution and blog coverage of the Future Blockchain Summit, noting the firm had “started our Blockchain initiative,” further substantiating non-financial, build/consulting exposure to crypto ecosystems. ([infobeans.ai](https://infobeans.ai/nft-marketplace/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
All evidenced crypto exposure arises from providing software/consulting around blockchain, Web3 and NFTs to clients; there is no indication of proprietary token issuance, exchange/custody, crypto mining, or treasury crypto holdings in filings or calls. Accordingly, exposure is assigned entirely to bizserv (1.0). Given disclosures describe capability focus and at least one enterprise blockchain client without segment revenue quantification, RevShare is coded 1 (<~5%) and AssetShare 0. ([infobeans.ai](https://infobeans.ai/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Annual-Report-FY-2022-2023.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Earnings-call references to blockchain/Web3 are brief and framed around marketing/events rather than quantified revenue; no separate crypto revenue line is disclosed. ([alphastreet.com](https://alphastreet.com/india/infobeans-technologies-ltd-infobean-q4-fy23-earnings-concall-transcript/))  
- Company materials emphasize many non-crypto practices; blockchain work appears ancillary, so investor-base “crypto proxy” effects are not evidenced. ([infobeans.ai](https://infobeans.ai/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Annual-Report-FY-2022-2023.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 12, 2023 (Q4 FY23) — “meeting clients on blockchain, on emerging technologies, Web3 and Design Trends.” — [AlphaStreet transcript] (https://alphastreet.com/india/infobeans-technologies-ltd-infobean-q4-fy23-earnings-concall-transcript/)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- FY2022–23 Annual Report — “increased focus on … AI, Blockchain… already landed our first client in Enterprise Blockchain.” — [Annual Report PDF] (https://infobeans.ai/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Annual-Report-FY-2022-2023.pdf)  
- FY2022–23 Annual Report — “Web3 Consulting, Implementation & Enterprise Blockchain” listed under services. — [Annual Report PDF] (https://infobeans.ai/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Annual-Report-FY-2022-2023.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Product page — “NFT Marketplace” solutions (multi‑chain, mint/buy/sell/trade). — [InfoBeans website] (https://infobeans.ai/nft-marketplace/)  
- Blog — Future Blockchain Summit write‑up noting the firm had “started our Blockchain initiative.” — [InfoBeans blog] (https://infobeans.ai/future-blockchain-summit-the-middle-easts-flagship-event-for-blockchain-and-cryptocurrency/)

</small>

## PAYPAL HOLDINGS

* Rank: 126 | Expo: 0.55 | id_str: US70450Y1038
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: 10-K FY2024; Q4 2020 buyside and analyst calls; Q3 2023 earnings call; PayPal newsroom releases (2020–2024); SEC 10-Q (Q3 2023)

### Firm Overview
PayPal Holdings, Inc. is a U.S.-based digital payments company operating PayPal and Venmo, providing online checkout, peer‑to‑peer transfers, merchant processing and related services globally. Since October 2020 it has offered consumer crypto services, and in August 2023 it introduced PayPal USD (PYUSD), a U.S. dollar‑denominated stablecoin issued by Paxos, with subsequent integrations across PayPal and Venmo. ([newsroom.paypal-corp.com](https://newsroom.paypal-corp.com/2020-10-21-PayPal-Launches-New-Service-Enabling-Users-to-Buy-Hold-and-Sell-Cryptocurrency?97df0f5c_page=1&ed4932d4_page=10&f9a368b5_page=12&utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
PayPal provides crypto financial services: consumers can buy, hold, sell, convert and transfer supported cryptocurrencies within PayPal and Venmo, use “Checkout with Crypto,” and access PYUSD for payments and transfers; custodial services are provided via licensed third‑party trust companies (e.g., Paxos). ([newsroom.paypal-corp.com](https://newsroom.paypal-corp.com/2020-10-21-PayPal-Launches-New-Service-Enabling-Users-to-Buy-Hold-and-Sell-Cryptocurrency?97df0f5c_page=1&ed4932d4_page=10&f9a368b5_page=12&utm_source=openai))  
Management highlighted PYUSD on earnings calls as “the first regulated stablecoin by a global payments company,” underscoring PayPal’s role in stablecoin‑enabled P2P and commerce flows. ([nasdaq.com](https://www.nasdaq.com/articles/paypal-pypl-q3-2023-earnings-call-transcript))  
Filings show PayPal records a “crypto asset safeguarding liability” with a corresponding asset for customer crypto held by custodians; these are measured at fair value and were $2.9B as of December 31, 2024 (Bitcoin, Ethereum and other), evidencing ongoing operational handling of digital assets for customers. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1633917/000163391725000019/pypl-20241231.htm))  
No evidence indicates PayPal mines crypto or holds crypto as a treasury investment; crypto activity is facilitative/custodial and product‑driven. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1633917/000163391725000019/pypl-20241231.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence overwhelmingly supports classification as finserv. PayPal directly enables retail crypto trading, payments, transfers and a USD stablecoin program (issued by Paxos) across PayPal and Venmo, with related custodial/safeguarding accounting in SEC filings; there is no indication of proprietary mining or deliberate treasury HODL. Therefore, finserv is set to 1.0 and all other mechanism weights to 0.0. Management has also stated crypto revenue is recognized on a net basis and was not a material driver at launch, guiding RevShare = 1 (<~5%). The presence of recognized safeguarding assets for customer crypto on the balance sheet (about $2.9B vs. total assets ~$81.6B in 2024) supports AssetShare = 1 (<~5%). ([newsroom.paypal-corp.com](https://newsroom.paypal-corp.com/2020-10-21-PayPal-Launches-New-Service-Enabling-Users-to-Buy-Hold-and-Sell-Cryptocurrency?97df0f5c_page=1&ed4932d4_page=10&f9a368b5_page=12&utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- PayPal recognizes crypto revenue on a net basis and does not break out a separate crypto revenue line, making precise revenue share estimation difficult. ([s205.q4cdn.com](https://s205.q4cdn.com/875401827/files/doc_financials/2020/q4/PayPal-Q4-2020-Buyside-Call-Transcript.pdf))  
- PYUSD is issued by Paxos; PayPal’s role spans distribution, wallet integration and payments. Regulatory posture evolved during 2023–2024 (including an SEC subpoena disclosure), but these factors do not alter the mechanism classification. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1633917/000163391723000156/pypl-20230930.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 29, 2024 (Q3 2024) — “we’ve launched the first regulated stablecoin by a global payments company in PYUSD, connecting our PayPal and Venmo ecosystems...” — [Nasdaq transcript mirror] (https://www.nasdaq.com/articles/paypal-pypl-q3-2023-earnings-call-transcript)  
- Feb 3, 2021 (Q4 2020 Analyst Call) — “But crypto… to see an outsized impact… we’re a little bit of a ways out right now.” — [PayPal IR PDF] (https://s205.q4cdn.com/875401827/files/doc_financials/2020/q4/PayPal-Q4-2020-Analyst-Call-Transcript.pdf)  
- Feb 4, 2021 (Q4 2020 Buyside Call) — “we account for this on a net revenue basis… recognize a spread… and a transaction fee… [it’s] not a material driver of our revenue or earnings performance in 2021.” — [PayPal IR PDF] (https://s205.q4cdn.com/875401827/files/doc_financials/2020/q4/PayPal-Q4-2020-Buyside-Call-Transcript.pdf)

**Filings (10‑K / 10‑Q):**
- FY2024 10‑K — “we are an agent when facilitating cryptocurrency transactions… we recognize a crypto asset safeguarding liability with a corresponding safeguarding asset”; crypto safeguarding asset/liability $2.886B at 12/31/2024. — [SEC 10‑K] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1633917/000163391725000019/pypl-20241231.htm)  
- Q3 2023 10‑Q — customer crypto assets (BTC, ETH, other) and matching safeguarding liability/asset; disclosure of third‑party custodians and PYUSD program/subpoena. — [SEC 10‑Q] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1633917/000163391723000156/pypl-20230930.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- Oct 21, 2020 — Launch enabling U.S. users to buy, hold and sell crypto (Bitcoin, Ethereum, Bitcoin Cash, Litecoin). — [PayPal Newsroom] (https://newsroom.paypal-corp.com/2020-10-21-PayPal-Launches-New-Service-Enabling-Users-to-Buy-Hold-and-Sell-Cryptocurrency?97df0f5c_page=1&ed4932d4_page=10&f9a368b5_page=12)  
- Mar 30, 2021 — “Checkout with Crypto” allows conversion to fiat at checkout for purchases. — [CNBC] (https://www.cnbc.com/2021/03/30/paypal-launches-crypto-checkout-service.html)  
- Apr 20, 2021 — Venmo rolls out crypto buy/hold/sell to its users. — [PayPal Newsroom] (https://newsroom.paypal-corp.com/2021-04-20-Introducing-Crypto-on-Venmo)  
- Aug 7, 2023 — PayPal launches PYUSD stablecoin (issued by Paxos). — [PayPal Newsroom] (https://newsroom.paypal-corp.com/2023-08-07-PayPal-Launches-U-S-Dollar-Stablecoin)  
- Apr 4, 2024 — Xoom enables PYUSD as a funding option for cross‑border transfers. — [PayPal Newsroom] (https://newsroom.paypal-corp.com/2024-04-04-Xoom-Enables-PayPal-USD-as-a-Funding-Option-for-Cross-Border-Money-Transfers)

</small>

## TCR2 THERAPEUTICS DEAD - DELIST.02/06/23

* Rank: 127 | Expo: 0.55 | id_str: US87808K1060
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2019–2021), Q1 2019 earnings call, 2023 merger press releases

### Firm Overview
TCR2 Therapeutics Inc. was a U.S. clinical‑stage biotechnology company focused on engineered T‑cell therapies using its TRuC T‑cell platform for solid tumors and hematologic malignancies. On June 1, 2023, TCR2 completed a strategic combination with Adaptimmune Therapeutics and ceased trading as a standalone company. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1750019/000156459020013856/tcrr-10k_20191231.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across SEC filings in 2019 and 2021 and earnings call materials reviewed for 2017–2024, there is no evidence that TCR2 engaged with cryptocurrencies through treasury holdings, mining, customer‑facing crypto financial services, ecosystem business services, or crypto‑directed infrastructure. The 10‑Ks detail R&D, clinical programs, and operating matters without discussion of digital assets, and the Q1 2019 earnings call centers on TRuC‑T platform progress and clinical development. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1750019/000156459020013856/tcrr-10k_20191231.htm))  
Given the company’s pure‑play biotechnology focus and subsequent merger into Adaptimmune in 2023, no crypto‑related mechanisms affecting equity appear during the sample period (January 2017–December 2024). ([adaptimmune.com](https://www.adaptimmune.com/investors-and-media/news-center/press-releases/detail/247/adaptimmune-announces-completion-of-strategic-combination))

### Weight Assignment
No substantive crypto exposure was identified in transcripts, SEC filings, or reputable financial news during the sample window. In line with the classification rules, all mechanism categories are set to 0.0 and unclass is set to 1.0. With no crypto revenue or asset disclosures, RevShare and AssetShare are coded NA, and there is no evidence of a crypto‑sensitive investor base, so invbase = 0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1750019/000156459020013856/tcrr-10k_20191231.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The company merged with Adaptimmune effective June 1, 2023, limiting standalone disclosures thereafter; however, pre‑merger materials likewise show no crypto involvement. No additional ambiguities identified. ([ir.stockpr.com](https://ir.stockpr.com/adaptimmune/sec-filings-email/content/0001558370-25-003544/adap-20241231x10k.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 13, 2019 (Q1 2019) — “we are pioneering the development of a novel T‑cell engineering platform…” — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/05/14/tcr2-therapeutics-inc-tcrr-q1-2019-earnings-call-t.aspx)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- FY2019 10‑K — Business description of TRuC‑T cell platform; no references to cryptocurrency/blockchain. — [SEC 10‑K](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1750019/000156459020013856/tcrr-10k_20191231.htm)

## IDEANOMICS

* Rank: 128 | Expo: 0.55 | id_str: US45166V2051
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.7, bizserv 0.3, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K (FY2018), S-1/A (2019), Q4 2018 earnings call, Q4 2020 earnings call, 2021 sustainability report

### Firm Overview
Ideanomics, Inc. is a U.S.-based company that, during 2018–2019, pursued a fintech strategy centered on blockchain-enabled digital asset creation and trading platforms before pivoting primarily to electric-vehicle-related businesses from 2020 onward. Earlier filings describe plans to tokenize securities and develop an ATS trading venue (DBOT) using blockchain technology. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/837852/000114420419017205/tv516467_10k.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management and SEC filings from 2018–2019 describe a business model focused on digital asset securitization and “tokenization,” including intentions to use Ethereum-standard tokens and to build blockchain-enabled issuance and secondary trading via DBOT ATS and Velocity Ledger. This constitutes direct exposure to crypto financial services (issuance, trading infrastructure, and advisory tied to digital assets). ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/837852/000114420419017205/tv516467_10k.htm))  
On the Q4 2018 call, leaders highlighted efforts to commercialize “AI and blockchain” and referenced digital asset management and a potential settlement token, reinforcing a services-based crypto strategy. ([nasdaq.com](https://www.nasdaq.com/articles/ideanomics-inc-idex-q4-2018-earnings-conference-call-transcript-2019-04-08-0))  
By Q4 2020, management noted that 2019 gross profit was “principally from the sale of digital asset management services,” but also stated that this contract produced no revenue in 2020, indicating the crypto-finserv exposure was time‑bound and later de‑emphasized. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/03/31/ideanomics-inc-idex-q4-2020-earnings-call-transcri/))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence shows substantive but time‑limited crypto involvement centered on issuing, managing, and enabling trading of tokenized assets (financial services), with additional activity framed as advisory/technology enablement around blockchain (non‑financial business services). Accordingly, finserv is set to 0.7 and bizserv to 0.3. No credible evidence was found of proprietary crypto treasury holdings, mining, or crypto‑directed hardware/hosting, so holders, miners, and infrastr are 0.0. Given clear transcript quotes and SEC filings, certainty is scored at 3. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/837852/000114420419017205/tv516467_10k.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The company pivoted away from crypto-related revenues after 2019; filings and disclosures indicate no digital asset management revenue in 2020 and subsequent years, so current-period materiality appears minimal despite earlier focus. Materiality coding reflects the most recent clearly evidenced period within 2017–2024 (RevShare = 0; AssetShare = 0), though 2019 saw crypto-linked revenue dominate results; this temporal shift is noted here. No specific evidence was found of a crypto‑sensitive investor base. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/03/31/ideanomics-inc-idex-q4-2020-earnings-call-transcri/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Mar 31, 2021 (Q4 2020) — “Gross profit for 2019 was $43.1 million, principally from the sale of digital asset management services… not expected to produce revenues for the foreseeable future.” — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/03/31/ideanomics-inc-idex-q4-2020-earnings-call-transcri/)  
- Apr 8, 2019 (Q4 2018) — “It is part of our initiative to commercialize our AI and blockchain efforts…”; discussion of digital asset management group and potential settlement token. — [Nasdaq](https://www.nasdaq.com/articles/ideanomics-inc-idex-q4-2018-earnings-conference-call-transcript-2019-04-08-0)

**Filings (10‑K / registration statements):**
- FY2018 10‑K — plans to securitize assets and tokenize securities on blockchain; ERC‑20 referenced; DBOT ATS and Velocity Ledger described. — [SEC 10‑K](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/837852/000114420419017205/tv516467_10k.htm)  
- Aug 2, 2019 S‑1/A — further detail on tokenization strategy and DBOT blockchain‑enabled issuance/trading platform. — [SEC S‑1/A](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/837852/000114420419024039/tv520550_s1a.htm)  
- Jan 2022 Sustainability Report — table showing $40.7M digital asset management services revenue in 2019 and none in 2020. — [Company report](https://ideanomics.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/01/2021_IDEX-Sustainability-Report-011021.pdf)  
- Jan 2023 S‑1 — confirms no digital asset management revenue in 2020 vs. $40.7M in 2019. — [SEC S‑1](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0000837852/000110465923005246/idex-20220930xs1.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- Apr 2021 — Coverage of FY2020 results reiterating the $40.7M 2019 digital asset management services contribution and none thereafter. — [Cantech Letter](https://www.cantechletter.com/2021/04/ideanomics-is-a-buy-says-roth-capital-partners/)  

</small>

### Firm Overview
Jazz Pharmaceuticals plc is an Ireland‑domiciled, Nasdaq‑listed biopharmaceutical company focused on medicines for sleep disorders, epilepsy and oncology. Its portfolio and disclosures center on prescription therapeutics and related R&D, not digital assets or blockchain. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1232524/000123252424000015/jazz-20231231.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
A review of earnings call transcripts within 2017–2024 and the FY2023 Form 10‑K shows no substantive references to cryptocurrency, blockchain, mining, digital‑asset custody, tokenization, or related financial products; management commentary consistently focuses on commercial and clinical activities for approved drugs and pipeline candidates. The FY2023 10‑K contains no mentions of “cryptocurrency,” “bitcoin,” “blockchain,” or “digital assets,” and discusses cash, investments and intangible assets in conventional terms. Given the absence of any crypto‑linked products, services, holdings, or infrastructure, no exposure mechanism applies. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/05/08/jazz-pharmaceuticals-jazz-q1-2019-earnings-call-tr.aspx))

### Weight Assignment
Because no credible evidence of crypto exposure was found across transcripts, filings, or reputable news during the 2017–2024 window, the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0) with all other mechanism weights at 0.0. No investor‑base amplifier is indicated.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified. Note: Jazz’s 2021 acquisition of GW Pharmaceuticals (Epidiolex, a cannabinoid‑based medicine) relates to biopharma and is unrelated to cryptocurrency. ([bloomberg.com](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2021-02-03/jazz-pharmaceuticals-to-buy-gw-for-7-2-billion-in-cash-stock?utm_source=openai))

## AUGMENTUM FINTECH

* Rank: 130 | Expo: 0.53 | id_str: GB00BG12XV81
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Annual Reports (FY2023–FY2024), interim results materials, external deal/news coverage (2021–2024)

### Firm Overview
Augmentum Fintech plc is a UK‑listed investment trust focused on early and later‑stage fintech companies across banking, payments, wealth/asset management and adjacent financial services in Europe. Its portfolio includes minority stakes in digital‑asset financial services businesses as part of a defined “digital assets” vertical within the broader fintech mandate. ([augmentum.vc](https://augmentum.vc/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/268270-Augmentum-AR-2024-WEB.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s crypto exposure arises indirectly through equity investments in crypto financial services businesses rather than by operating such services itself. The FY2024 Annual Report lists Gemini as a regulated exchange/custodian in which Augmentum invested in November 2021 and notes Gemini’s improvement alongside broader crypto market developments; the report also quantifies the firm’s “digital assets vertical” at 6.5% of net assets. In addition, Augmentum led Tesseract’s 2021 Series A round; Tesseract provides digital‑asset lending solutions to institutional market participants. External deal coverage from 2021 corroborates Augmentum’s role in the Tesseract financing. ([augmentum.vc](https://augmentum.vc/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/268270-Augmentum-AR-2024-WEB.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Exposure is through crypto financial services investees (e.g., Gemini exchange/custodian; Tesseract digital‑asset lending). There is no evidence that Augmentum mines crypto, holds crypto as treasury assets, or supplies mining/compute infrastructure. Accordingly, the mechanism is assigned entirely to finserv (1.0). The investor‑base amplifier is not indicated because management targets generalist fintech investors and no source evidences crypto‑specific retail flows into the stock.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Public, citable earnings‑call transcripts for 2017–2024 could not be retrieved without gated access; classification therefore relies on Annual Reports and reputable news sources, and certainty is capped at 2 per protocol. Exposure is indirect and minority‑stake based; while FY2024 discloses 6.5% of NAV in the “digital assets” vertical, the balance can vary over time with portfolio changes and valuations. ([uk.marketscreener.com](https://uk.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/AUGMENTUM-FINTECH-PLC-120974674/news-call-transcripts/?utm_source=openai))

## EQUALS GROUP DEAD - DELIST.15/04/25

* Rank: 131 | Expo: 0.53 | id_str: GB00BLS0XX25
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Company press releases (Mar 2021), Annual Reports (2020–2023), financial news coverage (2021)

### Firm Overview
Equals Group plc is a UK-based fintech focused on business payments, multi-currency accounts, international FX and corporate card solutions, historically listed on AIM in London. On March 1, 2021 the company announced a partnership with Tap Global to enable cryptocurrency exchange services for both B2B and B2C customers within its platform. ([equalsplc.com](https://www.equalsplc.com/content/news/archive/2021/010321.asp?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The sole identifiable crypto channel during the 2017–2024 window is financial services: Equals entered a partnership with Gibraltar-licensed Tap Global to let customers convert into and out of cryptocurrencies, adding crypto liquidity alongside its fiat payment rails. Management described this as a logical step to allow clients to convert between cryptocurrencies and fiat, delivered via a regulated partner rather than Equals taking custody directly. There is no evidence that Equals mined crypto, held crypto on its own balance sheet, or supplied mining/compute infrastructure; recent Annual Reports detail derivatives and FX collateral but do not show digital-asset holdings. ([equalsplc.com](https://www.equalsplc.com/content/news/archive/2021/010321.asp?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Because Equals’ crypto involvement in 2017–2024 is limited to offering crypto conversion/exchange access through a regulated partner (Tap Global), exposure is assigned entirely to finserv (1.0). There is no credible basis for weights in holders, miners, bizserv, or infrastr. Investor-base amplification is not indicated by sources.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Public, citable earnings-call transcript text covering 2017–2024 could not be accessed without subscription; a call listing for H1 2023 is available but gated, so per protocol certainty is capped at 2. Additionally, multiple materials about stablecoin off-ramps with BVNK surfaced in late 2025–2026; these fall outside the January 2017–December 2024 sample window and were not used for classification. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/EQUS-GROU-61568060/news/Transcript-Equals-Group-plc-H1-2023-Earnings-Call-Sep-12-2023-44824875/))

* Rank: 133 | Expo: 0.49 | id_str: IL0011691438
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Form 20‑F (FY2023, filed Apr 9, 2024), Q4 FY2024 earnings call (Apr 9, 2024), product pages and blogs (2021–2023)

### Firm Overview
Cognyte Software Ltd. is an Israel-based provider of investigative analytics software serving government, national security, intelligence and law‑enforcement customers worldwide. The company’s platform fuses large, fragmented data sources and applies AI/analytics to accelerate complex investigations across use cases including financial crime and cyber threats.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Cognyte markets a dedicated blockchain analytics offering (BLINK) used by authorities to detect illicit and suspicious cryptocurrency transactions, de‑anonymize wallet owners, and accelerate crypto‑related investigations. Management commentary also frames cryptocurrency as an evolving challenge for customers, with Cognyte’s analytics embedded to counter criminals’ use of crypto and other obfuscation tactics. There is no evidence that Cognyte holds crypto as treasury, operates mining, provides custody/trading, or supplies mining hardware or energy infrastructure.

### Weight Assignment
Exposure is classified entirely as bizserv (1.0). Cognyte sells non‑financial software and analytics services that support crypto investigations for public‑sector clients; it does not custody or transact in digital assets, nor does it provide mining hardware or compute/energy directed at miners. Given explicit product materials and filings describing blockchain/crypto‑investigation capabilities, a full 1.0 weight to bizserv is appropriate; all other categories are 0.0.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Revenue attributable specifically to blockchain analytics is not disclosed; crypto‑related revenue is inferred to be a minor share within a broader investigative‑analytics portfolio.
- Transcript access is via third‑party hosts for some quarters; wording may vary slightly from company/IR transcripts.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
