## B3 BRASIL BOLSA BALCAO ON

* Rank: 150 | Expo: 0.41 | id_str: BRB3SAACNOR6
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings calls (Q1 2024, Q3 2024), B3 circular letter (Mar 28, 2024), B3 press releases (2021), Reuters (Mar 28, 2024)

### Firm Overview
B3 S.A. – Brasil, Bolsa, Balcão is Brazil’s main exchange operator, running the country’s equities, derivatives, clearing and depository infrastructure from São Paulo. Its crypto linkage arises from listing crypto-asset ETFs since 2021 and, from April 2024, operating standardized Bitcoin futures contracts on its derivatives market.

### Exposure Channels Identified
- Financial services (finserv): B3 listed Brazil’s first crypto index ETF in April 2021 (HASH11) and subsequently admitted other crypto ETFs such as QBTC11, enabling regulated secondary-market trading of crypto-linked securities. These are exchange-traded products whose underlying crypto custody is handled by the issuers and their custodians, while B3 provides the trading and post-trade venue. ([b3.com.br](https://www.b3.com.br/en_us/news/b3-begins-trading-the-first-hashdex-etf.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- Financial services (finserv): In March/April 2024 B3 secured regulatory approval and launched a standardized Bitcoin futures contract (BIT), creating a direct crypto-derivatives venue within B3’s exchange and clearing ecosystem. Management subsequently highlighted the product’s traction and revenues on earnings calls. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/brazil-exchange-operator-b3-to-offer-bitcoin-futures-in-april-3357861?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
B3’s crypto exposure is through operating markets for crypto-linked financial products: listing/trading of crypto ETFs since 2021 and the launch of Bitcoin futures in April 2024. This is squarely a financial-services mechanism because B3 intermediates trading and clearing of crypto-referenced instruments, while it neither mines nor holds crypto on its balance sheet. Given the clear and material operational involvement in crypto product trading but the absence of other channels, the finserv category is set to 1.0 and all others to 0.0. ([b3.com.br](https://www.b3.com.br/en_us/news/b3-begins-trading-the-first-hashdex-etf.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- B3 does not issue or custody the crypto assets behind ETFs; those roles sit with asset managers and custodians, so exposure is via venue/trading fees rather than asset risk. ([b3.com.br](https://www.b3.com.br/en_us/news/b3-begins-trading-the-first-hashdex-etf.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- Product evolution beyond December 2024 (e.g., later derivatives or contract size changes) is outside the sample and not considered in weighting or materiality. ([b3.com.br](https://www.b3.com.br/data/files/77/D5/3E/DD/17BC69106B8BCB69AC094EA8/OC%20013-2025-VPC_ALTERACAO%20DO%20TAMANHO%20DO%20CONTRATO%20FUTURO%20DE%20BITCOIN_PORT.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 14, 2024 (Q3 2024): “the success of the Bitcoin futures, launched in April this year… volumes reaching 120,000 contracts traded daily and BRL19 million in revenue for the quarter.” — [Roic AI transcript] ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/BOLSY/transcripts/2024/3))  
- May 2024 (Q1 2024): “We finally were able to launch the Bitcoin future… very pleased with the results that we are seeing.” — [Roic AI transcript] ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/BOLSY/transcripts/2024/1))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Mar 28, 2024: Circular Letter 044/2024 — standardized Bitcoin futures (BIT) in BRL on B3’s exchange market. — [B3 circular (EN)] ([b3.com.br](https://www.b3.com.br/data/files/E1/51/3E/C9/0968E810C54843E8DC0D8AA8/OC%20044-2024%20PRE%20Lancamento%20do%20Contrato%20Futuro%20de%20Bitcoin%20e%20da%20Op.%20Estruturada%20de%20Rolagem%20de%20Contrato%20Futuro%20de%20Bitcoin%20%28EN%29.pdf?utm_source=openai))  
- Apr 26–28, 2021: “Hashdex Nasdaq Crypto Index ETF (HASH11) begins trading today at B3.” — [B3 news release (EN/PT)] ([b3.com.br](https://www.b3.com.br/en_us/news/b3-begins-trading-the-first-hashdex-etf.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- Jun 23, 2021: External communication on the start of trading for QR’s Bitcoin ETF (QBTC11). — [B3 External Communication 030/2021 (EN)] ([b3.com.br](https://www.b3.com.br/data/files/B7/A7/19/E6/AF53A710D25F12A7AC094EA8/EC%20030-2021-VPC%20QBTC_ING.pdf?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Mar 28, 2024: “Brazilian exchange operator B3… received a green light… to offer bitcoin futures, with trading to start on April 17.” — [Reuters via Investing.com] ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/brazil-exchange-operator-b3-to-offer-bitcoin-futures-in-april-3357861?utm_source=openai))  
- Jun 23, 2021: “Brazil Stock Exchange lists first bitcoin ETF in Latin America (QBTC11).” — [CoinDesk] ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2021/06/23/brazil-stock-exchange-lists-first-bitcoin-etf-in-latin-america?utm_source=openai))

## FUNKO CL.A

* Rank: 151 | Expo: 0.41 | id_str: US3610081057
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: SEC 10-K (2022), Q3 2021 earnings call, Funko press releases (2021), CNBC (Apr 1, 2021), MediaPost (Sep 2022)

### Firm Overview
Funko, Inc. is a U.S.-based pop culture consumer products company listed on Nasdaq (FNKO), best known for its Pop! vinyl collectibles. In 2021 the company entered digital collectibles by acquiring TokenWave and launching “Digital Pop!” NFTs on the WAX blockchain, distributed via its Droppp platform. ([investor.funko.com](https://investor.funko.com/news-and-events/press-releases/Press-Releases/2021/Funko-Enters-NFT-Market-with-Majority-Stake-in-TokenHead-Developer/default.aspx?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
- Financial services (finserv): Through its wholly owned subsidiary TokenWave, Funko operates Droppp.io to “facilitate the buying and selling of its NFTs,” and, under SAB 121, recognizes a safeguarding asset and corresponding liability for user crypto held on the platform. The company also notes receiving payments in digital assets and purchasing digital assets for certain expenses tied to its NFT business. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1704711/000170471123000008/fnko-20221231.htm))  
- Management highlighted on earnings calls that multiple Digital Pop! drops “sold out in minutes,” and announced the opening of the Droppp.io marketplace—evidence of an ongoing NFT program directly handled by Funko. ([tickertrends.io](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/FNKO/Q3-earnings-transcript-2021))  
- Funko extended this program through retail collaborations (e.g., a 2022 Walmart/DC Comics “phygital” bundle), reinforcing direct operational involvement with crypto-linked products. ([mediapost.com](https://www.mediapost.com/publications/article/378074/funko-warner-brothers-and-walmart-team-up-for-ne.html?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Funko’s crypto exposure is concentrated in operating and monetizing its own NFT program: issuing Digital Pop! NFTs and running the Droppp.io marketplace that handles user crypto under a safeguarding framework. This constitutes direct handling and platform facilitation of digital assets, aligning with the finserv channel. There is no evidence of mining or treasury-style crypto holdings, nor of hardware/infrastructure provision, so finserv is set to 1.0 and all other mechanisms to 0.0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1704711/000170471123000008/fnko-20221231.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Funko’s NFTs are consumer products rather than investment products; classification as finserv rests on the company directly facilitating NFT sales/custody on Droppp rather than merely licensing content. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1704711/000170471123000008/fnko-20221231.htm))  
- The “Other” brand category (which includes Digital Pop! among other brands) represented about 5% of sales in 2021–2022; a precise revenue split attributable solely to NFTs is not disclosed. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1704711/000170471123000008/fnko-20221231.htm))  
- Developments after December 2024 (e.g., any changes to Droppp operations) are outside the sample and not considered.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 4, 2021 (Q3 2021): “Each of our drops sold out in minutes… We’ve also… opened our new digital marketplace Droppp.io.” — [TickerTrends](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/FNKO/Q3-earnings-transcript-2021)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Mar 3, 2022: 10-K — “These exclusive digital card packs are currently sold on the WAX blockchain platform…” — [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1704711/000170471123000008/fnko-20221231.htm)  
- 2022 10-K (SAB 121): “Funko… operates the Droppp.io platform, to facilitate the buying and selling of its NFTs… recorded an $11.3 million safeguarding asset and corresponding liability…” — [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1704711/000170471123000008/fnko-20221231.htm)  
- Apr 1, 2021: “Funko Enters NFT Market with Majority Stake in TokenHead Developer” (TokenWave acquisition) — [Funko IR press release](https://investor.funko.com/news-and-events/press-releases/Press-Releases/2021/Funko-Enters-NFT-Market-with-Majority-Stake-in-TokenHead-Developer/default.aspx)  
- Nov 4, 2021: Q3 2021 results — “NFT Collections… sold out in minutes… opening Droppp.io” — [Funko IR press release](https://investor.funko.com/news-and-events/press-releases/Press-Releases/2021/Funko-Reports-Third-Quarter-2021-Sales-of-267.7-Million-Up-40.0/)

- Sep 23, 2022: Walmart/DC “phygital” comic cover bundle via Funko — [MediaPost](https://www.mediapost.com/publications/article/378074/funko-warner-brothers-and-walmart-team-up-for-ne.html)

## RAMBUS

* Rank: 152 | Expo: 0.41 | id_str: US7509171069
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.1, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.9
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q3 2018 earnings call; Rambus press releases (2018, 2019); 2019–2020 10-K filings; American Banker (Jun 25, 2019)

### Firm Overview
Rambus Inc. is a U.S.-based semiconductor company listed on Nasdaq (RMBS) that provides memory interface chips and licenses silicon IP, including security IP, to data center and broader electronics markets. During 2018, it also marketed a payments/security software offering before divesting its Payments and Ticketing businesses in 2019. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rambus?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
- Non-financial services (bizserv): In October 2018, management announced “Vaultify Trade,” a software solution using tokenization and encryption “to secure the transaction and storage of crypto‑assets on blockchain,” aimed at banks/exchanges via a white‑label app/SDK. This represents a technology provider role around the crypto ecosystem rather than direct custody or financial intermediation. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/10/30/rambus-inc-rmbs-q3-2018-earnings-conference-call-t.aspx?utm_source=openai))  
- In October 2019, Rambus completed the sale of its Payments and Ticketing businesses to Visa, indicating that any crypto‑adjacent payments/tokenization offering was short‑lived and subsequently outside Rambus’s core perimeter. Post‑sale, filings and investor materials emphasize chips and security IP for semiconductors, not crypto operations or holdings. ([rambus.com](https://www.rambus.com/rambus-closes-sale-of-payments-and-ticketing-businesses-to-visa/?utm_source=openai))  
- There is no evidence in filings or calls of crypto held on the balance sheet, crypto mining, or operation of a crypto exchange/custody product by Rambus during 2017–2024. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/r/NASDAQ_RMBS_2023.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Rambus’s brief 2018 initiative (Vaultify Trade) targeted crypto‑asset security as a software/technology provider to financial institutions, which fits the bizserv channel. Given the subsequent 2019 divestiture of Payments and Ticketing to Visa and the lack of ongoing crypto activity or assets, exposure is judged peripheral. Accordingly, bizserv is set to 0.1 to reflect the short, non‑core involvement, and unclass is set to 0.9; all other mechanisms are 0.0. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/10/30/rambus-inc-rmbs-q3-2018-earnings-conference-call-t.aspx?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- “Crypto” references in Rambus materials often pertain to cryptography IP rather than cryptocurrency; this classification relies specifically on the 2018 Vaultify Trade crypto‑asset product. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/10/30/rambus-inc-rmbs-q3-2018-earnings-conference-call-t.aspx?utm_source=openai))  
- The revenue contribution of Vaultify Trade was not disclosed and appeared immaterial; the business line was sold in 2019, limiting persistence of exposure within the 2017–2024 window. ([rambus.com](https://www.rambus.com/rambus-closes-sale-of-payments-and-ticketing-businesses-to-visa/?utm_source=openai))  
- No evidence of treasury crypto holdings or mining was identified in 10‑K filings or calls for the period. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/r/NASDAQ_RMBS_2023.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 30, 2018 (Q3 2018), Motley Fool: “we launched Vaultify Trade… to secure the transaction and storage of crypto‑assets on blockchain.” — [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/10/30/rambus-inc-rmbs-q3-2018-earnings-conference-call-t.aspx)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- 2019 10‑K: notes sale of the Payments and Ticketing businesses to Visa completed in Oct 2019 (former Security Division). — [Annual Report PDF](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/r/NASDAQ_RMBS_2019.pdf)  
- 2020 10‑K: reiterates the 2019 divestiture of Payments and Ticketing to Visa. — [Annual Report PDF](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/r/NASDAQ_RMBS_2020.pdf)  
- Oct 22, 2019: “Rambus Closes Sale of Payments and Ticketing Businesses to Visa.” — [Rambus IR](https://www.rambus.com/rambus-closes-sale-of-payments-and-ticketing-businesses-to-visa/)

Its activities center on R&D, clinical development, and related manufacturing for MAR‑T cell product candidates.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024 filings, Marker portrays itself as a clinical‑stage developer of T‑cell immunotherapies with grant revenue and R&D spend; there is no indication of cryptocurrency holdings, mining activity, digital‑asset financial products, or blockchain‑related operations. ## CELLEBRITE DI

* Rank: 154 | Expo: 0.39 | id_str: IL0011794802
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Form 20‑F (2023), Q3 2022 earnings call, 2020 product launch press release, 2022–2023 partnership/solution pages

### Firm Overview
Cellebrite DI Ltd. is an Israel‑based digital intelligence software and services company listed on Nasdaq (CLBT) that provides lawful device access, data extraction/decoding, investigative analytics, and case management to law‑enforcement and enterprise customers worldwide. Within its product suite, Cellebrite also offers capabilities for blockchain and cryptocurrency investigations delivered in collaboration with partners. ([investors.cellebrite.com](https://investors.cellebrite.com/static-files/c69d1e3a-363f-479c-af47-b3437a990ec5))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Cellebrite launched “Crypto Tracer” in July 2020, a cryptocurrency and blockchain investigation solution designed to help investigators trace illicit crypto transactions. Subsequent company materials describe an integrated crypto investigation offering, including Chainalysis data and tools embedded into Cellebrite’s digital‑intelligence workflow. The company’s 2023 Form 20‑F explicitly states it provides “CryptoCurrency Investigative Solutions for analyzing blockchain transactions together with related data,” indicating exposure via investigative software/services rather than financial intermediation or asset ownership. No evidence suggests treasury crypto holdings, mining, or direct custody/exchange activities. ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/cellebrite-adds-cryptocurrency--blockchain-investigations-solution-to-industry-leading-digital-intelligence-platform-301099234.html?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Exposure is classified as bizserv = 1.0 because Cellebrite supplies non‑financial investigative software, integrations, and training that assist clients with crypto‑related cases, without itself touching client assets or issuing crypto financial products. There is no evidence of mining, treasury holdings, or financial‑services style custody/trading; likewise, its role is not infrastructure for mining/compute. Given clear statements in filings, product pages, and an earnings‑call reference to cryptocurrency investigations, all other mechanism weights are 0.0. ([investors.cellebrite.com](https://investors.cellebrite.com/static-files/c69d1e3a-363f-479c-af47-b3437a990ec5))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The firm does not disclose a separate revenue contribution for crypto‑related offerings; RevShare is coded as minor based on qualitative disclosures. ([investors.cellebrite.com](https://investors.cellebrite.com/static-files/c69d1e3a-363f-479c-af47-b3437a990ec5))  
- Evidence points to software/service enablement of crypto investigations via partners (e.g., Chainalysis), not direct handling of digital assets; thus finserv/holders categories are set to 0.0 under the exclusion rules. ([cellebrite.com](https://cellebrite.com/en/cellebrite-crypto-solution-powered-by-chainalysis/?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 17, 2022 (Q3 2022), Strike.Market: “This new partnership announced in July expedites the resolution of digital investigations involving cryptocurrency by enabling customers to easily identify and assess criminal activity.” — [Strike.Market transcript](https://strike.market/stocks/CLBT/earnings-call-transcripts/182862) ([strike.market](https://strike.market/stocks/CLBT/earnings-call-transcripts/182862))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**

**Financial news / other:**
- Jul 28, 2020: “Cellebrite Adds Cryptocurrency & Blockchain Investigations Solution… [Crypto Tracer].” — [PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/cellebrite-adds-cryptocurrency--blockchain-investigations-solution-to-industry-leading-digital-intelligence-platform-301099234.html) ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/cellebrite-adds-cryptocurrency--blockchain-investigations-solution-to-industry-leading-digital-intelligence-platform-301099234.html?utm_source=openai))

### Firm Overview
PensionBee Group plc is a UK-based direct-to-consumer online pension provider listed on the London Stock Exchange. The company consolidates customers’ existing pensions into managed plans overseen by large third‑party asset managers and expanded its business to the United States in 2024.

### Exposure Channels Identified
A review of 2017–2024 period materials (annual report content for FY2023, results announcements/presentations, and IR FAQs) shows no evidence that PensionBee engaged in cryptocurrency activities such as treasury holdings of digital assets, mining, exchange/brokerage, custody, or crypto‑linked investment products. The FY2023 annual report describes the principal activity as a direct‑to‑consumer online pension provider and contains no references to cryptoassets. ([investegate.co.uk](https://www.investegate.co.uk/announcement/rns/pensionbee-group--pbee/fy-2023-results-announcement-/8086384))  
IR FAQs indicate customer assets are invested via mainstream managers and safeguarded with global custodians (e.g., BNY Mellon, State Street, Citibank), with no mention of crypto options within PensionBee plans. ([pensionbee.com](https://www.pensionbee.com/uk/faq?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of any substantive crypto exposure across filings, results materials, and investor communications during 2017–2024, the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0. There is no basis to assign weights to holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr categories under the project’s threshold principle and exclusion rules. ([investegate.co.uk](https://www.investegate.co.uk/announcement/rns/pensionbee-group--pbee/fy-2023-results-announcement-/8086384))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- No crypto‑related passages were identified in results announcements/presentations during 2021–2024; dedicated third‑party earnings call transcripts are limited for this UK small‑cap. Consequently, classification rests on filings and official IR materials. ([pensionbee.com](https://www.pensionbee.com/uk/investor-relations/results-and-reports))  
- If product scope changes after December 2024 (e.g., the US launch announced in 2024), exposure could evolve; nothing in the covered period indicates crypto involvement. ([pensionbee.com](https://www.pensionbee.com/press/pensionbee-launches-in-us?utm_source=openai))

## TEXAS PACIFIC LAND TRUST

* Rank: 156 | Expo: 0.38 | id_str: US88262P1021
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 1.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filing (FY2022), Q2 2022 earnings call, company press release (May 16, 2022), financial news coverage (2022–2023)

### Firm Overview
Texas Pacific Land is a U.S. land and resource company, among the largest private landowners in Texas, with significant acreage in the Permian Basin. Converted from a trust to a corporation in 2021, the company generates revenue from oil and gas royalties, surface leases/easements, and water services, and has explored “next‑generation” projects leveraging its surface footprint.

### Exposure Channels Identified
In May 2022, the company announced a strategic alliance with Mawson Infrastructure Group and JAI Energy to develop up to 60 megawatts of bitcoin mining on Texas Pacific Land surface, with Mawson to own and operate the facilities while TPL and JAI earn a royalty and hold an equity option. The Q2 2022 earnings call reiterated this initiative as part of “next‑gen opportunities,” highlighting the expected royalty‑style economics rather than direct mining operations. The FY2022 Form 10‑K references “development of bitcoin mining facilities” among projects structured as multi‑year royalty/fee/lease arrangements, and notes limited short‑term revenue impact. In April 2023, Mawson sold its Texas sites and related contracts/leases to a Singapore‑managed fund, suggesting counterparties changed while the basic exposure for TPL remains tied to hosting/royalty arrangements on its land. ([texaspacific.com](https://www.texaspacific.com/investors/news-events/press-releases/detail/131/texas-pacific-land-corporation-mawson-infrastructure-group))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence indicates exposure via providing land access and site enablement for bitcoin mining, earning royalties and potential equity upside, with third parties operating the mining facilities. This is infrastructure‑style support targeted at miners rather than direct mining, custody, or financial product activity. Accordingly, infrastr = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. Management disclosures and filings emphasize royalty structures and negligible near‑term financial contribution, so RevShare = 0 and AssetShare = 0. ([texaspacific.com](https://www.texaspacific.com/investors/news-events/press-releases/detail/131/texas-pacific-land-corporation-mawson-infrastructure-group))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Mawson’s sale of its Texas sites and contracts in April 2023 introduces uncertainty regarding timing, counterparties, and scale of eventual operations on TPL land; filings through the covered period do not quantify crypto‑related revenue. ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2023/04/19/bitcoin-miner-mawson-to-sell-texas-sites-for-85m-to-singapore-fund-manager?utm_source=openai))  
- No evidence that TPL itself mines or holds crypto on its balance sheet; exposure is contingent on third‑party development and power availability. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1811074/000181107423000014/tpl-20221231.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 4, 2022 (Q2 2022), transcript: “We announced the bitcoin mining venture with Mawson Infrastructure Group and JAI Energy to develop a 60‑megawatt facility on TPL surface.” — [Strike.Market transcript](https://strike.market/stocks/TPL/earnings-call-transcripts/164289) ([strike.market](https://strike.market/stocks/TPL/earnings-call-transcripts/164289))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- FY2022 Form 10‑K: references “development of bitcoin mining facilities” among next‑generation projects, structured with multi‑year royalty/fee/lease arrangements and limited short‑term impact. — [SEC 10‑K](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1811074/000181107423000014/tpl-20221231.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1811074/000181107423000014/tpl-20221231.htm))  
- Aug 3, 2022 8‑K exhibit (Q2 results release): “Executed new agreements for bitcoin mining …” — [SEC exhibit 99.1](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1811074/000181107422000050/exhibit991q22022earningsre.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1811074/000181107422000050/exhibit991q22022earningsre.htm?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- May 16, 2022: “Texas Pacific Land Corporation, Mawson Infrastructure Group Inc., and JAI Energy Announce New Bitcoin Mining Venture in West Texas” — [Company press release](https://www.texaspacific.com/investors/news-events/press-releases/detail/131/texas-pacific-land-corporation-mawson-infrastructure-group) ([texaspacific.com](https://www.texaspacific.com/investors/news-events/press-releases/detail/131/texas-pacific-land-corporation-mawson-infrastructure-group))  
- May 16, 2022: Coverage of alliance to add bitcoin mines on TPL land (60 MW, Mawson to operate). — [Bloomberg Law](https://news.bloomberglaw.com/crypto/texas-pacific-land-aims-to-add-bitcoin-mines-in-west-texas) ([news.bloomberglaw.com](https://news.bloomberglaw.com/crypto/texas-pacific-land-aims-to-add-bitcoin-mines-in-west-texas?utm_source=openai))

## PLAYSTUDIOS A

* Rank: 157 | Expo: 0.38 | id_str: US72815G1085
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q3 2022 earnings call, 8-K/press release (Aug 9, 2022), company press release (Aug 3, 2022), Form 10‑K (FY2023), tech press coverage (Aug 2022)

### Firm Overview
Playstudios, Inc. is a U.S.-based developer and publisher of free‑to‑play mobile and social games, best known for its myVEGAS franchise and the playAWARDS loyalty platform. Listed on Nasdaq as MYPS, the company expanded into Web3 initiatives in 2022 alongside its core gaming operations. ([ir.playstudios.com](https://ir.playstudios.com/financial-information/all-sec-filings/content/0001104659-24-051240/tm246741d2_ars.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
In August 2022, Playstudios announced a new blockchain division, playBLOCKS, and a strategic alliance with Forte; management said the effort would tokenize the playAWARDS loyalty program and enable players to gift and sell certain rewards via a marketplace. The Q3 2022 earnings call described playBLOCKS as “our Blockchain solutions business” and outlined plans to “tokeniz[e] our playAWARDS loyalty program” and build an exchange system and marketplace. The company’s FY2023 Form 10‑K also documents the acquisition of WonderBlocks to develop a play‑to‑earn loyalty platform on the Ethereum blockchain, supporting the Web3 build‑out. No filings in the 2017–2024 window quantify material revenue or balance‑sheet crypto holdings from these initiatives. ([ir.playstudios.com](https://ir.playstudios.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/52/playstudios-brings-its-real-world-gamified-rewards-to-web3))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence points to direct handling/issuance of digital assets within Playstudios’ products (tokenized rewards and a player marketplace), which fits Crypto Financial Services. There is no indication of crypto treasury holdings or mining, and the activity is not merely third‑party support or infrastructure. Accordingly, finserv = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. Given the strategic emphasis but absence of disclosed revenue contribution in 2017–2024, RevShare = 0 and AssetShare = 0. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/MYPSW/transcripts/2022-year/3-quarter))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Post‑launch commentary in 2023–2024 earnings calls emphasizes loyalty‑as‑a‑service generally, with fewer explicit Web3 updates; the scale and monetization of tokenized rewards during the sample period are not quantified. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/MYPS/documents/transcripts/q4-2023))  
- Classification relies on stated plans and disclosed acquisitions/partnerships; limited evidence of material revenue impact within 2017–2024 caps certainty at 3. ([ir.playstudios.com](https://ir.playstudios.com/financial-information/all-sec-filings/content/0001104659-24-051240/tm246741d2_ars.pdf?utm_source=openai))

## TEL AVIV STOCK EXCHANGE

* Rank: 158 | Expo: 0.38 | id_str: IL0011590291
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Periodic Report (FY2023), TASE-CH PFMI disclosure (Dec 31, 2023), partnership press release (Aug 2023), news coverage of Project Eden PoC (Jun 2023), coverage of blockchain securities lending platform (2020)

### Firm Overview
Tel Aviv Stock Exchange Ltd. operates Israel’s national securities exchange and related clearing and depository infrastructure. During the 2017–2024 period, management advanced initiatives to introduce regulated digital-asset services alongside its core trading and post‑trade businesses.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Company filings in 2023 state that the TASE Clearing House intends to offer custody (depository) services for digital assets, initially for selected cryptocurrencies and only to licensed entities, and that TASE engaged Fireblocks to provide the underlying digital‑asset infrastructure. ([content.tase.co.il](https://content.tase.co.il/media/nc5hnifg/tase_ltd_periodic_report_for_2023.pdf))  
TASE’s PFMI disclosure further details plans to provide depository services “with an emphasis on crypto currencies,” operated on blockchain with digital wallets, keys, and segregation from core systems, noting an agreement with Fireblocks. ([content.tase.co.il](https://content.tase.co.il/media/uz3bkihb/file_0770_pfmi_tasech_doc_20231231_eng.pdf))  
In August 2023 TASE announced a strategic agreement with Fireblocks to build a supervised framework for a range of digital‑asset products and services, and in June 2023 it completed “Project Eden,” a proof‑of‑concept with the Ministry of Finance to issue and clear a tokenized government bond on a dedicated blockchain platform. ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/tel-aviv-stock-exchange-tase-signs-agreement-with-fireblocks-to-provide-technology-infrastructure-for-a-comprehensive-range-of-digital-asset-products--services-301892555.html?utm_source=openai))  
Separately, TASE previously developed a blockchain‑based securities lending platform (DLT), evidencing technical capability though not direct cryptocurrency handling. ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2020/07/28/israels-stock-exchange-says-it-is-launching-a-blockchain-platform-for-securities-lending?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
The exchange’s documented plan to directly custody digital assets for regulated participants and to establish a trading framework places its crypto exposure squarely in Crypto Financial Services; there is no evidence of treasury crypto holdings, mining, or pure infrastructure sales to third parties. Accordingly, finserv = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. Because services were in planning/PoC and not quantified as revenue or balance‑sheet items within 2017–2024, RevShare = 0 and AssetShare = 0. ([content.tase.co.il](https://content.tase.co.il/media/nc5hnifg/tase_ltd_periodic_report_for_2023.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- No earnings‑call transcript within January 2017–December 2024 could be retrieved; while the company held calls (e.g., Q3 2023 was scheduled on November 21, 2023), a transcript quoting management was not publicly available, so certainty is capped at 2. ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/il/news-releases/the-tel-aviv-stock-exchange-tase-schedules-third-quarter-2023-results-release-and-conference-call-for-tuesday-november-21-2023-301974377.html?utm_source=openai))  
- Filings describe intent and partnerships; timing of commercial launch, regulatory approvals, and monetization of digital‑asset services during the sample window are not quantified. ([content.tase.co.il](https://content.tase.co.il/media/nc5hnifg/tase_ltd_periodic_report_for_2023.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 21, 2023: “TASE Schedules Third Quarter 2023 Results Release and Conference Call” (transcript not available). — [PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/il/news-releases/the-tel-aviv-stock-exchange-tase-schedules-third-quarter-2023-results-release-and-conference-call-for-tuesday-november-21-2023-301974377.html)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- FY2023 Periodic Report: “TASE‑CH intends to offer custody services for digital assets… For the purpose of providing the aforesaid services, TASE has engaged with Fireblocks.” — [TASE 2023 Periodic Report, p. 74–76](https://content.tase.co.il/media/nc5hnifg/tase_ltd_periodic_report_for_2023.pdf)  
- Dec 31, 2023 PFMI (TASE‑CH): “TASE‑CH intends to offer depository services in digital assets, with an emphasis on crypto currencies… Thus, TASE has entered into an agreement with Fireblocks.” — [TASE‑CH PFMI document, p. 77–78](https://content.tase.co.il/media/uz3bkihb/file_0770_pfmi_tasech_doc_20231231_eng.pdf)

- Jun 7, 2023: “TASE completes proof‑of‑concept to tokenize fiat and bonds; ERC‑1155 security token minted.” — [Cointelegraph](https://cointelegraph.com/news/tel-aviv-stock-exchange-completes-proof-of-concept-to-tokenize-fiat-and-bonds)  
- Jul 28, 2020: “Israel’s Stock Exchange Launching a Blockchain Platform for Securities Lending.” — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2020/07/28/israels-stock-exchange-says-it-is-launching-a-blockchain-platform-for-securities-lending)

## TMX GROUP

* Rank: 159 | Expo: 0.36 | id_str: CA87262K1057
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q2 2023 earnings call (July 28, 2023), MX Advisory A23-008 (Oct 3, 2023) and CDCC Notice 115-23 (Oct 3, 2023), TSX press release on first Bitcoin ETF (Feb 18, 2021), Shorcan DCN crypto initiative press release (Mar 22, 2018)

### Firm Overview
TMX Group Limited is a Canadian market infrastructure operator whose core businesses include the Toronto Stock Exchange, TSX Venture Exchange, TSX Alpha Exchange, Montréal Exchange derivatives market, and post‑trade services via CDS and CDCC. During 2017–2024, management explored and launched regulated products tied to digital assets within its exchange and clearing franchises.

### Exposure Channels Identified
On July 28, 2023 management stated it had published a consultation paper “seeking industry feedback regarding trading of digital assets, including cryptocurrency, on TSX Alpha Exchange,” highlighting direct engagement with spot crypto market structure. ([s21.q4cdn.com](https://s21.q4cdn.com/671813756/files/doc_events/2023/07/1/Transcript-TMX-Group-Ltd-Q2-2023-Financial-Results-July-28-2023.pdf))  
TMX’s Montréal Exchange subsequently listed cash‑settled Bitcoin Price Index Futures, with CDCC clearing, launching trading on January 16, 2024; the contract references CoinDesk’s XBX index. ([m-x.ca](https://www.m-x.ca/en/resources/notices/advisory-notices?id=42))  
Earlier, TSX listed third‑party crypto investment products, including the world’s first spot Bitcoin ETF in February 2021, evidencing additional fee exposure to crypto ETF listings and trading. ([s21.q4cdn.com](https://s21.q4cdn.com/671813756/files/doc_news/2021/02/1/Toronto-Stock-Exchange-Lists-Worlds-First-Bitcoin-ETF-2021-2-18-2021.pdf))  
TMX had also announced in 2018 a Shorcan Digital Currency Network initiative to broker Bitcoin and Ether, underscoring a multi‑year exploration of digital‑asset services, though the brokerage did not become a sustained line of business. ([investors.tmx.com](https://investors.tmx.com/English/News--Events/news/news-details/2018/TMXs-Shorcan-Announces-Cryptocurrency-Initiatives-2018-3-22/default.aspx))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence shows TMX directly offering and clearing a crypto‑derivative (Bitcoin Price Index Futures on MX/CDCC) and formally consulting on exchange‑traded spot digital assets on TSX Alpha. These are regulated financial services involving digital‑asset exposure via exchange/clearing operations rather than proprietary coin holding or mining. No credible evidence indicates TMX holds crypto as treasury assets or sells mining/infrastructure. Therefore, finserv = 1.0 and all other mechanisms = 0.0. Given lack of disclosed segment figures isolating crypto revenues and the relatively small footprint versus the group, RevShare is coded 1 (minor, <~5%) and AssetShare = 0.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The TSX Alpha consultation outlined potential spot digital‑asset trading; implementation status within 2017–2024 is exploratory, while the Bitcoin futures product did launch in January 2024. Volumes and revenue contribution for crypto‑linked products are not broken out in public filings. ([tsx.com](https://www.tsx.com/en/resource/3035/alpha-consultation-paper-digital-assets-2023-07-06-en.pdf?utm_source=openai))  
- Earlier brokerage plans (Shorcan DCN, 2018) illustrate interest but do not represent ongoing operations; classification relies on the 2023–2024 exchange/clearing initiatives. ([investors.tmx.com](https://investors.tmx.com/English/News--Events/news/news-details/2018/TMXs-Shorcan-Announces-Cryptocurrency-Initiatives-2018-3-22/default.aspx))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jul 28, 2023 (Q2 2023): “published a consultation paper seeking industry feedback regarding trading of digital assets, including cryptocurrency, on TSX Alpha Exchange.” — [TMX Group Q2 2023 call transcript (PDF)](https://s21.q4cdn.com/671813756/files/doc_events/2023/07/1/Transcript-TMX-Group-Ltd-Q2-2023-Financial-Results-July-28-2023.pdf)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**

- Oct 3, 2023: CDCC Notice 115‑23 — Listing of Bitcoin Price Index Futures; settlement in USD; BTG24/BTH24 series. — [CDCC (PDF)](https://www.cdcc.ca/u_avis_en/115-23_en.pdf)  
- Feb 18, 2021: “Toronto Stock Exchange Lists World’s First Bitcoin ETF (Purpose Bitcoin ETF).” — [TMX/TSX news release (PDF)](https://s21.q4cdn.com/671813756/files/doc_news/2021/02/1/Toronto-Stock-Exchange-Lists-Worlds-First-Bitcoin-ETF-2021-2-18-2021.pdf)

- Oct 3, 2023: “Canadian exchange TMX soon to start bitcoin futures trading.” — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2023/10/03/canadian-exchange-tmx-soon-to-start-bitcoin-futures-trading)

## IZEA WORLDWIDE

* Rank: 160 | Expo: 0.36 | id_str: US46604H2040
* Weight summary: holders 0.6, miners 0.2, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.2, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2017, 2022, 2023), Jan 11, 2022 payments press release, Q3 2021 earnings call

### Firm Overview
IZEA Worldwide, Inc. is a U.S.-listed influencer marketing company providing managed services and software platforms that connect brands with creators. Between 2017 and 2024, the company experimented with crypto-related initiatives, briefly mined and held small amounts of digital assets, and for a period accepted cryptocurrency as a payment method before discontinuing it.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Filings disclose that IZEA historically mined digital assets, with mining operations ceasing in 2019, and that it purchased and held crypto assets; as of December 31, 2023, it reported holdings of approximately $162,905 split between Bitcoin and Ethereum. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/i/NASDAQ_IZEA_2023.pdf))  
On January 11, 2022 the company announced it would accept Bitcoin and Ethereum as payment for influencer marketing services and could pay select influencers in crypto when received from marketers; management also referenced prior alt‑coin mining and conversions to BTC/ETH. ([izea.com](https://izea.com/press-releases/izea-begins-accepting-bitcoin-and-ethereum/?utm_source=openai))  
In 2020–2021, IZEA added cryptocurrencies and popular NFT collections to its BrandGraph tracking and launched MetaMod to facilitate sponsorships in virtual worlds and on NFT marketplaces, indicating ancillary, non‑financial services to crypto/NFT ecosystems. ([izea.com](https://izea.com/press-releases/izea-launches-metamod/?utm_source=openai))  
By December 11, 2024, IZEA stated it had discontinued crypto payments due to limited interest and that it does not support crypto payments to or from creators or brands, limiting ongoing exposure. ([izea.com](https://izea.com/blog/avoid-scams-izea-does-not-pay-in-crypto/))

### Weight Assignment
The primary mechanism is holders: the firm bought, held and accounted for crypto (BTC/ETH) on its balance sheet during 2021–2023, though amounts were small relative to total assets. Mining exposure is secondary and historical (2018–2019). A smaller bizserv component reflects BrandGraph coverage of crypto/NFT topics and MetaMod activations intersecting with NFT marketplaces. There is no evidence of IZEA operating financial services such as custody, exchange or issuing crypto investment products, nor of infrastructure sales, hence finserv and infrastr are 0.0. Overall mix: holders 0.6, miners 0.2, bizserv 0.2. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/i/NASDAQ_IZEA_2023.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Earnings call transcripts contain only high‑level references (e.g., “cryptocurrencies” in a metaverse context) rather than detailed crypto operational metrics; thus filings and company releases carry most evidential weight. Certainty set to 3. ([strike.market](https://strike.market/stocks/IZEA/earnings-call-transcripts/128606))  
- Crypto payment acceptance was time‑limited and discontinued by December 2024; the magnitude of any crypto‑denominated billings is not broken out. ([izea.com](https://izea.com/blog/avoid-scams-izea-does-not-pay-in-crypto/))  
- Digital asset balances are small versus cash and investments; RevShare coded 0 and AssetShare 1 accordingly. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/i/NASDAQ_IZEA_2023.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 10, 2021 (Q3 2021): “Data, artificial intelligence, cryptocurrencies, and a flexible workflow for brands and creators will become increasingly important in that world.” — [Strike.Market transcript](https://strike.market/stocks/IZEA/earnings-call-transcripts/128606) ([strike.market](https://strike.market/stocks/IZEA/earnings-call-transcripts/128606))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- 2023 Annual Report (10‑K): “Historically, we mined digital assets (mining operations ceased in 2019)… we will accept payments in digital assets…,” and year‑end crypto holdings detail; BTC $96,722 and ETH $66,183 as of Dec 31, 2023 (total $162,905). — [AnnualReports.com PDF](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/i/NASDAQ_IZEA_2023.pdf) ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/i/NASDAQ_IZEA_2023.pdf))  
- 2022 Form 10‑K: risk factor and accounting policy on holding/transacting in digital assets; impairment and disclosure of historical mining cessation in 2019. — [SEC filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1495231/000149523123000040/izea-20221231.htm)   
- 2017 Annual Report: describes a small cryptocurrency mining operation and decision not to release mining software amid regulatory uncertainty. — [AnnualReports.com PDF](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/i/NASDAQ_IZEA_2017.pdf) ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/i/NASDAQ_IZEA_2017.pdf))  
- Jan 11, 2022: “IZEA Begins Accepting Bitcoin and Ethereum Payments…” (also noting prior alt‑coin mining and BTC/ETH conversions). — [IZEA press release](https://izea.com/press-releases/izea-begins-accepting-bitcoin-and-ethereum/) ([izea.com](https://izea.com/press-releases/izea-begins-accepting-bitcoin-and-ethereum/?utm_source=openai))  
- Jan 16, 2018: “Announcing IZEA Crypto” outlining crypto mining initiative. — [IZEA press release](https://izea.com/press-releases/announcing-izea-crypto/) ([izea.com](https://izea.com/press-releases/announcing-izea-crypto/?utm_source=openai))

## CARECLOUD

* Rank: 161 | Expo: 0.36 | id_str: US14167R1005

* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filing (2024), Q4 2018 earnings call, Q4 2020 earnings call, Q3 2024 earnings call

### Firm Overview
CareCloud, Inc. is a U.S.-listed healthcare technology company that provides cloud-based electronic health records, practice management, and revenue cycle management services to medical practices. During 2017–2024 its public disclosures and calls focus on healthcare software and services (including AI and interoperability), not on cryptocurrency activities. ([ir.carecloud.com](https://ir.carecloud.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/Form10-K.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
A review of CareCloud’s 2017–2024 earnings calls and its FY2024 Form 10-K shows no evidence that the company mined, held, custodied, or transacted in crypto assets, nor that it operated crypto exchanges, brokerages, or issued crypto investment products. ([ir.carecloud.com](https://ir.carecloud.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/Form10-K.pdf))  
Management did discuss and demonstrate a blockchain-based health data interoperability concept around HIMSS 2019, and marketing content periodically references blockchain in a general healthcare IT context; these references do not constitute participation in cryptoasset markets under this framework. ([ir.carecloud.com](https://ir.carecloud.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/MTBC-Earnings-Call-Transcript.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of substantive crypto exposure across filings and calls, the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). Mentions of blockchain relate to healthcare interoperability concepts rather than cryptoasset custody, trading, payments, mining, or infrastructure directed at crypto workloads, so holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, and infrastr are each set to 0.0. ([ir.carecloud.com](https://ir.carecloud.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/Form10-K.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
General marketing and conference commentary referencing “blockchain” (e.g., HIMSS interoperability demonstrations) could be misconstrued as crypto involvement; however, filings and subsequent calls do not indicate any cryptoasset operations or holdings. None identified beyond this distinction. ([ir.carecloud.com](https://ir.carecloud.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/MTBC-Earnings-Call-Transcript.pdf))

* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings calls (Q4 2021, Q1 2022), MD&A (Q1 2022), press releases (Q2–Q3 2022)

### Firm Overview
Enthusiast Gaming Holdings Inc. is a Canada-based gaming media and esports company whose portfolio includes Luminosity Gaming, owned-and-operated media sites, and owned game properties such as EV.io. During 2021–2022 the company experimented with Web3 features in its gaming/content ecosystem, including NFT integrations within EV.io and related sponsorships. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Enthusiast_Gaming?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management discussed and executed NFT initiatives tied to its Addicting Games/EV.io title, including a strategic partnership with Fractal (an NFT marketplace) and multiple NFT “mints,” some of which sold out. The company also announced a multi‑year collaboration with Hut 8 Mining, integrating the miner’s branding into EV.io and exploring Web3, NFTs and crypto-themed content. These activities indicate operational use of crypto/NFT technology and marketing services around Web3 rather than custody, brokerage, or issuance of financial crypto products. There is no evidence that Enthusiast held crypto as treasury assets, mined crypto, or operated an exchange/custody platform within 2017–2024. ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2022/08/15/2498568/0/en/Enthusiast-Gaming-Announces-Second-Quarter-2022-Results.html?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence shows the firm used NFTs to enhance gameplay and fan engagement (minting and integrating NFTs into EV.io) and partnered with a crypto miner for content/sponsorships; these are non‑financial services to and use of the crypto ecosystem. Accordingly, bizserv is set to 1.0, with holders, miners, finserv, and infrastr at 0.0. Given multiple direct management statements in earnings calls and corroborating filings/press releases, certainty is 4. Reported NFT activity appears small relative to overall revenue, so RevShare is coded 1 and AssetShare 0. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/EGLX.TO/transcripts/2022-year/1-quarter))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
NFT minting and primary sales could be misconstrued as “financial services,” but Enthusiast relied on third‑party marketplaces (e.g., Fractal) and did not provide custody, exchange, or investment products; thus finserv is set to 0. Activity was most pronounced in 2022; public materials in later periods emphasize core media/advertising and do not indicate ongoing material crypto operations or balance‑sheet holdings. ([enthusiastgaming.com](https://www.enthusiastgaming.com/enthusiast-gaming-and-fractal-announce-strategic-nft-partnership/?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q4 2021 (transcript): “We’ve gone from mentioning NFTs to doing a multiyear partnership with one of the most innovative digital asset miners… [and] an NFT marketplace called Fractal… a few short days away from our first ever NFT mint.” — [ROIC.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/EGLX/transcripts/2021/4) ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/EGLX/transcripts/2021/4))  
- Q1 2022 (transcript): “Successful NFT launch… over 4,000… NFTs that we minted… you can wear them in the game as you’re playing EV.IO.” — [ROIC.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/EGLX.TO/transcripts/2022-year/1-quarter) ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/EGLX.TO/transcripts/2022-year/1-quarter))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Q1 2022 MD&A: details Hut 8 multi‑year partnership (Web3, NFTs, crypto) and March 15, 2022 Fractal partnership to integrate NFTs into an HTML5 game. — [MD&A PDF](https://www.enthusiastgaming.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/05/EGHI-MDA-Mar-31-2022-FINAL.pdf) ([enthusiastgaming.com](https://www.enthusiastgaming.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/05/EGHI-MDA-Mar-31-2022-FINAL.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**
- Mar 15, 2022: “Enthusiast Gaming and Fractal Announce Strategic NFT Partnership… EV.IO… Hut 8… lead sponsor… official EV.IO NFT mint.” — [Company press release](https://www.enthusiastgaming.com/enthusiast-gaming-and-fractal-announce-strategic-nft-partnership/) ([enthusiastgaming.com](https://www.enthusiastgaming.com/enthusiast-gaming-and-fractal-announce-strategic-nft-partnership/?utm_source=openai))  
- Aug 15, 2022: Q2 2022 results — “over 3,500 NFTs being minted by the Company and integrated into… EV.IO;… introduced… play‑to‑earn.” — [GlobeNewswire](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2022/08/15/2498568/0/en/Enthusiast-Gaming-Announces-Second-Quarter-2022-Results.html) ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2022/08/15/2498568/0/en/Enthusiast-Gaming-Announces-Second-Quarter-2022-Results.html?utm_source=openai))  
- Nov 14, 2022: Q3 2022 results — “EV.IO’s limited edition NFT mint of 2,500 tokens sold out in six hours at a price between 2.00–2.50 SOL.” — [Company news post](https://www.enthusiastgaming.com/enthusiast-gaming-to-announce-third-quarter-2022-results-2/) ([enthusiastgaming.com](https://www.enthusiastgaming.com/enthusiast-gaming-to-announce-third-quarter-2022-results-2/))

## MONEYLION A DEAD - DELIST.17/04/25

* Rank: 163 | Expo: 0.35 | id_str: US60938K3041
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2021–2024), Q2 2022 earnings call, company press releases (2021), SEC correspondence (Nov 2024)

### Firm Overview
MoneyLion Inc. is a U.S.-based digital financial platform known for banking, lending, investing, and embedded‑finance marketplace services delivered via its mobile app. Within the 2017–2024 period, the company added an in‑app cryptocurrency feature powered by a third‑party infrastructure provider and later decided to wind it down.

### Exposure Channels Identified
MoneyLion launched “MoneyLion Crypto” in 2021 to let customers buy and sell select cryptocurrencies inside its app; the service was powered by Zero Hash, which provided custody, trading and pricing while MoneyLion earned a share of fees. Company filings make clear MoneyLion did not directly custody or transact crypto itself and recognized partner revenue for this offering. In November 2024, MoneyLion informed the SEC it would fully wind down MoneyLion Crypto by the end of 2024 and disclosed that revenue from the program was less than 0.1% of total company revenue for the relevant 2024 periods. These activities constitute crypto financial services exposure (brokerage/custody via partner, fee share), not holding or mining. ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/09/13/fintech-platform-moneylion-to-enable-bitcoin-ethereum-purchases-within-app?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence consistently shows MoneyLion’s crypto linkage came from offering customers retail crypto trading and related rewards through a partner (Zero Hash), from which it received a revenue share; there is no evidence of balance‑sheet crypto holdings, mining, or crypto‑specific hardware/infrastructure. Therefore, finserv is set to 1.0 and all other mechanism weights are 0.0. Given explicit management/filing disclosures and an earnings‑call transcript quote confirming the crypto fee‑share model, certainty is 4. Reported contribution from the crypto feature was immaterial (<0.1% of revenue in Q3 2024), so RevShare is coded 0 and AssetShare 0. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/ML/documents/transcripts/q2-2022))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
MoneyLion invested in Zero Hash and marketed crypto‑based rewards/round‑ups, which might be misconstrued as treasury “holding,” but filings specify that crypto custody and transactions occurred at Zero Hash, not MoneyLion; thus holders = 0.0. Transcript availability from mainstream aggregators is mixed, but at least one transcript with relevant language was retrieved and corroborated by 10‑K disclosures. Activity was wound down by December 2024, but within the 2017–2024 sample window the appropriate mechanism remains finserv. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1807846/000095017024027617/ml-20231231.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q2 2022 (transcript): “Finally, MoneyLion Investing and MoneyLion Crypto generate revenue share on crypto transactions and a monthly per account fee on investment accounts.” — [Fintool](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/ML/documents/transcripts/q2-2022)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- 2023 10‑K (filed 2024): “MoneyLion Crypto: We earn revenue from Zero Hash… Zero Hash pays us a share of the fees… [and] we are not directly involved in any cryptocurrency transactions or the exchange of fiat funds for cryptocurrency.” — [SEC 10‑K FY2023](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1807846/000095017024027617/ml-20231231.htm)
- 2022 10‑K/Annual disclosures: lists available coins (Bitcoin, Ether; later Bitcoin Cash and Litecoin) and describes the Zero Hash partnership model. — [MoneyLion Annual filing/extract](https://investors.moneylion.com/filings-financials/sec-filings/annual-reports/content/0000950170-23-008455/0000950170-23-008455.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Apr 8, 2021: Company announces strategic investment in Zero Hash to power buy/sell/earn crypto features in MoneyLion app. — [MoneyLion press release](https://www.moneylion.com/investors/news/press-release/moneylion-to-empower-members-to-buy-sell-and-earn-cryptocurrencies-via-strategic-investment-in-zero-hash/)
- Sep 13, 2021: “MoneyLion to enable bitcoin, ether purchases within app… Zero Hash for transaction and custody services.” — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/09/13/fintech-platform-moneylion-to-enable-bitcoin-ethereum-purchases-within-app)
- Product help/article: clarifies that crypto assets are custodied by Zero Hash, not MoneyLion/Pathward. — [MoneyLion “Say hello to MoneyLion Crypto”](https://www.moneylion.com/learn/personal-finance/basics/say-hello-to-moneylion-crypto)
- Nov 26, 2024: SEC correspondence notes MoneyLion Crypto to be fully wound down by end‑2024; revenue from Zero Hash was <0.1% of MoneyLion’s total revenue for the three and nine months ended Sep 30, 2024. — [SEC correspondence](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1807846/000121390024102945/filename1.htm)

</small>

## COMSOVEREIGN HOLDING

* Rank: 164 | Expo: 0.35 | id_str: US2056504010
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2021–2022), Q2–Q3 2021 earnings calls, company press release (Oct 2021)

### Firm Overview
COMSovereign Holding Corp is a U.S.-based developer of 4G LTE Advanced and 5G communications systems and solutions sold to network operators, enterprises, and government customers. Its filings describe a portfolio spanning radios (DragonWave‑X, Fastback), mobile edge computing (Saguna), and related wireless infrastructure products. ([s3.amazonaws.com](https://s3.amazonaws.com/sec.irpass.cc/2434/0001213900-23-093792.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across earnings-call transcripts and 10‑K filings within the 2017–2024 sample window, there is no evidence that COMSovereign holds cryptocurrency on its balance sheet, operates mining, or provides crypto custody/trading/payment services. In October 2021 the company announced a standards‑oriented pilot with Total Network Solutions to use a blockchain ledger for an “Enhanced MEID” device‑verification concept in telecom networks; this is a cybersecurity/supply‑chain initiative and does not involve customer crypto assets or financial products. As such, no substantive crypto‑exposed revenue or asset channels are identified for this issuer. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1178727/000121390021043501/ea146045ex99-2_comsovereign.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Given the lack of crypto treasury, mining, exchange/brokerage, or crypto‑specific infrastructure activity, all mechanism categories are set to 0.0 and unclass is set to 1.0. The 2021 blockchain pilot relates to telecom device authentication and standards work and is not a service to the crypto ecosystem, so it does not meet the bizserv or finserv definitions for this project. ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/comsovereign-and-tns-advance-wireless-infrastructure-security-utilizing-the-blockchain-for-mobile-electronic-device-verification-301403424.html))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
COMSovereign’s public materials include the term “blockchain” in the context of E‑MEID device verification; readers could misconstrue this as crypto exposure, but neither filings nor calls indicate custody of or transactions in digital assets. Transcript coverage for this micro‑cap name is limited, but available SEC‑filed transcripts and recent 10‑K disclosures support a non‑exposed classification. ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/comsovereign-and-tns-advance-wireless-infrastructure-security-utilizing-the-blockchain-for-mobile-electronic-device-verification-301403424.html))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 16, 2021 (Q2 2021): “We announced our intent to enter the 5G Mobile Edge Compute Cloud market with the acquisition of SAGUNA.” — [SEC exhibit transcript](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1178727/000121390021043501/ea146045ex99-2_comsovereign.htm)
- Nov 15, 2021 (Q3 2021): “For the three months ended September 30, 2021, total revenues were approximately $4.1 million, driven by sales in our Global Telecom unit, led by Fastback, DragonWave‑X, and VNC.” — [SEC exhibit transcript](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1178727/000121390021060090/ea150877ex99-2_comsovereign.htm)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- FY2022 10‑K: Company description as a developer of 4G LTE Advanced and 5G communication systems and solutions; no crypto custody/mining disclosures. — [SEC 10‑K FY2022](https://s3.amazonaws.com/sec.irpass.cc/2434/0001213900-23-093792.pdf)
- FY2021 10‑K: Business description and segment detail focused on telecom hardware/software; no crypto‑related revenue lines. — [Annual Report FY2021](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/c/NASDAQ_COMS_2021.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Oct 19, 2021: “COMSovereign and TNS Advance Wireless Infrastructure Security Utilizing the Blockchain for Mobile Electronic Device Verification” (E‑MEID blockchain pilot for device authentication). — [PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/comsovereign-and-tns-advance-wireless-infrastructure-security-utilizing-the-blockchain-for-mobile-electronic-device-verification-301403424.html)

## GLOBANT

* Rank: 165 | Expo: 0.35 | id_str: LU0974299876
* Weight summary: holders 0.2, miners 0.0, finserv 0.1, bizserv 0.7, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: Form 20‑F filings (2021, 2023), Q4 2021 and Q2 2022 earnings calls

### Firm Overview
Globant S.A. is a Luxembourg‑registered, NYSE‑listed digital consulting and software development company that provides technology services globally. Its offerings span digital transformation, studios focused on emerging technologies, and platform products; blockchain/Web3 work has featured within these service lines during the 2017–2024 period. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/g/NYSE_GLOB_2021.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
- Bizserv: Management repeatedly described blockchain/Web3, NFTs and the Metaverse as client solution areas, including work with Web3 clients and launches such as the Metaverse Studio; Globant also acquired Atix Labs, a blockchain services firm, to “strengthen… blockchain and crypto‑related solutions.” ([investiment.io](https://www.investiment.io/symbols/GLOB/earnings/transcripts/2021/4))  
- Holders: The company disclosed crypto assets on its balance sheet, accounting for them as indefinite‑lived intangibles; as of December 31, 2021 they comprised Bitcoin and Ethereum, with subsequent filings noting Bitcoin, Ethereum and stablecoins. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/g/NYSE_GLOB_2021.pdf))  
- Finserv (minor): In Q2 2022, Globant announced “a new wallet for digital assets… an intuitive custodial wallet… completely operated via WhatsApp,” indicating limited direct provision of a crypto wallet product. Evidence of material revenue from this product was not found. ([investiment.io](https://www.investiment.io/symbols/GLOB/earnings/transcripts/2022/2))  
- No evidence was found of mining operations or crypto‑specific hardware/hosting infrastructure within the sample window. 

### Weight Assignment
Bizserv is assigned the majority weight (0.7) given sustained management emphasis on blockchain/Web3 services, explicit client examples (e.g., NFTs and Web3 platforms), a dedicated Metaverse Studio, and the Atix Labs acquisition. Holders receives 0.2 based on disclosed BTC/ETH (later including stablecoin) holdings recognized under IFRS as crypto assets; amounts appear small relative to total assets. A minor finserv weight (0.1) reflects management’s 2022 introduction of a custodial digital‑asset wallet concept, with no evidence of material scale. Miners and infrastr remain 0.0; unclass is 0.0. ([investiment.io](https://www.investiment.io/symbols/GLOB/earnings/transcripts/2021/4))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The 2022 “custodial wallet” announcement indicates capability, but public evidence of commercial uptake or revenue impact was limited; hence only a small finserv weight is applied. ([investiment.io](https://www.investiment.io/symbols/GLOB/earnings/transcripts/2022/2))  
- Crypto holdings were disclosed and appear immaterial relative to total assets; filings do not suggest a strategic HODL policy. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/g/NYSE_GLOB_2021.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 19, 2021 (Q3 2021), Motley Fool: “the ones that we just announced, Atix blockchain company and Navint…” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/19/globant-sa-glob-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcript/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/19/globant-sa-glob-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))
- Feb 17, 2022 (Q4 2021): “Pixel links… monetize them through NFTs… We’re also proud to be working with Dapper Labs… automate the creation of NFTs.” — [Investiment.io transcript](https://www.investiment.io/symbols/GLOB/earnings/transcripts/2021/4) ([investiment.io](https://www.investiment.io/symbols/GLOB/earnings/transcripts/2021/4))
- Aug 18, 2022 (Q2 2022): “We’re launching a new wallet for digital assets… an intuitive custodial wallet… operated via WhatsApp… send, receive and swap cryptocurrencies.” — [Investiment.io transcript](https://www.investiment.io/symbols/GLOB/earnings/transcripts/2022/2) ([investiment.io](https://www.investiment.io/symbols/GLOB/earnings/transcripts/2022/2))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- 2021 Form 20‑F: crypto assets accounting policy; as of Dec 31, 2021 crypto assets comprised Bitcoin and Ethereum. — [AnnualReports.com copy of Form 20‑F 2021](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/g/NYSE_GLOB_2021.pdf) ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/g/NYSE_GLOB_2021.pdf))
- 2023 Form 20‑F (filed Feb 29, 2024): policy note referencing Bitcoin, Ethereum and stablecoin as crypto assets. — [SEC filing (HTML)](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1557860/000162828024008037/glob-20231231.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1557860/000162828024008037/glob-20231231.htm?utm_source=openai))
- Oct 5, 2021: “Globant acquires Atix Labs to strengthen and empower its blockchain and crypto‑related solutions and products.” — [Globant press release](https://www.globant.com/pt-br/news/globant-acquires-atix-labs-strengthen-and-empower-its-blockchain-and-crypto-related-solutions) ([globant.com](https://www.globant.com/pt-br/news/globant-acquires-atix-labs-strengthen-and-empower-its-blockchain-and-crypto-related-solutions?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**

## SPROTT

* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: 2021 and 2023 annual reports; Q1 2021 earnings call; Q3 2024 MD&A

### Firm Overview
Sprott Inc. is a Toronto-based asset manager focused on precious metals and critical materials, with shares listed on the TSX and NYSE under the symbol SII. Its product set centers on physical bullion trusts, resource-focused ETFs and institutional strategies, rather than digital-asset businesses.

### Exposure Channels Identified
- Holders: Multiple filings disclose “digital gold strategies” recognized as financial instruments measured at fair value through profit or loss and carried within “Other assets,” indicating proprietary investments in tokenized/digital-gold instruments rather than customer-facing products. Amounts were small (for example, $7.06 million at December 31, 2021; $3.78 million at December 31, 2022; $3.41 million at December 31, 2023). ([sprott.com](https://sprott.com/media/4937/sii-2021-annual-report.pdf))  
- Management commentary referenced anticipated gold “tokenization,” reinforcing that any crypto-adjacent activity relates to digital representations of precious metals rather than Bitcoin/Ethereum exposure. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/05/07/sprott-inc-sii-q1-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))  
- No evidence in the 2017–2024 window that Sprott operated a crypto exchange, wallet/custody platform, mining operation, or crypto-backed ETP; its listed products and disclosures remain focused on physical bullion and resource equities. ([sprott.com](https://sprott.com/investment-strategies/exchange-listed-products/physical-bullion-funds/gold-and-silver/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Holders is set to 1.0 because the only substantiated crypto mechanism is Sprott’s proprietary “digital gold strategies” held on balance sheet as investment assets at FVTPL. There is no credible evidence of mining, crypto financial services to clients, non-financial crypto services, or crypto-specific infrastructure provision in 2017–2024. Given the small size of these digital‑asset positions relative to total assets and the absence of related revenue lines, RevShare is 0 and AssetShare is 1.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Filings label the assets “digital gold strategies” without detailing specific instruments or blockchain rails; classification therefore infers tokenized/digital-gold exposure but not broader cryptocurrency holdings. ([sprott.com](https://sprott.com/media/4937/sii-2021-annual-report.pdf))  
- Commentary about future tokenization did not coincide with a Sprott‑branded digital‑asset product launch during 2017–2024. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/05/07/sprott-inc-sii-q1-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 7, 2021 (Q1 2021): “we continue to believe that digital gold will be adopted… via gold tokenization.” — [Motley Fool transcript] ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/05/07/sprott-inc-sii-q1-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))

- 2023 Annual Report: Digital gold strategies carried at $3.78m (Dec 31, 2022) and $3.41m (Dec 31, 2023); classified at FVTPL. — [Sprott 2023 Annual Report PDF] ([sprott.com](https://sprott.com/media/22hj4rfz/sii-2023-annual-report.pdf))
- Q3 2024 MD&A/Financials: continuing disclosure of “Digital gold strategies” within Other assets. — [MD&A HTML copy] ([cdn.yahoofinance.com](https://cdn.yahoofinance.com/prod/sec-filings/0001512920/000162828024045448/sprott2024q3-mdaandfs.htm?utm_source=openai))

## DBS GROUP HOLDINGS

* Rank: 167 | Expo: 0.34 | id_str: SG1L01001701
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: DBS results media briefing transcripts (Q1 2021, Q4 2021, Q1 2022); Annual reports 2020–2023; DBS newsroom releases 2022–2024

### Firm Overview
DBS Group Holdings is a Singapore-headquartered banking group listed on the Singapore Exchange, operating across key Asian markets. Since late 2020, it has developed a bank-backed digital asset platform offering tokenisation, trading and custody for institutional and accredited investors via the DBS Digital Exchange, which remains a small but growing part of the group.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The bank directly provides crypto financial services through the members‑only DBS Digital Exchange (DDEx), including trading of digital payment tokens, institutional‑grade custody, and security token issuance/listing. Management repeatedly emphasised that access is restricted to institutional and accredited investors, not retail, and rolled out self‑directed crypto trading for these clients via its mobile banking app in 2022. Activity metrics disclosed by the bank show traction: DDEx crossed S$1 billion in trading value in its first full year (2021), reported an 80% YoY rise in Bitcoin trading in 2022 with a doubled customer base, and said trading value in the first five months of 2024 nearly tripled versus 2023. DBS Vickers, the brokerage arm, also obtained a Major Payment Institution licence under Singapore’s Payment Services Act to provide digital payment token services. ([dbs.com](https://www.dbs.com/iwov-resources/images/investors/quarterly-financials/2021/1Q21_media_briefing.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv is set to 1.0 because DBS operates a regulated crypto exchange and provides custody, tokenisation and listing services that generate fee‑based activity, while there is no evidence of proprietary treasury crypto holdings, mining, non‑financial crypto services, or crypto‑specific hardware infrastructure. Management described the digital exchange business as judiciously targeted at accredited/institutional clients and “still relatively small,” supporting RevShare = 1 (<~5%) and AssetShare = 0 (client assets under custody are off‑balance sheet). ([dbs.com](https://www.dbs.com/iwov-resources/images/investors/quarterly-financials/2021/1Q21_media_briefing.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Access policy evolved operationally (e.g., enabling self‑directed trading for accredited investors), but management stated retail crypto trading in Singapore would not launch in 2022; no contrary evidence was found for 2017–2024. ([dbs.com](https://www.dbs.com/iwov-resources/images/investors/quarterly-financials/2022/DBS_1Q22_media_briefing.pdf))  
- Reported “assets under custody” figures relate to client assets housed with DBS and do not evidence material on‑balance‑sheet crypto holdings by the group. ([dbs.com](https://www.dbs.com/iwov-resources/images/investors/annual-report/dbs-annual-report-2023.pdf))  
- Earnings materials are “media briefing” transcripts rather than third‑party transcript services; however, they are official company transcripts hosted on the investor relations site. ([dbs.com](https://www.dbs.com/iwov-resources/images/investors/quarterly-financials/2021/1Q21_media_briefing.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Apr 30, 2021 (Q1 2021): “we launched the digital exchange last quarter… our Digital Exchange capabilities are much like Coinbase… offering this to accredited investors and institutional counterparties… about $80m of assets under custody.” — [DBS IR transcript PDF](https://www.dbs.com/iwov-resources/images/investors/quarterly-financials/2021/1Q21_media_briefing.pdf)  
- Feb 14, 2022 (Q4 2021): “make trading access more convenient, but still restricting it to accredited investors… make trading online – self‑service and instantaneous.” — [DBS IR transcript PDF](https://www.dbs.com/iwov-resources/images/investors/quarterly-financials/2021/DBS_4Q21_media_briefing.pdf)  
- Apr 29, 2022 (Q1 2022): “We won’t do any retail crypto in Singapore this year… get our crypto offering available on mobile banking… [for] accredited investors.” — [DBS IR transcript PDF](https://www.dbs.com/iwov-resources/images/investors/quarterly-financials/2022/DBS_1Q22_media_briefing.pdf)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- 2020 Annual Report: “establishment of the DBS Digital Exchange… fund raising through asset tokenization and secondary trading of digital assets, including cryptocurrencies… for institutional and accredited investors.” — [DBS AR 2020 (pp. 44–60) PDF](https://www.dbs.com/annualreports/2020/files/media/dbs-annual-report-2020-p44-60.pdf)  
- 2022 Annual Report: “rolled out self‑directed crypto trading via DBS digibank, enabling accredited investors to trade cryptocurrencies on DBS Digital Exchange.” — [DBS AR 2022 PDF](https://www.dbs.com/annualreports/2022/files/media/dbs-annual-report-2022.pdf)  
- 2023 Annual Report: “DBS Digital Asset Ecosystem’s assets under custody… crossed SGD 1 billion.” — [DBS AR 2023 PDF](https://www.dbs.com/iwov-resources/images/investors/annual-report/dbs-annual-report-2023.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Dec 10, 2020: Bank‑backed exchange launch for tokenisation, trading and custody for institutional/accredited investors. — [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2020/12/10/singapore-bank-dbs-to-launch-digital-exchange-as-crypto-demand-soars.html)  
- Feb 15, 2022: “DDEx crosses billion‑dollar mark in trading value in first full year; digital assets under custody > S$800m.” — [DBS newsroom](https://www.dbs.com/newsroom/DBS_Digital_Exchange_crosses_billion_dollar_mark_in_trading_value_in_first_full_year_of_operations?pid=sg-group-pweb-media-hero-dbs-digital-exchange)  
- Oct 1, 2021: DBS Vickers receives MAS approval to provide digital payment token services as a Major Payment Institution. — [DBS newsroom](https://www.dbs.com/newsroom/DBS_Vickers_receives_regulatory_approval_under_Payment_Services_Act_to_provide_digital_payment_token_services)  
- Feb 15, 2023: “Bitcoin trading volume up 80% at DDEx in 2022; customer base doubled.” — [DBS newsroom](https://www.dbs.com/newsroom/Bitcoin_trading_volume_up_80pct_at_DBS_Digital_Exchange_in_2022_customer_base_doubles)  
- Feb 15, 2023: “DBS exchange not open to retail; close to 1,200 participants registered.” — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2023/02/15/dbs-says-bitcoin-trading-up-80-in-2022-on-ddex-exchange)  
- Jul 9, 2024: “Value of digital payment tokens traded on DDEx in Jan–May 2024 nearly tripled YoY; active trading clients +36%.” — [DBS newsroom](https://www.dbs.com/newsroom/DBS_Digital_Exchanges_trading_value_nearly_triples_in_first_five_months_of_2024_active_trading_clients_Up_36pct)
