## CONIFEX TIMBER

* Rank: 168 | Expo: 0.34 | id_str: CA2073241044
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 1.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q3 2023 earnings call, 2023 MD&A and AIF, investor presentations (2022–2024), legal/news coverage (2023–2024)

### Firm Overview
Conifex Timber Inc. is a Canadian forestry and bioenergy company listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange, operating a sawmill and a 36 MW biomass power plant in Mackenzie, British Columbia. Beginning in 2021–2022, management pursued a diversification strategy into high‑performance computing (HPC)/data center hosting, initially including cryptocurrency mining workloads, alongside its core lumber and power businesses.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Company materials and earnings remarks describe plans to build and host industrial‑scale HPC/data center capacity in British Columbia, drawing on Conifex’s power‑sector expertise. A 3 MW trial phase of hosting began at Mackenzie in late 2021/early 2022, and subsequent projects sought large interconnections to BC Hydro for expansion; these initiatives were explicitly affected by a provincial suspension on supplying power to new cryptocurrency mining projects. The model emphasized developing power and colocation infrastructure for next‑generation data center customers (including crypto miners), rather than operating a crypto exchange, holding crypto on the balance sheet, or providing non‑financial advisory services. Court and media coverage further corroborate that the initial purpose of certain projects was cryptocurrency mining, with later communications highlighting a pivot toward broader AI/ML HPC hosting. ([conifex.com](https://conifex.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/Conifex-Timber-Q2-2022-Corporate-Presentation.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Infrastructure is set to 1.0 because Conifex’s crypto‑adjacent activity is supplying and hosting compute/power infrastructure for mining/HPC clients, evidenced by the Mackenzie hosting trial and management’s stated plan to “develop industrial scale power infrastructure to serve next generation data center customers.” There is no evidence in the sample period of proprietary crypto treasury holdings, financial‑services activity, or the company operating its own mining fleet at scale; crypto exposure arises through intended colocation/energy infrastructure and was constrained by the BC moratorium. RevShare = 0 and AssetShare = 0 reflect the absence of disclosed, material crypto‑related revenue or on‑balance‑sheet digital assets during 2017–2024. ([conifex.com](https://conifex.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/Conifex-Timber-Q2-2022-Corporate-Presentation.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Some media and legal documents refer to the projects as “initially for the purpose of mining cryptocurrency,” which blurs whether Conifex would have self‑mined or strictly hosted third‑party miners; investor materials consistently frame the strategy as hosting/HPC infrastructure. ([timescolonist.com](https://www.timescolonist.com/economy-law-politics/bc-forestry-company-conifex-fights-to-revive-crypto-mines-6872910))  
- The BC power‑supply suspension materially limited execution; classification relies on stated plans and trial hosting rather than scaled operating metrics. ([conifex.com](https://conifex.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/2024-AIF-Final.pdf))  
- Only one accessible third‑party transcript (via Quartr embedded on MarketBeat) was identified for 2017–2024; additional transcripts exist behind paywalls. Certainty is therefore set to 3 with multi‑source corroboration. ([marketbeat.com](https://www.marketbeat.com/earnings/reports/2023-11-7-conifex-timber-inc-cffto-stock/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 7, 2023 (Q3 2023): “we intend to leverage our power sector expertise to develop industrial scale power infrastructure to serve next generation data center customers.” — [MarketBeat (Quartr transcript)](https://www.marketbeat.com/earnings/reports/2023-11-7-conifex-timber-inc-cffto-stock/) ([marketbeat.com](https://www.marketbeat.com/earnings/reports/2023-11-7-conifex-timber-inc-cffto-stock/))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Mar 27, 2024, 2023 MD&A: OIC “to suspend its obligation to supply service to certain new cryptocurrency mining projects… impacted two of our proposed high‑performance computing (HPC) projects.” — [Conifex MD&A](https://conifex.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/Conifex-2023-Management-Discussion-Analysis.pdf) ([conifex.com](https://conifex.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/Conifex-2023-Management-Discussion-Analysis.pdf))  
- Mar 12, 2025, 2024 AIF: recounts the December 2022 OIC halting service to new crypto mining projects and Conifex’s legal challenges; temporary suspension extended in 2024. — [Conifex AIF 2024](https://conifex.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/2024-AIF-Final.pdf) ([conifex.com](https://conifex.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/2024-AIF-Final.pdf))  
- Q2 2022 Corporate Presentation: “partnership… to develop a business hosting data center or other high‑performance computing (HPC) customers… commenced hosting an initial 1.5 MW in Dec 2021 and an additional 1.5 MW in Mar 2022.” — [Conifex Q2 2022 Presentation](https://conifex.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/Conifex-Timber-Q2-2022-Corporate-Presentation.pdf) ([conifex.com](https://conifex.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/Conifex-Timber-Q2-2022-Corporate-Presentation.pdf))  
- Mar 2024 Investor Presentation: “Conifex Data Center – Trial Phase… intent to use power sector expertise to develop industrial‑scale power infrastructure for next‑generation data center customers in BC.” — [Conifex Investor Presentation](https://conifex.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/Conifex-Q4-Investor-Presentation-1.pdf) ([conifex.com](https://conifex.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/Conifex-Q4-Investor-Presentation-1.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**
- Apr 19, 2023: “All the projects are being developed ‘initially for the purpose of mining cryptocurrency.’” — [Victoria Times Colonist](https://www.timescolonist.com/economy-law-politics/bc-forestry-company-conifex-fights-to-revive-crypto-mines-6872910) ([timescolonist.com](https://www.timescolonist.com/economy-law-politics/bc-forestry-company-conifex-fights-to-revive-crypto-mines-6872910))  
- Feb 2, 2024: BC Supreme Court dismisses Conifex petition; moratorium on power for crypto mining projects maintained. — [Norton Rose Fulbright](https://www.nortonrosefulbright.com/en/knowledge/publications/22aee927/bc-supreme-court-upholds-temporary-freeze-on-cryptocurrency-interconnections) ([nortonrosefulbright.com](https://www.nortonrosefulbright.com/en/knowledge/publications/22aee927/bc-supreme-court-upholds-temporary-freeze-on-cryptocurrency-interconnections?utm_source=openai))  
- 2024: Coverage of court decisions upholding the moratorium and its impact on Conifex mining/HPC plans. — [Business in Vancouver](https://www.biv.com/news/technology/court-upholds-moratorium-crypto-mining-8295314); [Prince George Citizen](https://www.princegeorgecitizen.com/local-news/conifex-loses-bid-to-overturn-ndp-pause-on-powering-cryptocurrency-mines-10319016) ([biv.com](https://www.biv.com/news/technology/court-upholds-moratorium-crypto-mining-8295314?utm_source=openai))

</small>

## USBC (ASE)

* Rank: 169 | Expo: 0.34 | id_str: US4992382022
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2023–2024), press releases (2023–2024), investor website

### Firm Overview
USBC, known as Know Labs, Inc. during the 2017–2024 sample period, is a U.S.-based health technology company focused on non-invasive medical diagnostics, particularly radio-frequency spectroscopy for glucose monitoring. Shares traded on NYSE American under the ticker KNW during this period, and the company’s disclosures emphasize development-stage medical devices rather than financial or crypto-related activities.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024 sources, there is no evidence that the company held cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet, mined digital assets, offered crypto financial services, provided non-financial services to the crypto sector, or supplied crypto-specific infrastructure. Management communications and filings consistently discuss Bio-RFID technology, clinical studies, and product prototypes for glucose monitoring. No earnings materials or filings in the period describe treasury Bitcoin holdings, exchange/custody operations, mining, or crypto-collateralized products. Accordingly, no crypto exposure mechanisms apply within the defined sample window.

### Weight Assignment
All mechanism categories are set to zero with unclass = 1.0 because the company’s documented activities during 2017–2024 relate exclusively to medical diagnostics R&D and product development, with no substantive, documented crypto linkage. With no qualifying evidence of holdings, mining, financial services, business services, or infrastructure targeted at crypto, classification defaults to Unclassified for the sample period.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The company underwent branding and strategic changes after the 2017–2024 window; out-of-sample developments are excluded by design.
- As a small-cap development-stage issuer, traditional third-party earnings call transcripts are limited; however, available company communications and SEC filings are sufficient to confirm lack of crypto exposure in-sample.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls / summaries:**
- Aug 2024: Discussion focused on Gen 1 prototype and KnowU wearable; no crypto references — [Investing.com earnings-call summary](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-know-labs-reports-q3-financials-and-development-progress-93CH-3573134)  
- May 2024: Q2 FY2024 call centered on R&D for non-invasive glucose monitor; no crypto references — [Investing.com earnings-call summary](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-know-labs-bolsters-rd-for-noninvasive-glucose-monitor-93CH-3444489)

**Filings (10‑K / SEC):**
- Nov 14, 2024, Form 10‑K: Business description is non-invasive diagnostics (Bio‑RFID), no crypto activities disclosed — [SEC filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1074828/000165495424014480/knwn_10k.htm)  
- Jun 17, 2024, Prospectus supplement: Describes KnowU wearable CGM and prototype progress; no crypto activities — [SEC filing](https://ir.knowlabs.co/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001654954-24-007853/0001654954-24-007853.pdf)  
- FY2023 Annual Report archive: Company focus on non-invasive glucose monitoring; no crypto references — [AnnualReports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/k/NYSE_KNW_2023.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Feb 27, 2024: Company announces KnowU wearable non-invasive CGM; focus remains medical diagnostics — [Press release (PDF)](https://d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net/_8530540ac2f1d6571bdb9360d96a66b7/knowlabs/news/2024-02-27_Know_Labs_Announces_the_KnowU_its_Wearable_Non_84.pdf)  
- Jan 4, 2023: Patent press release expanding IP for non-invasive glucose sensor; no crypto angle — [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20230104005268/en/Know-Labs-Inc.-Expands-IP-Leadership-in-Non-Invasive-Diagnostics-with-New-High-Performance-Glucose-Sensor-Patent)

## JSE

* Rank: 170 | Expo: 0.33 | id_str: ZAE000079711
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Financial news (2021, 2024), JSE market/regulatory notices

### Firm Overview
JSE Limited operates South Africa’s Johannesburg Stock Exchange, the largest securities exchange in Africa, offering listing, trading, clearing and data services across equities, derivatives and fixed income. During the 2017–2024 sample period, the group’s disclosures and market communications focus on traditional capital markets infrastructure rather than digital-asset businesses.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024, there is no evidence that JSE held cryptocurrencies, mined digital assets, or directly provided crypto financial services (such as operating a crypto exchange or issuing crypto ETPs). In fact, the exchange rejected applications to list crypto-linked ETFs in 2017 and again in June 2021, citing the absence of an appropriate domestic regulatory framework. ([mybroadband.co.za](https://mybroadband.co.za/news/cryptocurrency/401313-south-african-bitcoin-etf-torpedoed.html))  
As of May 15, 2024, industry reporting reiterated that the JSE could not consider the listing of ETFs or ETNs referencing crypto assets under prevailing rules. ([mybroadband.co.za](https://mybroadband.co.za/news/cryptocurrency/536957-big-changes-needed-for-bitcoin-etf-in-south-africa.html))

### Weight Assignment
Because JSE neither held crypto assets nor offered, issued, or operated crypto-asset products within the 2017–2024 window—and explicitly declined crypto ETP listings in that period—the firm is classified as Unclassified for crypto exposure. All mechanism weights are set to zero with unclass = 1.0, and the investor base amplifier is 0 given the lack of evidence that JSE’s equity price was used as a crypto proxy by investors. ([mybroadband.co.za](https://mybroadband.co.za/news/cryptocurrency/401313-south-african-bitcoin-etf-torpedoed.html))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Post-2024 regulatory proposals around permitting crypto-linked ETPs have been discussed publicly, but they fall outside the January 2017–December 2024 sample period and are therefore excluded from this classification.  
- Exchange-operator earnings call transcripts are limited in third-party databases; however, multiple credible news sources sufficiently document the absence of crypto product listings in-sample.

### Evidence

<small>

**Financial news / other:**
- Jun 12, 2021: JSE refused Sygnia’s Bitcoin ETF application, citing lack of a regulatory framework; notes a prior 2017 rejection. — [MyBroadband](https://mybroadband.co.za/news/cryptocurrency/401313-south-african-bitcoin-etf-torpedoed.html)  
- Jun 17, 2021: “Johannesburg Stock Exchange Rejects Bitcoin ETF… ‘lack of a regulatory framework for cryptocurrencies.’” — [ETF.com](https://www.etf.com/sections/bitcoin-crypto/johannesburg-stock-exchange-rejects-bitcoin-etf)  
- May 15, 2024: “As such, the Johannesburg Stock Exchange (JSE) cannot consider the listing of an ETF or exchange-traded note (ETN) that references crypto assets.” — [MyBroadband](https://mybroadband.co.za/news/cryptocurrency/536957-big-changes-needed-for-bitcoin-etf-in-south-africa.html)

</small>

## TWILIO 'A'

* Rank: 171 | Expo: 0.33 | id_str: US90138F1021
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings calls (Q4 2023, Q4 2024), Twilio customer case study, 10‑K filing

### Firm Overview
Twilio Inc. is a U.S.-based cloud communications platform company that provides APIs for programmable messaging, voice, email (SendGrid), and user verification to enterprises and developers. Its core business is customer engagement infrastructure rather than operating or investing in digital assets. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Twilio?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Within the 2017–2024 window, Twilio’s crypto exposure appears solely as a vendor to crypto firms that use its communications and verification APIs. Twilio’s own customer case study shows Crypto.com using Twilio Programmable Messaging, Verify, and SendGrid Email for ID verification, account security, and transaction protection—clear non‑financial, support‑service involvement with no asset custody. ([customers.twilio.com](https://customers.twilio.com/en-us/crypto?utm_source=openai))  
Management explicitly referenced “crypto customers” in earnings discussions: for Q4 2023, Twilio described organic growth headwinds tied to crypto customers; for Q4 2024, the CFO noted crypto customers were showing stronger volumes but remained “relatively immaterial.” These remarks anchor Twilio’s exposure as demand from crypto clients rather than any direct crypto activities. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-twilio-exceeds-q4-targets-with-strong-revenue-and-cash-flow-crypto-industry-challenges-to-persist-in-q1-93CH-3304672))

### Weight Assignment
All identified exposure channels are non‑financial business services delivered to crypto ecosystem participants (messaging, identity/OTP, email), with no evidence of holding crypto, mining, issuing crypto products, or dedicated mining/compute infrastructure. Therefore, bizserv = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. Consistent with management’s statement that crypto volumes are “relatively immaterial,” RevShare is coded 1 (<~5%) and AssetShare is 0. No evidence indicates Twilio’s equity trades as a crypto proxy, so invbase = 0. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2025/02/13/twilio-twlo-q4-2024-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Twilio does not disclose a crypto‑sector revenue breakout in filings; the magnitude is inferred from management commentary rather than segment reporting. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1447669/000144766925000035/twlo-20241231.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- The Crypto.com case study is a marketing/customer story without quantified revenue contribution. ([customers.twilio.com](https://customers.twilio.com/en-us/crypto?utm_source=openai))  
- No evidence in 10‑K filings of crypto asset holdings or mining; classification relies on transcripts and customer evidence for the crypto link. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1447669/000144766925000035/twlo-20241231.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 13, 2025 (Q4 2024 call): “From a crypto perspective, we do see those customers exhibiting stronger volume… it’s… relatively immaterial… not a big driver for the business.” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2025/02/13/twilio-twlo-q4-2024-earnings-call-transcript/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2025/02/13/twilio-twlo-q4-2024-earnings-call-transcript/))  
- Feb 15, 2024 (Q4 2023 call summary): “Total organic revenue growth excluding crypto customers was 10%… headwinds from the crypto industry expected to persist in Q1.” — [Investing.com summary](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-twilio-exceeds-q4-targets-with-strong-revenue-and-cash-flow-crypto-industry-challenges-to-persist-in-q1-93CH-3304672) ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-twilio-exceeds-q4-targets-with-strong-revenue-and-cash-flow-crypto-industry-challenges-to-persist-in-q1-93CH-3304672))

**Filings (10‑K / SEC):**
- 2024 Form 10‑K: Business remains customer‑engagement infrastructure; no disclosure of crypto holdings/mining activities. — [SEC filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1447669/000144766925000035/twlo-20241231.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1447669/000144766925000035/twlo-20241231.htm?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Crypto.com uses Twilio Messaging, Verify, and SendGrid Email for ID verification and account security. — [Twilio customer case study](https://customers.twilio.com/en-us/crypto) ([customers.twilio.com](https://customers.twilio.com/en-us/crypto?utm_source=openai))  
- Background: Twilio acquired Authy (2FA) with noted crypto‑exchange customers (e.g., Coinbase) in earlier years, illustrating longstanding usage of Twilio authentication in crypto. — [FinSMEs (Authy acquisition)](https://www.finsmes.com/2015/03/twilio-acquires-authy.html) ([finsmes.com](https://www.finsmes.com/2015/03/twilio-acquires-authy.html?utm_source=openai))

## TRINITY CAPITAL

* Rank: 172 | Expo: 0.33 | id_str: US8964423086
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: 10‑K filings (2023, 2024), Q2 2022 and Q3 2023 earnings calls, CleanSpark press release, Core Scientific filings

### Firm Overview
Trinity Capital Inc. is a U.S. business development company that provides growth-stage debt and equipment financing to venture-backed companies. Its business model centers on secured loans, equipment financings, and selective equity/warrant positions across diversified innovation sectors.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Within 2017–2024, Trinity’s crypto link is through lending and equipment financing to bitcoin miners and digital‑asset infrastructure firms. The company provided a $35 million equipment‑backed facility to CleanSpark, and Core Scientific disclosed drawdowns under a master equipment finance facility with Trinity. Trinity’s 2024 Form 10‑K explicitly classifies positions in “Digital Assets Technology and Services,” including Core Scientific and CleanSpark, and shows multiple equipment‑financing tranches to Applied Digital Corporation. Management also discussed bitcoin‑related borrowers and equipment‑lender restructurings on earnings calls, underscoring that exposure is via financing to miners rather than any direct crypto activity. ([investors.cleanspark.com](https://investors.cleanspark.com/news/news-details/2022/CleanSpark-Finalizes-35-Million-in-Financing-from-Trinity-Capital-04-26-2022/default.aspx?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
All observed exposure is financial intermediation to crypto ecosystem participants (equipment loans/secured credit to miners and digital‑asset data center operators). There is no evidence of proprietary crypto holdings, mining, or hardware provision by Trinity. Accordingly, finserv = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. Crypto remains a small slice of the book: Trinity’s 2023 Form 10‑K shows “Digital Assets Technology and Services” at roughly low‑single‑digit percentages of the portfolio, supporting RevShare = 1 (<~5%) and AssetShare = 1 (<~5%). ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/t/NASDAQ_TRIN_2023.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Magnitude varies over time with individual borrower dynamics (e.g., Core Scientific’s Chapter 11 and subsequent resolution); portfolio share can fluctuate between years. ([investors.corescientific.com](https://investors.corescientific.com/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001628280-22-014350/0001628280-22-014350.pdf?utm_source=openai))  
- Some transcripts are accessed via third‑party reposts (Motley‑style aggregators) due to primary site access limits; quotes are consistent with Seeking Alpha listings but hosted offsite. ([seekingalpha.com](https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TRIN/earnings/transcripts))

## AXOS FINANCIAL

* Rank: 173 | Expo: 0.33 | id_str: US05465C1009
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q1 FY2024 and Q4 FY2023 earnings calls; 2022 Form 10‑K risk factors; 2023 financial news (CNBC/Bloomberg‑syndicated)

### Firm Overview
Axos Financial, Inc. is a U.S. bank holding company based in Las Vegas, Nevada, whose primary subsidiaries provide digital consumer and commercial banking, and securities clearing and custody services. Within the 2017–2024 window, Axos’ crypto‑related activities were limited and ancillary to its broader banking and brokerage operations.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management disclosed a short‑lived “incubator” deposit‑gathering effort serving selected digital‑asset companies (e.g., exchanges, brokers, NFT‑related firms) and stated the bank maintained limited‑purpose operating accounts for Binance.US between March 14, 2023 and August 10, 2023; retail customer funds and wallet/retail crypto transactions were not handled. Axos subsequently exited this digital‑asset deposit initiative in 2023 amid regulatory developments. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/AX/documents/transcripts/q1-2024))  
Earlier, Axos’ 2022 Form 10‑K described a subsidiary (Axos Digital Assets LLC) that planned a retail crypto trading platform and a dollar‑backed stablecoin, but later reporting indicated those plans were put on hold with “no current plans” to proceed. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1299709/000129970922000232/ax-20220630.htm?utm_source=openai))  
External coverage around June 2023 also listed Axos among U.S. banks used by Binance.US, underscoring that Axos’ crypto exposure was via banking/payment rails, not proprietary crypto activity. ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2023/06/08/banks-to-cut-off-binance-access-to-us-banking-system-exchange-says.html?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
All identified exposure is financial intermediation for crypto clients (corporate operating accounts/fiat rails for digital‑asset firms) rather than mining, proprietary coin holdings, hardware, or non‑financial services. Management explicitly limited activities to operating accounts, then exited the vertical in 2023; there is no evidence of Axos holding crypto, mining, or launching retail crypto trading/custody. Accordingly, finserv = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. Given the narrow scope and duration, crypto‑attributable revenue appears peripheral (RevShare = 1) and there are no meaningful crypto assets on balance sheet (AssetShare = 0). ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/AX/documents/transcripts/q1-2024))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Magnitude of the crypto client deposit base was referenced qualitatively/with selected figures but not as a separate segment; precise revenue share is not disclosed. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/AX/documents/transcripts/q4-2023))  
- The 2022 10‑K outlined prospective crypto products via Axos Digital Assets LLC; subsequent statements suggest these were paused, but the subsidiary still appears on the list of subsidiaries. Timing of any future change is uncertain. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1299709/000129970922000232/ax-20220630.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- Some earnings‑call transcripts are accessed via reputable third‑party hosts due to platform access limits; quotes are consistent with standard call records. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/AX/documents/transcripts/q1-2024))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 26, 2023 (Q1 FY2024): “Between Mar 14, 2023 and Aug 10, 2023, Axos maintained limited purpose accounts for Binance.US… We… exited [the] deposit‑gathering business for digital‑asset companies.” — [Fintool transcript](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/AX/documents/transcripts/q1-2024) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/AX/documents/transcripts/q1-2024))  
- Jul 27, 2023 (Q4 FY2023): “Due to recent changes in the regulatory landscape… we’ve decided to exit our small incubator deposit gathering for digital asset companies.” — [Fintool transcript](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/AX/documents/transcripts/q4-2023) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/AX/documents/transcripts/q4-2023))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- 2022 Form 10‑K: Risk factor describing Axos Digital Assets LLC plans for retail crypto trading and a dollar‑backed stablecoin. — [SEC 10‑K (FY2022)](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1299709/000129970922000232/ax-20220630.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1299709/000129970922000232/ax-20220630.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- 2024 Form 10‑K exhibit: List of subsidiaries includes “Axos Digital Assets LLC.” — [SEC Exhibit 21.1](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1299709/000129970924000113/ex2112024063010-kexhibitq4.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1299709/000129970924000113/ex2112024063010-kexhibitq4.htm?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Jun 8, 2023: “Disclosed U.S. banking partners [for Binance.US] included Axos Bank…” — [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2023/06/08/banks-to-cut-off-binance-access-to-us-banking-system-exchange-says.html) ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2023/06/08/banks-to-cut-off-binance-access-to-us-banking-system-exchange-says.html?utm_source=openai))  
- Jun 2023: Axos opens accounts for some crypto firms; plans for retail crypto and a stablecoin “now on hold… no current plans.” — [Bloomberg (syndicated via NDTV Profit)](https://www.ndtvprofit.com/business/new-crypto-banking-system-emerges-as-regulators-crack-down-on-coinbase-binance-10040730) ([ndtvprofit.com](https://www.ndtvprofit.com/business/new-crypto-banking-system-emerges-as-regulators-crack-down-on-coinbase-binance-10040730))

## REMARK HOLDINGS

* Rank: 174 | Expo: 0.33 | id_str: US75955K3005
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.2, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.8
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q2–Q3 2021 earnings calls; 2022 Form 10‑K business description; Dec 2021 NFT platform press release; Q2 2024 earnings call

### Firm Overview
Remark Holdings, Inc. is a U.S. technology company headquartered in Las Vegas that develops and sells AI‑based computer vision software and related solutions across public safety, retail, transportation and other sectors. Within the 2017–2024 window, management briefly discussed plans to enter NFT/metaverse initiatives alongside its core AI operations.

### Exposure Channels Identified
On the August 23, 2021 and November 8, 2021 earnings calls, management outlined plans to “build” an NFT business and launch a Bikini.com‑anchored metaverse/NFT initiative (“Bikiniverse”) with a targeted Q4 2021 rollout. ([earningscall.biz](https://earningscall.biz/e/nasdaq/s/mark/y/2021/q/q2))  
A December 13, 2021 company press release announced a partnership with isMedia to launch an NFT platform with an initial drop tied to Bikini.com content. ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/remark-holdings-partners-with-ismedia-to-launch-nft-platform-301443065.html?utm_source=openai))  
Subsequent filings emphasize AI products and services as the primary business, with no segment disclosure of NFT‑related revenue or digital‑asset holdings, and later calls focus on AI safety/security deployments rather than NFTs or crypto. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1368365/000136836523000023/mark-20221231.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence of crypto exposure is confined to 2021–early 2022 announcements and earnings‑call commentary about launching an NFT/metaverse platform; there is no indication in later filings or calls of material revenue or assets from these activities. Therefore, a small weight is assigned to finserv (NFT platform/marketplace activity) with the balance to unclass given the firm’s primary AI focus and lack of demonstrated, ongoing crypto economics. RevShare is coded 0 and AssetShare 0 based on filings and calls showing no crypto‑attributable revenue or on‑balance‑sheet digital assets. ([earningscall.biz](https://earningscall.biz/e/nasdaq/s/mark/y/2021/q/q2))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Announced NFT/metaverse plans may have been exploratory; no clear launch, revenue, or asset disclosures were found in 2022–2024 filings. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1368365/000136836523000023/mark-20221231.htm))  
- Earnings‑call transcripts are sourced from reputable third‑party hosts; quotes are consistent with standard call records but may contain minor transcription errors. ([earningscall.biz](https://earningscall.biz/e/nasdaq/s/mark/y/2021/q/q2))  
- Trademark records (e.g., “BIKINIVERSE”) corroborate branding intent but do not evidence commercial traction. ([furm.com](https://furm.com/trademarks/bikiniverse-97144257?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 23, 2021 (Q2 2021): “...building a non‑fungible token… business in the US and Asia with a targeted Q4 launch…” — [EarningsCall.biz](https://earningscall.biz/e/nasdaq/s/mark/y/2021/q/q2)  
- Nov 8, 2021 (Q3 2021): “On our last earnings call, we announced our intention [to enter] the NFT metaverse… We are on track for a fourth‑quarter launch… with the launch of Bikiniverse.” — [EarningsCall.biz](https://earningscall.biz/e/nasdaq/s/mark/y/2021/q/q3)  
- Aug 19, 2024 (Q2 2024): “Our recent contract win for implementing weapon detection technology in the Clark County School District is a significant milestone…” — [Fintool transcript](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/MARK/documents/transcripts/q2-2024)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- 2022 Form 10‑K: “We currently earn the majority of our revenue from sales of AI‑based products and services.” — [SEC 10‑K (FY2022)](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1368365/000136836523000023/mark-20221231.htm)  
- Dec 13, 2021: “Remark Holdings… partners with isMedia to launch [an] NFT platform… initial drop anchored by… Bikini.com.” — [PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/remark-holdings-partners-with-ismedia-to-launch-nft-platform-301443065.html)

**Financial news / other:**
- Nov 26, 2021: “BIKINIVERSE” trademark application filed, owner: Remark Holdings, Inc. — [USPTO record (via Furm)](https://furm.com/trademarks/bikiniverse-97144257)  
- 2021 metaverse mentions surge; Remark cited among companies exploring metaverse initiatives — [GlobalData note](https://www.globaldata.com/media/business-fundamentals/metaverse-mentions-peaked-q4-2021-due-surge-metaverse-hype-says-globaldata/)

</small>

## NETS DEAD - DELIST.13/02/18

* Rank: 175 | Expo: 0.33 | id_str: DK0060745370
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.2, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.8
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 2017 Annual Report; 2016–2017 company news releases; Mar 2017 PYMNTS coverage

### Firm Overview
Nets A/S was a Nordic digital payments company headquartered in Denmark that provided merchant acquiring, card issuing and financial network services across the region. The company was taken private in 2018 (delisted on February 13, 2018) and, within the 2016–2017 period, explored blockchain-related innovations through its Digital Lab alongside its core payments activities.

### Exposure Channels Identified
In 2017, Nets entered a partnership with Chainalysis to enhance its fraud and dispute services for banks “working with virtual currencies on Blockchain,” indicating the provision of compliance/analytics tools around Bitcoin transaction validation rather than direct asset handling. ([rns-pdf.londonstockexchange.com](https://www.rns-pdf.londonstockexchange.com/rns/0541G_-2018-2-27.pdf))  
Company materials also describe a Digital Innovation Lab testing emerging technologies “such as … blockchain,” and contemporaneous posts highlight a micropayments proof‑of‑concept using blockchain for digital media. These point to R&D and advisory/enablement rather than exchange, custody, or treasury activity. ([rns-pdf.londonstockexchange.com](https://www.rns-pdf.londonstockexchange.com/rns/0541G_-2018-2-27.pdf))  
Trade press the same month reported the Nets–Chainalysis collaboration to help Nordic banks validate bitcoin transactions, consistent with a non‑financial service to the crypto ecosystem. ([pymnts.com](https://www.pymnts.com/news/security-and-risk/2017/danish-firms-partner-on-cryptocurrency-security-for-banks-blockchain-security-fraud-chainanalysis-nets-cryptosecurity/))

### Weight Assignment
Because evidence shows Nets providing compliance/analytics capabilities and running blockchain pilots without taking custody of digital assets, operating mining, or launching crypto financial products, exposure is assigned to bizserv (0.2) with the balance to unclass (0.8). No disclosures indicate crypto‑attributable revenue or on‑balance‑sheet digital assets, so RevShare = 0 and AssetShare = 0. Lack of retrievable earnings‑call transcripts for 2017–2018 (given the February 13, 2018 delisting) caps certainty at 2 despite clear filing and press evidence. ([rns-pdf.londonstockexchange.com](https://www.rns-pdf.londonstockexchange.com/rns/0541G_-2018-2-27.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- No earnings‑call transcript with crypto commentary was located; only call announcements were found, so transcript anchoring is not possible and certainty is capped at 2. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/NETS-31472033/news/Nets-A-S-Financial-results-for-Q3-2017-including-financial-calendar-for-2018-25470620/?utm_source=openai))  
- Blockchain initiatives appear exploratory/R&D and compliance‑oriented; material commercial uptake or segment revenue was not disclosed in 2017 filings. ([rns-pdf.londonstockexchange.com](https://www.rns-pdf.londonstockexchange.com/rns/0541G_-2018-2-27.pdf))  
- Post‑delisting ownership and later corporate combinations fall outside the scope of this entity’s classification window.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 9, 2017: “In connection with the publication of the results for Q3 2017, Nets will host a conference call…” — [MarketScreener](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/NETS-A-S-31423445/calendar/)  
- Aug 17, 2017: Q2 2017 results release noting a conference call for investors — [GlobeNewswire](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2017/08/17/1086898/0/en/Nets-A-S-Financial-results-for-Q2-2017.html)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- 2017 Annual Report (p. 8): “Nets enters into partnership with Chainalysis and strengthens product offering to banks working with virtual currencies on Blockchain.” — [Nets A/S Annual Report 2017 (PDF)](https://www.rns-pdf.londonstockexchange.com/rns/0541G_-2018-2-27.pdf)  
- 2017 Annual Report (p. 7): Digital Innovation Lab “trying out emerging technologies such as … blockchain.” — [Nets A/S Annual Report 2017 (PDF)](https://www.rns-pdf.londonstockexchange.com/rns/0541G_-2018-2-27.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Mar 27, 2017: “Danish Firms Partner On Cryptocurrency Security For Banks” (Nets + Chainalysis to help validate bitcoin transactions). — [PYMNTS](https://www.pymnts.com/news/security-and-risk/2017/danish-firms-partner-on-cryptocurrency-security-for-banks-blockchain-security-fraud-chainanalysis-nets-cryptosecurity/)  
- Mar 27, 2017: “Nets partners with Chainalysis for validation of cryptocurrency payments” with quotes from both firms. — [CryptoNinjas](https://www.cryptoninjas.net/2017/03/27/nets-partners-with-chainanalysis-for-validation-of-cryptocurrency-payments/)  
- 2017: “Blockchain could help online media monetise low‑value items” (Nets micropayments PoC). — [Nets Perspectives](https://www.nets.eu/perspectives/Pages/Blockchain-could-help-online-media-monetise-low-value-items.aspx)

</small>

## GFT TECHNOLOGIES

* Rank: 176 | Expo: 0.32 | id_str: DE0005800601
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.7, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.3
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q1 2023 earnings call; 2023 Annual Report; client success stories (VP Bank); press/news on UDPN and blockchain projects (2021–2024)

### Firm Overview
GFT Technologies SE is a Germany‑headquartered IT services company focused on digital transformation for financial services, insurance, and industrial clients, listed on Xetra. Within 2017–2024 it built and integrated blockchain/DLT solutions for banks and market infrastructures as part of broader consulting, engineering, and modernization engagements.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management explicitly cites blockchain and DLT among GFT’s core technology competencies on earnings calls, framing them as part of its digital transformation toolkit rather than as proprietary financial products or balance‑sheet assets. ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/xetra/gft/investor-relations/earnings-call/q1-2023))  
GFT’s project work includes building a tokenization and digital custody solution for VP Bank (Liechtenstein), integrating ERC‑1400 tokens on Ethereum with Avaloq core banking, and delivering DLT proof‑of‑concepts and platforms for regulated financial market use. ([gft.com](https://www.gft.com/pl/en/industries/success-stories/blockchain-based-asset-management))  
The company also collaborated on digital currency and payments initiatives (e.g., UDPN pilots and sandbox) and earlier partnered on DAML/Corda smart‑contract ecosystems; these place GFT as a technology and implementation partner rather than a custodian, exchange, or asset holder. ([gft.com](https://www.gft.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/press-and-news/2023/news/deutsche-bank-and-sc-ventures-by-standard-chartered-bank-complete-first-digital-currency-transfer-and-swap-on-the-universal-digital-payments-network?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence consistently shows GFT as a non‑financial service provider to the crypto/digital‑asset ecosystem: designing, integrating, and operating blockchain/DLT solutions for financial institutions. There is no indication that GFT directly issues, custodies, or trades crypto assets, operates mining, or holds crypto on its balance sheet. Accordingly, bizserv = 0.7 reflects repeated, substantive project delivery and management emphasis; the remaining 0.3 is left as unclass due to the lack of segment‑level crypto revenue disclosure and because blockchain is one of multiple service lines.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Client success stories and company press are marketing‑oriented; quantitative crypto revenue shares are not disclosed.  
- Some market‑infrastructure milestones occurred around 2025; classification relies on 2017–2024 activities and statements.  
- No evidence found of proprietary custody/exchange products or on‑balance‑sheet crypto holdings.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 11, 2023 (Q1 2023): “our expertise in technologies like cloud, blockchain, DLT, and AI … is well respected worldwide.” — [AlphaSpread transcript](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/xetra/gft/investor-relations/earnings-call/q1-2023) ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/xetra/gft/investor-relations/earnings-call/q1-2023))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- 2023 Annual Report: R&D “focus on … artificial intelligence, DLT/blockchain …” (R&D €18.19m); opportunities note highlights DLT/blockchain expertise. — [Annual Report 2023 (PDF)](https://www.hauptversammlung.de/fileadmin/public/user_upload/gft-annual-report-2023.pdf) ([hauptversammlung.de](https://www.hauptversammlung.de/fileadmin/public/user_upload/gft-annual-report-2023.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**
- VP Bank success story: tokenization and digital custody integrated into core banking; ERC‑1400 on Ethereum. — [GFT case study](https://www.gft.com/pl/en/insights/success-stories/blockchain-based-asset-management) ([gft.com](https://www.gft.com/pl/en/industries/success-stories/blockchain-based-asset-management))  
- UDPN: Deutsche Bank and SC Ventures complete first digital‑currency transfer/swap in UDPN sandbox; GFT contributed to development. — [GFT news](https://www.gft.com/vn/en/news/import/press-and-news/2023/News/deutsche-bank-and-sc-ventures-by-standard-chartered-bank-complete-first-digital-currency-transfer-and-swap-on-the-universal-digital-payments-network) ([gft.com](https://www.gft.com/vn/en/news/import/press-and-news/2023/News/deutsche-bank-and-sc-ventures-by-standard-chartered-bank-complete-first-digital-currency-transfer-and-swap-on-the-universal-digital-payments-network?utm_source=openai))  
- UDPN All‑in‑One Digital Currency Sandbox launched (Mar 13, 2024), with GFT involvement. — [GFT press release](https://www.gft.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/press-and-news/2024/press-releases/udpn-launches-all-in-one-digital-currency-sandbox-us) ([gft.com](https://www.gft.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/press-and-news/2024/press-releases/udpn-launches-all-in-one-digital-currency-sandbox-us?utm_source=openai))  
- SWIAT cooperates with GFT to offer global blockchain platform; GFT joins ecosystem as validator. — [GFT press release](https://www.gft.com/be/en/about-us/newsroom/press-and-news/2023/press-releases/swiat-cooperates-with-gft-to-offer-secure-compliant-global-blockchain-platform) ([gft.com](https://www.gft.com/be/en/about-us/newsroom/press-and-news/2023/press-releases/swiat-cooperates-with-gft-to-offer-secure-compliant-global-blockchain-platform?utm_source=openai))  
- GFT joins DAML for Corda program, supporting smart contracts on R3 Corda. — [Digital Asset blog](https://blog.digitalasset.com/news/daml-for-corda-gft) ([blog.digitalasset.com](https://blog.digitalasset.com/news/daml-for-corda-gft?utm_source=openai))  
- Early blockchain testing with Google Cloud; RBS Ethereum payments PoC referenced. — [CCN coverage](https://www.ccn.com/gft-google-cloud-platform-blockchain/) ([ccn.com](https://www.ccn.com/gft-google-cloud-platform-blockchain/))  
- BX Swiss (Dec 13, 2022): technical foundation for regulated trading of tokenized securities; Ethereum test blockchain; SIC cash settlement. — [BX Swiss press](https://www.bxswiss.com/news/20221213-BX-Swiss-legt-das-technische-Fundament-fuer-die-Zukunft-des-regulierten-Handels-mit-tokenisierten-Wertpapieren) ([bxswiss.com](https://www.bxswiss.com/news/20221213-BX-Swiss-legt-das-technische-Fundament-fuer-die-Zukunft-des-regulierten-Handels-mit-tokenisierten-Wertpapieren?utm_source=openai))

## VERISIGN

* Rank: 177 | Expo: 0.31 | id_str: US92343E1029
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filing (FY2024), Q4 2024 earnings call, trade press on patents (2020, 2024)

### Firm Overview
Verisign, Inc. is a U.S.-based provider of critical internet infrastructure best known as the registry operator for the .com and .net top-level domains and as an operator of two of the internet’s 13 root servers. Its business model centers on domain name registration services and DNS operations rather than financial services or asset holding.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024 filings and earnings communications, management positions Verisign squarely as a DNS and registry operator with no operational involvement in cryptocurrencies (no mining, custody/exchange, or treasury holdings). The FY2024 Form 10-K mentions “alternative namespaces … including blockchain namespaces” only as a potential substitution risk to traditional DNS, not as a product line or revenue driver. Verisign was granted patents related to associating domain names with blockchain addresses in 2020 and 2024, but there is no disclosed commercialization or segment revenue tied to these patents within the sample period. Taken together, evidence indicates no substantive, monetized crypto exposure during 2017–2024. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1014473/000101447325000006/vrsn-20241231.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Because Verisign’s references to blockchain are limited to competitive risk language in filings and uncommercialized patents, and there is no evidence of crypto asset holdings, mining, exchange/custody services, or crypto-directed infrastructure revenues in 2017–2024, the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0. Patents and generic risk-factor mentions are insufficient for assigning any non-zero mechanism weight under the project’s threshold principle.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Verisign holds blockchain-related patents; if these were commercialized into products or services after 2024, classification could change, but no such evidence appears in the 2017–2024 window. ([domainnamewire.com](https://domainnamewire.com/2024/06/04/verisign-granted-patent-for-connecting-domain-names-to-blockchain-addresses/?utm_source=openai))  
- Earnings call transcripts reviewed emphasize DNS security, registry programs, and ICANN/NTIA matters, with no crypto operational disclosures. ([thetranscript.net](https://thetranscript.net/transcript/7506/verisign-q4-2024-earnings-report))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 24, 2024 (Q3 2024): “we look-forward to working with the new CEO … particularly in the areas we engage in mostly, which is the security and stability of the DNS and DNS abuse.” — [The Transcript](https://thetranscript.net/transcript/7506/verisign-q4-2024-earnings-report) ([thetranscript.net](https://thetranscript.net/transcript/7506/verisign-q4-2024-earnings-report))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- FY2024 Form 10‑K (filed Feb 2025): “the demand for domain names … could be impacted by alternative namespaces … including blockchain namespaces.” — [SEC filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1014473/000101447325000006/vrsn-20241231.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1014473/000101447325000006/vrsn-20241231.htm))

**Financial news / other:**
- Jun 4, 2024: “Verisign granted patent for connecting domain names to blockchain addresses.” — [Domain Name Wire](https://domainnamewire.com/2024/06/04/verisign-granted-patent-for-connecting-domain-names-to-blockchain-addresses/) ([domainnamewire.com](https://domainnamewire.com/2024/06/04/verisign-granted-patent-for-connecting-domain-names-to-blockchain-addresses/?utm_source=openai))  
- Jul 21, 2020: “Verisign gets blockchain patent (Domain name blockchain user addresses).” — [Domain Name Wire](https://domainnamewire.com/2020/07/21/verisign-gets-blockchain-patent/) ([domainnamewire.com](https://domainnamewire.com/2020/07/21/verisign-gets-blockchain-patent/?utm_source=openai))

</small>

## JIAYIN GROUP ADR 1:4

* Rank: 178 | Expo: 0.31 | id_str: US47737C1045
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Form 20‑F (FY2023), Q3 2024 earnings call transcript

### Firm Overview
Jiayin Group Inc. is a China-based fintech platform listed on Nasdaq (JFIN) that facilitates unsecured consumer loans by connecting individual borrowers with institutional funding partners. The company operates a capital‑light loan facilitation model and focuses on risk management, data analytics and technology to support its lending marketplace.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across filings and investor materials for 2017–2024, Jiayin describes its business as facilitating consumer loans between borrowers and financial institutions; no operations involve cryptocurrency custody, exchange/brokerage, mining, token issuance, or treasury holdings. The FY2023 Form 20‑F frames the company as “one of the leading fintech platforms in China committed to facilitating … connections between underserved individual borrowers and financial institutions,” with revenue lines centered on loan facilitation and related services, not digital assets. On the Q3 2024 earnings call, management emphasized loan facilitation volume and technology (including large language models) for credit risk and operations—again without any mention of crypto activities. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/j/NASDAQ_JFIN_2023.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of any substantive, monetized crypto activity in filings and transcripts during 2017–2024—and no evidence of crypto asset holdings, mining, financial‑product issuance, or crypto‑directed infrastructure—Jiayin is classified as unclass = 1.0 under the project’s threshold principle. References across sources consistently position the firm as a consumer‑finance facilitator rather than a crypto‑exposed enterprise. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/j/NASDAQ_JFIN_2023.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Many third‑party transcript sites require log‑ins or are paywalled; one accessible transcript (Q3 2024) was located and reviewed. No transcript within 2017–2024 indicated crypto activity, but coverage density is limited. Certainty remains “NA” due to unclassified status. ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/2611633/q3-2024-jiayin-group-inc-earnings-call-transcript))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 20, 2024 (Q3 2024): “loan facilitation volume of approximately RMB 26.5 billion … revenue from loan facilitation services reached around RMB 1.1 billion … we further deepened the application of large language models.” — [GuruFocus transcript](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/2611633/q3-2024-jiayin-group-inc-earnings-call-transcript) ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/2611633/q3-2024-jiayin-group-inc-earnings-call-transcript))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- FY2023 Form 20‑F (filed Apr 29, 2024): “one of the leading fintech platforms in China committed to facilitating … connections between underserved individual borrowers and financial institutions.” — [AnnualReports.com (Form 20‑F PDF)](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/j/NASDAQ_JFIN_2023.pdf) ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/j/NASDAQ_JFIN_2023.pdf))
- FY2023 filing notice (Apr 2024): company announced filing of Form 20‑F for year ended Dec 31, 2023. — [IR news](https://ir.jiayin-fintech.com/news-releases/news-release-details/jiayin-group-inc-filed-annual-report-form-20-f-fiscal-year-2023) ([ir.jiayin-fintech.com](https://ir.jiayin-fintech.com/news-releases/news-release-details/jiayin-group-inc-filed-annual-report-form-20-f-fiscal-year-2023?utm_source=openai))

## ONECONNECT FINL. TECH. ADR 1:30

* Rank: 179 | Expo: 0.30 | id_str: US68248T2042
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q1 2020 earnings call transcript; FY2021 Form 20‑F; company website (Gamma platform); industry news/awards (2019–2020)

### Firm Overview
OneConnect Financial Technology Co., Ltd. is a Shenzhen-based technology-as-a-service provider to financial institutions, dual-listed on the NYSE (OCFT) and HKEX. The company offers software solutions across digital banking, digital insurance, and its Gamma platform, which includes enterprise blockchain capabilities for financial-sector use cases.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management and filings describe OneConnect’s use of blockchain technology (FiMAX architecture) within software solutions for banks, insurers, and government platforms, such as trade finance and logistics. This is enterprise distributed-ledger software embedded in client solutions, without the firm taking custody of cryptoassets or operating crypto financial products. Earnings call remarks and filings emphasize blockchain as an underlying technology among AI and cloud, with examples including a “blockchain‑backed SME financing platform” in Guangdong and a blockchain-enabled port logistics network with China Merchants Port Group. Industry awards and press further corroborate OneConnect as the technology provider behind eTradeConnect, Hong Kong’s blockchain-based trade finance platform.

### Weight Assignment
All evidence points to OneConnect providing non-financial technology services that leverage blockchain for client use cases; there is no indication of mining, exchange/custody operations, or proprietary crypto holdings. Therefore, the firm is classified entirely as bizserv = 1.0. Given blockchain’s role is as an enabling layer within broader fintech solutions, crypto-attributable revenue appears minor (RevShare code 1) and there is no balance-sheet crypto exposure (AssetShare 0).

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Blockchain is discussed as part of bundled solutions; revenue is not disclosed by technology, so the precise crypto-related share is not quantifiable.
- The company’s historical ownership of a Hong Kong virtual bank (sold in 2024) relates to digital banking, not crypto activity.
- No evidence of crypto custody, exchange, or mining was found; China’s regulatory environment likely limits such activities.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 7, 2020 (Q1 2020): “The blockchain‑backed SME financing platform in the Guangdong province achieved good results… we have recently signed an agreement with China Merchants Port Group to build a blockchain‑backed port logistics network.” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/05/07/oneconnect-financial-technology-co-ltd-ocft-q1-202.aspx) (fool.com) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/05/07/oneconnect-financial-technology-co-ltd-ocft-q1-202.aspx))
- Nov 14, 2024 (Q3 2024): Management outlined focus on Digital Banking, Digital Insurance, and Gamma platform; no crypto custody/exchange activities discussed. — [IR transcript PDF](https://s24.q4cdn.com/452244074/files/doc_financials/2024/q3/OneConnect-Financial-Techonology_Q3-20241114_Transcript.pdf) (ir.ocft.com) ([s24.q4cdn.com](https://s24.q4cdn.com/452244074/files/doc_financials/2024/q3/OneConnect-Financial-Techonology_Q3-20241114_Transcript.pdf))
- Nov 10, 2022 (Q3 2022): Discussion of Gamma platform and digital infrastructure for financial institutions. — [IR transcript PDF](https://s24.q4cdn.com/452244074/files/doc_financials/2022/q3/Transcript-OCFT-2022Q3-Earnings-Release-vF.pdf) (ir.ocft.com) ([s24.q4cdn.com](https://s24.q4cdn.com/452244074/files/doc_financials/2022/q3/Transcript-OCFT-2022Q3-Earnings-Release-vF.pdf))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- FY2021 Form 20‑F: “We use our blockchain technology, built upon our proprietary, secure and strong FiMAX architecture… Combining FiMAX’s patented crypto‑controlled data‑sharing algorithm and per‑field encryption technologies…” — [SEC filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1780531/000110465922041012/ocft-20211231x20f.htm) (sec.gov) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1780531/000110465922041012/ocft-20211231x20f.htm))
- FY2023 Form 20‑F/A (general business and listing details). — [SEC filing (cloudfront mirror)](https://d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net/CIK-0001780531/8e8021e0-e117-4b3b-9802-45e52a52e981.html) (d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net) ([d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net](https://d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net/CIK-0001780531/8e8021e0-e117-4b3b-9802-45e52a52e981.html))
- Corporate website: Gamma platform includes “blockchain” solutions for financial institutions. — [Company site](https://www.oneconnectft.com/en/) (oneconnectft.com) ([oneconnectft.com](https://www.oneconnectft.com/en/))

**Financial news / other:**
- Apr 3, 2020: “OneConnect… is building a logistics‑tracking blockchain platform with the China Merchants Port Group.” — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2020/04/03/softbank-backed-fintech-firm-joins-chinese-conglomerate-to-build-blockchain-platform) (coindesk.com) ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2020/04/03/softbank-backed-fintech-firm-joins-chinese-conglomerate-to-build-blockchain-platform?utm_source=openai))
- Nov 25, 2020: OneConnect and China Merchants Group launch Greater Bay Area port logistics/trade facilitation blockchain platform. — [Port Technology International](https://www.porttechnology.org/oneconnect-and-china-merchants-group-launch-linked-port-blockchain-project/) (porttechnology.org) ([porttechnology.org](https://www.porttechnology.org/oneconnect-and-china-merchants-group-launch-linked-port-blockchain-project/?utm_source=openai))

## INTERCONTINENTAL EX.

* Rank: 180 | Expo: 0.30 | id_str: US45866F1049
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2021–2024), Q4 2018 and Q2/Q3 2021 earnings calls, ICE/Bakkt press releases, major financial news (2019)

### Firm Overview
Intercontinental Exchange, Inc. (ICE) is a U.S.-based operator of exchanges, clearing houses and data services, including the New York Stock Exchange. Within the 2017–2024 sample period, ICE created and majority-owned Bakkt, which launched bitcoin futures on ICE Futures U.S. in September 2019; ICE later deconsolidated Bakkt when it went public on October 15, 2021 and thereafter accounted for it as an equity-method investment. ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2019/09/23/nyse-owner-ice-launches-deliverable-bitcoin-futures-contracts.html?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s crypto linkage is via financial services: through Bakkt and ICE Futures U.S., ICE offered regulated bitcoin futures and related clearing/custody arrangements (deliveries under the contracts were made via Bakkt Trust). Management repeatedly discussed Bakkt on earnings calls, first as a majority-owned investment and later as a standalone public company in which ICE held a minority stake. There is no evidence that ICE mined crypto or held crypto on its own balance sheet outside of operating needs, and its exposure is not hardware/infrastructure-related. ([bakkt.com](https://bakkt.com/newsroom/ice-announces-record-trading-volume-for-physically-delivered-bakkt-bitcoin-futures-contracts?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence across calls, filings, and news consistently shows ICE’s exposure arises from operating and enabling crypto financial products (exchange, clearing, custody via Bakkt), not from proprietary coin holdings or mining. Accordingly, finserv is set to 1.0, with all other mechanisms at 0.0. Given management’s characterization of Bakkt as an investment and the later deconsolidation with equity-method accounting, crypto-related revenue contribution to ICE appears minor (RevShare code 1) and there is no balance-sheet crypto asset exposure at ICE (AssetShare 0). ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/02/08/intercontinental-exchange-ice-q4-2018-earnings-con.aspx))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Bakkt’s crypto activity sits partly outside ICE post–October 15, 2021; ICE’s ongoing exposure is indirect (equity-method investment plus any product/clearing activity), and detailed revenue attribution to crypto is not separately disclosed. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1571949/000119312523079282/d441572d10ka.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- Volumes and financial materiality for Bakkt-branded bitcoin futures within the period were modest relative to ICE’s diversified businesses, limiting precision on RevShare. ([bakkt.com](https://bakkt.com/newsroom/ice-announces-record-trading-volume-for-physically-delivered-bakkt-bitcoin-futures-contracts?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 8, 2019 (Q4 2018): “We’re excited about… newer initiatives such as … Bakkt, which recently raised over $180 million in its first round of funding.” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/02/08/intercontinental-exchange-ice-q4-2018-earnings-con.aspx) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/02/08/intercontinental-exchange-ice-q4-2018-earnings-con.aspx))
- Jul 29, 2021 (Q2 2021): “Our investment in Bakkt contributed approximately $36 million to second quarter expenses… We now anticipate that the Bakkt… transaction… will close in the third quarter.” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/07/29/intercontinental-exchange-inc-ice-q2-2021-earnings/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/07/29/intercontinental-exchange-inc-ice-q2-2021-earnings/))
- Oct 28, 2021 (Q3 2021): “We… unlocked Bakkt via our New York Stock Exchange listing… Other than that, Bakkt will be doing its own earnings calls now as a public company.” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/28/intercontinental-exchange-inc-ice-q3-2021-earnings/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/28/intercontinental-exchange-inc-ice-q3-2021-earnings/))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Feb 3, 2022 (FY2021 10‑K): “We recorded… a $1.4 billion gain on the deconsolidation of Bakkt.” — [SEC filing](https://content.edgar-online.com/ExternalLink/EDGAR/0001571949-22-000006.html?dest=ice20211231ex211_htm&hash=23269905bdc7f232995a89f2693be793b488d891e4020c9f3eb7c12ad6ddbc3f) ([content.edgar-online.com](https://content.edgar-online.com/ExternalLink/EDGAR/0001571949-22-000006.html?dest=ice20211231ex211_htm&hash=23269905bdc7f232995a89f2693be793b488d891e4020c9f3eb7c12ad6ddbc3f&utm_source=openai))
- Feb 2, 2023 (10‑K/A): “Following the closing of the merger on October 15, 2021, we deconsolidated Bakkt… and treat it as an equity method investment.” — [SEC filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1571949/000119312523079282/d441572d10ka.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1571949/000119312523079282/d441572d10ka.htm?utm_source=openai))
- 2024 annual report excerpt: notes continued equity‑method treatment and prior impairment related to Bakkt in 2022. — [Annual report PDF](https://s2.q4cdn.com/154085107/files/doc_financials/2024/ar/cae03d3f-ac2f-4754-bb2c-9ee3defc4da3.pdf) ([s2.q4cdn.com](https://s2.q4cdn.com/154085107/files/doc_financials/2024/ar/cae03d3f-ac2f-4754-bb2c-9ee3defc4da3.pdf?utm_source=openai))
- Oct 15, 2021: “Bakkt… completed its merger with VPC Impact Acquisition Holdings and will begin trading… on October 18.” — [ICE press release](https://ir.theice.com/press/news-details/2021/ICE-Announces-Completion-of-Merger-Between-Bakkt-and-VPC-Impact-Acquisition-Holdings/default.aspx) ([ir.theice.com](https://ir.theice.com/press/news-details/2021/ICE-Announces-Completion-of-Merger-Between-Bakkt-and-VPC-Impact-Acquisition-Holdings/default.aspx?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Sep 23, 2019: “NYSE owner ICE launches deliverable bitcoin futures contracts.” — [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2019/09/23/nyse-owner-ice-launches-deliverable-bitcoin-futures-contracts.html) ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2019/09/23/nyse-owner-ice-launches-deliverable-bitcoin-futures-contracts.html?utm_source=openai))
- Jun 13, 2019: Bakkt to begin testing physically settled bitcoin futures in July; contracts listed and cleared through ICE venues. — [Reuters via Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/cryptocurrency-news/bakkt-says-bitcoin-futures-testing-to-begin-in-july-1896966) ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/cryptocurrency-news/bakkt-says-bitcoin-futures-testing-to-begin-in-july-1896966?utm_source=openai))
- May 13, 2019: ICE Futures U.S. contract spec notes deliveries “made through Bakkt Trust Company LLC.” — [CFTC filing](https://www.cftc.gov/sites/default/files/filings/ptc/19/05/ptc051319iceusdcm001.pdf) ([cftc.gov](https://www.cftc.gov/sites/default/files/filings/ptc/19/05/ptc051319iceusdcm001.pdf?utm_source=openai))

## INTERACTIVE BROKERS GROUP A

* Rank: 181 | Expo: 0.30 | id_str: US45841N1072
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10‑K filings (2022–2023), Q3 2021 and Q4 2021 earnings calls, company press releases (2021, 2023)

### Firm Overview
Interactive Brokers Group, Inc. is a U.S.-based global electronic brokerage serving individual and institutional clients across numerous markets and asset classes. Within the 2017–2024 sample window, its crypto activity consists of enabling clients to trade certain cryptocurrencies via regulated third-party partners rather than holding or custoding digital assets itself. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1381197/000138119724000083/ibkr-20231231x10k.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
IBKR launched cryptocurrency trading for U.S. clients on September 13, 2021 through Paxos Trust Company, later extending access to U.S. RIAs and expanding internationally (e.g., Hong Kong via OSL Digital Securities). ([interactivebrokers.com](https://www.interactivebrokers.com/en/general/about/mediaRelations/9-13-21.php?utm_source=openai))
Management explained on the Q3 2021 call that Paxos charges commissions and pays IBKR a fee share, confirming the firm’s role as a brokered access point rather than a principal or custodian. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/19/interactive-brokers-group-inc-ibkr-q3-2021-earning/))
On the Q4 2021 call, management also stated IBKR does not allow margin financing on cryptocurrencies, reinforcing that the company avoids direct balance‑sheet exposure to digital assets. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/01/18/interactive-brokers-ibkr-q4-2021-earnings-call-tra/))

### Weight Assignment
All credible sources indicate IBKR’s crypto exposure arises from offering clients access to buy/sell and custody crypto through partners (Paxos/Zero Hash; OSL in Hong Kong), generating fee income but not proprietary crypto holdings or mining. Therefore, finserv is set to 1.0 and all other mechanisms to 0.0. Given management’s characterization and the absence of separate, sizable line items in filings, crypto appears to be a minor contributor to overall revenue (RevShare code 1), with no crypto assets on IBKR’s balance sheet (AssetShare 0). ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/19/interactive-brokers-group-inc-ibkr-q3-2021-earning/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Geographic availability, partner mix (Paxos/Zero Hash; OSL in Hong Kong), and token coverage evolved over time; IBKR does not break out detailed crypto revenue, limiting precision on materiality estimates. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1381197/000138119724000083/ibkr-20231231x10k.htm?utm_source=openai))
- Media reports confirm the 2021 U.S. launch; however, external news rarely quantifies the crypto fee contribution to IBKR specifically. ([bloomberg.com](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2021-09-13/interactive-brokers-adds-cryptocurrency-trading-joining-rivals?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 19, 2021 (Q3 2021): “We are the customer to Paxos… Paxos charge[s] the commission and then gives us a fee.” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/19/interactive-brokers-group-inc-ibkr-q3-2021-earning/)  
- Oct 19, 2021 (Q3 2021): “At this moment this is only available in the United States… invited around 350,000 accounts… 17,000 requested permission to trade [crypto].” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/19/interactive-brokers-group-inc-ibkr-q3-2021-earning/)  
- Jan 18, 2022 (Q4 2021): “We are not allowing margin on cryptos… we cannot finance crypto.” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/01/18/interactive-brokers-ibkr-q4-2021-earnings-call-tra/)

**Filings (10‑K / annual report):**
- Feb 2024 (FY2023 10‑K): Customers “can trade Bitcoin, Ethereum, Litecoin and Bitcoin Cash through Paxos Trust Company or Zero Hash LLC… In Hong Kong, customers can trade and hold BTC and ETH in their account with [IBHK].” — [SEC 10‑K](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1381197/000138119724000083/ibkr-20231231x10k.htm)  
- 2023 (FY2022 Annual Report PDF): Notes crypto trading is provided “through a third‑party cryptocurrency” provider on IBKR’s platform. — [Annual Report PDF](https://investors.interactivebrokers.com/download/2022_IBG_AR.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Sep 13, 2021: IBKR launches U.S. crypto trading via Paxos (BTC, ETH, LTC, BCH). — [Company press release](https://www.interactivebrokers.com/en/general/about/mediaRelations/9-13-21.php)  
- Oct 18, 2021: IBKR enables U.S. RIAs to trade crypto via Paxos on behalf of clients. — [Company press release](https://www.interactivebrokers.com/en/general/about/mediaRelations/10-18-21.php)  
- Feb 14, 2023: IBKR Hong Kong launches BTC/ETH trading for Professional Investors with OSL. — [Company press release](https://www.interactivebrokers.com/en/general/about/mediaRelations/2-15-23.php)  
- Nov 28, 2023: OSL and IBKR enable retail digital asset trading in Hong Kong. — [OSL press release](https://www.osl.com/hk-en/press-release/osl-interactive-brokers-retail-digital-asset-trading-hong-kong/)  
- Sep 13, 2021: News coverage of IBKR adding crypto trading via Paxos. — [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2021-09-13/interactive-brokers-adds-cryptocurrency-trading-joining-rivals)  
- Sep 13, 2021: Coverage of the Paxos partnership and supported coins. — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/09/13/interactive-brokers-launches-crypto-trading-through-paxos)

</small>

## DIGITALOCEAN HOLDINGS

* Rank: 182 | Expo: 0.30 | id_str: US25402D1028
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 1.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 2 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q2 2022 earnings call transcript, 10‑K filing (2023), company website/policies (Blockchain solutions page; Acceptable Use Policy), Marketplace/press release (2019)

### Firm Overview
DigitalOcean Holdings, Inc. is a U.S.-based public cloud provider focused on developers, startups, and SMBs, offering virtual machines, managed databases, Kubernetes, and related services. Within the 2017–2024 window, its crypto-related activity is limited to hosting blockchain applications and nodes for customers rather than engaging in crypto custody, investing, or mining itself.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management explicitly discussed a “blockchain” customer vertical on the August 8, 2022 Q2 earnings call, noting it was experiencing macro-related weakness and that DigitalOcean “doesn’t do a lot of crypto” itself, with impact concentrated in blockchain workloads. ([s206.q4cdn.com](https://s206.q4cdn.com/265192696/files/doc_financials/2022/q2/DOCN-Q2-22-Earnings-Transcript.pdf))  
DigitalOcean markets a dedicated “Cloud Blockchain Servers” solution and its Marketplace has listed one‑click images for running full nodes (e.g., OneLedger Mainnet), indicating purposeful support for blockchain hosting on its infrastructure. ([digitalocean.com](https://www.digitalocean.com/solutions/blockchain))  
At the same time, the company’s Acceptable Use Policy prohibits cryptocurrency mining without explicit written permission, underscoring that DigitalOcean is not a mining operator and restricts such workloads on its platform. ([digitalocean.com](https://www.digitalocean.com/legal/acceptable-use-policy?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
All credible sources point to DigitalOcean’s crypto exposure arising from providing cloud compute and hosting to blockchain customers (infrastructure), not from holding digital assets, mining, or offering financial products. Management characterized blockchain as a distinct customer vertical (about 5% of revenue in Q2 2022) but emphasized the company does not “do a lot of crypto,” reinforcing the lack of direct balance‑sheet or financial‑services exposure. Accordingly, infrastr = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. Given the “about 5%” datapoint and no later disclosure of larger shares, RevShare is coded 2 (~5–15%), and AssetShare is 0. ([s206.q4cdn.com](https://s206.q4cdn.com/265192696/files/doc_financials/2022/q2/DOCN-Q2-22-Earnings-Transcript.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The only quantified share for the blockchain vertical (“about 5% of revenue”) is from Q2 2022; subsequent filings and calls did not provide updated percentages, so materiality may have shifted. ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/nyse/docn/investor-relations/earnings-call/q2-2022?utm_source=openai))  
- DigitalOcean’s policies limit mining workloads; Marketplace listings and solution pages indicate support for blockchain apps/nodes, but the company does not break out blockchain revenue in 10‑Ks, constraining precision. ([digitalocean.com](https://www.digitalocean.com/legal/acceptable-use-policy?utm_source=openai))  
- No evidence of proprietary crypto holdings or custody activities was found in the 2023 10‑K. ([investors.digitalocean.com](https://investors.digitalocean.com/files/doc_financials/2023/ar/digitalocean-2023-ar_web-ready.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 8, 2022 (Q2 2022): “Yeah. We don’t do a lot of crypto. It’s more in the blockchain… [blockchain is] by far the most meaningful vertical that we’re seeing real impact.” — [Q2 2022 corrected transcript (PDF)](https://s206.q4cdn.com/265192696/files/doc_financials/2022/q2/DOCN-Q2-22-Earnings-Transcript.pdf)  
- Aug 8, 2022 (Q2 2022): “Blockchain… in Q2 represented about 5% of revenue… [and] is under extreme pressure.” — [AlphaSpread transcript mirror](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/nyse/docn/investor-relations/earnings-call/q2-2022)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- FY2023 Form 10‑K: Company business overview; no disclosure of crypto asset holdings or custody operations — [2023 10‑K (PDF)](https://investors.digitalocean.com/files/doc_financials/2023/ar/digitalocean-2023-ar_web-ready.pdf)  
- Mar 5, 2019: DigitalOcean launches Marketplace; initial categories include “blockchain” — [Press release](https://investors.digitalocean.com/news/news-details/2019/DigitalOcean-Launches-Partner-App-Marketplace/default.aspx)

**Financial news / other:**
- Company solutions page: “Cost‑effective, scalable cloud hosting for blockchain businesses.” — [Cloud Blockchain
**Financial news / other:**
- Mar 5, 2019: DigitalOcean launches Partner App Marketplace; initial app categories include “blockchain.” — [Company news release](https://investors.digitalocean.com/news/news-details/2019/DigitalOcean-Launches-Partner-App-Marketplace/default.aspx)  
- Solutions page (accessed 2026): “Cost‑effective, scalable cloud hosting for blockchain businesses.” — [DigitalOcean Cloud Blockchain Servers](https://www.digitalocean.com/solutions/blockchain)  
- Jan 24, 2024: OneLedger “Mainnet Fullnode” 1‑Click App listing shows blockchain node deployment on DigitalOcean. — [DigitalOcean Marketplace docs](https://docs.digitalocean.com/products/marketplace/catalog/mainnet-fullnode/)  
- Policy reference (updated Mar 20, 2026): “Mining of Cryptocurrencies… without explicit written permission.” — [Acceptable Use Policy](https://www.digitalocean.com/legal/acceptable-use-policy)  
- Dec 21, 2020: Community Q&A confirms running full nodes is permitted while mining requires written permission, citing AUP. — [DigitalOcean Community](https://www.digitalocean.com/community/questions/does-cloud-provider-digitalocean-allow-users-to-run-crypto-full-nodes)

</small>
