A review of 4SC’s annual and interim reports across 2017–2024 shows no references to holding, mining, transacting in, or providing services related to cryptocurrencies or blockchain. The 2023 Annual Report describes business operations exclusively in biopharma R&D and commercialization planning, without crypto‑related assets, revenues, or products. No evidence was found of crypto treasury holdings, mining operations, crypto financial services, crypto‑focused business services, or targeted infrastructure offerings. ([4sc.com](https://www.4sc.com/investors/investor-information/financial-reports/))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of substantive crypto references across filings and company communications during the sample window, the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0) with all other mechanism weights = 0.0. There is likewise no evidence to code an investor‑base amplifier effect tied to crypto sentiment.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified beyond typical small‑cap disclosure constraints. Earnings call transcripts exist via third‑party aggregators, but accessible records reviewed did not indicate crypto involvement; classification relies primarily on company filings and official releases. ## TANLA PLATFORMS

* Rank: 184 | Expo: 0.29 | id_str: INE483C01032
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Annual/Integrated Reports (FY2024), product/press materials for Trubloq (2019–2024), Q3 FY24 earnings call transcript (Jan 24, 2024)

### Firm Overview
Tanla Platforms Limited is an India-based communications platform-as-a-service (CPaaS) provider that builds and operates large-scale messaging platforms for enterprises and telecom operators. Its portfolio includes Trubloq, a distributed-ledger-based system used to enforce Indian telecom anti-spam regulations, and other omnichannel offerings such as RCS, WhatsApp and voice; none of these involve cryptocurrency custody, mining, or trading. ([stockinsights-in-filings.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com](https://stockinsights-in-filings.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/annual-reports/TANLA-Tanla_Platforms_Ltd-AnnualReport-FY2024.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across filings, investor materials and news during 2017–2024, Tanla’s blockchain usage is confined to enterprise telecom compliance (TRAI’s DLT regime) via its Trubloq platform. The documentation describes Trubloq as a “world’s largest blockchain use case” for SMS template/consent registration and scrubbing; no crypto assets, tokens, or crypto-financial services are referenced. Management’s earnings-call commentary centers on CPaaS mix (domestic/ILD SMS, OTT channels like WhatsApp and RCS) rather than any crypto-related activity. Accordingly, there is no evidence of treasury crypto holdings, mining operations, crypto custody/exchange products, or mining-oriented infrastructure sales. ([stockinsights-in-filings.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com](https://stockinsights-in-filings.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/annual-reports/TANLA-Tanla_Platforms_Ltd-AnnualReport-FY2024.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Because Tanla’s blockchain deployment is an enterprise DLT application for telecom compliance rather than a service to the cryptocurrency ecosystem, and there is no indication of crypto assets on balance sheet or crypto financial services, the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). All mechanism categories tied to crypto (holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, infrastr) are set to 0.0 in the absence of substantive, crypto-linked exposure during 2017–2024. ([stockinsights-in-filings.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com](https://stockinsights-in-filings.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/annual-reports/TANLA-Tanla_Platforms_Ltd-AnnualReport-FY2024.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Tanla frequently markets Trubloq as a blockchain-based platform, which could be misconstrued as crypto exposure; however, available materials consistently frame it as a telecom DLT solution mandated by TRAI without any tokenization or crypto-asset component. No disclosures or transcripts within the sample window indicate crypto holdings, mining, custody, or trading services. ([tanla.com](https://www.tanla.com/newsroom/tanla-launches-trubloq-at-mwc-barcelona?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jan 24, 2024 (Q3 FY24): “we are having the largest share in the RCS market in India today that is upwards of 40%.” — Tanla Q3 FY24 Earnings Conference Call Transcript (PDF) ([NSE filing mirror](https://nsearchives.nseindia.com/corporate/TANLA_30012024151514_UFR_Q3FY24_Earnings_Call_Transcripts_to_SEs_Final.pdf)) ([nsearchives.nseindia.com](https://nsearchives.nseindia.com/corporate/TANLA_30012024151514_UFR_Q3FY24_Earnings_Call_Transcripts_to_SEs_Final.pdf))

**Filings (Annual/Integrated reports, exchange):**
- FY2023–24 Integrated Annual Report: “Trubloq, the world’s largest use-case on blockchain application in telecommunications…”; no mention of crypto assets or services — Tanla Platforms (PDF) ([Annual Report FY2024](https://stockinsights-in-filings.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/annual-reports/TANLA-Tanla_Platforms_Ltd-AnnualReport-FY2024.pdf)) ([stockinsights-in-filings.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com](https://stockinsights-in-filings.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/annual-reports/TANLA-Tanla_Platforms_Ltd-AnnualReport-FY2024.pdf))
- Jan 30, 2024: Transcript submission to stock exchanges for Q3 FY24 earnings call — Tanla Platforms (PDF) ([NSE filing](https://nsearchives.nseindia.com/corporate/TANLA_30012024151514_UFR_Q3FY24_Earnings_Call_Transcripts_to_SEs_Final.pdf)) ([nsearchives.nseindia.com](https://nsearchives.nseindia.com/corporate/TANLA_30012024151514_UFR_Q3FY24_Earnings_Call_Transcripts_to_SEs_Final.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**

- Feb 26, 2019: “World’s First Blockchain Enabled Commercial Communication Stack, TRUBLOQ” launch aligned with TRAI DLT rules — Tanla Platforms ([Press release](https://www.tanla.com/newsroom/tanla-launches-trubloq-at-mwc-barcelona)) ([tanla.com](https://www.tanla.com/newsroom/tanla-launches-trubloq-at-mwc-barcelona?utm_source=openai))

## 9F ADR 1:20

* Rank: 185 | Expo: 0.29 | id_str: US65442R2085
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Company earnings releases (2019), IR event calendar (2019); Form 20-F filings (2021–2024); press releases and crypto trade press (2021)

### Firm Overview
9F Inc is a China-based financial technology company listed on Nasdaq under the ticker JFU whose business has evolved from online consumer finance toward a licensed securities brokerage and wealth management footprint centered in Hong Kong. In 2021 the company announced a strategic transformation toward a “digital and blockchain-based securities brokerage” with “digital asset technology services,” while statutory filings through 2024 continue to describe brokerage/wealth management and insurance activities without disclosing crypto asset holdings. ([nasdaq.com](https://www.nasdaq.com/press-release/9f-inc.-rebrands-online-stock-brokerage-business-to-fuyuan-securities-2021-02-02?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across the January 2017–December 2024 window, management communications reference blockchain technology as part of an internal tech stack (for example, the 2019 “9F Superbrain” platform), and in August 2021 the company stated plans to make digital-asset-related services central to its offering. However, neither the available earnings materials nor Form 20-F disclosures in this period indicate that 9F directly operated a crypto exchange/custody business, issued crypto investment products, mined crypto, or held crypto on balance sheet. As such, substantive, realized crypto exposure during the sample period is not evidenced. ([ir.9fgroup.com](https://ir.9fgroup.com/image/2019Q3ER.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Because the evidence within 2017–2024 shows only (i) internal/blockchain technology references and (ii) an announced pivot toward digital-asset brokerage without demonstrable, operational crypto-financial services or balance-sheet holdings, the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). All crypto-linked mechanism categories (holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, infrastr) are set to 0.0 in the absence of concrete disclosures of custody/trading of digital assets, crypto revenue lines, mining metrics, or crypto asset balances during the sample window. ([ir.9fgroup.com](https://ir.9fgroup.com/image/2019Q3ER.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
9F’s August 30, 2021 press release and CoinDesk coverage characterize a strategy to become a blockchain-based securities and digital-assets brokerage; if this had been executed within 2017–2024, it would fall under finserv. However, the company’s filings in this period do not document actual crypto operations or assets, and no earnings-call transcripts were retrievable despite search efforts (certaintly therefore capped and firm classified as unclass). Future filings beyond December 2024 could alter this assessment if they evidence realized crypto services or holdings. ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/9f-inc-accelerates-its-transformation-into-a-new-digital-blockchain-securities-brokerage-platform-301365154.html?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Sep 27, 2019: “Second Quarter Earnings Conference Call” listing (transcript not posted) — 9F Inc IR Event Calendar (2019) ([ir.9fgroup.com](https://ir.9fgroup.com/Event-Calendar?item=6))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Dec 5, 2019: Q3 2019 earnings release; “9F Superbrain… integrates our core artificial intelligence, cloud and blockchain technologies.” — 9F Inc (PDF) ([2019Q3ER.pdf](https://ir.9fgroup.com/image/2019Q3ER.pdf))
- FY2021 Form 20-F (filed 2022): Company operations overview (brokerage/wealth/insurance; no crypto assets disclosed) — SEC EDGAR ([jfu-20211231x20f.htm](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1619544/000110465922061935/jfu-20211231x20f.htm))

## TWITTER DEAD - DELIST.28/10/22

* Rank: 186 | Expo: 0.29 | id_str: US90184L1026
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filing (2021); Q4 2020 and Q4 2021 earnings calls; official product blog posts (2021–2022); Stripe newsroom (Apr 2022); tech/business press on NFT profile pictures (Jan 2022)

### Firm Overview
Twitter Inc. was a U.S.-based social media company listed on the NYSE until it was acquired and delisted in late October 2022. During 2017–2022 it generated the vast majority of its revenue from advertising and experimented with limited crypto-related user features.

### Exposure Channels Identified
From 2021–2022, Twitter enabled crypto tipping by integrating Bitcoin Lightning payments via Strike and later allowed users to add Ethereum addresses to its Tips feature; payments were executed through third-party services with no custody by Twitter. It also launched an NFT profile-picture verification feature for Twitter Blue subscribers, which authenticated wallet ownership but did not involve asset custody. Management referenced Web3/crypto as an ecosystem relevant to Twitter’s creator and product roadmap on earnings calls, while SEC filings indicate no crypto assets held on balance sheet. Collectively, these amount to software integrations serving crypto users rather than direct financial intermediation. ([blog.x.com](https://blog.x.com/en_us/topics/product/2021/bringing-tips-to-everyone))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence shows Twitter did not mine crypto, operate an exchange/custody service, issue crypto investment products, or hold crypto as treasury. Its crypto features—Lightning/USDC payouts via partners and NFT verification—were integrations that left custody and settlement to external providers, matching the “bizserv” (non-financial services) channel rather than “finserv.” Accordingly, bizserv is set to 1.0 with all other mechanism weights at 0.0. ([blog.x.com](https://blog.x.com/en_us/topics/product/2021/bringing-tips-to-everyone))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Twitter briefly piloted creator payouts in USDC through Stripe Connect on Polygon and enabled Bitcoin/Ethereum tipping links, but there is no evidence of Twitter directly handling digital assets or earning meaningful crypto revenue during 2017–2024. Post-acquisition product changes (e.g., removal of NFT PFP support in 2024) fall outside the public-company window and do not alter the historical classification. ([stripe.com](https://stripe.com/en-jp/newsroom/news/crypto-payouts?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 10, 2022 (Q4 2021): “If you think about the broader crypto ecosystem… decentralized technologies and applications being built on top of the various blockchains…” — The Motley Fool (transcript) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/02/10/twitter-twtr-q4-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))
- Feb 9, 2021 (Q4 2020): Analyst Q: “…getting decentralization into the web and bitcoin and things like that.” — The Motley Fool (transcript) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/02/10/twitter-inc-twtr-q4-2020-earnings-call-transcript/))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Feb 16, 2022: Form 10-K — “The Company invests its excess cash primarily in short-term fixed income securities… and money market funds.” (no crypto assets disclosed) — SEC EDGAR ([sec filing](https://d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net/CIK-0001418091/b021055f-fb7c-4dff-889c-8ee1964d78c5.html))

**Financial news / other:**
- Sep 23, 2021: “Bringing Tips to everyone” — “Once you tap the service… you’ll be taken off Twitter… Twitter takes no cut… tip with Bitcoin using Strike.” — Twitter Product Blog ([blog.x.com](https://blog.x.com/en_us/topics/product/2021/bringing-tips-to-everyone))
- Apr 22, 2022: “Stripe introduces global crypto payouts… Twitter has chosen Stripe to enable select US creators to receive crypto payouts, starting with USDC.” — Stripe Newsroom ([stripe.com](https://stripe.com/en-jp/newsroom/news/crypto-payouts))
- Jan 20, 2022: Twitter Blue launches NFT profile pictures with wallet verification — TechCrunch ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2022/01/20/twitter-blue-subscription-users-are-first-gain-access-to-a-new-nft-profile-picture-feature/amp/))
- Feb 16, 2022: Twitter adds Ethereum addresses to Tips — CoinDesk ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2022/02/16/twitter-adds-ethereum-wallet-support-to-tipping-feature))

</small>

## FRANKLIN RESOURCES

* Rank: 187 | Expo: 0.28 | id_str: US3546131018
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Form 10-K (FY2024); Q3 and Q4 2024 earnings calls; ETF launch press releases (Jan and Jul 2024); CoinDesk coverage of tokenized fund (Apr 2024)

### Firm Overview
Franklin Resources, Inc. (Franklin Templeton) is a U.S.-based global asset manager listed on the NYSE (ticker BEN) with diversified strategies across public and private markets. In 2024 it expanded into digital assets through spot crypto ETFs and continued development of a tokenized U.S. government money fund that records share ownership on public blockchains. ([s201.q4cdn.com](https://s201.q4cdn.com/562074409/files/doc_financials/2024/ar/Franklin-Resources-Annual-Report-10K_FINAL.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company sponsors and manages regulated crypto investment products, notably the Franklin Bitcoin ETF (EZBC, launched January 11, 2024) and the Franklin Ethereum ETF (EZET, launched July 23, 2024). Management referenced these launches on earnings calls, and the FY2024 Form 10-K highlights both ETFs and ongoing use of blockchain for transfer agency/tokenization. Holdings of bitcoin or ether occur within the ETFs/trusts to back client products, not as corporate treasury assets. Franklin also operates the Franklin OnChain U.S. Government Money Fund, whose shares are represented as BENJI tokens on Stellar and Polygon, reinforcing the firm’s role in crypto financial services rather than custody-free integrations or mining. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/61b78e50f27fac0e188999f547c22b1e?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence shows clear “finserv” exposure: Franklin Templeton issues and manages crypto-backed ETFs and a tokenized money fund, with related disclosures in earnings calls, press releases, and the FY2024 10-K. There is no evidence the parent company holds crypto on its own balance sheet, operates mining, or sells non-financial services to crypto firms. Accordingly, finserv is set to 1.0 and all other mechanism weights to 0.0. ([s201.q4cdn.com](https://s201.q4cdn.com/562074409/files/doc_financials/2024/ar/Franklin-Resources-Annual-Report-10K_FINAL.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Some earnings call transcripts are behind paywalls; accessible mirrors (Insider Monkey, Fintool) were used for quotations and align with the company’s official filings and press releases. ETF trust filings show substantial crypto holdings backing client products, but these are not corporate treasury assets of Franklin Resources, which supports the “finserv not holders” classification. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/BEN/documents/transcripts/q4-2024?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- July 26, 2024 (Q3 2024): “Capitalizing on this trend, we launched our second digital asset backed ETF earlier this week, the Franklin Ethereum ETF…” — Insider Monkey (transcript) ([insidermonkey.com](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/franklin-resources-inc-nyseben-q3-2024-earnings-call-transcript-1326962/))
- Nov 4, 2024 (Q4 2024): “And this year, we launched Franklin Bitcoin ETF and Franklin Ethereum ETF…” — Fintool (transcript) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/BEN/documents/transcripts/q4-2024?utm_source=openai))

**Filings (10-K / product filings):**
- FY2024 Form 10-K (filed Nov 2024): “We launched Franklin Bitcoin ETF and Franklin Ethereum ETF…,” and continued blockchain-enabled transfer agency for funds — Franklin Resources Annual Report/10-K PDF ([s201.q4cdn.com](https://s201.q4cdn.com/562074409/files/doc_financials/2024/ar/Franklin-Resources-Annual-Report-10K_FINAL.pdf))
- Franklin Bitcoin ETF (EZBC) Form 10‑Q (Dec 31, 2024): “Cost of investment in bitcoin: $471,560,931 at December 31, 2024 … $280,623,942 at March 31, 2024.” — Franklin Templeton (EZBC-10Q4) ([franklintempleton.com](https://www.franklintempleton.com/forms-literature/download/EZBC-10Q4?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Jan 11, 2024: Bitcoin ETFs begin trading; Franklin ticker EZBC listed — AP News ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/61b78e50f27fac0e188999f547c22b1e?utm_source=openai))
- July 23, 2024: Franklin Templeton launches Franklin Ethereum ETF (EZET) — Company press release ([franklintempleton.com](https://www.franklintempleton.com/press-releases/news-room/2024/franklin-templeton-launches-franklin-ethereum-etf-ezet?utm_source=openai))
- Apr 25, 2024: Franklin enables peer-to-peer transfers for OnChain U.S. Government Money Fund BENJI tokens (Stellar/Polygon) — CoinDesk ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2024/04/25/franklin-templeton-upgrades-380m-tokenized-treasury-fund-to-enable-peer-to-peer-transfers?utm_source=openai))

## ALLIANCE RSO.PTNS.L P UT LP.

* Rank: 188 | Expo: 0.28 | id_str: US01877R1086
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.9, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.1, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: Form 10‑Q (Q3 2024); Q1 and Q2 2024 earnings calls; CoinDesk coverage (Apr 2024)

### Firm Overview
Alliance Resource Partners, L.P. is a U.S.-listed master limited partnership engaged primarily in coal production and marketing, with additional mineral (oil and gas) royalty interests. Beginning in 2020, the partnership also piloted small-scale bitcoin mining using excess electrical capacity at one of its mining complexes.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management stated on earnings calls that ARLP mines bitcoin to monetize underutilized power at its River View mine and that the company is not purchasing bitcoin; it sells coins as needed to cover operating costs. The company also disclosed fair‑value remeasurement of digital assets and reported specific bitcoin balances during 2024. Additionally, management noted renting out excess capacity to third‑party bitcoin miners within a purpose-built data center. These activities map primarily to the miners mechanism, with a smaller infrastructure component for hosting/colocation. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2024/04/29/alliance-resource-partners-arlp-q1-2024-earnings-c/))

### Weight Assignment
Miners = 0.9 reflects direct operation of mining rigs and recognition of mined bitcoin and related fair‑value changes in reported results. Infrastr = 0.1 captures the stated hosting of other miners in ARLP’s facility. Holders = 0.0 because management emphasized it is not buying bitcoin and sells mined coins to cover expenses (retained balances appear operational, not treasury HODL). No evidence supports finserv or bizserv exposure. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2024/04/29/alliance-resource-partners-arlp-q1-2024-earnings-c/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Crypto activities are small relative to ARLP’s core coal and minerals businesses; disclosures indicate modest balances and intermittent monetization tied to operating needs. Hosting scale was mentioned but not quantified, so the infrastructure weight is conservative. No evidence suggests a crypto‑sensitive investor base beyond incidental coverage. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2024/07/29/alliance-resource-partners-arlp-q2-2024-earnings-c/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Apr 29, 2024 (Q1 2024): “In the second half of 2020, we started mining Bitcoin… we have mined and now own Bitcoin valued at approximately $30 million… Our crypto mining net property, plant, and equipment… $7.3 million.” — The Motley Fool (transcript) (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2024/04/29/alliance-resource-partners-arlp-q1-2024-earnings-c/)
- Apr 29, 2024 (Q1 2024) Q&A: “We’re not actually out there buying Bitcoin… we are selling what we need to cover our expenses… renting out… capacity to other Bitcoin miners within the data center.” — The Motley Fool (transcript) (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2024/04/29/alliance-resource-partners-arlp-q1-2024-earnings-c/)
- Jul 29, 2024 (Q2 2024): “During the 2024 quarter, we saw a decrease in the fair value of the partnership’s digital assets of $3.7 million… We now own approximately 452 Bitcoins, valued at $28.3 million.” — The Motley Fool (transcript) (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2024/07/29/alliance-resource-partners-arlp-q2-2024-earnings-c/)

**Filings (10‑Q / 10‑K):**
- Q3 2024 Form 10‑Q (filed Nov 2024): Note 6 Digital Assets — dispositions of $1.4m (Q3) and $3.1m (9M) with realized gains; fair‑value basis disclosed. — SEC filing (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1086600/000155837024014867/arlp-20240930x10q.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- Apr 30, 2024: “Coal miner Alliance Resource dabbles in crypto mining, mines 425 BTC using excess power; holdings relatively small.” — CoinDesk (https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2024/04/30/coal-miner-alliance-resource-dabbles-in-crypto-mining-mines-425-btc)

</small>

### Firm Overview
Data#3 Limited is an ASX-listed Australian information technology solutions and services provider headquartered in Brisbane. Its core offerings span cloud, modern workplace, security, data and analytics, connectivity, and related services for enterprise and government customers across Australia and Fiji. ([announcements.asx.com.au](https://announcements.asx.com.au/asxpdf/20240821/pdf/066tsn8mmvtgf8.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
A review of multiple earnings-call transcripts across 2019–2024 shows no discussion of cryptocurrency holdings, mining activities, custody/trading services, or digital-asset product lines; management commentary focuses on software, services, cloud and AI-led opportunities. ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/stock/ASX%3ADTL/transcripts/2507148))
The FY2018 results briefing listed “Blockchain” under innovation themes and examples; however, subsequent filings and disclosures through FY2023 do not evidence any blockchain/crypto productization, balance-sheet holdings, or revenue segments tied to digital assets. No substantive evidence of exposure via the six mechanisms (holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, infrastr) was identified within the January 2017–December 2024 window. Mentions of blockchain as an innovation theme (2018) are high-level only and were not followed by disclosed offerings, assets, or revenues linked to crypto; therefore the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
A 2018 investor presentation referenced “Blockchain” among innovation priorities, but no later transcript or filing corroborates operational activity or revenue in this area; on this basis the reference is treated as incidental. None identified beyond this. ([investor.data3.com](https://investor.data3.com/DownloadFile.axd?file=%2FReport%2FComNews%2F20180822%2F02012201.pdf))

## BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON

* Rank: 190 | Expo: 0.28 | id_str: US0640581007
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings calls (Q1 2021; Q3–Q4 2022); 2021–2023 annual reports; Q1 2024 Form 10‑Q; company press release (Oct 2022); ETF prospectus/admin filings (2024); financial news (CNBC/Bloomberg Law)

### Firm Overview
The Bank of New York Mellon Corporation is a U.S.-based global financial services company best known for asset servicing, custody and related market infrastructure. Within this core franchise, the firm has developed a digital asset offering focused on institutional custody and servicing.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Evidence from management commentary and filings shows BNY Mellon provides digital‑asset financial services, not mining or proprietary coin holding. The bank launched a digital asset custody platform for select U.S. institutional clients in October 2022, initially supporting Bitcoin and Ethereum, alongside traditional asset servicing. ([bny.com](https://www.bny.com/corporate/global/en/about-us/newsroom/press-release/bny-mellon-launches-new-digital-asset-custody-platform-130305.html?utm_source=openai))
Annual reports and disclosures note the creation of an enterprise Digital Assets unit in 2021 and ongoing fund‑servicing roles for crypto‑linked products (e.g., Grayscale). ([bny.com](https://www.bny.com/corporate/global/en/investor-relations/annual-report-2021.html?utm_source=openai))
BNY Mellon has also acted as administrator/transfer agent and cash custodian for spot bitcoin ETFs (e.g., iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF), reinforcing its role in crypto financial services. ([ishares.com](https://www.ishares.com/us/literature/prospectus/p-ishares-bitcoin-trust-12-31.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
All substantiated exposure is via crypto financial services: institutional digital‑asset custody and fund/ETF servicing. There is no evidence of balance‑sheet crypto holdings beyond operational or regulatory accounting, nor any mining or hardware provisioning. Accordingly, the firm is classified as finserv = 1.0 (all other mechanisms = 0.0). Given that digital‑asset activity remains a small contributor relative to BNY Mellon’s overall fee base, RevShare is coded as 1 and AssetShare as 0. ([bnymellon.com](https://www.bnymellon.com/content/dam/bnymellon/documents/pdf/investor-relations/bny-mellon-form-10-q-1q24.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Public disclosures through 2024 emphasize platform launch and capabilities but provide limited quantification of digital‑asset custody balances or revenue contribution; American Banker reporting suggests activity was “trivial” at end‑2022, so materiality is conservatively coded. Regulatory treatment (e.g., safeguarding obligations/liability presentation) has also evolved, which may have influenced adoption pace during the sample window. ([americanbanker.com](https://www.americanbanker.com/news/new-document-highlights-conflict-over-bny-mellons-crypto-custody-business?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 17, 2022 (Q3 2022): “We are now live with our digital asset custody platform in the U.S.” — Fintool (transcript) (https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/BK/documents/transcripts/q3-2022)
- Jan 13, 2023 (Q4 2022): “We went live with our digital asset custody platform in the U.S. in October.” — AlphaStreet (transcript) (https://news.alphastreet.com/bank-of-new-york-mellon-corporation-bk-q4-2022-earnings-call-transcript/)
- Apr 16, 2021 (Q1 2021): “A little bit on the build‑out you’re doing around digital assets… We’re already involved in a consortium…” — AlphaStreet (transcript) (https://news.alphastreet.com/bank-of-new-york-mellon-corp-bk-q1-2021-earnings-call-transcript/)

**Filings (10‑K / annual reports / 10‑Q):**
- 2021 Annual Report: Formed an enterprise Digital Assets unit; providing fund services for digital‑asset‑linked products. — BNY Mellon (https://www.bny.com/corporate/global/en/investor-relations/annual-report-2021.html)
- 2023 Annual Report: References the October 2022 launch of Digital Asset Custody platform for select U.S. institutional clients. — BNY Mellon PDF (https://www.bnymellon.com/content/dam/bnymellon/documents/pdf/investor-relations/annual-report-2023.pdf)
- Q1 2024 Form 10‑Q: “Our Digital Asset Custody platform offers custody…” (description of capabilities/initiatives). — SEC/BNY Mellon (https://www.bnymellon.com/content/dam/bnymellon/documents/pdf/investor-relations/bny-mellon-form-10-q-1q24.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Oct 11, 2022: “BNY Mellon launches new digital asset custody platform… select clients can hold and transfer bitcoin and ether.” — BNY press release (https://www.bny.com/corporate/global/en/about-us/newsroom/press-release/bny-mellon-launches-new-digital-asset-custody-platform-130305.html)
- Oct 13, 2022: Context and implications of BNY Mellon’s custody launch. — CNBC (https://www.cnbc.com/2022/10/13/what-bny-mellons-digital-asset-custody-launch-means-for-the-crypto-market-.html)
- Oct 11, 2022: Coverage of custody launch for BTC/ETH. — Bloomberg Law (https://news.bloomberglaw.com/crypto/bny-mellon-debuts-management-platform-for-bitcoin-and-ether)
- Jul 13, 2021: Agreement to provide asset servicing and ETF services for Grayscale Bitcoin Trust upon conversion. — BNY press release (https://www.bny.com/corporate/global/en/about-us/newsroom/press-release/grayscale-investments-forges-agreement-with-bny-mellon-to-provide-asset-servicing-and-etf-services-for-grayscale-bitcoin-trust-130210.html)

## QUICKLOGIC

* Rank: 191 | Expo: 0.28 | id_str: US74837P4054
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 2024 Form 10‑K; Q3 2024 earnings call (8‑K exhibit); company press releases (2023–2024); product overview page

### Firm Overview
QuickLogic Corporation is a U.S.-based fabless semiconductor company that develops embedded FPGA (eFPGA) IP, ruggedized FPGAs, and related user tools, serving industrial, aerospace and defense, edge AI, consumer, and computing markets. Company filings and product materials emphasize eFPGA IP licensing and services, not digital-asset activities. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/882508/000143774925009211/quicklo20241211_10k.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024 disclosures and earnings materials, there is no evidence that QuickLogic holds cryptocurrency on its balance sheet, operates mining, or provides crypto financial services. Management discussion and filings focus on eFPGA IP deliverables, rad‑hard FPGA programs, and partnerships (e.g., Intel Foundry alliances), with no substantive references to bitcoin, blockchain, or mining. Press releases that mention “crypto‑agility” and post‑quantum security relate to cryptography (algorithm agility), not cryptocurrency. Given the absence of explicit crypto/mining workloads or custody/issuance activity, no exposure mechanism is supported. ([ir.quicklogic.com](https://ir.quicklogic.com/sec-filings/content/0001437749-24-034262/0001437749-24-034262.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Because the record through December 2024 shows no direct crypto exposure and only cryptography‑related offerings, the firm is assigned unclass = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. This reflects the threshold principle: general‑purpose semiconductors and IP that could be used in many applications do not qualify for infrastr or bizserv without explicit, material crypto‑directed evidence. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/882508/000143774925009211/quicklo20241211_10k.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
General‑purpose FPGAs and eFPGA IP could, in theory, be incorporated into third‑party products with blockchain use cases, but no customer or workload disclosures substantiate this within the 2017–2024 window. References to “crypto‑agility” in marketing/press materials denote cryptographic algorithm agility, not cryptocurrency exposure. ([ir.quicklogic.com](https://ir.quicklogic.com/press-releases/detail/757/idaho-scientific-selects-quicklogic-efpga-hard-ip-to-enable?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 11, 2024 (Q3 2024), 8‑K Exhibit 99.2: “On June 18, we announced that we joined the Intel Foundry’s Accelerator IP and USMAG Alliances.” — SEC filing (transcript) (https://ir.quicklogic.com/sec-filings/content/0001437749-24-034262/0001437749-24-034262.pdf)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Dec 11, 2024: Form 10‑K — Describes revenue primarily from eFPGA IP/professional services; no mention of bitcoin/crypto/mining. — SEC (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/882508/000143774925009211/quicklo20241211_10k.htm)
- Jun 18, 2024: “QuickLogic Joins Intel Foundry Accelerator IP and USMAG Alliance Programs” — QuickLogic press release (https://ir.quicklogic.com/press-releases/detail/702/quicklogic-joins-intel-foundry-accelerator-ip-and-usmag)

**Financial news / other:**
- Jan 19, 2023: Partnership with Xiphera to implement post‑quantum cryptographic IP cores on QuickLogic eFPGA (cryptography/security focus, not cryptocurrency). — QuickLogic press release (https://ir.quicklogic.com/press-releases/detail/672/securing-the-future-quicklogic-and-xiphera-partner-to)
- Company product overview: eFPGA IP, ruggedized FPGAs, user tools; no digital‑asset services. — QuickLogic website (https://www.quicklogic.com/)

### Firm Overview
Live Nation Entertainment, Inc. is a U.S.-based live entertainment company that promotes concerts globally and operates ticketing through its Ticketmaster division. Company filings describe a business focused on concerts, ticketing, and sponsorships, with Ticketmaster distributing hundreds of millions of tickets annually. ([investors.livenationentertainment.com](https://investors.livenationentertainment.com/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001335258-24-000017/lyv-20231231.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management explicitly discussed adding “blockchain and NFT ability at the Ticketmaster platform,” and described minting NFTs on top of Ticketmaster’s digital tickets during the May 7, 2021 earnings call. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/05/07/live-nation-entertainment-inc-lyv-q1-2021-earnings/))  
In August 2022, Ticketmaster announced it would use Dapper Labs’ Flow blockchain to let event organizers issue NFTs tied to tickets; Ticketmaster subsequently promoted “Digital Collectibles” and a collectibles marketplace, and the NFL expanded NFT commemorative ticket offerings with Ticketmaster. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2022/08/31/ticketmaster-taps-the-flow-blockchain-to-let-event-organizers-issue-nfts-tied-to-tickets/?utm_source=openai))  
In March 2023, Ticketmaster launched token‑gated ticket sales that verify ownership of specific NFTs to unlock presales and benefits (e.g., with Avenged Sevenfold), further integrating blockchain-based digital assets into its ticketing workflows. ([business.ticketmaster.com](https://business.ticketmaster.com/nft-token-gated-sales/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
The firm’s crypto link is operational and platform‑driven: Ticketmaster issues and facilitates custody/transfer of NFT “digital collectibles” for event organizers and fans (a crypto asset handling function), and uses NFTs for access control via token‑gated sales (a non‑financial service feature around the ecosystem). Accordingly, the exposure is assigned mainly to finserv (0.6) for custody/issuance/transfer of digital assets on the platform, and partially to bizserv (0.4) for token‑gated ticketing and Web3 fan engagement features. There is no evidence of treasury crypto holdings, mining, or crypto‑specific hardware provisioning. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/05/07/live-nation-entertainment-inc-lyv-q1-2021-earnings/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Company 10‑Ks reviewed do not quantify revenue or assets tied to NFTs/digital collectibles, suggesting the activity is peripheral; therefore RevShare is coded as minor and AssetShare as zero. Also, Ticketmaster’s “collectible marketplace” language emphasizes viewing and transferring collectibles rather than secondary trading volume, so a pure finserv-only classification could be debated; the split with bizserv reflects this nuance. ([investors.livenationentertainment.com](https://investors.livenationentertainment.com/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001335258-24-000017/lyv-20231231.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 7, 2021 (Q1 2021), Motley Fool transcript: “we continue to enhance our offering… as well as blockchain and NFT ability at the Ticketmaster platform.” — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/05/07/live-nation-entertainment-inc-lyv-q1-2021-earnings/)
- May 7, 2021 (Q1 2021), Motley Fool transcript: “You can mint a ticket and mint an NFT right on top of a Ticketmaster platform… Ticketmaster will be in the delivery business using their… blockchain.” — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/05/07/live-nation-entertainment-inc-lyv-q1-2021-earnings/)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**

## YOUGOV

* Rank: 193 | Expo: 0.27 | id_str: GB00B1VQ6H25

* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Annual Reports (2018–2024); FY24 and FY21 earnings call transcripts; FY20 half-year results announcement

### Firm Overview
YouGov plc is a UK-based international online market research and data analytics company best known for opinion polling and syndicated and custom research. Its products include panel-powered survey tools and audience datasets sold to brand, media, government, and other enterprise clients.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across earnings calls and filings from 2017–2024, there is no evidence that YouGov holds cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet, operates mining, offers crypto trading/custody, or issues crypto investment products. The company did develop YouGov Direct, described in 2018–2020 corporate materials as a blockchain-based data-permissioning and digital advertising platform, but these disclosures relate to underlying ledger technology rather than cryptocurrency usage or custody. More recent materials rebrand YouGov Direct under “YouGov Surveys,” continuing to emphasize survey delivery and analytics rather than any digital-asset services. Occasional YouGov-branded polls on topics such as CBDCs reflect its core polling role, not operational exposure to crypto assets. ([yougov.co.uk](https://yougov.co.uk/technology/articles/20234-yougov-announces-new-advertising-platform-yougov-d?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Because the available evidence points to general blockchain-as-infrastructure experimentation (YouGov Direct) without handling or holding crypto assets, and because neither transcripts nor filings show crypto-linked financial services, mining, treasury holdings, or crypto-specific hardware/infrastructure provisioning, the firm is assigned unclass = 1.0. This reflects no substantive, price-relevant crypto exposure channel under the project definitions for 2017–2024. ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/stock/AQSE%3AYOU.GB/transcripts/2379831?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Older disclosures explicitly labeled YouGov Direct as “blockchain-based,” which could be misconstrued as crypto activity; the documents and announcements indicate private-ledger recordkeeping for consent/ad interactions, not cryptocurrency usage or custody. No filings reviewed disclose crypto revenue lines or balance sheet crypto assets. ([corporate.yougov.com](https://corporate.yougov.com/documents/120/YouGov_Annual_Report_2019.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 29, 2024 (FY24), GuruFocus transcript: “This morning, we published our results for the 12 months to the 31st of July 2024… it’s been a year of transition.” — [GuruFocus](https://www.gurufocus.com/stock/AQSE%3AYOU.GB/transcripts/2573527)
- Mar 23, 2021 (H1 2021), GuruFocus transcript: “Expanding YouGov Direct into new markets and integrating YouGov Chat is another important development.” — [GuruFocus](https://www.gurufocus.com/stock/AQSE%3AYOU.GB/transcripts/2379831)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Nov 11, 2024: FY24 Annual Report — “YouGov Surveys is our panel powered, self‑service survey building platform, formerly known as YouGov Direct.” — [YouGov Investors](https://corporate.yougov.com/documents/420/YouGov_Annual_Report_2024.pdf)
- Apr 22, 2020: FY20 Half‑Year Results announcement — “For each use of their data, a receipt is automatically generated on our blockchain…” — [Investegate](https://www.investegate.co.uk/announcement/rns/yougov--you/half-year-results/6424650)
- Nov 7, 2019: FY19 Annual Report — case study describes “Our blockchain‑based platform for panellist permissioning and effective ad targeting (YouGov Direct).” — [YouGov Investors](https://corporate.yougov.com/documents/120/YouGov_Annual_Report_2019.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**

- Aug 2023: Coverage of Cato/YouGov survey on CBDC sentiment in the U.S. (polling only; no operational crypto activity). — [Ledger Insights](https://www.ledgerinsights.com/cbdc-survey-americans-skeptical-digital-dollar/)

## BRILLIANT EARTH GROUP A

* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0

* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10‑K filings (2022–2024); Q4 2023 earnings call; company press releases on blockchain traceability

### Firm Overview
Brilliant Earth Group, Inc. is a U.S.-based, digital-first fine jeweler focused on ethically sourced natural and lab-grown diamonds, operating an omnichannel model with showrooms across major U.S. cities and e-commerce under Nasdaq ticker BRLT. Company materials emphasize supply-chain transparency and technology, including blockchain-enabled diamond traceability, as part of its brand positioning.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across earnings calls and SEC filings within 2017–2024, there is no evidence that Brilliant Earth holds cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet, operates mining, offers crypto trading/custody, or issues crypto investment products. The firm markets “blockchain-verified” diamonds through traceability platforms (e.g., De Beers’ Tracr and earlier Everledger integrations), which are ledger-based provenance tools rather than cryptocurrency usage or custody. Filings describe “blockchain‑verified transparency” in the customer experience but do not disclose crypto assets or crypto-related revenue lines. On this basis, none of the defined crypto-exposure mechanisms apply.

### Weight Assignment
Because Brilliant Earth’s use of blockchain is limited to supply-chain traceability and there is no substantiated involvement in crypto holding, mining, financial services, non-financial crypto services, or crypto-specific infrastructure, the firm is assigned unclass = 1.0. This reflects the absence of a substantive, price-relevant crypto exposure channel under the project definitions for 2017–2024.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Third‑party blogs occasionally claim the company accepts cryptocurrency payments, but no current confirmation appears in company policies, filings, or investor communications; in any case, incidental merchant acceptance via a processor would not meet the project’s threshold for the finserv category. Transcripts are available primarily from 2021 onward (post‑IPO); the reviewed calls and filings do not reference crypto assets.

## FD TECHNOLOGIES DEAD - DELIST.23/07/25

* Rank: 195 | Expo: 0.27 | id_str: GB0031477770

* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Annual Reports (2019, 2024); H1 2024 and FY 2024 earnings calls; company and trade press releases on KX’s crypto use-cases

### Firm Overview
FD Technologies plc (formerly First Derivatives) is a Northern Ireland–based data and analytics software group whose principal product is KX (kdb+), supplying real-time and time‑series analytics to financial services and other data‑intensive industries. In FY2024 the group reported KX as the core continuing software unit, with consulting operations later divested in late 2024. ([fdtechnologies.com](https://fdtechnologies.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/FD-Technologies-plc-Annual-Report-2024.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024, the company did not hold cryptocurrency on its balance sheet, operate mining, or offer client custody/exchange services in its own name; disclosures and results materials focus on software subscriptions and services. However, KX/kdb+ has been adopted by crypto-market participants: BitMEX expanded its use of kdb+ in 2017 for its high‑frequency bitcoin derivatives exchange, and in 2018 KX for Flow added native support for cryptocurrency trading. In 2022, crypto liquidity provider B2C2 publicly highlighted expanded use of KX analytics and dashboards. These activities position FD Technologies as a non‑financial technology vendor into the crypto ecosystem. ([fdtechnologies.com](https://fdtechnologies.com/news/kdb-powers-trading-platform-bitmex-high-frequency-bitcoin-exchange/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence indicates FD Technologies’ crypto exposure arises from providing software and analytics used by crypto exchanges and trading firms, without the company itself taking digital‑asset risk or providing financial intermediation. Under the project definitions this is classified as bizserv (non‑financial services to the crypto ecosystem). Therefore, weights are assigned as: bizserv 1.0; all other mechanisms 0.0. Given transcript access (at least one call) plus corroborating filings and reputable trade press, certainty is set to 3 (Confident). ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/FD-TECHNOLOGIES-PLC-4003789/news/Transcript-FD-Technologies-plc-2024-Earnings-Call-May-21-2024-46785779/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Earnings call transcripts are partially paywalled; available excerpts confirm call provenance but do not quantify any crypto‑specific revenue; no segment disclosure isolates crypto within KX, so RevShare is coded as minor (1) based on external client evidence. 
- KX’s crypto‑related press items are concentrated in 2017–2018 with later client references (e.g., B2C2) in 2022; materiality within total revenue is not disclosed in filings. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/FD-TECHNOLOGIES-PLC-4003789/news/Transcript-FD-Technologies-plc-2024-Earnings-Call-May-21-2024-46785779/))

Today, as usual, I’m joined by Ryan Preston, our CFO…” — [MarketScreener](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/FD-TECHNOLOGIES-PLC-4003789/news/Transcript-FD-Technologies-plc-2024-Earnings-Call-May-21-2024-46785779/) ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/FD-TECHNOLOGIES-PLC-4003789/news/Transcript-FD-Technologies-plc-2024-Earnings-Call-May-21-2024-46785779/))

* Primary sources: Annual reports (FY2021–FY2024), ASX/press releases (2018–2021), FY2024 earnings call

### Firm Overview
Web Travel Group (formerly Webjet Limited) is an Australia‑listed online travel company operating the WebBeds B2B wholesale accommodation platform; following a demerger implemented on September 30, 2024, the B2C businesses were separated into Webjet Group Limited while the B2B WebBeds business remained with Web Travel Group. The company’s activities center on travel distribution, technology, and marketplaces rather than digital‑asset intermediation. ([webtravelgroup.com](https://www.webtravelgroup.com/about-us/our-story/?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024, no evidence indicates the company held cryptocurrency on its balance sheet, operated mining, or provided custody, exchange, token issuance, or other crypto financial services. The group developed Rezchain, a blockchain‑based booking‑reconciliation tool, and in March 2021 made a strategic equity investment (25%) in LockTrip, a blockchain‑based hotel marketplace whose platform uses the LOC token and HYDRA chain; these initiatives relate to enterprise blockchain and a minority equity stake rather than the company itself transacting in or holding crypto assets. Company filings through FY2024 discuss the LockTrip stake but do not disclose crypto asset holdings or crypto‑linked revenue lines at Web Travel Group. Consequently, under this project’s definitions, no crypto‑exposure mechanism applies. ([webtravelgroup.com](https://www.webtravelgroup.com/resources/asx-releases/download/788/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Disclosures show enterprise blockchain usage (Rezchain) and a minority equity investment in a blockchain company (LockTrip), but no direct handling, custody, issuance, trading, mining, or treasury holding of crypto assets by Web Travel Group during the 2017–2024 window. Therefore, all mechanism categories are set to 0.0 and unclass is set to 1.0.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Webjet accepted Bitcoin for certain offerings in 2015, but this predates the sample window and there is no firm evidence of continued acceptance or direct crypto handling during 2017–2024. ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2015/02/04/online-travel-agency-webjet-now-accepts-bitcoin?utm_source=openai))
- LockTrip’s platform involves tokens (LOC/HYDRA), but the company’s exposure in 2021–2024 is via an equity stake; filings reviewed do not indicate Web Travel Group itself held tokens or recognized crypto‑related assets or revenues. ([webjet.sfo2.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com](https://webjet.sfo2.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/public/Uploads/WEB_ASX_03_18_21_LockTrip-press-release.pdf?utm_source=openai))

* Primary sources: 10‑K filings (2022–2023), 2021–2022 earnings calls, press releases (UFC x Dapper Labs), mainstream business media

### Firm Overview
Endeavor Group Holdings, Inc. is a U.S.-based global sports and entertainment company spanning talent representation, owned sports properties, events and experiences, and media and technology. During 2017–2024, the company owned UFC (subsequently combined with WWE into TKO Group Holdings in September 2023) and operated businesses including IMG and On Location.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across the 2017–2024 window, management referenced NFT initiatives tied to UFC and partnerships with Dapper Labs, as well as sponsorship deals with Crypto.com at UFC. These activities reflect IP licensing, marketing, and digital collectibles rather than custody, issuance of financial products, exchange/brokerage, mining, or treasury investment in crypto assets. Company filings reviewed do not disclose cryptocurrency holdings or crypto‑linked financial services operated by Endeavor. Therefore, none of the defined crypto‑exposure mechanisms apply. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/03/16/endeavor-group-holdings-inc-edr-q4-2021-earnings-c/))

### Weight Assignment
Mentions of NFTs and a Crypto.com sponsorship indicate brand and licensing activity associated with digital assets, but there is no evidence that Endeavor itself held crypto on its balance sheet, mined, or provided exchange, custody, payments, or investment products. Under the project’s definitions, these are not qualifying mechanisms; accordingly all mechanism weights are set to 0.0 and unclass is set to 1.0. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/03/16/endeavor-group-holdings-inc-edr-q4-2021-earnings-c/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- UFC’s NFT program (UFC Strike) launched with Dapper Labs; Dapper operates the marketplace on Flow, suggesting Endeavor’s role was licensing/IP rather than financial intermediation. ([dapperlabs.com](https://www.dapperlabs.com/newsroom/dapper-labs-and-ufc-launch-ufc-strike-an-all-new-nft-experience-for-mma-fans-everywhere?utm_source=openai))
- Crypto.com’s UFC sponsorship reflects marketing revenue rather than crypto services; no evidence indicates Endeavor handled tokens or took on crypto asset risk for these deals. ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2021/07/07/ufc-gets-sports-cryptocurrency-money-with-fight-kit-sponsorship-.html?utm_source=openai))
- From September 12, 2023, UFC was contributed to TKO Group Holdings; post‑combination disclosures reside primarily at TKO, further reducing any direct Endeavor linkage. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1973266/000095017025027778/tko-20241231.htm?utm_source=openai))

## COGENT BIOSCIENCES

* Rank: 198 | Expo: 0.26 | id_str: US19240Q2012
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10‑K filings (2022–2023), 2018 earnings call, investor relations press releases

### Firm Overview
Cogent Biosciences, Inc. is a U.S.-based clinical‑stage biotechnology company focused on developing precision therapies for genetically defined diseases, including systemic mastocytosis and gastrointestinal stromal tumors. The company rebranded from Unum Therapeutics to Cogent Biosciences in October 2020 following the acquisition of Kiq Bio assets. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1622229/000119312524107137/d828655dars.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across January 2017–December 2024, company filings describe drug R&D activities, cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities, and customary biotech risk factors; they do not disclose cryptocurrency holdings, mining, exchange/brokerage, custody, payments, or digital‑asset investment products. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1622229/000095017023007729/cogt-20221231.htm))  
Earnings‑call and IR materials center on clinical programs (e.g., bezuclastinib) and financing events typical of pre‑revenue biotechs, with no substantive references to blockchain/crypto activities. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/08/14/umrx-q2-2018-earnings-conference-call-transcript.aspx?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
No credible evidence was found that Cogent Biosciences held crypto assets, mined, offered crypto‑financial services, provided non‑financial crypto services, or supplied crypto‑specific infrastructure during 2017–2024. Under the project rules, all mechanism categories are set to 0.0 and the firm is assigned unclass = 1.0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1622229/000095017023007729/cogt-20221231.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified. Searches of SEC filings and available transcripts/IR materials yielded no qualifying crypto exposure within the sample window. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1622229/000095017023007729/cogt-20221231.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 14, 2018 (Q2 2018), Motley Fool: “Unum is a clinical‑stage company, working to develop a next‑generation engineered T‑cell therapy.” — [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/08/14/umrx-q2-2018-earnings-conference-call-transcript.aspx)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Mar 14, 2023, Form 10‑K FY2022: Company overview; liquidity and risk factors; no crypto activities disclosed. — [SEC](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1622229/000095017023007729/cogt-20221231.htm)
- Oct 8, 2024 (filed for FY2023), Form 10‑K FY2023 (PDF): Business description; R&D expense detail; no crypto disclosures. — [SEC](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1622229/000119312524107137/d828655dars.pdf)
- Oct 6, 2020, press release: Unum Therapeutics changes name to Cogent Biosciences. — [Company IR](https://investors.cogentbio.com/news-releases/news-release-details/unum-therapeutics-announces-company-name-change-cogent)

**Financial news / other:**
- Jan 8, 2025, company announcement: CEO presentation at J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference (IR webcast info). — [Company IR](https://investors.cogentbio.com/news-releases/news-release-details/cogent-biosciences-announces-presentation-43rd-annual-jp-morgan)

</small>

## AQUIS EXCHANGE DEAD - DELIST.02/07/25

* Rank: 199 | Expo: 0.26 | id_str: GB00BD5JNK30
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Company RNS/news releases (2018), FY2023 investor meeting transcript listing, financial media coverage (2018)

### Firm Overview
Aquis Exchange PLC was a UK‑based exchange services group listed on AIM, operating the Aquis Markets multilateral trading facility and the Aquis Stock Exchange (AQSE), and licensing exchange and surveillance technology via its Aquis Technologies division. Within the 2017–2024 window, Aquis’ crypto adjacency came through its technology arm rather than through custody, trading or investment in digital assets. ([aquis.eu](https://www.aquis.eu/news/aquis-launches-the-world-s-first-regulated-market-grade-24x7-exchange?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Aquis Technologies entered into a technology licensing agreement to supply trading and market‑surveillance systems to Archax, described as an institutional‑grade crypto and security token exchange, with initial exclusivity for Aquis technology “in the digital asset space.” This agreement included an upfront implementation fee and recurring licence fees once the exchange launched. Financial media at the time also characterised Aquis Technologies as selling “crypto and security token exchange technology” to third parties, with Archax cited as a client. These activities indicate exposure as a non‑financial service provider to the crypto ecosystem (software/technology licensing), not as a direct handler or custodian of crypto assets. ([aquis-website-eu.s3.amazonaws.com](https://aquis-website-eu.s3.amazonaws.com/Agreement-with-Archax-RNS-final1.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
The only substantiated crypto channel is Aquis Technologies’ provision of exchange and surveillance software to a digital‑asset trading venue (Archax). Under the project rules, this is classified as bizserv because Aquis provided technology/services around the ecosystem without taking custody of crypto or operating a crypto‑financial product itself. Accordingly, weights are set to bizserv = 1.0 and all other mechanisms = 0.0. Given that transcript text could not be fully retrieved, certainty is capped at 2. ([aquis-website-eu.s3.amazonaws.com](https://aquis-website-eu.s3.amazonaws.com/Agreement-with-Archax-RNS-final1.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Earnings‑call transcripts for 2017–2024 are listed by third‑party aggregators, but full text access is paywalled; no crypto‑specific remarks from calls could be quoted directly, so certainty is capped at 2. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/AQUIS-EXCHANGE-PLC-44247338/news-call-transcripts/))  
- No evidence was found that Aquis held crypto on balance sheet, mined, or directly offered crypto exchange/custody/payment services within 2017–2024; exposure appears limited to technology licensing. ([aquis.eu](https://www.aquis.eu/news/aquis-launches-the-world-s-first-regulated-market-grade-24x7-exchange?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Mar 21, 2024 (FY 2023), GuruFocus transcript listing: “Good afternoon and welcome to Full Year 2023 Aquis Exchange PLC Investor Presentation.” — [GuruFocus](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/2395309/full-year-2023-aquis-exchange-plc-investor-meeting-transcript)  
- Sep 12, 2024 (H1 2024) transcript listed (content paywalled). — [MarketScreener](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/AQUIS-EXCHANGE-PLC-44247338/news/Transcript-Aquis-Exchange-PLC-H1-2024-Earnings-Call-Sep-12-2024-47862103/)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Sep 6, 2018, company news release: “Aquis Technologies… chosen by Archax, the institutional‑grade crypto and security token exchange, to supply… trading technology.” — [Aquis (news)](https://www.aquis.eu/news/agreement-archax)  
- Sep 25, 2018, interim results RNS: “Post period end… agreement signed with Archax, an institutional‑grade crypto exchange.” — [Aquis (RNS/news)](https://www.aquis.eu/news/interim-results-six-months-ended-30-june-2018)  
- Undated RNS PDF: details of Archax agreement incl. “exclusive use… in the digital asset space” and licence‑fee structure. — [Aquis (RNS PDF)](https://aquis-website-eu.s3.amazonaws.com/Agreement-with-Archax-RNS-final1.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Sep 6, 2018: Coverage of Aquis‑Archax crypto exchange technology partnership. — [The Fintech Times](https://thefintechtimes.com/aquis-partners-archax-crypto-exchange/aquis-partners-with-archax-for-institutional-crypto-exchange/)  
- Sep 25, 2018: Article noting Aquis “licenses and sells trading technology, such as crypto and security token exchange technology,” citing the Archax deal. — [City A.M.](https://www.cityam.com/aquis-exchange-grows-market-share-and-revenue-disrupts/)

## FIRST FOUNDATION

* Rank: 200 | Expo: 0.26 | id_str: US32026V1044
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Company press release (Apr 2021), Q1 and Q3 2021 earnings calls, FDIC RFI response (2021), 10-K FY2023

### Firm Overview
First Foundation Inc. is a U.S. financial services holding company for First Foundation Bank and First Foundation Advisors, offering personal and business banking, wealth management, and trust services across several states including California, Texas, Nevada and Hawaii. Within 2017–2024, its crypto-related activity arose from a partnership initiative to enable client access to bitcoin via third-party providers.

### Exposure Channels Identified
In April 2021, the company announced a strategic investment in NYDIG and a partnership with NYDIG and Fiserv to integrate bitcoin access into First Foundation’s banking platforms, enabling customers to buy, sell and hold bitcoin with NYDIG as sub-custodian. Management reiterated on earnings calls in 2021 that the “cryptocurrency project with NYDIG and Fiserv” was progressing, describing plans to let clients execute bitcoin transactions through the bank channel and highlighting anticipated fee-based income opportunities. In its response to the FDIC’s 2021 digital assets RFI, First Foundation Bank specified that NYDIG Trust would be the custodian, the bank would not custody crypto, and that fees on client bitcoin transactions through NYDIG would be shared with the bank, with an illustrative 1% fee. Subsequent filings reflect an equity investment in NYDIG but do not show crypto assets on the balance sheet or material crypto revenue disclosures.

### Weight Assignment
The evidence shows First Foundation pursued a crypto financial-services offering (client bitcoin trading and custody via NYDIG, integrated by Fiserv), with fee sharing but without the bank taking custody itself. Under the project rules, this is classified as finserv exposure. There is no evidence of proprietary crypto holdings, mining, or infrastructure provisioning. Accordingly, the weights are set to finserv = 1.0 and all other categories = 0.0. Certainty is set to 3 based on multiple transcript quotes plus company and regulatory-source corroboration, while filings indicate limited financial materiality (RevShare = 0; AssetShare = 0).

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Filings through FY2023 do not break out any realized crypto-related revenue; the status and scale of any launched retail bitcoin service within 2017–2024 are not explicitly detailed in 10‑K disclosures.  
- No evidence was found of crypto assets on the balance sheet; exposure appears limited to the NYDIG partnership and equity stake.  
- As with many banks, regulatory developments post‑2021 could have influenced the pace of rollout; the classification relies on management statements and the FDIC filing confirming the program design.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 26, 2021, Q3 2021 call: “As Scott mentioned, cryptocurrency project with NYDIG and Fiserv continues to successfully move forward.” — [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/26/first-foundation-inc-ffwm-q3-2021-earnings-call-tr/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/26/first-foundation-inc-ffwm-q3-2021-earnings-call-tr/))  
- Oct 26, 2021, Q3 2021 call: plan to “give [customers] the ability to have execution with Bitcoin… to execute with NYDIG,” delivered via the bank channel. — [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/26/first-foundation-inc-ffwm-q3-2021-earnings-call-tr/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/26/first-foundation-inc-ffwm-q3-2021-earnings-call-tr/))  
- Apr 27, 2021, Q1 2021 call: CFO: “we… made an investment with NYDIG… bring bitcoin into the traditional banking world… work with NYDIG [and] Fiserv to build this on our traditional banking model.” — [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/04/27/first-foundation-inc-ffwm-q1-2021-earnings-call-tr/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/04/27/first-foundation-inc-ffwm-q1-2021-earnings-call-tr/))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Apr 20, 2021, press release: strategic investment in NYDIG; partnership with NYDIG and Fiserv to enable customers to buy, sell and hold bitcoin, with NYDIG as sub‑custodian and Fiserv integration. — [Company press release](https://www.firstfoundationinc.com/news-insights/first-foundation-inc-announces-strategic-investment-institutional-bitcoin-provider) ([firstfoundationinc.com](https://www.firstfoundationinc.com/news-insights/first-foundation-inc-announces-strategic-investment-institutional-bitcoin-provider))  
- FY2023 Form 10‑K (filed Feb 28, 2024): notes a $6.3 million 2022 valuation loss on the NYDIG equity investment; no crypto asset holdings disclosed. — [Annual Report PDF](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/f/NASDAQ_FFWM_2023.pdf) ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/f/NASDAQ_FFWM_2023.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**
- 2021 FDIC RFI response: “NYDIG Trust will be the custodian and FFB will not be the custodian… Fees generated by Bitcoin purchases and sales by FFB customers through NYDIG will be shared with FFB… [FFB] will likely charge a fee of 1%.” — [FDIC submission (PDF)](https://www.fdic.gov/resources/regulations/federal-register-publications/2021/2021-rfi-digital-assets-3064-za25-c-015.pdf) ([fdic.gov](https://www.fdic.gov/resources/regulations/federal-register-publications/2021/2021-rfi-digital-assets-3064-za25-c-015.pdf))  
- Jun 24, 2021: Coverage noting First Foundation working with NYDIG and Fiserv to bring bitcoin trading services to customers. — [Crypto.news](https://crypto.news/bitcoin-trading-18-million-americans-nydig-internet-banking/) ([crypto.news](https://crypto.news/bitcoin-trading-18-million-americans-nydig-internet-banking/))

</small>

## NETSOL TECHS.

* Rank: 201 | Expo: 0.26 | id_str: US64115A4022
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K FY2023, company press releases (2019, 2021), Q2 FY2024 earnings call

### Firm Overview
NetSol Technologies, Inc. is a U.S.-listed software company that provides enterprise solutions for the global asset finance and leasing industry, with products including its NFS Ascent/Transcend platform and the Otoz/Transcend Retail digital auto retail suite. Its business is centered on SaaS, license and services revenue for automotive and equipment finance clients across North America, Europe and Asia, with mobility-oriented offerings deployed for OEMs and dealers.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Within the 2017–2024 window, management communications and filings emphasize auto finance software, digital retail and mobility solutions. A March 2019 press release described the OTOZ Mobility Innovation Lab and a planned blockchain-based platform that contemplated smart contracts and an internal incentive token (“OTOZ Mobility Coin”), and later Otoz materials continued to reference blockchain among enabling technologies. However, across filings and earnings calls in the period, there is no evidence that the company held cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet, offered crypto custody/trading or investment products, mined digital assets, or provided mining/compute infrastructure. Otoz deployments discussed in 2021–2024 were focused on automotive retail and mobility use cases rather than on crypto markets or services.

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of substantiated cryptocurrency activities in filings and on earnings calls, and because the Otoz blockchain references relate to mobility/auto retail functionality rather than crypto-asset services, no crypto-exposure mechanism is supported to a material degree. Accordingly, the firm is assigned unclass = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. The investor base amplifier is set to 0 due to a lack of evidence that the stock trades as a crypto proxy.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- A 2019 press release referenced a potential incentive token for the Otoz ecosystem; subsequent filings/calls within 2017–2024 do not discuss crypto assets, token issuance, or related revenues, leaving uncertainty about whether any tokenization advanced beyond concept/pilot stage.  
- Some third-party transcript aggregations provide limited context; accessible Q2 FY2024 remarks highlight Otoz and SaaS growth but contain no crypto references.  
- If any non-public pilots involved digital asset transactions, they were not reflected in publicly available filings/transcripts for 2017–2024.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 13, 2024, Q2 FY2024 call: “At the heart of our SaaS business are products like the Otoz digital retail platform and our API-first marketplace, Appex Now.” — [ROIC.ai transcript](https://www.roic.ai/quote/NTWK/transcripts/2024/2)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- FY2023 Form 10‑K: Business description of NFS Ascent and Otoz; no references to cryptocurrency holdings, mining, or crypto custody/trading. — [Fintel-hosted 10‑K](https://fintel.io/doc/sec-netsol-technologies-inc-1039280-10k-2023-september-22-19622-9061)
- Mar 27, 2019, press release: OTOZ lab launch; platform “designed to leverage blockchain… and provide a Digital Token (OTOZ Mobility Coin) for incentivized customer behavior.” — [Company press release](https://ir.netsoltech.com/press-releases/detail/3093/netsol-technologies-launches-otoz-mobility-innovation-lab)
- Aug 9, 2021, press release: Otoz update; technology “supported by… blockchain,” focused on digital auto retail/mobility. — [Company press release](https://ir.netsoltech.com/press-releases/detail/3148/otoz-provides-update-on-recently-launched-digital-retail)

**Financial news / other:**
- Jul 16, 2024, contract announcement: $16M, five‑year Otoz digital retail deal with major automaker (auto retail focus; not crypto). — [Company press release](https://ir.netsoltech.com/press-releases/detail/3215/netsol-signs-16-million-agreement-with-major-automaker-to)

</small>

## BED BATH AND BEYOND

* Rank: 202 | Expo: 0.26 | id_str: US6903701018
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.8, bizserv 0.2, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10‑K filings (2021–2023/2024), Q1 2019 and Q4 2019 earnings calls, Q2 2021 earnings call, 2023 financial news

### Firm Overview
Beyond, Inc. (formerly Overstock.com) is a U.S.-listed online retailer focused on home goods; in June 2023 it acquired the Bed Bath & Beyond intellectual property and subsequently rebranded its retail operations under that name while keeping its corporate listing. Alongside retail, the company has maintained legacy exposure to blockchain ventures through Medici Ventures, L.P. and direct minority interests in tZERO Group.

### Exposure Channels Identified
From 2017–2024, the company’s substantive crypto linkage was through tZERO and the Medici portfolio. Management repeatedly discussed tZERO’s digital asset and digital-securities businesses on earnings calls, including its crypto trading app and ATS for tokenized securities. In early 2021, the Medici assets were moved to a limited partnership managed by Pelion, and the company deconsolidated those operations while retaining a minority interest in tZERO; later, tZERO discontinued its standalone crypto app in March 2023 as it refocused on regulated securities. The stock has often traded as a crypto “proxy” during bitcoin and blockchain up-cycles, reflecting investor attention to its blockchain assets and strategy.

### Weight Assignment
Finserv (0.8) is primary because the company’s exposure has been via exchange/brokerage and digital‑securities activities at tZERO (and related capital‑markets initiatives) that directly tie results and sentiment to crypto markets. Bizserv (0.2) captures Medici’s historical provision of non‑financial blockchain development and enablement to portfolio companies and the broader ecosystem, without the parent holding or custoding crypto assets. There is no evidence of material coin holdings, mining operations, or crypto‑specific hardware/hosting activities by the parent in 2017–2024, so holders, miners, and infrastr are set to 0.0. Given repeated media framing and trading behavior around the firm as a blockchain/crypto play, invbase is set to 1.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- tZERO’s product mix changed over time (e.g., the 2023 shutdown of the tZERO Crypto app); remaining exposure is through digital securities rather than spot-crypto brokerage, and the parent company’s interest became non‑controlling after the 2021 Pelion transaction.  
- The company accepted bitcoin payments historically as a merchant, but public filings in 2017–2024 do not indicate strategic crypto‑asset treasury holdings.  
- Magnitude of crypto‑linked revenue at the consolidated level was immaterial relative to retail; materiality codes reflect that reality.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Mar 13, 2020 (Q4 2019), Motley Fool: “We launched the Crypto App… we expect to introduce the capability to trade security tokens on our app.” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/03/13/overstockcom-inc-ostk-q4-2019-earnings-call-transc.aspx)  
- May 9, 2019 (Q1 2019), Motley Fool: “At the end of the day, we see now that tZERO is this enormous opportunity.” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/05/09/overstockcom-inc-ostk-q1-2019-earnings-call-transc.aspx)  
- Aug 2021 (Q2 2021), ROIC.ai: “We… deconsolidated the Medici Ventures businesses… and recognized… fair value adjustments on the Medici assets and our direct minority interest in tZERO.” — [ROIC.ai transcript](https://www.roic.ai/quote/OSTKO/transcripts/2021-year/2-quarter)  
- Feb 21, 2024 (Q4 2023), Motley Fool: “A short summary on our 16‑company Medici portfolio, a noncore asset… [LP with Pelion].” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2024/02/21/beyond-inc-byon-q4-2023-earnings-call-transcript/)

**Filings (10‑K / 8‑K):**
- FY2023 Form 10‑K (filed Mar 2024): details Medici Ventures, L.P. structure and fair‑value accounting for direct minority interests in tZERO and SpeedRoute. — [Annual Report PDF](https://investors.beyond.com/files/doc_financials/2023/q4/2024/03/BYON-2023-12-31-10K-EDGAR-FINAL.pdf)  
- FY2022 Form 10‑K (filed Feb 24, 2023): discusses deconsolidation of Medici and accounting for tZERO following Pelion transaction. — [EDGAR filing](https://content.edgar-online.com/ExternalLink/EDGAR/0001130713-23-000014.html?dest=ostk-20221231_htm&hash=b9be1e0d3c67153f607cb6aa5373feeb7c2bd906b8142b6cdc4fb44ee14c747f)
