## ALLFUNDS GROUP

* Rank: 203 | Expo: 0.26 | id_str: GB00BNTJ3546
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Annual Reports (2022–2024), H1 2023 and H1 2024 earnings calls, 2022–2023 company and media announcements on Allfunds Blockchain

### Firm Overview
Allfunds Group plc is an Amsterdam‑listed B2B wealthtech platform that provides fund distribution, dealing and data/analytics solutions to distributors and fund houses globally. In addition to its core platform, the group develops blockchain-based market infrastructure through “Allfunds Blockchain” aimed at digitising fund operations and share registries. ([app.allfunds.com](https://app.allfunds.com/docs/cms/Allfunds_Annual_Report_2024_Strategic_Report_dae299f69e.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s crypto/blockchain linkage is via Allfunds Blockchain, which builds distributed‑ledger solutions for the investment‑fund value chain (e.g., tokenised fund shares, smart‑contract workflows, and transfer‑agency process automation). Allfunds reported that by December 2022 it had begun generating revenues from Allfunds Blockchain and described ongoing work with distributors, fund houses and regulators to bring efficiencies to fund distribution using blockchain. Partnerships and pilots during 2022–2023 included Spain’s first tokenised investment fund within the CNMV regulatory sandbox (with Renta 4 Banco and Onyze) and further collaborations with asset managers such as AllianceBernstein and La Française. There is no evidence that Allfunds mines crypto, holds coins on treasury, or operates a consumer crypto brokerage/custody business. ([app.allfunds.com](https://app.allfunds.com/docs/cms/Allfunds_2022_Annual_Report_2d8223ebf7.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Bizserv is set to 1.0 because Allfunds’ role is to provide blockchain‑enabled software and infrastructure to the traditional funds ecosystem without the firm itself issuing, trading, or custoding digital assets. Filings emphasise blockchain as a technology initiative (with revenue starting in 2022) rather than a balance‑sheet asset or a financial‑product line, so holders, miners, finserv and infrastr are 0.0. Given limited public framing of the stock as a crypto proxy, invbase is 0. ([app.allfunds.com](https://app.allfunds.com/docs/cms/Allfunds_2022_Annual_Report_2d8223ebf7.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Earnings call materials seldom detail blockchain revenues by segment; while 2022 filings confirm initial revenue, magnitude is not broken out thereafter. ([app.allfunds.com](https://app.allfunds.com/docs/cms/Allfunds_2022_Annual_Report_2d8223ebf7.pdf))  
- The firm announced expansion of an ETP platform in 2024 (launch targeted for 2025), but this refers to exchange‑traded products broadly and does not evidence direct crypto-financial services by Allfunds. ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/2547478/allfunds-group-plc-fra6uy-h1-2024-earnings-call-highlights-record-growth-and-strategic-expansions-amidst-market-challenges?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

**Earnings calls:**

- Jul 28, 2023 (H1 2023), GuruFocus/Refinitiv: “Good morning, and welcome to Allfunds’s financial results presentation for the first half of 2023… the results presentation and supplemental Excel information can be found… This call is being broadcast live.” — [GuruFocus transcript](https://www.gurufocus.com/stock/FRA%3A6UY/transcripts/2365373) ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/stock/FRA%3A6UY/transcripts/2365373))

## COMPAGNIE CHARGEURS INVEST

* Primary sources: Corporate sustainability/annual reports (2019–2023), investor presentations, product/brand pages, industry press (2020–2024)

### Firm Overview
Compagnie Chargeurs Invest (formerly Chargeurs SA) is a Paris‑listed French industrial and financial group whose portfolio spans protective films (Novacel), interlinings (Chargeurs PCC Fashion Technologies), museum services, and luxury fibers. Within the 2017–2024 window, the group highlighted supply‑chain traceability in its Luxury Fibers division via the NATIVA label, which uses blockchain to document provenance; this is framed as product transparency rather than a crypto‑asset activity. ([chargeurs.com](https://chargeurs.com/en/))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across filings and product materials, blockchain is referenced as an enabling technology for NATIVA’s fiber traceability (e.g., “proprietary blockchain technology” to certify quality and provenance). This is not accompanied by disclosures of crypto custody, exchange operations, token issuance, or treasury investments in digital assets. Trade‑press coverage in 2024 similarly describes “traçabilité sécurisée en blockchain” for NATIVA cashmere, again as supply‑chain transparency rather than a crypto‑financial product. There is no evidence of mining activities or crypto‑related fee income. ([chargeurs.com](https://www.chargeurs.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/Chargeurs_RSE_UK.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Unclass is set to 1.0 because the only relevant references are to blockchain as a supply‑chain tool embedded in Chargeurs’ products (NATIVA), with no indication of balance‑sheet crypto holdings, mining, or financial services that touch digital assets. As such, crypto‑asset market movements are not a documented driver of the company’s earnings or assets under the 2017–2024 sample. ([chargeurs.com](https://www.chargeurs.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/Chargeurs_RSE_UK.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- None identified beyond general blockchain usage for product traceability, which does not, on the evidence available, translate into crypto‑asset exposure. ([fr.fashionnetwork.com](https://fr.fashionnetwork.com/news/Nativa-offre-une-tracabilite-a-son-cachemire-remontant-jusqu-aux-elevages%2C1640745.html))

* Primary sources: Annual Information Form (FY2024), FY2024 MD&A, Q2–Q3 FY2024 earnings calls, company press releases (2021–2024)

### Firm Overview
Standard Lithium Ltd is a Canada‑based lithium development company listed in the U.S. (NYSE American: SLI) and Canada (TSX‑V: SLI). It focuses on direct lithium extraction projects in the Smackover Formation of Arkansas and East Texas, advancing its South West Arkansas and Lanxess projects and related demonstration activities.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across FY2024 filings and multiple earnings calls, management discusses lithium‑brine resources, direct lithium extraction technology, engineering studies, partnerships and project financing, with no references to cryptocurrencies, blockchain tokens, custody, exchange operations, or digital‑asset treasury holdings. The FY2024 AIF and MD&A outline a minerals project developer profile focused on lithium chemicals and related process technology. Earnings call remarks in 2024 similarly concentrate on lithium pricing, project milestones and the Equinor partnership, not crypto‑asset activities. Press releases from 2021–2024 cover Koch Strategic Platforms’ equity investment and the 2024 Equinor project JV, again without crypto touchpoints. ([standardlithium.com](https://www.standardlithium.com/_resources/financials/AIF2024.pdf?v=040701))

### Weight Assignment
Unclass is set to 1.0 because no substantive crypto‑asset exposure mechanism is evidenced in 2017–2024 sources. The company’s narrative centers on lithium resource development and DLE process commercialization, with partnerships and financing tied to battery materials, not digital assets. Accordingly, there is no basis to assign weights to holders, miners, finserv, bizserv or infrastr categories. ([standardlithium.com](https://www.standardlithium.com/_resources/financials/AIF2024.pdf?v=040701))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified beyond routine references to technology partnerships and capital formation for lithium projects; searches across filings, press releases and 2024 earnings calls yielded no crypto‑related disclosures. ([standardlithium.com](https://www.standardlithium.com/investors/reports-and-filings/))

## PLAYTECH

* Rank: 206 | Expo: 0.25 | id_str: IM00B7S9G985
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Annual Reports and Results (2018–2023), H1 2019/2022 RNS/Investegate, Financial Commission (2018), LeapRate/Finance Magnates (2017–2018)

### Firm Overview
Playtech plc is a UK‑listed gambling technology supplier providing B2B software and services across casino, sports, poker, bingo and related platforms, and historically owned B2C assets in Italy. Until July 2022, Playtech also operated a financial trading division (Finalto, formerly TradeTech Group) before divesting it to focus on gambling technology. ([investors.playtech.com](https://www.investors.playtech.com/~/media/Files/P/Playtech-IR-V2/documents/results-and-presentations/2018/full-year-results-21-02-2019.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Within the 2017–2019 period, Playtech’s TradeTech/Finalto division provided multi‑asset brokerage technology and liquidity, explicitly including cryptocurrencies among supported instruments and crypto‑CFD liquidity for brokers. Independent industry reports in 2017–2018 reference crypto’s impact on the unit’s results and detail TradeTech Alpha’s integration partners offering access to “FX, CFDs, Equities and Crypto currencies.” The division’s operations therefore constituted direct financial‑services exposure to crypto trading products. Playtech completed the sale of Finalto in July 2022, so the crypto finserv exposure pertains to the 2017–2022 window within the sample period. ([leaprate.com](https://www.leaprate.com/forex/liquidity/playtechs-tradetech-integrates-brokeree-multi-asset-liquidity-bridge/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv is set to 1.0 because the only evidenced crypto mechanism is through Playtech’s financial division offering crypto‑related trading/liquidity (including crypto‑CFDs) to brokers and clients; there is no evidence of mining, treasury holdings, non‑financial crypto services, or crypto‑specific infrastructure provision. Given that Finalto was divested in July 2022, the exposure is time‑bounded within the 2017–2022 portion of the sample, so materiality scores are kept low (RevShare=1, AssetShare=0) to reflect crypto’s peripheral role within a broader multi‑asset offering. ([investors.playtech.com](https://www.investors.playtech.com/~/media/Files/P/Playtech-IR-V2/documents/results-and-presentations/2018/full-year-results-21-02-2019.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Public, free full‑text earnings call transcripts were limited; a transcript header is available, but crypto‑specific discussion in calls was not retrievable without subscription. Evidence therefore relies on results materials and reputable industry news. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/PLAYTECH-PLC-11002037/news/Transcript-Playtech-plc-2020-Earnings-Call-Mar-11-2021-37857536/))
- Filings describe TradeTech/Finalto’s multi‑asset model but do not break out crypto revenue specifically; third‑party industry sources document crypto‑CFD/liquidity activity and 2017 “crypto hype” impacts. ([investors.playtech.com](https://www.investors.playtech.com/~/media/Files/P/Playtech-IR-V2/documents/results-and-presentations/2018/full-year-results-21-02-2019.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Mar 11, 2021 (FY2020), transcript header: “Good morning or good afternoon, all, and welcome to the Playtech 2020 Full Year Results Call.” — [MarketScreener transcript page](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/PLAYTECH-PLC-11002037/news/Transcript-Playtech-plc-2020-Earnings-Call-Mar-11-2021-37857536/) ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/PLAYTECH-PLC-11002037/news/Transcript-Playtech-plc-2020-Earnings-Call-Mar-11-2021-37857536/))

* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Annual Report 2022, MTG press releases (2022–2024), Kongregate blog posts, industry news; earnings-call event listings (2022–2023)

### Firm Overview
Modern Times Group MTG AB is a Sweden‑based digital entertainment group listed on Nasdaq Stockholm, focused on mobile and online gaming through studios such as InnoGames, Hutch, Ninja Kiwi and PlaySimple. Until early 2024 it also owned Kongregate, a U.S. developer/publisher that explored Web3/NFT initiatives before being combined with Monumental, leaving MTG with a minority stake in the new entity. ([mtg.com](https://www.mtg.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/MTG_ENG_230411.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
MTG’s crypto‑related exposure during the 2017–2024 window stemmed from Kongregate’s push into blockchain/NFT gaming. MTG’s 2022 Annual Report states the group “began to explore blockchain and NFT gaming through Kongregate’s increased focus on Web3,” and highlights the launch of Bitverse Web3 titles and a $40m Blockchain Developer Fund with Immutable X. In July 2022, MTG also disclosed that Kongregate acquired Gamaga specifically to expand blockchain capabilities. These activities indicate non‑financial, product/platform involvement with Web3 game and NFT mechanics rather than custody, exchange, or mining. ([mtg.com](https://www.mtg.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/MTG_ENG_230411.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Bizserv is set to 1.0. The evidenced exposure is Kongregate’s development and publishing of blockchain‑enabled games and related initiatives (e.g., Web3 titles, NFT functionality, partnership with Immutable X, dedicated developer capacity), which fits non‑financial services/software integration supporting crypto‑enabled products. There is no evidence that MTG held crypto on its balance sheet, mined crypto, provided exchange/custody/ETP services, or supplied mining hardware/energy. Note that this exposure is time‑bounded; MTG combined Kongregate with Monumental in February 2024 (deconsolidated thereafter), so crypto linkage is concentrated in 2022–early 2024 and appears peripheral to group revenues and assets. ([mtg.com](https://www.mtg.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/MTG_ENG_230411.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Public, free full‑text earnings call transcripts for MTG were not retrievable without subscription; only webcast/event listings were available, so the transcript anchor could not be quoted. Certainty is therefore capped at 2 per the instructions. ([financialhearings.com](https://financialhearings.com/event/44542))  
- Kongregate’s Web3 efforts were within a broader multi‑studio portfolio; MTG’s filings describe exploration and initiatives rather than discrete crypto revenue or asset balances at group level, so materiality scores are kept low. ([mtg.com](https://www.mtg.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/MTG_ENG_230411.pdf))  
- The Kongregate/Monumental transaction (closed Q1 2024) limits ongoing exposure within the sample to pre‑combination periods. ([mtg.com](https://www.mtg.com/press-releases/mtg-combines-kongregate-with-monumental-for-a-30-stake-in-the-joined-entity/?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 26, 2022 (Q3 2022), event listing: “Audiocast with teleconference; Speakers: CEO Maria Redin, CFO Lasse Pilgaard.” — [Financial Hearings event page](https://financialhearings.com/event/44542) (no public transcript) (financialhearings.com) ([financialhearings.com](https://financialhearings.com/event/44542))  
- Oct 25, 2023 (Q3 2023), transcript page (content gated) — [MarketScreener transcript page](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/MODERN-TIMES-GROUP-MTG-AB-6492174/news/Transcript-Modern-Times-Group
... Group MTG AB, Q3 2023 Earnings Call, Oct 25, 2023” — [MarketScreener transcript page](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/MODERN-TIMES-GROUP-MTG-AB-6492174/news/Transcript-Modern-Times-Group-MTG-AB-Q3-2023-Earnings-Call-Oct-25-2023-45151076/)   
- Feb 9, 2023 (Q4 2022), transcript page (content gated) — [MarketScreener transcript page](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/MODERN-TIMES-GROUP-MTG-AB-6492174/news/Transcript-Modern-Times-Group-MTG-AB-Q4-2022-Earnings-Call-Feb-09-2023-42946571/)   
- Apr 27, 2022 (Q1 2022), transcript page (content gated) — [MarketScreener transcript page](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/MODERN-TIMES-GROUP-MTG-AB-6492174/news/Transcript-Modern-Times-Group-MTG-AB-Q1-2022-Earnings-Call-Apr-27-2022-40174428/) ([]())

- 2024 Annual Report (Swedish): confirms deconsolidation of Kongregate in Q1 2024 and recognition of Monumental shares as a financial asset. — [MTG Års- och hållbarhetsredovisning 2024 PDF](https://mb.cision.com/Main/56/4130090/3366008.pdf) ([mb.cision.com](https://mb.cision.com/Main/56/4130090/3366008.pdf?utm_source=openai))

## SUBEX

* Rank: 208 | Expo: 0.25 | id_str: INE754A01055
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: earnings call transcripts (2023–2024), annual report FY2023–24, press releases (2019–2020)

### Firm Overview
Subex Limited is an India‑based telecom software company focused on business assurance, fraud management, partner ecosystem management and analytics for communications service providers. It is listed in India and positions itself as a “Telecom AI” vendor; any blockchain activity appears tied to enterprise telco use cases rather than cryptocurrency markets.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024 materials, management discusses enterprise blockchain collaborations for telco settlements and fraud data sharing, including a partnership with Tech Mahindra and participation in industry blockchain consortia. These initiatives relate to permissioned, enterprise DLT for telecom operations, not to cryptocurrencies, token issuance, exchange/custody, or mining. The FY2023–24 annual report explicitly states the company did not trade or invest in cryptocurrency or virtual currency during the year, and transcripts contain no references to holding crypto assets or providing crypto‑financial services. On this basis, no substantive, price‑relevant cryptocurrency exposure channel is evidenced for Subex within the sample period.

### Weight Assignment
Unclass is set to 1.0. While Subex references enterprise blockchain projects for telecom fraud mitigation and inter‑carrier/roaming settlement, there is no evidence of crypto treasury holdings, mining, crypto trading/custody, crypto‑backed financial products, or hardware/energy supply to miners. The annual report’s disclosure that the company has not traded or invested in cryptocurrency further supports a non‑exposed classification for crypto mechanisms during 2017–2024. Therefore, all mechanism weights are zero except unclass.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Subex’s use of the term “blockchain” could be misconstrued as crypto exposure; the cited sources indicate permissioned, enterprise DLT for telecom workflows, not cryptocurrency activities.  
- Public transcripts in 2023–2024 contain one mention of a blockchain collaboration (with Tech Mahindra) but no crypto‑specific activity.  
- If future disclosures beyond December 2024 introduce crypto‑related products or holdings, classification would need revisiting; none were found in the covered window.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 9, 2023 (Q1 FY2024) transcript: “In terms of our blockchain and TechM, this is one of the projects that we have been pursuing… first signs are very promising.” — [Subex IR transcript PDF] (subex.com) ([subex.com](https://www.subex.com/pdf/investors/news-events/investor-call/FY24/Q1/transcript-of-the-earnings-call-held-on-august-9-2023.pdf))  
- May 13, 2024 (Q4 FY2024) transcript: business update focused on AI/fraud management; no crypto holdings/services disclosed. — [Subex IR transcript PDF] (subex.com) ([subex.com](https://www.subex.com/pdf/investors/news-events/investor-call/FY24/Q4/transcript-of-the-earnings-call-held-on-may-13-2024.pdf))

**Filings (10‑K / annual reports):**
- Annual Report 2023–24, Notes: “The Company have not traded or invested in Crypto currency or Virtual Currency during the financial year.” — [Annual Report FY2023–24 PDF] (BSE filing) ([bsmedia.business-standard.com](https://bsmedia.business-standard.com/_media/bs/data/announcements/bse/09072024/8fa26f1d-6d79-48ad-bcce-6a0c919e2627.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**
- Nov 5, 2020: Tech Mahindra and Subex announce partnership to drive adoption of blockchain‑based solutions for telcos; integration with Subex’s blockchain network/enterprise DLT. — [Mahindra press release] (mahindra.com) ([mahindra.com](https://www.mahindra.com/news-room/press-release/en/tech-mahindra-and-subex-partner-to-drive-scale-adoption-of-blockchain-based-solutions-for-telecom-operators-globally))  
- Dec 10, 2019: Subex joins RAG Wangiri Blockchain Consortium to use blockchain for fraud intelligence sharing among telcos. — [Subex press release] (subex.com) ([subex.com](https://www.subex.com/press_release/subex-and-rag-announce-first-of-its-kind-alliance-to-leverage-blockchain-for-combatting-fraud/?utm_source=openai))  
- 2021: Industry coverage noting Subex’s membership in RAG consortium for blockchain‑based fraud efforts. — [Capacity Media article] (capacitymedia.com) ([capacitymedia.com](https://www.capacitymedia.com/article/29ot42ikril15nn8s4bwh/big-interview/in-blockchain-we-trust?utm_source=openai))

## ARQIT QUANTUM

* Rank: 209 | Expo: 0.25 | id_str: KYG0567U1278
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 20-F filing (FY2023), earnings calls (Q4 FY2023; H1/FY2024; Q4 FY2024), press releases and trade news (2022–2023)

### Firm Overview
Arqit Quantum Inc. is a UK-based cybersecurity company listed on Nasdaq that develops quantum-safe encryption software delivered via its SKA/QuantumCloud platform. During 2017–2024, the company focused on channel partnerships and application products for telecom, government/defense, and financial services; digital-asset-related activities are positioned as security layers rather than direct asset handling.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Arqit markets WalletSecure, an application designed to make Ethereum-based digital wallets and digital-asset transactions quantum-safe, and describes supporting the issuance, storage and transacting of digital assets along with real-time compliance controls. Management also introduced TradeSecure, which uses distributed ledger technology to create transferable digital finance instruments (e.g., digital promissory notes) for trade finance platforms. Earnings calls in 2023–2024 referenced WalletSecure and TradeSecure and anticipated initial transactions, but there is no indication that Arqit issued tokens, custodied crypto, or directly intermediated digital-asset flows. No evidence was found of crypto treasury holdings or mining operations.

### Weight Assignment
Bizserv is set to 1.0 because Arqit’s link to crypto markets is through non-financial support services: wallet security and DLT-enabled documentation for trade finance. These offerings target crypto/digital-asset users but do not involve custody, exchange/brokerage, issuance of crypto-backed products, mining, or mining hardware/energy provision. With no balance-sheet crypto holdings and no direct financial intermediation, holders, miners, finserv, and infrastr remain 0.0.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Historical media (2018) referenced a token pre-sale investment associated with Arqit prior to its SPAC listing; no evidence shows a launched token or ongoing token economics within 2017–2024 reporting as a public company.  
- WalletSecure/TradeSecure references are clear, but disclosed revenues tied specifically to crypto/digital assets appear immaterial during 2023–2024; classification reflects mechanism presence rather than materiality.  
- No disclosures of crypto assets on the balance sheet were identified in filings reviewed through FY2023.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 22, 2023 (Q4 FY2023): “WalletSecure, which provides compliance analytics and digital asset security, is our newest specialized application…” — [StockInsights transcript] (stockinsights.ai) (https://www.stockinsights.ai/us/ARQQ/earnings-transcript/fy23-q4-b4ec)  
- May 21, 2024 (H1/FY2024): “We also expect to announce soon the first transaction for our TradeSecure and WalletSecure products.” — [StockInsights transcript] (stockinsights.ai) (https://www.stockinsights.ai/us/ARQQ/earnings-transcript/fy24-q2-4fef)  
- Dec 11, 2024 (Q4 FY2024): Business update on products and channels (context for product suite and go‑to‑market). — [StockInsights transcript] (stockinsights.ai) (https://www.stockinsights.ai/us/ARQQ/earnings-transcript/fy24-q4-f672)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Nov 21, 2023 (FY2023 20‑F): Describes WalletSecure for “any Ethereum‑based digital wallet” and TradeSecure using distributed ledger technology for digital finance instruments. — [Arqit 20‑F] (ir.arqit.uk) (https://ir.arqit.uk/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001558370-23-019267/arqq-20230930x20f.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- Sept 11, 2023: Arqit announces WalletSecure to secure Ethereum‑based wallets and digital‑asset transactions. — [PR Newswire via GlobalFinTechSeries] (globalfintechseries.com) (https://globalfintechseries.com/digital-asset-management/arqit-new-product-launch-and-business-update/)  
- Dec 12, 2022: Arqit and Traxpay sign contract; TradeSecure to deliver quantum‑safe digital finance instruments using DLT. — [Company release PDF] (arqitgroup.com CDN) (https://d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net/_7ace036ab1106ce22b696fe2d3ed5005/arqit/news/2022-12-12_Arqit_and_Traxpay_sign_contract_to_deliver_more_48.pdf)  
- Jun 15, 2023: Consortium pilot delivers cross‑border quantum‑secure digital trade transaction using DLT, with Arqit TradeSecure. — [PR Newswire] (prnewswire.com) (https://www.prnewswire.com/apac/news-releases/arqit-consortium-delivers-worlds-first-cross-border-quantum-secure-digital-trade-transaction-301851551.html)  
- Sept 20, 2018: Media report notes token pre‑sale investment associated with Arqit in early plans; no launched token evidenced in later filings. — [CoinDesk] (coindesk.com) (https://www.coindesk.com/business/2018/09/20/rbs-blockchain-team-jumps-ship-to-build-new-startups-using-r3s-corda)

### Firm Overview
Quorum Information Technologies Inc. is a Canada-based SaaS software and services provider focused on automotive dealerships and OEMs across North America. Its portfolio spans dealership management, CRM, digital retailing, business development center services, and related tools used in daily dealership operations.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across earnings calls, filings, and press releases from 2017–2024, Quorum’s activities center on dealership enterprise software and BDC services with no direct involvement in cryptocurrencies or blockchain-based products. There is no evidence of mining operations, crypto custody/exchange services, token issuance, or treasury holdings of digital assets. The company’s disclosures and management commentary consistently describe a traditional SaaS business model serving auto retail clients. Accordingly, no crypto-exposure mechanism is identified.

### Weight Assignment
Unclass is set to 1.0 because credible sources across the 2017–2024 sample indicate no substantive crypto exposure. With the business model limited to dealership software and services, and no mining, infrastructure supply, financial intermediation in digital assets, or treasury holdings, all mechanism categories remain 0.0 and the firm is classified as unclassified for crypto exposure.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- None identified specific to 2017–2024. Note: the corporate name “Quorum” should not be confused with JPMorgan/ConsenSys “Quorum” enterprise blockchain software, which is unrelated to Quorum Information Technologies Inc.

## FIDELITY NAT.INFO.SVS.

* Rank: 211 | Expo: 0.24 | id_str: US31620M1062
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings calls (Q1 2021, Q1 2022), FIS press releases (2021–2022), SEC Form 10‑K (2023), financial news (2022)

### Firm Overview
Fidelity National Information Services, Inc. (FIS) is a U.S.-based financial technology provider offering core banking, payments, merchant acquiring (including the former Worldpay business during 2019–2023), and capital markets solutions. During 2017–2024, FIS launched crypto-related offerings for banks and merchants, including bitcoin enablement via a custodial partner, crypto-exchange acquiring, and stablecoin settlement options. ([fisglobal.com](https://www.fisglobal.com/about-us/media-room/press-release/2021/bitcoin-in-your-bank-account-fis-nydig-partner-to-enable-banks-to-offer-their-customers-the-ability?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s exposure is through financial services: in May 2021 FIS announced an “industry‑first” solution with NYDIG to enable banks to let customers buy, sell, and hold bitcoin from their bank accounts. In earnings remarks, management described FIS as “the leading acquirer for cryptocurrency,” serving several top global exchanges (e.g., Coinbase, BitPay). In 2022, FIS (via Worldpay) added merchant settlement in USDC through a partnership with Circle and piloted with Crypto.com; its capital markets segment also partnered with Fireblocks to help clients store and issue digital assets and access DeFi. ([fisglobal.com](https://www.fisglobal.com/about-us/media-room/press-release/2021/bitcoin-in-your-bank-account-fis-nydig-partner-to-enable-banks-to-offer-their-customers-the-ability?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 reflects direct provision of crypto‑related financial services: bank‑facing bitcoin enablement (with NYDIG), card‑to‑crypto acquiring for major exchanges, and merchant settlement in USDC. There is no evidence of proprietary coin holdings as treasury assets, mining operations, or hardware/energy infrastructure supply; thus holders, miners, and infrastr = 0.0. Services are not merely advisory/support, so bizserv = 0.0. Given clear, repeated disclosures and transcripts, invbase remains 0 and certainty is 4. ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/nyse/fis/investor-relations/earnings-call/q1-2021))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Worldpay was divested from FIS beginning in 2023, which may reduce direct crypto‑acquiring exposure post‑transaction; however, within the 2017–2024 sample, the cited crypto activities occurred while Worldpay operated under FIS and FIS also advanced digital‑asset initiatives in other segments. Public filings do not quantify crypto revenue share, so RevShare is coded as minor based on qualitative disclosures. The 2023 Form 10‑K references evolving regulations around “digital/crypto currency,” but provides limited segment granularity. ([tickertrends.io](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/FIS/Q1-earnings-transcript-2022))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 6, 2021 (Q1 2021): “We’ve also launched an industry‑first cryptobanking solution… in partnership with NYDIG… [so] customers will be able to view and transact their Bitcoin holdings alongside their traditional accounts.” — [AlphaSpread transcript] (https://www.alphaspread.com/security/nyse/fis/investor-relations/earnings-call/q1-2021)  
- May 6, 2021 (Q1 2021): “FIS is the leading acquirer for cryptocurrency… We serve 5 of the top 10 digital asset exchanges… including innovators like Coinbase and BitPay.” — [AlphaSpread transcript] (https://www.alphaspread.com/security/nyse/fis/investor-relations/earnings-call/q1-2021)  
- May 4, 2022 (Q1 2022): “We’ve recently formed a partnership with Circle to enable our merchants to receive settlement in USD Coin, and Crypto.com will be our first pilot customer.” — [TickerTrends transcript] (https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/FIS/Q1-earnings-transcript-2022)  
- May 4, 2022 (Q1 2022): “We processed for the top four out of five largest crypto exchanges… You saw us sign a partnership this quarter with Circle… [to be] the first provider of USDC crypto capabilities.” — [TickerTrends transcript] (https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/FIS/Q1-earnings-transcript-2022)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- May 5, 2021: “Enables banks to offer their customers the ability to buy, sell, and hold bitcoin via their bank accounts.” — [FIS press release (NYDIG)] (https://www.fisglobal.com/about-us/media-room/press-release/2021/bitcoin-in-your-bank-account-fis-nydig-partner-to-enable-banks-to-offer-their-customers-the-ability)  
- Apr 6, 2022: Worldpay from FIS “first global merchant acquirer to offer… settlement directly in USD Coin (USDC).” — [FIS investor news] (https://www.investor.fisglobal.com/news-releases/news-release-details/worldpay-fis-becomes-first-global-merchant-acquirer-offer-direct/)  
- Apr 13, 2022: FIS teams with Fireblocks “to enable firms… to access… crypto trading venues… and DeFi applications.” — [FIS press release] (https://www.fisglobal.com/about-us/media-room/press-release/2022/fis-teams-with-fireblocks-to-accelerate-crypto-adoption-within-capital-markets-industry-globally)  
- 2023 Form 10‑K: notes evolving laws may cover “alternative payment types, including digital/crypto currency.” — [SEC filing PDF] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1136893/000119312524118132/d680197dars.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Apr 6, 2022: “Circle enlists FIS, Crypto.com for merchant payments settled in USDC.” — [CoinDesk] (https://www.coindesk.com/business/2022/04/06/circle-enlists-fis-cryptocom-for-merchant-payments-settled-in-usdc)

([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pollen_Street_Group?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024 disclosures reviewed, there is no evidence that the company held cryptocurrencies, mined digital assets, provided crypto‑related financial services, or supplied crypto infrastructure. The 2024 Annual Financial Report contains no references to crypto, bitcoin, blockchain, or digital assets, consistent with a strategy focused on private credit and financial‑services private equity. ([pollenstreetgroup.com](https://www.pollenstreetgroup.com/app/uploads/Pollen-Street-Group-Limited-Annual-Financial-Report-2024-1.pdf))  
Additionally, a February 25, 2025 update from the Washington State DFI (citing outreach in 2024) records Pollen Street stating it “is not involved in cryptocurrency investing,” posted in the context of warning about third‑party brand misuse scams impersonating the firm. This supports the absence of direct crypto exposure in the sample period. ([dfi.wa.gov](https://dfi.wa.gov/consumer/alerts/alleged-cryptocurrency-trading-platform-sencapitals-appears-be-engaged-fraud))

### Weight Assignment
Unclass = 1.0 is assigned due to the absence of substantive, firm‑level crypto exposure in filings, presentations, or credible news over 2017–2024. No treasury crypto holdings, mining operations, crypto custody/trading/payment products, or mining hardware/energy infrastructure activities were identified. Accordingly, holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, and infrastr are each set to 0.0, with invbase = 0 given no evidence that the stock trades as a crypto proxy. ([pollenstreetgroup.com](https://www.pollenstreetgroup.com/app/uploads/Pollen-Street-Group-Limited-Annual-Financial-Report-2024-1.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified beyond standard limitations: the group invests in a range of financial‑services businesses, some of which may engage with fintech themes; however, no firm‑level crypto activity is disclosed. No dedicated earnings call transcripts were located for 2017–2024; investor communications are primarily RNS, annual/interim reports, and presentations. ([pollenstreetgroup.com](https://www.pollenstreetgroup.com/shareholders/results-centre/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Mar 25, 2025: Full‑year 2024 results presentation/webcast materials; no references to crypto/digital assets observed. — [Pollen Street 2024 Results Presentation] (https://www.pollenstreetgroup.com/app/uploads/Pollen-Street-Group-Limited-2024-Results-Presentation.pdf)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Mar 25, 2025: Annual Financial Report 2024 — strategy, AUM, revenue model; no crypto references. — [Annual Report 2024 (PDF)] (https://www.pollenstreetgroup.com/app/uploads/Pollen-Street-Group-Limited-Annual-Financial-Report-2024-1.pdf)  
- Sep 12, 2024: Q3 2024 Trading Update — focus on AUM growth and private credit deployment; no crypto references. — [RNS Q3 2024 (PDF)] (https://ir.pollenstreetgroup.com/media/haul1nqr/pollen-street-limited-q3-2024-trading-update.pdf)  
- Sep 4, 2024: H1 2024 Interim Results Presentation — fee‑based asset management and investment company model; no crypto references. — [H1 2024 Presentation (PDF)] (https://www.pollenstreetgroup.com/app/uploads/pollen-street-group-limited-interim-results-presentation-h1-2024.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Aug 28, 2024 (updated Feb 25, 2025): WA DFI alert notes firm statement that Pollen Street is not involved in cryptocurrency investing; warns of brand misuse scams. — [Washington State DFI] (https://dfi.wa.gov/consumer/alerts/alleged-cryptocurrency-trading-platform-sencapitals-appears-be-engaged-fraud)  
- Mar 21, 2024: Coverage of 2023 results and 2024 guidance focused on fundraising and private credit; no crypto angle cited. — [Alternative Credit Investor] (https://alternativecreditinvestor.com/2024/03/21/pollen-street-lifts-guidance-after-2023-growth-beats-targets/)

</small>

## REPOSITRAK

* Rank: 213 | Expo: 0.23 | id_str: US7002153044
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10‑K filings (FY2023, FY2024), Q4 FY2024 earnings call

### Firm Overview
ReposiTrak, Inc. (formerly Park City Group) is a U.S. SaaS provider focused on B2B compliance, food safety traceability, and supply chain solutions for retailers, wholesalers, and suppliers. In late 2023 the company rebranded/uplisted and continued operating a recurring‑revenue model centered on its ReposiTrak Traceability Network and compliance platform. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/50471/000143774924030271/trak20240630c_10k.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across FY2017–FY2024 filings and multiple earnings calls, there is no evidence that the company held crypto assets, mined digital assets, offered crypto trading/custody/payment products, or sold mining hardware/energy/hosting. Disclosures emphasize SaaS compliance and food traceability, with no references to cryptocurrency or blockchain as a revenue driver in the FY2023 or FY2024 10‑Ks. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/50471/000143774923027054/pcyg20230630_10k.htm))  
Management commentary in FY2024 calls focuses on accelerating enrollment and ARR from food traceability and cross‑selling compliance/supply‑chain modules. A FY2023 call described the platform as system‑agnostic that can interoperate “with any labeling… any bar coding… any blockchain system,” underscoring that blockchain is not the firm’s product but simply one of many systems it can connect to. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/TRAK/documents/transcripts/q4-2024))

### Weight Assignment
Unclass = 1.0 is assigned due to the absence of substantive crypto exposure. Filings contain no crypto/digital‑asset holdings or product lines; transcripts describe a SaaS network for food traceability/compliance rather than crypto financial services or mining/infrastructure. There is no evidence the stock trades as a crypto proxy, so invbase = 0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/50471/000143774924030271/trak20240630c_10k.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified beyond routine caveats. A 2023 transcript mentions interoperability with “any blockchain system,” but this reflects integration capability, not a blockchain product or crypto activity by the company. ([statementdog.com](https://statementdog.com/analysis/TRAK/earnings_calls/262245))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Sep 30, 2024 (Q4 FY2024), transcript: “We invested … to further accelerate our traceability solution… I believe we are and will continue to be the leader in traceability.” — [Fintool transcript] (https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/TRAK/documents/transcripts/q4-2024) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/TRAK/documents/transcripts/q4-2024))  
- Sep 30, 2024 (Q4 FY2024), transcript: “Compliance management, supply chain, [and] discovery continue to deliver solid growth… The same customers with a traceability problem also have a compliance and supply chain problem.” — [Fintool transcript] (https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/TRAK/documents/transcripts/q4-2024) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/TRAK/documents/transcripts/q4-2024))  
- Sep 28, 2023 (Q4 FY2023), transcript: “We work with any labeling system, any bar coding system, any blockchain system… we provide the traceability interoperability layer.” — [StatementDog transcript] (https://statementdog.com/analysis/TRAK/earnings_calls/262245) ([statementdog.com](https://statementdog.com/analysis/TRAK/earnings_calls/262245))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Sep 30, 2024: Form 10‑K (FY2024) — SaaS compliance/traceability business; no crypto/digital‑asset references found. — [SEC filing] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/50471/000143774924030271/trak20240630c_10k.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/50471/000143774924030271/trak20240630c_10k.htm))  
- Sep 28, 2023: Form 10‑K (FY2023) — Park City Group as SaaS parent of ReposiTrak; no crypto/digital‑asset references found. — [SEC filing] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/50471/000143774923027054/pcyg20230630_10k.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/50471/000143774923027054/pcyg20230630_10k.htm))  
- Sep 30, 2024: FY2024 results press release — reiterates positioning as “the world’s largest food traceability and regulatory compliance network.” — [Business Wire via Nasdaq] (https://www.nasdaq.com/press-release/repositrak-delivers-full-year-revenue-205-million-and-earnings-share-030-2024-09-30) ([nasdaq.com](https://www.nasdaq.com/press-release/repositrak-delivers-full-year-revenue-205-million-and-earnings-share-030-2024-09-30?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**

- May 15 & Sep 30, 2024: Third‑ and fourth‑quarter earnings call scheduling/coverage confirming cadence; business focus on traceability/compliance. — [Seeking Alpha Q3 FY2024 transcript page] (https://seekingalpha.com/article/4693700-repositrak-inc-trak-q3-2024-earnings-call-transcript), [Nasdaq call notice Q4 FY2024] (https://www.nasdaq.com/press-release/repositrak-schedules-fiscal-2024-fourth-quarter-earnings-conference-call-and-webcast) ([seekingalpha.com](https://seekingalpha.com/article/4693700-repositrak-inc-trak-q3-2024-earnings-call-transcript?utm_source=openai))

## IDACORP

* Rank: 214 | Expo: 0.23 | id_str: US4511071064
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 1.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10‑K/Annual Report (2022), IPUC orders and Schedule 20 tariff (2022–2023), Q2 2018 earnings call

### Firm Overview
IDACORP, Inc. is a U.S. utility holding company whose primary subsidiary, Idaho Power Company, is a vertically integrated electric utility serving customers in southern Idaho and eastern Oregon. The company’s operations and earnings are determined largely through state utility regulation and traditional electric service activities.

### Exposure Channels Identified
There is no evidence that IDACORP holds crypto assets, operates mining activities, or offers crypto financial services. Instead, the company’s exposure arises through energy infrastructure sold to cryptocurrency mining customers: in June 2022 the Idaho Public Utilities Commission (IPUC) approved Idaho Power’s new customer class and rate “Schedule 20 – Speculative High‑Density Load,” explicitly targeted at commercial cryptocurrency mining operations under 20 MW, with mandatory summer interruptibility and marginal‑energy pricing. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/i/NYSE_IDA_2022.pdf))  
IDACORP’s 2022 annual report states Idaho Power had received approximately 2,000 MW of potential interest from the crypto‑mining industry and sought to mitigate financial and system risks via Schedule 20’s design. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/i/NYSE_IDA_2022.pdf))  
Contemporaneous reporting and IPUC dockets further document the tariff’s application, including a dispute with GeoBitmine over Idaho Power’s Schedule 20 terms for a proposed 6 MW facility. ([utilitydive.com](https://www.utilitydive.com/news/geobitmine-idaho-power-cryptocurrency-mining-rate/626724/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Infrastr = 1.0 is assigned because IDACORP’s only substantiated crypto linkage is as an energy supplier with a miner‑specific tariff and operating terms; there is no treasury holding, mining, financial service, or non‑financial service activity. The 2018 earnings call acknowledged interest from “cryptocurrency and blockchain customers,” and later filings and commission orders formalized a dedicated rate class for such load. Given the absence of evidence that the stock trades as a crypto proxy, invbase = 0. RevShare and AssetShare are coded 0 reflecting no disclosed crypto revenue segment or crypto assets on balance sheet. ([s26.q4cdn.com](https://s26.q4cdn.com/720254477/files/doc_events/2018/IDA-USQ_Transcript_2018-08-02.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Evidence confirms explicit tariffing for potential crypto‑mining customers, but publicly available filings do not quantify actual Schedule 20 customer counts or revenue contribution; therefore the materiality to revenue is unclear. Crypto‑specific commentary in earnings calls is concentrated in 2018, with later references primarily appearing in regulatory filings rather than management discussion. ([s26.q4cdn.com](https://s26.q4cdn.com/720254477/files/doc_events/2018/IDA-USQ_Transcript_2018-08-02.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 2, 2018 (Q2 2018), transcript: “In a very different kind of mining, we continue to see active interest from cryptocurrency and blockchain customers.” — [Q2 2018 Corrected Transcript PDF] (https://s26.q4cdn.com/720254477/files/doc_events/2018/IDA-USQ_Transcript_2018-08-02.pdf) ([s26.q4cdn.com](https://s26.q4cdn.com/720254477/files/doc_events/2018/IDA-USQ_Transcript_2018-08-02.pdf?utm_source=openai))  
- May 2, 2024 (Q1 2024), corrected transcript: management discusses needed infrastructure amid high customer and load growth; no crypto products mentioned. — [Q1 2024 Corrected Transcript PDF] (https://s26.q4cdn.com/720254477/files/doc_financials/2024/q1/CORRECTED-TRANSCRIPT_-IDACORP-Inc-IDA-US-Q1-2024-Earnings-Call-2-May-2024-4_30-PM-ET.pdf) ([s26.q4cdn.com](https://s26.q4cdn.com/720254477/files/doc_financials/2024/q1/CORRECTED-TRANSCRIPT_-IDACORP-Inc-IDA-US-Q1-2024-Earnings-Call-2-May-2024-4_30-PM-ET.pdf?utm_source=openai))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- 2022 Annual Report/10‑K: establishes “Speculative High‑Density Load” class for commercial crypto miners (<20 MW), notes ~2,000 MW potential interest, outlines interruptibility and marginal‑energy pricing; subsequent IPUC actions pending. — [IDACORP 2022 Annual Report PDF] (https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/i/NYSE_IDA_2022.pdf) ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/i/NYSE_IDA_2022.pdf))  
- IPUC Final Order No. 35428 (June 15, 2022): approves Schedule 20; docket materials describe purpose for cryptocurrency operations. — [IPUC Order PDF] (https://puc.idaho.gov/Fileroom/PublicFiles/ELEC/IPC/IPCE2137/OrdNotc/20220615Final_Order_No_35428.pdf) ([puc.idaho.gov](https://puc.idaho.gov/Fileroom/PublicFiles/ELEC/IPC/IPCE2137/OrdNotc/20220615Final_Order_No_35428.pdf?utm_source=openai))  
- IPUC Reconsideration Order No. 35550 (Oct 5, 2022): affirms Schedule 20 with modifications. — [IPUC Order PDF] (https://puc.idaho.gov/Fileroom/PublicFiles/ELEC/IPC/IPCE2137/OrdNotc/20221005Reconsideration_Order_No_35550.pdf) ([puc.idaho.gov](https://puc.idaho.gov/Fileroom/PublicFiles/ELEC/IPC/IPCE2137/OrdNotc/20221005Reconsideration_Order_No_35550.pdf?utm_source=openai))  
- IPUC Notice/Order No. 35666 (Jan 18, 2023): docket notes Schedule 20 was created “specifically [for] large‑scale cryptocurrency mining operators.” — [IPUC Notice PDF] (https://puc.idaho.gov/Fileroom/PublicFiles/ELEC/IPC/IPCE2230/OrdNotc/20230118Notice_of_Application_Order_No_35666.pdf) ([puc.idaho.gov](https://puc.idaho.gov/Fileroom/PublicFiles/ELEC/IPC/IPCE2230/OrdNotc/20230118Notice_of_Application_Order_No_35666.pdf?utm_source=openai))  
- Idaho Power tariff — Schedule 20: Speculative High‑Density Load (current tariff sheet). — [Tariff PDF] (https://docs.idahopower.com/pdfs/AboutUs/RatesRegulatory/Tariffs/20.pdf) ([docs.idahopower.com](https://docs.idahopower.com/pdfs/AboutUs/RatesRegulatory/Tariffs/20.pdf?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Aug 2, 2022: Coverage of GeoBitmine challenge; recounts schedule’s crypto‑specific design and Idaho Power’s receipt of nearly 2,000 MW of crypto inquiries. — [Utility Dive] (https://www.utilitydive.com/news/geobitmine-idaho-power-cryptocurrency-mining-rate/626724/) ([utilitydive.com](https://www.utilitydive.com/news/geobitmine-idaho-power-cryptocurrency-mining-rate/626724/?utm_source=openai))  
- Aug 2, 2022: Background on Idaho Power’s crypto‑mining rate class and context of GeoBitmine dispute. — [Inside Climate News] (https://insideclimatenews.org/news/02082022/bitcoin-crypto-mining-idaho/) ([insideclimatenews.org](https://insideclimatenews.org/news/02082022/bitcoin-crypto-mining-idaho/?utm_source=openai))

## CUSTOMERS BANCORP

* Rank: 215 | Expo: 0.23 | id_str: US23204G1004
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10‑K filings (2021, 2024), Q3 2021 and Q4 2024 earnings calls, Q2 2022 press release, Federal Reserve enforcement notice (Aug 2024)

### Firm Overview
Customers Bancorp is a U.S. bank holding company headquartered in Pennsylvania, whose principal subsidiary Customers Bank provides commercial and consumer banking nationally. Beginning in 2021 the bank launched an instant USD payments network for institutional clients (initially CBIT, later migrated to an in‑house platform called cubiX), which has served many firms operating in the digital‑asset ecosystem. ([d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net](https://d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net/CIK-0001488813/58a3b0af-e427-4079-b2a0-50289a37f4c6.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s crypto linkage is through providing real‑time fiat payments and deposit services to digital‑asset clients rather than holding or trading crypto itself. Management highlighted CBIT’s role in attracting non‑interest‑bearing deposits in 2021, and later described transitioning those clients to the bank‑built cubiX platform while keeping related balances in cash for risk control. Regulatory materials and company filings characterize CBIT/cubiX as an instant payments platform serving digital‑asset clients and not a vehicle for crypto custody or trading by the bank. In August 2024, the Federal Reserve entered an enforcement action focused on risk management around the bank’s digital‑asset strategy, reinforcing that this channel is operational/financial services rather than balance‑sheet crypto exposure. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/28/customers-bancorp-inc-cubi-q3-2021-earnings-call-t/))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 is assigned because evidence consistently shows Customers Bancorp’s exposure arises from providing fiat banking and 24/7 payments rails (CBIT/cubiX) to digital‑asset clients, including significant non‑interest‑bearing deposit inflows tied to that platform. There is no evidence of proprietary crypto holdings, mining, or hardware infrastructure activities, and filings describe CBIT/cubiX as a closed, intrabank USD system rather than a crypto‑asset product. Given the absence of indications that the equity trades as a retail crypto proxy, invbase = 0. RevShare is coded 1 (minor) and AssetShare 0, reflecting modest disclosed fee contribution (e.g., ~$5 million annual run‑rate from cubiX in 2024) and no crypto assets on the balance sheet. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/28/customers-bancorp-inc-cubi-q3-2021-earnings-call-t/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
While deposits from the digital‑asset vertical were periodically quantified, the precise revenue share attributable to these clients (via net interest income on related balances plus fees) is not separately disclosed; therefore RevShare is conservatively coded as minor. The CBIT-to‑cubiX migration in late 2024 changes the technical implementation, but not the underlying exposure mechanism. Some transcripts and investor materials are hosted on third‑party sites, but core claims are corroborated in SEC filings and the Federal Reserve notice. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1488813/000148881325000013/cubi-20241231.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 28, 2021 (Q3 2021), transcript: “deposits grew by $6.1 billion… driven by our… CBIT launch, which brought in $1.5 billion of non‑interest bearing deposits as of September 30.” — [Motley Fool] (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/28/customers-bancorp-inc-cubi-q3-2021-earnings-call-t/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/28/customers-bancorp-inc-cubi-q3-2021-earnings-call-t/))  
- Q4 2024 results call: “we fully transferred all CBIT customers to our in‑house developed Cubix platform… [and] 15% used to be the old limit on crypto deposits… we’re holding all of this in cash.” — [Investing.com transcript] (https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-customers-bancorp-q4-2024-earnings-beat-forecasts-93CH-3843846) ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-customers-bancorp-q4-2024-earnings-beat-forecasts-93CH-3843846))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Q3 2021 filing: CBIT launched on TassatPay; “may only be created by… customers… CBIT is not listed or traded on any digital currency exchange… $1.5 billion of deposits… in preparation for the launch.” — [SEC filing PDF] (https://d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net/CIK-0001488813/58a3b0af-e427-4079-b2a0-50289a37f4c6.pdf) ([d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net](https://d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net/CIK-0001488813/58a3b0af-e427-4079-b2a0-50289a37f4c6.pdf))  
- Q2 2022 press release: “CBIT‑related deposits… $2.1 billion at June 30, 2022… onboarded 90 new CBIT customers in Q2, total 190.” — [Company press release PDF] (https://www.customersbank.com/pdf/220727-release.pdf) ([customersbank.com](https://www.customersbank.com/pdf/220727-release.pdf))  
- FY 2024 Form 10‑K: CBIT “provided a closed‑system for intrabank commercial transactions and was not intended to be a trading platform”; omnibus CBIT account balance $0 at Dec 31, 2024 (vs. $826.9m at Dec 31, 2023). — [SEC 10‑K HTML] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1488813/000148881325000013/cubi-20241231.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1488813/000148881325000013/cubi-20241231.htm?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Aug 8, 2024: Federal Reserve enforcement action requiring strengthened oversight and AML controls related to the bank’s digital‑asset strategy. — [Federal Reserve press release] (https://www.federalreserve.gov/newsevents/pressreleases/enforcement20240808a.htm) ([federalreserve.gov](https://www.federalreserve.gov/newsevents/pressreleases/enforcement20240808a.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- Aug 8–12, 2024: Coverage summarizing the action and describing CBIT as a 24/7 USD platform serving digital‑asset clients; bank deals only in U.S. dollars and does not accept cryptocurrency. — [Bloomberg] (https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2024-08-08/federal-reserve-hits-customers-bancorp-cubi-with-enforcement-action), [CoinDesk] (https://www.coindesk.com/policy/2024/08/08/crypto-friendly-bank-ordered-by-fed-to-limit-risks-from-digital-asset-clients) ([bloomberg.com](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2024-08-08/federal-reserve-hits-customers-bancorp-cubi-with-enforcement-action?utm_source=openai))

</small>

## BLACKROCK

* Rank: 216 | Expo: 0.23 | id_str: US09290D1019
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (FY 2024), Q1 2021 and Q1/Q2 2024 earnings calls, issuer press releases (Jan/Jul 2024), SEC approval statements (Jan 10, 2024)

### Firm Overview
BlackRock, Inc. is a U.S.-based global asset manager and the world’s largest by assets under management, with a leading iShares ETF platform. Within digital assets during 2017–2024, BlackRock introduced spot crypto ETPs (bitcoin and ether) and launched a tokenized U.S. Treasury liquidity fund for qualified investors.

### Exposure Channels Identified
BlackRock’s crypto linkage is squarely through financial products and services. In January 2024, the SEC approved the listing and trading of multiple spot bitcoin ETPs; BlackRock’s iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) began trading Jan 11, 2024 and subsequently gathered significant assets/inflows as discussed by management on earnings calls and in filings. (SEC statement: https://www.sec.gov/newsroom/speeches-statements/gensler-statement-spot-bitcoin-011023; IBIT press release: https://www.ishares.com/us/literature/press-release/blackrocks-bitcoin-etf-ibit-clears-final-sec-hurdle.pdf)  
BlackRock’s iShares Ethereum Trust ETF (ETHA) followed in July 2024 after SEC effectiveness, expanding client access to ether under a regulated wrapper. (Press release: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20240722299039/en/BlackRocks-iShares-Ethereum-Trust-ETF-Set-to-Launch)  
In March 2024, BlackRock launched its first tokenized fund, the USD Institutional Digital Liquidity Fund (BUIDL), issued on public blockchain via Securitize, further underscoring a financial-services delivery channel rather than proprietary coin holding. (Press release: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20240320771318/en/BlackRock-Launches-Its-First-Tokenized-Fund-BUIDL-on-the-Ethereum-Network)

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 is assigned because evidence shows BlackRock’s exposure operates through issuing and sponsoring crypto investment products (IBIT, ETHA) and a tokenized cash management fund (BUIDL), with related fees and flows reflected in company reporting (e.g., “cryptocurrency ETPs” driving alternative ETF inflows in 2024). There is no indication of corporate treasury crypto holdings, mining, or purpose-built mining infrastructure. (10-K FY2024 PDF: “Alternative ETFs had net inflows of $41 billion, driven by the launch of cryptocurrency exchange‑traded products (ETPs).” https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/2012383/000119312525073384/d865271dars.pdf; IBIT prospectus naming Coinbase Custody as bitcoin custodian: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1980994/000143774924006443/bit20240304_424b3.htm)

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified. Crypto activity is clearly product‑based (ETPs/tokenized fund); the underlying digital assets reside in the product vehicles or with appointed custodians, not on BlackRock’s corporate balance sheet.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Apr 12, 2024 (Q1 2024), transcript: “Our Bitcoin fund, which was launched in January, was the fastest‑growing ETF in history, and already has nearly $20 billion in AUM.” — [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2024/04/12/blackrock-blk-q1-2024-earnings-call-transcript/)  
- Jul 15, 2024 (Q2 2024), transcript: “BlackRock’s bitcoin ETF continues to lead, gathering another $4 billion in the second quarter for 18 billion of net inflows in its first six months.” — [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2024/07/15/blackrock-blk-q2-2024-earnings-call-transcript/)  
- Apr 16, 2021 (Q1 2021), transcript: “we are investigating… how we could create different products… related to crypto… [and] the activities around Bitcoin and other crypto, we’re fascinated about it.” — [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/04/16/blackrock-blk-q1-2021-earnings-call-transcript/)

**Filings (10‑K / prospectus):**
- FY 2024 Form 10‑K: “Alternative ETFs had net inflows of $41 billion, driven by the launch of cryptocurrency exchange‑traded products (‘ETPs’) in the year.” — [SEC PDF](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/2012383/000119312525073384/d865271dars.pdf)  
- IBIT prospectus: “Coinbase Custody Trust Company, LLC (the ‘Bitcoin Custodian’) is the custodian for the Trust’s bitcoin holdings.” — [SEC filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1980994/000143774924006443/bit20240304_424b3.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- Jan 10, 2024: SEC approval statement for listing/trading of spot bitcoin ETP shares
- Jan 10, 2024: SEC approval of spot bitcoin ETP listings and trading — [SEC Chair Gensler statement](https://www.sec.gov/newsroom/speeches-statements/gensler-statement-spot-bitcoin-011023)  
- May 24, 2024: SEC clears path for spot ether ETFs; issuers include BlackRock’s iShares — [Axios](https://www.axios.com/2024/05/23/sec-ethereum-etf-approval-crypto-news)  
- Apr 12, 2024: IBIT’s early inflows and “fastest‑growing ETF in history” remark highlighted in coverage of Q1 results — [Blockworks](https://blockworks.co/news/blackrock-q1-earnings-bitcoin-fund-inflows/)

</small>

## HAMILTON LANE CL.A

* Rank: 217 | Expo: 0.23 | id_str: US4074971064
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filing (FY 2024), Q2 FY2023 and Q3 FY2024 earnings calls, company/Securitize press releases (Jan/May 2023)

### Firm Overview
Hamilton Lane Incorporated is a U.S.-based global private markets investment manager listed on Nasdaq under HLNE. Between 2017 and 2024, the firm partnered with tokenization platforms to offer feeder funds providing on-chain access to certain Hamilton Lane strategies, including direct equity and private credit. ([hamiltonlane.com](https://www.hamiltonlane.com/en-us/news/direct-equity-fund-on-securitize))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s crypto linkage operates through financial services: tokenized feeder funds and distribution via regulated digital asset platforms. In January 2023, Hamilton Lane announced a Securitize feeder fund tokenized on Polygon for Equity Opportunities Fund V; in May 2023, access to its Senior Credit Opportunities Fund (SCOPE) was expanded via Polygon as well. Management also discussed tokenization partnerships on earnings calls (e.g., Securitize, ADDX, Libre) and the firm’s 2024 10‑K explicitly references making products available “on the blockchain” and “through tokenization and digital securities exchanges.” ([hamiltonlane.com](https://www.hamiltonlane.com/en-us/news/direct-equity-fund-on-securitize))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 is assigned because all evidenced exposure stems from offering and distributing investment products that are tokenized or available through digital-asset rails (feeder funds on Securitize/Polygon), generating fee income rather than proprietary crypto holdings or mining revenues. The FY2024 10‑K frames tokenization as a distribution/technology channel, and earnings calls describe multiple tokenization partnerships; there is no indication of balance-sheet crypto holdings, mining, or mining-focused infrastructure. ([hamiltonlane.com](https://www.hamiltonlane.com/en-us/news/direct-equity-fund-on-securitize))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Management noted tokenization-channel inflows but characterized them as “not … massively significant amounts of capital,” so the revenue contribution from tokenized products during the sample period appears minor; the firm does not provide a separate crypto revenue line. Accordingly, RevShare is coded as 1 (<~5%) and AssetShare as 0. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/HLNE/documents/transcripts/q3-2024))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 2022 (Q2 FY2023), transcript: “we … announced a partnership with … Securitize … to provide qualified U.S. investors with tokenized access to three Hamilton Lane funds.” — [TickerTrends](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/HLNE/Q2-earnings-transcript-2023) ([tickertrends.io](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/HLNE/Q2-earnings-transcript-2023))  
- Feb 6, 2024 (Q3 FY2024), transcript Q&A: “Tokenization is … better, faster, cheaper… We have been receiving capital … through the token channel… not … massively significant amounts.” — [Fintool](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/HLNE/documents/transcripts/q3-2024) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/HLNE/documents/transcripts/q3-2024))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- FY2024 Form 10‑K: risk factor references “making products available on the blockchain and through tokenization and digital securities exchanges.” — [SEC 10‑K HTML](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1433642/000143364224000064/hlne-20240331.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1433642/000143364224000064/hlne-20240331.htm))  
- Jan 31, 2023: Equity Opportunities Fund V tokenized feeder fund on Securitize (Polygon); reduced minimums. — [Hamilton Lane news](https://www.hamiltonlane.com/en-us/news/direct-equity-fund-on-securitize) ([hamiltonlane.com](https://www.hamiltonlane.com/en-us/news/direct-equity-fund-on-securitize))  
- May 2023: SCOPE private credit access expanded; shares issued/managed on Polygon. — [Securitize press](https://securitize.io/learn/press/securitize-expands-access-to-hamilton-lanes-senior-credit-opportunities-fund-via-polygon) ([securitize.io](https://securitize.io/learn/press/securitize-expands-access-to-hamilton-lanes-senior-credit-opportunities-fund-via-polygon))

**Financial news / other:**
- May 4, 2023: Coverage of Hamilton Lane offering tokenized access to a second fund. — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2023/05/04/hamilton-lane-offers-polygon-based-tokenized-access-to-a-second-fund) ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2023/05/04/hamilton-lane-offers-polygon-based-tokenized-access-to-a-second-fund?utm_source=openai))

## TP ICAP GROUP

* Rank: 218 | Expo: 0.23 | id_str: JE00BMDZN391
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Half‑year and full‑year results transcripts (2022, 2024), Annual Report 2023, press releases (2021 launch; 2022 FCA registration; 2023 first live trade)

### Firm Overview
TP ICAP Group plc is a UK‑based interdealer broker and market‑infrastructure provider listed in London. During 2017–2024 it built and launched Fusion Digital Assets, an FCA‑registered, institutional spot cryptoasset marketplace that connects to third‑party custodians rather than taking client assets onto its own balance sheet. ([tpicap.com](https://tpicap.com/media/press-releases/2022/tp-icap-receives-fca-registration-wholesale-cryptoasset-exchange?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s crypto exposure is via financial services: operating a wholesale spot cryptoasset exchange (Fusion Digital Assets) and broking crypto derivatives for institutional clients. The venue runs a segregated, non‑custodial model with execution on TP ICAP’s platform and independent custody/settlement initially via Fidelity Digital Assets; it completed its first live trade in May 2023. Management discussed the digital‑asset business on the H1 2024 results call, noting a soft launch with market makers streaming prices and average weekly activity of roughly five million orders and 1,200 trades. The 2023 Annual Report also records completion of the firm’s first OTC crypto‑asset derivative trade and outlines partnerships supporting its digital‑assets strategy. ([tpicap.com](https://tpicap.com/tpicap/media/press-releases/2023/tp-icap-announces-first-live-trade-through-fusion-digital-assets?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 is assigned because all evidenced exposure stems from TP ICAP operating and monetizing a regulated trading venue and related broking services for digital assets (execution, liquidity aggregation, and connectivity to custodians). There is no indication of proprietary crypto holdings or mining, and the venue’s non‑custodial design implies client assets remain with third‑party custodians rather than on TP ICAP’s balance sheet. ([tpicap.com](https://tpicap.com/media/press-releases/2022/tp-icap-receives-fca-registration-wholesale-cryptoasset-exchange?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Disclosures indicate that digital‑assets activities were in launch/early‑scale phase through 2023–2024; the firm does not break out a separate crypto revenue line, suggesting a minor contribution within group revenues. Given the non‑custodial setup and absence of disclosed treasury holdings, AssetShare is coded 0. RevShare is coded 1 (<~5%) pending any future segment disclosure. ([tpicap-media.com](https://tpicap-media.com/media/mkhfuxuf/tp-icap-half-year-results-transcript.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 7, 2024 (H1 2024 results), transcript: “Before I talk about these markets, I’d like to update you on our digital asset business. During the first half, we successfully soft launched our digital asset venue… Our exchange processes an average of five million orders and 1,200 trades a week.” — [TP ICAP H1 2024 transcript PDF](https://tpicap-media.com/media/mkhfuxuf/tp-icap-half-year-results-transcript.pdf) ([tpicap-media.com](https://tpicap-media.com/media/mkhfuxuf/tp-icap-half-year-results-transcript.pdf))  
- Mar 15, 2022 (Preliminary results 2021), transcript: “We brokered our first Bitcoin … trade in 2019… In 2021, we announced plans to launch a wholesale spot trading venue for digital assets… expected to be live later this year subject to FCA registration.” — [TP ICAP 2021 results transcript PDF](https://tpicap.com/tpicap/sites/g/files/escbpb106/files/2022-03/TP%20ICAP%20Preliminary%20results%202021%20%E2%80%93%20Presentation%20Transcript_0.pdf) ([tpicap.com](https://tpicap.com/tpicap/sites/g/files/escbpb106/files/2022-03/TP%20ICAP%20Preliminary%20results%202021%20%E2%80%93%20Presentation%20Transcript_0.pdf))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- 2023 Annual Report: “2023 saw TP ICAP complete its first OTC crypto asset derivative trade… partnership to build a digital assets marketplace with Flow Traders, Fidelity Digital Assets and Zodia Custody.” — [Annual Report 2023 PDF](https://www.directorstalkinterviews.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/TP-ICAP-Annual-Report-2023.pdf) ([directorstalkinterviews.com](https://www.directorstalkinterviews.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/TP-ICAP-Annual-Report-2023.pdf))  
- Dec 1, 2022: Fusion Digital Assets registered as an FCA cryptoasset exchange provider; venue design (non‑custodial execution plus independent custodian). — [TP ICAP press release](https://tpicap.com/media/press-releases/2022/tp-icap-receives-fca-registration-wholesale-cryptoasset-exchange) ([tpicap.com](https://tpicap.com/media/press-releases/2022/tp-icap-receives-fca-registration-wholesale-cryptoasset-exchange?utm_source=openai))  
- May 24, 2023: First live trade on Fusion Digital Assets; venue operates a segregated, non‑custodial model with Fidelity Digital Assets. — [TP ICAP press release](https://tpicap.com/tpicap/media/press-releases/2023/tp-icap-announces-first-live-trade-through-fusion-digital-assets) ([tpicap.com](https://tpicap.com/tpicap/media/press-releases/2023/tp-icap-announces-first-live-trade-through-fusion-digital-assets?utm_source=openai))  
- Jun 29, 2021: Announcement to launch cryptoasset trading platform with Fidelity Digital Assets, Zodia Custody and Flow Traders. — [TP ICAP press release](https://tpicap.com/tpicap/media/press-releases/2021/tp-icap-launch-cryptoasset-trading-platform-collaboration-fidelity-digital-assets-zodia-custody-and-flow-traders) ([tpicap.com](https://tpicap.com/tpicap/media/press-releases/2021/tp-icap-launch-cryptoasset-trading-platform-collaboration-fidelity-digital-assets-zodia-custody-and-flow-traders?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**

- Jun 29, 2021: Report on TP ICAP’s crypto trading effort with Fidelity and StanChart’s Zodia Custody. — [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2021-06-29/icap-to-launch-crypto-trading-platform-with-fidelity-stanchart) ([bloomberg.com](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2021-06-29/icap-to-launch-crypto-trading-platform-with-fidelity-stanchart?utm_source=openai))  
- May 25, 2023: Article on first live trade on TP ICAP’s new digital‑assets platform. — [GlobalTrading](https://www.globaltrading.net/tp-icap-executes-first-live-trade-on-new-digital-assets-platform/) ([globaltrading.net](https://www.globaltrading.net/tp-icap-executes-first-live-trade-on-new-digital-assets-platform/?utm_source=openai))

## BOC HONG KONG HOLDINGS

* Rank: 219 | Expo: 0.23 | id_str: HK2388011192
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Annual Report 2023; 2024 interim results analyst briefing transcript

### Firm Overview
BOC Hong Kong (Holdings) Limited is a leading Hong Kong banking group listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, operating retail, commercial and treasury businesses locally and across Southeast Asia. Management disclosures emphasize RMB clearing leadership and participation in central‑bank digital currency pilots (e‑HKD, mBridge) and tokenized bond initiatives, but do not describe proprietary cryptocurrency services. ([hkexnews.hk](https://www.hkexnews.hk/listedco/listconews/sehk/2024/0328/2024032800800.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across the 2017–2024 sample, public filings and management briefings do not indicate that BOCHK holds cryptocurrencies on balance sheet, operates mining, or offers direct crypto trading, custody, or brokerage of cryptoassets. Disclosures instead reference CBDC pilots (e‑HKD), cross‑border e‑CNY promotion, and support roles in tokenized green bond issuance—activities that are outside the project’s cryptocurrency mechanisms. ([bochk.com](https://www.bochk.com/dam/bochk/desktop/top/aboutus/ir/docs/presentations/2024ir/transcript_en.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Unclass = 1.0 is assigned because all identified “digital asset” activity relates to CBDCs and tokenized securities infrastructure rather than cryptocurrency custody, exchange, mining, treasury holdings, or monetized crypto financial products. No substantive evidence within 2017–2024 shows BOCHK directly handling or distributing cryptoassets under this project’s definitions. ([bochk.com](https://www.bochk.com/dam/bochk/desktop/top/aboutus/ir/docs/presentations/2024ir/transcript_en.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Hong Kong regulators have encouraged banks to serve licensed virtual‑asset firms, and some peers distribute crypto‑linked ETFs; however, no BOCHK‑specific evidence of crypto product distribution or custody was found in 2017–2024 sources. BOCHK’s mentions of e‑HKD, mBridge, and tokenized green bonds are retained as non‑crypto context and excluded from mechanism weights. ([fortune.com](https://fortune.com/crypto/2023/06/15/hong-kong-bank-of-china-hsbc-standard-chartered-crypto-exchanges/?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 29, 2024 (Interim 2024), transcript: “We engaged deeply in the e‑HKD Pilot Programme under the HKMA, became a founding member of the Project Ensemble Architecture Community, and vigorously promoted the implementation of cross‑border central bank digital currency projects.” — [BOCHK 2024 Interim Results transcript PDF](https://www.bochk.com/dam/bochk/desktop/top/aboutus/ir/docs/presentations/2024ir/transcript_en.pdf) ([bochk.com](https://www.bochk.com/dam/bochk/desktop/top/aboutus/ir/docs/presentations/2024ir/transcript_en.pdf))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Mar 28, 2024: Annual Report 2023 — participation in e‑HKD pilot and role as custodian for the HKSAR Government’s digital green bond issuance noted. — [BOC Hong Kong (Holdings) Annual Report 2023 PDF](https://www.hkexnews.hk/listedco/listconews/sehk/2024/0328/2024032800800.pdf) ([hkexnews.hk](https://www.hkexnews.hk/listedco/listconews/sehk/2024/0328/2024032800800.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**
- Jun 15, 2023: Report that HKMA urged banks, including Bank of China, HSBC and Standard Chartered, to accommodate licensed crypto exchanges (sector context; not a BOCHK product disclosure). — [Fortune coverage](https://fortune.com/crypto/2023/06/15/hong-kong-bank-of-china-hsbc-standard-chartered-crypto-exchanges/) ([fortune.com](https://fortune.com/crypto/2023/06/15/hong-kong-bank-of-china-hsbc-standard-chartered-crypto-exchanges/?utm_source=openai))
