## APPLOVIN A

* Rank: 220 | Expo: 0.23 | id_str: US03831W1080
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (FY2023); Q3 2023 and Q4 2022 earnings calls; MoPub acquisition press release and coverage

### Firm Overview
AppLovin Corporation is a U.S.-based advertising-technology and marketing software company focused on helping mobile app developers acquire users and monetize apps, anchored by products such as AppDiscovery, MAX mediation, and the AXON machine-learning engine, with expansion into connected TV via its Wurl acquisition. Filings and calls in 2021–2024 consistently frame the business around adtech/software rather than financial services or balance sheet investing. ([s21.q4cdn.com](https://s21.q4cdn.com/165405286/files/doc_financials/2023/ar/AppLovin-10K-2023.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024, public filings and earnings calls show no evidence that AppLovin mines cryptocurrencies, holds crypto as treasury assets, or operates crypto exchanges, custody, payments, or investment products. The FY2023 Form 10-K mentions potential expansion into “blockchain-related markets” only as a general future opportunity within a long list of adjacencies, with no related product launches or assets disclosed in the sample period. Transcripts emphasize adtech performance marketing (AXON/AppDiscovery), MAX mediation and CTV, not crypto services. ([s21.q4cdn.com](https://s21.q4cdn.com/165405286/files/doc_financials/2023/ar/AppLovin-10K-2023.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Unclass = 1.0 is assigned because available evidence indicates no substantive, monetized cryptocurrency exposure via treasury holdings, mining, financial services, non‑financial crypto services, or mining/infrastructure supply. Management and filings focus on advertising technology and connected‑TV initiatives; the only crypto‑adjacent reference is a generic risk‑factor note about “blockchain‑related markets,” which does not meet the threshold for a non‑zero mechanism weight. ([s21.q4cdn.com](https://s21.q4cdn.com/165405286/files/doc_financials/2023/ar/AppLovin-10K-2023.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Management once referenced “metaverse and blockchain” conceptually (Q3 2021), and the FY2023 10‑K lists “blockchain‑related markets” among many possible adjacencies; however, no concrete crypto product, custody, or asset holding was identified in 2017–2024 sources. AppLovin’s ad platforms could, in principle, serve advertisers from crypto sectors, but there is no evidence of the firm directly handling digital assets, so this is excluded under project rules. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/11/applovin-corporation-app-q3-2021-earnings-call-tra/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 8, 2023 (Q3 2023), transcript: “This quarter's record‑breaking performance is a testament to the success of our new AI‑based advertising technology, AXON 2… integrating AXON 2 into our CTV initiative.” — [Barchart (syndicated Motley Fool) transcript](https://www.barchart.com/story/news/21852332/applovin-app-q3-2023-earnings-call-transcript) ([barchart.com](https://www.barchart.com/story/news/21852332/applovin-app-q3-2023-earnings-call-transcript))  
- Feb 8, 2023 (Q4 2022), transcript: “We're a performance marketing business at its core… The Connected TV device is really exciting for us…” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2023/02/08/applovin-app-q4-2022-earnings-call-transcript/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2023/02/08/applovin-app-q4-2022-earnings-call-transcript/))  
- Nov 10, 2021 (Q3 2021), transcript: discussion of MAX/MoPub scale; exploratory remark about “metaverse and blockchain” as potential adjacencies. — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/11/applovin-corporation-app-q3-2021-earnings-call-tra/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/11/applovin-corporation-app-q3-2021-earnings-call-tra/))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Feb 26, 2024: Form 10‑K (FY2023) — core adtech focus; generic reference to potential “blockchain‑related markets” among future opportunities. — [AppLovin 2023 Form 10‑K PDF](https://s21.q4cdn.com/165405286/files/doc_financials/2023/ar/AppLovin-10K-2023.pdf) ([s21.q4cdn.com](https://s21.q4cdn.com/165405286/files/doc_financials/2023/ar/AppLovin-10K-2023.pdf))  
- Jan 3, 2022: Completion of MoPub acquisition, integrating features into MAX mediation. — [AppLovin press release](https://investors.applovin.com/news/news-details/2022/AppLovin-Closes-Acquisition-of-Twitters-MoPub-Business/default.aspx) ([investors.applovin.com](https://investors.applovin.com/news/news-details/2022/AppLovin-Closes-Acquisition-of-Twitters-MoPub-Business/default.aspx?utm_source=openai))

## MONEYONMOBILE

* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Form 10-K (FY2017); Q1 2018 earnings call (Aug 16, 2017); 2018 PR Newswire press releases

### Firm Overview
MoneyOnMobile, Inc. was a U.S.-listed mobile payments firm with primary operations in India, offering consumers the ability to pay for goods and services and remit funds via a large agent/retailer network using basic mobile/SMS functionality. Its filings describe a focus on domestic remittance, mobile/TV top-ups, and a lightweight “MOM ATM” cash‑out solution, not digital-asset services. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1414628/000162828017006916/momt201710-k.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across available 2017–2018 sources, there is no evidence that the company mined cryptocurrencies, held crypto as a treasury asset, or operated crypto exchanges, custody, investment products, or crypto payment processing. Management discussion on the Aug 16, 2017 earnings call centers on domestic remittance, mobile/TV top‑ups, and MOM ATM deployment and economics, with no blockchain/crypto references. Public press releases in 2018 similarly highlight payments partnerships, product launches (e.g., biometric MOM ATM), and capital raises, not crypto activities. ([preview.irdirect.net](https://preview.irdirect.net/template_files/1083/event-materials/2017-08-16/transcript.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of substantive crypto activity in filings, transcripts, or press during the 2017–2024 sample window, the firm is classified as Unclassified (unclass = 1.0). No mechanism category (holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, infrastr) is supported by evidence, so each is assigned 0.0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1414628/000162828017006916/momt201710-k.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
MoneyOnMobile appears to have limited coverage and investor materials beyond 2018, and only one in-sample earnings call transcript was located; if additional transcripts existed, they were not readily retrievable. A scheduled Q3 FY2018 earnings call was announced via Accesswire, but no transcript was found online. None of the reviewed sources indicated crypto involvement. ([accessnewswire.com](https://www.accessnewswire.com/newsroom/en/earnings/moneyonmobile-inc.-to-host-earnings-call-489717))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**

- Aug 16, 2017 (Q1 FY2018) transcript: “the retailer becomes a cash recycling point… MoneyOnMobile is the settlement network crediting or debiting the respective amounts…” — [MoneyOnMobile IR transcript (PDF)](https://preview.irdirect.net/template_files/1083/event-materials/2017-08-16/transcript.pdf)

## NAOS SMALL CAP OPPORTUNITIES COMPANY

* Rank: 222 | Expo: 0.22 | id_str: AU000000NSC4
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0

* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Annual Report 2024; Q4 FY2024 investor update deck; Q2 FY2018 investor update call deck; MarketIndex company snapshot

### Firm Overview
NAOS Small Cap Opportunities Company Limited is an Australian listed investment company (ASX: NSC) that provides concentrated exposure to Australian and New Zealand emerging companies and aims to deliver a sustainable stream of fully franked dividends. The company is externally managed by NAOS Asset Management and invests in a concentrated portfolio of small-cap industrials rather than operating financial or technology infrastructure itself. ([company-announcements.afr.com](https://company-announcements.afr.com/asx/nsc/38b8bab5-600b-11ef-beba-3ea1a61ffeaf.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024 materials, NSC is described as a listed investment company investing in small-cap equities; there is no indication it mines cryptocurrencies, holds crypto as a treasury asset, or operates crypto exchanges, custody, or investment products. The FY2024 Annual Report outlines objectives focused on long-term, concentrated exposure to Australian public emerging companies and dividend delivery, with no references to bitcoin, crypto, or digital assets in the report. Investor update decks similarly emphasize long-term equity investing in emerging companies and portfolio composition by industry, not digital-asset activities. A third-party snapshot likewise characterizes NSC simply as a listed investment company providing exposure to small-cap companies. ([company-announcements.afr.com](https://company-announcements.afr.com/asx/nsc/38b8bab5-600b-11ef-beba-3ea1a61ffeaf.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of substantive crypto activity in filings, investor presentations, or credible news during the 2017–2024 window, NSC is classified as Unclassified (unclass = 1.0). No evidence supports allocation to holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr, as the firm’s business is investing in listed equities rather than engaging in crypto operations or providing crypto-focused financial or infrastructure services. ([company-announcements.afr.com](https://company-announcements.afr.com/asx/nsc/38b8bab5-600b-11ef-beba-3ea1a61ffeaf.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
No conventional earnings call transcripts were located; NSC typically provides investor update decks and ASX-announced presentation materials rather than verbatim call transcripts. While portfolio companies could themselves have varying exposures over time, this framework classifies only NSC’s direct mechanisms; indirect exposure via investee firms does not meet category thresholds. ([company-announcements.afr.com](https://company-announcements.afr.com/asx/nsc/e390d6fa-4f8b-11ef-a765-2af604e39195.pdf))

### Evidence

**Earnings calls:**

## ASPEN TECHNOLOGY DEAD - DELIST.13/03/25

* Rank: 223 | Expo: 0.22 | id_str: US29109X1063
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (FY2021, FY2023); Q4 FY2021 earnings call

### Firm Overview
Aspen Technology, Inc. (AspenTech) was a U.S.-based industrial software company (Nasdaq: AZPN through 2024) that provided process modeling, asset optimization, and industrial AI software for asset‑intensive sectors such as energy, chemicals, and manufacturing. In May 2022, “new AspenTech” was formed when Emerson Electric combined two of its industrial software businesses with AspenTech, while AspenTech continued to operate as an industrial software provider. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/929940/000092994021000060/azpn-20210630.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across earnings call transcripts and SEC filings from 2017–2024, AspenTech’s business focus remains enterprise software for process industries (engineering design, manufacturing and supply chain, and asset performance management), with management commentary centered on operational efficiency and sustainability. No sources indicate the firm held cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet, mined digital assets, ran crypto exchanges/custody/payment products, or supplied mining‑specific hardware/energy infrastructure. The documents reviewed consistently describe software solutions (e.g., Aspen Mtell, hybrid modeling, “self‑optimizing plant”) rather than crypto‑asset activities. ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/nasdaq/azpn/investor-relations/earnings-call/q4-2021))

### Weight Assignment
With no substantive evidence of crypto holdings, mining, crypto financial services, non‑financial crypto services, or crypto‑targeted infrastructure during 2017–2024, AspenTech is classified as Unclassified (unclass = 1.0). The firm’s exposure channels are squarely enterprise software for industrial customers, which does not meet any crypto mechanism category thresholds under this framework. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/929940/000092994021000060/azpn-20210630.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The input label notes delisting on March 13, 2025 following Emerson’s subsequent buyout; that event lies outside the January 2017–December 2024 sample window and does not affect the classification. Management examples referencing “mining” pertain to metals/mining customers using APM software, not crypto‑asset mining. None identified that would change the mechanism assignment. ([aspentech.com](https://www.aspentech.com/-/media/aspentech/home/resources/press-release/pdfs/fy22/aspentech-completes-emerson-transaction-expanding-high-performance-global-industrial-software.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 11, 2021 (Q4 FY2021): “AspenTech solutions are very well aligned to the chemicals customers’ need for greater operational efficiency and sustainability.” — [AlphaSpread (Q4‑2021 transcript)](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/nasdaq/azpn/investor-relations/earnings-call/q4-2021) ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/nasdaq/azpn/investor-relations/earnings-call/q4-2021))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Aug 18, 2021: Form 10‑K — Company description and business overview; no references to bitcoin/cryptocurrency/blockchain. — [SEC EDGAR (FY2021 10‑K)](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/929940/000092994021000060/azpn-20210630.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/929940/000092994021000060/azpn-20210630.htm))
- Aug 2023: Form 10‑K — “new AspenTech” post‑Emerson transaction; no crypto references in business description or risk factors. — [SEC EDGAR (FY2023 10‑K PDF)](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1897982/000189798223000070/azpn2023063010k1024442pm.pdf) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1897982/000189798223000070/azpn2023063010k1024442pm.pdf))
- May 16, 2022: “AspenTech Completes Emerson Transaction” — transaction context and business scope. — [AspenTech press release (PDF)](https://www.aspentech.com/-/media/aspentech/home/resources/press-release/pdfs/fy22/aspentech-completes-emerson-transaction-expanding-high-performance-global-industrial-software.pdf) ([aspentech.com](https://www.aspentech.com/-/media/aspentech/home/resources/press-release/pdfs/fy22/aspentech-completes-emerson-transaction-expanding-high-performance-global-industrial-software.pdf?utm_source=openai))

## GEVO

* Rank: 224 | Expo: 0.22 | id_str: US3743964062
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0

* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (FY2023); Q2 2022 and Q3 2021 earnings calls; company press releases (2021–2024)

### Firm Overview
Gevo, Inc. is a U.S.-based renewable fuels and chemicals company headquartered in Colorado and listed on Nasdaq (GEVO). Its business centers on producing sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and other low‑carbon hydrocarbons through its Net‑Zero projects and related platforms. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1392380/000139238024000008/gevo-20231231x10k.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across multiple earnings calls and the FY2023 Form 10‑K, management discusses SAF production, offtake agreements with airlines, renewable natural gas, and project financing; there is no indication of cryptocurrency holdings, mining, or crypto financial services. The FY2023 10‑K references using distributed ledger technology (“blockchain”) within the Verity platform to document sustainability attributes and chain‑of‑custody, but this is an enterprise tracking application and does not involve transacting in or holding crypto‑assets. No sources indicate that Gevo runs a crypto exchange/custody service, supplies mining‑specific hardware/hosting/energy, or otherwise provides crypto‑focused services. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/08/08/gevo-inc-gevo-q2-2022-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of any substantive crypto‑asset activity in transcripts, filings, or credible news during the January 2017–December 2024 window, Gevo is classified as Unclassified (unclass = 1.0). Mentions of blockchain in the 10‑K relate to internal product‑tracking (Verity) and do not meet any mechanism category (holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, infrastr) under this framework. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1392380/000139238024000008/gevo-20231231x10k.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The company’s 10‑K mentions applying “Distributed Ledger Technology, commonly referred to as blockchain” in its Verity platform; per our rules, this non‑crypto enterprise use is not a crypto exposure channel. No earnings‑call or filing evidence suggests crypto holdings, mining, or crypto financial services during 2017–2024. None beyond this identified. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1392380/000139238024000008/gevo-20231231x10k.htm))

- Aug 8, 2022 (Q2 2022), Motley Fool: “We announced two new SAF supply agreements… Delta… British Airways… [and] additional… with American Airlines, Alaska Airlines, Japan Airlines, Finnair, and Aer Lingus.” — [Motley Fool transcript] (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/08/08/gevo-inc-gevo-q2-2022-earnings-call-transcript/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/08/08/gevo-inc-gevo-q2-2022-earnings-call-transcript/))

## MIZUHO FINL.GP.

* Rank: 225 | Expo: 0.21 | id_str: JP3885780001
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Financial results/IR Q&A summaries (2017–2024); Form 20‑F (FY2018, filed 2019); Mizuho Bank press release (2019); Mizuho Securities materials (2023); financial news (2019)

### Firm Overview
Mizuho Financial Group, Inc. is one of Japan’s largest financial institutions, operating through banking, trust banking, and securities subsidiaries. Within the review period, it emphasized digital transformation and payments initiatives such as J‑Coin Pay, but it did not operate a cryptocurrency exchange, custody platform, or mining business based on public materials.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across IR day Q&A summaries and financial results materials, management discusses digital banking and the J‑Coin Pay payment service; J‑Coin is positioned as a bank-linked electronic money/wallet rather than a crypto‑asset. Independent reporting contemporaneous with launch clarified that J‑Coin does not use cryptocurrency or blockchain technology. Group securities operations have participated in Japan’s security token market and tokenization platforms, but these relate to digitized traditional securities on distributed ledgers rather than custody, trading, or issuance of crypto‑assets.

### Weight Assignment
No credible evidence from 2017–2024 indicates that Mizuho Financial Group held crypto on balance sheet, mined crypto, or provided crypto financial services (exchange, custody, crypto investment products, or crypto payments using on‑chain assets). J‑Coin Pay is not a cryptocurrency, and the group’s involvement with security tokens pertains to tokenized traditional instruments. Therefore, the company is classified as Unclassified (unclass = 1.0) under the project’s rules.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Some transcripts are gated on third‑party portals; however, Mizuho’s own IR Q&A materials are available and reference J‑Coin without implying crypto‑asset usage. Mizuho Securities’ participation in security token offerings and tokenization platforms is noted but treated as outside scope for crypto‑asset exposure under this framework. None beyond these identified.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Sep 2021 (IR Day 2021) — Q&A: “What measures do you think are necessary to increase the business value of J‑Coin? … we hope to increase the number of J‑Coin users in the future.” — [Mizuho IR Day 2021: Summary of Q&A (PDF)](https://www.mizuhogroup.com/binaries/content/assets/pdf/mizuhoglobal/investors/financial-information/ir-information/briefing/202109_3.pdf) ([mizuhogroup.com](https://www.mizuhogroup.com/binaries/content/assets/pdf/mizuhoglobal/investors/financial-information/ir-information/briefing/202109_3.pdf))

- Feb 20, 2019: “J‑Coin Pay to launch in March 2019” — official service announcement. — [Mizuho Bank press release (PDF)](https://www.mizuhogroup.com/binaries/content/assets/pdf/mizuho-bank/news/2019/02/20190220release_eng.pdf) ([mizuhogroup.com](https://www.mizuhogroup.com/binaries/content/assets/pdf/mizuho-bank/news/2019/02/20190220release_eng.pdf?utm_source=openai))

## SAMSUNG ELECTRONICS

* Rank: 226 | Expo: 0.21 | id_str: KR7005930003
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.4, infrastr 0.6, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Samsung official product/developer pages (2019–2021); financial news (2018); industry press (2023); MicroBT press release (2023)

### Firm Overview
Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd. is a South Korea–based global manufacturer of semiconductors, smartphones and consumer electronics listed on the Korea Exchange. Within 2017–2024 it both manufactured application‑specific integrated circuits (ASICs) for third‑party Bitcoin mining hardware through its foundry business and embedded a non‑custodial blockchain wallet/keystore in certain Galaxy devices. ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2018/01/31/samsung-cryptocurrency-mining-semiconductor-chips.html?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Infrastructure: Multiple credible reports and teardowns confirm Samsung Foundry manufactured ASICs used in Bitcoin miners, initially acknowledged in 2018 and later identified at 3 nm in MicroBT’s WhatsMiner lineup (with Samsung executives appearing at the product debut), which positions Samsung as an upstream chip supplier to mining OEMs. This fits the infrastructure category as it provides mining‑targeted silicon rather than operating miners itself. ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2018/01/31/samsung-cryptocurrency-mining-semiconductor-chips.html?utm_source=openai))
Non‑financial business services: On the device/software side, Samsung released the Samsung Blockchain Wallet/Keystore and a developer SDK, enabling on‑device key management and DApp integration for users (including later support for Ledger hardware wallets). Samsung does not custody assets or operate an exchange, so this falls under non‑financial services to the crypto ecosystem rather than financial services. ([samsung.com](https://www.samsung.com/us/apps/samsung-blockchain/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence is strongest for infrastructure exposure given the foundry’s role in fabricating Bitcoin‑mining ASICs from 2018 onward and again in 2023 with 3 nm devices, while the Galaxy wallet/keystore and SDK represent a sustained but ancillary non‑financial enablement of crypto usage. Balancing frequency and materiality signals, weights are assigned as infrastr = 0.6 and bizserv = 0.4; holders, miners and finserv remain 0.0, and unclass = 0.0. ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2018/01/31/samsung-cryptocurrency-mining-semiconductor-chips.html?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Public, accessible earnings‑call transcripts with crypto‑specific remarks could not be retrieved despite search; classification therefore relies on official product pages and reputable news/industry sources and the certainty score is capped at 2 per protocol. Foundry revenue attributable to mining ASICs is not separately disclosed and likely intermittent; Galaxy blockchain features also vary by market/carrier and are not presented as a monetized financial service. ([samsung.com](https://www.samsung.com/global/ir/?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Notice of earnings conference calls and webcast access (2019–2024 framework; transcripts not posted publicly on IR site). — [Samsung IR “Investor Relations” hub](https://www.samsung.com/global/ir/) ([samsung.com](https://www.samsung.com/global/ir/?utm_source=openai))

**Filings / company materials / product documentation:**
- Samsung Blockchain Wallet app overview (non‑custodial wallet for virtual assets on Galaxy). — [Samsung Apps & Services](https://www.samsung.com/us/apps/samsung-blockchain/) ([samsung.com](https://www.samsung.com/us/apps/samsung-blockchain/?utm_source=openai))
- “Samsung Blockchain Wallet adds hardware wallet support (Ledger Nano S/Nano X).” — [Samsung Business Korea news post, May 14, 2021](https://www.samsung.com/sec/business/insights/news/news-20210514/) ([samsung.com](https://www.samsung.com/sec/business/insights/news/news-20210514/?utm_source=openai))
- Samsung Developer: Blockchain platform/SDK resources for integrating DApps and key management. — [Samsung Developers – Blockchain](https://developer.samsung.com/blockchain) ([developer.samsung.com](https://developer.samsung.com/blockchain?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / industry sources:**

## COSCO SHIPPING PORTS

* Rank: 227 | Expo: 0.21 | id_str: BMG2442N1048
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Annual reports (2017–2024), Interim Report 2024, IR webcasts

### Firm Overview
COSCO SHIPPING Ports Limited is a Hong Kong–listed global port logistics service provider operating and managing container terminals across China and multiple overseas regions. Company filings describe a portfolio of hundreds of berths and emphasize terminal operations, supply‑chain services, and “digital intelligence” initiatives rather than financial products or asset investments. ([doc.irasia.com](https://doc.irasia.com/listco/hk/coscoship/interim/2024/intrep.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across interim and annual reports reviewed within the 2017–2024 window, there are no disclosures of cryptocurrency holdings, mining, exchange/brokerage, custody, or other crypto financial services. References to “blockchain” appear only in the context of smart‑port digitization (e.g., 5G, AI, blockchain and big data) rather than cryptoassets. COSCO SHIPPING Ports is named among terminal‑operator members of the Global Shipping Business Network (GSBN), a permissioned enterprise blockchain consortium to digitize shipping processes; this is not a cryptoasset activity and does not imply custody, trading, or investment in digital currencies. ([doc.irasia.com](https://doc.irasia.com/listco/hk/coscoship/interim/2024/intrep.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of substantive cryptoasset exposure in filings and investor materials, and with any blockchain mentions confined to enterprise process digitization (not cryptocurrency), the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0. All mechanism categories (holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, infrastr) are assigned 0.0 in line with the exclusion rules for generic blockchain adoption unconnected to crypto markets. ([doc.irasia.com](https://doc.irasia.com/listco/hk/coscoship/interim/2024/intrep.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Earnings call/webcast listings are available via the IR site, but publicly accessible verbatim earnings call transcripts were not located; nonetheless, the finding here is an explicit “no meaningful crypto exposure” based on multiple company reports. GSBN membership and smart‑port references involve enterprise IT digitization, not crypto financial services or asset holdings. ([ports.coscoshipping.com](https://ports.coscoshipping.com/en/Investors/IRHome/Webcasts/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Filings / reports:**
- Interim Report 2024 (company profile, operations focus; no cryptoasset disclosures). — [COSCO SHIPPING Ports Interim Report 2024 (PDF)](https://doc.irasia.com/listco/hk/coscoship/interim/2024/intrep.pdf) ([doc.irasia.com](https://doc.irasia.com/listco/hk/coscoship/interim/2024/intrep.pdf))
- Annual Report 2023 excerpt (Chairman’s Statement on digital/green transformation; no crypto products). — [HKEXnews filing (PDF)](https://www.hkexnews.hk/listedco/listconews/sehk/2024/0417/11162805/2024041700510.pdf) ([hkexnews.hk](https://www.hkexnews.hk/listedco/listconews/sehk/2024/0417/11162805/2024041700510.pdf))
- Annual Report 2022 (smart‑port section: “applications such as 5G, artificial intelligence, blockchain and big data”). — [Company annual report 2022 (PDF)](https://doc.irasia.com/listco/hk/coscoship/annual/2022/ar2022.pdf) ([doc.irasia.com](https://doc.irasia.com/listco/hk/coscoship/annual/2022/ar2022.pdf))
- Financial reports archive (2017–2024 reports listed). — [Investor Relations: Financial Reports](https://ports.coscoshipping.com/en/Investors/IRHome/FinancialReports/) ([ports.coscoshipping.com](https://ports.coscoshipping.com/en/Investors/IRHome/FinancialReports/))

**Investor relations / results:**
- Webcasts archive (2017–2025 interim/final results announcements). — [IR Webcasts](https://ports.coscoshipping.com/en/Investors/IRHome/Webcasts/) ([ports.coscoshipping.com](https://ports.coscoshipping.com/en/Investors/IRHome/Webcasts/))

**Industry/consortium context (enterprise blockchain, not crypto):**

## HONG KONG EXCHANGES AND CLEARING

* Rank: 228 | Expo: 0.21 | id_str: HK0388045442
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: HKEX news releases (Dec 2022; Apr 30, 2024), SEHK participant circulars (Apr 2024), Annual Report/market disclosures, third‑party coverage of first‑day ETF trading volumes (Apr 2024)

### Firm Overview
Hong Kong Exchanges and Clearing Limited (HKEX) is the listed operator of Hong Kong’s securities and derivatives exchanges and associated clearing houses, and the owner of the London Metal Exchange. Its role in crypto is via exchange listing, trading and clearing of exchange‑traded products that reference digital assets.

### Exposure Channels Identified
HKEX facilitates crypto exposure by admitting and supporting trading in crypto‑linked exchange‑traded products. Asia’s first crypto‑asset futures ETFs listed in Hong Kong in December 2022, and on April 30, 2024 HKEX welcomed the listing of Asia’s first spot virtual asset (Bitcoin and Ether) ETFs, adding to Hong Kong’s ETP product set. In October 2024 HKEX also announced the HKEX Virtual Asset Index Series to provide Bitcoin and Ether reference benchmarks for the region. HKEX does not issue these ETFs or custody the underlying coins; its exposure is as the exchange and clearing venue for the securities. ([HKEX 2022 Year in Review]; [HKEX news release, Apr 30, 2024]; [HKEX Virtual Asset Index Series, Oct 28, 2024]) ([hkexgroup.com](https://www.hkexgroup.com/Media-Centre/News-Release/HKEX-Group/2022/221220news?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence shows a clear, direct role in crypto financial services through listing and enabling trading/clearing of crypto‑linked ETFs; therefore finserv is assigned 1.0. There is no indication of proprietary crypto holdings (holders = 0.0), mining activity (miners = 0.0), non‑financial services to the crypto ecosystem (bizserv = 0.0), or provision of mining hardware/compute/energy (infrastr = 0.0). Given the strong linkage is via crypto investment products on the exchange, and that first‑day/ongoing trading volumes are reported using HKEX data, the finserv classification is appropriate. ([SEHK circular preparing for spot VA ETF listing, Apr 17, 2024]; [The Block first‑day trading volumes citing HKEX data]) ([hkex.com.hk](https://www.hkex.com.hk/-/media/HKEX-Market/Services/Circulars-and-Notices/Participant-and-Members-Circulars/SEHK/2024/ETP_00124_e.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Public, verbatim earnings call transcripts could not be located; HKEX typically posts webcasts and presentation decks without transcript text, so certainty is capped at 2 as required. The exchange’s role is limited to securities‑market infrastructure for crypto‑linked products (and VA indices from late 2024); it does not itself operate a crypto‑asset trading platform for spot coins or provide custody, which has been noted in the classification. ([HKEX Financial Results and Presentations page]; [HKEX news releases and circulars]) ([hkexgroup.com](https://www.hkexgroup.com/Investor-Relations/Financial-Results-and-Presentations/2021?sc_lang=en))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls / results materials:**
- 2021 Annual Results Webcast (English only) — [HKEX IR (webcast)](https://edge.media-server.com/mmc/p/irqum6d8) (no public transcript located). — [HKEX IR results page](https://www.hkexgroup.com/Investor-Relations/Financial-Results-and-Presentations/2021?sc_lang=en)

**Filings / reports / market disclosures:**
- “HKEX in 2022: Year in Review” — notes Hong Kong “welcoming two of Asia’s first crypto asset ETFs in December 2022.” — [HKEX](https://www.hkexgroup.com/Media-Centre/News-Release/HKEX-Group/2022/221220news)
- HKSCC PFMI Disclosure 2023 — records “December 2022 – HKEX introduced Asia’s first crypto asset ETFs.” — [HKEX (PDF)](https://www.hkex.com.hk/-/media/HKEX-Market/Services/Clearing/Securities/PFMI/HKSCC_PFMI_Disclosure_2023.pdf)
- SEHK Participant Circular ETP/001/24 (Apr 17, 2024) — operational guidance “in preparation for the listing of spot virtual asset ETFs.” — [SEHK (PDF)](https://www.hkex.com.hk/-/media/HKEX-Market/Services/Circulars-and-Notices/Participant-and-Members-Circulars/SEHK/2024/ETP_00124_e.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- “HKEX Welcomes Asia’s First Spot Virtual Asset ETFs” (Apr 30, 2024). — [HKEX news release](https://www.hkex.com.hk/News/News-Release/2024/240430news)
- “Hong Kong’s six spot bitcoin, ether ETFs see $11 million in volume on first trading day” (Apr 30, 2024) — cites HKEX trading data. — [The Block](https://www.theblock.co/post/291644/hong-kong-bitcoin-ether-etf-volume-first-day)
- “HKEX to Launch HKEX Virtual Asset Index Series” (Oct 28, 2024) — BTC/ETH reference indices for the region. — [HKEX](https://www.hkex.com.hk/News/News-Release/2024/241028news)
- “HKEX ETF Spotlight: Invest in the digital era with crypto asset ETFs” (Jan 2023) — product facts incl. Dec 16, 2022 listings. — [HKEX (PDF)](https://www.hkex.com.hk/-/media/HKEX-Market/Products/Securities/Exchange-Traded-Products/Launch/HKEX-ETF-Spotlight_January-2023_for-Hong-Kong-and-Singapore-investors_English_digital.pdf)

## ATRECA DEAD - DELIST.17/06/24

* Rank: 229 | Expo: 0.21 | id_str: US04965G1094
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: SEC 10-K (FY2022), Nasdaq/GlobeNewswire results materials (Mar 3, 2022), proxy and delisting notices (Mar–Apr 2024)

### Firm Overview
Atreca, Inc. was a U.S.-based clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on discovering and developing antibody-based therapeutics for solid tumors. Its activities centered on oncology research and development rather than financial services or technology infrastructure.

### Exposure Channels Identified
A review of Atreca’s filings and investor communications within the 2017–2024 window shows a pure‑play biopharmaceutical focus, with management discussion centered on clinical programs, R&D and financing. There is no indication of proprietary cryptocurrency holdings, mining operations, customer crypto services (exchange, custody, payments, or ETPs), or crypto‑specific hardware/hosting activity. The company also transitioned off Nasdaq in March 2024 amid a plan to sell assets and pursue dissolution, with no crypto activities disclosed during this process. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1532346/000155837023004916/bcel-20221231x10k.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of any substantive crypto references across SEC filings, earnings materials, or credible news coverage during 2017–2024, all mechanism categories (holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, infrastr) are set to 0.0 and the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0. This reflects Atreca’s strictly biotech operating model and lack of documented exposure channels to digital assets. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1532346/000155837023004916/bcel-20221231x10k.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- FY2022 Form 10‑K — Business overview: “We are a clinical‑stage biopharmaceutical company utilizing our differentiated platform to discover and develop novel antibody‑based therapeutics to treat a range of solid tumor types.” — [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1532346/000155837023004916/bcel-20221231x10k.htm)
- Mar 28–Apr 8, 2024 — DEFA14A: adjourned special meeting; proposals to sell substantially all assets and proceed to liquidation/dissolution. — [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1532346/000110465924040784/tm249781d2_defa14a.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- Mar 21, 2024 — “Change of Listing: ATRECA INC — from Nasdaq to OTC on March 18, 2024.” — [Solactive notice](https://legacy2.solactive.com/change-of-listing-atreca-inc-21st-march-2024/)

## SKILLZ A

* Rank: 230 | Expo: 0.21 | id_str: US83067L2088
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: SEC 10-K filings (FY2021, FY2023), Q2 2021 and Q3 2021 earnings call transcripts

### Firm Overview
Skillz Inc. is a U.S.-based mobile games competition platform that enables developers to run real-money skill-based tournaments and players to compete for cash prizes. Company filings describe the business as “building the competition layer of the internet” by reinventing competitive mobile gaming; disclosures focus on gaming, user acquisition, and platform technology rather than digital-asset activities. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1801661/000162828022004552/sklz-20211231.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
A review of earnings call transcripts in 2021 and SEC annual reports through FY2023 shows no substantive references to cryptocurrency holdings, mining, crypto custody/trading services, or blockchain/NFT product lines. Management commentary in the sampled calls centers on user acquisition (Aarki integration), content, platform features, and operating metrics, with no discussion of crypto-related initiatives. The company’s player “Z” currency is presented in support materials as an internal virtual currency for non-cash play, not a blockchain token. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/08/04/skillz-inc-sklz-q2-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of documented crypto exposure across transcripts (2017–2024 window sampled via 2021 calls) and SEC filings (FY2021–FY2023), no mechanism category is supported. Accordingly, all mechanism weights are set to 0.0 and the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0. No evidence was found for treasury crypto holdings (holders), mining operations (miners), customer-facing crypto financial services (finserv), crypto-adjacent business services (bizserv), or mining/crypto hardware and hosting (infrastr). ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/04/skillz-inc-sklz-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- “Z” virtual currency terminology could be confused with crypto tokens; available support materials indicate it is simply an internal game currency for non-cash use, not a digital asset on a blockchain. ([support.skillz.com](https://support.skillz.com/hc/en-us/articles/203685829-What-is-Z-?utm_source=openai))
- Public transcripts accessed for 2021 contain no crypto references; additional calls in later years within 2017–2024 were either not necessary for classification or not required once the classification was stable from filings and representative calls. None of the reviewed sources indicated crypto activities.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 3, 2021 (Q3 2021): “We…integrate advanced synchronous technology onto the Skillz platform through Exit Games’ Photon Engine…accelerated our ability to support popular first-person shooter and racing games.” — [The Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/04/skillz-inc-sklz-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcript/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/04/skillz-inc-sklz-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))
- Aug 3, 2021 (Q2 2021): “We think that the future of competition will be centralized in one place on the Internet…going beyond mobile…” — [The Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/08/04/skillz-inc-sklz-q2-2021-earnings-call-transcript/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/08/04/skillz-inc-sklz-q2-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- FY2023 Form 10‑K — Business overview emphasizes building a mobile gaming competition platform; no crypto-related lines disclosed. — [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1801661/000180166124000100/sklz-20231231.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1801661/000180166124000100/sklz-20231231.htm))
- FY2021 Form 10‑K — “We are building the competition layer of the internet by re‑inventing competitive mobile gaming.” — [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1801661/000162828022004552/sklz-20211231.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1801661/000162828022004552/sklz-20211231.htm))

**Financial news / other:**

## TXNM ENERGY

* Rank: 231 | Expo: 0.21 | id_str: US69349H1077
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.1, unclass 0.9
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: SEC 10-K filing (FY2023), Q4 2023 earnings call, Q3 2018 earnings call

### Firm Overview
TXNM Energy is a U.S. energy holding company for two regulated electric utilities: Public Service Company of New Mexico (PNM) and Texas-New Mexico Power Company (TN
…P). Through these regulated utilities, the company provides electric service in New Mexico and Texas; its core activities are transmission and distribution and regulated generation in New Mexico. In August 2024 the holding company changed its name from PNM Resources to TXNM Energy.

### Exposure Channels Identified
No evidence indicates that TXNM Energy holds cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet, runs mining operations, or provides custody/trading or other crypto financial services. However, management and investor materials explicitly note crypto-mining customers within TNMP’s West Texas service territory contributing to demand-based load growth, and TNMP describes transmission projects that accommodate load from “virtual currency mining facilities.” These references reflect adjacency via electric infrastructure serving miners rather than direct participation in digital-asset markets. Accordingly, any crypto linkage is categorized as infrastructure exposure and appears minor relative to the overall regulated utility business. ([txnmenergy.com](https://www.txnmenergy.com/~/media/Files/P/PNM-Resources/quarterly-results/2022/Q4%202022%20Earnings%20Call%20Transcript.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
The company is a regulated electric utility holding company with broad-based residential, commercial, industrial and government customers; crypto miners are one subset of large flexible loads in parts of TNMP’s territory. Given explicit but limited mentions of crypto-mining customers and planned lines that cite virtual currency mining alongside other drivers (oil and gas, data centers), a small infrastructure weight is assigned to reflect utility infrastructure serving miners, with the remainder unclassified due to the absence of direct crypto activities (no treasury holdings, mining, or financial services). Final weights: infrastr 0.1, unclass 0.9. ([txnmenergy.com](https://www.txnmenergy.com/~/media/Files/P/PNM-Resources/quarterly-results/2022/Q4%202022%20Earnings%20Call%20Transcript.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The magnitude of crypto-related load is not broken out as a separate segment in filings; disclosures characterize it as incremental demand within TNMP rather than a standalone line of business. ([txnmenergy.com](https://www.txnmenergy.com/~/media/Files/P/PNM-Resources/events-and-presentations/2024/February%202024%20Investor%20Presentation.pdf))
- Evidence of crypto exposure arises from serving mining customers; there is no indication of digital-asset custody, trading, or strategic coin holdings by TXNM Energy. The classification would change only if the company began offering crypto financial services or adopted a treasury allocation policy involving crypto assets.
- Corporate identity transitioned from PNM Resources to TXNM Energy in August 2024; references before that date use the former name. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1108426/000110842624000082/pnm-20240805.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 24, 2023 (FY2022): “At TNMP…load…exceeded expectations…plus the added growth from crypto mining customers entering the market.” — [TXNM/PNMR FY2022 earnings call PDF] (lines P4 L141–146) ([txnmenergy.com](https://www.txnmenergy.com/~/media/Files/P/PNM-Resources/quarterly-results/2022/Q4%202022%20Earnings%20Call%20Transcript.pdf))
- Feb 7, 2024 (Q4/FY2023): “Investing in the infrastructure needed to meet customer needs and enable the clean energy transition.” — [Investing.com transcript summary/full text] 
- Nov 6, 2018 (Q3 2018): Discussion focused on load growth, capex plans and regulatory items; no crypto activities referenced. — [The Motley Fool transcript] 

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- FY2023 Form 10‑K — Annual report for PNM Resources/TXNM Energy (business description and regulated utility operations). — [SEC EDGAR index] 
- Aug 5, 2024 — Form 8‑K: press release regarding company name change to TXNM Energy and ticker update. — [SEC EDGAR] ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1108426/000110842624000082/pnm-20240805.htm?utm_source=openai))
- Aug 2 & 5, 2024 — Press releases announcing completion of name change to TXNM Energy and trading effectiveness. — [PR Newswire] ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/txnm-energy-nyse-txnm-completes-name-change-from-pnm-resources-302213844.html?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Feb 2024 — Investor Presentation: TNMP “West Texas: oil and gas, crypto mining industrials”; demand-based load increases reflect incremental crypto-mining customer demand. — [TXNM Energy investor presentation] ([txnmenergy.com](https://www.txnmenergy.com/~/media/Files/P/PNM-Resources/events-and-presentations/2024/February%202024%20Investor%20Presentation.pdf))
- TNMP “Current Transmission Line Projects”: proposal cites growing demand “particularly from…data centers, and virtual currency mining facilities.” — [TNMP project page] ([tnmp.com](https://tnmp.com/transmission-line-projects))
- Oct 2023 — Report on ERCOT controllable load qualification involving a bitcoin mining facility served in TNMP territory, illustrating miner presence as a customer class. — [Webull news item] ([webull.com](https://www.webull.com/news/13566705734800384?utm_source=openai))

## SEQUOIA FINL.GP.

* Rank: 232 | Expo: 0.21 | id_str: AU000000SEQ3
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: ASX announcements (2019–2024), 2024 Annual Report notice, corporate website

### Firm Overview
Sequoia Financial Group Limited is an Australia-based, ASX-listed diversified financial services company offering wealth management, professional services for advisers and accountants, stockbroking/trading platforms, and media content. Its businesses serve self-directed retail and wholesale clients as well as third‑party professional service firms in Australia. ([sequoia.com.au](https://www.sequoia.com.au/?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Within the January 2017–December 2024 window, the company explicitly stated it had no exposure to cryptocurrencies or exchanges in an ASX “Media Clarification” announcement dated November 24, 2022. Corporate materials describe conventional financial products and services (advice, platforms, brokerage, administration) and do not indicate crypto custody, issuance, trading, mining, or treasury holdings by the company. Some marketing/editorial content on affiliated sites discusses crypto market developments or generic SMSF considerations for crypto investing, but that reflects news/advice content rather than the firm directly handling digital assets. Accordingly, no crypto exposure mechanism is supported by the evidence. ([company-announcements.afr.com](https://company-announcements.afr.com/asx/seq/69c9b9b2-6b87-11ed-8d7d-ba667c702a54.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Given the explicit ASX clarification of no crypto exposure and the absence of disclosures showing the firm holding crypto assets, running mining operations, or providing crypto financial services (custody, exchange/brokerage in digital assets, or issuing crypto‑backed products), the firm is classified as unclassified. All mechanism weights are set to zero with unclass = 1.0; invbase is 0 because there is no evidence that its shareholder base trades the stock as a crypto proxy. ([company-announcements.afr.com](https://company-announcements.afr.com/asx/seq/69c9b9b2-6b87-11ed-8d7d-ba667c702a54.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- ASX small-cap coverage means earnings call transcripts are not readily available; however, for an unclassified assignment this is not required. A FY24 results webinar is listed without a publicly accessible transcript. ([marketindex.com.au](https://www.marketindex.com.au/asx/seq/announcements/fy24-results-webinar-2A1542600))
- The group’s platforms and media occasionally reference crypto markets or discuss SMSF pathways to crypto at third‑party venues; this is not evidence of the company itself taking custody of, issuing, or investing in digital assets. ([sequoiadirect.com.au](https://www.sequoiadirect.com.au/latest-market-news/bank-crisis-spooks-markets/?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Filings (ASX / reports):**
- Nov 24, 2022 — Media Clarification: “Sequoia Financial Group Ltd confirms that it has no exposure to crypto currencies or exchanges.” — [ASX company announcement (PDF)](https://company-announcements.afr.com/asx/seq/69c9b9b2-6b87-11ed-8d7d-ba667c702a54.pdf)
- Aug 27, 2024 — 2024 Annual Report and Appendix 4E (business description; no crypto holdings disclosed). — [MarketIndex announcement entry](https://www.marketindex.com.au/asx/seq/announcements/2024-annual-report-and-appendix-4e-2A1543530)
- Aug 19, 2021 — Appendix 4E and Annual Report FY2021 (business lines and segment descriptions). — [MarketIndex announcement entry](https://www.marketindex.com.au/asx/seq/announcements/appendix-4e-and-annual-report-for-year-ended-30-june-2021-2A1316703)

**Corporate/operations:**
- Corporate overview: integrated financial services to self-directed and wholesale clients; advisers/accountants. — [Sequoia Financial Group website](https://www.sequoia.com.au/)
- Stockbroking and clearing services for advisers, traders, funds and fintechs (equities/options; exchange connectivity). — [Sequoia corporate/institutional page](https://www.sequoia.com.au/corporate-institutional/stockbroking-and-clearing/)
- Sequoia Direct trading platform (equities/options; not a crypto exchange). — [Sequoia Direct site](https://www.sequoiadirect.com.au/)

## CAPSTONE GREEN ENERGY DEAD - DELIST.08/12/23

* Rank: 233 | Expo: 0.21 | id_str: US14067D5086
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 1.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 2 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q1 FY2023 earnings call; corporate press releases (June 2022); product case study (2021–2022 deployment, published 2024); 10-K/A and 10-K filings (filed June 13, 2024)

### Firm Overview
Capstone Green Energy Corporation (formerly Capstone Turbine) is a U.S.-based manufacturer and lessor of microturbine energy systems, offering on‑site power and Energy‑as‑a‑Service solutions for industrial and commercial customers worldwide. Its products are used in combined heat and power, microgrids, oil and gas, data centers and other power‑reliant applications. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Capstone_Green_Energy?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
During 2017–2024, management explicitly cited bitcoin/cryptocurrency mining as an end‑market for its microturbine rentals and systems, particularly where miners use associated gas at well sites to generate low‑cost power. On its Q1 FY2023 call, the CEO described “a big influx of Bitcoin operators” using Capstone microturbines and noted bitcoin as one of the company’s largest EaaS market verticals at that time. Corporate disclosures further announced EaaS contracts totaling 6 MW for remote data centers handling cryptocurrency mining and referenced an installed base of Capstone‑powered cryptocurrency customers. A Louisiana case study documents two C1000S microturbines (1 MW each) commissioned in October 2021 and April 2022 for a blockchain and cryptocurrency mining data center using on‑site associated gas. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/CGRN/transcripts/2023/1))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence shows Capstone supplies prime power (equipment and rentals) to crypto‑mining data centers but does not itself mine coins, hold crypto on its balance sheet, or provide custody/exchange services. This maps directly to infrastructure provision (generation hardware and EaaS to mining workloads). Accordingly, infrastr = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. Given multiple, consistent sources (earnings call, press release, case study), certainty is 4. RevShare is set to 2 (~5–15%) to reflect meaningful but not dominant contribution from cryptocurrency‑mining customers during periods when management cited bitcoin as one of the largest EaaS verticals; AssetShare is 0 as there is no indication of crypto assets held by the company. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/CGRN/transcripts/2023/1))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The company does not break out revenue attributable specifically to cryptocurrency clients in filings; RevShare therefore reflects a conservative, ordinal estimate from management commentary and disclosed MW deployments. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/CGRN/transcripts/2023/1))
- The firm underwent restructuring and filing catch‑up in 2023–2024; classification relies on activity and disclosures within the 2017–2024 window and does not infer any post‑2024 changes. ([capstonegreenenergy.com](https://www.capstonegreenenergy.com/news/press-releases/detail/4024/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 11, 2022 (Q1 FY2023): “We’ve seen a big influx of Bitcoin operators…to generate low‑cost energy for their Bitcoin mining operations.”; “Most of the Bitcoin we’re doing is all megawatt machines…especially in Pennsylvania.” — [Roic AI transcript page](https://www.roic.ai/quote/CGRN/transcripts/2023/1)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Jun 13, 2024 — Company announced filing of FY2022 10‑K/A and FY2023 10‑K (business description and EaaS model). — [Capstone press release](https://www.capstonegreenenergy.com/news/press-releases/detail/4024/)

**Financial news / other:**
- Jun 8, 2022 — “Two Energy‑as‑a‑Service contracts totaling 6 MW for remote data centers handling cryptocurrency mining… When these two sites are operational… we will have 13 MW of Capstone powered cryptocurrency customers.” — [Capstone press release](https://www.capstonegreenenergy.com/news/press-releases/detail/3942/)
- Case study — “Louisiana Blockchain and Cryptocurrency Mining Data Center”: two C1000S microturbines; first commissioned Oct 2021, second Apr 2022; uses on‑site associated gas. — [Capstone case study (PDF)](https://d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net/_41a9a39e4a0434ef12d7d4e56e9302cd/capstonegreenenergy/db/185/10342/pdf/CS_CAP486_Bitcoin_Mining_lowres.pdf)
- Product page listing the above “Louisiana Blockchain and Cryptocurrency Mining” case study under C1000S. — [Capstone C1000S page](https://www.capstonegreenenergy.com/products/capstone-microturbines/c1000s)
- Corporate profile/background (name change, business scope). — [Wikipedia overview](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Capstone_Green_Energy)

## CRANE

* Rank: 234 | Expo: 0.21 | id_str: US2244081046
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Earnings calls (Q4 2019, Q3 2021); 2019 Annual Report; 2023 Form 10‑K; corporate press releases around April 2023 separation

### Firm Overview
Crane Company is a U.S.-based industrial manufacturer listed on the NYSE, with operations primarily in Aerospace & Electronics, Process Flow Technologies, and Engineered Materials following its April 2023 separation from Crane Holdings Co. Prior to the separation, the legacy group also included a Payment & Merchandising Technologies segment (Crane Payment Innovations, Crane Currency, and Crane Merchandising Systems).

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across January 2017–December 2024, there is no evidence in earnings calls or SEC filings that Crane held cryptocurrencies, mined digital assets, or operated crypto financial services. Pre‑separation, the group’s Payment & Merchandising Technologies segment (CPI/CMS/Currency) focused on cash handling, vending, and banknote security; management commentary around that segment does not reference crypto activity. While CPI markets service capabilities for “Cash ATM & Crypto ATM Repair,” this pertains to third‑party equipment service and does not indicate material or direct crypto exposure at the consolidated company level, and following April 3, 2023 those businesses were part of Crane NXT, not Crane Company.

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of substantive crypto references in earnings calls and filings during 2017–2024, and the fact that post‑separation Crane Company does not include the payments businesses (with only peripheral “crypto ATM” service marketing noted at CPI under Crane NXT), the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0. All other mechanism weights are set to 0.0, with invbase = 0 and materiality measures not applicable.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- CPI’s service site mentions “Cash ATM & Crypto ATM Repair,” suggesting limited third‑party service adjacency; however, there is no disclosure of crypto‑related revenues or operations at Crane, and CPI resided in the Payment & Merchandising segment that was spun into Crane NXT on April 3, 2023. Classification therefore remains unclassified.  
- No earnings‑call transcript within 2017–2024 contained crypto references; quotes below are included only to anchor segment context.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jan 28, 2020 (Q4 2019): “new retail solutions at Crane Payment Innovations, new technology advancements and continued wins at Currency…” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/01/28/crane-co-cr-q4-2019-earnings-call-transcript.aspx) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/01/28/crane-co-cr-q4-2019-earnings-call-transcript.aspx))
- Oct 26, 2021 (Q3 2021): “At Payment & Merchandising Technologies, Crane Payment Innovations will be $200 million below pre‑COVID levels in 2021…” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/26/crane-co-cr-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcript/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/26/crane-co-cr-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- 2019 Annual Report: Payment & Merchandising Technologies segment consists of CPI, Crane Currency, and Crane Merchandising Systems; product lines include payment acceptance/dispensing and banknotes/security. — [Crane 2019 AR PDF](https://investors.craneco.com/files/doc_financials/2019/ar/Crane_2019_AR.pdf) ([investors.craneco.com](https://investors.craneco.com/files/doc_financials/2019/ar/Crane_2019_AR.pdf))
- Feb 26, 2024 — Form 10‑K (FY2023): separation accounting; P&M Technologies reported as discontinued ops; current segments are Aerospace & Electronics, Process Flow Technologies, and Engineered Materials. — [Crane Company 2023 Form 10‑K PDF](https://investors.craneco.com/files/doc_financials/2023/q4/dbbc18e4-042d-451c-9408-bfa22dba29d7.pdf) ([investors.craneco.com](https://investors.craneco.com/files/doc_financials/2023/q4/dbbc18e4-042d-451c-9408-bfa22dba29d7.pdf))
- Apr 3, 2023 — “Crane Company Completes Separation from Crane Holdings, Co.” (Crane trades as CR; Crane NXT holds the payment businesses). — [Crane press release](https://investors.craneco.com/news/news-details/2023/Crane-Company-Completes-Separation-from-Crane-Holdings-Co/default.aspx) ([investors.craneco.com](https://investors.craneco.com/news/news-details/2023/Crane-Company-Completes-Separation-from-Crane-Holdings-Co/default.aspx?utm_source=openai))

## TOSHIBA DEAD - DELIST.20/12/23

* Rank: 235 | Expo: 0.21 | id_str: JP3592200004
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Integrated Reports (FY2019–FY2021); Earnings releases (FY2022); IR delisting notices (Oct–Dec 2023); Toshiba Digital Solutions blockchain press (2024)

### Firm Overview
Toshiba Corporation is a Japan-based industrial conglomerate focused on energy systems, infrastructure, electronic devices, and digital solutions. The company was taken private by a Japan Industrial Partners–led consortium and its shares were delisted from the Tokyo Stock Exchange and the Nagoya Stock Exchange on December 20, 2023. ([global.toshiba](https://www.global.toshiba/ww/ir/corporate/finance/annual-report.html?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across January 2017–December 2024, there is no evidence that Toshiba held cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet, mined digital assets, or operated crypto exchanges, custodians, or investment products. Toshiba Digital Solutions promoted an enterprise/permissioned blockchain platform (DNCWARE Blockchain+), including a logistics deployment in August 2024 and a new version announcement in December 2024; these initiatives concern enterprise blockchain infrastructure for data integrity and workflows rather than custody or transacting in crypto assets, and therefore do not meet the financial‑services or holders criteria. Toshiba’s retail technology unit emphasized traditional and biometric payment solutions (e.g., ELERA/PopPay integrations), not crypto acceptance or custody. ([global.toshiba](https://www.global.toshiba/jp/news/digitalsolution/2024/08/news-20240807-01.html?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of substantive crypto-asset exposure in filings, investor materials, and press through 2024—and the fact that identified blockchain activities are enterprise/permissioned use cases unrelated to cryptocurrency custody, trading, mining, or investment products—the company is classified as unclass = 1.0. All other mechanism weights are set to 0.0, with invbase = 0 and materiality measures not applicable. ([global.toshiba](https://www.global.toshiba/ww/ir/corporate/finance/annual-report.html?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- English earnings-call transcripts are sparse and in some cases paywalled; however, available IR materials and news around the period do not indicate crypto-asset activities.  
- Toshiba’s enterprise blockchain announcements (DNCWARE Blockchain+) could be misconstrued as “crypto,” but these relate to permissioned blockchain for supply-chain/logistics and similar applications, not digital-asset services.  
- The company’s delisting on December 20, 2023 reduces ongoing public disclosures; classification is based on materials available through the 2017–2024 window. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/TOSHIBA-6493713/news/Transcript-Toshiba-Corporation-2020-Earnings-Call-Jun-05-2020-37889113/))

## SUN HUNG KAI & COMPANY

* Rank: 236 | Expo: 0.21 | id_str: HK0086000525
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Investor presentations (AR 2023); Earnings call transcript (Mar 22, 2024); SHK Capital Partners site; Financial news (2022 Sygnum round; 2018 Animoca investment)

### Firm Overview
Sun Hung Kai & Co. Limited is a Hong Kong–listed principal-led alternative investment platform with businesses spanning investment management, funds management and credit (including consumer finance via UAF and mortgage lending via SHK Credit). The group is listed on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong (stock code 86) and positions itself as an alternative asset manager serving institutional and professional investors. ([shkco.com](https://www.shkco.com/en/InvestorInfo.aspx?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s crypto exposure is through financial services: its funds management arm (Sun Hung Kai Capital Partners) partners with managers running cryptocurrency strategies and distributes related products to professional investors. Company investor materials for 2023 explicitly list “Market Neutral Crypto Strategy” and “Quantitative/Directional Crypto Strategy” within fund partnerships, and show “Digital Asset” as a sleeve within external hedge fund exposure. Separately, the group led Sygnum Bank’s $90 million Series B financing in January 2022 and previously invested in Animoca Brands (2018), adding indirect exposure to digital-asset businesses. There is no evidence that the company mines crypto or holds crypto as a treasury asset. ([shkco.com](https://www.shkco.com/pdf/arreport_2023_en.pdf?ver=3))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 reflects: (i) distribution/partnerships for crypto hedge fund strategies through SHK Capital Partners, including a Cayman open-ended fund focused on quantitative crypto arbitrage, and (ii) principal investments in regulated digital-asset financial institutions (e.g., Sygnum). No evidence supports holders, miners, infrastrukture, or non-financial biz services categories, so those are set to 0.0. Given that crypto appears as a small sleeve of hedge fund exposure (e.g., “Digital Asset” 6.8% within external hedge funds in 2023) and no dedicated on-balance-sheet crypto, RevShare is coded 1 (minor) and AssetShare 0. ([shkcapital.com](https://www.shkcapital.com/en/partner/kernel.aspx))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The primary earnings-call transcript retrieved (March 22, 2024) anchors the period but does not itself discuss crypto; the fin
the financial‑services exposure is established through investor materials and SHK Capital Partners disclosures, with corroboration from third‑party news about Sygnum and Animoca. The company does not break out crypto-related revenue separately, and “Digital Asset” appears as a sleeve within external hedge fund exposure rather than a standalone segment; thus crypto revenue materiality is coded as minor. Product availability is oriented to professional investors and may vary by jurisdiction, so exposure could fluctuate with mandate changes. ([shkco.com](https://www.shkco.com/pdf/arreport_2023_en.pdf?ver=3))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Mar 22, 2024: Full Year 2023 earnings call — “Morning, ladies and gentlemen, thank you for joining our 2023 Annual Results Investor Presentation.” — [GuruFocus transcript](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/2395754/full-year-2023-sun-hung-kai-co-ltd-earnings-call-chinese-english-transcript)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**

AR 2023 (PDF)](https://www.shkco.com/pdf/arreport_2023_en.pdf?ver=3)
- Company history page — “Seed commitment in Kernel Fund, a fully regulated crypto-currency hedge fund” (milestone list). — [SHK & Co. Company History](https://www.shkco.com/en/Anniversary.aspx)
- SHK Capital Partners partner page — “Quantitative crypto strategy… arbitrage strategies… Cayman open ended Fund.” — [SHK Capital Partners – Partner: Kernel](https://www.shkcapital.com/en/partner/kernel.aspx)

**Financial news / other:**

### Firm Overview
CoreCard Corporation is a U.S.-based financial technology company headquartered in Norcross, Georgia that provides software and processing services for card issuing and account management to banks, fintechs, and program managers. Company filings describe the business as “providing technology solutions and processing services to the financial technology and services market,” with products certified for major card networks. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/320340/000143774924006215/ins20231231_10k.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across earnings-call transcripts and SEC filings reviewed for January 2017–December 2024, there is no substantive indication that the company operates crypto financial services, mines cryptocurrency, holds crypto as a treasury asset, or supplies crypto‑specific infrastructure. Management commentary and disclosures instead focus on card‑issuing software, processing, and customer relationships (e.g., program managers and banks), with revenue components such as license, professional services, and processing/maintenance. Given the absence of credible crypto references in filings and calls, no crypto‑exposure mechanism is assigned. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/320340/000143774924006215/ins20231231_10k.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Unclass = 1.0 is applied because the evidence base (10‑K filings and earnings‑call transcripts within the 2017–2024 sample) shows a conventional card‑issuing/processing software business without documented crypto activities. All other mechanism categories are set to 0.0 accordingly. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/320340/000143774924006215/ins20231231_10k.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified within the 2017–2024 sample window; Seeking Alpha content is sometimes paywalled, so third‑party transcript aggregators were used to retrieve at least one call transcript. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/CCRD/documents/transcripts/q3-2023))

### Evidence

<small>

## KT

* Rank: 238 | Expo: 0.21 | id_str: KR7030200000
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.4, bizserv 0.6, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: KT earnings presentation (Q2 2019); KT consolidated interim financial statements (Q2 2021, Q1 2022); Korea Times and Asia Business Daily coverage (2019–2021); Yonhap News (2020)

### Firm Overview
KT Corporation is a South Korea‑based telecommunications operator providing mobile, broadband, fixed line and media services with a portfolio of domestic and overseas subsidiaries. The group also owns financial services affiliates, including BC Card and a significant interest in online‑only K Bank via KT Group holdings.

### Exposure Channels Identified
KT developed and commercialized enterprise blockchain offerings under its GiGA Chain platform, positioning them for security, logistics and other B2B applications; this is a non‑financial service to the crypto/blockchain ecosystem. Through group affiliates, KT also has indirect financial exposure: BC Card became the largest shareholder of K Bank in 2020, and K Bank provides the real‑name bank accounts used by Upbit, Korea’s largest crypto exchange, driving deposit and fee growth during periods of active crypto trading. KT’s filings further document equity investments in K Bank in 2021 and ongoing related‑party activity in 2022. No evidence was found that KT holds crypto on its treasury, operates mining, or supplies crypto‑specific hardware.

### Weight Assignment
Bizserv = 0.6 reflects KT’s direct provision of blockchain platform services (GiGA Chain) marketed across multiple enterprise verticals. Finserv = 0.4 captures KT Group’s exposure via K Bank’s real‑name deposit/withdrawal linkage to Upbit, which generated meaningful customer and deposit inflows during crypto up‑cycles but remains one component of a much larger telecom‑centric group. Holders, miners, and infrastr are set to 0.0 based on lack of supporting evidence; unclass = 0.0 by construction.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
K Bank exposure is indirect through KT Group subsidiaries and equity stakes; contributions attributable specifically to crypto activity are not broken out in KT’s consolidated reporting. KT’s blockchain initiatives emphasize enterprise use cases and local services rather than cryptocurrency trading or custody. All evidence is confined to the January 2017–December 2024 window.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jul 2019 — Q2 2019 earnings presentation: “KT also was first in the world to launch a blockchain development platform called ‘KT Giga Chain’… spearheading… security, finance, logistics and local currencies.” — [KT 2Q19 Earnings Presentation (PDF)]
- Jul 2019 — Q2 2019 earnings presentation: “KT also was first in the world to launch a blockchain development platform called ‘KT Giga Chain’… spearheading… security, finance, logistics and local currencies.” — [KT 2Q19 Earnings Presentation (PDF)](https://corp.kt.com/eng/attach/record/2019/2Q19_KT_ENG.pdf)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Aug 26, 2021 — Q2 2021 consolidated interim FS (Events after the reporting period): “On July 9, 2021, the Group acquired 65,377,980 shares of common stock of K-Bank Co., Ltd. for ₩425 billion.” — [KT 2Q21 Consolidated FS (PDF)](https://corp.kt.com/eng/attach/report/2021/KT%202021%20Q2%20Con.pdf)
- May 27, 2022 — Q1 2022 consolidated interim FS: group structure and related‑party disclosures (BC Card and major affiliates). — [KT 1Q22 Consolidated FS (PDF)](https://corp.kt.com/eng/attach/report/2022/KT_2022%201Q%20%28Con%29_FS%20Report%20%28ENG%29_0527_signed.pdf)
- 2024 results materials: segment commentary noting (K bank) profit improvement driven by deposit/loan growth and cost stabilization. — [KT 2024 Earnings Release (PDF)](https://docs.publicnow.com/viewDoc.aspx?filename=33742%5CEXT%5C4A9914C9AA9E588F2EBF4CE70E46B22EAC27F6C6_E7D34526C106BAB40EF5B4DC9901CA40E8B3C2CE.PDF)

**Financial news / other:**
- Jul 22, 2020 — Financial Services Commission approves BC Card becoming K Bank’s largest shareholder (34%). — [Asia Economy (English)](https://cm.asiae.co.kr/en/article/2020072217333703238)
- Jul 15, 2020 — Upbit’s new partnership with K Bank formed on June 23; implications for users and real‑name account requirements. — [The Korea Times](https://www.koreatimes.co.kr/economy/20200715/foreigners-face-barriers-in-crypto-exchanges)
- May 15, 2022 — Fees paid by crypto exchanges to partner banks; Upbit’s fees to K Bank surged roughly 30x from 2020 to 2021. — [Asia Economy (English)](https://cm.asiae.co.kr/en/article/2022051514265268684)
