## NU HOLDINGS A

* Rank: 239 | Expo: 0.21 | id_str: KYG6683N1034
* Weight summary: holders 0.2, miners 0.0, finserv 0.8, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: Form 20‑F (FY2022), Form 6‑K (Q1 2023); Q4 2024 earnings call; Paxos partnership blog; Reuters/MarketScreener coverage (May 2022); CryptoBriefing (Apr 2024)

### Firm Overview
Nu Holdings Ltd is the Cayman Islands holding company for Nubank, a leading Latin American digital banking platform with core operations in Brazil and growing businesses in Mexico and Colombia. Beyond cards, deposits, and lending, Nubank added cryptocurrency features within its consumer app during 2022–2024.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Nu offers retail crypto brokerage within its app under the NuCrypto brand, with trading and custody powered by a regulated partner (Paxos); company filings describe the product and its features, and a 2024 article notes the rollout of crypto withdrawals for BTC, ETH and SOL. In February–March 2023, Nu launched Nucoin, a native token used for a loyalty program. Separately, at the time of its 2022 crypto rollout, Nu’s parent said it would allocate roughly 1% of its cash to bitcoin as a treasury asset, indicating a modest holder exposure. There is no evidence of mining or provision of crypto‑specific hardware/infrastructure.

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 0.8 reflects Nu’s direct provision of crypto trading/custody to retail customers (via a partner), ongoing product expansion, and related safeguarding disclosures. Holders = 0.2 captures the 2022 decision to place ~1% of cash into bitcoin, which is small versus group assets and not central to the model. Miners, bizserv, and infrastr are set to 0.0 for lack of supporting evidence; unclass = 0.0 by construction.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Public sources report the 1% bitcoin treasury allocation in May 2022, but subsequent filings do not break out ongoing balances; the current magnitude within 2017–2024 cannot be precisely verified from public filings. Earnings call transcripts in 2023–2024 emphasize broader business metrics and do not consistently quantify crypto revenues; crypto is likely <5% of revenue in the sample period. Nu’s crypto brokerage relies on a third‑party custodian under arrangements described in filings, which may evolve with regulation.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 20, 2025 — Q4 2024 earnings call: “Revenues grew 58% year over year… and our efficiency ratio… ended the year at 29.9%.” — [The Motley Fool transcript] (Note: call covers FY2024 results). ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2025/02/20/nu-nu-q4-2024-earnings-call-transcript/))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Form 20‑F (FY2022): “As part of our suite of products and services, we enable our customers to purchase and sell certain crypto assets in our platform (NuCrypto)… Current Cryptocurrencies that can be traded: Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH), Uniswap (UNI), Polygon (MATIC)… NYDFS regulated custodian, private keys management, and brokerage solutions.” — [AnnualReports.com hosted filing] ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/n/NYSE_NU_2022.pdf))
- Form 20‑F (FY2022): “In February 2023, we started offering Nucoin, our own token to be used in our loyalty network (‘Nucoin Network’).” — [AnnualReports.com hosted filing] ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/n/NYSE_NU_2022.pdf))
- Form 6‑K (Q1 2023): “In February 2023, Nu initiated the distribution of NuCoin, the native crypto token… [Loyalty network].” — [SEC 6‑K] ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1691493/000129281423002286/nufs1q23_6k.htm))
- Form 6‑K (Q1 2023): Custody performed by broker; customers retain legal ownership;
— [SEC 6‑K](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1691493/000129281423002286/nufs1q23_6k.htm). 

**Financial news / other:**
- May 11, 2022 — Nubank to allow crypto transactions; Nu Holdings bought bitcoin equal to about 1% of its cash holdings. — [MarketScreener (Reuters)](https://www.marketscreener.com/news/latest/Brazil-s-Nubank-to-allow-cryptocurrency-transactions-for-clients-40406494/) ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/news/latest/Brazil-s-Nubank-to-allow-cryptocurrency-transactions-for-clients-40406494/?utm_source=openai))
- May 11, 2022 — Partnership announcement: Nubank expands crypto market access powered by Paxos. — [Paxos blog](https://www.paxos.com/blog/nubank-expands-crypto-market-access-to-tens-of-millions-of-users-powered-by-paxos) 
- May 23, 2024 — Official announcement: Nubank launches crypto transfers (send/receive) for BTC, ETH and SOL. — [Nu International (PT)](https://international.nubank.com.br/pt-br/companhia/nubank-anuncia-envio-e-recebimento-de-criptomoedas-diretamente-pela-plataforma/) | [Nu International (ES)](https://international.nubank.com.br/es/compania/nubank-anuncia-el-envio-y-recepcion-de-criptomonedas-directamente-a-traves-de-la-plataforma/) ([international.nubank.com.br](https://international.nubank.com.br/pt-br/companhia/nubank-anuncia-envio-e-recebimento-de-criptomoedas-diretamente-pela-plataforma/?utm_source=openai))

## CANTALOUPE

* Rank: 240 | Expo: 0.20 | id_str: US1381031061
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Press releases (2021–2022), Form 10‑K (FY2022); Payments Dive and CoinDesk coverage (May 2021)

### Firm Overview
Cantaloupe, Inc. is a U.S.-based digital payments and software company focused on unattended retail, providing card readers, point-of-sale devices, and the Seed software platform for vending, micro markets, and other self‑service environments. Its core business is cashless payment acceptance and fleet/route management software for operators.

### Exposure Channels Identified
In May 2021 Cantaloupe announced a partnership with Bakkt to enable consumers to spend bitcoin and selected loyalty points at Cantaloupe‑enabled vending machines and other unattended retail devices; the companies stated the Bakkt app would support “Pay with Crypto/Points” at over a million devices, with Bakkt handling the digital asset functionality. Cantaloupe’s product communications and IR press releases from 2021–2022 reiterated acceptance of “all payments, including cryptocurrency,” and that cryptocurrency would be converted to cash for merchant settlement via Bakkt, indicating Cantaloupe acts as an integration layer rather than a crypto custodian. The company’s FY2022 Form 10‑K also references plans to “offer consumers more ways to pay leveraging digital wallets such as pay with crypto” and a platform including “acceptance of crypto payments.” There is no evidence of proprietary custody/exchange services, crypto treasury holdings,
There is no evidence of mining or provision of crypto‑specific hardware/infrastructure. ([paymentsdive.com](https://www.paymentsdive.com/news/cantaloupe-automated-retail-machines/600020/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Bizserv = 1.0 reflects that Cantaloupe’s role is to integrate a third‑party crypto platform (Bakkt) so consumers can pay at unattended retail, while Cantaloupe’s merchants are settled in fiat and the company does not itself custody or trade digital assets. No credible evidence supports holders, miners, finserv, or infrastr exposure under the project’s definitions; unclass is 0.0 by construction. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20210506005345/en/Bakkt-and-Cantaloupe-Announce-Partnership-to-Accept-Cryptocurrency-and-Participating-Loyalty-Points-in-Cantaloupes-Unattended-Retail-Network?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Company press releases and trade‑press coverage from 2021 describe “pay with crypto” enablement and signage starting Fall 2021, but subsequent SEC filings through FY2024 do not quantify crypto transaction volumes or discuss the feature’s materiality. Earnings calls in 2022–2024 emphasize broader cashless adoption and product updates rather than crypto specifically; classification therefore relies primarily on press releases and industry coverage. Certainty is capped at 2. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20210818005310/en/Cantaloupe-Innovation-Summit-2021-Showcases-New-Products-and-Services?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Sep 10, 2024 — Form 10‑K (FY2024): Business description and payments focus; no specific crypto metrics disclosed. — [SEC filing (PDF)](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/896429/000162828024042316/a20240630-ctlp10xk.pdf)
- May 6, 2021 — “Bakkt and Cantaloupe Announce Partnership to Accept Cryptocurrency… at Cantaloupe’s Unattended Retail Network.” — [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20210506005345/en/Bakkt-and-Cantaloupe-Announce-Partnership-to-Accept-Cryptocurrency-and-Participating-Loyalty-Points-in-Cantaloupes-Unattended-Retail-Network)
- Jan 20, 2022 — “Cantaloupe Celebrates 30 Years…” notes Bakkt integration enabling pay with crypto/points on >1M devices. — [Business Wire via Cantaloupe IR](https://cantaloupeinc.gcs-web.com/news-releases/news-release-details/cantaloupe-celebrates-30-years-innovation-autonomous-retail/)

## SEPTENI HOLDINGS

* Rank: 241 | Expo: 0.20 | id_str: JP3423300007
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Company press releases (2023–2024), Annual Securities Report (FY2024), FY2024 2Q–3Q earnings call transcripts

### Firm Overview
Septeni Holdings Co., Ltd. is a Japan‑based digital marketing and media group listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange, operating segments centered on marketing communications, direct business, and data/solutions. Its core activities are internet advertising and related services, with no disclosure indicating proprietary crypto asset custody or treasury holdings. ([septeni-holdings.co.jp](https://www.septeni-holdings.co.jp/en/ir/library/securities-report/2024_yuho_en.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
In June 2023, group subsidiary Septeni Incubate launched “Ocean Dict,” a Web3‑focused marketing SaaS that provides on‑chain analytics and wallet‑based signup features to support NFT, blockchain‑game (BCG), and dApp projects. This product explicitly targets blockchain projects and analyzes multi‑chain transaction data for marketing use cases. ([septeni-holdings.co.jp](https://www.septeni-holdings.co.jp/news/release/2023/06013526.html))  
In March 2024, Septeni Incubate and Septeni Global joined Dentsu, double jump.tokyo, and SHINSEKAI Technologies to form “Web3 Gaming Booster,” a specialized team offering comprehensive marketing support for Web3 games. These activities position the firm as a non‑financial service provider to crypto projects rather than an exchange, custodian, or miner. ([septeni-holdings.co.jp](https://www.septeni-holdings.co.jp/news/release/2024/03013702.html?utm_source=openai))  
Across FY2024 filings and briefings, the company describes its operations in advertising and solutions, with no indication of crypto asset treasury holdings or mining operations. ([septeni-holdings.co.jp](https://www.septeni-holdings.co.jp/en/ir/library/securities-report/2024_yuho_en.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Bizserv = 1.0 best reflects Septeni’s exposure: the firm (through subsidiaries) offers marketing and analytics services to Web3/NFT/blockchain‑game clients but does not issue crypto products, custody client assets, mine coins, or supply specialized mining hardware. Other mechanism categories (holders, miners, finserv, infrastr) receive 0.0 due to lack of evidence in filings and investor communications. ([septeni-holdings.co.jp](https://www.septeni-holdings.co.jp/news/release/2023/06013526.html))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Evidence of crypto exposure is concentrated in subsidiary‑level press releases (Japanese‑language) and partnership announcements; earnings call transcripts reviewed did not specifically discuss Web3/NFT revenues, and no segment detail quantifies crypto‑related contribution. Classification therefore emphasizes documented Web3 marketing offerings rather than quantified financial materiality. ([septeni-holdings.co.jp](https://www.septeni-holdings.co.jp/en/ir/library/FY2024_3Q_Transcript_E.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 7, 2024 — Q3 FY2024 results briefing: “Net sales grew steadily, growing by 8.1% YoY. Revenue increased by 2.7%.” — [Septeni IR transcript (PDF)](https://www.septeni-holdings.co.jp/en/ir/library/FY2024_3Q_Transcript_E.pdf)

## RCI HOSPITALITY HDG.

* Rank: 242 | Expo: 0.20 | id_str: US74934Q1085
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Company press releases (2022, 2024), Q4 2023 and Q4 2024 earnings call transcripts, FY2023 Form 10-K

### Firm Overview
RCI Hospitality Holdings, Inc. is a U.S. operator of adult nightclubs and sports bar/restaurants under brands such as Rick’s Cabaret, Tootsie’s Cabaret, Scarlett’s Cabaret and Bombshells, with 69 establishments across multiple states as of September 30, 2023. The FY2023 Form 10-K describes operations concentrated in Nightclubs and Bombshells segments and does not indicate any crypto assets on the balance sheet. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/r/NASDAQ_RICK_2023.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
RCI began accepting Bitcoin as a form of payment at Tootsie’s Cabaret and Scarlett’s Cabaret in the Miami area in March 2022, with the company specifying that Bitcoin payments would be converted to cash. Management reiterated in February 2024 that these clubs continue to accept Bitcoin and that transactions had been increasing. ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/tootsies--scarletts-cabaret-miami-to-accept-bitcoin-301492740.html))  
On the Q4 FY2023 earnings call (December 14, 2023), management said, “crypto is up, Bitcoin is killing it,” noting about $170,000 in Bitcoin processed over two days at Tootsie’s and interest in enabling Bitcoin acceptance in New York and Chicago. On the Q4 FY2024 call (December 16, 2024), the CEO added that payments are converted to U.S. dollars at the point of transaction and that acceptance had begun in New York and Chicago as well. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/RICK/documents/transcripts/q4-2023))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 best captures the firm’s crypto exposure, which is limited to accepting Bitcoin payments at select clubs. This activity links revenue to crypto payment volumes and sentiment but does not involve issuing crypto products, custodying client assets, mining, or holding crypto on the treasury; management explicitly stated conversion to USD at the point of sale. Accordingly, holders, miners, bizserv, and infrastr are set to 0.0. ([insidermonkey.com](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/rci-hospitality-holdings-inc-nasdaqrick-q4-2024-earnings-call-transcript-1410415/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Acceptance appears limited to specific venues and is not broken out as a separate revenue line; company-level crypto revenue contribution is not quantified in filings. Classification as “finserv” reflects merchant crypto payments rather than provision of payment rails to third parties. Management indicates immediate conversion to USD, supporting no balance-sheet crypto exposure. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/r/NASDAQ_RICK_2023.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Dec 14, 2023 — Q4 FY2023: “We actually processed, I think $170,000 in Bitcoin in two days… we’re getting other requests on… whether we’re taking Bitcoin in New York… probably in Chicago.” — [Fintool transcript](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/RICK/documents/transcripts/q4-2023)
- Dec 16, 2024 — Q4 FY2024: “We convert to US dollars at point of transaction… Bitcoin has been very integral, especially in Miami… started in New York and Chicago as well.” — [Insider Monkey transcript](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/rci-hospitality-holdings-inc-nasdaqrick-q4-2024-earnings-call-transcript-1410415/)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- FY2023 Form 10-K — Business overview; segments; no disclosure of crypto asset holdings. — [Annual Report PDF](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/r/NASDAQ_RICK_2023.pdf)
- Mar 1, 2022 — “Tootsie’s & Scarlett’s Cabaret Miami to Accept Bitcoin” (pilot; converted to cash). — [PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/tootsies--scarletts-cabaret-miami-to-accept-bitcoin-301492740.html)

## OXFORD NANOPORE TECHNOLOGIES

### Firm Overview
Oxford Nanopore Technologies plc is a United Kingdom–based life science tools company that develops and sells nanopore-based DNA and RNA sequencing platforms and consumables used across research, clinical, and applied markets. The company is listed on the London Stock Exchange and reports in GBP.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across company filings and results announcements during 2017–2024, there is no evidence that the firm holds cryptocurrencies, mines digital assets, provides crypto financial services, or supplies crypto‑specific infrastructure. Disclosures and commentary focus on sequencing instruments (e.g., MinION, GridION, PromethION), consumables, and software supporting genomics applications, with no references to blockchain or crypto payment/custody activities. Accordingly, no substantive crypto exposure channels are identified for the sample period. ([a.storyblok.com](https://a.storyblok.com/f/196663/x/26419309c8/annual-report-2023.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Because no credible crypto‑related activity is evidenced in transcripts/announcements or in the Annual Report & Accounts over the 2017–2024 window, the company is assigned unclass = 1.0 and 0.0 for all other mechanism categories. There is also no basis to code a crypto‑sensitive investor base amplifier, so invbase = 0. ([a.storyblok.com](https://a.storyblok.com/f/196663/x/26419309c8/annual-report-2023.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified within the 2017–2024 sample window; transcripts accessible via third‑party sites are paywalled, but company filings and results communications provide sufficient basis to determine no crypto exposure. If any blockchain or digital‑asset initiatives began after December 2024, they fall outside the scope of this assessment. ([nanoporetech.com](https://nanoporetech.com/about/investors/reports?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Mar 6, 2024 — FY2023 preliminary results: management hosted a conference call/webcast; materials describe sequencing platforms and markets; no crypto content noted. — [Oxford Nanopore news](https://nanoporetech.com/zh/news/news-preliminary-results-year-ended-31-december-2023)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Annual Report & Accounts 2023 — Business description, segments, and financial statements; no disclosure of crypto holdings, mining, or crypto services. — [Oxford Nanopore Annual Report 2023 (PDF)](https://a.storyblok.com/f/196663/x/26419309c8/annual-report-2023.pdf)
- Mar 21, 2023 — Final results for year ended Dec 31, 2022; business update with no crypto references. — [Company announcement](https://nanoporetech.com/news/news-final-results-year-ended-31-december-2022)

**Financial news / other:**
- Jul 25, 2023 — Interim results (H1 2023) RNS summary; focus on sequencing products and cash/investments; no crypto activity disclosed. — [Investegate](https://www.investegate.co.uk/announcement/rns/oxford-nanopore-technologies--ont/interim-results-for-the-6-months-ended-30-june-23/7737653)

## MERCADOLIBRE

* Rank: 244 | Expo: 0.19 | id_str: US58733R1023
* Weight summary: holders 0.2, miners 0.0, finserv 0.8, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: Form 10-K (FY2023); Q4 2021 earnings call; Financial news (2021–2023)

### Firm Overview
MercadoLibre, Inc. is a Latin American e‑commerce and fintech platform operating the Mercado Libre marketplace and the Mercado Pago digital wallet, with reporting as a U.S. registrant. Within its fintech suite, the company added digital‑asset features during 2021–2023 across Brazil, Mexico, and Chile. ([investor.mercadolibre.com](https://investor.mercadolibre.com/sites/mercadolibre/files/mercadolibre/results/sec-filing-q4-2023.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company provides crypto financial services via Mercado Pago, enabling users to buy, hold and sell selected cryptocurrencies and stablecoins in Brazil (2021), Mexico (2022) and Chile (2023). It also launched a loyalty token program (MercadoCoin) in Brazil in August 2022. In filings, MercadoLibre recognizes customer crypto‑asset safeguarding assets and liabilities under SAB 121, reflecting custodial arrangements with third‑party service providers. Separately, the company invested corporate cash in bitcoin and ether in 2021 and continued to hold digital assets on balance sheet in 2023. ([investor.mercadolibre.com](https://investor.mercadolibre.com/sites/mercadolibre/files/mercadolibre/results/sec-filing-q4-2023.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv is weighted most heavily (0.8) because the buy/hold/sell feature is live across multiple countries and supported by disclosures about customer crypto safeguarding assets/liabilities and reliance on third‑party custodians. Holders receives a smaller weight (0.2) given explicit disclosure of treasury investments in bitcoin and ether and a 2023 year‑end fair value table, but the amounts are small relative to total assets. No evidence was found of mining, infrastructure supply, or non‑financial crypto services. ([investor.mercadolibre.com](https://investor.mercadolibre.com/sites/mercadolibre/files/mercadolibre/results/sec-filing-q4-2023.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
MercadoLibre states it does not itself custody client crypto or control private keys; services are provided by designated third parties, which slightly reduces direct operational exposure. Crypto‑related revenues are not separately disclosed in filings, so revenue materiality is inferred as minor. Loyalty‑token and country‑specific regulatory treatments evolved during the period, but evidence within the 2017–2024 window is sufficient to classify mechanisms. ([investor.mercadolibre.com](https://investor.mercadolibre.com/sites/mercadolibre/files/mercadolibre/results/sec-filing-q4-2023.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 22, 2022 — Q4 2021: “Within the digital account, all users in Brazil can now buy, hold and sell Bitcoin, Ethereum and the USDP stable coin.” — [The Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/02/22/mercadolibre-meli-q4-2021-earnings-call-transcript/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/02/22/mercadolibre-meli-q4-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))
- Feb 22, 2022 — Q4 2021 Q&A: “Crypto definitely is another net positive, but it’s still early. We only launched crypto in mid‑December.” — [The Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/02/22/mercadolibre-meli-q4-2021-earnings-call-transcript/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/02/22/mercadolibre-meli-q4-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- FY2023 Form 10‑K — “A cryptocurrency buy, hold and sell feature of our wallet in Brazil, Mexico and Chile…” — [MercadoLibre FY2023 10‑K (PDF)](https://investor.mercadolibre.com/sites/mercadolibre/files/mercadolibre/results/sec-filing-q4-2023.pdf) ([investor.mercadolibre.com](https://investor.mercadolibre.com/sites/mercadolibre/files/mercadolibre/results/sec-filing-q4-2023.pdf))
- FY2023 Form 10‑K — Customer crypto‑asset safeguarding assets/liabilities $34m (2023) and $15m (2022) by asset (BTC/ETH/other). — [MercadoLibre FY2023 10‑K (PDF)](https://investor.mercadolibre.com/sites/mercadolibre/files/mercadolibre/results/sec-filing-q4-2023.pdf) ([investor.mercadolibre.com](https://investor.mercadolibre.com/sites/mercadolibre/files/mercadolibre/results/sec-filing-q4-2023.pdf))
- FY2023 Form 10‑K — Treasury digital assets table: BTC (cost $6m, FV $17m; 412.7 units) and ETH (cost $3m, FV $7m; 3,041.6 units) at Dec 31, 2023. — [MercadoLibre FY2023 10‑K (PDF)](https://investor.mercadolibre.com/sites/mercadolibre/files/mercadolibre/results/sec-filing-q4-2023.pdf) ([investor.mercadolibre.com](https://investor.mercadolibre.com/sites/mercadolibre/files/mercadolibre/results/sec-filing-q4-2023.pdf))
- FY2023 Form 10‑K — “During 2021, we invested an aggregate $30 million in bitcoin and ether… [no additional purchases in 2022 or 2023].” — [MercadoLibre FY2023 10‑K (PDF)](https://investor.mercadolibre.com/sites/mercadolibre/files/mercadolibre/results/sec-filing-q4-2023.pdf) ([investor.mercadolibre.com](https://investor.mercadolibre.com/sites/mercadolibre/files/mercadolibre/results/sec-filing-q4-2023.pdf))

## PALANTIR TECHNOLOGIES A

* Rank: 245 | Expo: 0.19 | id_str: US69608A1088
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings calls (Q1 2021, Q3 2021), product documentation (2022), 2024 10-K, financial news (2021)

### Firm Overview
Palantir Technologies Inc. is a U.S.-based enterprise software company best known for its Gotham and Foundry platforms used by government and commercial clients for data integration, analytics and operations. Within crypto, Palantir positions Foundry-based tooling for compliance/AML use cases and has accepted bitcoin as a customer payment method since 2021. ([investors.palantir.com](https://investors.palantir.com/files/2024%20FY%20PLTR%2010-K.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company markets “Foundry for Crypto,” an offering aimed at crypto exchanges and fintechs needing industrialized AML/KYC and compliance analytics; management highlighted this as a strong fit for fast‑growing crypto companies and noted customers can pay in crypto. This constitutes non‑financial services sold into the crypto ecosystem rather than custody, trading or issuance. No evidence indicates Palantir holds crypto assets for treasury purposes, mines digital assets, or operates financial products tied to crypto. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/09/palantir-technologies-inc-pltr-q3-2021-earnings-ca/))

### Weight Assignment
Bizserv is set to 1.0 based on explicit product positioning and repeated management commentary that Foundry for Crypto targets exchanges/fintechs for AML/KYC and compliance workflows. No disclosures in recent filings indicate direct financial services in digital assets, mining activity, or treasury crypto holdings; acceptance of bitcoin as a form of payment is operational and incidental, not a balance‑sheet strategy. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/09/palantir-technologies-inc-pltr-q3-2021-earnings-ca/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Public filings through FY2024 do not break out crypto-related revenue, so revenue materiality is inferred as minor. Filings also do not disclose any digital-asset holdings on the balance sheet, and external reporting does not quantify adoption of Foundry for Crypto across customers. Evidence rests primarily on 2021 management commentary and official product materials within the 2017–2024 window. ([investors.palantir.com](https://investors.palantir.com/files/2024%20FY%20PLTR%2010-K.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 11, 2021 — Q1 2021: “The short answer is yes… [bitcoin on balance sheet is] on the table… we do accept [Bitcoin]… we’re open for business there.” — [The Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/05/11/palantir-technologies-inc-pltr-q1-2021-earnings-ca/) (fool.com) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/05/11/palantir-technologies-inc-pltr-q1-2021-earnings-ca/))
- Nov 9, 2021 — Q3 2021: “We are super excited about Foundry for Crypto… unique fit with fast‑growing crypto companies that need industrialized compliance solutions… they are welcome to pay us in crypto.” — [The Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/09/palantir-technologies-inc-pltr-q3-2021-earnings-ca/) (fool.com) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/09/palantir-technologies-inc-pltr-q3-2021-earnings-ca/))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- FY2024 Form 10‑K — Company description and risk factors; no disclosure of digital‑asset holdings or crypto‑specific revenue lines. — [Palantir FY2024 10‑K (PDF)](https://investors.palantir.com/files/2024%20FY%20PLTR%2010-K.pdf) (investors.palantir.com) ([investors.palantir.com](https://investors.palantir.com/files/2024%20FY%20PLTR%2010-K.pdf))
- Solution overview — Foundry for Crypto capabilities and compliance focus. — [Palantir Foundry for Crypto (PDF)](https://www.palantir.com/assets/xrfr7uokpv1b/5hqNsoSteUiu7V7ROybTtR/575d2a9ebded4a6fbbacd80f76bffedc/Palantir_Foundry_For_Crypto.pdf) (palantir.com) ([palantir.com](https://www.palantir.com/assets/xrfr7uokpv1b/5hqNsoSteUiu7V7ROybTtR/575d2a9ebded4a6fbbacd80f76bffedc/Palantir_Foundry_For_Crypto.pdf?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Nov 9, 2021 — Palantir “expecting big things” from new crypto security software; Foundry for Crypto aimed at compliance needs. — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/11/09/palantir-expecting-big-things-from-new-crypto-security-software) (coindesk.com) ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/11/09/palantir-expecting-big-things-from-new-crypto-security-software?utm_source=openai))
- May 11, 2021 — Company now accepts bitcoin payments; remarks made alongside Q1 2021 results. — [Cointelegraph](https://cointelegraph.com/news/data-analytics-giant-palantir-now-accepts-bitcoin-payments) (cointelegraph.com) ([cointelegraph.com](https://cointelegraph.com/news/data-analytics-giant-palantir-now-accepts-bitcoin-payments?utm_source=openai))

## FATE THERAPEUTICS

* Rank: 246 | Expo: 0.19 | id_str: US31189P1021
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filing (FY2023, filed Feb 26, 2024), earnings calls (Q1 2021, Q3 2021), investor relations events (2023)

### Firm Overview
Fate Therapeutics, Inc. is a U.S. clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing off‑the‑shelf, iPSC‑derived NK and T‑cell immunotherapies for cancer and autoimmune diseases. Public filings and company profiles consistently describe a biotechnology focus with no indication of cryptocurrency-related activities. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1434316/000095017024020158/fate-20231231.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across the 2017–2024 sample window, available earnings call transcripts emphasize cell therapy R&D progress and collaborations, without any references to crypto holdings, mining, exchange operations, or blockchain initiatives. The FY2023 Form 10‑K centers on iPSC‑derived cellular immunotherapies and contains no disclosures of digital‑asset holdings or crypto financial services. Given the absence of substantive crypto links in calls, filings, or investor materials, the firm is classified as unclassified for crypto exposure. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/05/06/fate-therapeutics-inc-fate-q1-2021-earnings-call-t/))

### Weight Assignment
All mechanism weights are set to zero with unclass = 1.0. This reflects a lack of credible evidence that Fate Therapeutics held crypto assets, mined digital assets, provided crypto financial services, sold non‑financial services to the crypto ecosystem, or supplied crypto‑specific infrastructure during 2017–2024. The assignment is based on review of the FY2023 10‑K and multiple 2021 earnings call transcripts, all of which focus on iPSC‑based cell therapies and related clinical development. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1434316/000095017024020158/fate-20231231.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified beyond the inherent limitation that an absence‑of‑evidence conclusion relies on sampled transcripts and filings; if the company later initiates crypto‑related activity, this classification should be revisited.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 5, 2021 — Q3 2021: “We absolutely think we have a platform that can be broadly applicable… IPS‑derived cell therapy.” — The Motley Fool transcript. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/05/fate-therapeutics-inc-fate-q3-2021-earnings-call-t/))
- May 6, 2021 — Q1 2021: “We will be in a position to treat patients with 819… in the next weeks to months.” — The Motley Fool transcript. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/05/06/fate-therapeutics-inc-fate-q1-2021-earnings-call-t/))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Feb 26, 2024 — FY2023 Form 10‑K: “We are a clinical‑stage biopharmaceutical company dedicated to… programmed cellular immunotherapies.” — SEC filing. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1434316/000095017024020158/fate-20231231.htm))
- Nov 5, 2019 — Press release (8‑K exhibit): “Opened state‑of‑the‑art cGMP facility dedicated to manufacturing iPSC‑derived cell therapies.” — Company press release filed with SEC. ([fatetherapeuticsinc.gcs-web.com](https://fatetherapeuticsinc.gcs-web.com/static-files/742382f9-1eab-4d13-b562-116ca20c3c1f))

**Financial news / other:**

- IR Events: Q3 2023 earnings conference call listing (no crypto context). — Fate Therapeutics IR. ([ir.fatetherapeutics.com](https://ir.fatetherapeutics.com/events/event-details/fate-therapeutics-q3-2023-earnings-conference-call?utm_source=openai))

## INFORMATION SVS.GP.

* Rank: 247 | Expo: 0.19 | id_str: US45675Y1047
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (FY2023), earnings calls (Q2 2022, Q3 2024), ISG Provider Lens research brochures (2019–2021)

### Firm Overview
Information Services Group, Inc. (ISG) is a U.S.-based global technology research and advisory firm listed on Nasdaq under ticker III. Its core activities include advisory, benchmarking and research services to enterprise and public-sector clients worldwide.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across the 2017–2024 window, ISG’s earnings calls and investor materials consistently focus on technology research and advisory offerings, utilization, recurring research revenue, and regional demand; there are no substantive references to crypto asset holdings, mining, exchange/custody operations, or crypto-linked financial products. The FY2023 Form 10‑K describes ISG’s business as a “leading global technology research and advisory firm,” with no disclosures of digital‑asset holdings or crypto-specific revenue lines. ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/stock/III/transcripts/2594885))  
ISG’s Provider Lens materials occasionally evaluate “Blockchain Services” as part of broader digital transformation coverage, but this reflects sector research rather than ISG transacting in, holding, or operating crypto infrastructure. ([ei.isg-one.com](https://ei.isg-one.com/Research/GoToDocument?code=Quadrant-DigitalBusiness-US-2021&dashboardId=201a11ca-c115-4a33-a9f7-941eceefbbd1&utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of evidence that ISG holds crypto on balance sheet, operates mining, offers crypto financial services, provides non‑financial services uniquely targeted to crypto firms, or supplies crypto/mining infrastructure, the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). Mentions of blockchain in ISG’s research are treated as general technology market coverage rather than a revenue stream derived from crypto asset activity, so no mechanism category receives a non‑zero weight. ([ar.isg-one.com](https://ar.isg-one.com/2023/assets/downloads/ISG_2023_AR_Form_10k.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
ISG publishes research that includes blockchain among many enterprise technologies; this could involve advisory discussions with clients evaluating blockchain, but no direct, material crypto-ecosystem service line was evidenced in calls or filings. Some transcript sources are behind paywalls, so representative open excerpts were used; none indicated crypto exposure. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/INFORMATION-SERVICES-GROU-879237/news/Transcript-Information-Services-Group-Inc-Q3-2024-Earnings-Call-Nov-08-2024-48310501/))

**Earnings calls:**

### Firm Overview
VZ Holding AG is a Switzerland-based financial services group focused on fee-based wealth management, retirement planning, and related banking services offered through VZ Depotbank. The company is listed on SIX Swiss Exchange and operates primarily in Switzerland with additional presence in Germany and the UK.

### Exposure Channels Identified
In November 2021, VZ VermögensZentrum (VZ Group) partnered with Sygnum Bank to give VZ clients access to digital asset services via Sygnum’s regulated B2B banking platform, enabling clients to buy, hold, and trade leading cryptocurrencies under a bank-grade framework. ([sygnum.com](https://www.sygnum.com/news/vz-vermoegenszentrum-partners-with-sygnum-bank/?utm_source=openai))  
VZ’s own guidance for clients (updated September 19, 2024) states that cryptocurrencies can be traded and safeguarded “at the VZ Depotbank,” with crypto assets held
…off the bank’s balance sheet and shown in clients’ custody statements; VZ Depotbank safekeeps digital assets in omnibus wallets and routes client orders via selected brokers/exchanges under Swiss regulatory standards.   
This is direct crypto financial services (trading/custody) delivered under VZ’s brand, initially enabled by a November 23, 2021 B2B partnership with Sygnum Bank. ([sygnum.com](https://www.sygnum.com/news/vz-vermoegenszentrum-partners-with-sygnum-bank/?utm_source=openai))  
Across reviewed filings, there is no disclosure suggesting the group holds cryptocurrencies on its own balance sheet or engages in mining or hardware infrastructure activities. ([vermoegenszentrum.ch](https://www.vermoegenszentrum.ch/sites/default/files/documents/investor-relations-annual-report-2023-chen.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
The finserv mechanism is set to 1.0 because VZ Depotbank offers regulated client trading and custody of cryptocurrencies, with explicit statements about execution, segregation, and safekeeping. There is no evidence of treasury crypto holdings (holders = 0.0), mining operations (miners = 0.0), crypto‑specific non‑financial services (bizserv = 0.0), or mining/infrastructure supply (infrastr = 0.0). Given strong company and partner confirmations plus at least one earnings‑call transcript anchor, certainty is 3. Crypto revenue and assets appear peripheral to group totals (client assets are off balance sheet), so RevShare is coded 1 and AssetShare 0. ([sygnum.com](https://www.sygnum.com/news/vz-vermoegenszentrum-partners-with-sygnum-bank/?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Management’s earnings calls reviewed did not detail crypto revenues or segment metrics; the offering is facilitated via Sygnum’s B2B platform, and VZ does not disclose take‑rates or volumes attributable to digital assets. As client crypto assets are custodied off balance sheet, the balance‑sheet impact is minimal; revenue materiality is inferred as minor due to lack of segment disclosure. Future changes in service scope or disclosure could warrant an update. ([sygnum.com](https://www.sygnum.com/news/vz-vermoegenszentrum-partners-with-sygnum-bank/?utm_source=openai))

A review of the company’s 2024 annual report and FY2024/H1 2025 results materials found no references to cryptocurrency, bitcoin, blockchain, mining, exchanges, custody, crypto trading for clients, or treasury holdings of digital assets. The filings describe revenue from classifieds (B2C/C2C) and ancillary services; a COO note mentions implementing “new payment methods,” without identifying crypto as a payment option. Dedicated earnings call transcripts were not publicly available; evaluation relied on FY2024 and H1 2025 results releases and the 2024 annual report. * Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Company press releases (2020, 2022); Annual Reports 2023–2024; Q2–Q3 2024 quarterly results materials

### Firm Overview
Next Biometrics Group ASA is a Norway‑based developer of fingerprint sensor technology, listed on the Oslo Stock Exchange, with products used across payments/fintech, government ID, access control and notebook markets. The company markets sensor modules and readers built on its proprietary Active Thermal technology and operates globally through subsidiaries in the U.S., China, Taiwan and India. ([nextbiometrics.com](https://nextbiometrics.com/wp-content/uploads/NEXT-Biometrics-Annual-report-2023.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Evidence within the 2017–2024 window shows exposure to crypto via hardware supplied to a crypto wallet manufacturer. In November 2020, the company announced an initial order for 10,000 sensors for an “ultra-secure blockchain wallet” built for NGRAVE; in February 2022 it disclosed a NOK 3.1 million recurring follow‑up order for its Access 200 fingerprint sensor from NGRAVE. These sales indicate participation in the crypto ecosystem as a component supplier, not as a custodian, exchange, miner, or investor. A company “Insights” post from January 2021 also referenced NGRAVE’s digital wallet using NEXT’s sensors “linked to the use of blockchain‑technology.” ([nextbiometrics.com](https://nextbiometrics.com/news_archive_2020/next-receives-purchase-order-of-initial-10000-sensors-for-crypto-currency-secure-blockchain-digital-wallet/))

### Weight Assignment
The firm is classified as providing non‑financial services to the crypto ecosystem, specifically hardware components integrated into third‑party crypto wallets; therefore bizserv = 1.0. There is no evidence that NEXT Biometrics holds crypto on balance sheet, mines crypto, offers exchange/custody/trading services, or supplies mining compute or energy; thus holders = miners = finserv = infrastr = 0.0. Given the limited and indirect nature of the linkage and the absence of any suggestion that the investor base treats the stock as a crypto proxy, invbase = 0. ([nextbiometrics.com](https://nextbiometrics.com/wp-content/uploads/NEXT-Biometrics-Annual-report-2023.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
No dedicated earnings‑call transcript within January 2017–December 2024 was retrievable; assessment relies on annual reports, quarterly results reports, and company/news releases, so certainty is capped at 2 per protocol. The NGRAVE‑related orders provide clear evidence of crypto‑adjacent activity, but the company does not disclose a separate crypto revenue line; materiality is inferred as minor. If future filings begin segmenting wallet‑related revenue or if the firm expands into direct crypto financial services, this classification should be revisited. ([nextbiometrics.com](https://nextbiometrics.com/investors/financial_reports_presentations/))

* Primary sources: Annual Reports 2023–2024; 1H 2024 results materials; 2024 OCBC press releases; 2021 Bloomberg interview

### Firm Overview
Oversea-Chinese Banking Corporation (OCBC) is a Singapore-headquartered banking group with operations across ASEAN and Greater China, offering consumer and corporate banking, treasury, wealth management (via Bank of Singapore) and insurance (via Great Eastern). Within 2017–2024, OCBC’s digital‑asset activities appear in treasury and corporate solutions initiatives rather than retail crypto products.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Evidence in 2024 shows OCBC launching an asset tokenisation platform and completing an inaugural tokenised bond for a corporate accredited investor in November 2024, with the bank describing customisable tokenised products and custody provided to clients. OCBC also became the first Singapore bank to enable institutional intraday lending using J.P. Morgan’s Onyx Digital Assets platform via blockchain‑based intraday reverse repos in October 2024. Separately, OCBC piloted a blockchain‑based conditional payments solution with Singapore’s Land Transport Authority in 2024. Earlier, in November 2021, the CEO stated the bank was studying setting up a crypto exchange; no subsequent evidence in 2017–2024 indicates a launch. These activities indicate exposure through providing regulated digital‑asset financial services; there is no evidence of crypto mining, proprietary crypto holdings, or exchange operations.

### Weight Assignment
OCBC’s exposure channel is classified as financial services due to documented issuance/custody of tokenised securities and participation in blockchain‑enabled financing and payments pilots; therefore finserv = 1.0. There is no support for holders, miners, infrastr or bizserv categories in the 2017–2024 window, so these are 0.0. Given the bank’s diversified franchise and lack of market‑proxy behavior tied to crypto cycles, invbase = 0. Materiality appears minor (RevShare = 1) and the balance sheet shows no crypto asset accumulation (AssetShare = 0).

### Ambiguities / Limitations
No earnings‑call transcript text could be retrieved; assessment relies on annual reports, results materials and press releases, so certainty is capped at 2 per protocol. OCBC’s initiatives center on tokenised securities and blockchain infrastructure rather than cryptocurrencies per se, which may have weaker linkage to crypto price movements; classification is based on the closest applicable mechanism (finserv) under the taxonomy. If future transcripts or filings disclose broader digital‑asset products (e.g., exchange, custody at scale, or stablecoin/crypto services), this classification should be revisited.

## OPERA AMERICAN DEPOSITARY SHARES 1:1

* Rank: 253 | Expo: 0.19 | id_str: US68373M1071
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Form 20‑F (FY2024), Q4 2023 earnings call, Opera Newsroom/blog posts (2018–2024)

### Firm Overview
Opera Limited is a Norway‑founded, NASDAQ‑listed internet company best known for its Opera, Opera GX and Opera Mini web browsers and related advertising/search monetization. Since 2018 the company has incorporated Web3 features, including an integrated non‑custodial wallet and, in 2023, the MiniPay stablecoin wallet inside Opera Mini for select markets.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Opera provides software that enables end‑users to access and transact with digital assets without Opera taking custody: it introduced a built‑in non‑custodial crypto wallet in 2018, launched the dedicated Crypto Browser in 2022, and in 2024 folded those Web3 features into its flagship browsers. In September 2023, Opera introduced MiniPay,
…a non-custodial stablecoin wallet built on the Celo blockchain and integrated into Opera Mini to enable low‑cost peer‑to‑peer payments; Opera’s release stresses that MiniPay is non‑custodial and relies on local partners for cash‑in/out rather than Opera taking custody or touching banking rails. In February 2024 Opera delisted the separate Crypto Browser and migrated its Web3 features into its flagship desktop and mobile browsers, reinforcing that Opera’s role is providing browser and wallet software rather than running an exchange or custody service. Within the 2017–2024 window there is no evidence that Opera mined crypto, issued crypto‑backed financial products, or held digital assets on its own balance sheet. ([investor.opera.com](https://investor.opera.com/node/9276/pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Opera’s exposure to crypto stems from building and distributing end‑user software (browsers and non‑custodial wallets) that interfaces with public blockchains and stablecoins; it does not take custody, operate a brokerage/exchange, or otherwise handle client assets. Therefore the appropriate mechanism is bizserv. No transcript, filing, or press source indicates proprietary crypto holdings, mining operations, or crypto‑backed financial products during 2017–2024. Accordingly, the mechanism weights are assigned as: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0. invbase = 0 reflects that Opera is primarily viewed as a browser/ads company rather than a market proxy for crypto prices. ([investor.opera.com](https://investor.opera.com/node/9276/pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Opera disclosed MiniPay activations but did not break out a crypto‑specific revenue segment in 2017–2024, suggesting crypto monetization was immaterial to reported revenues in the window. If Opera were to introduce custodial services, exchange functionality, or hold digital assets on its balance sheet in future periods, a finserv or holders weight could become appropriate; within the sample window, available sources indicate a non‑custodial, software‑only approach. ([blogs.opera.com](https://blogs.opera.com/africa/2024/03/minipay-reaches-2m-activations/?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 29, 2024 — Q4 2023 earnings call: “we are well‑positioned for the rebounding interest in fintech applications on Web3, where we continue to invest in technology and build use cases.” [Fintool transcript](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/OPRA/documents/transcripts/q4-2023) (also mirror PDF) (reportify) (https://reportify-1252068037.cos.ap-beijing.myqcloud.com/media/production/s_4674802_ea9b5dc4fb0071cdc7ad4c315e8e0487.pdf)
- Aug 30, 2022 — Q2 2022 earnings call: “the creation of new versions of our browsers has only just begun with GX and our crypto browsers.” [AlphaStreet transcript](https://news.alphastreet.com/opera-limited-opra-q2-2022-earnings-call-transcript/)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Apr 24, 2024 — Opera files its 2023 Form 20‑F (FY2023). [IR press release](https://investor.opera.com/news-releases/news-release-details/opera-files-its-2023-annual-report-form-20-f/)
- 2023 Annual Report (FY2023) notes launch of MiniPay stablecoin wallet in Sept 2023. [Annual report PDF](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReports/PDF/NASDAQ_OPRA_2023.pdf)
- Sep 13, 2023 — “MiniPay… integrated into Opera Mini… MiniPay is a non‑custodial wallet… [and] relies on local partners so users can add and withdraw stablecoins.” [Opera IR press release (PDF)](https://investor.opera.com/node/9276/pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Jan 2022 — Opera launches dedicated Crypto Browser beta (Web3 focus, built‑in wallet/NFT integrations). [Opera Crypto blog](https://blogs.opera.com/crypto/2022/01/opera-crypto-browser-project-web3/)
- Feb 2024 — Opera folds Crypto Browser features into flagship browsers and delists the experimental app. [Opera News blog](https://blogs.opera.com/news/2024/02/opera-delists-the-experimental-crypto-browser/)
- Mar 2024 — MiniPay reaches 2M activations across Africa six months post‑launch. [Opera Africa blog](https://blogs.opera.com/africa/2024/03/minipay-reaches-2m-activations/)
- Sep 14, 2023 — Coverage of MiniPay launch on Celo. [The Paypers](https://thepaypers.com/cryptocurrencies/opera-launches-minipay-a-stablecoin-wallet-on-the-celo-blockchain--1264408)

## HAPPIEST MINDS TECHNOLOGIES PVT

* Rank: 254 | Expo: 0.19 | id_str: INE419U01012

* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings call transcripts (Q1 FY2023, Q2 FY2023), Integrated Annual Report 2023–24, Blockchain services page (corporate website)

### Firm Overview
Happiest Minds Technologies Limited is an India‑based digital engineering and IT services company listed on the NSE/BSE, with offerings spanning product and digital engineering, infrastructure management and security services, and domain-focused solutions. Within its broader portfolio, the company has developed capabilities in emerging technologies including blockchain applied to enterprise use cases.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Happiest Minds provides enterprise blockchain services — consulting, platform engineering, smart‑contract development, dApp engineering, and blockchain security — as part of its digital transformation offerings, positioning it as a non‑financial services provider to the crypto/Web3 ecosystem. ([happiestminds.com](https://www.happiestminds.com/services/blockchain-solutions-services/))  
The company’s Integrated Annual Report 2023–24 highlights a case study implementing a blockchain‑enabled platform for trading pre‑owned luxury watches, including smart contracts for automated reconciliation. ([happiestminds.com](https://www.happiestminds.com/digital-integrated-annual-report-2023-2024/product-and-digital-engineering-services.php))  
Management commentary on earnings calls during 2022 referenced investing ahead in blockchain capabilities and building proofs‑of‑concept with customers, evidencing active delivery in this area during the 2017–2024 window. ([happiestminds.com](https://www.happiestminds.com/investors/Earnings%20Call/2022-2023-Q1/HappiestMinds_Earnings%20Call%20Transcript_July%2025%2C%202022.pdf))  
There is no indication that Happiest Minds operates an exchange, provides custody, mines cryptoassets, or holds crypto on its own balance sheet; its role is software and consulting enablement for clients. ([happiestminds.com](https://www.happiestminds.com/services/blockchain-solutions-services/))

### Weight Assignment
The firm’s crypto exposure mechanism is via non‑financial services to the ecosystem: it designs and builds blockchain solutions and smart contracts for enterprise clients without taking custody of client assets or issuing crypto‑backed financial products. No filings or transcripts indicate proprietary crypto holdings or mining. Accordingly, the classification is concentrated in bizserv (1.0) with zero weights for holders, miners, finserv, and infrastr. ([happiestminds.com](https://www.happiestminds.com/digital-integrated-annual-report-2023-2024/product-and-digital-engineering-services.php))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Crypto/Web3 appears as part of a broader digital services mix and is not reported as a separate revenue segment; materiality therefore cannot be precisely quantified from public disclosures in 2017–2024. Earnings call references focus on capabilities and use‑cases rather than wallet/custody or token holdings, supporting a bizserv (not finserv/holders) classification. ([happiestminds.com](https://www.happiestminds.com/investors/Earnings%20Call/2022-2023-Q1/HappiestMinds_Earnings%20Call%20Transcript_July%2025%2C%202022.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jul 22, 2022 — Q1 FY2023: “Whether Blockchain, Metaverse… [we are] building POCs… already working with customers to get a few use cases out.” [Happiest Minds transcript PDF] (https://www.happiestminds.com/investors/Earnings%20Call/2022-2023-Q1/HappiestMinds_Earnings%20Call%20Transcript_July%2025%2C%202022.pdf) ([happiestminds.com](https://www.happiestminds.com/investors/Earnings%20Call/2022-2023-Q1/HappiestMinds_Earnings%20Call%20Transcript_July%2025%2C%202022.pdf))
- Oct 21, 2022 — Q2 FY2023: “Like blockchain… we continue to invest quite aggressively before the real need is identified.” [Happiest Minds transcript PDF] (https://www.happiestminds.com/investors/Earnings%20Call/2022-2023-Q2/HappiestMinds_Earnings%20Call%20Transcript_Oct%2026%2C%202022.pdf) ([happiestminds.com](https://www.happiestminds.com/investors/Earnings%20Call/2022-2023-Q2/HappiestMinds_Earnings%20Call%20Transcript_Oct%2026%2C%202022.pdf))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- 2024 — Integrated Annual Report 2023–24: case study “Blockchain‑enabled Platform for Trading of Pre‑owned Luxury Watches” (smart contracts for reconciliation). [Annual report page] (https://www.happiestminds.com/digital-integrated-annual-report-2023-2024/product-and-digital-engineering-services.php) ([happiestminds.com](https://www.happiestminds.com/digital-integrated-annual-report-2023-2024/product-and-digital-engineering-services.php))
- FY2022–FY2024 — Investor materials/annual reports list blockchain among core next‑gen tech capabilities. **Financial news / other:**

[Blockchain services] (https://www.happiestminds.com/services/blockchain-solutions-services/) ([happiestminds.com](https://www.happiestminds.com/services/blockchain-solutions-services/))

## INDUSTRIAL & COML.BK.OF CHINA 'A'

* Rank: 255 | Expo: 0.19 | id_str: CNE000001P37
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Investor/analyst results briefings (Apr 2024, Aug 2024), Annual/Interim results materials (2019–2024), Regulatory context (PBOC 2021), Financial news (2018 blockchain patent)

### Firm Overview
Industrial and Commercial Bank of China Limited (ICBC) is a leading state-owned commercial bank headquartered in Beijing and listed in Shanghai and Hong Kong. It provides corporate, retail, treasury, and custody services domestically and internationally, and is among the world’s largest banks by assets.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across the 2017–2024 window, no evidence was found that ICBC held cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet, operated mining, or offered crypto‑asset trading, custody, or exchange services to clients. Mainland China’s regulatory stance made crypto transactions illegal in September 2021, and ICBC’s investor materials emphasize conventional custody and digital transformation rather than crypto services. ([loc.gov](https://www.loc.gov/item/global-legal-monitor/2021-10-13/china-central-bank-issues-new-regulatory-document-on-cryptocurrency-trading/?utm_source=openai))  
ICBC discusses “digital” initiatives (e.g., digital finance, digital assets as part of internal digitization themes), but these references are not to customer crypto‑asset products. The bank’s custody disclosures relate to traditional asset classes, and 2024 Hong Kong spot Bitcoin/Ether ETFs were launched with other custodians/sub‑custodians, not ICBC. ([v.icbc.com.cn](https://v.icbc.com.cn/userfiles/Resources/ICBCLTD/download/2024/ResultsBriefing20240422.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of any substantiated activity in crypto holding, mining, client crypto financial services, crypto‑oriented business services, or crypto‑specific infrastructure, ICBC is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). Mentions of “digital assets” in 2024 results briefings occur within a broader digital‑transformation program (D‑ICBC) rather than crypto‑asset offerings, and regulatory constraints in mainland China during 2017–2024 further support a zero‑exposure assessment across all mechanism categories. ([v.icbc.com.cn](https://v.icbc.com.cn/userfiles/Resources/ICBCLTD/download/2024/ResultsBriefing20240422.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- ICBC engages in technology innovation and has explored blockchain (e.g., a 2018 patent report), but this pertains to enterprise technology usage rather than crypto‑asset services and does not meet the threshold for bizserv/finserv classification. ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2018/04/30/icbcs-first-blockchain-patent-is-now-public?utm_source=openai))  
- Hong Kong’s 2024 spot crypto ETFs launched with other trustees/custodians; any later‑dated media speculation about stablecoin licensing interest falls outside the 2017–2024 sample and/or lacks primary confirmation for ICBC, so it is not used for classification. ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/04/15/hong-kong-regulators-approve-spot-bitcoin-and-ether-etfs-.html?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 30, 2024 — 2024 Interim Results Briefing (Q&A): “the scale of the mega asset management business exceeded RMB30 trillion, of which the domestic assets under custody reached RMB24.5 trillion.” [ICBC Q&A record] (https://v.icbc.com.cn/userfiles/resources/icbcltd/download/2024/QARecord20240914.pdf) ([v.icbc.com.cn](https://v.icbc.com.cn/userfiles/resources/icbcltd/download/2024/QARecord20240914.pdf))  
- Apr 22, 2024 — 2023 Annual Results Briefing (minutes): “We will step up the D‑ICBC drive, especially in the five fields of digital ecosystem, digital assets, digital technology, digital infrastructure and digital genes.” [ICBC briefing minutes] (https://v.icbc.com.cn/userfiles/Resources/ICBCLTD/download/2024/ResultsBriefing20240422.pdf) ([v.icbc.com.cn](https://v.icbc.com.cn/userfiles/Resources/ICBCLTD/download/2024/ResultsBriefing20240422.pdf))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- 2019 — Annual Results Announcement (overview of results and operations). [ICBC investor relations] (https://www.icbc.com.cn/icbcltd/investor%20relations/financial%20information/financial%20reports/2019annualresultsannouncement.htm) ([icbc.com.cn](https://www.icbc.com.cn/icbcltd/investor%20relations/financial%20information/financial%20reports/2019annualresultsannouncement.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- Sep 24, 2024 — Publication (custody business scale references). **Financial news / other:**
- Sep 24, 2021 — PBOC circular made all cryptocurrency transactions illegal in mainland China (regulatory context). [Library of Congress summary] (https://www.loc.gov/item/global-legal-monitor/2021-10-13/china-central-bank-issues-new-regulatory-document-on-cryptocurrency-trading/) ([loc.gov](https://www.loc.gov/item/global-legal-monitor/2021-10-13/china-central-bank-issues-new-regulatory-document-on-cryptocurrency-trading/?utm_source=openai))  
- Apr 15, 2024 — Hong Kong approved spot Bitcoin and Ether ETFs; arrangements involved other custodians/sub‑custodians (e.g., OSL, BOCI‑Prudential). ## ANSARADA GROUP DEAD - DELIST.09/09/24

* Rank: 256 | Expo: 0.19 | id_str: AU0000124422
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: ASX filings (FY2024 Preliminary Final Report), Q3–Q4 FY2024 earnings calls, Transaction/delisting notices (ASX, ACCC, deListed)

### Firm Overview
Ansarada Group Limited was an Australia‑based software company providing virtual data rooms and workflow tools for dealmaking, governance, risk, compliance and infrastructure procurement. It was listed on the ASX prior to its 2024 acquisition and subsequent delisting.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across the 2017–2024 window, Ansarada’s materials describe a single SaaS business that sells virtual data rooms and adjacent GRC/procurement software; no filings or earnings calls indicate crypto holdings, mining, crypto‑asset custody/trading, or other client crypto financial services. Its FY2024 Preliminary Final Report frames the company as a one‑segment B2B SaaS provider of information governance, board management, risk and compliance, and online deal rooms, with no crypto‑specific products or balance‑sheet assets disclosed. ([cdn-api.markitdigital.com](https://cdn-api.markitdigital.com/apiman-gateway/ASX/asx-research/1.0/file/2924-02837961-2A1540574%26v%3Dfc9bdb61fe50ea61f8225e24ce041a0e155a9400))  
Management’s Q3–Q4 FY2024 call commentary focuses on ARR, deal volume by region, pricing/packaging and cash flow mechanics, again without any references to crypto or blockchain activities. ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/asx/and/investor-relations/earnings-call/q3-2024))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of evidence that Ansarada held crypto assets, conducted mining, provided crypto financial services, delivered dedicated non‑financial services to the crypto ecosystem, or supplied crypto‑mining infrastructure, the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). The company’s filings and transcripts consistently position it as a horizontal VDR/GRC SaaS platform, and transaction/delisting notices in 2024 do not alter this assessment for the 2017–2024 sample period. ([cdn-api.markitdigital.com](https://cdn-api.markitdigital.com/apiman-gateway/ASX/asx-research/1.0/file/2924-02837961-2A1540574%26v%3Dfc9bdb61fe50ea61f8225e24ce041a0e155a9400))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- As a general‑purpose VDR provider, Ansarada likely served clients across many industries (potentially including crypto firms), but such incidental usage does not meet the threshold for bizserv/finserv exposure absent explicit, material crypto activity by Ansarada itself. ([ansarada.com](https://www.ansarada.com/data-room?utm_source=openai))  
- The company was acquired and delisted in 2024; available FY2024 filings and late‑period calls show no crypto involvement, and no earlier‑period disclosures (2017–2023) indicating crypto exposure were found in ASX reports reviewed for this classification. ([accc.gov.au](https://www.accc.gov.au/public-registers/mergers-and-acquisitions-registers/public-informal-merger-reviews-register-2002-25/datasite-llc-ansarada-group-limited?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**

- Apr 29, 2024 — Q3 FY2024 Earnings Call: “We continue to see positive cash flow.” [AlphaSpread] (https://www.alphaspread.com/security/asx/and/investor-relations/earnings-call/q3-2024) ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/asx/and/investor-relations/earnings-call/q3-2024))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Aug 13, 2024 — Preliminary Final Report FY2024 (unaudited): “online deal rooms for customers via a business to business (‘B2B’) software (‘SaaS’) based platform.” [ASX filing] (https://cdn-api.markitdigital.com/apiman-gateway/ASX/asx-research/1.0/file/2924-02837961-2A1540574%26v%3Dfc9bdb61fe50ea61f8225e24ce041a0e155a9400) ([cdn-api.markitdigital.com](https://cdn-api.markitdigital.com/apiman-gateway/ASX/asx-research/1.0/file/2924-02837961-2A1540574%26v%3Dfc9bdb61fe50ea61f8225e24ce041a0e155a9400))

## B RILEY FINANCIAL

* Rank: 257 | Expo: 0.19 | id_str: US05580M1080
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (FY2022), Q3 2021 earnings call, company press/IR on crypto conference, financial news on Core Scientific DIP financing

### Firm Overview
B. Riley Financial, Inc. is a U.S.-based diversified financial services holding company with businesses spanning investment banking, brokerage/capital markets, wealth management, financial consulting, retail liquidation, brands and principal investments. Its filings describe these segments and do not disclose a crypto exchange/custody business or treasury crypto holdings over the 2017–2024 period. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1464790/000121390023030681/fars0423_brileyfinancial.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management stated on the Q3 2021 earnings call that B. Riley Securities was “very active in raising capital for miners” and was hosting a dedicated “Crypto Conference” in New York, adding that it had been “the first bank to bring a crypto miner public with Stronghold Digital Mining’s recent IPO.” These activities expose the firm to crypto through client advisory/underwriting fees and related corporate access events rather than through custody or trading of digital assets. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/28/b-riley-financial-inc-rily-q3-2021-earnings-call-t/?source=isafpbcs0000001&utm_campaign=firehose&utm_medium=feed&utm_source=sharewise))  
Beyond underwriting, B. Riley (through affiliates) provided debtor‑in‑possession financing to Bitcoin miner Core Scientific during its Chapter 11 process, creating credit exposure to a crypto‑sector client; Core Scientific later announced full pay‑down of this DIP facility in January 2024. These relationships tie results to crypto‑sector conditions without the company itself mining, holding, or custoding crypto assets. ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2023/03/02/core-scientific-bankruptcy-judge-set-to-approve-70m-financing-deal-from-b-riley?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
The identified exposure is best characterized as providing services around the crypto ecosystem (investment banking/advisory, conferences, and credit to sector companies) without directly handling digital assets. Consistent with the taxonomy, this maps to bizserv rather than finserv, since the company is not operating a crypto exchange, custody platform, or crypto investment product and does not hold crypto on its balance sheet in filings reviewed. Accordingly, bizserv = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/28/b-riley-financial-inc-rily-q3-2021-earnings-call-t/?source=isafpbcs0000001&utm_campaign=firehose&utm_medium=feed&utm_source=sharewise))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Investment banking is a financial activity, but under the project’s definitions it only counts as finserv if the firm directly issues, trades, custodies, or operationally holds crypto assets; available sources indicate activity centered on underwriting/advisory and events, so bizserv is used. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/28/b-riley-financial-inc-rily-q3-2021-earnings-call-t/?source=isafpbcs0000001&utm_campaign=firehose&utm_medium=feed&utm_source=sharewise))  
- The company’s credit/DIP financing to Core Scientific represents balance‑sheet exposure to a crypto client but not to crypto assets themselves; no disclosures reviewed show treasury crypto holdings. ([d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net](https://d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net/_247b48dff9d7f88dd9555f480fabbde8/corescientific/news/2024-01-04_Core_Scientific_Inc_Announces_Full_Pay_Down_of_43.pdf))  
- Evidence is anchored in Q3 2021 call remarks and corroborating news/press; later transcripts in 2022–2024 reviewed provided limited additional crypto specificity, so certainty is set to 3 (clear transcript plus corroboration). ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/28/b-riley-financial-inc-rily-q3-2021-earnings-call-t/?source=isafpbcs0000001&utm_campaign=firehose&utm_medium=feed&utm_source=sharewise))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 28, 2021 — Q3 2021: “We’re not trading crypto, we have been very active in raising capital for miners… [and] we are hosting an in‑person Crypto Conference in New York… [the] first bank to bring a crypto miner public with Stronghold Digital Mining’s recent IPO.” [The Motley Fool] (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/28/b-riley-financial-inc-rily-q3-2021-earnings-call-t/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/28/b-riley-financial-inc-rily-q3-2021-earnings-call-t/?source=isafpbcs0000001&utm_campaign=firehose&utm_medium=feed&utm_source=sharewise))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Mar 15, 2023 — FY2022 Form 10‑K: diversified segments (capital markets, wealth, consulting, liquidation, brands/principal investments); no crypto exchange/custody or treasury crypto holdings disclosed. ## NOVAGOLD RESOURCES

* Rank: 258 | Expo: 0.18 | id_str: CA66987E2069
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (FY2023), Q4 2023 earnings call, company project overview

### Firm Overview
NOVAGOLD RESOURCES is a Canadian mineral development company listed in the U.S. and Canada whose principal asset is the Donlin Gold project in Alaska, held through Donlin Gold LLC. During 2017–2024 the project remained in development with no mining operations or production revenue. ([novagold.com](https://novagold.com/donlin-gold/project-overview/?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across filings and earnings calls within 2017–2024, management consistently presents NOVAGOLD as a pure-play gold developer focused on advancing Donlin; there is no evidence of crypto mining, treasury crypto holdings, or digital-asset financial services. The FY2023 Form 10-K emphasizes that Donlin is not in production and the company has no revenue from mining operations, with disclosures centered on gold prices, permitting and project development. Consequently, no mechanism in the crypto taxonomy applies. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/NG/documents/transcripts/q4-2023))

### Weight Assignment
No substantive references to cryptocurrency exposure were identified in 2017–2024 transcripts, filings, or news. The company neither mines crypto, holds crypto on the balance sheet, nor provides crypto financial or non-financial services, and there is no evidence of targeted crypto-related infrastructure sales. Under the project’s rules, the firm is therefore coded unclass = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1173420/000117184324000389/ng20231130_10k.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Searches included multiple earnings-call transcripts and investor materials; none in 2017–2024 showed crypto exposure. Occasional rhetorical references to “crypto” by management appear in later commentary (2025) but are outside the sample window and do not indicate any operational exposure under this taxonomy. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/NG/documents/transcripts/q4-2023))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jan 25, 2024 — Q4 2023: “We see NovaGold as a pure play on Donlin…” — Fintool transcript. [Fintool] (https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/NG/documents/transcripts/q4-2023)  
- Oct 3, 2024 — Q3 2024 earnings call listing (transcript index). [MarketScreener] (https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/NOVAGOLD-RESOURCES-INC-1410991/news-call-transcripts/)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Jan 24, 2024 — Form 10‑K (FY2023): “The Donlin Gold project is not in production or currently under construction, and we have no ongoing mining operations or revenue from mining operations.” [SEC EDGAR] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1173420/000117184324000389/ng20231130_10k.htm)
- Company project overview describing Donlin Gold as NOVAGOLD’s principal asset. [NOVAGOLD IR] (https://novagold.com/donlin-gold/project-overview/)

**Financial news / other:**
- Oct 2, 2024 — NOVAGOLD reports Q3 2024 results; update focuses on Donlin Gold development. [GlobeNewswire] (https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2024/10/02/2957384/0/en/NOVAGOLD-Reports-Third-Quarter-2024-Financial-Results.html)

## DOUBLEDOWN INTERACTIVE ADR 20:1

* Rank: 259 | Expo: 0.18 | id_str: US25862B1098

* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 20-F filing (FY2023, filed March 28, 2024), Q1 2024 earnings call, Q4 2022 earnings call

### Firm Overview
DoubleDown Interactive Co., Ltd. is a Korea-based developer and publisher of digital games best known for social casino titles such as DoubleDown Casino, with operations and listing in the U.S. via ADRs. In late 2023 the company expanded into European real‑money iGaming through the acquisition of SuprNation AB. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1799567/000119312524080583/d750578d20f.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024 filings and earnings calls, management consistently characterizes DoubleDown as a social casino and iGaming operator; no disclosures indicate crypto mining, holding crypto on the balance sheet, or providing crypto‑asset financial services. Filings describe revenue flows through mainstream app‑store and platform payment systems (Apple, Google, Facebook, Amazon) and related processing fees, not digital‑asset rails. Multiple transcripts during 2023–2024 focus on social casino KPIs (ARPDAU, payer conversion) and the SuprNation iGaming business, with no material references to blockchain, NFTs, or cryptocurrencies. Consequently, none of the crypto‑exposure mechanism categories apply. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1799567/000119312524080583/d750578d20f.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of any substantive crypto activity in transcripts, the 2024 Form 20‑F, and related press materials, DoubleDown is coded unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism weights at 0.0. The firm’s operations (social casino and regulated iGaming) and payment channels are conventional and do not involve mining, custody, issuance, or crypto‑specific infrastructure that would map to the taxonomy. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1799567/000119312524080583/d750578d20f.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified within the January 2017–December 2024 sample window. Public materials accessed occasionally require third‑party transcript aggregators, but the content is consistent across sources and does not suggest crypto exposure. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/DOUBLEDOWN-INTERACTIVE-CO-154724785/news-call-transcripts/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 8, 2024 — Q1 2024: “Total Q1 revenue was $88.1 million, with $79.8 million from social casino free‑to‑play and $8.3 million from SuprNation.” [Fintool] (https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/DDI/documents/transcripts/q1-2024)  
- Feb 7, 2023 — Q4 2022: “In addition to maintaining a strong base business in social casino, we… announced our upcoming entry into the iGaming space in Europe with our planned acquisition of SuprNation.” [Fintool] (https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/DDI/documents/transcripts/q4-2022)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Mar 28, 2024 — Form 20‑F: “We develop and publish digital gaming content on various mobile and web platforms.”; platform payments (Apple, Facebook, Google, Amazon) detailed. [SEC EDGAR] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1799567/000119312524080583/d750578d20f.htm)  
- Nov 11, 2024 —
- Nov 11, 2024 — Form 6‑K: press release and unaudited financials for Q3 2024 furnished. [SEC EDGAR] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1799567/000162828024047001/ddi-20240930_d2.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- Feb 11, 2025 — “Earnings call transcript: Doubledown Interactive Q4 2024 shows steady growth.” [Investing.com] (https://m.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-doubledown-interactive-q4-2024-shows-steady-growth-93CH-3863212?ampMode=1)
