## CHIN.COMMS.SVS.'H'

* Rank: 260 | Expo: 0.18 | id_str: CNE1000002G3
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Annual/Interim Reports (2021–2024), H1 2024 earnings call

### Firm Overview
China Communications Services Corporation Limited is a Hong Kong–listed H‑share company (stock code 552) that provides telecommunications infrastructure services, business process outsourcing, and applications/content/other ICT services primarily in mainland China, with additional non‑operator and overseas customers. Filings emphasize digital infrastructure, smart city, emergency management/security, and green/low‑carbon projects, reflecting a broad ICT services mandate rather than financial or asset‑holding activities. ([chinaccs.com.hk](https://www.chinaccs.com.hk/en/ir/reports/ir2024/ir2024.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024 investor materials and transcripts, management discusses results and strategy around telecom infrastructure, data centers, cloud/AI computing, and systems integration; there are no disclosures of cryptocurrency mining, treasury crypto holdings, exchange/custody operations, or crypto‑linked financial products. The H1 2024 earnings call and multiple reports focus on service revenue across TIS, BPO, and ACO segments. While corporate materials mention R&D in “blockchain” and a “blockchain data service platform,” these are framed as enterprise software/IT capabilities for government and industry digitalization rather than products that involve handling or transacting in cryptoassets. Under the project’s rules, such generic blockchain R&D does not constitute a crypto exposure mechanism. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/CHINA-COMMUNICATIONS-SERV-6165947/news/Transcript-China-Communications-Services-Corporation-Limited-H1-2024-Earnings-Call-Aug-27-2024-47763705/))

### Weight Assignment
No substantive crypto activity is evidenced within the January 2017–December 2024 window. The company’s disclosures and call content center on conventional ICT services (consultation/design, construction, O&M, software/platform integration) and sectoral initiatives (smart city, computing power, green energy). Mentions of “blockchain” appear only as part of broader technology portfolios and are not tied to cryptoasset custody, issuance, trading, or mining. Accordingly, all mechanism weights are set to 0.0 and unclass = 1.0; invbase = 0 due to the absence of an investor‑base proxy to crypto sentiment. ([chinaccs.com.hk](https://www.chinaccs.com.hk/en/ir/reports/ir2024/ir2024.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Earnings call transcripts for this Hong Kong–listed issuer are limited and often behind paywalls; one H1 2024 call transcript was identified and an FY2018 results presentation transcript is indexed, but detailed content is sparse publicly. Company reports reference “blockchain” platforms, yet with no link to cryptoassets; if future disclosures were to tie these platforms to digital‑asset custody or tokenized products, classification would need revision. Within the 2017–2024 sample, no evidence indicates material crypto exposure. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/CHINA-COMMUNICATIONS-SERV-6165947/news/Transcript-China-Communications-Services-Corporation-Limited-H1-2024-Earnings-Call-Aug-27-2024-47763705/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 27, 2024 — H1 2024: “Good afternoon, ladies and gentlemen. I’m Terence from China Com Service.” — transcript header. [MarketScreener] (https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/CHINA-COMMUNICATIONS-SERV-6165947/news/Transcript-China-Communications-Services-Corporation-Limited-H1-2024-Earnings-Call-Aug-27-2024-47763705/)

### Firm Overview
Tubacex, S.A. is a Spain‑based industrial group that manufactures seamless stainless steel and high‑alloy tubular solutions, with global operations and customers across energy, industrial, and mobility/aerospace end markets. The company communicates through annual reports, results presentations, and regulatory press releases.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024 investor documents and news coverage, Tubacex discusses manufacturing, backlog, margins, geographic mix, and sector exposure (OCTG, industrial, powergen, aerospace); there is no discussion of cryptocurrency mining, cryptoasset treasury holdings, or digital‑asset financial services. Annual reports and consolidated financial statements focus on steel tube production and related services without balance‑sheet cryptoassets or crypto‑linked product lines. Results presentations and 2024 press releases similarly emphasize order book, EBITDA, and OCTG/industrial strategy, with no references to crypto, exchanges, custody, tokenization, or mining workloads. These sources collectively support a classification of no material crypto exposure within the 2017–2024 sample. ([tubacex.com](https://www.tubacex.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/Annual-report-2023-Tubacex.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
No substantive crypto activity is evidenced in filings, presentations, or reputable financial news during January 2017–December 2024. Mentions center on stainless steel/OCTG and industrial solutions rather than any crypto‑related mechanisms. Therefore, all mechanism weights are set to 0.0 with unclass = 1.0. Investor‑base amplifier is 0 given the absence of market characterization as a crypto proxy. ([tubacex.com](https://www.tubacex.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/Annual-report-2023-Tubacex.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Public, English‑language earnings call transcripts for Tubacex within 2017–2024 are limited; coverage is primarily via filings, presentations, and brief news summaries. If future disclosures were to indicate activities such as crypto mining hardware supply, dedicated datacenter/compute for blockchain workloads, crypto custody/payment products, or treasury crypto holdings, this assessment would need revision. Within the sample period, available evidence indicates no material crypto exposure. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/TUBACEX-S-A-69371/news/Tubacex-S-A-Reports-Earnings-Results-for-the-Third-Quarter-and-Nine-Months-Ended-September-30-202-48272328/))

## FINANCIAL INSTITUTIONS

* Rank: 262 | Expo: 0.18 | id_str: US3175854047
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 2021 annual report; Q4 2021 and Q2 2022 earnings calls; PR/news coverage (PR Newswire Jan 2022; CoinDesk Oct 2021)

### Firm Overview
Financial Institutions, Inc. is a U.S. financial holding company headquartered in Warsaw, New York, and is the parent of Five Star Bank and other subsidiaries. The company is listed on Nasdaq under ticker FISI and provides community banking, insurance brokerage, and wealth management services primarily in New York State.

### Exposure Channels Identified
During 2021–2022, Five Star Bank announced and discussed an integrated consumer bitcoin service enabling customers to buy, sell and hold bitcoin within its digital banking platform, in partnership with Q2 Holdings and NYDIG. Management described the NYDIG-powered service on earnings calls and in the 2021 annual report, positioning it as a digital offering with transaction-fee economics while NYDIG provided custody. External coverage identified Five Star Bank as one of the first U.S. banks to roll out the NYDIG/Q2 solution. No evidence indicates proprietary crypto custody by the bank, crypto holdings on the balance sheet, or crypto mining activities. ([earningscall.biz](https://earningscall.biz/e/nasdaq/s/fisi/y/2021/q/q4))

### Weight Assignment
The company’s exposure to crypto arises through providing a consumer-facing bitcoin trading/holding service via third-party partners, which fits the finserv category. Earnings call remarks emphasize a “safe and sound” NYDIG-custodied solution and fee-at-the-margin economics, with no treasury crypto holdings or mining operations. Accordingly, finserv is set to 1.0 and all other mechanism weights to 0.0. Given the scale described and lack of segment breakouts, crypto-related revenue appears minor (<~5%), and no crypto assets appear on the balance sheet. ([earningscall.biz](https://earningscall.biz/e/nasdaq/s/fisi/y/2021/q/q4))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
While 2021–2022 sources describe launch and partnerships, later filings (e.g., 2023 Form 10‑K) do not detail the product’s uptake or revenues, and do not reference bitcoin explicitly. Industry reporting indicates certain NYDIG consumer trading integrations were later retooled or sunset at some institutions, which may have affected program continuity; however, Five Star Bank did not provide specific public updates in filings reviewed. Classification is therefore anchored to documented 2021–2022 activity within the sample window. ([s201.q4cdn.com](https://s201.q4cdn.com/409218819/files/doc_financials/2023/q4/fisi-10k-20231231-with-exhibits.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q4 2021 earnings call: “That’s why our partnership with NYDIG is an opportunity… responsive to customer interest and demand for the ability to buy and sell and hold Bitcoin… we would make money at the margin in terms of the transactions…” [EarningsCall.biz] (https://earningscall.biz/e/nasdaq/s/fisi/y/2021/q/q4)  
- Q2 2022 earnings call: “We continue to collaborate with our regulators on our exciting efforts to offer Bitcoin to consumers in partnership with NYDIG.” [ROIC.ai] (https://www.roic.ai/quote/FISI/transcripts/2022/2)

**Filings (10‑K / annual report):**
- 2021 Annual Report: “We… enable our customers to transact in Bitcoin… inside the Five Star Bank Digital Banking platform… partners… Q2 and… NYDIG… among the first banks to deliver… buy, sell and hold Bitcoin…” [Financial Institutions, Inc.] (https://s201.q4cdn.com/409218819/files/doc_financials/2021/ar/FISI-2021-Annual-Report-for-Websites.pdf)  
- 2023 Form 10‑K: Company/business description; no explicit bitcoin service disclosure located. [Financial Institutions, Inc.] (https://s201.q4cdn.com/409218819/files/doc_financials/2023/q4/fisi-10k-20231231-with-exhibits.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Oct 22, 2021 — Five Star Bank named among first to roll out NYDIG/Q2 bitcoin buys. [CoinDesk] (https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/10/22/bitcoin-at-your-bank-nydig-names-first-2-firms-to-roll-out-btc-buys)  
- Jan 25, 2022 — Five Star Bank announces integrated bitcoin investment services via NYDIG. [PR Newswire] (https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/five-star-bank-and-nydig-announce-partnership-to-bring-safe-and-secure-integrated-bitcoin-investment-services-to-existing-and-future-checking-account-holders-301468191.html)  
- Context: Some institutions later paused NYDIG consumer trading integrations as the vendor retooled offerings. [CU Today] (https://www.cutoday.info/THE-feature/Deal-Is-Off-CUs-Are-Telling-Members)

* Primary sources: 2022–2023 Form 10‑K filings; Lemonade Foundation/L3C press and blog (Mar 2022–Mar 2023)

### Firm Overview
Lemonade Inc. is a U.S.-based digital insurer listed on the NYSE (ticker LMND) that offers renters, homeowners, pet, life, and car insurance across the U.S. and parts of Europe. Within its filings, the company notes that the separate Lemonade Foundation launched a blockchain-based, parametric climate microinsurance initiative for smallholder farmers, but the program sits at the Foundation level rather than within Lemonade Inc.’s operating entities. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/l/NYSE_LMND_2023.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024, no evidence indicates Lemonade Inc. held crypto on its balance sheet, mined digital assets, ran a crypto exchange/custody service, or otherwise generated crypto-related revenue. Company 10‑K disclosures reference the Lemonade Foundation’s “first‑of‑its‑kind blockchain‑based insurance product” and name partners such as Chainlink and Avalanche, but do not attribute crypto assets or income to Lemonade Inc. itself. A March 28, 2023 Business Wire release further clarifies that “The Lemonade Foundation is an independent legal entity, and is not owned or controlled by Lemonade, Inc. or its shareholders.” Consequently, the Foundation’s blockchain activity does not constitute a corporate crypto mechanism for LMND. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/l/NYSE_LMND_2023.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Because all substantive blockchain/crypto activity identified is conducted by the separate Lemonade Foundation and Lemonade’s SEC filings do not disclose corporate crypto holdings, mining, or financial services touching crypto, the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). No other mechanism category meets the threshold for a non‑zero weight. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/l/NYSE_LMND_2023.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Metromile, acquired by Lemonade in July 2022, previously disclosed bitcoin holdings in 2021; however, Lemonade’s subsequent filings do not indicate inherited or ongoing digital-asset holdings at the LMND level, suggesting any such exposure was immaterial or disposed before/after closing. The classification therefore anchors to Lemonade Inc.’s own disclosures and the explicit separation of the Foundation’s crypto initiative. ([fintel.io](https://fintel.io/doc/sec-metromile-inc-1819035-10q-2021-november-15-18946-464?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Filings (10‑K / annual report):**
- Apr 24, 2024 — 2023 Form 10‑K: “the Foundation launched a first of its kind blockchain based insurance product… launched as part of the Lemonade Foundation Crypto Climate Coalition… partners: Hannover Re, Pula, Chainlink, Avalanche, Etherisc and DAOstack.” [Lemonade, Inc.] (https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/l/NYSE_LMND_2023.pdf)  
- Apr 27, 2023 — 2022 Form 10‑K: same Foundation description; no corporate crypto assets or services disclosed. [Lemonade, Inc.] (https://s24.q4cdn.com/139015699/files/doc_financials/2022/ar/lmnd-2022-annual-report-final-4-27-23.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Mar 28, 2023 — “Nearly 7,000 Kenyan Farmers Protected by the Lemonade Crypto Climate Coalition”; notes Foundation is “an independent legal entity… not owned or controlled by Lemonade, Inc.” [Business Wire] (https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20230328005342/en/Nearly-7000-Kenyan-Farmers-Protected-by-the-Lemonade-Crypto-Climate-Coalition)  
- Mar 22, 2022 — “Introducing the Lemonade Crypto Climate Coalition” (Foundation-led initiative; partners include Avalanche and Chainlink). [Lemonade Blog] (https://www.lemonade.com/blog/crypto-climate-coalition/)

**Background/legacy (Metromile):**
- Nov 15, 2021 — Metromile 10‑Q: accounting for bitcoin as digital assets; disclosed BTC holdings pre‑acquisition. [SEC via Fintel] (https://fintel.io/doc/sec-metromile-inc-1819035-10q-2021-november-15-18946-464)

</small>

## BNY MEL.STGC.MUN. BF.

* Rank: 264 | Expo: 0.18 | id_str: US09662E1091
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 2024 Form N‑CSR annual report; fund website; third‑party closed‑end fund profile

### Firm Overview
BNY Mellon Strategic Municipal Bond Fund, Inc. is a U.S. exchange‑listed closed‑end fund (ticker DSM; CUSIP 09662E109) that seeks federally tax‑exempt income by investing in municipal bonds. It is managed by BNY Mellon Investment Adviser with Insight North America as sub‑adviser, and its portfolio and disclosures indicate a municipal fixed‑income strategy.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024, the fund’s stated objective and fundamental policy are to invest primarily in municipal obligations that provide income exempt from U.S. federal personal income tax; no activities involving cryptoasset custody, trading, mining, or balance‑sheet holdings are indicated in shareholder reports. The 2024 Form N‑CSR explicitly sets an 80%+ municipal‑obligations policy under normal conditions, and performance benchmarks reference the Bloomberg U.S. Municipal Bond Index, consistent with a muni bond focus. The sponsor’s fund page and third‑party profiles likewise describe a municipal‑only mandate. Accordingly, no crypto‑exposure mechanism applies and the fund is classified as unclassified. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/855887/000085588725000001/lp10852.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Because the fund invests in municipal securities and does not operate crypto financial services, hold crypto on its balance sheet, provide mining or crypto‑directed infrastructure, or deliver non‑financial services to the crypto ecosystem, all mechanism weights are set to zero and unclass is set to 1.0. No investor‑base crypto amplifier is evident for this municipal bond CEF. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/855887/000085588725000001/lp10852.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Closed‑end funds like DSM typically do not host earnings calls; classification therefore relies on shareholder reports and fund literature. While BNY Mellon (the parent corporation) has separately publicized digital‑asset initiatives, those do not pertain to this municipal bond fund and are not reflected in DSM’s filings or objectives. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/855887/000085588725000001/lp10852.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Filings (shareholder reports / SEC):**
- Nov 30, 2024 — Annual Report (Form N‑CSR): “investment objective is to maximize current income exempt from federal income tax… [and] invest… at least 80% of its net assets in municipal obligations.” [SEC EDGAR] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/855887/000085588725000001/lp10852.htm)

**Fund literature:**
- Fund overview page: “Seeks to provide… monthly federal tax‑exempt dividend by investing primarily in high quality municipal bonds”; shows CUSIP 09662E109. [BNY Investments] (https://www.bny.com/investments/us/en/individual/products/closed-end-funds/fund/bny-mellon-strategic-municipal-bond-fund-inc.html)

**Third‑party profile:**
- Fund profile summarizing objective and 80%+ municipal‑obligation policy; category: Municipal. [CEF Connect] (https://www.cefconnect.com/fund/DSM)

</small>

## DWS GROUP

* Rank: 265 | Expo: 0.18 | id_str: DE000DWS1007
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q2 2024 earnings call; 2023 Annual Report; 2023–2024 DWS press releases; 2024 exchange/news coverage

### Firm Overview
DWS Group GmbH & Co. KGaA is a Frankfurt‑based global asset manager listed in Germany and majority‑owned by Deutsche Bank. Its franchise spans active, passive (Xtrackers) and alternatives, with digital‑asset exposure arising through branded crypto exchange‑traded products and a joint venture to issue a euro‑denominated stablecoin.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management explicitly discussed the establishment of AllUnity, a joint venture with Galaxy Digital and Flow Traders, to launch a BaFin‑regulated euro stablecoin, positioning DWS in crypto financial services. The firm also launched Xtrackers Galaxy Physical Bitcoin and Ethereum ETCs in 2024, providing investors with crypto exposure via listed exchange‑traded commodities in Germany and at SIX. These activities involve issuing and managing crypto‑linked investment products, a textbook fit for the finserv category, with no evidence of proprietary crypto treasury holdings, mining, or crypto‑directed infrastructure. ([download.dws.com](https://download.dws.com/download?elib-assetguid=87b9c1fe3e474582b234a6608d8b4724&publishLocationGuid=eacbc9cf4b8e4d2189eb69cd09e2ff4f))

### Weight Assignment
All substantiated crypto activity is financial‑product based: sponsoring/launching Xtrackers crypto ETCs and forming AllUnity to issue a regulated stablecoin. There is no evidence that DWS mines crypto, holds digital assets for treasury purposes, or supplies mining hardware/compute; nor is there a separate non‑financial services role to the crypto ecosystem. Accordingly, finserv is set to 1.0 and all other mechanism weights to 0.0. Given DWS’s large diversified AUM base, crypto fee income appears minor (<5%), and the collateral for ETCs is held at the product issuer/custodian, not on DWS’s balance sheet, implying low asset share. ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2024/04/05/dws-galaxy-digital-list-exchange-traded-commodities-offering-btc-eth-exposure-in-germany?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The Xtrackers Galaxy Physical Cryptocurrency ETC securities are issued by Xtrackers Digital Markets ETC AG (not part of DWS), though they are launched and marketed under the DWS Xtrackers brand in partnership with Galaxy; classification therefore focuses on DWS’s role as sponsor/manager of crypto financial products rather than consolidation. AllUnity’s intended euro stablecoin was announced in December 2023 with launch targeted for 2025; within the 2017–2024 sample, evidence reflects the JV’s formation and strategic intent rather than product launch economics. No indications were found that DWS is treated by markets as a pure crypto proxy; invbase is set to 0. ([etf.dws.com](https://etf.dws.com/de-de/ueber-uns/pressemitteilungen/dws-bietet-mit-neuen-xtrackers-etcs-einfachen-zugang-zu-bitcoin-und-ethereum/?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jul 24, 2024 — Q2 2024 results call transcript: “we have established a joint venture called AllUnity… which aims to launch the first BaFin regulated Euro stablecoin in 2025.” [DWS IR] (https://download.dws.com/download?elib-assetguid=87b9c1fe3e474582b234a6608d8b4724&publishLocationGuid=eacbc9cf4b8e4d2189eb69cd09e2ff4f)

**Filings (Annual Report / reports):**
- 2023 Annual Report: notes intention to form AllUnity with Galaxy and Flow Traders to issue a euro‑denominated stablecoin. [AnnualReports.com (DWS 2023)] (https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/d/dws-group-gmbh-co-kgaa_2023.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Apr 5, 2024 — “DWS, Galaxy Digital List Exchange‑Traded Commodities Offering BTC, ETH Exposure in Germany.” [CoinDesk] (https://www.coindesk.com/business/2024/04/05/dws-galaxy-digital-list-exchange-traded-commodities-offering-btc-eth-exposure-in-germany)
- Apr 2024 — “DWS launches new Xtrackers ETCs providing easy access to bitcoin and ethereum (in partnership with Galaxy Digital).” [Xtrackers by DWS] (https://etf.dws.com/en-ch/about-us/pressemitteilungen/dws-launches-new-xtrackers-etcs-providing-easy-access-to-bitcoin-and-ethereum/)
- Oct 29, 2024 — “DWS Xtrackers Becomes New Crypto ETP Issuer at SIX Swiss Exchange” (BTC and ETH products listed). [SIX Swiss Exchange] (https://www.six-group.com/en/newsroom/media-releases/2024/20241029-xtrackers-crypto-etp-issuer.html)
- Dec 13, 2023 — “DWS, Flow Traders and Galaxy announce the intention to launch AllUnity.” [DWS press release] (https://www.dws.com/our-profile/media/media-releases/dws-flow-traders-and-galaxy-announce-the-intention-to-launch-allunity/)

## IBEX

* Rank: 266 | Expo: 0.18 | id_str: BMG4690M1010
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 2 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q4 2022 earnings call; FY2024 Form 20‑F; Q3 FY2022 investor presentation

### Firm Overview
IBEX Limited is a Nasdaq‑listed business process outsourcing and customer experience company with delivery centers across onshore U.S., nearshore (Jamaica, Honduras, Nicaragua) and offshore (Philippines, Pakistan) locations. Its industry verticals include Retail & E‑commerce, HealthTech, Telecommunications and FinTech, among others. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1720420/000172042024000042/ibex-20240630.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
IBEX’s crypto exposure arises from providing non‑financial CX/BPO services to crypto companies rather than holding or transacting in digital assets itself. In the Q4 FY2022 call, management noted that “the one market that we have that we’ve done a really good job of winning in is in the crypto world,” adding that exposure was “about 5% of total” during the 2022 downturn. An investor presentation for Q3 FY2022 lists “#1 Crypto Exchange” among IBEX’s top client positions, indicating contracted CX support for a leading exchange. There is no evidence of proprietary crypto holdings, mining, issuance/custody of crypto products, or crypto‑directed hardware/compute. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/09/23/ibex-limited-ibex-q4-2022-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence shows IBEX engages with crypto firms strictly as a CX/BPO vendor, including support for a top crypto exchange, with management quantifying crypto‑client exposure at roughly 5% at one point in 2022. As such, the exposure mechanism is classified entirely as bizserv, with no basis to assign weights to holders, miners, finserv or infrastr. Bizserv is therefore set to 1.0 and all other categories to 0.0. Given management’s “~5%” comment within the 2017–2024 window, RevShare is coded as 2 (~5–15%), while AssetShare is 0 as no crypto assets are held on balance sheet. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/09/23/ibex-limited-ibex-q4-2022-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Do not confuse IBEX Limited (the Nasdaq‑listed BPO/CX provider) with the unrelated “IBEX” bitcoin‑payments firm (e.g., IBEX Pay/Wallet); the latter is a separate private fintech entity. The specific crypto clients are not named publicly, and the share of revenue from crypto clients likely varied after 2022; filings in 2024 discuss FinTech mix but do not separately break out crypto. ([ibexpay.io](https://www.ibexpay.io/?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Sep 22, 2022 — Q4 FY2022 earnings call: “the one market that we have that we’ve done a really good job of winning in is in the crypto world… ours was probably about 5% of total.” [The Motley Fool] (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/09/23/ibex-limited-ibex-q4-2022-earnings-call-transcript/)

**Filings (10‑K / 20‑F / 6‑K):**
- Sep 12, 2024 — Annual Report (Form 20‑F): describes global delivery footprint and target verticals including FinTech. [SEC EDGAR] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1720420/000172042024000042/ibex-20240630.htm)
- May 2024 — Q3 FY2024 filing: notes FinTech vertical percentage movement (context for vertical mix). [SEC EDGAR] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1720420/000172042024000031/ibex-20240331.htm)

**Financial news / other (investor materials):**
- May 18, 2022 — Q3 FY2022 investor presentation: “#1 Crypto Exchange” listed among top client positions. [IBEX IR (PDF)] (https://investors.ibex.co/static-files/691948f5-07c6-4cf4-8ee9-9d32f27127c8)
- May 18, 2022 — Press release on Q3 FY2022: highlights FinTech/HealthTech growth and vertical mix. [IBEX IR] (https://investors.ibex.co/news-releases/news-release-details/ibex-limited-announces-third-quarter-fiscal-year-2022-financial)

## ONMOBILE GLOBAL

* Rank: 267 | Expo: 0.18 | id_str: INE809I01019
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.7, bizserv 0.3, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: FY2022–FY2024 annual reports; Q1 FY2024 earnings call; May 2022 ONMO crypto-payments press release

### Firm Overview
OnMobile Global Limited is a Bengaluru‑based mobile entertainment company listed in India that has pivoted from ringback tones and content services toward mobile gaming, including its ONMO cloud gaming platform and Challenges Arena offerings for telecom operators and consumers. Management communications during FY2023–FY2024 emphasize gaming as the growth focus. ([onmobile.com](https://www.onmobile.com/?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
OnMobile’s direct crypto link is through ONMO enabling cryptocurrency payments: in May 2022 the company announced players outside India could use Bitcoin to pay entry fees on ONMO, with plans to add more cryptocurrencies to the ONMO wallet. This constitutes a crypto‑payments feature offered to end users. ([onmobile.com](https://www.onmobile.com/home/media/onmo-launches-crypto-payments-for-players-globally))  
Separately, OnMobile invested in Tech4Billion Media (Chingari), a short‑video app that launched the $GARI token, and ONMO went live inside the Chingari app in February 2022, indicating commercial ties to a Web3‑oriented platform; however, the investment itself was in equity/pre‑Series A preference shares, not crypto tokens. ([onmobile.com](https://www.onmobile.com/home/media/onmobile-leads-dollar-13-million-investment-round-in-chingari?utm_source=openai))  
Annual reports during the sample window explicitly state the company had not traded or invested in cryptocurrency or virtual currency in the relevant years, suggesting no treasury‑style crypto holdings on balance sheet. ([onmobile.com](https://www.onmobile.com/sites/default/files/FY-2022-Annual-Report.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence supports classifying the primary mechanism as finserv because ONMO directly offers crypto payments to its users (Bitcoin acceptance and an ONMO wallet roadmap). A secondary, smaller bizserv weight reflects OnMobile’s role as a gaming/content partner to a Web3 platform (Chingari), which ties a portion of its activity to crypto‑ecosystem clients without OnMobile itself taking custody of digital assets. There is no indication of proprietary coin holdings, mining, or crypto‑specific hardware provision, so holders, miners and infrastr are set to 0.0. The finserv/bizserv split is set at 0.7/0.3 given the directness of the payments feature versus the ancillary nature of the Chingari partnership. ([onmobile.com](https://www.onmobile.com/home/media/onmo-launches-crypto-payments-for-players-globally))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The scale and persistence of crypto‑payments usage on ONMO are not disclosed in filings, so revenue materiality appears minor and unquantified within FY2017–FY2024. OnMobile’s exposure via Chingari is through equity, not token holdings; while transcripts discuss Chingari’s funding and blockchain associations, they do not provide crypto‑asset balances for OnMobile. Only one clearly crypto‑relevant earnings‑call quote within the window was identified; additional calls did not materially change the classification. ([onmobile.com](https://www.onmobile.com/sites/default/files/Q1%20FY2024%20Investor%27s%20Call%20Transcript.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 8, 2023 — Q1 FY2024 earnings call: “As far as Chingari is concerned, they are working well… they’ve got some investments recently from Aptos, which is one of a Tier 1 blockchain company.” [OnMobile IR (PDF)](https://www.onmobile.com/sites/default/files/Q1%20FY2024%20Investor%27s%20Call%20Transcript.pdf)

**Filings (Annual Report / reports):**
- FY2024 Annual Report: “The Company has not traded or invested in Crypto currency or virtual currency during the current year.” [OnMobile AR FY2024 (PDF)](https://www.onmobile.com/sites/default/files/FY-2024-Annual-Report.pdf)
- FY2022 Annual Report: similar statement; also discloses investment in Tech4billion Media Pvt. Ltd. (Chingari) as 22,508 Pre‑Series A CCPS. [OnMobile AR FY2022 (PDF)](https://www.onmobile.com/sites/default/files/FY-2022-Annual-Report.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- May 4, 2022 — “ONMO launches Crypto payments for players globally” (Bitcoin accepted; more cryptocurrencies planned in ONMO wallet). [OnMobile press release](https://www.onmobile.com/home/media/onmo-launches-crypto-payments-for-players-globally)
- Feb 6, 2022 — “ONMO social esports gaming platform goes live on Chingari.” [ONMO Media](https://www.onmo.com/media/)
- Apr 1, 2021 — “OnMobile leads $13 million investment round in Chingari.” [OnMobile press release](https://www.onmobile.com/home/media/onmobile-leads-dollar-13-million-investment-round-in-chingari)

## ASE TECHNOLOGY HOLDING

* Rank: 268 | Expo: 0.18 | id_str: TW0003711008
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 1.0, unclass 0.0

* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Counterparty 20-F filings (2023–2024); ASE 20-F; Q4 2020 earnings call

### Firm Overview
ASE Technology Holding Co., Ltd. is a Taiwan‑based global leader in outsourced semiconductor assembly and test (OSAT), providing IC packaging, testing and system‑in‑package solutions across end markets including mobile, automotive, and high‑performance computing. The company is listed in Taiwan (TWSE: 3711) and via ADR in the U.S. (NYSE: ASX), operating extensive packaging and test facilities worldwide. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ASE_Group?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
ASE’s crypto linkage arises through infrastructure: it provides packaging and testing services to fabless designers of blockchain/cryptocurrency ASICs. For example, Intchains Group Limited (a blockchain ASIC designer) discloses, “We started our cooperation with ASE Group in 2021,” listing ASE as an OSAT partner for packaging and testing its ASIC chips. There is no indication that ASE holds crypto on its own balance sheet or offers custody, exchange, or payments products; the exposure is via supplying back‑end semiconductor services to crypto‑mining chip vendors. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1895597/000119312524070138/d574315d20f.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Classification is set to infrastr = 1.0 because the only evidenced mechanism is ASE’s role as a semiconductor packaging/testing supplier to crypto‑ASIC designers; there is no evidence of proprietary coin holdings (holders), mining operations (miners), or crypto financial/non‑financial services (finserv/bizserv). Given ASE’s scale and diversified end markets, crypto‑related work appears incidental relative to total revenue and assets, so RevShare and AssetShare are coded 0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1895597/000119312524070138/d574315d20f.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Earnings call transcripts reviewed did not contain explicit crypto references; the infrastructure linkage is established primarily through counterparty filings (e.g., Intchains 20‑F) rather than ASE’s own calls. The magnitude of crypto‑related packaging/testing within ASE’s revenue mix is not disclosed in ASE filings, so materiality is inferred as negligible. Historical Bitmain draft IPO materials reportedly referenced ASE among packaging/testing providers, but a stable primary copy was not retrieved in this run and thus is not relied upon. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1895597/000119312524070138/d574315d20f.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 5, 2021 — Q4 2020 earnings call: “With high‑performance computing, the cloud… we’re seeing more application released into smart devices, electrical vehicle, and IoT.” [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/02/05/ase-technology-holding-co-ltd-asx-q4-2020-earnings/)

**Filings (10‑K / 20‑F / reports):**

- 2023/2024 — Intchains Group Ltd. Form 20‑F: “We started our cooperation with ASE Group in 2021” for packaging and testing of ASIC chips for blockchain applications. [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1895597/000119312524070138/d574315d20f.htm)

### Exposure Channels Identified
No crypto-related activities were identified for Compugen during 2017–2024. Earnings call transcripts focus on clinical trial progress and finances and do not reference cryptocurrencies, mining, blockchain, or digital-asset services. The company’s Form 20-F filings similarly contain no references to cryptocurrency holdings, mining operations, or crypto financial products, indicating no material linkage to the crypto ecosystem. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/02/26/compugen-ltd-cgen-q4-2020-earnings-call-transcript/))

With no evidence of treasury crypto holdings, mining operations, crypto financial services, non-financial services to crypto firms, or crypto-directed infrastructure, the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/05/20/compugen-ltd-cgen-q1-2019-earnings-call-transcript.aspx))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified regarding crypto exposure. Note: Compugen Ltd. (biotech; CGEN) is distinct from Compugen Inc. (Canadian IT services), which is unrelated to this issuer. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Compugen_Inc.?utm_source=openai))

## DIACEUTICS

* Rank: 270 | Expo: 0.18 | id_str: GB00BJQTGV64
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0

* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Annual reports (2019–2023); RNS/Investegate results announcements (2021–2024); H1 2024 results presentation

### Firm Overview
Diaceutics plc is a UK-based healthcare technology company listed on AIM (ticker: DXRX) that provides data, analytics and tech-enabled services to pharmaceutical and biotech companies to improve the commercialization of precision medicine diagnostics. Its offerings are delivered via the DXRX platform, which integrates global real‑world diagnostic testing data and a network of laboratories to help clients identify and reach eligible patients.

### Exposure Channels Identified
A review of results announcements, investor materials and annual reports shows Diaceutics focuses on precision‑medicine data and analytics (DXRX platform, Insight and Engagement solutions) for pharma and biotech customers; no activities involve holding cryptocurrencies, mining, or offering crypto‑financial products/custody. Materials emphasize platform adoption, recurring revenue growth and diagnostic data/engagement capabilities, with no references to cryptocurrencies, bitcoin or blockchain as part of operations. A keyword check of the 2021 annual report returned no instances of “cryptocurrency,” “bitcoin,” or “blockchain,” supporting the absence of crypto‑related exposure. Company profile and RNS releases consistently describe a diagnostics data platform business model rather than any digital‑asset activity. ([investegate.co.uk](https://www.investegate.co.uk/announcement/rns/diaceutics--dxrx/final-results/7491767))

### Weight Assignment
No evidence indicates treasury crypto holdings (holders), mining (miners), crypto exchanges/custody/ETPs (finserv), non‑financial services specifically for crypto firms (bizserv), or hardware/hosting/energy sold to miners (infrastr). All retrieved sources describe a healthcare data platform and services company whose revenue is tied to pharma diagnostics commercialization, not digital assets. Accordingly, the firm is coded unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism weights = 0.0. ([investegate.co.uk](https://www.investegate.co.uk/announcement/rns/diaceutics--dxrx/final-results/7491767))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified regarding crypto exposure. Public transcripts of earnings calls were not located on third‑party transcript aggregators; evidence relies on company RNS/results materials and annual reports, which contained no crypto/digital‑asset disclosures or discussion. A keyword search of the Annual Report 2021 PDF found no occurrences of “cryptocurrency,” “bitcoin,” or “blockchain.” ([investors.diaceutics.com](https://investors.diaceutics.com/articles/h1-2024-results-presentation))

## VONTOBEL HOLDING

* Rank: 271 | Expo: 0.17 | id_str: CH0012335540
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Press releases on Bitcoin-linked products and custody (2016–2019); Half‑Year Report 2019; Annual reports; SIX Structured Products listings; Financial news; Transcript indexes (2019–2024)

### Firm Overview
Vontobel Holding AG is a Zurich‑based investment firm active in wealth and asset management and structured investment solutions, listed on SIX Swiss Exchange (ticker: VONN). Since 2016–2017 it has expanded into digital‑asset investment products and, from 2019, B2B custody for digital assets within its regulated banking infrastructure.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Vontobel was among the first Swiss issuers to list Bitcoin tracker certificates (2016–2017) and subsequently launched short mini‑futures on Bitcoin, enabling listed long and short exposure for investors on SIX; these are crypto investment products issued by the bank. It also introduced an open‑end Bitcoin tracker and crypto portfolio certificates that trade on SIX Structured Products, evidencing ongoing issuance activity in crypto‑linked securities. In January 2019, Vontobel launched “Digital Asset Vault,” a custody and execution solution allowing financial intermediaries (banks, asset managers, wealth managers) to offer clients buy/sell, transfer and custody of digital assets within Vontobel’s banking infrastructure. Management reporting in H1 2019 explicitly notes extending offerings “in the area of cryptocurrencies” and providing institutional custody via Digital Asset Vault. ([vontobel.com](https://www.vontobel.com/en-ch/about-vontobel/media/communications/tracker-certificates-on-bitcoin-vontobel-is-increasing-the-investment-volume-3197/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence shows direct provision of crypto financial services: issuance of listed crypto‑linked structured products (tracker certificates, mini‑futures) and a regulated B2B custody/execution platform (Digital Asset Vault). There is no indication that Vontobel mines digital assets, strategically HODLs crypto on its own balance sheet, or supplies mining hardware/energy. Consequently, the exposure is coded entirely as finserv = 1.0, with other mechanism weights = 0.0. Given that crypto activity appears to be a small portion of group revenues, RevShare is set to 1 and AssetShare to 0. ([vontobel.com](https://www.vontobel.com/en-ch/about-vontobel/media/communications/tracker-certificates-on-bitcoin-vontobel-is-increasing-the-investment-volume-3197/?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Public, crypto‑specific remarks in earnings call transcripts for 2017–2024 were not retrievable without paywalls; transcript listings confirm call coverage but full texts are gated, so the certainty score is capped at 2 per protocol. The economic materiality of crypto within group revenue is not separately disclosed; tracker certificates may be hedged synthetically and custody assets are off‑balance‑sheet, limiting precision on revenue/asset shares. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/VONTOBEL-HOLDING-AG-69606/news-call-transcripts/))

## OGE ENERGY

* Rank: 272 | Expo: 0.17 | id_str: US6708371033
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 1.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2023), Q3–Q4 2024 earnings calls, SEC-filed electric service agreement (2024)

### Firm Overview
OGE Energy Corp. is a regulated utility holding company headquartered in Oklahoma City, operating primarily through its subsidiary Oklahoma Gas & Electric Company, which provides electric service in Oklahoma and western Arkansas. Within this core utility business, exposure to cryptocurrencies arises from serving power to third‑party crypto mining facilities under utility tariffs and load‑control programs.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Evidence indicates OG&E supplies electricity to at least one dedicated cryptocurrency mining customer under an executed “Will Serve” electric service agreement specifying a 17 MW mining load. Management also discusses “crypto load” on earnings calls, noting it is subject to direct load control and contributes to commercial load growth. There is no indication that the company mines digital assets, holds cryptocurrency on its balance sheet, or provides financial services related to crypto. Accordingly, the exposure mechanism is energy infrastructure supporting miners rather than direct participation in crypto markets.

### Weight Assignment
The company’s documented activity is limited to supplying power infrastructure to crypto miners, including a signed 17 MW service agreement and management commentary describing crypto load under direct load control. There is no evidence of holdings, mining, financial services, or ancillary non‑financial services. Therefore, the exposure is coded entirely as infrastr = 1.0, with all other mechanism weights = 0.0. Given the utility’s large revenue base, crypto‑related revenue appears minor; RevShare is set to 1 and AssetShare to 0.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Crypto‑specific revenue or customer concentration is not separately disclosed in filings, so materiality is inferred as minor relative to total revenue. Some transcript access is via third‑party aggregators; however, the statements quoted are specific and corroborated by company IR event listings. The scale and persistence of crypto load may change as large‑load customers (data centers/crypto) are negotiated over time.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 5, 2024 — Q3 2024 Earnings Call: “the driver within that has been the crypto mining load that we’ve had now for quite some time.” — [Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-oge-energy-corp-q3-2024-performance-and-outlook-93CH-3707501)
- Feb 19, 2025 — Q4 2024 Earnings Call: “some of the crypto load … do not really have a net addition to our capacity needs because they’re on direct load control.” — [Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-oge-energy-beats-q4-2024-forecasts-stock-rises-93CH-3877678)
- IR events page confirming Q3 and Q4 2024 earnings call dates/presentations. — [OGE Energy IR — Events & Presentations](https://ogeenergy.gcs-web.com/events-presentations)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Feb 2024 — 2023 Annual Report (10‑K): risk discussion references “industries such as cryptocurrency.” — [SEC filing PDF](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1021635/000095017024039019/2023_oge_ars.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Feb 13, 2024 — Electric Service “Will Serve” Agreement between OG&E and SPRE Watonga OK, LLC: “Cryptocurrency mining operation to total 17 MW.” — [SEC EDGAR exhibit EX‑10.14](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/2084026/000143774925039073/ex_902244.htm)

## DATAVAULT AI

* Rank: 273 | Expo: 0.17 | id_str: US86633R6099
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.7, bizserv 0.3, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filing (FY2024), Q3 2024 earnings call, SEC 8-Ks (September and December 2024)

### Firm Overview
Datavault AI Inc. (formerly WiSA Technologies, Inc.) is a U.S.-based technology company that historically developed wireless spatial audio semiconductors and, as of December 31, 2024, acquired information technology assets, patents and trademarks associated with “Data Vault” and related platforms. Following this late‑2024 asset purchase, the firm describes a pivot toward Web3‑enabled data management and monetization software leveraging blockchain, tokenization and AI. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1682149/000141057825000600/dvlt-20241231x10k.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management discussed the Data Vault transaction on the November 15, 2024 Q3 earnings call, highlighting a move into “blockchain and artificial intelligence” and an “information data exchange” to monetize data assets, including described revenue splits on transactions. These activities entail issuing and enabling transactions in tokenized data and other digital assets, which aligns with crypto financial services rather than merely providing peripheral services. Filings and company materials further portray the flagship Data Vault platform as a “cyber‑secure asset tokenization platform” with capabilities to mint NFTs and generate smart contracts, reinforcing a direct crypto/ Web3 product footprint. There is no evidence that the company mines or treasury‑holds cryptocurrencies, nor that it supplies mining hardware or energy infrastructure. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-wisa-technologies-reports-robust-growth-in-q3-93CH-3726601))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence centers on the launch and commercialization plans for Data Vault’s Information Data Exchange and tokenization stack, which directly touch digital assets and on‑chain transactions; therefore finserv is primary. A secondary allocation to bizserv reflects broader AI/HPC and data‑over‑sound capabilities and software licensing that may not always require on‑chain components. No indication of mining, proprietary crypto treasury holdings, or crypto‑specific infrastructure provision was found. Hence: finserv 0.7, bizserv 0.3; others 0.0. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-wisa-technologies-reports-robust-growth-in-q3-93CH-3726601))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The pivot occurred at year‑end 2024, so crypto‑attributable revenue during 2017–2024 appears negligible and disclosures emphasize future commercialization; RevShare and AssetShare are set accordingly. While multiple filings and the Q3 2024 earnings call describe blockchain/tokenization features, quantified crypto revenue or customer adoption within the sample window are not reported. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1682149/000141057825000600/dvlt-20241231x10k.htm))

### Evidence

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 15, 2024 — Q3 2024 Earnings Call: “we announced… our purchase of assets from Data Vault Holdings for the IP and trademarks around Data Vault and Adio.” — [Investing.com transcript](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-wisa-technologies-reports-robust-growth-in-q3-93CH-3726601) ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-wisa-technologies-reports-robust-growth-in-q3-93CH-3726601))
- Nov 15, 2024 — Q3 2024 Earnings Call: “We have a blockchain and artificial intelligence play at Data Vault…” — [Investing.com transcript](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-wisa-technologies-reports-robust-growth-in-q3-93CH-3726601) ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-wisa-technologies-reports-robust-growth-in-q3-93CH-3726601))
- Nov 15, 2024 — Q3 2024 Earnings Call: “We… simplify… into an exchange. We sell data on behalf of our clientele… on our information data exchange.” — [Investing.com transcript](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-wisa-technologies-reports-robust-growth-in-q3-93CH-3726601) ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-wisa-technologies-reports-robust-growth-in-q3-93CH-3726601))

## ICHIGO

* Rank: 274 | Expo: 0.17 | id_str: JP3120010008
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 2 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings calls (FY2023, Q1 FY2025), TDnet filings (September–October 2024), corporate presentations (2023–2024)

### Firm Overview
Ichigo Inc. is a Tokyo-listed Japanese real estate and asset management company (TSE: 2337) focused on value‑add investments and sponsorship of listed J‑REITs and renewable assets. In recent years the company has also developed a “digital real estate” line issuing tokenized real estate investment products in Japan. ([ichigo.gr.jp](https://www.ichigo.gr.jp/en/company/about_company.html?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Beginning in 2022, Ichigo (via wholly‑owned Ichigo Owners) launched the Ichigo Residence Token program and, by 2024, had issued multiple real‑estate security tokens, with sales of a fifth token announced on September 20, 2024; management and filings explicitly describe these as security tokens recorded on a blockchain platform and regulated under Japan’s Financial Instruments and Exchange Act. Secondary trading occurs on Osaka Digital Exchange’s START market, and Ichigo disclosures note use of Boostry’s blockchain platform. Earnings calls in 2023–2024 referenced “securitized token offerings” and subsequent residence token deals as part of the firm’s asset‑management growth. There is no evidence that Ichigo mines crypto, holds crypto as treasury, or supplies mining hardware/energy infrastructure. ([release.tdnet.info](https://www.release.tdnet.info/inbs/ek/140120240920587053.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Ichigo directly issues and distributes blockchain‑based security tokens backed by real estate and reports related asset sales and revenues, which squarely fits crypto financial services (custody/issuance/trading of digital assets). No materials indicate proprietary crypto treasury holdings, mining activities, or crypto‑specific infrastructure provision. Accordingly, the assignment is finserv 1.0 with all other mechanisms 0.0. ([release.tdnet.info](https://www.release.tdnet.info/inbs/ek/140120240920587053.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Ichigo’s products are regulated security tokens (digital securities) rather than volatile, free‑floating cryptocurrencies; classification here treats tokenized real‑asset products as part of “finserv.” Revenue quantification by token deal is disclosed (e.g., FY25/2 revenue impact and Q1 FY25/2 flow earnings tied to residence tokens), but granular crypto‑attributable revenue shares within the 2017–2024 window are not broken out beyond presentation disclosures. No evidence suggests a crypto‑themed investor base in Ichigo’s equity. ([release.tdnet.info](https://www.release.tdnet.info/inbs/ek/140120240920587053.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**

## STONEX GROUP

* Rank: 275 | Expo: 0.17 | id_str: US8618961085
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings call (Q4 2022), Form 10-K (FY2024), press releases (June 2022 launch; Dec 2024 Ireland VASP), product PR (May 2022 BTC cash‑settled swap)

### Firm Overview
StoneX Group Inc. is a U.S.‑listed global financial services network providing execution, clearing, market access and payments across asset classes to commercial, institutional and retail clients. The company operates brands including FOREX.com and City Index and has developed a digital assets offering alongside its core brokerage and risk‑management businesses.

### Exposure Channels Identified
StoneX operates a dedicated digital‑assets unit, StoneX Digital LLC, launched in June 2022 to provide institutional clients access to trading and custody solutions for cryptocurrencies and related products. In its FY2024 10‑K, the company disclosed that StoneX Digital International Limited (Ireland) pursued virtual‑asset registration and the EU’s MiCA authorization pathway; on December 4, 2024, StoneX announced Central Bank of Ireland approval to operate as a Virtual Asset Service Provider. Management also stated on the November 22, 2022 earnings call that StoneX would “continue to support [clients] by facilitating trading in a growing number of listed [crypto] derivatives as well as publicly listed ETFs,” and that it would “soon have crypto FX and physical gold” on StoneX One platforms. Earlier in May 2022, StoneX executed its first cash‑settled BTC/USD swap for institutional FX clients, evidencing direct participation in crypto financial services. ([ir.stonex.com](https://ir.stonex.com/node/21016/pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence shows StoneX provides crypto financial services — issuance/operation of trading access, derivatives execution, custody and VASP‑regulated services — but does not indicate proprietary crypto treasury holdings, mining, or hardware/energy infrastructure directed at mining. Therefore, the mechanism is assigned entirely to finserv (1.0), with all other categories at 0.0. The FY2024 10‑K regulatory disclosures and VASP approval, together with management’s 2022 remarks and the BTC swap PR, support this classification. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/913760/000091376024000187/intl-20240930.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
StoneX does not separately disclose crypto‑specific revenues or assets in a way that allows precise sizing within total company results; digital‑asset activity appears to be primarily client execution/custody and derivatives access rather than balance‑sheet holdings. No evidence indicates a crypto‑themed investor base in StoneX’s equity during 2017–2024.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 22, 2022 — Q4 2022: “The crypto market has gone through some… changes… We will continue to support these clients by facilitating trading in a growing number of listed derivatives as well as publicly listed ETFs…”; “We will soon have crypto FX… to both international and domestic StoneX One platforms.” — [Strike.Market transcript](https://strike.market/stocks/SNEX/earnings-call-transcripts/175934)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Nov 29, 2024 — Form 10‑K (FY2024): Notes Irish VASP regime/MiCA and that “StoneX Digital International Limited… is in the closing stages of registration as a VASP… with a view to applying for authorization as a CASP.” — [SEC filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/913760/000091376024000187/intl-20240930.htm)
- Dec 4, 2024 — PR: “StoneX Digital International Limited… approved by the Central Bank of Ireland, as a Virtual Asset Service Provider.” — [GlobeNewswire](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2024/12/04/2991645/11961/en/StoneX-Group-Inc-Approved-to-Operate-Digital-Asset-Business-in-Ireland-as-a-Virtual-Asset-Service-Provider.html)
- Jun 8, 2022 — PR: Company launches StoneX Digital LLC to provide institutional access to digital‑asset products and services. — [StoneX IR (PDF)](https://ir.stonex.com/node/21016/pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- May 17, 2022 — PR: StoneX executes first cash‑settled BTC/USD swap via StoneX Markets LLC for institutional FX clients. — [PR Newswire (via StockTitan)](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/stonex-executes-first-bitcoin-cash-settled-swap-301548799.html)

</small>

## WARNER MUSIC GROUP A

* Rank: 276 | Expo: 0.17 | id_str: US9345502036
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings calls (Q1–Q4 2022), company press releases (2022–2023), Form 10‑K (FY2024)

### Firm Overview
Warner Music Group Corp. is a U.S.-listed global music company operating recorded music labels and music publishing (Warner Chappell), with revenues primarily from streaming, licensing, and artist services across more than 70 countries. Its FY2024 Form 10‑K shows Recorded Music at ~81% of revenue and Music Publishing at ~19%, providing context for the scale against which any Web3/NFT initiatives sit. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1319161/000131916124000039/wmg-20240930.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
From 2019 onward, management discussed and pursued Web3 initiatives including NFTs, metaverse experiences, and blockchain‑based fan engagement in partnership with third parties. Earnings calls in 2022 highlighted investments/partnerships (e.g., Dapper Labs, The Sandbox, POAP, Splinterlands) and artist NFT drops, positioning these as emerging, non‑financial, experimental revenue streams. The company announced press‑release partnerships with OpenSea and The Sandbox and a multi‑year collaboration with Polygon/LGND Music for a collectibles platform, underscoring ongoing Web3 activity. None of the evidence indicates proprietary crypto custody/treasury holdings, mining, or provision of financial services. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/11/22/warner-music-group-corp-wmg-q4-2022-earnings-call/))

### Weight Assignment
WMG’s crypto link arises from delivering entertainment and fan‑engagement experiences on crypto‑native platforms (NFTs, metaverse concerts, POAPs) without touching customer assets, operating an exchange, or mining. This fits the non‑financial “business services” channel to the crypto ecosystem (events/experiences, content collaborations). Therefore: bizserv = 1.0; all other mechanism categories = 0.0. Evidence is drawn from multiple 2022 earnings‑call statements and company press releases; filings provide scale context and the absence of balance‑sheet crypto holdings. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/02/08/warner-music-group-corp-wmg-q1-2022-earnings-call/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Management framed Web3/NFTs as early‑stage experiments and did not break out crypto‑specific revenue or assets in filings, making sizing imprecise; classification rests on qualitative disclosures and PRs rather than quantified segments. Categorizing artist NFT/ metaverse initiatives as “bizserv” requires interpretation under the taxonomy, but no evidence supports finserv, holders, miners, or infrastr. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/11/22/warner-music-group-corp-wmg-q4-2022-earnings-call/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 8, 2022 — Q1 FY2022: “From collectibles to music royalties, Web3 represents an exciting future… Since our investment in Dapper Labs in 2019… In January alone, we launched… Blockparty… OneOf… [and]… partner[ed] with The Sandbox to build out WMG Land… virtual experiences in NFT.” — [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/02/08/warner-music-group-corp-wmg-q1-2022-earnings-call/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/02/08/warner-music-group-corp-wmg-q1-2022-earnings-call/))
- May 10, 2022 — Q2 FY2022: “There is now a lot of buzz about Web3… partnerships… Roblox, Fortnite and The Sandbox… relationships… Genies, Blockparty, OneOf and Dapper Labs… partnered with blockchain gaming developers Splinterlands… [and] POAP to mint shared memories as NFTs.” — [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/05/10/warner-music-group-corp-wmg-q2-2022-earnings-call/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/05/10/warner-music-group-corp-wmg-q2-2022-earnings-call/))
- Aug 9, 2022 — Q3 FY2022: “We continue to harness… Web3… Warner Records UK… NFT collection Stick Men Toys… peaking at No. 2 on OpenSea… We look at areas like Web3 with NFTs… having huge potential.” — [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/08/09/warner-music-group-corp-wmg-q3-2022-earnings-call/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/08/09/warner-music-group-corp-wmg-q3-2022-earnings-call/))
- Nov 22, 2022 — Q4 FY2022: “Our work in Web3… accelerated over the last 12 months… started back in 2019 when we invested in… Dapper Labs… WMG Land… is now live in The Sandbox.” — [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/11/22/warner-music-group-corp-wmg-q4-2022-earnings-call/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/11/22/warner-music-group-corp-wmg-q4-2022-earnings-call/))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Nov 21, 2024 — Form 10‑K (FY2024): Recorded Music 81% / Publishing 19% of revenue; no crypto‑asset holdings disclosed. — [SEC filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1319161/000131916124000039/wmg-20240930.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1319161/000131916124000039/wmg-20240930.htm))
- Sep 29, 2022 — “Warner Music Group and OpenSea join forces to expand Web3 opportunities for artists.” — [WMG press release](https://www.wmg.com/news/warner-music-group-and-opensea-join-forces-to-expand-web3-opportunities-for-artists) ([wmg.com](https://www.wmg.com/news/warner-music-group-and-opensea-join-forces-to-expand-web3-opportunities-for-artists?utm_source=openai))

## MITSUBISHI UFJ FINL.GP.

* Rank: 277 | Expo: 0.17 | id_str: JP3902900004
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Company investor presentations/Q&A (2023–2024), MUFG Trust press releases (2023), SEC Form 20‑F (FY2024), financial news (2021–2023)

### Firm Overview
Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group is Japan’s largest banking group, operating global commercial banking, trust banking, securities, and asset management businesses. Its crypto‑adjacent activities sit primarily within Mitsubishi UFJ Trust and Banking’s “Progmat” tokenization and stablecoin platforms and through a past on‑/off‑ramp partnership supporting Coinbase’s Japan launch. ([mufg.jp](https://www.mufg.jp/dam/ir/report/annual_report/pdf/ar2024_en.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The group’s trust banking arm leads “Progmat,” a tokenization stack (Progmat ST/UT) and “Progmat Coin,” a stablecoin issuance/management platform aligned with Japan’s revised Payment Services Act, enabling bank/trust‑issued stablecoins on public chains and supporting security‑token workflows. In September 2023, MUFG’s trust bank and Binance Japan began a joint study to issue fiat‑pegged stablecoins using Progmat Coin, with MUTB as trustee/issuer. MUFG previously partnered with Coinbase to provide MUFG Quick Deposit fiat access for Coinbase Japan customers. There is no evidence the group mines crypto or holds crypto assets as treasury. ([mufg.jp](https://www.mufg.jp/dam/ir/presentation/2023/pdf/speech2309_en.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence shows MUFG’s role is as a regulated financial‑services enabler: trust‑type stablecoin issuance infrastructure, tokenized securities rails, and (historically) banking access for a crypto exchange. These are classic “finserv” mechanisms (custody/issuance/transaction enablement) rather than proprietary coin holding, mining, non‑financial services, or hardware/compute provision. Accordingly, finserv = 1.0 and all other mechanism categories = 0.0. ([tr.mufg.jp](https://www.tr.mufg.jp/english/pressreleases/pdf/20230926_1.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Coinbase exited Japan in January 2023, so the on‑ramp link is historical; current earnings materials do not quantify crypto revenues, and the FY2024 Form 20‑F provides no discrete crypto‑asset line items. Progmat was spun out as Progmat, Inc. in October 2023 with MUFG Trust as the largest shareholder, but revenue contribution sizing to MUFG remains unclear within 2017–2024 disclosures. ([coinbase.com](https://www.coinbase.com/en-it/blog/halting-operations-in-japan?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 16, 2023 — FY2023 H1 IR Presentation (Speech Script): “Progmat… is a platform to enable distribution of assets… in the form of small‑lot digital securities… [and] was spun out in October as Progmat, Inc.” — [MUFG IR (PDF)](https://www.mufg.jp/dam/ir/presentation/2023/pdf/speech2309_en.pdf) ([mufg.jp](https://www.mufg.jp/dam/ir/presentation/2023/pdf/speech2309_en.pdf))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Jul 30, 2024 — Form 20‑F (FY2024): Annual report; no crypto‑asset treasury holdings broken out. — [SEC filing (PDF)](https://www.mufg.jp/dam/ir/report/annual_report/pdf/ar2024_en.pdf) ([mufg.jp](https://www.mufg.jp/dam/ir/report/annual_report/pdf/ar2024_en.pdf))
- Sep 26, 2023 — “MUTB and Binance Japan commence joint study to explore new stablecoin issuance using Progmat Coin (trust‑type under Japan’s PSA).” — [MUTB press release (PDF)](https://www.tr.mufg.jp/english/pressreleases/pdf/20230926_1.pdf) ([tr.mufg.jp](https://www.tr.mufg.jp/english/pressreleases/pdf/20230926_1.pdf))
- Sep 11, 2023 — “Establishment of Progmat, Inc.” (shareholder composition; business purpose covering security tokens, utility tokens and stablecoins). — [MUTB press release (PDF)](https://www.tr.mufg.jp/english/pressreleases/pdf/20230911_1.pdf) ([tr.mufg.jp](https://www.tr.mufg.jp/english/pressreleases/pdf/20230911_1.pdf?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Aug 19, 2021 — “Coinbase names MUFG as banking partner in Japan; MUFG Quick Deposit to support access.” — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2021/08/19/coinbase-names-mitsubishi-ufj-as-banking-partner-in-japan) ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2021/08/19/coinbase-names-mitsubishi-ufj-as-banking-partner-in-japan?utm_source=openai))
- Jan 18, 2023 — “Coinbase halts Japan operations; prior MUFG partnership noted.” — [The Japan Times](https://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2023/01/18/business/corporate-business/coinbase-japan) ([japantimes.co.jp](https://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2023/01/18/business/corporate-business/coinbase-japan/?utm_source=openai))
- Jun 2, 2023 — “MUFG’s Progmat Coin to support bank‑backed stablecoins on Ethereum, Avalanche, Cosmos, Polygon.” — [Ledger Insights](https://www.ledgerinsights.com/mufg-bank-backed-stablecoin-public-blockchains/) ([ledgerinsights.com](https://www.ledgerinsights.com/mufg-bank-backed-stablecoin-public-blockchains/?utm_source=openai))
- May 24, 2024 — “Security token issuance using Progmat; MUTB to issue ST and provide private‑key custody.” — [Tosei Corp. release](https://www.toseicorp.co.jp/english/news/2024/20240524_1353.html) ([toseicorp.co.jp](https://www.toseicorp.co.jp/english/news/2024/20240524_1353.html?utm_source=openai))

## LOTUS TECH.AMER. DEPY. SHS.1:1

* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: SEC Form 20‑F (FY2024), company IR press releases (2024), earnings call announcements (2024), depositary receipt directory

### Firm Overview
Lotus Technology Inc is a Geely‑affiliated luxury “intelligent mobility” company focused on battery electric vehicles and advanced driving technologies, with operations across the UK, EU, and China and Nasdaq listing of its American depositary shares under ticker LOT beginning February 23, 2024. Its business centers on designing, manufacturing, and selling premium EVs such as the Lotus Eletre and related software/ADAS features. ([ir.group-lotus.com](https://ir.group-lotus.com/news-releases/news-release-details/lotus-tech-and-l-catterton-asia-acquisition-corp-complete/?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
A search of Lotus Technology’s FY2024 annual report on Form 20‑F, 2024 earnings materials, and IR press releases reveals no substantive references to cryptocurrency holdings, mining operations, custody/trading services, tokenized products, or mining‑focused infrastructure revenues. Disclosures and communications instead emphasize vehicle deliveries, financial performance, and autonomous/electrification roadmaps. The company’s U.S.‑listed instrument is a sponsored ADR with a 1:1 ordinary share ratio, which is unrelated to crypto. ([last10k.com](https://last10k.com/sec-filings/lot))

### Weight Assignment
Because credible evidence of crypto exposure was not identified across filings and investor communications within the 2017–2024 sample window, the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism categories = 0.0. This reflects an automotive/technology operating profile without material crypto‑asset, mining, financial‑services, non‑financial services, or hardware supply exposure.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Lotus Cars (brand) publicized an NFT partnership in June 2022 with Ripple and NFT PRO; however this appears to have been a marketing initiative by Lotus Cars/Group Lotus and not an ongoing, material activity of Lotus Technology Inc., and no crypto‑related assets or revenue lines are disclosed in Lotus Technology’s FY2024 filings. Lotus Technology only became a U.S.‑listed issuer in February 2024, so transcript availability within 2017–2024 is limited; no crypto references were found in 2024 results materials. ([automotive.lulop.com](https://automotive.lulop.com/en_EN/post/show/243220/lotus-cars-and-nft-pro-announc.html?utm_source=openai))

### Firm Overview
China Reinsurance (Group) Corporation is a Beijing‑based state‑owned reinsurance group listed in Hong Kong via H shares (stock code 1508). Its principal businesses are P&C reinsurance, life and health reinsurance, primary P&C insurance, and asset management. ([eng.chinare.com.cn](https://eng.chinare.com.cn/zzywwzgb/attachDir/2024/09/2024090515104772956.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
A review of China Re’s investor presentations and webcasts spanning 2017–2024, together with its 2023 annual report, found no evidence of cryptocurrency treasury holdings, mining operations, crypto‑exchange/custody products, tokenised investment products, or mining‑directed infrastructure revenues. Instead, disclosures focus on core insurance and reinsurance lines and broader digitalisation initiatives. Where “blockchain” is mentioned, it appears alongside cloud computing, AI and big data as general technology enablement rather than asset‑related activity. There is no indication in filings or IR materials that the stock is treated by investors as a crypto proxy. ([eng.chinare.com.cn](https://eng.chinare.com.cn/zzywwzgb/Investor_Relatuons/593301/index.html))

### Weight Assignment
Because credible evidence of crypto exposure was not identified across filings and investor communications within the January 2017–December 2024 window, the company is classified as unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism categories = 0.0. This reflects an insurance/reinsurance operating profile without material crypto‑asset, mining, financial‑services, non‑financial services, or hardware‑supply exposure. ([eng.chinare.com.cn](https://eng.chinare.com.cn/zzywwzgb/Investor_Relatuons/593301/index.html))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Management materials occasionally reference “blockchain” as part of technology initiatives, but this is not tied to digital‑asset custody, issuance, or trading activities and does not meet the threshold for classification. Public, English‑language earnings call transcripts are limited; evidence relies on annual reports, HKEX documents, and results presentations/press conferences. ([eng.chinare.com.cn](https://eng.chinare.com.cn/zzywwzgb/attachDir/2024/09/2024090515104772956.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls / presentations:**
- Mar 28, 2023 — “2022 Annual Results Presentation” listed on IR site. — [China Re IR presentations] (https://eng.chinare.com.cn/zzywwzgb/Investor_Relatuons/593301/index.html) ([eng.chinare.com.cn](https://eng.chinare.com.cn/zzywwzgb/Investor_Relatuons/593301/index.html))
- Mar 31, 2020 — “2019 Annual Result Presentation” listed on IR site. — [China Re IR presentations] (https://eng.chinare.com.cn/zzywwzgb/Investor_Relatuons/593301/index.html) ([eng.chinare.com.cn](https://eng.chinare.com.cn/zzywwzgb/Investor_Relatuons/593301/index.html))
- Dec 25, 2020 — “2020 China Re Investor Day Webcast.” — [China Re IR presentations] (https://eng.chinare.com.cn/zzywwzgb/Investor_Relatuons/593301/index.html) ([eng.chinare.com.cn](https://eng.chinare.com.cn/zzywwzgb/Investor_Relatuons/593301/index.html))
