## SECOO HOLDING ADR 1:5

* Rank: 280 | Expo: 0.17 | id_str: US81367P2002
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Earnings calls (Q2 & Q3 2019), 2020 Form 20‑F, press releases on blockchain/NFT initiatives (2018–2022)

### Firm Overview
Secoo Holding Limited is a Beijing‑based luxury e‑commerce and lifestyle platform listed in the United States via American Depositary Shares. Its core business is the sale of upscale goods and related services through online and offline channels in China. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/08/29/secoo-holding-limited-seco-q2-2019-earnings-call-t.aspx))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across earnings call transcripts and the FY2020 Form 20‑F, there is no evidence that Secoo held cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet, operated mining, ran a crypto exchange/custody service, or supplied mining‑directed infrastructure. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/08/29/secoo-holding-limited-seco-q2-2019-earnings-call-t.aspx))
Management materials and press releases show the company used blockchain for product authentication and tracking (described as a “luxury blockchain”) and, in 2022, announced a cooperation to explore NFTs with a cultural partner; these initiatives appear as technology/marketing extensions of its retail business rather than crypto‑asset services. ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/secoo-launched-the-first-luxury-blockchain-establishing-the-commodity-tracking-system-300675357.html?utm_source=openai))
Earnings calls in 2019 emphasized AI, big data and cloud services supporting the retail platform, with no discussion of cryptocurrencies or token‑based products. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/12/05/secoo-holding-limited-seco-q3-2019-earnings-call-t.aspx))

### Weight Assignment
Because credible, material crypto‑asset exposure was not identified within the January 2017–December 2024 window, the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism categories = 0.0. The blockchain authentication and NFT mentions are treated as ancillary technology/marketing activities that do not involve crypto custody, trading, investment products, mining, or mining‑driven infrastructure revenue, and thus do not meet the threshold for assignment to other categories. ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/secoo-launched-the-first-luxury-blockchain-establishing-the-commodity-tracking-system-300675357.html?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Public, English‑language transcripts after 2019 are limited, and NFT‑related communications are primarily press releases rather than detailed financial disclosures; however, available filings and calls do not indicate crypto‑asset services or holdings. If future filings disclose concrete NFT/crypto product revenues or asset balances, classification could be revisited. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/08/29/secoo-holding-limited-seco-q2-2019-earnings-call-t.aspx))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 29, 2019 — Q2 2019 call: “GMV of approximately RMB3.24 billion, up 95.9% year‑over‑year.” — [The Motley Fool] (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/08/29/secoo-holding-limited-seco-q2-2019-earnings-call-t.aspx) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/08/29/secoo-holding-limited-seco-q2-2019-earnings-call-t.aspx))
- Dec 5, 2019 — Q3 2019 call: “utilizing leading‑edge technologies, such as AI, Big Data and cloud services.” — [The Motley Fool] (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/12/05/secoo-holding-limited-seco-q3-2019-earnings-call-t.aspx) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/12/05/secoo-holding-limited-seco-q3-2019-earnings-call-t.aspx))

**Filings (10‑K / 20‑F / press releases):**

* RevShare:  | AssetShare: 
* Primary sources: 10‑K filings (2022–2024), Q4 2019 and Q4 2023 earnings calls

### Firm Overview
Cue Biopharma, Inc. is a U.S.-based clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing Immuno‑STAT biologics to selectively engage disease‑relevant T cells for cancer and autoimmune disorders. It is listed on Nasdaq under ticker CUE and reports typical biotech cash and collaboration-revenue profiles in its SEC filings. ([cuebiopharma.com](https://www.cuebiopharma.com/investors/))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across earnings call transcripts and SEC filings from 2019–2024, there is no evidence that the company held cryptocurrencies as treasury assets, operated mining, offered crypto financial services (exchange, custody, investment products), or supplied mining‑directed infrastructure. Management commentary on calls focuses on clinical programs (e.g., CUE‑101/102 oncology, CUE‑401/500 autoimmune) and collaboration revenue, while filings detail cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities—not digital assets—on the balance sheet. Taken together, these sources indicate no material crypto‑asset exposure during the sample window. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/CUE/documents/transcripts/q4-2023))

### Weight Assignment
Because credible, material crypto‑asset exposure was not identified within January 2017–December 2024, the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism categories = 0.0. The absence of crypto holdings, mining, crypto‑financial products, or targeted mining infrastructure in both transcripts and 10‑K disclosures supports this assignment. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1645460/000095017025047677/cue-20241231.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Transcripts from some years are hosted by third‑party aggregators rather than the company or major portals, but the content is consistent with SEC filings and shows no crypto activity. If future filings disclose digital‑asset holdings or crypto‑related services, the classification could be revisited. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/CUE/documents/transcripts/q4-2023))

- Feb 4, 2020 — Q4 2019 call: “We finished the year with approximately $59.4 million in cash and cash equivalents …” — [ROIC AI](https://www.roic.ai/quote/CUE/transcripts/2019/4) ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/CUE/transcripts/2019/4?utm_source=openai))

## CHINA MOBILE 'A'

* Rank: 282 | Expo: 0.17 | id_str: CNE1000055G1
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Annual Report 2023, Sustainability Report 2023, H1 2023 earnings presentation transcript

### Firm Overview
China Mobile Limited is a leading state-owned telecommunications operator in mainland China and Hong Kong, providing mobile, broadband, and digital services; its A-shares were listed on the Shanghai Stock Exchange on January 5, 2022. Its 2023 annual report shows revenue primarily from telecommunications and information services, with large cash and bank balances and no disclosure of digital-asset holdings. ([chinamobileltd.com](https://www.chinamobileltd.com/en/ir/reports/ar2023.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024 investor materials and transcripts, there is no evidence that China Mobile held cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet, engaged in crypto mining, or offered crypto-financial products such as exchanges, custody, or investment vehicles. The 2023 annual report details cash and cash equivalents and other financial assets (e.g., bank deposits, WMPs, debt investments) without any digital-asset line items. Management’s interim 2023 results presentation focused on operating performance rather than digital assets. While the company participates in enterprise blockchain initiatives (e.g., a blockchain-based supply-chain emissions platform and involvement in China’s BSN), these are permissioned, enterprise uses and not tied to cryptocurrency markets. ([chinamobileltd.com](https://www.chinamobileltd.com/en/ir/reports/ar2023.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Because credible, material cryptocurrency exposure is not evidenced in filings, transcripts, or financial news within January 2017–December 2024, China Mobile is classified as unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism categories = 0.0. Internal/blockchain applications and participation in BSN are excluded under the project’s rules as they do not involve custody, issuance, trading, mining, or balance-sheet holdings of crypto assets, and thus are unlikely to transmit crypto-market shocks to the equity. ([chinamobileltd.com](https://www.chinamobileltd.com/en/ir/reports/ar2023/sd2023.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Earnings materials are often provided as slide decks and brief presentation transcripts rather than full Q&A transcripts; one transcript was accessible via a third-party aggregator. China Mobile’s enterprise blockchain work (e.g., SMBaaS/BSN) could superficially resemble crypto activity, but sources indicate these are permissioned, non-crypto infrastructures; therefore, they are not counted toward crypto exposure categories. ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/2368738/half-year-2023-china-mobile-ltd-earnings-presentation-transcript))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 10, 2023 — H1 2023 earnings presentation transcript: “Welcome to China Mobile Limited’s 2023 Interim Results Presentation.” — [GuruFocus](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/2368738/half-year-2023-china-mobile-ltd-earnings-presentation-transcript) (gurufocus.com)
- Mar 21, 2024 — 2023 Annual Results Presentation (slides). — [China Mobile IR](https://www.chinamobileltd.com/en/ir/webcasts/pre240321.pdf) (chinamobileltd.com)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Mar 21, 2024 — Annual Report 2023: consolidated balance sheet shows “Cash and cash equivalents RMB141,559 million” and notes define cash equivalents as bank deposits/short-term liquid investments; no digital-asset balances disclosed. — [Annual Report 2023](https://www.chinamobileltd.com/en/ir/reports/ar2023.pdf) (chinamobileltd.com)
- 2023 Sustainability Report: “creates a greenhouse gas emission management platform based on blockchain technology,” built on SMBaaS (enterprise/permissioned). — [Sustainability Report 2023](https://www.chinamobileltd.com/en/ir/reports/ar2023/sd2023.pdf) (chinamobileltd.com)

**Financial news / other:**

## FEDEX

* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Form 10‑K (FY2024), Q2 FY2018 and Q3 FY2018 earnings calls

### Firm Overview
FedEx Corporation is a U.S.-based global transportation, logistics, and e‑commerce services company operating express air, ground, and freight networks worldwide. Its core revenues derive from shipping, logistics, and related business services.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024, there is no evidence that FedEx held cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet, mined crypto assets, or offered crypto financial products such as trading, custody, or investment vehicles. The FY2024 Form 10‑K presents standard cash and investment line items without any digital-asset holdings disclosed. ([d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net](https://d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net/CIK-0001048911/8b5589de-a765-4fc7-b41b-162ef233ee5e.pdf))  
Management did discuss “blockchain” on earnings calls in late 2017 and 2018 and highlighted involvement with industry groups (e.g., BiTA) and later with Hyperledger, but these are enterprise/permissioned blockchain initiatives, not cryptocurrency activities captured by this project’s categories. ([nasdaq.com](https://www.nasdaq.com/articles/fedex-fdx-q2-2018-earnings-conference-call-transcript-2017-12-20))

### Weight Assignment
Because credible, material cryptocurrency exposure is not evidenced in filings, transcripts, or reliable news for 2017–2024, all mechanism categories are assigned 0.0 and unclass = 1.0. References to enterprise blockchain standards and pilots are excluded under the rules (no custody/issuance/trading/mining or balance‑sheet crypto), and thus are unlikely to transmit crypto‑market shocks to FedEx’s equity.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Mentions of “blockchain,” BiTA membership, and Hyperledger membership could superficially resemble crypto activity, but sources indicate these are enterprise technology and standards efforts rather than crypto‑asset exposure. No transcripts or filings indicate acceptance of cryptocurrency payments, crypto holdings, mining operations, or crypto financial services within the 2017–2024 window. ([nasdaq.com](https://www.nasdaq.com/articles/fedex-fdx-q2-2018-earnings-conference-call-transcript-2017-12-20))

## LITE STRATEGY

* Rank: 284 | Expo: 0.16 | id_str: US55279B3015
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0

* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Form 10‑K (FY2023–FY2024), company press releases (2023–2024)

### Firm Overview
Lite Strategy is the current corporate name of the U.S. company that operated as MEI Pharma, Inc. during 2017–2024, a San Diego–based clinical‑stage oncology firm listed on Nasdaq. In that period, the business focused on developing drug candidates and managed a balance sheet centered on cash, cash equivalents, and short‑term U.S. government securities.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across the January 2017–December 2024 window, there is no evidence that the company held cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet, mined digital assets, or offered crypto financial services such as exchange, custody, or investment products. The FY2024 Form 10‑K describes liquid assets as cash, cash equivalents, and short‑term investments, with short‑term investments consisting of U.S. government securities; no digital‑asset holdings are disclosed. The FY2023 Form 10‑K shows the same pattern and explicitly characterizes short‑term investments as U.S. government securities. Public communications during 2023–2024 center on strategic and cash‑management updates related to the biotech business and do not introduce crypto activities. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1262104/000095017024108185/meip-20240630.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Because filings and investor communications in 2017–2024 provide no credible indication of crypto‑asset holding, mining, crypto financial services, non‑financial crypto services, or mining‑related infrastructure provisioning, all mechanism categories are set to 0.0 and unclass = 1.0. The absence of any digital‑asset disclosures in consecutive 10‑Ks and the operational focus on oncology support this assignment. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1262104/000095017024108185/meip-20240630.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Evidence and classification are restricted to January 2017–December 2024 per the project scope. Company activities disclosed after this period are not considered in the weights above. None identified within the sample period that would change the unclassified assignment.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**

## REMITLY GLOBAL

* Rank: 285 | Expo: 0.16 | id_str: US75960P1049
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.2, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.8
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Form 10‑K (FY2023), Q2 2024 and Q4 2023 earnings calls, company/partner news (Oct 2021–Feb 2022)

### Firm Overview
Remitly Global, Inc. is a Seattle‑based digital remittance company listed on Nasdaq (ticker RELY) that enables customers to send money to recipients in over 170 countries via bank deposit, mobile wallets, and cash pickup. Its FY2023 Form 10‑K describes a transaction‑fee and FX‑spread driven business with principal liquidity held in cash and cash equivalents, and does not discuss crypto asset holdings. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1782170/000178217024000037/rely-20231231.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Within the sample period, Remitly partnered with crypto platforms to support fiat off‑ramps tied to digital‑asset wallets. In October 2021, it announced a partnership with Meta’s Novi wallet pilot, where Novi used the USDP stablecoin and leveraged Remitly’s payout network for cash pickup in Guatemala. ([ir.remitly.com](https://ir.remitly.com/news-releases/news-release-details/remitly-announces-new-partnership-novi-pilot-its-digital-wallet)) In February 2022, Coinbase launched a Mexico cash‑out pilot that media reported was enabled through a partnership with Remitly’s payout relationships at Mexican retailers, allowing recipients to redeem crypto balances for pesos at thousands of locations. ([geekwire.com](https://www.geekwire.com/2022/coinbase-partners-with-remittance-company-remitly-to-let-people-in-mexico-cash-out-crypto/))
Earnings calls over 2023–2024 emphasize Remitly’s cross‑border payments platform and global pay‑in/disbursement partner network, not crypto custody or investment; for example, management highlighted nearly $40 billion of 2023 send volume “flowing through our pay‑in and disbursement partners” and that more than 90% of transactions are dispersed in under an hour. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2024/02/22/remitly-global-rely-q4-2023-earnings-call-transcri/))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence supports a limited crypto financial‑services channel: Remitly provided payout/off‑ramp capabilities to crypto wallets and exchanges, facilitating conversion of crypto balances into local‑currency cash via its licensed money‑transmission network. However, filings indicate no crypto assets on balance sheet and calls focus on traditional remittance economics, suggesting this activity was peripheral to core revenue. Accordingly, weights are set to finserv 0.2 and unclass 0.8, with holders, miners, bizserv, and infrastr at 0.0. ([ir.remitly.com](https://ir.remitly.com/news-releases/news-release-details/remitly-announces-new-partnership-novi-pilot-its-digital-wallet))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Scale and duration of the Coinbase/Novi‑related flows within 2017–2024 are not quantified in filings or calls; the off‑ramp initiatives appear pilot‑oriented and may not have been material. No transcript in the period explicitly discusses crypto rails by name, so the finserv weight relies on partner announcements rather than management commentary.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 1, 2024 — Q2 2024: “Our transactions continue to get faster with more than 90% dispersed in less than an hour…” — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2024/08/01/remitly-global-rely-q2-2024-earnings-call-transcri/)  
- Feb 22, 2024 — Q4 2023: “…nearly $40 billion of send volume in 2023 flowing through our pay‑in and disbursement partners…” — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2024/02/22/remitly-global-rely-q4-2023-earnings-call-transcri/)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Feb 23, 2024 — Form 10‑K FY2023: liquidity in “cash and cash equivalents”; business overview; no crypto‑asset holdings discussed. — [SEC 10‑K HTML](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1782170/000178217024000037/rely-20231231.htm)  
- Oct 19, 2021 — “Remitly Announces New Partnership With Novi For The Pilot Of Its Digital Wallet” (USDP stablecoin; Remitly cash‑pickup network). — [Remitly IR](https://ir.remitly.com/news-releases/news-release-details/remitly-announces-new-partnership-novi-pilot-its-digital-wallet)

**Financial news / other:**
- Feb 16, 2022 — “Coinbase partners with remittance company Remitly to let people in Mexico cash out crypto.” — [GeekWire](https://www.geekwire.com/2022/coinbase-partners-with-remittance-company-remitly-to-let-people-in-mexico-cash-out-crypto/)  
- Feb 16, 2022 — “Coinbase offers free crypto cashouts for transfers to Mexico… partnered with Remitly.” — [fintech.io news brief](https://news.fintech.io/post/102hiw8/coinbase-offers-free-crypto-cashouts-for-transfers-to-mexico)

</small>

## ENFUSION A DEAD - DELIST.21/04/25

* Rank: 286 | Expo: 0.16 | id_str: US2928121043
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.2, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.8
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: FY2024 Form 10‑K, Q4 2021 and Q3 2023 earnings calls, Dec 2021 Coinbase Prime integration coverage

### Firm Overview
Enfusion Inc. was a Chicago‑based provider of cloud‑native front‑to‑back investment management software (PMS/OMS/EMS, accounting and managed services) serving hedge funds and institutional asset managers; its Class A shares traded on the NYSE as ENFN beginning October 21, 2021. FY2024 filings describe a SaaS, subscription‑driven model with liquidity in cash and money market funds, and do not indicate digital‑asset holdings. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1868912/000155837025002043/enfn-20241231x10k.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Within the 2017–2024 window, Enfusion enabled clients to trade and monitor crypto assets by linking its OEMS/PMS to Coinbase Prime. The December 22, 2021 announcement explained that Enfusion customers with a Coinbase Prime account would be able to trade crypto directly and view crypto positions alongside the rest of their portfolios through Enfusion’s interface, with custody and prime brokerage handled by Coinbase. Management highlighted this Coinbase partnership on the Q4 2021 earnings call as part of a broader partner‑ecosystem strategy. These activities position Enfusion as a software/connectivity provider to crypto‑trading institutions rather than a custodian, broker, or principal investor in digital assets. ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/12/22/coinbase-prime-to-streamline-institutional-crypto-trading-with-link-to-enfusion-system?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence supports a non‑financial services, software‑integration exposure: Enfusion’s role was to provide connectivity and workflow tools that allowed institutional clients to access Coinbase Prime and manage crypto alongside other asset classes inside Enfusion’s platform. Filings show no crypto on Enfusion’s balance sheet and no indication that Enfusion itself provided custody, trading, or treasury investments in digital assets. Given the clear but ancillary nature of this linkage to client crypto activity, weights are set to bizserv 0.2 and unclass 0.8, with holders, miners, finserv, and infrastr at 0.0. ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/12/22/coinbase-prime-to-streamline-institutional-crypto-trading-with-link-to-enfusion-system?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Public disclosures do not quantify revenue attributable specifically to crypto‑connected workflows, so materiality is inferred to be minor. Earnings calls in 2023 emphasize multi‑asset, front‑to‑back software capabilities but do not break out digital‑asset metrics; thus, the bizserv weight relies primarily on the 2021 Coinbase Prime integration and management commentary rather than recurring transcript detail in later periods. ([marketbeat.com](https://www.marketbeat.com/earnings/reports/2023-11-7-enfusion-inc-stock/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Dec 2021 — Q4 2021: “In December, we announced a strategic partnership with Coinbase that allows investment managers on Enfusion's platform to monitor their crypto assets and seamlessly trade cryptocurrency at Coinbase…” — [Roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/ENFN%3AUS/transcripts/2021-year/4-quarter)  
- Nov 7, 2023 — Q3 2023: “With Enfusion, the client now has a unified view across all funds and products… and benefits from our multi‑asset class… capabilities.” — [MarketBeat (Quartr transcript)](https://www.marketbeat.com/earnings/reports/2023-11-7-enfusion-inc-stock/)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Mar 3, 2025 — Form 10‑K FY2024: business overview; cash and cash equivalents policy; money market balances disclosed (no digital‑asset holdings). — [SEC 10‑K HTML](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1868912/000155837025002043/enfn-20241231x10k.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- Dec 22, 2021 — “Coinbase Prime to Streamline Institutional Crypto Trading With Link to Enfusion System.” — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/12/22/coinbase-prime-to-streamline-institutional-crypto-trading-with-link-to-enfusion-system/)  
- Dec 22, 2021 — Company announcement: Enfusion and Coinbase to bring seamless digital‑asset connectivity to institutions. — [Enfusion Newsroom](https://www.enfusion.com/news/enfusion-and-coinbase-to-bring-seamless-digital-asset-connectivity-to-institutions/)

## THOUGHTWORKS HOLDING DEAD - DELIST.14/11/24

* Rank: 287 | Expo: 0.16 | id_str: US88546E1055
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.2, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.8
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: FY2023 Form 10‑K; Q4 2021 earnings call; Thoughtworks client case studies (Xapo, Secco); Sept 2023 Amber Group digital‑asset security white paper

### Firm Overview
Thoughtworks Holding, Inc. was a global technology consultancy focused on strategy, design, and software engineering, with operations across North America, Europe, Asia‑Pacific and Latin America. The company was publicly listed on Nasdaq as TWKS from 2021 until its take‑private by Apax funds completed in November 2024; filings and disclosures describe a services business with liquidity held in cash and cash equivalents, and no digital‑asset holdings. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1866550/000186655024000022/twks-20231231.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Within the 2017–2024 window, Thoughtworks delivered consulting and engineering services to firms operating in digital‑asset ecosystems. Notably, it partnered with Xapo Bank, a dual‑licensed bank and Virtual Asset Service Provider enabling customers to transact in fiat and Bitcoin, to modernize technology, architecture and operating model; Thoughtworks also announced partnering with Xapo as the company evolved from a Bitcoin custodian to a global bank. Additional client work included prototyping Secco’s “Aura,” a proximity‑based token/barter concept testing blockchain and cryptocurrencies. In September 2023, Thoughtworks co‑authored a white paper with Amber Group and Web3 security firms on digital‑asset security, compliance and risk management. These activities indicate indirect, non‑financial services exposure as a technology/consulting supplier to crypto‑adjacent clients. ([thoughtworks.com](https://www.thoughtworks.com/en-us/clients/financial-services-insurance/xapo-bank?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence shows Thoughtworks acting as a non‑financial services provider to digital‑asset clients (software modernization, product/architecture work, and industry thought leadership) while not itself issuing crypto products, providing custody/exchange services, mining, or holding crypto on its balance sheet. Therefore, exposure is assigned primarily to bizserv (0.2) to reflect identifiable crypto‑ecosystem client work, with the remainder unclass (0.8) to capture that most revenue is unrelated to crypto and disclosures contain no crypto asset holdings. Holders, miners, finserv, and infrastr are set to 0.0 based on filings and the nature of engagements. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1866550/000186655024000022/twks-20231231.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Management’s earnings‑call commentary references “crypto currency” in sector trends but does not quantify crypto‑related revenue, and filings provide no segment breakout for digital‑asset work; materiality is inferred to be minor. Some evidence (Xapo and Secco) comes from company client stories and press announcements rather than third‑party financial media, though the Amber Group white paper provides additional third‑party corroboration. No indications were found of Thoughtworks offering crypto financial services, mining, or holding crypto assets. ([tickertrends.io](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/TWKS/Q4-earnings-transcript-2021))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Mar 1, 2022 — Q4 2021: “There’s always third‑party payment, neo‑bank and then crypto currency.” — [TickerTrends transcript](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/TWKS/Q4-earnings-transcript-2021) ([tickertrends.io](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/TWKS/Q4-earnings-transcript-2021))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Feb 27, 2024 — Form 10‑K FY2023: business overview; services model; no digital‑asset holdings disclosed. — [SEC 10‑K HTML](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1866550/000186655024000022/twks-20231231.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1866550/000186655024000022/twks-20231231.htm))
- Nov 12, 2024 — Take‑private transaction completion (context on delisting). — [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20241112193185/en/Thoughtworks-Completes-Transaction-to-Go-Private-in-%241.75-Billion-Deal-with-Apax-Funds) ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20241112193185/en/Thoughtworks-Completes-Transaction-to-Go-Private-in-%241.75-Billion-Deal-with-Apax-Funds?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Sept 27, 2023 — “Amber Group, Thoughtworks, and Web3 Security Leaders Release White Paper on Digital Asset Security, Compliance, and Risk Management.” — [PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/amber-group-thoughtworks-and-web3-security-leaders-release-white-paper-on-digital-asset-security-compliance-and-risk-management-301940318.html) ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/amber-group-thoughtworks-and-web3-security-leaders-release-white-paper-on-digital-asset-security-compliance-and-risk-management-301940318.html?utm_source=openai))
- 2021 — “Xapo partners with Thoughtworks to scale from Bitcoin vault to global bank.” — [Thoughtworks News](https://www.thoughtworks.com/en-br/about-us/news/2021/xapo-partners-with-thoughtworks-to-scale-from-bitcoin-vault-to-global-bank) ([thoughtworks.com](https://www.thoughtworks.com/en-br/about-us/news/2021/xapo-partners-with-thoughtworks-to-scale-from-bitcoin-vault-to-global-bank?utm_source=openai))
- 2023–2024 — Client story: “Building a faster, more scalable, future‑ready digital bank” (Xapo Bank; dual‑licensed VASP enabling fiat and Bitcoin). — [Thoughtworks client case study](https://www.thoughtworks.com/en-us/clients/financial-services-insurance/xapo-bank) ([thoughtworks.com](https://www.thoughtworks.com/en-us/clients/financial-services-insurance/xapo-bank?utm_source=openai))
- Client story: “Secco Aura” — MVP and tech validation including P2P, blockchain and cryptocurrencies. — [Thoughtworks client case study](https://www.thoughtworks.com/clients/secco) ([thoughtworks.com](https://www.thoughtworks.com/clients/secco?utm_source=openai))

## INFIBEAM AVENUES

* Rank: 288 | Expo: 0.16 | id_str: INE483S01020
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.2, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.8
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: FY2018–19 earnings calls and investor materials; 2018–2019 Indian business media; product/FAQ pages (CCAvenue CBDC)

### Firm Overview
Infibeam Avenues Limited (rebranded AvenuesAI Limited in January 2026) was an India‑based fintech and digital payments technology company best known for its CCAvenue payment gateway and BillAvenue bill‑payment platform, with operations in India and select international markets. The company also operated a data‑center/infrastructure segment and historically pursued enterprise blockchain initiatives alongside its core payments and platform businesses.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Within the 2017–2024 window, management discussed collaborations to develop and host enterprise blockchain applications, including a cross‑border blockchain invoicing platform to run on the company’s data‑center infrastructure; these are indirect, non‑financial services to blockchain use‑cases rather than cryptocurrency products. Earnings‑call remarks in FY2019 outlined work with IBM India on blockchain applications and with Primechain Technologies on a trade‑finance invoicing platform, positioning blockchain as an additional revenue stream built on Infibeam’s infrastructure. Conversely, its payments arm (CCAvenue) emphasized CBDC (e‑rupee) acceptance and explicitly distinguished CBDC from cryptocurrencies; CCAvenue’s own FAQ lists “Merchant dealing in Crypto Currencies” among prohibited business types, indicating avoidance of direct crypto financial services. Overall, the identifiable crypto‑adjacent activity is limited to enterprise blockchain enablement, not exchange/custody, mining, or treasury holding. ([ia.ooo](https://www.ia.ooo/wp-content/uploads/2021/02/TranscriptCall-Q3FY2018-19.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence supports a modest bizserv classification for Infibeam Avenues as a technology/infrastructure provider to enterprise blockchain initiatives (software/integration/hosting) without taking custody of, issuing, or transacting in cryptocurrencies. No credible indications were found of balance‑sheet crypto holdings, mining, or crypto financial services; on the contrary, the payments gateway disallows crypto‑merchant onboarding and promotes CBDC acceptance as distinct from crypto. Accordingly, bizserv is set to 0.2 to reflect identifiable but peripheral blockchain work, with the remainder unclass (0.8) given the dominance of non‑crypto payments/platform revenue and lack of ongoing, material crypto exposure in later disclosures. Holders, miners, finserv, and infrastr are 0.0 under the project definitions. ([ia.ooo](https://www.ia.ooo/wp-content/uploads/2021/02/TranscriptCall-Q3FY2018-19.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Blockchain initiatives were described in FY2019 calls and investor materials, but subsequent transcripts in 2023–2024 focus on core payments and AI with no quantification of blockchain revenue; materiality is therefore inferred as minor. CBDC (e‑rupee) acceptance is not cryptocurrency exposure under this framework. Some annual‑report PDFs from earlier years are large/less accessible online; however, multiple contemporaneous news items and company filings corroborate the IBM/Primechain collaborations. ([avenuesai.com](https://www.avenuesai.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/11/Earnings-Call-Transcript.pdf))

### Evidence

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 15, 2019 — Q3 FY2019: “We have collaborated with IBM India to develop, implement and promote blockchain applications on our company’s upcoming cloud data center.” — [Company transcript PDF] (https://www.ia.ooo/wp-content/uploads/2021/02/TranscriptCall-Q3FY2018-19.pdf) ([ia.ooo](https://www.ia.ooo/wp-content/uploads/2021/02/TranscriptCall-Q3FY2018-19.pdf))
- May 31, 2019 — Q4 FY2019: “We have also collaborated with Primechain Technologies to develop [a] cross‑border Blockchain invoicing platform… [to] run on our data center infrastructure.” — [Company transcript PDF] (https://www.ia.ooo/wp-content/uploads/2021/02/TranscriptCall-Q4FY2018-192.pdf) ([ia.ooo](https://www.ia.ooo/wp-content/uploads/2021/02/TranscriptCall-Q4FY2018-192.pdf))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Oct 25, 2019 — Investor presentation (BSE filing): “Blockchain Platform… Infrastructure as a Service ‘IaaS’… on LinuxONE.” — [BSE filing PDF] (https://www.bseindia.com/xml-data/corpfiling/AttachHis/af1af8a5-197a-
**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Oct 25, 2019 — Investor presentation: “Blockchain Platform … first time ever on LinuxONE.” — [BSE presentation PDF](https://www.ia.ooo/wp-content/uploads/2021/02/Presentation-25.10.2019.pdf)

## WESTERN UNION

* Rank: 289 | Expo: 0.16 | id_str: US9598021098
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K/Annual Report wrap (2022), Q4 2017 earnings-call quotes (media transcript coverage), Fortune interview (2018), Reuters-syndicated analysis (2021)

### Firm Overview
The Western Union Company is a U.S.-based global money-transfer and cross‑border payments firm, offering retail and digital remittances in over 200 countries and territories. Across 2017–2024, management emphasized digital expansion and compliance intensity typical of licensed money transmitters; crypto references appear mainly as technology pilots or in regulatory‑risk context rather than as commercial products. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/02/07/the-western-union-company-wu-q4-2018-earnings-conf.aspx))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Within the sample window, management publicly discussed testing Ripple’s blockchain-based solutions in 2018, with CEO remarks on an earnings call that Western Union was examining blockchain for settlement, working‑capital optimization, compliance, and that it had “some tests with Ripple.” Later that year and in subsequent commentary, leadership indicated the pilot had not produced meaningful cost savings and remained too expensive relative to existing rails. Company filings list “cryptocurrency licensing and other regulations” among regulatory frameworks relevant to its business, but there is no evidence of Western Union launching custody, exchange, crypto payments, or balance‑sheet holdings through December 2024. Consequently, no substantive mechanism category (holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, infrastr) is supported for this period. ([cointelegraph.com](https://cointelegraph.com/news/western-union-confirms-testing-ripple-technology-for-money-transfers//?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence indicates exploratory pilots and general‑purpose risk disclosures rather than operational or revenue‑generating crypto activity. Under the project definitions, pilots without productization and generic compliance references do not warrant non‑zero weights in holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr. Therefore, unclass is set to 1.0, and all other mechanism weights are 0.0. ([cointelegraph.com](https://cointelegraph.com/news/western-union-confirms-testing-ripple-technology-for-money-transfers//?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Crypto‑media frequently recycled or overstated Western Union–Ripple stories; one outlet later issued a formal retraction clarifying that prior “integration” claims referred only to an old pilot. Broader industry pieces in 2021 also characterized large remitters (including Western Union) as cautious on adding bitcoin services. These dynamics may create noise, but within 2017–2024 there is no credible evidence of Western Union operating a crypto financial service, mining, or holding crypto as treasury. ([mariblock.com](https://www.mariblock.com/stories/retraction-article-western-union-and-ripple-integration-story-clarification?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 13, 2018 — Q4 2017 call: “We are looking … at blockchain capabilities … and we do have some tests with Ripple.” — [CoinTelegraph coverage quoting the call](https://cointelegraph.com/news/western-union-confirms-testing-ripple-technology-for-money-transfers/) ([cointelegraph.com](https://cointelegraph.com/news/western-union-confirms-testing-ripple-technology-for-money-transfers//?utm_source=openai))
- Feb 14, 2018 — Q4 2017 call recap: management examining blockchain and testing transactions with Ripple; context from call remarks — [PYMNTS](https://www.pymnts.com/earnings/2018/western-union-q4-earnings-stock-ripple-hikmet-ersek/) ([pymnts.com](https://www.pymnts.com/earnings/2018/western-union-q4-earnings-stock-ripple-hikmet-ersek/))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- 2022 Annual Report wrap (includes 2022 Form 10‑K content): regulatory scope lists “cryptocurrency licensing and other regulations.” — [SEC filing PDF](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1365135/000120677423000421/wu4120391_ars.pdf) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1365135/000120677423000421/wu4120391_ars.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**

## NETDRAGON WEBSOFT

* Rank: 290 | Expo: 0.16 | id_str: KYG6427W1042
* Weight summary: holders 1.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: HKEX interim/annual reports (2023–2024); IR webcasts archive (2017–2024)

### Firm Overview
NetDragon Websoft Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong–listed Chinese developer and operator of online games and education technology platforms, known for titles such as Eudemons Online and Conquer Online and for its Promethean interactive displays business. In 2023, it announced annual results and continued positioning as a global internet community builder across gaming and edtech. ([netdragon.com](https://www.netdragon.com/?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Within the 2017–2024 window, the company disclosed direct cryptocurrency activity on its own balance sheet. In its 2024 interim report (for the six months ended June 30, 2024), NetDragon recorded approximately USD33 million of “acquisition of a cryptocurrency,” disposed of cryptocurrencies with an aggregate carrying amount of about USD41 million, and recognized a disposal gain; it also reported “other receivables from agents for acquisition of cryptocurrencies.” 
The 2023 annual report further states that certain unlisted equity investments held at fair value are in private entities “principally engaged in investments in cryptocurrencies, tokens, blockchain-based assets and other digital assets.” There is no evidence in filings or investor materials of the firm operating crypto exchanges, custody, payments, mining, or crypto-specific infrastructure through December 2024. 

### Weight Assignment
Disclosures indicate crypto exposure arises from the company’s own investment/treasury activity and related investment vehicles, not from providing financial services or infrastructure to third parties. Under the project definitions, this maps to holders = 1.0 and all other mechanisms = 0.0. The presence of direct crypto purchases and sales and crypto-focused investees supports a pure holders classification; no corroborated evidence supports miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr for the 2017–2024 period. 

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Earnings-call transcripts are listed on the company’s IR site for 2019–2024, but the transcript PDFs could not be retrieved in this run; per protocol, certainty is capped at 2 and the classification is anchored on audited/interim filings. Also, filings do not specify which cryptocurrency was acquired, and the magnitude appears episodic (principally evident in H1 2024), so RevShare and AssetShare are conservatively coded as “1” (<5%). ([ir.nd.com.cn](https://ir.nd.com.cn/en/earnings-releases?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- 2019–2024 IR “Earnings Releases” archive — transcripts listed but PDFs not retrievable in this run. — [NetDragon IR](https://ir.nd.com.cn/en/earnings-releases)
- Mar 30, 2022 — 2021 Annual Results Conference Call page lists “DOWNLOAD TRANSCRIPT.” — [NetDragon IR event page](https://ir.nd.com.cn/en/25860.html)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Sep 26, 2024 — Interim Report 2024 (H1): “USD33m… for acquisition of a cryptocurrency… disposed of certain cryptocurrencies… gain on disposal RMB51m; … other receivables from agents for acquisition of cryptocurrencies.” — [HKEXnews PDF](https://www.hkexnews.hk/listedco/listconews/sehk/2024/0926/2024092600457.pdf)
- Apr 23, 2024 — Annual Report 2023: Note states unlisted equity investments are in entities engaged in “investments in cryptocurrencies, tokens, blockchain-based assets and other digital assets.” — [HKEXnews PDF](https://www.hkexnews.hk/listedco/listconews/sehk/2024/0423/2024042300404.pdf)
- Financial Reports index (downloads for 2024 Interim, 2023 Annual, etc.). — [NetDragon IR](https://ir.nd.com.cn/en/financial-reports)

**Financial news / other:**

## MADISON SQUARE GARDEN ENTERTAINMENT A

* Rank: 291 | Expo: 0.16 | id_str: US5582561032
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 2024 Form 10‑K; NFT.NYC 2022 event pages; earnings call transcripts (2021–2024)

### Firm Overview
Madison Square Garden Entertainment Corp. is a U.S.-based live entertainment company that owns and operates iconic venues, including Madison Square Garden, Radio City Music Hall, the Beacon Theatre, The Theater at Madison Square Garden, and The Chicago Theatre. The company generates revenue from hosting concerts, family shows, special events and related venue operations in New York and Chicago.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Within the 2017–2024 window, the company’s venues hosted third‑party crypto industry events, most notably NFT.NYC 2022 programming at Radio City Music Hall. This constitutes providing event services to the crypto ecosystem without taking custody of digital assets or offering financial products. No evidence was found of the company holding cryptocurrency on its balance sheet, operating mining activities, or running crypto trading/custody/payment services. Accordingly, exposure aligns with non‑financial services to the ecosystem (events) rather than financial or balance‑sheet channels.

### Weight Assignment
Because the corroborated crypto touchpoint is venue hosting of NFT‑focused events, the appropriate mechanism is bizserv. There is no support for holders, miners, finserv or infrastr classifications in filings or calls. Given the limited, event‑driven nature of exposure, all weight is assigned to bizserv (1.0), with RevShare and AssetShare coded 0 to reflect negligible crypto‑related revenue or assets.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Earnings call transcripts reviewed for 2021–2024 contain no crypto‑related discussion; classification relies on external event evidence instead of management commentary, so certainty is set to 2. Marketing partnerships involving blockchain brands tied to the New York Knicks or Rangers belong to Madison Square Garden Sports Corp., a separate company, and are excluded from this assessment. Hosting NFT events appears episodic, so crypto exposure may be immaterial and intermittent.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 9, 2021 — Q1 FY2022 Earnings Call Transcript (reviewed; no references to cryptocurrency/digital assets). — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/09/madison-square-garden-entertainment-corp-msge-q1-2/)
- Feb 9, 2022 — Q2 FY2022 Earnings Call Transcript (reviewed; no references to cryptocurrency/digital assets). — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/02/09/madison-square-garden-entertainment-corp-msge-q2-2/)
- Nov 2024 — Q1 FY2025 Earnings Call Transcript (reviewed; no references to cryptocurrency/digital assets). — [Seeking Alpha](https://seekingalpha.com/article/4735662-madison-square-garden-entertainment-corp-msge-q1-2025-earnings-call-transcript)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Aug 16, 2024 — FY2024 Form 10‑K: business description lists venues and event operations; no crypto asset holdings disclosed. — [SEC filing PDF](https://s202.q4cdn.com/493887674/files/doc_financials/2024/q4/MSGE-10K-8-16-24-Filed.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- NFT.NYC 2022 overview listing Radio City Music Hall among official venues (June 21–23, 2022). — [NFT.NYC official page](https://www.nft.nyc/nftnyc2022)
- “NFT.NYC to kick off its 4th Annual NFT Community Event at Radio City Music Hall” (tickets accepted via credit card or cryptocurrency; sponsors include Polygon and MoonPay). — [News Direct](https://newsdirect.com/news/nft-nyc-to-kick-off-its-4th-annual-nft-community-event-at-radio-city-music-hall-731380331)

## UNIVERSAL MUSIC GROUP

* Rank: 292 | Expo: 0.16 | id_str: NL0015000IY2
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: Earnings call transcripts (Q4 2021, Q1 2022); Annual Reports (2021–2022); company and partner press releases (2022)

### Firm Overview
Universal Music Group N.V. is a Netherlands-based global music company listed on Euronext Amsterdam, operating recorded music, music publishing and merchandising businesses worldwide. Within the 2017–2024 period, management discussed Web3 initiatives and the company pursued NFT collaborations and licensing programs tied to artists and labels. ([universalsimplify.com](https://www.universalsimplify.com/reports.html))

### Exposure Channels Identified
From 2021–2022, UMG partnered with NFT platforms to launch officially licensed digital collectibles, including a deal making Curio a dedicated outlet for future UMG NFT projects, and a Billboard partnership to debut “ChartStars” NFTs on Flow. Its Web3-focused label 10:22PM created the virtual band KINGSHIP using Bored Ape Yacht Club IP, and a UMG imprint acquired a Bored Ape NFT for the project. Management framed NFTs/Web3 as part of broader artist marketing and e‑commerce strategy during earnings calls. These activities constitute non‑financial services and content licensing to the crypto ecosystem rather than running a financial product or exchange. ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/universal-music-group-partners-with-curio-to-develop-nft-fan-collections-for-its-record-labels-and-artists-301484447.html?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence points to exposure via content/IP licensing and collaborations around NFTs (e.g., Curio, Billboard ChartStars, Snowcrash, LimeWire) without UMG itself providing custody, trading, or crypto financial products. There is no support for mining or treasury crypto holdings beyond an NFT acquired for creative use, and no crypto-focused investor-base dynamics are evident. Accordingly, all weight is assigned to bizserv (1.0). Given that NFT revenues and assets appear immaterial relative to group totals, RevShare and AssetShare are coded “1” (<5%). ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/universal-music-group-partners-with-curio-to-develop-nft-fan-collections-for-its-record-labels-and-artists-301484447.html?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
UMG’s filings and calls discuss Web3/NFT initiatives qualitatively; granular revenue or balance‑sheet quantification is limited, and activity appears episodic within 2021–2022. One label purchased a Bored Ape NFT for a virtual act, but there is no indication of a broader treasury‑style crypto holding policy. If future disclosures separate NFT revenues or assets, RevShare/AssetShare coding may warrant revision. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/UNIVERSAL-MUSIC-GROUP-N-V-127080831/news/Universal-Music-label-acquires-ape-NFT-to-lead-virtual-music-group-39804676/?utm_source=openai))

## CHEETAH MOBILE ADR 1:50

* Rank: 293 | Expo: 0.16 | id_str: US1630752038
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Form 20-F (2018), Q4 2017 earnings call, company press releases (2018), CoinDesk coverage

### Firm Overview
Cheetah Mobile Inc. is a Beijing-based mobile internet company listed on the NYSE (ticker: CMCM) known for utility apps and mobile games, and later AI/robotics products. Within the 2017–2024 window, the company also launched a mobile cryptocurrency wallet (SafeWallet) and related blockchain-facing tools as ancillary offerings.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company entered crypto financial services in early 2018 by launching SafeWallet, a mobile digital wallet allowing users to store keys for crypto assets such as Bitcoin and Ether; its 2018 Form 20-F details the service and notes availability to non-PRC users due to local rules. ([annualreports.co.uk](https://www.annualreports.co.uk/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/c/NYSE_CMCM_2018.pdf))  
On its Q4 2017 earnings call, management said blockchain could create opportunities and explicitly cited the release of SafeWallet and a decentralized app browser, with additional blockchain products in the pipeline. ([filecache.investorroom.com](https://filecache.investorroom.com/mr5ir_jinshan/134/download/CMCM_4Q17%20Earnings%20Call%20Transcript_EN.pdf))  
Press and trade coverage at the time corroborated the wallet launch and subsequent product updates (e.g., QR code–based user ID), reinforcing characterization as a wallet provider rather than a miner or treasury holder. ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/cheetah-mobile-releases-safewallet-cryptocurrency-wallet-300590982.html?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence supports classification solely through crypto financial services: the firm offered a non‑custodial wallet and dapp browser and discussed blockchain opportunities, but there is no indication of mining, hardware supply, or treasury crypto holdings. Therefore, finserv is assigned 1.0, with other categories 0.0. Given the wallet appeared ancillary relative to core revenues and filings provide no segment-scale contribution or balance-sheet crypto assets, RevShare is coded 1 (<5%) and AssetShare 0. ([annualreports.co.uk](https://www.annualreports.co.uk/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/c/NYSE_CMCM_2018.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Post-2018 disclosures emphasize AI/robotics; later calls do not prominently feature crypto, suggesting activity was limited or de‑emphasized, but this does not negate the documented wallet product within the sample period. No evidence was found of balance‑sheet crypto holdings or mining operations. If future filings break out explicit wallet revenues or assets, materiality codes may warrant revision. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/08/20/cheetah-mobile-inc-cmcm-q2-2018-earnings-conferenc.aspx?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Mar 19, 2018 — Q4 2017 Earnings Call: “We also believe blockchain technologies may create additional opportunities for us… [Cheetah Mobile] has already released… SafeWallet, and… a decentralized app browser.” — [Cheetah Mobile IR transcript (PDF)](https://filecache.investorroom.com/mr5ir_jinshan/134/download/CMCM_4Q17%20Earnings%20Call%20Transcript_EN.pdf) ([filecache.investorroom.com](https://filecache.investorroom.com/mr5ir_jinshan/134/download/CMCM_4Q17%20Earnings%20Call%20Transcript_EN.pdf))

## REDWOOD TST.

* Rank: 294 | Expo: 0.16 | id_str: US7580754023
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Q3 2021 earnings call; 2021–2022 company press releases; Q3 2021 earnings release (8‑K); industry coverage (National Mortgage News, HousingWire)

### Firm Overview
Redwood Trust, Inc. is a U.S. specialty finance company and mortgage REIT listed on the NYSE (ticker: RWT) focused on residential and business‑purpose housing credit through its Sequoia and CoreVest platforms. Within 2017–2024, management pursued technology initiatives (including a venture arm, RWT Horizons) and piloted blockchain‑based reporting in certain RMBS deals; these activities did not involve holding or transacting in cryptocurrencies.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management publicly discussed using a partner’s distributed‑ledger platform to publish loan remittance data on a blockchain for Sequoia RMBS (e.g., SEMT 2021‑6) and described it as a first step toward greater transparency in private‑label RMBS. These disclosures relate to enterprise blockchain infrastructure adopted for mortgage reporting, not to crypto‑asset custody, trading, mining, or treasury holdings. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/27/redwood-trust-reit-inc-rwt-q3-2021-earnings-call-t/))  
RWT Horizons made minority investments in fintechs exploring tokenization/ledger rails (e.g., Liquid Mortgage; industry articles also mention Oasis Pro Markets, Vesta Equity, and Canopy). These are passive venture stakes and partnerships; Redwood itself did not launch crypto financial products or take balance‑sheet exposure to digital assets during the sample period. ([redwoodtrust.com](https://www.redwoodtrust.com/investor-relations/news-events/press-releases/detail/30/redwood-trust-supports-digitization-of-the-mortgage))

### Weight Assignment
There is no evidence from calls, filings, or credible news that Redwood held cryptocurrencies, mined crypto, operated a crypto exchange/custody service, sold crypto investment products, or supplied mining hardware/energy. The company’s references are to blockchain‑enabled reporting in RMBS and small venture investments in related fintechs, which are outside the defined crypto‑exposure mechanisms. Accordingly, the firm is coded unclass = 1.0, with all other mechanism weights = 0.0. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/27/redwood-trust-reit-inc-rwt-q3-2021-earnings-call-t/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Redwood’s partnerships and venture stakes touch digital‑asset market infrastructure (e.g., Oasis Pro Markets’ ATS supporting stablecoin settlement, per industry reporting), which could lead to future tokenized products; however, within 2017–2024 there is no clear, material, direct crypto‑asset exposure. If Redwood were to issue tokenized securities, custody digital assets, or hold crypto on balance sheet, classification may change. ([housingwire.com](https://www.housingwire.com/articles/redwood-trust-and-angel-oak-preparing-for-a-blockchain-future/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 27, 2021 — Q3 2021 earnings call: “first of its kind use of blockchain‑based technology within private‑label RMBS… Liquid Mortgage… payment information… published on the blockchain daily.” — [The Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/27/redwood-trust-reit-inc-rwt-q3-2021-earnings-call-t/)  
- Apr 28, 2022 — Q1 2022 call (as reported): five Sequoia deals used Liquid Mortgage’s blockchain tracking through Jan 2022. — [HousingWire summary](https://www.housingwire.com/articles/redwood-trust-and-angel-oak-preparing-for-a-blockchain-future/)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Apr 19, 2021 — Press release: investment in Liquid Mortgage to digitize and record information on a blockchain; white paper link. — [Redwood Trust IR](https://www.redwoodtrust.com/investor-relations/news-events/press-releases/detail/30/redwood-trust-supports-digitization-of-the-mortgage)  
- Sep 17, 2021 — Press release: pricing of SEMT 2021‑6, “first non‑agency RMBS leveraging blockchain‑based technology.” — [Redwood Trust IR](https://www.redwoodtrust.com/investor-relations/news-events/press-releases/detail/16/redwood-trust-announces-pricing-of-the-markets-first)  
- Oct 27, 2021 — Q3 2021 earnings release: confirms SEMT 2021‑6 leveraging Liquid Mortgage blockchain tech. — [Redwood Trust IR (8‑K/Earnings Release)](https://www.redwoodtrust.com/investor-relations/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0000930236-21-000029/q32021earningsrelease.htm)

**Financial news / other:**

## WAITR HLDGS

* Rank: 295 | Expo: 0.16 | id_str: US9307522097
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 2019 earnings call (Motley Fool); 2020 Form 10‑K; 2021–2022 company press releases; 2024 news coverage of shutdown/bankruptcy

### Firm Overview
Waitr Holdings Inc. was a U.S. on‑demand food ordering and delivery platform that operated the Waitr, Bite Squad and later ASAP brands, rebranding to ASAP in 2022 as it pursued a broader “deliver anything” model. The company ceased operations and filed for Chapter 7 bankruptcy in April 2024. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20220726005718/en/Waitr-Holdings-Inc.-Soon-to-be-Known-as-ASAP-Scores-Exclusive-Mobile-Ordering-Deal-with-MetLife-Stadium-New-York-Giants-New-York-Jets?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across earnings call transcripts, SEC filings and press releases from 2017–2024, management discusses restaurant delivery, in‑stadium/mobile ordering, and facilitation of merchants’ access to third‑party payment processors; there is no evidence of crypto custody, exchange/brokerage, crypto investment products, mining, or mining hardware/energy businesses. In 2021 the firm announced a move into cannabis delivery/payments with a partner; later updates emphasized referring merchants to third‑party payment processing, not digital‑asset handling by the company. Subsequent news focuses on rebranding and the 2024 liquidation, with no crypto‑specific initiatives documented. ([nasdaq.com](https://www.nasdaq.com/press-release/waitr-expanding-into-cannabis-market-2021-03-04?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of credible evidence that Waitr/ASAP held crypto assets, mined crypto, operated crypto financial services, provided crypto‑specific business services, or supplied crypto/mining infrastructure, the firm is coded unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism weights = 0.0. References to facilitating “third‑party payment processing” for merchants and moves into cannabis‑related commerce do not meet the thresholds for the defined crypto‑exposure mechanisms.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Waitr acquired and partnered with payment processors (e.g., Flow Payments) that serve higher‑risk merchant categories; while some processors in the market offer crypto acceptance, the firm’s disclosures describe this line as referrals to third‑party payment providers, with no indication that Waitr itself handled digital assets. No materials in the 2017–2024 window show material, direct crypto exposure; if future archival materials surface showing direct crypto custody/payment processing by Waitr, classification would need revisiting. ([nasdaq.com](https://www.nasdaq.com/press-release/waitr-expanding-into-cannabis-market-2021-03-04?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 10, 2019 — Q1 2019 call: “Our technology platform is purpose built to serve restaurants and consumers in small to medium markets.” — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/05/10/waitr-holdings-inc-wtrh-q1-2019-earnings-call-tran.aspx)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Mar 8, 2021 — Form 10‑K (FY2020): business description focuses on food delivery; no crypto activities disclosed. — [StockLight copy of 10‑K PDF](https://stocklight.com/stocks/us/nasdaq-wtrh/waitr-holdings/annual-reports/nasdaq-wtrh-2021-10K-21722239.pdf)
- Aug 8, 2022 — Q2 2022 results: rebranding to ASAP; notes facilitation of access to third‑party payment providers. — [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20220808005741/en/Waitr-Holdings-Inc.-Soon-to-be-Known-as-ASAP-Reports-Second-Quarter-2022-Results)
- Nov 9, 2022 — Q3 2022 results: “approximately 2,900 merchants are using third‑party payment processing services referred by ASAP.” — [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20221109006007/en/ASAP.com-the-On-Demand-Delivery-Brand-for-Waitr-Holdings-Inc.-Reports-Third-Quarter-2022-Results)

**Financial news / other:**
- Mar 4, 2021 — Company announces move into cannabis delivery/payments via partnership (not crypto). — [Nasdaq press release repost](https://www.nasdaq.com/press-release/waitr-expanding-into-cannabis-market-2021-03-04)
- Aug 16, 2022 — Name change to ASAP tied to strategic shift. — [MarketScreener](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/WAITR-HOLDINGS-INC-65219287/news/Waitr-Holdings-Inc-has-Changed-its-Name-to-ASAP-40961798/)
- Apr 2–3, 2024 — ASAP (formerly Waitr) ceases operations and files Chapter 7 bankruptcy. — [Restaurant Dive](https://www.restaurantdive.com/news/asap-files-chapter-7-bankruptcy-ceases-business/712016/) | [Food On Demand](https://foodondemand.com/04032024/asap-food-delivery-ceases-operations-files-bankruptcy/)

## NEXON

* Rank: 296 | Expo: 0.16 | id_str: JP3758190007
* Weight summary: holders 1.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: IR press release (Apr 28, 2021), Q4 2021 earnings call transcript, 2023–2024 MapleStory Universe announcements (Polygon, Avalanche)

### Firm Overview
Nexon Co., Ltd. is a Tokyo‑listed global online game publisher best known for franchises such as MapleStory and Dungeon&Fighter, operating across Japan, Korea, China, North America and other regions. In April 2021, the company disclosed a treasury purchase of bitcoin, while separately exploring blockchain-enabled game initiatives tied to the MapleStory IP. ([pdf.irpocket.com](https://pdf.irpocket.com/C3659/BbNL/qYha/Sr9r.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Holders: On April 28, 2021 Nexon purchased 1,717 BTC for approximately $100 million, describing the move as a disciplined strategy to protect shareholder value and the purchasing power of cash; subsequent earnings materials referenced revaluation impacts from bitcoin price movements. ([pdf.irpocket.com](https://pdf.irpocket.com/C3659/BbNL/qYha/Sr9r.pdf))  
No evidence was found that Nexon operates a crypto exchange, brokerage, custody, payments network, or crypto investment products; likewise there is no indication of mining operations or mining infrastructure businesses. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/3659.T/transcripts/2021/4))  
Product initiatives: Nexon has pursued MapleStory Universe, an NFT/Web3 ecosystem initially aligned with Polygon Supernets in March 2023 and later announcing an Avalanche partnership in March 2024; these are product uses of blockchain for gameplay rather than financial services or infrastructure provision. ([polygon.technology](https://polygon.technology/blog/nexon-selects-polygon-supernets-for-smash-global-hit-maplestory-universe?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
All observed, substantive crypto exposure during 2017–2024 stems from Nexon’s deliberate treasury holding of bitcoin; management’s earnings call remarks explicitly cite bitcoin-related revaluation effects, and the initial purchase represented a modest share of liquid resources. Therefore, holders = 1.0 and all other mechanism categories = 0.0. MapleStory Universe is treated as a blockchain-enabled content initiative rather than a crypto-financial service or ecosystem support function, so it does not shift weight to finserv, bizserv, or infrastr. ([pdf.irpocket.com](https://pdf.irpocket.com/C3659/BbNL/qYha/Sr9r.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
MapleStory Universe development and related Web3 tooling advanced during 2023–2024 but remained in testing/rollout phases within the sample window; any future launch dynamics that would entail operating a wallet/custody marketplace or tokenized economic layer at scale could warrant reconsideration of category mix. No evidence indicates Nexon reduced or sold its bitcoin holdings within 2017–2024, but the firm’s detailed balance-sheet treatment beyond call commentary is primarily visible via IR releases rather than US-style 10‑K filings. ([za.investing.com](https://za.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-nexon-posts-record-q3-revenue-bullish-on-longterm-growth-93CH-3428543?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 8, 2022 — Q4/FY2021 call: “... a revaluation gain of ¥1.2 billion due to movements in the market price of Bitcoin during the quarter.” — [Roic AI transcript](https://www.roic.ai/quote/3659.T/transcripts/2021/4) ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/3659.T/transcripts/2021/4))
- Aug 2021 — Q2 2021 call: “... we recognized a revaluation loss of JPY 4.5 billion due to movements in the market price of Bitcoin.” — [AlphaSpread (Q2 2021 call page)](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/tse/3659/investor-relations/earnings-call/q2-2021) ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/tse/3659/investor-relations/earnings-call/q2-2021?utm_source=openai))
- Nov 2024 — Q3 2024 call: management noted preparation for large‑scale Q4 testing of MapleStory N within the MapleStory Universe. — [Investing.com transcript](https://za.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-nexon-posts-record-q3-revenue-bullish-on-longterm-growth-93CH-3428543) ([za.investing.com](https://za.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-nexon-posts-record-q3-revenue-bullish-on-longterm-growth-93CH-3428543?utm_source=openai))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**

- Feb 8, 2022 — Q4/FY2021 results release references bitcoin revaluation gain in the period; transcript/audio available via IR. — [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20220207006027/en/Nexon-Releases-Earnings-for-Fourth-Quarter-and-Full-Year-2021) ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20220207006027/en/Nexon-Releases-Earnings-for-Fourth-Quarter-and-Full-Year-2021?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Apr 28, 2021 — Coverage of Nexon’s $100m bitcoin purchase (1,717 BTC at ~$58,226 average). — [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2021-04-28/nexon-joins-tesla-in-bitcoin-bet-with-100-million-purchase) ([bloomberg.com](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2021-04-28/nexon-joins-tesla-in-bitcoin-bet-with-100-million-purchase?utm_source=openai))

## ACI WORLDWIDE

* Rank: 297 | Expo: 0.16 | id_str: US0044981019
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Press releases (2021–2022), Q3 2021 earnings call transcript, product/solutions pages, 2023 Form 10‑K

### Firm Overview
ACI Worldwide, Inc. is a U.S.-based provider of mission‑critical, real‑time payments software serving banks, merchants and billers, listed on Nasdaq under ACIW. Within the 2017–2024 window, its crypto involvement appears through enabling merchant acceptance of cryptocurrencies as part of its Secure eCommerce and Omni‑Commerce offerings. ([aciworldwide.com](https://www.aciworldwide.com/?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Finserv: In October 2021, ACI announced a strategic partnership with RocketFuel Blockchain to offer cryptocurrency payment acceptance via ACI Secure eCommerce, marketed as “zero‑fee crypto” with next‑day settlement. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20211025005051/en/ACI-Worldwide-and-RocketFuel-Launch-Cryptocurrency-Payment-Acceptance-for-Merchants-GloballyPioneering-Zero-Fees?utm_source=openai))  
In June 2022, ACI and Vendo Services extended their partnership to enable merchants to offer more than 127 cryptocurrencies, further evidencing ACI’s role in crypto payments enablement. ([investor.aciworldwide.com](https://investor.aciworldwide.com/news-releases/news-release-details/aci-worldwide-and-vendo-services-partner-enable-merchants-offer?utm_source=openai))  
Management commentary on the Q3 2021 earnings call noted “increasing demand for our services from fintech companies, including cryptocurrency and buy‑now‑pay‑later firms,” indicating exposure via customer demand rather than balance‑sheet activity. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/04/aci-worldwide-inc-aciw-q3-2021-earnings-call-trans/))  
No evidence was found that ACI holds cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet or engages in mining during 2017–2024; filings and company materials frame crypto as a merchant acceptance capability delivered through partners. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/935036/000119312524104853/d62871dars.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
All observed crypto exposure is through payments enablement (acceptance and settlement of crypto for merchants) delivered via ACI’s commerce platforms and partners (e.g., RocketFuel; later references to “Zero‑Fee Crypto”). There is no credible evidence of treasury holdings, mining, or hardware/hosting activities. Accordingly, finserv = 1.0 with all other mechanism categories = 0.0. Materiality appears minor relative to total revenue, so RevShare is coded 1 and AssetShare 0. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20211025005051/en/ACI-Worldwide-and-RocketFuel-Launch-Cryptocurrency-Payment-Acceptance-for-Merchants-GloballyPioneering-Zero-Fees?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Product pages are updated over time and emphasize capability, but uptake and revenue contribution are not broken out in filings or calls within 2017–2024. Evidence of crypto enablement relies on company releases and solutions collateral plus one earnings‑call reference; transcripts do not quantify crypto volumes, and filings do not separately disclose crypto assets or income lines. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20211025005051/en/ACI-Worldwide-and-RocketFuel-Launch-Cryptocurrency-Payment-Acceptance-for-Merchants-GloballyPioneering-Zero-Fees?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 4, 2021 — Q3 2021 call: “We also see increasing demand for our services from fintech companies, including cryptocurrency and buy‑now‑pay‑later firms.” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/04/aci-worldwide-inc-aciw-q3-2021-earnings-call-trans/)  

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Oct 25, 2021 — Press release: ACI and RocketFuel launch crypto payment acceptance via ACI Secure eCommerce, “enabling merchants worldwide to accept cryptocurrency payments—with no processing fees.” — [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20211025005051/en/ACI-Worldwide-and-RocketFuel-Launch-Cryptocurrency-Payment-Acceptance-for-Merchants-GloballyPioneering-Zero-Fees)  
- Jun 29, 2022 — Press release: ACI and Vendo Services extend partnership to support “more than 127 cryptocurrencies” for merchants via ACI Secure eCommerce. — [ACI Worldwide IR](https://investor.aciworldwide.com/news-releases/news-release-details/aci-worldwide-and-vendo-services-partner-enable-merchants-offer)

**Financial news / other:**

## VOXTUR ANALYTICS SUSP - SUSP.09/09/25

* Rank: 298 | Expo: 0.16 | id_str: CA9290821052
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Company news releases (2022–2024), Q2 2024 earnings call transcript, SEDAR+ results release (Q3 2024)

### Firm Overview
Voxtur Analytics Corp is a Canada‑based real estate technology company listed on the TSX Venture Exchange (VXTR) and OTCQB (VXTRF), offering data analytics, valuation, title/settlement and assessment solutions primarily for U.S. and Canadian mortgage markets. Within 2017–2024, crypto relevance appears via its Blue Water Financial Technologies unit exploring tokenization of mortgage assets through partnerships, while core operations remain mortgage tech and services. 

### Exposure Channels Identified
Bizserv: On December 22, 2023 Voxtur announced a strategic partnership between its Blue Water Financial Technologies unit and Security Token Group to deliver “on‑chain ready solutions” for investors, tokenization providers and marketplaces in the mortgage space (pricing, trading, tracking, and quality control). This positions Voxtur as a technology and services provider into the tokenization ecosystem rather than a direct issuer or custodian of digital assets.   
A January 31, 2024 follow‑on post by Security Token Group reiterated the partnership and clarified that Blue Water did not at that time issue tokens or provide tokenization services, underscoring that activity in the period was partnership‑ and enablement‑focused.   
Earnings materials in 2024 focused on Voxtur’s operational restructuring and the planned divestiture/classification of Blue Water as discontinued operations; no treasury crypto holdings or mining were indicated. 

### Weight Assignment
Because the documented crypto adjacency in 2017–2024 arises from providing technology and workflow support into prospective security‑token markets (rather than issuing, custoding, trading, or holding digital assets), the exposure is classified as non‑financial business services. Accordingly, bizserv = 1.0 and all other mechanisms = 0.0. Given the absence of disclosed crypto revenue or assets and the partnership’s pre‑launch status during the sample window, materiality is coded as RevShare = 0 and AssetShare = 0. 

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Partnership announcements describe “on‑chain ready” solutions but do not evidence executed token issuances or custodial activity by Voxtur/Blue Water within 2017–2024. Company transcripts in 2024 do not discuss crypto specifically; Blue Water was being divested/held for sale in late 2024, complicating forward‑looking assessment of crypto involvement. Certainty is capped at 2 due to reliance on company news and absence of crypto‑specific remarks in calls. 

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 30, 2024 — Q2 2024 call: “In July 2024, the company entered into a definitive agreement to divest 50.5% of the interest of Blue Water Financial Technology… [and] has been accounted for as a discontinued operation.” — [Alpha Spread transcript (Q2 2024)](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/xtsx/vxtr/investor-relations/earnings-call/q2-2024) 

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Nov 28, 2024 — Q3 2024 results release: Blue Water presented as disposal group held for sale; continuing operations metrics disclosed. — [Voxtur PDF](https://www.voxtur.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/2024-11-28-Voxtur-Analytics-F24-Q3-Results-News-Release_FINAL.pdf)   
- Aug 29, 2022 — Q2 2022 results release: announcement of the purchase agreement for Blue Water, establishing the capital‑markets unit within Voxtur. — [Voxtur news release PDF](https://www.voxtur.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/TSVX-l-Financial-Results-l-Q2-2022.pdf) 

**Financial news / other:**
- Dec 22, 2023 — “Voxtur Announces Strategic Partnership with Security Token Group,” describing on‑chain ready solutions for mortgage asset tokenization via Blue Water and STA. — [Voxtur website](https://www.voxtur.com/voxtur-announces-strategic-partnership-with-security-token-group/)   
- Jan 31, 2024 — Security Token Group post reiterating the partnership and noting Blue Water did not then issue tokens or provide tokenization services. — [Medium (Security Token Group)](https://medium.com/security-token-group/voxtur-announces-strategic-partnership-with-security-token-group-for-the-tokenization-of-mortgage-5b3f1b12e807) 

</small>
