## IMAGEWARE SYSTEMS

* Rank: 299 | Expo: 0.16 | id_str: US45245S1087
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 2021 Form 10‑K (filed Apr 2022), Q1 2021 earnings call transcript, Oct 2021 company press release, industry coverage (Biometric Update)

### Firm Overview
ImageWare Systems, Inc. is a U.S.-based biometric identity and authentication software provider focused on law enforcement, government and enterprise customers. Within 2017–2024 it modernized its product suite, including an initiative to integrate biometrics with decentralized/self‑sovereign identity capabilities built on blockchain.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Bizserv: Management and filings describe “Imageware Digital ID,” a decentralized identity service enabling self‑sovereign identity underpinned by blockchain technology tied to biometrics. This positions ImageWare as a technology supplier to blockchain‑based identity use cases rather than a financial intermediary.  
In Q1 2021, management discussed self‑sovereign identity (SSI) as a strategic focus and referenced a patent for “zero‑knowledge anonymous verification … using immutable databases, such as a blockchain,” indicating development of enabling software rather than custody or trading of digital assets.  
An October 4, 2021 press release announced entry into the SSI market and membership in decentralized identity standards groups (DIF and Trust Over IP), further reinforcing the role as a non‑financial services provider to blockchain/crypto identity ecosystems.

### Weight Assignment
Evidence shows ImageWare providing non‑financial technology and software that leverages blockchain for decentralized identity, without operating exchanges, custody, investment products, mining, or holding crypto on treasury. Accordingly, bizserv = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. No disclosure indicates identifiable crypto‑specific revenues or balance sheet crypto assets during 2017–2024, so RevShare = 0 and AssetShare = 0. There is no evidence that the equity trades as a crypto proxy, so invbase = 0.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The SSI initiative appears early‑stage during 2021–2022; public materials do not quantify blockchain‑related revenue. Transcripts and filings confirm product direction but not commercial scale. No evidence of direct handling of crypto assets was found.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jun 2, 2021 — Q1 2021 call: “Another… initiative … is self‑sovereign identity (SSI)… we recently received one noteworthy patent for zero‑knowledge… using immutable databases, such as a blockchain.” — [SEC exhibit transcript](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/941685/000165495421006614/ex99-1.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/941685/000165495421006614/ex99-1.htm))  
- Dec 10, 2021 — Q3 2021 call (company overview and operations update). — [AlphaStreet transcript](https://news.alphastreet.com/imageware-systems-inc-iwsy-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcript/) ([news.alphastreet.com](https://news.alphastreet.com/imageware-systems-inc-iwsy-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- 2021 Form 10‑K (filed Apr 2022): “Imageware Digital ID is a decentralized identity service that enables self‑sovereign identity underpinned by blockchain technology tied to biometrics.” — [SEC filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/941685/000143774922008996/iwsy20211231_10k.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/941685/000143774922008996/iwsy20211231_10k.htm))  
- Oct 4, 2021 — Press release: Company entering SSI market; joining Decentralized Identity Foundation and Trust Over IP. — [PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/imageware-to-add-biometrics-to-blockchain-powered-self-sovereign-identity-ssi-301391686.html) ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/imageware-to-add-biometrics-to-blockchain-powered-self-sovereign-identity-ssi-301391686.html))

**Financial news / other:**
- Oct 2021 — Coverage of ImageWare’s SSI initiative, “first biometrically‑enabled solution for blockchain‑based identification platforms.” — [Biometric Update](https://www.biometricupdate.com/202110/imageware-claims-to-be-first-provider-to-include-biometrics-in-ssi) ([biometricupdate.com](https://www.biometricupdate.com/202110/imageware-claims-to-be-first-provider-to-include-biometrics-in-ssi?utm_source=openai))

### Firm Overview
Victory Capital Holdings, Inc. is a U.S.-based diversified asset manager headquartered in San Antonio and listed on Nasdaq under the ticker VCTR. Within the 2017–2024 period, the firm introduced a private crypto index fund for accredited U.S. investors and filed to launch a multi‑coin ETF strategy based on the Nasdaq Crypto Index.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Finserv: In 2021, its wholly owned adviser launched the Victory Hashdex Nasdaq Crypto Index Fund LLC, a private vehicle tracking the Nasdaq Crypto Index for accredited U.S. investors, and concurrently filed for an exchange‑traded product using the same multi‑coin index. Earnings calls in 2021 discussed the crypto product suite, positioning it as diversified exposure to a basket of digital assets rather than a single‑coin offering. SEC filings (Form D) identify the fund, manager and placement agent within the Victory Capital group and disclose capital raised, confirming ongoing product activity. No evidence indicates crypto mining or corporate‑treasury coin holdings.

### Weight Assignment
Evidence shows direct provision of crypto investment products (private fund; planned ETF) and related distribution/management by Victory Capital affiliates. There is no indication of mining, hardware infrastructure, or corporate‑level HODL activity. Accordingly, finserv = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. Form D amounts suggest crypto‑related AUM and fees were small relative to consolidated revenue during 2017–2024, so RevShare = 1 and AssetShare = 0. No sources indicate the stock trades as a crypto proxy, so invbase = 0.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The multi‑coin ETF was filed in 2021 but U.S. regulatory approval for such products was evolving through 2024; uptake of the private fund appears modest based on Form D disclosures, and Victory does not break out crypto revenue. No evidence of balance‑sheet crypto assets at the parent level was found.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 6, 2021 — Q2 2021: “We have a live product today… it’s not a bitcoin ETF… it’s the NASDAQ Crypto Index that’s rebalanced every quarter.” — [The Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/08/06/victory-capital-holdings-inc-vctr-q2-2021-earnings/)  
- Nov 5, 2021 — Q3 2021: “We launched the Crypto product a few months ago… We have seen really good interest… we’re in an education phase.” — [The Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/05/victory-capital-holdings-inc-vctr-q3-2021-earnings/)

- Aug 3, 2021 — SEC S‑1: Registration for VictoryShares Hashdex Nasdaq Crypto Index ETF (multi‑coin index ETF). — [SEC filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1876560/000119312521235281/d102675ds1.htm)

## ING GROEP

* Rank: 301 | Expo: 0.16 | id_str: NL0011821202
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.6, bizserv 0.4, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 2Q 2020 analyst call transcript; 2022 ING press release; 2020–2022 financial news coverage; 2019 annual report

### Firm Overview
ING Groep N.V. is a Netherlands-based universal bank listed in Amsterdam (INGA) with ADRs on the NYSE (ING). Between 2017 and 2024, the group explored digital-asset custody and post-trade infrastructure via an internal platform (Pyctor) and contributed to crypto AML standards work, while not acting as a miner or holding crypto as treasury assets.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Finserv: ING incubated Pyctor, described as a digital-asset custody and post-trade platform covering a range of assets, which the bank spun out to GMEX Group in July 2022. ING also initiated and participated in the Travel Rule Protocol (TRP) working group alongside Standard Chartered, Fidelity Digital Assets and BitGo to operationalize FATF’s Travel Rule for crypto transfers. Bizserv: Beyond custody R&D, ING’s blockchain team worked on privacy-preserving tooling (e.g., zero‑knowledge “Bulletproofs”) and published thought leadership, which supports the wider digital-asset ecosystem without the bank itself taking asset custody for clients at scale. No evidence indicates corporate crypto holdings or mining activities.

### Weight Assignment
Evidence points to direct but limited crypto financial‑services activity through ING’s incubation of a custody/post‑trade platform and crypto AML tooling, balanced by broader blockchain enablement work that does not involve taking client assets. With Pyctor spun out in 2022 and no disclosure of meaningful crypto revenues or balance‑sheet assets, exposure is characterized as peripheral: finserv = 0.6 for custody/AML initiatives, bizserv = 0.4 for technology/policy contributions. Holders, miners and infrastr are set to 0.0. RevShare = 0 and AssetShare = 0 reflect negligible contribution to group revenues and no reported crypto assets.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Pyctor’s spin‑out in 2022 limits ongoing direct custody exposure at the parent; public disclosures do not quantify any crypto‑related revenues or assets. Earnings calls seldom discuss crypto specifically; the available transcript reference is framed as “blockchain” experimentation within trade processes. Classification therefore relies on documented initiatives and press disclosures rather than segmented financial reporting.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 6, 2020 — 2Q 2020 analyst call: “we are also experimenting with blockchain because blockchain is in terms of an end‑to‑end payment system, especially for trade, much safer than all the paper floating around on a global basis.” — [ING analyst call transcript (PDF)](https://ing.com/binaries/content/assets/documents/files/ing-analyst-call-transcript-3.pdf)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Jul 11, 2022 — Press release: “ING spins out digital assets platform Pyctor,” describing custody and post‑trade services for a broad range of digital assets. — [ING newsroom](https://www.ing.com/Newsroom/News/ING-spins-out-digital-assets-platform-Pyctor.htm)
- 2019 Annual Report: Notes ING’s open‑source privacy work on distributed ledgers, including zero‑knowledge range proof codes and “Bulletproofs.” — [Annual report (PDF)](https://www.annualreports.co.uk/HostedData/Annual
- 2019 Annual Report: Notes ING’s blockchain privacy R&D alongside broader innovation initiatives. — [Annual report (PDF)](https://ing.com/binaries/content/assets/documents/files/2019_annual_report_ing_groep_n.v.pdf)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Mar 11, 2022 — 2021 Annual Report on Form 20‑F: Group‑wide activities and risk disclosures; no crypto asset holdings disclosed. — [ING 20‑F (PDF)](https://ing.com/investors/financial-performance/annual-reports/2021)  
- Jul 11, 2022 — Press release: “ING spins out digital assets platform Pyctor,” describing custody and post‑trade services and ongoing collaboration with ING’s digital assets team. — [ING Newsroom](https://www.ing.com/Newsroom/News/ING-spins-out-digital-assets-platform-Pyctor.htm)  
- Jul 2020 — “ING ranks fifth in Forbes for blockchain,” highlighting open‑source ZKRP/ZKSM and Bulletproofs work. — [ING News](https://ing.com/news/2020/07/ing-ranks-fifth-in-forbes-for-blockchain.html)  
- Nov 2018/Oct 2019 — “Blockchain innovation improves data privacy for clients”: ING publishes ZKRP/ZKSM code and explains applications. — [ING Newsroom](https://www.ing.com/Newsroom/News/Blockchain-innovation-improves-data-privacy-for-clients.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- Jun 23, 2020 — “In banking first, ING develops FATF‑friendly protocol for tracking crypto transfers” (TRP; banks + VASPs incl. Standard Chartered, Fidelity DA, BitGo). — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2020/06/23/in-banking-first-ing-develops-fatf-friendly-protocol-for-tracking-crypto-transfers)  
- Oct 8, 2020 — “Group backed by ING, Fidelity and Standard Chartered releases crypto AML tools” (TRP API v1.0). — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2020/10/08/group-backed-by-ing-bank-fidelity-and-standard-chartered-releases-crypto-aml-tools)

- Jul 11–12, 2022 — Additional Pyctor spin‑out coverage. — [Bloomberg Law](https://news.bloomberglaw.com/crypto/ing-sells-digital-assets-platform-pyctor-to-gmex-no-terms) | [FinTech Futures](https://www.fintechfutures.com/fintech/ing-spins-out-digital-post-trade-market-infrastructure-tech-to-gmex-group)

## SHOPIFY SUBD.VTG.SHS.'A'

* Rank: 302 | Expo: 0.16 | id_str: CA82509L1076
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q1 2022 earnings call; 2022–2023 annual filings; 2021–2022 product announcements and financial news

### Firm Overview
Shopify Inc. is a Canada-based, dual-listed e‑commerce software company that provides a commerce platform and related tools for merchants globally. Between 2017 and 2024, Shopify introduced optional crypto‑adjacent features—such as NFT storefront support and integrations with third‑party crypto payment providers—while continuing to focus on its core merchant SaaS and payments stack.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Bizserv: Shopify enabled NFT minting/sales via partner apps and launched “tokengated commerce” features that let merchants gate experiences for NFT holders; these capabilities rely on partners and wallet connections rather than Shopify taking custody of digital assets. ([help.shopify.com](https://help.shopify.com/en/manual/products/digital-service-product/nfts?utm_source=openai))  
Financial news coverage and company materials also highlight third‑party crypto payments integrations (e.g., Strike for Lightning, Crypto.com Pay), again implemented as external gateways for merchants on Shopify. ([forbes.com](https://www.forbes.com/sites/ninabambysheva/2022/04/07/strike-announces-shopify-integration-partnerships-with-ncr-and-blackhawk-bringing-bitcoin-lighting-payments-to-major-merchants/?utm_source=openai))  
An earnings call reference confirms NFTs being sold via Shopify storefronts (e.g., Chicago Bulls/NBA), but filings reviewed show no corporate crypto assets on Shopify’s balance sheet. ([nasdaq.com](https://www.nasdaq.com/articles/shopify-shop-q1-2022-earnings-call-transcript))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence shows Shopify primarily as an enabler of crypto‑related commerce experiences (NFT storefronts, token‑gated access, and optional third‑party crypto payment gateways) without directly issuing, trading, or custoding digital assets. Under the project rules, that constitutes non‑financial business services exposure rather than financial services. Accordingly, bizserv is set to 1.0, with holders, miners, finserv, and infrastr at 0.0. Given the scale of Shopify’s overall revenues and the ancillary nature of these features, crypto‑attributable revenue is assessed as minor (RevShare = 1) and no crypto assets are evident on the balance sheet (AssetShare = 0). ([help.shopify.com](https://help.shopify.com/en/manual/products/digital-service-product/nfts?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Shopify’s features evolved during 2021–2022 and are largely delivered through partners; disclosures do not quantify any crypto‑specific revenue, and earnings calls seldom detail crypto beyond brief mentions. Membership in the Libra/Diem Association in 2020 indicated strategic interest but does not establish an ongoing financial‑services role. Certainty is set to 3 based on a clear transcript quote plus corroborating filings and news. ([nasdaq.com](https://www.nasdaq.com/articles/shopify-shop-q1-2022-earnings-call-transcript))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Searches across earnings call transcripts, SEC annual reports, press releases, and the company website from 2017–2024 show no involvement with cryptocurrencies or blockchain. There is no indication of treasury crypto holdings, crypto mining, crypto financial services, crypto-related business services, or crypto-focused infrastructure. Materials during the period consistently describe a pure biopharma profile centered on NBTXR3 and clinical collaborations (e.g., with Janssen/Johnson & Johnson). Given the absence of any substantive or recurring crypto references across core disclosures and calls, the firm is assigned unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism weights at 0.0. No evidence supports an investor-base crypto proxy effect.

None identified.

### Firm Overview
SuRo Capital Corp. is a U.S.-listed, externally managed business development company that invests primarily in late-stage, high-growth, venture-backed private companies across sectors such as education technology, marketplaces, fintech, and enterprise software. Company filings describe a strategy focused on capital appreciation from equity and equity-like investments; they do not indicate any direct cryptocurrency operations or treasury holdings. ([stocklight.com](https://stocklight.com/stocks/us/nasdaq-ssss/suro-capital/annual-reports/nasdaq-ssss-2023-10K-231175817.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
A review of earnings call transcripts and SEC filings from 2017–2024 shows no evidence that SuRo Capital mines crypto, offers crypto financial services (exchange, custody, payments, funds/ETPs), provides crypto-focused infrastructure, or holds crypto on its own balance sheet. Management has occasionally discussed Web 3.0 and broader blockchain/crypto venture trends as an investment theme, but such references relate to potential or indirect portfolio exposure rather than SuRo Capital itself engaging with digital assets. For example, in Q4 2021 management noted “significant opportunity in Web 3.0,” citing the number of “crypto unicorns” that year; this does not imply direct crypto operations by SuRo Capital. Accordingly, none of the six mechanism categories apply. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/03/09/suro-capital-corp-ssss-q4-2021-earnings-call-trans/))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of direct crypto involvement in disclosures and calls, SuRo Capital is classified as unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism weights at 0.0. Incidental commentary about Web 3.0/crypto venture activity and any indirect exposure via portfolio companies do not meet the defined thresholds for holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr categories, which require direct asset exposure or operating activity. ([stocklight.com](https://stocklight.com/stocks/us/nasdaq-ssss/suro-capital/annual-reports/nasdaq-ssss-2023-10K-231175817.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
SuRo Capital’s portfolio strategy can include investments in companies related to emerging technologies (at times including Web 3.0 themes), which may create indirect sensitivity to crypto markets; however, this does not constitute direct crypto activity by SuRo Capital under the project definitions. Transcript availability varies by source; the classification relies on accessible calls and the most relevant 10‑K within the 2017–2024 window. ([stocklight.com](https://stocklight.com/stocks/us/nasdaq-ssss/suro-capital/annual-reports/nasdaq-ssss-2023-10K-231175817.pdf))

## UNITED OVERSEAS BANK

* Rank: 305 | Expo: 0.16 | id_str: SG1M31001969
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0

* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: UOB Group research (Nov 2023), MAS/Marketnode Project Guardian materials (Nov 2022), Business Times feature (Nov 2023)

### Firm Overview
United Overseas Bank Limited (UOB) is a Singapore‑headquartered regional bank providing retail, corporate and investment banking, wealth management and treasury services across ASEAN and key global markets. Within digital assets, UOB has participated in Singapore’s tokenisation pilots for capital markets products and distribution, while explicitly avoiding retail cryptocurrency offerings during the 2017–2024 period. ([uobgroup.com](https://www.uobgroup.com/annualreport?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
- Financial services (finserv): UOB worked with HSBC and Marketnode under MAS’s Project Guardian to pilot “native digital issuance” of wealth management products using distributed ledger technology, and concluded a technical pilot on issuance and distribution of a digitally native structured product in June 2023, with UOB distributing to private‑bank clients. UOB also highlights being the first financial‑institution issuer in Singapore to tap Marketnode’s digital‑asset issuance, depository and servicing platform, and has collaborated with ADDX on the digitisation and digital custody of sustainability‑linked bonds. ([marketnode.com](https://www.marketnode.com/media-centre/mas-launches-new-industry-pilots-hsbc-and-uob-are-working-with-marketnode-to-enable-native-digital-issuance-of-wealth-management-products))  
- Scope boundaries: UOB stated it refrained from offering cryptocurrencies to clients pending stronger investor‑protection rules; UOB Malaysia’s custody FAQs similarly note they do not support crypto/digital‑asset custody. This supports classification as digital‑asset financial services (tokenised securities, pilots) rather than crypto exchange/custody for retail. ([businesstimes.com.sg](https://www.businesstimes.com.sg/companies-markets/banking-finance/future-finance-2023/digital-assets-improve-capital-market))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence centers on UOB’s direct role in issuing/distributing tokenised structured products and engaging in MAS‑led tokenisation pilots, which fits the finserv category’s criteria (handling or issuing digital‑asset products and related distribution/custody). There is no credible evidence of proprietary crypto holdings, mining, or crypto‑specific infrastructure provision. Accordingly, finserv is set to 1.0 and all other mechanism weights to 0.0. Given the lack of a retrievable public earnings‑call transcript to quote, certainty is capped at 2 despite multiple corroborating sources. ([marketnode.com](https://www.marketnode.com/media-centre/mas-launches-new-industry-pilots-hsbc-and-uob-are-working-with-marketnode-to-enable-native-digital-issuance-of-wealth-management-products))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Earnings‑call transcript access: MarketScreener lists multiple UOB calls from 2017–2024, but full text is behind login; no quote could be retrieved publicly, capping certainty at 2. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/UNITED-OVERSEAS-BANK-LIMI-50911274/news-call-transcripts/))  
- Tokenised‑asset activity largely reflects pilots and early commercialisation; disclosures do not quantify material revenue contribution within 2017–2024, so RevShare is coded as minor and AssetShare as 0 based on available evidence. ([businesstimes.com.sg](https://www.businesstimes.com.sg/companies-markets/banking-finance/future-finance-2023/digital-assets-improve-capital-market))

### Evidence

## FAR EAST CONSORT.INTL.

* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Annual Report 2024 (MD&A, Chairman’s statement), results presentation listings (2017–2024), HKEX filings, trade press on gaming unit

### Firm Overview
Far East Consortium International Limited is a Hong Kong–based developer and operator focused on property development, hotel operations and management, car park operations, and a smaller gaming segment mainly in Europe. Company reports and third‑party profiles consistently describe a diversified real estate–anchored group without reference to cryptocurrency activities. ([fecil.com.hk](https://www.fecil.com.hk/Product_Photo/files/annual-report/en/2024/annual/08management-discussion-and-analysis2024.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across FY2024 disclosures, the group’s liquidity and investment securities are described as fixed‑income instruments, listed shares, and notes linked to a mortgage portfolio; there is no mention of digital assets or cryptocurrencies held for treasury or product purposes. No evidence was found of mining operations, crypto exchange/brokerage, custody, tokenised products, or blockchain‑specific infrastructure sales. The company’s gaming arm (Palasino) added online operations under a standard iGaming platform agreement and obtained a Malta online gaming licence in 2022, but these activities are unrelated to crypto. Results presentation/webcast materials spanning 2017–2024 similarly show a focus on property, hotels, car parks and gaming. ([fecil.com.hk](https://www.fecil.com.hk/Product_Photo/files/annual-report/en/2024/annual/08management-discussion-and-analysis2024.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
No substantive crypto exposure is evidenced in filings, presentations or credible media during the 2017–2024 sample period. Investment and liquidity disclosures point to traditional securities only, and public materials describe core operations in real estate, hospitality, car parks and gaming, without crypto touchpoints. Accordingly, the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0); all other mechanism weights are set to 0.0. ([fecil.com.hk](https://www.fecil.com.hk/Product_Photo/files/annual-report/en/2024/annual/08management-discussion-and-analysis2024.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Earnings-call transcripts: While investor presentations and webcasts are available for 2017–2024, public transcript text is not provided on the IR site; no crypto references were observed in accessible decks or filings. ([fecil.com.hk](https://www.fecil.com.hk/?route=presentations))  
- Scope: Searches across company filings and trade press during 2017–2024 yielded no credible crypto links; should future disclosures change, reassessment would be warranted. ([fecil.com.hk](https://www.fecil.com.hk/Product_Photo/files/annual-report/en/2024/annual/08management-discussion-and-analysis2024.pdf))

## S4 CAP.ORD.SHS.

* Rank: 307 | Expo: 0.15 | id_str: GB00BFZZM640
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Annual Report 2023, H1 2022 and FY2023 results decks, H1 2024 earnings call transcript, Monks Web3 partner/case pages

### Firm Overview
S4 Capital plc is a London‑listed digital advertising, marketing and technology services group founded by Sir Martin Sorrell and operating globally under its unitary brand Media.Monks (rebranded to Monks in 2024). Company filings describe a focus on content, data and digital media, and technology services for large enterprise clients; they do not indicate any treasury crypto holdings or crypto financial products. ([annualreports.co.uk](https://www.annualreports.co.uk/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/s/LSE_SFOR_2023.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s crypto exposure arises from providing non‑financial services to brands and platforms building Web3/NFT experiences. Evidence includes S4’s own results deck highlighting “Launching the Vault, a full‑blown NFT primary marketplace, for Gucci,” and noting it was an “NFT Cloud pilot launch partner” with a “Global GTM lead position for Commerce/Web3.” ([s4capital.com](https://www.s4capital.com/data/production/2023-03/S4%20H12022%20Results%20Presentation.pdf?VersionId=.L.ezyYPkN7QEq54A3XM43vEg8xH.mS4))  
Media.Monks (Monks) is also identified as a Salesforce Web3 launch partner supporting NFT Management and Web3 Connect, and showcases a Web3 platform build for BITKRAFT—activities that involve strategy, design and integration but not custody, exchange, or issuance of digital assets by S4 itself. ([monks.com](https://www.monks.com/articles/mediamonks-named-salesforce-web3-launch-partner))  
Earnings‑call materials through 2024 frame the business in content, data & digital media, and technology services with technology clients a large share of revenue, reinforcing that any crypto linkage is via client work rather than balance‑sheet exposure or financial intermediation. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/SFOR.L/transcripts/2024/2))

### Weight Assignment
Classified as bizserv = 1.0. Across 2017–2024, the clearest, repeated linkage to crypto is S4/Monks’ Web3/NFT consulting, creative and platform integration work (e.g., Gucci Vault/NFT initiatives; Salesforce Web3 launch partner status). There is no evidence of crypto holdings, mining, custody, brokerage/exchange operations, or crypto‑specific hardware/energy infrastructure sales. As such, all weight is assigned to non‑financial services supporting the crypto ecosystem. ([s4capital.com](https://www.s4capital.com/data/production/2023-03/S4%20H12022%20Results%20Presentation.pdf?VersionId=.L.ezyYPkN7QEq54A3XM43vEg8xH.mS4))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Earnings‑call transcripts reviewed do not explicitly reference “crypto” or “Web3”; the classification relies on investor decks and Monks case/partner documentation that substantiate Web3/NFT client work. Certainty is set to 3 accordingly. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/SFOR.L/transcripts/2024/2))  
- Self‑published case studies and partner pages are used alongside company results materials; no segment‑level crypto revenue disclosure is provided in filings, suggesting crypto revenues are minor within overall operations. ([annualreports.co.uk](https://www.annualreports.co.uk/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/s/LSE_SFOR_2023.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Sep 19, 2024 — H1 2024 call: “technology [was] still by far the main category, representing 44% of our revenue.” — [Roic AI transcript] (https://www.roic.ai/quote/SFOR.L/transcripts/2024/2) ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/SFOR.L/transcripts/2024/2))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**

### Firm Overview
NCC Group plc is a UK‑headquartered cyber security and software escrow company listed in London, providing technical assurance, managed services and consulting to enterprise and public‑sector clients globally. Company filings describe a cyber and software resilience business model and do not indicate any treasury crypto holdings. ([online.flippingbook.com](https://online.flippingbook.com/view/942751376?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The firm offers dedicated blockchain/Web3 security services, including smart‑contract audits, Web3 application reviews, and crypto‑custody/operational security assessments. ([nccgroup.com](https://www.nccgroup.com/technical-assurance/blockchain-security/))  
NCC Group has published public security assessments for crypto protocols and infrastructure, such as Electric Coin Company’s Zcash NU4 upgrade (2020), the Zcash Foundation’s Zebra node implementation (2023), and Hedera Hashgraph (2024). These activities reflect consulting and assurance work for crypto clients rather than custody, exchange operations, or proprietary token holdings. ([nccgroup.com](https://www.nccgroup.com/us/research-blog/public-report-electric-coin-company-nu4-cryptographic-specification-and-implementation-review/))

### Weight Assignment
Classified as bizserv = 1.0. Across 2017–2024, the only substantiated linkage to crypto is NCC Group’s non‑financial, third‑party security assurance for blockchain ecosystems (audits/assessments and related consulting). There is no evidence of mining, balance‑sheet crypto holdings, issuance/custody of crypto financial products, or crypto‑specific hardware/energy infrastructure sales. ([nccgroup.com](https://www.nccgroup.com/technical-assurance/blockchain-security/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Earnings‑call materials reviewed focus on core cyber services and organizational performance; crypto is not presented as a standalone segment, and no segment‑level crypto revenue disclosure is provided in filings. Certainty is set to 3 based on a transcript anchor plus multiple corroborating sources for blockchain assurance work. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/NCC-GROUP-PLC-4004767/news/Transcript-NCC-Group-plc-2024-Earnings-Call-Aug-01-2024-47537635/))

* Rank: 309 | Expo: 0.15 | id_str: CA1254911003

* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q1 2021 earnings call transcript; 2021 Annual Information Form; CI GAM press releases (2021)

### Firm Overview
CI Financial Corp is a Canadian-based wealth and asset management company whose operating subsidiaries include CI Global Asset Management and wealth advisory platforms. Company filings describe a diversified product lineup; in 2021 CI added “Digital Assets” products including bitcoin and ether ETFs and mutual funds to modernize its asset management offering. ([s28.q4cdn.com](https://s28.q4cdn.com/266555903/files/doc_financials/2021/ar/CI-Financial-AIF-2021.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
CI’s crypto linkage is through financial products that provide investors with direct exposure to digital assets. In March and April 2021, CI Global Asset Management launched the CI Galaxy Bitcoin ETF (BTCX) and the CI Galaxy Ethereum ETF (ETHX), alongside bitcoin and ether mutual funds; these products are advised in partnership with Galaxy Digital. These activities constitute crypto financial services (issuance/management of crypto investment products) rather than proprietary coin holding, mining, or infrastructure provision. ([cifinancial.com](https://www.cifinancial.com/ci-gam/ca/en/newsroom/press-releases/ci-galaxy-bitcoin-etf-to-launch-on-the-tsx-on-march-9.html?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Assigned finserv = 1.0. Management explicitly highlighted “an incredibly successful launch of our cryptocurrency strategies,” stating CI had established a leadership position “in both Bitcoin and Ethereum,” and uniquely offered both mutual fund and ETF structures; filings list the CI Galaxy Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs and related mutual funds under new “Digital Assets” products. There is no evidence of mining operations, crypto held on CI’s own balance sheet, or hardware/energy infrastructure sales. ([s28.q4cdn.com](https://s28.q4cdn.com/266555903/files/doc_financials/2021/q1/CI-Financial-conference-call-transcript-Q1-2021.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Crypto ETFs and mutual funds are separate investment funds; their assets reside off CI’s corporate balance sheet, so balance‑sheet crypto exposure appears negligible. CI does not disclose a standalone crypto revenue segment; given CI’s broader AUM base, crypto‑related fees are assessed as minor within 2017–2024. None of the reviewed sources indicate treasury crypto holdings or mining. ([s28.q4cdn.com](https://s28.q4cdn.com/266555903/files/doc_financials/2021/ar/CI-Financial-AIF-2021.pdf))

## MITEK SYSTEMS

* Rank: 310 | Expo: 0.15 | id_str: US6067102003
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: earnings calls (Q4 2019; Q4 2023), 10-K (2024), company press releases/case studies (2018–2024)

### Firm Overview
Mitek Systems is a U.S.-based software company focused on digital identity verification, liveness detection/biometrics, and mobile check deposit solutions serving financial institutions, fintechs and other regulated digital platforms. Within this core, it markets KYC/AML-focused identity products that are used by cryptocurrency platforms for onboarding and compliance.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company provides non‑financial services to crypto platforms via identity verification and KYC/AML tooling. Mitek publicly highlights cryptocurrency exchanges and wallets as a use case for its KYC solutions and has published crypto‑specific materials and customer case studies. Press materials show direct deployments with crypto brokers/exchanges (e.g., BTCDirect; Coinme) to automate KYC and reduce onboarding friction. These activities fit business services to the crypto ecosystem rather than coin holding, mining, financial product issuance, or hardware/infrastructure provision. ([miteksystems.com](https://www.miteksystems.com/glossary/know-your-customer-kyc?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Assigned bizserv = 1.0 based on multiple company‑hosted sources demonstrating Mitek’s digital identity/KYC products are used by cryptocurrency exchanges/brokers (press release for BTCDirect; Coinme case study) and educational content positioning crypto compliance as a core KYC use case. No evidence indicates treasury crypto holdings, mining operations, custody/exchange services, or sales of mining hardware/energy capacity. Earnings calls during 2019–2024 consistently frame Mitek as an identity verification and fraud‑prevention software provider to banks/fintechs, aligning with a non‑financial services role to crypto clients. ([miteksystems.com](https://www.miteksystems.com/press-releases/mitek-delivers-digital-kyc-for-cryptocurrency-broker-btcdirect?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Earnings call transcripts reviewed in the 2017–2024 window emphasize identity verification broadly but do not explicitly name crypto customers; crypto linkage relies on company press/case studies and product content. The firm does not disclose a crypto‑specific revenue segment; crypto‑related revenue is assessed as minor within diversified identity and deposit businesses. ([assets.thevendorgroup.com](https://assets.thevendorgroup.com/site/97b87faa-91f0-4edd-a3ac-ecbbcfffe370/2025/07/25/6882d7975ed99accd4afcb36/MITK%20-%20Q4%202023%20Earnings%20Call%20Transcript.pdf))

**Earnings calls:**

## PROSEGUR CIA.SECURIDAD

* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: earnings calls (Q3 2021; Q2 2024), audited consolidated annual accounts (2024), company press releases (2020–2023)

### Firm Overview
Prosegur Compañía de Seguridad, S.A. is a Madrid‑based multinational private security group listed in Spain, operating guarding, cash‑in‑transit (Prosegur Cash), alarms, and cybersecurity businesses across multiple regions. Within Prosegur Cash, the group launched “Prosegur Crypto,” an institutional digital‑asset custody service positioned as a secure, cold‑storage solution for financial institutions and platforms.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s exposure to crypto comes from operating institutional digital‑asset custody under the Prosegur Crypto brand within Prosegur Cash. Corporate materials and press releases describe the service as a comprehensive custody and governance platform, including a “Crypto Bunker” cold‑storage environment, and name exchange/platform clients such as Bit2Me. In 2023 the group partnered with Minos Global to combine trading and custody for institutional clients; audited 2024 financials note an increased stake in Minos, described as providing “brokerage and custody services for digital crypto‑assets.” These activities fit the financial‑services channel (custody/crypto products) rather than coin holding, mining, or hardware provision. ([prosegurcrypto.com](https://www.prosegurcrypto.com/en?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Assigned finserv = 1.0 based on multiple company sources and audited filings confirming Prosegur operates an institutional crypto‑asset custody platform (Prosegur Crypto), built around cold storage and physical security, and that it deepened its strategic collaboration/investment with Minos Global to deliver custody/trading for tokenized instruments. No evidence indicates proprietary crypto treasury holdings, mining operations, or crypto‑specific hardware/energy infrastructure; earnings calls frame growth via “new products” and transformation, consistent with custody being a service line within Prosegur Cash. ([prosegur.es](https://www.prosegur.es/dam/jcr%3A1d8756bb-e163-452d-b0f3-bedc0f78f6bc/201118_Prosegur_CRYPTO_OK%20%28002%29.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Earnings‑call transcripts reviewed in 2021–2024 discuss transformation and “new products” but do not frequently reference Prosegur Crypto by name; linkage to crypto relies primarily on company press materials and audited accounts. The group does not disclose a standalone crypto‑custody revenue line; crypto revenue is assessed as minor within diversified operations. Classification is at the parent level; the crypto activity sits within subsidiary Prosegur Cash. ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/mad/cash/investor-relations/earnings-call/q3-2021))

## TETRAGON FINANCIAL GROUP

* Rank: 312 | Expo: 0.15 | id_str: GG00B1RMC548
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0

* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 2 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Annual reports (2020, 2023), FY2022 earnings call (Mar 6, 2023), Delaware Chancery filings and Bloomberg coverage (Jan–Apr 2021)

### Firm Overview
Tetragon Financial Group is a Guernsey‑registered, closed‑ended investment company listed in Amsterdam and London that invests across asset classes including asset management stakes, CLOs, real estate and private equity. Within the 2017–2024 window, the company held a direct private equity position in Ripple Labs, a blockchain technology firm, alongside a diversified portfolio.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Tetragon’s crypto exposure arises from its private equity holding in Ripple Labs (Series A/B/C preferred shares), which the company discusses in investor communications and lists among top positions. The 2023 annual report notes Ripple as a top‑10 holding and attributes part of annual gains to court developments around XRP and broader crypto market dynamics. Management references Ripple explicitly on earnings calls when detailing direct private investments. There is no evidence that Tetragon holds cryptocurrencies on its own balance sheet, operates mining, offers custody/exchange services, or supplies crypto‑specific infrastructure. ([tetragoninv.com](https://www.tetragoninv.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/Tetragon-Financial-Group-Annual-Report-2023.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Classified fully as finserv because the channel is financial in nature: equity ownership in a crypto‑focused technology company that affects NAV and period gains/losses. This is distinct from treasury “holders” (no on‑balance‑sheet tokens), “miners” (no mining), “bizserv” (no non‑financial services to the crypto sector), and “infrastr” (no crypto hardware/energy provision). Evidence spans annual reports (valuation, position size, attribution) and transcripts explicitly naming Ripple; legal/news items further corroborate the linkage and materiality to results. ([tetragoninv.com](https://www.tetragoninv.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/Tetragon-Financial-Group-Annual-Report-2023.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Exposure is indirect (via equity in Ripple Labs rather than direct token holdings or client crypto services), so mapping to finserv reflects the financial‑investment channel rather than operational crypto activity. Tetragon is diversified; Ripple’s contribution varied by year, so crypto sensitivity may be episodic rather than continuous. Public, free transcripts are limited; quotes are sourced from third‑party transcript publishers. ([tetragoninv.com](https://www.tetragoninv.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/Tetragon-Financial-Group-Annual-Report-2023.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Mar 6, 2023 — FY2022 earnings call: “This category contains the Ripple Labs investments as well as 3 other unlisted positions.” — [GuruFocus transcript](https://www.gurufocus.com/stock/XAMS%3ATFG/transcripts/2377984) ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/stock/XAMS%3ATFG/transcripts/2377984))
- Q4 2020 — Earnings call: “When we invested in the Series C stock in December 2019, we bargained for the right
... to redeem if the SEC determined XRP to be a security on a go‑forward basis.” — [2020 Annual Report reference, reiterated in results materials] (https://www.tetragoninv.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/12/2020-annual-report.pdf)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- 2023 Annual Report: “Direct: This category produced gains of $32.2 million during the period, related to positive performance in the investment in Ripple Labs Inc.” — [Tetragon Financial Group Annual Report 2023 (PDF)](https://www.tetragoninv.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/Tetragon-Financial-Group-Annual-Report-2023.pdf)
- 2023 Annual Report: “In July 2023, a U.S. judge ruled that Ripple Labs did not violate federal securities law by selling its XRP token on public exchanges… [and] the court dismissed the SEC’s request for an interlocutory appeal.” — [Tetragon Financial Group Annual Report 2023 (PDF)](https://www.tetragoninv.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/Tetragon-Financial-Group-Annual-Report-2023.pdf)
- 2020 Annual Report: “As of 31 December 2020, Tetragon’s investment in Ripple Labs Series C Preferred stock was valued at $174.6 million… [Tetragon] bargained for the option to redeem its investment earlier if the SEC determined that XRP is a security… Ripple Labs is contesting in Delaware Chancery Court.” — [Tetragon Financial Group Annual Report 2020 (PDF)](https://www.tetragoninv.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/12/2020-annual-report.pdf)

### Firm Overview
Cellectis S.A. is a French clinical-stage biotechnology company developing allogeneic CAR T-cell therapies using its TALEN gene-editing platform, with ADSs listed on the Nasdaq Global Market. Company filings and press materials emphasize oncology programs such as UCART22, UCART123, and UCART20x22 and collaborations with partners including AstraZeneca. ([cellectis.com](https://www.cellectis.com/uploads/files/CLSS_20-F_YE2023_VF.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024 filings, earnings materials and press releases, Cellectis’ activities are biopharmaceutical in nature (gene editing, cell therapy R&D and clinical trials) with no indication of cryptocurrency holdings, mining, financial services, non‑financial crypto services, or crypto‑directed infrastructure. A full-text search of the 2023 Form 20‑F shows no references to “blockchain,” “bitcoin,” or “crypto,” and management’s earnings call remarks focus on CAR‑T programs and manufacturing. No evidence suggests any material operational, treasury, or client-service linkage to digital assets. ([cellectis.com](https://www.cellectis.com/uploads/files/CLSS_20-F_YE2023_VF.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of credible, substantive crypto-related references across filings, calls, or reputable news, the firm is assigned unclass = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. This reflects that equity performance would not be expected to move through crypto-specific channels (holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, infrastr) based on evidence within the January 2017–December 2024 window. ([cellectis.com](https://www.cellectis.com/uploads/files/CLSS_20-F_YE2023_VF.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified. Public materials reviewed consistently describe a pure-play biopharma profile; transcripts available in the window are limited but align with filings and press releases. * Primary sources: 10-K filing (FY2024), Q2 2021 earnings call (Aug 9, 2021), FDA approval news release (Aug 2, 2024)

### Firm Overview
Adaptimmune Therapeutics plc is a commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company developing engineered T‑cell receptor (TCR) therapies for solid tumors, with American Depositary Shares listed on Nasdaq. In August 2024, the FDA granted accelerated approval to its product Tecelra (afamitresgene autoleucel) for certain adults with unresectable or metastatic synovial sarcoma. ([adaptimmune.com](https://www.adaptimmune.com/investors-and-media/sec-filings/annual-reports/content/0001558370-25-003544/adap-20241231x10k.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024, Adaptimmune’s disclosures and earnings discussions focus on oncology programs, clinical development, and commercialization of TCR therapies, with no indication of cryptocurrency holdings, mining, crypto financial services, non‑financial crypto services, or crypto‑directed infrastructure. A full‑text search of the company’s 2024 Form 10‑K shows no references to “blockchain,” “bitcoin,” or “crypto.” Management’s calls in 2019 and 2021 center on trials such as SPEARHEAD‑1/SURPASS and preparation for BLA filing and commercialization, not digital‑asset activities. ([adaptimmune.com](https://www.adaptimmune.com/investors-and-media/sec-filings/annual-reports/content/0001558370-25-003544/adap-20241231x10k.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of any substantive crypto-related activity in filings, transcripts, or reputable news during the January 2017–December 2024 window, the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism weights = 0.0. This reflects that Adaptimmune’s equity would not be expected to move via crypto-specific channels (holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, infrastr) based on available evidence. ([adaptimmune.com](https://www.adaptimmune.com/investors-and-media/sec-filings/annual-reports/content/0001558370-25-003544/adap-20241231x10k.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 9, 2021 — Q2 2021: “we’re working hard to file our BLA next year and achieve the first element of our 2‑2‑5‑2 strategy to have a product on the market targeting MAGE‑A4.” — The Motley Fool (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/08/09/adaptimmune-therapeutics-plc-adap-q2-2021-earnings/)
- Aug 1, 2019 — Q2 2019: “We have already seen compelling data in sarcoma with ADP‑A2M4… and have recently started a trial with the next‑generation SPEAR T‑cell.” — The Motley Fool (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/08/01/adaptimmune-therapeutics-adap-q2-2019-earnings-cal.aspx)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- 2024 Form 10‑K: company described as a commercial‑stage biopharma focused on cell therapies; no mentions of blockchain/bitcoin/crypto. — Adaptimmune (https://www.adaptimmune.com/investors-and-media/sec-filings/annual-reports/content/0001558370-25-003544/adap-20241231x10k.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- Aug 2, 2024 — FDA news release: accelerated approval of Tecelra (afamitresgene autoleucel) to Adaptimmune LLC. — U.S. FDA (https://www.fda.gov/news-events/press-announcements/fda-approves-first-gene-therapy-treat-adults-metastatic-synovial-sarcoma)
- Aug 1, 2024 — Company press release announcing FDA accelerated approval of TECELRA. — Adaptimmune/Business Wire (https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20240801538240/en/Adaptimmune-Receives-U.S.-FDA-Accelerated-Approval-of-TECELRA%C2%AE-afamitresgene-autoleucel-the-First-Approved-Engineered-Cell-Therapy-for-a-Solid-Tumor)

</small>

## SURGEPAYS

* Rank: 315 | Expo: 0.15 | id_str: US86882L2043
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.1, finserv 0.1, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.8
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2018, 2022), Q3 2023 earnings call, 2018 ERC‑20 token press releases

### Firm Overview
SurgePays, Inc. is a U.S.-based technology and telecommunications company focused on providing prepaid wireless and fintech services to underbanked consumers through a nationwide network of independent convenience and community stores. The business mixes MVNO wireless (including subsidized broadband programs) with a fintech point‑of‑sale platform used by store clerks to process top‑ups and other transactions.

### Exposure Channels Identified
In late 2018, when the company operated as Surge Holdings, it launched an ERC‑20 “Surge Utility Token” on Ethereum as a loyalty/rewards instrument and described having a cryptocurrency asset mining operation involving Ethereum and Litecoin; it also referenced a goal of holding certain digital assets, but then sold all cryptocurrency assets in December 2018. ([d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net](https://d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net/_4edc38913a8028c0cb9bdb95f412b562/surgeholdings/news/2018-11-14_SURGE_BLOCKCHAIN_TO_LAUNCH_SURGE_UTILITY_TOKEN_ON__25.pdf))  
Subsequent filings characterize “Surge Blockchain” primarily as a software/wholesale marketplace embedded in the SurgePays fintech portal, with minimal segment revenue (about $113k, ~0.09% of total revenue in 2022), and no ongoing crypto custody, exchange, or mining activities disclosed. ([materials.proxyvote.com](https://materials.proxyvote.com/Approved/86882L/20230801/AR_548604.PDF))  
More recent earnings calls emphasize prepaid wireless, ACP/MVNO growth and fintech distribution to underbanked customers rather than digital‑asset initiatives, indicating crypto is not a current driver of operations. ([insidermonkey.com](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/surgepays-inc-nasdaqsurg-q3-2023-earnings-call-transcript-1225992/))

### Weight Assignment
A minority weight is assigned to miners (0.1) based on the company’s explicit 2018 disclosure of operating a crypto mining setup, which was exited the same year. A minority weight is assigned to finserv (0.1) for issuing and operating an ERC‑20‑based loyalty token in 2018. No weight is given to holders due to sale of all crypto assets in December 2018 and lack of evidence of deliberate, ongoing treasury crypto holdings. The remaining 0.8 is assigned to unclass to reflect that 2019–2024 disclosures and calls focus on telecom/fintech operations with only nominal “blockchain” segment activity unrelated to crypto asset exposure.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The “Surge Blockchain” label in filings refers to a software/wholesale module within the fintech portal and may be mistaken for crypto‑asset activity; segment disclosures and narrative indicate limited relevance to digital assets post‑2018. Evidence of token usage and mining is confined to 2018; no later transcripts or filings substantiate ongoing crypto activities in the 2019–2024 window.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 2, 2023 — Q3 2023: “the core business of wireless and Fintech was up over $2 million in Q3,” and the company is “providing wireless telecom and fintech products to the underbanked and underserved.” — Insider Monkey (Q3 2023 transcript) (https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/surgepays-inc-nasdaqsurg-q3-2023-earnings-call-transcript-1225992/) ([insidermonkey.com](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/surgepays-inc-nasdaqsurg-q3-2023-earnings-call-transcript-1225992/))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- 2018 Form 10‑K: “Surge Cryptocurrency strategically mines Ethereum, Litecoin and cryptocurrencies… The goal for this subsidiary is to hold Bitcoin, Litecoin, Ripple and Stellar as digital assets… In December 2018, the Company sold all of its Cryptocurrency assets.” — CompaniesMarketCap mirror of 2018 10‑K (https://companiesmarketcap.com/inr/surgepays/sec-reports-10k/0001493152-19-004471/) ([companiesmarketcap.com](https://companiesmarketcap.com/inr/surgepays/sec-reports-10k/0001493152-19-004471/))
- 2022 Form 10‑K (filed Mar 30, 2023): describes a “blockchain fintech platform” as part of the portal and shows Surge Blockchain, LLC revenue of $112,911 (~0.09% of total) for 2022. — SurgePays 2022 10‑K PDF (https://materials.proxyvote.com/Approved/86882L/20230801/AR_548604.PDF) ([materials.proxyvote.com](https://materials.proxyvote.com/Approved/86882L/20230801/AR_548604.PDF))

**Financial news / other:**
- Nov 14, 2018 — “Surge Blockchain to Launch Surge Utility Token on Ethereum Network… ERC‑20 Surge Utility Token…” — GlobeNewswire (press release PDF) (https://d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net/_4edc38913a8028c0cb9bdb95f412b562/surgeholdings/news/2018-11-14_SURGE_BLOCKCHAIN_TO_LAUNCH_SURGE_UTILITY_TOKEN_ON__25.pdf) ([d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net](https://d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net/_4edc38913a8028c0cb9bdb95f412b562/surgeholdings/news/2018-11-14_SURGE_BLOCKCHAIN_TO_LAUNCH_SURGE_UTILITY_TOKEN_ON__25.pdf))
- Nov 28, 2018 — “SURGE BLOCKCHAIN ANNOUNCES SURGEPAYS 2.0 RELEASE,” company “holdings include… Cryptocurrency Asset Mining.” — GlobeNewswire (https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2018/11/28/1658264/0/en/SURGE-BLOCKCHAIN-ANNOUNCES-SURGEPAYS-2-0-RELEASE.html) ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2018/11/28/1658264/0/en/SURGE-BLOCKCHAIN-ANNOUNCES-SURGEPAYS-2-0-RELEASE.html))

</small>

## HCH.TELECOM.HK.HOLDINGS

* Rank: 316 | Expo: 0.14 | id_str: KYG4672G1064
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.1, bizserv 0.1, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.8
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Company press releases (2021–2022), 2023 annual report, 2017 disposal call transcript, trade press coverage (2022)

### Firm Overview
Hutchison Telecommunications Hong Kong Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong–listed mobile network operator under CK Hutchison that provides consumer and enterprise telecom services in Hong Kong and Macau under the 3/3Business/SUPREME brands. The core business centers on mobile connectivity and 5G solutions, with strategy and disclosures focused on telecom operations.

### Exposure Channels Identified
In March 2022, the company’s mobile arm 3 Hong Kong announced it would accept cryptocurrency payments for bills, devices and services via FTX Pay, positioning the offer as an industry first for Hong Kong telecoms. This is a crypto payments initiative and therefore a financial‑services exposure, albeit intermediated by a third‑party exchange. In July–August 2021, 3 Hong Kong partnered with XCEPT and MOVE Network on an NFT “Big Bang” experiential marketing project, indicating limited non‑financial crypto engagement tied to content/marketing rather than custody or trading. Recent annual disclosures emphasize 5G and digital solutions and only high‑level references to Web3.0, with no evidence of balance‑sheet holdings or mining activity. ([web.three.com.hk](https://web.three.com.hk/pressrelease/20220303e.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
A small finserv weight (0.1) is assigned for the 2022 crypto payments acceptance via FTX Pay, which directly touches crypto payments but appears ancillary and partner‑driven. A small bizserv weight (0.1) captures the 2021 NFT experiential project as a marketing/event use of blockchain‑based media rather than a financial product. No evidence supports holders, miners, or infrastructure roles. The remainder (0.8) is allocated to unclass to reflect that 2017–2024 communications and filings consistently frame the company as a conventional telecom operator with crypto only at the periphery.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The 2022 payments initiative depended on FTX; following FTX’s November 2022 collapse, continuity of this option is unclear and not discussed in subsequent annual reports. Publicly available transcripts are limited; the only on‑site transcript in the window relates to the 2017 HGC disposal rather than periodic earnings calls. No filing quantifies any crypto‑related revenue or assets. ([web.three.com.hk](https://web.three.com.hk/pressrelease/20220303e.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 1, 2017 — Analyst/Investor session on HGC disposal: “The transaction … will enable the company to focus its resources more effectively on its core business of providing mobile services…” — HTHKH transcript (https://www.hthkh.com/en/ir/presentation/pre170801.pdf) ([hthkh.com](https://www.hthkh.com/en/ir/presentation/pre170801.pdf))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Mar 3, 2022 — Press release: “3 becomes Hong Kong’s first telecoms operator to accept cryptocurrency payments… powered by FTX Pay.” — 3 Hong Kong (https://web.three.com.hk/pressrelease/20220303e.pdf) ([web.three.com.hk](https://web.three.com.hk/pressrelease/20220303e.pdf))
- 2023 Annual Report — Corporate profile: commitment to smart city development and “opening up new opportunities in the era of Web3.0.” — HTHKH (https://www.hthkh.com/en/ir/reports/ar2023/ar2023.pdf) ([hthkh.com](https://www.hthkh.com/en/ir/reports/ar2023/ar2023.pdf))
- Jul
- Jul 30, 2021 — Press release: “Unveiling Hong Kong’s first 3. Orbstellar by XCEPT NFT Big Bang Immersive Experience Hall … creating collective digital art with blockchain technology … transformed into NFT.” — 3 Hong Kong (https://web.three.com.hk/pressrelease/20210730e.pdf) (web.three.com.hk)
- 2023 Annual Report — Corporate profile notes the Group “opening up new opportunities in the era of Web3.0.” — HTHKH (https://www.hthkh.com/en/ir/reports/ar2023/ar2023.pdf) (hthkh.com)

**Financial news / other:**
- Apr 5, 2022 — Trade press: “Three Hong Kong starts accepting payments via cryptocurrencies … powered by FTX Pay.” — Mobile News (https://www.mobilenewscwp.co.uk/News/article/three-hong-kong-starts-accepting-payments-via-cryptocurrencies) (mobilenewscwp.co.uk)

* Primary sources: 10-K filing (FY2023), Q2 2024 earnings call, company press releases (2023–2024)

### Firm Overview
Precigen, Inc. is a U.S. biopharmaceutical company focused on advancing next‑generation gene and cell therapies across immuno‑oncology, autoimmune disorders and infectious diseases. Its 2023 Form 10‑K describes lead programs on the AdenoVerse immunotherapy and UltraCAR‑T platforms. ([materials.proxyvote.com](https://materials.proxyvote.com/Approved/74017N/20240503/10K_575508.PDF))

### Exposure Channels Identified
A review of earnings call transcripts and SEC filings from 2017–2024 shows no material involvement with cryptocurrencies: no treasury holdings of cryptoassets, no mining operations, no crypto financial services or custody, no crypto‑focused business services, and no mining or blockchain‑directed infrastructure activity. The FY2023 10‑K business description centers entirely on gene/cell therapy platforms, and a Q2 2024 earnings call transcript discusses clinical and regulatory progress without any mention of crypto or blockchain. Accordingly, no crypto exposure channel is supported for this firm in the 2017–2024 window. ([materials.proxyvote.com](https://materials.proxyvote.com/Approved/74017N/20240503/10K_575508.PDF))

### Weight Assignment
With no credible evidence of crypto exposure across holders, miners, financial services, non‑financial services, or infrastructure, the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). No investor‑base amplifier is applied, as there is no indication that the stock trades as a crypto proxy or that management references crypto‑focused shareholders. ([materials.proxyvote.com](https://materials.proxyvote.com/Approved/74017N/20240503/10K_575508.PDF))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified for the 2017–2024 assessment window. Note: later developments in 2025–2026 (e.g., regulatory milestones and commercialization of PRGN‑2012) do not alter the crypto classification for the covered sample period. ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/precigen-reports-full-year-2024-financial-results-and-business-updates-302406034.html?utm_source=openai))

## CHINA UNICOM (HONG KONG)

* Rank: 318 | Expo: 0.14 | id_str: HK0000049939
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Annual reports (2021–2023), FY2018–FY2019 earnings presentation transcripts, SEC Form 20‑F (2019)

### Firm Overview
China Unicom (Hong Kong) Limited is a Hong Kong–listed telecommunications operator that consolidates and reports the China Unicom Group’s core communications and digital services activities, including mobile, broadband, cloud, data center and enterprise ICT services primarily in mainland China. The 2023 Annual Report emphasizes focus on two main businesses: Connectivity and Communications, and Computing and Digital Smart Applications. ([ar2023.chinaunicom.com.hk](https://ar2023.chinaunicom.com.hk/English/chairmans-statement.html))

### Exposure Channels Identified
A review of 2017–2024 earnings presentation transcripts and filings shows no material involvement with cryptocurrencies: no treasury holdings of cryptoassets, no mining operations, and no crypto exchange, custody, or payments services. Management communications center on mobile/broadband, 5G deployment, cloud, big data and enterprise ICT, with “industry internet” revenues driven by cloud computing, IoT and related services. While corporate sustainability reports list “blockchain” among enabling technologies, this is in the context of enterprise digital solutions rather than handling cryptoassets. An external report notes SIM‑based pilots for China’s e‑CNY with China Unicom and a state bank; this is a CBDC initiative and not cryptocurrency exposure. ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/hkex/762/investor-relations/earnings-call/q4-2019))

### Weight Assignment
With no evidence of balance‑sheet crypto holdings, mining, crypto financial services, crypto‑focused business services, or mining‑directed infrastructure, the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). No investor‑base amplifier is applied, as there is no indication that the stock trades as a crypto proxy or that management references crypto‑focused shareholders. ([ar2023.chinaunicom.com.hk](https://ar2023.chinaunicom.com.hk/English/chairmans-statement.html))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
“Blockchain” appears in sustainability materials as a general‑purpose technology, which does not meet the project’s crypto‑exposure thresholds. SIM‑based e‑CNY pilots involved a central bank digital currency, not cryptocurrency. Public transcripts for Hong Kong–listed firms are typically result‑presentation scripts; nonetheless, multiple years of materials reviewed consistently focus on core telecom and cloud/ICT services without crypto involvement. ([chinaunicom.com.hk](https://www.chinaunicom.com.hk/en/esg/reports/csr2022.pdf?utm_source=openai))

## SOLVAY INDUPA

* Rank: 319 | Expo: 0.14 | id_str: ARP533511050

* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Corporate filings and financial statements (2019–2022), CNV delisting/OPA approval (2019), Unipar corporate pages, third‑party company profiles

### Firm Overview
Solvay Indupa, now Unipar Indupa S.A.I.C., is an Argentina-based petrochemical manufacturer focused on PVC and caustic soda with a primary plant in Bahía Blanca. The company became part of Brazil’s Unipar group following an acquisition completed in 2016 and was subsequently delisted from the Buenos Aires exchange after a 2019 tender offer.

### Exposure Channels Identified
A review of Unipar/Indupa group filings and corporate materials from 2017–2024 shows no involvement with cryptocurrencies: no treasury holdings of cryptoassets, no mining activity, and no crypto exchange, custody, or payments services. Documents consistently emphasize core chemicals operations (PVC, chlorine/caustic) in Argentina and Brazil. Full‑text checks of Unipar’s consolidated financials that include Unipar Indupa show no occurrences of “bitcoin,” “blockchain,” or “cripto/criptomoeda.” The firm’s public profiles and industry association listings describe it as a chemical producer, not a crypto‑adjacent business. ([s3.amazonaws.com](https://s3.amazonaws.com/mz-filemanager/3c0b3516-7dff-44a5-946f-20e7ec87dfa0/f98ea55e-2865-473e-8270-15be97309c90_2019_Demonstra%C3%A7%C3%B5es%20Financeiras%20Anuais%20Completas.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
With no evidence of balance‑sheet crypto holdings, mining, crypto financial services, crypto‑focused business services, or mining‑directed infrastructure, the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). No investor‑base amplifier is applied, as there is no indication the stock trades as a crypto proxy or that management addresses a crypto‑focused shareholder base. ([cnv.gov.ar](https://www.cnv.gov.ar/SitioWeb/Prensa/Post/1367/1367aprobacion-de-opa-sobre-acciones-de-unipar-indupa-saic?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
No standalone earnings‑call transcripts for the Argentine subsidiary were found; Unipar hosts group‑level results materials. This absence does not affect the classification given the strong chemical‑sector focus across filings and corporate materials and the negative full‑text checks for crypto terms. ([ri.unipar.com](https://ri.unipar.com/informacoes-aos-investidores/central-de-resultados/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- No dedicated Unipar Indupa S.A.I.C. transcripts located for 2017–2024; group‑level results materials are hosted on Unipar’s RI site. — Unipar RI Central de Resultados (https://ri.unipar.com/informacoes-aos-investidores/central-de-resultados/) ([ri.unipar.com](https://ri.unipar.com/informacoes-aos-investidores/central-de-resultados/))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**

## SVENSKA HANDELSBANKEN A

* Rank: 320 | Expo: 0.14 | id_str: SE0007100599
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Annual and sustainability reports (2019, 2022), investor relations pages, consumer advisory on virtual assets (ongoing), Riksbank e‑krona pilot materials (2021–2023), Safello company releases (2023–2024)

### Firm Overview
Svenska Handelsbanken AB is a Sweden‑based universal bank with operations across the Nordics and the UK, listed on Nasdaq Stockholm. Public materials emphasize traditional banking, savings and asset management; the bank explicitly advises retail customers against direct trading in cryptocurrencies and other virtual assets. ([handelsbanken.com](https://www.handelsbanken.com/en/investor-relations?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024, no evidence indicates balance‑sheet crypto holdings, mining activity, or the provision of crypto exchange, custody, payments, or investment products by the bank itself. Handelsbanken’s published guidance to customers takes a cautious stance on cryptocurrencies and sets documentation requirements for proceeds from virtual‑asset activity, reflecting an AML/consumer‑protection approach rather than product enablement. While Handelsbanken participated with the Riksbank and Tietoevry in technical testing during the e‑krona (CBDC) pilot’s second phase, this work related to a central‑bank digital currency and not to private cryptoassets. Safello, a Nordic crypto exchange, also disclosed that Handelsbanken terminated its Swish arrangement and later that it migrated all banking services away from Handelsbanken. ([handelsbanken.se](https://www.handelsbanken.se/en/personal/savings/crypto-currencies-virtual-assets?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of substantiated activity in any of the six mechanism categories (holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, infrastr) and the bank’s own published caution against consumer crypto trading, the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). The CBDC pilot involvement is excluded as it pertains to state digital money, not cryptocurrency markets. No investor‑base amplifier is applied, as there is no evidence the stock trades as a crypto proxy or that management engages a crypto‑focused shareholder base. ([handelsbanken.se](https://www.handelsbanken.se/en/personal/savings/crypto-currencies-virtual-assets?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Some Swedish investors can access exchange‑traded crypto‑tracking certificates via third‑party brokerages, but there is no evidence that Handelsbanken issues or custodies such products; therefore this does not meet the finserv threshold in this framework. No earnings‑call transcripts reviewed contained crypto‑related discussion, but this does not affect an unclassified assignment. ([bulletin.webull.com](https://bulletin.webull.com/20180207/150343817/2610ed318686248f03489d35afa2802b.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 7, 2018, Q4 2017 results: transcript reviewed; no crypto discussion observed. — Thomson Reuters StreetEvents PDF (Webull) (https://bulletin.webull.com/20180207/150343817/2610ed318686248f03489d35afa2802b.pdf) ([bulletin.webull.com](https://bulletin.webull.com/20180207/150343817/2610ed318686248f03489d35afa2802b.pdf))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Annual Report 2019: core banking focus and disclosures. — Handelsbanken Annual Report 2019 (https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/h/handelsbanken_2019.pdf) ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/h/handelsbanken_2019.pdf?utm_source=openai))
- Annual and Sustainability Report 2022: group activities and segment descriptions. — Handelsbanken 2022 Report (PDF) (https://mb.cision.com/Main/3555/3723067/1874784.pdf) ([mb.cision.com](https://mb.cision.com/Main/3555/3723067/1874784.pdf?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Consumer advisory: “Handelsbanken advises against direct trading in cryptocurrencies and other virtual assets.” — Handelsbanken (EN) (https://www.handelsbanken.se/en/personal/savings/crypto-currencies-virtual-assets) ([handelsbanken.se](https://www.handelsbanken.se/en/personal/savings/crypto-currencies-virtual-assets?utm_source=openai))
- AML guidance on virtual‑asset proceeds/documentation. — Handelsbanken (EN) (https://www.handelsbanken.se/en/personal/savings/crypto-currencies-virtual-assets) ([handelsbanken.se](https://www.handelsbanken.se/en/personal/savings/crypto-currencies-virtual-assets?utm_source=openai))
- Riksbank e‑krona pilot phase 2: integration tested with Handelsbanken and Tietoevry. — Sveriges Riksbank (https://www.riksbank.se/en-gb/payments--cash/payments-in-sweden/payments-report-2022/the-riksbanks-work-and-policy/the-riksbank-is-making-preparations-for-a-possible-e-krona-/the-riksbank-is-testing-a-technical-solution-and-preparing-for-a-possible-issuable-e-krona-/) ([riksbank.se](https://www.riksbank.se/en-gb/payments--cash/payments-in-sweden/payments-report-2022/the-riksbanks-work-and-policy/the-riksbank-is-making-preparations-for-a-possible-e-krona-/the-riksbank-is-testing-a-technical-solution-and-preparing-for-a-possible-issuable-e-krona-/?utm_source=openai))
- E‑kronapiloten etapp 2: technical note mentioning cooperation with Handelsbanken. — Sveriges Riksbank (SV) (https://www.riksbank.se/sv/betalningar--kontanter/e-krona/e-kronarapporter/e-kronapiloten-etapp-2/) ([riksbank.se](https://www.riksbank.se/sv/betalningar--kontanter/e-krona/e-kronarapporter/e-kronapiloten-etapp-2/?utm_source=openai))
- “Handelsbanken terminates Safello’s Swish.” — Safello (Cision release) (https://mb.cision.com/Main/20146/3760214/2019142.pdf) ([mb.cision.com](https://mb.cision.com/Main/20146/3760214/2019142.pdf?utm_source=openai))
- “Safello completes migration from Handelsbanken.” — Inderes release (https://www.inderes.fi/en/releases/safello-completes-migration-from-handelsbanken) ([inderes.fi](https://www.inderes.fi/en/releases/safello-completes-migration-from-handelsbanken?utm_source=openai))
