## CORSAIR GAMING

* Rank: 321 | Expo: 0.14 | id_str: US22041X1028
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filing (FY2024), Q3 2021 and Q4 2021 earnings calls, Q2 2022 earnings call

### Firm Overview
Corsair Gaming, Inc. is a U.S.-based designer and marketer of gaming and creator peripherals, as well as PC components such as DRAM, power supplies, cooling, and cases, selling globally through retail channels and direct-to-consumer. Its operating results are closely tied to new GPU/CPU cycles and the broader PC gaming hardware market. ([materials.proxyvote.com](https://materials.proxyvote.com/Approved/22041X/20250414/FO10K_605240.PDF))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024 there is no evidence that Corsair holds crypto on its balance sheet, operates mining, provides crypto exchange/custody/payments, or issues crypto investment products. Management’s own risk disclosures reference crypto only indirectly: demand for Corsair’s products can be affected when consumers purchase GPUs for non‑gaming uses such as cryptocurrency mining. Earnings calls in 2021–2022 referenced Bitcoin/Ethereum mining primarily as a factor constraining or normalizing GPU availability, not as a Corsair product focus. Consequently, no mechanism category (holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, infrastr) meets the threshold for non‑zero weight. ([materials.proxyvote.com](https://materials.proxyvote.com/Approved/22041X/20250414/FO10K_605240.PDF))

### Weight Assignment
Mentions of crypto appear only as exogenous market dynamics around GPU supply (e.g., mining absorbing GPU supply; later, mining profitability changes improving availability). Corsair does not sell mining‑specific hardware, does not custody or trade digital assets, and does not target crypto clients. Under the framework’s exclusion rules, such indirect effects do not constitute a mechanism of crypto exposure; therefore the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). No investor‑base amplifier is applied, as the stock is not characterized in credible sources as a crypto proxy. ([materials.proxyvote.com](https://materials.proxyvote.com/Approved/22041X/20250414/FO10K_605240.PDF))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Corsair’s PSUs and other components can be used in mining rigs, but there is no evidence of mining‑directed product lines or material crypto‑mining revenue; thus infrastr is not assigned. Transcripts are available only post‑IPO (2020 onward), but they provide sufficient coverage within the 2017–2024 window to assess crypto relevance. ([materials.proxyvote.com](https://materials.proxyvote.com/Approved/22041X/20250414/FO10K_605240.PDF))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 2, 2021 (Q3 2021): “you’ve got Bitcoin mining that’s absorbing a lot of the [GPU] supply.” — The Motley Fool transcript ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/02/corsair-gaming-inc-crsr-q3-2021-earnings-call-tran/))
- Feb 8, 2022 (Q4 2021): “mining … is going to be less profitable or in fact, impossible to do [the] way it’s being done now,” implying improved GPU availability thereafter. — The Motley Fool transcript ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/02/08/corsair-gaming-inc-crsr-q4-2021-earnings-call-tran/))
- Aug 5, 2022 (Q2 2022): “GPU prices have moderated and GPUs are generally available now.” — The Motley Fool transcript ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/08/05/corsair-gaming-inc-crsr-q2-2022-earnings-call-tran/?utm_source=openai))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Feb 2025, Form 10‑K (FY2024): risk factors note GPU supply/demand can be affected by “consumers purchasing GPUs for non‑gaming purposes such as cryptocurrency mining.” — SEC filing (PDF) ([materials.proxyvote.com](https://materials.proxyvote.com/Approved/22041X/20250414/FO10K_605240.PDF))
- Feb 2025, FY2024 results release: market context and filing reference. — Corsair IR newsroom ([ir.corsair.com](https://ir.corsair.com/news-releases/news-release-details/corsair-gaming-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2024?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- General coverage of Corsair transcripts and filings repository. — Morningstar earnings transcript hub (index) ([morningstar.com](https://www.morningstar.com/stocks/xnas/crsr/earnings-transcript?utm_source=openai))

</small>

## POSTAL SAVINGS BANK OF CHINA 'A'

* Rank: 322 | Expo: 0.14 | id_str: CNE100003PZ4
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Annual reports (2021, 2024), 2022 CSR/ESG report, HKMA e‑CNY pilot materials

### Firm Overview
Postal Savings Bank of China Co., Ltd. is a large state‑owned retail bank headquartered in Beijing and dual‑listed in Shanghai (A‑share) and Hong Kong (H‑share). Its core franchise spans mass retail and inclusive finance, with extensive rural and county‑level coverage; digital finance initiatives (including e‑CNY) are part of its technology‑driven strategy. ([psbc.com](https://www.psbc.com/en/about_psbc/psbc_news/202204/t20220412_176277.html?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Within the 2017–2024 window, PSBC is an authorised e‑CNY operating institution and provides digital RMB wallet services, payments, smart‑contract “secured pay” features, and scenario applications (e.g., medical insurance, utilities, consumer loans). It also issues themed e‑CNY hardware wallets and has partnered with vendors to supply hard‑wallet cards. Earlier, the bank implemented blockchain in core custody/business processes. These activities fit squarely under crypto financial services (CBDC/payment rails), with no evidence of proprietary coin holdings or mining. ([hkma.gov.hk](https://www.hkma.gov.hk/media/eng/doc/smart-consumers/institutions_participating_eng.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence shows substantive, bank‑operated e‑CNY services (wallet management, payments, smart‑contract use cases, hardware wallets, and participation in Hong Kong’s cross‑boundary e‑CNY pilot). Under the framework, this is classified as finserv exposure. There is no indication of treasury crypto holdings, mining activity, or crypto‑specific infrastructure provision. Therefore, finserv is assigned 1.0, with all other mechanism weights at 0.0. Given the lack of an accessible earnings‑call transcript quote, certainty is capped at 2 per protocol. ([hkma.gov.hk](https://www.hkma.gov.hk/media/eng/doc/smart-consumers/institutions_participating_eng.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- No accessible earnings‑call transcript text could be retrieved for direct quotation despite locating transcript listings; certainty is capped at 2. ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/stock/PSBKF/transcripts/2761176))
- PSBC’s use of “blockchain” in internal systems does not itself constitute crypto exposure under the framework (no custody/trading of cryptoassets), so no separate bizserv or infrastr weight is assigned. ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2017/01/12/postal-savings-bank-of-china-has-already-sent-100-transactions-on-its-internal-blockchain?utm_source=openai))
- Revenue contribution from e‑CNY appears minor relative to the bank’s size; precise segment revenue is not separately disclosed. ([psbc.com](https://www.psbc.com/en/about_psbc/psbc_news/202404/t20240407_238378.html?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Listing of FY2023 and HY2023 earnings call transcripts (text paywalled; no accessible quote). — GuruFocus transcript index (PSBKF/01658) (Apr 2, 2024; Aug 31, 2023) [GuruFocus](https://www.gurufocus.com/stock/PSBKF/transcripts/2333132) ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/stock/PSBKF/transcripts/2333132?utm_source=openai))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- 2024 Annual Report (published Mar 27, 2025): “e‑CNY personal wallet customer base – registered via the e‑CNY App – ranked first among all operating institutions,” and launched e‑CNY FX into seven currencies (USD, EUR, HKD, etc.). — PSBC Annual Report (EN) [PDF] ([psbc.com](https://www.psbc.com/en/investor_relations/finance/financial_reports/202503/P020250327809149171009.pdf?utm_source=openai))
- 2022 CSR/ESG Report: describes building “outlet + e‑CNY business circle,” medical/social insurance e‑CNY payments, loan disbursement/repayment in e‑CNY, and outputting e‑CNY service capabilities (wallet management, coupons, gateway payment) to other banks. — PSBC CSR 2022 (EN) [PDF] ([psbc.com](https://www.psbc.com/en/investor_relations/social_responsibility/202304/P020230425541190362625.pdf))
- 2021 Annual Report/CSR: active participation in the e‑CNY pilot; signed e‑CNY service cooperation agreements with 60+ banks and rolled out branch‑based e‑CNY business circles. — HKExnews filing (EN) [PDF] ([www1.hkexnews.hk](https://www1.hkexnews.hk/listedco/listconews/sehk/2022/0330/2022033003002.pdf?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- HKMA list of institutions in Hong Kong’s cross‑boundary e‑CNY pilot: PSBC named as an e‑CNY wallet operating institution. — HKMA info sheet [PDF] ([hkma.gov.hk](https://www.hkma.gov.hk/media/eng/doc/smart-consumers/institutions_participating_eng.pdf))
- Vendor release: PSBC selected Eastcompeace to supply digital currency hard‑wallet card manufacturing services. — Eastcompeace news release ([eastcompeace.com](https://www.eastcompeace.com/967.html))
- Historical reference: PSBC launched a blockchain‑based asset custody system (IBM Hyperledger Fabric) in 2017. — CoinDesk report ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2017/01/12/postal-savings-bank-of-china-has-already-sent-100-transactions-on-its-internal-blockchain?utm_source=openai))

## OTC MARKETS GP.CL.A

* Rank: 323 | Expo: 0.14 | id_str: US67106F1084
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Annual Reports (2023), Annual Market Review (2023), Q1 2023 and Q4 2023 earnings calls, press releases on OTCQX digital‑asset products

### Firm Overview
OTC Markets Group Inc. operates regulated over‑the‑counter markets in the United States, including the OTCQX Best Market, OTCQB Venture Market and Pink Open Market, and runs the OTC Link ATS/ECN trading systems. Its revenue model spans corporate services (market tiers and issuer services), market data licensing, and OTC Link connectivity/transaction services; crypto exposure arises through the trading and quotation of digital‑asset securities and crypto investment products that access its markets.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Within the 2017–2024 window, numerous crypto investment products (e.g., Grayscale trusts such as ETHE, ETCG, BCHG, LTCN, GXLM, HZEN, ZCSH) were publicly quoted and traded on OTCQX/OTCQB, creating an operational link to crypto markets through listing/quotation and trading activity. In May 2023, management announced receiving FINRA approval permitting broker‑dealers to trade digital asset securities on OTC Link ATS, and in March 2024 reiterated they “have a license to put [digital asset securities] on our ATS” while noting the ecosystem (custody/clearing/accounting) is still maturing. There is no indication the company mines crypto or holds crypto on its own balance sheet; exposure is through operating regulated trading venues and related services. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/OTCM/documents/transcripts/q1-2023))

### Weight Assignment
OTC Markets’ crypto channel is financial services: it operates markets and ATSs on which crypto‑linked securities trade, and it obtained regulatory approval to enable broker‑dealer trading of digital asset securities. Management’s transcripts and the company’s annual materials explicitly document this role; there is no evidence of mining, treasury crypto holdings, or crypto‑specific hardware/compute. Accordingly, finserv is assigned 1.0 and all other mechanism weights 0.0. Note that while crypto‑related volumes (e.g., GBTC and other trusts) have influenced market activity historically, the firm emphasizes diversification beyond any single product; thus crypto revenue share is coded minor. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/OTCM/documents/transcripts/q1-2023))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The firm’s approval to trade digital asset securities on OTC Link ATS is clear, but near‑term monetization depends on broader industry plumbing (broker‑dealer custody, omnibus clearing, accounting treatment), which management characterizes as developing. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/OTCM/documents/transcripts/q4-2023))
- Prominent crypto trusts that once traded on OTCQX (e.g., GBTC) uplisted to NYSE Arca as ETFs on January 11, 2024, reducing direct OTC quotation of that product; this does not negate the firm’s approved capability for digital asset securities but tempers immediate volume linkage. ([etfs.grayscale.com](https://etfs.grayscale.com/gbtc?utm_source=openai))
- No evidence that OTC Markets itself takes custody of digital assets or issues crypto products; hence no holders/miners/infrastructure exposure is assigned. Company materials and transcripts frame the role as a regulated market operator only. ([otcmarkets.com](https://www.otcmarkets.com/otcapi/company/financial-report/393940/content))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Mar 7, 2024 (Q4 2023), Fintool: “Given the recent approval of spot crypto ETFs… GBTC was a fantastic story… Digital asset securities, we have a license to put them on our ATS… we will be ready when it shows up.” — Fintool transcript (OTCM Q4 2023) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/OTCM/documents/transcripts/q4-2023)) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/OTCM/documents/transcripts/q4-2023))
- May 10, 2023 (Q1 2023), Fintool: “Last Friday, we received FINRA approval to permit digital asset securities to be traded by broker‑dealers on our OTC Link ATS.” — Fintool transcript (OTCM Q1 2023) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/OTCM/documents/transcripts/q1-2023)) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/OTCM/documents/transcripts/q1-2023))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- 2023 Annual Report (issuer filing): “During 2023, we received approval from FINRA to permit digital asset securities to be traded by broker‑dealers on OTC Link ATS…” — OTC Markets Group 2023 Annual Report [PDF] ([otcmarkets.com](https://www.otcmarkets.com/otcapi/company/financial-report/393940/content)) ([otcmarkets.com](https://www.otcmarkets.com/otcapi/company/financial-report/393940/content))
- 2023 Annual Market Review: “On May 5th 2023, OTC Markets received FINRA approval to permit digital asset securities to be traded by broker‑dealers on OTC Link ATS.” — OTC Markets 2023 Annual Market Review [PDF] ([otcmarkets.com](https://www.otcmarkets.com/files/2023_Annual_Market_Review.pdf)) ([otcmarkets.com](https://www.otcmarkets.com/files/2023_Annual_Market_Review.pdf?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Oct 18, 2021: “OTC Markets Group Welcomes Grayscale Stellar Lumens Trust, Grayscale Horizen Trust, and Grayscale Zcash Trust to OTCQX.” — PR Newswire release ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/otc-markets-group-welcomes-grayscale-stellar-lumens-trust-grayscale-horizen-trust-and-grayscale-zcash-trust-to-otcqx-301401883.html)) ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/otc-markets-group-welcomes-grayscale-stellar-lumens-trust-grayscale-horizen-trust-and-grayscale-zcash-trust-to-otcqx-301401883.html?utm_source=openai))
- Jan 11, 2024: “Effective as of the open of business on 1/11/2024, [GBTC] shares were listed to NYSE Arca as an exchange‑traded product.” — Grayscale GBTC ETF page ([grayscale.com](https://etfs.grayscale.com/gbtc)) ([etfs.grayscale.com](https://etfs.grayscale.com/gbtc?utm_source=openai))

## AMPLITUDE A

* Rank: 324 | Expo: 0.14 | id_str: US03213A1043
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2022–2024), Q2 2024 and Q3 2023 earnings calls

### Firm Overview
Amplitude Inc. is a U.S.-based software company listed on Nasdaq under the ticker AMPL that provides a digital analytics platform used by product, data, and marketing teams to analyze customer behavior and optimize digital experiences. Public filings describe a SaaS business focused on analytics, experimentation, and related tools; no filings or investor materials indicate any crypto‑related operations. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1866692/000095017025024135/ampl-20241231.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024 filings and earnings call transcripts, Amplitude discusses its core platform (analytics, session replay, experimentation, activation) and go‑to‑market execution, without reference to cryptocurrencies, digital assets, mining, custody, or blockchain operations. For example, management frames the company as “the enterprise digital analytics platform,” highlighting ARR, product launches, and customer wins in non‑crypto verticals. There is no evidence of crypto held on the balance sheet, crypto mining, or crypto financial services. While third‑party partners market on‑chain data connectors that can feed data into Amplitude workspaces, these are external integrations and do not imply Amplitude handles digital assets or provides crypto services. ([investors.amplitude.com](https://investors.amplitude.com/static-files/857ebda0-0ad9-4d29-8d67-71bc1e45a9a3))

### Weight Assignment
No substantive crypto exposure was found in Amplitude’s disclosures or calls during the 2017–2024 sample window. The absence of any mention of crypto holdings, mining activity, crypto financial products, ecosystem services, or dedicated infrastructure results in an unclassified outcome. Accordingly, all mechanism weights are set to 0.0 and unclass = 1.0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1866692/000095017025024135/ampl-20241231.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Name ambiguity: “Amplitude” is also the name of a separate crypto/blockchain network on Kusama; this is unrelated to Amplitude Inc. (Nasdaq: AMPL). ([pendulumchain.org](https://www.pendulumchain.org/amplitude?utm_source=openai))  
- Third‑party vendors advertise “on‑chain data to Amplitude” integrations; these do not evidence Amplitude itself engaging in crypto financial services or custody. ([multibase.co](https://www.multibase.co/resources/connect-onchain-data-to-amplitude?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 8, 2024 (Q2 2024): “Our second quarter revenue was $73.3 million… We have a differentiated platform approach.” — Amplitude Q2 2024 transcript (Refinitiv) ([investors.amplitude.com](https://investors.amplitude.com/static-files/857ebda0-0ad9-4d29-8d67-71bc1e45a9a3)) ([investors.amplitude.com](https://investors.amplitude.com/static-files/857ebda0-0ad9-4d29-8d67-71bc1e45a9a3))
- Nov 7, 2023 (Q3 2023): “Amplitude tells you what your customers do and how they behave across the entire customer journey.” — Amplitude Q3 2023 transcript (Refinitiv) ([investors.amplitude.com](https://investors.amplitude.com/static-files/0bb63b3e-8dff-4664-803c-fc3671fa13a9)) ([investors.amplitude.com](https://investors.amplitude.com/static-files/0bb63b3e-8dff-4664-803c-fc3671fa13a9))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Form 10‑K for year ended Dec 31, 2024: Company description and risk factors; no references to cryptocurrency, digital assets, or blockchain. — SEC EDGAR filing ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1866692/000095017025024135/ampl-20241231.htm)) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1866692/000095017025024135/ampl-20241231.htm))
- Form 10‑K for year ended Dec 31, 2022: Business overview of Amplitude’s digital analytics platform; no crypto references. — SEC EDGAR filing ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1866692/000095017023003045/ampl-20221231.htm)) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1866692/000095017023003045/ampl-20221231.htm?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Example third‑party integration: “Blockchain data streamed directly to your Amplitude project.” — Multibase on on‑chain data connectors (vendor site; not Amplitude) ([multibase.co](https://www.multibase.co/resources/connect-onchain-data-to-amplitude)) ([multibase.co](https://www.multibase.co/resources/connect-onchain-data-to-amplitude?utm_source=openai))
- Name disambiguation: “Amplitude is the sister network of Pendulum on Kusama.” — Pendulum/Amplitude network page (not related to Amplitude Inc.) ([pendulumchain.org](https://www.pendulumchain.org/amplitude)) ([pendulumchain.org](https://www.pendulumchain.org/amplitude?utm_source=openai))

</small>

## VIRACTA THERAPEUTICS DEAD - DELIST.17/07/25

* Rank: 325 | Expo: 0.14 | id_str: US92765F1084
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2023–2024), Q3 2024 10-Q, Q2 2024 financial results press release

### Firm Overview
Viracta Therapeutics Inc. is a U.S.-based clinical-stage precision oncology company focused on the treatment and prevention of virus-associated cancers, with its lead program “Nana‑val” (nanatinostat plus valganciclovir) targeting EBV‑positive malignancies. Public filings and company materials describe a biotechnology R&D enterprise; no documents indicate any cryptocurrency-related operations.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024 filings and investor materials, Viracta details its pipeline, clinical trials, financing arrangements, and operating runway typical of a pre‑commercial biotech. There are no references to cryptocurrency holdings, mining, custody, exchange/brokerage services, blockchain infrastructure, or crypto-adjacent service lines. The 2024 Form 10‑K and 2024 quarterly filings consistently frame the business around EBV‑associated oncology and related clinical development activities. Press releases likewise focus on study updates, operating expense trends, and cash runway, without digital‑asset content. 

### Weight Assignment
No substantive crypto exposure was found in disclosures or investor materials during the 2017–2024 sample window. With no evidence of treasury crypto holdings, mining activity, crypto financial products, ecosystem services, or dedicated infrastructure, the firm is assigned unclass = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Viracta underwent corporate changes (formerly Sunesis Pharmaceuticals) and faced Nasdaq bid‑price non‑compliance in 2024; neither implies crypto exposure.  
- Earnings-call transcripts were not required for an unclassified outcome; filings and press releases provided sufficient evidence of no crypto exposure.  
- The sector label provided in the input (“Energy”) appears inconsistent with the company’s biotechnology focus.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- IR events page (no archived call materials currently displayed): “Webcasts and presentations that have been archived are available by viewing historical event details.” — Viracta IR Events & Presentations (viracta.investorroom.com/events-and-presentations) ([viracta.investorroom.com](https://viracta.investorroom.com/events-and-presentations))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Mar 7, 2024: Form 10‑K — “Viracta is a clinical-stage precision oncology company focused on the treatment and prevention of virus-associated cancers.” — SEC EDGAR ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1061027/000119312524118083/d770324dars.pdf))
- Nov 13, 2024: Form 10‑Q — “lead product candidate is an all‑oral combination therapy… nanatinostat and… valganciclovir (collectively, ‘Nana‑val’).” — SEC EDGAR ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1061027/000095017024126385/virx-20240930.htm))
- Mar 7, 2024: Form 10‑K — background/name history (“formerly known as Sunesis Pharmaceuticals, Inc.”). — SEC EDGAR ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1061027/000119312524118083/d770324dars.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**
- Aug 14, 2024: “Reports Second Quarter 2024 Financial Results and Provides Business Update” (cash runway, pipeline prioritization; no crypto content). — Viracta IR news release ([viracta.investorroom.com](https://viracta.investorroom.com/2024-08-14-Viracta-Therapeutics-Reports-Second-Quarter-2024-Financial-Results-and-Provides-Business-Update))
- Nov 2024: Coverage of Nasdaq bid‑price non‑compliance window ending Nov 25, 2024 (context; no crypto angle). — Investing.com ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/sec-filings/viracta-therapeutics-faces-nasdaq-delisting-over-noncompliance-93CH-3747915))

## WARSAW STOCK EXCHANGE

* Rank: 326 | Expo: 0.14 | id_str: PLGPW0000017
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: quarterly reports (2020–2023), Q3 2020 and Q4 2021 earnings calls, 2024 strategy presentation, financial news on GPW Private Market and tokenization initiatives

### Firm Overview
The Warsaw Stock Exchange (Giełda Papierów Wartościowych w Warszawie S.A.; ticker GPW) operates Poland’s main securities and derivatives markets and is the parent of the GPW Group, which includes market data, indices, and energy/commodities venues. Within 2017–2024, management also developed initiatives around digital-asset tokenization and a blockchain-based “GPW Private Market” alongside its core exchange operations.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management repeatedly described a blockchain/DLT initiative to issue and trade digital tokens representing assets (security and non‑financial tokens) under the GPW Private Market project, positioning GPW to provide regulated digital‑asset issuance/trading services. In the Q3 2020 results call, GPW said it partnered on a crowdfunding platform and secured funding to build a secondary market “trading platform for tokens.”   
Subsequent filings reiterated that the project’s aim was to build a platform where tokens representing property rights are issued and traded, while a 2024 strategy deck referenced development of a digital‑asset platform, blockchain, and tokenization of non‑financial assets. ([pb.pl](https://www.pb.pl/static/att/emitent/2023-08/Skonsolidowane-sprawozdanie-finansowe-GK-GPW-sig-sig-sig-sig_202308100913213463.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
The documented focus is on enabling issuance and secondary trading of tokenized assets (security tokens and other non‑financial tokens) on GPW‑affiliated infrastructure, which squarely fits Crypto Financial Services. There is no evidence that GPW holds crypto on its balance sheet or engages in mining; nor is it supplying mining hardware/compute. Accordingly, the classification assigns finserv = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0, with low materiality scores given the pre‑revenue/early‑stage nature of these initiatives within the 2017–2024 window. ([pb.pl](https://www.pb.pl/static/att/emitent/2023-08/Skonsolidowane-sprawozdanie-finansowe-GK-GPW-sig-sig-sig-sig_202308100913213463.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Evidence indicates the tokenization platform’s timeline was affected by regulatory and implementation factors; press and investor materials suggested technological readiness targeted for 1H 2024, but launch timing remained contingent on regulation as of end‑2024. This tempers materiality but not the mechanism classification. None of the sources indicate crypto treasury holdings, mining, or crypto‑specific ETPs listed on GPW within the 2017–2024 sample. ([sii.org.pl](https://www.sii.org.pl/16795/analizy/newsroom/rum-wejdzie-na-gpw-inwestowanie-w-alkohole-na-private-market.html?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 5, 2020 (Q3 2020): “Second phase of private markets, our blockchain initiative… partners in Raisemana… received financing… for the secondary market trading platform for tokens.” — Alpha Spread transcript (Q3 2020) ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/wse/gpw/investor-relations/earnings-call/q3-2020))  
- Mar 2022 (Q4 2021): “We progressed… in our private markets of blockchain… This year we’re going to start with the first trading of… tokens on fine arts.” — Alpha Spread transcript (Q4 2021) ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/wse/gpw/investor-relations/earnings-call/q4-2021))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Nov 5, 2020: GPW Group quarterly report Q3 2020 — description of Private Market: blockchain platform; trade in digital assets; tokenization and secondary trading. ([bankier.pl](https://www.bankier.pl/static/att/emitent/2020-11/GPW_Group_quarterly_report_for_Q3_2020_202011050382377939.pdf))  
- Aug 10, 2023: Condensed interim consolidated financial statements — “Celem projektu jest zbudowanie platformy, na której… będą tokeny… [z] obrotem…” (aim to build a platform to issue and trade tokens). ([pb.pl](https://www.pb.pl/static/att/emitent/2023-08/Skonsolidowane-sprawozdanie-finansowe-GK-GPW-sig-sig-sig-sig_202308100913213463.pdf))  
- Nov 27, 2024: GPW Group Strategic development directions 2025–2027 — references to “digital assets,” “development of a blockchain platform,” and “tokenization of non‑financial assets.” ([pap-mediaroom.pl](https://pap-mediaroom.pl/sites/default/files/attachments/202411/DOC.20241127.50218544.zal01_Strategic_development_directions_GPW_Group_2025-2027.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**
- Sep 28, 2020: “Platforma crowdfundingowa Raisemana… w zamian za tokeny blockchain… pierwszy etap… GPW Private Market… platformą obrotu wtórnego aktywami cyfrowymi.” — Bankier.pl ([bankier.pl](https://www.bankier.pl/wiadomosc/Raisemana-we-wspolpracy-z-GPW-uruchomi-platforme-crowdfundingowa-dla-producentow-gier-7969827.html))  
- Nov 18, 2021: Cooperation with Artinfo.pl to tokenize artworks under GPW Private Market. — Strefa Inwestorów ([strefainwestorow.pl](https://strefainwestorow.pl/artykuly/inwestycje-alternatywne/20211118/gpw-tokenizacja-dziel-sztuki))  
- 2023 feature: Platform technologically targeted for 1H 2024; launch dependent on regulation. — Stowarzyszenie Inwestorów Indywidualnych (SII) ([sii.org.pl](https://www.sii.org.pl/16795/analizy/newsroom/rum-wejdzie-na-gpw-inwestowanie-w-alkohole-na-private-market.html))  
- Jun 27, 2023: EU’s DLT pilot regime context for tokenized securities in Poland. — Gazeta Prawna analysis ([serwisy.gazetaprawna.pl](https://serwisy.gazetaprawna.pl/orzeczenia/artykuly/8740230%2Ckiedy-stokenizowane-papiery-wartosciowe.html))

### Firm Overview
Adcock Ingram Holdings Ltd is a South African pharmaceutical and consumer healthcare company with operations focused on prescription medicines, over‑the‑counter products, consumer brands and hospital/critical care supplies. Public filings during 2017–2024 describe a traditional pharma manufacturer and marketer with no stated activities in digital assets. ([adcock.com](https://www.adcock.com/Content/FinReports/2024_Integrated_Report.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Targeted review of the company’s Integrated Reports for 2023 and 2024 shows no references to cryptocurrencies, blockchain, tokenization, mining, exchanges, custody, or related digital‑asset services; keyword searches within the PDFs for “crypto,” “bitcoin,” “blockchain,” and “token” returned no matches. The reporting instead details market share, product portfolios, manufacturing, governance, sustainability and financial performance. Supplementary filings (annual financial statements and SENS/press results) likewise contain no crypto‑related disclosures. No evidence was found that Adcock Ingram held crypto assets, operated mining activities, provided crypto financial services, offered non‑financial services to the crypto ecosystem, or supplied crypto‑specific infrastructure within the 2017–2024 window. Accordingly, the classification assigns unclass = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. ([adcock.com](https://www.adcock.com/Content/FinReports/2024_Integrated_Report.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified. Public investor materials are comprehensive for 2023–2024; no credible crypto exposure is indicated, and no earnings‑call transcript repositories with crypto content were found for the firm over the sample period. ([adcock.com](https://www.adcock.com/Content/FinReports/2024_Integrated_Report.pdf))

## RISKIFIED A

* Rank: 328 | Expo: 0.14 | id_str: IL0011786493
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 20-F filings (2023), Q3 2021 earnings call, Q1 2024 earnings call, company case study (Uphold), company press release (Plaid partnership)

### Firm Overview
Riskified Ltd is an Israel‑based technology company that provides an AI‑driven ecommerce fraud and risk intelligence platform, best known for its Chargeback Guarantee product. It listed Class A ordinary shares on the NYSE and serves large enterprise merchants across multiple verticals and regions. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1851112/000185111224000010/rskd-20231231.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Riskified explicitly lists “Payments, Money Transfer & Crypto” among its served verticals in its 2023 Form 20‑F, indicating direct provision of fraud‑mitigation services to crypto‑related merchants. On the Q3 2021 earnings call, management said “remittance and crypto have both seen an increase in attention from our perspective and a growth in the pipeline,” and also referenced “higher‑risk industries like … digital tokens,” confirming active engagement with crypto clients. The company showcases a case study with Uphold, a cryptocurrency exchange, detailing how Riskified’s platform reduces fraud and improves acceptance. In addition, a 2023 press release about its Plaid partnership cites cryptocurrency as a use case for ACH payments protected by Riskified. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1851112/000185111224000010/rskd-20231231.htm))

### Weight Assignment
The evidence shows Riskified delivers non‑financial, crypto‑adjacent services: fraud prevention, chargeback guarantee, and policy abuse mitigation for merchants that include crypto exchanges and money‑transfer platforms. There is no indication that Riskified mines crypto, holds crypto on its balance sheet, issues crypto‑backed financial products, or provides hardware/compute to the crypto stack. Accordingly, the firm’s crypto exposure is classified entirely as bizserv = 1.0, with all other mechanism weights = 0.0. Revenue linked to crypto clients appears minor relative to total company revenue, so RevShare is coded 1 and AssetShare 0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1851112/000185111224000010/rskd-20231231.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Later transcripts (e.g., 2024) emphasize verticals such as “Payments and Money Transfer” without always naming “crypto,” but filings during the sample window retain explicit “Crypto” vertical language. ([ir.riskified.com](https://ir.riskified.com/static-files/d5611f31-0bc7-4cdc-a264-538b34f77936))  
- A court filing alleges Binance was onboarded as a client pre‑IPO; this supports breadth of crypto exposure but is litigation‑sourced. None of the materials indicate Riskified taking custody of digital assets or operating financial crypto services. ([law.justia.com](https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/new-york/nysdce/1%3A2022cv03545/579155/92/?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 17, 2021 (Q3 2021): “remittance and crypto have both seen an increase in attention from our perspective and a growth in the pipeline.” — The Motley Fool (transcript) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/17/riskified-ltd-rskd-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcrip/)) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/17/riskified-ltd-rskd-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcrip/))  
- Nov 17, 2021 (Q3 2021): “higher‑risk industries like … digital tokens.” — The Motley Fool (transcript) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/17/riskified-ltd-rskd-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcrip/)) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/17/riskified-ltd-rskd-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcrip/))  
- May 15, 2024 (Q1 2024): “over 30% growth in Payments and Money Transfer” — Riskified IR (PDF transcript) ([ir.riskified.com](https://ir.riskified.com/static-files/d5611f31-0bc7-4cdc-a264-538b34f77936)) ([ir.riskified.com](https://ir.riskified.com/static-files/d5611f31-0bc7-4cdc-a264-538b34f77936))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Mar 6, 2024: 2023 Form 20‑F — served verticals include “Payments, Money Transfer & Crypto.” — SEC EDGAR ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1851112/000185111224000010/rskd-20231231.htm)) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1851112/000185111224000010/rskd-20231231.htm))

- Oct 2023: “Riskified Partners with Plaid to Enhance Risk Protection for ACH Bank Payments” — press release notes ACH use in cryptocurrency transactions. — Riskified ([riskified.com](https://www.riskified.com/press/riskified-partners-with-plaid-to-enhance-risk-protection-for-ach-bank-payments/)) ([riskified.com](https://www.riskified.com/press/riskified-partners-with-plaid-to-enhance-risk-protection-for-ach-bank-payments/?utm_source=openai))

## H&R BLOCK

* Rank: 329 | Expo: 0.14 | id_str: US0936711052
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filing (2023), Q3 2022 earnings call, company newsroom (2023 CoinTracker partnership), product help pages (crypto integrations)

### Firm Overview
H&R Block, Inc. is a U.S.-based provider of tax preparation services operating through assisted in‑office and DIY digital channels, with additional operations in Canada and Australia. The company’s offerings include tax software, online filing, and professional preparation, complemented by select consumer financial products.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management stated on the Q3 FY2022 earnings call that the company “expanded [its] ability to serve … cryptocurrency investors,” adding a dedicated DIY questionnaire and additional assisted-channel training, and noted “a nearly 20% increase” in retail and crypto filers. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/05/11/hr-block-hrb-q3-2022-earnings-call-transcript/))  
In March 2023, H&R Block announced a partnership with CoinTracker to streamline crypto tax filing; the news release added that the company had seen an “85% rise” in clients reporting crypto transactions. Company resources also describe seamless integrations with CoinTracker and Coinbase for importing crypto activity. ([hrblock.com](https://www.hrblock.com/tax-center/newsroom/innovation/cointracker-partners-with-hr-block-to-streamline-crypto-tax-filing-process/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
H&R Block’s crypto linkage is through non‑financial services: tax preparation and software flows tailored to crypto users, plus integrations that import transaction histories from exchanges/aggregators. There is no evidence the company holds crypto on its balance sheet, mines digital assets, provides custody/trading, or supplies mining hardware/compute. Accordingly, exposure is classified entirely as bizserv = 1.0; all other mechanism weights are 0.0. The absence of crypto assets or products in the 2023 Form 10‑K supports AssetShare = 0, and the crypto‑related revenue contribution appears minor relative to total revenue, so RevShare = 1. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/12659/000183886223000027/hrb-20230630.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Crypto is not presented as a distinct revenue line in filings, and later transcripts in the sample window do not consistently reiterate crypto‑specific commentary; thus, the classification relies on the 2022 call plus 2023 partnership disclosures and product documentation. None of the reviewed sources indicate direct handling or custody of digital assets by H&R Block. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/12659/000183886223000027/hrb-20230630.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 11, 2022 (Q3 FY2022): “expanded our ability to serve … cryptocurrency investors … [and] saw a nearly 20% increase … in our retail and crypto filers.” — The Motley Fool (transcript) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/05/11/hr-block-hrb-q3-2022-earnings-call-transcript/))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**

- Mar 10, 2023: “CoinTracker Partners With H&R Block to Streamline Crypto Tax Filing Process” — notes 85% rise in clients reporting crypto transactions. — H&R Block Newsroom / Business Wire ([hrblock.com](https://www.hrblock.com/tax-center/newsroom/innovation/cointracker-partners-with-hr-block-to-streamline-crypto-tax-filing-process/))

## NEOGEN

* Rank: 330 | Expo: 0.14 | id_str: US6404911066
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0

* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filing (2024), Q2 2024 earnings call, Q3 2024 earnings call, Q2 2023 earnings call

### Firm Overview
Neogen Corporation is a U.S.-based provider of food and animal safety solutions, including diagnostic test kits, sanitation monitoring, and animal genomics services, with global operations. In 2022–2024 the company integrated 3M’s Food Safety business, broadening its portfolio across food safety testing and related software.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across earnings call transcripts from 2017–2024 and the company’s most recent Form 10‑K, there are no disclosures of cryptocurrency holdings, mining activities, crypto trading/custody products, or other direct digital-asset exposure. Neogen references “Neogen Analytics” software and has public materials on traceability and blockchain applications tied to food and livestock supply chains; however, these are enterprise data/traceability tools and do not involve cryptocurrencies or crypto‑financial services. Accordingly, no crypto‑exposure mechanisms apply under the project definitions.

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of crypto treasury holdings, mining, crypto financial products, crypto‑focused services, or mining‑directed infrastructure, the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism weights = 0.0. No evidence indicates a crypto‑sensitive investor base, so invbase = 0. Because the firm has no identifiable crypto revenue or assets in scope, RevShare and AssetShare are both NA.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Neogen’s partnership/history around blockchain traceability (e.g., with Ripe Technology) and website references to “Traceability & Blockchain Applications” relate to enterprise supply‑chain data and do not constitute cryptocurrency involvement under these taxonomy rules; thus they are excluded from mechanism weighting. No crypto‑related discussion appears in reviewed transcripts or the FY2024 Form 10‑K.

### Evidence

**Earnings calls:**
- Jan 9, 2024 (Q2 FY2024): “This is an exciting time at Neogen, as we’re approaching several near‑term milestones on the journey of integrating the former 3M Food Safety business.” — Company transcript (PDF) ([neogen.com](https://s21.q4cdn.com/672210836/files/doc_financials/2024/q2/2024-01-09_transcript_fy24q2-earnings.pdf))
- Apr 9, 2024 (Q3 FY2024): “The third quarter marked a significant step in the integration and our ultimate journey towards full…” — Company transcript (PDF) ([neogen.com](https://www.neogen.com/4a91b1/globalassets/pdfs/quarterly-and-annual-forms/2024-04-09_transcript_fy24q3-earnings.pdf))
- Jan 5, 2023 (Q2 FY2023): “helping our customers around the world keep the people and animals they care about safe.” — Company transcript (PDF) ([neogen.com](https://www.neogen.com/4a3602/globalassets/pdfs/quarterly-and-annual-forms/2023-01-05_transcript_fy23q2-earnings.pdf))
- Jul 20, 2021 (Q4 FY2021): Business update focused on food/animal safety; no crypto references in transcript. — The Motley Fool ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/07/20/neogen-corporation-neog-q4-2021-earnings-call-tran/))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Jul 30, 2024 (FY2024 Form 10‑K): Business segments, revenue discussion; no disclosure of cryptocurrency assets, mining, or crypto products; no mentions of “crypto/bitcoin/blockchain” in filing. — SEC EDGAR ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/711377/000095017024087670/neog-20240531.htm))

**Financial news / other:**
- Feb 5, 2020: “Neogen partners with ripe.io to bring blockchain to food safety and animal genomics” — enterprise blockchain traceability; not a cryptocurrency product. — PR Newswire ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/neogen-partners-with-ripeio-to-bring-blockchain-to-food-safety-and-animal-genomics-300999452.html))
- Dec 15, 2021: “Neogen CEO: 3M food‑safety unit deal improves blockchain innovation” — discussion of blockchain innovation context; not crypto. — CNBC ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2021/12/15/neogen-ceo-3m-food-safety-unit-deal-improves-blockchain-innovation.html))

## LY

* Rank: 331 | Expo: 0.14 | id_str: JP3933800009
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings call transcript (Q1 2023), company press releases (2019–2022), FY2023 results release (May 8, 2024)

### Firm Overview
LY Corporation is a Japan-based internet services group formed through the integration of Z Holdings (Yahoo! Japan) and LINE, with businesses spanning media/advertising, e‑commerce, and fintech including PayPay. On October 1, 2023, Z Holdings changed its trade name to LY Corporation as part of a group reorganization consolidating LINE and Yahoo Japan under one entity. ([lycorp.co.jp](https://www.lycorp.co.jp/en/ir/news/auto_20240507582159/pdfFile.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The group operates crypto financial services primarily via LINE’s crypto and blockchain subsidiaries: it launched the BITMAX crypto-asset exchange for Japan in September 2019, added trading of the LINE‑issued LINK token in August 2020, and operates the LINE NFT marketplace (Japan, launched April 2022). The FY2023 results release also lists LINE BITMAX and DOSI (global NFT platform by LINE NEXT) under Strategic Business “Other fintech,” confirming these activities are ongoing within the group. LY did not disclose proprietary crypto treasury holdings or any mining activity in the review period. ([lycorp.co.jp](https://www.lycorp.co.jp/en/ir/news/irnews20190917103029/main/0/link/LINE_20190917EN.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence shows direct involvement in crypto exchanges, token issuance support, wallets/custody, and NFT marketplaces—activities that squarely fall under crypto financial services. No sources indicate balance‑sheet HODL behavior, mining operations, or hardware/infrastructure provisioning. Accordingly, the classification assigns finserv = 1.0 with all other mechanism weights = 0.0. Given LY’s diversified revenue base, crypto‑related revenue appears minor relative to group totals (RevShare = 1) and no material crypto asset balances are evident on the consolidated balance sheet (AssetShare = 0). ([lycorp.co.jp](https://www.lycorp.co.jp/en/ir/news/auto_20240507582159/pdfFile.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- LINE’s global exchange BITFRONT ceased operations in late 2022; Japan-focused BITMAX and NFT initiatives continue, but granular revenue disclosure for these units is limited, so materiality is inferred as minor. ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2022/11/28/social-media-giant-line-closes-its-crypto-exchange-bitfront?utm_source=openai))  
- Plans discussed in 2023 to link PayPay with crypto/NFT services reflect strategic intent; execution timing and scale were not fully quantified within the 2017–2024 window. ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/tse/4689/investor-relations/earnings-call/q1-2023))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q1 FY2023 (Z Holdings): “We will develop new markets by linking LINE’s crypto assets and NFT services with PayPay’s payments.” — Alpha Spread transcript (Q1‑2023) [Alpha Spread](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/tse/4689/investor-relations/earnings-call/q1-2023) ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/tse/4689/investor-relations/earnings-call/q1-2023))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- May 8, 2024: “Results for the Fiscal Year Ended March 31, 2024” — Strategic Business ‘Other fintech’ lists LINE BITMAX and DOSI among major services/products. — LY Corporation IR [PDF](https://www.lycorp.co.jp/en/ir/news/auto_20240507582159/pdfFile.pdf) ([lycorp.co.jp](https://www.lycorp.co.jp/en/ir/news/auto_20240507582159/pdfFile.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**
- Sep 17, 2019: “BITMAX Cryptocurrency Exchange for Japan Begins Operations” — LVC (LINE) press release. — LY Corporation IR [PDF](https://www.lycorp.co.jp/en/ir/news/irnews20190917103029/main/0/link/LINE_20190917EN.pdf) ([lycorp.co.jp](https://www.lycorp.co.jp/en/ir/news/irnews20190917103029/main/0/link/LINE_20190917EN.pdf))
- Aug 4, 2020: “BITMAX… Begins Offering LINE‑branded Crypto Asset ‘LINK’” — LVC press release. — LY Corporation IR [PDF](https://www.lycorp.co.jp/en/ir/news/irnews20200804103050/main/0/link/LINE_20200804_EN.pdf) ([lycorp.co.jp](https://www.lycorp.co.jp/en/ir/news/irnews20200804103050/main/0/link/LINE_20200804_EN.pdf))
- Jul 27, 2021: Yahoo! JAPAN and LINE to collaborate on NFT secondary market integration with Yahoo! Auctions — Company release (JP). — LY (LINE) News [PDF](https://www.lycorp.co.jp/news/archive/L/ja/ja20210727_B.pdf) ([lycorp.co.jp](https://www.lycorp.co.jp/news/archive/L/ja/ja20210727_B.pdf))
- Apr 13, 2022: “LINE Launches LINE NFT, an NFT Marketplace in Japan” — Company press release. — LINE Corp [link](https://www.linecorp.com/en/pr/news/en/2022/4200) ([linecorp.com](https://www.linecorp.com/en/pr/news/en/2022/4200?utm_source=openai))
- Nov 28, 2022: “Social‑Media Giant LINE Closes Its Crypto Exchange Bitfront” — CoinDesk. — CoinDesk [link](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2022/11/28/social-media-giant-line-closes-its-crypto-exchange-bitfront) ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2022/11/28/social-media-giant-line-closes-its-crypto-exchange-bitfront?utm_source=openai))

## BELLICUM PHARMACEUTICALS DEAD - DELIST.05/03/24

* Rank: 332 | Expo: 0.13 | id_str: US0794814048

* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2019, 2022), SEC 8-K (May 2023), results press release (Q4 2019), Nasdaq delisting notice (2023)

### Firm Overview
Bellicum Pharmaceuticals, Inc. was a U.S.-based clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing controllable cellular immunotherapies for cancers, including CAR-T and CAR-NK approaches. The company conducted R&D and clinical programs during 2017–2024 and later moved through delisting and deregistration steps as operations wound down.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024, Bellicum’s SEC filings and company communications consistently describe a pure-play biotechnology business centered on cellular immunotherapy R&D, with no products, services, or policies related to cryptocurrencies. There is no evidence of treasury crypto holdings, crypto mining activity, operating a crypto exchange/custody platform, or providing crypto-focused infrastructure or non-financial services. The disclosures focus on pipeline status, clinical trial updates, cash runway, and capital markets events rather than any digital-asset initiatives. Consequently, no crypto exposure mechanism applies. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1358403/000135840320000007/a201910k1.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of any substantive crypto references in filings, press releases, or financial news during the 2017–2024 window, the firm is classified as unclassified with respect to crypto exposure. All six mechanism categories are set to 0.0 except unclass = 1.0. No evidence indicates that Bellicum’s investor base treated the equity as a crypto proxy, so invbase = 0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1358403/000135840320000007/a201910k1.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The company’s website-hosted earnings call transcripts are limited and some third-party archives are paywalled; however, SEC filings and company releases provide sufficient clarity that no crypto activities were pursued. ([seekingalpha.com](https://seekingalpha.com/article/4331711-bellicum-pharmaceuticals-inc-blcm-ceo-richard-fair-on-q4-2019-results-earnings-call?utm_source=openai))  
- Corporate actions around delisting/deregistration in 2023–2024 do not introduce any crypto angle; they are referenced here solely for corporate context. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1358403/000162828023020109/blcm-20230530.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

## WISE A

* Rank: 333 | Expo: 0.13 | id_str: GB00BL9YR756
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Help Centre/Acceptable Use Policy (multi-year), HY FY23 interim results transcript (Nov 29, 2022), FY2023 annual report

### Firm Overview
Wise plc is a UK-based financial technology company listed in London that provides cross-border payments, multi-currency accounts, cards, and embedded payments infrastructure for banks and platforms. Its annual report describes the group’s principal activity as the provision of cross‑border money transfer services to consumers and businesses. ([wise.com](https://wise.com/imaginary-v2/Wise-Annual-Report-Accounts-FY2023.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across the 2017–2024 window, there is no evidence that Wise operated a crypto exchange, offered crypto custody/trading, mined digital assets, or held crypto as treasury. On the contrary, Wise’s policy explicitly prohibits sending money to cryptocurrency exchanges or using Wise to facilitate crypto trading, indicating the firm deliberately avoids direct digital-asset activity. ([wise.com](https://wise.com/help/articles/2v3OMcg97Z7gObSifJbjsu/understanding-wises-position-on-cryptocurrency?utm_source=openai))  
Earnings-call materials reference “crypto currency” only in the context of broader competitive landscape debates, not as a Wise product line. There are no disclosures in the FY2023 annual report of crypto products or balance‑sheet crypto assets. ([wise.com](https://wise.com/imaginary-v2/images/059d290564f11b83ebaad1795f07df37-WISE%20FY23%20HALF%20YEAR%20RESULTS%20PRESENTATION%20-%20TRANSCRIPT%20%28Final%20for%20Website%29.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Given the explicit prohibition of crypto-related usage in Wise’s policies and the absence of crypto products, custody, mining, treasury holdings, or purpose-built crypto infrastructure in filings or calls, no crypto-exposure mechanism applies. The firm is therefore assigned unclass = 1.0, with all other mechanism weights at 0.0. There is no indication that Wise trades as a crypto proxy with investors, so invbase = 0. ([wise.com](https://wise.com/help/articles/2v3OMcg97Z7gObSifJbjsu/understanding-wises-position-on-cryptocurrency?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Wise’s public transcripts focus on core payments and account features; crypto is mentioned only as part of market context, not product strategy.  
- Policies can evolve by jurisdiction; this classification reflects disclosures and policies observed during 2017–2024. ([wise.com](https://wise.com/imaginary-v2/images/059d290564f11b83ebaad1795f07df37-WISE%20FY23%20HALF%20YEAR%20RESULTS%20PRESENTATION%20-%20TRANSCRIPT%20%28Final%20for%20Website%29.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 29, 2022: HY FY23 Interim Results — “we have had things like crypto currency, IXB, HSBC global money.” — Wise transcript. [Wise IR (PDF)](https://wise.com/imaginary-v2/images/059d290564f11b83ebaad1795f07df37-WISE%20FY23%20HALF%20YEAR%20RESULTS%20PRESENTATION%20-%20TRANSCRIPT%20%28Final%20for%20Website%29.pdf) (mentions crypto as market context) ([wise.com](https://wise.com/imaginary-v2/images/059d290564f11b83ebaad1795f07df37-WISE%20FY23%20HALF%20YEAR%20RESULTS%20PRESENTATION%20-%20TRANSCRIPT%20%28Final%20for%20Website%29.pdf))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- FY2023 Annual Report and Accounts — principal activity is provision of cross-border money transfer services; no crypto products disclosed. **Financial news / other:**
- Help Centre: “You can’t send money to cryptocurrency exchanges … or use Wise to facilitate crypto trading.” [Wise Help Centre](https://wise.com/help/articles/2v3OMcg97Z7gObSifJbjsu/understanding-wises-position-on-cryptocurrency) ([wise.com](https://wise.com/help/articles/2v3OMcg97Z7gObSifJbjsu/understanding-wises-position-on-cryptocurrency?utm_source=openai))

## SAP

* Rank: 334 | Expo: 0.13 | id_str: DE0007164600
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 2017 Form 20-F; Q1 2017 and Q2 2018 earnings calls; 2018 SAP Cloud Platform Blockchain announcement; SAP News Center (2021)

### Firm Overview
SAP SE is a Germany-based global enterprise application and cloud software company listed in Frankfurt and on the NYSE, best known for ERP suites such as S/4HANA and its Business Technology Platform. Its blockchain-related activity sits within innovation offerings that support enterprise use cases like supply-chain traceability rather than cryptocurrency trading or custody. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/s/NYSE_SAP_2017.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024, SAP promoted blockchain capabilities for enterprise processes, including a blockchain-as-a-service offering on SAP Cloud Platform/Leonardo and participation in industry programs and consortia. Management discussed blockchain on earnings calls (e.g., joining Hyperledger and forming a customer consortium) as an innovation area, not as a financial product or treasury asset. There is no evidence of SAP mining digital assets, operating a crypto exchange, providing crypto custody, or holding cryptocurrency on its balance sheet. Later communications highlight traceability solutions such as GreenToken by SAP that use blockchain for supply-chain transparency. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/SAP%3ACT/transcripts/2017-year/1-quarter))

### Weight Assignment
SAP’s exposure to crypto-related developments is via non-financial services that support blockchain adoption by enterprise customers. Given the absence of mining, financial services, treasury holdings, or purpose-built mining infrastructure, the firm’s crypto-exposure mechanism is classified entirely as bizserv. Consequently, weights are set to bizserv = 1.0 and all other mechanisms = 0.0; invbase = 0 as SAP is not characterized in financial media or by management as a crypto proxy. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/s/NYSE_SAP_2017.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- SAP’s blockchain branding evolved (Leonardo/BTP) and public emphasis varied over time; revenue attribution from blockchain services is not separately disclosed, so crypto-related materiality appears negligible. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/s/NYSE_SAP_2017.pdf))  
- Later-period earnings transcripts rarely feature blockchain explicitly; classification relies on multiple earlier calls plus filings and news confirming enterprise blockchain offerings and programs. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/07/19/sap-se-sap-q2-2018-earnings-conference-call-transc.aspx))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jul 19, 2018 (Q2 2018): “the hype and the excitement around blockchain is accelerating… we… created a [blockchain] consortium… more than 50 customers… now past 70.” — The Motley Fool transcript. ## WEBSTEP

* Rank: 335 | Expo: 0.13 | id_str: NO0010609662
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 2021–2022 annual reports; Q4 2021 earnings call; Q4 2021 investor presentation; company press releases (2021)

### Firm Overview
Webstep ASA is a Norway-based IT consulting company listed on the Oslo Stock Exchange that provides software development, cloud, data/AI, and technology advisory services for public- and private-sector clients across Norway (previously also Sweden). Its business model centers on experts-for-hire, team-as-a-service, and project deliveries rather than operating financial products or holding investment assets. ([webstep.com](https://www.webstep.com/about-us/?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management explicitly included blockchain among the “emerging technologies” the firm delivers as part of advisory and IT services, alongside cloud, AI, IoT, and security, indicating non-financial services around blockchain rather than direct crypto activity. Investor materials from the same period list “Blockchain” within Webstep’s delivery model focus areas. There is no evidence that Webstep mines cryptoassets, operates a crypto exchange or custody service, or holds cryptocurrency on its balance sheet. Overall, references to blockchain are capability-oriented and positioned within general digital transformation work. ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/ose/wstep/investor-relations/earnings-call/q4-2021))

### Weight Assignment
Webstep’s exposure to crypto is limited to non-financial consulting and implementation services touching blockchain use cases for clients. With no treasury holdings, mining operations, crypto-financial services, or crypto-specific infrastructure sales, the firm is classified entirely as bizserv. We therefore assign bizserv = 1.0 and all other mechanisms = 0.0; invbase = 0 due to the absence of evidence that Webstep trades as a crypto proxy or that management highlights a crypto-focused investor base. ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/ose/wstep/investor-relations/earnings-call/q4-2021))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Blockchain is referenced as part of a broad innovation stack and not broken out in segment reporting; no crypto-related revenue is disclosed separately, suggesting negligible materiality. ([live.euronext.com](https://live.euronext.com/sites/default/files/company_press_releases/attachments_oslo/2023/04/13/587572_Webstep_ASA_Annual_report_2022.pdf))  
- Later transcripts and filings emphasize general IT consulting; specific blockchain mentions appear episodic, but the classification remains stable as non-financial services. ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/ose/wstep/investor-relations/earnings-call/q1-2022))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 17, 2022 (Q4 2021): “Cloud, AI, blockchain, IoT and security are all drivers… we are delivering advice and IT services leveraging these key technologies.” — CEO remarks. [AlphaSpread (Q4 2021)](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/ose/wstep/investor-relations/earnings-call/q4-2021) ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/ose/wstep/investor-relations/earnings-call/q4-2021))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**

[Webstep 2021 Annual Report (PDF)](https://live.euronext.com/sites/default/files/company_press_releases/attachments_oslo/2022/04/05/558596_Webstep_ASA_Annual_report_2021.pdf) ([live.euronext.com](https://live.euronext.com/sites/default/files/company_press_releases/attachments_oslo/2022/04/05/558596_Webstep_ASA_Annual_report_2021.pdf))

### Firm Overview
Matinas BioPharma Holdings, Inc. is a U.S.-based clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company listed on NYSE American that develops oral, lipid nanocrystal (LNC) drug delivery formulations. Its lead program is MAT2203, an oral formulation of amphotericin B for invasive fungal infections; the company’s activities are focused on drug R&D and partnerships.

### Exposure Channels Identified
A review of earnings call transcripts and SEC filings over 2017–2024 shows no involvement in cryptocurrency holding, mining, exchanges, custody, payments, or blockchain-related services; management commentary focuses on LNC-enabled drug programs and partnering. The 2022 Form 10-K details the business and financials with no references to bitcoin, blockchain, or cryptocurrency. Reported revenues in the period stem from R&D collaborations (e.g., BioNTech) rather than any digital-asset activity, and balance sheet assets are cash, marketable securities, and typical R&D-related items. Consequently, no crypto-exposure mechanism applies. ([stockinsights.ai](https://www.stockinsights.ai/us/MTNB/earnings-transcript/fy24-q2-cb84))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of substantive crypto exposure across transcripts, filings, and news during the 2017–2024 sample, the firm is classified as unclassified for crypto channels. We assign unclass = 1.0 and all other mechanism categories = 0.0. There is no evidence that the stock trades as a crypto proxy or that management highlights a crypto-focused shareholder base; invbase = 0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1582554/000149315223007726/form10-k.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified beyond the general limitation that absence-of-evidence is inferred from filings and multiple transcripts within the 2017–2024 window. Searches of filings and disclosures found no mentions of cryptocurrency, bitcoin, or blockchain. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1582554/000149315223007726/form10-k.htm))

* Primary sources: Form 10-K (FY2023); Q2–Q3 2022 earnings calls; company press releases (2022–2023)

### Firm Overview
Shutterstock, Inc. is a U.S.-based provider of stock images, video, music, and creative tools, serving enterprise and self-serve customers globally, and listed on the NYSE as SSTK. Its strategy in recent years has centered on content marketplaces, acquisitions (e.g., Giphy), and partnerships to enable generative AI capabilities rather than crypto-related activities. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1549346/000154934624000007/sstk-20231231.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
A review of earnings call transcripts from 2017–2024 shows discussion focused on subscription mix, enterprise growth, 3D content, and creative workflow tools, with no substantive references to cryptocurrency, mining, exchanges, custody, or blockchain services. ([news.alphastreet.com](https://news.alphastreet.com/shutterstock-inc-sstk-q2-2022-earnings-call-transcript/))  
The FY2023 Form 10-K explicitly states the company “does not hold any Crypto Assets as of December 31, 2023,” and contains no disclosures of crypto products or services. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1549346/000154934624000007/sstk-20231231.htm))  
Company news in 2022–2023 highlights partnerships with OpenAI and Meta to power generative AI features on its platform—initiatives unrelated to crypto-financial services or blockchain infrastructure. ([investor.shutterstock.com](https://investor.shutterstock.com/news-releases/news-release-details/shutterstock-introduces-generative-ai-its-all-one-creative?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Because there is no evidence in transcripts, filings, or reputable news that Shutterstock holds crypto, mines, offers crypto financial services, provides crypto-specific business services, or supplies crypto-mining infrastructure, all mechanism categories are set to 0.0 and unclass = 1.0. There is also no indication that SSTK trades as a crypto proxy or targets a crypto-focused investor base, so invbase = 0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1549346/000154934624000007/sstk-20231231.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified beyond reliance on absence of evidence across 2017–2024 sources. Note: reports about the founder’s personal angel investment in an NFT marketplace (Mintable, 2021) pertain to individual activity and not to Shutterstock corporate operations, and thus do not affect this classification. ([crowdfundinsider.com](https://www.crowdfundinsider.com/2021/07/177350-nft-marketplace-mintable-raises-13-million-series-a/?utm_source=openai))

## ENDAVA ADR 1:1

* Rank: 339 | Expo: 0.13 | id_str: US29260V1052
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Form 20-F (FY2024); Annual Report (FY2020); Q4 FY2024 earnings call

### Firm Overview
Endava plc is a London-headquartered provider of next‑generation technology services and digital transformation solutions operating globally, listed on the NYSE via ADRs under ticker DAVA. Its client base and revenue are concentrated in payments, banking/capital markets, insurance, TMT, and other enterprise verticals.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Company filings describe project work “on distributed ledger technology systems and cryptocurrency technologies such as exchanges and non‑fungible token issuance systems,” indicating Endava serves crypto‑native clients as an engineering partner rather than as an asset holder or financial intermediary. ([investors.endava.com](https://investors.endava.com/sec-filings/content/0001656081-24-000037/0001656081-24-000037.pdf))  
Earlier annual reporting also highlights development of “blockchain payment gateways,” reinforcing a consistent positioning as a software/services provider around blockchain initiatives. ([s22.q4cdn.com](https://s22.q4cdn.com/220269410/files/doc_downloads/AGM/2020/FINAL-ENDAVA-PLC-annual-report-FY20-r188-SIGNED.pdf))  
Earnings calls across the sample period emphasize Endava’s deep exposure to payments and financial services clients—environments where some blockchain/DLT and digital‑asset projects arise—without suggesting the company itself runs exchanges, holds crypto, or offers custody/trading products. ([stockinsights.ai](https://www.stockinsights.ai/us/DAVA/earnings-transcript/fy24-q4-d6fc))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence points to Endava delivering non‑financial technology and engineering services to crypto ecosystem participants (e.g., exchanges/NFT issuance systems) rather than directly handling digital assets. Under the taxonomy, this is classified as bizserv. No disclosures indicate balance‑sheet crypto holdings, mining, financial‑services intermediation, or crypto‑specific infrastructure supply. Accordingly, bizserv = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. ([investors.endava.com](https://investors.endava.com/sec-filings/content/0001656081-24-000037/0001656081-24-000037.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Filings confirm engagement with DLT/crypto projects but do not quantify revenue attributable to these activities; RevShare is therefore coded as minor. ([investors.endava.com](https://investors.endava.com/sec-filings/content/0001656081-24-000037/0001656081-24-000037.pdf))  
- Earnings-call transcripts reviewed within 2017–2024 do not explicitly discuss crypto; the classification relies primarily on SEC filings plus generic transcript context about client verticals, which caps certainty at 3. ([stockinsights.ai](https://www.stockinsights.ai/us/DAVA/earnings-transcript/fy24-q4-d6fc))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Sep 19, 2024 (Q4 FY2024): “Our concentration on payments as a vertical… has, for many years, driven significant growth for our business.” — [StockInsights transcript](https://www.stockinsights.ai/us/DAVA/earnings-transcript/fy24-q4-d6fc). ([stockinsights.ai](https://www.stockinsights.ai/us/DAVA/earnings-transcript/fy24-q4-d6fc))  
- Oct 11, 2018 (Q4 FY2018): “We’re particularly strong in the payments and financial services vertical in [the Nordic] market…” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/10/11/endava-plc-dava-q4-2018-earnings-conference-call-t.aspx). ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/10/11/endava-plc-dava-q4-2018-earnings-conference-call-t.aspx))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**

## HANG SENG BANK

* Rank: 340 | Expo: 0.13 | id_str: HK0011000095
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Annual Reports (2022–2024); 2024 Interim Results speech; e-HKD pilot press releases (2023–2024)

### Firm Overview
Hang Seng Bank Limited is a leading Hong Kong-based bank and a subsidiary of The Hongkong and Shanghai Banking Corporation, providing retail, commercial and wealth management services in Hong Kong and Mainland China. The group also operates an asset management arm (Hang Seng Investment Management) and an index business (Hang Seng Indexes Company).

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across the 2017–2024 sample window, public filings, result announcements and press materials do not evidence the bank holding cryptocurrencies on balance sheet, running a crypto exchange/custody business, mining, or offering crypto investment products as a core service. Management communications in 2023–2024 highlight participation in the Hong Kong Monetary Authority’s e‑HKD/CBDC pilots and exploration of tokenised deposits—initiatives that use DLT but are distinct from cryptocurrencies and do not imply balance‑sheet or product exposure to crypto assets. (hangseng.com; 31 Jul 2024 Interim Results speech)  
Hong Kong approved spot Bitcoin and Ether ETFs in April 2024, but no firm‑specific disclosure was found indicating Hang Seng Bank offered crypto ETF dealing/custody beyond its general securities platform or that its asset‑management unit managed crypto ETPs during the period. (cnbc.com; HSVM ETF research paper context)

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of substantive, firm‑level crypto activity during 2017–2024—and the available disclosures focusing on CBDC/e‑HKD pilots and tokenisation exploration rather than cryptocurrency products or holdings—the company is classified as unclass = 1.0. No evidence supports weights in holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr under the project’s definitions for the sample period.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Hang Seng’s involvement in HKMA e‑HKD pilots and tokenised‑deposit exploration (2023–2024) is adjacent to the crypto ecosystem but does not constitute cryptocurrency exposure per project definitions.  
- Post‑period developments (e.g., tokenisation features linked to a gold ETF in 2026) fall outside the 2017–2024 window and are not considered for classification.  
- Earnings call transcripts are limited in public accessibility; classification relies on company result speeches/press releases and filings.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls / results speeches:**
- Jul 31, 2024 (Interim Results speech): “We continued to be a leading advocate of Central Bank Digital Currency (‘CBDC’) in Hong Kong… exploring use cases for tokenised deposit solutions…” — [Hang Seng Bank](https://cms.hangseng.com/cms/fin/file/result/speech_i_2024_en.pdf)  
- Feb 21, 2023 (2022 Annual Results speech): CEO remarks on performance and outlook; no crypto product/holding disclosures — [Hang Seng Bank](https://www.hangseng.com/cms/fin/file/result/speech_a_2022_en.pdf)

**Filings (annual reports / investor materials):**
- Mar 27, 2025 (Annual Report 2024; covers FY2024): Group financials and business review; no cryptocurrency holdings or crypto product lines disclosed — [HKEXnews filing](https://www1.hkexnews.hk/listedco/listconews/sehk/2025/0327/2025032700485.pdf)  
- Annual Report hubs and five‑year summaries for 2022–2023 (context; no crypto disclosures) — [Hang Seng Bank 2023 Summary](https://cms.hangseng.com/cms/fin/fld/statement/annual-report-2023/five-year-financial-summary-eng.html)

**Press releases / other company materials:**
- Sep 23, 2024: “Hang Seng Participates in Phase 2 of HKMA’s e‑HKD Pilot Programme.” — [Hang Seng Bank](https://www.hangseng.com/cms/ccd/eng/PDF/230924e.pdf)  
- Sep 15, 2023: “Hang Seng Completes Real‑life Simulations of Hypothetical e‑HKD Use Cases.” — [Hang Seng Bank](https://cms.hangseng.com/cms/ccd/eng/PDF/150923e.pdf)  
- May 18, 2023: “Hang Seng Use Cases Selected for HKMA e‑HKD Pilot Programme.” — [Hang Seng Bank](https://www.hangseng.com/cms/ccd/eng/PDF/180523e.pdf)

**Financial news / broader context:**
- Apr 15, 2024: Hong Kong regulators approve spot Bitcoin and Ether ETFs (market context; no Hang Seng‑specific crypto disclosure). — [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/04/15/hong-kong-regulators-approve-spot-bitcoin-and-ether-etfs-.html)  
- 2025 (context paper referencing 2022–2024 developments): HSVM ETF research paper notes SFC approvals of VA futures (2022) and spot VA ETFs (2023/2024); no indication HSVM launched crypto ETPs in 2017–2024. — [Hang Seng Investment Management](https://cms.hangsenginvestment.com/cms/ivp/hsvm/document/ETF_Research_Paper.pdf)
