## UBISOFT ENTERTAINMENT CAT A

* Rank: 341 | Expo: 0.13 | id_str: FR0000054470
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings call transcripts (Oct 26, 2023); Company press releases (Dec 2021); Financial/industry news (2021–2024)

### Firm Overview
Ubisoft Entertainment SA is a France‑based global video game publisher and developer known for franchises such as Assassin’s Creed, Tom Clancy’s Rainbow Six, and Far Cry. During 2017–2024, its core business remained video game development and publishing, with selective experimentation in blockchain/NFT features and a Web3 title alongside its mainstream portfolio.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company piloted blockchain/NFT features via Ubisoft Quartz, a platform launched in December 2021 that enabled limited in‑game digital collectibles (“Digits”) on Tezos for Ghost Recon Breakpoint; Ubisoft later ended Breakpoint updates but indicated Quartz/NFTs could be used in other games. ([staticctf.ubisoft.com](https://staticctf.ubisoft.com/8aefmxkxpxwl/2Oe3Awist4SYDKCVt4wRsv/557e39030e8e3b4cd1dc3bb1d7fdce20/Ubisoft_Quartz_Announcement_12.07.21_EN.pdf?utm_source=openai))  
Ubisoft also pursued a dedicated Web3 game, Champions Tactics: Grimoria Chronicles, with NFT‑based characters and mints in 2023–2024; reporting noted launches/mints and marketplace activity, and the firm published Web3‑specific user terms. These initiatives reflect product experimentation with blockchain items rather than custody, brokerage, or financial products. ([jp.beincrypto.com](https://jp.beincrypto.com/ubisoft-championtactics-nft-free-mint-oasys//?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence indicates Ubisoft’s crypto link is through non‑financial product features (NFT collectibles in a legacy title) and the development/launch of a Web3 game. There is no indication of crypto held as treasury assets, mining, exchange/custody operations, or mining‑directed infrastructure. Accordingly, the exposure is classified as bizserv = 1.0 under this project’s definitions (non‑financial services/content around the ecosystem). Given the small scale of observed activity relative to group revenues during 2017–2024, RevShare is coded 1 and AssetShare 0.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Transcript coverage did not include a crypto‑specific passage; however, multiple primary press materials and reputable industry reports detail Quartz and the Web3 title.  
- The 2021 Quartz beta drew limited commercial traction and was discontinued for Breakpoint, while Ubisoft signaled potential reuse in future titles; activity levels likely fluctuated. ([pcgamer.com](https://www.pcgamer.com/ubisoft-ends-its-nft-experiment-in-ghost-recon-breakpoint-but-promises-theyre-coming-to-other-games/?utm_source=openai))  
- No reliable evidence of balance‑sheet crypto holdings or regulated financial‑product activity was found for 2017–2024.

### Evidence
<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 26, 2023 (H1 FY2024): “Welcome, everyone, and thank you for joining the call today. Ubisoft delivered an excellent second quarter well above target.” — [FactSet CallStreet via Scribd](https://www.scribd.com/document/690454896/CORRECTED-TRANSCRIPT-Ubisoft-Entertainment-SA-UBI-FR-Q2-2024-Earnings-Call-26-October-2023-12-15-PM-ET)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Oct 28, 2021 (H1 FY2021–22 earnings figures; call notice included) — [Ubisoft press release PDF](https://staticctf.ubisoft.com/8aefmxkxpxwl/667U3sKCZltRVFBUMN0jM8/c9dbc2fd1ddbf14c9def4cfb5fe90fd2/Ubisoft_FY22_H1_Earnings_PR_English.pdf)
- Oct 27, 2022 (H1 FY2022–23 earnings figures) — [Ubisoft press release PDF](https://staticctf.ubisoft.com/8aefmxkxpxwl/796qQk01yMVE43ObpkcA0Q/cdffc5deb747da211e0436fdfe297c2d/Ubisoft_FY23_H1_Earnings_PR_English_Final.pdf)
- Dec 7, 2021: “Introducing Ubisoft Quartz… the first platform for playable and energy‑efficient NFTs in AAA games.” — [GlobeNewswire (Ubisoft)](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2021/12/07/2347700/0/en/Introducing-Ubisoft-Quartz-The-First-Platform-for-Playable-and-Energy-Efficient-NFTs-In-AAA-Games.html)

## ACCENTURE CLASS A

* Rank: 342 | Expo: 0.13 | id_str: IE00B4BNMY34
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings call transcripts (Dec 16, 2022; Jun 22, 2023), 10-K filings (2022, 2024), Company press releases (Nov 8, 2022), Financial/industry news (Nov 8, 2022)

### Firm Overview
Accenture plc is a global professional services firm headquartered in Dublin and listed on the NYSE under ACN, providing strategy and consulting, technology, and managed services to enterprise and public-sector clients worldwide. During 2017–2024, blockchain/Web3 appeared as one of several emerging technology capabilities within its broader digital, cloud, security, and AI offerings.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management has referenced client work and partnerships tied to blockchain/Web3, such as collaborating with NTT DOCOMO to “speed up the adoption of Web3,” indicating product design, platform development, and training rather than asset handling. Accenture’s filings list blockchain among emerging technologies incubated across its Labs and innovation programs, consistent with advisory, systems integration, and solution-building roles. The firm also partnered with the Digital Dollar Foundation in the not‑for‑profit Digital Dollar Project, evidencing consulting/technology work around CBDC exploration rather than balance‑sheet holdings or financial products. There is no evidence of crypto treasury positions, mining, custody/exchange operations, or mining‑oriented hardware/energy infrastructure. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/ACN/documents/transcripts/q1-2023))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence points to Accenture’s exposure occurring through non‑financial services delivered to clients in and around the crypto ecosystem (consulting, architecture, integration, pilots), not through direct asset exposure or financial intermediation. Accordingly, the classification is bizserv = 1.0, with all other mechanisms = 0.0. Given blockchain/Web3 is a small capability within a diversified portfolio, RevShare is coded 1 (<~5%) and AssetShare 0 (no crypto assets). ([accenture.com](https://www.accenture.com/content/dam/accenture/final/accenture-com/a-com-custom-component/iconic/document/Accenture-2024-10-K.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Earnings transcripts and filings reference blockchain/Web3 as part of broader technology trends; they do not quantify crypto‑specific revenue, so materiality is inferred as minor. ([accenture.com](https://www.accenture.com/content/dam/accenture/final/accenture-com/a-com-custom-component/iconic/document/Accenture-2024-10-K.pdf))  
- Partnerships such as the NTT DOCOMO Web3 collaboration and CBDC work via the Digital Dollar Project support a services‑only role; however, revenue attribution by sub‑theme is not disclosed. ([newsroom.accenture.com](https://newsroom.accenture.com/news/2022/ntt-docomo-and-accenture-collaborate-to-accelerate-adoption-of-web3?utm_source=openai))  
- No reliable evidence within 2017–2024 indicates crypto custody, exchange operations, mining, or treasury holdings.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Dec 16, 2022 (Q1 FY2023): “We’re partnering with NTT DOCOMO … to speed up the adoption of Web3.” — [Fintool](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/ACN/documents/transcripts/q1-2023) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/ACN/documents/transcripts/q1-2023))

## AMERICAN EXPRESS

* Rank: 343 | Expo: 0.13 | id_str: US0258161092
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.2, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.8
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings calls (Oct 22, 2021; Jan 25, 2022), 10-K (2023), Company/partner press releases (Nov 16, 2017; Jun 10, 2022), Financial/industry news (2017, 2022)

### Firm Overview
American Express Company (NYSE: AXP) is a global integrated payments company headquartered in New York that issues charge and credit cards, operates a proprietary network, and provides related banking and travel services. During 2017–2024, its engagement with crypto/blockchain appeared primarily through selective partnerships and pilots rather than direct asset custody or trading.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management has discussed crypto primarily as a payments‑adjacent phenomenon, noting card acceptance at certain NFT marketplaces (e.g., NBA Top Shot) and potential future redemption options, while emphasizing that cryptocurrency is viewed “more as an asset class” and not a near‑term threat to card payments. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/22/american-express-axp-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcr/)) The company pursued blockchain‑enabled cross‑border B2B payments via Ripple beginning in 2017 through its FX International Payments business, evidencing limited use of distributed‑ledger rails for payments. ([ripple.com](https://ripple.com/ripple-press/american-express-introduces-blockchain-enabled-cross-border-payments/?utm_source=openai)) In 2022, crypto platform Abra announced a crypto‑rewards credit card to run on the American Express network; American Express framed this as a network partnership, and its venture arm has invested in crypto firms such as FalconX and Abra. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20220610005460/en/Abra-Announces-the-Abra-Crypto-Card-on-the-American-Express-Network-and-a-New-NFT-Service-in-the-Abra-App?utm_source=openai)) The 2023 Form 10‑K references “digital currencies … and blockchain” among competitive/payment technology developments, with no indication of balance‑sheet crypto holdings or direct exchange/custody operations. ([s26.q4cdn.com](https://s26.q4cdn.com/747928648/files/doc_financials/2023/ar/American-Express-Annual-Report-2023.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence supports a small but clear “finserv” exposure channel: AmEx enables select crypto‑related payments/benefits through its network and partners (NFT marketplace acceptance; announced Abra crypto‑rewards card; Ripple‑based payments pilot). There is no evidence of treasury crypto holdings, mining, custody/exchange operations, or mining‑directed infrastructure. Accordingly, finserv is set to 0.2 to capture this limited, partnership‑based exposure; all other mechanism weights are 0.0, with the residual 0.8 assigned to unclass reflecting that crypto remains peripheral to AmEx’s diversified business. Management’s framing and filings support RevShare = 0 and AssetShare = 0. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/22/american-express-axp-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcr/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The Abra “Crypto Card” was announced for late‑2022 availability; subsequent third‑party reviews suggest rollout was limited/unclear, which tempers the assessed materiality. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20220610005460/en/Abra-Announces-the-Abra-Crypto-Card-on-the-American-Express-Network-and-a-New-NFT-Service-in-the-Abra-App?utm_source=openai))  
- References to NBA Top Shot acceptance indicate card‑network enablement rather than AmEx handling digital assets; revenue attribution specific to crypto is not disclosed in filings. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/22/american-express-axp-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcr/))  
- Ripple‑based cross‑border activity evidences blockchain use but does not imply AmEx custody of crypto assets. ([ripple.com](https://ripple.com/ripple-press/american-express-introduces-blockchain-enabled-cross-border-payments/?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 22, 2021 (Q3 2021): “We think about cryptocurrency much more as an asset class… we’re on NBA Top Shot. You can use the card there.” — [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/22/american-express-axp-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcr/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/22/american-express-axp-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcr/))  
- Jan 25, 2022 (Q4 2021): “We think about the spectrum of digital currencies… probably not going to offer a crypto card.” — [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/01/25/american-express-axp-q4-2021-earnings-call-transcr/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/01/25/american-express-axp-q4-2021-earnings-call-transcr/))

**Filings (10‑K / company materials):**
- 2023 Form 10‑K: competition includes “digital currencies… and blockchain and similar distributed ledger technologies.” — [American Express 2023 Annual Report/10‑K PDF] (p. 18–19) ([s26.q4cdn.com](https://s26.q4cdn.com/747928648/files/doc_financials/2023/ar/American-Express-Annual-Report-2023.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**
- Nov 16, 2017: AmEx FXIP partners with Ripple and Santander for blockchain‑enabled cross‑border B2B payments — [Ripple press release] | [CNBC] ([ripple.com](https://ripple.com/ripple-press/american-express-introduces-blockchain-enabled-cross-border-payments/?utm_source=openai))

### Firm Overview
Plus Therapeutics, Inc. is a U.S. clinical-stage pharmaceutical company developing targeted radiotherapeutics and diagnostics for central nervous system cancers, including its rhenium-186 obisbemeda program and CNSide diagnostic assay. The company’s 2023 Form 10-K describes a focus on radiopharmaceutical R&D, grants, and clinical progress, with no reference to cryptocurrency or blockchain activities. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1095981/000095017024025851/pstv-20231231.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Searches of earnings call transcripts and company filings for January 2017–December 2024 show no evidence that Plus Therapeutics holds crypto assets, mines digital assets, provides crypto financial services, or supplies mining-focused infrastructure. The 2023 Form 10-K contains no occurrences of “cryptocurrency,” “bitcoin,” “digital asset,” or “blockchain,” and outlines cash and cash equivalents as the company’s liquid assets. Earnings calls during 2023 discuss clinical trial updates, regulatory milestones, and grant funding; there is no discussion of crypto-related operations. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1095981/000095017024025851/pstv-20231231.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of any substantiated crypto exposure mechanisms across transcripts, SEC filings, and news/IR materials, all mechanism categories are set to 0.0 with the residual assigned to unclass = 1.0. There is no basis to code a crypto‑sensitive investor base amplifier. As no crypto revenue or assets are disclosed or discussed, RevShare and AssetShare are NA for this unclassified firm. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1095981/000095017024025851/pstv-20231231.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified. Multiple transcript aggregators list calls for 2020–2024 and the company’s IR releases confirm routine earnings calls; available materials consistently focus on radiotherapeutics with no crypto content. ([strike.market](https://strike.market/stocks/PSTV/earnings-call-transcripts))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 14, 2023 (Q2 2023): “We completed enrollment in the Phase I Part A… [and] FDA approved us to continue to Phase I Part B.” — [StockInsights AI transcript](https://www.stockinsights.ai/us/PSTV/earnings-transcript/fy23-q2-08f0) ([stockinsights.ai](https://www.stockinsights.ai/us/PSTV/earnings-transcript/fy23-q2-08f0))

**Filings (10‑K / company materials):**
- 2023 Form 10‑K (filed Mar 5, 2024): Company describes radiotherapeutics focus; no references to cryptocurrency/blockchain terms found. — [SEC filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1095981/000095017024025851/pstv-20231231.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1095981/000095017024025851/pstv-20231231.htm))

**Financial news / other:**
- Oct 31, 2023: “Plus Therapeutics Reports Third Quarter 2023 Financial Results and Business Highlights; management to host conference call.” — [Company press release](https://ir.plustherapeutics.com/news-releases/news-release-details/plus-therapeutics-reports-third-quarter-2023-financial-results) ([ir.plustherapeutics.com](https://ir.plustherapeutics.com/news-releases/news-release-details/plus-therapeutics-reports-third-quarter-2023-financial-results?utm_source=openai))
- Listing of Plus Therapeutics earnings call transcripts across 2017–2024. — [Strike.Market transcripts index](https://strike.market/stocks/PSTV/earnings-call-transcripts) ([strike.market](https://strike.market/stocks/PSTV/earnings-call-transcripts))

## DOCUSIGN

* Rank: 345 | Expo: 0.13 | id_str: US2561631068
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2023–2024), Q2 FY2019 earnings call, company press release (2018), media interview (2020)

### Firm Overview
DocuSign, Inc. is a U.S.-based software company best known for cloud e-signature and broader agreement management products serving enterprises and governments globally. Within the 2017–2024 period, the company explored blockchain features but its core operations and disclosures focus on subscription software for agreement workflows. ([investor.docusign.com](https://investor.docusign.com/files/doc_financials/2023/ar/docusign-inc-_annual-report_form-10-k.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Searches of earnings call transcripts and SEC filings show no evidence that DocuSign held cryptocurrencies, mined digital assets, operated a crypto exchange, or provided custody/trading products. In June 2018 the company demonstrated an optional feature to anchor evidence of completed agreements to the Ethereum blockchain and joined relevant industry groups, framed as a technology integration rather than a crypto product. In September 2018 management reiterated this on the earnings call. In 2020, the CEO said blockchain was “too expensive” for DocuSign’s typical use cases, reinforcing that blockchain was not being adopted as a core platform. ([investor.docusign.com](https://investor.docusign.com/files/doc_financials/2023/ar/docusign-inc-_annual-report_form-10-k.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Because there is no substantiated activity in treasury crypto holdings, mining, crypto financial services, or mining-focused infrastructure, all mechanism categories are set to 0.0 with the residual assigned to unclass = 1.0. The 2018 Ethereum anchoring capability and standards memberships are treated as limited, optional technology features without disclosed crypto assets, fee lines, or volume metrics; they do not create a material crypto exposure mechanism for equity pricing under this framework. The investor-base amplifier is set to 0 given the lack of evidence that DOCU trades as a crypto proxy. ([investor.docusign.com](https://investor.docusign.com/files/doc_financials/2023/ar/docusign-inc-_annual-report_form-10-k.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
DocuSign’s 2018–2019 materials indicate experimentation with blockchain integrations and industry memberships; however, subsequent filings through 2024 do not quantify revenue, assets, or adoption tied to crypto, and recent filings contain no “blockchain/cryptocurrency” references. No evidence was found of DocuSign directly transacting in or custodying crypto assets during 2017–2024. ([investor.docusign.com](https://investor.docusign.com/files/doc_financials/2023/ar/docusign-inc-_annual-report_form-10-k.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Sep 5, 2018 (Q2 FY2019): “We announced a blockchain integration… [so] customers will… record their DocuSigned agreement on the Ethereum blockchain.” — [Motley Fool transcript] (short excerpt) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/09/05/docusign-inc-docu-q2-2019-earnings-conference-call.aspx))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- 2023 Form 10‑K: Business focuses on eSignature and agreement management; no references to cryptocurrency/blockchain terms found. — [DocuSign 2023 10‑K PDF] ([investor.docusign.com](https://investor.docusign.com/files/doc_financials/2023/ar/docusign-inc-_annual-report_form-10-k.pdf))  
- 2024 Form 10‑K: Continues subscription software focus; no references to cryptocurrency/blockchain terms found. — [DocuSign 2024 10‑K PDF] ([d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net](https://d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net/CIK-0001261333/75d75c18-cc65-4d3b-854b-4b0b5df125b3.pdf))  
- Jun 20, 2018: “DocuSign demonstrated a live integration with the Ethereum blockchain… [and] announced membership in the Enterprise Ethereum Alliance and Chamber of Digital Commerce.” — [Company press release] ([docusign.com](https://www.docusign.com/company/news-center/docusign-unveils-system-of-agreement-vision-at-momentum-2018))

**Financial news / other:**
- Oct 2020: CEO Daniel Springer: blockchain is “too expensive” for DocuSign’s use cases; prior Ethereum partnership cited. — [Quartz] ([qz.com](https://qz.com/1942479/docusign-ceo-says-blockchain-is-too-expensive-for-wide-adoption/))

</small>

## UNITED MICRO ELTN.

* Rank: 346 | Expo: 0.13 | id_str: TW0002303005
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 1.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings calls (Q4 2017; Q4 2018; Q2 2023), Form 20‑F filings (2022)

### Firm Overview
United Microelectronics Corporation is a Taiwan-based pure-play semiconductor foundry that manufactures integrated circuits on mature and specialty process nodes for global customers across communications, consumer, computing, and automotive end markets. Within 2017–2024, management discussed demand tied to cryptocurrency mining ASICs at 14nm, while the company remained focused on core foundry services rather than holding or transacting in crypto assets.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s only crypto-related channel is as an infrastructure supplier to the mining ecosystem. Management linked 14nm FinFET production and revenue swings to cryptocurrency-mining customers and to changes in crypto valuations. There is no evidence that UMC held cryptocurrency on its balance sheet, mined coins, or offered exchange/custody/payment products. Filings describe a traditional foundry model, reinforcing the absence of treasury or financial-services exposure.

### Weight Assignment
Evidence from multiple earnings calls shows UMC fabricated ASICs used for cryptocurrency mining at 14nm and that crypto market cycles affected node-level demand and pricing. No sources indicate any role as a miner, asset holder, or financial-services provider; the business remains wafer fabrication. Therefore, 100% of the exposure is assigned to infrastr (manufacturing chips that power crypto mining). The investor-base amplifier is set to 0, as there is no indication that UMC’s equity trades as a crypto proxy.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Mining-related demand appears episodic and most clearly documented around 2017–2019; later references (e.g., 2023) frame crypto-ASIC output as prior activity rather than an ongoing growth focus. The company does not quantify crypto-attributable revenue; the RevShare code is inferred from management’s qualitative comments and node-level mix disclosures. No transcript or filing indicates any crypto asset holdings.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jan 29, 2019 (Q4 2018): “14‑nanometer revenue has been suffering very weak demand from cryptocurrency related customers… [fell] to 1% from 5% in Q3… due to the volatility in the cryptocurrency market.” — [MLQ.ai transcript] (Q4’18) ([mlq.ai](https://mlq.ai/stocks/UMC/earnings-call-transcript/Q4-2018))  
- Jan 31, 2018 (Q4 2017): “Foundry growth… is driven by many different applications, including from cryptocurrency… Our addressable area… is mainly in the cryptocurrency or AI area.” — [Roic.ai transcript] (Q4’17) ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/UMC/transcripts/2017/4))  
- Jul 26, 2023 (Q2 2023): “We have been producing some 14 nm crypto coin related product a few years back.” — [Fintool transcript] (Q2’23) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/UMC/documents/transcripts/q2-2023))

**Filings (20‑F / company materials):**
- 2022 Form 20‑F: Describes UMC as a pure-play foundry serving communications, consumer, computer and other markets; no disclosure of cryptocurrency holdings or crypto financial products. — [SEC filing] ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1033767/000119312522125284/d168667d20f.htm?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- 2018 milestone: UMC notes entry into 14nm mass production for customer ICs, providing context for node used in crypto ASIC demand. — [UMC Milestones page] ([umc.com](https://www.umc.com/zh-CN/Milestone/UMC_milestone/Index/2010-2019?utm_source=openai))

</small>

## DRAFTKINGS A

* Rank: 347 | Expo: 0.13 | id_str: US26142V1052
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.9, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.1, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings calls (Q3 2021; Q1–Q2 2022), SEC filings (2022–2024), company press releases/news (2021–2022), financial news (2022, 2024)

### Firm Overview
DraftKings Inc. is a U.S.-based digital sports entertainment and gaming company operating online sports betting, iGaming, and daily fantasy sports across multiple jurisdictions. Between 2021 and July 30, 2024, it also operated DraftKings Marketplace and the “Reignmakers” NFT-based fantasy game built on Polygon; both were discontinued in Q3 2024.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s crypto exposure occurred primarily through operating a custodial NFT marketplace and NFT-based fantasy contests, including facilitating primary drops and secondary trading while safeguarding users’ NFTs under SAB 121 disclosures. DraftKings also entered blockchain infrastructure via Polygon, first through a strategic agreement that included the option to validate the network and later by operating a validator node in collaboration with Zero Hash. There is no evidence the firm mined cryptocurrencies or held tokens on its balance sheet for treasury purposes.

### Weight Assignment
Finserv is weighted at 0.9 because the core crypto activity involved issuing/custodying NFTs, running a marketplace with transaction fees, and integrating NFTs into fantasy contests—activities directly handling digital assets for customers. Infrastr is weighted at 0.1 to reflect the Polygon validator role, which supports blockchain operations but appears economically minor relative to core gaming revenues. No weights are assigned to holders, miners, or bizserv. The investor-base amplifier is set to 0 given the stock is generally traded as a sports betting/iGaming name rather than a crypto proxy.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
DraftKings discontinued Reignmakers and the NFT Marketplace effective July 30, 2024, so ongoing exposure after that date is limited; historical exposure within 2021–2024 is clear, but recent revenue contribution is not broken out in filings. Filings disclose safeguarding of users’ NFTs and legal matters around Marketplace NFTs but do not quantify NFT revenue as a share of total revenue. Details on validator economics (e.g., staking rewards) are not itemized in financial statements.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 5, 2021 (Q3 2021): “announcing a blockchain collaboration with Polygon… [and] option to… help secure the network as a validator node.” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/05/draftkings-inc-dkng-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcri/)
- Aug 5, 2022 (Q2 2022): “our recent investments in Web3 technology and DraftKings Marketplace have positioned us… to launch… Reignmakers football.” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/08/05/draftkings-inc-dkng-q2-2022-earnings-call-transcri/)
- May 6, 2022 (Q1 2022): “DraftKings Marketplace introduced the Primetime NFT Series…” — [Nasdaq transcript](https://www.nasdaq.com/articles/draftkings-inc.-dkng-q1-2022-earnings-call-transcript)

**Filings (10‑K / 10‑Q / disclosures):**
- 2022 Form 10‑K: Describes Marketplace primary/secondary NFT sales and revenue model; notes SAB 121 asset/liability for safeguarding users’ NFTs. — [SEC filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1883685/000188368523000013/dkng-20221231.htm)
- 2023 Form 10‑K: Continues NFT Marketplace/Reignmakers description; details related litigation and SAB 121 safeguarding disclosure. — [SEC filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1883685/000188368524000010/dkng-20231231.htm)
- Q2 2024 report: “Effective July 30, 2024, the Company discontinued Reignmakers and our NFT Marketplace.” — [DraftKings Q2 2024 filing](https://ir.aboutdraftkings.com/files/doc_financials/2024/q2/55bb1fee-2b8a-406f-8891-7c2c1689d967.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Oct 18, 2021: Polygon and DraftKings announce strategic blockchain agreement; DK given option to become a validator. — [Company press release](https://draftkings.gcs-web.com/news-releases/news-release-details/polygon-and-draftkings-enter-strategic-blockchain-agreement/)
- Mar 7, 2022: DraftKings becomes Polygon validator (with Zero Hash partnership), following through on the plan. — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2022/03/07/draftkings-becomes-polygon-validator-after-nft-marketplace-clocks-44m-in-sales); [DraftKings news post](https://www.draftkings.com/about/news/2022/03/draftkings-joins-forces-with-zero-hash-to-earn-staking-rewards-on-polygon/)
- Jul 30–31, 2024: DraftKings discontinues Reignmakers and NFT Marketplace, citing legal developments. — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2024/07/30/draftkings-dumps-nft-business-citing-legal-developments); [Covers](https://www.covers.com/industry/draftkings-cans-reignmakers-and-nft-marketplace-due-to-legal-concerns-july-31-2024)

</small>

## SYMBOLIC LOGIC

* Rank: 348 | Expo: 0.13 | id_str: US30049R2094
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: SEC filings (2021–2022), Q2 2021 earnings call, company press releases (2021)

### Firm Overview
Symbolic Logic, Inc. is a U.S.-based micro-cap technology company formerly known as Evolving Systems, Inc. After selling substantially all operating assets to PartnerOne Capital in December 2021, it delisted from Nasdaq in April 2022 and repositioned as a research and development organization focused on self-learning algorithms and symbolic tagging of physical objects.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management discussed potential future use of crypto tokens within a loyalty platform during the August 12, 2021 earnings call while the business was still operating as Evolving Systems; this was framed as prospective product direction rather than an active, revenue-generating line. ([tickertrends.io](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/EVOL/Q2-earnings-transcript-2021))  
Subsequent SEC filings around and after the 2021 pivot indicate the company became pre-revenue, with activities focused on R&D, including modeling crypto-based financial instruments, but do not show evidence of crypto custody, exchange operations, mining, or balance-sheet holdings. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1052054/000110465922044364/evol-20211231x10k.htm))  
Given the sale of its legacy software businesses in December 2021 and the lack of disclosed crypto products or assets through 2022 filings, no substantive, monetized crypto exposure during the 2017–2024 sample window is evidenced. ([evolving.com](https://www.evolving.com/press-release/evolving-systems-enters-definitive-agreements-to-sell-activation-and-marketing-businesses-to-partnerone-capital-for-40-million/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Despite a clear on-call statement of intent to explore crypto tokens for loyalty use cases in 2021, there is no corroborating evidence of launched products, customer assets under custody, mining activity, or treasury crypto holdings through the end of 2024. Filings instead emphasize an R&D posture following the 2021 asset sale. Accordingly, all mechanism weights are assigned to unclass (1.0) to reflect absence of material, verifiable exposure within the sample period; invbase is set to 0 as the stock is not characterized in credible sources as a crypto proxy. ([tickertrends.io](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/EVOL/Q2-earnings-transcript-2021))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The August 2021 call included forward-looking statements about issuing crypto tokens in loyalty programs, but no later disclosures confirm implementation; the firm “went dark” in early 2023, limiting public visibility after 2022. If any small pilots occurred, they were not disclosed in filings or credible coverage found for 2017–2024. ([tickertrends.io](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/EVOL/Q2-earnings-transcript-2021))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 12, 2021 (Q2 2021): “we will be issuing crypto tokens in the future… [crypto] is squarely in our product development plans.” — [TickerTrends transcript](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/EVOL/Q2-earnings-transcript-2021)

**Filings (10‑K / 10‑Q / company materials):**
- 2021 Form 10‑K (filed Apr 2022): Describes R&D focus; notes modeling of “chaotic crypto‑based financial instruments” and references success of self‑learning algorithms in the “crypto marketplace”; no crypto assets disclosed. — [SEC filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1052054/000110465922044364/evol-20211231x10k.htm)
- Q3 2022 Form 10‑Q: Confirms post‑sale pivot to R&D, delisting process, and lack of operating products; no crypto holdings/custody/mining disclosed. — [SEC filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1052054/000110465922116514/symb-20220930x10q.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- Oct 18, 2021: Evolving Systems enters definitive agreements to sell Activation and Marketing (incl. loyalty) businesses to PartnerOne for $40M. — [Company press release](https://www.evolving.com/press-release/evolving-systems-enters-definitive-agreements-to-sell-activation-and-marketing-businesses-to-partnerone-capital-for-40-million/)
- Apr 2022: Company notifies Nasdaq of intent to delist; Form 25 to be filed on or about April 25, 2022. — [MarketScreener summary of 8‑K](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/SYMBOLIC-LOGIC-INC-9224/news/EVOLVING-NOTIFIES-NASDAQ-OF-ITS-INTENTION-TO-DELIST-ITS-STOCK-Form-8-K-40052137/)

</small>

## TECH MAHINDRA

* Rank: 349 | Expo: 0.13 | id_str: INE669C01036
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Integrated annual reports (2018–2019, 2020–2021), Q3 FY2018 and Q4 FY2018 earnings calls, company press releases and news (2018–2022)

### Firm Overview
Tech Mahindra is an India‑based global IT services and consulting firm within the Mahindra Group, providing digital transformation services across telecom, BFSI, manufacturing, and other industries. Between 2017 and 2024, management highlighted enterprise blockchain work as part of its next‑generation offerings, including recognition in Forbes’ Blockchain 50 and multiple client implementations. ([mahindra.com](https://www.mahindra.com/news-room/press-release/en/tech-mahindra-emerges-as-the-only-indian-company-in-the-forbes-blockchain-50-list?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s exposure to crypto‑adjacent activity is through enterprise blockchain consulting, integration, and solutions delivery. Management stated on earnings calls that Tech Mahindra completed in‑production blockchain implementations for a large Indian public‑sector bank and would maintain “extraordinary focus on Blockchain” within its digital portfolio; the firm also joined external partnerships and consortia to scale such solutions. Notable projects include a TRAI‑compliant DLT anti‑spam ecosystem with Microsoft, a blockchain payments/BaaS tie‑up with Quantoz, and a global partnership with BlockApps; Tech Mahindra was also the only Indian firm named to Forbes’ Blockchain 50 in 2021–2022. There is no credible evidence that the company custodies cryptoassets, operates an exchange, mines coins, or holds crypto on its balance sheet. ([insights.techmahindra.com](https://insights.techmahindra.com/investors/Q3-FY18-call-transcript.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
All exposure is assigned to bizserv (1.0). Multiple earnings‑call statements and filings describe blockchain as a systems‑integration and solutions capability for enterprise clients, supported by press‑reported partnerships and case studies across telecom anti‑spam, BFSI, and supply chain use cases. No sources indicate financial intermediation in digital assets, mining operations, specialized mining hardware supply, or treasury crypto holdings, so holders/miners/finserv/infrastr are set to 0.0. ([insights.techmahindra.com](https://insights.techmahindra.com/investors/Q3-FY18-call-transcript.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Blockchain activity is framed broadly as part of digital transformation, and disclosures do not isolate crypto‑related revenue; RevShare is therefore coded as minor (1) based on qualitative evidence rather than explicit segment data. Indian annual reports are less granular than U.S. 10‑Ks, but available documents and press coverage during 2017–2024 consistently portray Tech Mahindra’s role as a service provider that does not take custody of client cryptoassets. ([files.techmahindra.com](https://files.techmahindra.com/cache/investors/Annual-Report-fy18-19.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q3 FY2018 (Dec 31, 2017 quarter): “one of the first system integrators to complete an in‑production BlockChain implementation that inter‑mingles with bank legacy systems for one of the largest public sector banks in India.” — [Tech Mahindra Q3 FY18 transcript (PDF)](https://insights.techmahindra.com/investors/Q3-FY18-call-transcript.pdf) ([insights.techmahindra.com](https://insights.techmahindra.com/investors/Q3-FY18-call-transcript.pdf))
- Q4 FY2018 (Mar 31, 2018): “extraordinary focus on Blockchain… there is no solution which does not go through a filter of… Blockchain.” — [Tech Mahindra Q4 FY18 transcript (PDF)](https://insights.techmahindra.com/investors/Q4-FY18-call-transcript.pdf) ([insights.techmahindra.com](https://insights.techmahindra.com/investors/Q4-FY18-call-transcript.pdf))

**Filings (annual reports / company materials):**
- FY2018–2019 Annual Report: references to building blockchain capabilities and centers of excellence. — [Tech Mahindra Annual Report 2018–19 (PDF)](https://files.techmahindra.com/cache/investors/Annual-Report-fy18-19.pdf) ([files.techmahindra.com](https://files.techmahindra.com/cache/investors/Annual-Report-fy18-19.pdf?utm_source=openai))
- FY2020–2021 Integrated Annual Report: highlights recognition among the Top‑50 global blockchain companies by Forbes and showcases enterprise blockchain case studies. — [Tech Mahindra Integrated Annual Report 2020–21 (PDF)](https://insights.techmahindra.com/investors/annual-report-20-21.pdf) ([insights.techmahindra.com](https://insights.techmahindra.com/investors/annual-report-20-21.pdf?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Aug 27, 2018: Collaboration with Microsoft to build a TRAI‑compliant DLT anti‑spam ecosystem. — [Microsoft News India](https://news.microsoft.com/en-in/tech-mahindra-and-microsoft-enable-a-robust-blockchain-based-ecosystem-to-fight-spam-calls-in-india/) ([news.microsoft.com](https://news.microsoft.com/en-in/tech-mahindra-and-microsoft-enable-a-robust-blockchain-based-ecosystem-to-fight-spam-calls-in-india/?utm_source=openai))
- Aug 7, 2019: Partnership with BlockApps to accelerate blockchain business networks. — [Tech Mahindra/BlockApps release (PDF)](https://cache.techmahindra.com/cache/events/Tech-Mahindra-and-BlockApps-Partner-to-Accelerate-Adoption-of-Blockchain-Business-Networks-Globally.pdf); [PR Newswire coverage](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/blockapps-and-tech-mahindra-partner-to-accelerate-adoption-of-blockchain-business-networks-300898548.html) ([cache.techmahindra.com](https://cache.techmahindra.com/cache/events/Tech-Mahindra-and-BlockApps-Partner-to-Accelerate-Adoption-of-Blockchain-Business-Networks-Globally.pdf?utm_source=openai))
- Oct 25, 2019: Partnership with Quantoz for blockchain‑based payments/BaaS. — [Ledger Insights](https://www.ledgerinsights.com/tech-mahindra-qauntoz-blockchain-payments/) ([ledgerinsights.com](https://www.ledgerinsights.com/tech-mahindra-qauntoz-blockchain-payments/?utm_source=openai))

## HF SINCLAIR

* Rank: 350 | Expo: 0.13 | id_str: US4039491000
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K (FY2024, filed Feb 2025), Q1 2022 and Q3 2023 earnings calls, merger/transaction press releases and Reuters coverage

### Firm Overview
HF Sinclair is a U.S.-based independent energy company formed in March 2022 following HollyFrontier’s acquisition of Sinclair Oil and related assets; it produces and markets gasoline, diesel, jet fuel, renewable diesel, and lubricants, and owns midstream and branded marketing operations primarily across the Southwest, Rocky Mountains, and Pacific Northwest. Its 2024 Form 10-K describes operations spanning refining, renewables, lubricants and specialties, and midstream logistics, with no stated involvement in digital assets. ([d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net](https://d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net/CIK-0001915657/6992bf4a-32d6-4e64-acd5-30b635baf2b3.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
A review of multiple HF Sinclair earnings-call transcripts within 2017–2024 (including Q1 2022 and Q3 2023) shows discussion focused on refining throughput, renewables ramp, lubricants performance, marketing footprint, and HEP logistics, with no references to bitcoin, cryptocurrencies, digital assets, blockchain, mining, custody, or crypto-linked products. The FY2024 Form 10-K (covering the sample period) similarly contains no mentions of “bitcoin,” “crypto,” “cryptocurrency,” “digital asset(s),” or “blockchain,” and outlines only traditional energy and renewables businesses. There is also no credible news or filing evidence that HF Sinclair accepts crypto payments at corporate level, holds crypto on its balance sheet, operates financial services involving crypto, supplies mining-specific infrastructure, or mines digital assets. ([s29.q4cdn.com](https://s29.q4cdn.com/382181944/files/doc_financials/2022/q1/DINO_1Q22-Transcript.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
All categories are assigned 0.0 with unclass = 1.0. Across calls, filings, and major-news coverage in 2017–2024, there is no substantive indication of crypto exposure through treasury holdings, mining, crypto financial intermediation, non-financial crypto services, or mining-focused infrastructure; management emphasis and disclosures remain centered on refining, renewable diesel, lubricants, and logistics segments. ([d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net](https://d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net/CIK-0001915657/6992bf4a-32d6-4e64-acd5-30b635baf2b3.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
HF Sinclair licenses the Sinclair brand to many branded stations; individual site operators could theoretically adopt third‑party crypto payment processors, but no corporate‑level policy or disclosure indicates company participation or exposure, and such franchisee actions would not meet the threshold for firm‑level mechanisms here. None identified beyond that nuance. ([d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net](https://d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net/CIK-0001915657/6992bf4a-32d6-4e64-acd5-30b635baf2b3.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q3 2023 (Nov 2, 2023): “strong third quarter results, driven by… our Refining, Lubricants, HEP and Marketing segments.” — [HF Sinclair Q3 2023 transcript (PDF)](https://s29.q4cdn.com/382181944/files/doc_financials/2023/q3/DINO-Transcript_3Q23-dot.pdf) ([hfsinclair.com IR]) 
- Q1 2022 (May 9, 2022): “closed on the acquisition of the Sinclair Companies… and we made our first sales of renewable diesel from Cheyenne.” — [HF Sinclair Q1 2022 transcript (PDF)](https://s29.q4cdn.com/382181944/files/doc_financials/2022/q1/DINO_1Q22-Transcript.pdf) ([hfsinclair.com IR])
- Q2 2019 (Aug 1, 2019): discussion centers on refining margins, throughput, and lubes (no crypto). — [HollyFrontier Q2 2019 transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/08/01/hollyfrontier-hfc-q2-2019-earnings-call-transcript.aspx) ([The Motley Fool])

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**

- Mar 14–15, 2022: completion of Sinclair Oil combination and establishment of HF Sinclair (ticker DINO). — [Company press release](https://investor.hfsinclair.com/investor-relations/press-releases/press-releases-details/2022/HollyFrontier-and-Holly-Energy-Partners-Announce-Completion-of-Transactions-with-The-Sinclair-Companies-and-Establishment-of-New-Parent-Company-HF-Sinclair-Corporation/default.aspx) ([HF Sinclair IR])

## BITTIUM

* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Annual Report 2023; H1 2024 and FY2023 earnings calls

### Firm Overview
Bittium Oyj is a Finland‑based information technology company listed on Nasdaq Helsinki that develops secure communications and connectivity solutions for defense, public safety and other professional users, alongside medical biosignal monitoring products and engineering services. Its 2023 Annual Report describes three main business segments: Defense & Security, Medical, and Engineering Services. ([bittium.com](https://www.bittium.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/Bittium-AnnualReport-2023-EN-1.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
A review of earnings-call transcripts within the 2017–2024 window shows operational discussions focused on secure mobile communications, tactical radios, medical technology and company strategy, with no references to bitcoin, cryptocurrencies, digital assets, blockchain, mining/custody, or crypto‑linked financial products. For example, the H1 2024 call opens as a standard results press conference, as do the FY2023 and H1 2023 calls, with no crypto content evident in their text snippets. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/BITTIUM-OYJ-1412471/news/Transcript-Bittium-Oyj-H1-2024-Earnings-Call-Aug-09-2024-47609736/))  
The 2023 Annual Report similarly outlines products, markets and financials with no mentions of “bitcoin,” “crypto,” “cryptocurrency,” “digital asset(s),” or “blockchain,” and no indication of treasury holdings, mining operations, crypto financial services, or mining‑focused infrastructure exposure. ([bittium.com](https://www.bittium.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/Bittium-AnnualReport-2023-EN-1.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of any substantive crypto exposure across calls, filings and company materials during January 2017–December 2024, all mechanism categories are set to 0.0 and the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0. Management emphasis and disclosures center on secure communications, tactical networking and medical biosignal solutions rather than digital‑asset activities. ([bittium.com](https://www.bittium.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/Bittium-AnnualReport-2023-EN-1.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Bittium leads a Seamless and Secure Connectivity R&D program that notes blockchain‑based consensus protocols as a possible research mechanism, but this is a broad networking/security research reference rather than evidence of a crypto business line or product; per the classification rules, such exploratory mentions do not meet the threshold for a non‑zero weight. None otherwise identified. ([bittium.com](https://www.bittium.com/about-bittium/technology-innovation/seamless-and-secure-connectivity-program/?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**

## KUDELSKI 'B'

* Rank: 352 | Expo: 0.12 | id_str: CH0012268360
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Annual Reports 2019 and 2023; H1 2018 earnings call; 2019–2020 blockchain security press releases

### Firm Overview
Kudelski SA is a Switzerland‑based digital security and technology group listed on SIX Swiss Exchange, operating through businesses including NAGRA (digital television/content security), Kudelski Security (cybersecurity services), and Kudelski IoT. Company reports describe a focus on digital security solutions and managed cybersecurity services across Europe and the United States. ([nagra.com](https://www.nagra.com/sites/default/files/2024-03/2023_Annual_Report.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Within the 2017–2024 window, the company launched and expanded a Blockchain Security Center that provides code audits, cryptography reviews and security assessments for blockchain projects, exchanges and wallets. The 2019 Annual Report states the team audited “high‑profile cryptocurrency and crypto exchanges including Binance, KZen, Rust, and Zcash,” and announced completion of a security assessment for the Crypto.com exchange. In 2023, Kudelski IoT reported an agreement with Ledger to audit third‑party crypto wallet applications, resulting in 45 audits that year. These activities are service provision around crypto projects without the firm taking custody of digital assets, issuing products, or mining. ([nagra.com](https://www.nagra.com/sites/default/files/migration/2019_Kudelski%20Group_Annual%20Report.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence shows crypto exposure is through non‑financial professional services (security audits, code reviews, assessments) delivered to blockchain/crypto clients. There is no indication of treasury crypto holdings, mining, exchange/custody operations, or mining‑directed infrastructure. Accordingly, 100% of the weight is assigned to bizserv. Given multiple direct company sources and third‑party confirmations across 2019–2023, classification certainty is high. ([nagra.com](https://www.nagra.com/sites/default/files/migration/2019_Kudelski%20Group_Annual%20Report.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Earnings‑call transcripts located do not explicitly discuss blockchain; the classification relies on detailed disclosures in the 2019 and 2023 Annual Reports and contemporaneous press releases. No quantified revenue share from blockchain security is disclosed; RevShare is therefore coded as minor (<~5%) based on qualitative prominence. No evidence indicates crypto assets on balance sheet. ([bulletin.webull.com](https://bulletin.webull.com/20180815/150340574/8968d61293550831badf2c4bed506d9f.pdf))

**Earnings calls:**

## SINGAPORE EXCHANGE

* Rank: 353 | Expo: 0.12 | id_str: SG1J26887955
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: SGX press releases (2020–2021), Annual Report 2023, H1 2018–2019 earnings call transcript (anchor), financial news (2024)

### Firm Overview
Singapore Exchange (SGX Group) is a Singapore-based multi-asset market infrastructure provider offering listing, trading, clearing, settlement, data and index services across equities, derivatives, fixed income and FX. Its investor relations materials describe SGX as Asia’s most international, multi-asset exchange with an expanding indices and data business. ([investorrelations.sgx.com](https://investorrelations.sgx.com/?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Within the 2017–2024 window, SGX expanded its iEdge index franchise to include cryptocurrency benchmarks: on September 1, 2020 it announced the iEdge Bitcoin Index and iEdge Ethereum Index in collaboration with CryptoCompare. These indices were positioned as inputs for financial products in the region. ([links.sgx.com](https://links.sgx.com/1.0.0/corporate-announcements/F4D0MAFH74Y2F26O/20200901_SGX_Index_Edge_to_launch_crypto_indices_in_collaboration_with_CryptoCompare.pdf?utm_source=openai))  
On November 2, 2021, SGX licensed its iEdge Bitcoin Index to Propine, a MAS‑licensed digital asset custodian and Bitcoin ETF service provider, highlighting a role in enabling crypto investment products via index licensing rather than direct asset handling. ([links.sgx.com](https://links.sgx.com/1.0.0/corporate-announcements/2JWGGB441HPJOI81/aac8e1579831eda227c982950411477eeceb37ee1948a853244f7da5ae3c7282?utm_source=openai))  
Separately, SGX and Temasek formed Marketnode in January 2021 to build digital asset infrastructure for capital markets (e.g., tokenised bonds and post‑trade services). While “digital assets” activity is blockchain‑based and adjacent to crypto, it is oriented to tokenised securities rather than cryptocurrencies. 

### Weight Assignment
Evidence shows SGX’s crypto exposure arises through financial services tied to crypto indices and related licensing that can underpin investment products, without SGX taking custody of, mining, or holding cryptocurrencies on its own balance sheet. Accordingly, the classification assigns 100% to finserv. Given multiple primary SGX announcements (2020–2021) and mainstream coverage of the broader digital‑asset JV (2024), certainty is coded as 3; the indices’ contribution to group revenue appears minor, so RevShare = 1 and AssetShare = 0. ([links.sgx.com](https://links.sgx.com/1.0.0/corporate-announcements/F4D0MAFH74Y2F26O/20200901_SGX_Index_Edge_to_launch_crypto_indices_in_collaboration_with_CryptoCompare.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- SGX’s Marketnode activities relate to tokenised capital‑markets workflows and do not, by themselves, imply cryptocurrency exposure; they are treated as non‑crypto adjacent.   
- Public comments in July 2024 indicated SGX did not plan to allow cryptocurrency listings “soon,” limiting direct exchange‑level crypto exposure. ([crypto.news](https://crypto.news/singapores-sgx-exchange-does-not-plan-to-allow-crypto-listings-soon/?utm_source=openai))  
- An earnings‑call transcript was retrieved to satisfy anchoring, but located call text did not specifically discuss crypto; classification relies primarily on SGX announcements and reputable news. Certainty set to 3 accordingly. 

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Apr 20, 2018, Q3 FY2018 results briefing: “Thank you very much for taking the time to attend our third quarter FY 2018 results briefing.” — [AlphaSpread transcript] (https://www.alphaspread.com/security/sgx/s68/investor-relations/earnings-call/q3-2018)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Sep 1, 2020: “SGX Index Edge to launch crypto indices… iEdge Bitcoin Index and iEdge Ethereum Index.” — [SGX announcement PDF] (https://links.sgx.com/1.0.0/corporate-announcements/F4D0MAFH74Y2F26O/20200901_SGX_Index_Edge_to_launch_crypto_indices_in_collaboration_with_CryptoCompare.pdf)
- Nov 2, 2021: “SGX iEdge licenses its Bitcoin index to Propine (MAS‑licensed custodian and Bitcoin ETF service provider).” — [SGX announcement] (https://links.sgx.com/1.0.0/corporate-announcements/2JWGGB441HPJOI81/aac8e1579831eda227c982950411477eeceb37ee1948a853244f7da5ae3c7282)
- Jan 22 & 29, 2021: SGX–Temasek digital‑asset JV “Marketnode” and partnership with Covalent for end‑to‑end fixed income infrastructure. — [SGX announcements] (https://links.sgx.com/1.0.0/corporate-announcements/JQDKY8RHYPAVCHB0/7b30ff72ec0844c4d17e43544205bd8513432730c5918e0af3e9a20e61751d71) (https://links.sgx.com/1.0.0/corporate-announcements/CE089RJIGTGV4CEJ/38239afb85978e9352fd01e827d31d3518cba7f74ea79daa88082f9878ae0ede)
- Annual Report 2023: Notes Marketnode as a digital‑asset issuance JV and describes Gateway/tokenisation solutions. — [SGX AR 2023 PDF] (https://investorrelations.sgx.com/static-files/4c5c96a6-c1a3-42a6-8f80-6fa318a823f5)

**Financial news / other:**
- Jun 3, 2024: “Temasek- and SGX‑backed Marketnode to roll out funds ecosystem (blockchain‑based) as early as June.” — [The Straits Times] (https://www.straitstimes.com/business/temasek-and-sgx-backed-marketnode-to-roll-out-funds-ecosystem-as-early-as-june)
- Jul 9, 2024: “SGX does not plan to allow crypto listings soon; supports iEdge Bitcoin/Ethereum indices.” — [Crypto.news] (https://crypto.news/singapores-sgx-exchange-does-not-plan-to-allow-crypto-listings-soon/)

## SK HYNIX

* Rank: 354 | Expo: 0.12 | id_str: KR7000660001
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.1, unclass 0.9
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q4 2017 earnings call transcript (Jan 25, 2018 anchor), TrendForce DRAM market reports (2018), Reuters (2018), SK hynix annual/IR materials (2018–2020)

### Firm Overview
SK hynix is a South Korea–based global semiconductor manufacturer specializing in dynamic random access memory (DRAM) and NAND flash memory for servers, PCs, mobile devices and graphics. Its investor communications describe a diversified memory portfolio with graphics DRAM as one application segment. ([news.skhynix.com](https://news.skhynix.com/sk-hynix-inc-reports-first-quarter-2018-results/?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Within the 2017–2018 period, cryptocurrency mining temporarily boosted demand for graphics cards, which in turn required additional GDDR memory supplied by firms such as SK hynix. On SK hynix’s January 25, 2018 earnings call, management noted that for GPU‑algorithm mining (e.g., Ethereum at the time) “GPU companies are saying… there would be a demand for about 20% to 30% additional GDDR5 [memory]… approximately 10%” of PC graphics, while ASIC‑based mining used negligible DRAM. ([bulletin.webull.com](https://bulletin.webull.com/20180125/150376768/cafd32ec40b36765fd71534c667d427d.pdf))  
Industry data corroborated that graphics DRAM prices surged nearly 15% quarter‑over‑quarter in 2Q18 “on the back of cryptocurrency‑related demand,” indicating a tangible but transitory pull from mining into the graphics memory market. ([trendforce.com](https://www.trendforce.com/presscenter/news/20180813-10036.html?utm_source=openai))  
Reuters reporting in June 2018 also tied DRAM strength to “servers, gaming PCs and cryptocurrency mining devices,” naming SK hynix among the top DRAM suppliers, further supporting an indirect exposure pathway via component supply. ([business-standard.com](https://www.business-standard.com/article/reuters/china-launches-dram-chip-price-probe-into-samsung-electronics-sk-hynix-and-micron-118060500646_1.html?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence indicates an indirect, upstream exposure through supplying graphics DRAM used in GPUs during mining booms, not through holding tokens, mining coins, or offering financial/crypto services. Because the company’s own commentary framed the effect as incremental to the graphics segment and limited in overall DRAM share, the exposure is classified as infrastructure and sized modestly at 0.1, with the remaining 0.9 assigned to unclass to reflect crypto’s peripheral role in the business model across 2017–2024. RevShare is coded 1 (<~5%) and AssetShare 0, consistent with no crypto assets on balance sheet. ([bulletin.webull.com](https://bulletin.webull.com/20180125/150376768/cafd32ec40b36765fd71534c667d427d.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The 2018 crypto‑mining pull on graphics DRAM was temporary and not sustained; later years’ materials emphasize servers/AI rather than crypto.  
- Direct revenue attribution to crypto is not disclosed; sizing relies on management’s qualitative call remarks and industry pricing data.  
- No evidence of holdings, mining operations, or direct crypto financial services by SK hynix was found in the 2017–2024 window. ([news.skhynix.com](https://news.skhynix.com/sk-hynix-inc-reports-first-quarter-2018-results/?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jan 25, 2018 (Q4 2017 earnings call): “GPU companies are saying… demand for about 20%–30% additional GDDR5… approximately 10% [of PC graphics]. … ASIC… uses… DDR3… share… very small.” — [Thomson Reuters StreetEvents (Webull PDF)](https://bulletin.webull.com/20180125/150376768/cafd32ec40b36765fd71534c667d427d.pdf) ([bulletin.webull.com](https://bulletin.webull.com/20180125/150376768/cafd32ec40b36765fd71534c667d427d.pdf))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**

- 2018–2020 Annual Report (company overview, segment context). — [Korea FSS DART viewer](https://englishdart.fss.or.kr/dsbh002/viewer.do?rcpNo=20210330000776) ([englishdart.fss.or.kr](https://englishdart.fss.or.kr/dsbh002/viewer.do?rcpNo=20210330000776&utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**

## KINGDEE INTL. SOFTWARE GROUP

* Rank: 355 | Expo: 0.12 | id_str: KYG525681477
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.1, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.9
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 2018 IR Newsletter; 2019 earnings call transcript; 2019 Annual Report; Tencent corporate article (2018)

### Firm Overview
Kingdee International Software Group is a Shenzhen‑based enterprise software company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, focused on cloud ERP and related SaaS solutions for Chinese and international enterprises. Its core offerings span financial management, tax, supply chain and broader digital transformation platforms.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Within the 2017–2024 window, Kingdee partnered with the Shenzhen Tax Service and Tencent to launch China’s first blockchain‑based electronic invoice pilot in August 2018, integrating WeChat Pay, invoice issuance and reimbursement workflows. Company investor materials contemporaneously highlighted Kingdee’s role and brand initiative around blockchain applications (e.g., “Confidence Chain”) applied to finance/taxation and other enterprise scenarios. These activities relate to enterprise/blockchain software enablement rather than cryptocurrency custody, trading, or mining. No evidence was found that Kingdee holds crypto assets, runs a crypto exchange, or mines tokens. ([investor.kingdee.com](https://investor.kingdee.com/static/newsEn/November%207%2C%202018__Kingdee%20IR%20Newsletter%203Q%202018.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
The observable linkage to the crypto ecosystem is indirect and software‑oriented: Kingdee supplied enterprise blockchain functionality (not tokenized financial products or mining infrastructure) for invoicing and related enterprise processes. Accordingly, exposure is classified as non‑financial business services (bizserv) at a modest 0.1 weight, with the balance assigned to unclass to reflect crypto’s peripheral role in the company’s overall operations and revenue mix during 2017–2024. Supporting disclosures and third‑party coverage center on blockchain invoicing pilots and product pages, with no trace of treasury holdings or crypto‑financial services. ([investor.kingdee.com](https://investor.kingdee.com/static/newsEn/November%207%2C%202018__Kingdee%20IR%20Newsletter%203Q%202018.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The invoicing initiative uses permissioned blockchain for tax compliance; it does not imply cryptocurrency usage or exposure to token price movements.  
- Kingdee’s filings and calls emphasize cloud ERP/SaaS and do not attribute material revenue to blockchain; sizing therefore treats crypto‑adjacent exposure as minor.  
- At least one earnings call transcript is accessible, but it focuses on results and strategy rather than blockchain/crypto specifics. ([investor.kingdee.com](https://investor.kingdee.com/static/financialReportsEn/Annual%20Report%202019.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Mar 14, 2019 (2018 results): “Revenue grew 21.9%… net profit was up 32.9%.” — [GuruFocus transcript] (https://www.gurufocus.com/news/2320457/full-year-2018-kingdee-international-software-group-co-ltd-earnings-call-transcript) ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/2320457/full-year-2018-kingdee-international-software-group-co-ltd-earnings-call-transcript))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- 2019 Annual Report: business description and segment disclosures (no crypto holdings indicated). — [Kingdee Investor Relations] (https://investor.kingdee.com/static/financialReportsEn/Annual%20Report%202019.pdf) ([investor.kingdee.com](https://investor.kingdee.com/static/financialReportsEn/Annual%20Report%202019.pdf))
- Nov 7, 2018 IR Newsletter (3Q18): “Kingdee and Tencent jointly issue China’s first digital invoice on the Blockchain… full‑process invoice management from WeChat Pay to reimbursement.” — [Kingdee IR] (https://investor.kingdee.com/static/newsEn/November%207%2C%202018__Kingdee%20IR%20Newsletter%203Q%202018.pdf) ([investor.kingdee.com](https://investor.kingdee.com/static/newsEn/November%207%2C%202018__Kingdee%20IR%20Newsletter%203Q%202018.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**
- Aug 2018: “First blockchain e‑invoice in Shenzhen” with Shenzhen Tax Service, Tencent and Kingdee participation. — [Tencent corporate article] (https://www.tencent.com.cn/en-us/articles/2000006.html) ([tencent.com.cn](https://www.tencent.com.cn/en-us/articles/2000006.html?utm_source=openai))
- 2018: Overview of Shenzhen blockchain e‑invoice pilot and partners. — [EEWorld/Leiphone summary] (https://en.eeworld.com.cn/mp/leiphone/a28061.jspx) ([en.eeworld.com.cn](https://en.eeworld.com.cn/mp/leiphone/a28061.jspx?utm_source=openai))
- 2018: Media coverage of the pilot and tech providers. — [The Asset] (https://www.theasset.com/treasury/35181/china-races-ahead-in-blockchain-adoption) ([theasset.com](https://www.theasset.com/treasury/35181/china-races-ahead-in-blockchain-adoption?utm_source=openai))
- Product context: Kingdee Constellation/Tax Cloud mentions blockchain electronic invoice platform integration. — [Kingdee product page] (https://en.kingdee.com/products/taxcloud/) ([en.kingdee.com](https://en.kingdee.com/products/taxcloud/?utm_source=openai))

## BELIEVE DEAD - DELIST.05/08/25

* Rank: 356 | Expo: 0.12 | id_str: FR0014003FE9
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Earnings call transcripts (H1 2021; Q1 2022; H1 2022); Universal Registration Document 2022; FY2022 results press release; Q3 2023 results page

### Firm Overview
Believe SA is a Paris‑based digital music company (Euronext Paris) whose businesses include artist and label services as well as TuneCore’s self‑serve distribution platform; the group focuses on revenue‑sharing deals with major DSPs and technology‑enabled audience development. Its ISIN is FR0014003FE9. ([believe.com](https://www.believe.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/BELIEVE_Universal-registration-document-2022_ENG.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Within the 2017–2024 window, management discussed NFTs conceptually in 2021, describing early experiments and potential for artist monetization, but without announcing a concrete product line or balance‑sheet exposure. Subsequent results communications and regulatory filings center on music distribution, DSP partnerships and pricing, with no evidence of crypto custody, exchange operations, mining, or crypto‑backed financial products. Accordingly, no substantive crypto exposure channel is identified for the firm. ([believe.com](https://www.believe.com/sites/believe/files/2022-03/2021-09-15-Transcript-H1-2021-Results.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Because evidence across earnings calls and filings points to a traditional digital‑music operating model and only exploratory remarks on NFTs, the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). No weights are assigned to holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr, as there is no credible indication that Believe held crypto assets, provided crypto‑financial services, supplied crypto‑specific infrastructure, or derived material revenue from non‑financial services to the crypto ecosystem in 2017–2024. ([believe.com](https://www.believe.com/sites/believe/files/2022-03/2021-09-15-Transcript-H1-2021-Results.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Management indicated in September 2021 that Believe was “working on a few things” around NFTs; no subsequent filings or calls in the sample period substantiate material rollout or revenue contribution from crypto‑related offerings.  
- European mid‑cap coverage means some transcripts are provided as company PDFs rather than third‑party platforms; however, available documents are sufficient to anchor the assessment. ([believe.com](https://www.believe.com/sites/believe/files/2022-03/2021-09-15-Transcript-H1-2021-Results.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Sep 15, 2021 (H1 2021 results): “On the NFTs… we have already done a number of early experiments. This is something where you are going to see us being quite active in the coming months.” — [Believe H1 2021 transcript] (https://www.believe.com/sites/believe/files/2022-03/2021-09-15-Transcript-H1-2021-Results.pdf) ([believe.com](https://www.believe.com/sites/believe/files/2022-03/2021-09-15-Transcript-H1-2021-Results.pdf))
- May 4, 2022 (Q1 2022 revenues): “All of our deals with DSPs are revenue‑share based… Every time they increase subscription, as Amazon just did, we benefit directly.” — [Believe Q1 2022 transcript] (https://www.believe.com/sites/believe/files/2022-05/Believe%20Q1%202022%20Revenues%20-%20Webcast%20transcript%20-%204%20May%202022.pdf) ([believe.com](https://www.believe.com/sites/believe/files/2022-05/Believe%20Q1%202022%20Revenues%20-%20Webcast%20transcript%20-%204%20May%202022.pdf))
- Aug 3, 2022 (H1 2022 results): “With the launch of a new pricing model [for TuneCore] we have been able to see the early signs of accelerated growth.” — [Believe H1 2022 transcript] (https://www.believe.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Believe-First-Half-2022-Results-Transcript-03.08.pdf) ([believe.com](https://www.believe.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Believe-First-Half-2022-Results-Transcript-03.08.pdf))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Universal Registration Document 2022: company business model and segment disclosures (no crypto treasury holdings indicated). — [Believe URD 2022] (https://www.believe.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/BELIEVE_Universal-registration-document-2022_ENG.pdf) ([believe.com](https://www.believe.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/BELIEVE_Universal-registration-document-2022_ENG.pdf))
- Mar 26, 2024: Filing notice for Universal Registration Document 2023. — [GlobeNewswire] (https://www.globenewswire.com/dc/news-release/2024/03/26/2852614/0/en/Regulated-information-Filing-of-the-2023-Universal-registration-document.html) ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/dc/news-release/2024/03/26/2852614/0/en/Regulated-information-Filing-of-the-2023-Universal-registration-document.html))

**Financial news / other:**

## VISLINK TECHNOLOGIES

* Rank: 357 | Expo: 0.12 | id_str: US92836Y4098
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0

* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (FY2021, FY2024); Q1 2024 and Q4 2024 earnings calls; FY2024 results press releases

### Firm Overview
Vislink Technologies is a U.S.-based provider of live video transmission and data communication solutions used by broadcasters, defense, and public safety customers. Its portfolio spans RF, bonded cellular/5G transmitters and receivers, airborne video downlink systems, and related software/services.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024 filings and earnings calls, management describes a business focused on live video acquisition, contribution, and distribution, including 5G and RF systems and the AVDS airborne video downlink category. There is no evidence that Vislink holds cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet, operates a crypto exchange/custody/brokerage, issues crypto‑backed financial products, mines crypto, or supplies crypto‑specific hardware (e.g., ASICs/GPUs) to mining clients. References instead emphasize media, Mil/Gov, and public‑safety video transport and service contracts. ([vislink.com](https://www.vislink.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Vislink-Technologies-Inc-03-05-2025-Form-10-K-December-31-2024-Filed-Version.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Because sources consistently characterize Vislink as a live video communications equipment and services company with no substantive crypto activities, all mechanism categories are set to zero and the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). No investor‑base amplifier is applied, as financial media and calls do not depict the stock as a crypto proxy. ([vislink.com](https://www.vislink.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Vislink-Technologies-Inc-03-05-2025-Form-10-K-December-31-2024-Filed-Version.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Transcript and press‑release coverage is adequate and spans FY2024; however, none indicate crypto‑related operations or assets. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-vislink-technologies-q4-2024-sees-restructuring-93CH-4020025))  
- The company changed its name from xG Technology to Vislink Technologies in 2019, which may affect retrieval of older materials; this does not alter the classification outcome. ([vislink.com](https://www.vislink.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Vislink-Technologies-Inc-03-05-2025-Form-10-K-December-31-2024-Filed-Version.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 2, 2025 (FY2024/Q4 2024), transcript: “multimillion dollar orders and product shipments for our airborne video downlink systems.” — [Investing.com transcript] (https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-vislink-technologies-q4-2024-sees-restructuring-93CH-4020025) ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-vislink-technologies-q4-2024-sees-restructuring-93CH-4020025))
- May 2, 2025 (FY2024/Q4 2024), transcript: “Our bonded cellular technology remains a linchpin... notably for public safety.” — [Investing.com transcript] (https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-vislink-technologies-q4-2024-sees-restructuring-93CH-4020025) ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-vislink-technologies-q4-2024-sees-restructuring-93CH-4020025))
- May 15, 2024 (Q1 2024), transcript: “Service and software revenue [grew] to 16% of total revenue in the first quarter… LinkMatrix software into our hardware offerings.” — [Fintool transcript] (https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/VISL/documents/transcripts/q1-2024) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/VISL/documents/transcripts/q1-2024))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**

- Mar 31, 2022: FY2021 Form 10‑K — company description (broadcast, surveillance/defense focus; no crypto disclosures). — [Vislink 2021 10‑K] (https://www.vislink.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/04/VISL_10_K_2021-Filed-Version.pdf) ([vislink.com](https://www.vislink.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/04/VISL_10_K_2021-Filed-Version.pdf))
