## MANCHESTER UNITED CL.A

* Rank: 358 | Expo: 0.12 | id_str: KYG5784H1065
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.2, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.8
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 20-F annual reports (FY2022–FY2023); Q4 2022 earnings call; Club press releases (2022–2023)

### Firm Overview
Manchester United plc is a UK-based professional football club operator listed on the NYSE that generates revenue from sponsorships, retail/licensing, broadcasting, and matchday activities. Within the 2017–2024 window, crypto-related activity appeared in the commercial segment through a training kit sponsorship with blockchain platform Tezos and the launch of club-branded digital collectibles on Tezos. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/m/NYSE_MANU_2023.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The club entered a multi‑year agreement naming Tezos as its official blockchain and training kit partner in February 2022, a classic advertising/sponsorship arrangement that contributes to commercial revenue. Management also pursued blockchain-enabled fan offerings, including NFTs/digital collectibles issued on Tezos, positioned as engagement products rather than financial services. There is no evidence of crypto held on the balance sheet, mining activity, exchange/custody operations, or crypto‑specific hardware provision. Overall, the exposure is best characterized as non‑financial services to the crypto ecosystem (advertising/brand activation and licensed digital collectibles). ([manutd.com](https://www.manutd.com/en/news/detail/tezos-becomes-official-training-kit-and-blockchain-partner-of-man-utd?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
A small bizserv weight (0.2) is assigned to reflect: (i) the Tezos training‑kit sponsorship (an advertising service provided to a crypto protocol) and (ii) the club’s licensed NFT drops on Tezos. The remaining 0.8 is left as unclass because these activities are peripheral to the core business and there is no evidence of treasury holdings, mining, crypto financial products, or infrastructure provisioning. RevShare is coded 1 given that sponsorship/NFT contributions appear minor relative to total revenue; AssetShare is 0 due to no disclosed crypto assets. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/m/NYSE_MANU_2023.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The Tezos partnership extended beyond 2022 and the digital collectibles program continued through FY2023; however, disclosures do not break out crypto‑specific revenue, so materiality is inferred as minor within commercial revenue. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20231024641888/en/Manchester-United-PLC-Reports-Fourth-Quarter-and-Full-Year-Fiscal-2023-Results))  
- The assessment finds no indication of Manchester United holding crypto on balance sheet or operating any crypto financial service; if such practices existed but were undisclosed, they would not be captured here. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/m/NYSE_MANU_2023.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Sep 22, 2022 (Q4 FY2022), transcript: “In collaboration with our partnership with Tezos, we are exploring a range of blockchain‑enabled fan offerings, including digital collectibles…” — [EarningsCall.ai] (https://www.earningscall.ai/stock/transcript/MANU-2022-Q4) ([earningscall.ai](https://www.earningscall.ai/stock/transcript/MANU-2022-Q4))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- FY2022 Form 20‑F: “During fiscal year 2022, we announced a new training shirt partnership with Tezos… continuing to develop plans to enter the NFT space.” — [Manchester United 20‑F] (https://ir.manutd.com/~/media/Files/M/Manutd-IR/documents/manu-20f-2022-09-24.pdf) ([ir.manutd.com](https://ir.manutd.com/~/media/Files/M/Manutd-IR/documents/manu-20f-2022-09-24.pdf))
- FY2023 Annual Report: “For the 2022/23 season, our shirt sponsor was TeamViewer and our training kit partner was Tezos… Total sponsorship revenue… £189.5 million.” — [AnnualReports.com] (https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/m/NYSE_MANU_2023.pdf) ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/m/NYSE_MANU_2023.pdf))
- FY2023 Annual Report: “In the year ended 30 June 2023, we launched our digital collectibles range [on] Tezos…” — [AnnualReports.com] (https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/m/NYSE_MANU_2023.pdf) ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/m/NYSE_MANU_2023.pdf))
- Q4 FY2023 results release: “During FY23 Club launched 3 phases of its digital collectibles in partnership with Tezos…” — [Business Wire] (https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20231024641888/en/Manchester-United-PLC-Reports-Fourth-Quarter-and-Full-Year-Fiscal-2023-Results) ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20231024641888/en/Manchester-United-PLC-Reports-Fourth-Quarter-and-Full-Year-Fiscal-2023-Results))

**Financial news / other:**

## GOLDMAN SACHS GP.

* Rank: 359 | Expo: 0.12 | id_str: US38141G1040
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings call transcript (Q1 2021); press releases and financial news (2021–2022); HKMA tokenized bond materials (2023)

### Firm Overview
The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. is a U.S.-based global investment banking and financial services firm listed on the NYSE under ticker GS. During 2017–2024, its crypto-related activity centered on client facilitation in markets and selected digital-asset/tokenization initiatives within institutional finance.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Goldman Sachs relaunched a cryptocurrency trading effort in 2021 to meet institutional client demand, initially focusing on CME bitcoin futures and non-deliverable forwards, and subsequently expanded into crypto derivatives. In March 2022 the firm executed its first over-the-counter bitcoin options transaction, facilitated by Galaxy Digital, signaling a further buildout of crypto-related market-making and hedging services for clients. Separately, Goldman developed GS DAP, a digital-asset platform used by the Hong Kong Monetary Authority to issue a tokenized green bond in February 2023, reflecting blockchain-based financial product enablement for institutions. There is no evidence of crypto held on the corporate balance sheet for treasury purposes, no mining operations, and no provision of mining hardware or energy infrastructure. ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2021/03/01/goldman-sachs-restarts-cryptocurrency-desk-amid-bitcoin-boom.html?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv is set to 1.0 because the company’s exposure manifests through client-facing crypto financial services: trading and structuring (CME futures, NDFs, OTC options) and institutional tokenization solutions (GS DAP). Management commentary on the Q1 2021 earnings call acknowledged cryptocurrencies and regulatory limits while emphasizing support for client needs, which aligns with a pure financial-services channel rather than proprietary holding or infrastructure. No substantiated activity fits holders, miners, bizserv, or infrastr under the project’s definitions, so those weights are 0.0. RevShare is coded 1 given that crypto-related revenues appear minor relative to the firm’s diversified business; AssetShare is 0 due to the absence of disclosed crypto assets on balance sheet. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/04/14/goldman-sachs-group-inc-gs-q1-2021-earnings-call-t/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Goldman does not separately disclose a crypto revenue line, so materiality is inferred as minor within Global Banking & Markets. ([thetranscript.net](https://thetranscript.net/transcript/5509/the-goldman-sachs-group-q4-2023-earnings-call-transcript))  
- Tokenization activities (e.g., HKMA’s 2023 issuance via GS DAP) involve blockchain rails for traditional securities; these are classified as finserv here because they entail direct provision of digital-asset financial infrastructure to clients. ([hkma.gov.hk](https://www.hkma.gov.hk/eng/news-and-media/press-releases/2023/02/20230216-3/?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Apr 14, 2021 (Q1 2021), transcript: “I also want to touch on the topics of cryptocurrency, blockchain and the digitization of money… Of course, we need to operate within the current regulatory guidelines. For example, we cannot own Bitcoin or trade it as principal.” — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/04/14/goldman-sachs-group-inc-gs-q1-2021-earnings-call-t/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/04/14/goldman-sachs-group-inc-gs-q1-2021-earnings-call-t/))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Mar 21, 2022: “Galaxy Digital facilitates Goldman Sachs’s first OTC crypto options trade.” — [Galaxy Digital press release](https://www.galaxy.com/newsroom/galaxy-digital-facilitates-goldman-sachss-first-otc-crypto-options-trade) ([galaxy.com](https://www.galaxy.com/newsroom/galaxy-digital-facilitates-goldman-sachss-first-otc-crypto-options-trade?utm_source=openai))
- Feb 16, 2023: “HKSAR Government’s Inaugural Tokenised Green Bond Offering… leveraging Goldman Sachs’ tokenisation platform GS DAP.” — [HKMA press release](https://www.hkma.gov.hk/eng/news-and-media/press-releases/2023/02/20230216-3/) ([hkma.gov.hk](https://www.hkma.gov.hk/eng/news-and-media/press-releases/2023/02/20230216-3/?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Mar 1, 2021: “Goldman Sachs restarts cryptocurrency desk; to begin dealing bitcoin futures and NDFs.” — [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2021/03/01/goldman-sachs-restarts-cryptocurrency-desk-amid-bitcoin-boom.html) ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2021/03/01/goldman-sachs-restarts-cryptocurrency-desk-amid-bitcoin-boom.html?utm_source=openai))
- Jun 18, 2021: “Goldman ramps up trading; Galaxy to act as liquidity provider on CME bitcoin futures.” — [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2021/06/18/bitcoin-goldman-sachs-ramps-up-trading-in-partnership-with-mike-novogratz-galaxy-digital.html) ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2021/06/18/bitcoin-goldman-sachs-ramps-up-trading-in-partnership-with-mike-novogratz-galaxy-digital.html?utm_source=openai))

### Firm Overview
Gold Royalty Corp is a Vancouver-based precious-metals royalty company listed on the NYSE American under ticker GROY. The firm’s 20-F describes a portfolio of 240+ royalties and its strategy of acquiring royalties, streams and similar interests across the mine life cycle; it does not conduct mining operations itself. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1834026/000095017024037537/groy-20231231.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across earnings calls and filings in 2017–2024, there is no indication that the company holds cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet, mines digital assets, provides crypto financial services, or supplies crypto-specific infrastructure. Management commentary focuses on gold royalty cash flows and portfolio growth, with no crypto-related business lines discussed. The 2023 20-F contains no references to “crypto,” “bitcoin,” “blockchain,” or similar terms. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/GROY/documents/transcripts/q3-2023))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of credible, material crypto exposure in transcripts, filings, or news within the sample window, the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights are set to 0.0. This reflects that its business model (gold royalties/streams) is unrelated to digital assets and there is no evidence of balance-sheet holdings, mining, client-facing crypto financial services, or crypto-focused infrastructure supply. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1834026/000095017024037537/groy-20231231.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- None identified within the 2017–2024 sample period; the company listed in 2021 and disclosures/transcripts consistently pertain to gold royalties rather than digital assets. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1834026/000095017024037537/groy-20231231.htm))

## PIONEER POWER SOLUTIONS

* Rank: 361 | Expo: 0.12 | id_str: US7238363003
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2021, 2023); Q3 2021 earnings call; Q4 2023 earnings call

### Firm Overview
Pioneer Power Solutions, Inc. is a U.S.-based provider of electrical power systems and distributed energy solutions listed on Nasdaq as PPSI. Its business includes engineered power distribution equipment, microgrid/DER integration, and mobile EV charging solutions sold to utility, industrial and commercial customers.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across earnings calls (2017–2024) and SEC filings, management focuses on distributed generation, data center and EV-charging use cases; there is no indication of holding cryptocurrencies, mining digital assets, offering crypto financial products, or providing crypto-specific infrastructure. The FY2023 10-K describes the company as designing and integrating electric power systems and mobile EV charging solutions, with no disclosures of digital-asset activities. Q4 2023 remarks emphasize EV charging growth and rising power demand (including AI), not crypto. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1449792/000149315224029269/form10-k.htm))

Pioneer did extend a 2021 distribution agreement with CleanSpark (a firm that subsequently became a prominent bitcoin miner), but the arrangement pertained to microgrid software and Pioneer's PowerBloc solution for EV infrastructure rather than crypto-mining equipment or services. Subsequent press releases and orders highlight EV charging, microgrids, utilities, airports, and data centers as end markets. ([pioneerpowersolutions.com](https://www.pioneerpowersolutions.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Pioneer-2021-10-K-including-Wrap.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of substantive, direct crypto activity in transcripts, filings, or credible news within January 2017–December 2024—and the fact that the CleanSpark link concerns EV/microgrid solutions rather than mining—the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism weights at 0.0. This reflects no evidence of balance-sheet crypto holdings, mining operations, crypto financial services, crypto-targeted business services, or infrastructure purpose-built for miners. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1449792/000149315224029269/form10-k.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Adjacency via CleanSpark (a bitcoin miner) could create indirect exposure if Pioneer's solutions were later marketed to miners; however, available 2017–2024 disclosures tie the relationship to EV charging/microgrids, not crypto workloads. No earnings-call or filing evidence indicates crypto-directed products or clients in the sample window. ([pioneerpowersolutions.com](https://www.pioneerpowersolutions.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Pioneer-2021-10-K-including-Wrap.pdf))

### Evidence

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 16, 2021 (Q3 2021): “strategic shift to… products and services that directly respond to the rapidly growing distributed generation and electric vehicle charging markets.” — [Pioneer IR PDF](https://www.pioneerpowersolutions.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/11/PPSI-3Q-2021-Earnings-Call-Transcription.pdf) ([pioneerpowersolutions.com](https://www.pioneerpowersolutions.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/11/PPSI-3Q-2021-Earnings-Call-Transcription.pdf))
- Apr 1, 2024 (Q4 2023): “Demand for electric power is growing rapidly… Our products specifically support, harden, and accelerate these efforts.” — [Fintool transcript](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/PPSI/documents/transcripts/q4-2023) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/PPSI/documents/transcripts/q4-2023))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**

- 2021 10-K (company PDF): “we entered into a Distribution Agreement with CleanSpark… [to] serve as our exclusive distributor of the Products… through Dec 31, 2023.” — [Pioneer 2021 10-K](https://www.pioneerpowersolutions.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Pioneer-2021-10-K-including-Wrap.pdf) ([pioneerpowersolutions.com](https://www.pioneerpowersolutions.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Pioneer-2021-10-K-including-Wrap.pdf))

## GRINDROD

* Rank: 362 | Expo: 0.12 | id_str: ZAE000072328

* Primary sources: Press releases (2023–2024), Integrated Annual Report (2022), MarketScreener earnings-call listings (2024), South African Competition Tribunal ruling (2022), Bloomberg/Business Day news (2024)

### Firm Overview
Grindrod Limited is a JSE-listed South African freight logistics company focused on ports and terminals, rail, and integrated logistics in Southern Africa, notably along the Maputo corridor. Its activities center on moving bulk commodities such as chrome, coal and magnetite through terminals and related logistics operations.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across earnings calls and company disclosures in 2023–2024, management discusses terminal throughput, port concessions and rail strategy; there is no indication of holding cryptocurrencies, mining digital assets, or offering crypto financial products. FY2023 results communications emphasize port volumes and integrated logistics, not digital-asset activities. ([grindrod.com](https://www.grindrod.com/press-release/30202-grindrod-delivers-strong-results-buoyed-by-operational-resilience?utm_source=openai))  
Interim 2024 disclosures likewise focus on Maputo and South African dry-bulk terminals, ships agency, and clearing and forwarding performance with no crypto references. ([grindrod.com](https://www.grindrod.com/press-release/30204-grindrod-limited-announces-its-interim-financial-results-for-the-six-months-ended-30-june-2024?utm_source=openai))  
The group exited direct financial-services exposure by selling Grindrod Bank to African Bank (approved in 2022 and referenced in 2024 press), further reducing any pathway to crypto-financial offerings. ([comptrib.co.za](https://www.comptrib.co.za/info-library/press-room/merger-alert-outcome-of-mergers-decided-by-the-tribunal-23-september-2022?utm_source=openai))  
Contemporaneous media coverage in 2024 centers on port concessions and rail capacity upgrades at Maputo, consistent with a traditional logistics profile rather than crypto-related operations. ([bloomberg.com](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2024-01-23/dp-world-s-2-billion-mozambique-port-expansion-gets-approval?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of substantive, direct crypto activity in transcripts, filings, or credible news within January 2017–December 2024—and with communications focused on ports, terminals, and rail logistics rather than digital assets—the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism weights at 0.0. This reflects no evidence of balance-sheet crypto holdings, mining operations, crypto financial services, crypto-targeted business services, or infrastructure purpose-built for miners. ([grindrod.com](https://www.grindrod.com/press-release/30202-grindrod-delivers-strong-results-buoyed-by-operational-resilience?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Formal earnings-call transcripts for 2024 are cataloged by MarketScreener; only partial text is visible without subscription, but available snippets and surrounding materials contain no crypto mentions. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/GRINDROD-LIMITED-3743309/news/Transcript-Grindrod-Limited-H1-2024-Earnings-Call-Aug-23-2024-47721433/))  
- South African logistics operators could theoretically service crypto-mining equipment or related cargo flows; however, no disclosures indicate such activity in 2017–2024. None identified beyond this general possibility.

## CGI A

* Rank: 363 | Expo: 0.12 | id_str: CA12532H1047

* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: SEC Form 40-F filings (FY2023–FY2024), Q2 2024 earnings call transcript, company press releases

### Firm Overview
CGI Inc. is a Montreal-based global IT and business consulting firm listed on the TSX (GIB.A) and NYSE (GIB). It provides systems integration, managed services and proprietary IP-based solutions to clients across government and commercial sectors worldwide.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024, CGI’s public filings and earnings discussions focus on traditional IT services (managed services, systems integration, and IP-based software) with no indication of holding cryptocurrencies on balance sheet, mining digital assets, or offering crypto financial products. The FY2024 and FY2023 Form 40-F filings detail revenue segments, backlog and risk management without references to crypto, bitcoin or blockchain, supporting an absence of balance-sheet or product-level crypto exposure. ([cgi.com](https://www.cgi.com/sites/default/files/2024-12/cgi-2024-sec-form-40-f.pdf))  
Representative transcripts in 2024 emphasize bookings, backlog, margins and AI-enabled offerings; they do not discuss crypto activities. ([strike.market](https://strike.market/stocks/GIB/earnings-call-transcripts/264402))  
CGI does market enterprise blockchain consulting and ran a 2017 interoperability initiative between its Trade360 platform and Skuchain’s blockchain, but these are enterprise DLT use cases and do not involve custody or issuance of crypto assets. ([cgi.com](https://www.cgi.com/en/2017-10-16-CGI-and-its-Bank-partners-achieve-ground-breaking-interoperability-between-CGI-Trade360-and-Skuchains-blockchain-platform?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Given the lack of substantive, direct crypto involvement in filings, earnings calls, or credible news within January 2017–December 2024—and with any blockchain activity limited to enterprise DLT consulting rather than crypto-asset products—CGI is classified as unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism weights at 0.0. This reflects no evidence of treasury crypto holdings, mining, crypto financial services, crypto-focused business services, or mining-directed infrastructure. ([cgi.com](https://www.cgi.com/sites/default/files/2024-12/cgi-2024-sec-form-40-f.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- CGI’s historical marketing materials reference blockchain consulting and proofs-of-concept (e.g., Trade360 with Skuchain), which could theoretically serve crypto-native clients; however, no disclosures indicate material revenue, asset holdings, or recurring activity tied to cryptocurrencies in 2017–2024. ([cgi.com](https://www.cgi.com/en/2017-10-16-CGI-and-its-Bank-partners-achieve-ground-breaking-interoperability-between-CGI-Trade360-and-Skuchains-blockchain-platform?utm_source=openai))  
- Some detailed transcript repositories are paywalled; accessible 2024 transcripts reviewed show no crypto references.

**Earnings calls:**
- May 1, 2024 (Q2 FY2024), transcript: “Good morning, ladies and gentlemen. Welcome to CGI's Second Quarter Fiscal 2024 Conference Call.” — [Strike.Market transcript] (https://strike.market/stocks/GIB/earnings-call-transcripts/264402)

* Primary sources: SEC filings (Form 20-F/6-K, 2022–2024), Q3 2023 earnings call transcript, company press releases and IR presentations (2023–2024)

### Firm Overview
CI&T Inc. is a Brazil-founded, NYSE-listed IT and software engineering services company that provides digital transformation, AI, design and development services to large enterprises across the Americas, Europe and APAC. Its business model is services-led and focused on enterprise technology programs rather than proprietary financial or trading activities.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024, CI&T’s public materials emphasize enterprise software and AI-enabled transformation; there is no evidence the company holds cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet, operates mining, or offers crypto financial products. The Q3 2023 earnings call centers on revenue, client mix and AI initiatives with no crypto-asset discussion. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/CINT/documents/transcripts/q3-2023))  
Regulatory filings over 2022–2024 describe CI&T as a global digital/technology transformation specialist and document accounting restatements and operating metrics, without indicating crypto holdings, mining activities, custody, exchanges, or other crypto-financial services. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1868995/000155485524000218/MainDocument.htm))  
CI&T marketing content occasionally references blockchain/Web3 conceptually, but these are high-level thought‑leadership notes rather than evidence of material crypto-asset services or custody. ([ciandt.com](https://ciandt.com/br/pt-br/article/blockchain-como-ele-vai-impactar-sua-empresa-e-seu-modelo-de-negocios?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of substantive crypto exposure in transcripts, filings and result announcements during January 2017–December 2024—and with any blockchain mentions limited to general enterprise/marketing content rather than crypto-asset activity—the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0. No weights are assigned to holders, miners, finserv, bizserv or infrastr because there is no documented balance-sheet holding, mining operation, crypto financial product, crypto-focused service line, or mining‑directed infrastructure business. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/CINT/documents/transcripts/q3-2023))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Some transcript repositories (e.g., Seeking Alpha) are paywalled; accessible Q3 2023 materials were reviewed and showed no crypto references. ([seekingalpha.com](https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CINT/earnings/transcripts?utm_source=openai))  
- A legacy article on CI&T’s site discusses blockchain conceptually; no filings or calls indicate material revenue or assets linked to cryptocurrencies. ([ciandt.com](https://ciandt.com/br/pt-br/article/blockchain-como-ele-vai-impactar-sua-empresa-e-seu-modelo-de-negocios?utm_source=openai))

* Primary sources: SEC filings (Form 40-F, FY2023–FY2024), Annual Information Form (FY2023), Sprott press releases (2024), Sprott Inc. Q2 2024 earnings call

### Firm Overview
Sprott Physical Gold Trust is a Toronto-based, NYSE Arca/TSX‑listed closed‑end mutual fund trust managed by Sprott Asset Management LP. Its stated mandate is to invest and hold substantially all assets in physical gold bullion, with units redeemable for bullion.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024, the trust’s regulatory filings and investor materials consistently describe a single‑metal mandate focused on physical gold bullion; for example, FY2024 filings detail the ounces of physical gold held and related unrealized/realized gains, with no reference to digital assets. There is no evidence of cryptocurrency treasury holdings, mining operations, or crypto financial services such as custody, exchange, or tokenized products. Sprott Inc. earnings calls discuss flows into Sprott’s precious‑metals physical trusts broadly, but do not indicate any crypto‑asset activities at the gold trust. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1477049/000199937125002923/ex99-6.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Because the entity is a single‑purpose vehicle that holds allocated physical gold and does not touch crypto assets operationally or financially, no crypto‑exposure mechanism applies. Accordingly, the classification assigns unclass = 1.0 and 0.0 to holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, and infrastr. This is supported by the trust’s FY2023–FY2024 filings and 2024 press disclosures stating its mandate and use of proceeds strictly for acquiring physical gold bullion. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1477049/000199937125002923/ex99-6.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The trust does not host standalone earnings calls; relevant commentary appears on Sprott Inc. calls at the manager level and is not crypto‑related. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-sprott-inc-highlights-robust-aum-growth-and-new-copper-fund-93CH-3564052))  
- No indications were found of any crypto‑asset holdings or services during 2017–2024; monitoring is warranted only if Sprott were to launch a separate crypto product, which would be outside this entity’s gold‑only mandate. ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2024/12/07/2993306/0/en/Sprott-Physical-Gold-Trust-Updates-Its-At-the-Market-Equity-Program.html?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 7, 2024 (Q2 2024, Sprott Inc.), transcript summary: “we benefited from market value appreciation in our precious metals physical trusts.” — [Investing.com] (https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-sprott-inc-highlights-robust-aum-growth-and-new-copper-fund-93CH-3564052)

- Mar 25, 2024: Annual Information Form (FY2023) — states the Trust was created to invest and hold substantially all assets in physical gold bullion. — [Sprott AIF PDF] (https://www.sprottwealth.com/media/7022/phys-q4-2023-aif.pdf)  
- Dec 31, 2023: Annual financial statements — reports unrealized gains on physical gold bullion of $711.2 million for 2023. — [Sprott report PDF] (https://www.sprottusa.com/media/7021/phys-q4-2023.pdf)

### Firm Overview
Chow Tai Fook Jewellery Group Limited is a Hong Kong–headquartered jewellery retailer listed on the HKEX, operating an extensive store network across Mainland China, Hong Kong and selected overseas markets. The FY2024 annual report describes a core business focused on gem‑set jewellery, gold products and related retail operations. ([ctfjewellerygroup.com](https://www.ctfjewellerygroup.com/content/dam/ctfjewellerygroup/ir/report/arir/ar2024_en.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024, no evidence was found that the company held cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet, engaged in mining, offered crypto‑financial services (exchange, custody, tokenized products), or accepted crypto payments; listed payment options emphasize cards and local e‑wallets (e.g., WeChat Pay/Alipay). ([chowtaifook.com](https://www.chowtaifook.com/en-hk/customer-service/payment/?utm_source=openai))  
In 2018 the group collaborated with GIA and Everledger to place diamond grading information for its T MARK diamonds on a permissioned blockchain built on IBM’s platform—an internal supply‑chain/traceability application rather than a digital‑asset activity. Subsequent coverage and case studies reiterate this blockchain‑for‑traceability use case and do not indicate crypto‑asset custody or exposure. ([gia.edu](https://www.gia.edu/gia-news-press/chow-tai-fook-gia-blockchain?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Because Chow Tai Fook’s activities during 2017–2024 reflect jewellery retailing and supply‑chain digitization without transacting in, holding, or providing services for crypto assets, none of the mechanism categories apply. The classification therefore assigns unclass = 1.0 and 0.0 to holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, and infrastr, based on company filings and public materials describing retail operations and the limited, non‑asset blockchain initiative with GIA/Everledger. ([ctfjewellerygroup.com](https://www.ctfjewellerygroup.com/content/dam/ctfjewellerygroup/ir/report/arir/ar2024_en.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Transcript content is typically distributed via third‑party portals that may require login; listings confirm regular earnings calls, but no crypto references were identified in accessible summaries. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/CHOW-TAI-FOOK-JEWELLERY-G-9604011/news-call-transcripts/))  
- The 2018 blockchain project could be misconstrued as “crypto” exposure; it is a provenance/traceability ledger and does not involve crypto‑asset holdings or customer crypto transactions. ([gia.edu](https://www.gia.edu/gia-news-press/chow-tai-fook-gia-blockchain?utm_source=openai))

## APPEN

* Rank: 367 | Expo: 0.12 | id_str: AU000000APX3
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: Annual reports/ASX filings (2021–2022), acquisition press release (2021), FY2020 earnings call transcript

### Firm Overview
Appen Limited is an Australia‑headquartered provider of AI training data and model evaluation services listed on the ASX, serving large technology customers globally. In September 2021, it acquired Quadrant Global Pte Ltd, a Singapore‑based provider of mobile location/POI data whose platforms include a blockchain‑enabled consent system and the Geolancer data‑collection app. ([announcements.asx.com.au](https://announcements.asx.com.au/asxpdf/20220224/pdf/456bjczfhh2wzh.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The crypto‑relevant exposure arises through Quadrant. Appen’s 2021 and 2022 reports disclose that Quadrant holds cryptocurrency assets (e.g., Ethereum and USDC) used to pay Geolancer contributors and suppliers, and that eQUAD utility tokens issued pre‑acquisition were recognized as deferred revenue representing future service obligations; crypto assets were classified as inventory at the lower of cost and NRV. ([announcements.asx.com.au](https://announcements.asx.com.au/asxpdf/20220224/pdf/456bjczfhh2wzh.pdf))  
Appen’s press release for the Quadrant deal highlighted a blockchain‑based consent management platform; Quadrant Protocol materials describe eQUAD’s role in platform economics and licensing flows. Together, these indicate the company directly handles and settles certain operational payments in crypto rather than merely using “blockchain for record‑keeping.” ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20210825005844/en/Appen-to-Acquire-Quadrant-to-Expand-Mobile-Location-Based-Data-Collection-Offering?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence shows direct handling of digital assets and token‑based obligations within Quadrant’s operations, which best maps to finserv (crypto payments/handling). There is no indication of proprietary treasury HODL, mining, dedicated crypto‑financial products for investors, or crypto‑specific hardware/hosting. Consequently, finserv is set to 1.0, with other mechanisms at 0.0. The reports characterize cryptocurrency balances as immaterial and operational (inventory), supporting no allocation to holders. ([announcements.asx.com.au](https://announcements.asx.com.au/asxpdf/20220224/pdf/456bjczfhh2wzh.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Earnings‑call coverage accessible without subscriptions is limited; the available FY2020 call anchors the business description but predates the Quadrant acquisition, so most crypto specifics come from filings and press materials. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/APXYY/transcripts/2020/4))  
- Management characterizes Quadrant’s cryptocurrency balances as immaterial and operational, so crypto’s economic significance to consolidated results appears small within 2017–2024. ([announcements.asx.com.au](https://announcements.asx.com.au/asxpdf/20220224/pdf/456bjczfhh2wzh.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 24, 2021 (FY2020 results): “Thank you for standing by and welcome to the Appen Limited FY 2020 full year results conference call… We have a long track record and considerable expertise in speech and natural language data…” — [Roic AI transcript] (https://www.roic.ai/quote/APXYY/transcripts/2020/4)  
- Feb 27, 2023 (FY2022 results) — transcript listing — [MarketScreener] (https://ch.marketscreener.com/kurs/aktie/APPEN-LIMITED-19777560/news/Transcript-Appen-Limited-2022-Earnings-Call-Feb-27-2023-43096287/)

### Exposure Channels Identified
Searches across earnings call transcripts (2019–2021), SEC filings (Form 10-Ks through year-end 2023) and company press releases revealed no substantive references to cryptocurrency, digital assets, blockchain activities, mining, custody, exchange operations, or crypto‑related payment flows. No balance‑sheet or revenue disclosures suggest crypto holdings or crypto‑linked products/services within the 2017–2024 sample window.

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of any credible, material crypto linkages in filings, calls, or news during 2017–2024, the firm is assigned unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism weights at 0.0. There is also no evidence that the stock trades as a crypto proxy with a crypto‑focused investor base, so invbase = 0.

None identified. Routine cybersecurity risk disclosures are present in filings, but there are no indications of crypto usage or exposure.

## BK TECHNOLOGIES

* Rank: 370 | Expo: 0.12 | id_str: US05587G2030
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2024), Q3 2024 earnings call, company press releases (2022–2024)

### Firm Overview
BK Technologies Corporation is a U.S.-based manufacturer of land mobile radio communications equipment for public safety and government customers, with products such as the BKR 5000/9000 series and related services. The company has also introduced InteropONE, a push-to-talk-over-cellular software service, as part of a SaaS initiative alongside its core radio hardware business. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/2186/000143774925009464/bkti20241231_10k.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across the 2017–2024 window, the firm’s activities center on public-safety radios and communications software, not digital assets. A keyword search of the Form 10‑K for the year ended December 31, 2024 shows no instances of “crypto,” “bitcoin,” or “blockchain,” and disclosures describe traditional communications products, supply chain, inventory, and financing arrangements. Recent earnings-call discussion in 2024 highlights radio sales backlog, margins, and the BKR 5000/9000 product lines, with no references to mining, exchanges, custody, or digital‑asset payments. InteropONE is described as a push‑to‑talk‑over‑cellular service to enhance radio interoperability, not a crypto-related product. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/2186/000143774925009464/bkti20241231_10k.htm))

### Weight Assignment
With no evidence of balance‑sheet crypto holdings, mining operations, crypto financial services, crypto‑specific infrastructure, or non‑financial services to the crypto ecosystem, the firm is assigned unclass = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. There is likewise no indication that the stock has traded as a crypto proxy or that management identifies a crypto‑oriented investor base; therefore invbase = 0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/2186/000143774925009464/bkti20241231_10k.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified. Routine cybersecurity and technology risk disclosures in filings do not imply crypto involvement. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/2186/000143774925009464/bkti20241231_10k.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 14, 2024 (Q3 2024): “...backlog remains strong at $27 million supported by solid order activity for our BKR 5,000 and BKR 9,000 radios...” — GuruFocus transcript (edited Refinitiv) — [GuruFocus](https://www.gurufocus.com/stock/BKTI/transcripts/2605112)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Mar 27, 2025 (Form 10‑K for year ended Dec 31, 2024): company description and risk factors; no “crypto/bitcoin/blockchain” mentions on keyword search — [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/2186/000143774925009464/bkti20241231_10k.htm)
- Sep 5, 2024: BKR 9000 receives NIFC approval; overview of radios and SaaS initiative — [BK Technologies IR](https://investors.bktechnologies.com/pr/bkr-9000-multiband-radio-receives-approval-from-the-national-interagency-fire-center)
- Oct 11, 2022: InteropONE push‑to‑talk‑over‑cellular service launch — [BK Technologies IR](https://investors.bktechnologies.com/pr/bk-technologies-launches-interopone)

**Financial news / other:**
- Oct 3, 2023: Sandoval County purchase order for BKR 9000 radios — [BK Technologies IR](https://investors.bktechnologies.com/pr/bk-technologies-receives-purchase-order-from-sandoval-county-new-mexico-for-bkr-9000)
- Aug 2024: Investor presentation overview of InteropONE/LMR focus — [BK Technologies](https://www.bktechnologies.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/BK-Investor-Presentation-August-2024-v1.pdf)

## STATE STREET

* Rank: 371 | Expo: 0.11 | id_str: US8574771031
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 2023 Annual Report; Q4 2020 earnings call; company press releases (2021–2022); financial news (2023)

### Firm Overview
State Street Corporation is a U.S.-based global custodian and asset manager listed on the NYSE (ticker STT), providing investment servicing, investment management, and data/analytics solutions to institutional clients worldwide. In 2021 it established “State Street Digital” to focus on services tied to digital assets and related technologies such as blockchain, tokenization, cryptocurrency and CBDCs. ([Annual Report 2023]). ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/s/NYSE_STT_2023.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s crypto linkage is through financial services: administration/transfer agency for crypto‑linked funds and the build‑out of institutional digital‑asset custody and servicing capabilities. In March 2021, State Street was named fund administrator and transfer agent for VanEck’s proposed Bitcoin Trust ETF, and in 2022 it announced a licensing agreement with Copper.co to develop institutional digital‑asset custody (later ended in 2023 as the regulatory environment evolved). These activities align with the finserv mechanism, rather than holding crypto on balance sheet or providing mining/infrastructure. Management has also discussed client interest in digital currencies creating custody opportunities. ([investors.statestreet.com](https://investors.statestreet.com/investor-news-events/press-releases/news-details/2021/State-Street-Named-Fund-Administrator-and-Transfer-Agent-for-VanEcks-Bitcoin-Trust-ETF-03-02-2021/default.aspx?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence across filings, press releases and an earnings‑call exchange indicates State Street’s exposure to crypto is as a financial‑services provider (fund administration/transfer agency for Bitcoin products and development of digital‑asset custody/servicing). There is no indication of proprietary crypto holdings or mining, and technology initiatives (e.g., blockchain FX workflows) are ancillary to its financial‑services role. Therefore, finserv = 1.0, all other mechanisms = 0.0. Given at least one transcript quote plus corroborating filings/news, certainty is set to 3. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/s/NYSE_STT_2023.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
U.S. digital‑asset custody remained subject to regulatory approvals during 2022–2024 and the Copper licensing agreement was terminated in March 2023, so realized revenues from digital‑asset custody in that window appear limited; RevShare is coded minor (1) on a precautionary basis. Some roles (e.g., ETF administration) depend on product approvals and mandates that can change over time; no evidence of balance‑sheet crypto assets was found. ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2023/03/16/banking-giant-state-street-cuts-ties-with-crypto-custody-firm-copper?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jan 19, 2021 (Q4 2020): “Anything that stimulates more interest in blockchain and particularly … digital currency is going to create a custody opportunity for us… we see digital coins, cryptocurrency as part of the holdings within our client base.” — Fintool transcript — [Fintool](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/STT/documents/transcripts/q4-2020) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/STT/documents/transcripts/q4-2020))

**Filings (10‑K / annual report / company materials):**
- 2023 Annual Report: “In 2021, we established State Street Digital to focus on the development of services related to digital assets and related technologies, such as blockchain, tokenization, cryptocurrency…” — [AnnualReports.com PDF](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/s/NYSE_STT_2023.pdf) ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/s/NYSE_STT_2023.pdf))
- Jul 16, 2021: Q2 2021 earnings press release noting the formation of State Street Digital — [State Street IR PDF](https://s203.q4cdn.com/888565246/files/doc_events/2021/07/16/STT-2Q21-Earnings-Press-Release.pdf) ([s203.q4cdn.com](https://s203.q4cdn.com/888565246/files/doc_events/2021/07/16/STT-2Q21-Earnings-Press-Release.pdf?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Mar 2, 2021: “State Street Named Fund Administrator and Transfer Agent for VanEck’s Bitcoin Trust ETF” — [State Street press release](https://investors.statestreet.com/investor-news-events/press-releases/news-details/2021/State-Street-Named-Fund-Administrator-and-Transfer-Agent-for-VanEcks-Bitcoin-Trust-ETF-03-02-2021/default.aspx) ([investors.statestreet.com](https://investors.statestreet.com/investor-news-events/press-releases/news-details/2021/State-Street-Named-Fund-Administrator-and-Transfer-Agent-for-VanEcks-Bitcoin-Trust-ETF-03-02-2021/default.aspx?utm_source=openai))
- Jun 10, 2021: “State Street Launches New Digital Unit To Take On $1.5 Trillion Crypto Market” — [Institutional Investor](https://www.institutionalinvestor.com/article/2bswspfidykkxf4a78p34/portfolio/state-street-launches-new-digital-unit-to-take-on-1-5-trillion-crypto-market) ([institutionalinvestor.com](https://www.institutionalinvestor.com/article/2bswspfidykkxf4a78p34/portfolio/state-street-launches-new-digital-unit-to-take-on-1-5-trillion-crypto-market?utm_source=openai))
- Mar 9, 2022: “State Street to Develop Digital Custody in Collaboration with Copper.co” — [State Street press release](https://investors.statestreet.com/investor-news-events/press-releases/news-details/2022/State-Street-to-Develop-Digital-Custody-in-Collaboration-with-Copper.co-03-09-2022/default.aspx) ([investors.statestreet.com](https://investors.statestreet.com/investor-news-events/press-releases/news-details/2022/State-Street-to-Develop-Digital-Custody-in-Collaboration-with-Copper.co-03-09-2022/default.aspx?utm_source=openai))

## CONCENTRIX

* Rank: 372 | Expo: 0.11 | id_str: US20602D1019
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Company case studies (2021–2023), product/awards press releases (2023–2024), 2023 10-K filing, Q1 2024 earnings call

### Firm Overview
Concentrix Corporation is a global provider of customer experience solutions and technology, serving enterprise clients across multiple verticals including technology, retail, travel and e-commerce, and banking, financial services and insurance. Its business model centers on outsourced CX operations, digital operations, analytics, and enterprise technology integration rather than financial intermediation or asset ownership. ([s21.q4cdn.com](https://s21.q4cdn.com/257053467/files/doc_financials/2023/ar/2023-Form-10-K-FINAL.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Evidence from Concentrix’s own client case studies shows it provides non-financial services to crypto ecosystem companies, such as rapidly scaling multilingual customer support and compliance-aware operations for a digital crypto exchange platform. A separate case study describes end-to-end CX operations spanning acquisition, onboarding, and support for a client in the “crypto economy.” These are classic business services around the crypto ecosystem without custodying assets or providing financial products. ([concentrix.com](https://www.concentrix.com/insights/case-studies/pioneering-support-crypto-economy/?utm_source=openai))  
Concentrix also markets CNXWallet, a Web3 wallet management software offering positioned for enterprise governance and customer experience, and highlighted in awards and company news; this suggests software enablement rather than Concentrix itself taking custody of client crypto assets. The 2023 10‑K contains no crypto balance‑sheet holdings and reads as a CX services business. ([concentrix.com](https://www.concentrix.com/about/news/press-release/concentrix-webhelp-recognized-for-technology-innovation/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
All identified crypto exposure arises from providing CX/BPO and technology enablement to crypto-native clients (exchanges, wallets) and from offering a wallet management software product, with no indication of proprietary crypto holdings, mining, or direct financial intermediation. Under the definitions, this maps to bizserv rather than finserv, holders, miners, or infrastr. Therefore, bizserv = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. Given the absence of crypto-specific discussion on earnings calls and reliance on company materials, certainty is set to 2.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
No crypto-specific references were found in reviewed earnings call transcripts; classification relies on company case studies and product/awards materials, so certainty is capped at 2 per project rules. The CNXWallet positioning references “custody and governance,” but available descriptions indicate an enterprise wallet management solution rather than Concentrix custodying client assets; realized crypto-attributable revenue is not broken out, so RevShare is coded minor (1) on a precautionary basis. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/CNXC/documents/transcripts/q1-2024))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Mar 26, 2024 (Q1 2024): “Our growth reflects the differentiated offerings across our key verticals… and banking and financial services.” — Fintool transcript — [Fintool](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/CNXC/documents/transcripts/q1-2024) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/CNXC/documents/transcripts/q1-2024))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- FY 2023 Form 10-K: Company described as a CX solutions and technology provider across several verticals; no crypto asset holdings disclosed. — [Concentrix 2023 10-K PDF](https://s21.q4cdn.com/257053467/files/doc_financials/2023/ar/2023-Form-10-K-FINAL.pdf) ([s21.q4cdn.com](https://s21.q4cdn.com/257053467/files/doc_financials/2023/ar/2023-Form-10-K-FINAL.pdf))
- Jun 6, 2024: Awards press release highlighting CNXWallet (Web3 wallet management solution) among winning technologies. — [Concentrix press release](https://www.concentrix.com/about/news/press-release/concentrix-wins-five-globee-awards-for-customer-excellence/) 
- 2023 Merit Awards note recognizing the Concentrix Web3 Wallet (CNXW). — [Concentrix news item](https://www.concentrix.com/about/news/press-release/concentrix-webhelp-recognized-for-technology-innovation/) ([concentrix.com](https://www.concentrix.com/about/news/press-release/concentrix-webhelp-recognized-for-technology-innovation/?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Sep 15, 2023: Case study detailing rapid scale-up of 24/7 global support for a crypto exchange platform. — [Concentrix case study](https://www.concentrix.com/insights/case-studies/supporting-the-crypto-surge-with-strategic-brand-partnership/) 
- 2021–2022: Case study describing end-to-end CX operations for a client in the crypto economy (onboarding through retention). — [Concentrix case study](https://www.concentrix.com/insights/case-studies/pioneering-support-crypto-economy/) ([concentrix.com](https://www.concentrix.com/insights/case-studies/pioneering-support-crypto-economy/?utm_source=openai))
- Investor materials include a “Global Leading Crypto Wallet/Exchange” case reference. — [Investor presentation excerpt](https://ir.concentrix.com/static-files/f7f81cfa-e71c-4593-b359-8e8dc0811107) ([ir.concentrix.com](https://ir.concentrix.com/static-files/f7f81cfa-e71c-4593-b359-8e8dc0811107?utm_source=openai))

## PROSEGUR CASH

* Rank: 373 | Expo: 0.11 | id_str: ES0105229001
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Company press releases (2021–2024), 2022 Annual Accounts (CNMV filing), Q3 2021 earnings call

### Firm Overview
Prosegur Cash, S.A. is the Madrid‑listed subsidiary of Prosegur focused on cash‑in‑transit and integrated cash management services across Europe and Latin America. As part of its “new products” transformation, the company has added institutional digital‑asset custody under the Prosegur Crypto brand. ([prosegur.com](https://www.prosegur.com/en/business-lines/cash?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Multiple company communications state that Prosegur Crypto is “Prosegur Cash’s institutional digital asset custody service,” providing secure, offline custody (“crypto bunkers”) and related operational safeguards for institutions. The service has been used by crypto‑native clients such as Bit2Me and has expanded internationally with custody bunkers in Brazil (2023) and Argentina (2024). Prosegur Cash also invested in and partnered with Minos Global to offer institutional trading and custody of tokenized assets, aligning with Europe’s MiCA regime. These activities involve direct provision of custody and crypto‑financial services rather than mining, proprietary coin holding, or general tech infrastructure. ([prosegurcash.com](https://www.prosegurcash.com/en/media/article/press/prosegur-crypto-anuncia-creacion-bunker-custodia-activos-digitales-brasil?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence shows Prosegur Cash’s crypto exposure arises from running an institutional digital‑asset custody platform and related tokenization/trading enablement through partnerships. Under the project definitions, this maps squarely to finserv. There is no indication of proprietary crypto‑treasury holdings (holders), mining (miners), or mining‑oriented infrastructure (infrastr). Therefore, finserv = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. Given strong company documentation plus an earnings‑call anchor (general business discussion) but limited crypto‑specific call discussion, certainty is set to 3. ([prosegurcash.com](https://www.prosegurcash.com/en/media/article/press/prosegur-crypto-anuncia-creacion-bunker-custodia-activos-digitales-brasil?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Earnings call transcripts reviewed seldom mention crypto explicitly, and management does not break out crypto‑related revenue; custody assets are client assets and thus off‑balance‑sheet. Some expansion items appear in late‑2024 press coverage and are included; post‑2024 developments were not considered for classification. ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/mad/cash/investor-relations/earnings-call/q3-2021))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 5, 2021 (Q3 2021): “new product share over total sales at a solid 21%.” — [Alpha Spread transcript](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/mad/cash/investor-relations/earnings-call/q3-2021)  
- Nov 5, 2021 (Q3 2021): “we have invested EUR 16 million year‑to‑date [in digital transformation].” — [Alpha Spread transcript](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/mad/cash/investor-relations/earnings-call/q3-2021)

**Filings (10‑K / annual reports / IR):**
- 2022 Annual Accounts (Management Report): “we expect further consolidation of initiatives such as Prosegur Crypto or Cash Today.” — [Prosegur Cash Annual Accounts 2022 (PDF)](https://www.prosegurcash.com/dam/jcr%3A7da6370f-1b22-40df-ad70-2bc15aee6103/Indiv%202022%202.pdf)
- Business lines overview noting Prosegur Crypto as digital‑asset custody within the group’s cash business — [Prosegur: Cash business page](https://www.prosegur.com/en/business-lines/cash)

**Financial news / other:**
- Jul 14, 2021: Prosegur announces creation of “crypto bunker” custody model with GK8. — [Prosegur press release](https://www.prosegur.com/en/media/article/press/prosegur-creates-worlds-first-crypto-bunker-custody-digital-assets)
- Nov 23, 2021: Bit2Me to keep part of its funds with Prosegur Crypto (Prosegur Cash’s custody service). — [Prosegur press release](https://www.prosegur.com/en/media/article/press/Prosegur-Crypto-becomes-the-crypto-custodian-of-the-Spanish-protocol-Bit2Me)
- Nov 2, 2023: Prosegur Crypto launches a Brazil‑based custody bunker. — [Prosegur Cash press release](https://www.prosegurcash.com/en/media/article/press/prosegur-crypto-anuncia-creacion-bunker-custodia-activos-digitales-brasil)
- Dec 4, 2024: Prosegur Crypto opens Argentina custody bunker (service of Prosegur Cash). — [Cinco Días (El País)](https://cincodias.elpais.com/criptoactivos/2024-12-04/prosegur-crypto-inaugura-el-primer-bunker-de-custodia-de-argentina.html)

## VALERO ENERGY

* Rank: 374 | Expo: 0.11 | id_str: US91913Y1001
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filing (FY2023), Q2 2022 and Q3 2018 earnings calls

### Firm Overview
Valero Energy Corporation is a U.S.-based independent manufacturer and marketer of petroleum-based and low‑carbon liquid transportation fuels and petrochemical products, with operations primarily in the United States, Canada, the United Kingdom, Ireland and Latin America. As of FY2023, it owned 15 petroleum refineries (about 3.2 million barrels per day), held a joint venture interest in Diamond Green Diesel (about 1.2 billion gallons per year of renewable diesel capacity), and operated 12 ethanol plants (about 1.6 billion gallons per year). ([d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net](https://d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net/CIK-0001035002/b8d4c6a6-7d8b-4bce-945d-e2a865f03e38.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024 earnings calls and filings reviewed, Valero’s management discussion centers on refining, renewable diesel via Diamond Green Diesel, and ethanol; there is no indication of proprietary crypto holdings, mining, digital‑asset financial services, or crypto‑specific infrastructure. Typical call content covers throughput, margins, capital allocation and low‑carbon fuel projects (e.g., DGD expansions and SAF options). ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/07/29/valero-energy-vlo-q2-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))
One potentially confusing term in a Q2 2022 call reference to “BTC” is the U.S. blenders tax credit for renewable diesel, not Bitcoin; the context is renewable fuel policy and credits. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/07/28/valero-energy-vlo-q2-2022-earnings-call-transcript/))
The FY2023 Form 10‑K likewise details petroleum refining and renewable fuels operations without any crypto‑asset activities. ([d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net](https://d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net/CIK-0001035002/b8d4c6a6-7d8b-4bce-945d-e2a865f03e38.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
No substantive crypto exposure is evidenced in transcripts, filings, or reputable trade/news coverage for 2017–2024. Mentions of “BTC” relate to the renewable fuels blenders tax credit, not cryptocurrency. Under the project definitions, all mechanism categories are set to 0.0 and the firm is assigned unclass = 1.0. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/07/28/valero-energy-vlo-q2-2022-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified beyond the “BTC” terminology overlap noted above (blenders tax credit vs. Bitcoin). The searches reviewed multiple transcripts and the FY2023 10‑K within the sample window; no crypto‑related business lines were found. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/07/29/valero-energy-vlo-q2-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jul 28, 2022 (Q2 2022): “The renewable diesel segment had record production volumes as the DGD expansion, DGD 2, ramped up to full capacity.” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/07/28/valero-energy-vlo-q2-2022-earnings-call-transcript/)  
- Jul 28, 2022 (Q2 2022): “The BTC obviously is part of the business model that we capture with renewable diesel...” [blenders tax credit, not Bitcoin]. — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/07/28/valero-energy-vlo-q2-2022-earnings-call-transcript/)  
- Oct 25, 2018 (Q3 2018): “The Diamond Green Diesel expansion was completed in August, bringing the current renewable diesel production capacity to 16,500 barrels per day.” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/10/25/valero-energy-corp-vlo-q3-2018-earnings-conference.aspx)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Feb 2024 (FY2023 Form 10‑K): “We own 15 petroleum refineries… We are a joint venture member in Diamond Green Diesel… and we own 12 ethanol plants…” — [Valero Form 10‑K (FY2023)](https://d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net/CIK-0001035002/b8d4c6a6-7d8b-4bce-945d-e2a865f03e38.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Feb 1, 2023: Valero/Darling JV approves SAF upgrade project at Port Arthur renewable diesel plant. — [Reuters via Hydrocarbon Processing](https://www.hydrocarbonprocessing.com/news/2023/02/valero-jv-approves-sustainable-aviation-fuel-project-at-texas-plant)

## AKER

* Rank: 375 | Expo: 0.11 | id_str: NO0010234552
* Weight summary: holders 0.9, miners 0.1, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: Annual report (2021), Q1 2021 and Q2 2022 earnings calls, stock‑exchange press release (Mar 8, 2021)

### Firm Overview
Aker ASA is a Norway‑based industrial investment company listed on the Oslo Stock Exchange, with holdings across energy, industrial technology, software and related sectors. In March 2021, Aker established Seetee AS as a majority‑owned subsidiary to invest in Bitcoin and related projects within the Bitcoin ecosystem.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The principal crypto exposure is through Seetee, which was initially capitalized with NOK 500 million and states that Bitcoin is its treasury asset; Aker’s 2021 filings and subsequent calls reference the investment and associated fair‑value movements. Seetee’s launch strategy also included “launching Bitcoin verification operations” and a collaboration with Blockstream that initially focused on mining, indicating a secondary, smaller mining linkage. There is no evidence that Aker operates exchanges, custody or other crypto financial services, nor that it supplies crypto‑specific hardware or infrastructure.

### Weight Assignment
Given explicit disclosures that Seetee keeps its liquid investable assets in Bitcoin and Aker’s continued discussion of Seetee’s value changes on results calls, the “holders” mechanism is dominant. A smaller “miners” weight is assigned based on Aker’s announcement that Seetee would launch Bitcoin verification (mining) operations in partnership with Blockstream, though detailed mining revenues were not reported in the 2017–2024 window. No evidence supports financial‑service, business‑service or infrastructure supplier roles for Aker in crypto. Hence: holders 0.9, miners 0.1, all others 0.0.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Earnings‑call and filing disclosures confirm the Bitcoin treasury strategy via Seetee, but do not quantify any sustained mining revenue or hashrate during 2017–2024; the miners weight is therefore conservative. Seetee’s Bitcoin position was a small share of Aker’s asset base over the period, which informed AssetShare = 1. At least one transcript quote is sourced from third‑party transcript repositories rather than Aker’s site; underlying audio/webcasts were not directly cited.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q1 2021: “The main cash outflows in the quarter were … the NOK500 million investment into [the] new Bitcoin and blockchain technology company, Seetee.” — [MLQ.ai transcript](https://mlq.ai/stocks/AKAAF/earnings-call-transcript/Q1-2021) ([mlq.ai](https://mlq.ai/stocks/AKAAF/earnings-call-transcript/Q1-2021?utm_source=openai))  
- Q2 2022: “The decrease in other equity investments is mainly explained by negative value development in Seetee … [and] negative value changes … Seetee of NOK 230 million.” — [AlphaSpread transcript](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/ose/aker/investor-relations/earnings-call/q2-2022) ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/ose/aker/investor-relations/earnings-call/q2-2022))

**Filings (annual/interim reports):**
- Q1 2021 report: “Aker also invested NOK 0.5 billion in the newly established bitcoin and blockchain‑technology company Seetee.” — [Aker ASA Q1 2021 report (PDF)](https://akerasa.ams3.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/interim-reports/Aker-ASA-Q1-2021-Report_2021-05-07-050750.pdf) ([akerasa.ams3.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com](https://akerasa.ams3.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/interim-reports/Aker-ASA-Q1-2021-Report_2021-05-07-050750.pdf))  
- Annual report 2021: “Seetee — Investment in bitcoin and projects and companies throughout the Bitcoin ecosystem. Ownership interest 90.01%.” — [Aker ASA Annual Report 2021 (PDF)](https://akerasa.ams3.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/images/Aker-ASA-Annual-report-2021.pdf) ([akerasa.ams3.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com](https://akerasa.ams3.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/images/Aker-ASA-Annual-report-2021.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**
- Mar 8, 2021: “Aker … will keep all its liquid investable assets in bitcoin [via Seetee] and will also enter the bitcoin mining industry.” — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/03/08/norway-listed-aker-to-put-100-bitcoin-in-treasury-reserves-of-new-investment-unit) ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/03/08/norway-listed-aker-to-put-100-bitcoin-in-treasury-reserves-of-new-investment-unit?utm_source=openai))  
- Mar 8, 2021: Stock‑exchange announcement launching Seetee; Letter to Shareholders at seetee.io. — [Aker ASA via Cision](https://news.cision.com/aker-asa/r/aker-asa-launches-seetee-to-invest-in-bitcoin-and-blockchain-technology%2Cc3301475) ([news.cision.com](https://news.cision.com/aker-asa/r/aker-asa-launches-seetee-to-invest-in-bitcoin-and-blockchain-technology%2Cc3301475?utm_source=openai))  
- Mar 8, 2021: Seetee Shareholder Letter detailing strategy to hold bitcoin and partner with Blockstream. — [Seetee.io](https://www.seetee.io/blog/2021-03-08-shareholder-letter/) ([seetee.io](https://www.seetee.io/blog/2021-03-08-shareholder-letter/?utm_source=openai))  
- Mar 8, 2021: Launch note summarizing initial focus including “launching Bitcoin verification operations.” — [MarketScreener](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/AKER-ASA-1413076/news/Aker-ASA-Launches-Seetee-to-Invest-in-Bitcoin-and-Blockchain-Technology-33489528/) ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/AKER-ASA-1413076/news/Aker-ASA-Launches-Seetee-to-Invest-in-Bitcoin-and-Blockchain-Technology-33489528/?utm_source=openai))

## BUILD A BEAR WORKSHOP

* Rank: 376 | Expo: 0.11 | id_str: US1200761047
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q2 2022 earnings call; Company press release (Sept 28, 2022); Sweet (partner) FAQ

### Firm Overview
Build‑A‑Bear Workshop, Inc. is a U.S. specialty retailer known for its experiential stores where customers design and purchase customized stuffed animals; it is listed on the NYSE under ticker BBW. The company emphasizes omnichannel retail and brand extensions across licensing and digital initiatives. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/b/NYSE_BBW_2023.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company directly entered Web3 in 2022 by launching a branded NFT program in partnership with Sweet, positioning the effort as part of its 25th anniversary initiatives. Management stated on an earnings call that Build‑A‑Bear planned “to enter the Metaverse with the introduction of our first NFTs,” supported by its relationship with Web3 firm Sweet. A contemporaneous corporate press release detailed the NFT collection, associated physical collectibles, and the Sweet partnership. There is no evidence of crypto treasury holdings, mining activities, custody/exchange operations, or crypto‑specific infrastructure supply. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/BBW/documents/transcripts/q2-2023))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence points to a narrow, direct engagement with digital assets via issuance/sale of NFTs through a third‑party platform, which falls under crypto financial services for classification purposes. No sources indicate any other mechanism of exposure. Accordingly, the assignment is concentrated in finserv: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0. Given the one‑off/limited nature of the initiative and lack of separate segment disclosure, crypto‑related revenue and assets appear immaterial (RevShare = 1, AssetShare = 0). ([ir.buildabear.com](https://ir.buildabear.com/news-releases/news-release-details/build-bear-unveil-nft-collection-and-one-kind-collectible-bear?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The NFT activity was announced and discussed in 2022; there is limited evidence of sustained, material NFT revenues thereafter within the 2017–2024 window, and filings do not separately disclose crypto assets or detailed NFT revenue. Classification therefore captures presence (finserv) rather than magnitude, and certainty is set to 3 based on an earnings‑call quote plus corroborating corporate materials. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/BBW/documents/transcripts/q2-2023))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q2 2022: “Build‑A‑Bear has plans to enter the Metaverse with the introduction of our first NFTs … This launch is supported by a relationship with Web3 firm Sweet, which specializes in global brand NFT experiences.” — [Fintool transcript](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/BBW/documents/transcripts/q2-2023) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/BBW/documents/transcripts/q2-2023))  
- Q2 2022 (alt. source with same remarks) — [ROIC.ai transcript](https://www.roic.ai/quote/BBW/transcripts/2022-year/2-quarter) ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/BBW/transcripts/2022-year/2-quarter?utm_source=openai))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Sept 28, 2022 press release announcing NFT collection and Sweet partnership — [Build‑A‑Bear IR](https://ir.buildabear.com/news-releases/news-release-details/build-bear-unveil-nft-collection-and-one-kind-collectible-bear) ([ir.buildabear.com](https://ir.buildabear.com/news-releases/news-release-details/build-bear-unveil-nft-collection-and-one-kind-collectible-bear?utm_source=openai))  
- FY2023 Form 10‑K (filed April 18, 2024) for company description and disclosures; no separate crypto asset line items identified — [AnnualReports.com host of 10‑K PDF](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/b/NYSE_BBW_2023.pdf) ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/b/NYSE_BBW_2023.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**

None identified within the January 2017–December 2024 sample period; earnings-call transcripts and the FY2023 Form 10‑K provide consistent, non-crypto business descriptions. ([stockinsights.ai](https://www.stockinsights.ai/us/NDRA/earnings-transcript/fy23-q2-f045))

* Primary sources: Form 10-K (filed Mar 1, 2023 for FY2022); Q2 2023 earnings call; merger press releases and mainstream financial news (2023)

### Firm Overview
PacWest Bancorp was a U.S. bank holding company based in Beverly Hills/Los Angeles, California, with Pacific Western Bank as its principal subsidiary. In July 2023 it agreed to merge with Banc of California; the transaction closed on November 30, 2023, after which the combined institution operated under the Banc of California name. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PacWest_Bancorp?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across the 2017–2024 window, earnings calls and SEC filings focus on community/commercial banking, deposit mix, liquidity, and balance‑sheet repositioning; there is no indication of crypto custody, brokerage/exchange operations, payments products tied to digital assets, mining, or corporate treasury crypto holdings. The FY2022 Form 10‑K contains no references to cryptocurrency, bitcoin, blockchain, or digital assets, and provides no crypto‑specific revenue or asset disclosures. News coverage around 2023 centers on deposit flows and the subsequent merger, not crypto activity. ([earningscall.ai](https://www.earningscall.ai/stock/transcript/PACW-2023-Q2))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of substantive crypto involvement in filings, transcripts, or credible news through December 2024, the firm is coded unclassified: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0. There is no evidence the stock functioned as a recognized crypto proxy for investors; invbase = 0. As no crypto revenues or assets are disclosed, RevShare and AssetShare are NA. ([earningscall.ai](https://www.earningscall.ai/stock/transcript/PACW-2023-Q2))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The company underwent significant restructuring during 2023 and merged into Banc of California on November 30, 2023. While community banks may incidentally bank customers active in digital assets, no sources in 2017–2024 document PacWest offering crypto‑specific financial services or holding crypto assets; therefore no mechanism category is assigned. ([bancofcal.com](https://bancofcal.com/about-us/newsroom/banc-of-california-announces-completion-of-transformational-merger-with-pacwest-bancorp-and-400-million-equity-raise/?utm_source=openai))

- Q2 2023: “We accelerated these efforts during the second quarter in light of the turmoil in the banking market … [and] are very pleased that our deposit base stabilized in the quarter and has now begun to grow.” — [EarningsCall.ai transcript](https://www.earningscall.ai/stock/transcript/PACW-2023-Q2) ([earningscall.ai](https://www.earningscall.ai/stock/transcript/PACW-2023-Q2))

### Firm Overview
Li & Fung Limited was a Hong Kong–based global supply chain manager for consumer brands and retailers, historically listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under ticker 494. The company was privatized in May 2020 and delisted, and it notes that from 2020 onward it no longer publicly shares financial information as a private company. ([lifung.com](https://www.lifung.com/press-release/li-fung-announces-shareholder-approval-privatization/?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2020, company materials center on supply chain solutions, logistics and a broader digital transformation (e.g., 3D product development and a “Supply Chain of the Future” initiative), with no indication of cryptocurrency holdings, mining, custody, trading, or crypto‑linked financial products. The 2019 Annual Report and related results materials emphasize operating performance, logistics growth and capital structure actions, not digital‑asset activity. Even post‑privatization investor communications in 2024 reference an investor call presentation without suggesting crypto services or assets. As such, no substantive crypto exposure mechanism applies. ([lifung.com](https://www.lifung.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/08/LiFung_AR_2019.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of evidence that Li & Fung held crypto assets, mined, offered crypto financial services, provided crypto‑specific non‑financial services, or supplied crypto‑targeted infrastructure during the 2017–2024 window, the firm is coded unclassified: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0. There is no indication that the equity historically served as a recognized proxy for crypto sentiment; invbase = 0. ([lifung.com](https://www.lifung.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/08/LiFung_AR_2019.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The company was delisted on May 27, 2020 following shareholder approval of privatization on May 12, 2020; post‑2020 disclosures are limited. Public earnings call transcripts for 2017–2020 were not located; classification relies on annual reports, results announcements and press materials. While Li & Fung pursued digital initiatives in supply chain management, no sources indicate direct involvement with cryptocurrencies as assets or services per the classification criteria. ([lifung.com](https://www.lifung.com/press-release/li-fung-announces-shareholder-approval-privatization/?utm_source=openai))

- Results materials archive indicating prior public reporting and that Li & Fung became a private company in 2020 — [Li & Fung Annual Reports page](https://www.lifung.com/investors/annual-reports/) ([lifung.com](https://www.lifung.com/investors/annual-reports/))
