## LEXICON PHARMACEUTICALS

* Rank: 380 | Expo: 0.11 | id_str: US5288723027
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (FY2023, filed March 2024); Q3 2020 earnings call; Q3 2024 earnings call summary/transcript; 2023 FDA approval press release; 2024 quarterly results press release

### Firm Overview
Lexicon Pharmaceuticals, Inc. is a U.S.-based biopharmaceutical company listed on Nasdaq (LXRX) that develops and commercializes therapeutics, including INPEFA (sotagliflozin) for heart failure. Its 2023–2024 disclosures focus on commercializing INPEFA and advancing late‑stage pipeline programs such as LX9211 and LX9851. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1062822/000106282224000008/lxrx-20231231.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across the January 2017–December 2024 period, company filings and earnings calls describe a conventional biopharma business model centered on drug R&D, regulatory milestones, and commercialization, with no indication of cryptocurrency holdings, mining, custody/trading services, or crypto‑specific infrastructure. The FY2023 Form 10‑K contains no references to “bitcoin,” “cryptocurrency,” or “digital assets,” and instead details INPEFA commercialization and pipeline activities. Earnings call transcripts similarly discuss drug programs, launches and revenues without crypto context. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1062822/000106282224000008/lxrx-20231231.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of evidence that Lexicon held crypto assets, mined, offered crypto financial services, provided crypto‑specific non‑financial services, or supplied crypto‑targeted infrastructure in 2017–2024, the firm is coded unclassified: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0. There is no evidence that the stock is treated by investors as a crypto proxy; invbase = 0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1062822/000106282224000008/lxrx-20231231.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified. Standard biotech disclosures and transcripts were available; searches of SEC filings and investor materials during 2017–2024 revealed no substantive crypto exposure. Note: Some third‑party transcript pages include site‑level crypto tickers/menus which are not company statements. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1062822/000106282224000008/lxrx-20231231.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 29, 2020 (Q3 2020), transcript: “we shifted our focus to LX9211 and our other scientific assets” and “completed the sale of XERMELO” — [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/10/30/lexicon-pharmaceuticals-inc-lxrx-q3-2020-earnings/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/10/30/lexicon-pharmaceuticals-inc-lxrx-q3-2020-earnings/))  
- Nov 1, 2024 (Q3 2024), transcript summary: “Net sales for the third quarter of 2024 were $1.7 million” and pipeline updates (INPEFA, LX9211, LX9851) — [Investing.com transcripts](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-lexicon-pharmaceuticals-reports-on-q3-2024-progress-93CH-3719966) ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-lexicon-pharmaceuticals-reports-on-q3-2024-progress-93CH-3719966))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**

## OTTER TAIL

* Rank: 381 | Expo: 0.11 | id_str: US6896481032
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 1.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 2 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K/Annual Report filings (2022–2023); Q3 2021 earnings call; North Dakota PSC filing; local/regional news on Jamestown data center

### Firm Overview
Otter Tail Corporation is a U.S. diversified holding company whose primary business is its regulated electric utility, Otter Tail Power Company, serving Minnesota, North Dakota and South Dakota, alongside manufacturing and plastics operations. The company’s disclosures during 2017–2024 emphasize regulated utility investment and diversified industrial activities. ([s1.q4cdn.com](https://s1.q4cdn.com/276295446/files/doc_financials/2023/interactive/Otter_Tail_Corp-AR_2022_ClientDL/images/Otter_Tail_Corp-AR2022.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Crypto exposure arises indirectly through energy infrastructure: Otter Tail Power sells electricity to a data center developer/operator in its territory with a concentration of customers engaged in cryptocurrency mining and related activities. Management highlighted in late 2021 a “new load” from a business focused on high‑performance crypto‑mining infrastructure, noting potential demand near 100 MW with curtailment capability. Company risk disclosures in the 2022 Annual Report reiterate that demand from the customer’s crypto‑mining clients can directly affect the customer’s electricity needs, and therefore Otter Tail’s revenues. Local reporting documents Applied Blockchain/Digital’s 100+ MW Jamestown, North Dakota facility co‑located with an Otter Tail Power substation, supporting the linkage between the utility and mining‑related load. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/02/otter-tail-corporation-ottr-q3-2021-earnings-call/))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence indicates the company does not hold crypto on balance sheet, mine crypto, or provide crypto financial services. Its exposure is as an energy supplier to a data‑center operator serving crypto miners—a clear fit with the infrastructure category (“energy infrastructure targeted at miners”). Accordingly, the classification allocates 1.0 to infrastr and 0.0 to all other mechanism categories. Given the size of the identified load (up to ~100 MW) and external reporting describing the facility as a major customer, crypto‑linked revenue is coded as moderate (RevShare = 2), while AssetShare = 0 since no crypto assets are held by the firm. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/02/otter-tail-corporation-ottr-q3-2021-earnings-call/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Otter Tail does not separately disclose the exact revenue share attributable to crypto‑mining‑related customers, and the “over 10% of segment revenue” customer references in filings may or may not refer to the same data‑center customer. Additionally, Applied Digital began emphasizing high‑performance computing/AI hosting in 2023–2024, which may reduce the crypto‑specific mix over time; the classification reflects evidence within the 2017–2024 sample. ([s1.q4cdn.com](https://s1.q4cdn.com/276295446/files/doc_financials/2023/interactive/Otter_Tail_Corp-AR_2022_ClientDL/images/Otter_Tail_Corp-AR2022.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 2, 2021 (Q3 2021), transcript: “addition of a new load … focused on … crypto mining … Demand … could approach 100 megawatts.” — [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/02/otter-tail-corporation-ottr-q3-2021-earnings-call/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/02/otter-tail-corporation-ottr-q3-2021-earnings-call/))

**Filings (10-K / annual report):**
- Annual Report 2022: data‑center developer/operator customer with concentration in cryptocurrency mining; demand from miners can impact electricity sales/revenues. — [Otter Tail 2022 AR (PDF)](https://s1.q4cdn.com/276295446/files/doc_financials/2023/interactive/Otter_Tail_Corp-AR_2022_ClientDL/images/Otter_Tail_Corp-AR2022.pdf) ([s1.q4cdn.com](https://s1.q4cdn.com/276295446/files/doc_financials/2023/interactive/Otter_Tail_Corp-AR_2022_ClientDL/images/Otter_Tail_Corp-AR2022.pdf))  
- Form 10‑K for year ended Dec 31, 2022 (filed Feb 15, 2023): similar disclosure on data‑center customer serving crypto‑mining clients. — [SEC EDGAR/EDGAR Online](https://content.edgar-online.com/ExternalLink/EDGAR/0001466593-23-000053.html?dest=ottr-20221231_htm) ([content.edgar-online.com](https://content.edgar-online.com/ExternalLink/EDGAR/0001466593-23-000053.html?dest=ottr-20221231_htm&hash=73e9fc9f4d6b0b1082230bba0c324be5e772f7301e1423c9b4762cf7460e4df6&utm_source=openai))  
- ND Public Service Commission filing (Case 23‑0342): includes the same risk language regarding the crypto‑mining‑concentrated customer. — [ND PSC (PDF)](https://www.psc.nd.gov/webdocs/case/23-0342/076-010.pdf) ([psc.nd.gov](https://www.psc.nd.gov/webdocs/case/23-0342/076-010.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**
- May 5, 2022: Ribbon‑cutting for Applied Blockchain’s Jamestown crypto‑mining hosting facility; co‑located with an Otter Tail Power substation. — [NewsDakota/KMHC](https://www.newsdakota.com/2022/05/05/applied-blockchain-celebrates-finished-project-with-ribbon-cutting/) ([newsdakota.com](https://www.newsdakota.com/2022/05/05/applied-blockchain-celebrates-finished-project-with-ribbon-cutting/?utm_source=openai))  
- Sept 14–15, 2021: Groundbreaking; partnership with Otter Tail Power to provide electricity to Jamestown facility. — [NewsDakota](https://www.newsdakota.com/2021/09/14/powerhouse-crypto-mining-business-expects-operations-by-years-end/) | [Blockchain News Group](https://blockchainnewsgroup.com/2021/09/15/groundbreaking-held-for-applied-blockchains-cryptocurrency-hosting-facility/) ([newsdakota.com](https://www.newsdakota.com/2021/09/14/powerhouse-crypto-mining-business-expects-operations-by-years-end/?utm_source=openai))  
- Applied Digital backgrounder: Jamestown (JMS01) 100–106 MW hosting facility; company evolved from Applied Blockchain. — [APLD IR/filings](https://ir.applieddigital.com/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001144879-24-000189/apldprospectussupplement-j.htm) | [Press release](https://ir.applieddigital.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/12/applied-blockchain-inc-expands-its-blockchain-industry) ([ir.applieddigital.com](https://ir.applieddigital.com/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001144879-24-000189/apldprospectussupplement-j.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- Consumer advocate note: crypto‑mining facility to become Otter Tail Power’s second‑largest customer; interruptible customer status. — [Citizens Utility Board of Minnesota](https://cubminnesota.org/cryptocurrency-minings-risk-to-otter-tails-ratepayers) ([cubminnesota.org](https://cubminnesota.org/cryptocurrency-minings-risk-to-otter-tails-ratepayers?utm_source=openai))

## GREE HOLDINGS

* Rank: 382 | Expo: 0.11 | id_str: JP3274070006
* Weight summary: holders 0.1, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.3, infrastr 0.6, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: press releases (2022–2023), financial results briefings coverage (2023), securities filings (FY2024)

### Firm Overview
GREE Holdings is a Japan-based digital entertainment group best known for mobile and online games, and since 2022 it has built a metaverse/VTuber segment around its REALITY platform. Through subsidiary BLRD it entered Web3, developing blockchain games and operating validator nodes on multiple networks. ([gree.co.jp](https://gree.co.jp/jp/ja/corporate/group/?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management announced entry into Web3 on September 27, 2022, stating it would develop/publish blockchain games, operate validator nodes, and even make token investments (including into the Oasys project). BLRD subsequently disclosed validator operations on Polygon and stated it was already running validators for Oasys and Avalanche; in June 2023 GREE also announced validation on Sui alongside plans to deploy Web3 titles using Japanese IP. These activities indicate direct operational involvement in blockchain network infrastructure and first‑party game development using blockchain rails; there is no evidence the company mines proof‑of‑work coins or operates exchange/custody services. Securities filings through FY2024 describe segment makeup but do not show crypto holdings as a separate material balance‑sheet class. ([gree.co.jp](https://www.gree.co.jp/jp/ja/news/press/2022/0927-01.html?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Validator operations are the clearest, repeated disclosure and map to providing blockchain infrastructure capacity; this is weighted highest (infrastr = 0.6). Web3 game development/publishing is non‑financial, product/software activity around the crypto ecosystem, so a secondary weight is assigned to bizserv (0.3). The company also references token investments (e.g., Oasys tokens), which warrants a small holders weight (0.1). No evidence supports miners or finserv exposure in 2017–2024 disclosures, hence 0.0 in those categories. Given reliance on press releases and secondary coverage rather than a retrievable transcript, certainty is set to 2, and crypto revenue/asset materiality is coded as minor (RevShare = 1; AssetShare = 1). ([gree.co.jp](https://www.gree.co.jp/jp/ja/news/press/2022/0927-01.html?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
A verbatim earnings-call/briefing transcript mentioning Web3 could not be retrieved; Japanese IR for GREE primarily provides videos/slides, and media coverage summarizes comments. Token investment magnitude and any mark‑to‑market accounting are not broken out in FY2017–FY2024 filings, limiting precision on AssetShare. Reported validator operations span multiple PoS networks, but the revenue contribution from validation and Web3 titles within the sample period is not disclosed separately; weights reflect qualitative disclosures rather than quantified segment lines. ([moguravr.com](https://www.moguravr.com/gree-2024-1q-financial-result/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 9, 2023 (FY2024 Q1) coverage: “順調に先行投資が進捗している（決算説明会より）” — [Mogura VR News](https://www.moguravr.com/gree-2024-1q-financial-result/) ([moguravr.com](https://www.moguravr.com/gree-2024-1q-financial-result/))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Securities Report (FY2024, 20th term): segment breakdown and business description (games, metaverse/DX, investment) — [Zaimdoc mirror of 有価証券報告書](https://zaimdoc.com/docs/s100ufy3) ([zaimdoc.com](https://zaimdoc.com/docs/s100ufy3))
- FY2024 Q3 earnings release (May 8, 2024) — [JPX/catr entry](https://catr.jp/tss/10eb9/177100) ([catr.jp](https://catr.jp/tss/10eb9/177100?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Sep 27, 2022: GREE announces entry into Web3 via REALITY; plans blockchain game development, validator operations, and token investment (incl. Oasys). — [GREE press release](https://www.gree.co.jp/jp/ja/news/press/2022/0927-01.html) ([gree.co.jp](https://www.gree.co.jp/jp/ja/news/press/2022/0927-01.html?utm_source=openai))
- Oct 27, 2022: BLRD (GREE) strategic partnership with Ava Labs; to publish games and run Avalanche validators. — [GREE press release](https://hd.gree.net/jp/ja/news/press/2022/1027-01.html) ([hd.gree.net](https://hd.gree.net/jp/ja/news/press/2022/1027-01.html?utm_source=openai))
- Dec 20, 2022: BLRD begins operating Polygon validator nodes
- Dec 20, 2022: BLRD begins operating Polygon validator nodes — [GREE press release](https://www.gree.co.jp/jp/ja/news/press/2022/1220-03.html)  
- Oct 27, 2022: Strategic partnership with Ava Labs; BLRD to publish games and run Avalanche validators — [GREE press release](https://www.gree.co.jp/jp/ja/news/press/2022/1027-01.html)  
- Sep 27, 2022: GREE enters Web3; plans blockchain game development, validator node operations, and token investment in Oasys — [GREE press release](https://www.gree.co.jp/jp/ja/news/press/2022/0927-01.html)  
- Dec 20, 2022: Polygon validator operations coverage — [PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/japanese-game-giant-gree-to-run-nodes-on-polygon-301706977.html)

## DECIPHERA PHARMS. DEAD - DELIST.12/06/24

* Rank: 383 | Expo: 0.11 | id_str: US24344T1016
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: SEC 10-K filing (FY2023, filed Feb 7, 2024), Q1–Q3 2021 earnings call transcripts, acquisition press releases (April–June 2024)

### Firm Overview
Deciphera Pharmaceuticals, Inc. was a U.S.-based oncology biopharmaceutical company focused on the discovery, development, and commercialization of kinase inhibitor therapies, including QINLOCK for GIST. Ono Pharmaceutical completed its acquisition on June 11, 2024; Deciphera’s shares ceased trading on Nasdaq upon closing and were subsequently delisted. ([fintel.io](https://fintel.io/doc/sec-deciphera-pharmaceuticals-inc-1654151-10k-2024-february-07-19760-2425?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across earnings-call transcripts in 2021 and the company’s FY2023 Form 10-K (filed February 7, 2024), management and filings discuss clinical programs, commercialization, and oncology pipeline priorities with no references to cryptocurrency holdings, mining, blockchain infrastructure, or crypto financial services. No disclosures indicate digital asset balances, token investments, validator/mining operations, or crypto-custody/exchange activities during 2017–2024. Given the company’s healthcare focus and the absence of any crypto-operational or balance-sheet links, no crypto-exposure mechanism applies. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/05/05/deciphera-pharmaceuticals-inc-dcph-q1-2021-earning/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
With no substantive crypto involvement identified in transcripts, SEC filings, or transaction announcements over the 2017–2024 sample window, the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0) and all other mechanism weights are 0.0. No evidence suggests a crypto‑sensitive investor base, so invbase = 0; revenue and asset materiality related to crypto are not applicable. ([fintel.io](https://fintel.io/doc/sec-deciphera-pharmaceuticals-inc-1654151-10k-2024-february-07-19760-2425?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified. Multiple independent sources (earnings calls, SEC filings, and acquisition materials) consistently describe a pure‑play oncology biopharma with no crypto exposure; the company ceased trading following completion of the Ono acquisition in June 2024. ([deciphera.com](https://www.deciphera.com/sites/default/files/news-files/newsarticle-asset-body-20240611-11116.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 5, 2021 (Q1 2021): “QINLOCK is now established as monotherapy as the standard of care in the fourth line.” — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/05/05/deciphera-pharmaceuticals-inc-dcph-q1-2021-earning/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/05/05/deciphera-pharmaceuticals-inc-dcph-q1-2021-earning/?utm_source=openai))

- Nov 3, 2021 (Q3 2021): “We’re excited to move forward with vimseltinib, our inhibitor of CSF1R.” — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/03/deciphera-pharmaceuticals-inc-dcph-q3-2021-earning/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/03/deciphera-pharmaceuticals-inc-dcph-q3-2021-earning/?utm_source=openai))

## BURSA MALAYSIA

* Rank: 384 | Expo: 0.11 | id_str: MYL1818OO003
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Earnings call transcript (H1 2024), Integrated annual report pages (2023–2024), Company press releases on DLT PoC (2019–2020), Financial news (Mar 2024)

### Firm Overview
Bursa Malaysia Berhad operates Malaysia’s national securities and derivatives exchange, providing listing, trading, clearing and depository services across the country’s capital markets. Its investor materials emphasize core exchange activities and financial performance; no digital-asset exchange operations are described. ([bursa.listedcompany.com](https://bursa.listedcompany.com/financial_highlights.html?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across earnings briefings, investor materials and company communications from 2017–2024, there is no evidence that Bursa Malaysia holds cryptocurrencies, mines digital assets, or offers crypto trading, custody, or investment products. In March 2024, the CEO publicly ruled out offering cryptocurrencies on the multi-asset exchange platform, reinforcing the absence of crypto financial services. Malaysia’s licensed digital asset exchanges are registered with the Securities Commission Malaysia as Recognised Market Operators, separate from Bursa’s exchange infrastructure. Bursa did run a dematerialisation proof‑of‑concept using distributed ledger technology for securities processes, but this was an internal market‑infrastructure test and did not involve issuing or handling cryptocurrencies for customers. ([fintechnews.my](https://fintechnews.my/43685/blockchain/bursa-malaysia-rules-out-offering-crypto-on-its-multi-asset-exchange-platform/))

### Weight Assignment
Given explicit management statements that the exchange will not list or offer cryptocurrencies, the lack of disclosures indicating crypto custody, mining, or product issuance, and the fact that regulated digital‑asset exchanges in Malaysia operate outside Bursa’s remit, all mechanism categories are assigned 0.0 and the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). No evidence indicates a crypto‑sensitive investor base, so invbase = 0. ([fintechnews.my](https://fintechnews.my/43685/blockchain/bursa-malaysia-rules-out-offering-crypto-on-its-multi-asset-exchange-platform/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified. Bursa’s limited DLT proof‑of‑concept was confined to internal dematerialisation/market‑infrastructure processes and does not constitute crypto financial services exposure. ([bursamalaysia.com](https://www.bursamalaysia.com/bm/about_bursa/media_centre/bursa-malaysia-partners-with-digital-asset-and-vmware-to-develop-dematerialisation-proof-of-concept?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**

## CLOUDFLARE A

* Rank: 385 | Expo: 0.11 | id_str: US18915M1071
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.7, infrastr 0.3, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Cloudflare blog posts (2018, 2019, 2022); Q2 2022 earnings call; 10-Q risk factors (Q2 2024); Transparency Report (2H 2023)

### Firm Overview
Cloudflare, Inc. provides internet infrastructure and cybersecurity services through a global edge network and is listed in the United States. Within the 2017–2024 window, its core business remained network security and performance; Web3-related activity was ancillary.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Cloudflare offered developer-facing “Web3” gateways that proxied traffic to IPFS and Ethereum, enabling users to access decentralized content or make Ethereum JSON‑RPC calls without running their own nodes. These services are non‑financial, do not involve custody, and mainly function as networking, security, and developer tooling around blockchain use cases, consistent with business services exposure. In May 2022 the company announced a limited research experiment to operate and fully stake Ethereum validator nodes on its network; this is infrastructure directed at blockchain workloads but not mining or financial services. In 2024 Cloudflare disclosed that traffic for its public IPFS and Ethereum gateways would transition to an IPFS Foundation‑operated gateway, underscoring that these activities were not a core revenue driver. ([blog.cloudflare.com](https://blog.cloudflare.com/crypto-week-2018/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Weights lean to bizserv (0.7) because the company’s IPFS and Ethereum gateways and related developer documentation position Cloudflare as a networking/security provider to crypto projects rather than a custodian, exchange, or asset holder. A smaller infrastr weight (0.3) reflects the operation of blockchain nodes and gateway endpoints, which constitute compute/storage dedicated to blockchain workloads but are ancillary to the company’s broader business; management has also characterized Cloudflare as “agnostic on crypto” and said the firm has not “bet heavily” on it. No evidence supports miners, finserv, or holders exposure in the 2017–2024 period. ([blog.cloudflare.com](https://blog.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-ethereum-gateway/?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Risk factors in 2024 note that, in connection with Web3 products and potential protocol governance, the company expects to hold certain types of cryptocurrency or digital assets; no amounts or material balance‑sheet impact were disclosed for 2017–2024. The gateway‑transition disclosure in the 2H 2023 Transparency Report indicates deprioritization of public Web3 gateways, which may reduce any future exposure via these services. ([d5bp8bijhhba2.cloudfront.net](https://d5bp8bijhhba2.cloudfront.net/pdf/aa22517f91aff33519eb38b7b0c4672baa2507c473e6fa6e92ed9ae5cf5dc40c.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 4, 2022 (Q2 2022), Motley Fool: “I feel like I'm the only kind of agnostic on crypto, where I don't think we have bet heavily on it.” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/08/05/cloudflare-inc-net-q2-2022-earnings-call-transcrip/) (search within transcript) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/08/05/cloudflare-inc-net-q2-2022-earnings-call-transcrip/))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Aug 1, 2024 (Q2 2024 10‑Q): “In connection with our Web3 suite of products and our potential future participation in various Web3 protocol governance activities, we expect to hold certain types of cryptocurrency and similar types of digital assets…” — [SEC filing (PDF)](https://d5bp8bijhhba2.cloudfront.net/pdf/aa22517f91aff33519eb38b7b0c4672baa2507c473e6fa6e92ed9ae5cf5dc40c.pdf) ([d5bp8bijhhba2.cloudfront.net](https://d5bp8bijhhba2.cloudfront.net/pdf/aa22517f91aff33519eb38b7b0c4672baa2507c473e6fa6e92ed9ae5cf5dc40c.pdf))
- 2H 2023 Transparency Report: “In 2024, Cloudflare’s IPFS and Ethereum gateway traffic will be transitioning to IPFS Foundation’s gateway.” — [Cloudflare Transparency Report (PDF)](https://cf-assets.www.cloudflare.com/slt3lc6tev37/6g60HDCGGkpcsUyLYlLt72/53bbd3da918b5fdbba3a731e50672e14/2H_2023_Transparency_report.pdf) ([cf-assets.www.cloudflare.com](https://cf-assets.www.cloudflare.com/slt3lc6tev37/6g60HDCGGkpcsUyLYlLt72/53bbd3da918b5fdbba3a731e50672e14/2H_2023_Transparency_report.pdf?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Sep 17, 2018: Cloudflare launches a public IPFS gateway during “Crypto Week.” — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2018/09/17/cloudflare-launches-decentralized-web-gateway-at-its-first-crypto-week) ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2018/09/17/cloudflare-launches-decentralized-web-gateway-at-its-first-crypto-week?utm_source=openai))
- Jun 19, 2019: “Cloudflare’s Ethereum Gateway” announcement and docs — [Cloudflare blog](https://blog.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-ethereum-gateway/) and [Web3 docs](https://developers.cloudflare.com/web3/how-to/use-ethereum-gateway/) ([blog.cloudflare.com](https://blog.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-ethereum-gateway/?utm_source=openai))
- May 16, 2022: “Proof of Stake and our next experiments in web3” — Cloudflare to launch and fully stake Ethereum validator nodes as a research experiment. — [Cloudflare blog](https://blog.cloudflare.com/next-gen-web3-network) ([blog.cloudflare.com](https://blog.cloudflare.com/next-gen-web3-network?utm_source=openai))

## NERDWALLET A

* Rank: 386 | Expo: 0.11 | id_str: US64082B1026
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filing (2022); Q1 2024 earnings call; NerdWallet crypto ratings methodology; Business Wire press releases (Best‑Of Awards)

### Firm Overview
NerdWallet, Inc. is a U.S.-listed personal finance platform providing editorial content, comparison marketplaces, and tools that help consumers and SMBs evaluate financial products. Within 2017–2024, its core business centered on guidance and lead generation across categories like credit cards, loans, insurance, banking, and investing, with cryptocurrency covered primarily as an editorial/review vertical rather than a financial product it issues or custodies.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management and filings indicate crypto as a content/ratings category on the site, not a product NerdWallet operates. The 2022 10‑K described expansion into new verticals “like Medicare and cryptocurrency,” framing crypto as part of its information and marketplace coverage. NerdWallet maintains a published ratings methodology for cryptocurrency platforms and produces “Best Crypto Exchanges” lists, consistent with advertising/lead‑gen around third‑party providers rather than custody or brokerage. On its Q1 2024 call, management noted that when “investing or crypto take off,” parts of that content may not monetize as well, reinforcing that crypto coverage is largely editorial/top‑of‑funnel rather than a direct revenue product. ([content.edgar-online.com](https://content.edgar-online.com/ExternalLink/EDGAR/0001625278-23-000021.html?dest=nrds-20221231_htm&hash=9994a2cc9890f4ec5aebf441fa3ea2f02d176d09fdb5c85847605678a62c1c4a))

### Weight Assignment
All identified exposure is through non‑financial business services: editorial reviews, ratings, and comparison pages pointing users to third‑party crypto platforms. There is no evidence in 2017–2024 of NerdWallet mining crypto, holding crypto on its balance sheet, running a crypto exchange, or providing custody. Accordingly, bizserv = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. The separate launch of NerdWallet+ with an Atomic‑powered Treasury/automated investing account is not a crypto product and does not alter the crypto classification. ([nerdwallet.com](https://www.nerdwallet.com/l/nerdwallet-plus-treasury?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The precise share of revenue attributable to crypto‑related referral or advertising is not disclosed; management has suggested some crypto content is lower‑monetizing, implying immateriality. Crypto “Best‑Of” awards and exchange reviews exist, but detailed financial contribution by sub‑vertical is not broken out; RevShare is coded conservatively as minor. No transcript passages explicitly reference crypto custody, mining, or balance‑sheet holdings. ([investors.nerdwallet.com](https://investors.nerdwallet.com/static-files/c39b5e7a-621e-452e-a694-780ebe80c15c))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Apr 25, 2024 (Q1 2024): “When things like investing or crypto take off, there's parts of that content that also doesn't monetize as well.” — [Refinitiv/IR transcript (PDF)](https://investors.nerdwallet.com/static-files/c39b5e7a-621e-452e-a694-780ebe80c15c) ([investors.nerdwallet.com](https://investors.nerdwallet.com/static-files/c39b5e7a-621e-452e-a694-780ebe80c15c))

Bragg Gaming Group Inc. is a Canada-based B2B iGaming technology and content provider whose offerings include remote game server technology, proprietary and exclusive online casino content, and platform/PAM and turnkey services for regulated markets in North America and Europe. Its filings and news releases consistently describe the company as a content-driven iGaming supplier rather than an operator. Across earnings calls and filings in 2017–2024, management discussions center on iGaming content, distribution, and platform services, with no substantive references to cryptocurrencies, mining, crypto custody, or blockchain-based products. A search within the FY 2024 Form 40‑F returned no matches for “crypto,” “bitcoin,” or “blockchain,” reinforcing the absence of crypto-related activity. Earnings calls similarly focus on PAM/turnkey customers and content strategy. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1867834/000155837025004123/brag-20241231x40f.htm))

No credible evidence indicates exposure via the six crypto mechanisms. Filings and call transcripts emphasize B2B iGaming technology and content, not crypto holdings, mining, financial services, non‑financial crypto services, or mining-targeted infrastructure. Therefore, all mechanism weights are set to zero with unclass = 1.0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1867834/000155837025004123/brag-20241231x40f.htm))

### Firm Overview
Acacia Research Corporation is a U.S.-listed holding company that acquires and operates businesses, historically active in intellectual property monetization and more recently in industrial and energy verticals. In October 2021 it acquired Printronix, a printing technology business, and in November 2023 it acquired a majority stake in Benchmark Energy II, an oil and gas operator in Texas and Oklahoma. ([printronix.com](https://printronix.com/emea/acacia-research-acquires-printronix-a-leading-printing-technology-business/?utm_source=openai))

Across earnings calls and SEC filings during 2017–2024, there are no substantive references to cryptocurrency holdings, mining activities, crypto financial services (exchanges, custody, payments), non‑financial crypto services, or mining‑targeted infrastructure. A search within the FY 2022 Form 10‑K returned no matches for “crypto,” “bitcoin,” or “blockchain,” and discussion centers on IP licensing, Printronix operations, and corporate investments. Earnings calls similarly focus on portfolio monetization, Printronix, and Benchmark Energy rather than digital assets or blockchain initiatives. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/934549/000093454923000031/actg-20221231.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Because no credible evidence indicates exposure through any of the six crypto mechanisms, all mechanism weights are set to zero with unclass = 1.0. Filings and calls consistently describe a strategy around acquiring and operating traditional businesses (e.g., Printronix, Benchmark Energy) and IP monetization, with no crypto‑related operations or treasury holdings disclosed. ([printronix.com](https://printronix.com/emea/acacia-research-acquires-printronix-a-leading-printing-technology-business/?utm_source=openai))

None identified.

* Primary sources: S&P Dow Jones Indices press releases (2021); S&P Global Ratings press release (Dec 2023); Q2 2021 and Q4 2023 earnings calls; FY 2024 Form 10-K

### Firm Overview
S&P Global Inc. is a U.S.-based provider of credit ratings, benchmarks, and financial data and analytics operating globally across divisions including Ratings, Indices, Market Intelligence, and Commodity Insights. Its crypto-related activity centers on launching digital asset indices and publishing analytical assessments covering stablecoins within the sample period.

### Exposure Channels Identified
S&P Dow Jones Indices, an S&P Global division, launched the S&P Digital Market Indices in May 2021, including benchmarks for Bitcoin, Ethereum, and broader crypto baskets; these are benchmark/index licensing activities and do not involve custody or trading of digital assets. In December 2023, S&P Global Ratings introduced a Stablecoin Stability Assessment framework that analyzes the ability of major fiat-referenced tokens to maintain their pegs; again, this is an analytical/rating product, not an investable product or custody service. Under the taxonomy, these are non‑financial services delivered to the crypto ecosystem (index/benchmark licensing, research, and ratings), which fit the bizserv category rather than finserv. Management discussed both the crypto indices and the nascent size/opportunity on earnings calls during the period. ([spglobal.com](https://www.spglobal.com/spdji/en/index-launches/article/sp-dow-jones-indices-launches-cryptocurrency-index-series-including-sp-bitcoin-index/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
All observed crypto exposure is through non‑financial services: index creation/licensing and analytical/rating products (stablecoin assessments). There is no evidence that S&P Global holds crypto on its balance sheet, runs exchange, custody, payments, or issues/manages crypto ETPs, nor that it supplies mining-targeted hardware/compute. Accordingly, bizserv is set to 1.0 and all other mechanism weights to 0.0. Revenue and asset exposure from these activities appear peripheral relative to the consolidated company, so RevShare is coded 1 and AssetShare 0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/64040/000006404025000052/spgi-20241231.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
No indication of direct handling or custody of digital assets was found; index and rating activities are clearly service-based. The company does not disclose a standalone revenue line for crypto indices/assessments, so materiality is inferred as minor from qualitative disclosures and scale of consolidated revenues.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jul 29, 2021 (Q2 2021), Motley Fool: “We announced the first indices… using pricing from Lukka… launched S&P Bitcoin, S&P Ethereum and a Cryptocurrency MegaCap Index… [and] a couple that provide broad index….” — [Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/07/29/sp-global-inc-spgi-q2-2021-earnings-call-transcrip/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/07/29/sp-global-inc-spgi-q2-2021-earnings-call-transcrip/))
- Feb 8, 2024 (Q4 2023), “we… leveraged our expertise in blockchain technology and cryptocurrencies to launch the first stablecoin stability assessment in late 2023.” — [Transcript](https://thetranscript.net/transcript/5764/sandp-global-q4-2023-earnings-call-transcript) ([thetranscript.net](https://thetranscript.net/transcript/5764/sandp-global-q4-2023-earnings-call-transcript))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Feb 2025 (FY 2024 Form 10‑K): Indices segment description and revenue drivers (benchmark licensing; no crypto custody/issuance). — [SEC filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/64040/000006404025000052/spgi-20241231.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/64040/000006404025000052/spgi-20241231.htm))
- May 4, 2021: “S&P Dow Jones Indices Launches Cryptocurrency Index Series Including S&P Bitcoin Index.” — [S&P DJI press release](https://www.spglobal.com/spdji/en/index-launches/article/sp-dow-jones-indices-launches-cryptocurrency-index-series-including-sp-bitcoin-index/) ([spglobal.com](https://www.spglobal.com/spdji/en/index-launches/article/sp-dow-jones-indices-launches-cryptocurrency-index-series-including-sp-bitcoin-index/))
- Dec 12, 2023: “S&P Global Ratings Launches Stablecoin Stability Assessment.” — [S&P Global press release](https://press.spglobal.com/2023-12-12-S-P-Global-Ratings-Launches-Stablecoin-Stability-Assessment) ([press.spglobal.com](https://press.spglobal.com/2023-12-12-S-P-Global-Ratings-Launches-Stablecoin-Stability-Assessment))

**Financial news / other:**
- Dec 13, 2023: Coverage of S&P Global Ratings’ new stablecoin assessment and initial scores. — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/policy/2023/12/13/tether-scores-second-lowest-rank-in-sp-global-stablecoin-stability-assessment) ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/policy/2023/12/13/tether-scores-second-lowest-rank-in-sp-global-stablecoin-stability-assessment))
- Dec 3, 2020: Announcement that S&P DJI would launch crypto indices in 2021 (with Lukka data). — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2020/12/03/sp-dow-jones-indices-to-launch-crypto-indexes-in-2021) ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2020/12/03/sp-dow-jones-indices-to-launch-crypto-indexes-in-2021))

## NCB FINANCIAL GROUP

* Rank: 390 | Expo: 0.11 | id_str: JME201700064
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Annual reports (2022–2024); Bank of Jamaica press releases (2021–2022); Jamaica Observer (Apr 2022); MoneyGram press release (Mar 2023)

### Firm Overview
NCB Financial Group Limited is a Jamaica‑headquartered financial services conglomerate with operations across the Caribbean spanning banking, payments, wealth management, and insurance. Its digital‑asset exposure is through TFOB (2021) Limited’s Lynk mobile wallet, which supports domestic payments and remittances and has been used to distribute Jamaica’s central bank digital currency (JAM‑DEX). ([myncb.com](https://www.myncb.com/annualreport2024))

### Exposure Channels Identified
NCB introduced the Lynk mobile wallet in December 2021; the company reported early scale and disclosed that Lynk was the only digital wallet integrated with JAM‑DEX at that time. Bank of Jamaica confirms that during the phased rollout, National Commercial Bank (through Lynk) offered JAM‑DEX to the public. In March 2023, MoneyGram announced a partnership enabling Lynk users to receive remittances directly to the wallet, expanding Lynk’s crypto/digital‑asset‑adjacent financial services footprint. These activities constitute provision of crypto/digital‑asset financial services (wallet/custody of CBDC, payments and remittances) rather than asset holding or mining. ([myncb.com](https://www.myncb.com/NCBFinancialGroup/media/NCB-Financial-Group/Reports/NCBFG_AnnualReport_2022-web2.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
All identified exposure is via financial services delivered to users transacting in a digital asset (JAM‑DEX) and through a regulated wallet/remittance platform (Lynk). There is no evidence that NCBFG holds cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet, engages in mining, issues crypto ETPs, or supplies mining‑targeted hardware/compute. Accordingly, finserv is set to 1.0 and all other mechanism weights to 0.0. Given the group’s consolidated scale and disclosures, revenue attributable to Lynk/CBDC appears minor, so RevShare is coded 1 and AssetShare 0. ([myncb.com](https://www.myncb.com/annualreport2024))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
No publicly accessible earnings‑call transcripts with crypto/CBDC commentary were retrievable; investor briefings are webcast but transcripts are not posted, so certainty is capped at 2 per protocol. Classification relies on company reports and central‑bank/public‑news sources; NCBFG does not break out Lynk/CBDC revenue, so materiality is inferred as minor. Note: the exposure here involves a CBDC (JAM‑DEX); if a narrower taxonomy excludes CBDCs from “crypto,” the effective exposure would be lower, but still falls under digital‑asset financial services in this framework. ([myncb.com](https://www.myncb.com/News/NCB-Finanacial-Group-reports-79-net-profit-inc-1?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 9, 2024 (Q3 2024) investor briefing recap; company notes a virtual Investor Briefing was held (no public transcript posted). — [Company news](https://www.myncb.com/News/NCB-Finanacial-Group-reports-79-net-profit-inc-1) ([myncb.com](https://www.myncb.com/News/NCB-Finanacial-Group-reports-79-net-profit-inc-1?utm_source=openai))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- FY 2024 Annual Report (CEO message): “Lynk… facilitated over 1.3 million transactions in 2024, with a total transaction value of $14.5 billion. This year, Lynk introduced 24/7 remittance services…” — [NCBFG AR 2024](https://www.myncb.com/annualreport2024/) ([myncb.com](https://www.myncb.com/annualreport2024))
- FY 2022 Annual Report: “Additionally, Lynk is the only digital wallet integrated with JAM‑DEX, Jamaica’s central bank digital currency.” — [NCBFG AR 2022](https://www.myncb.com/NCBFinancialGroup/media/NCB-Financial-Group/Reports/NCBFG_AnnualReport_2022-web2.pdf) ([myncb.com](https://www.myncb.com/NCBFinancialGroup/media/NCB-Financial-Group/Reports/NCBFG_AnnualReport_2022-web2.pdf?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Bank of Jamaica: “JAM‑DEX phased rollout… Currently National Commercial Bank (NCB) offers JAM‑DEX through Lynk, its digital wallet.” — [BOJ notice](https://boj.org.jm/jam-dex-phased-rollout-underway/) ([boj.org.jm](https://boj.org.jm/jam-dex-phased-rollout-underway/?utm_source=openai))
- Apr 13, 2022: “Currently, National Commercial Bank (NCB) is the only wallet provider operating in the market through its digital wallet, Lynk.” — [Jamaica Observer](https://www.jamaicaobserver.com/2022/04/13/boj-to-widen-wallet-availability/) ([jamaicaobserver.com](https://www.jamaicaobserver.com/2022/04/13/boj-to-widen-wallet-availability/?utm_source=openai))

## DIGITAL CHINA HDG.

* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Annual reports (2020, 2024); DCITS product pages (Sm@rtGAS; Digital RMB scenarios); 2019 interim investor call deck

### Firm Overview
Digital China Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong–listed technology group focused on big data operations, smart city solutions, and supply‑chain/IT services across mainland China and overseas. Through its fintech arm DCITS (an associate/major subsidiary during the period), the group commercializes software platforms for banks and enterprises, including blockchain and Digital RMB (DCEP) scenario solutions. ([hkexnews.hk](https://www.hkexnews.hk/listedco/listconews/sehk/2021/0423/2021042300645.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management disclosures indicate the group develops blockchain platforms and “digital currency (DCEP) solutions” for financial‑sector clients via DCITS, notably the Sm@rtGAS blockchain technology platform used in supply‑chain finance and other applications. These activities are technology provision and systems integration for institutions; there is no evidence the company itself issues, trades, custodies, mines, or holds cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet. DCITS also publishes Digital RMB scenario application materials, reinforcing that exposure is to CBDC/enterprise blockchain implementations rather than speculative crypto assets. Accordingly, exposure is coded as non‑financial business services to the digital‑asset ecosystem. ([hkexnews.hk](https://www.hkexnews.hk/listedco/listconews/sehk/2021/0423/2021042300645.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Because the identified link to digital assets is the provision of blockchain/DCEP software and integration for third‑party financial institutions, bizserv is set to 1.0 and all other mechanism weights to 0.0. Filings do not show crypto assets held or crypto‑related financial products operated by the company, and there is no mining or crypto‑specific hardware focus. Given the breadth of group activities, any crypto‑adjacent revenue is inferred to be minor relative to consolidated revenue (RevShare = 1; AssetShare = 0). ([dcholdings.com](https://www.dcholdings.com/img/cms/2024report/2024%20Annual%20Report.pdf)
.) 

### Ambiguities / Limitations
No publicly accessible, full earnings-call transcripts with crypto commentary were found for 2017–2024; the closest available record is the 2019 interim investor teleconference deck posted via HKEX, so certainty is capped at 2 per protocol. Also, much of the company’s disclosed activity relates to enterprise blockchain and China’s Digital RMB (CBDC) scenarios rather than decentralized cryptocurrencies; if a taxonomy excludes CBDCs from “crypto,” effective exposure would be lower, but still falls under non‑financial services to digital-asset infrastructure.

**Earnings calls:**
- Sep 2, 2019: Investor teleconference deck for H1 2019 interim results; “Tele-conference to report the unaudited interim results of the Company for the six months ended 30 June 2019.” — [HKEX filing (Investor Conference deck)](https://www1.hkexnews.hk/listedco/listconews/sehk/2019/0902/ltn201909021871.pdf)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**

- FY 2020 Annual Report: DCITS described as fintech-focused; “CloudCore is a professional solution provider of internet banking… focusing on zero-touch financial services on mobile banking, internet banking, transaction banking, and direct banking.” — [Digital China Holdings 2020 AR](https://www.hkexnews.hk/listedco/listconews/sehk/2021/0423/2021042300645.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- DCITS product page: “Sm@rtGAS… 金融级的区块链基础技术平台… 结合支付结算、数字货币、贸易融资、数字票据、商业积分等应用场景推出一整套的金融区块链综合解决方案.” — [DCITS Sm@rtGAS (Blockchain platform)](https://www.dcits.com/product-and-solution/Data-Intelligence-Product-Suite/product-713-4889-1.html)
- DCITS solution page: “数字人民币场景应用… ‘吾卡’平台… 基于区块链的各方数据共享、同步及存证.” — [DCITS Digital RMB scenarios](https://www.dcits.com/product-and-solution/Digital-Yuan-Scenario-Applications)

## GLOBAL PAYMENTS

* Rank: 392 | Expo: 0.10 | id_str: US37940X1028
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filing (FY 2024); Q1 2022 earnings call; Bakkt press release (Feb 2022)

### Firm Overview
Global Payments Inc. is a U.S.-based payments technology and software company that provides merchant acquiring, ecommerce, and issuer processing solutions globally. Its activities center on enabling card and digital payments for merchants and financial institutions.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management disclosed enabling cryptocurrency-related functionality via partners rather than directly holding or issuing digital assets. On May 2, 2022, the company said it would “support [PayPal’s] cryptocurrencies for the first time” and announced a strategic alliance with Bakkt to enable cryptocurrency redemption in bankcard loyalty programs and to expand banking-as-a-service offerings to include consumer access to cryptocurrency. ([d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net](https://d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net/_ab3fb5b38abfb02a5164d778cf6df083/globalpayments/db/2250/20737/webcast_transcript/Global-Payments-Inc.%28GPN-US%29-Q1-2022-Earnings-Call-2-May-2022-8_00-AM-ET-%281%29.pdf))  
The Bakkt alliance was also detailed in a contemporaneous press release describing the loyalty redemption and BaaS crypto access use cases. ([investors.bakkt.com](https://investors.bakkt.com/news/news-details/2022/Bakkt-and-Global-Payments-Announce-Strategic-Alliance/default.aspx?utm_source=openai))  
There is no evidence in filings that Global Payments mines or holds crypto on its own balance sheet; the 2024 Form 10-K focuses on merchant and issuer solutions with no disclosed digital-asset holdings. ([investors.globalpayments.com](https://investors.globalpayments.com/financial-information/all-sec-filings/content/0001123360-25-000011/gpn-20241231.htm))

### Weight Assignment
The identified link to crypto is through financial services enablement: supporting PayPal’s crypto checkout capabilities and collaborating with Bakkt to let bank clients’ end users redeem and access crypto within loyalty/BaaS programs. These are crypto financial services delivered via partners and rails, not custody or investment by Global Payments itself. Therefore, finserv is set to 1.0 and all other mechanism weights to 0.0. Given the scale of Global Payments’ broader business and absence of discrete crypto segment disclosures, crypto-related revenue is assessed as minor (<~5%; RevShare = 1) and there are no crypto assets on the balance sheet (AssetShare = 0). ([d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net](https://d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net/_ab3fb5b38abfb02a5164d778cf6df083/globalpayments/db/2250/20737/webcast_transcript/Global-Payments-Inc.%28GPN-US%29-Q1-2022-Earnings-Call-2-May-2022-8_00-AM-ET-%281%29.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The company’s role appears largely as an integrator/distributor of partners’ crypto capabilities; uptake and economics are not broken out in filings, so materiality is inferred as small. Evidence is anchored in at least one earnings call plus corroborating press; later filings do not quantify crypto volumes or revenues, which limits precision on RevShare.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 2, 2022 (Q1 2022): “...we expect to be live with PayPal this quarter in new geographies and additional verticals and will support their cryptocurrencies for the first time...” and “We are pleased to announce a strategic alliance with Bakkt… starting with enabling cryptocurrency redemption in customer loyalty programs… [and] expanding our banking-as-a-service offerings to include cryptocurrency.” — [Global Payments Q1 2022 transcript (FactSet/CallStreet)](https://d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net/_ab3fb5b38abfb02a5164d778cf6df083/globalpayments/db/2250/20737/webcast_transcript/Global-Payments-Inc.%28GPN-US%29-Q1-2022-Earnings-Call-2-May-2022-8_00-AM-ET-%281%29.pdf) ([d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net](https://d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net/_ab3fb5b38abfb02a5164d778cf6df083/globalpayments/db/2250/20737/webcast_transcript/Global-Payments-Inc.%28GPN-US%29-Q1-2022-Earnings-Call-2-May-2022-8_00-AM-ET-%281%29.pdf))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- FY 2024 Form 10-K: Business description and segment reporting; no disclosure of crypto asset holdings on balance sheet. — [Global Payments 2024 10-K (filed Feb 2025)](https://investors.globalpayments.com/financial-information/all-sec-filings/content/0001123360-25-000011/gpn-20241231.htm) ([investors.globalpayments.com](https://investors.globalpayments.com/financial-information/all-sec-filings/content/0001123360-25-000011/gpn-20241231.htm))

- May 2022: Bakkt Q1 2022 results reiterate the Global Payments alliance and use cases. — [Bakkt Q1 2022 results release](https://investors.bakkt.com/news/news-details/2022/Bakkt-Reports-First-Quarter-2022-Results/default.aspx) ([investors.bakkt.com](https://investors.bakkt.com/news/news-details/2022/Bakkt-Reports-First-Quarter-2022-Results/default.aspx?utm_source=openai))

## SYNCHRONOSS TECHNOLOGIES

* Rank: 395 | Expo: 0.10 | id_str: US87157B4005
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filing (FY 2023); Q2 2018 earnings call; Q4 2022 earnings call; Q1 2023 earnings call; Company press releases (2018–2019); Company blog (2021)

### Firm Overview
Synchronoss Technologies is a U.S.-based software company best known for white‑label personal cloud platforms and, historically, messaging and digital products for telecom operators. In October 2023, the company divested its Messaging and NetworkX businesses to focus on cloud‑centric solutions, as reflected in its FY 2023 Form 10‑K. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1131554/000113155424000018/sncr-20231231.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024 materials, there is no evidence that the company holds cryptocurrencies, mines digital assets, or operates crypto financial services such as exchanges, brokerage, custody, or crypto‑backed investment products. Management communications and filings emphasize personal cloud subscribers, carrier partnerships, and feature development (e.g., AI‑enabled photo tools) rather than crypto activities. While Synchronoss collaborated on blockchain‑based, cross‑carrier payment proofs‑of‑concept with SoftBank and TBCASoft and discussed private‑blockchain use cases in a 2021 blog, these initiatives relate to enterprise/telecom applications and do not involve cryptocurrencies or crypto balance‑sheet items. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/SNCR/documents/transcripts/q4-2022))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of cryptocurrency holdings, mining, or direct crypto financial services in filings and earnings communications through December 2024—and the fact that the identified blockchain efforts were permissioned/enterprise pilots not tied to crypto assets—the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). No evidence indicates a crypto‑sensitive investor base, and no disclosures suggest revenue or assets derived from digital assets within the sample window. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1131554/000113155424000018/sncr-20231231.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Synchronoss’ 2018–2019 blockchain PoCs and a 2021 blog indicate technical exploration of distributed ledgers in telecom contexts; however, these do not constitute cryptocurrency exposure under this framework and appear non‑material to revenues or assets. Some transcript repositories are paywalled or intermittently blocked, but accessible transcripts and the FY 2023 10‑K were sufficient to assess crypto exposure. ([synchronosstechnologiesinc.gcs-web.com](https://synchronosstechnologiesinc.gcs-web.com/news-releases/news-release-details/softbank-synchronoss-and-tbcasoft-partner-proof-concept-rcs-and?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 10, 2018 (Q2 2018), transcript: “Our cloud offering is a comprehensive, value‑added solution… helping our target customers improve revenue and customer loyalty.” — [Fox Business transcript](https://www.foxbusiness.com/markets/synchronoss-technologies-sncr-q2-2018-earnings-conference-call-transcript) ([foxbusiness.com](https://www.foxbusiness.com/markets/synchronoss-technologies-sncr-q2-2018-earnings-conference-call-transcript))
- Mar 2023 (Q4 2022 call): “We’d be adopting Verizon’s private storage infrastructure to manage all digital content for Verizon customers using the Synchronoss Personal Cloud platform.” — [Fintool transcript](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/SNCR/documents/transcripts/q4-2022) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/SNCR/documents/transcripts/q4-2022))
- May 2023 (Q1 2023 call): “We expanded high‑margin cloud revenue… and exceeded the milestone of 10 million global cloud subscribers.” — [Fintool transcript](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/SNCR/documents/transcripts/q1-2023) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/SNCR/documents/transcripts/q1-2023))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- FY 2023 Form 10‑K: Focus on “Synchronoss Personal Cloud”; divestiture of Messaging and NetworkX businesses (Oct 31, 2023) to “solely focus on providing cloud‑centric solutions.” — [SEC 10‑K (filed 2024)](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1131554/000113155424000018/sncr-20231231.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1131554/000113155424000018/sncr-20231231.htm))

**Financial news / other:**
- Sep 2018: SoftBank, Synchronoss and TBCASoft announce RCS and blockchain‑based mobile payments PoC; demo at MWC Americas. — [Company press release](https://synchronosstechnologiesinc.gcs-web.com/news-releases/news-release-details/softbank-synchronoss-and-tbcasoft-partner-proof-concept-rcs-and) ([synchronosstechnologiesinc.gcs-web.com](https://synchronosstechnologiesinc.gcs-web.com/news-releases/news-release-details/softbank-synchronoss-and-tbcasoft-partner-proof-concept-rcs-and?utm_source=openai))
- Jan 2019: Synchronoss wins “Most Innovative Blockchain for Telecoms Strategy” (collaboration with SoftBank/TBCASoft). — [Company press release](https://synchronosstechnologiesinc.gcs-web.com/news-releases/news-release-details/synchronoss-wins-2019-leading-lights-award-most-innovative) ([synchronosstechnologiesinc.gcs-web.com](https://synchronosstechnologiesinc.gcs-web.com/news-releases/news-release-details/synchronoss-wins-2019-leading-lights-award-most-innovative?utm_source=openai))
- Jun 10, 2021: Company blog notes work on “blockchain‑based projects,” including payments on consortium blockchains and an RCS payments PoC using a private blockchain. — [Synchronoss blog](https://www.synchronoss.com/the-benefits-of-blockchain/) ([synchronoss.com](https://www.synchronoss.com/the-benefits-of-blockchain/))

</small>

## CHANGE HEALTHCARE DEAD - DELIST.04/10/22

* Rank: 396 | Expo: 0.10 | id_str: US15912K1007
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (FY 2021–FY 2022); Q3–Q4 FY 2020 earnings calls; Company/industry coverage on blockchain pilots (2018–2019); Acquisition-close news (Oct 2022)

### Firm Overview
Change Healthcare Inc. was a U.S.-based healthcare technology company providing revenue cycle, claims clearinghouse, and related network solutions to payers and providers. It was acquired by UnitedHealth Group’s Optum and delisted in October 2022.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across filings and earnings materials in 2017–2022, there is no evidence that the company held cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet, mined digital assets, or provided crypto financial services such as exchanges, brokerage, custody, or crypto-backed investment products. The firm did publicize deployment of a permissioned blockchain (Hyperledger Fabric) within its Intelligent Healthcare Network beginning in 2018 to improve claims transparency and auditability; this relates to distributed ledger technology rather than cryptocurrency activity. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1756497/000119312521175345/d156422d10k.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of cryptocurrency holdings, mining, or direct crypto financial services—and the fact that the company’s blockchain initiatives were permissioned/enterprise DLT use cases not involving digital assets—the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). The October 2022 acquisition and delisting further limit subsequent transcript coverage, but available filings and credible industry sources are sufficient to conclude no crypto-asset exposure under this framework. ([fiercehealthcare.com](https://www.fiercehealthcare.com/payers/unitedhealth-closes-acquisition-change-healthcare?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Blockchain announcements in 2018–2019 could be conflated with cryptocurrency by casual observers; however, sources indicate these were Hyperledger Fabric–based, permissioned implementations without native crypto tokens or custody. Change Healthcare’s delisting in October 2022 reduces post-2022 primary disclosures. The 2024 ransomware incident affecting the business does not constitute corporate crypto-asset exposure. ([lfdecentralizedtrust.org](https://www.lfdecentralizedtrust.org/case-studies/change-healthcare-case-study?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jun 4, 2020 (Q4 FY 2020) transcript: “We closed out the fiscal year with another strong quarter… delivering over $300 million in free cash flow for the year.” — [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/06/04/change-healthcare-inc-chng-q4-2020-earnings-call-t.aspx) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/06/04/change-healthcare-inc-chng-q4-2020-earnings-call-t.aspx?utm_source=openai))
- Feb 13, 2020 (Q3 FY 2020) transcript: “Adjusted free cash flow was $151 million for the three months ended December 31, 2020…” — [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/02/13/change-healthcare-inc-chng-q3-2020-earnings-call-t.aspx) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/02/13/change-healthcare-inc-chng-q3-2020-earnings-call-t.aspx?utm_source=openai))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- FY 2021 Form 10-K: Business description and segment disclosures (Software & Analytics; Network Solutions; Technology-Enabled Services). — [SEC 10-K (filed May 27, 2021)](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1756497/000119312521175345/d156422d10k.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1756497/000119312521175345/d156422d10k.htm?utm_source=openai))
- FY 2022 Form 10-K: Business overview and references to the pending UnitedHealth/Optum transaction. — [SEC 10-K (filed May 26, 2022)](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1756497/000175649722000007/chng-20220331x10k.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1756497/000175649722000007/chng-20220331x10k.htm?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Jan 12, 2018: Company announces general availability of Intelligent Healthcare Network with blockchain (Hyperledger Fabric) for claims transparency. — [Healthcare Innovation summary of PR](https://www.hcinnovationgroup.com/policy-value-based-care/article/13008936/change-healthcare-announces-general-availability-of-enterprise-scale-blockchain-solution-for-healthcare) ([hcinnovationgroup.com](https://www.hcinnovationgroup.com/policy-value-based-care/article/13008936/change-healthcare-announces-general-availability-of-enterprise-scale-blockchain-solution-for-healthcare?utm_source=openai))
- Jun 24, 2019: Hyperledger case study details Change Healthcare’s Fabric-based deployment and throughput claims. — [Hyperledger Foundation](https://www.lfdecentralizedtrust.org/blog/2019/06/24/change-healthcare-50-million-transactions-a-day-using-hyperledger-fabric) ([lfdecentralizedtrust.org](https://www.lfdecentralizedtrust.org/blog/2019/06/24/change-healthcare-50-million-transactions-a-day-using-hyperledger-fabric?utm_source=openai))
- Oct 3, 2022: UnitedHealth Group closes acquisition of Change Healthcare; Change suspends trading/delists. — [FierceHealthcare](https://www.fiercehealthcare.com/payers/unitedhealth-closes-acquisition-change-healthcare) | [Healthcare Dive](https://www.healthcaredive.com/news/unitedhealth-completes-13b-change-healthcare-buy-doj/633154/) ([fiercehealthcare.com](https://www.fiercehealthcare.com/payers/unitedhealth-closes-acquisition-change-healthcare?utm_source=openai))

## DAVE A

* Rank: 397 | Expo: 0.10 | id_str: US23834J2015
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2022–2024); March 21, 2022 Form 8-K; Q3–Q4 2022 earnings calls; Company news releases (Mar 2022, Jan 2024); Reuters coverage (Nov 2022)

### Firm Overview
Dave Inc. is a U.S.-based neobank offering consumer financial services such as cash advances (ExtraCash), checking, and debit card products, publicly listed on Nasdaq under “DAVE.” Filings emphasize core operations around ExtraCash originations, card spend, and related fee/transaction revenues rather than crypto-asset activities. ([investors.dave.com](https://investors.dave.com/news-events/events))

### Exposure Channels Identified
In March 2022 Dave announced a strategic partnership with FTX US and a $100 million convertible note from FTX Ventures; a concurrent Services Agreement contemplated enabling Dave customers to open FTX accounts and transact in eligible cryptocurrencies, with FTX as the exclusive crypto services provider during the term. However, following FTX’s collapse in November 2022, Dave stated that no customers were exposed and that no products had been launched in partnership with FTX. Dave subsequently repurchased the outstanding FTX Ventures convertible note at a discount in January 2024, further reducing any residual tie to the crypto platform. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1841408/000119312522080609/d325306d8k.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Despite the 2022 Services Agreement indicating an intent to integrate crypto functionality via FTX US, the company did not launch crypto financial services during the 2017–2024 sample period and later unwound the financing relationship after FTX’s failure. There is no evidence of crypto holdings, mining, custody/exchange operations, or crypto-backed products reflected in Dave’s revenues or assets over the window. Accordingly, the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/mark-cubanbacked-fintech-dave-says-no-customers-exposed-to-ftx-2947248?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The March 21, 2022 announcements (press release and 8-K) could be misconstrued as ongoing crypto activity; Dave’s 8-K described functionality to enable crypto trading via FTX, but the company later disclosed that no such product was launched and customers were not exposed. The note repurchase in January 2024 ended the FTX financing link; if Dave were to introduce crypto features after December 2024, classification would need revisiting. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1841408/000119312522080609/d325306d8k.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Mar 6, 2023 (Q4 2022) transcript: “We also grew revenue by nearly 34%… The anchor of our banking value proposition ExtraCash provides up to $500 of short-term interest-free advances to members within minutes of joining.” — [Dave IR transcript PDF](https://investors.dave.com/static-files/f460e5d5-64da-45c7-ac3b-65842e678a18) ([investors.dave.com](https://investors.dave.com/static-files/f460e5d5-64da-45c7-ac3b-65842e678a18))
- Nov 10, 2022 (Q3 2022) transcript: “Dave’s value prop of no-hidden fee banking with up to $500 of interest free ExtraCash continues to resonate… [record] non-GAAP total operating revenue of approximately $58.6 million, +41% Y/Y.” — [Dave IR transcript PDF](https://investors.dave.com/static-files/f6573a03-0ef7-4193-8213-e49830e373f2) ([investors.dave.com](https://investors.dave.com/static-files/f6573a03-0ef7-4193-8213-e49830e373f2))

**Filings (10-K / 8-K / 10-Q):**
- Mar 21, 2022 Form 8-K: “The Services Agreement allows the Company’s customers to establish accounts with FTX to place orders for eligible cryptocurrencies… FTX will be the Company’s exclusive provider of such cryptocurrency services.” — [SEC 8-K](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1841408/000119312522080609/d325306d8k.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1841408/000119312522080609/d325306d8k.htm))
- Q1 2022 Form 10-Q: Discloses $100.0 million convertible note with FTX Ventures Ltd. entered March 21, 2022. — [SEC 10-Q](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1841408/000119312522150058/d340063d10q.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1841408/000119312522150058/d340063d10q.htm?utm_source=openai))
- FY 2024 Form 10-K: Notes January 2024 repurchase of the $105.7 million outstanding balance of the FTX Ventures convertible note for $71.0 million and related gain. — [SEC 10-K](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1841408/000095017025032164/dave-20241231.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1841408/000095017025032164/dave-20241231.htm))

**Financial news / other:**
- Mar 21, 2022: Dave announces strategic partnership with FTX US and $100 million investment from FTX Ventures, exploring digital asset payments. — [Dave IR news release](https://investors.dave.com/news-releases/news-release-details/ftx-ventures-invests-100-million-dave-companies-form-strategic) ([investors.dave.com](https://investors.dave.com/news-releases/news-release-details/ftx-ventures-invests-100-million-dave-companies-form-strategic?utm_source=openai))
- Nov 2022: “Dave says no customers were exposed to FTX and it has not launched any products in partnership with [FTX].” — [Reuters via Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/mark-cubanbacked-fintech-dave-says-no-customers-exposed-to-ftx-2947248) ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/mark-cubanbacked-fintech-dave-says-no-customers-exposed-to-ftx-2947248?utm_source=openai))
- Jan 5, 2024: Dave announces closing of repurchase of FTX Ventures convertible note for $71 million. — [Dave IR news release](https://investors.dave.com/news-releases/news-release-details/daves-repurchase-ftx-convertible-note-71-million-finalized) ([investors.dave.com](https://investors.dave.com/news-releases/news-release-details/daves-repurchase-ftx-convertible-note-71-million-finalized?utm_source=openai))
- Mar 21, 2022: Coverage of FTX/Dave partnership to enable crypto payments. — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2022/03/21/ft
- Mar 21, 2022: Coverage of FTX Ventures’ $100M investment and partnership to explore crypto payments. — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2022/03/21/ftx-invests-100m-in-banking-app-dave-forms-partnership-for-crypto-payments)
- Jan 5, 2024: Dave finalizes repurchase of the FTX Ventures note for $71 million (closed Jan 29, 2024). — [Dave IR news release](https://investors.dave.com/news-releases/news-release-details/daves-repurchase-ftx-convertible-note-71-million-finalized)
- May 7, 2024: Q1 2024 results release notes cash decrease driven by repurchase of the FTX Ventures note for $71 million (33% discount). — [Dave IR press release](https://investors.dave.com/news-releases/news-release-details/dave-reports-record-first-quarter-2024-results)

</small>

## THERATECHNOLOGIES DEAD - DELIST.29/09/25

* Rank: 398 | Expo: 0.10 | id_str: CA88338H7040
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: SEC/SEDAR filings (2019–2023), Q4 2019 and Q2 2020 earnings calls, FY 2022 results press release

### Firm Overview
Theratechnologies Inc. is a Montreal-based commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company focused primarily on HIV-related therapies, including EGRIFTA SV (tesamorelin) for HIV-associated lipodystrophy and Trogarzo (ibalizumab) for heavily treatment-experienced HIV patients. Its public communications during 2017–2024 consistently center on these pharmaceutical products, R&D programs, and financing matters. ([theratech.com](https://www.theratech.com/news-releases/news-release-details/theratechnologies-reports-financial-results-and-business-updates/))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across multiple earnings-call transcripts and company filings from 2017–2024, there is no evidence that the company held cryptocurrencies, mined digital assets, offered crypto financial services, provided crypto-focused business services, or supplied mining/infrastructure hardware or capacity. Management commentary and disclosures focus on product sales of EGRIFTA SV and Trogarzo, R&D progress (e.g., tesamorelin in NASH, SORT1+ oncology platform), and operating metrics. The absence of crypto-related activities in these materials indicates no material linkage between Theratechnologies’ equity performance and crypto markets during the sample period. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/02/25/theratechnologies-inc-thtx-q4-2019-earnings-call-t.aspx))

### Weight Assignment
Given the lack of substantiated crypto activity in transcripts, filings, or press releases over 2017–2024, Theratechnologies is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). No weights are assigned to holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr, and the investor-base amplifier is set to 0 due to no evidence that its shareholder base trades the stock as a crypto proxy. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/02/25/theratechnologies-inc-thtx-q4-2019-earnings-call-t.aspx))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified within the January 2017–December 2024 window; if any crypto-related initiatives were to arise after this period, the classification would need to be revisited.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 25, 2020 (Q4 2019) transcript: “After our first full year of commercialization of Trogarzo, we ended just shy of $28 million in sales for this product.” — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/02/25/theratechnologies-inc-thtx-q4-2019-earnings-call-t.aspx) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/02/25/theratechnologies-inc-thtx-q4-2019-earnings-call-t.aspx))
- Jul 15, 2020 (Q2 2020) transcript: “Our second quarter consolidated revenues were up 10%… reaching $17.2 million… Trogarzo sales… $7.9 million compared to $7 million last year.” — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/07/15/theratechnologies-inc-thtx-q2-2020-earnings-call-t.aspx) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/07/15/theratechnologies-inc-thtx-q2-2020-earnings-call-t.aspx))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Sep 27, 2019 Form 40-F (registration under Section 12; NASDAQ listing details and exhibits). — [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1512717/000119312519257311/d805932d40fr12b.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1512717/000119312519257311/d805932d40fr12b.htm))
- FY 2023 Consolidated Financial Statements (years ended Nov 30, 2023/2022/2021): product-focused operations and financial statement notes; no crypto assets disclosed. — [SEDAR+/newsfile PDF](https://newsfile.futunn.com/public/NN-PersistNoticeAttachment/7781/20241119/SEDAR_PLUS/CSA_SEDAR_PLUS_NOTICE_RECORD_ID_161781.pdf) ([newsfile.futunn.com](https://newsfile.futunn.com/public/NN-PersistNoticeAttachment/7781/20241119/SEDAR_PLUS/CSA_SEDAR_PLUS_NOTICE_RECORD_ID_161781.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**
- Mar 14, 2023: FY 2022 results release detailing revenue from EGRIFTA SV and Trogarzo and R&D updates; no crypto activities discussed. — [Theratechnologies press release](https://www.theratech.com/news-releases/news-release-details/theratechnologies-reports-financial-results-and-business-updates/) ([theratech.com](https://www.theratech.com/news-releases/news-release-details/theratechnologies-reports-financial-results-and-business-updates/))

## AFFIMED
