* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 20-F filings (2018–2023), Q3 2021 and Q4 2019 earnings calls, press releases (2018–2023)

### Firm Overview
Affimed N.V. is a Dutch clinical-stage immuno-oncology company with operations in Germany, developing innate cell engagers such as AFM13 and AFM24 and funding operations primarily through equity raises and R&D collaborations, including a notable Genentech agreement. Public disclosures focus on R&D progress, collaborations, and financing, consistent with a biotech profile rather than financial or crypto activities. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1608390/000110465924040310/afmd-20231231x20f.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across earnings-call transcripts and SEC filings from 2017–2024, there is no evidence that the company held cryptocurrencies, mined digital assets, offered crypto financial services, provided crypto-focused business services, or supplied mining/infrastructure hardware or capacity. Management commentary centers on clinical programs (e.g., AFM13, AFM24), collaboration revenue (e.g., Genentech), and funding runway. The absence of crypto-related disclosures in the 20-F and the content of calls indicates no material linkage to crypto markets during the sample window. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/10/affimed-nv-afmd-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcript/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Given the lack of substantiated crypto activity in transcripts, filings, or press materials over 2017–2024, Affimed is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). No weights are assigned to holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr, and the investor-base amplifier is set to 0 due to no evidence that its shareholder base trades the stock as a crypto proxy. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1608390/000110465924040310/afmd-20231231x20f.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified within the January 2017–December 2024 window; if any crypto-related initiatives were to arise after this period, the classification would need to be revisited. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1608390/000110465924040310/afmd-20231231x20f.htm?utm_source=openai))

- Apr 28, 2020 (Q4 2019) transcript: “Regarding AFM13 we dosed the first patient in the fourth quarter of 2019 in our registration-directed Phase 2 study… and enrollment is ongoing.” — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/04/28/affimed-nv-afmd-q4-2019-earnings-call-transcript.aspx) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/04/28/affimed-nv-afmd-q4-2019-earnings-call-transcript.aspx?utm_source=openai))
- Nov 19, 2019 (Q3 2019) transcript: Discussion focused on IND clearance and trial initiation plans; no crypto topics referenced. — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/11/19/affimed-nv-afmd-q3-2019-earnings-call-transcript.aspx) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/11/19/affimed-nv-afmd-q3-2019-earnings-call-transcript.aspx?utm_source=openai))

## GRESHAM TECHNOLOGIES DEAD - DELIST.10/07/24

* Rank: 400 | Expo: 0.10 | id_str: GB0008808825
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Annual reports (2018, 2022), FY2023 final results RNS, H1 2022 investor presentation (transcript)

### Firm Overview
Gresham Technologies plc was a UK-based financial technology company focused on enterprise data automation and reconciliation solutions for banks and asset managers, centered on its Clareti platform. Public disclosures in 2018–2023 describe a software-led, subscription business serving financial institutions globally, with occasional wins in adjacent sectors. ([annualreports.co.uk](https://www.annualreports.co.uk/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/g/LSE_GHT.L_2018.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Evidence indicates Gresham’s crypto linkage is via software sold to digital-asset clients rather than any direct handling of crypto. The 2018 annual report notes that following the B2 Group acquisition, the business added “a Luxembourg-based bitcoin exchange” as a customer, and later disclosures reference wins in the “digital asset” sector and client demand to reconcile and report transactions for “digital asset classes.” There is no indication that Gresham held crypto on balance sheet, mined assets, or offered custody/exchange products itself. ([annualreports.co.uk](https://www.annualreports.co.uk/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/g/LSE_GHT.L_2018.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Classified as bizserv = 1.0. Management materials show Gresham provides non-financial, software-based reconciliation and data-control services to financial institutions, including at least one crypto exchange and broader digital-asset use cases; there is no evidence of direct financial intermediation, mining, asset holding, or crypto-specific infrastructure provision. Investor-base effects were not evident, so invbase = 0. ([annualreports.co.uk](https://www.annualreports.co.uk/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/g/LSE_GHT.L_2018.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Earnings-call transcript access is limited; while a 2022 investor presentation transcript is available, it does not directly discuss crypto and broader call transcripts are gated, so certainty is set to 3 based on filings and RNS disclosures. Digital-asset engagements appear client-specific and not separately quantified, so crypto revenue share is coded as minor. ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/2397314/half-year-2022-gresham-technologies-plc-earnings-presentation-transcript))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jul 26, 2022 (H1 2022) investor presentation transcript: “Their first relationship with Gresham was a connect agreement in July of 2018.” — [GuruFocus](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/2397314/half-year-2022-gresham-technologies-plc-earnings-presentation-transcript) ([gurufocus.com](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/2397314/half-year-2022-gresham-technologies-plc-earnings-presentation-transcript?utm_source=openai))

**Filings (Annual reports / RNS):**
- 2018 Annual Financial Report: “The business added four customers… including a high-profile German challenger bank and a Luxembourg-based bitcoin exchange.” — [AnnualReports.co.uk (PDF)](https://www.annualreports.co.uk/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/g/LSE_GHT.L_2018.pdf) ([annualreports.co.uk](https://www.annualreports.co.uk/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/g/LSE_GHT.L_2018.pdf))
- FY2023 Final Results (RNS): banks “explore how to integrate blockchain… and specifically how they can reconcile and report transactions for digital asset classes.” — [Investegate](https://www.investegate.co.uk/announcement/rns/gresham-technologies--ght/final-results/8160696) ([investegate.co.uk](https://www.investegate.co.uk/announcement/rns/gresham-technologies--ght/final-results/8160696))
- 2023 Earnings Release/Overview: “wins in the payments, energy and digital asset sectors”; strategy includes “control of digital asset data processing.” — [FinancialReports.eu](https://financialreports.eu/filings/gresham-technologies-plc/earnings-release/2023/1860351/) ([financialreports.eu](https://financialreports.eu/filings/gresham-technologies-plc/earnings-release/2023/1860351/))

## SEI INVESTMENTS

* Rank: 401 | Expo: 0.10 | id_str: US7841171033
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filing (2024), Q4 2024 earnings call, 2018 blockchain press release

### Firm Overview
SEI Investments Company is a U.S.-based provider of technology, operations, and asset management solutions to financial institutions, advisors, and other asset owners, centered on its SEI Wealth Platform and trust-based custody. In 2024, SEI reported that a majority of revenue came from technology and operations outsourcing and asset management fees across its client segments. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/350894/000035089425000028/seic-20241231.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across January 2017–December 2024, no substantive evidence indicates that SEI held crypto on its balance sheet, mined digital assets, or directly operated crypto financial services such as exchange, custody of crypto, or fund products backed by crypto. References to “digital assets” in disclosures appear in the context of evolving client preferences and technology change, not as an SEI-operated business line. SEI did explore distributed-ledger technology through a 2018 partnership with Synechron to build internal expertise and evaluate use cases via a Blockchain Center of Excellence, but this was framed as R&D/enablement rather than a client-facing crypto service. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/350894/000035089425000028/seic-20241231.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of explicit, material crypto activities during the 2017–2024 window, SEI is coded unclass = 1.0. Mentions of blockchain and “digital assets” relate to technology exploration and market commentary rather than mechanisms in this taxonomy (holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, infrastr). There is also no evidence of retail or “crypto proxy” investor-base effects, so invbase = 0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/350894/000035089425000028/seic-20241231.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
SEI’s 2018 blockchain initiative signals internal interest but does not evidence a revenue-bearing crypto service within the sample period. Public transcripts reviewed do not discuss crypto in a way that changes classification. Note: SEI’s tokenization-related activity in the Bank of England’s Digital Securities Sandbox was announced on April 8, 2025, outside the 2017–2024 scope and is therefore not used for classification. ([ir.seic.com](https://ir.seic.com/press-releases/detail/69/sei-and-synechron-join-forces-for-blockchain-utilization))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jan 31, 2025 (Q4 2024) earnings call: “Sales events for Q4 2024 were $38,000,000 with $28,000,000 net recurring.” — [Investing.com transcript](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-sei-investments-q4-2024-shows-eps-growth-93CH-3843983)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- 2024 Form 10-K: company description of technology, operations, custody, and asset management mix. — [SEC filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/350894/000035089425000028/seic-20241231.htm)
- 2024 Form 10-K: “We are at risk of failing to keep pace with significant new technologies… such as… blockchain.” — [SEC filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/350894/000035089425000028/seic-20241231.htm)
- 2024 Form 10-K: risk discussion referencing “changing client preferences for sustainable and digital assets.” — [SEC filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/350894/000035089425000028/seic-20241231.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- Mar 7, 2018: “SEI and Synechron join forces for blockchain utilization… SEI’s Blockchain Center of Excellence.” — [SEI IR press release](https://ir.seic.com/press-releases/detail/69/sei-and-synechron-join-forces-for-blockchain-utilization)
- SEI Private Trust Company custody model overview (traditional custody structure; not crypto-specific). — [SEI site](https://www.seic.com/about-sei/our-insights/sei-private-trust-company-safeguards-your-assets)

### Firm Overview
Adverum Biotechnologies, Inc. is a U.S.-based clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on ocular gene therapies, including ixo-vec (formerly ADVM-022) for wet age-related macular degeneration. The company is listed on Nasdaq under the ticker ADVM and is headquartered in Redwood City, California.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across January 2017–December 2024, Adverum’s public materials and earnings communications focus on development of gene therapy candidates for ophthalmology, with no indication of cryptocurrency holdings, mining, crypto financial services, or crypto-focused business or infrastructure lines. Management’s discussions center on clinical programs (OPTIC, LUNA) and corporate financing typical of a biotech, not digital assets or blockchain-related revenue. Filings and company press releases similarly present no evidence of crypto exposure. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/08/09/adverum-biotechnologies-inc-advm-q2-2019-earnings.aspx))

Given the absence of any substantive crypto-related activity in transcripts, SEC filings, and press releases during 2017–2024, the firm is coded unclass = 1.0, with all mechanism categories set to 0.0. There is no evidence that retail or “crypto proxy” dynamics influence its shareholder base, so invbase = 0. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/08/09/adverum-biotechnologies-inc-advm-q2-2019-earnings.aspx))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified. Sources reviewed consistently describe a clinical-stage biotech focus without crypto involvement. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1501756/000162828024011648/0001628280-24-011648-index.html))

### Firm Overview
Cyren Ltd. was an Israel-based cybersecurity company that provided cloud-delivered email security, web security and threat intelligence services to enterprises and OEM partners; its shares traded on Nasdaq under ticker CYRN. On February 22, 2023, the company announced it would cease operations and initiate insolvency/liquidation proceedings following unsuccessful efforts to secure additional liquidity. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/121390022014854/0001213900-22-014854-index.html))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across the 2017–2024 window, Cyren’s SEC filings and public communications describe a traditional cybersecurity business model focused on email/web security and threat intelligence, with no indication of cryptocurrency holdings, mining, crypto financial services, or targeted crypto-infrastructure activities. Product descriptions and segment disclosures in its 10-K/10-Q filings do not reference digital-asset custody, trading, tokenization, or mining operations. The February 2023 liquidation announcements and related coverage similarly contain no evidence of crypto-related business lines or balance sheet assets. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1084577/000121390021018692/f10k2020_cyrenltd.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of explicit, material crypto activities in Cyren’s filings and contemporaneous news during 2017–2024, the firm is coded unclass = 1.0, with all mechanism categories set to 0.0. There is also no evidence that Cyren’s investor base treated the equity as a crypto proxy, so invbase = 0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1084577/000121390021018692/f10k2020_cyrenltd.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The company ceased operations in February 2023, limiting later disclosures; historical earnings-call transcripts are not essential here because classification is unclass. Older press items about phishing targeting Bitcoin users reflect generic threat research rather than a crypto-revenue line. None of the reviewed sources show revenue, assets, or services that meet this taxonomy’s crypto mechanisms. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1084577/000121390023013435/ea174057ex99-1_cyrenltd.htm?utm_source=openai))

## MOLTEN VENTURES

* Rank: 404 | Expo: 0.10 | id_str: GB00BY7QYJ50
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0

* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Annual reports (FY2022–FY2024), company portfolio/news posts (2018–2023), financial press coverage (2018)

### Firm Overview
Molten Ventures plc (formerly Draper Esprit) is a London-listed venture capital firm that invests in high‑growth European technology companies across fintech, enterprise software, AI/deeptech and other segments. Any crypto linkage arises indirectly through equity stakes in portfolio companies rather than through direct digital‑asset operations.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across the 2017–2024 window, Molten highlighted venture investments in crypto/blockchain businesses such as Ledger, a hardware wallet and digital‑asset security company, and SettleMint, an enterprise blockchain platform. These positions are portfolio equity stakes; Molten does not present them as operating activities of the plc. Company materials and annual reports describe these as part of its broader tech portfolio and do not indicate that the plc holds cryptocurrencies, mines, runs exchanges, or offers crypto‑backed financial products. Accordingly, no direct mechanism category from this taxonomy applies. ([moltenventures.com](https://www.moltenventures.com/news/draper-esprit-invests-in-380m-series-c-in-portfolio-company-ledger))

### Weight Assignment
Because Molten’s crypto connection is via venture equity investments in third‑party companies—not through holding coins, mining, providing crypto financial services, non‑financial services to the crypto ecosystem, or supplying crypto‑specific infrastructure—the firm is coded unclass = 1.0. There is no evidence of a distinct crypto‑oriented investor‑base dynamic tied to Molten’s own shares, so invbase = 0. ([investors.moltenventures.com](https://investors.moltenventures.com/storage/uploads/PLC/Results-and-Reports/FY24_Annual-Report.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
As a listed VC, Molten’s NAV could be sensitive to crypto market cycles via portfolio companies like Ledger; however, this indirect valuation effect falls outside the defined mechanism categories, which require direct operational exposure. No conventional earnings‑call transcripts were found for the plc during the period, but this is not required for unclassified firms. ([moltenventures.com](https://www.moltenventures.com/news/draper-esprit-invests-in-380m-series-c-in-portfolio-company-ledger))

### Evidence

**Filings (annual reports / presentations):**

- FY2024 Annual Report: business model/portfolio overview (no direct crypto operations indicated). — [Molten Ventures FY24 Annual Report (PDF)](https://investors.moltenventures.com/storage/uploads/PLC/Results-and-Reports/FY24_Annual-Report.pdf)
- FY2023 Annual Results presentation: references portfolio funding rounds including Ledger. — [FY23 Results Presentation (PDF)](https://investors.moltenventures.com/storage/uploads/PLC/Results-and-Reports/Molten_Ventures_plc_Annual_Report_Presentation_FY23_nirwf.pdf)

- Mar 30, 2023: Molten participates in Ledger’s Series C extension; notes scale of Ledger’s crypto security footprint. — [Molten Ventures newsroom](https://www.moltenventures.com/news/molten-participates-in-series-c-extension-for-ledger)

## WITAN INV.TRUST DEAD - DELIST.03/10/24

* Rank: 405 | Expo: 0.10 | id_str: GB00BJTRSD38
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Annual report (FY2023), monthly factsheet (February 2024), RNS/circulars on merger (September–October 2024), industry news (October 2024)

### Firm Overview
Witan Investment Trust plc was a London-listed, multi‑manager global equity investment trust that invested shareholders’ capital across external managers and selected specialist funds. In October 2024, Witan’s assets were transferred to Alliance Trust and the combined vehicle rebranded Alliance Witan; Witan’s listing was subsequently cancelled.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Company materials describe a traditional listed‑equity investment trust using a multi‑manager approach, with assets spread across third‑party mandates and specialist collective funds. There is no indication in recent reports or factsheets that the trust held cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet, mined digital assets, operated a crypto exchange/custody service, or issued crypto‑backed products. Its portfolio and disclosures focus on quoted equities and investment companies, consistent with a conventional equity trust mandate, not digital‑asset activities. ([media.umbraco.io](https://media.umbraco.io/alliance-trust/czmkcjru/witan-ar2023.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of any direct crypto mechanisms — no treasury coin holdings, no mining, no crypto financial services, no crypto-specific infrastructure or non‑financial services — the firm is coded unclass = 1.0. There is no evidence that Witan’s shareholder base treated the stock as a crypto proxy, so invbase = 0. ([media.umbraco.io](https://media.umbraco.io/alliance-trust/czmkcjru/witan-ar2023.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified. Note that the trust combined with Alliance Trust in October 2024 under a section 110 scheme; the classification here pertains to Witan during the 2017–2024 sample period prior to completion. ([media.umbraco.io](https://media.umbraco.io/alliance-trust/2q1glkuu/results-of-class-meeting-and-first-gm-240930.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Filings (annual reports / factsheets):**
- Mar 15, 2024: Annual Financial Report (FY2023) — “Our third‑party managers implement mandates set by the Company… Four of our six core external managers outperformed their benchmarks.” — [Investegate](https://www.investegate.co.uk/announcement/rns/witan-inv-trust--wtan/annual-financial-report/8091562)
- 2023 Annual Report: strategy/portfolio disclosure (benchmark, sector mix; multi‑manager context). — [Witan Annual Report 2023 (PDF)](https://media.umbraco.io/alliance-trust/czmkcjru/witan-ar2023.pdf)
- Feb 29, 2024: Monthly factsheet — “A multi‑manager strategy for global equity investment, offering growth in capital and income.” (manager breakdown, top 20 holdings) — [Factsheet (PDF)](https://media.umbraco.io/alliance-trust/a43kj1ip/witan-investment-trust-factsheet-29-02-24.pdf)

**RNS / corporate documents (merger context):**
- Sept 30, 2024: “Results of Class Meeting and First GM… combination of the assets of Witan Investment Trust plc with Alliance Trust PLC… scheme of reconstruction… under section 110 of the Insolvency Act 1986.” — [Company RNS (PDF)](https://media.umbraco.io/alliance-trust/2q1glkuu/results-of-class-meeting-and-first-gm-240930.pdf)
- Oct 9, 2024: Alliance Witan announcement — WTAN shareholders approved the scheme; c. £1.539bn of net assets to be acquired in exchange for 120.95m new shares. — [Alliance Witan announcement hub](https://www.alliancewitan.com/landing-pages/announcement)

**Financial news / other:**
- Oct 2, 2024: “Alliance Trust knocks on FTSE 100 after just 9% of Witan shares cash out” — industry report on merger progress and election outcomes. — [The AIC](https://www.theaic.co.uk/aic/news/industry-news/alliance-trust-knocks-on-ftse-100-after-just-9-of-witan-shares-cash-out)
- Oct 11, 2024: Listing/rebrand coverage as Alliance Witan joins the LSE following combination with Witan. — [Portfolio Adviser](https://portfolio-adviser.com/alliance-witan-listed-on-london-stock-exchange/)

</small>

## ARCUS BIOSCIENCES

* Primary sources: 10-K (FY2023), 10-Q (Q2 2023), FY2023 results press release (Feb 21, 2024)

### Firm Overview
Arcus Biosciences, Inc. is a U.S.-based, clinical‑stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing immuno‑oncology therapies, including anti‑TIGIT and PD‑1 programs, in partnership with Gilead Sciences. Its activities center on research and clinical development rather than financial services or digital‑asset operations.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across SEC filings and investor materials, Arcus describes itself as a single‑segment biopharmaceutical R&D company advancing oncology drug candidates and collaborations; there is no indication of cryptocurrency holdings, mining, or digital‑asset product operations. The company does not run a crypto exchange, custody platform, or payments service, and makes no disclosures of crypto‑backed investment products. There is likewise no evidence it supplies crypto‑specific hardware, datacenter hosting, or mining infrastructure, nor that it provides non‑financial services targeted at crypto firms. These sources consistently frame Arcus as a conventional biotech with clinical programs and collaborations in oncology. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1724521/000172452124000052/rcus-20231231.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of any treasury crypto holdings, mining, crypto financial services, crypto‑adjacent business services, or targeted infrastructure, the firm is assigned unclass = 1.0. There is no evidence that Arcus trades as a crypto proxy or has a crypto‑sensitive investor base, so invbase = 0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1724521/000172452124000052/rcus-20231231.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified.

### Evidence

<small>

**Filings (10-K / 10-Q):**
- Feb 21, 2024: Form 10‑K (FY2023) — “We are a clinical‑stage biopharmaceutical company focused on creating best‑in‑class therapies.” — [SEC filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1724521/000172452124000052/rcus-20231231.htm)
- Aug 7, 2023: Form 10‑Q (Q2 2023) — Company describes single operating segment focused on developing and commercializing differentiated therapies. — [SEC filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1724521/000095017023038760/rcus-20230630.htm)

**Financial news / other (IR press releases):**

## BEL FUSE 'A'

* Rank: 407 | Expo: 0.10 | id_str: US0773472016
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 1.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Company press releases and product literature (2018–2019), Q4 2018 earnings call, FY2024 Form 10-K

### Firm Overview
Bel Fuse Inc. is a U.S.-based manufacturer of electronic components that power, protect and connect electronic circuits, operating through Power Solutions & Protection, Connectivity Solutions, and Magnetic Solutions. Its end markets include servers, storage, networking, aerospace/defense, industrial and other applications; power products are used in server, networking and data center equipment. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/729580/000143774925005652/belfb20241231_10k.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Bel markets dedicated power hardware for high‑performance compute workloads that explicitly include blockchain processing and cryptocurrency mining (e.g., the PET2000 2 kW AC‑DC front‑end supply), positioning it as an upstream infrastructure supplier to mining/HPC installations. Management communication and filings also describe strong participation in server and data‑center power, a neighboring demand pool that often overlaps with mining deployments. There is no evidence that Bel holds crypto on its balance sheet, runs mining operations, or offers crypto financial services; its role is strictly component supply. ([belfuse.com](https://www.belfuse.com/resources/news/bel-power-solutions-announces-platinum-efficiency-2000-w-ac-dc-power-supply-for-blockchain-cryptocurrency-and-ai-applications))

### Weight Assignment
The company’s only substantiated crypto‑related mechanism is infrastructure: purpose‑built power supplies and related hardware promoted for blockchain and cryptocurrency‑mining workloads, plus historic comments tying power‑supply demand to blockchain‑related data‑center needs. Accordingly, infrastr = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. Given the absence of any disclosure of crypto‑specific revenue segmentation or asset holdings, RevShare is coded as 1 (minor, <~5%) and AssetShare as 0. ([belfuse.com](https://www.belfuse.com/resources/news/bel-power-solutions-announces-platinum-efficiency-2000-w-ac-dc-power-supply-for-blockchain-cryptocurrency-and-ai-applications))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Earnings call transcripts reviewed did not explicitly reference “crypto” or “mining,” though they did cite data‑center power demand; the explicit crypto linkage comes from company product releases and brochures. The magnitude of sales into crypto‑mining is not disclosed and likely cyclical with broader HPC/datacenter demand; no separate crypto revenue line is available. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/02/21/bel-fuse-belfb-q4-2018-earnings-conference-call-tr.aspx))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 21, 2019 (Q4 2018): “driven by continued demand for our power supplies in [a] variety of data center applications.” — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/02/21/bel-fuse-belfb-q4-2018-earnings-conference-call-tr.aspx)

**Filings (10-K / annual report):**
- FY2024 Form 10‑K: Power Solutions & Protection “include… AC/DC power supplies… primarily used in… Servers, Storage, Networking…”; 2024 variance driven by lower sales of front‑end and board‑mount power “used in networking and datacenter applications.” — [SEC 10‑K (filed 2025)](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/729580/000143774925005652/belfb20241231_10k.htm)

**Financial news / other (company materials):**
- Feb 14, 2019: “2000 W AC‑DC Power Supply for Blockchain, Cryptocurrency and AI Applications” (PET2000‑NAS446) — [Bel Fuse news](https://www.belfuse.com/resources/news/bel-power-solutions-announces-platinum-efficiency-2000-w-ac-dc-power-supply-for-blockchain-cryptocurrency-and-ai-applications)
- Oct 31, 2018: Q3 2018 results release noted strong demand for data‑center power, “including… power‑intensive requirements needed for the encryption and security aspects of blockchain technology.” — [Bel IR news](https://ir.belfuse.com/news-releases/news-release-details/bel-reports-third-quarter-2018-results)
- 2018 brochure: “Power Your Blockchain” solutions — [Bel brochure (PDF)](https://www.belfuse.com/resources/brochures/powersolutions/br-BPS-bitcoing-cryptocurrency-solutions.pdf)
- PET2000 “Blockchain Series” datasheet — [Bel datasheet (PDF)](https://www.belfuse.com/resources/datasheets/powersolutions/ds-BPS-PET2000-blockchain-series.pdf)

## FLUSHING FINANCIAL

* Rank: 408 | Expo: 0.10 | id_str: US3438731057
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Company press release (Jan 2022), Banking Dive CEO interview (Jul 2024), Q4 2024 earnings call transcript

### Firm Overview
Flushing Financial Corporation is the bank holding company for Flushing Bank, a New York–based community/commercial bank focused on deposit gathering and real estate/commercial lending in the NYC metro area. It is listed on Nasdaq under ticker FFIC. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/923139/000110465926024646/ffic-20251231x10k.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
In January 2022, Flushing announced a partnership with NYDIG to enable customers to buy, sell, and hold bitcoin through the bank’s digital channels, with NYDIG providing the underlying bitcoin services and custody. This is direct provision of a crypto financial service to the bank’s customers (via a partner), not proprietary trading or balance‑sheet holding. Subsequently, management stated the initiative was run as a pilot and discontinued within roughly six months due to concerns over bitcoin volatility and customer appropriateness. There is no evidence the bank mined crypto, supplied infrastructure, or held crypto on its own balance sheet. ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2022/01/25/2372566/8417/en/Flushing-Bank-Announces-Partnership-with-NYDIG-to-Offer-Bitcoin-Services.html?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
The only substantiated crypto mechanism is financial services: a 2022 NYDIG‑powered pilot allowing retail clients to transact in bitcoin inside the bank’s digital experience. With management later confirming the pilot was ended and no continuing crypto activity appearing in subsequent earnings calls, the exposure is coded entirely as finserv = 1.0 and all other categories = 0.0. Given the pilot nature and the lack of any disclosed crypto revenue or assets, RevShare = 0 and AssetShare = 0. ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2022/01/25/2372566/8417/en/Flushing-Bank-Announces-Partnership-with-NYDIG-to-Offer-Bitcoin-Services.html?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The NYDIG offering appears to have been short‑lived and limited in scope; Flushing did not disclose separate crypto revenue or asset balances. Later earnings calls focus on core banking topics with no recurring crypto commentary, suggesting immateriality after 2022. No transcript located during the 2017–2024 window explicitly discussed the NYDIG pilot; classification rests on the company’s press release and the CEO’s on‑record 2024 comments. ([bankingdive.com](https://www.bankingdive.com/news/flushing-bank-ceo-John-Buran-cybersecurity/722950/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 1, 2025 (Q4 2024 results): “The operating environment has turned more favorable and we continue to invest in the business to drive future profitability.” — [Investing.com transcript](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-flushing-financial-q4-2024-misses-forecasts-93CH-3843854) ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-flushing-financial-q4-2024-misses-forecasts-93CH-3843854))

**Filings (10‑K / annual report):**
- Mar 13, 2025: Form 10‑K (company/business description; bank operations and markets) — [SEC filing index](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0000923139/000110465926024646/ffic-20251231x10k.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/923139/000110465926024646/ffic-20251231x10k.htm?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Jan 25, 2022: “Flushing Bank Announces Partnership with NYDIG to Offer Bitcoin Services” — [GlobeNewswire](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2022/01/25/2372566/8417/en/Flushing-Bank-Announces-Partnership-with-NYDIG-to-Offer-Bitcoin-Services.html) ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2022/01/25/2372566/8417/en/Flushing-Bank-Announces-Partnership-with-NYDIG-to-Offer-Bitcoin-Services.html?utm_source=openai))
- Jul 31, 2024: CEO John Buran on ending the NYDIG pilot (“It was a pilot… we decided… it really wasn’t the right thing for us… [bitcoin] volatility and appropriateness”) — [Banking Dive interview](https://www.bankingdive.com/news/flushing-bank-ceo-John-Buran-cybersecurity/722950/) ([bankingdive.com](https://www.bankingdive.com/news/flushing-bank-ceo-John-Buran-cybersecurity/722950/))
- NYDIG trading platform terms used by Flushing (bitcoin buy/sell/hold via NYDIG account) — [Flushing Bank PDF](https://www.flushingbank.com/media/041dscu3/nydig-trading-platform-terms-12-15-21-1.pdf) ([flushingbank.com](https://www.flushingbank.com/media/041dscu3/nydig-trading-platform-terms-12-15-21-1.pdf?utm_source=openai))

## TOSEI CORPORATION

* Rank: 409 | Expo: 0.10 | id_str: JP3595070008
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Company news releases (2021–2024), Integrated Report 2024, Q2 2024 earnings call transcript

### Firm Overview
Tosei Corporation is a Japan-based real estate group listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange (Prime) and Singapore Exchange, operating revitalization, development, rental, fund/consulting, property management, and hotel businesses. Management highlights digital initiatives and fund operations alongside core real estate activities in recent investor materials.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Tosei has directly issued and managed real-estate–backed security tokens (digital securities recorded on blockchain) for investors. It listed its first issues on ADDX (Singapore) in 2021 and 2023, then in 2024 completed a publicly offered real-estate ST in Japan using the Progmat platform and commenced handling on ODX’s “START” PTS, Japan’s secondary market for STs. On the Q2 FY2024 earnings call (July 5, 2024), management said the group “has been ahead of the curve in issuing ST… the first of which was listed on ADDX,” and noted it was managing four ST funds totaling about JPY 11.1 billion in AUM and considering listing on ODX. There is no evidence the firm mines crypto, provides mining infrastructure, or holds cryptocurrencies on its own balance sheet. ([toseicorp.co.jp](https://www.toseicorp.co.jp/news/2023/20230217_734.html))

### Weight Assignment
The exposure mechanism is squarely crypto financial services: Tosei (via Tosei Asset Advisors and group companies) structures and offers blockchain-recorded security tokens backed by real estate, and discusses pipeline and AUM on earnings calls. With recurring ST issuances and secondary-market handling disclosed in 2024, the firm is coded finserv = 1.0 and all other mechanism categories = 0.0. Given the scale of the broader group (tens of billions of yen in annual revenue), the ST line appears minor; thus RevShare = 1 and AssetShare = 0. ([pdf.irpocket.com](https://pdf.irpocket.com/C8923/Oxqc/rQ94/RYOs.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Security tokens reflect beneficial interests in real-estate trusts issued to investors; they are not proprietary crypto holdings by Tosei, and related revenues are not broken out separately in filings. The Fantom partnership reported by third-party blogs in 2019 is not used as primary evidence due to source quality and lack of follow-through in company IR materials. Overall, the classification is stable given multiple official disclosures and an earnings-call transcript in 2024. ([toseicorp.co.jp](https://www.toseicorp.co.jp/english/business/dx/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jul 5, 2024 (Q2 FY2024): “The group has been ahead of the curve in issuing ST… the first of which was listed on the ADDX in Singapore.” — [FY2024 2Q transcript PDF](https://pdf.irpocket.com/C8923/Oxqc/rQ94/RYOs.pdf) ([pdf.irpocket.com](https://pdf.irpocket.com/C8923/Oxqc/rQ94/RYOs.pdf))
- Jul 5, 2024 (Q2 FY2024): “In the security token business, we are managing 4 funds with total AUM of JPY 11.1 billion… considering listing… on the Osaka Digital Exchange (ODX).” — [FY2024 2Q transcript PDF](https://pdf.irpocket.com/C8923/Oxqc/rQ94/RYOs.pdf) ([pdf.irpocket.com](https://pdf.irpocket.com/C8923/Oxqc/rQ94/RYOs.pdf))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Oct 30, 2024: Integrated Report — notes being “first to list a security token on ADDX in 2021.” — [Integrated Report 2024](https://www.toseicorp.co.jp/english/files/241030_integratedreport.pdf) ([toseicorp.co.jp](https://www.toseicorp.co.jp/english/files/241030_integratedreport.pdf))
- May 24, 2024: Public offering/issuance of real‑estate ST using Progmat; 1.74 billion yen issue; plan to handle on ODX “START.” — [Tosei news release](https://www.toseicorp.co.jp/english/news/2024/20240524_1353.html) ([toseicorp.co.jp](https://www.toseicorp.co.jp/english/news/2024/20240524_1353.html))
- Sep 4, 2024: Notice that TOSEI Property Fund (Series 3) Ichigaya ST to be handled on ODX “START”; Progmat platform specified. — [Tosei news release](https://www.toseicorp.co.jp/english/news/2024/20240904_1361.html) ([toseicorp.co.jp](https://www.toseicorp.co.jp/english/news/2024/20240904_1361.html))

**Financial news / other:**
- Feb 17, 2023: Second Japan real‑estate–backed ST listed on ADDX (series details and figures). — [Tosei JP
… JP news](https://www.toseicorp.co.jp/news/2023/20230217_734.html)

**Financial news / other:**

- Feb 17, 2023: Second real-estate ST issuance; listed on ADDX — [Tosei JP news release](https://www.toseicorp.co.jp/news/2023/20230217_734.html)
- May 24, 2024: Public offering and issuance of real-estate ST in Japan using Progmat; plan to handle on ODX “START” — [Tosei EN news (PDF)](https://www.toseicorp.co.jp/english/news/files/20240524-01.pdf)
- Sep 4, 2024: Notice that TOSEI Property Fund (Series 3) Ichigaya ST will be handled on ODX “START” — [Tosei EN news](https://www.toseicorp.co.jp/english/news/2024/20240904_1361.html)

## SIMCORP DEAD - DELIST.31/10/23

* Rank: 410 | Expo: 0.10 | id_str: DK0060495240
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Company news releases (2021–2024), Annual Report 2022, Q3 2022 earnings call transcript

### Firm Overview
SimCorp A/S was a Denmark-based provider of front-to-back investment management software and services to global asset managers, asset owners, and asset servicers. The company was acquired by Deutsche Börse and its shares were removed from trading on Nasdaq Copenhagen on October 30–31, 2023.

### Exposure Channels Identified
SimCorp enabled clients to manage digital assets via technology integrations, not by providing financial services itself. In December 2023, it announced a partnership with Alloy to allow institutional clients to manage cryptocurrencies, tokenized securities, and DeFi exposures within SimCorp’s platform. In 2024, SimCorp described an industry-first proof of concept integrating Franklin Templeton’s BENJI tokenized U.S. government money fund into SimCorp One, again via Alloy’s digital-asset capabilities. There is no evidence that SimCorp mined crypto, directly custodied or traded digital assets for clients, or held crypto on its own balance sheet. ([simcorp.com](https://www.simcorp.com/about-us/news/2023/SimCorp-partners-with-Alloy?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
The exposure mechanism is non-financial business services: SimCorp’s software/platform and partner ecosystem added connectivity and workflow support for clients’ digital-asset portfolios, but SimCorp did not itself issue, custody, or intermediate crypto assets. Accordingly, bizserv = 1.0 and all other mechanism categories = 0.0. Given that digital-asset functionality emerged late in the sample (2023–2024) and no separate revenue breakout is disclosed, crypto-related revenue is assessed as minor (RevShare = 1) and there is no balance-sheet crypto (AssetShare = 0). ([simcorp.com](https://www.simcorp.com/about-us/news/2023/SimCorp-partners-with-Alloy?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Digital-asset capabilities were introduced near the end of the sample period, and management did not provide segment revenue detail, limiting materiality precision. Earnings-call transcripts reviewed emphasized platform, SaaS and partner strategy but did not explicitly quantify digital-asset activity; the classification relies on official product/partnership disclosures. SimCorp was delisted in October 2023 following the Deutsche Börse takeover; post-delisting developments are referenced only where published by the company. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/SMCYY/transcripts))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 15, 2022 (Q3 2022): “Growth lever number 3, the ecosystem scaling… fintech partnerships… and making sure that SimCorp connects with the rest of the financial world… whether it’s integration to primary settlement and custodian.” — [ROIC AI transcript](https://www.roic.ai/quote/SMCYY/transcripts) ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/SMCYY/transcripts))
- Nov 12, 2021 (Q3 2021): Discussion of SaaS and data services expansion supporting standardized, partner-enabled delivery — [AlphaSpread transcript](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/cse/sim/investor-relations/earnings-call/q3-2021) ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/cse/sim/investor-relations/earnings-call/q3-2021))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- 2022: Annual Report — company description and financial statements; no crypto assets disclosed on balance sheet — [Annual Report 2022 (PDF)](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReports/PDF/simcorp_2022.pdf) ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReports/PDF/simcorp_2022.pdf))
- Oct 3, 2023: Nasdaq Copenhagen accepts delisting; last trading day Oct 30, 2023 — [Nasdaq press notice](https://www.nasdaq.com/press-release/simcorp-a-s-removal-from-trading-2023-10-03) ([nasdaq.com](https://www.nasdaq.com/press-release/simcorp-a-s-removal-from-trading-2023-10-03?utm_source=openai))

## COWEN CLASS A DEAD - DELIST.02/03/23

* Rank: 411 | Expo: 0.10 | id_str: US2236226062
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Earnings calls (Q2–Q3 2021), 2022 10-K and Q2 2022 10‑Q, press releases (2021–2022), Bloomberg (May 2023)

### Firm Overview
Cowen Inc. was a U.S.-based diversified financial services firm offering investment banking, research, sales and trading, prime brokerage, and investment management before its acquisition by TD Bank Group in March 2023 and subsequent delisting. During 2021–2023 Cowen added a digital-assets effort aimed at institutional clients under the Cowen Digital brand.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Cowen launched Cowen Digital in March 2022 to provide institutional trading and custody access to cryptocurrencies, partnering with Standard Custody & Trust (a PolySign subsidiary) for custody and Digital Prime Technologies for prime-brokerage solutions. This follows Cowen’s May 2021 $25 million strategic investment in PolySign and earlier formation of a digital-asset investment unit/fund. There is no evidence that Cowen mined cryptocurrencies or held crypto on its own balance sheet; custody was handled by a regulated third party. TD Cowen shut the digital unit in May 2023, curtailing activity late in the sample window. ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2022/03/23/2408450/0/en/Cowen-Launches-Digital-Asset-Division-Offering-Trading-and-Custody-Solutions-for-Institutional-Investors.html?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
The firm’s crypto exposure stemmed from providing financial intermediation—execution access, custody via a qualified partner, and related institutional services—rather than from proprietary holdings or mining. Management referenced the “digital assets initiative” and work toward trading and custody capabilities on earnings calls in 2021, and the 2022 filings document Cowen Digital’s incentive plan and unit structure. Therefore, finserv = 1.0 and all other mechanism categories = 0.0. Given limited duration, lack of separate revenue disclosure, and the 2023 wind‑down, crypto revenue is assessed as minor (RevShare = 1) and no crypto assets appear on Cowen’s balance sheet (AssetShare = 0). ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/07/28/cowen-inc-cown-q2-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The digital-asset business operated for a relatively short period (primarily 2022–H1 2023), and Cowen did not disclose standalone revenue or AUM for Cowen Digital, limiting materiality precision. Post‑acquisition shutdown in May 2023 reduces ongoing relevance but does not negate exposure within the 2017–2024 sample. ([bloomberg.com](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2023-05-31/td-cowen-closes-digital-unit-that-focused-on-cryptocurrencies?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jul 28, 2021 (Q2 2021), Motley Fool: “We’re also making progress on our digital assets initiative… developing capabilities to meet emerging client demand for trading and custody of a range of digital assets.” — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/07/28/cowen-inc-cown-q2-2021-earnings-call-transcript/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/07/28/cowen-inc-cown-q2-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))
- Oct 29, 2021 (Q3 2021), Motley Fool: “While we’re making progress on the build-out of Cowen Digital, our digital assets initiative… [client] engagement… is very high.” — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/29/cowen-inc-cown-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcript/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/29/cowen-inc-cown-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**

- May 2, 2022: Q2 2022 Form 10‑Q — “On March 1, 2022, Cowen Digital Holdings LLC authorized 2,000,000 Class B Units…” under the 2022 Equity Unit Incentive Plan — [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1466538/000146653822000082/cown-20220630.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1466538/000146653822000082/cown-20220630.htm?utm_source=openai))
- Mar 23, 2022: “Cowen Launches Digital Asset Division Offering Trading and Custody Solutions for Institutional Investors” — [GlobeNewswire release](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2022/03/23/2408450/0/en/Cowen-Launches-Digital-Asset-Division-Offering-Trading-and-Custody-Solutions-for-Institutional-Investors.html) ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2022/03/23/2408450/0/en/Cowen-Launches-Digital-Asset-Division-Offering-Trading-and-Custody-Solutions-for-Institutional-Investors.html?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- May 13, 2021: “Cowen to Offer Crypto Custody… $25M Stake in PolySign” — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/05/13/cowen-to-offer-crypto-custody-services-through-25m-stake-in-polysign) ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/05/13/cowen-to-offer-crypto-custody-services-through-25m-stake-in-polysign?utm_source=openai))

## EPIZYME DEAD - DELIST.15/08/22

* Rank: 412 | Expo: 0.10 | id_str: US29428V1044
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2021), Q4 2019 earnings call, 2022 acquisition/delisting announcements

### Firm Overview
Epizyme, Inc. was a U.S.-based commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on epigenetic therapies for cancer, including the EZH2 inhibitor TAZVERIK (tazemetostat). The company was acquired by Ipsen and ceased to be independently listed in August 2022.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across earnings-call transcripts and SEC filings during 2017–2022, Epizyme’s activities center on oncology drug development and commercialization; no crypto, blockchain, mining, custody, payments, or digital-asset product lines are described. The 2021 Form 10-K details product revenue from TAZVERIK and R&D programs but contains no references to cryptocurrency or blockchain terms. The company’s independent listing ended with Ipsen’s acquisition in August 2022, further limiting any potential late-window crypto activity within the 2017–2024 sample. Accordingly, no mechanism of crypto exposure is supported by the evidence. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1571498/000095017022002552/epzm-20211231.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Because there is no evidence that Epizyme held crypto on its balance sheet, mined digital assets, provided crypto financial services, supplied crypto-specific infrastructure, or delivered non-financial services to the crypto ecosystem, the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0) with all other mechanism weights = 0.0. The absence of crypto-related disclosures in the 2021 10‑K and the focus of earnings-call discussions on oncology products support this assignment. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1571498/000095017022002552/epzm-20211231.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified; the company’s business scope and disclosures are squarely biopharmaceutical. The firm was acquired and delisted in August 2022, but this does not affect the conclusion for the 2017–2024 window. ([ipsen.com](https://www.ipsen.com/annualreport/press-releases/ipsen-completes-acquisition-of-epizyme-expanding-its-portfolio-in-oncology/?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 24, 2020 (Q4 2019), Motley Fool: “We will begin reporting product revenues in the first quarter since TAZVERIK was approved in January.” — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/02/24/epizyme-inc-epzm-q4-2019-earnings-call-transcript.aspx)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Mar 2022: 2021 Form 10‑K — company described as a commercial‑stage biopharma focused on novel epigenetic medicines; no crypto-related disclosures — [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1571498/000095017022002552/epzm-20211231.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- Aug 12, 2022: “Ipsen completes acquisition of Epizyme” — [Ipsen press release](https://www.ipsen.com/annualreport/press-releases/ipsen-completes-acquisition-of-epizyme-expanding-its-portfolio-in-oncology/)

* Primary sources: 10-K/annual reports (2022–2023), Q3 2019 earnings call, 2024 company press release

### Firm Overview
RBB Bancorp is a U.S. bank holding company headquartered in Los Angeles, California, whose primary subsidiary is Royal Business Bank, a community-focused commercial bank with operations across California, Nevada, New York, New Jersey, Illinois, and Hawaii. The company’s filings describe traditional banking activities including lending, deposits, mortgage and SBA loan servicing, and related services. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1499422/000143774924011386/RBB2023ARS.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across earnings-call transcripts and SEC filings during 2017–2024, there is no evidence that RBB Bancorp or Royal Business Bank held cryptocurrency, mined digital assets, offered crypto trading/custody, or provided other crypto-specific products. The 2023 Annual Report on Form 10-K and the 2022 annual report contain no references to cryptocurrency, blockchain, or digital‑asset services, and the available earnings-call transcript (Q3 2019) focuses on conventional community banking operations (loans, deposits, NIM, branches). Accordingly, no crypto exposure mechanisms are supported. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1499422/000143774924011386/RBB2023ARS.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Because no substantive crypto-related activities were identified in transcripts, filings, or contemporaneous news within the 2017–2024 window, the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0) with all other mechanism weights at 0.0. There is also no indication that the stock trades as a crypto proxy; therefore, the investor base amplifier is set to 0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1499422/000143774924011386/RBB2023ARS.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified within the January 2017–December 2024 sample window; company disclosures and the reviewed transcript are squarely focused on traditional banking. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1499422/000143774924011386/RBB2023ARS.pdf))

### Evidence

## SALESFORCE

* Rank: 414 | Expo: 0.09 | id_str: US79466L3024
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filing (FY2024), Q2 FY2020 and Q1 FY2020 earnings calls, 2023 Salesforce Web3 product announcement, financial press (CoinDesk)

### Firm Overview
Salesforce is a U.S.-based enterprise cloud software company and the market leader in customer relationship management, offering its Customer 360 platform across sales, service, marketing, commerce and analytics. In March 2023 the company announced “Salesforce Web3,” including NFT Management and Web3 Connect, tools that let brands mint and manage NFT collections and connect blockchain activity to CRM data. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1108524/000119312524140606/d753990dars.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Salesforce’s crypto exposure is through non-financial enterprise software that helps clients interact with public blockchains for marketing, loyalty and customer engagement use cases. Management discussed “Salesforce Blockchain” on earnings calls in 2019–2020, and in 2023 the company expanded to Web3 tools that monitor on‑chain activity and integrate wallet/NFT data with CRM; pilot programs ran hundreds of thousands of transactions for brands like Mattel and Crown Royal. There is no evidence Salesforce custodies crypto assets, runs an exchange, or offers crypto trading or investment products; filings present a standard SaaS business without digital‑asset holdings. Partnerships and ecosystem activity (e.g., Polygon collaboration; Salesforce Ventures’ investment in Web3 data firm Mnemonic) support the characterization as enterprise software/services rather than financial intermediation. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/08/22/salesforcecom-inc-crm-q2-2020-earnings-call-transc.aspx))

### Weight Assignment
The firm’s exposure arises from providing non‑financial services to crypto/Web3 users: software to mint/manage NFTs and integrate blockchain data into CRM workflows. As Salesforce does not hold crypto on its balance sheet, mine coins, provide custody/trading, or supply mining/ASIC infrastructure, the appropriate mechanism is bizserv = 1.0 with all others = 0.0. The earnings‑call remarks on Salesforce Blockchain and the 2023 Web3 product launch anchor this assignment; filings indicate no direct digital‑asset holdings or crypto‑specific financial products. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/08/22/salesforcecom-inc-crm-q2-2020-earnings-call-transc.aspx))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Salesforce does not disclose a separate revenue line for Web3/NFT tools, so the revenue contribution within 2017–2024 appears minor; classification relies on qualitative product descriptions and partner announcements. Branding evolved from “Salesforce Blockchain” (2019) to “Salesforce Web3” (2023), but this does not alter the mechanism categorization. No evidence indicates use of corporate funds to hold crypto or operate financial products during the sample window. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/08/22/salesforcecom-inc-crm-q2-2020-earnings-call-transc.aspx))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 22, 2019 (Q2 FY2020), Motley Fool: “Salesforce Blockchain is the world's first declarative blockchain solution for CRM, built natively on our platform.” — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/08/22/salesforcecom-inc-crm-q2-2020-earnings-call-transc.aspx) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/08/22/salesforcecom-inc-crm-q2-2020-earnings-call-transc.aspx))
- Jun 4, 2019 (Q1 FY2020), Thomson Reuters: “…we integrated into the blockchain, where you could see the whole transparency of the supply chain…” — [Thomson Reuters transcript PDF](https://s23.q4cdn.com/574569502/files/doc_financials/2020/2019-Jun-04-CRM.N-137908243223-Transcript.pdf) ([s23.q4cdn.com](https://s23.q4cdn.com/574569502/files/doc_financials/2020/2019-Jun-04-CRM.N-137908243223-Transcript.pdf))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Mar 6, 2024: FY2024 Form 10‑K — company described as a global CRM platform (Customer 360); no digital‑asset holdings disclosed — [SEC EDGAR (PDF)](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1108524/000119312524140606/d753990dars.pdf) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1108524/000119312524140606/d753990dars.pdf))
- Mar 15, 2023: “Salesforce Web3 Tech Helps Brands Build Trusted, Sustainable Digital Communities” — [Salesforce Newsroom](https://www.salesforce.com/news/stories/web3-nft-news/web3_announcement/) ([salesforce.com](https://www.salesforce.com/news/stories/web3-nft-news/web3_announcement/?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Mar 17, 2023: “Polygon Partners With Salesforce for NFT‑Based Loyalty Program” — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2023/03/17/polygon-partners-with-salesforce-for-nft-based-loyalty-program) ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2023/03/17/polygon-partners-with-salesforce-for-nft-based-loyalty-program?utm_source=openai))
- Jun 14, 2023: “Salesforce Leads $6M Round for Web3 Data Platform Mnemonic; to integrate with Salesforce Web3” — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2023/06/14/salesforce-leads-6m-round-for-ai-backed-web3-data-platform-mnemonic) ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2023/06/14/salesforce-leads-6m-round-for-ai-backed-web3-data-platform-mnemonic?utm_source=openai))

## ONESPAN

* Rank: 415 | Expo: 0.09 | id_str: US68287N1000
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filing (FY2023), Q2 2023 and Q3 2023 earnings calls, company press releases (ProvenDB acquisition; Trust Vault launch)

### Firm Overview
OneSpan Inc. is a U.S.-based provider of digital agreement security and authentication solutions, formerly known as VASCO Data Security. Its portfolio includes e-signature (OneSpan Sign), notary, identity verification, and related security products; in 2023 the company added blockchain‑backed document storage capabilities via the acquisition of ProvenDB and the launch of OneSpan Trust Vault.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s crypto/Web3 exposure comes from non‑financial enterprise software that uses public blockchain technology to provide tamper‑evident storage and audit trails for high‑value documents (OneSpan Trust Vault). Management announced the acquisition of ProvenDB in January 2023 to extend its platform to public and private blockchains and later launched Trust Vault in November 2023. Filings describe Trust Vault as a “blockchain‑based decentralized digital storage solution,” with blockchain anchoring used to record document creation and subsequent actions. There is no evidence the company holds crypto on its balance sheet, runs an exchange, or offers custody/trading products. ([investors.onespan.com](https://investors.onespan.com/news-releases/news-release-details/onespan-acquire-blockchain-technology-provider-provendb-bring/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
OneSpan’s exposure is squarely in non‑financial services to the crypto ecosystem: it sells software that leverages blockchain for document integrity and compliant e‑vaulting, without taking custody of digital assets or providing financial intermediation. Management discussed bringing “secure e‑Vaulting for documents and artifacts based on blockchain technology to market” in Q2 2023, and highlighted the Trust Vault launch on the Q3 2023 call; the FY2023 10‑K formalizes Trust Vault as part of the Digital Agreements segment. Accordingly, bizserv = 1.0 and all other mechanisms = 0.0. ([s24.q4cdn.com](https://s24.q4cdn.com/314592314/files/doc_earnings/2023/q2/transcript/Q2-2023-OneSpan-Earnings-Conference-Call-Transcript-FINAL.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The company does not break out revenue attributable specifically to blockchain/Trust Vault within 2017–2024; the contribution appears minor relative to total revenue. Evidence indicates blockchain anchoring and e‑vaulting features, but there is no indication of corporate crypto holdings or operation of financial products during the sample window. None identified that would alter the mechanism categorization. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1044777/000162828024009466/ospn-20231231.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 9, 2023 (Q2 2023), investor transcript: “We are also on track to bring secure e‑Vaulting for documents and artifacts based on blockchain technology to market later this year.” — [OneSpan IR transcript PDF](https://s24.q4cdn.com/314592314/files/doc_earnings/2023/q2/transcript/Q2-2023-OneSpan-Earnings-Conference-Call-Transcript-FINAL.pdf) ([s24.q4cdn.com](https://s24.q4cdn.com/314592314/files/doc_earnings/2023/q2/transcript/Q2-2023-OneSpan-Earnings-Conference-Call-Transcript-FINAL.pdf?utm_source=openai))
- Nov 8, 2023 (Q3 2023), call: “OneSpan Trust Vault … helps guarantee the integrity and long‑term viability of documents through the use of immutable storage capabilities based on blockchain technology.” — [Insider Monkey transcript](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/onespan-inc-nasdaqospn-q3-2023-earnings-call-transcript-1221045/) ([insidermonkey.com](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/onespan-inc-nasdaqospn-q3-2023-earnings-call-transcript-1221045/))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Mar 6, 2024: Form 10‑K (FY2023) — “OneSpan Trust Vault is a blockchain‑based decentralized digital storage solution…”; also notes ProvenDB technology leverages blockchain. — [SEC EDGAR (HTML)](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1044777/000162828024009466/ospn-20231231.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1044777/000162828024009466/ospn-20231231.htm))

## ONCOINVENT

* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Annual reports (2020–2021), investor pages (2017–2021), Dec 2024 private placement press release

### Firm Overview
Oncoinvent is a Norway-based clinical-stage radiopharmaceutical company developing alpha-emitting therapies for solid tumors; its lead candidate is Radspherin, a radium‑224 based microsphere suspension for peritoneal carcinomatosis. Company materials and filings describe a focus on radiopharmaceutical R&D, manufacturing capabilities, and clinical trials. ([oncoinvent.com](https://www.oncoinvent.com/wp-content/uploads/Oncoinvent-Annual-Report-2021-EN.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across the 2017–2024 window, investor and filing documents focus exclusively on oncology drug development and related clinical operations; no activities involving cryptocurrencies, blockchain, mining, custody, trading, or crypto‑backed financial products are disclosed. The Dec 5, 2024 press release concerns a private placement and anticipated Euronext Growth Oslo listing, with no crypto references. A text search of annual reports for 2020 and 2021 finds no instances of “blockchain,” “Bitcoin,” or “crypto.” ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/oncoinvent-asa-announces-oversubscribed-private-placement-302323732.html))

### Weight Assignment
No substantive crypto exposure channel is evidenced in filings or investor materials for 2017–2024. Accordingly, the firm is assigned unclass = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Oncoinvent had limited public reporting before its late‑2024 listing; no earnings‑call transcripts from 2017–2024 were retrievable. Available annual reports and investor pages were reviewed and contained no crypto‑related disclosures or terminology. These limitations do not alter the unclassified outcome. ([oncoinvent.com](https://www.oncoinvent.com/investors/reports-and-presentations/reports-and-presentation-archive/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Filings / company reports:**
- Mar 23, 2022: Annual Report 2021 — “Oncoinvent was founded with the objective of becoming a global leader in the development of alpha‑emitting radiotherapeutics… [lead product] Radspherin.” — [Oncoinvent Annual Report 2021 (PDF)](https://www.oncoinvent.com/wp-content/uploads/Oncoinvent-Annual-Report-2021-EN.pdf)
- Mar 1, 2021: Annual Report 2020 — “Radspherin is a suspension of novel alpha‑emitting radioactive microspheres designed for treatment of metastatic cancers in body cavities.” — [Oncoinvent Annual Report 2020 (PDF)](https://www.oncoinvent.com/wp-content/uploads/Annual-report-2020-ENG.pdf)
- Investors page (archive): Reports and Presentations (2018–2022 listings). — [Oncoinvent IR: Reports and presentation – Archive](https://www.oncoinvent.com/investors/reports-and-presentations/reports-and-presentation-archive/)

**Financial news / other:**
- Dec 5, 2024: “Oncoinvent ASA announces oversubscribed private placement… will apply for admission to trading on Euronext Growth Oslo.” — [PR Newswire release](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/oncoinvent-asa-announces-oversubscribed-private-placement-302323732.html)

## TESLA

* Rank: 417 | Expo: 0.09 | id_str: US88160R1014
* Weight summary: holders 1.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (FY2022–FY2023), Q1 2021 and Q2 2022 earnings calls

### Firm Overview
Tesla is a U.S.-based manufacturer of electric vehicles and energy storage/solar products with global operations and listings on Nasdaq. During the period reviewed, the company also held Bitcoin as a treasury asset and briefly accepted cryptocurrency as a form of payment for products.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s crypto exposure stems primarily from holding Bitcoin on its balance sheet beginning in early 2021, with subsequent partial sales in 2022 and ongoing smaller holdings thereafter. Management explicitly discussed the investment and long-term view on Bitcoin during earnings calls. Tesla also briefly accepted Bitcoin for vehicle purchases in 2021 and has accepted Dogecoin for select merchandise, but these activities were ancillary and not material drivers of revenue. Financial media repeatedly linked Tesla’s share performance to Bitcoin moves during 2021, indicating a crypto‑sensitive investor narrative.

### Weight Assignment
Evidence from filings and calls shows direct balance‑sheet exposure to Bitcoin and associated impairment/realized gains, making holders the clear and economically meaningful channel. Mining, infrastructure, and non‑financial services do not apply. Crypto payments existed only intermittently (BTC for cars briefly; DOGE for merchandise) and were peripheral, so they do not rise to a separate finserv weight. Accordingly, holders = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Tesla briefly accepted Bitcoin for vehicle purchases in 2021 and has accepted Dogecoin for select merchandise; these are treated as immaterial to revenue and not classified as finserv. The company sold ~75% of its Bitcoin in Q2 2022; remaining holdings in 2023 were small relative to total assets. No additional transcripts beyond those cited were required once the classification stabilized.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Apr 26, 2021 (Q1 2021): “Tesla did invest $1.5 billion into bitcoin… We do believe long term in the value of bitcoin.” — [The Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/04/27/tesla-tsla-q1-2021-earnings-call-transcript/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/04/27/tesla-tsla-q1-2021-earnings-call-transcript/?utm_source=openai))
- Jul 20, 2022 (Q2 2022): “We converted a majority of our Bitcoin holdings to fiat for a realized gain, offset by impairment charges…” — [AlphaStreet transcript](https://news.alphastreet.com/tesla-inc-tsla-q2-2022-earnings-conference-call-transcript/) ([news.alphastreet.com](https://news.alphastreet.com/tesla-inc-tsla-q2-2022-earnings-conference-call-transcript/?utm_source=openai))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Feb 5, 2024: Form 10‑K (FY2023) — Digital assets, net $184m; estimated fair value $487m; notes 2022 impairment $204m and $64m realized gains on conversion. — [SEC EDGAR (HTML)](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1318605/000162828024002390/tsla-20231231.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1318605/000162828024002390/tsla-20231231.htm))
- Jan 31, 2023: Form 10‑K (FY2022) — “As of December 31, 2022, we have converted approximately 75% of our [Bitcoin] purchases into fiat currency… carrying value $184m; fair value $191m.” — [Tesla IR PDF (10‑K)](https://ir.tesla.com/_flysystem/s3/sec/000095017023001409/tsla-20221231-gen.pdf) ([ir.tesla.com](https://ir.tesla.com/_flysystem/s3/sec/000095017023001409/tsla-20221231-gen.pdf))

**Financial news / other:**
- Jul 20, 2022: “As of the end of Q2, we have converted approximately 75% of our Bitcoin purchases into fiat currency,” reported with Q2 results. — [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2022/07/20/tesla-converted-75percent-of-bitcoin-purchases-to-fiat-currency-in-q2-2022.html) ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2022/07/20/tesla-converted-75percent-of-bitcoin-purchases-to-fiat-currency-in-q2-2022.html?utm_source=openai))
- Mar 24, 2021: Tesla began accepting Bitcoin for U.S. vehicle purchases (later suspended in May 2021). — [Axios](https://www.axios.com/2021/03/24/elon-musk-tesla-bitcoin-cryptocurrency) ([axios.com](https://www.axios.com/2021/03/24/elon-musk-tesla-bitcoin-cryptocurrency?utm_source=openai))
- Jun 21, 2022: Musk reiterated Tesla accepts Dogecoin for merchandise. — [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2022-06-21/musk-says-he-supports-dogecoin-after-people-encouraged-him-to) ([bloomberg.com](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2022-06-21/musk-says-he-supports-dogecoin-after-people-encouraged-him-to?utm_source=openai))
- Feb 23, 2021: Analyst note reported Tesla’s share price “now directly linked to the price of bitcoin” after the treasury purchase. — [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2021/02/23/tesla-and-bitcoins-value-are-now-linked-analyst-warns.html) ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2021/02/23/tesla-and-bitcoins-value-are-now-linked-analyst-warns.html?utm_source=openai))

</small>

## GOLD

* Rank: 418 | Expo: 0.09 | id_str: US00181T1079
* Weight summary: holders 0.9, miners 0.0, finserv 0.1, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 1
* Primary sources: 10-Q filing (Dec 31, 2022), FY2023 annual report, Q2 2023 earnings call, company press releases (2022–2023)

### Firm Overview
Gold.com, Inc. (formerly A‑Mark Precious Metals) is a U.S.-based precious metals trading and logistics company with wholesale operations and a direct‑to‑consumer arm that includes JM Bullion and Goldline. The company expanded its DTC footprint through acquisitions such as Goldline (2017) and JM Bullion (2021). ([ir.gold.com](https://ir.gold.com/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001019056-24-000138/0001019056-24-000138.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Filings during the sample period disclose that the company purchased and held “digital assets (crypto currencies)” for investment, accounting for them as indefinite‑lived intangibles with impairments recognized through other income. Press releases in 2022–2023 also show small “write‑down of digital assets” and a “purchase of digital assets,” indicating active, albeit minor, balance‑sheet exposure to crypto. Separately, subsidiary JM Bullion accepted cryptocurrency as a payment method but converted receipts immediately to U.S. dollars, avoiding price exposure. Management earnings calls discussed the company’s digital precious‑metals platform CyberMetals, which is not a cryptocurrency but helps contextualize the firm’s broader digital strategy. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1591588/000095017023002032/amrk-20221231.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Direct, disclosed investment holdings of cryptocurrencies in 2022–2023 support a primary “holders” classification. The crypto‑payments acceptance at JM Bullion existed but was immediately converted and immaterial, so it is coded as a minor “finserv” exposure. There is no evidence the company mined crypto, provided crypto‑specific non‑financial services, or supplied crypto infrastructure. Accordingly, weights are set to holders 0.9 and finserv 0.1, with all other categories 0.0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1591588/000095017023002032/amrk-20221231.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The magnitude of digital‑asset holdings appears small based on impairment and purchase line‑items; precise balances by period are not broken out beyond inclusion in other long‑term assets. Crypto‑payments acceptance was operational and hedged by instant conversion, so revenue impact is negligible. Earnings‑call discussion of CyberMetals pertains to digital precious metals rather than cryptocurrencies; crypto exposure relies primarily on filings and press releases. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1591588/000095017024112408/amrk_ars_fy24.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 6, 2023 (Q2 FY2023): “we’ve brought on a couple of new people to focus on marketing for CyberMetals… we have seen some redemptions and people actually trading metal back to us on the platform…” — [Insider Monkey transcript](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/a-mark-precious-metals-inc-nasdaqamrk-q2-2023-earnings-call-transcript-1117281/) ([insidermonkey.com](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/a-mark-precious-metals-inc-nasdaqamrk-q2-2023-earnings-call-transcript-1117281/))

**Filings (10‑K / 10‑Q / prospectus):**
- Dec 31, 2022 (Form 10‑Q): “The Company has purchased certain digital assets (crypto currencies) that are held for investment purposes… recorded at cost less impairment.” — [SEC EDGAR](https://ir.amark.com/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/000095017023002032/amrk-20221231.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1591588/000095017023002032/amrk-20221231.htm?utm_source=openai))
- FY2023 Annual Report: Accounting policy note “Digital Assets (crypto currencies)… held for investment purposes… impairment recognized through other income, net.” — [SEC AR PDF](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1591588/000095017023052127/amrk_ars_fy23.pdf) 
- Mar 4, 2021 (424(b)(2) prospectus): “JM Bullion accepts… cryptocurrency, [but] converts… into U.S. dollars and does not maintain a position or exposure to fluctuations…” — [Company filing PDF](https://ir.amark.com/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/000
…0001104659-21-033038/0001104659-21-033038.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- May 9, 2023: Press release includes “Write-down of digital assets” in EBITDA reconciliation and shows “Purchase of digital assets” in cash flows. — [A‑Mark fiscal Q3 2023 release (PDF)](https://ir.amark.com/_assets/_059213c56a0b4006ddc410aa8d42253d/amark/news/2023-05-09_A_Mark_Precious_Metals_Reports_Fiscal_Third_164.pdf)
- FY2024 Annual Report highlights CyberMetals digital metal platform and notes GoldPrice.org/SilverPrice.org publish precious metal and cryptocurrency pricing. — [FY2024 Annual Report (PDF)](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1591588/000095017024112408/amrk_ars_fy24.pdf)

</small>
