## NANO DIMENSION SPONSORED 1 ADR 1:1

* Rank: 419 | Expo: 0.09 | id_str: US63008G2030
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Form 20‑F filings (2023–2024), Q3 2023 earnings call, company press releases (2021–2022)

### Firm Overview
Nano Dimension Ltd. is an Israel‑based additive manufacturing company focused on additively manufactured electronics and industrial 3D printing systems, materials and software. The business emphasizes digital manufacturing solutions for customers in aerospace and defense, high‑tech industrials, specialty medical, and R&D.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across the 2017–2024 sample, filings and investor materials describe a pure‑play digital/additive manufacturing business (hardware, materials, software) with no disclosed cryptocurrency holdings, mining operations, crypto‑financial services, or crypto‑specific infrastructure activities. A review of the company’s 2023 and 2024 Form 20‑F filings shows no references to “cryptocurrency,” “bitcoin,” or “digital assets,” and discusses risks, accounting policies, and strategy solely in the context of manufacturing and M&A. Earnings call commentary similarly centers on additive manufacturing products, materials development, and end‑markets like defense and electronics, with no crypto‑related operations mentioned. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1643303/000121390024024783/ea0201615-20f_nanodimen.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of any substantive, explicit crypto exposure in transcripts, filings, or press materials over 2017–2024, the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). There is no evidence of treasury crypto holdings (holders), mining (miners), custody/exchange/payment products (finserv), non‑financial crypto services (bizserv), or crypto‑directed hardware/hosting/energy supply (infrastr). Therefore all mechanism weights are set to 0.0 except unclass = 1.0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1643303/000121390024024783/ea0201615-20f_nanodimen.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified beyond the standard caveat that general‑purpose manufacturing hardware could indirectly be used by crypto ecosystem customers; however, no sources document material, crypto‑directed demand or strategy at the company level during 2017–2024. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1643303/000121390024024783/ea0201615-20f_nanodimen.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 28, 2023 (Q3 2023): “the development of additive manufacturing and the network we are building of cloud manufacturing… at the end of the day there is one thing that’s important – materials and process.” — [EarningsCall.ai](https://www.earningscall.ai/stock/transcript/NNDM-2023-Q3)

**Filings (10‑K / 20‑F / 6‑K):**
- Mar 21, 2024 (Form 20‑F for FY2023): Company description, risk factors and accounting policies with no references to cryptocurrencies or digital assets. — [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1643303/000121390024024783/ea0201615-20f_nanodimen.htm)
- Apr 2025 (Form 20‑F for FY2024): Strategic focus on digital manufacturing; no references to cryptocurrencies or digital assets. — [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1643303/000141057825001194/nndm-20241231x20f.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- Jul 11, 2022: Press release on acquiring Admatec and Formatec to expand metal and ceramic additive manufacturing portfolio. — [Nano Dimension IR](https://investors.nano-di.com/press-releases/news-details/2022/Nano-Dimension-Acquires-Admatec-and-Formatec-Creating--Metal--Ceramic-Additive-Manufacturing-Portfolio/default.aspx)
- Dec 23, 2023: Report on unsolicited bid related to 3D‑printing industry consolidation; unrelated to crypto activity. — [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2023-12-23/nano-dimension-offers-to-buy-stratasys-in-1-1-billion-deal)

The FY2022 Form 10‑K business description focuses on digital media tools and platform strategy and contains no references to “cryptocurrency,” “bitcoin,” or “digital assets.” Earnings calls emphasize subscription growth in creative tools and enterprise offerings (MediaCentral, NEXIS) rather than any crypto‑related activities. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReports/PDF/NASDAQ_AVID_2022.pdf))

Given the absence of substantive, explicit crypto exposure in filings and multiple earnings calls over the sample period, the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). There is no documentation of treasury crypto holdings (holders), mining (miners), custody/exchange/payment products (finserv), non‑financial crypto services (bizserv), or crypto‑directed hardware/hosting/energy supply (infrastr). ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReports/PDF/NASDAQ_AVID_2022.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified.

## TON STRATEGY

### Firm Overview
During the 2017–2024 sample period, the entity operated as a U.S.-based software company focused on interactive video–enabled sales tools and its MARKET.live livestream social shopping platform for commerce and media clients, alongside related SaaS offerings. Filings and calls in this window consistently describe a social commerce/SaaS business model, without reference to cryptocurrency activities. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1566610/000149315224012416/form10-k.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across FY2021 and FY2023 SEC filings, there are no occurrences of “crypto,” “cryptocurrency,” or “digital asset,” and the business description centers on MARKET.live and SaaS products (verbCRM, verbLIVE, verbLEARN, verbPULSE). Earnings call remarks in 2022 emphasize the architecture, scalability and rollout of the MARKET.live livestream shopping platform, not crypto-related products or balance sheet holdings. 2024 press materials similarly focus on e-commerce integrations (e.g., TikTok Shop partner designation) and operating progress in social shopping. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0001566610/000149315222008470/form10-k.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Given the lack of substantive, explicit crypto exposure in filings, earnings calls, or credible news within January 2017–December 2024, the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). There is no evidence of treasury crypto holdings (holders), mining operations (miners), crypto-financial services (finserv), non-financial crypto services (bizserv), or crypto-directed hardware/hosting supply (infrastr) over the sample period. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1566610/000149315224012416/form10-k.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Classification is limited to the January 2017–December 2024 sample period; sources in that window document no crypto exposure. None identified beyond this standard scope limitation. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1566610/000149315224012416/form10-k.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

## FOX A

* Rank: 422 | Expo: 0.09 | id_str: US35137L1052
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.6, bizserv 0.4, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Form 10-K (FY2022, FY2023), Q1 FY2022 earnings call (Nov 7, 2021), company press releases and trade coverage (2021–2023)

### Firm Overview
Fox Corporation is a U.S.-based media company focused on news, sports, entertainment and digital streaming properties, with primary brands including FOX News Media, FOX Sports, FOX Entertainment and Tubi. Within the 2017–2024 window, Fox’s direct crypto-related activity sat inside Fox Entertainment’s Web3/NFT unit, Blockchain Creative Labs (BCL), formed in 2021 to support tokenized media and fan experiences. ([investor.foxcorporation.com](https://investor.foxcorporation.com/?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
- Crypto financial services (finserv): Fox, via BCL, directly issued and sold NFTs tied to FOX shows (e.g., The Masked Singer “MaskVerse”) and operated marketplace and wallet technology, including a creator fund and platform partnership with Eluvio. Management highlighted early traction in wallets and NFT pack downloads on the Nov 7, 2021 earnings call. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/07/fox-corp-foxa-q1-2022-earnings-call-transcript/))  
- Crypto non-financial services (bizserv): Fox monetized advertising from crypto-native marketers; management described the “crypto category” as a new and booming ad vertical in sports during the same FY2022 call, indicating indirect revenue exposure as a media platform selling reach to crypto companies. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/07/fox-corp-foxa-q1-2022-earnings-call-transcript/))  
- No evidence was found of corporate treasury crypto holdings, mining operations, or crypto-specific hardware/hosting supply. ([content.edgar-online.com](https://content.edgar-online.com/ExternalLink/EDGAR/0001628280-23-029065.html?dest=foxa-20230630x10kex41_htm&hash=1667bb69b3eb74d18beae21cdc4e31dc0c0a3ad9cda7c74114eb600fb06d360d))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv receives the larger share (0.6) because BCL directly handled digital assets by minting, selling and managing NFT marketplaces/wallets, supported by a strategic Eluvio platform tie-up and a $100 million creator fund. Bizserv (0.4) captures Fox’s role as an advertising seller to crypto firms, which management characterized as a fast-growing category in sports. There is no basis for holders, miners or infrastr weights over 2017–2024, and unclass is therefore 0.0. ([investor.foxcorporation.com](https://investor.foxcorporation.com/news/business/2021/fox-corporation-makes-strategic-investment-in-eluvio-and-selects-eluvio-as-platform-for-fox-entertainments-and-bento-box-entertainments-recently-launched-nft-business-blockchain-crea/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
BCL’s financial contribution is not separately broken out in segment disclosures, so crypto-attributable revenue appears minor relative to consolidated sales; RevShare is coded conservatively. Activity was concentrated in 2021–2023; persistence beyond the period is not assessed here. No transcript evidence indicates treasury crypto holdings. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1754301/000162828022022584/fox-20220630.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 7, 2021 (Q1 FY2022): “We are very pleased with the early results of our investment in Blockchain Creative Labs… fans… downloaded over 120,000 NFT packs and created over 100,000 wallets.” — [The Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/07/fox-corp-foxa-q1-2022-earnings-call-transcript/)  
- Nov 7, 2021 (Q1 FY2022): “the crypto category, which is a brand new category, particularly in sports, is booming” — [The Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/11/07/fox-corp-foxa-q1-2022-earnings-call-transcript/)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Aug 12, 2022 (Form 10‑K FY2022): “Blockchain Creative Labs… is focused on establishing a leadership position in Web3 content creation, distribution and monetization.” — [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1754301/000162828022022584/fox-20220630.htm)
- Aug 11, 2023 (Form 10‑K FY2023): Lists “Blockchain Creative Labs, LLC” among subsidiaries. — [EDGAR/Edgar-Online](https://content.edgar-online.com/ExternalLink/EDGAR/0001628280-23-029065.html?dest=foxa-20230630x10kex41_htm&hash=1667bb69b3eb74d18beae21cdc4e31dc0c0a3ad9cda7c74114eb600fb06d360d)

**Financial news / other:**
- Aug 25, 2021: Fox invests in Eluvio; BCL to use Eluvio as underlying tech; FOX/Bento Box launched a $100M creator fund. — [Fox Corp Investor press release](https://investor.foxcorporation.com/news/business/2021/fox-corporation-makes-strategic-investment-in-eluvio-and-selects-eluvio-as-platform-for-fox-entertainments-and-bento-box-entertainments-recently-launched-nft-business-blockchain-crea/)
- Oct 13, 2021: Fox’s BCL launching the “MaskVerse” NFT marketplace for The Masked Singer; Fox had invested in Eluvio earlier. — [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/10/13/nfts-are-coming-to-fox-tv-show-masked-singer)
- Oct 27, 2021: WWE partners with Fox’s Blockchain Creative Labs on a multi‑year NFT marketplace deal. — [Forbes](https://www.forbes.com/sites/alfredkonuwa/2021/10/27/wwe-partners-with-foxs-blockchain-creative-labs-in-nft-deal/)
- Nov 4, 2021: “Fox bets big on blockchain,” with
- Nov 4, 2021: “Fox bets big on blockchain,” detailing Fox’s plan to double down on NFTs and the $100M creator fund behind BCL; MaskVerse wallet/pack metrics cited. — [Axios](https://www.axios.com/2021/11/04/fox-blockchain-nfts-investment)
- Jan 9, 2024: Fox launches “Verify,” a blockchain-based platform to help media firms license content to AI companies, underscoring continued blockchain initiatives. — [Axios](https://www.axios.com/2024/01/09/fox-corp-blockchain-platform-ai-licensing-verify)

## DLOCAL A

* Rank: 423 | Expo: 0.09 | id_str: KYG290181018
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Form 20-F (FY2023), Q4 2021 and Q4 2022 earnings calls, 4Q22 earnings presentation

### Firm Overview
dLocal Limited is a Uruguay-based fintech that provides a single-API payments platform enabling global enterprise merchants to accept pay-ins and make payouts across emerging markets in Latin America, Africa and Asia. Within the 2017–2024 window, management added “crypto” as a merchant vertical, serving crypto-related clients primarily by handling local fiat rails tied to exchange on/off-ramp needs rather than by transacting in digital assets itself. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/03/15/dlocal-limited-dlo-q4-2021-earnings-call-transcrip/?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
- Crypto non-financial services (bizserv): On the March 15, 2022 Q4 2021 earnings call, management stated, “We have added crypto as a new vertical,” describing early pilots and use cases with several merchants across geographies. Subsequent disclosures quantify the vertical’s small scale: in 2022, “wallets” accounted for 1% of TPV and “crypto” for 0.2% of TPV. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/03/15/dlocal-limited-dlo-q4-2021-earnings-call-transcrip/))  
- dLocal’s FY2023 Form 20-F clarifies the firm’s role relative to digital assets: it “do[es] not receive from or pay to end users and merchants in cryptocurrencies,” though it may engage crypto brokers for FX/expatriation needs (and reported a $5.6 million deposit trapped at a crypto exchange in November 2022 that was sold for $3.4 million in December 2023). This supports classification as a service provider to crypto clients without direct handling or custody of digital assets. ([investor.dlocal.com](https://investor.dlocal.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/DLO_2023-Annual-Report.pdf))  
- No evidence indicates corporate treasury crypto holdings, mining operations, or crypto-specific hardware/hosting supply during 2017–2024. ([investor.dlocal.com](https://investor.dlocal.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/DLO_2023-Annual-Report.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Bizserv is set to 1.0 because dLocal’s crypto exposure arises from providing payments infrastructure and fiat rails to crypto-ecosystem merchants, without taking custody of, issuing, or transacting in digital assets on behalf of end users. Management’s own filing language explicitly distinguishes its activities from direct crypto handling, and the quantified 2022 TPV share for “crypto” further indicates the exposure is present but peripheral. With no mining, treasury holdings, or infrastructure angles in evidence, other mechanism weights are 0.0. ([investor.dlocal.com](https://investor.dlocal.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/4Q22-earnings-transcript.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Crypto-related TPV and revenue are not broken out after 2022, so post-2022 vertical sizing cannot be independently verified from the public materials reviewed here. The 2023 20-F notes limited operational interaction with crypto brokers for FX, which adds risk exposure but does not change the mechanism classification because it is not a customer-facing digital-asset product. Evidence indicates minimal materiality; RevShare is coded conservatively. ([investor.dlocal.com](https://investor.dlocal.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/DLO_2023-Annual-Report.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Mar 15, 2022 (Q4 2021): “We have added crypto as a new vertical. We are still in our early stages of pilots…” — The Motley Fool transcript. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/03/15/dlocal-limited-dlo-q4-2021-earnings-call-transcrip/))  
- Apr 4, 2023 (Q4 2022): “We also serve wallets, with 1% of our total TPV, and crypto, with 0.2% of our total TPV.” — Company IR transcript PDF. ([investor.dlocal.com](https://investor.dlocal.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/4Q22-earnings-transcript.pdf))

**Filings (20‑F / annual report and presentations):**
- Mar 19, 2024 (Form 20‑F FY2023):
- Mar 19, 2024 (Form 20‑F FY2023): “we do not receive from or pay to end users and merchants in crypto currencies,” and disclosed a US$5.6m deposit stuck at a crypto provider in Nov 2022, with a US$3.4m recovery recognized in 2023. — Form 20‑F PDF (Item 3/5 notes) (investor.dlocal.com)  
- Apr 4, 2023: “In 2022, financial services include wallets (1% of total TPV) and crypto (0.2% of total TPV).” — 4Q22 earnings presentation PDF (investor.dlocal.com)

**Financial news / other:**
- Apr 4, 2023: dLocal notes a $5.6m deposit with bankrupt crypto exchange FTX recorded as a loss allowance; shares fall on mixed results. — Proactive Investors

</small>

### Firm Overview
IRIDEX Corporation is a U.S.-based ophthalmic medical technology company that develops and sells laser systems, delivery devices, and disposable probes used to treat glaucoma and retinal diseases. It is listed on the Nasdaq under ticker IRIX and describes its primary platforms as the Cyclo G6 for glaucoma and photocoagulation lasers for retina. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1006045/000095017024038650/irix-20231230.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
A review of IRIDEX’s business model, SEC filings, and investor materials over 2017–2024 shows no involvement with cryptocurrency activities such as mining, custody/trading, digital-asset products, or blockchain-focused services. The FY2023 Form 10‑K describes a pure-play ophthalmic device company and contains no references to “crypto,” “cryptocurrency,” “Bitcoin,” or “blockchain,” consistent with a non-crypto operating profile. No disclosures indicate corporate treasury holdings of digital assets or services provided to crypto-native clients. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1006045/000095017024038650/irix-20231230.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of any substantive crypto touchpoints across filings and investor materials during the 2017–2024 window, IRIDEX is assigned unclass = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. The company’s activities center on regulated medical devices for eye care, with no evidence of crypto-related holdings, mining, financial services, business services, or infrastructure provisioning to the crypto ecosystem. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1006045/000095017024038650/irix-20231230.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified.

[SEC](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1006045/000095017024038650/irix-20231230.htm)  
- Nov 9, 2023 (Form 10‑Q Q3 2023): business and risk discussion consistent with medical devices; no crypto-related disclosures. [SEC](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1006045/000095017023065162/irix-20230930.htm)

## SSH COMMUNICATIONS SECURITY

* Rank: 425 | Expo: 0.09 | id_str: FI0009008270

* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Annual Reports (2020, 2022), financial statement releases and interim reports (2022–2024), company product/solutions pages

### Firm Overview
SSH Communications Security Oyj is a Finland-based cybersecurity software company listed on Nasdaq Helsinki (ticker SSH1V). The firm develops secure access and communications solutions such as PrivX (privileged access/zero trust), Universal SSH Key Manager, and NQX (network encryption), with a focus on cryptography, key management, and quantum‑safe security for enterprise and critical infrastructure customers. ([ssh.com](https://www.ssh.com/hubfs/SSH%20Annual%20Report%202022.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
A review of SSH’s filings and investor materials from 2017–2024 shows no business activities related to cryptocurrencies such as mining, exchange/brokerage, custody, payments, or digital‑asset investment products. The company’s disclosures consistently describe a software and solutions vendor in privileged access, key management, secure file transfer, and network encryption, including quantum‑safe features, without touching digital assets or blockchain services. No treasury or operational holdings of cryptocurrencies are disclosed in annual reports or financial statement releases in the covered period. References to “crypto” in product naming (e.g., legacy CryptoAuditor) relate to cryptography and encrypted session auditing rather than cryptocurrency. ([ssh.com](https://www.ssh.com/hubfs/SSH%20Annual%20Report%202022.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of any substantive cryptocurrency touchpoints across annual reports, interim/financial statement releases, and product materials over 2017–2024, the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism weights = 0.0. SSH’s activities are squarely in defensive cybersecurity and cryptography (not cryptocurrency), with no evidence of mining, balance‑sheet coin holdings, crypto financial services, ecosystem business services, or crypto‑specific infrastructure provisioning. ([ssh.com](https://www.ssh.com/hubfs/SSH%20Annual%20Report%202022.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls / IR materials:**
- Feb 23, 2023: Financial Statement Release FY2022 — business overview and figures; no digital-asset activities disclosed. [GlobeNewswire](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2023/02/23/2613904/0/en/SSH-COMMUNICATIONS-SECURITY-CORPORATION-FINANCIAL-STATEMENT-RELEASE-JANUARY-1-DECEMBER-31-2022.html)  
- Aug 10, 2023: Interim Report Jan–Jun 2023 — operating review; focus on access management, key management, network encryption. [SSH IR PDF](https://www.ssh.com/hubfs/Investor%20documents/2025/SSH%20CMD%20master%20presentation.pdf)

**Filings (annual/financial statements):**
- 2022 Annual Report: company description, products (PrivX, UKM, NQX); no crypto services/holdings disclosed. [SSH IR PDF](https://www.ssh.com/hubfs/SSH%20Annual%20Report%202022.pdf)

## SRAX A

* Rank: 426 | Expo: 0.09 | id_str: US78472M1062
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2018, 2021), Q4 2018 earnings call, company press releases (2017, 2021)

### Firm Overview
SRAX, Inc. is a U.S.-based technology company whose primary business since 2019–2021 has centered on Sequire, a SaaS investor-intelligence and virtual events platform serving public companies. Prior to 2021, SRAX also developed BIGtoken, a consumer data marketplace built on a blockchain identification graph, which it later divested in February 2021. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20210517005860/en/SRAX-Reports-Continued-Revenue-Growth-in-Q1-2021-and-Increases-Full-Year-Guidance?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
SRAX’s current crypto touchpoints are indirect and service-led. Through Sequire, the company organized a dedicated Sequire Blockchain and Crypto Conference in July 2021 featuring multiple blockchain/crypto companies, positioning SRAX as an events/marketing conduit to the crypto ecosystem rather than a handler of digital assets. Earlier, BIGtoken was described in SEC filings as a “blockchain identification graph” platform; however, SRAX divested this subsidiary in 2021 and its core operations since then have focused on Sequire. There is no evidence that SRAX mined crypto, held crypto on its treasury, ran a crypto exchange/custody service, or issued crypto investment products during 2017–2024. ([nasdaq.com](https://www.nasdaq.com/press-release/srax-to-host-the-sequire-blockchain-and-crypto-conference-on-july-15th-2021-2021-06?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
The company’s observable crypto linkage is providing non‑financial services (marketing/investor events, audience access) to crypto‑focused issuers via Sequire, and historical blockchain technology development within BIGtoken prior to its 2021 divestiture. As such, the appropriate mechanism is bizserv = 1.0, with all other categories at 0.0. Given the peripheral nature of these activities to consolidated SRAX operations in the covered period, RevShare is coded 1 (<~5%) and AssetShare 0. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20210709005040/en/SRAX-to-Host-Brock-Pierce-Maja-Vujinovic-Saum-Noursalehi-and-more-as-Keynote-Speakers-at-the-2021-Sequire-Blockchain-Conference?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
SRAX announced in 2017 an intention to pay a “cryptocurrency dividend” tied to an ICO for BIGtoken; this appears not to have materialized and predates the 2021 divestiture of BIGtoken, so it is not treated as an ongoing finserv or holders exposure. Earnings transcripts used to anchor the period highlight BIGtoken’s launch and growth but do not themselves evidence custody, exchange, or treasury holdings of crypto. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1538217/000155335017001303/srax_ex99z01.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Apr 1, 2019 (Q4/FY2018), transcript: “We also developed BIGtoken… completed the Beta in 2018, [and] launched general availability in 2019.” [SEC Exhibit 99.01] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1538217/000155335019000335/srax_ex99z01.htm)  
- Aug 12, 2021 (Q2 2021), transcript: “We’ve held two conferences in the quarter… LD Micro and a Cannabis Conference.” [AlphaSpread transcript] (https://www.alphaspread.com/security/otc/srax/investor-relations/earnings-call/q2-2021)

**Filings (10‑K / 10‑Q / 8‑K):**
- 2018 Form 10‑K: “The core of the project is our blockchain identification graph platform… The regulatory regime governing blockchain technologies, cryptocurrencies, [and] digital assets… is uncertain.” [SEC] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1538217/000155335018000287/srax_10k.htm)  
- 2021 Form 10‑K: “BIGToken was divested pursuant to a share exchange agreement that closed on February 4, 2021.” [StockLight copy of 10‑K] (https://stocklight.com/stocks/us/nasdaq-srax/srax/annual-reports/nasdaq-srax-2021-10K-21794791.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Jun 22, 2021: “SRAX to Host the Sequire Blockchain and Crypto Conference on July 15th, 2021.” [Nasdaq Press Release] (https://www.nasdaq.com/press-release/srax-to-host-the-sequire-blockchain-and-crypto-conference-on-july-15th-2021-2021-06)  
- Jul 22, 2021: “Replays Available from the… Sequire Blockchain Conference.” [Business Wire] (https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20210722005173/en/Replays-Available-from-the-Influential-Companies-of-the-Sequire-Blockchain-Conference)  
- Nov 28, 2017: “SRAX Intends to Pay Cryptocurrency Dividend with BIGtoken” (intention; not treated as ongoing exposure). [SEC Exhibit 99.01] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1538217/000155335017001303/srax_ex99z01.htm)

## SEAGATE TECHNOLOGY HOLDINGS

* Rank: 427 | Expo: 0.09 | id_str: IE00BKVD2N49
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 1.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q4 2021 earnings call; company 10-K (2024); industry/news coverage on Chia-driven HDD demand and decentralized storage alliances (2021–2022)

### Firm Overview
Seagate Technology Holdings plc is a global data storage manufacturer headquartered in the U.S./Ireland and listed on Nasdaq (ticker: STX). Its core business is hard disk drives and related mass-capacity storage systems for cloud, enterprise, and consumer markets; any crypto touchpoints during 2017–2024 were indirect and hardware-driven rather than financial. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1137789/000113778924000068/stx-20240628.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
The company’s crypto linkage stems from supplying storage hardware used by storage-centric blockchain and decentralized storage activity (e.g., Chia farming and IPFS/Filecoin ecosystems). On its July 21, 2021 earnings call, management cited “meaningful increase in HDD demand due in part to the initial buildout of the Chia net space,” describing the contribution as at most mid-single-digit percent of industry exabyte shipments and noting incremental sales to “Chia partners.” ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/07/21/seagate-technology-plc-stx-q4-2021-earnings-call-t/))
Beyond that episodic lift, Seagate engaged with decentralized storage initiatives as a founding member of the Decentralized Storage Alliance and through the Circular Drive Initiative alongside Chia Network, both aligning its hardware with Web3/storage-centric blockchain use cases rather than taking custody of digital assets. ([blocksandfiles.com](https://blocksandfiles.com/2022/10/31/decentralized-storage-alliance-web3/))
There is no evidence in filings or calls that Seagate mined crypto, held crypto on its balance sheet, or offered crypto financial services. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1137789/000113778924000068/stx-20240628.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Seagate’s exposure is best classified as infrastructure: it provides physical storage hardware that can be (and was, in 2021) purchased by crypto users for storage-centric blockchain activity. Management’s contemporaneous commentary frames crypto-related demand as a modest, largely channel-driven uplift rather than a core segment, supporting RevShare = 1 (<~5%) and AssetShare = 0. With no treasury holdings, mining, custody, or product issuance, holders/miners/finserv/bizserv receive 0.0, and infrastr = 1.0. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/07/21/seagate-technology-plc-stx-q4-2021-earnings-call-t/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Crypto-related demand (e.g., Chia) appears transient and was not broken out as a discrete segment in filings; subsequent calls in 2023–2024 focus on cloud/AI-driven storage demand rather than crypto, limiting precision on sustained revenue share. Participation in industry alliances signals relevance to decentralized storage but does not by itself quantify materiality. ([s24.q4cdn.com](https://s24.q4cdn.com/101481333/files/doc_financials/2024/q4/CORRECTED-TRANSCRIPT_-Seagate-Technology-Holdings-Plc-STX-US-Q4-2024-Earnings-Call-23-July-2024-5_00-PM-ET.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jul 21, 2021 (Q4 FY2021), transcript: “meaningful increase in HDD demand due in part to the initial buildout of the Chia net space… at most a mid-single digit percentage of total industry exabyte shipments” and “incremental sales… to Chia partners.” [Motley Fool] (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/07/21/seagate-technology-plc-stx-q4-2021-earnings-call-t/)  
- Jul 21, 2021 (Q4 FY2021), corrected transcript copy with same remarks on Chia/store‑centric blockchain demand. [Seagate IR PDF] (https://s24.q4cdn.com/101481333/files/doc_financials/2021/q4/FINAL-STX-CORRECTED-TRANSCRIPT-Seagate-Technology-Holdings-Plc(STX-US),-Q4-2021-Earnings-Call,-21-July-2021-900-AM-ET-21-Jul-21.pdf)

**Filings (10-K / annual):**
- Jun 28, 2024: Form 10-K — business remains HDD/mass-capacity focused; no treasury crypto holdings or crypto-financial products disclosed. [SEC] (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1137789/000113778924000068/stx-20240628.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- Jun 9, 2021: Seagate CFO notes “increase in the crypto farming demand” (Chia) at BofA conference. [Blocks & Files] (https://www.blocksandfiles.com/2021/06/09/chia-space-crypto-mining-puts-seagate-disk-drive-revenues-in-a-spin/)  
- Oct 31, 2022: Seagate named founding member of the Decentralized Storage Alliance with Protocol Labs/Filecoin Foundation and others. [Blocks & Files] (https://www.blocksandfiles.com/disk/2022/10/31/web3-bros-form-decentralized-storage-alliance/1589621)  
- Nov 2, 2021: Circular Drive Initiative launches; Seagate cited as founding storage vendor; second-use cases include “storage-centric blockchains.” [PR Newswire] (https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/major-storage-vendors-join-circular-drive-initiative-to-combat-e-waste-301413614.html)  
- Oct 20, 2021: Overview noting Seagate told investors it saw more demand tied to Chia. [Fortune] (https://fortune.com/2021/10/20/seagate-ceo-dave-mosley-crypto-chip-shortage-covid/)

</small>

### Firm Overview
GEE Group Inc. is a U.S.-based staffing and recruiting company that provides professional and commercial staffing services across information technology, engineering, finance and accounting, legal, and healthcare end markets. It is listed on NYSE American under ticker JOB and operates nationally through multiple brands. ([ir.geegroup.com](https://ir.geegroup.com/all-sec-filings/content/0001477932-24-008212/job_10k.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across earnings calls and SEC filings reviewed for 2017–2024, there is no credible evidence that GEE Group mined cryptocurrencies, held crypto on its balance sheet, or provided crypto financial services such as custody, exchange, or crypto investment products. The FY2024 10-K contains no references to cryptocurrency, blockchain, digital assets, mining, or related activities. The company’s earnings calls in 2024 focus on staffing market conditions, technology tools like AI for recruiting efficiency, and operating performance, with no crypto-related discussion. Given the absence of substantive crypto linkage, the firm is classified as unclassified for crypto exposure. ([ir.geegroup.com](https://ir.geegroup.com/all-sec-filings/content/0001477932-24-008212/job_10k.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Because no meaningful crypto exposure was identified in transcripts, filings, or press releases for the 2017–2024 period, all mechanism categories are set to 0.0 and unclass = 1.0. There is likewise no evidence that the stock trades as a crypto proxy among investors, so invbase = 0. RevShare and AssetShare are marked NA because no crypto-attributable revenue or assets are disclosed. ([ir.geegroup.com](https://ir.geegroup.com/all-sec-filings/content/0001477932-24-008212/job_10k.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified; multiple filings and calls within the sample window show no crypto-related activities or disclosures. As with any “absence of evidence” assessment, future activities beyond December 2024 were not considered for classification. ([ir.geegroup.com](https://ir.geegroup.com/all-sec-filings/content/0001477932-24-008212/job_10k.htm))

## JP MORGAN CHASE & CO.

* Rank: 429 | Expo: 0.09 | id_str: US46625H1005
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Investor Day 2023 transcript; Q4 2021 earnings call; 2023 Form 10-K; Reuters/Bloomberg/CoinDesk/CNBC (2019–2024)

### Firm Overview
JPMorgan Chase & Co. is a U.S.-based global financial services firm operating across consumer and commercial banking, payments, markets, and asset and wealth management. Its blockchain and tokenization initiatives sit within wholesale payments and securities services and are focused on institutional clients.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The bank operates blockchain-based financial services for institutions, including JPM Coin/Onyx (now Kinexys) deposit-token rails for near real-time payments and programmable payments; management cited “JPM Coin at a $1 billion a day” in transaction volume at the 2023 Investor Day and highlighted “programmable money” use cases. ([jpmorganchase.com](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/content/dam/jpmc/jpmorgan-chase-and-co/investor-relations/documents/events/2023/jpmc-investor-day-2023/JPM-Investor-Day-2023-Final-Transcript_Corporate-Investment-Bank.pdf))  
In October 2023, JPMorgan executed the first live transaction on its Tokenized Collateral Network, where BlackRock tokenized money-market fund shares and transferred them to Barclays as collateral, evidencing on-chain collateral and settlement capabilities for traditional assets. ([bloomberg.com](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2023-10-11/jpmorgan-jpm-launches-blockchain-settlement-in-blackrock-barclays-trade?utm_source=openai))  
Separately, in 2021 the bank opened access for wealth management clients to multiple crypto funds (including a new Bitcoin fund with NYDIG) on a request-only basis, indicating client-facing crypto investment product access. ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2021/08/05/bitcoin-jpmorgan-led-by-jamie-dimon-quietly-unveils-access-to-a-half-dozen-crypto-funds.html?utm_source=openai))  
Ahead of the January 2024 spot bitcoin ETF approvals, BlackRock named JPMorgan Securities as an authorized participant for its iShares Bitcoin Trust, reflecting the bank’s market-structure role in crypto-linked ETFs. ([bnnbloomberg.ca](https://www.bnnbloomberg.ca/business/technology/2023/12/29/blackrock-names-jane-street-jpmorgan-as-bitcoin-etf-brokers/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
All identified exposure is through crypto financial services provided to institutional and wealth clients: blockchain-based payments and tokenization (JPM Coin/Onyx/Kinexys, programmable payments, TCN), plus facilitation roles around crypto investment products (wealth access to crypto funds; AP for spot BTC ETF). There is no evidence of proprietary crypto mining, nor of the firm holding crypto on its corporate balance sheet as a treasury investment; the 2023 Form 10-K and disclosures do not indicate treasury digital asset holdings. Given the breadth and prominence of these activities and their direct handling of digital-asset/payment tokens for clients, the classification is finserv = 1.0, with all other mechanism weights at 0.0. ([jpmorganchase.com](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/content/dam/jpmc/jpmorgan-chase-and-co/investor-relations/documents/events/2023/jpmc-investor-day-2023/JPM-Investor-Day-2023-Final-Transcript_Corporate-Investment-Bank.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
JPM Coin and deposit tokens are permissioned representations of bank deposits rather than public cryptocurrencies; this sits at the boundary of “crypto” definitions but fits the project’s finserv category (payments/custody/investment products). Revenue attributable to blockchain/crypto is not disclosed separately and is immaterial relative to firmwide revenue; RevShare is coded as 1 (<~5%) on this basis. ETF authorized participant and research roles imply market-structure involvement rather than proprietary “holder” exposure; any incidental fund positions held by affiliates for market-making are treated as operational and not as treasury “holders.” ([jpmorganchase.com](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/content/dam/jpmc/jpmorgan-chase-and-co/investor-relations/documents/quarterly-earnings/2023/4th-quarter/corp-10k-2023.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 14, 2022 (Q4 2021), transcript: “On the wholesale side, we’ve continued to innovate on our Execute trading platform, commercialized blockchain through Onyx, and are building out real-time payments capabilities.” [JPMorgan IR PDF] (https://www.jpmorganchase.com/content/dam/jpmc/jpmorgan-chase-and-co/investor-relations/documents/quarterly-earnings/2021/4th-quarter/JPM-4Q21-Earnings-Call-Final-Transcript.pdf)  
- May 22, 2023 (Investor Day – CIB), transcript: “It remains the leading blockchain franchise… you heard about the JPM Coin at a $1 billion a day. Programmable money is the next frontier…” [JPMorgan Investor Day PDF] (https://www.jpmorganchase.com/content/dam/jpmc/jpmorgan-chase-and-co/investor-relations/documents/events/2023/jpmc-investor-day-2023/JPM-Investor-Day-2023-Final-Transcript_Corporate-Investment-Bank.pdf)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Feb 16, 2024: Form 10-K — firmwide business lines and risk disclosures; no treasury digital-asset holdings indicated. [JPMorgan 2023 Form 10-K PDF] (https://www.jpmorganchase.com/content/dam/jpmc/jpmorgan-chase-and-co/investor-relations/documents/quarterly-earnings/2023/4th-quarter/corp-10k-2023.pdf)  
- Feb 16, 2024: Press release noting filing of 2023 Form 10-K. [JPMorgan IR] (https://jpmorganchaseco.gcs-web.com/node/626116/pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Oct 11, 2023: BlackRock tokenized MMF shares posted to Barclays as OTC collateral via JPMorgan’s Tokenized Collateral Network (Onyx). [Bloomberg] (https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2023-10-11/jpmorgan-jpm-launches-blockchain-settlement-in-blackrock-barclays-trade) | [CoinDesk] (https://www.coindesk.com/business/2023/10/11/jpmorgan-debuts-tokenized-blackrock-shares-as-collateral-with-barclays)  
- Jun 23, 2023: JPM Coin expands to euro payments; cumulative volumes hundreds of billions since 2019. [CoinDesk] (https://www.coindesk.com/business/2023/06/23/jpmorgan-expands-its-blockchain-based-token-to-euro-payments-bloomberg)  
- Nov 10, 2023: JPMorgan launches “programmable payments” on JPM Coin for institutional clients. [Investing.com] (https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/jpmorgan-introduces-programmable-payments-feature-on-jpm-coin-93CH-3231252) | [Blockworks] (https://blockworks.com/news/jpmorgan-blockchain-payments)  
- Aug 5, 2021: JPMorgan opens access to a half-dozen crypto funds for wealth clients; new Bitcoin fund with NYDIG. [CNBC] (https://www.cnbc.com/2021/08/05/bitcoin-jpmorgan-led-by-jamie-dimon-quietly-unveils-access-to-a-half-dozen-crypto-funds.html)  
- Dec 29, 2023: BlackRock names JPMorgan Securities as an authorized participant for its proposed spot bitcoin ETF. [BNN Bloomberg] (https://www.bnnbloomberg.ca/business/technology/2023/12/29/blackrock-names-jane-street-jpmorgan-as-bitcoin-etf-brokers/)

</small>

## SEVEN BANK

* Rank: 430 | Expo: 0.09 | id_str: JP3105220002
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: IR presentations and earnings call transcripts (FY2018–FY2021), SBI Holdings/Money Tap shareholder disclosure (2020), Bank of Japan CBDC Forum member list (2024)

### Firm Overview
Seven Bank, Ltd. is a Japan-based retail bank best known for operating a nationwide ATM network centered in 7‑Eleven convenience stores and offering consumer banking and international remittance services. The bank is part of the Seven & i group and complements its ATM platform with app-based money transfer offerings and partnerships. ([sevenbank.co.jp](https://www.sevenbank.co.jp/corp/news/2021/pdf/20211105_E2_PR.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Evidence indicates exposure through crypto/blockchain-enabled financial services rather than balance-sheet holdings. Seven Bank is listed as a shareholder in Money Tap Co., Ltd., a bank-to-bank mobile payments platform that uses Ripple’s blockchain technology; Seven Bank appears by name in Money Tap’s official shareholder roster published when Ripple invested in Money Tap in October 2020. ([sbigroup.co.jp](https://www.sbigroup.co.jp/english/news/pdf/2020/1028_a_en.pdf))  
Separately, the Bank of Japan’s CBDC Forum documents list Seven Bank as a forum member and working-group participant, tying the bank to digital-currency infrastructure discussions and pilots. ([boj.or.jp](https://www.boj.or.jp/en/paym/digital/d_forum/dfo240115a.pdf))  
Core operating disclosures and presentations emphasize international remittance services and retail banking; there is no indication the bank mines crypto or holds tokens on its treasury balance sheet. ([sevenbank.co.jp](https://www.sevenbank.co.jp/corp/news/2021/pdf/20211105_E2_PR.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
All substantiated exposure arises from
### Weight Assignment
All substantiated exposure arises from blockchain-enabled payments and digital-currency initiatives rather than proprietary coin holdings or mining. Seven Bank is a named shareholder in Money Tap, a Ripple-technology payments platform, and is a listed participant in the Bank of Japan’s CBDC Forum working groups; these support a financial-services classification. No disclosures indicate treasury digital-asset holdings or mining activity. Accordingly, finserv = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. 

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Money Tap exposure is via shareholding/participation; public sources do not explicitly show Seven Bank running crypto custody, exchange, or XRP/ODL-based remittances itself. “Blockchain-enabled payments” (Ripple/Money Tap, CBDC pilots) may not involve public cryptocurrencies directly, but they fit the project’s finserv channel (payments). The bank’s IR and annual reports do not break out any crypto-attributable revenue or digital asset balances, so RevShare and AssetShare are coded 0 based on available evidence. ([sevenbank.co.jp](https://www.sevenbank.co.jp/english/ir/library/disclosure/pdf/20210930_E1_AR.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 1, 2019 (Q3 FY2018), transcript: “New payment service providers in virtual or cryptocurrency operators have expanded their business very rapidly, but people want security and safety enhancement these days.” — [AlphaSpread transcript] (https://www.alphaspread.com/security/tse/8410/investor-relations/earnings-call/q3-2019)  
- Nov 2018 (Q2 FY2018), transcript: Management reviews interim results and outlines settlement/account and remittance initiatives; no indication of crypto holdings or mining. — [AlphaSpread transcript] (https://www.alphaspread.com/security/tse/8410/investor-relations/earnings-call/q2-2019)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Nov 5, 2021: Financial Results for the Second Quarter of FY2021 (presentation) — operating overview of ATM, payments, and international money transfer services; no treasury digital-asset holdings indicated. — [Seven Bank IR PDF] (https://www.sevenbank.co.jp/corp/news/2021/pdf/20211105_E2_PR.pdf)  
- Sep 30, 2021: Integrated Report 2021 — business mix and segment disclosures; no crypto asset balances reported. — [Seven Bank Integrated Report 2021 PDF] (https://www.sevenbank.co.jp/english/ir/library/disclosure/pdf/20210930_E1_AR.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Oct 28, 2020: Ripple invests in Money Tap; official shareholder list includes “Seven Bank.” — [SBI Holdings press release PDF] (https://www.sbigroup.co.jp/english/news/pdf/2020/1028_a_en.pdf)  
- Jan 2024: Bank of Japan CBDC Forum member list — “Seven Bank, Ltd.” named as a forum member and WG participant. — [Bank of Japan presentation PDF] (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/paym/digital/d_forum/dfo240115a.pdf)

## MCEWEN MINING

* Rank: 431 | Expo: 0.09 | id_str: US58039P3055
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filing (FY2023), earnings call transcripts (Q4 2019), company/Reuters press releases (2022–2023)

### Firm Overview
McEwen Mining Inc. is a gold and silver mining production and exploration company with an advanced copper development project focused on the Americas, including the Gold Bar mine (Nevada), the Fox Complex/Froome mine and Stock mill (Ontario), a 49% interest in the San José mine (Argentina), and ownership in McEwen Copper, which holds the Los Azules copper project (Argentina). These activities define the company’s core operations during the 2017–2024 period. ([mcewenmining.com](https://www.mcewenmining.com/files/doc_financials/mcewen/2023/Q4/McEwen_Mining_Inc_10-K_EDGAR_20231231.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across filings and earnings calls from 2017–2024, McEwen Mining’s disclosures center on precious‑metals production and copper project development; there is no evidence that the company mined cryptocurrencies, held crypto on its balance sheet, or offered crypto‑related financial services. The FY2023 10‑K describes a traditional metals mining portfolio without digital‑asset holdings or services. Earnings calls discuss ounces produced, costs, mine plans, and the Los Azules copper project, not crypto activities. Major strategic news in 2022–2023 involved investments by Rio Tinto’s Nuton and Stellantis in McEwen Copper, which are unrelated to cryptocurrencies. ([mcewenmining.com](https://www.mcewenmining.com/files/doc_financials/mcewen/2023/Q4/McEwen_Mining_Inc_10-K_EDGAR_20231231.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Because no substantive crypto exposure is documented in transcripts, regulatory filings, or credible news during January 2017–December 2024, the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). There is no basis for assigning weights to holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr, and no evidence that the investor base treats the stock as a crypto proxy; therefore invbase = 0.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified. Source materials consistently describe conventional gold/silver mining and copper project development with no crypto references. If future disclosures indicate tokenization, digital‑asset treasury activity, or mining‑specific infrastructure services, the classification would need to be revisited.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Mar 17, 2020 (Q4 2019), transcript: “For 2020, our share of production from San José is expected to be 45,700 oz gold and 3.2M oz silver.” — [The Motley Fool] (https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/03/17/mcewen-mining-inc-mux-q4-2019-earnings-call-transc.aspx)  
- Nov 2024 (Q3 2024), transcript header and discussion focus on gold output and Los Azules; no crypto mentioned. — [Investing.com transcript summary] (https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-mcewen-mining-reports-robust-q3-2024-results-93CH-3709139)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Mar 15, 2024: “McEwen Mining Inc. is a gold and silver mining production and exploration company with an advanced copper development project, focused on the Americas.” — [Form 10‑K (FY2023) PDF] (https://www.mcewenmining.com/files/doc_financials/mcewen/2023/Q4/McEwen_Mining_Inc_10-K_EDGAR_20231231.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Feb 27, 2023: Stellantis invests $155M to acquire a minority stake in McEwen Copper (Los Azules). — [Reuters via Investing.com] (https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/stellantis-invests-155-million-in-argentine-copper-mine-3015979) | [Stellantis press release PDF] (https://www.stellantis.com/content/dam/stellantis-corporate/news/press-releases/2023/february/27-02-2023/en/20230227-Stellants-Invests-in-Argentina-Copper-Mine-EN.pdf)

## DENA

* Rank: 432 | Expo: 0.09 | id_str: JP3548610009
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Company news (2021–2022), earnings-call transcript (FY2018/FY

### Firm Overview
DeNA Co., Ltd. is a Japan-based internet company with core businesses in mobile games, live streaming (e.g., Pococha), sports operations (Yokohama DeNA BayStars and Kawasaki Brave Thunders), and healthcare/medical services. Within 2021–2022, the group experimented with blockchain/NFT initiatives that sat alongside its entertainment properties rather than constituting a separate financial segment. ([irwebcasting.com](https://www.irwebcasting.com/20240508/4/c54592e9f5/media/presentation_ja_02.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
DeNA issued and sold NFTs tied to its sports franchises, launching the “PLAYBACK 9” digital movie collection for the Yokohama DeNA BayStars on November 30, 2021, using LINE Blockchain to mint the tokens and initially requiring LINE BITMAX Wallet for users. In April 2022, a user-to-user market function enabled secondary trading on LINE NFT with settlement via LINE Pay. Separately, DeNA’s auction platform Mobaoku integrated Uniqys Garage in November 2022 to run NFT auctions. There is no evidence of cryptocurrency mining, balance‑sheet crypto treasury holdings, or crypto hardware/data‑center provisioning during 2017–2024. 

### Weight Assignment
The company directly issued and facilitated trading of NFTs and enabled NFT auctions, which fits “finserv” (issuing/managing crypto products and facilitating crypto transactions). The activity appears ancillary to DeNA’s broader entertainment portfolio and not a stand‑alone reportable segment; filings during the period focus on games, live streaming, sports, and healthcare with no crypto assets disclosed, supporting a low revenue share and no asset share. Accordingly, weights are set to finserv 1.0 and all others 0.0. ([www2.jpx.co.jp](https://www2.jpx.co.jp/disc/24320/140120240508584367.pdf?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The NFT initiatives were limited in scope and tied to specific entertainment properties; their economic materiality is not broken out. Public briefings and transcripts seldom mention crypto explicitly; one English transcript from 2019 anchors the earnings‑call source requirement but predates the 2021–2022 NFT launches. No evidence indicates DeNA served as a major crypto proxy for investors.

## MARKETAXESS HOLDINGS

* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2023), earnings-call transcripts (2020–2021), company press releases (2024)

### Firm Overview
MarketAxess Holdings Inc. is a U.S.-listed financial technology company that operates electronic trading platforms for global fixed‑income markets, including corporate bonds, EM debt, Eurobonds, munis and U.S. government bonds; it also offers market data and post‑trade services. Its 2023 Form 10‑K emphasizes trading, data and post‑trade solutions in credit and rates, with no digital‑asset products noted. ([d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net](https://d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net/CIK-0001278021/5e3b8017-f165-40c4-8e10-d0572745e200.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024 sources, there is no evidence that MarketAxess held cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet, mined digital assets, offered crypto trading/custody, or provided infrastructure purpose‑built for crypto workloads. The 2023 Form 10‑K references “blockchain technologies” only in the context of general technology evolution and potential resource needs, not as an active product line or revenue stream. Company press releases and presentations in 2024 similarly focus on credit and rates trading innovations (e.g., X‑Pro, Mid‑X, Open Trading) without introducing crypto or tokenization services. On this basis, no substantive crypto exposure channel is identified for the period. ([d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net](https://d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net/CIK-0001278021/5e3b8017-f165-40c4-8e10-d0572745e200.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Because the firm’s disclosures and communications during 2017–2024 show no material activity in any crypto‑related mechanism — holders, miners, financial services, business services, or infrastructure — the classification is unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism weights = 0.0. The generic risk‑factor mention of “blockchain technologies” is insufficient to justify a non‑zero category under the threshold principle.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified beyond standard horizon limits: while industry discussion of tokenization exists generally, MarketAxess’s filings and 2017–2024 communications provide no concrete crypto/digital‑asset offering or balance‑sheet exposure.

Across 2017–2024 sources, there is no evidence that CuriosityStream held cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet, mined digital assets, offered crypto trading/custody products, or provided crypto-focused infrastructure. The FY2023 Form 10‑K contains no references to “crypto,” “cryptocurrency,” “blockchain,” or “bitcoin,” and discusses only streaming and content/licensing activities. 2024 investor communications and press materials similarly focus on operating metrics, revenue, cash flow, and content, without introducing any crypto products or balance‑sheet exposure. Because disclosures and communications during 2017–2024 show no material activity in any crypto‑related mechanism category — holders, miners, financial services, business services to the crypto ecosystem, or infrastructure — the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism weights = 0.0 under the project’s threshold principle.

## GLOBE TELECOM

* Rank: 435 | Expo: 0.09 | id_str: PHY272571498
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Consolidated financial statements (FY2024), Q1 2022 earnings call, product/help pages and 2024 PH business media

### Firm Overview
Globe Telecom, Inc. is a Philippines-based telecommunications group providing mobile, broadband and enterprise connectivity, with expanding digital platforms. Through its fintech affiliate Mynt (operator of GCash), Globe participates in payments and other financial services alongside its core telecom operations.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Globe’s crypto linkage comes via Mynt/GCash, whose in‑app feature GCrypto enables users to buy, sell and manage cryptocurrencies; GCrypto is powered by locally licensed exchange PDAX. In July 2024, GCash added PayPal USD (PYUSD) support on GCrypto, underscoring active crypto product distribution within the ecosystem. Globe’s FY2024 audited statements detail Mynt as a joint venture accounted for under the equity method, with GCash operations (via GXI) described as an e‑money issuer and payment/remittance platform under the GCash brand. There is no evidence that Globe mines crypto, holds crypto as treasury assets, or supplies crypto‑specific infrastructure. ([help.gcash.com](https://help.gcash.com/hc/en-us/articles/47624429776025-What-is-GCrypto?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence points solely to a financial‑services channel via GCrypto distributed through GCash (Mynt), which Globe reports as a joint venture and from which it recognizes equity earnings. With no indications of direct balance‑sheet crypto holdings, mining, or infrastructure provisioning, the classification concentrates entirely in finserv. Given crypto’s role as one feature within GCash’s broader wallet/fintech suite, the mechanism is coded as finserv =
... 1.0 and all other mechanisms = 0.0. The investor-base amplifier is set to 0 because Globe is not characterized in financial media or management commentary as a crypto proxy; crypto is a minor feature within a broader super‑app JV. 

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Globe does not disclose a crypto-specific revenue split within Mynt/GCash; GCrypto’s financial contribution is therefore inferred to be minor relative to Globe’s consolidated revenues. Several transcript repositories list Globe’s calls behind paywalls; one accessible 2022 call is quoted below but does not specifically discuss crypto. Classification relies on audited filings describing Mynt as a JV plus GCash/GCrypto official product documentation. ([help.gcash.com](https://help.gcash.com/hc/en-us/articles/47624429776025-What-is-GCrypto?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**

- Aug 7, 2024 (Q2 2024) — transcript listing (content gated). — [MarketScreener](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/GLOBE-TELECOM-INC-6492511/news/Transcript-Globe-Telecom-Inc-Q2-2024-Earnings-Call-Aug-07-2024-47579843/)   
- Feb 7, 2024 (Q4 2023) — transcript listing (content gated). — [MarketScreener HK](https://hk.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/GLOBE-TELECOM-INC-6492511/news/Transcript-Globe-Telecom-Inc-Q4-2023-Earnings-Call-Feb-07-2024-45901425/) 

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**

- 2Q 2023 SEC Form 17‑Q — describes GCash product suite including “investments (via GFunds and the recently launched GStocks and GCrypto).” — [Globe 2Q23 17‑Q PDF](https://www.globe.com.ph/sites/default/files/reports/secpse/2023/B.%20Quarterly/IV.%20Quarterly%20Report%20%2817Q%29/GLO-17Q-2Q23.pdf) ([globe.com.ph](https://www.globe.com.ph/sites/default/files/reports/secpse/2023/B.%20Quarterly/IV.%20Quarterly%20Report%20%2817Q%29/GLO-17Q-2Q23.pdf?utm_source=openai))

## LUCKIN COFFEE ADR 1:8

* Rank: 436 | Expo: 0.09 | id_str: US54951L1098
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 20-F annual report (FY2023), Q3 2019 earnings call, 2024 earnings presentations

### Firm Overview
Luckin Coffee Inc. is a China-based coffee chain operator with a digital-first ordering model centered on its mobile apps and Weixin mini‑program, and its American depositary shares trade over-the-counter in the United States. The company’s SEC filings describe its core business as retail coffee and related beverages with payments primarily through Chinese electronic payment rails. ([investor.luckincoffee.com](https://investor.luckincoffee.com/static-files/87983fca-f5ff-4ece-acf8-f445a7c3b949))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across the January 2017–December 2024 sample, a review of Luckin’s SEC annual report and available earnings materials shows no evidence that the company held cryptocurrencies as treasury assets, mined crypto, offered crypto trading/custody/investment products, or supplied crypto‑specific infrastructure. Instead, the company discloses that it accepts “a variety of payment methods, including Weixin Pay, Alipay and Union Pay,” which are conventional Chinese electronic payment systems, not cryptocurrencies. Searches of Luckin’s 2019 and 2024 earnings materials similarly revealed no crypto‑related content. Third‑party services selling Luckin gift cards for crypto are independent workarounds and do not indicate that Luckin itself accepts or handles crypto. ([investor.luckincoffee.com](https://investor.luckincoffee.com/static-files/87983fca-f5ff-4ece-acf8-f445a7c3b949))

### Weight Assignment
Because no substantive, company‑level crypto linkage was found in filings, transcripts, or reputable news during 2017–2024, Luckin is coded as unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism weights = 0.0. There is no basis to assign the investor‑base amplifier, as neither management nor financial media characterize the stock as a crypto proxy. ([investor.luckincoffee.com](https://investor.luckincoffee.com/static-files/87983fca-f5ff-4ece-acf8-f445a7c3b949))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Some transcript repositories gate content; however, available company transcripts and disclosure documents did not reference crypto. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/LUCKIN-COFFEE-INC-58388990/news/Transcript-Luckin-Coffee-Inc-Q2-2024-Earnings-Call-Jul-30-2024-47504278/))  
- Third‑party gift card platforms allow purchases with crypto but explicitly note Luckin does not itself accept crypto; these do not constitute company‑level crypto activity. ([bitrefill.com](https://www.bitrefill.com/cn/en/gift-cards/luckin-coffee-china/?utm_source=openai))  
- No indications were found that Luckin participated in China’s digital‑yuan pilots during the sample window; regardless, a CBDC would not meet this project’s cryptocurrency criteria.

## SOFTBANK GROUP

* Rank: 437 | Expo: 0.09 | id_str: JP3436100006
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: SoftBank Group Report 2023 (financial section), Q3 FY2022 investor call Q&A

### Firm Overview
SoftBank Group Corp. is a Japan-based investment holding company that manages the SoftBank Vision Funds and other investment vehicles, with a portfolio spanning global technology sectors. Its core activities are investment and capital allocation rather than operating telecommunications or financial services directly at the parent-company level. ([group.softbank](https://group.softbank/en/ir/financials/annual_reports/2023))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across January 2017–December 2024, no evidence indicates that SoftBank Group held cryptocurrency as a treasury asset, mined crypto, provided crypto trading/custody, or supplied crypto-specific infrastructure in its own operations. Crypto linkages arose via minority/venture investments held by its Vision Funds (e.g., portfolio listings include FTX Trading Ltd., West Realm Shires Inc. (FTX US), Digital Currency Group, Hashdex, Bitso, Sorare, CertiK, and The Sandbox), but these are portfolio stakes rather than company-operated crypto services. Management disclosed on an investor call that the “FTX investment $97 million was completely written off … combined … [and] captures both” FTX entities, underscoring that exposure was via investee equity, not direct crypto activity. Under the project’s taxonomy, passive equity stakes in crypto firms do not map to holders/finserv/miners/infrastructure/bizserv categories. ([group.softbank](https://group.softbank/media/Project/sbg/sbg/pdf/ir/financials/annual_reports/annual-report_fy2023_06_en.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Because SoftBank Group’s crypto interaction during the sample window was through venture/equity holdings rather than operating businesses that custody or transact digital assets, it is coded unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism weights = 0.0. The stock is not characterized in filings or mainstream coverage as a crypto proxy; therefore invbase = 0. Supporting materials (annual report portfolio lists; investor call Q&A on FTX write-down) corroborate that crypto exposure was incidental to the investment portfolio rather than a corporate operating channel. ([group.softbank](https://group.softbank/media/Project/sbg/sbg/pdf/ir/financials/annual_reports/annual-report_fy2023_06_en.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The Vision Funds’ portfolio composition changed over time and included several crypto-related companies; however, the taxonomy excludes passive/portfolio-investor exposure from mechanism categories. ([group.softbank](https://group.softbank/media/Project/sbg/sbg/pdf/ir/financials/annual_reports/annual-report_fy2023_06_en.pdf))  
- Press coverage confirmed FTX exposure was written down to zero, consistent with the call Q&A; this does not imply SoftBank operated crypto services. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/softbank-reports-q2-profit-but-announces-more-vision-fund-pain-with-ftx-writedown-2940983?utm_source=openai))  
- Some transcript repositories and IR videos are gated; available investor-call Q&A and the annual report’s financial section provided sufficient context. ([group.softbank](https://group.softbank/system/files/pdf/ir/presentations/2022/earnings-investor-call_q3fy2022_01_en.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 7, 2023 (Q3 FY2022) investor call Q&A: “the FTX investment $97 million was completely written off … [and] it is combined. It captures both.” — [SoftBank Group Q3 FY2022 Investor Call Q&A PDF](https://group.softbank/system/files/pdf/ir/presentations/2022/earnings-investor-call_q3fy2022_01_en.pdf)  

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- SoftBank Group Report 2023 (Financial section) — Vision Fund portfolio lists include FTX Trading Ltd., West Realm Shires Inc. (FTX US), Digital Currency Group, Hashdex, Bitso, Sorare, CertiK, The Sandbox (Bacasable Global), among others. — [SoftBank Group Report 2023 – Financial Section PDF](https://group.softbank/media/Project/sbg/sbg/pdf/ir/financials/annual_reports/annual-report_fy2023_06_en.pdf)

## NOVABRIDGE BIOSCIENCES ADR 10:23

* Rank: 438 | Expo: 0.09 | id_str: US44975P1030
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Form 20-F filing (FY2024), Q2 2023 earnings call transcript (Motley Fool)

### Firm Overview
NovaBridge Biosciences is the new corporate name (effective October 2025) for I-Mab, a Cayman-incorporated, Nasdaq-listed clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on developing immuno-oncology and immuno-inflammation biologics. Within the 2017–2024 sample window, the company operated as I-Mab and reported typical biotech activities (R&D, clinical pipeline progress, collaborations) in its SEC filings and results updates. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1778016/000095017025050344/imab-20241231.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across January 2017–December 2024, no evidence indicates that the company held cryptocurrency as a treasury asset, mined crypto, operated a crypto exchange/custody/payment service, or supplied crypto-specific hardware, data-center, or energy infrastructure. Public disclosures and earnings calls consistently describe a pure biopharma profile centered on antibody and bispecific drug candidates and industry partnerships (e.g., AbbVie collaboration in 2020), with no digital-asset operations mentioned. Therefore, none of the crypto-exposure mechanism categories apply. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1778016/000095017025050344/imab-20241231.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of any substantive crypto links in filings, earnings-call transcripts, or reputable news sources during the sample period, the firm is assigned unclass = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. There is no evidence that the stock trades as a crypto proxy, so invbase = 0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1778016/000095017025050344/imab-20241231.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The company rebranded to NovaBridge Biosciences and updated its ADR materials in late 2025, which is outside the January 2017–December 2024 sample window; these sources are used only for post-period identification (including ADR ratio 23:10) and do not affect the classification. ([depositaryreceipts.citi.com](https://depositaryreceipts.citi.com/adr/guides/pgm_d.aspx?cusip=44975P103&pageId=15&subpageID=105&typeDisplay=C&utm_source=openai))
- Some transcript repositories are paywalled; accessible Motley Fool transcripts and company releases provided sufficient context to establish the lack of crypto activity. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/08/31/i-mab-imab-q2-2021-earnings-call-transcript/?utm_source=openai))

## GETTY IMAGES HOLDINGS A

* Rank: 439 | Expo: 0.09 | id_str: US3742751056
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q2 2023 earnings call; Form 10-Q (Q2 2023); press releases on Candy Digital partnership (2022–2023)

### Firm Overview
Getty Images Holdings, Inc. is a NYSE‑listed global visual content company whose core business is licensing imagery and video through its Getty Images, iStock, and Unsplash brands to media, advertising, and corporate clients worldwide. Within the 2017–2024 window, management emphasized core licensing and subscriptions, while selectively experimenting with digital collectibles tied to its photo archive. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1898496/000162828024011316/gety-20231231.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
In May 2022 Getty Images announced a multi‑year, exclusive partnership with Candy Digital making Candy the developer and marketplace for “Getty Images NFTs,” with primary and secondary market transactions supported and payments available via credit card or crypto; NFTs are minted on the Palm blockchain. Subsequent drops through 2023 (e.g., the ‘70s Music & Culture and City Diaries collections) show continued—but niche—activity in digital collectibles. On the Q2 2023 earnings call and in the corresponding 10‑Q, management noted reduced activity in the “crypto and NFT space” affecting certain editorial verticals, indicating exposure is adjacent rather than core. There is no evidence the company mines crypto, runs custody/exchange services, or holds crypto as a treasury asset. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20220517005370/en/Getty-Images-Enters-Exclusive-Partnership-with-Candy-Digital-to-Introduce-its-First-Ever-NFTS))

### Weight Assignment
Getty’s crypto linkage is limited to content/licensing collaborations that result in blockchain‑minted digital collectibles, executed via a partner‑run marketplace (Candy). This fits non‑financial services provided to the crypto ecosystem rather than direct financial intermediation or asset holding. Accordingly, bizserv is set to 1.0 and all other mechanisms to 0.0. The activity appears minor relative to total revenue; there is no indication of balance‑sheet crypto assets, hence RevShare = 1 and AssetShare = 0. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20220517005370/en/Getty-Images-Enters-Exclusive-Partnership-with-Candy-Digital-to-Introduce-its-First-Ever-NFTS))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- The Candy partnership handles minting and marketplace operations; disclosures do not quantify Getty’s collectible‑related revenues, and filings do not specify whether any crypto is ever received or custodied by Getty (classification therefore leans bizserv over finserv). ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20220517005370/en/Getty-Images-Enters-Exclusive-Partnership-with-Candy-Digital-to-Introduce-its-First-Ever-NFTS))
- Earnings materials during 2017–2024 mention crypto/NFTs mainly in the context of demand trends; crypto exposure appears economically peripheral. ([insidermonkey.com](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/getty-images-holdings-inc-nysegety-q2-2023-earnings-call-transcript-1183544/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 14, 2023 (Q2 2023): “The largest decline was in sports, where we have seen reduced activity in the crypto and NFT space...” — [Insider Monkey](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/getty-images-holdings-inc-nysegety-q2-2023-earnings-call-transcript-1183544/) ([insidermonkey.com](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/getty-images-holdings-inc-nysegety-q2-2023-earnings-call-transcript-1183544/))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Form 10‑Q (quarter ended Jun 30, 2023): editorial commentary noting “reduced activity in the crypto and NFT space.” — [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1898496/000162828023030120/gety-20230630.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1898496/000162828023030120/gety-20230630.htm))
- Annual Report (Form 10‑K, FY2023): business description focused on licensing/subscriptions; no treasury crypto holdings indicated. — [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1898496/000162828024011316/gety-20231231.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1898496/000162828024011316/gety-20231231.htm?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- May 17, 2022 — “Exclusive partnership with Candy Digital to develop and marketplace Getty Images NFTs; credit card or crypto payments; Palm blockchain.” — [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20220517005370/en/Getty-Images-Enters-Exclusive-Partnership-with-Candy-Digital-to-Introduce-its-First-Ever-NFTS) ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20220517005370/en/Getty-Images-Enters-Exclusive-Partnership-with-Candy-Digital-to-Introduce-its-First-Ever-NFTS))
- Mar 17, 2023 — “Introducing ‘Exposure: by Getty Images & Candy’; drop details incl. USD/USDC and credit‑card support.” — [Candy Digital blog](https://webflow.candy.com/blogposts/introducing-exposure-by-getty-images-candy) ([webflow.candy.com](https://webflow.candy.com/blogposts/introducing-exposure-by-getty-images-candy))
- Jun 1, 2023 — “City Diaries” digital photograph collection launch in ongoing Getty‑Candy partnership. — [GlobeNewswire](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2023/06/01/2680378/0/en/Candy-Digital-and-Getty-Images-Unveil-Second-Digital-Photograph-Collection-called-City-Diaries-Visual-Narratives-From-Around-the-World.html) ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2023/06/01/2680378/0/en/Candy-Digital-and-Getty-Images-Unveil-Second-Digital-Photograph-Collection-called-City-Diaries-Visual-Narratives-From-Around-the-World.html))
