## PLDT

* Rank: 440 | Expo: 0.09 | id_str: PHY7072Q1032
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q1 2022 earnings call; Q3 2022 earnings call; Form 20‑F (FY2024); 2022 press/news on PayMaya/Maya crypto feature

### Firm Overview
PLDT Inc. is a Philippines-based integrated telecommunications company listed in Manila and on the NYSE via ADRs. Through its significant minority stake in Voyager Innovations (operator of the Maya money app and Maya Bank), PLDT is indirectly linked to digital financial services, including a crypto trading feature introduced in 2022. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/78150/000095017025038448/phi-20241231.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management highlighted during 2022 earnings calls that the Maya app (formerly PayMaya) was revamped into an all‑in‑one platform adding “features like cryptocurrency” and “crypto trading,” confirming direct customer crypto dealing within the investee’s product suite. Subsequent calls referenced Maya’s ecosystem spanning payments, credit, savings, insurance, investments “or crypto.” PLDT’s SEC filings describe its ownership in Voyager/Maya via PCEV and the equity‑method treatment, indicating exposure occurs through an associate rather than consolidated operations. There is no evidence that PLDT mines crypto, runs custody/exchange services itself, or holds crypto on its treasury. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/PHI/transcripts/2022/1))

### Weight Assignment
PLDT’s linkage to crypto stems from its investment in Voyager/Maya, whose app offers retail crypto trading; this fits the crypto financial services mechanism. Because the activity resides at the associate level and PLDT recognizes share of profits/losses rather than direct crypto revenues, the exposure is categorized purely as finserv and judged economically peripheral to PLDT’s consolidated results. Accordingly, finserv is set to 1.0 and all other mechanisms to 0.0; investor‑base amplification is not evident. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/78150/000095017025038448/phi-20241231.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Crypto activity occurs at the associate (Voyager/Maya) level; PLDT accounts for this under the equity method, and filings do not quantify crypto‑specific revenues or assets attributable to PLDT. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/78150/000095017025038448/phi-20241231.htm?utm_source=openai))
- Transcript access is via third‑party sites; quotes align with PLDT’s official disclosures but original call PDFs are behind portals. None of the sources indicate PLDT treasury crypto holdings or mining. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/PHI/transcripts/2022/1))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- May 5, 2022 (Q1 2022): “...all‑in‑one money platform... other features like cryptocurrency... we added on new services, like crypto trading...” — [ROIC AI](https://www.roic.ai/quote/PHI/transcripts/2022/1) ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/PHI/transcripts/2022/1))
- Nov 3, 2022 (Q3 2022): “...Everything is within the same app. Payments, credit, savings, insurance, investments, or crypto, whatever.” — [Fintool](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/PHI/documents/transcripts/q3-2022) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/PHI/documents/transcripts/q3-2022))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Form 20‑F (FY2024): ownership and governance details for Voyager/Maya Bank HoldCos; equity‑method treatment noted. — [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/78150/0000950170-25-038448-index.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/78150/0000950170-25-038448-index.htm?utm_source=openai))
- Form 6‑K exhibit/press release (Apr 2022): “PLDT remains the largest shareholder of Voyager… synergies with Maya.” — [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/78150/000156459022018053/phi-ex991_6.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/78150/000156459022018053/phi-ex991_6.htm?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Apr 6, 2022 — “PayMaya rolling out crypto feature (buy/sell).” — [ABS‑CBN News](https://www.abs-cbn.com/business/04/06/22/want-bitcoin-paymaya-says-rolling-out-crypto-feature) ([abs-cbn.com](https://www.abs-cbn.com/business/04/06/22/want-bitcoin-paymaya-says-rolling-out-crypto-feature?utm_source=openai))
- Apr 11, 2022 — “PayMaya ventures into crypto trading.” — [Philstar](https://qa.philstar.com/business/2022/04/11/2173642/paymaya-ventures-crypto-trading) ([qa.philstar.com](https://qa.philstar.com/business/2022/04/11/2173642/paymaya-ventures-crypto-trading?utm_source=openai))
- May 1, 2022 — “PayMaya rebrands to Maya; integrates digital banking and crypto trading.” — [NoypiGeeks](https://www.noypigeeks.com/business/maya-digital-banking-service/) ([noypigeeks.com](https://www.noypigeeks.com/business/maya-digital-banking-service/?utm_source=openai))
- Sep 20, 2021 — “Voyager secures digital bank license (Maya Bank) from BSP.” — [Reuters via Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/philippines-voyager-obtains-digital-banking-license-from-central-bank-2620301) ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/philippines-voyager-obtains-digital-banking-license-from-central-bank-2620301?utm_source=openai))

## PROCESSA PHARMACEUTICALS

* Rank: 441 | Expo: 0.09 | id_str: US74275C3043
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Form 10‑K (FY2023, filed Mar 2024); Form 10‑K (FY2022, filed Mar 2023); Q2 2022 earnings call

### Firm Overview
Processa Pharmaceuticals, Inc. is a U.S.-based clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company listed on Nasdaq under the ticker PCSA and headquartered in Hanover, Maryland. The company focuses on developing Next Generation Chemotherapy oncology drug candidates and has not commercialized any products during the period reviewed.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across earnings call transcripts in 2020–2023 and SEC filings for fiscal years 2022 and 2023, there is no indication that Processa engaged in cryptocurrency-related activities. There is no evidence of crypto treasury holdings, mining operations, crypto-financial services (exchanges, custody, trading products), or non-financial services to the crypto ecosystem. Disclosures emphasize drug development activities, licensing agreements, clinical trials, and financing typical of small-cap biotech issuers. Consequently, no crypto exposure mechanism applies for the January 2017 – December 2024 sample window.

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of any substantive crypto references in the company’s SEC filings and earnings call materials during the sample period, Processa is assigned unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism weights at 0.0. No evidence suggests a crypto-sensitive investor base, so invbase = 0. This reflects that the firm’s operations and disclosures are centered entirely on biopharmaceutical R&D rather than digital assets.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified. Transcript access for some calls is via third‑party aggregators, but the available text and SEC filings consistently indicate no crypto activity within 2017–2024.

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 11, 2022 (Q2 2022): “Welcome to Processa’s second‑quarter 2022 results and clinical pipeline update conference call.” — [GuruFocus (Refinitiv StreetEvents transcript)](https://www.gurufocus.com/stock/PCSA/transcripts/2213338)

- Form 10‑K (FY2022, filed Mar 30, 2023): business description as a clinical‑stage biopharma; no references to cryptocurrency/digital assets. — [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1533743/000149315223009730/form10-k.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- Company IR events page showing scheduled earnings calls (e.g., Q2 2022). — [Processa IR — Events](https://www.processapharmaceuticals.com/news-media/events/detail/10225/second-quarter-2022-earnings-conference-call)

## MICRON TECHNOLOGY

* Rank: 442 | Expo: 0.08 | id_str: US5951121038
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 1.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10‑K filing (FY2018); Q1–Q3 FY2018 earnings calls; FY2021 Q1 earnings call; Micron corporate blog (2019); Micron press release (June 2018)

### Firm Overview
Micron Technology, Inc. is a U.S.-based semiconductor manufacturer listed on Nasdaq (ticker MU) that designs and produces DRAM and NAND memory and storage products for data center, mobile, client, graphics and embedded markets. Its activities related to crypto are indirect, primarily via graphics and high‑bandwidth memory used in GPUs and certain mining hardware rather than holding or transacting in digital assets. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/723125/000072312518000092/a2018q4.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management explicitly linked cryptocurrency mining to demand for Micron’s graphics memory in multiple FY2018 earnings calls, noting that crypto mining added pressure to graphics memory supply and augmented sales in gaming/graphics. The FY2018 Form 10‑K further states Micron’s graphics DRAM products are incorporated into “crypto‑mining technology” and that the company achieved volume production of GDDR6 “for the graphics and crypto‑mining markets” in 2018. In FY2021 Q1, management reiterated that some portion of Micron’s graphics revenue serves cryptomining applications, emphasizing the need for high‑performance memory in such workloads. Micron’s own 2019 corporate blog and a June 2018 press release also position its GDDR memory as suitable for crypto mining use cases. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2017/12/20/micron-technology-mu-q1-2018-earnings-conference-c.aspx))

### Weight Assignment
Micron does not mine, custody, or offer financial products in digital assets; its exposure operates through supplying memory components integral to mining‑related GPUs/ASICs and blockchain workloads. Given explicit disclosures and repeated call commentary tying graphics DRAM/GDDR6 to crypto‑mining demand, the “infrastr” mechanism is assigned 1.0. Management also indicated that graphics is a single‑digit share of revenue and cryptomining is a subset within graphics, supporting a minor revenue share code (RevShare = 1) and no balance‑sheet crypto assets (AssetShare = 0). No evidence indicates a crypto‑sensitive investor base, so invbase = 0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/723125/000072312518000092/a2018q4.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Exposure is indirect and unquantified: Micron does not break out crypto‑mining demand within graphics, and references to cryptomining intensity declined outside the 2017–2018 boom. The classification rests on explicit but segment‑level links rather than product‑line revenue attribution. None of the reviewed sources indicate crypto treasury holdings, mining operations, or crypto financial services. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/723125/000072312518000092/a2018q4.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Dec 19, 2017 (Q1 FY2018): “Although smaller in size, recent interest in cryptocurrency mining has put further pressure on graphics memory supply.” — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2017/12/20/micron-technology-mu-q1-2018-earnings-conference-c.aspx)
- Mar 22, 2018 (Q2 FY2018): “We also benefited from strong demand for graphics/memory, with crypto‑currency mining augmenting sales for gaming applications.” — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/03/22/micron-technology-inc-mu-q2-2018-earnings-conferen.aspx)
- Jun 20, 2018 (Q3 FY2018): “...graphics in console and gaming as well as some crypto‑driven demands...” — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/06/20/micron-technology-inc-mu-q3-2018-earnings-conferen.aspx)
- FY2021 Q1: “Some of the graphics cards that are built using GDDR6 and GDDR6X... are used in cryptomining applications... cryptomining needs high performance memory and Micron DRAM.” — [StockInsights transcript](https://filings.stockinsights.ai/us/earnings-transcripts/723125/MU-Micron_Technology_Inc-2021-Q1-3601-r.html)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**

- Press release (June 25, 2018): Micron begins mass production of 8Gb GDDR6; cites crypto mining among applications. — [Micron investors](https://investors.micron.com/static-files/b64f18a3-463c-4592-ae94-11ebf3df743b)

**Financial news / other:**
- Corporate blog (Sept 2019): Micron GDDR memory “ideal for cryptocurrency mining”; miners require fast high‑bandwidth memory. — [Micron blog](https://www.micron.com/about/blog/company/insights/blockchain-never-forgets)

## MSCI

* Rank: 443 | Expo: 0.08 | id_str: US55354G1004
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: MSCI press releases (2022–2023); MSCI Digital Assets product page; FY2023 Form 10‑K; Q4 2022 earnings call transcript

### Firm Overview
MSCI Inc. is a U.S.-based provider of indexes, portfolio analytics, ESG and climate data, and related decision-support tools for global investors. Within the 2017–2024 window, MSCI launched and marketed digital-asset index and classification solutions but did not operate trading, custody, or investment vehicles holding crypto on its own balance sheet. ([ir.msci.com](https://ir.msci.com/static-files/86f9baec-38ce-4ff2-858b-6c340f8b71c5))

### Exposure Channels Identified
MSCI’s exposure to crypto is through non-financial services to the ecosystem: it designs and licenses digital-asset indexes and a classification framework (developed with partners such as Goldman Sachs and Coin Metrics) that investors and product issuers can use for benchmarking and research. In March 2023, ETC Group announced the first digital-asset ETP based on an MSCI index, underscoring MSCI’s role as an index licensor rather than a product issuer or asset custodian. No evidence indicates that MSCI mines crypto, runs an exchange, or holds crypto as treasury assets. ([msci.com](https://www.msci.com/our-solutions/indexes/digital-assets-solutions?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
The firm’s crypto linkage is providing index and taxonomy content to market participants without taking custody of or transacting in digital assets; per the project definitions, that is classified as bizserv rather than finserv. Accordingly, bizserv = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. Given that digital-asset offerings appear to be a small subset of MSCI’s broader index/analytics portfolio, revenue materiality is coded as minor (RevShare = 1) and there is no indication of crypto assets on balance sheet (AssetShare = 0). No evidence suggests MSCI trades as a crypto proxy, so invbase = 0. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20221103005909/en/MSCI-Launches-First-Suite-of-Digital-Assets-Indexes?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Earnings-call transcripts reviewed in 2022–2024 contained little or no explicit discussion of crypto/digital-asset products, so the classification rests primarily on press releases, product pages, and third-party announcements. MSCI does not break out digital-asset index revenues, so the RevShare code is an inference based on scope and positioning rather than disclosed figures. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2023/01/31/msci-msci-q4-2022-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jan 31, 2023 (Q4 2022): “We can offer one‑stop shop for different types of indices across many layers, including asset classes, exposures, styles and investment themes.” — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2023/01/31/msci-msci-q4-2022-earnings-call-transcript/)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Form 10‑K (FY2023, filed Feb 9, 2024): business mix across Index, Analytics, ESG/Climate, and Private Assets; no crypto asset holdings indicated. — [SEC EDGAR (MSCI 2023 10‑K)](https://ir.msci.com/static-files/86f9baec-38ce-4ff2-858b-6c340f8b71c5)
- Nov 3, 2022: “MSCI Launches First Suite of Digital‑Assets Indexes…” — [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20221103005909/en/MSCI-Launches-First-Suite-of-Digital-Assets-Indexes)
- Jan 18, 2022: “MSCI announces strategic alliance with Menai Financial Group” (to develop digital‑asset solutions). — [MSCI IR](https://ir.msci.com/news-releases/news-release-details/msci-announces-strategic-alliance-menai-financial-group)

**Financial news / other:**
- Mar 29, 2023: “ETC Group to Launch the First Digital Asset ETP Based on an MSCI Index.” — [GlobeNewswire](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2023/03/29/2636379/0/en/ETC-Group-to-Launch-the-First-Digital-Asset-ETP-Based-on-an-MSCI-INDEX.html)

## NETGEAR

* Rank: 444 | Expo: 0.08 | id_str: US64111Q1040
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: FY2023 Form 10-K; NETGEAR press releases and product pages (2021–2023); Q1 2023 earnings call transcript

### Firm Overview
NETGEAR, Inc. is a U.S.-based provider of consumer and small‑business networking hardware and related services, known for brands such as Orbi and Nighthawk in home Wi‑Fi and Pro AV switches for business. Within 2017–2024, NETGEAR also marketed Meural digital art displays, which incorporated features to authenticate and display blockchain‑verified digital art and NFTs.

### Exposure Channels Identified
NETGEAR’s crypto linkage arises from the Meural digital canvas product line and associated software integrations that allow users to connect crypto wallets (e.g., MetaMask, Coinbase) to verify and display NFTs, and from partnerships with NFT platforms (e.g., Async Art, SuperRare DAO). The company’s FY2023 Form 10‑K explicitly lists “non‑fungible token (NFT) artwork” among technology trends it targets, and 2023 product announcements emphasized “blockchain‑verified” art display capabilities. There is no evidence the company mines crypto, runs an exchange or custody service, or holds crypto on its balance sheet.

### Weight Assignment
The Meural‑NFT functionality is a non‑financial product/service that supports the crypto ecosystem without the firm taking custody of assets or operating financial products; per the taxonomy, this maps to bizserv. No indications support weights for holders, miners, finserv, or infrastr. Given Meural’s small share of overall revenue and no disclosed crypto asset holdings, RevShare is coded minor (1) and AssetShare 0. No evidence suggests NETGEAR trades as a crypto proxy, so invbase = 0. Bizserv = 1.0; all others = 0.0.

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Earnings‑call transcripts reviewed did not explicitly discuss NFTs/crypto; the anchor quote confirms Meural as part of the consumer brand set, while the crypto linkage relies on filings and press materials. No transcript within 2017–2024 directly tying Meural to NFTs was located; therefore certainty is capped at 2.

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Apr 26, 2023 (Q1 2023): “The Connected Home segment, which includes our industry leading Orbi, Nighthawk, Nighthawk Pro Gaming, Armor, and Meural brands, generated net revenue of $102.7 million during the quarter…” — [Insider Monkey](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/netgear-inc-nasdaqntgr-q1-2023-earnings-call-transcript-1143696/)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Form 10‑K (FY2023, filed Feb 2024): “We… invest in R&D to… capitalize on… trends, such as… non‑fungible token (‘NFT’) artwork,” and lists Meural among trademarks. — [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1122904/000095017024016525/ntgr-20231231.htm)
- Oct 31, 2023: “NETGEAR Unveils Meural Opus… showcasing blockchain‑verified art… next‑generation NFT viewing experience… collectors can connect to their crypto wallets.” — [NETGEAR IR press release](https://investor.netgear.com/releases/news-details/2023/NETGEAR-Unveils-Meural-Opus-in-Support-of-Digital-Art-Collectors-and-Creators/default.aspx)
- July 6, 2021: “NETGEAR Partners With Async Art to Add Dynamic, Programmable NFTs to the Meural Library.” — [NETGEAR IR press release](https://investor.netgear.com/releases/news-details/2021/NETGEAR-Partners-With-Async-Art-to-Add-Dynamic-Programmable-NFTs-to-the-Meural-Library/default.aspx)

**Financial news / other:**
- July 28, 2022: “NETGEAR… joining SuperRare DAO… develop a licensing and royalty model for displaying NFTs… recent integrations with Coinbase and MetaMask.” — [NETGEAR Pressroom](https://www.netgear.com/hub/pressroom/netgear-nft-superrare-dao/)
- 2022: “Collectors can now connect their Coinbase wallets to show off their NFT collections on Meural… expands on NETGEAR’s work with MetaMask.” — [NETGEAR Pressroom](https://www.netgear.com/hub/pressroom/nft-crypto-wallets-on-meural/)

## USA TODAY

* Rank: 445 | Expo: 0.08 | id_str: US36472T1097
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q2 2021 earnings call; 2023 Form 10‑K; 2021 NFT press releases; Q2 2021 earnings presentation

### Firm Overview
USA TODAY is the flagship national news brand of Gannett Co., Inc., a U.S.-listed media and digital marketing solutions company that operates the USA TODAY Network across the United States and Newsquest in the U.K. Within 2017–2024, Gannett experimented with blockchain-linked digital collectibles associated with USA TODAY content, while its core business remained publishing and advertising.

### Exposure Channels Identified
In June 2021 the USA TODAY Network launched its inaugural non-fungible token (NFT) auction, “The First Newspaper Delivered to the Moon,” using Origin’s NFT Launchpad, with proceeds directed to charitable causes. Management highlighted the launch on the Q2 2021 earnings call as part of broader product experimentation. In November 2021 Gannett followed with a second NFT initiative in collaboration with artist Peter Tunney, hosted on OpenSea, again framed as an engagement/brand initiative. There is no evidence the company mined crypto, operated an exchange, provided custody, or held crypto assets on its balance sheet during the sample period. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20210622005663/en/Gannett-Launches-Its-First-Non-Fungible-Token))

### Weight Assignment
The NFT drops are non-financial, content/brand products that interact with the crypto ecosystem without the company providing financial intermediation or taking asset custody; under the taxonomy this maps to bizserv. No disclosures indicate crypto treasury holdings, mining, financial services, or purpose-built crypto infrastructure, so those categories are set to zero. Given the small, largely charitable nature of the NFT activity relative to total revenue, RevShare is coded minor (1) and AssetShare 0; there is no evidence of a crypto‑sensitive investor base, so invbase = 0. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20210622005663/en/Gannett-Launches-Its-First-Non-Fungible-Token))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
The NFT initiatives appear episodic and primarily promotional/charitable, with no ongoing segment disclosure; filings reviewed do not reference crypto assets or material NFT revenue. Only one earnings call explicitly mentions the NFT effort; later calls and filings focus on core publishing and digital marketing operations. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/08/06/gannett-co-inc-gci-q2-2021-earnings-call-transcrip/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 6, 2021 (Q2 2021): “In the quarter, Gannett also launched its inaugural NFT… The first newspaper delivered to the Moon collection…” — [The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/08/06/gannett-co-inc-gci-q2-2021-earnings-call-transcrip/)

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- Form 10‑K (FY2023, filed Feb 22, 2024): business segments and operations; no crypto asset holdings disclosed. — [SEC filing PDF](https://d18rn0p25nwr6d.cloudfront.net/CIK-0001579684/488a13b0-5849-4064-a7ed-cca0d0ee1c05.pdf)

- Jun 22, 2021: “Gannett Launches Its First Non‑Fungible Token… powered by Origin’s NFT Launchpad” (charitable auction). — [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20210622005663/en/Gannett-Launches-Its-First-Non-Fungible-Token)
- Nov 22, 2021: “Gannett Launches NFTs in Collaboration With Peter Tunney… auction hosted on OpenSea; second NFT auction.” — [Nasdaq/Business Wire](https://www.nasdaq.com/press-release/gannett-launches-non-fungible-tokens-in-collaboration-with-american-artist-peter)

Because no substantiated crypto exposure channel was identified in filings or IR materials during 2017–2024, the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism weights set to 0.0. ### Firm Overview
BE Semiconductor Industries N.V. (Besi) is a Netherlands-based supplier of semiconductor assembly equipment, including die attach, packaging, and related plating systems, listed on Euronext Amsterdam. Across 2017–2024, corporate materials and results communications focus on advanced packaging technologies such as hybrid bonding for logic, memory, and photonics applications. ([vanslingerlandt.com](https://www.vanslingerlandt.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/Annual_Report_2023.pdf-Besi.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
A review of earnings call transcripts and filings in 2017–2024 reveals no activity related to cryptocurrency treasury holdings, mining operations, crypto exchanges/custody, or products/services directed at digital assets. 2018 call transcripts discuss demand drivers in smartphones, automotive, computing, and cloud server applications, without mentioning crypto or bitcoin. The 2023 Annual Report references “blockchain software” only as a broad technology trend in the digital economy, not as a product line or balance-sheet asset. Together, these sources indicate no substantive or targeted crypto exposure under the taxonomy. ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/aex/besi/investor-relations/earnings-call/q1-2018))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of explicit or material crypto-related activity in transcripts, filings, or news within the 2017–2024 window, the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism categories set to 0.0. Besi’s equipment is general-purpose for semiconductor packaging and is not evidenced as purpose-built or primarily directed to crypto/mining workloads; under the rules this precludes an infrastr assignment. There is also no evidence of crypto‑sensitive investor-base dynamics; thus invbase = 0. ([vanslingerlandt.com](https://www.vanslingerlandt.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/Annual_Report_2023.pdf-Besi.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified. Transcripts for later periods are sometimes gated, but accessible 2018 transcripts plus 2021–2024 filings and press releases consistently focus on semiconductor packaging and hybrid bonding with no crypto-specific disclosures. ### Firm Overview
Five Below, Inc. is a U.S.-based specialty value retailer focused on trend-right merchandise for tweens, teens and beyond, generally priced at $1–$5 with a higher-priced “Five Beyond” assortment. The company is listed on the Nasdaq under ticker FIVE and operates a rapidly expanding nationwide store base. ([investor.fivebelow.com](https://investor.fivebelow.com/investors/?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
A review of earnings call transcripts from 2018 through 2024 shows management discussing merchandising trends, store expansion, Five Beyond conversions and shrink mitigation, with no references to cryptocurrency holdings, mining, exchanges, custody, blockchain initiatives, or crypto-directed services. The FY2022 Form 10‑K contains no occurrences of “crypto,” “bitcoin,” “digital asset,” or “blockchain,” and describes a conventional retail model without digital-asset exposure. Five Below’s in‑store payment policy lists accepted methods (cash, major cards, and mobile wallets) and excludes crypto payments, further indicating no operational crypto integration during the 2017–2024 sample window. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2018/06/06/five-below-inc-five-q1-2018-earnings-conference-ca.aspx))

### Weight Assignment
With no evidence of treasury crypto holdings, mining, financial services involving digital assets, crypto-directed non-financial services, or targeted infrastructure supply, Five Below is classified as unclass = 1.0 and all other mechanism categories = 0.0. The absence of crypto acceptance in payment policies and the lack of any crypto-related disclosures in transcripts and filings support a zero-weight assignment across all exposure mechanisms. ([support.fivebelow.com](https://support.fivebelow.com/hc/en-us/articles/223778147-forms-of-payment-in-store))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified.

## DP WORLD DEAD - DELIST.23/06/20

* Rank: 449 | Expo: 0.08 | id_str: AEDFXA0M6V00
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 2018–2020 earnings call listings; 2019 Annual Report; 2020 delisting notices and press releases; H1 2019 results coverage; TradeLens blockchain integration news

### Firm Overview
DP World is a Dubai‑based global ports and logistics operator with terminals and related logistics assets across multiple regions. The company was publicly listed until mid‑2020 and subsequently became privately held.

### Exposure Channels Identified
A review of earnings call listings for 2017–2020 shows routine discussions of throughput, revenue, acquisitions, and logistics strategy, with no references to cryptocurrency holdings, mining, exchanges, custody, crypto payments, or other digital‑asset activities. The group’s 2019 annual report and results coverage focus on container volumes, portfolio additions (e.g., P&O Ferries, Topaz Energy & Marine), and logistics platforms. Separately, DP World participated in supply‑chain blockchain initiatives (e.g., IBM/Maersk TradeLens) during 2020; these relate to logistics data/traceability and do not imply crypto asset involvement or financial services exposure to digital assets. The firm delisted from Nasdaq Dubai in June 2020 and remains a conventional ports/logistics operator through the 2017–2024 sample window. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/DP-WORLD-PLC-6500032/news-call-transcripts/))

### Weight Assignment
No evidence indicates treasury crypto holdings, mining operations, issuance/custody/trading of digital assets, crypto‑directed non‑financial services, or purpose‑built crypto infrastructure supply. Blockchain use was limited to logistics digitisation and is not a crypto exposure mechanism under this framework. Accordingly, unclass is set to 1.0 and all other mechanism weights to 0.0; invbase is 0 given the absence of any market narrative tying DP World’s equity to crypto sentiment. ([en.flows.be](https://en.flows.be/shipping/2020/05/dp-world-connects-all-terminals-to-blockchain-platform-tradelens/?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
DP World’s transcripts on MarketScreener are listed for 2017–2020 but full text access is paywalled; classification relies on listings, annual report content, and contemporaneous news coverage during 2017–2020. Post‑2020 the company is private (following its June 2020 delisting), which reduces third‑party transcript availability; however, investor materials and press releases remain accessible. Mentions of blockchain (e.g., TradeLens) are logistics‑technology items and not crypto‑asset exposure. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/DP-WORLD-PLC-6500032/news-call-transcripts/))

* Primary sources: Annual reports (2018, 2021), RNS de‑listing notice (Oct 2023), MarketScreener earnings‑call listings (2017–2023), Edison research note (2018)

### Firm Overview
EMIS Group plc was a UK healthcare information technology company that developed and supported clinical software for primary care, community, and other NHS settings (e.g., EMIS Web). The company agreed to be acquired by UnitedHealth Group’s Optum affiliate and its shares were cancelled from trading on AIM on October 30, 2023. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/e/LSE_EMIS_2021.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024 sources, EMIS’s activities center on healthcare software and data connectivity; no evidence indicates treasury crypto holdings, mining, crypto financial services, or purpose‑built crypto infrastructure supply. In November 2018 EMIS acquired Dovetail Digital (Dovetail Lab), a healthcare blockchain software start‑up, to explore secure data‑sharing applications; this is enterprise technology adoption and does not entail crypto‑asset custody, trading, issuance, or investment. The market narrative around EMIS relates to NHS IT and the Optum acquisition process, not to crypto proxies or investor sentiment tied to digital assets. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/e/LSE_EMIS_2021.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of crypto‑asset holdings, mining, financial services touching digital assets, non‑financial services to the crypto ecosystem, or crypto‑directed hardware/energy supply, all mechanism categories are set to 0.0 and unclass is set to 1.0. The 2018 blockchain acquisition (Dovetail) is treated as healthcare data‑sharing technology and excluded per framework rules that distinguish blockchain digitisation from crypto exposure; invbase is 0 as there is no evidence EMIS traded as a crypto proxy. ([investegate.co.uk](https://www.investegate.co.uk/index.php/announcement/rns/emis-group--emis/acquisition-of-dovetail-digital-limited/5689628?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- UK small/mid‑cap transcripts are less consistently available than US peers; MarketScreener lists EMIS earnings calls for 2017–2023 but full text may require login.  
- The firm used “blockchain” in a healthcare data context via Dovetail; no sources show EMIS handling or investing in crypto assets.  
- De‑listing was effective October 30, 2023 per RNS; some third‑party references round to late October 2023. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/EMIS-GROUP-PLC-161106929/news-call-transcripts/))

### Firm Overview
Open Lending Corporation is a U.S.-based financial technology company that provides loan analytics, risk-based pricing, risk modeling, automated decision technology, and default insurance enablement to automotive lenders through its Lenders Protection platform. Its business centers on facilitating near-prime and non‑prime auto loans for credit unions, banks, finance companies and OEM captives in the United States. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1806201/000180620124000036/lpro-20231231.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across filings and earnings calls within 2017–2024, Open Lending’s activities are focused on automotive credit enablement and insurance-related profit‑share economics; there is no indication that the firm holds crypto assets, mines cryptocurrencies, offers crypto trading/custody, or supplies crypto‑specific hardware or infrastructure. A text search of the FY2023 Form 10‑K shows no occurrences of “crypto,” “bitcoin,” or “blockchain,” reinforcing the absence of crypto‑asset involvement. Third‑party and company communications during the period discuss loan certifications, program fees, and insurer profit‑share rather than any digital‑asset products. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1806201/000180620124000036/lpro-20231231.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Given the lack of evidence for any of the six crypto‑exposure mechanisms, all mechanism categories are set to 0.0 and unclass is set to 1.0. There is likewise no evidence that Open Lending trades as a crypto proxy or that its shareholder base is materially tied to crypto sentiment, so invbase is 0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1806201/000180620124000036/lpro-20231231.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- U.S. transcripts are available for multiple quarters, but none indicate crypto activity; the analysis relies on filings and representative calls rather than exhaustive call coverage for every quarter.  
- FY2024 results and calls were published in early 2025; for consistency with the 2017–2024 sample window, the classification emphasizes FY2023 10‑K and 2021–2023 calls/press releases. None suggest crypto involvement. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1806201/000180620124000036/lpro-20231231.htm))

* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: MarketScreener earnings call listings (2017–2018), NSD press releases and 2017 annual report, Reuters (May 8, 2018), bne IntelliNews (Jan 2019)

### Firm Overview
MegaFon PJSC is a Russian telecommunications operator providing mobile, fixed-line and digital services across Russia. The company was historically listed internationally but is now an inactive instrument on several screens, with public results and calls documented around 2017–2018.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across sources in the 2017–2024 window, there is no evidence that MegaFon held cryptocurrencies on its balance sheet, mined digital assets, or operated crypto‑financial services such as an exchange, custody, brokerage, or tokenized investment products. Available earnings call listings for 2017–2018 and corporate news focus on core telecom operations rather than digital‑asset activities. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/PUBLIC-JOINT-STOCK-COMPAN-13690419/news-call-transcripts/))  
In October 2017, Russia’s National Settlement Depository (NSD) and Raiffeisenbank used a Hyperledger‑based platform to issue and settle RUB 500 million of MegaFon commercial bonds — a distributed‑ledger pilot for securities lifecycle, not a cryptocurrency service or holding by MegaFon under this classification’s rules. ([nsd.ru](https://www.nsd.ru/en/publications/news/press-releases/nsd-runs-commercial-bonds-platform-based-on-hyperledger-fabric-1-0-and-smart-contracts-2017-10-02_161600/?utm_source=openai))  
MegaFon’s broader “digital projects” initiatives around 2018 (with partners Gazprombank and Rostec) referenced developing blockchain‑based solutions, again indicating DLT experimentation rather than crypto‑asset exposure. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/technology-news/russias-megafon-cuts-stake-in-mailru-to-form-digital-venture-1435492))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of credible evidence that MegaFon directly holds crypto assets, mines, offers crypto financial services, provides crypto‑specific non‑financial services, or supplies mining/crypto‑targeted infrastructure, all operational mechanisms are set to 0.0 and unclass is set to 1.0. Its stock is not documented as a crypto proxy, so the investor‑base amplifier is 0. The 2017 DLT bond pilot and later blockchain references are excluded from mechanism weights because they do not entail cryptocurrency custody, issuance, or fee‑earning services by MegaFon. ([nsd.ru](https://www.nsd.ru/en/publications/news/press-releases/nsd-runs-commercial-bonds-platform-based-on-hyperledger-fabric-1-0-and-smart-contracts-2017-10-02_161600/?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Full earnings call texts on some portals are behind paywalls; accessible listings confirm call dates but not full content. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/MEGAFON-13491458/news/Transcript-Public-Joint-Stock-Company-MegaFon-Q4-2017-Earnings-Call-Mar-15-2018-37965410/))  
- Some third‑party payment aggregators allow topping up MegaFon accounts using cryptocurrencies by converting to fiat; these are not MegaFon‑operated services and thus do not indicate direct crypto exposure. (Examples exist mostly outside the 2017–2024 sample window.) ([coinsbee.com](https://www.coinsbee.com/ru/mobile-top-ups/megafon//?utm_source=openai))

## SK TELECOM

* Rank: 458 | Expo: 0.08 | id_str: KR7017670001
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.8, bizserv 0.2, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Company investor presentation (Q3 2023), Earnings calls (Q3 2022; Q3 2023), Company press releases (2022–2023), SEC Form 20‑F (2022)

### Firm Overview
SK Telecom Co., Ltd. is South Korea’s largest mobile network operator with additional businesses in broadband, media, and enterprise services. During 2017–2024, the company also pursued Web3 initiatives around NFTs, wallets, and metaverse monetization alongside broader AI strategy and telecom operations.

### Exposure Channels Identified
SK Telecom launched “TopPort,” a curated NFT marketplace, and tied it to a company Web3 wallet, enabling users to mint, buy, and store NFT items with simplified KRW payments rather than crypto exchange onboarding. In its Q3 2023 investor briefing, SK Telecom described an ifland economic system that includes a virtual currency (“Stone”), content trading, and a Web3 wallet integration with NFT items via TopPort. The company also announced plans for “T wallet,” collaborating with Aptos Labs and Atomrigs Lab to expand Web3 wallet services for cryptocurrencies and NFTs, and signed an MOU with Mirae Asset Securities to build a security‑token business consortium. Separately, SK Telecom has promoted blockchain-based decentralized identity (DID) services (“Initial”) with financial and technology partners, a non-financial service adjacent to the crypto ecosystem. ([news.sktelecom.com](https://news.sktelecom.com/180313))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence supports classifying SK Telecom primarily under finserv: it directly operates an NFT marketplace, provides a Web3 wallet that can hold NFTs/crypto, and has pursued security‑token initiatives with a major securities firm. A smaller bizserv weight captures its blockchain/DID (“Initial”) deployments that support crypto/Web3 identity without directly taking asset custody. No evidence indicates treasury crypto holdings, mining, or crypto‑directed hardware/hosting. Accordingly, finserv is set to 0.8 and bizserv to 0.2; all other mechanisms are 0.0. ([sktelecom.com](https://www.sktelecom.com/img/eng/presen/20240802/3Q23InvestorBriefingENG.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Earnings call transcripts emphasize metaverse monetization and AI strategy; explicit crypto/NFT terms appear more clearly in investor presentations and press releases rather than on calls. Classification relies on those official materials for the Web3 wallet/NFT details. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/SKM/documents/transcripts/q3-2023))  
- SK Square (separately listed) invested in the Korbit exchange; that entity is outside this firm’s scope.  
- Revenue/asset contributions from NFT/wallet/security‑token initiatives are not broken out; RevShare is coded as minor based on available disclosures. SEC Form 20‑F (2022) provides overall business context but does not detail Web3 revenue lines. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1015650/000119312523120018/d408889d20f.htm))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 10, 2022 (Q3 2022): “We introduced the ifland point program… the beginning of the introduction of the Metaverse economic ecosystem… transactions using ifland points will be enabled.” — Fintool transcript. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/SKM/documents/transcripts/q3-2022))
- Nov 8, 2023 (Q3 2023): “Ifland… will begin to use the in‑app transaction‑based economic system… facilitating… monetization.” — Fintool transcript. ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/SKM/documents/transcripts/q3-2023))

### Firm Overview
IAC Inc. is a U.S.-based holding company focused on digital media and internet businesses, including publishing (Dotdash Meredith/People Inc.), home services (historically Angi), search (Ask Media Group), and marketplaces/care services. During 2017–2024, public disclosures center on advertising, content, marketplaces and corporate investments, with no emphasis on cryptocurrency operations.

### Exposure Channels Identified
A review of earnings-call transcripts from 2018 through 2024 shows no substantive references to cryptocurrency initiatives or digital-asset strategies by IAC or its primary operating units over the period. Filings similarly do not disclose treasury crypto holdings, mining, custody/trading products, or other direct crypto financial services. Given the absence of explicit crypto-related activity, no mechanism category is supported. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/investing/2019/02/08/iacinteractivecorp-iac-q4-2018-earnings-conference.aspx))

### Weight Assignment
With no evidence of crypto holdings, mining, financial services, non-financial crypto services, or dedicated infrastructure, the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. No signs of a crypto‑sensitive investor base were found in calls or coverage, so invbase = 0. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-iac-q4-2024-misses-eps-stock-rises-56-93CH-3864996))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- IAC owns media properties (e.g., Investopedia/People Inc.) that publish crypto‑related educational content; however, content publishing without direct asset handling or service provision to crypto firms does not meet mechanism thresholds under these rules.  
- If smaller subsidiaries pursued minor Web3/NFT experiments that were not disclosed in company-level filings or calls, they were not evident in available sources. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0001800227/000110465924054563/tm2411134d2_ars.pdf))

- Feb 12, 2025 (covering Q4 2024): Strategic/AI commentary; no digital‑asset products or holdings — Investing.com transcript. [Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-iac-q4-2024-misses-eps-stock-rises-56-93CH-3864996)

* Primary sources: Annual reports (2018–2020), Earnings calls (2017; 2018; 2024), Trade press/news (2021–2024)

### Firm Overview
De La Rue plc is a UK-based producer of secure currency and authentication solutions, historically the world’s largest commercial banknote printer with additional activities in brand protection and government solutions. During 2017–2024, public disclosures emphasize banknote design/production, security features, and product authentication software and labels rather than cryptocurrency operations.

### Exposure Channels Identified
A review of earnings-call transcripts and investor presentations from 2017, 2018 and 2024 shows no disclosure of treasury cryptocurrency holdings, crypto mining, or digital-asset financial services. One filing notes the Authentication division was “exploring blockchain technologies” alongside tools like DLR Validate, but this is framed as general product authentication/track-and-trace, not crypto-asset services or custody. Consequently, no substantive mechanism of crypto exposure is supported by the evidence base for 2017–2024. ([bulletin.webull.com](https://bulletin.webull.com/20170523/925302823/a05c72dd5abccbde475642eda8a3beb8))

### Weight Assignment
Because there is no evidence of balance-sheet crypto holdings, mining activities, crypto trading/custody products, or dedicated crypto infrastructure, the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. The 2020 annual report’s reference to “exploring blockchain technologies” pertains to brand/authentication solutions rather than cryptocurrency businesses, which does not meet the threshold for bizserv or finserv under these rules. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/d/LSE_DLAR_2020.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Limited transcript availability for some years; however, accessible calls in 2017 and 2018 plus a 2024 transcript listing showed no crypto-related initiatives. ([bulletin.webull.com](https://bulletin.webull.com/20170523/925302823/a05c72dd5abccbde475642eda8a3beb8))  
- The Authentication division’s exploration of blockchain and its subsequent agreed sale to Crane NXT in October 2024 indicate strategic changes but do not constitute crypto-asset activity within the sample window. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/d/LSE_DLAR_2020.pdf))

In November 2023 it agreed to acquire CRH’s European lime operations, underscoring its core positioning in industrial minerals and construction materials rather than digital‑asset activities. A review of SigmaRoc’s annual/interim reports and results presentations across 2017–2024 shows no disclosure of cryptocurrency treasury holdings, crypto mining, digital‑asset trading/custody products, or other crypto‑financial services. Materials consistently emphasize aggregates, lime and limestone production, and industrial/mineral end‑markets. An FY23 investor presentation transcript listing similarly contains no substantive references to crypto activity. Taken together, available sources do not support any of the defined crypto‑exposure mechanisms. ([sigmaroc.com](https://www.sigmaroc.com/investors/results-presentations))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of evidence for balance‑sheet crypto assets, mining operations, crypto exchanges/custody/payment products, crypto‑focused services, or mining‑directed infrastructure, SigmaRoc is classified as unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism weights = 0.0. Its disclosures and news flow over 2017–2024 align with a traditional building‑materials/lime business model, not cryptocurrency end‑markets. ## BUREAU VERITAS

* Rank: 462 | Expo: 0.08 | id_str: FR0006174348
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Registration documents (2018–2019), BV newsroom (2018, 2022), sector media (2018), financial reports index (2017–2024)

### Firm Overview
Bureau Veritas SA is a France‑headquartered global testing, inspection and certification group listed on Euronext Paris, operating across consumer products, buildings and infrastructure, marine and offshore, industry, and commodities. Within its digital initiatives, the company has developed blockchain‑enabled traceability offerings in food and supply chains alongside broader TIC services. ([group.bureauveritas.com](https://group.bureauveritas.com/sites/g/files/zypfnx196/files/media/document/2018_Registration_Document.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Evidence from company materials shows Bureau Veritas launched “Origin,” a blockchain‑based end‑to‑end food traceability label in 2018, and has partnered on blockchain platforms and audits (e.g., joining VAKT for commodities post‑trade, and validating smart contracts for ENGIE’s green‑energy traceability). These activities reflect non‑financial, trust/assurance and traceability services delivered to clients that interact with distributed‑ledger technology; the firm does not custody digital assets, run an exchange, or issue crypto‑financial products. No disclosures were found of treasury crypto holdings or mining operations in registration documents or the financial‑reports archive covering 2017–2024. Accordingly, exposure aligns with bizserv only. ([group.bureauveritas.com](https://group.bureauveritas.com/newsroom/bureau-veritas-launches-origin-worlds-first-blockchain-based-complete-food-traceability?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
The documented blockchain initiatives (Origin traceability, VAKT participation, smart‑contract validation with ENGIE) are service offerings around traceability and digital trust that do not involve holding or transacting in cryptoassets. With no evidence of mining, crypto custody/trading products, or crypto on the balance sheet, the classification is concentrated in bizserv. Thus, weights are assigned as: bizserv 1.0; all other mechanism categories 0.0. ([group.bureauveritas.com](https://group.bureauveritas.com/sites/g/files/zypfnx196/files/media/document/2018_Registration_Document.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- At least one earnings‑call transcript quote could not be retrieved free‑of‑charge for 2017–2024; several transcript listings exist behind publisher gates. Certainty is capped at 2 per protocol. ([seekingalpha.com](https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BVRDF/earnings/transcripts?utm_source=openai))
- “Blockchain” references concern supply‑chain traceability and smart‑contract assurance, not cryptocurrency custody or financial products; no explicit revenue split for these services is disclosed, so RevShare is coded as minor (1) based on presence but presumed small scale. ([group.bureauveritas.com](https://group.bureauveritas.com/sites/g/files/zypfnx196/files/media/document/2018_Registration_Document.pdf))

### Firm Overview
OMNIQ Corp is a U.S.-based provider of AI-driven machine vision, data collection, and automation solutions for supply chain, public safety, traffic/parking, and access control. Company materials and filings emphasize computer vision, IoT, and fintech point-of-sale software following the 2024 acquisition of CodeBlocks; no role for cryptocurrencies is indicated. ([globenewswire.com](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2024/08/01/2922779/0/en/OMNIQ-CORP-ANNOUNCES-UPLISTING-TO-OTCQB-VENTURE-MARKET.html?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across the 2017–2024 window, filings and company communications describe product lines in AI/vision hardware and software, labels/RFID, and service contracts, without references to cryptoassets, blockchain, mining, or custody/trading activities. The FY2023 Form 10-K MD&A attributes revenue to hardware, service contracts, software, labels and ribbons; text searches of the filing contain no occurrences of “crypto,” “bitcoin,” or “blockchain.” The Q3 2024 earnings call discusses fintech partnerships for payments (e.g., CodeBlocks, Ingenico, SHVA) but not digital assets. Accordingly, no crypto-exposure mechanism is supported. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/278165/000149315224012457/form10-k.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Because there is no credible evidence of treasury crypto holdings, mining operations, crypto-financial services (exchange/custody/products), crypto-adjacent non-financial services, or mining-focused infrastructure, the firm is classified as unclassified. Weights are therefore assigned as unclass 1.0 and 0.0 for all other categories. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/278165/000149315224012457/form10-k.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- None identified; multiple primary sources were consulted and an earnings-call transcript within the sample window was available, but none referenced cryptocurrency or blockchain. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/278165/000149315224012457/form10-k.htm))

## TUI N

* Rank: 464 | Expo: 0.08 | id_str: DE000TUAG505
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Annual reports (2017, 2023), Q1 2024 and FY2023 earnings call transcripts, 2017–2018 trade press coverage

### Firm Overview
TUI AG is a Germany-based integrated tourism group with operations spanning tour operating, airlines, hotels and cruises. Its public materials during 2017–2024 focus on travel and leisure services rather than crypto-related activities.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across the 2017–2024 window, company disclosures and calls emphasize bookings, capacity, aviation, hotels and cruises; there is no evidence of cryptoasset holdings, mining, exchange/custody services, or payment acceptance in crypto. The 2017 Annual Report discussed using blockchain technology to centralize hotel bed inventory (the “One Inventory/One Purchasing” initiative, often referenced as BedSwap), which is an internal IT use of blockchain rather than exposure to cryptocurrencies. Trade-press pieces from 2017–2018 covered this blockchain inventory concept, but they likewise describe enterprise software, not crypto custody, trading, or treasury activity. Recent financial statements and earnings-call materials from 2023–2024 continue to focus on core travel operations with no crypto references. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/t/LSE_TUI_2017.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
No credible evidence supports classification as holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr. The 2017 blockchain initiative is an internal operational database use case and does not constitute a service to the crypto ecosystem or any financial exposure to cryptoassets. Accordingly, the firm is assigned unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism weights at 0.0. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/t/LSE_TUI_2017.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Media coverage in 2017–2018 could be mistaken as crypto exposure; however, sources explicitly describe a private blockchain for internal hotel-inventory management rather than cryptocurrency activity. No transcripts or filings in 2023–2024 indicate crypto involvement. ([skift.com](https://skift.com/2017/07/11/blockchain-will-disrupt-expedia-and-airbnb-tui-ceo-says/?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 13, 2024 (Q1 2024): Marketscreener transcript listing (call on bookings/trading; no crypto context). [MarketScreener](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/TUI-AG-470539/news/Transcript-TUI-AG-Q1-2024-Earnings-Call-Feb-13-2024-45939292/)
- Dec 6, 2023 (FY2023): Marketscreener transcript listing (results discussion; no crypto context). [MarketScreener](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/TUI-AG-470539/news/Transcript-TUI-AG-2023-Earnings-Call-Dec-06-2023-45511982/)

**Filings (Annual reports / financial statements):**
- 2017 Annual Report: “Building on the Blockchain technology, we are striving to centralise our inventory on one database… bed capacity allocation and/or bed swap.” [AnnualReports.com (TUI 2017)](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/t/LSE_TUI_2017.pdf)
- 2023 Annual Financial Statements of TUI AG: financial statements and notes; no cryptoasset disclosures observed. [TUI Group IR](https://www.tuigroup.com/damfiles/default/tuigroup-15/en/investors/6_Reports-and-presentations/Reports/2023/TUI_23_003_AG_Bericht_2023_EN_231205.pdf-ed51e697b03b4f0768ccccb9ca7acce8.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Jul 11, 2017: CEO interview on blockchain’s potential in travel distribution; internal tech focus. [Skift](https://skift.com/2017/07/11/blockchain-will-disrupt-expedia-and-airbnb-tui-ceo-says/)
- Feb 15, 2018: “TUI Is Relying on Its Blockchain to Help Boost Profits” (hotel-inventory management). [Skift](https://skift.com/2018/02/15/tui-is-relying-on-its-blockchain-to-help-boost-profits/)
- Aug 25, 2017: “Travel Giant TUI Airs Plan to Move All Data to the Blockchain” (BedSwap). [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2017/08/25/travel-giant-tui-airs-plan-to-move-all-data-to-the-blockchain)

## AGRICULTURAL BANK OF CHINA 'A'

* Rank: 465 | Expo: 0.08 | id_str: CNE100000RJ0
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Annual report (2023), Bank policy statement on virtual currencies (June 2021), financial news (June–Sept 2021)

### Firm Overview
Agricultural Bank of China is one of China’s large state-owned commercial banks listed in Shanghai (A-share 601288) and Hong Kong. Its disclosures emphasize traditional retail and corporate banking and digital transformation, including participation in China’s digital renminbi initiatives and internal blockchain applications, not cryptocurrency businesses. ([abchina.com.cn](https://www.abchina.com.cn/en/investor-relations/performance-reports/annual-reports/202404/P020240426659355378697.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024 there is no evidence that the bank holds cryptoassets, mines, runs a crypto exchange, provides crypto custody, or accepts crypto payments. On June 21, 2021, the bank issued a notice prohibiting use of its services for virtual-currency transactions such as Bitcoin, aligning with People’s Bank of China directives to clamp down on crypto-related activities. ([abchina.com](https://www.abchina.com/cn/PersonalServices/SvcBulletin/202106/t20210621_2011168.htm?utm_source=openai))
The 2023 annual report discusses “Digital RMB Projects” and internal “application of blockchain technology” for risk monitoring, evidence storage and traceability—these are CBDC/enterprise IT initiatives, not cryptoasset services. ([abchina.com.cn](https://www.abchina.com.cn/en/investor-relations/performance-reports/annual-reports/202404/P020240426659355378697.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Given the explicit prohibition on virtual-currency use of its services, the absence of any disclosures about crypto holdings or services, and the focus on CBDC and internal tech rather than market-facing crypto activities, the firm is assigned unclass = 1.0 with all other mechanism weights at 0.0. CBDC-related work (digital RMB) and private blockchain usage do not meet the project’s crypto-exposure definitions for holders, miners, financial services, business services, or infrastructure. ([abchina.com](https://www.abchina.com/cn/PersonalServices/SvcBulletin/202106/t20210621_2011168.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Hong Kong has advanced a regulated virtual-asset framework, but no disclosures were found indicating that Agricultural Bank of China (including its Hong Kong operations) provides virtual-asset custody, trading, or other crypto services; the 2023 annual report contains no such references. None identified beyond this contextual point. ([abchina.com.cn](https://www.abchina.com.cn/en/investor-relations/performance-reports/annual-reports/202404/P020240426659355378697.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- No transcript evidence required for unclassified firms; primary evidence relies on filings and policy statements.

**Filings (Annual reports / policy statements):**
- 2023 Annual Report: “Digital RMB Projects… we strengthened our application in various scenarios and product innovation.” [Agricultural Bank of China](https://www.abchina.com.cn/en/investor-relations/performance-reports/annual-reports/202404/P020240426659355378697.pdf) ([abchina.com.cn](https://www.abchina.com.cn/en/investor-relations/performance-reports/annual-reports/202404/P020240426659355378697.pdf))
- 2023 Annual Report: “Regarding the application of blockchain technology, we completed blockchain infrastructure construction… promoted multi-scenario pilot applications.” [Agricultural Bank of China](https://www.abchina.com.cn/en/investor-relations/performance-reports/annual-reports/202404/P020240426659355378697.pdf) ([abchina.com.cn](https://www.abchina.com.cn/en/investor-relations/performance-reports/annual-reports/202404/P020240426659355378697.pdf))
- June 21, 2021: “Statement on prohibiting the use of our services for virtual currency transactions such as Bitcoin.” [Agricultural Bank of China (Chinese)](https://www.abchina.com/cn/PersonalServices/SvcBulletin/202106/t20210621_2011168.htm) ([abchina.com](https://www.abchina.com/cn/PersonalServices/SvcBulletin/202106/t20210621_2011168.htm?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- June 21, 2021: PBOC summons major banks (incl. Agricultural Bank of China) and Alipay to crack down on crypto-related services. [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2021/06/21/china-central-bank-urges-alipay-banks-to-crack-down-on-crypto.html) ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2021/06/21/china-central-bank-urges-alipay-banks-to-crack-down-on-crypto.html?utm_source=openai))

## CVR ENERGY

* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2024), earnings calls (2019, 2021), investor relations news releases (2024–2025)

### Firm Overview
CVR Energy is a U.S.-based holding company whose principal businesses are petroleum refining/marketing and nitrogen fertilizer manufacturing, with an added Renewables segment focused on renewable diesel. As of the 2024 Form 10-K, the company reports three segments: Petroleum, Renewables, and Nitrogen Fertilizer. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1376139/000137613925000008/cvi-20241231.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
A review of the company’s 2024 Form 10-K reveals no disclosures of cryptocurrency holdings, mining, custody, exchange/brokerage, or other crypto financial services. Management commentary in earnings calls across the sample focuses on refining operations, renewables projects (e.g., Wynnewood renewable diesel conversion), and fertilizer markets, with no substantive crypto-related activity discussed. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1376139/000137613925000008/cvi-20241231.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of any stated cryptoasset holdings, mining activity, crypto financial services, crypto-adjacent business services, or targeted infrastructure supply to crypto/mining workloads, CVR Energy is assigned unclass = 1.0 and all other mechanism weights = 0.0. This assignment is based on segment disclosures and management discussion centered exclusively on petroleum, renewables, and fertilizer operations through 2017–2024. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1376139/000137613925000008/cvi-20241231.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified.

## JULIUS BAER GRUPPE

* Rank: 467 | Expo: 0.08 | id_str: CH0102484968
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 2
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Press releases (2019–2020), Strategy update (May 2022), FY 2022 results presentation (Feb 2023), financial news (2019–2022)

### Firm Overview
Julius Baer Gruppe AG is a Swiss private banking group headquartered in Zurich and listed on the SIX Swiss Exchange, focused on wealth management for private clients. Within its broader advisory and investment offerings, the bank has added client access to digital assets over the last several years through partnerships and dedicated services.

### Exposure Channels Identified
The bank entered a partnership with SEBA Crypto AG (later SEBA Bank) in 2019 to extend its service range to storage, transaction and investment solutions for digital assets, following a minority equity investment in 2018. Julius Baer subsequently launched digital-asset services for clients booked in Switzerland in January 2020, leveraging SEBA’s licensed platform for trading and custody. In its May 2022 strategy update, management explicitly positioned Julius Baer “at the interface of digital assets and the fiat world,” integrating the asset class into its wealth offering. Slide materials for the FY 2022 results reiterated “Digital assets in a wealth management context” as an active strategic workstream. ([juliusbaer.com](https://www.juliusbaer.com/sg/en/news/julius-baer-enters-partnership-for-digital-asset-services-with-seba-crypto-ag/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Evidence shows Julius Baer provides crypto financial services (client trading, custody and related solutions) via a licensed partner platform; there is no indication the group mines crypto, supplies mining infrastructure, or holds crypto on its own balance sheet as treasury assets. Accordingly, 100% of the exposure is assigned to finserv, with other mechanisms at 0. Given the lack of transcript-accessible crypto quotes despite multiple investor calls over 2017–2024, certainty is set to 2. RevShare is coded 1 (minor, <~5%) based on qualitative disclosures with no segmental crypto revenue breakout; AssetShare is 0 as no proprietary crypto holdings are indicated. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/JULIUS-BAR-GRUPPE-AG-5713105/news-call-transcripts/?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Could not retrieve a crypto-specific passage from a publicly accessible earnings-call transcript; access to transcript content on common aggregators was restricted. Certainty capped at 2 per protocol.
- No quantified disclosure of crypto-attributable revenues or assets in available filings/presentations; exposure appears service-based and likely small relative to group totals.
- The client digital-asset offering initially applied to clients booked in Switzerland and is delivered via a partner platform; scope by jurisdiction and direct balance-sheet involvement are not fully detailed in public materials. ([juliusbaer.com](https://www.juliusbaer.com/en/media/news-portal/julius-baer-launches-digital-assets-services/?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 2, 2023 (FY 2022 results presentation): “Digital assets in a wealth management context.” [Julius Baer – FY 2022 Results Presentation PDF](https://juliusbaer.mediarentlive.ch/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/2023-02-02_JuliusBaer_FYR2022_Presentation_EN.pdf) ([juliusbaer.com](https://juliusbaer.mediarentlive.ch/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/2023-02-02_JuliusBaer_FYR2022_Presentation_EN.pdf))
- Feb 1, 2024 (2023 earnings call; transcript listing reference). [MarketScreener transcript index](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/JULIUS-BAR-GRUPPE-AG-5713105/news-call-transcripts/) ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/JULIUS-BAR-GRUPPE-AG-5713105/news-call-transcripts/))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Feb 26, 2019: “Julius Baer enters partnership for digital asset services with SEBA Crypto AG… to provide storage, transaction and investment solutions for digital assets.” [Julius Baer press release](https://www.juliusbaer.com/sg/en/news/julius-baer-enters-partnership-for-digital-asset-services-with-seba-crypto-ag/) ([juliusbaer.com](https://www.juliusbaer.com/sg/en/news/julius-baer-enters-partnership-for-digital-asset-services-with-seba-crypto-ag/))
- Jan 21, 2020: “Julius Baer now offers its clients… a selection of digital assets services by making use of the… platform and capabilities of SEBA.” [Julius Baer press release](https://www.juliusbaer.com/en/media/news-portal/julius-baer-launches-digital-assets-services/) ([juliusbaer.com](https://www.juliusbaer.com/en/media/news-portal/julius-baer-launches-digital-assets-services/))
- May 19, 2022: Strategy update: “Integrating digital assets into its holistic wealth management proposition… at the interface of digital assets and the fiat world.” [Julius Baer media release](https://www.juliusbaer.com/sg/en/news/julius-baer-presents-updated-strategy-medium-term-targets-for-2023-2025-and-sharpens-capital-distribution-policy/) ([juliusbaer.com](https://www.juliusbaer.com/sg/en/news/julius-baer-presents-updated-strategy-medium-term-targets-for-2023-2025-and-sharpens-capital-distribution-policy/))

## MORGAN STANLEY

* Rank: 468 | Expo: 0.08 | id_str: US6174464486
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2021), Q3 2021 earnings call, press releases (2021), financial news (2021, 2024)

### Firm Overview
Morgan Stanley is a U.S.-based global financial services firm listed on the NYSE, operating through Institutional Securities, Wealth Management, and Investment Management. Within its wealth and asset management franchises, the firm has provided client access to digital assets primarily via third‑party bitcoin funds and, later, spot bitcoin ETFs.

### Exposure Channels Identified
In March 2021, Morgan Stanley began offering certain wealth management clients access to bitcoin through external funds, including vehicles managed by Galaxy Digital and a joint NYDIG/FS Investments product. This is a direct crypto financial-services channel focused on client investing rather than proprietary balance‑sheet exposure. In 2021 Morgan Stanley Investment Management funds also disclosed positions in Grayscale Bitcoin Trust (GBTC), evidencing additional product‑level exposure within asset management. In August 2024, media reported the firm authorized its ~15,000 financial advisors to recommend select spot bitcoin ETFs to eligible clients, reinforcing the finserv channel. ([galaxy.com](https://www.galaxy.com/newsroom/galaxy-digital-announces-first-quarter-2021-financial-results?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
All substantiated exposure is through client investment products and distribution (wealth management access to bitcoin funds/ETFs and MSIM fund holdings), with no evidence of proprietary treasury holdings, mining, or infrastructure supply. Therefore, 100% of the weight is assigned to finserv and 0% to other mechanisms. Given at least one transcript quote plus corroborating filings/press/news, certainty is set to 3. Revenue attributable to crypto appears peripheral relative to group totals, so RevShare is coded 1 (<~5%); no indication of material crypto assets on the consolidated balance sheet leads to AssetShare = 0. ([news.alphastreet.com](https://news.alphastreet.com/morgan-stanley-ms-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcript/?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Public filings do not provide a quantified breakdown of crypto-related revenues or assets; client access is delivered via third‑party funds/ETFs rather than direct custody on Morgan Stanley’s balance sheet. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/895421/000089542122000400/ms-20211231.htm?utm_source=openai))
- Some detailed access policies and eligibility criteria were communicated via internal memos and reported by media rather than via standalone investor presentations. ([bloomberg.com](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2024-08-02/morgan-stanley-to-allow-advisers-to-offer-bitcoin-etfs-to-wealthy-clients?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 14, 2021 (Q3 2021) earnings call: “We’re not directly trading crypto for retail clients… I don’t know what the value of Bitcoin should or shouldn’t be.” [AlphaStreet transcript](https://news.alphastreet.com/morgan-stanley-ms-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcript/) ([news.alphastreet.com](https://news.alphastreet.com/morgan-stanley-ms-q3-2021-earnings-call-transcript/?utm_source=openai))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Dec 31, 2021 Form 10-K (company annual report reference; overall business and risk framework consulted). [SEC EDGAR – MS 2021 10‑K index page](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/895421/000089542122000400/ms-20211231.htm) ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/895421/000089542122000400/ms-20211231.htm?utm_source=openai))
- Mar 2021: “Morgan Stanley began offering its wealth management clients access to bitcoin funds, including the Galaxy Bitcoin Fund LP and the Galaxy Institutional Bitcoin Fund LP.” [Galaxy Digital press release/newsroom](https://www.galaxy.com/newsroom/galaxy-digital-announces-first-quarter-2021-financial-results) ([galaxy.com](https://www.galaxy.com/newsroom/galaxy-digital-announces-first-quarter-2021-financial-results?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**
- Mar 17, 2021: “Morgan Stanley to offer rich clients access to Bitcoin funds.” [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2021-03-17/morgan-stanley-to-offer-rich-clients-access-to-bitcoin-funds) ([bloomberg.com](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2021-03-17/morgan-stanley-to-offer-rich-clients-access-to-bitcoin-funds?utm_source=openai))
- Jun 29, 2021: MS Europe Opportunity Fund bought 28,289 GBTC shares (per SEC filing).
