## ELIA GROUP

* Rank: 469 | Expo: 0.08 | id_str: BE0003822393
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Annual reports/prospectus (2021–2024), investor relations releases (2024), earnings call transcript (H1 2021)

### Firm Overview
Elia Group is a Belgian electricity transmission system operator listed on Euronext Brussels that owns Elia Transmission Belgium and indirectly Germany’s 50Hertz via Eurogrid. The group’s core business is regulated high‑voltage grid operation, planning and investment across Belgium and northeastern Germany.

### Exposure Channels Identified
A review of Elia Group’s earnings materials, investor publications and regulatory filings over 2017–2024 shows no evidence of crypto‑asset holdings, mining activity, client trading/custody services, or hardware/hosting directed at cryptocurrency workloads. The disclosures focus on regulated transmission activities, grid investments and digitalisation, with no mention of digital‑asset balance‑sheet exposure or crypto financial products. ([eliagroup.eu](https://www.eliagroup.eu/-/media/project/elia/shared/documents/elia-group/investor-relations/reports-and-results/2021/20210802_elia-group-half-year-results-2021-transcript.pdf))  
The group has explored blockchain/DLT in energy‑market pilots (e.g., identity and data exchange with Energy Web; R&D involving Powerledger’s xGrid/traXes), but these are framed as digital infrastructure or market‑design initiatives for the power system rather than activities involving custody or trading of crypto assets. Participation in Energy Web initiatives dates back several years and is described as sector innovation, not financial exposure. ([innovation.eliagroup.eu](https://innovation.eliagroup.eu/-/media/project/elia/innovation/documents/press-release/20231020_pressrelease_energyweb1.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Because all substantiated activity relates to regulated electricity transmission and non‑financial digitalisation pilots, with no credible mechanism linking crypto‑asset price moves to Elia Group’s equity through holdings, mining, client crypto services, or mining‑directed infrastructure, the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). No investor‑base amplifier is indicated. ([eliagroup.eu](https://www.eliagroup.eu/-/media/project/elia/shared/documents/elia-group/investor-relations/reports-and-results/2021/20210802_elia-group-half-year-results-2021-transcript.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Elia’s DLT pilots (Energy Web, Powerledger) indicate interest in blockchain‑based applications for the energy sector; however, there is no disclosure that these involve handling or holding crypto assets, and no quantified crypto revenue or assets are reported in the reviewed period. Publicly accessible transcripts prior to 2022 are limited, but available materials and the 2025 prospectus (covering 2024 results) are consistent with a lack of crypto exposure. ([innovation.eliagroup.eu](https://innovation.eliagroup.eu/-/media/project/elia/innovation/documents/press-release/20231020_pressrelease_energyweb1.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Jul 28, 2021: “We… are fully committed to ensure that we deliver the infrastructure to make sure that this integration can happen.” [Elia Group H1 2021 transcript (PDF)](https://www.eliagroup.eu/-/media/project/elia/shared/documents/elia-group/investor-relations/reports-and-results/2021/20210802_elia-group-half-year-results-2021-transcript.pdf) ([eliagroup.eu](https://www.eliagroup.eu/-/media/project/elia/shared/documents/elia-group/investor-relations/reports-and-results/2021/20210802_elia-group-half-year-results-2021-transcript.pdf))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- 2025 Prospectus (covers FY 2024–2022): references R&D with “blockchain‑enabled xGrid and traXes platforms” for peer‑to‑peer energy trading; no crypto‑asset holdings disclosed. [Elia Group Prospectus 2025 (PDF)](https://investor.eliagroup.eu/-/media/Project/Elia/shared/Documents/Investor-Relations/Offering/2025/2025_Elia-Group_Prospectus_EN) ([investor.eliagroup.eu](https://investor.eliagroup.eu/-/media/Project/Elia/shared/Documents/Investor-Relations/Offering/2025/2025_Elia-Group_Prospectus_EN))
- Mar 6, 2024: Q4 2023 results press release; operational highlights and investments; no crypto exposure. [Elia Group press release](https://investor.eliagroup.eu/-/media/project/elia/shared/documents/press-releases/2024/20240306_press-release_elia-group-q4-2023_en.pdf) ([investor.eliagroup.eu](https://investor.eliagroup.eu/-/media/project/elia/shared/documents/press-releases/2024/20240306_press-release_elia-group-q4-2023_en.pdf?utm_source=openai))
- Annual report archive consulted for baseline business description. [AnnualReports.com – Elia 2021 (PDF)](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/e/elia_2021.pdf) ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/e/elia_2021.pdf?utm_source=openai))

**Financial news / other:**

[Elia Innovation press release (PDF)](https://innovation.eliagroup.eu/-/media/project/elia/innovation/documents/press-release/20231020_pressrelease_energyweb1.pdf) ([innovation.eliagroup.eu](https://innovation.eliagroup.eu/-/media/project/elia/innovation/documents/press-release/20231020_pressrelease_energyweb1.pdf))
- Aug 24, 2018: “Elia tests blockchain in the energy sector” (pilot using Energy Web technology). [Computable.be](https://www.computable.be/2018/08/24/elia-test-blockchain-in-de-energiesector/) ([computable.be](https://www.computable.be/2018/08/24/elia-test-blockchain-in-de-energiesector/?utm_source=openai))

### Firm Overview
ATA Creativity Global is a China-focused international education services company listed on Nasdaq that provides portfolio training, research-based learning programs, and overseas study counselling centered on the creative arts. The group adopted its current name and AACG ticker in 2019 following the consolidation of its Huanqiuyimeng art education business.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Review of earnings-call transcripts from 2019–2021 consistently describes operations in art/creativity education, portfolio training, research-based learning, and study-abroad counselling, with no references to cryptocurrency holdings, mining, client custody/trading, or crypto-related financial products. Filings during 2018–2024 describe revenue entirely from education services and provide no disclosure of digital-asset balances or crypto product lines. Press releases in 2024 similarly emphasize student enrollments and program delivery rather than any crypto activities. ([d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net](https://d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net/_43a5dcf5cf03fe2ed4685571c9209903/ata/db/163/3359/webcast_transcript/AACG_Transcript_Q2_2021_Earnings.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Because all evidenced activity in the 2017–2024 window pertains to non-financial education services and there is no substantiated mechanism linking crypto-asset price moves to the firm’s equity through holdings, mining, client crypto financial services, or mining-directed infrastructure, the company is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). No evidence indicates a crypto-sensitive investor base during the sample period, so invbase = 0. ([d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net](https://d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net/_b63ccd9640a87ddf5d204a3dc36a65b1/ata/db/163/3353/webcast_transcript/AACG_Transcript_Q4-YE_2019_Earnings.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified for the 2017–2024 sample period; accessible transcripts and filings are consistent with an absence of crypto exposure. Earlier archived materials prior to 2019 relate to the predecessor testing/education business but likewise do not evidence crypto activity. ([ir.atai.net.cn](https://ir.atai.net.cn/financial-information/financial-results))

## BOLSA MEXICANA DE VALORES

* Rank: 471 | Expo: 0.08 | id_str: MX01BM1B0000
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0

* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Earnings calls (2019–2022), Press release (Q4/FY 2023 results), Annual report listings (2017–2023)

### Firm Overview
Bolsa Mexicana de Valores (Grupo BMV) is Mexico’s primary exchange operator, providing cash equities, derivatives (via MexDer), post-trade, market data and related services. Filings and investor materials through 2017–2024 emphasize traditional exchange and market infrastructure activities across multiple business lines. ([bmv.com.mx](https://www.bmv.com.mx/docs-pub/SALA_PRENSA/CTEN_BOLE/Grupo%20BMV%20reporta%20al%204T23%20y%20a%C3%B1o%20completo%2013.02.24.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across earnings calls from 2019–2022 and FY 2023 results materials, management discusses revenues from cash equities, derivatives (e.g., TIIE swaps and IPC futures), custody/clearing, and information services, with no evidence of corporate crypto holdings, mining, or client-facing crypto custody/trading products. ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/bmv/bolsaa/investor-relations/earnings-call/q1-2019))  
In October 2021 the CEO said BMV was seeking authorization to list crypto futures on MexDer and to allow crypto ETFs in the SIC cross-listing venue; however, the report was explicitly about applications/authorizations and not active listings. Subsequent materials reviewed through December 2024 do not evidence approved or launched crypto futures or crypto ETF listings on BMV/MexDer/SIC. ([eleconomista.com.mx](https://www.eleconomista.com.mx/mercados/BMV-listaria-criptomonedas-a-traves-del-Mexder-20211004-0135.html))  
MexDer’s product information and SIC/Global Market pages describe listed instruments generally (equities, ETFs, local derivatives), and available contract lists do not indicate crypto-linked futures during the period. ([mexder.com.mx](https://mexder.com.mx/wb3/wb/MEX/contratos_futuro?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of substantiated crypto activities in 2017–2024—no treasury holdings, no mining, no approved crypto financial products, and no crypto-focused infrastructure lines—the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). No evidence suggests a crypto-sensitive investor base for the stock, so invbase = 0. ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/bmv/bolsaa/investor-relations/earnings-call/q1-2019))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Management’s 2021 comments indicate interest in pursuing regulatory approval for crypto futures and potential crypto ETF cross-listings via SIC, but there is no corroborating evidence of approved listings by December 2024 in BMV/MexDer disclosures. If such products were authorized after the sample window, that would be out of scope for this classification. ([eleconomista.com.mx](https://www.eleconomista.com.mx/mercados/BMV-listaria-criptomonedas-a-traves-del-Mexder-20211004-0135.html))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Apr 24, 2019 (Q1 2019): “BMV has a strong and well-diversified business model… results were driven by our fee-related businesses, especially Custody and Information Services.” [AlphaSpread – Q1 2019 transcript](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/bmv/bolsaa/investor-relations/earnings-call/q1-2019) ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/bmv/bolsaa/investor-relations/earnings-call/q1-2019))  
- Oct 17, 2019 (Q3 2019): “In derivative trading and clearing, revenue increased… CCV provides clearing services for all Mexican equities.” [AlphaSpread – Q3 2019 transcript](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/bmv/bolsaa/investor-relations/earnings-call/q3-2019) ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/bmv/bolsaa/investor-relations/earnings-call/q3-2019))  
- Jul 21, 2021 (Q2 2021): “Investors in Mexico have the option of investing in almost 2,800 foreign securities that are listed on the SIC.” [AlphaSpread – Q2 2021 transcript](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/bmv/bolsaa/investor-relations/earnings-call/q2-2021) ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/bmv/bolsaa/investor-relations/earnings-call/q2-2021))  
- Jul 20, 2022 (Q2 2022): “Cash equities trading revenue was [up]… Investors in Mexico have the option of investing in over 140 local securities and over 3,000 foreign securities.” [AlphaSpread – Q2 2022 transcript](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/bmv/bolsaa/investor-relations/earnings-call/q2-2022) ([alphaspread.com](https://www.alphaspread.com/security/bmv/bolsaa/investor-relations/earnings-call/q2-2022))

**Financial news / other:**

## EXPENSIFY A

* Rank: 472 | Expo: 0.08 | id_str: US30219Q1067
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 10-K filings (2021–2023), Q2 2023 and Q4 2023 earnings calls

### Firm Overview
Expensify, Inc. is a U.S.-based software company that provides cloud expense management, corporate cards, and reimbursement tools to businesses worldwide. Its revenue model centers on software subscriptions and card interchange rather than holding or transacting in digital assets.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across earnings calls in 2022–2023, management discusses paid member trends, subscription metrics, card interchange and product development; there is no mention of corporate cryptocurrency holdings, mining, or crypto custody/trading products. For example, in Q2 2023 the CFO highlighted revenue, paid members, and interchange growth — standard payments metrics with no crypto angle. ([ir.expensify.com](https://ir.expensify.com/static-files/5994cfcb-0796-4c96-840e-fbdc32704fc3))  
The company’s FY 2023 Form 10‑K describes its business as expense management, reimbursements, and the Expensify Card; searches of the filing do not surface “bitcoin,” “crypto,” or “digital asset,” suggesting no material crypto activity disclosed during 2017–2024. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1476840/000147684024000016/exfy-20231231.htm))  
Help Center materials outlining reimbursement payment methods describe bank- and wallet-based rails (e.g., ACH/global bank transfers) but do not reference cryptocurrency reimbursements during the period. ([help.expensify.com](https://help.expensify.com/articles/new-expensify/wallet-and-payments/Reimbursement-Payment-Methods?utm_source=openai))  
Historically, Expensify announced a bitcoin reimbursement option in March 2013; however, this predates the 2017–2024 sample, and no 2017–2024 sources indicate that crypto reimbursements or other crypto-financial services remained active. ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/expensify-brings-bitcoin-to-the-enterprise-for-international-expense-reports-that-dont-suck-200235751.html?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of substantiated crypto activities in 2017–2024 — no treasury crypto holdings, no mining, and no customer-facing crypto custody/trading or crypto-backed products — the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). Management communications and filings focus on software subscriptions and card interchange rather than digital assets. No evidence suggests a crypto-sensitive investor base, so invbase = 0. ([ir.expensify.com](https://ir.expensify.com/static-files/5994cfcb-0796-4c96-840e-fbdc32704fc3))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Expensify’s 2013 bitcoin reimbursement announcement shows historical experimentation with crypto, but there is no support that this feature persisted into the 2017–2024 window; current Help Center reimbursement documentation omits crypto. If cryptocurrency features were reintroduced after December 2024, that would be outside scope. ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/expensify-brings-bitcoin-to-the-enterprise-for-international-expense-reports-that-dont-suck-200235751.html?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Q2 2023 (transcript): “Our revenue in Q2 was $38.9 million… our gross interchange was $2.7 million… [up] 56% year-over-year.” [Expensify IR – Q2 2023 transcript (PDF)](https://ir.expensify.com/static-files/5994cfcb-0796-4c96-840e-fbdc32704fc3) ([ir.expensify.com](https://ir.expensify.com/static-files/5994cfcb-0796-4c96-840e-fbdc32704fc3))  
- Q4 2023 (transcript excerpt): Discussion centers on “chat, payments, and documents” as the core of the product strategy; no crypto references. [Fintool – Q4 2023 transcript](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/EXFY/documents/transcripts/q4-2023) ([fintool.com](https://fintool.com/app/research/companies/EXFY/documents/transcripts/q4-2023))

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Mar 2024: FY 2023 Form 10‑K describes expense management, reimbursements, and card interchange; no “bitcoin/crypto/digital asset” terms found. ### Firm Overview
eGain Corporation is a U.S.-based provider of cloud software for customer engagement and AI-powered knowledge management, listed on Nasdaq. Its business centers on SaaS subscriptions and related services for enterprises across regulated and non‑regulated industries.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across available 2017–2024 sources, management communications and filings focus on AI knowledge, SaaS metrics, and customer wins; there is no indication of cryptocurrency treasury holdings, mining activity, or crypto custody/trading products. In the FY24 Q3 earnings call, the CEO emphasized “AI knowledge” momentum and product capabilities, with no crypto references. ([egain.com](https://www.egain.com/fy24q3-earnings-call-transcript/))  
A review of the company’s FY 2024 Form 10‑K shows no occurrences of “bitcoin,” “crypto,” or “digital asset,” suggesting no disclosed crypto activity during the sample period. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1066194/000155837024013686/egan-20240630xars.pdf))  
Financial news coverage around the September 14, 2023 results similarly discusses revenue and earnings guidance without any crypto angle. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/10bdef52cd12853cac81e3bd67153435?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of evidence that eGain holds crypto assets, operates mining, provides crypto financial services, or supplies crypto-specific infrastructure, the firm is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). Management commentary and filings consistently position eGain as an AI knowledge and CX software provider, not a crypto-exposed business. No sources indicate a crypto-sensitive investor base, so invbase = 0. ([egain.com](https://www.egain.com/fy24q3-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified within the January 2017–December 2024 window; if eGain introduced crypto-related products or treasury holdings after December 2024, that would be outside scope.

Aemetis, Inc. is a U.S.-based renewable fuels and renewable natural gas producer with operations that include a California ethanol plant, a California dairy renewable natural gas project, and an India biodiesel business. Management communications and filings emphasize low‑carbon fuels, sustainable aviation fuel development, and carbon capture projects.

Across 2017–2024 sources, there is no evidence that Aemetis holds cryptocurrency on its balance sheet, mines digital assets, provides crypto custody/trading, or supplies crypto‑specific hardware or hosting. The FY 2024 Form 10‑K contains no occurrences of “bitcoin,” “crypto,” “digital asset,” or “blockchain,” and focuses on ethanol, RNG, biodiesel and SAF initiatives. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/738214/000143774925007768/amtx20241231_10k.htm))  
Earnings calls during the sample window discuss revenues from ethanol, RNG LCFS/RIN credits, biodiesel contracts in India, and SAF project milestones, with no crypto references. Company press releases in 2023–2024 similarly center on IRA tax credit sales, biodiesel allocations, and quarterly results without any digital‑asset products. ([aemetis.com](https://www.aemetis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/2024-05-09-Q1-2024-Earnings-Call-Transcript.pdf))

Given the absence of any crypto‑treasury holdings, mining, financial services involving digital assets, crypto‑adjacent business services, or crypto‑directed infrastructure, Aemetis is classified as unclassified (unclass = 1.0). None identified within the January 2017–December 2024 window; if Aemetis initiated crypto‑related activities after December 2024, that would be outside scope.

## 3I INFOTECH

* Rank: 475 | Expo: 0.07 | id_str: INE748C01038
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.1, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.9
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Annual Reports (FY 2022–23, FY 2023–24), Q2 FY25 earnings call (Nov 6, 2024), company media/press (2018–2023)

### Firm Overview
3i Infotech Limited is an India‑based information technology and digital transformation services company with operations across North America, India, the Middle East and other regions. Its portfolio spans cloud, cybersecurity, application modernization, data/analytics, business process services, and select “future tech” offerings. ([3i-infotech.com](https://www.3i-infotech.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/3i-Infotech_2023_AR_Cover-to-cover_V4_Hyperlinked.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Within 2017–2024 disclosures, the company markets “future technologies” that explicitly include blockchain/NFT solutions as part of its services portfolio; these are positioned as enterprise digital transformation capabilities rather than crypto financial products. ([3i-infotech.com](https://www.3i-infotech.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/3i-Infotech_2023_AR_Cover-to-cover_V4_Hyperlinked.pdf))  
Annual reports also state that the company “has not traded or invested in Crypto currency or Virtual Currency,” indicating no treasury or product‑backing holdings of digital assets. ([3i-infotech.com](https://www.3i-infotech.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/3i-Infotech_AR_2023-24.pdf))  
No evidence was found of mining operations, crypto custody/trading, or issuance of crypto‑backed financial products in earnings communications or filings through December 2024. ([3i-infotech.com](https://www.3i-infotech.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/Q2FY25-Earnings-Call-Transcript.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
A small bizserv weight (0.1) is assigned based on repeated, formal disclosures that blockchain/NFT solutions form part of 3i Infotech’s service catalog, consistent with non‑financial, enterprise blockchain work that does not involve taking custody of digital assets. All other channels are set to 0.0, with the residual 0.9 placed in unclass to reflect that crypto‑related work appears peripheral and not broken out as a material revenue contributor; filings explicitly rule out crypto trading/investment. ([3i-infotech.com](https://www.3i-infotech.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/3i-Infotech_AR_2023-24.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
“Blockchain/NFT solutions” are referenced at a portfolio level without segment revenue disclosure; the extent to which these offerings intersect with the crypto ecosystem (vs. private/enterprise blockchain) is unclear. The available earnings call in the sample window (Q2 FY25 call dated November 6, 2024) does not discuss crypto, so the classification relies on filings and company media for the blockchain aspect. ([3i-infotech.com](https://www.3i-infotech.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/Q2FY25-Earnings-Call-Transcript.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Nov 6, 2024 (Q2 FY25), company transcript: “we are expanding into high growth areas such as healthcare, manufacturing and telecommunications.” [3i Infotech Q2 FY25 Earnings Call Transcript (PDF)](https://www.3i-infotech.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/Q2FY25-Earnings-Call-Transcript.pdf) ([3i‑infotech.com](https://www.3i-infotech.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/Q2FY25-Earnings-Call-Transcript.pdf))

**Filings (10‑K / press releases):**
- FY 2023–24 Annual Report: “blockchain/NFT solutions” listed under “Future Technologies” offerings. Also discloses: “The company has not traded or invested in Crypto currency or Virtual Currency.” [Annual Report 2023–24 (PDF)](https://www.3i-infotech.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/3i-Infotech_AR_2023-24.pdf) ([3i‑infotech.com](https://www.3i-infotech.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/3i-Infotech_AR_2023-24.pdf))  
- FY 2022–23 Annual Report: “blockchain/NFT solutions” included in products and services; crypto/virtual currency non‑investment disclosure repeated. [Annual Report 2022–23 (PDF)](https://www.3i-infotech.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/3i-Infotech_2023_AR_Cover-to-cover_V4_Hyperlinked.pdf) ([3i‑infotech.com](https://www.3i-infotech.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/3i-Infotech_2023_AR_Cover-to-cover_V4_Hyperlinked.pdf))

* Primary sources: Interim results earnings call (Q2 2023), Annual/Interim results press releases (2022–2023), Investor relations Results and Presentations library

### Firm Overview
Ping An Insurance (Group) Company of China, Ltd. is a China-based financial services conglomerate listed in Shanghai and Hong Kong, with core businesses spanning life and property insurance, banking, and asset management. Public investor materials during 2017–2024 emphasize insurance-led operations and technology-enabled services across the group.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across earnings communications and filings during 2017–2024, there are no disclosures of cryptocurrency holdings, mining, crypto custody/trading, or crypto investment products. Management commentary in results calls focuses on insurance, banking, dividends, and technology enablement rather than digital-asset activities. Ping An’s affiliate OneConnect is frequently cited in media for enterprise blockchain projects (e.g., trade finance and logistics), which are permissioned blockchain applications for financial institutions, not crypto-asset services and therefore do not constitute crypto exposure under the project’s definitions. No evidence indicates that the group or its key subsidiaries offered crypto-financial services or maintained treasury crypto assets in the sample window. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/PNGAY/transcripts))

### Weight Assignment
No substantive crypto exposure was identified for 2017–2024. Consistent with the decision rules, all mechanism categories are set to 0.0 and unclass to 1.0, reflecting the absence of credible links between Ping An’s equity value and cryptocurrency developments in transcripts, filings, or news during the sample period. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/PNGAY/transcripts))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
References to OneConnect’s blockchain initiatives can be misconstrued as crypto exposure; however, these are enterprise/permissioned blockchain solutions for banks and ports rather than crypto-asset services. Seeking Alpha hosts multiple Ping An results-call transcripts across the period, but direct access may require a subscription; at least one accessible transcript (Q2 2023) was reviewed for this classification. None identified beyond this. ([coindesk.com](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2018/12/03/ping-ans-new-pitch-for-blockchain-shared-ledger-but-banks-keep-clients?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Aug 31, 2023 (Interim results), transcript: “Our goal is to become a world-leading integrated financial and healthcare company, and we will promote our development with technological empowerment.” — Q&A/management remarks. ## CHINA FINANCE ONLINE ADR 1:50

* Rank: 479 | Expo: 0.07 | id_str: US1693792038
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: 20-F filings (2018–2020), Q1 2018 earnings call, results press releases and delisting notice (2018–2022)

### Firm Overview
China Finance Online Co., Ltd. is a China-based online financial services and media company that operated retail securities brokerage, wealth-management products, and financial data/analytics, alongside the financial portal JRJ. It was listed in the U.S. via American depositary shares during the period before being delisted in January 2022. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1297830/000156459019013204/jrjc-20f_20181231.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across earnings communications (2017–2020) and SEC filings (2018–2020), there is no evidence that the company held cryptocurrencies, mined digital assets, or provided crypto-financial services such as crypto trading, custody, or ETPs. Management commentary and product descriptions focus on securities brokerage, robo-advisory (Lingxi) using mutual funds, financial information subscriptions, and advertising. Given China’s regulatory stance that effectively banned domestic crypto exchange trading from September 2017, the firm’s retail brokerage and fintech offerings remained in traditional financial instruments. Editorial coverage of crypto topics on JRJ’s news pages, if any, does not constitute crypto exposure under this project’s definitions. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/JRJCY/transcripts/2018-year/1-quarter))

### Weight Assignment
No substantive crypto exposure channel was identified for 2017–2024. Filings and calls describe conventional brokerage, data, and advisory businesses without digital-asset operations or balance-sheet holdings. Accordingly, all mechanism categories are set to 0.0 and unclass to 1.0. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/c/NASDAQ_JRJC_2019.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Earnings call access is via third‑party aggregators; only a subset of 2017–2020 calls was reviewed, but none indicated crypto activity. ([roic.ai](https://www.roic.ai/quote/JRJCY/transcripts/2018-year/1-quarter))  
- The firm’s financial news portal may have reported on cryptocurrencies editorially; such content is excluded as it does not entail custody, issuance, or trading by the company. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/c/NASDAQ_JRJC_2019.pdf))  
- The company was delisted in January 2022, limiting later disclosures within the sample window. ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/china-finance-online-co-ltd-receives-notice-of-delisting-from-nasdaq-301464954.html?utm_source=openai))

## HALL OF FAME RESORT ENTERTAINMENT

* Rank: 480 | Expo: 0.07 | id_str: US40619L2016
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 10-K/annual report materials (2021–2022), Q2 2021 and Q4 2022 earnings calls, corporate press releases (Mar–May 2021), financial news (Mar 2021)

### Firm Overview
Hall of Fame Resort & Entertainment Company is a U.S.-based resort, entertainment and media business centered on the Hall of Fame Village in Canton, Ohio, leveraging partnerships connected to professional football. Within the 2017–2024 window it expanded into digital collectibles/NFTs as part of its media and fan-engagement initiatives. ([2021annualreport.hofreco.com](https://2021annualreport.hofreco.com/))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management launched NFT initiatives in 2021 via a partnership with Dolphin Entertainment, debuting athlete-themed NFTs on OpenSea and later listing collections tied to Enshrinement Week on FTX. In 2022 the company introduced a “Hall of Fame Village Pass” NFT that functions as a digital collectible with access/entitlements for fans. There is no evidence of crypto mining, crypto treasury holdings, or provision of mining hardware or energy infrastructure. Stock price action in March 2021 spiked on the NFT announcement, suggesting sensitivity to crypto/NFT sentiment among parts of the investor base. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20210323006052/en/Hall-of-Fame-Resort-Entertainment-Company-Signs-Partnership-with-Dolphin-Entertainment?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 reflects that the company directly created and sold digital-asset collectibles (NFTs) to consumers, including a membership-style “Village Pass,” and listed collections on established NFT marketplaces. No credible evidence indicates crypto balance‑sheet holdings (holders), mining operations (miners), crypto-adjacent professional services without asset handling (bizserv), or infrastructure aimed at mining/blockchain workloads (infrastr). Given the repeated, explicit management commentary and disclosures, all crypto exposure identified flows through the issuance and monetization of NFTs, which is categorized here as crypto financial services. ([earningscall.biz](https://earningscall.biz/e/nasdaq/s/hofv/y/2021/q/q2))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- NFT activity appears ancillary to the core resort/entertainment business and may be episodic; granular revenue attribution is not broken out in filings reviewed. ([2022annualreport.hofreco.com](https://2022annualreport.hofreco.com/assets/Uploads/2022-HOFREco-10-K.pdf))  
- Some transcripts are accessed via third‑party aggregators; however, the key NFT references are explicit and corroborated by company materials and press releases. ([earningscall.biz](https://earningscall.biz/e/nasdaq/s/hofv/y/2021/q/q2))  
- The company used third‑party marketplaces (OpenSea/FTX) for issuance and distribution, limiting direct custody/marketplace operations. ([2021annualreport.hofreco.com](https://2021annualreport.hofreco.com/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- August 13, 2021 (Q2 2021): “We… launched our next round of NFTs… partnered with Dolphin and… listed on FTX… well received.” — CEO remarks. [EarningsCall.biz](https://earningscall.biz/e/nasdaq/s/hofv/y/2021/q/q2) ([earningscall.biz](https://earningscall.biz/e/nasdaq/s/hofv/y/2021/q/q2))  
- March 28, 2023 (Q4 2022): “I use the Hall of Fame Village pass, the NFT, the digital collectible that comes with entitlements…” — CEO remarks. [Insider Monkey transcript](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/hall-of-fame-resort-entertainment-company-nasdaqhofv-q4-2022-earnings-call-transcript-1136144/) ([insidermonkey.com](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/hall-of-fame-resort-entertainment-company-nasdaqhofv-q4-2022-earnings-call-transcript-1136144/))

* Primary sources: results call CEO scripts (2020–2021); media releases (May 2023 Kaiko partnership; Apr 2021 blockchain DDO); 2024 annual report

### Firm Overview
Iress Limited is an Australia‑based, ASX‑listed financial technology company that provides trading, market data and wealth management software to financial institutions across APAC, the UK/Europe, North America and Africa. Within 2017–2024, its crypto‑adjacent activity consisted of delivering digital asset market data to clients and deploying a blockchain‑based solution for Australian financial product compliance. ([marketindex.com.au](https://www.marketindex.com.au/asx/ire))

### Exposure Channels Identified
In May 2023, Iress announced a partnership with Kaiko to distribute institutional‑grade digital asset and cryptocurrency market data to its clients via Iress data feeds, enabling back‑testing, benchmarking and surveillance use cases. This is a non‑custodial, data‑delivery integration; Iress does not issue crypto products, custody assets, or intermediate client trading. Separately, in April 2021 Iress disclosed a blockchain‑based regtech solution to support Design and Distribution Obligations (DDO) compliance, again without taking balance‑sheet exposure to crypto. No evidence was found of mining operations, crypto treasury holdings or mining‑focused hardware/energy provisioning. ([iress.com](https://www.iress.com/support/media-centre-old/2023/05/iress-partners-with-kaiko-to-offer-digital-assets-data/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Bizserv = 1.0 reflects that Iress’ documented linkage to digital assets is through software and data services (market data distribution via Kaiko; blockchain workflow for DDO), which fit the non‑financial services category because Iress does not handle, custody or issue digital assets. There is no support for holders, miners, finserv, or infrastr exposures over 2017–2024 based on calls, filings and company communications reviewed. ([iress.com](https://www.iress.com/support/media-centre-old/2023/05/iress-partners-with-kaiko-to-offer-digital-assets-data/?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Earnings call scripts reviewed did not reference crypto/digital assets explicitly; classification relies on press releases and product materials. ([iress.com](https://www.iress.com/media/documents/2020_Results_Conference_Call_-_CEO_script.pdf))  
- Revenue attributable to digital‑asset data is not broken out in annual reports; materiality appears negligible relative to total revenue. ([iress.com](https://www.iress.com/media/documents/IRESS_Annual_Report_2024.pdf?utm_source=openai))  
- Third‑party corroboration of the Kaiko partnership is limited; Kaiko’s own partner listing corroborates Iress as a partner. ([kaiko.com](https://www.kaiko.com/about-kaiko/partnerships?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

**Earnings calls:**
- Feb 18, 2021 (FY2020 results CEO script): “We expect Segment Profit in 2021 to increase by between 7%–10%.” — CEO remarks. [Iress](https://www.iress.com/media/documents/2020_Results_Conference_Call_-_CEO_script.pdf) ([iress.com](https://www.iress.com/media/documents/2020_Results_Conference_Call_-_CEO_script.pdf))  
- Feb 17, 2022 (FY2021 results CEO script): “We delivered on full year segment profit and net profit guidance for FY21.” — CEO remarks. * Primary sources: earnings call transcripts (Q3 2020, Q4 2021, Q2 2022); 2022 Form 10-K (filed Mar 1, 2023); FY2023 results press release (Feb 29, 2024)

### Firm Overview
Repay Holdings Corporation is a U.S.-based payments technology company that provides integrated electronic payment processing across verticals such as consumer lending, automotive finance, mortgage servicing, healthcare and B2B accounts payable/receivable. Its platform centers on card, ACH and virtual card solutions delivered through software integrations and proprietary tools. ([repay.com](https://repay.com/?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024 materials reviewed, management discussions and filings focus on card and ACH acceptance, virtual card issuance and B2B AP/AR automation; no crypto products, custody, exchange services, mining, or balance‑sheet digital asset holdings are disclosed. Earnings calls consistently describe growth from software integrations and virtual card/ACH payment volumes, not from digital assets. A 2021 board appointment referenced a director’s distributed‑ledger background at an external firm, but no associated blockchain or crypto product at REPAY is indicated. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/08/10/repay-holdings-corporation-rpay-q2-2022-earnings-c/))

### Weight Assignment
Unclass = 1.0 is assigned because there is no substantive, evidenced linkage to any of the six crypto‑exposure mechanisms during 2017–2024. Transcripts and the Form 10‑K emphasize traditional electronic payment modalities (card/ACH/virtual card) and software integrations without touching digital assets; therefore holders, miners, finserv, bizserv or infrastr categories are not supported. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/03/01/repay-holdings-corporation-rpay-q4-2021-earnings-c/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- A December 2021 press release highlights a director with distributed‑ledger expertise; this signals awareness but does not evidence operational crypto exposure. ([repay.com](https://repay.com/repay-appoints-emnet-rios-to-board-of-directors/?utm_source=openai))  
- No explicit references to cryptocurrency, blockchain, or digital assets were identified in the 2022 Form 10‑K; materiality of any crypto activity, if present, appears negligible. ([stocklight.com](https://stocklight.com/stocks/us/nasdaq-rpay/repay-holdings/annual-reports/nasdaq-rpay-2023-10K-23693971.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**

- Mar 1, 2022 (Q4 2021), Motley Fool: “BNPL … relying on payment processors like us and Payix to facilitate the processing of these card transactions.” [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/03/01/repay-holdings-corporation-rpay-q4-2021-earnings-c/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/03/01/repay-holdings-corporation-rpay-q4-2021-earnings-c/))  
- Aug 10, 2022 (Q2 2022), Motley Fool: “Secular trends toward frictionless digital payments … will continue to be a tailwind that will drive our business.” [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/08/10/repay-holdings-corporation-rpay-q2-2022-earnings-c/) ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2022/08/10/repay-holdings-corporation-rpay-q2-2022-earnings-c/))

### Firm Overview
Y-mAbs Therapeutics, Inc. is a U.S.-based commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing and commercializing antibody-based therapeutics for cancer; its FDA‑approved product is DANYELZA (naxitamab). Filings and calls emphasize oncology programs (e.g., DANYELZA, omburtamab) and the SADA pretargeted radioimmunotherapy platform. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1722964/000155837023005073/ymab-20221231x10k.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across 2017–2024 transcripts and filings, the company discusses oncology drug development, regulatory milestones, and commercial execution for DANYELZA; there is no indication of cryptocurrency holdings, mining, custody, exchange/brokerage services, or blockchain initiatives. Calls focus on clinical programs and DANYELZA sales momentum, not digital assets. Recent 10‑K disclosures similarly describe a cancer‑therapeutics business with no crypto products or balance‑sheet digital assets noted. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/05/09/ymabs-therapeutics-inc-ymab-q1-2020-earnings-call.aspx))

### Weight Assignment
Unclass = 1.0 is assigned because no substantive linkage to any crypto exposure mechanism was found in transcripts, filings, or press releases during the 2017–2024 period. There is no evidence of treasury crypto holdings, mining activity, crypto financial services, crypto‑specific business services, or targeted infrastructure supply; therefore all mechanism weights other than unclass are set to 0.0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1722964/000155837023005073/ymab-20221231x10k.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified.

### Evidence

**Earnings calls:**
- May 9, 2020 (Q1 2020), Motley Fool: “We are very much on track to…launch both naxitamab and omburtamab…[and] entered into…SADA…a radioimmunotherapy platform.” [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/05/09/ymabs-therapeutics-inc-ymab-q1-2020-earnings-call.aspx) (fool.com)  
- Aug 7, 2020 (Q2 2020), Motley Fool: “We…completed the BLA submission [for omburtamab]…[and] are extremely excited about our SADA technology.” [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/08/07/ymabs-therapeutics-inc-ymab-q2-2020-earnings-call.aspx) (fool.com)  
- Nov 6, 2020 (Q3 2020), Motley Fool: “We are very close to a potential marketing authorization for naxitamab…[with] our 10‑person sales organization…ready to go.” [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/11/06/ymabs-therapeutics-inc-ymab-q3-2020-earnings-call/) (fool.com)  
- Nov 2023 (Q3 2023), “$20 million in net product sales of DANYELZA, up 59% YoY.” [Insider Monkey transcript](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/y-mabs-therapeutics-inc-nasdaqymab-q3-2023-earnings-call-transcript-1223804/) (insidermonkey.com)

### Firm Overview
Kingsoft Corporation Limited is a China-based software and internet company listed in Hong Kong. Its operations center on office productivity software (WPS Office via Kingsoft Office Group) and online games (via Seasun), with recent strategy emphasizing AI-enabled office products and continued development of its JX game franchises. ([ir.kingsoft.com](https://ir.kingsoft.com/system/files-encrypted/nasdaq_kms/assets/2024/04/19/18-21-22/1.2023%20ANNUAL%20REPORT.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across filings and results materials for 2017–2024, there is no indication that Kingsoft holds cryptocurrency as treasury assets, mines digital assets, operates an exchange/custody platform, or offers crypto-backed financial products. Management commentary in earnings calls and results releases focuses on office software subscriptions, AI features (WPS AI), and online game performance, not crypto assets or blockchain products. The 2023 Annual Report and 2024 Interim Report detail revenue by office software and online games without crypto-related business lines or balance sheet digital assets. Recent quarterly announcements similarly report operating and segment metrics with no crypto references. ([ir.kingsoft.com](https://ir.kingsoft.com/system/files-encrypted/nasdaq_kms/assets/2024/04/19/18-21-22/1.2023%20ANNUAL%20REPORT.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Unclass = 1.0 is assigned because no substantive linkage to any of the six crypto-exposure mechanisms is evidenced in 2017–2024 transcripts, filings, or financial news. There is no support for holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr classifications; therefore those weights are set to 0.0. ([ir.kingsoft.com](https://ir.kingsoft.com/system/files-encrypted/nasdaq_kms/assets/2024/04/19/18-21-22/1.2023%20ANNUAL%20REPORT.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Potential name confusion with Kingsoft Cloud Holdings (separately listed) was addressed by verifying the ISIN KYG5264Y1089 and Hong Kong–listed parent entity. Terminology such as “digital asset management” appears in the context of enterprise content management within WPS, not cryptocurrencies. No transcript quotes indicating crypto activities were found for the parent; focus remained on AI office and gaming. ([marketscreener.com](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/KINGSOFT-CORPORATION-LIMI-6171050/news-call-transcripts/))

## MATTEL

* Rank: 485 | Expo: 0.07 | id_str: US5770811025
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 3
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: earnings calls (Q3 2022, Q4 2023); SEC Form 10‑K (FY2023); company marketplace/FAQ pages (2022–2024); press coverage of marketplace launch and partnerships (2022)

### Firm Overview
Mattel, Inc. is a U.S.-based global toy and entertainment company known for brands such as Barbie, Hot Wheels, Fisher-Price, American Girl and Masters of the Universe. During 2022–2024 the company expanded its digital initiatives for collectors, including launching a branded digital collectibles marketplace tied to its toy IP.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management explicitly discussed NFTs on the Q3 2022 earnings call, noting a Barbie collaboration for 15,000 one‑of‑a‑kind NFTs to debut on Mattel Creations. ([tickertrends.io](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/MAT/Q3-earnings-transcript-2022))  
In November 2022 Mattel launched the Mattel Creations Digital Collectibles Marketplace on the Flow blockchain; coverage emphasized that purchases could be made without owning cryptocurrency, while marketplace trading uses Flow tokens. ([ledgerinsights.com](https://www.ledgerinsights.com/mattel-toy-nft-marketplace-for-barbie-hot-wheels/?utm_source=openai))  
Company FAQs and marketplace guidance show Mattel enabling custody and peer‑to‑peer trading of its branded digital collectibles, with wallets created for users and transactions conducted in Flow or via fiat on‑ramps. ([creations.mattel.com](https://creations.mattel.com/pages/virtual-collectibles-frequently-asked-questions?utm_source=openai))  
Earnings commentary in 2023–2024 referenced growth of Mattel Creations as a direct‑to‑consumer channel for adult fans, reinforcing that the marketplace is part of its D2C strategy; there was no indication of treasury crypto holdings or mining activities. ([s21.q4cdn.com](https://s21.q4cdn.com/605014885/files/doc_financials/2023/q4/MAT-USQ_Transcript_2024-02-07.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Finserv = 1.0 is assigned because Mattel directly operates a digital collectibles marketplace that facilitates buying, selling, trading and custody of blockchain‑based assets (NFTs) tied to its brands, and supports crypto (Flow) transactions alongside fiat. There is no evidence of balance‑sheet crypto investments (holders), mining operations (miners), infrastructure provision (infrastr), or non‑financial support services to third‑party crypto firms (bizserv). Materiality appears minor relative to total company revenue, so RevShare = 1 and AssetShare = 0. ([creations.mattel.com](https://creations.mattel.com/en-gb/pages/virtual-marketplace-frequently-asked-questions?utm_source=openai))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Mattel’s marketplace activity centers on its own IP and collector community rather than broad, third‑party asset trading, and the company does not break out NFT/marketplace revenue as a separate line in filings, limiting precise calibration of materiality. The earnings‑call references around 2022–2024 confirm NFT initiatives and D2C strategy but do not quantify financial impact; filings reviewed did not disclose treasury crypto holdings. ([tickertrends.io](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/MAT/Q3-earnings-transcript-2022))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 25, 2022 (Q3 2022): “In the growing space of NFTs, we announced a digital art collaboration for Barbie with women’s empowerment brand Boss Beauties for 15,000 one‑of‑a‑kind NFTs to debut later this year on Mattel Creations.” [TickerTrends](https://tickertrends.io/transcripts/MAT/Q3-earnings-transcript-2022)  
- Feb 7, 2024 (Q4 2023): “Mattel Creations, our D2C business serving adult fans, continue to grow, with user traffic up over 90%.” [Refinitiv transcript, Mattel IR](https://s21.q4cdn.com/605014885/files/doc_financials/2023/q4/MAT-USQ_Transcript_2024-02-07.pdf)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Mar 15, 2024: 2023 Form 10‑K — business overview and segment reporting; no disclosure of treasury crypto holdings. [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/63276/000162828024016584/mattel2023annualreportfinal.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Nov 23, 2022: Mattel launches Mattel Creations Digital Collectibles Marketplace on Flow; purchases can be made without owning crypto. [Ledger Insights](https://www.ledgerinsights.com/mattel-toy-nft-marketplace-for-barbie-hot-wheels/)  
- Nov 22, 2022: Launch details and Flow blockchain noted for the marketplace. [The Pop Insider](https://thepopinsider.com/mattel-creations-digital-collectibles-marketplace/)  
- Jun 24, 2022: Mattel participated in Cryptoys’ $23M raise for a digital toy/NFT platform, underscoring broader NFT engagement. [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2022/06/24/digital-toy-platform-cryptoys-raises-23m-from-a16z-dapper-labs-mattel)  
- Marketplace/FAQ: Mattel wallet creation, P2P trading, and Flow token usage for the Marketplace. [Mattel Creations FAQ](https://creations.mattel.com/en-gb/pages/virtual-marketplace-frequently-asked-questions) | [Virtual Collectibles FAQ](https://creations.mattel.com/pages/virtual-collectibles-frequently-asked-questions)

</small>

* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: SEC Form 10‑K (FY2024); earnings call transcript (Q1 2021)

### Firm Overview
FB Financial Corporation is a U.S. bank holding company headquartered in Nashville, Tennessee, operating FirstBank across Tennessee, Alabama, Kentucky and North Georgia with a community and commercial banking focus. The company offers traditional banking and mortgage services through its branch and loan production network. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1649749/000164974925000100/fbfinancialcorp_annualre.htm))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Company filings explicitly state that FB Financial does not currently hold crypto assets or facilitate transactions with them, and that it is only evaluating the accounting guidance for potential future applicability. There is no evidence of crypto custody, exchange/brokerage, mining, or treasury investment activity. Risk disclosures mention technology partnerships may include blockchain-based products, but this is framed as a general innovation area rather than an implemented or revenue‑generating crypto service. Across available 2017–2024 earnings materials, management discussions focus on core banking performance and technology investment without indicating digital‑asset activities. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1649749/000164974925000100/fbfinancialcorp_annualre.htm))

### Weight Assignment
Unclass = 1.0 is assigned because filings affirmatively indicate no current crypto holdings or facilitation of crypto transactions, and there is no credible evidence of mining, financial‑services handling of digital assets, infrastructure provision to the crypto ecosystem, or treasury “holder” behavior. Any blockchain references are prospective and generic to technology strategy rather than documented crypto exposure during 2017–2024. Consequently, all other mechanism weights are 0.0. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1649749/000164974925000100/fbfinancialcorp_annualre.htm))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Filings note potential future offerings that could include blockchain‑based products, but there is no disclosure of execution or related revenues during the sample period. No transcripts located for 2017–2024 contained substantive crypto discussion; classification therefore relies primarily on filings. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1649749/000164974925000100/fbfinancialcorp_annualre.htm))

**Earnings calls:**

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**

## FTI CONSULTING

* Rank: 487 | Expo: 0.07 | id_str: US3029411093
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: 2023 Form 10‑K; Q1 2023 earnings call; FTI Technology case studies (FTX and crypto bankruptcy); 2023 press release on Blockchain & Digital Assets practice

### Firm Overview
FTI Consulting Inc. is a U.S.-based global business advisory firm listed on the NYSE (ticker: FCN) with five operating segments: Corporate Finance & Restructuring, Forensic and Litigation Consulting, Economic Consulting, Technology, and Strategic Communications. Within its Technology segment, the company offers blockchain advisory, cryptocurrency disputes and investigations, and digital‑asset advisory services as part of its broader e‑discovery and digital risk portfolio. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/f/NYSE_FCN_2023.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management has explicitly discussed crypto‑related demand across multiple practices, noting opportunities tied to crypto bankruptcies and litigation, with Technology involved in e‑discovery and investigations and other segments providing disputes and expert‑witness work. Public filings list “Blockchain Advisory Services,” “Cryptocurrency Disputes and Investigations,” and “Digital Asset Advisory Services” among the Technology segment’s offerings, and identify “Blockchain & Digital Assets” as an industry specialization. FTI Technology case studies document engagements such as advising the FTX Unsecured Creditors’ Committee and a distressed crypto exchange on bankruptcy, recovery and digital‑asset tracing. There is no evidence that FTI operates a crypto exchange, provides custody, mines crypto, or holds crypto on its balance sheet. ([insidermonkey.com](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/fti-consulting-inc-nysefcn-q1-2023-earnings-call-transcript-1143902/))

### Weight Assignment
The firm’s role is advisory and investigative around the crypto ecosystem (forensics, e‑discovery, restructuring, expert testimony, and blockchain analytics) rather than direct financial intermediation, mining, or treasury investment. Accordingly, 100% of exposure is assigned to bizserv, reflecting non‑financial professional services delivered to crypto‑related clients and matters. Other mechanism categories are set to 0.0 because filings and case materials show no custody/exchange products, mining operations, or crypto asset holdings. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/f/NYSE_FCN_2023.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
Filings and transcripts during 2017–2024 do not disclose a separate revenue split for crypto‑related work within Technology; available evidence indicates the activity is present but likely a small share of firmwide revenues. No balance‑sheet crypto assets are disclosed. ([annualreports.com](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/f/NYSE_FCN_2023.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Apr 27, 2023 (Q1 2023): “There’s been bankruptcies in crypto… Technology can get involved in e‑discovery. You can end up with investigations… disputes and expert witnesses.” [Insider Monkey](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/fti-consulting-inc-nysefcn-q1-2023-earnings-call-transcript-1143902/)

**Filings (10‑K / investor materials):**
- 2023 Form 10‑K/Annual Report: Technology segment offerings include “Blockchain Advisory Services,” “Cryptocurrency Disputes and Investigations,” and “Digital Asset Advisory Services”; industry specializations include “Blockchain & Digital Assets.” [AnnualReports.com (PDF)](https://www.annualreports.com/HostedData/AnnualReportArchive/f/NYSE_FCN_2023.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Case study: FTX Unsecured Creditors’ Committee retained FTI Technology’s Blockchain & Digital Assets practice; scope included custody solution evaluation, token monetization/risk analysis, and on‑chain tracing. [FTI Technology](https://www.ftitechnology.com/resources/case-studies/ftx-bankruptcy-and-technical-diligence)  
- Case study: Distressed cryptocurrency exchange engaged FTI for bankruptcy/restructuring advice, digital‑asset tracing, staking analyses and testimony. [FTI Technology](https://www.ftitechnology.com/resources/case-studies/distressed-cryptocurrency-exchange-selects-fti-consulting-for-bankruptcy-and)  
- Case study: Tracing unhosted wallets via tokenized notices/NFTs to support asset recovery in large crypto bankruptcy litigation. [FTI Technology](https://www.ftitechnology.com/resources/case-studies/fti-technologys-innovative-solution-for-tracing-unhosted-wallets-supports)  
- Press release: “FTI Consulting Announces Further Growth of Blockchain and Digital Assets Practice,” detailing hires and client demand. [FTI Consulting](https://www.fticonsulting.com/about/newsroom/press-releases/fti-consulting-announces-further-growth-of-blockchain-and-digital-assets-practice)

</small>

### Firm Overview
Sudarshan Chemical Industries Ltd is an India-based specialty chemicals company focused on pigments and color solutions for coatings, plastics, inks, and cosmetics, with global sales and manufacturing in Maharashtra. Investor materials position the company as one of India’s largest pigment producers with exports to 85+ countries. ([sudarshan.com](https://www.sudarshan.com/annual-report-2023-24.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across multiple earnings call transcripts and investor materials, management discusses pigment segment performance, manufacturing footprint, export mix, and margins; there is no mention of cryptocurrency activities, mining, custody, exchange operations, or blockchain services. The FY2023–24 Annual Report explicitly states that the Company “has not traded or invested in crypto currency or virtual currency” for FY2023 and FY2024; the consolidated report repeats the same disclosure for the Group. No financial news in 2017–2024 links the firm to crypto; coverage instead focuses on its October 2024 definitive agreement to acquire Heubach’s global pigment business. ([sudarshan.com](https://www.sudarshan.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/transcript-of-earnings-conference-call-quarter-and-year-ended-31st-march-2024.pdf))

### Weight Assignment
Given explicit filings that the Company and Group did not trade or invest in cryptocurrencies and the absence of any crypto-related products or services in transcripts or investor materials, the firm is classified as unclassified for crypto exposure. All mechanism categories are set to 0.0 with unclass at 1.0, reflecting no substantive crypto channel through which digital-asset developments would impact the equity. ([sudarshan.com](https://www.sudarshan.com/annual-report-2023-24.pdf))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
None identified within the January 2017–December 2024 sample; disclosures and transcripts reviewed are consistent in showing no crypto involvement. ([sudarshan.com](https://www.sudarshan.com/annual-report-2023-24.pdf))

## ICON

* Rank: 489 | Expo: 0.07 | id_str: IE0005711209
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Form 20-F filings (2019, 2023); Q3 2020 and Q2 2021 earnings calls; July 2021 acquisition press release

### Firm Overview
ICON plc is an Ireland-based contract research organization that provides outsourced clinical development and related services to biopharma and medical device companies; its SEC filings describe ICON as a clinical research organization founded in Dublin in 1990. The company expanded materially with its July 1, 2021 acquisition of PRA Health Sciences, creating a larger healthcare intelligence and clinical research group. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1060955/000106095520000004/iconplc20-f2019.htm?utm_source=openai))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Across multiple earnings call transcripts (e.g., 2020–2021) and annual filings through 2023, management discusses backlog, book‑to‑bill, trial execution and integration of PRA; there is no indication of cryptocurrency holdings, mining, exchange/custody activities, or products for crypto clients. The business model centers on CRO services (clinical trials, data/analytics, labs) for life‑sciences sponsors, not crypto‑asset markets. One 2021 call references “tokenized” patient populations in the context of de‑identifying and linking healthcare data—this describes data‑privacy tokenization, not blockchain/crypto usage or exposure. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/10/22/icon-plc-iclr-q3-2020-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Weight Assignment
Given the absence of any substantive crypto activity in earnings calls, annual filings, or reputable news during the 2017–2024 sample period, ICON is classified as unclassified for crypto exposure. All six mechanism categories are set to 0.0 with unclass at 1.0, and the investor‑base amplifier is 0 because there is no evidence the stock trades as a crypto proxy or that management highlights crypto‑focused shareholders. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/10/22/icon-plc-iclr-q3-2020-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
“Tokenization” mentioned by management in 2021 refers to de‑identified patient tokens used for linking real‑world data, not blockchain assets; this does not constitute crypto exposure under the project definitions. None otherwise identified within January 2017–December 2024. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/07/22/icon-plc-iclr-q2-2021-earnings-call-transcript/))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 22, 2020 (Q3 2020): “enhance our position as the CRO partner of choice.” [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2020/10/22/icon-plc-iclr-q3-2020-earnings-call-transcript/)  
- Jul 22, 2021 (Q2 2021): “On a stand‑alone basis during the quarter, ICON increased net business wins to a record $1.1 billion… our backlog grew to a record $10.2 billion.” [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/07/22/icon-plc-iclr-q2-2021-earnings-call-transcript/)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Form 20‑F (FY2019): “ICON public limited company (‘ICON plc’) is a clinical research organization (‘CRO’), founded in Dublin, Ireland in 1990.” [SEC EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1060955/000106095520000004/iconplc20-f2019.htm)  
- Form 20‑F (FY2023): Annual report describing CRO business and risk factors; no crypto activities disclosed. [SEC EDGAR (PDF)](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1060955/000106095524000055/icon_plcx20-fx2023xrv2xao.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Jul 1, 2021: “ICON plc… announced the completion of its acquisition of PRA Health Sciences.” [ICON press release (PDF)](https://investor.iconplc.com/node/15406/pdf)  
- Jul 2, 2021: “ICON closes $12bn acquisition of PRA Health Sciences.” [Clinical Trials Arena](https://www.clinicaltrialsarena.com/news/icon-buys-pra-health-sciences/)

</small>

## STARBUCKS

* Rank: 490 | Expo: 0.07 | id_str: US8552441094
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: NA
* RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
* Primary sources: Form 10-K (FY2023); Q4 FY2021 earnings call; trade/crypto press on Bakkt payment reloads and Odyssey program

### Firm Overview
Starbucks Corporation is a U.S.-based global coffee retailer and roaster operating company‑owned and licensed stores worldwide, with additional channel development through consumer packaged goods and ready‑to‑drink partnerships. Its FY2023 Form 10‑K outlines core activities in retail beverages/food and loyalty and stored‑value programs; it does not disclose cryptocurrency holdings or digital‑asset financial services. ([investor.starbucks.com](https://investor.starbucks.com/files/doc_financials/2023/q4/q423-10k.pdf))

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management discussed a payment partnership with PayPal and Bakkt in Q4 FY2021, noting that customers could “reload their Starbucks Card with a range of cryptocurrencies … by converting digital currencies to physical currency,” i.e., fiat before spend in the Starbucks app. This indicates Starbucks itself does not take custody of crypto or process on‑chain payments. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/28/starbucks-corporation-sbux-q4-2021-earnings-call-t/))  
Trade coverage at the time similarly framed the arrangement as allowing customers to convert bitcoin to U.S. dollars to reload Starbucks Cards rather than Starbucks accepting crypto directly at point of sale. ([nrn.com](https://www.nrn.com/quick-service/consumers-can-now-convert-bitcoin-or-frequent-flier-miles-to-cash-and-reload-their-starbucks-cards-to-pay-for-lattes?utm_source=openai))  
Separately, Starbucks piloted a Web3/NFT loyalty extension (“Starbucks Odyssey”) in 2022–2024; it launched NFT “stamps” on Polygon in late 2022/early 2023 and subsequently ended the beta on March 31, 2024. These initiatives do not imply custody of crypto assets or provision of crypto financial services by Starbucks. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2022/12/08/starbucks-opens-up-its-blockchain-based-loyalty-program-and-nft-community-to-first-beta-testers/?utm_source=openai))

### Weight Assignment
Across earnings calls and filings in 2017–2024, there is no evidence Starbucks held crypto on its balance sheet, mined assets, offered exchange/custody, or supplied mining/compute infrastructure. The Bakkt arrangement routed customer crypto through conversion to fiat before loading to a Starbucks Card, and the Odyssey NFT pilot was a marketing/engagement program later discontinued, not a financial product or service touching digital assets. Therefore all mechanism weights are 0.0 with unclass at 1.0, and invbase remains 0 given no evidence the stock trades as a crypto proxy. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/28/starbucks-corporation-sbux-q4-2021-earnings-call-t/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Payment integrations via partners (e.g., Bakkt) could be misconstrued as “accepting bitcoin;” management explicitly described conversion to fiat before card reloads. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/28/starbucks-corporation-sbux-q4-2021-earnings-call-t/))  
- Starbucks’ NFT/Web3 “Odyssey” beta (Dec 2022–Mar 2024) involved digital collectibles for loyalty but no disclosed crypto custody or financial‑service activities; the beta was sunset March 31, 2024. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2022/12/08/starbucks-opens-up-its-blockchain-based-loyalty-program-and-nft-community-to-first-beta-testers/?utm_source=openai))  
- A keyword search of the FY2023 Form 10‑K for “bitcoin/crypto/digital asset/Bakkt” returns no results, supporting the absence of crypto‑balance‑sheet or financial‑service disclosures (inference based on filing text). ([investor.starbucks.com](https://investor.starbucks.com/files/doc_financials/2023/q4/q423-10k.pdf))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Oct 28, 2021 (Q4 FY2021): “reload their Starbucks card with a range of cryptocurrencies … by converting digital currencies to physical currency.” [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/10/28/starbucks-corporation-sbux-q4-2021-earnings-call-t/)  
- Oct 30, 2019 (Q4 FY2019): “the important strategic role that our digital flywheel plays in growing digital customer relationships.” [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2019/10/30/starbucks-corp-sbux-q4-2019-earnings-call-transcri.aspx)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- Nov 17, 2023: Annual Report on Form 10‑K (FY2023); business description and risk factors; no crypto activities disclosed. [Starbucks IR (PDF)](https://investor.starbucks.com/files/doc_financials/2023/q4/q423-10k.pdf)

**Financial news / other:**
- Mar 2021–Apr 2021: “Customers can convert bitcoin to cash and reload their Starbucks cards to pay for lattes.” [Nation’s Restaurant News](https://www.nrn.com/quick-service/consumers-can-now-convert-bitcoin-or-frequent-flier-miles-cash-and-reload-their-starbucks)  
- Feb 13, 2023: Bakkt to discontinue its consumer app on March 16, 2023 (partner context). [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2023/02/13/digital-asset-platform-bakkt-to-discontinue-consumer-app-after-two-years)  
- Dec 8, 2022: Starbucks opens Web3/NFT “Odyssey” beta on Polygon. [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2022/12/08/starbucks-opens-up-its-blockchain-based-loyalty-program-and-nft-community-to-first-beta-testers/)  
- Mar 16, 2024: Starbucks to end Odyssey beta on Mar 31, 2024. [Engadget](https://www.engadget.com/it-took-starbucks-a-little-too-long-to-realize-coffee-nfts-arent-it-170132305.html)

</small>

## NORTHERN TRUST

* Rank: 491 | Expo: 0.07 | id_str: US6658591044
* Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.9, bizserv 0.1, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
* Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
* Certainty: 4
* RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
* Primary sources: Q1 2021 earnings call; press releases on Zodia Custody (2020–2021); Broadridge blockchain platform transfer (2019); BondEvalue blockchain bond trade (2020); trade/financial media coverage (2021–2024)

### Firm Overview
Northern Trust Corporation is a U.S.-based financial services company specializing in asset servicing, wealth management, and asset management for institutional and high‑net‑worth clients. Its digital‑asset activities during 2017–2024 centered on institutional custody and related servicing innovations developed with partners.

### Exposure Channels Identified
Management discussed a partnership with Standard Chartered to provide institutional cryptoasset custody via a separate venture, Zodia Custody, highlighting Northern Trust’s role in bringing traditional custody standards to digital assets. On July 29, 2021, Zodia received FCA registration and began commercial services in the U.K., positioning the venture to custody bitcoin and ether for institutions. Northern Trust also worked on tokenization and private‑markets infrastructure, including transferring its private‑equity blockchain platform to Broadridge in 2019 and collaborating with BondEvalue on the first fractionalized blockchain‑based bond trade in 2020. There is no evidence the company mined cryptocurrencies or held crypto on its balance sheet; exposure occurs through institutional custody/servicing and blockchain initiatives. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/04/20/northern-trust-corp-ntrs-q1-2021-earnings-call-tra/))

### Weight Assignment
The dominant mechanism is crypto financial services: Northern Trust co‑developed and backs Zodia Custody, an institutional digital‑asset custodian that launched following FCA registration in 2021, and management described the initiative on the Q1 2021 earnings call. Secondary, smaller weight is assigned to non‑financial services (bizserv) reflecting blockchain/tokenization work (private‑equity administration with Broadridge and the BondEvalue collaboration). There is no basis for holders, miners, or infrastructure weights. Hence: finserv 0.9, bizserv 0.1, others 0.0. ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/04/20/northern-trust-corp-ntrs-q1-2021-earnings-call-tra/))

### Ambiguities / Limitations
- Zodia Custody operates as a separate venture; Northern Trust’s exact revenue contribution from digital assets is not disclosed, so crypto revenue/assets appear negligible at the consolidated level. ([sc.com](https://www.sc.com/en/press-release/standard-chartered-and-northern-trust-announce-zodia-custody-receives-fca-registration/))  
- Filings reviewed did not show crypto held on Northern Trust’s balance sheet during the period; disclosures emphasize digital innovation rather than proprietary holdings. ([sec.gov](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/73124/000007312423000049/ntrs-20221231.htm?utm_source=openai))  
- Broader market developments post‑2021 (e.g., Zodia funding/expansion) support ongoing involvement but do not change the mechanism mix. ([bloomberg.com](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2023-04-26/standard-chartered-s-crypto-custodian-zodia-raises-36-million-from-japan-s-sbi?utm_source=openai))

### Evidence

<small>

**Earnings calls:**
- Apr 20, 2021 (Q1 2021): “we’ve partnered with Standard Chartered in a venture called Zodia, which will provide that crypto custody… [and] tokenization… with BondEvalue; [and] developing a PE capability on blockchain… with Broadridge.” [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2021/04/20/northern-trust-corp-ntrs-q1-2021-earnings-call-tra/)

**Filings (10-K / press releases):**
- 2022 Form 10‑K: Business/risk factor context; no crypto holdings disclosed. [SEC](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/73124/000007312423000049/ntrs-20221231.htm)

**Financial news / other:**
- Dec 9, 2020: Standard Chartered and Northern Trust to launch U.K. crypto custody (Zodia). [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2020/12/09/standard-chartered-northern-trust-to-launch-crypto-custody-service-in-the-uk)
- Jul 29, 2021: Zodia Custody receives FCA registration; begins commercial services. [Standard Chartered press release](https://www.sc.com/en/press-release/standard-chartered-and-northern-trust-announce-zodia-custody-receives-fca-registration/)
- Sep 14, 2021: Zodia product tested on Pure Digital’s platform. [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2021/09/14/standard-chartered-and-northern-trusts-crypto-custody-tests-londons-pure-digital)
- Jun 26, 2019: Northern Trust transfers private‑equity blockchain platform to Broadridge. [Broadridge](https://www.broadridge.com/press-release/2019/northern-trust-transfers-pioneering-blockchain-platform-to-broadridge)
- Aug 11, 2020: Northern Trust and BondEvalue complete first fractionalized blockchain‑based bond trade. [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20200811005881/en/BondEvalue-and-Northern-Trust-Collaborate-to-Complete-Worlds-First-Blockchain-based-Bond-Trade)
- Apr 26, 2023: Zodia raises $36M; previously backed by Standard Chartered and Northern Trust. [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2023-04-26/standard-chartered-s-crypto-custodian-zodia-raises-36-million-from-japan-s-sbi)
- Jun 19, 2024: NAB Ventures invests in Zodia Custody (shareholder expansion). [CoinDesk](https://www.coindesk.com/business/2024/06/19/national-australia-banks-venture-arm-invests-in-crypto-focused-zodia-custody)

</small>
